text: Standard Vino Volo food, standard Vino Volo wine. My real turn off was the service. Nice enough employees but frankly inattentive. One poor mate was doing all the work.

Review is for the bar only...

A gorgeous, wood-paneled bar mixed with a cozy fireplace lounge amidst 5-star skyline views give XIX great potential, but the quality of cocktails unfortunately could not match the rest of the experience.  The drinks were overpriced and underwhelming.  Some were too watered down and certain key ingredients could not be tasted.

Come here for the atmosphere, not to drink and you'll leave happy.

This is my second time here and I must say that everything from the service to the food to the ambiance was OUTSTANDING. My first time here, my server was Justin and he did such an awesome job. This time, my server was Rachel, she was super sweet, very attentive and did an amazing job making sure our drinks didn't get low. I want to just take the time to shout out both Justin and Rachel for being great at what they do. It's rare to find an establishment where everyone is just friendly and attentive. I brought up Justin's name to Rachel and she must've told him, so when he got on shift, he actually stopped by our table to say hello. 
The ambiance was awesome, they had some speakers and a dj set up and he was definitely playing some good music, because we were vibing out. The outdoor dining had some tables with a sort of enclosed area that was warm because I didn't even need to wear my jacket. It definitely was enjoyable. 

Moving on to the food, everything was good. I ordered the morning tacos which were plenty and yummy. my brother ordered the chicken chilaquiles and it looked amazing, I didn't have enough room to try it but he enjoyed it (I'll get it next time). But the star of the show was the coconut French toast... idk what they do to it but it was so gooooood! My brother even ordered an other serving (extra charge) because he liked it so much. 

This place is definitely a hidden gem... food, service and ambiance A++++.

Subway is a Subway- the sammiches do in a pinch but in a city known for hoagies there's always a lot to be desired. I enjoyed my lunch, the kids liked their apples and milk that came with theirs. 

It's a tight squeeze but there is ample seating upstairs. They had one high chair but I had two babies with me. 

I can imagine with the location they get a lot of riffraff so they locked bathroom door nearly wasn't opened in time for my 3 year old potty trainer. 

It was good- I'd stop by again if we were on foot and hungry like we were that day.

was kind of in a rush on friday night to get food and head out. i called for delivery, dreading the possible 1 hr delay til the food came but the food came surprisingly quick... maybe in like 25 minutes. the food was pretty decent too. i had the shrimp fried rice and my buddy had boneless spare ribs with chicken fried rice.

So good. Awesome place to stop during the day for a drink and snack. The hummus is easy to share and pitas are so fluffy and delicious. I usually order the hummus with meat, this time was chicken which was fantastic. Reasonable prices for what you get and awesome location. Outdoor seating as well when it's nice out which is a plus. The hummus also comes with a dish of pickled veggies which are great too.
Horrible customer service for A-list.  No communication and just rude very disappointing flying out of philly:(

Seems like a fun place. Great to just sit around and talk with friends, a truly chill bar. Not a place for dancing, but then again not many guys really care about that too much.

PBR pounders are cheap here.

Watch out, seems like a hipster gathering point, because it's juuuuuuuust ironic enough to attract them.

Awesome divey atmosphere, plenty of room to accomodate a lot of people.

A Fitting late night review for a fitting late night hang out spot. Upon going to dinner here with Aaron and Baby Badness for what I thought to be the first time I was able to piece together that it's been at least the 3rd time. When you have as many friends who've played the Troc as I have it just ends up being one of those places that you end up going to afterwards. But while I've always been able to place the saucy fried goodness of Kung Pao, I haven't always been able to place the name. But now that I have I can sum up that it's equally legendary.

Don't expect the best service and the best quality food. But do expect something that's worth what you pay for, and more if your 21+ since you can score a Kirin for $3.50 and plenty of other cheap drinks. You'll be able to get a piled high plate of food and they hook up some satisfying soups, but remember to ask for that plate of rice otherwise you're not going to get it (d'oh!). But despite some of the few hang up's David's can make the late night hours pure bliss and egg rolls!

I went on a date here for Center City's restaurant week, and had a great time. The food, which was reasonable portioned, was excellent. My date got a tomato salad, salmon, and lemon parfait, while I got the New England clam chowder (with real clams!), chicken, and a huckleberry crumble. They also had a very diverse wine selection.
Our waitress was incredibly attentive and polite, which just added to the really cool ambience of the place. Overall, it was a great experience, and I'd love to come back.

Undoubtedly my take on Distrito's is skewed by the fact that my friend and I were seated in the hellmobile -- a sickly pink-lit taxi in the window of Distrito's.  The fluorescent pink made my dinner companion look like a cross between a creature from a regendered Avatar and someone with very high blood pressure.  And it made all the food look uncooked and revolting, which in turn undoubtedly affected its flavor, which tasted uncooked and revolting.  The service was friendly and great and maybe even a bit friendlier and greater because they knew what it meant to be placed in the pink hellmobile.

I love that this place is here and enjoy the fresh pasta. The bolognese, lasagna and mezzemaniche are always superb. Next time I'm trying the cacio e pepe. In the cooler months this is excellent comfort food, and the owner is clearly a very kind man that deserves some Philly love.
ordered rare filet mignon over mashed potatoes. one of the best filets i've ever had! limited menu, but everyone i went with was satisfied w/their meal. def on the list of my fave byobs!

Food is good. Drinks are tasty. Try the blackberry martini or the pineapple martini. This is a solid four stars restaurant. The fifth star is for their CARROT CAKE! If you are a lover of carrot cake...get it! Perfect cream cheese icing and well balanced and moist cake. Will not disappointed and it is huge.
Great haircuts, work with you and will squeeze you in. Are willing to whelp you find what you want.

We live half a block from Kim van and honestly couldn't ask for a better shop to be close too. Always super friendly and 95% of the time they're prices match or beat any grocery store. They always seem to carry exactly what you are looking for and on the occasion will slip in something extra. They just recently renocated and did an amazing job. Highly highly recommended.

Stopped in here yesterday to grab some slices since it was down the street from a training I attended. I've had the pizza here once before at a work lunch and while it wasn't my favorite, I remembered it being decent. I went with a pepperoni and a plain slice.. the slices were huge but extremely greasy and very doughy. Even though I was hungry, I couldn't even force myself to finish one slice. It was that bad. Lots of people mentioned the $1 happy hour slices.. maybe this pizza would be more appetizing after a few drinks? I still don't think I'd want to waste another $1 here.

The building is gorgeous with multiple rooms and levels to eat in, perfect for a date night. The tea sandwiches and alcoholic beverages during Afternoon Tea were exquisite! We had the Down the Rabbit Hole tier of tea sandwiches. The Chamomile Lemonade was the best drink I've ever had! We will definitely be back to try more food and drinks.
This place is gross. Pizza cookies rude as hell. Looks like he's gonna kill you for ordering pizza.

This restaurant is a very nice and relaxed place that you can come to chill in. you can get a range of Mexican American meals for a nice price . the drinks range from margaritas to coca cola's so it is okay for youth to come here. Though I would not suggest you bring your family here it is a good one on one restaurant to catch up with someone.
    At this local Bar and Grill on South Street, customers get what they paid for. This Mexican American eatery is ready to fill all of your Mexican cravings. From Tacos to Margaritas this place has it all. The menu usually stays the same but every week they have a weekly special. It is not very Family friendly restaurant since the base of the menu is alcohol and fancy dishes. But even without a kids menu they can still seem to keep their customers satisfied. One of there most known for sections on their menu is their Mexican take on American burgers. Some examples are Acapulco, the Benedict Burger, and The Toluca Burger. My personal favorite entree is the Acapulco burger witch consist of Guacamole and salsa with an all beef patty. Everything in the restaurant has a very "chill" vibe with no stress.
    The General feel of the restaurant is very nice there is no stress. People come to this place to relax and have a drinks there are very litle families and this place is a place you would go to see your friends of catch up with a relative. There prices are relatively cheap. Ranging from $8.00 to $18.00 meals. It is also highly decorated and very nice looking.

Solid choice if you want traditional breakfast from a diner in Center City. Staff is nice, service fast. Prices are what you'd expect. So all around nothing to complain about. Glad to have a diner in CC now that's not all dated looking like Midtown.

I was a skeptic of Buff Bills. It looked like a dive from the outside - and in a way, it is - but it's very well kept and charming with some great food, great specials, and a great low-key atmosphere. 

I went on Tuesday for happy hour, and decided to stay for poker night. Everyone was really friendly and welcoming in the game. The buffalo chicken dip was incredible - by far the best thing on the menu. I can't resist half price apps!

I'll definitely be coming here again!

Forbes Forbes Forbes best pizza !!!! Cute spot to eat in. Has an ice cream parlor adjacent to it. Quality pizza coming from an NY native. Can get a pie order or you can grab slices. They do not have every slice available but they change it up daily. BYOB and vegan friendly.

Celebrated my 40th birthday here on December 2nd. Had a blast. Our server and the bar staff were amazing. Bar staff really, I can't say enough about. Super dope!! The hostess was rude but whatever/: Try the lamb sliders and calamari. Also, the DJ that night, OFF THE HOOK! He had us singing out loud and dancing our butts off!

a. Kitchen lives up to the hype. This Rittenhouse Square eatery is filled with sunlight, flavorful food, and friendly vibes.  A meal here is such a rewarding experience.  Don't miss the Smoked Char on toast.  This is a very tasty appetizer.  The entrée of grilled chicken with sweet & sour eggplant was perfectly prepared - tender and succulent.  A nice oat streusel & apple pudding completed the meal.  Their wine list is equally impressive - especially when I saw several Austrian wines on the drink card and in talking with the sommelier I could tell she was enthusiastic about Austrian wines.  The wait staff was incredibly friendly and attentive.  Now all this does come at a price...a. Kitchen is not priced like your neighborhood diner.  But if you have a chance to splurge and want a feel-good meal, head to a. Kitchen!
The pizza was amazing and economical! I ate 7 out of the 8 slices in just one sitting all by myself. I will definitely be going back!

I don't get why there are so many negative reviews for Genos.  I loved Genos!   I was in town and on the hunt to find the best cheesesteaks and out of the 4 I tried, Genos was my favorite!  And I even ate my Genos after it was sitting around in my fridge for a few hours so it wasn't even fresh!   

Genos - so juicy, tasty and oozing with cheese.  All of the flavors were there and it was so so so so so good.  When I reheated it, I was expecting it to be bad, but the flavors were all there.  I could just imagine how good it would have been if I ate it fresh.  The sandwich was also a perfect size!

I tried Pats, Ishkabibble's and Jim's the same weekend and my leftover/sitting in the fridge for a few hours Geno's sandwich was actually the best out of the 4 believe it or not.  Pats was my second favorite since it was also very juicy and tasty.  I ate that fresh and made the choice to save Geno's for later.  Jim's was good, but it was tiny in size compared to the 4.  Jim's was also lacking the flavor Genos and Pats had.  Ishkabibble was the worst of the 4 since it lacked flavor =(   

I will definitely be back to try Genos fresh and we'll see if my mind changes!

Frequented this place last week as a "check-off-the-bucket-list" type of place. Obviously could not get a reservation for the next like 3 months, so bit the bullet and waited on a Wednesday evening for any available seating. 

Man it was worth the wait!

The wait wasn't even that bad. In terms of time it was probably 45 minutes, but there is a small area inside with seats for waiting. As you wait they offer you drinks - a nice touch.

The bartender, and our server, was incredibly friendly and helpful with the menu. He gave us the low-down a few suggestions and then left us to our conversation. Perfect. My girlfriend and I went for the tasting menu, because when in Rome . . . it was fantastic! Started off with a variety of Middle Eastern salads, all quite exotic including a heart of palm tree. The hummus was incredible knock your socks off good. In retrospect, I wish I had three orders of the hummus, it was just that good. 

We had four small plates, fried cauliflower was delicious, but I remember not being a fan of the tuna tartare. For the main courses, we got hangar steak, which was excellently cooked, and the chicken shishlik, which was dry and overcooked (the only down part to the meal). The drinks were delicious. She got the lemonnana, which was incredibly sweet. She loved it, it was a bit too sweet for me. I went with the 50/50 split three ways, nice name for a drink, and much more balanced between sweet and bitter. 

Lovely experience, I look forward to going again in the future.

Food was decent and service was good. The only reason I didn't give it 5 stars is because I found the price to be too high for the quality of the food. We got BBQ so it totaled $32 per person. It's all you can eat, so that would be the reason for the higher price and I am aware that Korean BBQ is generally on the pricier side. However, I wouldn't come here any other day aside from my birthday (free for bday person if ID is provided and review is written). The food is good but I found the price not worth it.

Good spot in the northeast for pho. Nice decor on the inside and prices are solid. Food is good and comparable to what you would find in south Philly. Also a lot of other dishes like general tsos and pad Thai are on the menu for people who know what they like.
A lot of varieties of delicious Indonesian dessert and prepared food. The groceries is vast too. Great store for your Indonesian food needs. Very friendly staff

I LOVE this place. One of the best vegan spots in Philly. It is always busy but I've never had to wait for a spot at the counter, they are super efficient. Their tofu scramble is unreal. I usually get the breakfast burrito, but I'm also a fan of the bagel breakfast sandwich. Really anything here is good!! The coffee is good too, essential for a diner. I'm a fan of their chai lattes. I live maybe 40 miles out of Philly but whenever I'm in the city I try and make a stop here. My omnivore boyfriend also loves it here; he gets jealous if I go without him! Highly recommend this perfect spot for some yummy, no fuss vegan food in a cute diner-vibe space.

The iced tea is good. But....
I've gone into this coffee shop twice and probably will not be going back. 
The staff is not very friendly, it appears as if you are bothering them to ask any questions on what kind of coffee or mech they sell.  On one of my visits to Ox, someone in my party asked how to use one of the devices that they sell for at home use. When the barista finally answered it appeared as if he was so annoyed by answering it, which was then followed with a semi eye roll.  I'm in queen village 5 days a week for school and with the dozen other coffee shops in the area I can just go somewhere else.

I really love sushi. Really love it. In all its many forms, it's one of my favorite foods - up there with pizza and cheeseburgers. I moan like a walrus when I eat it, and I'm pretty much always happy it, even in those rare instances when it came from an Acme or Genuardi's.

Call them "sushi feelings," those blissfully indescribable feelings that go along with eating sushi; I had none of those sushi feelings this time around. The burnt shrimp tempura, the funky salmon flavor, the hollow (besides for rice) rainbow roll, the lack of any distinction whatsoever between the spicy tuna and spicy salmon... all of these things made me feel like I was eating something else, some food unworthy of the sacrosanct mantle of "sushi."

A pox on you, you purveyor of mediocrity!

We went here for New Years Eve, and it was SUPERB!  It's a small quaint place, you would not want to bring more than 8 people.  The menu is always changing.  The chef is in view of every table, and the staff is fantastic.  Every single thing our table ordered was amazing, and everyone raved about each course.  Dessert was great!  It's a BYO

Great food, great prices. Carnitas burrito was the best I've had in Philly. We moved back from LA and find it hard to get really good Mexican food here. Anyways, they were out of aguas frescas so the guy made me a fresh Horchata to order, which was awesome. Will be back to try the sopes. Really low-key spot. Really really good
Great buffet to go get lunch cheap.  It's not just salad, they have Chinese food selections too. I like their dolmas, which were surprisingly decent considering the many Asian dishes.

Great food, reasonable prices, fast and courteous. If you are in rush and looking for a quick local eats in south Philly, a definitely grab a slice here more often instead of succumbing to crappy places. If you get a Pizza from Franco I definitely ask them to put light sauce on the pizza.

Upon 2nd visit, I enjoyed JG Domestic more than my first time.

The lobster cappuccino is like a stoner joke turned culinary masterpiece. It is basically lobster bisque+cappuccino=excellent. I don't know what part of the netherworld Garces uses to channel his creativity, but I am glad he signed a deal with Beelzebub to have access to it.

The potato cassoulet was pretty darn great. The cabbage, cheese, andouille, and potatoes are like a superhero team that beats the crap out of my mouth.

Sadly, I did not find everything to be amazing.

The potted duck foie gras with orange gelee was fine.
The crab croquettes with avocado and pumpkin were fine.
The Pennsylvania Pekin duck was fine.
The beignets were fine.

JG Domestic is a very good restaurant. Despite its strengths, I feel it is my least favorite of the Garces joints. I think the only one I haven't tried in Philly is Guapos Tacos.

On the action-movie-parallel-satisfaction-scale, this establishment is Gladiator.

This place was pretty awesome!
 
Walking down the street exploring Philadelphia and came across this place with my girlfriend.
So many comics out there and they seem to have them all!
Wish i knew about this place a head of time , wouldnt mind coming back to buy some comics. Workers were very nice there and also helpful. Clean and cool vibe inside. Action fingers they had a glass case was pretty awesome. 

Not big into comics but can look into going back here again someday to buy some.
Dr. Pagnani is a very compassionate and professional doctor. He makes you feel welcomed, respected, and heard. I appreciate his inclusiveness toward patients of all backgrounds!
The beer selection was good, but limited for the novice beer drinker. The food was great- had the burger. Wonderful atmosphere.  Will go back

As a former member of Planet Fitness in South Philly, I was spoiled by their inexpensive $10-$20 monthly membership fees. But the trip from Fairmount to South Philadelphia proved to be too much at 5:30pm during the week. So I have joined with LifeSport and, even though it's more expensive per month, I am glad I did. It's an eight minute walk from my apartment, so there is no excuse not to go, other than sheer laziness. It's small, but casual and comfortable. The equipment isn't ultra-modern, but it is functional, relatively up-to-date, and works. They offer a decent variety of classes and, even though the gym appears small, there's lots of seperate areas for free weights, lifting, cardio, stretching, etc. It's a little sweaty in there, but I don't see that as a bad thing. No one seems to care. 

Lifesport is Average Joe's to PSC's Globogym. Dig it, four stars.

They have a good honest falafel sandwich. I have no opinion on their other menu items, since I only go for falafel. Always fresh, and today with a nice minty flavor. Of course, these things are a mess to eat, but that's not their fault. Counter staff is always friendly and very pleasant. I'll be back. Special note: whole wheat pita for no extra charge. They take LevelUp.

We recently stayed in Philadelphia for the Mid-Atlantic Region Irish Step dance competition over Thanksgiving.  BurgerFi was one of the restaurants very close to the convention center and across from the Reading Market.  The menu looked great on the website and it said it was open until 11 pm.  The awards ceremony usually runs late and we entered the restaurant at 10:30 pm to find the register closed and the person behind the counter cleaning up.  She told us the restaurant was open until 11 but the counter closed at 10:30.  We were very disappointed.  The website should have denoted this.

I decided to try the next day at around 7:30 pm.  There were only 3 people ahead of me and 7 behind.  I waited 20 minutes for my food- a veggie burger and fries.  The beverage area was almost out of stock, there were no lids for the cups, and the people up front were too swamped to help.  I had to go under the cabinet to find stuff to stock the beverage area.  There was one person on the register, 3 people on the line cooking, and one person preparing things from the frozen custard menu.  

This all falls on the management, the restaurant is next to a convention center and the management should pay attention to events being held and staff the restaurant appropriately so the staff can serve the customers in a timely manner.  Further, it was a holiday weekend and that alone should have called for more staff.  

The only positive thing was the food and it was quite good.

I wanted to tried mee Siam from a different restaurant although I like it from banana leaf . I like trying different places . So I ordered mee Siam and my boyfriend  ordered Roti canai . My mee Siam was good , very tasty and full of flavor . As for the Roti canai my boyfriend was upset with it because it was all soup with a piece of meat and potato . It was nothing compared to banana leaf's Roti canai . So that was a disappointment and the reason I took a star off . For drink , I ordered Thai tea . It was perfect .
Decent food
Easy walk from the Conwell Inn.  I had a sesame bagel toasted dark w/ butter and an Ever Fresh OJ.  Hubby had a bagel w/ Strawberry cream cheese.  Not open on the weekend.  Our food was very good.

A cute little yarn store in Washington Square West.  You won't find your Red Heart or Lionsbrand yarns.  The yarn selection is beautiful.  Each yarn is paired with a swatch to help you envision the yarn colour and texture.  The woman working there was helpful & gave me advice on how many skeins I would need for my project.  She rang me up & then offered to wind my skeins.  We shared nice conversation while she spun the yarn. 
*Note: if you can't find her right away, she may be walking her dog quickly.

Ok, call me Guilty - ended up at Barbuzzo today bc it was part of Restaurant Week.  Slap my face and call me Funny!  Barbuzzo did not disappoint!  I had the sheep's milk ricotta (super creamy ricotta drizzled with aged balsamic and extra virgin olive oil , seasoned with a pinch of salt and thyme, and served with grilled bread slices), the branzino over Israeli couscous and artichoke with a lemony sauce, and the caramel dessert.  I liked the ricotta, but what made the dish was the sweet aged balsamic - mmmm, so good.  My favorite part of the whole meal was this red-colored tapenade that topped my branzino - really to die for.  I tried to ask the waitress what it was, but she couldn't hear me correctly or something bc she kept saying, "I'm so happy you're enjoying it!"....ummm, "But what's this made of?" to which she asks,"It's really good isn't it?"...lol, I just gave up asking.  The dessert was a little rich for me, but it wasn't bad...it just didn't really hit the spot.  

I sat at the kitchen bar and got to stare at the cooks a cooking, but I didn't mind.  I was curious as to what each person was making, but that just means I have to come back until I taste everything on the menu.  Great little cozy place.  Great waitstaff and kitchen staff.  Nice atmosphere...can't wait to come back.  Why hadn't I come here earlier?????

The bathrooms are never clean, electrical outlets are inconsistent, I/95 to and from the airport is littered with trash, and the guard rails to and from the airport have weeds overgrown, clean this "international airport" up. It's a disgrace.

Super friendly guy running a solid food cart. I ordered the brisket cheesesteak which was okay. He seemed to be a bit rushed at the time I ordered and maybe not setup all the way yet. I did enjoy but it did lack the cheese (see picture) and it could of been a bit more tender. Other stuff on the menu looked and smelled good. I will give 2 Street Sammies another try when I come across them and hopefully upgrade this 3 star review. Cheers!

Came here with my friend due to the rooftop atmosphere among the pandemic.   It was my first time attending and I loved the vibe.   The crowd was diverse and my drink "Mykonos sunset" was tasty.  Since travel is suspended why not visit via beverage? 

I wasn't too hungry so I ordered an appetizer, potato gnocchi.  It was really delicious with a slight citrus element to it.  I'll definitely be back to try more items.

Dear Zahav,
Sometimes it's hard to say those things that you really feel, to put your heart down into words. However, in the absence of other expressive modes to share my feelings (painting, singing, interpretive dance), let me just say this: your Rosh Hashanah tasting menu rocked my world. It was a devilish combination of wonderful ingredients that were beautifully presented. Is there anything that you can't do with a duck? I'd like to see you try... Kind and attentive (and attractive - yowza!) service made it a wonderful evening. Dragged my friend and her husband along for the ride, and they also had a great time. Next time, I will definitely bring a hot date - don't get jealous. ;)
Sincerely,
Moi

Came here tonight to grab drinks and dinner by the warm heater's glow! We sat on a quaint side street where the servers kept hot mulled cider coming and good food rolling in. I'm verrrry picky about my burgers, and I think this is one of the best in the city! Has a tang to it, and the meat isn't overly dense. Thank you for a lovely experience dining al fresco!

What a very apt name for a very cool little tavern situated across the street from Benjamin Franklin's final resting place, Christ's Church. Stop in here and raise a toast for one of our most influential founders.

I came here to return blinds that broke outside of my 90 day window, and to buy a couple hundred dollars worth of merchandise for my house. I had the receipt for the blinds, and only asked for store credit to buy new blinds, not even a cash refund. The customer service lady who helped me was very rude and accusatory, and told me it was the manufacturer's issue now, not theirs. If she had told me any of this in a helpful and polite way, I wouldn't have had such a problem with it. However, I was so turned off by her attitude that I immediately went to Lowe's down the street and happily spent my couple hundred dollars there instead.
Great pork sandwich with long hots. The spaghetti with clams was smooth and sultry. Great lunch nice comfortable atmosphere.

[Sigh]

We've been back to McCrossens a half a dozen times since last year and every times has been a solid B+ experience.  Always good, but nothing extremely noteworthy. 

 .... Then we went back on Saturday night....  

I dont know when their menu changed but they're not vegetarian friendly anymore.  There wasn't a single vegetarian entree on the menu, and not a vegetarian sandwich or pasta either.  There was a beet salad (that sadly I didn't see until after I had ordered), hummus, and a cheese board.  That is it!  Even their side of "roasted brussel sprouts" has ham in it.  I was able to order the Butternut Squash Bisque in the specials without the bacon.  It may be a meat-lover's paradise, but there are enough nauseating words on the menu to make me forgo dinner altogether: veal, fois gras, bone marrow, sweet breads, tripe. I dont know when McCrossens turned into a meat market, but it makes me sad.  

Also, I can appreciate trying new wines and beers, but there is a fine line between expanding your customers horizons and being snooty.  As far as the by-the-glass-selections go, there isn't a word I recognize in the entire name of the wine.  In the reds, there was no cabernet, merlot, pinot, tempranillo, malbec, shiraz, syrah, sangiovese, or chianti.  I stared at whatever wines they had for ten minutes and I'm still not sure which words were the name of the winery and which was the type of wine.  I'm no wine slouch, but I have no idea what a "Gaglioppo, Ciro Rossso, Librandi, Calabria, Italy 2010" is.  If they're only offering five or so red wines by the glass, why are they all so obscure?  The beers aren't quite as obscure, and they have a wide selection so you can find something you like.. as long as what you like isn't a Bud or a Miller Lite.  

The only reason I'm giving two stars is that the cheese board is still fantastic.

I didn't know what to expect when I walked into the Igloo but I was impressed with their organized and clean approach to Fro-yo as well as the many options they offer.  In addition to Fro-Yo, they have all kinds of Gelato, Sorbet, and Water Ice.
I was in a Gelato mood and settled on the Salted Caramel and Dark Chocolate Hazelnut flavors.  I should probably tell you that the Salted Caramel was life-changing and probably the best Gelato flavor I've ever had.  The Dark Chocolate Hazelnut was really good too and added a nice balance to the 2 flavors. My girlfriend soon regretted her decision to get Fro-Yo and couldn't get enough of my Gelato. She was in dessert heaven and I was impressed by this new neighborhood spot.
If you are searching for some good Gelato and don't live around the corner from Capigiro, check this place out.

I ate here when they were by Broad and Pine.  They were very friendly and their sushi was very good.
When I saw they closed up, I was very relieved to learn that they only moved a short distance away.  Shewww!!
We ordered take out delivery this past weekend.  It came FAST!  And their sushi was wonderful!  We will be ordering from them on a regular basis from now on!!

I came in the other day for a blow dry. Everything was fine except for the strong smell of a dead animal in the shampoo room!!!! What's going on with this place? My hair turned out great but because of the smell and the messy condition of the salon i will NEVER return.

Not a great experience here. I've been too a few other McCormick & Schmick's elsewhere and have generally been pleased. 

The waitress in the bar seemed to really struggle with understanding the menu and the options available. She also struggled to keep up service-wise with the handful of us in the bar area drink-wise. 

When the food arrived it was--meh. The fish was chewy and overcooked and lacked the expert prep I would expect from a place that specializes in Seafood.

The preparation is stellar (crispy edges, pillowy base of pizza), but the flavor was lacking for me. White pizza's cheese was underseasoned and could have used a lot more salt. I've had better luck with the red pizzas here.

Great sandwiches! Highly recommend! It's BYOB so make sure you bring your favorite beverage.
The staff is super nice and the food was great!

I went here with a friend after purchasing a deal on Living Social that included a bottle of wine, choice of 2 entrees or 4 tapas/salads, and 2 desserts.  I was pretty surprised that we got a decent bottle of Chianti, which wasn't the lowest priced wine on the wine list - it was not bad.  

For food, we decided on the tapas because we'd get to try a variety of things:  jerk chicken skewers, cheesesteak empanadas, chicken dumplings, and the braised pork taquitos.  Not one of these tapas blew me away whatsoever - the flavors were just not there.  The most disappointing dish was the jerk chicken skewers with the dijon aioli - absolutely no flavor and the aioli was a joke (tasted like mayo and plain greek yogurt).

For dessert, we ordered the cheesecake and red velvet cake.  The cheesecake tasted like it was taken right out of a box however the red velvet cake wasn't too bad and had a hint of strawberry flavor.

I know a lot of people posted about how bad the service was, but we had a totally different experience (maybe because it seemed we were the only ones in there actually ordering anything from the kitchen).  Our server, Frank, was very friendly and on point.  Also, the food came out pretty quickly (even though it was blah).

Other than the awful tapas, I have two complaints:
1.  What the hell is going on with the musical selection you have here?  When I walked in, Frank Sinatra was blaring - fine by me.  By the time we were finishing up, it was a TLC dance party.  As much as I enjoy a little old school TLC, I'm not really sure what you're trying to get at here with the random change of tunes.  Oh, and what's up with having the hockey game turned up over the already loud music?  I don't get it.

2.  The 20% tip was automatically included on the bill as a "service charge."  Don't get me wrong, I'm not cheap and I will typically give a 20% tip, but to automatically include it seems like bad restaurant etiquette.

I will not be going here again...there are too many good restaurants in the area that perform way better than Gigi's.

Still our favorite steak place. Lines a deceiving Long but quick!  Cheesesteak sandwiches are so gooey and delicious.  Only missing some steak fries so loose one star for that.  Can't have a meal with out my fries!

If I could give this place negative stars I would they deliver my $12 salad with out dressing and when I called for some they kept saying it was coming and never came I called back and they stop answering my calls. Needless to say I did not enjoy my dry salad at all. And the phone person and driver person was both very rude. DO NOT ORDER FROM HERE!!!

Not that good. I asked for American wit fried onions and they soaked the sandwich with that cheap melted canned cheese. Mind boggling that they boast quality and then they put cheap cheese on it. Fresh deli cheese would have been much better.
One of the best places to see a live show. Period. Not too large or too small. Club attracts a nice variety of music.
$50 for a day pass?  For reals?  I can't really even review it because I couldn't afford to workout while in town. Looks nice though. That per day guest pass is outrageously high.

I stopped into Thai Singha during my lunch break one day.  Everything looked delicious but I decided to try the bamboo shoot dish.  I asked them to make it very spicy.  I always ask this but most places seem to think I'm joking and barely add spice.  I can say this meal was one of the spiciest of my life.  I literally had to take breaks every few bites because my face was red, I was drenched in sweat and my nose was running a dang marathon.  It was so delicious I couldn't stop despite the heat and pain!  I will definitely be trying these guys out again.  Delicious food!

The Body shop is good - but not what it was in the old days when Anita Roddick was more involved.  I do like many of the products and the prices are moderate.

The body butters (gee whiz, there are now about 16 types of body butter to choose from) are good - I like the Mango and Shea - both for VERY dry skin.  The new Lemon one is lighter and nice.

I also love the Satsuma fragrance in things like body wash and lotion.

Fun to shop and the Body Shop.

I've never had a bad drink of food item at Tria. Hard to get a seat if you aren't there early, but this place never disappoints. My wife and I try and sneak in around 4 to enjoy the bar with the baby asleep on our chest. We love it here, and you will too.
The best vegan Reuben I've ever had. Took a while to find this place since Reading Terminal gets so crowded but it was so excellent. Didn't fill me up but the flavors were amazing!
Just stopped in for a quick cup of coffee. Really didn't stay but like the funky atmosphere in the friendly staff to make you feel you're part of the community

Quick lunch pick-up order through the Ritual app. The Southwest beef brisket burrito bowl was delicious, but the rice was a bit watery at the bottom. The Mediterranean burrito was dense and filling, but had an overwhelming amount of hummus.

The frozen banana bowl tasted exactly as expected-- cold, smoothly blended banana with toppings. A healthy vegan alternative, but unsatisfying if you're hoping for a substitute to yogurt/ice cream/custard. Also pretty filling.

The space itself was nicely decorated with seating and pillows. Wish I had time to sit and eat-in instead of take out. Note-- if ordering frozen banana for pick-up, it is separate from the rest of your order and you have to ask someone to grab it from the fridge. I had to run back for it because I forgot to check before I left.

I must say I kept saying I wanted to have lunch here and when I did I was a little disappointed by the portions. The portions were very small with only 4 pieces of meat for an oxtail platter, but the jerk chicken was full portion. The drinks were very good and ambiance pleasant, staff was also very nice and there are fine men in the kitchen wink wink lol. Perfect place for appetizers and drinks with friends, which was why I went. But I'd say it was just ok.

Been here multiple times and every time I loved my meal and experience.  I always start with the mussels.  On the main entree list, I enjoyed everything I had - duck, pasta dish, pork chops. The specials are usually great too.  Love that it's BYO. Remember to bring cash!

Oh my goodness!!! Best food! And great service. Dinner service is always crowded so make a reservation. My husbands best friend discovered this place while living in Phili and now we go every time we visit... My husband even stops and brings home some food for me when he's there on business.  The charcoals dinner for two is enough food for four and absolutely delicious! You will not be sorry you tried Zorba's!!!! Yummy!!!!

Rite Aid tried but they are just the plain disheveled chain store as always. The Rite Aid I speak of is at 2301 Walnut Street in center city Philadelphia. This is about the same at most of the chain stores. The difference here are the workers. The pharmacists are excellent. Unfortunately too much of the business is conducted with rude clerks behind the pharmacy counter. These women have a nasty attitude, partly one of superiority since they know way too little and think way too much of themselves. They don't even make the effort to put on the illusion of common courtesy and seem to hate their jobs almost as much as they hate their customers. The first answer to any question is "no" always. When pressed, they usually come back with the truth. They are so unpleasant that they will not even look at customer in the eye when talking.  There are always enough workers but these women act like they have too much to do just by walking up to the counter from their chairs. Chain drug stores are not usually the best, but this particular Rite Aid wins an award for making your shopping experience an unpleasant one.

The guy taking our order rushed us and was very impatient even though I'm spending $55 dollars for a family dinner. It took forever to get the food and when we did, 3 out of the 4 shakes were melted (not good).  There was no seating on the first floor and the upstairs was reserved by some private party.

Well, I'm not afraid to admit when I'm wrong. I decided to try Bluestone again on a weekday and I was pleasantly surprised. As I said in my last review, this is a great place for a coffee and a pastry: tons of gluten-free options and a perfectly delicious cold brew; free-flowing wifi; light and airy decor. I stand by my earlier comments that brunch is likely not their strong suit, but I would definitely recommend coming here for a coffee date or study time.

I love this location!! Rich does an amazing job on my hair. I have super thick, long hair and I usually end up fixing my blow outs when I go anywhere else but he totally knows how to control my hair and make it look flawless! Highly recommend this location and Rich! He is fantastic!

Kung-fu Necktie is definitely my kind of place and reminds me of one my favorite bars/dance venues back here in good ol' Allston.

Bar in the front, dancing/ stage in the back. Music was fun for dancing, and they even played one of my favorite Radiohead songs! whoohoo! I enjoyed the $1 PBRs (although they were in smaller cups, oh well still can't complain!) as well as the super strong Jack and coke I ordered. 

Enjoyed the overall vibe of this place, small and intimate, although it did get a little crowded as the night went on.

This is an American south BBQ spot: no steak. JUST ribs and chicken. Some of the sides are better than one would expect, like collard greens, and the cucumber salad. The Mac isn't anything special. It's fun to dip the meats int the house BBQ sauce. I think I'd go again.

I love this place. The owner is a great guy who gets to know his customers very well. The fact that it is a BYO makes it even better. However, because you can BYO, the prices for dinner are a little high.

For an appetizer I had the grilled caesar salad. It was delicious!! Not too much dressing and the right amount of char on the romaine to make a tasty salad.

For an entree I had pasta. The pasta itself tasted homemade and everything was fresh. Since I live close by, this is definitely a great neighborhood place to have around.

This is officially my favorite Rita's because they ALWAYS have my mint chocolate chip!  But like all the other Rita's, you really can't go wrong whether you're a lover of gelati, blendini, or plain ol water ice.
My new favorite place for Pho in the city. Delicious and cheap!

This is a good mix of books, temple apparel and knick-knacks for the casual fan. You can get better stuff at the bookstore in the SAC, but this is a good location for it. Don't buy books here, the textbooks aren't always labelled for specific classes like they are in the SAC bookstore and, even at that, I would recommend buying books online whenever possible anyway.

The waiter was really nice, the restaurant was nice too and ofcourse the food was good. I ordered lamb chops and my hubby ordered marinated chicken. Both good. They add an 18% service fee on your bill, so i didn't expect that. our bill was $67 and we ended up paying $85. The menu was really small as well which was a bummer but I think I'd still go back and try some manti and calamri.

Ordered Veal Porterhouse, with mushrooms, lobster mac, and garlic mash as sides. The food was above par, which is expected at Del Frisco's. The building used to be a bank, which is obvious with the super high, detailed ceilings, the towering columns on the first floor, and the vault downstairs. It all makes for a nice experience, which is great when paired with good food, but I must say the service lacked personality. I was served by a robotic (but polite) waiter and maître d, which made the whole experience feel very impersonal. You can't go wrong with a meal at Del Frisco's, but I was left wanting something more special, as I believe one should at this price point. If you're paying for the bill at this place, look elsewhere.

Restaurant Week!
Ordered the eggplant appetizer for starter. The crispy bread and eggplant were delicious! Move on to the scallop fettucini with LOBSTER butter Enough said! Loved that it was balanced out with roasted corn! (Ate it so fast). Dessert .. Ice cream terrine! A combination of three flavors of ice cream! GREAT! Service was great. Food was hot each course presented and fast. WE were fat and chatty afterward and they didn't rush us! Great experience. GOOD FOOD. They also have outside seating and a parking lot near by that charged 16 bucks for two and a half hours being greedy! Will definitely be back. Did I mention the cbd cocktails?. Just did. Cool spot!

ordered general chicken for take out asked for extra sauce, the lady who answered said there's a charge for extra sauce. I said wait what you know who I'am I come here very often, her answer after a chuckle was its not me its the computer. I decided not to order that dish and went with something else . When I arrived no hello, a very unfriendly look this  lady gave me . she swiped my credit card and it was time for me to go she stood there without saying thank you (I hate that)So I said your welcome no response from her and I know she heard me . I decided to get my money back and this rude women pretended to not know how to give me my refund back on her credit card machine she took what seemed like forever. I got tired of waiting and stormed out with food I no longer wanted. There nice when taking your money rude as all hell when asked for a refund. I Will never return , I will take my money where it's appreciated!!!

If you are an active athlete in Philly -- you might as well just get on the bandwagon early and sign up for this race.

It is definitely THE Philly race to do each year.

However, be ready to run with every person in the entire city.... It is a PACKED house all 10 miles from spectators to runners. You will never be alone.

Definitely take public transport.. It's free for runners in the AM. Holler.

Maybe it's hard to make a decent cappuccino (I don't know, I never tried), but Lucky Goat gets it right each time. Some of the other places in the neighborhood, not so much. The pastries are good too, always fresh. Friendly staff. Nice help-yourself jawn of water which always has some kind of fruit in it, which is good on a hot morning while waiting for the coffee. If you sit in the front you can see when the trolly's coming so you can go to work if you're not into the whole "work all day from a coffeeshop while milking a single cup of coffee" thing. (SRSLY, people, move it along after a few hours. It's not your office.)

The class was fabulous, the location was a little off the border of where I'm comfortable (see: near an auto body shop and some ehhh-ness). The space is really lovely though, and the people were SO nice. I went for a sampler class with a local group, and it was so nice that they opened their doors for us!

Note: When I went, they had a new student special of $25 for two weeks of unlimited classes. If I lived in West Philly/U City, I'd definitely take them up on it.

Weakest/worst $6 cold brew I have ever had. I can't speak for the smoothies but I would not order a coffee. This review is shorter than most so I have to keep writing in order to tell you that I will never go back here.

My boyfriend and I went out for dinner and drinks before ending our date night, and we will not be back to this place nor recommend it. 

We ordered a frozen margarita and a Moscow mule. We didn't watch the bartender mix the mule, but we saw them serve the frozen Margarita and it was a premade drink that they just poured out of the tap and mixed with the premade frozen mix. I imagine the mule was made the same way. 

My boyfriend ordered the chicken sandwich and I ordered the shrimp tostada salad. I was celebrating the presentation of what I thought was someone's dip coming out, when I realized that it was my salad in a toasted tortilla bowl with the shrimp on the side. The shrimp had basic seasoning and there could've been more greens/lettuce for a $17 salad ($11 + $6 for the shrimp). My boyfriend's sandwich was the most disappointing. It was $11 for 85% bun, 15% flat/skinny chicken fillet with minimal seasoning. 

Overall we were disappointed with the food portions and authenticity. It was like an Applebee's of Mexican food. We would've given our feedback on the food in pero, but were never asked how everything was.
This place is great!! Used to call on the phone, now I use grub hub. They get your order exactly how you ask and in a timely fashion. Love them!!!

I would have never known about Barra if I hadn't stopped in a Vape shop I go to in Old City. I saw this burger that looked like a hot pocket on steroids with a mouthwatering  aroma that would stop traffic make its way to the counter. I had to find out where he had gotten this marvel , as I also take notice to the hand cut fries with a homemade dipping sauce accompanied with his burger. A tag team, better yet a dynamic duo that would make you smack someone in the face just for a taste.  

I ventured off into Barra to get me a taste of this masterpiece I had just witnessed. As you enter you'll see a wood grilled fire pit located in the corner of the restaurant. This is where the magic happens from their burgers to their pizza . Speaking of pizza (which I love) they use pizza dough for their burgers. A shelled, wrapped delight waiting for you to devour, or manhandle. 

Along with their pizza and burgers Barra has different plates you can enjoy, with an interesting antipasti section that will need some of my attention. Barra does have some unique creations with a braised short rib ravioli along with a mac and cheese that is just screaming my name to try. 

I did mention unique, or better yet the creativity of their chef is pretty intense. Steve is the chef at Barra and he has come up with some of these dishes, along with dishes not on the menu. I will jump into that in a minute, but lets get onto the burger scene right now.

"The Americano" ~ This a brick oven Kobe beef burger that is wrapped in a pizza shell with American cheese, chopped or diced onions, with their own special sauce, with a pickle on the side.(I added bacon to mine btw.) This also comes with their house cut fries served in a bucket which is simply awesome.  Now the issue I had was I had asked for this to be cooked to a medium rare to a medium , seeing I was dealing with Kobe beef. This was over cooked, to the point where it was well done, basically  when it is over cooked it is just a waste of time, taste and meat.  (Steve was not in that day, so I will give it another chance)

Here comes the unique portion of this review, you ready? Peanut Butter and Jelly wings!           http://youtu.be/LVPNXsc4wsQ     

Yea buddy, you read that right , and they were friggin AMAZING! Nice sized wings with a Thai peanuts with a peanut oil , mixed with a homemade jelly covered these bad boys. This clearly makes up for the burger fail I had experienced with my first visit.  This is a special order that Steve makes himself, so when you are there remember 2 things. 1st, ask for Steve, 2nd get the wings, you'll thank me!
   
Barra has a nice size bar as you enter with TVs located at the bar, along with  ample seating lining the bar. Barra's dining area has plenty of  tables and booths also, so don't worry you'll have a seat somewhere . Beer selections are ok,  they sometimes rotate a few drafts here and there with the seasons. The liquor and wine selections have more to it than their beer selections which is safe to say. The décor has a comfortable relaxed vibe to it, where you can just come in and enjoy your evening.

Go in and give these guys a try, they're still good in my book. 

 Side note : They also have a "hookah" bar located next door which you can access while dining in Barra.

If I could zero stars I would. My boyfriend and I are on a road trip and decided to stop at Geno's... bad idea, we were both disappointed. By far the worst cheesesteak we've ever had. Absolutely don't recommend unless you are getting the fries, we did enjoy that.

It is so easy to eat at Mumbai Bistro as a vegan, everything is labelled, so you don't have to ask what has dairy/ghee/etc.! I really enjoy the vegan dal, but the buffet rotates daily (so check the menu before you go). You can also get a la carte items. I aways grab a vegan samosa and vegan naan! The interior has only a few tables, but you can eat around the Jefferson University campus if it is a nice day.

This was our 2nd time here and we were treated like VIP right from the door. We we're even given a history tour by one of the managers. We love the ambiance of this spot and will come back often. You should too!!!

Had breakfast at this location many times and it's actually not too bad, might be one of the better ones in Philly. I usually go early in the morning during the weekday so I never had to wait for a table. Although the service is really slow, the food is pretty decent and portions are huge...it's IHOP, what do you expect? 

I always get the quick 2-egg breakfast, which comes with two eggs, two bacon strips, hash browns and toast and my brother would always get his favorite, strawberry banana french toast and the breakfast sampler. I've never tried anything else other than what I usually get but I should give their crepes a try.

The waitress we had was very nice, attentive to our needs and always checking on us, she even brought out a coffee refill without me asking. All the food was cooked well and still warm, tasted like your typical diner food but slightly better. I'm not a fan of their hash browns but everything else was fairly decent.
Stayed 2 nights with husband and pitbull dog. The staff couldn't have been better, from the guy out the front, to the staff in the garage. Clean room, bed comfortable.

Nothing really positive I could find to say about this place. The food doesn't have much flavor. Don't bother trying to even find vegetarian options. There are maybe 2 in the menu. By far the worst Chinese I have ever eaten in my life. I hate giving bad reviews, but I'm sorry to say that this is definitely a place I would never go again.
Not a vegan, but the pizza was still one of the best I have ever had. The portobellos on the pizza I still have dreams about. I will definitely stop here every time I am in Philly.

I did an online search and came across Precision. I read a couple reviews and decided to check them out for myself. The staff was friendly and very pleasant. I spoke with Juan prior to going but was pleasantly greeted by his cousin. We were in and out in less than 30min and 2 months later my windows look as good as when I first got them.

The tinting helps my Jeep stay cooler longer and it gives it an elegant sleek look.

I love it & highly recommend :)
Excellent sushi, friendly service. We found a parking spot right out front, and in Phila, parking is free in the evening on Wed. and some holidays.

Visiting Philly and found this place on Yelp. It's basic, tucked into a grotto-like food court, across from a bar and alongside other eateries. Man at the counter was very pleasant. The fries were soft (I like crispy) and the burger was average. Seems the patty may have come frozen, I liked the lettuce, tomato, and onion

Overall, your experience at any given Starbucks mainly depends on the quality of any given barista that may make your order; the food and drinks are consistently pretty darned good across any of the chain's locations that you may visit. And for this first visit, my greet tea Frappuccino is as delicious as any that I have gotten before at other Starbucks.

So let's talk about what is unique about this particular Starbucks location.

Starbucks moved to this much more spacious location, formerly a nice Cosi, from the smaller one it had across the street at 1201 Walnut. Starbucks leveled the entire floor, from the step-up dining area that the former Cosi had. This is a great location within Philadelphia's bustling "Gayborhood", through which a lot of customers funnel throughout the day.

There is plenty of space to move around within the restaurant, which actually is the one big fault I have of it. There is not an ample amount of seating here, especially when you take the cavernous size of available space here into consideration. Starbucks could easily seat many more people, if it bought more tables & chairs to reconfigure a denser dining area. I was here just before the dinner hour on a Sunday, and I was lucky someone moved so I could have a seat.

The restroom area is sizable, and interesting that each restroom does not have sinks inside them - the hand-washing sink area is on the outside of each restroom. It's a pretty cool setup, until you consider that you will be touching other people's urine and feces on the door handle when you exit the room. Having the sinks on the outside of the restroom is fine, if you have door-less walk-in restrooms (which this location does not have). So there's that.

There doesn't seem to be any outdoor seating, and the weather has been nice for the past few weeks that this location has been open. Cosi used to have to deal with a lot of less desirable types of people lingering with its outdoor seating; perhaps Starbucks just doesn't want to deal with that element at all, by forgoing outdoor tables and chairs?

Otherwise, Starbucks should be applauded for moving to this larger location. It should allow greater amounts of people to get in and out quicker.

I give an average score because lack of group classes and the Thrive classes. The Thrive classes are insanely expensive! over $300 a month on top or your membership so over $400!.  You need to sign up w. only one teacher and know your schedule on Sunday for the week, they don't like you switching out the times or teachers.  The classes are an hour and 15 mins is just warm up. They position it as "semi private" training but the classes are crowded with average of 8 students so not semi private really. I've taken a lot of HIIT classes at other gyms and they aren't nearly this expensive and inflexible.   The Thrive area also takes up a huge portion of the gym.  CF No Libs, please add more group classes back to the schedule. You owe it to your members who were loyal to you during the pandemic!

WOW! Seriously authentic and delicious! 

Hardena is a super casual and homey spot fit for foodies, groups and solo diners. They offer a wide range of authentic Indonesian eats, served hot-line/buffet style, which is great for those that appreciate visuals like myself! 

My friends and I ordered a number of dishes to go but if you dine there, I highly recommend ordering the sampler platter, which seems quite popular. It seems like each table had ordered one and we were jealous since ordering the platter to go wouldn't be the same! 

The goat curry, beef rendang, eggplant, collard greens and the rujak cingur salad were all delicious and big hits within our group. I, especially, really enjoyed the rujak cingur salad, which is a nice mix of greens served with sticky rice cakes, beef tendon, and veggie fritters served with a chunky peanut/shrimp dressing on the side. It was savory with a nice umami taste profile and all-around delicious!!

Filthy...tables all dirty, workers handling food with their hands...disgusting!
Afraid to eat there because of filth & germs!

Great location. Prices are decent and beer selection is as good as expected from a beer garden. Cornhole/foot-pong for activities and a decent area. Plenty of tables, chairs, and umbrellas for shade. Would recommend

If you are in Philly, don't miss this restaurant.  We shared small plates.  Standouts included swordfish meatballs, fried cauliflower, squid salad, almond dusted sweetbreads. But, the truly wonderful dish was the mortadella with hazelnut honey.  We had 2 servings and I would have loved to have a jar of that wonderful honey just to have spoonful whenever I want it!

The staff at this restaurant is as impressive as the food.  We had one dish that was not what we expected.  When our server checked on us, we mentioned this dish and he reacted immediately.  You would have thought we told him our entire meal was a disaster!  They brought another small plate for us, gave us an extra dessert and were truly concerned about the quality of everything they served.

By the way, the extra dessert they brought was Mom's Rice Pudding-outstanding!

Not great for dietary restrictions. Chose Cleavers when I was in town for business because of the reviews about them being great for veg/vegans and dietary restrictions. Another Yelper who had a dairy restriction said they enjoyed a great sub here. Ordered for pick-up a byo sub and dug right in. The issue is that I requested no cheese as I can't eat dairy. The box said no cheese, but deep in my sub was cheese. Super disappointed and now sick. I'll give it 3 stars because my boyfriend LOVED his.

We both ordered the breakfast sandwich and it was amazing. Perfect size, I was full but not stuffed. The pretzel croissant was great and the cream cheese spread was a surprising but fantastic addition. I can't wait to come back and try the lunch sandwiches.
Best fried rice and California rolls I've had. Beautiful restaurant too. Hoping to do my birthday there. Affordable too.

I recently bought a house that needed a new roof. The seller of the house recommended Tufano and sons so I decided to go with them. Everything from the phone call to the job was very professional and reasonably priced. The owner was very nice and the workers were respectful. They cleaned up nicely after they were done. They even did a wonderful job on the roof. We've had many down pours since and I haven't had an issue with water.

Not sure what all the hoopla is about this restaurant, but it was fine and price is good.  Typical one star on service and decor as most Chinese restaurant.  Shaved noodles are definitely better than the long noodles which were soft and too long so it was difficult when trying to scoop out a portion.  Menu is very limited, only two items did not come in soup.  And soup was nothing special.  Would go again, but would not wait if there's a line since there are plenty of other good restaurant next door and across the street.  Also, tables are crowded together so seating is tight.

I had (sadly) never been to Vietnam Cafe before, even though I've lived nearby for years. (I had gone to once-sister-restaurant, Grill Fish.) 

The space is HUGE. I was really taken aback by how upscale, clean, and big the space was. It's such a hidden gem and I was so thrilled to see it pretty busy on a rainy summer Sunday night.

I ordered the Salt & Pepper Tofu special, which was an extremely generous portion of crisp and salty tofu, tossed with some sauteed veggies. The tofu was near perfect. Every bite had the right balance of salt with the natural tofu flavor. Each piece was perfectly fried and damn near addicting. The plate as a whole was lacking some sauce, though. (But I know that's the style/intentional.)

My BF ordered the Chicken in black bean sauce, which was tasty - and added some much needed sauce to my own S+P tofu. 

The Vermicelli bowls look super delicious and I can't wait to try some of their curries and noodle dishes.

Friendly workers, interesting flavors of cupcakes (lavender, tiramisu, etc.), various pastries (cupcakes, whoopie pies, cookies, brownies, etc.).

We ordered one red velvet whoopie pie and a meyer lemon cupcake (both $2.50 each).  The red velvet whoopie pie was good...a dense, moist pastry with fluffy cream cheese filling.  The meyer lemon was so-so...lemon cupcake with lemon filling.  The filling was a jam or jelly like consistency and tasted like something from a lemon pie.

Not the best, but not the worst bakery I've been to.

Wow, what a disappointment. I came in for a quick lunch after having a mostly positive experience for breakfast a few weeks ago. I ordered a half-Ceaser salad, half pastrami sandwich. Initially, I was served turkey (which looked pretty disgusting, I must say), and the waitress was very nice and fixed my order. The salad was soggy and did not taste fresh at all. The sandwich was stuffed with the fattiest pastrami meat I have ever eaten, and had lettuce that was browning on the edges thrown on it. After a very unsatisfying meal, I was out 10 bucks. For a place that claims to be a classic Jewish deli, they completely failed to deliver.

What a bad experience. And, sadly, not my first with Enterprise.

We had reserved a vehicle weeks in advance to pick up at this location and then drive up and through New England on a fall color trip. As usual for us, I had reserved an intermediate SUV (RAV4 or Ford Escape or similar).

We showed up here at the appointed hour only to be told that they had no such intermediate SUV available. And weren't expecting one anytime soon. What's the point of reserving a specific type of vehicle if Enterprise feels no obligation to then provide it??

Worse, the two people behind the counter seemed at first quite non-plussed and unapologetic about the whole deal. They said that a reservation only obligates Enterprise to provide that category of car...or higher. So their solution to this problem was to give us a Nissan Armada - a behemoth of a vehicle with atrocious gas mileage and that I had zero interest in driving on the back roads of Maine and New Hampshire. Or they said that they could find an economy car for us. So either several categories above what we reserved or several categories below.

When I indicated that neither was acceptable, they then proposed that I drive one of their economy cars to Trenton, NJ (sort of on-route) and exchange it there at some out-of-the-way location for a smaller SUV. With a new round of paperwork and everything required there.

While discussing this, someone showed up to return a VW Golf wagon. (I didn't even know such a car existed.) This seemed to us better than any of the other bad options so we grabbed it. It proved marginally adequate - smaller and lower to the ground that what we wanted, and not a terribly well-made vehicle. But it did the trick.

To be clear, the fault here was less with the two Enterprise staff members who in the end tried to fix the problem and accommodate us. Rather, the problem is with an Enterprise system that treats reservations for a specific car-class as something far less than a guarantee.

This will be our last time using Enterprise. From now on, we'll go with the greater reliability and service level of Alamo or Budget or Avis, etc.

recently stepped in here for the first time and ended up purchasing a new pair of earrings and new "evil eye" bracelet. the owner was very welcoming and i'm glad i stopped in. i really had my eye on the jewelry however there are baby clothes and recycled furniture, some clothing and scarves, etc.

I unexpectedly loved Chubby Cattle. To start, the service was excellent! Each time a new dish came out the server or host took the time to explain how long each item should be cooked for. 

The concept of the restaurant is clearly unique since it's the first refrigerated conveyor belt hot pot restaurant (not exclusive to Philly though.) 

I tried a variety of the menu and loved every item except one. The rainbow soup dumpling, while aesthetically pleasing, were only okay. It is a fun dish and worthwhile to order for pics.

The tiger shrimp and scallops were super fresh and really good. My favorite meat for the hot pot was the lamb shank and it took no time to cook. I was obsessed with the mushroom basket! I obviously love mushrooms but they were clearly so fresh and the basket offered an excellent variety. Another favorite was the Wagyu nigiri. It had beautiful marbling and just melted in your mouth. I would go back for this alone. A can't miss item is the housemade purple yam noodles. When can you ever pass up on housemade noddles?

I highly recommend going to Chubby Cattle with a group of friends since the portions are huge and clearly meant to be shared. I was so impressed with the freshness of the food and can't wait to go back!

I cannot say enough great things about Top Tails. I use them when I am working late and have yet to be disappointed! My dog walkers, Megan and Jenn, have taken such great care of my fur babies! They leave me detailed notes on how the pups were during their visits.   The owner Tanya truly cares about her clients and their pets. I highly recommend this company for anyone looking for reliable pet sitters/walkers around the Manayunk area!!

thin slices of bread and butter
tap water
2 glasses of good red wine
hamachi tiny cubes of fish with lime and spice tasty
warm butter sauce with peas mushrooms ramps asparagus yummy
white meat chicken slices and a slice of chicken sausage tasty 
5 warm thin duck slices on beans yummy, pic below shows 10 slices

154 for 2, tax and tip included
warm thin 
intimate candelit

There are much better places to go. This place is divey and dirty. The wine glasses are dirty. The bathrooms have never been cleaned. It's really a sad place. The service was good but my god wash the glasses you are serving. And try a spritz of Lysol every now and again. Dirty glasses, dirty bar, gross music.

We visited the beer garden about a week ago and were very pleased with the reservation process, 90 minute reservation, cleaning and social distancing. Yesterday, however, we had a completely different experience despite recent updated/more strict regulations regarding COVID, bars, and restaurants.
We were standing behind a party of 10 people, openly drinking white claw, at least 2 of them not wearing masks, waiting to be seated together. The beer garden was packed and the 30 minutes in between reservations between cleaning seemed to have been eliminated as people we're leaving well after 6 and we couldn't even check in until at least 10 after 6. We were very disappointed and felt unsafe and left and will not be back. Other places are doing things much more responsibly. It's a shame because the beer is really excellent which is why I gave 2 stars.

Small menu and very good food.  The wings were some of the best I've ever had. I will definitely be back. My wife also enjoyed the salad though I forget which one she ordered.  Small place though and kind of expensive though the food and fresh ingredients are used.  Though it's hard to justify paying $1.00 per wing when the place down the street has 25 cent wings on Wednesday.  

This is a great place to take a date.

Tasty food, great drink menu, incredibly affordable, really funky atmosphere... but holy crap, our server was terrible. She  immediately asked for our food order, and stood there staring at us when we mentioned we'd need some more time. She repeatedly informed us of discounts we weren't eligible for (that we weren't aware of in the first place), and after our food came out, she disappeared. Completely. For over an hour. She finally brought our check, and then disappeared for another half hour or so. It wasn't until we threw down some cash and stood up to leave that she ran over and said something about "being right with us." Uhhh... sure.

The food was great but I'd be hesitant to come back until they step up their staff training, especially after reading similar reviews.

All things considered this is a decent option if you're in the area and craving Indian food. Nothing mindblowing by any means but again, decent enough! The inside is quite nice - a little bit bare but it's very warm and welcoming indoors especially if there are a few people around - and delightfully clean and neat.

Out late for food after a bit of a stressful day, we walked in at 9:34 and asked when they closed as the hours aren't posted - 9:30 but the waitress still told us to sit down for half an hour. There was a big family already eating when we arrived which made for pleasant background noise. I went with someone who speaks Hindi fluently and there was definitely a bit of an uptick in the pleasant nature of service once he started speaking, which makes sense of course, but the waitress was still very considerate in speaking directly to me (in English) which I did appreciate very much!

We ordered the lamb roganjosh and kofta lajawab; the lamb was very tender and flavorful but perhaps a touch on the salty side. Having never had kofta lajawab before, I was delighted with the texture and the fullness of flavor - there is also something to be said for the fact that the lajawab and the sauce were the same temperature and heated all the way through, leading me to believe they were not simply microwaved from frozen! Portion sizes were exactly right and even though they didn't have naan at the time we did get roti which I wasn't inclined to be fussy about.

Would return if I were in the area and craving Indian again but only after I explore other nearby places for the sake of comparison!
I've been coming to Toppers for years. I love getting my nails done with Maria Hannah. It's a nice atmosphere, great location, and beautiful decor. Thanks!!

"come to philly for the CRACK"

get it?  the crack in the liberty bell?

yea this place is as cool as that joke.
My husband was most excited for this place since he hasn't had funnel cake and corn dogs in years due to celiacs. This place was great, gluten free or not. All the food was great.

I've visited Club Ozz a couple of times over the years and visited it again last night.

It has definitely changed for the better.  The have gotten rid of the center bar and installed a main stage with very comfortable chairs and mini tables facing the stage.

After speaking to one of the dancers I found out that they are under new management.  The managers that now own this club are the same ones that own The Gold Club.  How that will affect this place I have no clue.

The place was clean, there was a nicely dressed bouncer at the door to keep the riffraff out, the bar is now along one wall and there's plenty of seating.

There were roughly 10 different dancers on Friday night.  I would say that there were two 8's, five 6's, two average, and one woman that shouldn't be dancing.  

Having been here in the past there's been great improvement.

No Cover
$20 - Couch dances 
$30 - Private dance
$150 - 30 minute private dance
$4 bottled beer

Came back here with my family for dimsum. We had 8 people and they sat us near their kitchen. The table surface was so flimsy and it was unbalanced. We tried to fix it before requesting for another table. They gave us an attitude and sat us at the table next to it. Food was only average and it took forever to get their attentions. Mom wanted to order something but the waiters never come around. At one point they were standing at one corner chatting.

Pretty unimpressed by the crate and barrel decor and overall blandness of the place.  The beer selection looked pretty good but the staff was too busy talking to each other and looking at their phones to ask if I wanted another beverage after 15 minutes went by I decided not to have one.

Not sure how I've missed this place all the time I've lived nearby but I'm glad I found it now. I've been twice--one for a mani/pedi and once just for a mani. Love! Great service, great product, awesome selection. I loved everything. I'll be a regular now!

1. It's a good cheesesteak, not going to lie.

2. The line is not worth it. I feel bad for tourists. 

3. My first time I stood in line and when I left I had to wash my clothing because the cheesesteak smell was IN my clothing.

4. Indoor dining, upstairs a plus. Who wants to sit outside when it's chilly and the weathers changing like today...

5. I felt unbelievably pressured to order quickly my first time around... I don't like that feeling.

6. only 3 stars because I've had better for cheaper.

I went here for the first time to get a pedicure. The women were extremely nice and made me feel at home. It was reasonable priced and they did an amazing job. The massage they gave was amazing. They even did a hot stone massage which was great. I've never had one before so it was a nice experience. I didn't have time for a manicure so I can't speak for that but I will definitely be going back here for my pedicures.

I used Jack's services twice and will do so again in the future for the following reasons:

- transparent pricing: you get a quote right at the first visit and they stick to that (unless you ask extra things of course, which I did).  No surprises!
- Punctual down to the minute
- fast: while one person comes for the quote, on the day of a whole crew shows up and finished the job quickly. 
- you never know they've been there:  every hole is patched and ready to paint.

Haven't been in there in a while, but know the food is good. Went last night to find they changed their dinner menu. Ordered the crab cake and seared tuna big plates. The crab cake big plate was two small crab cakes, with no sides. The tuna was small as well! For the price we paid, it was seriously disappointing and went home hungry. 

If you want a snack and don't mind spending the money, this place is for you.

We did not enjoy our meal here at all. We went after reading raves about it on our neighborhood Facebook page and were very disappointed not sharing the same enthusiasm. We went for lunch on the weekend, and the place was fairly crowded. We were a bit confused about how to order at the counter since there didn't seem to be any sort of line, but after we determined everyone else had ordered, we placed ours. 

I was really disappointed that no lamb (real) gyros or skewers were offered at the time. This is what cast a shadow over anything we ordered. The beef was dry and bland, the yogurt sauce decidedly not typical Mediterranean. The fries were ordinary.

The service was abrupt, and the whole experience was like eating at Ikea without the space or windows. 

I'll take a Center City shwarma cart over this next time I get the craving.

I was unimpressed with the food here.  The fried eggplant sticks were good but all else was ho-hum.  The rest room was amazingly spotless.  The staff was pleasant too; it's just the food was uninspiring and not all that delicious.

Yet another place in the market that's a must stop. As others have said - big line, but don't let that stop you. Super fresh donuts at a cheap price that's unbeatable.
