Waiting at the end of line, you can see the bakers spreading/cutting the dough in the initial process. Keep walking down and you see the fryer cooking said masterpieces. Towards the final stages you see the large collection of tasty treats, and you want to try all of them, but you know you can't (or shouldn't). Between two people we settled on the Chocolate Frosted and Peanut Butter filled donuts ($1.90 TOTAL). Pure doughy heaven. Everything is gooey and melts in your mouth with each bite. For some it might be a little chewy/oily, but honestly it's just the way I like it.

Next time I come here I'm getting a dozen to go.

Eh. 

best I can say to be honest is eh.  Food is eh, beer is beer, drinks are pricey and watered.  Been to a few happy hours here and honestly there are better options on this block.

This is truly top notch vegan eating. 

I adore the stuffed avocado - it is as beautiful as it is delicious and it is absolutely worth the price. The rutabaga fondue is to die for (and they will give you extra bread if you just ask). Also, DO NOT UNDERESTIMATE THE WOOD ROSTED CARROT, as it is truly a magical dish. Love the drinks, love the atmosphere, love the restaurant. Happy hour is also a great bet - just get there at 5 or you won't get a seat :)

We had a great time here last night and the food was amazing as always.  This is a small homey BYOB restaurant but inside I always feel as if I am on vacation in a unique city.  Inside the place is accented with little lights hanging over the preparation table with a retro/oriental look as modern art has been placed on the walls. The food is consistently good here and the staff is always very friendly when we come into eat.

Dishes that I found to be exceptionally good were the King Kong specialty roll and the Miso soup.  I have also had the pleasure of trying the "well know" Godzilla roll before and I would with out a doubt recommend trying it.

The reason I give 4 and not 5 stars is because I felt the presentation of the food could of been better.  Maybe some cool designs or play with some of there sauces. Overall the food is fantastic. 

Thanks Umai Umai for a great time and good food!

What can I say that hasn't been said thus far.
I've been to this place few times over the years.  For Free every single time.  I forget how though.
This is a great museum for all; but for any self respecting medical professional, this is a must visit place (if and when you are in the philly area).
My greatest moment from this place was when during one of the visits, we were invited to a behind the scene room and shown some medical text books HUNDREDS of years old.  I thought that was a pretty cool experience, especially when we had only heard of those books prior to that time.

When I was at Drexel and still frequented UC for a happy hour or after work drink with friends this was a great watering hole.  Awesome friendly bartenders, cheap beer, and the best cheese fries Philly has to offer.  Great place to bring a group of friends for a beer or just a quick after a night of drinking place to eat that has consistent good food.

Although the ambiance is mediocre (a bit cheesy and impersonal) and the prices are not cheap, the pizza is perfect. I recently visited Italy this spring and can honestly say that I find Osteria's pizza to have a more flavorful, interesting, and refined taste than pizza in Italy. The tomato sauce has a complex sweet, salty, and tangy taste and you will find an unusual array of toppings such as octopus, red chili flakes, and smoked mozzarella... but the combinations are delectable. 

The pasta is not quite up to par with their pizza. The sauce is just good, but the pasta has a great texture: cooked al dente with just the right amount of doughiness. 

The grilled vegetables on their special menu are delicious, and make for a perfect appetizer with the pizza. I have never been much of a pizza fan, but Osteria gives pizza a different name, and it is definitely worth the trip to try it.

Small coffee shop but amazing homemade pastries and great drinks. The service is also super nice. Come early to pick a good spot, its often filled with people (but you can always find a comfy spot on the couch)
Great lunchtime escape for awesome chicken Pho

this review is for 2121 market st...I wish I could go below 1 star...although apartments are decent but the management is just plain awful and they go to great lengths to maintain this reputation...no maintainence...no customer service...first of all it's been over an year and they still haven't figured out why their fire alarm starts ringing in middle of night almost every 2 weeks and if you ask them their answer is they are working on it...second their is no front desk person during daytime and if you have a panel/package coming just forget about it...I have lost 4 packages in last 6 months and when I complained to the management their answer is "their is nothing we can do about it, you should get a PO box if you are want your packages" & if you get them delivered at 2040 market then your mail just sits there for weeks before anyone cares to put a slip on your mailbox informing you about mail. And moreover there is no after hour maintenance service, washer/ dryer breaks down in middle of laundry loads and no one is there to fix it because management cannot get hold of the maintenance people...trash room is  overflowing almost everyday and the list goes on. Oh and one thing they are good at is sending you notices about your pets peeing on sidewalk outside the building especially during the time when new leases are about to be signed to attract new people...overall I would it's just awful experience to be with them and I would never ever recommend these apartments to anyone.

My favorite place to go for Vietnamese hoagies. I have tried a few different sandwiches and they were all delicious. There are tables in there for you to sit and eat but I feel like its more for their friends who stop by. The only issue is for me is the parking.

I went here on a tuesday with my friend who recommended it and if you like Lebanese food this place is great. It is not fancy like a restaurant in northern libs, Suraya. But it's really delicious.  The platters are generous in their helpings and it really does fill you up. 

I got the chicken schwarma platter with the white toum sauce. ( I had to google this because the man behind the counter did not give me the name.) It tastes like garlic in dip form. I dipped the chicken in it and the pita and it was the perfect compliment to one another. It was my first time having that sauce so of course I asked for seconds! (They kindly gave me more.) 

My friend got the beef kebab platter and we pretty much got the same sides of tabouleh and baba ghanoush. The sides were fresh and delicious. The beef kebab though tasty it was a little dry to my liking so I preferred the chicken over the beef. 

I didnt get to try the dessert because we were so full but I hear that they are delicious as well. 

I'd definitely recommend if you want tasty food at a low key, and affordable place to eat lebanese food. I kind of enjoy these small tasty places because it's  not loud and the affordability makes it easy to come back often!

As a health and fitness professional for the past 6 years, I've met and had experiences with my fair share of massage therapists. Vernell is top notch. She truly and genuinely cares about each client and their experience -beyond the massage itself. I've watched Vernell grow her business from day one, and I have seen the amount of time, energy, and LOVE that she and Leighton have poured into their business. Vernell checks in with me every time to discuss problems areas and concerns, and she mixes in the perfect amount of attention to those areas in combination with relaxing techniques. As a fitness freak who lifts heavy six days a week, my muscles are often wound up and tight. I've never been disappointed after leaving Vernell. Most importantly, Vernell is well educated and truly understands the body. She combines her extensive knowledge with her amazing intuition and insight, and provides for an exceptional experience every time. Thank you, Vernell and Leighton! My search for a compassionate, qualified, and genuine massage therapist has ended with AVA Wellness. HIGHLY recommended!

Had generally a tasty experience here. I really wanted to try the lox but they sold out 6 of them to one person RIGHT before I ordered which was really unlucky. Luckily I also wanted to try the breakfast pierogis.

Breakfast pierogis were awesome - but I may be biased because I loved potato-based anything and sour cream, and pierogis in general are just the best. Our table had chicken and waffles which were also tasty, some regular dish with homefries and meat, and a vegan breakfast burrito.

I will say when I tried the breakfast burrito I did not like it at all. It tasted burned, but the person who was eating it didn't have any complaints so maybe it was just me.

Overall would still recommend trying this place for being cute,  and having some unique breakfast choices.

Brownies is a pretty cool pub on 2nd st. which sits amongst clubs like GLAM and 32 Degrees.  The downstairs is a little tight, but upstairs there is usually a little more room..."And they got a pool table too."  The food is your typical pub fare...wings,fries,grilled cheese.  The crowd is mostly laid back and friendly.  If you're looking for a place in Old City to grab a beer, don't want to pay $5 or hang out with Popped Collars...head to Brownies.
Sit at the bar for best service. The tomato soup with grilled cheese combos are great, and there's always something good on the draft list. It's just a nice, laid back neighborhood bar, otherwise.

Yummy! Chicken is well-seasoned and well-portioned. Sides: plantains, rice, string beans, all good!
Not a fancy place whatsoever, but I'll take delicious food over fancy any day. 
Food is so cheap, you don't feel bad about sampling everything. Will definitely return next time I'm in Philly!

I have known the owner Jeff Rosen for about 20 years, he is a great mechanic and has all the tools to insure whatever you need done is done without excuses, he has worked on my car's and never had a single issue with anything other than due to friendship he gives things away he really doesn't have to, not many people who do that these days, if you own an exotic car this is the place to come...... and not get ripped off

Have you ever walked into a building and one scent transports you somewhere else? One whiff of Suraya rocketed JJ and I right across the Atlantic.

Upon walking into Suraya, we were greeted at the bakery counter before ordering coffee, a za'atar man'oushe, and the slow roasted lamb hummus. We admired the beautiful tiled bar and surprisingly elegant seating area while Lebanese music piped softly overhead--and before long we were rewarded with our meal. We could have stopped at the hummus and flatbread, but the jalousie had caught my eye and JJ wanted a kruller, so we lingered over another cup of coffee and watched the morning patrons come and go.

The lovingly prepared food and general ambiance at Suraya makes me want to return again and again.

Dined at Ambra a couple of weekends ago with another couple. I believe the restaurant does not take more than 4 people at time and I was lucky enough to be able to snag a pretty last minute reservation for 4 on a Saturday night. 
While we did wait for almost 30 minutes past our reservation time, the restaurant offered us a drink on the house, which ended up being very delicious. Keep in mind that they only serve beer or wine and not hard liquor. 
The decor is spectacular. It's so cozy and beautiful. Absolutely lovely, romantic and graceful. 
The service was also spectacular. The servers were all dressed to impress. Very attentive and very professional. 
The food however, was less than impressive. The dishes were breathtakingly beautiful. Arranged to perfection and placed in front of us at the same time. With that said, my husband and I mutually agreed that we were not impressed with the actual flavors. Between the two of us, we liked maybe 2 or 3 dishes out of 7. Some dishes were extremely under-seasoned and one was extremely salty. Flavor combinations in some dishes didn't make sense and when eaten together just cancelled each other out basically. Some on the other hand tasted bland and unoriginal. For $350+ for a couple I expected way more and thought of this experience as a disappointing one and will not be returning. 
But on a very last and positive note, the restaurant did call me a few days prior to find out of our food restrictions if any. I was impressed. Perhaps some of our restrictions (no pork, shrimp or salmon) took away from our experience, but I would imagine that a creative chef could work around this and still deliver amazing flavors.

El Jarocho (I pronounced it El Jare-A-Co upon our first visit) is the first (and only make it) no frills, authentic, Mexican eatery we have dined at while living in the city limits. In fact I haven't had mexican home cooking like this since visiting New Mexico & 'Rado  (and it's better!). The servings are hefty and comforting to the foodie soul. Me and hubs love talking random shop with the PPD, who are also regular diners, trying different Mexi dishes that we can't even pronounce or understand, and tip our hearts out to the service (speedy & to the point - just how I like it) with a smile.

meh.
my brother lives near the italian market in south philly.  I went for a visit.  Luckily for me my brother and his girlfriend are foodies.  I was able to taste many different cuisines in Philly.  Coming from San Francisco, there are places I don't go due to the tourist trap aura, due to the non-authenticity of it all (fisherman's wharf, chinatown etc).  But when I was in Philly I had to have a cheesesteak...and I had to go to the two most famous places, which of course are right across the street from one another,  in a big rivalry, and featured on the Food Netowrk!  How cheesy, but essential.  
We split two, both 'onions wit whiz '?(cheese whiz)One from Geno's and one from Pat's.  
Aside from the memorabilia, everything about Geno's was a disappointment...the meat unsalted, the cheese whiz totally bland( which I find totally strange) and the bread completely bland...I hear Jim's is really the place...
Awesome!!!! The previous reviews pretty much covered it. Bring friends, try everything. Book your reservation waaaaay in advance.

When Best Buy first came into being, what wasn't there to love? 

Women & beer aside, it was a guy's paradise. A shining bastion of electronics, music, movies & video games. I've spent some dough there over the years.

I don't visit Best Buy nearly as often as I used to. However, in the handful of times I have stopped in to find particular CDs or DVDs over the past few years, they frequently do not have what I am looking for. It may be just this location in particular, I'm not sure. 

The music section has been shoved off to the side and the music DVD selection is a shadow of its former self. You can blame it on the digital revolution if you want, but I still fucking buy and collect CDs! Let the naysayers say nay. 

They have small signs in the aisles which read, "Don't see what you're looking for. We'll gladly order it for you and ship it to you free of charge." 

When I go walk into a brick & mortar store for an item, it's so I am able to take it home that day and listen to/watch it. Why the hell would I physically go to a Best Buy to have them ship me something at a later date? Ever hear of Amazon.com?

I can't say I would never go back to a Best Buy ever again, but it would most likely be to just window shop for audio/video components. I've found better prices on appliances and some electronics elsewhere.
Food was on point with the quality and timing it was served the ONLY downfall was that it was really hot and muggy thus making you drink a lot more than enjoying your food

Stopped by for a quick lunch.  Ordered a Korean wrap with pork - no kimchi to go. It was delicious and filling.  The place has plenty of seating and the atmosphere is nice. Will definitely be back to try some more things out!

This is the swankiest place I think in all of Philadelphia.  This hotel is seriously decadent.  It's got a HUGE parlor downstairs with lots of nice furniture, a Steinway, and a garden with a fountain out back.  The staff is impeccably helpful and polite.  The rooms are sumptuous and pricey.  They also rent out space for weddings and stuff.  I certainly can't afford this place, but if you have the money and are from out of town, definitely stay here.
Went there for karaoke. It was a lot of fun, can't speak for the food, but not bad overall place.

I was very excited to find some solid reviews for cleaning wedding dresses. Unfortunately this place ruined the bottom of mine. After charging me $85 for cleaning a dress that didn't even go outside due to my wedding being solely on zoom in my living room, my dress now has what looks like burn marks or that someone dragged it through dirt and a huge hole in the netting. So what exactly did I pay for? AVOID!

Terrible service. The girl with BBs in her face has a lot of attitude and blames it on other workers when she hands you the wrong food. Terribly managed and staffed. Check to make sure you get your order! They gave the people in front of us the wrong order too.

PlayArts changed my life! It's the absolute best addition to the neighborhood - super friendly staff, beautifully designed space, and most of all kiddos love it. If you're raising a kid in the city, you NEED to become a member, especially in the winter months.

I was very excited about visiting another Jose Garces restaurant, and after getting in after a 2 HOUR WAIT (they don't take reservations) I was minimally pleased.  Needless to say, a 2 hour wait for 4 people is excessive at best.  

I think their worst feature is their drinks.  Despite being called a Whiskey Bar, it was one of the weakest and smallest Manhattans that I have ever had.  $13 for less than 3 oz of a drink with a minimal liquor presence is inexcusable.  

The burger is good, however it is way over priced for what you get, I know it is Rittenhouse square neighborhood, but damn!  So the barebones Village Burger with any cheese is $14, including no fries at all, which are $5 and very good, but that is almost $20 for a sandwich and fries.  

For a great burger with even better fries go to Good Dog Bar for their Roquefort Cheese stuffed burger with copious amounts of sweet / idaho potato fries for $13.  Or even Five Guys.  Just saying that Village Whiskey is about $5 over-priced across the board.

Final words:  Try the Kentucky Hot Brown instead of the burger and skip the drinks.

WHAT A DELICIOUS MESS.  Really though, that juice gets everywhere. Down the chin, down the arms, etc.  But the roast beef is tender and the sandwiches delicious.  Came here before heading to the stadiums.  Left feeling full, satisfied, and only hating myself a little for the number of calories consumed.  Atmosphere is hole in the wall chic. And by that I mean dark lighting, small tables, bar.

Even if I had something bad to say about this place I wouldn't tell you lol Miguel was just that good. The food was good as well. For starters they give you nice hot what I believe was Ciabata bread and a white bean paste with olive oil for dipping it was verrrryyyyy different but good. Pizza crust was nice a good I knew it was good before I even took a bite. My girlfriend got the lunch portion salmon fettuccine she liked it as well. The caprese is always my favorite sweet tomatoes with the savory fresh moz  gets me every time not to heavy either it's just right. Back to Miguel the star of the afternoon lol great waiter attentive I mean ATTENTIVE the sun was beaming on us and as soon as other guest left their table he immediately grabbed with umbrella and put it at our table for shade. That's when I knew we were coming back lol jk but every was really great here I'd recommend it.
Awesome food , awesome service . My favorite thing on he menu is the wedge salad with the steak on it .
Often underappreciated deli with great hoagies, sausage sandwiches, etc.

Darling's is just okay - I live around the corner so end up here more often than I would like given it's late hours etc.

Don't expect to go here to get your typical diner expierence, i.e greasy, cheap and delicious food....This is place is attempting to be a high class diner which I could do without.  Give me a $4 BLT at 2AM and I am a happy girl.

All in all the servers are attentive, somewhat slow, but attentive and the food is mediocre.  But paying over $10 bucks for an omlet is just not necessary!
Delicious pastries and breads. Everything is fresh baked and amazing. Their pancakes are fluffy and delicious. Try their Thai turkey salad.

Went with a bunch of friends for lunch. A lot of them were not familiar with Chinese food, and there were even some things I wasn't sure what it was. The problem is that for certain items, nobody could really give us a good description (in English) of what it was. The whole place is a bit loud and chaotic as the servers come by with carts of food, and when you order something they put circles on your ticket to charge you for your orders. When you are done dining, you take the ticket up to the front to pay, and you have no idea what they are charging you for so you basically have to trust that each server properly notated your ticket. On top of that, a lot of the plates, silverware, and cups were not very clean. They don't even give you any ice water unless you wave somebody down and ask for some. The food was alright, so I'll give them 3 stars, but the cleanliness and service were a bit lacking.

I'd eat Chinese food every single day. Some weeks I almost do just that, and those are the weeks that I pop up at King's Garden very, very often. The staff is so sweet and welcoming every single time and my food is always PIPING hot and scrumptious.

I always order to-go and no matter the order's size or how busy they seem to be, I'm always set to grub within 10-15 minutes. Their egg rolls make me happy. I tend to judge restaurants by the fresh crispness of their egg rolls--as well as the filling--and these are perfect every single time. I'll eat them hot or cold, for breakfast, lunch, dinner or the midnight snack. 

The menu is pretty extensive. I haven't had anything yet that I wouldn't order again. I started off by ordering 1 of the 3 things I usually order (in addition to the egg rolls), and since each one was tasty I felt very comfortable exploring other parts of the menu. The staff is also very eager to help you expand your palate, which I appreciate as a repeat customer. They also have specials so if you're calling in, be sure to ask about them.

It's wonderful to have an ol' faithful in your neighborhood that will stand in faithfully on those days when you just need some comfort food. It's a great location--midway down the Hill next to the Post Office and the CH Hotel & Grill--with very reasonable portions and prices. My usuals are the Singapore Rice Noodles, the Orange Beef and the Four Flavor Beef. Don't forget the egg rolls!
Soft bread, excellent overall steak and cheese. whiz wit!

5 stars for customer service.  When they first opened, they did not carry a hard to find food that I had been traversing across town to pick up for my dog with allergies.  Knowing that I was local, they started to carry the food, yay.  

Over the New Year holiday I ran out of food. Meant to pick it up, forgot.  First time I have actually ran out of food and when I realized, it was too late....holiday weekend and the store was closed.  So I borrowed some food from a neighbor...not ideal because it wasn't the special food...and  I called / e-mailed the store to see if they could help me over the holiday weekend and sure enough after coordinating his hectic walking schedule, he contacted us to meet him at the store to pick up the food.  

Thank you Walk It Like A Dog!

For tapas portions I expected much more savory dishes. The best thing that we ordered was the lamb.  Overall I was mislead and let down.  I guess if you are interested in drinking wine and having a small accompaniment this could be a good place.  I unfortunately arrived a little hungry and left $80 later and went to Dos Segundos.  Other reviewers are being way too generous.  I agree though that the atmosphere is inspired.
Don't be deceived by the looks.... This place might look a little "shady" but it's what's inside that counts... I'll keep it brief : the cheesestake was awesome. Go there and get it. Now.
Very neat place. I love the decor and relaxed atmosphere. Coffee is delicious. I love their peaberry coffee. Friendly service too.

This is our first experience at this kind of restaurant and was very happy with the food and service.  The restaurant was clean and the meat was delicious.  For me, the salad bar was nothing special but I was there to try the different kinds of meat.  I felt that the price was about right.  We didn't order much alcohol though.    I didn't realize there was a dress code.  It didn't seem strict though because our friend was wearing a t-shirt and they didn't say anything.  The price was a bit high but given the level of service, cleanliness, and quality, I can say that it's worth it.  If the price was better, I would have easily given Chima 5 stars.  Overall, a great experience and we'll definitely go back sometime.

This place is absolutely terrible!!! My friend and I both signed up at the same time and cancelled at the same time.  We went in and filled out the cancellation form.  Two months later my friend realized that she was getting charged so she told me to check.  Needless to say, I was getting charged as well.  We both called they said they would refund us our money and stop charging us.  My friends money was refunded mine never was.  6 months later I notice they have still been charging me.  I go in today and they are completely unorganized and unprofessional.  They claim they can not find the paperwork that I signed to cancel therefore I would not get my money back.  They made me sign the same form I signed in March.  They are so outdated with technology.  The gym is small and horrible.  The locker room is small and terrible.  More importantly they don't conduct business the way it should be conducted.  My issue is you should not steal money from people and that's exactly what they did to me.  Please I suggest that if you are looking for a gym  this place is not the answer....don't waste your time.  HORRIBLE!!!!!

This is my secret, late night, post-bar hoagie stop.  The morning after going there I woke up and thought... 1) ma, my head is pounding; 2) why am I wearing a sombrero; and 3) did I dream about that hoagie, or did I really have a world class hoagie at 3am?  
Never did get the answer to my first question, but I've been back to what I affectionately call "The Full Neslon" several times and know for sure that this place makes a well crafted hoagie.  Good meats, plenty of toppings, and very fresh veggies (they add crunch to the experience - perfect for a drunken snack).  I recommend the Italian with everything on it.  Tip the nice girls up front for putting up with your shenanigans.

I have been craving for their wings for a while based on all the good reviews, and I finally stopped in today to give it a shot.

It's Saturday lunch time, I was surprised that there was no line there. They raised price for $9.39/pound of wings which is not a big deal. 

Since it was my first time to try their wings, I ordered a mix of one pound of classic, smoked and San Antonio wings. The wings were good, but not as good as I was expecting. They are absolutely tender, somewhat flavorful, but not impressive. I would say it's just average chicken wings. 

The staff were nice and friendly. 

If you like chicken wings, Moriarty's is absolutely the first choice!

WOWZA!!  I've made Crown Roast of Lamb for special occasions several times over the years, and this was the absolute best I've ever tasted!  Flavorful but not game-y, and just delicious.  Nick made 2 racks into a beautiful crown with frenched tips, and it was perfect for a gathering of 4.  Price was great for all of the labor that went into it, too.  Thank you, and see you again soon!

YIKES!!! I have been a longtime customer of Oteri's 5th St. location...choosing them to fulfill all of my cakes for our special occasions...but the LAST 4 cakes that I've gotten from them have been off...VERY FAR FROM PERFECT...the most recent, my 50th Bday cake last month...they used to do an extremely wonderful job...I don't know what's been going on over these last few years, but it may be time for me to stop patronizing them...and entrust another bakery with my needs.
**FOOTNOTE...THIS WAS NOT THE LOOK OF THE CAKE THAT I ENVISIONED/ORDERED...WANTED IT SIMILIAR TO THE DESIGN OF MY BDAY PARAPHERNALIA...ONLY WITH WHITE ICING...BUT YOU SEE WHAT I GOT**
We ordered red velvet pancakes, creme brûlée French toast, Mac and cheese, and Spanish fries. Too much two people, but very good!

So in Philly there are 3 places that i get french toast for brk.  Here, Sabrina's and Green Egg cafe.  The portion they give you here, as the pictures show put this just past the other 2 for this one item.

Canolli filling, shaved chocolate, pistashios, bananas, and that thick cut challah bread make this a must visit.

When you come, come for this.


Cons:
Cash only.  The bathrooms have no ventilation and are like 90 degrees year-round.

You know you'll get a consistently decent pizza every time you come here.  And their other menu options such as salads and entrees are pretty good too.  It is great for large parties or a table for two.  My only qualm was at a recent visit, we ordered the 4 cheese pizza, but they would not allow us to put any toppings on it because it was a "special."  And we weren't allowed to "make our own" with the four cheeses either, with no valid reason.  Hmph.

Overall, a reasonably priced pizza joint that is a good standby.

This place was beautiful and the food was delicious but I'm taking away a star because the staff was unsettlingly gentle in their interactions with the customers and transfixed with the menu. I understand that's a weird criticism but it was honestly distracting from the meal. Maybe I've been living on the east coast too long... 

But you should still go and get the scallops. They're the bomb.

Unassuming spot but service and food was excellent!  I'm Philly for EW&F concert. Found this spot from Philly visitors site. Walked into a pool hall but followed road to restaurant side. Glad we didn't follow our instinct to flee. Good was fresh and they have daily special additions on a chalkboard. I had the seared salmon which was delicious. Hubby had the grilled chicken. Yummy. Priced perfectly (entrees under $20 bucks). Will go back next time we're in Philly. The other patrons were super friendly and kind as well.

Like several of the other reviewers, I got stuck with this worthless company when I tried to use my equally worthless home warranty from HSA to fix my broken refrigerator. The first repairman they sent out was very rude and in my house less than 10 minutes to diagnose the problem. He gruffly told me it was beyond repair and he recommended a replacement. I honestly think he just didn't want to be bothered doing work. Nearly a week goes by and I can't get in touch with the insurance company or Always Affordable, but then they text me to send out another repairman for a second opinion. The second guy they sent out was more professional, but I still wasn't completely buying his diagnosis of the problem. He said "I'll submit the issue to your insurance company to see if it's covered, but I doubt it. They never want to cover anything". Again, I think they just wanted to collect the $100 deductible and be on their way. When I asked if I could just pay him to fix the issue immediately he told me he didn't have the proper equipment and that I should call to set up a follow up visit. I have have made multiple phone calls to their multiple numbers, sent emails, and even tried text messages for close to a week. No response what so ever.  Stay far, FAR away from this company!

So when I first came here I felt out of place on the Super Free Sundays because it felt like a big inside joke that everyone who attends class here was in. If you weren't in then you didn't feel at home.

But I've taken improv classes here and I love the place. The instructors are talented and skilled. I learned valuable life lessons here. The community is super friendly. The shows are cheap (or free if you're a current student). There's ample seating and most days of the week there are shows going on. So come on out and learn some improv! It's SO much fun!

This is one of my favorites! I moved up to north east Philly from VB, VA and living at the beach meant always eating at waterfront restaurants. This place reminds me of home and I love bringing visitors to enjoy the great food and atmosphere!

Great shoe repair. Prices are fair, he uses very good materials (Vibram soles, etc...), he asks all the right questions before doing repairs.  He even managed to remove a lousy sole on a pair of sneakers and replace it with a great vibram sole. I've had him repair shoes and soles, and he sells Tingley over-shoes! I've since moved to West Philadelphia and tried other closer places, but there's no point--Villa's is the best. 

Give him your business!

Walked in after doing some research and knew what I wanted. I came in with a few options. My mother was with me. Walter, our salesperson, was very patient and answered our questions courteously. He didn't rush us and let us take our time. We ended up picking one of the couches I researched. The price I saw online was the price I paid with financing, which I feel is a good deal.

Beautiful stone house turned into a restaurant.  This place is packed every night.  Great place to go for a cozy meal.  The grass-fed burger is very delicious.  The menu is somewhat overpriced though.

After reading reviews I was skeptical.  Maybe it's because I'm from New York but we found like 2 things not working??  The exhibits change (at least since I was hear last) and there was really good live music celebrating Hanukkah which thrilled my family and I had the drag the kids away from each exhibit!  All in all awesome time!!!

Best Italian meal I ever had out at a restaurant. My two friends and I shared three different appetizers, entrees and desserts. Every last drop was gone! David was a doll and I can't wait to return. My only regret is not going sooner!

I came here this past Friday with a friend of mine and overall it was a really good experience: knowledgeable and friendly staff, good (some amazing) food, and a great price point for the quantity and quality. I'd give it a 4.5 if I could. 

The two of us shared five small dishes, which our waitress coursed for us. Five dishes between two was plenty since they brought out complimentary dishes as well (butternut squash soup with a bread puff, as well as crispy potatoes with shishito peppers). I'm not sure if they always do this, but it seemed that everyone around us received the same dish, a nice touch. 

The food:
Butternut squash soup w/bread puff. Normally I'm not a big fan of butternut squash soup because it's a bit too sweet, but this was perfect. Creamy, a little salty, and a little sweet. 

[Raw] 
Off the raw menu we ordered the striped jack with avocado and grilled jalapeno oil. The dish is simple, fresh, and beautifully assembled. The sashimi is melt in your mouth tender, the jalapeno oil and scallion add the perfect amount of tanginess and spice, and the avocado puree adds a creaminess that complements the fish beautifully. Probably my favorite dish of the night. 

[Vegetables] 
Beets with sheep's milk ricotta. I liked this dish a lot, but it was also highly reminiscent of my morning yogurt with granola.... good but not that interesting. 

Crispy potatoes with shishito peppers. The potatoes were definitely crispy and very flavorful (they incorporated a lot of Indian spice). The peppers added a nice little kick as well.

[Small plates]
Spaghetti squash salad, crispy egg, mushroom & leek vinigrette. The components of the dish were definitely interesting, but the dish itself just wasn't very good. The panko breaded sunny side egg was cold and the spaghetti squash was bland. I could have seen the dish being better had the egg been warm, but the cold yolk mixed with crunchy spaghetti squash just didn't do it for me. 

Salt cold and semolina croquette, saffron cream. This was my friend's favorite dish, but it was a bit too heavy and salty for my taste. 

Grilled octopus and escarole caesar. I really enjoyed this dish: large chunks of tender and crispy octopus, buckwheat lettuce and endives for freshness and pomegranate seeds for a nice crunch. The dressing was really refreshing as well. 

Date cake with coconut ice cream. Yummy, but very sweet!

We ended up paying about $50 a person for all of that, not too bad. 
Overall I had a really good meal and I definitely want to come back and try a few more dishes at some point.

I ordered vegetable fried rice and when I went to pick it up every time I asked for something like sauce or utensils they saiid something to coworkers and laughed.
Then they wouldn't let me use my card to pay.
To top it off there was PORK IN MY VEGETABLE FRIED RICE!
And the rice and vegetables were the most bland I had from anywhere. I thought there reviews were fake because so low but accurate asf

Update: I received a nice note from the owner explaining that there had been a mixup in assembling my order while putting another order together at the same time. I've been assured that their marinara is always meat-free unless you request meat to be added to it. With this knowledge, we'll be adding Evo back into our delivery rotation, although might be a bit wary of the sauce at first. 

Original: Loved this place until this last order... the "marinara" that accompanied our garlic knots came with a nice chunk of beef in it. Makes me question whether any of their red sauce is meat-free.

Fast and good!! Pleasant staff even with the never ending line!  Started on line at the door and was able to put in my order in about 2 mins and picked up the order in @ 12 mins.  Hot, juicy and flavorful!!

This place was seriously the only breakfast place that seemed to be open on a Monday morning! Then again it was pretty early. However! The breakfast food definitely hit the spot. I ordered a Western omelet and hash browns. The hash browns were really perfect. I mean soft yet crunchy, not burnt or overly crispy. This place seems like a whatever, mediocre/average place to grab food but I think it's worth checking out to satisfy any breakfast needs. It definitely satisfied mine! I also ordered a coffee. It was affordable, quick, and portions were generous. 

Go if you're in the area!

Mission Taqueria is one of my favorite mexican restaurants in philly! Not only do they have a great happy hour, but the regular menu is reasonably priced as well. My favorite is the green juice margie and the chicken tacos. But honestly, everything is delicious. It is a great fun atmosphere in here, but it can get really loud and crowded.

3 words--Red Velvet Pancakes. If you're looking for a more savory dish, I'm into the chicken and waffles. It's not your traditional home style C&W, but has an over easy egg on top with the perfect combo of sweet and salty. They always have rotating specials, but stick to the OG dishes. This is my favorite brunch spot in Philly--multiple locations, but this one I've frequented the most. Make sure you account for wait times on weekends--it's always at least a 30 min wait. Also, it's cash only.

holy moly jesus help me

i think my taste buds died and went to pastry heaven!

this place is the real deal holyfield, some gluten-free places pass off pastries that still have added sugar, and other dairy/soy products. but sweet freedom! these guys use a majority of coconut based (milk & oil) and the natural agave sweetener! their ingredients are helpfully posted with all of their lovely choices (how do you decide! i wanted to try them all!) and on their website. this is helpful because i hate being that 'what's in this' guy esp when the bakery gets busy (in a span of 15 minutes that i was in there there was definitely a lot of traffic). 

the space is pretty small, but this bakery is all about the food goods. i also overheard them able to make cakes if you order ahead of time! 

honestly, if not for bulk up (and compared to the opposition these are a LOT healthier for you) but still sweets nevertheless, and they are a bit on the pricey side, but understandingly, there is so much devotion, love, and deliciousness in these sweet baked amazing goods of wonderfulness.

Good hotel.  Standard Marriott. Room service food wasn't great. I had dinner and breakfast. There's a Starbucks and the hotel is clean. There was a mouse in our meeting room. There's a nice bar and it's been remodeled. Great location to really good dining and shopping.
Super tasty! So glad to have this yummy restaurant in the neighborhood! Everything we've had has been delicious and service is great and helpful for newbies
Fantastic food! Great place: warm and welcoming. Great beer selection. Chicken sandwich was off the chain! There's a bar and more seating in the back. I'll be back!

If you like french wine, this is your place. I find white (a jacquere from savoie) and red varietals here by the glass that do not exist elsewhere in our largely wine devoid city. Small rose selection as well. The food is consistent so we eat there as well. The portions are not huge but the flavors and ingredients are well done. The plates are well presented. Yes, the service can be slow but always friendly.

Over-hyped and underwhelming translates to significant disappointment. Based on Ruth Reichl's recent review which considered Vetri to be one of the best Italian restaurants on the East coast, we visited last night and tried the tasting menu. While items sound interesting: sweet onion crepe, duck terrine with quince mostarda, chestnut fettucine with wild boar ragu, etc., it was the spinach gnocchi that wins the prize. The gnocchi was excellent, though the many of the other 8 dishes seemed overdone and incongruous. Many small plates were technically well executed, though together lacked coherence. The restaurant itself is a bit too homey, with commercial radio playing in the background and sound reverberating off the walls. The entrance to the restaurant is too small and a 30 minute delay spent standing in confined quarters adds little to the experience. 

If this is the best of Philadelphia, I would recommend saving at least $200 and trying ANY other neighborhood restaurant. -The ambiance will be similar and while the spinach gnocchis will not be the same, you will have a much more pleasant dining experience and you might be pleasantly surprised.

I want to give four stars -- delicious food; appealing, atypical menu and authentic items, compared to other local Indian restaurants; pleasant and efficient service -- but the main dish prices hold me back.   The apps stood out for value and taste -- outstanding, big, fresh dosas; delicious gobi manchurian cauliflower; medhu vada, etc.  But at $16, $17, $18, the main chicken and mutton dishes were tiny, barely enough for two to share.  Also, there's nothing "resplendent" about the Chestnut Street location.  For sure, it's clean, spare, plain, but very noisy because high ceiling and bare floors and tables.  BYOB.

Lee's is a neighborhood gem! Their standard cheesesteaks and hoagies are all great, but where they really shine is in their creative ones like the Korean cheesesteak or the Game Over. 

They have probably my favorite chicken cheesesteak out there, the chicken is never dry. A chicken cheesesteak with spinach and fried onions (basically the Game Over without the shrimp) is my go to.

They do great greasy brunch/breakfast too. The steak egg and cheese cheesteak is fantastic, and their chicken and waffles are great (their wing dings are on point!) Beyond that, their friend rice is great as well. The creamy srirachi sauce is AMAZING.

Best of all is how nice Lee and the whole staff are. Always puts me in a good mood when I'm in there!

I like the Chicken French Toast.
It is great.

Its a shame they got rid of Chef Val. 
I like his food.

I used to be a rare customer - maybe an omlet here and there...(the omlets were always excellent btw)

Until I discovered the COFFEE.  They serve Douwe Egberts coffee, and it is amazing.  Since I tried it, I can't have coffee anywhere else! It really puts HubBub (located across the foyer) and all of the Starbucks' in the city combined to shame because the coffee here costs 3 times less and is infinitely better!  It is also served at COFFEE temperature - i.e. HOT, and not lukewarm like most other places.  A lukewarm cup of coffee that doesn't even taste good for $5? No, thank you.  An awesomely tasty, steaming cup of wonderful dutch coffee for under $2? YES PLEASE, I'll take 2!

And as if this alone weren't enough:
- The staff is super friendly.  They remember their customers and can have the orders ready pretty much when they see you coming. They always greet you with a smile! 
- LevelUp is accepted here.
- Did I mention, the coffee is great?

I have never eaten here.. strictly for delivery.  I was pleasantly surprised by the turkey hoagie I ordered.  Loved it.. and will definitely order again.  If you like a hoagie full of meat, this is a great option.

This place has the best burritos and tacos on campus. The ingredients are fresh and the service is good.

Unlike chipotle, they do not use copious amounts of msg in their food so you will be less likely to have a food considered after eating here.

Everything is so flavorful and they offer a variety of hot sauces if you want more of a kick. Each hot sauce is distinct so I recommending trying all of them yo see which one you prefer. 

The guac deserves a mention here too. The guac has hints of lime and onion and little chunks of avacado in it so even if it is put in food, you can distinguish it from what you are eating. It does not seem like you are eating mush.

The sweet potato tacos and burritos are very good here. The taco shells are firm but soft and enjoyable to eat. They put a lot but not too much in it so it is hard to eat and you can eat it without fearing that the contents are going to spill over.

The burritos here are so much better than chipotle. It is not runny and not full of liquid or juice depending on what you call it like those of chipotles. This means witheach bit you can taste the different ingredients instead of having everything meld into one like the Chipotle burritos.

The only good thing about this place is the view. They charge ridiculous prices for you to be drinking out of plastic cups and eating on paper plates. The staff members were also extremely rude about their "no standing policy". I am 100% willing to follow COVID rules (ex wearing masks at all times except when sitting), but they were being ridiculous about it. When me and my friends had finished up, we stood up to leave and were yelled at across the restaurant immediately after getting on our feet to sit down due to their no standing policy. We were all confused because the only reason we stood up is because we were getting ready to leave. The employees quite literally made a scene, yelling and waving at us like we were children, over us standing for 10 seconds. When we told them we were only standing because we were grabbing our belongings and leaving, the host says "then you need to put on your masks and leave." Wtf? That's exactly what we were getting ready to do. I understand having rules and restrictions, of which we followed, but they don't have to be so rude about it. Will definitely not be returning.

Lots of fun to walk around and sip coffee and enjoy all the colors and smells and sounds. A good place to look and taste. I walked around in the morning and got breakfast and then got my coffee to go and went to all the little shops around looking for treats for the family.

So delicious and awesome service! The second you turn over your card saying you want more food there is about 4 people lining up at your table to serve you! The buffet is also delicious. It's impossible to leave hungry! It's a little expensive but worth it.

So... only one guy working on a Saturday morning? Oh I'm sorry..there were two other people there... one asking about "stuff" that was left around and walked out of the bathroom with her shirt off.... and 1 who took about 30 minutes to read a receipt and then finally checked in someone who walked in after us.   Neither of the other 2 could help us  (Why they were there I have NO idea) and the customer in front of us was taking a super long time.  NOT at the fault of the ONE guy working.. can't remember his name but he was great.. Kept apologizing while trying his best to help the girl in front of us.  Once it was our turn, he was great. Smooth transaction. (Reserved online)   Only 4 stars because obviously the scheduler doesn't know how to schedule and why pay 2 worthless people ? other than that.. great place.

If you're looking for an experience reminiscent of being the main character in a horror film, look no further. Since elevators sometimes don't work, the stairwell is the perfect place for a mugging. The dimly lit hallways capture that serial-killer ambiance we all want in a hotel. You can rip up your zoo passes, as this quaint delight comes with its own array of tiny wildlife. Beds are set to stone hard and the walls are ethereal. Live-action entertainment much like reality TV echoes in the hallways all night long. My experience included a yelling man banging on my neighbor's door at 3am, "cmon hun, lemme in." I instantly stepped into an episode of Keeping Up with the Kardashian's and was at the edge of my seat with anticipation. Last but certainly not least was the promised breakfast. The vast selection of 5 Nature Valley granola bars and coffee was a delicious treat to wake up to. I must have just missed the herd of breakfast eaters as breakfast was 6am-9am, and at 7am when I arrived on the floor the basket of granola bars was nearly empty. If this hotel is booked when you pass through, I'd suggest finding a nearby hostel or car for a similar experience.

I cannot express enough how nice this woman is.  I got all four wisdom teeth taken out at once and did not feel a thing or experienced alot of pain afterwards. They are just honest people working hard there.  The procedure was so fast too.  She was great.  However, my appointment was at 1PM and did not get seen until 215.  That was a bit nerve racking.

came here for a nice night out with my girls. Sat at a high top, waitress was less than sufficient. Waited 20 minutes for an older waitress with dark curly hair to even come over and greet us. Walked past us several times after making eye contact, still kept walking. She kept going out of her way to assist a table at the back with complete disregard to us.

Amazing cocktails!
Had the Dr. Zhivago and tried a few others.  Loved the vibe of the whole place and the bartenders are definitely legit.

Can't wait to go back and try the food, because it all looked and smelled delicious.

A haiku:

Acceptable food,
above average coffee,
and no wifi? Word.

This place is amazing.  The cookies are kept warm, so each one you order seems like it just came out of the oven.  The staff is very friendly and delivery options are amazing.  Where else could you order a chocolate peanut butter cup cookie (the best option) at 2am?!

Really, don't bother. I have a reason.


I had a lunch planned at a restaurant that seats only til 3:00pm in the Center City. 

[1:30pm] 
Made a call for a cab. Someone who clearly feels miserable working there takes my reservation and said it would arrive in 15 minutes. 

[1:56pm] 
Wanted to check up and called. Some nice lady picked up and said there's no such reservation made for me. She was actually really nice and was apologizing so I let it go and made another reservation. She said she'll try her best to expedite the process but the normal wait is around 15-20min. I said ok.

[2:18pm]
Called to check where the driver was so I can get ready to be out. She said she will call the driver to see and call me right back. 

[2:20pm]
She called me and said the cab already came and left because I didn't pick up my phone. I never got the call. I asked which number the driver used and they had my number wrong, the last digit. At this point I am getting very annoyed because it's clear I won't make it to lunch in time. She said she'll call the driver again and call me right back.

[2:25pm] 
She wasn't calling me right back so I called again. asking what's going on and she said that driver is too far off so she's going to send me closer one. 

[2:33pm]
I get a call from her that the driver should be here soon so I should go outside. I told her I was already outside and she said 'look for the blue car'.... So I did but I saw no taxi. She said she'll check and call back.
My boyfriend got furious watching me go through this nonsense and started calling other cab companies. 

[2:43pm]
Again, no call back so I called. She said the driver is on his way so I should wait. She apologized but nothing really mattered at this point. She said she'll do her best to get me the cab, 'soon'


[2:50pm]
We got into the cab that my boyfriend called about less than 20 min ago.


I called the company to cancel my reservation because I didn't want the driver to make worthless trip since It's not really the driver's fault that I was stood up for so long. She apologized and all when I was canceling it but I was just upset. The system is just out of control and the service sucks. It's really questionable how they're still in business...
We didn't make it to the restaurant we originally had planned obviously but thank God it wasn't anything super important.

So, please do yourself a favor and cross this company out from the list.

I live 3 blocks from Locust Bar.  It was great to see Locust Bar go SMOKELESS during the pandemic.  Many times I've walked by and felt like a beer and some lunch but the smoking always makes me forego it.  What is puzzling is with a hospital across the street with hundreds of non-smoking medical professionals, this bar appeals to a dying (literally) niche market.  Keep Locust Bar SMOKE FREE!

A wonderful cheesesteak and beer oasis at 1533 Chestnut in Center city. The cheesesteak was very tasty but small but inexpensive especially considering you're in Center City. The beer was priced right. Big screen TVs and you don't feel like you're in Center City. I liked it. Finding a parking spot on Chestnut helped the whole experience.

He did a terrific job my toes. I used to have bad fungus on my toenails but using his anti fungal tea tree ointment my nails are perfectly healthy again. Other doctors to me there was no treatment and I would have to suffer with disfigured toenails for life. With regular check ups and ointment treatments I can finally not feel shamed wearing flip-flops ever again. I would highly recommend!

The wait is long but it's totally worth the wait. A bit disappointed that I ordered the lemon pound cake and it wasn't in my bag but I still wasn't disappointed with my experience. I got shrimp, greens, and mac n cheese and the Mac n cheese alone puts me in heaven. It's refreshing to see this black owned business stay afloat during the pandemic, the customer service was A1, and I will continue to support!

Little Fish came up small...  there wasn't much ambience to speak of in this restaurant, a no frills restaurant that seats 22.  It's very loud and the two waitresses while nice, did not do much more than serve us our courses- she initially filled our glasses with water and wine but after the first pour, she never came around again. The big disappointment was that she never came back to ask us how our food was which we actually needed to send something back.
I ordered the east coast selection of oysters to start, I can not recall the name but they were perfectly shucked.

I ordered the peekatoe crab appetizer, this dish was spicy and delicious- it was presented beautifully and was very satisfying. My husband had the octopus dish and he rave about how tender the meat was with just the perfect hint of smokey to it.

On to the entree, I had the tuna- it was a substantial size and the combination of greens- I was a bit underwhelmed with it- it was fine but for the price, I thought there would something more than ordinary.
DH ordered the monkfish- which was described to him as the consistancy of salmon. It was described as having bits of monkfish tartar on the dish as well. The center portion of his fish was cold and gelatinous. We had to flag down the other waitress )as ours disappeared) and asked her to sent it back. We were disappointed that they threw the same fish back on the grill to finish cooking, instead of cooking a new portion. However, it was replated, but once it came back, it was once again still squishy. We finally got a hold of our waitress and she told us that their chef's cook their fish this way because flaky is a well done. Since my husband had an expectation of a flaky salmon like fish, he was put off a little. Back it went and it came back again. No apology for the miscommunication or anything. Our evening started off with flying colors but alas fell flat. I don't know if they were trying to turn the table over, but to have a $40 entree treated in such a blase attitude, really didn't sit well this us. We decided to leave and not indulge in dessert. I was overall disappointed as this was one of our few dates nights since our second baby arrived:(

This practice is amazing, and Dr. Dalessandro is the best. 

Seriously, I don't have a lot more to say than that! Scheduling is easy, parking is convenient, the billing is transparent and easy to work with, the wait times were never more than 20 minutes, and I really felt like Dr. Dalessandro went above and beyond in terms of patient care. I'm actually going to miss going to her now that my feet are getting better! 

Would recommend this practice to anyone!

Great food. I don't enjoy Mexican food, but thats because I never had authentic Mexican food like this before. Fresh, and delicious. Not a typical Mexican restaurant.  
Staff friendly. Just be patience, everything is made from scratch so it's little bit of a wait.
Went to Nook tonight for a gallery showing. Did not have the coffee, but the company, the baked goods,  and atmosphere were spelindid. Will be going back for coffee soon!

Pizza by the slice, Open late when everything in Center City is closed. And the pizza is delicious. What's there not to like? If you stay and eat in there is a bigger menu including vegetarian options and alcohol

This is probably the best option in the area for basic American style take out Chinese food. The quality is a cut above the average and the portions are quite large.  Overall they seem to use fresh, high quality ingredients.  I recommend the Mango Chicken and Mongolian Beef.
The owner and staff are always very friendly and orders are prepared promptly. 

My only wish is that this restaurant would add some real authentic Chinese dishes to their menu.  For the most part, I think their menu was designed to appeal to the large number of senior citizens who live the high rise residential complexes near the restaurant, but a lot of Asian Penn grad students have also been moving into the area, and I would think they could be quite successful with some real Chinese dishes on the menu, so people don't have to go all the way to Chinatown.

I went to this location to have a damaged product replaced, that was brand new. Spoke with customer service and the called over the outdoor department sales associate. She went looking for the replacement and came back and said they had none and no other Lowes in the area had it either. I asked her three times if she could take down my information to call me if any came in or if the manager could order the piece and she kept brushing me off and telling me it was between me and the manufacturer. I called another Lowes in the area and asked if they had the item in stock and they said they had three currently available. So, either their stock counts are incorrect or the employee did not know the appropriate channels to look through. We even called customer service prior to going to this particular Lowes.
The hot fresh donuts are soooo good! And a very good price. Only 15 dollars for a dozen.

Dined at the Love on NYE with my love and it was an absolutely fabulous experience! Kind hosts, wonderful ambiance, amazing food! We tried the NYE special pistachio cheese ball and the torched broccoli for apps, and the truffle gnudi (pear, toasted chestnut brown butter sauce). We lovedddd everything! Especially the broccoli! They had a special NYE house punch which was absolutely delish as well! Cannot wait to go back! This place hit the spot. See it being a popular fine dining spot in the near future.

I'm reviewing the brunch here specifically since I've never been here for dinner. My buddy picked this place based on a sandwich he was hoping would be available from the Philadelphia Magazine list (pirogi sandwich?). 

Anyway, I had an egg white frittata while he had the burger. So egg whites in a skillet, whatever...tastes just like it sounds. The potatoes were good though. But it's his burger that really deserves this review. He was pretty impressed by it, said the patty had a nice consistency. Shame I'm on this health kick, and eating vicariously through him. As he's yelping vicariously through me I suppose. The burger was even aesthetically pleasing. 

The service and place was nice and pleasant. I would give it a higher rating if they had a better menu for the vegetarian/ health conscious person. But that's really my problem, not theirs....*sigh*

Not worth it at all. I wouldn't go back and if I hear anyone considering it I'd warn against it.

I got calamari to start, and was beyond salty. Think salt water. The flavor was nothing special, mundane food. I wish it had been at least just boring tasting, without the incredible, overwhelming presence of salt so I could have dismissed the mediocrity. Unfortunately, though the saltiness ruined any chances of it being even average. 

I paid $10 for that salty experience.

We also got chicken and waffles, which was around $15. For $15 I was expecting a few waffles, at the very least a whole waffle. What we got was half a waffle and two pieces of average tasting chicken. The gravy sauce that came with it was good, but not enough to make it worth $15 dollars. 

$15 dollars for average chicken, half a mediocre waffle and six or seven spoonfuls of chicken gravy. 

For the price the quality of the food here is extremely lacking.
I won't ever be going back.

I finally got a chance to try Honey's. Its been hyped by a few of my friends, and for a good reason. Honey's is a great atmosphere for a Sunday morning breakfast or brunch. Their portions are small enough to fill your appetite without taking a doggie bag or stuffing your self senseless. 

Their menu is massive and usually less than $11 per meal. With so many delicious choices, don't be surprised to find your self staring at the menu for a few minutes. It was extremely busy, but the service was still speedy. My coffee mug and my glass of water were never empty, very impressive. 

I plan on going back to Honey's in the near future for another go at the menu. I highly suggest trying Honey's if you are patient enough for a long wait, its worth it though.

Came here to celebrate a friend's birthday months ago, and I finally realized I never left a review for this enjoyable restaurant in Center City!

Overall, I'm surprised that Valanni doesn't have the 4-star rating it deserves. Throughout our dinner, we were served with delicious food of all sorts of variety - for both the vegetarians and non-vegetarians. I still remember to this date the yummy Oreo dessert :) More importantly, the placed seemed very trendy in comparison to the average Philly restaurants, and it was packed when we arrived. Most of the people here were young, which could be good or bad depending on what you're looking for. 

I always wished I could go back but never had the chance, and now I've left Philly... Maybe for the 5-year college reunion? Haha.

I've ordered food to go here 3 or 4 times hoping for a change in the service but the female staff at the desk is still just as rude as the first time I went. She will make you feel stupid if you ask any questions about the menu and is extremely short with her responses. Not sure how this restaurant has managed to get such high reviews with the most unfriendly staff at the front desk. I will not be back.

My boyfriend and I went here for his 21st birthday dinner and it was so good! It's a very upscale place, a little on the expensive side, but definitely worth it. The Shirley Temple drink I ordered was amazing and our waitress was very helpful and knowledgeable about the menu.

Since all of the sushi restaurants seem to keep closing in Center City, I've been going to Wasabi House.  The service is so-so, but the sushi is good.  The rolls are varied and my favorite is the Crab Roll.  I've never had their take out, but I do suspect that its better suited at home.  For an inexpensive sushi dinner, head here. Enjoy!!!

Get the taco tasting platter. Seriously, do it now!

Eat in this order: chicken, carnitas, carne asada, mahi mahi and sea bass. Ask for extra jalapenos. 

Don't get the red sangria unless you love bitters. Instead get the Grenada margarita. 

Totally worth it to get the chips and salsa. Comes with three different salsas: pico, chipotle and salsa verde. 

Only regret: didn't try the guac (next time!!). 

Great atmosphere, staff and clean facilities. Call ahead for a table outside because it fills up. Enjoy!!!

Waitresses offered terrible services, seriously!
While I was sitting and waiting for a delayed airplane, one of the waitresses came over me (not offering menu), and said it's not allowed to sit here, if you want order beer go to the bar! How rude! And no one is sitting and eating at where I was, I wonder if she knows what tip is made of.  Anyway, BIG MISTAKE BIG MISTAKE!
Absolutely amazing ribs. No need for sauce just good seasoning and flavor. A+. Dates appetizer are awesome too.
I love it here. Went last year in the summer and had a blast. Good food and drinks. Plan on going here again this spring

Greasy ,low quality ,small portion unhealthy food, I'm wondering who gives this food 4,5 star !!! 
I rather eat at McDonalds, at least it tastes good !

One word sums up Kanella: Yum.

Kanella is the building situated across from Varga Bar with the cerulean blue awning.  I have never been to Greece, although I feel like I took a trip there and was waiting on a terrace overlooking the ocean until my food arrived.  My visit occurred last Wednesday evening.  My friend whom had dined here previously made us a reservation for 6:15 since the restaurant has a tendency to get crowded midweek to the weekend.  

Draw back -- not trip! -- on the curtains like I almost did and there are booths lined up against the wall with a facing seat in the aisle.  The booths are cushioned and are outfitted with thin, blue pillows. The place is *that* cozy!

First you are given a complimentary appetizer of sourdough bread with tahini dipping sauce.  Second, you have you choice of the appetizers from the main menu.  Go for the Octopus (incredible!) and the Kanella platter.  If you are a fan of Dolmas (stuffed grape leaves) Kanella knows their stuff. Literally.  The waiter encouraged us to try what we liked; you could even make a meal out of the starters; the portions are generous!

For dessert, you can't go wrong with the Pagoto ice cream.  The trio of three different flavors (I can't recall the flavors!) is really something to enjoy.  Plus, it's light enough that you don't feel like you've ate the entire restaurant.  

Next time I look forward to staying for an entree (since some people wanted to catch a drink at Varga)!

I had the pan seared salmon with sweet potatoes and green beans it ok. The salmon was seasoned nice and cooked perfectly but the sides were very unimpressive. The staff were very courteous. I may give them another shot.

I just came here for a couple of beers with my brother. The place is cutely decorated with a plethora of bobble heads. The service was nice and I really liked the homey feel of the place. I tried the Bill Payer Ale and another brew that I couldn't remember. They were both pretty decent. 

The only thing that bothered me about this place was that it wasn't that clean. The vat's I suspect are used to brew the beer are overflowing with grains/barley overflowing out into a garbage can laid out next to the vat. The view kind of threw me off. 

Other than that, it is a pretty decent place, especially for a good beer.

Such an awesome place to get cocktails! Yes, the drinks are a bit pricey (to me at least), but even the glassware is cool and designs you've rarely seen. The cocktails are delicious- Dragon's Tear, Blackmail and I'm sure I had a third. My friends couldn't get enough either. 

The music fits just perfectly with the laid back, mix-matched decor. I wasn't nuts about their choice of food, but for drinks I'll definitely be back.
No go. Not good food and very rude manager / owner.  Didn't seem clean. Water was cloudy  and they looked at me like I was crazy.

This new brewery is exactly what the neighborhood needed. As new parents, a place where you can go grab a bite and a brew without babysitter hassle is a sweet treat! We went in earlier than most to get the best attitude from our kid. They even have a place to change your baby! No BS a family friendly bar!!! 
I love that the three of us sat on a couch, drank beer and split a 5-snack "board" (beef, eggs, sunchokes , cheddar, duck Rangoon )
I had two small beers, husband had three regular sized beers.
The smokey one was not for me, but my hubby loved it and gladly drank it for me. 
I loved the dark malty beer.
We both agreed that we couldn't wait to "bring the baby to the bar" again.
Cool vintage-rustic decor!

This is by far the best roast beef sandwich I ever had in my WHOLE ENTIRE LIFE. I grew up in South Philly and love Nick's Roast Beef..

I always get a combo and extra gravy.. Also you have to try the GRAVY FRIES ! delicious!
WHY DOES PATS THE CREATOR OF CHEESTEAKS HAVE 3 stars ?!!!!! That's BS . Probably a bunch of yuppies tourist rates this place. The started the CHEESTEAKS ! The steaks are always consistent

I found Zesto to be an average pizza place with nice employees. Everyone works quickly to take your order and prepare your food. The pizza itself was a bit greasy and not in a good way (like when a certain cheese blend naturally gives off good tasty oils). The sauce was ok and the crust was not overly flavorful (I tried regular cheese pizza and white pizza). Slices are huge, and they have plenty of specialty pizzas with generous amounts of toppings. There is a small seating area with a TV (usually tuned to ESPN but kept low). 

This is a good place to grab a quick slice during a break.

Despite the fact that I live about two blocks away from Harvest, I have never ventured inside. Luckily I was able to fix that. I went here with a large party of about 25-30 people and were seated in a large closed hall all by ourselves.

Ambiance: The restaurant ad a very "home-y" vibe to it. Exposed rock walls with numerous fireplaces made me feel I was walking into a large home that happened to have a bar and full fledged kitchen. The decorations are kept to a minimum which reflects the feel the place was going for. Natural woods abound and complement the stone.

Food: Since our party was so large we had a predesignated menu for us. Starting out, we were given a parmesan encrusted flatbread with oregano seasoning. Lets just say that the bread disappeared very fast. Next up was a butternut squash soap that was flavored with a bit of honey. I've never been a fan of butternut squash but the soup converted me. I scarfed it down and could hardly wait for my bison burger which was the next dish. The burger was cooked to perfection and was still incredibly juicy. The bun itself was delicious as well the mushroom sauce that came with it. The side salad was good though the greens weren't that crisp as I would have liked them.  

Service: Quite nice. The waiter was prompt and friendly, always coming back to double check on us. The service was quick too, the food came out together (which was impressive considering there was about 8 of us).

Drinks: The restaurant has a full bar on the second floor but I didn't get to use it so I can't attest to the drinks there. However their beer selection was quite large from what I could tell. I ended up having a nice glass of chardonnay. I'm not a big fan of the dryer chardonnays and I was glad the wine had a nice balance between dryness and sweetness. 

Overall I am pretty impressed with this place. Though its in the heart of University City, its a place I would definitely come back to and would be a great spot for a date as well!
This place was nice the food was good the bread was amazing. Just the menu needs to be more detailed and the service need a little work. But the food is nice I will go back again

Slightly above average food. Fair to poor service. There are lots of choices on the menu, so you will probably be able to find something here. I don't eat shellfish, so I can't vouch for any of that. However I did notice lobster, crab, etc. 
 They have all the usual Chinese dishes you would expect and they are just ok. But they are usual cheap, so you get what you pay for. 
My last visit was on New Year's Day and they charged a day long, overpriced, $14 rate. It's usually $7 for lunch and $11 for dinner. The service was pretty bad. It's a buffet, so you don't need much from your servers other than drinks and silverware. But that seemed challenging on this day. They argued with my sister and her husband over whether the asked for ice. Then another server got an attitude when my other sister asked for a spoon because there weren't any in the bin. 
 I'm not going to say don't go here, but be prepared for bad service if you're servers are having a bad day like they were my last time here. Food's ok and there's lots of variety and it's usually pretty affordable.

Friend and I dined in. Cheap, no frills experience. Chips and salsa were served before the meal. These were the deep-fried tortilla chips that are awesome. $10 for a taco platter that included two tacos and a side of Mexican rice and beans (black, pinto, or with chilis). Honestly, there was nothing spectacular about the meat or the way that it was seasoned, but the service and price is on-point. There was Tapatio, Chalula, and red and green El Yucateco Hot Sauce. This alone made it worthwhile.

I was sadly pretty disappointed with my experience at Alma de Cuba.

I went to Alma for Restaurant Week--in which you get an appetizer, entree, and dessert for $35. Upon entering the restaurant, it's clear that Stephen Starr & Co. put a lot of work into cultivating the ambiance--it's a gorgeous space with low lighting, beautiful imagery, and lush furniture. The ground floor is for drinks only, and the upper floor is for dinner.

My party of 4 were ushered to the upper floor and seated promptly. Service was great throughout our time at Alma, with waitstaff consistently at our table asking us if we needed anything, refilling our water / AMAZING BREAD BALLS, and clearing/cleaning our messes.

Before we ordered our food, I ordered a Mango Martini, which I was a big fan of--it tasted and felt almost like an alcoholic mango smoothie, and for $10, it was certainly worth it.

However, as great as the service, drinks, and interior were, I can't say the same about the food.

The Restaurant Week menu did not have a vegetarian entree option, but my waitress said the chef was willing to substitute the Crisp Rice and Black Lentil Cake for the meaty options, which I was grateful for. For an appetizer, I ordered Gloria's Black Bean Soup which was decent--it just tasted like... black bean soup I can get anywhere. Nothing to write home about. Soon afterwards, my Crisp Rice and Black Lentil Cake came out and was really just... not at all appealing in any way. The mounds of rice and lentils were all right, but the essentially-mashed vegetable mess on top just didn't taste or look good. It was all just really bland, chewy, and was a burden to eat. I still took the dish home because I was unable to eat it there in hopes that it would somehow, like, get better with age or something, but that didn't work out...
For dessert, I ordered the Coconut Tapioca Pudding, which was also just mediocre. It was good but not phenomenally amazing. The flavors were present but didn't really go well together. Overall, I'm not really sure any vegetarian should pay the $35 for this really mediocre meal, even if the portions were big--my omnivorous friends loved their dishes, though. 

In conclusion, I really won't recommend Alma's dinner to any vegetarian. The drinks and lounge space, maybe, yes, but I was sorely disappointed with my dining experience there.

Prompt friendly service from Dave.  The Shrimp Alexander is top notch but I was a little disappointed with the quality of my Cajun ribeye and believe my daughters Manhattan Cut was not cooked properly.  Sour creme mashers weren't mashed all the way through with a few large chunks of potatoes- poor quality to detail. This review is about quality to price ratio- to charge elite prices, the the quality and execution must be flawless.

Had a chance to visit Beiler's since the yearly convention I attend moved to Philly this year.  Of course, had to visit Beiler's again for the pastries. Was surprised to see they've now occupied two counters: one for pastries and one for donuts.  Of course, went to the donut counter.

Once there, I found out that the apple fritter I've tried awhile back is different.  It's no longer the huge fritter I remember and now it's more of a regular sized donut.  Ordered one of the new versions and got a glazed donut as well as a chocolate iced.

The donuts have a completely different texture than your standard donuts. Hard to describe it but the yeast donuts are very "bouncy."  It's soft but doughy at the same time.  Pretty damn awesome.  The apple fritter was still good although different and I really liked the glazed.  Didn't like the chocolate iced as much because I'm not a fan of the icing?  That's just me though.  There's also a lot of filled donuts, which a lot of people ordered.  Again, not for me, but literally over 10 kinds of filled donuts.

There's most likely going to be a short line and just hope the people before you are decisive because it's hard to make decisions when there's so many different types in front of you!
