Angel is an SNS whiz. French mani SNS is $45 and she is so meticulous. She doesn't use the drill at all which I love. Only downside is like other reviewers have said, it's a no frills kind of place. She's the only person there so call ahead to make an appointment. And there's no music or tv but I just have a headphone in and listen to music.

My first visit to Terron was last night for dinner.  I had the zesty Morrocan sandwich' and the hot greens salad .  These were both so outstandingly delicious.  I will be returning here soon to try other menu items.  I expect those to be just as delicious.  To top it off - I had the cherry brownie, which was also super delicious.

I love Nodding Head. The beers are fantastic, and the staff are so friendly. They were more than willing to give samples of different beers to try for my indecisive friend. The entrees are affordable and good quality. They also have a good selection of vegetarian entrees for said friend. The fries with dipping aioli are fantastic. I agree it is a calmer atmosphere than other CC bars. It's definitely a good place for meeting up after work for drinks and dinner.

OH MY GOD this place is good. I had their pasta special, in addition to the cabernet/merlot on tap and the biscotti. It's CRAZY good here. Very nice staff too. Fellini movie playing on the screen outside for excellent atmosphere. Run, don't walk.

Love the F and international terminals here. They are awesome. When this was US Air the terminal staff were surly combative and had a don't give a crap attitude. Oh and they were empowered to do zip!  Not so now IMHO and the impact on me as a traveler is solid positive. Plenty of foodie, drinkie options both terminals and lots of room in most gates!  No really this is goodness!

I've been to chart house several times throughout the years with my boyfriend and we both really like it. The view is romantic and the cocktails are always well made. We both enjoy the coconut shrimp and crab cakes. Everytime we go, we get something different and we never have any complaints. I definitely recommend this restaurant for a date night for two that enjoy seafood.

This practice came recommended to me seven years ago and in that time I have gone through two nurse practitioners and two doctors that have left the practice, the last of which I was never even informed about her departure. 

At this point I am planning on leaving this practice as I need to have a constant medical practitioner to be available for my care. I feel like I have started and stopped countless of times and this place seems to operate like a revolving door instead of a practice that truly cares about their patients.

Though the new offices are spacious, bright and new - there are only three elevators that service the huge building and the wait is always abysmally slow.

On the bright side, there are daily walk-in hours and you almost can always get same day appointments, but even the allure of this won't keep me coming back.

Hippot's service is efficient and their All you can Eat Menu is very worth it!!
Authentic Hot pot experience, many choices of fillings.
Gogogo!

This place is a good time. Unless you're behind someone buying 5 packs of Newps and paying in change. Which is like every other time. Then it's not so fun. Usually you can get in and get out, and once they know you, they don't bother with your ID, which saves me time, because I don't want to be standing in there for more than a minute, tops. One time the lady who works there told me I need to pray more. She said "You remember to eat, you remember to pray." Then I went home and instead of praying I drank 30 beers and played Modern Warfare 2.

In a city, I am often loath to try new coffee shops because of the distinct possibility of disappointment. Personality can be hard to come by in a world of Starbucks, Cosis, and Le Pain Quotidiens. For a change of scenery and a genuine touch of authenticity, Cake & the Beanstalk is a solid choice. 

I give them a B- for the store name, an A for the vibe, an A- for the coffee, and a B for the pastries. The blondie love is all over Yelp, and I have to admit, it was just alright. Nothing terrible, but certainly nothing to write home about. The pastries - brownies, blondies, cookies - are all very heavy ('bready' for lack of a better word). I've heard some say rubbery, and that's not too far off. They're tasty, just a little like bricks. 

C&B is a place to sit, to read, to order a second coffee. It's as close to a campus coffee shop as you'll find in Center City. Bring work, bring cash. They are very laid back here and the main seating area, surrounded on all sides by large, bay windows, is as mellow as can be.

Not surprisingly, Bar Hygge was poppin' early last night. While walking down the street, I noticed hoards, literally like 3 or 4 groups of people just funneling in the doors of this restaurant. In my selfish/hungry state, I was thinking, "oh, great." This didn't seem to phase the staff one bit. 
The establishment is a restaurant that "hosts" a brewery and features its beers, alongside a wine and cocktail list. Essentially, it is part brew pub, part restaurant, and they have a fully functioning full bar. The brews are great. Techne, the brewer, is putting out some really solid brews. I really enjoying the porter and the H.O.P., a hoppy ale. 
I tried the vegan "pulled pork" sliders, which is incorrectly advertised and also happens to be delicious. This item might make you think there is some of that fake meat shit on it, but in reality its mushrooms. Just mixed mushrooms in an awesome smoky sauce on a really buttery roll. With really, really delicious fries. We also had the mac and cheese which i think has some  Israeli cous cous, or some other different grain in there mixed along with actual mac. Also, several cheeses, veggies, and some roasted tomatoes on the side. Our friends got the waffle wings which came with mixed reviews, but to be fair were completely devoured, and the Hygge frites, hooked up style. This, apparently, comes covered in cheese, brisket, and is served along side "awesome sauce."
What really made our experience wonderful was the exceptional, top notch service. Our server was efficient, fast, kind, and accommodating. We were there for awhile, ordered a ton of beers, and even extended our party beyond the confines of our table. Essentially, we were kinda annoying and we knew it. We were never rushed and the service did not decline over the several hours we inhabited our table, despite the very packed restaurant. I will most definitely be back, and will keep note of the fact that there will be hoards of other people waiting to try the awesome sauce they are serving here. Literally.

I'm really torn on this place.  Its a neighborhood joint and I truly want to see it succeed!  I think their margaritas are great so no complaints there.  I have enjoyed their chips and salsa and guacomole quite a bit.  I have ordered 2 entrees there, however, and was not impressed with either.  I first ordered the mahi mahi tacos and they were just ok.  There was no sauce and overall the dish was very dry.  The second time I went I got the vegetarian enchiladas and didn't like that at all.  I will say my boyfriend got the pork belly tacos and really liked them.  I think the other thing that bothered us the last time we were there was the service.  We didn't get drinks until we were seated for at least 20-30 minutes and every step in ordering and getting additional drinks was a process.  I look forward to trying it again and having a better experience but for now I would say, A-OK.

This is probably the best place on Rittenhouse Square for vegan food. I'm a newly converted vegan and I'm  working my way through the menu. So far tried the Udon Noodle salad.  Very good, but like many of the other reviewers have said, you want more volume for $9.  Also had the Asian side salad.  Very good and at $4 is a better deal. Tried their vegan brownie, which runs $4 a square. Delicious but should have been $2.50, $3.00 at the most.  Then again, nobody held a gun o my head and forced me to buy it.

It's Chipotle...can't go wrong. 
Quick service at this location and the cleanliness is up there too.
Has some tables set up outside if the weather is nice for you to chow down with friends.

I'm not overstating: this is the best Japanese restaurant I've been in the United States. From the beautiful design to flawless food, including very nice service, it was a perfect dinner. Miso soup is fantastic, the rolls were amazing and the kobe beef niguiri, so good!

Before leaving, the waitress called the owner to greet me and I even got a small dessert on the house. 

The only bad thing about Tuna Bar: it's too many miles from my home in Orlando. I could eat here every week if it were not by the distance.

This is a neighborhood gem! I come here regularly and my go to is the beef burrito. It's almost always two meals for me. Comes with guac and a bunch of other delicious fillings. Way better than any chain Mexican restaurant.

Megabus was impressively on time from start to finish. While I did have some trouble locating the pickup spot (it's on a side street instead of where the other buses pickup/drop off), good thing Google Maps knows where the stop is (3105-3125 John F Kennedy Blvd, Philadelphia, PA 19104)! Granted, it was in the same spot that they dropped me off... I'm just unfamiliar with the territory and there's no signage. As other Yelpers have mentioned, this pickup/drop-off spot is completely outside with no awnings/coverings. (Similarly, the Baltimore stop appears to be a completely random place in the White Marsh parking lot.) Since it was raining, I stayed inside 30th Street Station until it was time to board. Pro tip: While the ticket says to arrive 15 minutes early, get there earlier that! Megabus offers reserved seating for an upcharge; if you want a decent seat and only paid for general seating, you better arrive early. While technically there are two-seaters on the bus, the bus was empty enough that *practically* everyone had their own set of seats (*practically*... my condolences to those that didn't). Fortunately but unfortunately, I got the very last two-seater to myself, but it was by the bathroom :/.

This location was a little disorganized. I got in line, showed my ticket to the Megabus staff member, he checked it, and told me that I was "good to go." But, as I began to board the bus, another Megabus staff member stopped me urgently and asked to see my ticket. I understand the need to prevent stowaways, but another staff member literally just cleared me, so the encounter was a little jarring. It wasn't a big issue because I did have my ticket verification on hand, but their practices could be more orderly to prevent duplication of efforts and accusatory tones.

I sat on the lower level and it was a bumpy ride, so it was difficult to get any reading done without the threat of getting motion sickness. There were outlets overhead, though I didn't test to see if they worked. The WiFi was semi-functional. It worked fine to browse the web and check email, but any streaming services to watch videos or listen to music didn't work; instead I kept getting redirected to the Megabus page asking me to download their app. Apparently if you download their app, you can watch certain movies and tv shows, just like on an airplane.

Despite the rain, the bus was perfectly on schedule departing Philly, stopping in Baltimore, and arriving in DC. I've travelled by Peter Pan and Greyhound before, and their service has been inconsistent. Megabus has been the best bus experience, in terms of value and schedule, coming and going, by far.

Yo.

First off I appreciate this place for being a laid back, Philly flavor bar close to the Barbary, where you can kick some drinks between bands when the Barb is doing their all ages thing.  

Second off...friend face and I dropped in here (night before Memorial Day weekend) and got...about eight thousand free shots, a history lesson on the gentrification of the neighborhood from a lifer (it was actually really interesting), tons of attention for dancing like scandalous whores, an invitation to a block party tonight, a story about how there were a bunch of drunk cops a block away shooting off their guns for no good reason........and a "just for fun" fist fight in an alley at 2 AM.  My knuckles look so hot right now.  

If my description hasn't sold you on this place...you should probably not hang out in Fishtown by yourself after dark...or ever.

Total gross out. Both sanitation and karma/ service. Uncleared tables/hothouse environment.  One employee accused the proprietors of stealing from her tip jar - I believe it!  Another was dressed down in very nasty fashion in front of customers for what appeared to be trivialities. I went there for awhile for convenience - I live around the corner - but after they tried to charge me for a soda w/ my combo plate cause it was a bottle not a can (the menu just offered a soda as an alternative to soup or egg roll) and then got shortchanged - got change for a $10, not the $20 I gave them - after I complained about the soda. So I've decided enough is enough - voting with my feet. Food is probably typical of these places - not worse or better. Hey now I have an excuse to myself not to eat that garbage!

I ordered some book shelves from a thrift shop that did not have delivery service.  None of the movers want to take my business.  Eric at Flat Rate Labor returned my call in 5 minutes and in a few hours, had the bookshelves delivered to my place. For someone to very quickly come and do business when other movers wouldn't return my calls speaks volumes of Flat Rate Labor. Not to mention, Flat Rate Labor is very inexpensive. I did check some of the other sites and found Flat Rate beat their prices.

Flat Rate Labor is an one man show. Eric runs everything, but he is excellent at setting up appointments, taking phone calls and coming down. And since most of us aren't experts at moving, he comes with truck, items and stuff to make moving a breeze.  Will use him again when I need to move.
Purchased a few ounces of "breakfast blend" . Looks like the sweeping of finely ground second tier tea. Don't waste your money as quality has left this stand.Threw the "tea" out that afternoon.

Sorry to be writing this...

So I got baguette #2 from the Rittenhouse Square market this past weekend. I hate to say it, because I have only the best feeling about this bakery, but it wasn't up to par this time. Here's the reason why: there was NO salt in the baguette (I mean none at all.) I'm assuming this was a mistake that the baker made - someone just forgot to add the salt in one batch. It happens. The bread was still okay, but a baguette without salt just tastes really lacking. I can get past it for Tuscan bread or a soft pretzel, but for this loaf, I just couldn't get past it. 
I will definitely be trying again next week and hopefully I will get another stellar loaf like the previous one I had. I also can't wait to try their croissants and brioche loaf! So I will try them again soon and re-update this, hopefully with better news!

The food here was delicious!! We got 2 banh mi sandwiches (I got the tofu) and it was soo good, the sauce on it was amazing!!! 

Service was great, we ordered pickup and it was ready within 10minutes.

My only complaint and the reason for the 4 stars is it wasn't very filling. It was a thin sandwich maybe if it had more or came with a side I would have felt 100% satisfied.

Recently visited and while I didn't think it could get any better, our server was easily one of the best we've ever had.  My wife and I have a combined 15 years in fine hospitality and we pay attention.  This gentleman was simply masterful!

I and my Hunee went to this place and the experience was HORRIBLE! We walked in and got seated and they had Maury Povich "You Are or Are Not the Father" segment on, who does that in a place of business. It was apparent that the tv was for the Hostess and NOT the guest. I requested that the channel be changed and the hostess said that she would have to ask the cook, HUH??

My Hunee asked for OJ and the waitress said that they just ran out, what diner runs out of OJ!! My inner voice was telling me to leave. I ordered broccoli, "Roasted" potatoes and fried whiting. We waited about a half hour for our food, my you there was onlt 3 other booths occupied. I asked the waitress to change the channel from Maury, they had the news on two other tv's but it was mute. Needless to say the Ratchet channel was never turned.

I had asked for the Manager and was told that they weren't there. Mind you it was after 1pm. So who was in charge.

My food finally came out and the "Roasted" potatoes were in huge chunks and cold. They weren't even cooked all the way. The broccoli was warm, it wasn't even hot enough to melt the butter. I asked the waitress to warm it up (microwave) and when she brought it back I had one stalk and pushed it aside. The fish was ok. 

This was the WORST restaurant experience that I've ever had. Ratchet tv/atmosphere, bad food, no OJ and two other couples left while we were there. One couple tried to order but they didn't have what they wanted and another got tired of waiting.

This place changed hands back in June, so sad to see a decent business go to someone who either doesn't care or just doesn't know how to run a business.  
I wish we would have seen some of these reviews prior to going, it would have saved timed and money. 

Shame on you Manayunk Diner and you had a card on the table that read "Under New Ownership With A Commitment To Make Every Dining Experience A Quality & Exceptional Encounter" what a LIE and a JOKE!

You won't be open much longer with service and food like this!

Lunch time on a Sunday afternoon.  It's raining, and I have to head to the airport.  A block from my hotel is this place, or I continue walking to Chili's.  Do I take a chance on a location joint, or keep going for established mediocrity? Yep, I took the chance. So the place is huge, and mostly dead.  Yet the TVs are very very loud.  That was annoying.  Service was nice when she acknowledged me, but there was no hostess so you seat yourself and it was a good while before anyone took my order.  I had a veggie burger, it was large, but it was kind bland, and mostly just a fused pile of black beans.  Yeah, I think unless you were there to watch some game it's safe to avoid this joint.

Portobello tartar was underwhelming, the hearts of palm left me wanting, but golden beets were amazing. The plate of carrots and squash was tasty, but the best was the maitake mushroom dish. Service was lacking. To start, we were asked to come in earlier as they had many tables available. When arrived we were stuck in a very tight corner.  We requested a new table and were sat at a high top just outside the kitchen. Ok, this happens. But after spending over $125 on veggies and two drinks, we  met with stares as we left. 

Some dishes were stellar but the inconsistency coupled with the overall unwelcoming atmosphere makes it a place i will likely not frequent again any time soon.

Update: I am finishing this after writing it at the restaurant. It has been at least 2 years and I have not been back.
Awesome food - great vegan selections! (I'm not...)  great service!  Great beer list! :)

So happy that Smileys is back open for business and that the owner is doing so well after his surgery.  Been craving it for weeks - and as usual, I wasn't disappointed!  Amazing Greek Salad with Chicken and the best Hummus I've ever eaten!  Welcome Back!
Great find in our neighborhood.  So much better than anything in center city.  Come here and bring a couple bottles of red wine.  You will he happy!  We will certainly be back!

One of my favorite restaurants in Philadelphia. I came here in a group of 4 during restaurant week and was just blown away. A very memorable evening. The appetizers were probably my favorite part of the meal, especially the escargot and fishermans style poached egg with taragon cream, which was so wonderfully simple and complex at the same time.The foie gras was solid, but a little boring. The main courses did not disappoint. I had the beef cheek bourginon, my friend had the duck confit with white beans. It was all spectacular. The wine list was solid too. The ambience was friendly and romantic, but not stuffy. Definitely the best bistrot style French food in Philly as far as I can tell.
Went here for a Thursday nights football game, they couldn't figure out their own TV and sound system to play the game. However, they did have good service

Great tacos to go! It was $6 for an order of three traditional Mexican tacos on soft corn tortillas. I had the chicken tacos and really enjoyed them. They came with a lime wedge and two salsas, red and verde.
The Barbary Is Kooool, great for the twenty One Twenty two something, New Hip Bar

I just love Doma!!!

Sushi Is always fresh and duhlicious and the service is always super friendly, warm and inviting.

For those that don't love sushi, Doma offerw many cooked items like bibimbap and udon soup among others.

Di Bruno Bros. is the crème de la crème of Italian specialty stores and, of all their locations, I find this one to be the best.

The staff here is incredibly knowledgable and you could easily spend an hour talking with the cheesemongers and sampling all the incredible cheeses they have to offer. 

Are you looking for some Italian delicacy? No problem. They have it. How about good meats, cheeses, and breads to pair up up with wines for a dinner or party? They've got you covered. Need a prepared meal, sauces, or a dessert? Done.

Let's cut to the chase. I would work here just for the discounts on purchases and, if they rolled out a cot in the basement, I'd live there too.
Doorman was a dick. Place is nice. Bunch of hipsters drinking beer and hanging out. Worth visiting on a nice day. Bartender was rushy. Had a drink played Jenga and left

Brett & his staff set my party up flawlessly. We came in for my birthday during restaurant week; I was eager to share my favorite restaurant with friends! We each tried different items from the menu and loved everything, as usual. If you're looking for delicious, consistant French cuisine, impeccable service with great beverage pairing suggestions, all for a reasonable price, Bistrot la Minette is for you.

The food here is okay, the service here isn't. 

Out of the three times I've been there, two of the times the service was horrible. The first time I went there I was seated in an empty section with my friend. The server came promptly, but we weren't ready yet, so we asked her for a few minutes to look over the menus, well, she never came back. We saw her go over and check her only other table a few times, and then stand behind the bar doing absolutely nothing. After 15-20 minutes of sitting there, waiting to be helped, we just left. 

The other time, the server didn't come for at least 15 minutes. It wasn't even busy. 

Even though I like the deserts here, I only would come here if I had extra time to sit and wait on waiters who don't seem to really care to be there.

Chicken is always fresh and delicious. You can watch them make it if you choose to wait. The original is probably my go to next to the sweet chili. The staff is always courteous and can answer all your questions. Highly recommend.

We held the post-rehearsal cocktail party for 70 people for my son's wedding at Rembrandt's last month and I cannot say enough good things about the way they handled the event.  The room was lovely, the food was delicious and beautifully presented and the staff worked unbelievably hard to make sure everyone was happy.  We had the room from 5 to 8 but the party was still going strong so the event manager asked me if I wanted them to keep serving and said that they would stay as long as we wanted them to.  The bill was so reasonable, even though we went 1.5 hours over time.  I would go back there in a heartbeat and so would most of our party guests.

Love this salon, Linda has been doing my hair for years and she is always up tp date with the styles and can do whatever you like.  Barb is very good at washing and very personable.  They are reasonably priced and have a lovely salon.  Thanks so much for always making my hair look good.  Cindy Prouse

My 2 year old had an awful night of sleep, teething and crying. He was sleeping on my chest all through the night so I woke up with a lot of pain and a terrible kink in my neck. I couldn't turn my head to the right at all. My mother-in-law googled the best massage in Philly and found Eviama "Houdini Jini" Intensive therapeutic techniques unlock tension in head, jaw, neck & shoulders. Perfect. I was desperate and since I don't live in Philly I didn't have any clue what massage therapists were good or not. I had my fingers crossed and booked a 45 minute session. Cooper was the gracious man who was appointed to fix me into correction. I originally said I preferred deep pressure but he gently worked through my problem area and pulled and pushed the balance between my sides. It was a miracle, I stood up from the table with almost full ability to look to my right. The majority of my pain was gone and I felt like a completely new person. Cooper had mentioned that two more sessions would put be back like normal. I didn't have the luxury of booking another session but I woke the next morning, a little tender where my huge knot was on my back but almost 100% I couldn't believe it. I was glad that he didn't smush my muscles into oblivion,I would have been much more sore. He knew what he was doing. I had never bounced back from a neck kink so quickly. The power of a good massage mixed with the right therapist is key. Book Cooper if you need to be fixed!

Philly isn't known as the Windy City here in the old U S of A but this weather has been making the nights long and the old ball and chain just hasn't been cutting it for something warm and sweet. Me and the hubby stopped in to celebrate a GLORIOUS Eagles victory and I couldn't think of anything sweeter than a chocolate martini from the famous Max Brenners. The hosts were sweet as pie (they really should offer a decent rhubarb on the menu, I have a phenomenal recipe) but when we sat at the bar, the sweet-ness took a bigger nosedive than the 2017 Falcons in the Super Bowl! We initially cashed out with the one bartender and treated her accordingly to the Eagles score that Sunday, but after that you would think the old ball and chain and myself had leprosy! We sat in those too tight bar stools with empty decorative glasses for longer than my first divorce. My settlement could have easily afforded them another round of drinks but instead the focus was on training another bartender and not on the only guests at the bar! Seems like I should look for my choco-fix elsewhere.... Valentines Day will be a little different this year thank you very much!

My wife and I arrived and there was a small wait.  The hostess said 10-15 minutes to be on the safe side, we said not a problem.  While we were waiting, several other parties arrived of various sizes.  After watching table after table being sat, a few couples left (which were there before us) and that seemed to move us up in the line.  We then watched every single party that came in after us get sat.  40 minutes later we were sat and told by the same hostess that if anyone asked we should say there was third person in our party because we were in a 4 top.  I looked around the dining room and everyone was in a 4 top.  We read the reviews on Yelp and everyone said the food was great however when were served our food was either a) burnt to a crisp or b) the wrong order.  It took 30 minutes for the waitress to check back on us to even see how our meal was going.  She asked if everything was ok and when my wife said she got the wrong order the waitress said ok, i guess we will put the right order in this time.  My wife said no thanks and the waitress acted as if we were putting her out.  The waitress than looked at me and asked if I was ok.  I showed her my burnt skillet (not served in a skillet) and she then realized what was going on.  She grabbed my plate and took it back to the kitchen.  20 minutes later she said the owner would take care of our entrees but not the coffee and bagel.  I didn't ask for any comps or anything but to imply that I would need to pay for those items and that I was asking too much (again, never asked for anything) was off-putting.  The table next to us said that they used to get amazing food here but only when the owner cooks.  To me, it's not worth the gamble to see if the owner is cooking that day and the service alone would keep me away.  Skip this one altogether and go to Honey's or Sabrina's.

We decided to try Sabrina's Cafe after another restaurant we typically go to in Philly was closed.  Between the stuffed Challah French toast and the breakfast burrito, it was good, but didn't 'wow' us as we hoped.  The food was a little bland.  The place had a cute atmosphere with friendly servers.

Love beer? You need to come to this place and merrily drink. Don't drink beer? Come and just try the Lost Abby's Red Poppy Ale and enjoy the great atmosphere. So I'm the latter. I didn't have bottles after bottles of beer, but I still had a great time here because this place was just... interesting. The beer bible--LOVE IT! I immediately instagramed that shiz. Matching glasses for every beer--They have you guessing what sort of goblet, thistle, or tulip glass comes with your next beer. More instagrams!
The mussels were very good... for $10, get it!
Next time a beer lover friend of mine visits Philly, I'll definite take them to Monks.

I found Penrose diner on Yelp and on various websites about dining in Philadelphia and since this diner intrigued me, my boyfriend andrst night in Philadelphia.

It looks almost identical to your typical diner with comfortable booth seats, a refrigerator with cakes rotating inside, and a menu with typical diner food. However, there was just something about this diner that sets itself apart from many of the diners I've been to.The décor is very simple. The diner is truly spacious and comfortable to dine in. Our waitress was very nice. I just wish I remembered her name because she was very friendly to us and even kindly answered any questions we ha

Our waitress brought out a bread basket for us, butter, and our drinks.  Our food took a little while for it to come, but as soon as it hit our table - we knew it was all worth the wait because after attempting to finish our dinner -we were actually happy campers!

So I ordered the Chicken Francese which came with a choice of a soup or salad and it was freezing cold in the diner; I opted for the Shrimp Bisque.  The Shrimp Bisque  was creamy and had baby shrimp in it (not the best kind of shrimp).  The soup had a little too much salt in it.  The Chicken Francese was scrumptious! The chicken was moist, tender, and just absolutely delectable. There was only two pieces which was just enough. The rice was moist and I love how it was drenched with the lemon and butter sauce because it gave the rice extra flavor and gave it a buttery taste. 

My boyfriend had a dinner special which consisted of a cup of soup or salad, his main entrée which was Lobster Ravioli, and a plate of dessert and he chose Jell-O. This special came with a glass of wine, but he substituted for a soda instead. My boyfriend had the Chicken Bowtie soup which looked really healthy and hearty.  I really enjoyed his soup because it was just the right amount of salt and flavor. The chunks of chicken was tender and it was just a really good and hearty soup. His Lobster Bisque looked amazing and it came with a slice of garlic bread so he can drench the bread in the warm, red, and creamy lobster sauce. 

Man... that experience of smothering a piece of bread in the sauce and having that first bite - is just...total foodgasm.  He gave me three pieces of the lobster ravioli and I absolutely loved it. The ravioli was cooked perfectly and just amazing. I've never had lobster ravioli before and I'm glad I got to sample a bit of it. My boyfriend made an excellent decision in ordering his dinner that night. 

Good food and good diners are hard to come by. I must say Penrose Diner is a true gem. Food is amazing here. Staff is truly friendly and welcoming here. I highly recommend coming to Penrose diner for a nice and cozy comfort food dinner.

I love Hop Angel! When I moved to the Northeast I was a little afraid there wouldn't be a good local beer bar or brunch spot near me, and Hop Angel is both! Good food, decent beer selection, brunch, optional outdoor drinking. AND it's in a gorgeous historic building. What more could I ask?

Great food!!! We loved everything.
Eggplant koora was amazing same as the chicken tika masala and the garlic nan.
Samosas were amazing same as the chocolate desert.
Will come again!

A big group of us came to Distrito for celebrating our graduation from a track program. We arrived and were seated in a nice room close to the entrance, walled off from the rest of the restaurant with a TV/AV setup presumably linked to karoake. 

Our food came out in waves: greek (mixed with a mexican theme) salad, chicken tortillas, seafood dish and fried plantains to name but a few. While most of the dishes had a nice presentation, the ingredients did not taste particularly fresh. The margheritas were filled with ice cubs, and after dinner, we even struggled to get the karaoke setup up-and-running. Once we even got the karoake working, it continued to freeze and didn't have many of the hit songs you would expect. Acoustics were also pour given that our private room was separated from the rest of the restaurant with only a thin, plastic sliding door that allowed for plenty of noise to seep through.

Overall, I was not impressed by my dining experience at Distrito even though I have been told the interior is quite fun, the nachos are delicious and happy hour specials are solid.

Made a 9am appointment. Took time off of work and waited two hours. No call, no show. Called them while waiting, no answer.  He calls me over two hours after my appointment and leaves a message that he is on his way. I called him back and said I waited two hours then went to work (having already lost hundreds of dollars at my job by waiting for him and having to work extra tonight to make up for it).  He says, "oh yeah, I got stuck at a job. I saw you called.  It is what it is."   Not even an apology, just excuses.  (And by the way, when I booked the appointment he told me 9am would be his first job of the day.) He obviously didn't care, so I just hung up the phone.  I'm so over bad service and a lack of concern for others' time and money, that instead of arguing with him I have taken to making it known to others here what kind of service they may receive if they book with this company.

Holly benedicts! The chicken & waffles benedict with a maple syrup and hot sauce hollandaise is out of this world! The cheesy grit side is a perfect addition. Honestly, all of the benedicts are perfection...eggs cooked perfectly every time! 

My last trip to Green Eggs I was in the mood for something light and summery...the crab, shrimp and bacon salad with a fresh sneeze berry lemonade was the perfect choice. Honestly one of the best salads I have ever had, the dressing was light allowing the deliciousness of the shrimp, crab and bacon to shine through! In addition to the amazing grub served, the friendliness of everyone who works there makes the brunch experience worth repeating every weekend!

I had a good experience at A+A. Although it's not much to look at, I actually drove past it the first time. It's tucked away behind a gas station. I pulled up and someone asked me what I wanted. I got an in and out, $25. Vacuum and wipe down inside, hand wash and tire shine. I parked and got out of my car, someone started vacuuming it right away. They don't have any sort of place to wait, it's an outdoor business. So on a cold day like today that sucks a little but I was prepared for the cold. An hour later my car was finished and looked great. I gave 4 stars because they didn't vacuum my trunk and the outside of my car wasn't THAT clean. He did wipe down spots I pointed out to him. I am absolutely satisfied with what I got for what I paid.
Incredible food and Service.  By far the best I have experienced in Philadelphia!  Highly recommend!

What makes a perfect dive bar?

Old, musty smokey smell - CHECK
Floors and walls that haven't been updated since the Reagan administration - CHECK
Sports on TV while music blares from the juke box - CHECK
Being served a Coke and Malibu in an ale glass with a "bendy" straw - CHECK! CHECK! CHECK!
Entertainment in the form of dog-eared early 80s boxes of Trivial Pursuit cards placed across the bar - CHECKAROONI
Pringles as pretty much the only dining option - (crunch) CHECK
The bartender coming out on the floor to join you as you regale other patrons with your best and funniest stupid-people stories - FO SHIZ!

Seriously.

Usually making a reservation is a good idea, but we luckily walked in and were seated promptly! I forgot how good this place is.
Ordered the salad with glass noodles and shrimp on top (can't remember the name), fish cakes, and pad kee mao/drunken noodles. Would recommend them all!!

The service here is horribly inattentive. The servers don't bother coming to your table to check up on you or even remotely follow up with your order. I would not recommend sitting in the restaurant to eat - as my party waited significantly longer than those who were seated after us as well as those who were taking out food. We weren't significantly hungry when we arrived, but we sure left with empty stomachs - as we decided not to wait for an order that seemed like it was never going to be served.

I went to vedge right when it opened and I was pleasantly surprised.  The food is amazing.  My friend and I ordered a bunch of different dishes and shared.  Their beer list is also interesting, as it's not all just local beers.  Both my friend and I are vegan and we loved the place.  The true test is when I went back with my mom (who is not) and she loved it as well.  Omnivores can go here and not miss meat at all.  What I like most about the place is that they don't make it all about being vegan like most other restaurants in the same category do.  It's also a lot nicer inside then most vegan places.  I think this is a restaurant that helps make veganism mainstream and chic.  I'll be back!

The greatest tacos in the world--they are worth the wait. Pork or beef. They come with cilantro and onion, no cheese or lettuce, these are authentic. Three kinds of homemade salsa, I go hot. If you want guacamole, they will put a slice of  fresh avocado in your taco. Grab a bottle of cold coke and three tacos and thank me later!
Got in at 9pm on a Sunday and figured, why not? Ugh. $12 later, the bread was stale, meat was meh. Now I've got a gut bomb. Should've gone to subway right nearby, or even the fast food Chinese.

Neighborhood gem. If you're in Point Breeze area, come here for a great cup of coffee and something to eat. Breakfast is amazing. Highly recommend the French toast and/or crepes. Great addition to the South Philly area!

Been stewing for over a week about last visit 
Love sitting outside watching people on a beautiful evening 
Usually have a burger and adult beverage 
Staff is very professional 
Last week decided to try an entree and ordered the duck breast
I asked our server which he preferred the half chicken or duck
He highly recommended the duck
So I did
Well that was the worst entree for value
2 pieces of tough bird lost in garnishes
Parc should be embarrassed of this entree and pricing at$$$$
Thinking twice about returning 
Just saying 
Rick

The concept of healthy food isn't new to Rittenhouse. There are tons of local, fast, casual eateries around the city that one can go to. The store itself is great: well-lit, clean, and plenty of seating to go around. I came on a Saturday for lunch with a friend and the place was dead empty. Not a great sign but I came for the food. And the food was decent. I ordered the steak and while it was dry (presmuably because they pre-cook the meat and sear it before finish), the sides were all decent but didn't knock my socks off. I didn't find too much value in the meal either. Portions were pretty small and for $12.50, I kind of expected more. Despite only living two blocks away, I don't see myself coming back until they fix the portion issue. You can go to Farmer's Keep and get a rotating variety of options, more value, and better tasting food.

Great atmosphere. Smells great inside like baked goods. Service was extra friendly. They have a small student discount and free wifi. It was a peaceful place to send emails and enjoy a refreshing spring fling ice tea.

I was in Philadelphia for 48 hours and went here twice. This place is awesome! Very small with limited seating, so I recommend taking out -- which is actually perfect due to proximity and the beautiful weather Philly is having.

All the bagels are made on-site in what appears to be a pizza oven. One of my friends remarked that they're denser than you're typical bagel. I agree, and that's part of what makes them so delicious.

I ordered the Turkey Club twice. It's fresh pulled turkey meat (not cold cuts!), like you'd have as left overs from Thanksgiving, along with cheddar cheese, granny smith apple and some type of mustard dijon sauce. I had mine on a toasted whole wheat bagel. Delicious! Also comes with a bag of chips. Believe it's around $9 -- as are most lunch sandwiches here. Breakfast sandwiches and bagels run cheaper. Iced coffee is pretty delicious, too.

Great place for a quick breakfast or lunch. Definitely recommend picking up some sandwiches and going out for a picnic.

This is a quaint BYOB fantastic Italian restaurant located in The Old City neighborhood.   The food and service were outstanding and it's definitely a place you want to visit when you are in Philadelphia!
They offer a 3 course dinner special that is absolutely reasonably priced from Sunday - Thursday!  Out of this world tiramisu and creme brûlée... Nothing else to say other than don't miss out visiting this restaurant!!

Fergies Outside (my name) is a great place to get some food & drinks while enjoying the still pleasant weather. Having Sansom street closed and used this way is fantastic. Hats off to the crew at Fergies for being so attentive, friendly and accommodating.

Excellent! The Parisian Za is one if the best things Ive ever eaten. The menu is super inventive and interesting. Delicious coffee and the nicest management and service. Thrilled to find this place with the help of yelp. The only con is that its small and can be hard to get a seat and it may be a bit pricey for breakfast, brunch and lunch for some. Expect to pay about $20. But it's worth it!
Man, their groothies are good. 'Nuff said.

Me and my boy Weeta ate here some nights ago. We happen to be in the area after his tinder date and thought we stopped. Unfortunetley it was a cold saturday night so the wait was really long but well worth it. We got the chicken cheese steak, standard cheese steak, and a burger all of which were great. You could add any topping you want free of charge we didnt overwhelm them and just added standard pepper/onion/cheese/ketchup/mayonaise. This is where me and Weeta slightly disagrees as he prefers canned peppers/mushroom cause his mom uses them but i prefer the fresh ones. Even with the longwait time and the difference in opinion we still found it great so that should speak to the quality/portions of the food.

In the middle of the winter during the pandemic, Mark from JB Plumbing was there for us. Twice. First, our trap to the main drain to the street finally went bad and our hot water heater died a couple of months after that. I did my research and called around.  The big companies were hard to contact and the customer service was really cold. Not to mention those big chains and franchises rates were much higher because of the advertising and middleman.  Mark really took care of us.  He put a plan in place, got his incredible, timely crew out and made two really big problems go away.  Each time, he was texting back and forth with me, walking me through it with snapshots and directions.  JB provides that old school customer service that I thought went to the wayside a long time ago. Thanks Mark!

Lovely bike trail/path. Nice ramp view of a large dog park. Boardwalk is pleasant. If running/biking avoid 10am saturday, most crowded. MLK drive is better midmorning Saturday spot. Rest of time it seems to handle large volume of people well!
Below average Chinese-American food based on the few meals I've had.
Very unsuspecting place! Absolutely fantastic burgers (especially the homemade veggie burger!) and sweet potato fries.

I literally signed up for a yelp account to review this beautiful studio. Nestled on the top of a theater building, the studio is across from Shambala Meditation Center. When you enter you feel like you are entering a completely different universe from the rest of the building: the walls are all welcoming colors, and there is gorgeous exposed brick in each of the studios. I also love the huge arch windows. In the mornings, the light is lovely. 

I like that you can come here for an array of classes, and that classes are categorized by mood(and time of day). Only con is that sometimes there aren't as many afternoon classes that are for a sweaty workout that fit with my schedule, but that just means I have to get up early which is better for me ultimately to get a workout. 

There are also multiple changing stalls and two bathrooms. 

The teachers here are fabulous, and very focused on making sure your alignment is proper and that the class is consonant with what you want. So far I've taken classes with Tracie, Brooke, and Stephanie. All three are extremely knowledgeable teachers who will pay attention to your individual needs.

UPDATE: Wanted to say that I took a class witht he studio owner Corina(and I was SO impressed  by her knowledge + her wonderful sequences. Also took a class with Elliot and it was great.

Love this market! Definitely check out the RAW milk!!! And also the grilled cheese place :) 
I do wish there were more organic options, but it's a really fun place to look around, and great food!
had an issue with management and went in and they resolved it quickly with the help of a salesperson. thanks.

I came her during Philly beer week for the 3 course/3 beers specials and it was great. My girlfriend and I both loved the mussels and car-bomb bread pudding. Service was good as well, and it was an overall great experience. 

I will definitely come back.

Try the soup! I always get the Hershel's special with pastrami (which is obviously incredible), but today decided to try the soup of the day, which was barley-beef soup. Incredible! I'll definitely be trying their soups from now on. The hot dog with sauerkraut was great as well. I love this stand.

Oh my goodness! The sandwiches here are incredible! Especially for airport food! I had just a regular Philly Cheese Steak sandwich and it was probably one of the best sandwiches I have had. The fries are great, the sandwiches are great, everything is great. If you have a layover in Philadelphia and need some food, this is the place to be!

Very Impressive. Love how spacious this location is and I quite enjoy the view from the second floor. 

However, they could definitely keep the gym floor a little cleaner because just about every machine or piece of equipment I went to use had used paper towels on them or near them. Meanwhile the staff at the front desk were too busy chit chatting with each other to clean up the paper towels all over the place.

Great location, so I will definitely be back.
The lunchbox was the perfect amount of food. The sides, green beans and corn, were perfectly seasoned. The turkey was incredibly moist with good flavor. Also, the line moved quickly.

I've heard so much great things about Giwa but........
I absolutely loved the atmosphere and the restaurant itself. I called for pick up, the gentleman that answer the phone was extremely nice and patient. He answers all of my questions calmly and I didn't feel like I was being rush at all. 

I wanted Galbi and it is on the Yelp's menu, but when I call he say that Galbi is no longer available. I was a bit bummed but he suggest the BBQ bowl so that's what I got. Picking up the food was very smooth.

The presentation of the order was good. When I think of Korean food, first it comes to my mind is their fluffy, soft white rice. Unfortunately Giwa's rice reminds me of chinese take out rice, hard and chewy:( Second, the steak was inconsistent, like some pieces were nice and soft, other pieces were tough. Oh yeah, the vegetables that comes with the bowl is just plain salad, and they were quite bitter. The BEST thing about the bowl was the kimchi, they were very flavorful and good. That was the only thing I ate all, the rest I let the trash have it. I also got the peach aloe drink, they have a funny taste to them, trashed it.

Decent selection of cigars. Lounge is in desperate need of an upgrade. Chairs are worn to the point where the springs are poking through the bottom. 
1 Tv only I guess about 50 inches. 
Great location just needs a little TLC.
Extremely rude, terrible service & subpar food. I definitely will not be back. Didn't even provide a bag or napkins.

I would say the place is okay. Really hyped up. Maybe I had really high expectations. I got the slammin salmon and my sister got the flaming tuna. Bland when it came to taste... If you try it, think of a big ol handroll.

Yeah, I did it.  I stood at the top of the steps, threw my hands in the air, and yelled Adrian!!!   I didn't run the stairs, but lets be realistic... I don't run anywhere.  Be sure to tip the guy that wanders the stairs to take your picture.
Worst Dunkin' Donuts location ever. Coffee taste like water and if it wasn't for the perks app I'd be pissed about the milk infused sugarless water. Now I feel I like lost 0.54 in the couch.

Bad service if i can rate no stars i would. The pedicure was fine but the lady kept cutting my nails so short that it hurt and when it was time for my manicure i showed her how i wanted my nails done and design she said she can do it so i was a little happy but the end result are not wht i wanted. When she finished paint my nails i asked what the design again and she grabbed my phone and showed the other nail tech and they both laughed and said no we dont do. And everything came out to be $51! So something i couldnt get.. very disappointed never coming here again. Plus they are real rude to the customers.

U want New York pizza in Philadelphia?   This is it!!
We were here by accident a few years ago and could not believe that we ran into a gold mine.   3 years later we were back in Philadelphia and this time Petes Pizza was a destination place.  Luckily for us we found it and the quality was just as I remembered it to be.  Great! 
If you want great quality New York pizza that is just about the food and not the decor,  this is the place.
Love this place. Oysters are great, and the crabcake is phenomenal as well. Pricey, but you pay for quality.

Ambiance is nice but either the owner or the staff's kids and relatives hang out there.  The little boy was crying and screaming at times and the girl was running back and forth behind the counter and the seating area.  Kitchen staff were walking in and out for smoking break.  This was during peak dining hour, 7:30 PM on a busy Friday.  We had 4 dishes and they were all too salty to us.  The soup dumplings weren't tasty.  We had been to many that were way better than this.  After dinner, we both kept drinking water so we suspected there were MSG.

Great quality printing! My print's colors were slightly off but what do you expect with next day printing and cheap pricing. Their available papers are really nice quality. They really need to redo their website. Call over the phone to bypass their vague printing instructions online. Prepare to venture into the badlands of kensington. I suggest getting your product shipped to you to avoid any shady encounters.

The food was pretty decent. However, the restaurant does not honor reservations. Our group made a reservation for a private room at 7:30P.M. we arrived at 7:20P.M. so we were a bit early and understandably they told us to wait a bit since the party before us was not finished yet. At 8:10P.M. we still had not been led to our room. We asked our host what the issue was and they said that the people before us were still not finished. The point of having a reservation is so that the room would be reserved for us at that specific time. Why have reservations when you're not going to honor that? They ended up telling us that we would have to be seated at a random open table in the restaurant. To which we begrudgingly accepted due to our time constraints. Our party was rather large, they didn't have any tables open that could accommodate all of us which is why we reserved a private room. We ended up pulling up chairs to a much smaller table with each of us having no elbow room to move, much less eat. While the food may have been good, we could barely enjoy it due to not being able to move our arms to use any utensils to eat.

went here for dinner with my boyfriend, I'm a huge fan of tapas and of bar food but valanni is disappointing as to both. Service was also pretty bad, the waitress only stopped by the table to take our order then we never managed to get her attention again so I the oversalted foods and never-refilled drinks and water left us parched and unhappy. It's also a very noisy restaurant, not the best spot if you're looking to have conversation during your meal. The only thing we liked about our meal was the lobster mac and cheese, it was very tasty, but unfortunately not good enough to get us to go back again.

Had dinner here on Wednesday night and was glad we made reservations because the place was packed. We started with the KFC (spicy) and octopus. For dinner, we shared the dolsot bibimbap with beef and bulgogi burger with a side of kimchee. The octopus was our favorite of the night, followed by the bibimbap. I had pretty high expectations for the KFC and burger. Both were good and definitely tasty, but not in the running for the best I've had. Based on first impressions, I'd give this place 3.5 stars for now.

I have tried a few cheesesteaks since coming to philly and so far, this is the best. They have sides as well to go with your sandwich and although they are a bit pricier than other places, their portion sizes are fairly large. I have recommended it to a few others and they have enjoyed it as well.

I like going to this Loft store on Sundays because parking is easy and the store isn't crowded!  Definitely beats making the trek to Springfield or King of Prussia or dealing with traffic and parking downtown. With this location, I can be in and out.

My whole life, the human body has astounded me. Its abnormalities, even more so. Though I never went into the clinical side of things, I have worked in the health care field for more than six years, and I've seen some pretty interesting things, many things I must keep to myself due to HIPAA. (Of course.) Still, I've never seen anything truly abnormal in person, merely in the pages of books or the screens of computers. Mütter Museum definitely gave me a memorable few hours. 

Relatively small in size but jammed packed with a rich history, Mütter Museum has some mind-boggling permenent displays and provoking exhibits. I stared slightly slack-jawed at the rows of human skulls and their descriptions, tubes housing various body parts, and photos of people dealing with almost inconcievable medical issues. As someone that has worked in Infection Control for many years, I was intrigued by "Broken Bodies, Suffering Spirits: Injury, Death, and Healing in Civil War Philadelphia". (Oddly fitting to see on Memorial Day, such as I did...) I read about how many health care professionals thought disease was spread through unclean air or misama, so they never bothered to sterilize instruments or bandages. Needless to say, that would NOT fly in the operating rooms of my hospital today. 

Their motto is to become "Disturbingly Informed", and boy, was I! If coming to Philly, this should definitely be on everyone's "to do" list. 

HOWEVER: Points off for not having a single spleen on display! As somone that lost theirs in 2008 due to a interesting medical anomoly, I was excited to see one in a jar. I saw hearts, livers, kidneys, conjoined fetuses, even a penis and scrotum covered in tumors. BUT NO SPLEEN? *sigh* I'm not even being that factecious when I say I was a bit disappointed in that. Want my old one? I'll go back to the hospital I had it removed at. Maybe they never incinerated it...haha... ;-)

I order food from Core De Roma at leas once per week through DiningIn. Everything that I have received has been extremely well prepared and is always delicious. I cannot speak for the atmosphere as I have only gotten delivery but everything I have ordered has been great

Ordered ravioli, pizza, and ricotta stuff veal meatballs. Everything was delicious my most favorite being the meatballs. The ravioli was very small in portion size compared to the meatballs, which were listed as a small plate. Overall great experience!

the 24.95 sushi buffet special which is now available every day is a deal if you eat a lot and chose well

the options on the menu at the restaurant are more extensive than what is online

the specialty rolls are a bargain in their sushi buffet, i am fond of the Samurai Roll, spciy snow crab, spicy salmon, and avocado wrapped in a thin pink soybean wrap with special sauce, regularly $14 by itself

the sashi appetizer is 3 pieces of tuna and 3 pieces of salmon.

the handrolls are also lovely though i found the maki to be a bit unflattering looking.

also, when the restaurant is busy it takes a long time for the sushi to arrive

Sigh, I really, really wanted to like this place, and I think that based on the quantity of food alone, Han Dynasty deserves some recognition, but there are a few shortcomings that should be mentioned. As a native-born Chinese, I appreciate specialized cultural food that remain true to their roots without resorting to the inevitable transition to the Americanized taste-palate route. There are some dishes that I think should garner a sample: the dan dan noodles, the double cooked/fried fish, and the peapod leaves. Some dishes are ok: hot wok chicken, eggplant, pork buns (much more Northern Chinese in taste: fluffier, heartier buns w/sweeter pork filling), and mapo tofu. I was really disappointed by the rabbit (more peanuts and small chunks of bone+meat) and wontons in chilly oil (all wrapper and no protein). Please be aware that the food is very salty and I do recommend getting the spiciest option as it truly brings out the flavor of the cuisine. My biggest disappointment is that even though I called a month in advance for a special chef-tasting menu with specific request for food, spices and other important requests, the staff completely disregarded it and brought numerous dishes that were not able to be enjoyed by the bulk of our party. Also, the special reserved space was $125 and inappropriately cooled during the entire meal. In conclusion, I think there is much room for improvement for Han Dynasty in my opinion, and hope to return when some of these issues can be addressed.

Food 3 stars
Service: 2 stars
Price: 4 stars (for the food to cost ratio)
Ambiance; 2 stars

There's a reason why - with all of the amazing yummy goodness within Reading Terminal Market - Miller's Twist is amongst the best-reviewed of them all. I went to DiNic's like any good touristy food whore would, but Miller's Twist was my favorite. If there is one place that I would say I MUST go back to on a second trip to RTM, it's Miller's Twist. Hands down.

DiNic's isn't my wife's thing, although she did have about a third of a Roast Pork sandwich. But soft pretzels - those are definitely her thing. So when we saw Miller's Twist, we had to try them. Although they have a few different types of soft pretzels to choose from, my wife kept it traditional and got a plain soft pretzel. As for me, my eyes were drawn to the various "Pretzel Roll Ups" they offered. Pretzel-wrapped dogs and sausages, they were all well and good, but a cheesesteak roll-up? Yummy, gooey cheesesteak wrapped in a pretzel? Yes please! 

And WOW, that cheesesteak roll-up was AMAZING. I mean, seriously, one of the most delicious and decadent things I had ever had. The pretzel was perfect - soft and delicious - and the meat and cheese inside were blended at the perfect ratio. My wife was raving about her soft pretzel, so I tried a bite. Warm, buttery, delicious .... to say that this was the best soft pretzel I ever had would be a vast understatement. I felt like the soft pretzels I had been eating were like driving a Chevy, and I'd just taken off in a Ferrari. 

As I reflect on the wonderful food I ate on our Philly mini-vacation, and the pounds I gained from it, I look back on Miller's cheesesteak roll-up and pretzels as fondly as anything else I had.

Being able to enjoy a nice meal in a restaurant with friends and family is something I so enjoy. Doing it gluten free is another story.  Giorgio on Pine is a fantastic find. Not only did I enjoy the service, the food was fantastic. Finding a restaurant that is willing to accommodate those of us who are gluten free with the foods we miss the most (pizza and pasta) is a rare find. Be prepared to add salt. It does get loud but enjoy it. It's Italian after all.

Interesting concept. First time having rolled ice cream. It is probably double the price of what a normal ice cream would cost. Got nothing extra other than to watch them "roll it out." 

Cool to do it once but probably will just enjoy a traditional ice cream in the future. Once rolled it looks great but when you start to eat it and it starts to melt, it goes back to its clumped ice cream form. Basically you paid an extra $4 to see someone roll it out and then it melt back in 10 mins.

On the other hand, they had some great flavors and plenty of options for toppings. The price was about $7 per cup. Unfortunately you can rise a credit card unless you buy $10 minimum so they know you have to add additional toppings or buy more if you are an exclusive cc user.

Staff was friendly. We got there right when they opened so it wasn't busy and we had all 3 of the staff helping us. They explained everything well. Helpful for sure.

Will not be back, but it was an interesting experience.

If I lived in this hood, I would be here at least once a week. That being said, it's a lovely place, but not sure I would travel for it. 
The drinks are great with generous pours. The food sounds amazing... but maybe in real life doesn't live up to its hype. I was expecting all things hygge and I think I got lukewarm. The fries looked that perfect shade of crispy brown by were slightly soggy. My spaetzle needed more salt and richness. 
The service was lovely and attentive.
So if you're in the hood definitely check it out but don't make it a destination spot

I called for the past today's and they keep giving me the run around with my results and not ready and finding out they didn't test first more than one thing l, which the doctor originally said he would. The secretary said she can't prescribe anything new to me until I come back into the office for a $35 co-pay to see a different physician. I told them, no,  I'm not paying anymore until I find out my results. This is the worst experience!

We were happy to see that someone else bought this place because the previous cafe was garbage. As soon as it opened, my husband went over to try out their coffee and breakfast. He used to roast coffee for a living and has pretty high standards. He raved about how great the coffee was and how delicious his breakfast sandwich was, and how nice the owners were and suggested we go there for dinner that weekend. I figured "why not?" And didn't get my hopes up for much since it's a tiny place but I figured it would be a nice night out for the family. 

We went on a Friday night about 2-3 weeks after they opened. We were greeted by Lisa and Michael who seemed so happy that we came in and made us feel like family right away. I was actually surprised how busy it was since the previous place never had a soul in it. As we sat down, I realized from looking at the other tables that I should have brought my own wine but never fear! Gianna and Lisa had plenty of wine! I was already happy as we ordered our entrees and they both made sure we were happy and well taken care of. The food was amazing and the meatballs and sauce tastes just like the old country. You can't get any closer to your Italian mom's cooking than this, even though it's done by the wonderful Michael, who is as cheerful and friendly as they come. 

We have since been back for another dinner, which was even better. Christina waited on us the second time and was an absolute delight. The bruschetta is so delicious. The chicken Angelina is wonderful, and the cannoli's are amazing. We've had breakfast a few times which is always great. The cappuccino is absolutely PERFECT. I want to drink them all day, even though I shouldn't lol. Definitely stop into this hidden gem in Tacony and you will be treated as part of the family and you will leave stuffed, hopefully tipsy (make sure you bring that whole bottle of wine!) and very happy. Can't speak more highly of these guys. They're doing an amazing job! Keep it up guys! We will definitely be back soon. Oh and make sure to bring enough cash because you will want to order everything!!!

I don't know why they received 4 stars. I drove 2.5 hrs to try their chicken pho the reason I came because of their reviews. Earnestly speaking this is not an authentic Vietnamese chicken pho the broth tasted bland and need more flavor, the service was suck and I believe the owner is not care if you're Vietnamese I came and wait for 15 minutes to be seated and another white couple came in and was greeted and seated before us so I told the man I have been waiting for 15 minutes and he pointed at the table at the corner and told us to seat there. This is so freaking rude but hey I drove that long so might as well try their pho.
First and last never come back.

I wanted to get my grandmothers wedding ring/band separated after being soldered together for over 50 years. I looked around and researched many spots on Jewelers Row when I finally came across Fishtown Jewelers instead. Based on all the positive reviews I decided to take my rings there, and I am unbelievably thankful I did. They took such good care of the rings and did a beautiful job, all while explaining the process and using their expertise to make sure they were not only beautiful but structurally sound. 

I cannot recommend Fishtown Jewelers enough for their personal attention to detail and workmanship.

Looks can be deceiving. 


This place looks like some sort of club from the waiting room super slick and chic but the inside is a mess. The staff is poorly trained and the equipment is terrible. The first person I saw tried to perform an x-ray 4 times. I kept my cool and was super understanding. But man was it uncomfortable. After the fourth time, he told me that the machine wasn't working and I had to go to another room. The fifth x-ray went fine but then came the hygienist. 

She was very friendly but her skills were lacking. she stabbed my gums several times and after my teeth didn't feel much cleaner. She said that they only do a light cleaning. 

Then came the dentist. I don't know whether I had sucker written on my forehead but he must have thought so. I have a cavity on one of my molars that tingles sometimes. He said that was fine it just needed a filling. whew, what a relief. 

He then tells me that one of my front teeth was dead and had to be pulled. and a few of my other teeth had serious decay that hadn't made it to the surface. I told him I didn't feel anything. he said that the x-rays clearly showed that the teeth were completely rotted and that I should go to the dentist more. Often. He was really condescending and blame me for not taking better care of my teeth. In all the wanted to pull four of my teeth and none was the one I have a  noticeable cavity in. 

When I went to the front desk the lady very politely told me that since the teeth were in my "smile zone" I should get porcelains because otherwise, they wouldn't look very good. she did note they were the most expensive option but swore they were completely worth it. They wanted to charge me about $6000 for teeth that I never had an issue with.  she told me it was okay because my insurance would cover most of it. They checked and saw I hadn't used any of my deductible. 

Then came the kicker. She said that since it was so late in the year I could get half of the work done this year and next year I would my deductible would reset and I could use that for the other half! I'm glad she had that all figured out FIVE MINUTES after my exam. And surprise it would be just enough to cover all the works that I suddenly needed. 

I will never go to this place again. If you have decent insurance beware, you might find that you need work that's just enough to max it out.

We are so happy to have authentic Greek food back in the neighborhood.  It's BYO and the food Is fresh, reasonable and incredible.  The staff is welcoming to families and the open windows are awesome.   Try it!
Amazing vegan food!! Quick and very friendly service! I'll be a regular here for sure.
Atmosphere and vibe is super cool... staff very nice. Drinks were great and food was amazing!! The burgers and the grilled cheese a must try!

I really like this place.  I've been a few times now and have always really enjoyed it.  The food is consistently tasty, the menu changes from time to time, the service is lovely.  I have only gone on weeknights, when there are other diners but it's not overly crowded.  It's so great to have a comfortable neighborhood joint that you can rely on for a nicer (but not fancy) evening.  Noise level is tolerable and it's great to have the option of indoor or outdoor seating.

This place is tops. I came off the hot street into the super stylish shop where the AC was ON. 

Had a chemix brewed coffee-- tough to find, a nice surprise. Staff was super helpful, square checkout with emailed receipt is awesome for my corporate expense account.
they're merely okay. the best part is really the truck, and the niftiness seeing it engenders.
The pizza is amazing! The vodka pizza was super flavorful and saucy! Honestly some of the best vodka sauce I have had, and the garlic powder on the crust was a nice touch!

This Radian is a great place to live, especially if you are a student at Penn. I am going on my third year of living here and couldn't imagine living anywhere else. It is a new building (built in 2008) and has a great variety of apartment floor plan options (1BR/1BA to 4BR/2BA apartments) as well as amenities (24hrs Fitness center, 11th floor rooftop lounge, Computer Center With B.Y.O.Paper Printer, Study Room). 

My top 3 reasons for choosing to live here are as follows:

1. Location - Located at 39th and Walnut St., The Radian is right across the street from UPENN's on-campus housing options such that your walk to class may only take 5-10min! Moreover, CVS, Bank of America, and a number of awesome food places to choose from (Chipotle, Bobby Flay's Burger Palace, Jimmy Johns, Capogiro, Sweetgreen) are right downstairs and a grocery store is just across the street. Literally EVERYTHING you need may be within a 1-2 block radius.

2. Safety  - Access Card Entry for Residents Only and a Security Guard present after business hours. Also because of the Radian's retail spaces all along 39th and Walnut St., the walk and entrance way is very well lit especially for West Philadelphia. The area is also covered by UPENN's 24/7 private security force.

3. Convenience/Privacy - The building is fully furnished which is especially helpful for residents from far away places. The year lease also ensures that I don't have to pack-up and store all my belongings for the summer over the summer but instead leave things under the bed while a re-letter stays there for the 3 month duration. All residents also have their own bedrooms and since the common areas offer ample space, I never feel like I am fighting for space with my roommates and can always retreat to my room at which I feel right at home.

I've actually been to the Fieldhouse a number of times for happy hours, game watching and other events. The space is fine but can definitely get packed for a popular game or event. This isn't one of my favorite places but it's fine. I recently went here on a Saturday afternoon and sat down for lunch. the menu isn't very big or impressive. I settled on one of the personal pizza selections. Again, it wasn't bad but for the $10 it wasn't great either. We ended up spending like $40 for the two of us, no drinks, and a very average lunch. Def not worth it. It's a fine spot for a happy hour, game watch or event but I would never make it my destination of choice.

Review is based on restaurant wk menu, we stop by for weekday lunch. Shared butternut squash soup, Rockefeller oyster for appetizer. Soup was hot and creamy just I like. Oyster was delicious as well. We got burger and crab sandwich for entree. Burger meat was well cooked since my mother in law ordered well done but was still juicy. I thought was pretty delicious. Fries were salty but I found most fries are salty to me in Philadelphia so.. Crab sandwich was my favorite, perfectly seasoned and thin slice chips were delicious as well. For dessert we got carrot cake and I thought was little bland. Salted chocolate and caramel tart was very delicious. Good portion of food and great service. I would go back.

A diner at BIBOU is a travel, a travel in a world of taste, spice and perfection, a travel to heaven.
It is difficult, almost impossible to get on the guest list. After trying for months when we finally got a reservation, we loved our experience so much that we booked for the following month before we had even finished our meal. Last Sunday was our fourth time but not the last believe me. We'll keep coming back ...

Very surprised that I had the best fish tacos of my life in Philly.  Great selection of beers, great employees and an amazing tuna-guac.

I went at lunch time and it was pretty empty, but I heard this place is packed come dinner and the weekends.  

Seriously thinking about making the 100 mile trek to eat here one of these weekends.  

One last thing - love the bartender's selection of music.  I even heard some schoolboy.

I've tried pretty much all of the foowiches this place serves and they are all very good (the Lemongrass Chicken is a little dry for my taste). But the Thai Curry Quinoa is amazing--my absolute favorite! A tiny bit spicy for those who might be sensitive, but for those of you who love things spicy, you can also get a side of Sirachi to give it extra kick! You can get all their foowiches in a bowl. I get my Thai Curry Quinoa in a bowl with an added meatball--must try! Everyone who works there is really friendly and nice. They are out near Drexel's main campus (University City) on most days. My friends and I try to make every Friday a Foowich Friday!

Very good ramen, will be back to this regularly for lunch. The only thing keeping this from 5 stars is the portion of pork was kind of small, and they charge extra for toppings that other ramen spots include in the dish at no charge. Good service and atmosphere.

We were out with my sister in Chinatown and wanted to grab dinner. As we wandered toward the place we ate last time (bc I'm a creature of habit), we stumbled across China King. A quick peek at Yelp on my phone showed Yelpers gave this place an A-OK. 

The place isn't in top shape; quite the contrary, it's a bit divey and run down looking but the woman working there was nice. There was a bit of a language barrier but we managed to place our orders without a ton of difficulty. 

I thoroughly enjoyed my steamed chicken and broccoli with fried rice. In fact, I ate nearly my whole plate (which is really uncommon for me!). The food tasted fresh and came out at the perfect temperature. 

The only downside is that the fortune cookies were stale. I don't usually eat them anyway though. And it can get very loud in there if a big group comes in. There was a table of about 4-5 that came in while we were eating and they were so loud; we couldn't even hear one another. Oh, they are cash only as well!

I have been here a few times and never fail to have an awesome time.  The food is awesome, service is perfect, drinks delicious.  Last time I was here it  was my birthday and we sat at the bar. The cutest bartender with a NY accent treated me like a birthday queen all night.  Whatever dish you get you have to get the tostones and sweet plaintains as side dishes... both are perfect accent to meal!

I was excited to try a new Lebanese/Mediterranean place in the city as I'm Lebanese but I was so disappointed. The food isn't authentic, it's Americanized. The service was fine, but due to the underwhelming food, and overpriced menu I won't be back. Also, they don't have traditional Lebanese coffee, or baklawa, but they have peanut butter hot chocolate?! I don't get it.

Overall, the Independence Beer Garden is one of my favorite beer gardens in Philadelphia. It's located right in Old City, and close to a lot of other attractions. The ambiance is what you would expect of a beer garden - open concept, large picnic tables, outside games (corn hole, ping pong etc.). During our last visit, the beer and cocktails were pretty good and reasonably priced. We ordered a couple of small plates - pretzel bites, wings, and fries - as well as a cheeseburger. All the food was good except the chicken wings. They were not cooked all the way through. The inside of one of the chicken wings was pink. After reporting it to the waiter, the manager came out and apologized. He even gave us a round of beer on the house.

A hidden gem if I have to say so myself, this small cafe in Philly is a great brunch place with a very distinct character.  I was drawn to this place one day when I wanted brunch food right after my monthly visit to the St. Johh Neumann shrine nearby.  The space is quite small, like every thing else in this part of the city and the age of the building really shows, but for this little coffee house it works perfectly.  And  they certainly have garnered quite a following especially with the solid four star rating on Yelp.  The prices are quite on the expensive side just as a heads up but the flavor and quality is all there.  I ordered the One Shot Breakfast Sandwich which is scrambled eggs and cheddar cheese on a brioche bun.  I added sausage to it because every breakfast sandwich needed sausage, bacon, or ham.  It's one of those unwritten rules.
