The sandwich will set you back $7.75 and some change which like I said before is pricey but I didn't mind because it was pretty big and they cooked in perfectly.  The scrambled eggs were light and fluffy.  I'm not kidding it really was light and fluffy and the sausage didn't taste like Jimmy Dean's.  At that price point I should have expected it but I was still pleasantly surprised.  I paired my sandwich with a gingerbread orange tea from Tea Source and it was heavenly.  It was sweet and aromatic with just the right amount of citrus flavor which I associate with a good brunch drink.  If you're not a tea aficionado One Shot serves the best java with their nice selection from Stumptown Coffee.  You can tell that their baristas truly know what's up.  I'm glad I made my way to this coffee shop.  It's a great place to have breakfast, lunch, or a combination of both.  I can say one thing for sure; it may have the name One Shot but they truly have a bulls-eye in my book!

It seems like a regular, wood oven type pizza place. 

Got a pizza where the most noticeable topping was soppresata. The soppressata was incredibly salty and had a spice similar to Chinese peppercorns, which was unpleasant. It was also liberally layered over the pizza, so it was in every bite and made it not particularly enjoyable. 

Also got the arrancini and the house salad, which were both alright.

Came here with my husband and a friend after a trip to Longwood Gardens :) Overall, i love the interior decors with Japanese animation playing on a projector against a brick wall (very nice concept!). We were seated right across from the sushi counter so it was nice to see the chef at work, since those table seems to require reservation for the omakase course.

We ordered nigori sake and some tapas-style food to kick off out meal. To start, we enjoyed their negitoro roll, which was packed and had more volume than i had anticipated (not complaining, though)! We also tried few of their tasty dishes such as the oboro tofu, hijiki, mentaiko fries (it's a pretty big portion), kalbi, etc.

I would definitely check this place out again (maybe for the omakase?) the next time i'm back in PA :)

Wedding Cake: AMAZING!!!!! One of my highlights of my wedding. It was so easy to pick a cake, pick a design, pick a taste. Irene is such a doll! She is the person on the block that's like a grandmother to everyone and feeds everyone. I absolutely love her! She kept it real, told us to just buy our own rhinestones to put on the cake to make it blingy. 

I liked that they make the cake out of butter cream and not only out of fondant which is pretty but quite hard. Originally I wanted that but she told us upfront what the cake would be like and I'd be happier with a softer cover. We couldn't decide on a cake topper so they put lavender roses on it. Delivery was free to our South Philly venue, they charge for the full cake not by slices (cost saver and thank GOD). 

Conclusion: Cake was delivered on time. All guests lived the cake. Cake was perfectly moisten and iced. Decorations was what I wanted. Service/ Customer  was great. Cannolis ROCK! Yes they are better than Carlos Bakery in Hoboken  and more reasonably priced. But seriously THANK YOU IRENE, thank you Oteri's for your service. You all were a great experience apart of our wedding day. Photos attached. I do wish for better parking too that way I can come and get more cannolis. :-D

Nice corner bar/pub. Ordered the short rib grilled cheese/cheesesteak spring rolls/mussels in a red marinara sauce. The mussels came out smelling very fishy and gravy tasted very bland. We only ate a few..I wasnt sure if they had gone bad. I said something abt the smell to the bartender (we sat at the bar) she just shrugged her shoulders. The rest of the order was great. Very nice bartenders. Quick with our order of drinks & food. Would return but I wouldnt get the mussels again. Oh & if you were wondering,  yes they chrgd us full price for the mussel we barely touched.

There are so many pluses about this hotel like the location, the comfy beds, the rooms, the staff. I've stayed in several Kimptons and I enjoy the constants like the free wine happy hour. Sadly, this time I wasn't wowed. The elevators were so slow that I thought at least one of them must be broken. I called for room service and had a less than stellar experience but once I let the staff now, it was removed from my bill. 

I wasn't able to check in early and that's understandable but I needed to get my luggage and they'd taken it upstairs so it took a little longer to get my luggage and sometimes you just don't have time to spare. 

I'd definitely give this place another chance though because between the location, the bed, and L'Occitane they've earned it.

Havent been here at night and im not from philly, but  the only 3 things To do in philly is get a cheesesteak from pats, the rocky steps, and get brunch here. Service is slow and a lil tude always lowers the gratuity, but the food selection and quality definitely makes up for phillys lack of amusement.  

the Silk brunch salad was a little bland but last time i had the corn beef hash and that was very flavorful.  

also the outside patio is nice especially hung over sunday morning!! 

Michelle the bartender outside is hot!

#1 
I called before the ceremony because I had a question about where to get the marriage license and she in formed me rather abruptly, that I need to go to city hall in Philadelphia , NOT to the Media court house, because Media makes you swear on a bible.  "Unless YOU dont have a problem with THAT" she said. Now, I am far from a 'religious' person, but as a spiritual person, if I was going to swear on a bible at any point in my life, it would be for my wedding, and maybe for jury duty. 
 
#2
Our wedding day approaches, and it is like most wedding days, running late, had to pick up my shoes, cant find the ring, etc etc...  We call and let them know we are running a few minutes late.  "Oh, no problem, just bring your MONEY, and your License."  So we get there 20 minutes late.  Come to find out the person who was marrying us is running late as well.  Great, Ill plug in my curling iron, and change, and then get married!  I let them know I had no problem and would be happy to wait.  They had another wedding coming in at 4 (in 25 minutes).  I even offered to go after them, and we would go get a sandwich across the street and wait.
 
#3
The woman marring us enters the building.  She runs into the room where I was changing and points at our bags, "NO", points at my curling iron "NO", points at me with my shirt unbuttoned about ready to change, "NO!","This is a civil ceremony!!  Get it your dress, get into the chapel and then you're OUT!"
 
 #4 
The lady starts her ceremony, and now with all the cameras pointed on her and us, she is a happy smiling person.  She looks like an angel, and now all is going smoothly.  Until, there is whispering out in the hall!  She stops the ceremony, runs to the door, yells "BE QUIET!", slams the door, comes back muttering about people being disrespectful.

#5 
Now, at this point, I was over her.  So, I am doing my best to hold my fiancés energy and concentration.  I lean in to give him a little kiss on the nose, kinda to bring him back into what we are doing, oh yeah, we are getting married!  I lean towards him to be brought out of it by a yell!  "NOOOOO!!!", the witch yells!  And she claps like she is scaring a cat away from a fish bowl.  Apparently no kissing before the ceremony has been completed.  
 
#6
I ignore her.  I'm not letting anything disconnect my soon to be husband and I from the reason why we are there.  We do the vows, we exchange rings, we kiss for the first time as a married couple and we look into each others eyes, while she barks, "Take a minute, take photos, all your stuff will be at the door."
We take our time, take some photos, and enjoy our happiness.  It took a little work, but we made it through with out being affected by her.  We walk into the hall after shooting some nicely lit shots, and go to get our marriage license.  All of our stuff is pilled at the door, and she is STILL complaining about how we were late, and this was ONLY a civil marriage.  I remind you, that she was 10 minutes later than us.
 
Luckily, I have a sense of humor, and this wasn't our actual wedding, just the 'paperwork' part. We were having another ceremony for our family and friends at a later date.  But, what if this was it for me?  What if this was going to be my one blessed day?  

I hope she reads this, so that she can be aware of her actions.  Just a little something in the back of her head that says "this is only a cheap 'civil' to me, but could be a lifetime of memories to them."

I used to love coming to this place for brunch, lunch, dinner or just drinks. However, the quality of service from the waitstaff has gone down and the quality of food flavors and presentation has really diminished. Aside from it being in a gorgeous location it's really not worth the trip. This used to be my go to place but I will be trying other locations in Manayunk just because this place is no longer worth visiting. Bring the quality back up Manayunk Brewery I miss your former self.

Wasn't crazy about trying this place at first, but once I tried it, I was sold! Food is delicious and fresh. Friendly staff--which is equally important to me as much as the food. If you watch your calorie intake closely, but also love tasty meals, this is the best place for you. Meals prepared and ready to eat now or later. You can stock up meals for the entire week here. I'm also the type that's against eating food out of the microwave (giving my food that microwave taste), but I've tried a handful of meals here already and none of them tasted like I just ate a microwave dinner. Again, delicious and fresh food. Love love love.

It surprised me that in a city like Philadelphia, I couldn't find a single window contractor on Yelp that wasn't a large franchise company.  So, I did it the old fashioned way... Google.  What I came up with was Emerald Windows, among other window/door contractors.  Low and behold, they came out today to install a single window in my home...

A.)  The original estimator was clean-cut, professional, knowledgeable and on time.  He made some solid recommendations and had the estimate out exactly when he said he would.  The estimate was detailed, he sent the company site where they order their windows from and made great recommendations.  The price was also fair and competitive.
B.)  The two guys who came out were also on time, clean-cut, professional, friendly, clean and quick.  They even made some recommendations for our front doors, which we'll be sure to call Emerald on when we're ready.  Nice "up-sell" guys!
C.)  Emerald's reputation as a long-standing window contractor in the area with humble beginnings made our decision very easy.  Plus, they installed some windows in Elfreths Alley, one of the city's most historic sites (my wife is a historian).

Naturally from my 5-star review I'd recommend Emerald to anyone looking to have windows installed.  These guys were just flat-out great to deal with and I was surprised the level of service we received since we were only having one window installed.  You would have thought we signed up to have 50 done!

Definitely cheap and delicious.  Me with three others ordered two orders of soup dumplings, scallion pancakes and four separate noodle dishes for 38.80 and we were stuffed.  Definitely well hidden but definitely worth stopping by

Delicious. I actually like that they don't take reservations. It filters out the uncommitted. It also gives you a few hours to work up an appetite. The menu was simple but perfectly executed. I loved the pork Milanese although I did feel the apple accompaniment was quite boring and too acidic. The lasagna was great too. I will definitely be back. This is a great romantic option in the area and it won't break you financially.

Dr. Lieberman has been caring for both of our daughters since they were born.  Right away we knew that Fairmount Pediatrics was a great fit for our family and will continue to be as our children grow.  During well visits, Dr. Lieberman addresses all aspects of our family's well-being, including sleep issues, eating well, and even welcoming a new sibling into the home.  Dr. Lieberman gives us direct and honest answers to our questions while respecting our opinions and concerns.  When our children are sick, Dr. Lieberman is available and responsive via email on the patient portal and by cell phone. I don't know of another pediatrician who personally responds on holidays, weekends, and after-hours.  Further, we are easily able to make appointments online and have never waited in the waiting room for an appointment.  We are always greeted warmly and treated respectfully by the staff.  Our 3 1/2 year old actually looks forward to visiting the office.  We feel lucky to have Dr. Lieberman and everyone at Fairmount Pediatrics caring for our children and if you are in search of a pediatrician, I cannot recommend this practice enough.

So I hyped myself up for Indian food. And since this place had the cheap lunch special I decided to try it. I got 4.99 chicken kabob, and naan. But should have went with rice. Anyway I see myself paying 5$ for it but I don't think I would pay more than that. If someone would gave me kabob I would just guess it to be some vegetarian. There was meet but very little. ( I get that is lunch special but still.) Naan was good I guess I didn't try any other places so I can't compare. But here the mistake I made I remember ordering just one special but they gave me too, regret not saying anything. I just got confused so just payed and left. Anyhoo, next time will just pay straight after ordering.  I didn't try sauces that came with meal, but I got chick fill a for they spicy sauce I had in my car. 
I did it got much flavor from chicken kebab.
Would I come back??, maybe just to try lunch special with rice.

Good, consistent and super-friendly. 

This place is around the corner from our house so we have been coming here at least once a week for the past 10 years or so. The food is good -- it's not a place I would bring a first date or business client to impress them -- but if you just want a good, filling meal at a reasonable price, you can't do much better than Pete's. It's been around forever, and when you are inside you can see a microcosm of the neighborhood sitting at tables or coming in to pick up -- everyone in Logan Square goes to Pete's!

In all the years we have been coming here, we have had exactly one minor issue with the food which they quickly rectified AND took the item off the bill. Service is friendly and efficient. Beer and wine list is decent for the kind of casual place it is. Prices range from reasonable to great value. (Their happy hour in particular is dirt cheap.) Our family of three can typically get dinner (and a round or two of drinks for mom and dad) for $30-40, and still have leftovers for the next day. 

Overall a great place to pop in for a quick bite or casual meal a step above basic takeout.
My notes say, "not bad". We just had the cappuccino. Not bad...

First night in Philly after flying all day and suffering rotten, lousy and expensive food, my wife and I were too tired to go far from our B&B (La Reserve, on Pine - go there!). Found this place a few steps away. Went there! 

Friendly and local vibe, vibrantly noisy, but not anywhere near so much that conversation is difficult. A small coterie of tables inside - be sure to reserve before you go. We didn't, but found a spot at the bar. Bartenders were helpful and nice. 

The menu is short, but you'll still have a hard time choosing. Fortunately, the young woman next to us got the hot pot. It sizzled hotly, scents and spices urging us to order the same. 

So we did. My wife got beef, I got tofu dolsot bibimbap. We tied for best dinner. No, seriously. The tofu was perfectly prepared, with a crisp just slightly chewy crust, yummily seasoned by its veggie environment topped off with a tingly red sauce. Get that. 

A small but tasty selection of beers and such went nicely with dinner. We could have stayed for another, sitting and yakking in the midst of other couples and friends enjoying the company and atmosphere, but we had pie to eat. Head to Magpie a couple of blocks away for dessert. 

Southgate is great. Go there.

I have a mailbox at this store. They have always been great notifying me of packages and having them ready when I get there. I've shipped packages both self-packed and that they packed. Earlier this year a package I shipped got lost and I couldn't find my receipt for shipping only for packing materials around that day. This UPS mailbox store went out of their way to help search transactions around that day and found my receipt and they contacted UPS for me so I could make a claim which was resolved.
They did an amazing job. The house was a mess after a renovation and is now completely spotless from top to bottom. I would highly reccomend and would hire them again.
Cancelling has been the biggest scam with this gym and their financial center. ABC Financial and they are all big scam artist. I would not join this gym if you ever plan to cancel.

Temple star's a great spot for that late-night thirst. They offer great food and awesome drinks and tobacco products. Gets crowded sometimes on a busy weekend but food is always on point. Call ahead to save time.

Recently tried their hot yoga and barre classes. Both instructors were very nice and helpful. I enjoyed classes with full of women in the room. The classes had great energy. I personally try to avoid hot yoga after 3 years of my Bikram yoga addiction which left me broken capillaries but I did not know they had hot yoga room until I get there.  Even though I am trying to avoid hot yoga, their class quality was great and instructor was knowledgable. Barre class was harder than I thought. All that small muscle work out was definitely tough! Overall great studio to have near by.

I know everyone reading is asking the same question, so lets get right to the point!  Better than Gino's but not as good as Pat's.  South street location is preferred simply because it is busier and you will get a better hot sandwich.  All these places have the meat precooked and just throw it on the griddle prior to assembling.  The Roosevelt mall location just seems as if the meat has been sitting on the sidelines for too long.   Even the Wiz was thickened and chalk like.  The south street location is preferred but not worth the wait.  Double Wiz is the way to go!

I can't say anything about the food since I only drank heavily before my flight, but my overpriced vodka wasn't bad.  Then again, it's vodka in an airport and at that point, I would have drank Malort out of the bottle.

The food here is really really good. Some highlights...

Dan Dan Noodles
Green Beans with Pork
Garlic Shrimp
Three Cup Chicken

I don't think you can go wrong with anything you order here. The service is also fast and friendly. The space/ambiance is just ok...usually very loud, very casual, but i think they are expanding soon.

I would advise one not to go here. First of all, I walked in and the place felt like a sauna. I then checked the temperature and it said that it was 76 degrees in the restaurant. I instantly started sweating especially after waiting in line for such a long time. I had to press my body up against the counter to keep myself up because the heat in the place was overwhelming me. Also, Immediately after I ate my sushi burrito I started feeling nauseous, followed by a headache. The next two days I was constantly throwing up with severe stomach pain. Everyone I talked to said I must have been given bad sushi, which is the only reasonable explanation I can think of being that the sushi burrito was the last thing I consumed before my sickness started. I have had a good experience here once before months ago but needless to say I am scarred from this past one and will stay away from now on.
Always hot and ready to go. Big or small, they have whatever you need. Great for meetings, organized events or just good old fashion party.

Great place!  Good food, had a sample on the house ( fried chips, aioli, pico de gallo and gazpacho). OMG! I also had the teriyaki pineapple burger and it was amazing. 
The bartender Lindsay was really nice and attentive.. Definitely a good time.

I really like this establishment and I would give the outward appearance and up keep 5 stars but the staff and the child care staff are some of the most disrespectful and rude people I have ever done business with.
Great place we will definitely come back. The arepas are just delicious. Great flavor! Simon was awesome definitely a must go

If I had to review based on the dine in experience alone then this would without doubt be a 5 star review.

I started with chicken curry dumplings. The dumplings were soft. They didn't fall apart despite a desirable amount of curry sauce.

The chicken stir fry was excellent. It was prepared spicy, as I requested, and was very tasty.

Even more pleasing was the complimentary hot tea. Yeah, you have to ask for it, but it's free.

I'm dropping a star for the take out soup. The chicken was not fully cooked and the soup was congealed. Steer clear of ordering take out soup.

Establishment is cash only.

First off, I want to preface this review by saying it's not a knock against the food, the service or my experience..that being said I placed my order via 3rd party and there was not a long wait time between then and my food being ready. My only issue, and this is only my opinion, I don't think you get enough for your money. I spent $11 and only got a sandwich, no fries, no drink. And it's not like the chicken itself was huge either. The lettuce stuck out more than the actual chicken. I could go to some other place get a spicy chicken sandwich fries and a soda for the same price or even less. That's my only issue but I do like to support local so I would definitely return and try some other things from the menu.

So this review is after the 2nd visit. I wanted to wait to post anything since I wanted to see what he would do after I grew out my eyebrows. Seriously....amazing! Of the 5 years of me living in Philly, I don't know why I haven't stumbled across this brow God until now, but I'm happy I did. I used to go to any old nail salon to get them waxed and over time my eyebrows were so thin. I then tried the HD Brows to make them appear thicker at Koco Nails...spoiler alert: don't do it. Nicck KNOWS what he is doing. His studio is very warm and welcoming and he takes his time to make your brow perfect. This isn't a in and out, wait in line type and pay your $5 type of deal. This is luxury for your brows, I don't even feel the need to fill them in afterwards. If you TRULY care about your brows and want them to be naturally beautiful call Nicck...seriously.
Very unique and entering place to grab a sweet drink and a nice dessert.  It is somewhere you want to return once you discover it.

Stopped in here on a trek around the city. I had this bookmarked for a while and when I found myself a few blocks away I couldn't miss the chance to drop by. 

I was eating "healthy" and ordered what is easily the hugest "garden salad" I've ever been served. I expected a little bowl of greens and was pleasantly surprised with the amount of food that was delivered. Plenty to tide me over to the next mean. Unfortunately, the wings that my boyfriend ordered were dry and not super flavorful so that was disappointing.

I also had a tasting pour of the pretzel beer. I can't recall the precise name but it was pretty boring. I think if I hadn't had expectations that it would have some pretzeliness to it, I would have liked it just fine but flavor profile had no hint of pretzel. It was just a basic, boring beer. 

I will be back if I am in the area - maybe try more food/beer - but I probably won't go out of my way.

OMG yesssssss. Sometimes I think I love Phileo more than I love my boyfriend. Then I rationalize, sure that's crazy, it's just yogurt you say. And yes, you're right, it's just yogurt, just delicious, just in several different flavor options, just with many many different topping options. Oh hell, I probably do love it more than him. (sorry alex!) The place is pristine-ly cleaned and staffed, and if you try some self restraint- it really is not that expensive. 

I mean- if you go in starving and have no self restraint- well it's gonna be like 10 bucks for ice cream- so watch it mister (and ms.)!

Other than that I really like the color scheme and the fact that I generally walk out of here with fro-yo and a smile.
I absolutely LOVE the service the service I get at Cedar Point. The whole staff is super friendly, easy to chat with, and looking to bend over backwards to make your visit happy and boozy.

A friend and I were in town for the Rock 'n Roll Philadelphia Half Marathon, and asked friends for good "carb load" places.  Since we were in Center City, we tried almost a week out to get reservations at Maggiano's, but no dice.  Thanks to Open Table, I got a reservations to Ralphs's on a Wednesay for a Saturday.  On a nice day, this is a fine walk from Center City.  For those who are looking for a fancy atmosphere, this probably isn't the place for you.  I loved the homey "vibe" or Ralph's, and saw regulars walk in and get hugs and kisses from the waiters.  We did get shoved in a corner and didn't receive the best service, but the food was really great.  I got the gnocci special, which was HUGE and divine.  Make sure you always ask for the price...I wasn't aware that spinach gnocci with 4 shrimps would turn out to be $28.  Yikes!  I would definitely recommend this place to people who love an authentic Italian experience without all of the bells and whistles of a chain restaurant.  Do be sure to make reservations.  The maitre'd turned down countless walk-ins.

I think this place is a VERY welcome addition to the neighborhood.  The decor is very modern and upscale - beautiful job there.  The menu is large enough to provide plenty of choice for most diners.  It is a mix of Vietnamese and Chinese dishes.

Although most of what's offered is standard fare for Vietnamese restaurants, Le Viet has elevated most dishes by using fresh ingredients and a very light hand in preparation.  The fried rice with shrimp and BBQ pork I ordered was incredibly delicious and not the least bit greasy.  Ours was a large party and we ordered two of the calamari salads served in a half pineapple - that was a big hit with the entire party.  Perfect for warm weather. 

I thought the prices were on par with those at other Vietnamese restaurants.  Most entrees were under $15.    I will definitely be making this place a regular haunt.

Keswick is a great addition to the neighborhood.  I brought a very busted up bike in thinking I could fix the problem with some oil on the chain.  They helped me out and realigned and diagnosed problems.  15 minutes and $15 later I have a working bike.  The mechanic was very nice and very helpful as was the guy at the register.

I wouldn't buy a bike here as they're super pricey.  Gotta capitalize on those rich college people in the area  I guess.  But, as a repair shop/place to pick up something small for your bike, it's a really great consumer experience.

Considering I'm now the duchess of ABP, I guess I should write a review!!  I come here soooooo often for breakfast as I pass through the mall on my way to work.  ABP breakfast is great because: 
1.  Salmon lox bagel with cream cheese only  $4.49
2. Large coffee only $0.99 with breakfast sandwich 
3.  Egg white and avocado on a bagel or croissant only $4.79
4.  Fast, very friendly service 
5.  Fresh do-it-yourself coffee 

Basically I'm always here because there's no where else you can get a salmon lox bagel with a coffee for less than $6!!  Thanks ABP!  (No comment on lunch, Ive never had it here).  

Also I like the touch screen in-restaurant that shows you the nutrition facts.  Helpful!
Terrible.  Immediately got sick after eating.  Then when I arrived to my hotel I checked my bank account and I had been charged twice!  I DO NOT recommend.

When driving to/from south philly from the Expressway there is a this little corner store that has been there for as long as I can remember and it is excellent.

It's the simple things in life like a vanilla cone, or some water ice or as you would expect to hear in South Philly, "Wooder" Ice.

Great selections, ridiculous portions and overall amazing experience.   If you can find parking and need a sugar fix...stop immediately.

I went last night with 5 of my gf's.  All and all it was a great meal! Wine list still needs some work and when two of my gf's were asking about some sparkling wine the waitress and one of the host/owner didn't seem to have a clue. 
We ordered a bunch of plates from the 3 areas- highlights for me- the calamari, curried cauliflower, lamb meatballs, and kale salad. We also had the duck dumplings- ok, grilled octopus- ok and some others.  
All and all- food was good, service was ok, our waitress forgot one of the specials and never came back to tell us; we sat upstairs- another large party was up there so it was loud and the bar downstairs seemed nice.  Definitely a nice new addition and will be back!

Yelp for the win, AGAIN! I brought a friend here last week for a bday dinner (he requested seafood) & we were both very pleased with the food & service. I had some of the best crab cakes since I've been living in Philly!!! My only complaint is that because this is also a seafood market, the raw fish smell is pretty strong, after you sit there for awhile you don't notice it as much. The prices are very reasonable for the quality of food, the crab cake dinner was $16 and it was all crab meat! The fries were good and seasoned well, and we were pleasantly surprised at how yummy the condiments were, I believe all the sauces are house made. I asked for honey mustard and instead I go some sort of lemon flavored mustard that was divine, I think I dipped everything in it. My date had the seafood platter with EVERYTHING, his flounder was HUGE, Neither of us finished our food. I will most definitely be back, next up is the soft shell crab!

Is halal food Arabic ? Why is there Chinese people working there? Why did their food not taste like halal? This place sucks I recommend going to the food cart near the Roosevelt mall I love it there!!

Second time here.  First time wasn't bad and figured we'd go again.  Our waiter was great but the "supporting service" not so much.  I was finished while the other person I was with wasn't.  One of the servers came over to take our plates which I was fine with because I like my plate cleared when I'm done.  But the server said I'll just take it when you're both ready.  I said, no, that's ok you can take my plate and she responded, "No, I'll take them at the same time."  It wasn't terribly busy so there was no reason I could tell.  This small incident really turned me off and was the deciding factor that I won't come back.  There are too many great places in Philly to go to.  The appetizer I had was amazing but the steak was so-so.

I visited Philly Feb' of last year, and I decided to stop here (Before I knew about Yelp, sadly) because it's the touristy thing to do. I wasn't impressed, service was decent and I liked the hot sauce and peppers. To be honest, I've had better philly cheese steaks at hole-in-the-walls around the tristate, but I enjoyed the experience. Didn't go to Pat's it was too busy, and it was too cold and windy.

Stopped here on a whim one night after dropping a friend off on south street. I LOVE poutine. If it wasn't so bad for you I would probably eat it more. This place is SO good. I had the traditional poutine and it was phenomenal. The fries perfectly hot and crispy, gravy was piping hot, and the curds were delicious.

The fries also withstood all the gravy and curds, I'm impressed. The place was clean and the service was very quick. Will definitely be back again sometime soon with friends!
First repair shop ever to have my car finished when they said it would be. Cleaned and vacuumed on top of it. Ez in ez out. Would highly recommend.

extremely charming,intimate and plain old nice place to go and have an excellent traditional italian meal. i went the first time last year with mom,aunt cher,and cousin same age as myself 30. we all had a wonderful time and exceptional meal.we are all foodies and were extremely satisfied with both the quanity and quality of the food.in addition to an excellent meal served by a very friendly and polite staff,the owner was on location and truley made us all feel like family.he was a true gentleman and offered us each a drink,and suggested what meals may be best for each of us after a short discussion to enlighten himself with each of our individual tastes.i had the veal shank over risotto with some grilled italian vegs,which quite honestly was the most delectable shank i've ever had and def the lowest priced.im not one to concern myself with the price of a meal thats worth it,but a low price for a meal u'd gladly pay more for,is a definate perk.the eggplant and seafood is also very good and plentiful.the meal was finished with a complimentary shot of lemonchello which is a delight all its own homemade daily,and was paired with a very sincere thankyou from the owner personally.classy touch... 2 thumbs up for sure, the atmosphere is intimate,dim lights,quaint place maybe 7 tables,traditional white table cloths,very clean,simple bar fully stocked.great for a date, maybe better to be with someone special as there is not much distraction,its very quiet.great for family like granny or mom or husband especially such a nice place.. do try you wont regret it.

I went here for my birthday dinner and it was a great decision! The food was excellent and the service was even better! Very friendly and personable. The atmosphere is also very nice. No complaints at all. I can't wait to go back!

This salon is the best in Philly! You won't be disappointed. The prices are great and so is the service. The ladies working here are so kind, sweet, and they know what they are doing! 

The shop is very cute and modernized with a nail bar set up. Not to mention there are so many colors, designs, and trends to choose from. 

I personally get long acrylics with gel polish and when it came to shaping and polishing, my tech was very precise. 

After finding this spot, I will never go anywhere else.
UNDER NEW MANAGEMENT!!! All you can eat sushi come hungry!!!! Bartender is friendly food is quick at lunch time. I think that's the best time to do no crowds!!

This place is an absolute must try! Their donuts are unlike you anything you've ever had before, they make and bake em right in front of you and they have soooo many to choose from! I went with the cookie n cream donut, I giggled after every bite, it was great! You gotta get their early cause the line will get long fairly soon
Jim's is the life of cheese steaks

I was born really far from Ethiopia, but I love Ethiopian food because it reminds me of food from the mother country.  When I came it had only opened for the day so it was still dead, but the bartender in the back kindly explained the menu to me and we both decided the best choice for me would be the vegetable tibs.  It was just delicious.  I really wish I got twice as much food because I would have eaten all of it.  The best way I can describe it is by calling it a vegetable stew.  But it tastes so much better than what I think most people picture when they hear the word 'stew'.  Anyway, great food, full bar, TVs, and a little outside area to sit out back too.  What else do you need?  Oh, a free parking lot in the back too!  Ten dollar minimum for credit cards - not bad at all.

Pizza is good but 4 of the last 5 orders from this location were incorrect. Incorrect items, missing  items... and I usually only order three things.  I've had great service from other dominos, but this one seems to have sub-par service.

If I could give 0 stars I would.  I had one of the worst meals of my life from Crazy Sushi.  We got delivery to my office which is a few blocks away.  I got a teriyaki salmon bento box which came with a California roll, salmon, veggie tempura, a salad and soup.  It sounded delicious but it was inedible.  The salmon was covered in a thick brown tasteless goo.  I LITERALLY peeled the sauce off of the salmon.  It was served on a bed of wilted, brown mung beans.  The salad was hot and they didn't give me any salad dressing.  The veggie tempura was not cooked.  I mean at all.  The batter was sticky and weird and the veggies inside (potatoes...for real) were completely raw.  My coworker for the teriyaki chicken, fried rice (a $2 upcharge for egg in your rice) and a salad.  She couldn't even eat it.  

Save your money and go somewhere else...anywhere else.

A very nice and well designed space.  The crowd seemed to be skewing more toward 30-somethings, but a good mix.  Our server was very energetic and attentive and couldn't say enough about the different menu items.  

We choose the Mac & Cheese (which came recommended from a coworker) and it was quite good.  The dish was served very warm, with the cheese still bubbling when it came to the table.  

Our meals were quite good. I had the Calamari Salad, which was a mix of greens/veggies and a "right" amount of calamari mixed through out.  

Christopher recommended the Chocolate Chunk Bread Pudding with Ovalatine Ice Cream for desert.  Not bad.  It was the first time I had had bread pudding in about 15 years.  If all the bread puddings were like those at Jones, I wouldn't have waited so long.  

An overall enjoyable evening and the first Starr restaurant that I truly enjoyed.
Never Again! Racist!!! Manger stated thats they don't like serving to "you people" when told didn't like one of the dishes that me and my friend ordered. Never again

~I have been to BRÜ Craft & Wurst 3 times now & plan to come back. First impressions: décor is rustic & aesthetically pleasing, & staff was very nice & more than accommodating. 

The quality of the German inspired food doesn't get much better. Stopped in for a brew & Bavarian pretzel the 1st time, & I'll just say all pretzels before this cannot compete to this barbarian of a pretzel. Now the 2nd time I stop in for dinner, & got a chance to ponder through their wide array of drink selections. As for the main course, I went with the "Wurst Plate", and let me tell you, it should be named the "Best Plate." The 3rd time was for more of a pregame for the club below (U-Bahn), drinks were fairly priced, & there was even a small dance floor with DJ to start getting "jiggy-with-it" before U-Bahn.

Recommendations: the customer flow pattern could use a little work when there is a crowded bar, although the self serve beer wall dissipates some of the crowded areas.

The Don approves of this establishment & will be back~

We have tried RevolutionHouse several times.  It is close by, and we really want to like the place, but...

The chairs are not comfortable, so I guess they want to keep the turnover happening, the service is mediocre at best, our wait staff disappears for long periods of time, and the cook staff does not seem to know how to cook a burger to temperature.  Ask for Medium Rare if you want any pink in your burger, otherwise it will be well done.

Firstly, this a great place to study. The music is not very loud and there is usually a table or two free. The environment is relaxing. The coffee is alright, but nothing special. The baked goods are delicious and they aways have something different.

The reason I am giving OCF four starts is because it is FREEZING in here throughout the winter and they refuse to turn the heating up. People are literally wearing their winter coats and hats in here. The guy sitting next to me is warming his hands on his coffee cup and shivering. It is known that OCF is super cold and people dress in more layers so they can survive while they study there. You should not have to wear a coat indoors at a coffee shop. Come on guys, TURN UP THE HEAT Please!

The  jazz  (Musami and the High Hopes) alone would have lured me in.   Large open windows with jazz  pouring out.....what a perfect way to spend a hot summer night.  The musicians all very much so got into their music and are awesome!

The reggae on the way out caught the attention of many also.

While here, we had dinner followed by absinthe.  Our dinners were very good with my partners stuffed peppers being outstanding.  I had the red snapper which was good.  The food is upscale and creatively delicious.

Our waiter was quite conversational and informative.  He patiently helped me navigate the absinthe menu.  His pick was right on.....St Georges as they were out of Muse Verte.  My second round (Pernod) not nearly as interesting as the St Georges.
I did not hallucinate as others have sworn I would.  I usually detest anything black licorice but St Gerorges is more than just sambuca smell/ taste.  Several other tastes invaded my palate.  Couldn't pinpoint exactly what they were but I did enjoy them.  
Wish they had sidewalk seating if only it were physically possible.

Had a very pleasant lunch here. I ordered the seitan Bahn mi and the seitan was kind of bland, but the rest of the sandwich and sides were delicious. I've had other stuff from there that was great. It gets crowded so don't expect to study. But very cute place, would def go back!

Wish I had taken pictures of my food but WOW. Everything was amazing. Can't remember the name of what I ordered but essentially short rib and noodles, so so good. The brussel sprouts were also great, not the best I've ever had at a restaurant but definitely top five and I've had lots of sprouts in my days. The edamame dumplings were pretty good didn't knock me out of the park-- my friend loved them though and she comes here regularly so if that's any indication of the quality of dumplings, I guess I'm just not the biggest dumpling fan. The pad Thai was also really good. Our waitress was friendly and helpful, we had a reservation for 6:45 and we were seated no more than ten minutes after that-- not a big deal though, it was a busy Saturday night so we expected it. Their desserts looked good but we both weren't in the mood for what was offered. Will def come back next time I'm in philly!

I'm obsessed. I've been on a neverending business trip for the last month during weekdays and this was the one thing I wanted to do this weekend, after seeing everyone's instagrams blowing up my usually food and yoga dominated feed. It did not disappoint.

Highly highly recommend checking it out!

PROS
+ Open every day until 1am.
+ HAMMOCKS. Although if couples don't stop canoodling in there, they will all be so low to the ground that your butt will touch the ground. Let's keep it 1 to a hammock shall we folks? I don't even get how that's comfortable.
+ Games! You can get bocce ball sets and there's shuffleboard and ping pong.
+ Rentable boats! including swan boats and dragon boats!
+ Just lots of room to hang, people watch, take pictures and remember why PHILLY IS THE BEST.

CONS
-Unfortunately lots of people have caught on to this place's awesomeness. Crowds and lines are an issue, although the beer line moved suprisingly fast.
-Food options are kinda limited- Garces has a shipping container dishing out burgers and fries and a cobb salad (?). The short rib fries were good ($8).
-Drink options are kinda limited too. $8 rum punch, $6 beers and they were out of some when we got there.
- What are we calling this place? The Park? The Harbor? Spruce Street Harbor Park doesn't exactly roll off the tongue and SSHP is a stupid acronym. Let's work on this.

I'LL BE BACK. MISS YOU ALREADY SSHP!

I love living in Park Towne Place because the residents and concierge staff are very courteous and friendly, the grass/parks are well-kept, the swimming pool is clean and big (the biggest residential swimming pool in Philadelphia), got the best view of the parkway, the room size, shelf space, and windows are huge. Now we have a new gym with boxing studio, spin/yoga studio, marketplace, dog park, skyline lounge, art gallery/studio, technology lounge, conference rooms, dry cleaners, and lots of green space! Sometimes I feel like I'm already part of the concierge team whenever I show off the place to my friends. And who can beat the social events like art events, Wine Thursdays at the Skyline Lounge, Yappy Hour, and mimosas by the pool on Sundays! I'm a proud resident of Park Towne Place, I love spreading the word about it. Got the best place to live in the city. Can't imagine living anywhere else.

We found this place for a quick dinner while staying at the nearby Omni hotel at Independence Park and liked it enough to return again for lunch 36 hours later!  Fresh food and creative menu choices in a great ambience thats at once modern but also quirkly with some nods to the traditional former firehouse in which it is housed.  Loved the duck breast with rice and grains, the free range chicken with brussel sprouts,  the Vietnamese fried squid appetizer,the pumpkin ravioli, and The yummy apple cherry cobbler dessert.  Will definitely return when in Philly!!

Wag the Dog (Stephanie and Doug) are the best. I am so glad we found them! My husband and I recently moved to Philadelphia from Tampa and needed to find someone to watch our 10 year old lab, Molly. Since Molly was a puppy our neighbors in Tampa would watch her when we traveled. I was very nervous about leaving her with someone different. As soon as we met with Stephanie and Doug we were instantly at ease and knew Molly would have a great time and be in good hands. Steph and Doug have doggie sat Molly now several times. They definitely go the extra mile (including backing homemade dog treats). I have since recommended Wag the Dog to several friends who have all reported back glowing reviews.

If you're looking for a place to go to after work or to go for a few relaxed drinks with friends, this is your place to go. The set up inside is absolutely beautiful and the prices are great. Everyday they have specials for pints and pitchers. The one let down I found about this place was the wings. Overall I had a good experience and definitely is a place I would return to.
Best Thai food I've ever had. Delicious flavor and packed with fresh veggies and top of the line meats.

Visited: 02/25/2016

We came here after dinner and it was average because in Duluth, GA there are so many of these types of drinks, this place doesn't really phase me. Actually, there are two Kung Fu Tea places in GA now :D I'm more biased towards Kung Fu Tea because of the tea taste. This time I ordered a warm drink and it was delicious :D So, whether you're in Duluth, GA or Philadelphia, PA, know you can visit a Kung Fu Tea :D

CPBK is awesome!! They've come so far in the few months they've been open.  The beer selection is great and only stands to get better as they establish themselves.  The outdoor deck is perfect for summer and fall.  The food is unique and delicious- beet ravioli!  I grew up in a house where my mom made borsch and we all ran away, so I was apprehensive to try this dish but my mind was blown!! Definitely a must try dish. Plus, the staff is really friendly and knowledgable.

The crust is thin and crunchy, the texture is similar to the crust of sourdough bread. So if you are expecting a stretchy kind of dough this isn't for you. 

Because it's thin, the pizza is very light. I ordered margarita pizza. I was expecting more ratio of the tomato sauce and more basil topping. In my opinion what I got is cheese pizza with some basil on top. 

Super friendly staff, there are some menu outside. I was disappointed for not getting a tiramisu because it's not available although it's on the menu

I was visiting Philly so I figured I have to pay this place a visit. The bread was little tough and dry but the steak was tasty. The staff was friendly but service was slow. I am not a fan of their fries. If I'm every in Philly, I will give them another try.

We  have dined here on 4 occasions over the last 3 months, each time with different persons in our parties, and each time raves all around.  Have not been less than very happy with any entree or appetizer which as been ordered.  Eclectic and well prepared offerings. Great service and very nice ambiance.  Reasonably priced for the quality of the experience.
Best Damn sandwich going.Beef brisket, carved pork are da bomb. Get either spinach or rabe with the provolone

I had been to the Vegan Commissary for a FooBooz After Dark Event and after looking at the menu board really wanted to come back and check it.  So when a group of yelpers were headed here for brunch I jumped at the opportunity to go back and brought a friend giving me the opportunity to try more dishes.  The poached avocado and hash was amazing and super filling, I'm really glad I ordered this item, I think half the table did and no one seemed disappointed.  My friend got the mushroom grits they were alright; I think I'd skip them for other items on the menu.  But he also got the side of potatoes and those were amazing, melted in your mouth.  I absolutely love that tip and tax is included in all the prices makes the bill so much easier and with our large group they were awesome enough to give everyone individual checks.  I wish this place was a little closer to home I might find myself here more often if it was.

Jeff is my secret weapon!  Although I feel compelled to keep my nails and feet in good shape, I've always hated wasting time at noisy salons.  Because Jeff only makes individual appointments, I can now enjoy the peace and quiet or even concentrate on reading or work projects.  Jeff is a true perfectionist, so I can mind my own business while he works his magic.

I should also note that Jeff appears to be the only person in town who offers axxium polish.  It costs more than regular polish and takes longer to apply, but it is definitely worth it because this stuff never chips.  I can go three weeks with my (natural) red nails without a single chip.  Where has this new technology been all my life?  

My only criticism is that Jeff's second floor shop is easy to miss.  (It's directly over Signatures hair salon.)  Look up!  Jeff is worth the hunt.

Apparently famous in Boston but highly do not recommend for the wonderful state of Pennsylvania! 

Ordered a selection of fried fish, oyesters, and shrimp, and it was all very below average. The fish made me want to run the other way, but the shrimp didn't cause too much worry- I could walk the other way? 

On the bright side, the calamari was good! ( Who messes up calamari though?)

Before I say anything negative, I have to say that a grocery store in this area was much needed. Provides mostly fresh fruits and veggies to that area of Philadelphia versus the local corner store. So thumbs up for that. 

BUT I would rather go somewhere else. 
1. lines were long... and even if there were 3 of us in line it took forever
2. cashier was inept (kept asking me "what is this?" and i would say things like "lettuce" or "corn" etc... i mean, I know I'm not your FIRST customer, I've been in line this whole time)
3. NO PAPER BAGS i.e. killing me with the ridiculous amounts of plastic bags. I like to rely on the paperbags from the grocery store because we do all our recycling those bags. So that sucked. Makes me hate them kinda.
4. Couldn't bring our cart to the car because it had those metal upsidedown U shaped barriers to prevent shoppers from stealing the carts. So we had to pull the car up front and it was just a lot of unnecessary traffic in front of the store because of that. Poor planning.
5. My peaches went bad in 2 days. Riddle me that. 
6. Kinda of an expensive place for that area. Boo. 

Sigh

Have only ordered off the snack menu here but it's always does the job and gets your tastebuds happy and belly full. The cheeseboard is an awesome value, $14 gets you three cheeses and an accompaniment of fresh fruits, compotes and crostini. Fish tacos and scallop ceviche are also excellent. Can't wait to come back and try more.

If you are looking for a new hairstylist - look no further. Ty is your person!

I had the great pleasure of meeting Ty this past week who worked wonders on my hair. I had some previous mishaps with my blonde and he worked some magic on making me brighter and more fluid. I also wanted some curtain bangs but like the good stylist he is - he suggested otherwise based on my needs. 

I am absolutely enthralled with the entire colour and cut of my hair and I cannot wait to go back and have him work his talents on my hair in a few months.

We thoroughly enjoyed our dinner at Aether. The selection of oysters was outstanding, fresh and delicious. Try the cauliflower tahini appetizer with fresh naan - a wonderful explosion of flavors.  The octopus appetizer is the best I have ever had.  Many of the grilled octopus appetizer at other restaurants have a stronger somewhat bitter taste. The mix of spices was a wonderful surprise and my guests who usually leave the octopus to me tried it and love it.  The scallops according to one guest was one of the best scallops dishes she has ever had.  Two of us had the bronzino and we both loved it.  The service was outstanding!  Great addition to the Fishtown restaurant scene.  Will definitely be back!!!!

There's Geno's, there's Pat's, AND there is Sonny's. Our first time in Philadelphia and wanted to hit up and try the city's famous food namesake...the Philly Cheesesteak. So glad we did not opt for neither of the two other establishments... we were on a time crunch and in the Old City and so we came across Sonny's. Lucky us, indeed.

The menu is pretty straightforward; pick your meat beef or chicken (pick beef), want onions? (say yes), cheese? What kind? (Say Whiz...as in cheese whiz). I got the loaded cheesesteak...can't compare to any other cheesesteak, but it was sooo goood! This should be the gold standard of cheesesteaks. Nice, warm roll. Thinly sliced steak, sweet peppers, grilled onions, and velvety cheese whiz. OMG!!

This is not a full service restaurant meaning there is no table service. You order at the counter and then you get called when your order is ready. Nice sized place but I can see the need to wait for a table when it gets busy.  The place was clean and staff was friendly. 

A must stop for lunch if you find yourself in the area.

I love one shot but I can't order food from here anymore until they fix the kitchen cluster, t's beyond slow the last 3 visits.  Please get an expediter or one that knows how to move the orders. So sad...
Emad Eid is the reason I will never rent here. Rude, nasty, and the worst customer service I have ever received. I love Avis, but will never rent from this location again because of him.

Phoebe's is okay. Some of the food was good, some not so much.

The pork ribs were huge. They had the amount of meat you typically find on beef ribs. They were tasty, but a bit fatty. I'd prefer less and more lean meat, but they were okay.

The cornbread was decent. It tasked good, but could have been a little sweeter for my taste.

The candied yams were good. Cooked well and very flavorful.

The coleslaw was a letdown. I prefer a sweet more mayo based slaw. This was light on the mayo and seemed to have some mustard in it. I did not like it at all.

The baked beans were okay. They were a little plan though. I'd like something like a brown sugar or honey kick to it. I like baked beans with a sauce that is a bit creamy, as opposed to watery.

The mac n cheese was decent. It was gooey and tasty. The one thing that was missing was a sprinkling of bread crumbs.

All and all, this is an okay spot. I would not write home about it, but would not run away from it either.

This is a good place to establish an alibi for a complicated murder plot; conduct an affair with your best friend's wife; or sell secrets to a foreign power. This airport  Ruby's location has a strange, 'remote outpost' type atmosphere. And why not?  Its located in something called the 'Philadelphia Airport Interplex'; which is a little compound of familiar business traveler's hotels (Embassy, Comfort, Marriott) sandwiched between the runway and the wildlife preserve. Is it inacessible? Not at all. The lonely, desolate, 'Eastwick' whistle-stop of SEPTA's R2 regional rail line is within walking distance. Just leg it down there via the busy highway full of cars speeding by at 65 mph. Only a thousand yards. No sidewalks. What could happen to you?

Seemingly serving only the dozen travelers ever residing at one time in "the 'plex", the restaurant is usually vacant; with a skeleton crew in place to serve you (they look desperate for customers, perhaps this is some kind of disciplinary location for Ruby's staff, as its so far out in the boondocks). How does it ever get enough customers to break even? You'll probably have the place to yourself. Still, the bar wench (usually Latina) makes a good margarita. You can hear her full life story. Twice. While tumbleweeds roll by outside and coyotes howl in the distance.

Looking on the bright side: its one of the best places to bring, meet, or pick up a hooker (if you ever see one in here, go for it), seduce a fellow business-traveler, or proposition a waitress by flashing a wad of greenbacks (as IF you've never done that before?). Hey, loneliness brings people together. Even the busboys conceivably have an opportunity here. Great site, just in general, to 'make a gol-dang fool o' yerrself, boy' --with little chance that it will come back to haunt you. Boy! if these menus could talk!

Okay, okay....so I traveled all the way BACK to south street to try this restaurant again. Me and my wife decided to pop in for lunch. We walked in and was instructed to pick a seat. Soft Arabic music was playing. When I sat down, the table cloth was dirtyyyyy. Old food was on it. The guy changed the table Cloth but I honestly would've felt better if they just had plastic table covers or even if he took the fresh table linen out of plastic. I'mNot gonna lie, I almost left. I don't know if they washed the table Linen or not. You have no peace of mind. I just hope I wasn't eating over top of anybody else's germs‍ a sista was starving tho so yup I took one for the team and ate anyway! Lmao

But the food was all that! We were both left completely satisfied. EVERYTHING was good. Lamb was  sooooo amazing. I had lamb, shrimp, rice, fries, salad w/homemade dressing, pita chips &hummus and white sauce. The pic speaks for itself. I can't remember the last time I ate something so good.

I took two stars off because they reallyyyy need to clean in there. It isn't dirty at all. Just ALOT of dust and no tlc put into the actual store where the customers eat. That dust really messed with my allergies. And them table covers. I don't know if they wash them. I really suggest using table clothes that can be disposed in front of customer. It wasn't a good feeling to be seated at a dirty table. If I wasn't so hungry, I would've said nevermind and went to another food establishment. 

SN: Hopefully they'll step it up next time on the cleaning tip. Cleanliness is next to godliness  If you pop in, please update us about the cleanliness!


Don't hesitate to eat here!! You won't be disappointed at all folks. Oh yea, I had the fat boy (I think that's the name) and I paid $30. It was soooo worth my money
I will be back and will be updating again

I am a loyal of the other -not to be mentioned other kati roll place in center city west-. 

I was returning to Philly and my bestie Anupma S. suggested we try this place for our drunk cravings. 

It's full of drunk young American Indians after 2am. 

I got the masala paneer roll and didn't like it. Too much fake curry it was so liquidy. And none of the crunchy onions like I like and fresh cilantro. And the white sauce didn't hit the spot. The curry was just that standard gross stuff you get like in the box at Trader Joes *exotic indian curry in 2 min*. 

Leaving at 3 stars cause service was nice, and fast, and maybe it was just my roll.

You can't go wrong with fried chicken and donuts. I recommend the tie seasoned chicken. They have two types of donuts one type that has two or three standard flavors that is like a cinnamon sugar coating and is available all day.  their specialty donuts are definitely the ones to go for. I got the grapefruit crème brûlée and a few other flavors and none disappointed. Make sure to call ahead if you're going to pick up a large order.

We came to the market while traveling for work and thought this place looked good so we gave it a try. 

I'll start by saying that we both love Indian food. The food looked good and smelled good, but unfortunately we were pretty disappointed. 

We got the chicken korma with a side of naan (they were out of garlic naan). My biggest issue was that it didn't have any flavor. The chicken was kind of dry and the sauce was missing the rich, spicy flavor you would expect from Indian food. 

There were so many other great things at the market that we ended up not eating most of it because it just wasn't very good. 

Maybe we ordered the wrong thing or maybe it was an off batch, but I probably wouldn't come back.

A very nice place for middle eastern/Mediterranean platters. The owner is a very nice man who genuinely cares about his business and customer satisfaction and comfort. Everything I've ordered there was fresh and prepared to order. The "three on one" juice is fantastic and freshly pressed to order, as well as the falafel Mediterranean platter. I'd eat here again.

- TIP:  Dive into the apps & street food sections of the menu.  Quesadilla with zucchini blossom, shredded beef arepas, chorizo montaditos -- all were solid.

- CON: Entrees were hit & miss. The one chicken dish was a glorified chicken-wrapped hot dog.  

- BOTTOM LINE: Impressively friendly & attentive service and delicious food.  There are a slew of solid Mexican hole-in-the-wall spots in South Philly, but this is a somewhat classier edition vs. the competition and definitely worth a visit.  I'll be back.

Yes please. Awesome ladies brunch. Bloody Marys were amazing... the bellinis were delicious... and yes, we did actually eat here too. The goat cheese and leek tart was wonderful. It wasn't at all what I was expecting. It was more like a pastry shaped like a flatbread and cut into triangles. The goat cheese was light and the leeks were blanched ever so slightly to get rid of that awful raw onion taste. The smoked salmon spread was also something I wouldn't normally order, but I'm so glad we did! It was perfect. The burger was a good burger, though I ordered medium, it was pretty bloody. The fries? Well, they were like popcorn... I wanted to keep eating and eating! I could have popped those in my mouth for hours and forgot was I was doing. Definitely make sure you order something with the fries. They must be double fried or well done as they are perfectly small and crispy. I also recommend the moules frites! Yes please.

Beautiful shop and very nice staff, but $5 for a mediocre cupcake? Not worth it at all. The cake is not very moist and lacks flavor. The icing is way to sweet and there's way too much. Better off going somewhere else to satisfy your sweet tooth.

Heffe delivers on both flavor and value.  I've only had the poleYO and the Tijuana poutine so far but I trust the menu completely.  The poutine was a generous portion that would be great to share with 2, 3 or maybe 4 people.  Even though it's cold that are still serving up hot food.
Donuts here are to DIE FOR!!!! We always bring out of town guests here for the donuts. The salted Carmel is my favorite omg. Worth trying one...., or five!

I had a great experience at Philadelphia museum of art. It was the first museum that I had to visit in Philadelphia. I was impressed by all the art work this location has. I can see my self returning again

PBR...I don't get it...Why isn't this place packed all the time?!  I wanted everything on the menu.  So of course, I settled on wings.  They weren't just buffalo...they were amazing, crunchy, cheesy and delicious.  This place is great to watch the game and relax!  Will definitely be back!!

Great Buffalo wings with real blue cheese dressing.  It took 20 minutes to get them, so maybe order them rather than stopping by to wait for them.  The sides typically could use better seasoning, but that's to each individual's taste I guess. The mains are usually spot on. I keep coming back!

This review might be a bit biased since I work right above the place. But i'm inclined to say that this bar is one of the best in the city, the food is great and they always have something going on. Phillies games during the playoffs, old city sideshow, video games on sunday nights, quizzo, cute wait staff, you name it they probably have it.

I've been renting at Hertz for 25 years. Like some of the other reviews posted here, I waited for a good 30 minutes for the shuttle to pick me up upon my arrival to PHL. They advertise a shuttle coming by every 5 minutes. When the driver finally showed up he did not look good...was bent over in the driver seat, head in his hands. If I wasn't so frustrated with the long wait it would have been comical. I'm on my way back to PHL tonight with a 2:00 am arrival. We'll see how THAT goes.

Absolutely amazing!  My meal at Jamonera ranks as one of my top 10 meals.  The food combines unique and original flavors.  They have classic Spanish tapas but add creative twists.  I would highly recommend the dates, crispy calasparra rice and the albondigas!  The only dish that wasn't a wow were the churros, but honestly after all those other dishes we hardly noticed.
The decor was very trendy and the service was good but I felt as though the portions were small and overpriced.

Nice ladies but horrible service. Supposedly they windows, I wished they never touched them because after they left windows looked absolutely horrible. The floors, they had clean the middle but never touched around the room. I have three small kids, they hadn't even touched the stairs. They are extremely disorganized, their vacuum was broken when they came and I went upstairs to be out of their way, at the end of the day, they told me they didn't know how to use my vacuum!!!! Like they couldn't ask me earlier! After they left I had to clean the house all over again! And I had paid $150.00!!!

Twenty Manning Grill is definitely not the kind of restaurant you go to everyday, but with a such a charming neighborhood feel, it's hard to stay away.  Located just off Rittenhouse Square, Twenty Manning Grill puts modern spins on classic American dishes.  Their inventive plating, combinations, and overall dining experience will make you feel like you're having both an exceptional and homestyle meal all at the same time.

From the house-made rye bread to the farm-fresh salads and exquisite entrees, Twenty Manning knows how to make even the most humdrum of plates taste like something wildly different.  For dinner, we split the farmers market beets.  Paired with goat cheese and lavender honey, this was definitely a sweeter salad than we expected, but still a great dish nonetheless.  My Berkshire Pork chop with honeyed figs, melted gorgonzola, and brussel sprouts was still on the sweet side, but also somewhat savory.  The entire service was also near perfect.

My main complaint and why Twenty Manning doesn't get five stars was the drinks.  My mojito was definitely not mixed well; it started out way too sweet and ended with almost no flavor.  Otherwise, this is a great place to go for that special occasion when you don't feel like getting all dressed up for some of Philadelphia's finer restaurants.
The pizza's are small and expensive, but I will say both times I ordered from this place they were delicious. I love the Moonlight pizza!!

Very short drive from NJ, about 10-15min on a Sunday morning and easy to locate. Parking wasn't too bad either but we also came at 8:30am
Normally we would sit inside but it looked crowded so we sat outside under an umbrella. There was a nice breeze so it felt great and relaxing!
The tables are a little tight and you can hear everyone's conversations but I wasn't here to talk I just wanted to try the amazing food that I have been hearing about lol
The Lady at the front desk was pleasant along with all the wait staff, everyone was attentive and accommodating! We didn't have to ask for anything before it was delivered
The menu looked great! My husband got the Steak n Eggs with Potatoes and Cheese Grits and I ordered the Creme Brulee French Toast! and Peach Tea (not too sweet with hint of peach)
It didn't take long for the food to be served, and I was so impressed! The food is so pretty with great presentation and it tasted amazing!!
The Creme Brulee Fr Toast was perfectly paired with the Fruit and the French Vanilla Glaze! Nice Brulee Crunch on Some Pieces of the Toast
The Steak was perfect medium and tender and the Cheese Grits???? The creamiest cheesiest grits they were amazing!
I was so happy we finally came it was a delicious beginning to our Sunday

Went for an early valentines dinner last night. Food was pretty good (not great), service wasn't. We were served an appetizer and but no plates to eat them over, so we ate over the soy sauce dish? I thought okay they're kind of busy it's fine. Our sushi finally came and again no plates. We politely ask the server for some plates and she says "oh sure" and walks away. We sit there for about 5 minutes just staring at the sushi and I was pretty miffed at this point so I get up and walk about three feet away from my table to the server stand and "oh look plates" so I grab two and bring them to the table myself. Server shows up about 5 or so minutes later, no plates in hand, clearly confused, probably wondering how the heck we got plates. Oh and it also took about 30 minutes for my beer to show up. How long does it take to pour a beer? As I said, food was decent, but not worth the bad service. Won't be returning.

I had two Swiss army watches that needed the batteries replaced. This was done quickly in under an hour and they look better than when I brought them in. The battery replacements came with a 3 year warranty. Will definitely be using them in the future.

Great food, great service, one star off for being overpriced.

House made guacamole with mango and papaya was really good. Chips are fresh and delicious. The mahi tacos are great, although the portion is two tacos for $11. This place is more or less like Mexican tapas.

We actually went into this place on New Years Day thinking it was a diner. We decided to stay anyway and it was a good choice despite spending a bit more than we would have liked for lunch.

Been here several times before and always love their "gates of hell". Not huge portion but it's reasonable with good flavors. $10 per bowl and so about 12-13 with tips & taxes. It is cash only though but they do have an ATM on site. Also it's BYOB. Overall, if you are in the city and looking for a nice quick ramen bite, I would definitely recommend this place.

This is such a hidden gem on South Street. I've come to the store twice for fingerprint card and USPS shipping services. Both times, the employees managed the line very efficiently. The first time, I was in and out in literally 5 minutes with my fingerprint card. The second time, there were about 3 employees servicing 8 customers, but I still managed to be done in 10-15 minutes. I will definitely be returning with my business in the future.

This was my first trip to Thread Bar and I have no complaints! I got my brows done by Ratna ( sorry if I butchered your name!) and she did a great job. She listens to what you want - and after the work is done she will make sure you're happy with what you have. If not, you can always communicate the changes you'd like with them and she'll do em! I probably asked for at least 3 editions after she was completed and I looked in the mirror, my guess is she wasn't sure of my preferences since I was a first time customer. Overall I was very pleased with the level of service and will certainly be returning in the future

Ok, so actually this is a 5 star store.  However I have a problem with one of the managers (skinny guy with short hair, I never saw him at the old store).  We have bought our beautiful chandelier with PVC drum as well as a multitude of other things (including a birthday present for my father in law).  And I swear every time we go in there this guy is instructing the sales folks on how not to be helpful.  The other time my wife was standing right next to the counter after I had asked one of them about a product that ended up being back ordered, and he was saying to the other staff how they shouldn't help people if the product is not available because they'll just keep bothering them, all in earshot of said customer.  Thanks, but I'll take my money somewhere else.
However if you want cool modern accessories or furniture for the home/kitchen, this is definitely the place.  Their choice of books is also excellent.

A very reasonably priced casual BYOB Italian joint.  The staff is friendly and outdoor seating is available when the weather is nice.  The Orecchiette Alla Romana is simply awesome (pasta dish with chicken and sausage).

The place is not large so it probably gets crowded on a friday or saturday evening -- so it might be best to avoid those nights if you're worried about a crowd.  And its cash ONLY so be prepared for that.

Wings: Classic pizza shop wings

Pizza: So so cheesy, soft crust and good ratio of cheese to sauce. Great price and the best I've had in the neighborhood. 

Stromboli: SO much cheese. Comes with onions and green peppers, so ask if you don't want those. The cheese was awesome, the crust was light and tasty, and the size was huge. 

I will gladly be back, but based on other reviews, I might stick to the wings, pizza and stromboli.

The reason why I have given this hotel a 2 star rating is because of Amanda Washington and Alexis (at the front desk).  This hotel was 3rd rate at best.  However, these 2 employees went out of their way to make our stay as comfortable and enjoyable as possible.

In particular Ms. Amanda Wahington, warned us not to take a taxi to our concert on Friday evening.  She attempted to give us directions to the subway, but when she saw that we were not grasping the information, she actually walked us part of the way. We were able to make it to our concert in very little time with no rush hour traffic hassle.

Upon our check out we asked her if we should take an uber or a taxi, she told us to take a taxi (with a flat rate).  She called "her guy" who showed up in 2-3 minutes and charged us considerably less then uber would have during Monday morning rush hour.

Ms. Alexis checked us in.  She was pleasant and courteous, of course.  However, when we complained about the room (it was a low floor with an ugly view of an alley way and noisy), she immediately came upstairs in person with an apology and vouchers for uber and drinks at the hotel bar.

Now the hotel itself is substandard for a Sheraton.  The room was quite small, the bathroom had cracks in the tiles and mildew.  The air conditioner was noisy and worked sporadically.  The location is in a tougher part of town.  If you walk through the terminal, the neighborhood gets better. Unfortunately, you have to step over homeless people and vagrants sleeping to get there.

Housekeeping did not clean our room on one of the days that we stayed there.  When we complained the front desk said they would give us extra SPG points.  Hopefully she will follow through on that.  

The gym was fine.  Relatively clean and stocked with clean towels.  No complaints there.

Finally, on our last day.  Someone checked in with a dog and left him/her alone in the room next door to us.  This poor animal was howling for hours!  We called to complain and we were told that is was a service dog.  After making several complaints the howling eventually stopped.  Perhaps they moved the other guest or something.

At any rate, if you do stay at this hotel, I highly recommend that you request to deal with Ms. Alexis and Ms. Amanda Washington.  They really helped to make our stay enjoyable.  I hope that management reads this and rewards those two ladies.  Furthermore, it is my hope that mangement will take steps to improve your hotel where possible.

I took my cat Finley in yesterday because he was itching profusely. Dr. Andeer gave him a flea treatment to rule that out and gave him a check-up, and sent me home saying if it didn't clear up by Friday to bring him back and we'd talk about changing his diet.

Finley was itchy all night, as was to be expected because the medicine takes 24-48 hours to kick in, but because I am such a worry wart, I e-mailed Dr. Andeer. She was so great and understood my concerns and said to bring him in before work (which was very convenient for me) and pick him up after so she could give him a more in-depth evaluation. This may not have been totally necessary, but I knew it would calm my nerves to rule out other skin issues as I had just adopted Finley on Friday.

I went to work and have been worried, but Dr. Andeer has been taking time out of her (no doubt) very busy schedule to update me periodically on Finley. She answered all my questions and concerns calmly, politely, and promptly, even though I was probably bugging her asking questions and over-worried about my cat.

I still don't know what's wrong with Finley, but I am confident Dr. Andeer will figure it out. In the meantime, I just wanted to write a review about how amazingly attentive, considerate, and sweet it was for her to constantly keep my posted on my cat's progress.

Thanks, Dr. Andeer! :)

Excellent happy hour.

I've now been to El Rey twice for Happy Hour. Happy Hour is a bit short, running only 5-6:30. Still, the deals are unbeatable. $4 margaritas, $3 Corona drafts, and $1 tacos! Service is quick... Tacos came out right away. Still, no pressure to leave. Great service, great drinks, great tacos.
It officially takes longer to get a mobile order than to wait in the line. If you mobile order during busy times, your food comes 25 minutes before your drink.

Third visit here. Ordered the crazy noodles which wasn't as spicy as previous times. Hubby said pad ga prao was great and the 4 star rating is for him. Mine was a 3. 

Great service as always. Cozy decor.

This is a fun place, well decorated  with good service, but most of the loser hipster clientele are snippy and trying to act too cool for their own good. 
Good place, lame people, so go with friends and ignore everyone else.

The food is very average for what you will pay for it. The only thing you are paying for is the view you get while you eat. I think this place should only be used for drinks. That way you can have a few drinks and enjoy the few, then head out to a better dinning experience.

One of the best Lan Zhou (not nan zhou, it is a city in China, retard) hand pulled noodles in the U.S!! Lan Zhou is known for their hand pulled noodles in China.
