I discovered this place when I was attending a conference in Philly. I am SO glad that i did. The restaurant is located in Chinatown. It is very small and easily overlooked from the outside. But the food is another story.

They have both hand drawn and shaved noodles but I prefer the hand drawn one. The noodle was so authentic and mouth watering that I went there almost everyday when I was at the conference. I told several people at the conference about it and they all went and loved it! It reminds me of the hand pulled Lan Zhou noodle I had with my grandma in China. Now you know THAT is the real deal!
All the positive reviews are true! Responsive, quick, thorough, and very fairly priced. A very good contact to remember and to recommend.
Our favorite Friday night dinner spot ! Great food great prices  friendly staff !!! See you tonight for dinner!!!

Schiesse show.

When I first walked into sugar moms, my reaction was "oh christ, it's a frat party."  Then I looked around a bit and I liked the exposed brick and the layout of the bar.  Next, my ears heard Wu-Tang, and The Clash and I was happy.  Moments later, I was drinking a Dogfishhead Aprihop for a very reasonable price and the happiness continued.

I only stayed for one drink and played some Golden Tee.  I had fun.  I don't think I'll come back to this place frequently but I had a surprisingly good time.  I think Sugar Mom's is unlike the normal Olde City place that most of us avoid and that's part of the charm for me.  It's got a cool setup, a very good beer list and enough space and distractions so you don't get bored.  Works for me.

this place is terrible the owners have a manager who is very nasty to the customers.  I had an item stolen from a dryer after using the bathroom. I asked the manager to review the tape , he told me he was busy loading a vending machine,  I expressed the urgency of seeing who went in my dryer because I didn't want the person to get away. The manager ignored me completely and then went into his office and never returned. 
I reported this to the owners who didn't seem to care and they never followed up.
I do not recommend this place its poorly ran.

A few years ago we hired Jordan as our realtor. On initial contact, he is charming and enthusiastic. Soon he became difficult to contact, put very little work and effort behind our listing and generally made us feel that our questions and inquiries were a burden to him. Instead of being supportive of our family and our needs, he was more like dealing with a used car salesman.

We were also almost immediately farmed out to his trainee, who was not very helpful.

We later worked with a realtor who was much more appreciative of our business and easy to contact.

Unfortunately, I would not recommend him at all.

All I can think about are chicken cheesesteaks. It's been hours since I had one at the Ugly Moose last night and still, sitting here at work, they're still all I can think about. 

The Moose is a comfortable getaway, offset from the road and offset from the rest of Philly. Good beers and great food- well-placed tv's make it a top notch joint to watch games at. They have the Garage too, even bigger and better tv's for the bigger games. 

It's a quality spot, a real honest & reliable place to go with a special lady or man friend or with a group of folks. You can't beat it and knowing that is one of the fun things that makes the Moose even better. If you want to experiment, get out there and get crazy- there are other places for that. But if you want to something you can trust, love, and really enjoy because of how it easy it is- the Moose is perfect.

My parents and I had been visiting the Constitution Center. When we discovered that the Red Owl Tavern does not serve classic American/"tavern" fare, our waitress recommended Jones. 

What a keen suggestion, and thank goodness we heeded it! Although it was after 8pm, Jones seated us right away and brought our food out very quickly. My dad and I each enjoyed what was objectively the best burger either of us had ever had, and my mom loved her club sandwich (she bright half of it home and enjoyed it for lunch the next day, but dad and I left satisfied and empty handed). Very reasonably priced as well. 

The lighting was a little dark and the other patrons a little younger than my parents--it was clear that this Stephen Starr establishment is frequented by young professionals more often than retirees, but we didn't feel at all out of place. 

I would highly recommend to ANYONE looking for some American fare to complement the historic American context in which Jones is located.

I have had bad experiences at this place before but I came back today on a whim because I really just wanted my head shaved and beard trimmed. I got a bad vibe immediately. I was assigned to a woman who was very nice but just seemed not 'all there'. She was very scattered and confused, and as much as I hate to suggest this, I feel like she may very well have been on something.

I had to repeat everything like 3 times, and she was having trouble figuring out where various styling tools were at her station. I wanted to get up and leave before she began but I just wanted to give her a chance.

But then just as she began trimming my beard there was an incident where a traffic cop got into a loud argument with someone else that worked there. It was a screaming match, it was loud, obnoxious and distracting. It not only distracted me, it distracted my stylist, who was interacting with that argument and fully stopping her work and staring off blankly at it.

At this point I didn't feel *safe* let alone comfortable. I asked her to stop, she had done minimal work at this point. I gave her $5 for her trouble and left.

I cannot in good conscience recommend this place.

Dissappointing. Enjoyed the food. But when we got the bill none of the happy hour prices were on it. We were charged full price. I pointed out to the waiter. He apologized and brought back the bill. The drinks were happy hour price but the food was not. So I asked him again to take the bill back. This time the bill was correct, $40.00 less. The waiter did apologize but I still felt like I was being scammed. The owner saw me but never asked why I sent the bill back twice. I like the bar. I live across the street from Prime Stache but I will be very cautious if I go again because I feel like they were trying to fleece me. Always check your bill here.

I started with an Eastside; gin, lime, cucumber, mint. It was great and I'm going to start making those at home all the time. Then I tried a Penicillin; scotch, honey ginger, lemon. It was too sweet, nowhere near as good as Ranstead Room. Drinks were around $10-$13, they also have beers for $4-$6, and the food looked good.

The back of the establishment was extremely loud even though the place wasn't packed.

My wife and I are vegetarian but not vegan though we use our trips into Philly to try and explore new vegan options because in our own small city the nascent plant-based dining scene is still very limited. Bombon in that regard was a treat. We were not prepared for it to be a small-plates restaurant and I don't know that I would have chosen it had I known it was. The server mentioned this was a change so maybe it was recent? Either way it should not distract from my appreciation of the food. 

The first course we ordered was the Hearts of Palm Ceviche. This was tougher than a fish civiche I am sure but the heat and acidity of the sauce was odd and addictive and well paired with the mild palm. The Spanish Fries were good but nothing special. We got them primarily out of interest in the vegan cheese which was very good. The tacos we got, fish, and others seemed like the real winner on the menu. The "fish" was clearly a tofu but it tasted and had the texture of a whitefish. Final course was a Tofu steak in a Spanish tomato and pepper sauce. While never fooled that the tofu was not beef it seemed to be purely a vehicle for the excellent sauce. 

The drinks were good but not above ones at restaurants in a similar class but we did not have margaritas which seemed to be a big draw. The desert was a coconut custard with a chocolate hazelnut crust and a citrus fruit foam. The foam was very tart and I only wish there was more of it to enjoy.

If I were to go again I think i would have just loaded up on the tacos and/or empanadas which are more bang for your buck versus the rest of the "small plates" racket.

There are pros and cons to every situation. After living here for 1 year, these are our observations:
1) new management, Amanda, is a breath of fresh air. Not only is she helpful but authentically kind and courteous, especially to staff. The change in the climate is palpable. Staff is friendlier, presumably, because they are now being treated respectfully. 
The new head of maintenance, Cliff, is a master problem solver.
There are regularly held resident events. We appreciate finally having our vegetarian dietary needs/requests honored.
2) venice lofts is horribly understaffed. This type of problem originates at the corporate level. We have lived in 3 other true "luxury" facilities that have dedicated housekeeping in addition to grounds, concierge, maintenance, and management. I would love to see housekeeping staff maintain the carpets on the residence floors. Some areas have been patch replaced otherwise they are stained, worn, and malodorous. Currently, the vacuuming/cleaning task is delegated to already taxed staff in other departments.
Trash pickup is an issue, again, because of inadequate staffing- a corporate issue. The part time staff member tasked with trash has additional duties and cannot keep up. Summer is upon us and I do not again look forward to the smell in the hallways. Would love to see dedicated full time staff to remove trash from the trash room/chute in a timely manner.
3) would love to have 24 hour concierge as is advertised.
4) appliances are old and breaking down. Makeshift remedies are applied instead of simply ordering new. Again, issues of inadequate funding at the corporate level. Would love appliances replaced with new.
5) DOG PARK: I can confidently state I speak for the majority of dog owners on this subject as we frequently discuss our concerns. Would love our repeated requests honored regarding removal of black mulch to be replaced with the AstroTurf so common in dog parks. The mulch is dirty, especially in rainy conditions, requiring dogs paws to be cleaned after use. Again, even in dry weather, dogs get filthy from the black mulch. Some dogs like to eat mulch which brings with it a whole host of separate problems.
It is sad to hear residents say they never utilize this park because of the mess. A truly wasted amenity.
Another wasted amenity is a huge grassy lot dedicated to bocce. In a year, I've seen people playing on one occasion. Surely that space could be better utilized, like, for dogs or just a family play space. Nice, clean grass....
6) Venice Lofts is conveniently located to the Septa station and Main Street Manayunk.
7) staff is very friendly, helpful, and welcoming. The front desk/concierge staff is a great group of ladies and gentlemen!
8) currently there is a stacked small capacity washer/dryer. Would love these replaced with full capacity. I concurrently have a lease in a true luxury apartment building in Florida. In the same amount of space I have a stacked full capacity washer/dryer....I measured. Either you do a load of laundry daily or you spend your Saturday catching up....
9) we have coffee available in the lobby. I would love to see a commercial coffee setup with on demand espresso/lattes/ brewed coffee available. This is what I have enjoyed in other luxury apartment buildings and it is a nice touch befitting your expense. Currently we have a Keurig with less than appealing options. Again-a corporate funding issue.

As I said, every situation has pros and cons. Convenient location.
Amazing staff especially now that management has changed hands.
Understaffed and underfunded.

We use Anthony and his crew for all of our painting needs! They have painted our business (interior and exterior) and our home. We needed help choosing colors, and Anthony helped us pick colors that made our space beautiful! He is quick and gets the jobs done fast. Always responsive (how many painters have you called and they never call back?!)!

Very, very reasonably priced and they do quality work! Highly recommend!!!
Wow!  What a great find.  Really different menu, but something for everyone.  Highly recommend the burger, amazing!  Great food, service and Stumptown Coffee too!

"Delicatessen" for me conjures images of hearty sandwiches stuffed to the gills with a fresh meats, cheeses, and fresh veggies.  Buddy, keep walking to the next deli; much better places in the area to get food, groceries, and beer.  I give them two stars for the limited selection of Belgians and American craft brews but as a complete beer snob, I'll walk the extra blocks (east to Foodery or west to Colney) for a much better beer selection and customer service.

If you're looking for great banh mi at a fantastic price, this is it. The space is small, but I noticed that in the time I was there, everyone took their orders to go. I sat comfortably at a table while watching endless people coming in and going out. The staff is very friendly- they handled a special request from me without giving me any problems.

I had their special along with a Thai iced tea. I did forget to ask for less sugar in the tea, so the tea was just a little to sweet- which is normal and I should have remembered. The sandwich was generously filled and the tasted very fresh. The pickled vegetables gave it a good kick and balanced out the meat.

Usually I am torn with banh mis because the bread is too rough after being toasted, leaving me with cuts on the roof of my mouth after eating a quarter of the thing. The bread here was somehow crispy but still soft enough to not hurt me- this is an odd compliment, I know. But you can imagine that I can't have too much of these too often because of this problem. 

I noticed that if you order through their app, you can get a discount. Plus you can order in advance and pick up when it's ready.

The style of cooking is close to other renowned ramen shops I have tried in New York City. I ordered the Hiro Ramen: The half-boiled egg, the pork slice with a hint of burned-in seaweed flavor, and the soup base all met my expectation.  My biggest issue is size. Compared to Terakawa ramen, I did not feel satisfied when I finished my meal at Hiro. I may come back when my mood strikes. But overall, it's a okay ramen shop.
Pretty darn good hotdogs. Several options to choose from and the service is quick. The dipping sauces that come with the french  fries are AWSOME!!

Hands down one of the best meals I ate in Philadelphia. We ordered the lamb shoulder and had all of the other plates as well. Our server Julie was amazing, very knowledgable and friendly when making drink recommendations. Hoping to go back soon!
This place has excellent food & service,  have been here a few times for lunch and dinner and have never been disappointed.  This is some of the best Italian food I've ever had,  you'll love it.

No thanks. I travel throughout the east coast (work transient) in search of a decent barber, well this place wasn't it. Walked in at 4:00 an hour later i was in the chair. After the owner finished telling me about his hairdresser family was he started cutting, he had no idea what he was doing. At 6:00 the nightmare was over cut was uneven and mall worthy, dropped $50 for a $15 dollar cut usually would have asked for a better fade or a lil more off the top but just wanted to leave. Waste of time and $$

In Philadelphia and want to sample the real local fare? Come to Campos to get a great cheesesteak from this family-run business. You might need to take it to-go because they don't have much seating.

Cash-only, but they have an ATM inside.

Never again!  The hostess staff is utterly incompetent.  There were multiple 6 and 4 top tables they could have put together for our party of 10 but they did not and the "one hour" wait was "unknown" after 1.5 hours so we left.   And after finishing our meal at a BWWs 20 miles away, we actually received a text that our table was ready at D&Bs, 2.5 hours after we were Tx it would be an hour....pathetic!  I guess the owners and mgt doesn't care anymore.  The games stink too...no old school games.  I'm done.  Never again.  I hope D&Bs sinks into the mud.

Such a funky, cute place! This would probably be a fun spot to bring a casual date to. For a Saturday brunch, service was reasonably quick.

The food itself was good - I ordered the huevos rancheros. The eggs were seasoned and prepared well. Overall, I wasn't dissatisfied, but I did think the dish was a little overwhelming bc of the amount of sauce and the massive pile of chorizo black beans under the tortilla. By the end, I wished maybe I had tried something else. My friends ordered omelets and the salmon hash though and seemed pleased with their food! I would like to come back for happy hour, but maybe not brunch
I mean does this place even need my review... one of my favorite places in the city. I honestly just came here to share my pic :)
Love this place. They make delicious breads and stuff delicious things in them. Call ahead and pick some up on your way to an Eagles game.

Have dined at this restaurant every time I travel to Chestnut Hill and while I have always enjoyed the restaurant their newly revised menu hit the spot.  The burgers and pizza were fabulous.  They even have gluten free dough for their pizza and are so accommodating for a gluten allergy.  (the owner's mom is gluten free).  Outdoor seating is fabulous for those great fall days/nights and the service is truly exceptional.  Give this place a try or a second visit if you haven't been in a while.  It is truly a worthwhile dining experience.  Their bar selectin is great.  Excellent martinis and great beer selection.  And if you love the pulled pork nachos they are still available.  Just ask for them.  The byob (build your own burger) was so unique.  If they have it and you want it they will add it.  Very fun and extremely delicious!   Kudos to CH7 for their continued efforts to perfect their restaurant and their menu selections.

The building is what caught my eye at first. Nestled right under an apartment complex this establishment was made perfectly for the working class millennials. My friends had a birthday party their and when I tell you this place brought me back to college with a long beer pong table and king size jinga blocks.

Disgusting...rude...unprofessional...slop...
I guess it was my fault for ordering shrimp and grits but i didn't expect for them to serve me slop. When I complained about it the waitress looked like a deer in headlights...talk about dumb..,she had to get the manager who informed me of course i could get it made differently but I had to pay for both. Huh? Really...you serve me slop and i have to pay for your incompetent dish. Wait he had the nerve to ask me if I wanted to take it home. So I sat there and ate nothing while the rest of my party had their breakfast. I already knew in my head there was no way i was paying for food i didn't eat so i asked to look at the menu again....$12.00 shrimp and grits...perfect cause the tip on $58.38 would have been $12.00 even swap....meanwhile they were so rude the waitress never even came back to our table she sent someone else. For the first time ever i left exact change $58.38 there was no way the were getting a tip out of me.  How could a manager/owner leave a customer so unhappy and not give a shit. I will never spend my money here again. I hate to even give it one star.
O. M. G. This is the store that dreams are made of.  Unique clothes and accessories that you won't find anywhere else.  The owners are fantastic. Friendly, welcoming, but not pushy. Love love love.

We were disappointed with this hotel for many reasons. Room was not ready at 4pm check in. So I had to give up having a room with micro/fridge to check in right then. They did comp me parking for the inconvenience which I was glad since it's $35 per day!!!!! We spent the day at the Zoo & looked forward to cooling off at the rooftop pool. Only to find it packed to over the capacity amount of non family friendly people. Shortly after the staff had to close the pool to be shocked and cleaned due to the overload and behavior of this huge rude group. 
I purposely looked for a hotel with pool to enjoy with my kids and it was locked up after that and we never got to swim!
Our room was not clean. Sheets were clean but the floors, shelves and bath were not. We decided to stay there for dinner and sat for 20 mins without even being Acknowledged by staff. (It wasn't busy) it was 5pm! I finally went to the bar stood for another few mins and got me and my children drinks which I carried to them myself! Then again had to go to the bar and order food which seemed to really annoy the bartender as she chatted with other bar customers about how busy her night would be with the airline people coming in and that she didn't really like it!  We then waited over 45 mins for simple menu items. We were not brought silverware or napkins. I basically acted as our waitress. It was extremely disappointing because the hotel portrays a higher class image online. But it is Not. Again had the same experience at the morning breakfast buffet. Sat at the table with no acknowledgment from staff or servers. I walked up to the bar and got menus and silverware myself Again! Finally a man said he was new and not sure what to do. We helped ourselves to the buffet and finally 10 mins later had a waiter who wasn't even sure if you could order from a menu or the price of buffet.  Needless to say I won't stay at This hotel on my next trip in Philly. Only good part (if you can stand all the rest) is it's close to historic sites and you can walk, but I would do so with great caution. Not the best part of town to walk alone or unalert in.

My girlfriend and I were in the area the other day and decided to come in and eat here. She suggested we look for a place with a higher rating but I persuaded her to come here despite the reviews because I had heard good things about it. 

Our waitress was a little cold at first but she let us get the margarita happy hour special with only a few minutes left. It was only $3 for a margarita that was pretty strong and really good. My girlfriend ordered the pulled pork. The sandwich came with a side of fries and was decently portioned. I got the shrimp tacos. They were really good. 

My only real complaint was the service. It took over 40 minutes for our food to come out when the restaurant wasn't busy. Also, when we were finished with our food we couldn't find our waitress and had to wait around. This turned what could have been a 45 minute quick trip into an almost 1.5 hour dinner.
Philadelphia Tradition. Geno has taken it to a new level. What a great guy!

I think I'm too old for this place. Or too clean. I did see my friends' band play one of the best shows I have seen them play to date here, but overall I thought the place was gross. The place was hot and smelly and so were the people there.

Hey, this spot is cool. Original feeling grocery store with more items than a corner one. Has a middle eastern flare. I like that it's under the El and brings more stuff to the historically-neglected area. Extra star for that.

As far as prepared food, I had the falafel sandwich. Great, moist, and large, too. Fresh tasting pita. Great baba ghanouj. But little things can go a long way--how about hand cut fries instead of frozen, for one?

There was a time when I thought I could never ever drink boba (or bubble tea) unless I was in Orange County, CA. My cravings for the chewy black balls immersed in varieties of teas, coffee, fruit smoothies, etc... were never satisfied for all the years I've lived in Philly (born and bred!) but only when I traveled to the West Coast and subsequently down south. Philly "bubble tea" was always bland, watered down drinks with hard balls sunk onto the bottom with no flavor when you bit into them.

Now I've heard that one of the owners of Tea Do was originally from SOCAL and he brought the idea here as well as the technique. I couldn't help myself. I had to break my own vow: "I will never ever get bubble tea from the east coast ever again!" 

Around its first month of being open, I ventured into Tea Do hesitant upon what I would find. Of course, I ventured in when there was a mass crowd of high school teenagers sitting on the couch in the front area being loud. I pushed forward. 

The counter space had some cute decorations and I looked up at the menu and already knew this place was different than the regular places in Philly that claimed to be a "tea place." First off, they actually brew real tea as opposed to these instant powder drinks that are available (what an anomaly!) and their menu was impressive considering the size of the place. After a few minutes, I decided to order a Tropical Twister. This way, I can test the drink itself without forcing myself to suffer through some terribly flavorless hard boba. Pretty good.

Considering I approved of their unique and fun drink creations and concepts, I came back the next day and ordered a classic: milk tea with boba. Pretty good. I went home satisfied with my drink but thought it was a fluke. Since I haven't had boba literally in years, it could just be that I forgot what "real good" boba tastes like and was overselling their product in my own head because my craving was so intense.

So of course, I went back. I ordered another "classic," Thai tea with boba for the boyfriend and a Passionfruit Green Tea with the mango jelly with a small spoon of boba. Dammit I realized I was hooked.

My sister then traveled into town (she's the one that lives in SOCAL that I used to visit) and I told her I found a comparable boba place. She didn't believe me of course but I took her to Tea Do where she got a Milk Tea with boba. Her boyfriend got some kind of extremely sweet drink (but he liked it like that.. he kept asking for more syrup haha I think it was the Hulk ...?) Now, when my sister travels into town and she has her daily hankering for boba, we head to Tea Do and I don't mind the 20 minute drive into the city and the battle for parking.

I went back almost everyday for a month straight and tried a bunch of things off the menu but keep finding myself venturing back into my favorite, the Passionfruit Green Tea. I still visit Tea Do quite regularly; but all-in-all, Tea Do just can't quite top my list of best tea places since I've been to some impressive ones in the west coast, but it is definitely one of the best in Philly.

First time trying Ekta last night and we were not disappointed! We made sure to order the Chicken Tikka Masala and garlic naan per my fellow Yelpers' recommendation, and also the Lamb Rogan Josh. The chicken tikka masala and garlic naan were on point, and my husband and I could not get enough of it. Next to the Chicken Tikka Masala, it was hard for the Lamb Rogan Josh to shine. It was good and the lamb was nicely cooked and all but the spotlight was all on the Chicken Tikka Masala. Yum! Can't wait to try their other stuff!

Clean, simple, well set up, quiet(ish) and never too crowded.  It is not a gym where people pump in front of mirrors and try to get dates.  It is a gym where serious people go to get in shape or maintain.  You have everything from people who are a little heavy and trying to lose to people who look like they spend much of their life working out.  

The view is awesome.  The owner, Phil, is almost as awesome as the view.  I love the fact you don't need to have a contract.  My wife decided to stop going and avail herself to our home gym and her cancellation was easy. Phil ended the contract and simply told her "you are still always welcomed back."  Classy.

Everything you need is here, free weights, machines, tons of cardio machines, shoes, drinks, trainers and more.  I have just never had such a great vibe at a gym.  And nor have I ever seen one where everything looks new and spotless.

Yes this is in historic place, In South Philly. Love , love this place. I do love my cheeses, cured hams. I went to Sabrina's For brunch, wandered over here afterwards with my friend, Its very small..crowded, but so worth it..$45.00 later..cheese at hand..I have never -on cheeses. But my weekness.OMG!! And their specialty oils, balsamic vinegar , etc.You would have thought it was Xmas as crowded as it was..
The crispy spinach chaat is a must here!! Brunch and drinks are also really good!

Too BAD!  Good deli food but POOR delivery ... take forever to deliver and 2x have cancelled delivery after waiting an hour because the driver refuses to come into the Domus Apartment building only a few blocks from the deli ... he wants you to come out!  Why pay for deliver?  There are other deli stores that deliver to the door!

Horrible decision to go here! I stopped  going years ago because of the service. I was hoping with time they would get better. We went through the drive thru and the person on the intercom seemed totally disinterested. After we ordered the food, we come to the window and the person is drinking straight from the cup. Then, we get our food and the order is wrong and we didn't receive straws either! I guess the person wanted us to drink directly from the cup too.

This place just does it for me. There are so many small touches that make you think that they just think of everything. Only reason I didn't give 5 stars is that a free refill and a board with a "pay it forward for a stranger" would make this good karma effort perfect. I put my suggestions in their suggestion box, so next time may just be able to bump my review to 5!

Horrendous!!!! Do NOT use this company!! We were lied to and told we could be picked up at our hotel and no one ever showed up. They also said that we could be dropped off at our hotel after the tour as it is on their way, then after the tour they refused to bring us back and let me and my children walk a mile in a bad area at 10pm at night. Then, they refused to reimburse us for the 3 day tour we purchased. We spent one of our only days in Philly constantly calling and arguing with the manager. What a nightmare- don't use them!!! They deserve zero stars!

I just stopped in for one of the best lunches I have had - wonderful! The atmosphere is delightful - soft lighting and comfortable seating, wonderful service and exceptional food. I can't believe I haven't been in here before but will be telling everyone, and can't wait to return in the evening to try the wine list! 

Quite a gem, really recommend!
Love the cheese steak, best I've had in a long time. Will definitely be ordering from here again soon. Cheese fries were also good. Delivered sooner than expected, food was still hot.

The last time I was here was over 12 years ago. But now that I live in NYC I thought a day trip to Philadelphia since it is so close-But my main reason for coming to Philadelphia was to visit this museum.

This time I came on a Saturday and it was overcrowded the last time I came here I think it wasn't as popular and I came on a weekday and it was empty (which I preferred). I love medical history and anatomy. 

Love: 

1. The many different kinds of items they have. 
2. It is intimate 
3. A lot of great history on medical information. 

Hate: 

1. The price is outrageous for how small the place is $18.00? seriously for the size not impressive. 
2. That you can't take pictures...except in the lobby. Because the dead can't consent...come on. 
3. Some of the items are not marked well...
4. unless you are 6'6" some of the items you can't see. They need to work on how they display items. Some of us are short. 
5. There are typos on their signage. 
6. When they say the have a special exhibit...it's not that special or that large. 

This will be the last time I come here...I wouldn't recommend of you are a repeat client like I was the second time it is just not that amazing... However it was my friends first time and he LOVED it.

Five stars all the way!

A friend of ours kept raving about this place, and armed with a gift certificate, our group finally made it to Bella Vista to try the yumminess that is Salt & Pepper. 

I loved how we could see the kitchen from where we sat. The guy who served us wasn't kidding when he said they don't pre-make anything. I saw my chicken completely raw.

That being said, I was very impressed with the menu. Whoever said a small menu wasn't impressive obviously hasn't been here, because I wanted to order EVERYTHING. Alas, I started off with the seared scallops, followed by the roasted citrus chicken.

The scallops were perfect. The flavors made me want more, but at the same time, they made me want to stop so I could save my appetite for the main course. One person in our group had the wasabi crab cake, which was yummy as well, but I'm not a fan of wasabi so I didn't care for it too much.

Main course was roasted citrus chicken with potatoes, salad, and a poached egg for half our party, and the seared bronzino with basil gnocchi for the other half. We were all impressed with our food selections. 

Seasonal menus hold a special place in my heart, and I can't wait to see what Salt & Pepper has in store. This place has become a favorite, and we're definitely coming back!

Aqua is located in center city Philadelphia. This is a great location for folks that are on foot or public transportation - but parking can sometimes be a hassle.

I have been here twice for lunch. I love their tom yum rice noodle soup with chicken - which is a lemon grass soup base, with thin rice noodles, chicken, onions, bamboo shoots, orange pepper, tomatoes, and mushrooms. Super refreshing meal for around $8. Not too heavy, not too greasy. 

The inside of the restaurant is very hip. They have nice soothing new age music playing. Perfect to go on a quiet date with. Staff is super friendly and vegetarian options are available if you want to substitute it with a meat dish. 

PS. I love their bathroom, smells and looks good. This place is always a good quiet time. A little pricey for Thai/ Malaysian food but what more can you expect from center city Philadelphia?

Beyond your expectations in every way.  My dog Rufus is treated so well and groomed perfectly.  I am always so excited to pick him up to see how great he looks.  He is so happy there as they treat the animals so well.  

Truly reasonable, wonderful, and an amazing overall experience.

Both times I've been here, never got to see my movie. Bought tickets for 'Buried', but wrong ticket information led me to Easy A, and soon thereafter a refund.

Tried viewing Salt, but the showtime got cancelled 2-3 times until they finally admitted the DLP Projectors were broken. Wasted wayy too much time in this theater.

I've been following this place for some time. I was thrilled to finally had snagged a reservation. Sure it was 5 or 6 weeks before we were in town but it worked out. We also got an early reservation like 545 which ended up working well since we were able to hang out in Fishtown afterwards. 

Please note that the restaurant entrance is in an alley- look for the peace sign. 

Sure the space is cool. They do have a large outside area to wait (with heaters when cold out) and they do have service for drinks that is transferred to your table. They also have a pretty large bar area (full when we were there). 

Anyway we started with the beans. And you say why would you have beans at a pizza place? Well- I don't know. But I do know that they were fantastic. Buttery and lemony and I just wished the dish came with bread because you want to sop up that oil. Would really recommend. Next came there ceasar salad- interesting twist with brussels sprouts and it was really very good. 

We had the spicy pizza and the white pizza with garlic & greens. We should have ordered pepperoni on the spicy pizza. I could have eaten the white pizza and not shared if I could. And I am not usually a white pizza eater. 

We ordered some cheese for our pizza and it was $4! Yes it was really good cheese but daaaaangggg for like a table spoon or 2?!?! Our server did warn us on the price.  

I know that this place has gotten a bad rap but when you come from a place that has little options for amazing pizza, this place is really very good. Now would I wait 1-1/2 hours? nah. I would make reservations ahead of time. So make sure you get your reservations and make a trek out to Fishtown. Support your local pizza joint that has achieve national recognition. 

What I don't get is how you go from being a primary take out place (their first small joint) to no take out at all- ?? But overall- I would have to say, you should go.

I wish I could add more starts.   Let's start off with the staff.  As soon as you walk-In there is a staff member wiping something down or making sure the line isn't getting out of hand.  Or the last time I was there 2 construction guys didn't have a mask and was covering their face with their shirt while ordering at the kiosk!  A wawa employee handed them both mask which they kindly obliged.  I thought that was very nice. This location not only cares about their customers they care about the food.   My sandwich never looked this pretty and tasty.    I love this location.   Hands down.   Now to find a location near my home.  Or I just may drive all the way down town knowing I'll be satisfied with whatever I order.   Keep up the great work guys.

Have no idea when this showed up here, but I love the West Philly Dock Street and was really excited when this popped up. And this place is busy! I personally kind of hate communal seating at bars, which there's a lot of here, but we didn't have much trouble finding bar seats after a few minutes. Beer is great, as with the other location. My only complaint is that the food and service was a little lackluster, and I missed the pizza choices (and cozy grunginess) available in the WP location. Hope they add an oven and make the space a little more comfy - it feels a bit IKEA at the moment.

Frontier Airlines.... The worst! I have a service dog so Paid extra money to have the front seat to utilize the extra leg room for my service animal. Before the trip was over I was move several times, told I would have to store my dog in his carrier in the overhead bin and made to feel like crap. I was able to show my ESA papers and was still challenged by overzealous, over the hill, rude flight attendant! The only way I would fly in Frontier again is in a coffin, and I wouldn't have any say in that..... Which is why I will make sure I have a will and travel insurance.

I stayed here for a conference Oct 14-16.  About 15 minutes after I got to my room, I found out that I had a "roommate" in the form of a live mouse.  I packed my stuff and went to change my room.  The lady at the front desk was very nice and apologetic, and gave me a different room and 2 breakfast vouchers.

However, in talking to a few other conference attendees, I found out a few of them had mice in their rooms too.  Yuck.  Would not stay here again

Great burgers, fries and shakes. They use high quality beef (with options to upgrade to Kobe - read Wagyu-or grass fed). Nicely cooked with great toppings and interesting combos and a very good bun. The burgers are accompanied by thin, crisp fries that taste of potato. I had a Kobe truffle burger, topped with truffled mushrooms and truffle cheese, that was very decadent. Added truffle fries and it was truffle heaven. Add it all up and you've got one great burger joint!

This little hole in the wall restaurant delivers! It's simple but to the point. The food is delicious, inexpensive, and big portions. The waiters listen and will actually make substitutions without any hassle. 
We came on a cold and rainy Philly afternoon hoping to find a delicious bowl of pho to warm our souls and we were not disappointed. We got the XL bowl of pho $8.95 (ours had different cuts of beef and meatballs). This bowl was definitely XL it fed two adults and we had leftovers. The broth was full of flavor and just perfectly cooked. They do not skimp on anything here. My favorite were the meatballs next time I'm getting extra of these.
We also got the shrimp summer roll for $3 you get two huge rolls per order. It was very fresh and satisfying. 
All around 5 stars. This place delivers what it promises at such a low price. Food is at your table in no time. And servers are attentive, I can't wait to go back and explore their menu!

After reading so many disappointing reviews, I felt like dining at Effie's would be a serious risk. However, the bad reviews must be from people who are trying to make Effie's something that it is not. Come here for authentic and no-frills Greek. Don't go too crazy ordering something that wouldn't be a Greek staple and you'll leave happy. Everything we ordered was great- Mezze dips, dolamades, chicken gyro and orzo with seafood. Bonus for constant refills of warm pita and friendly service!

I guess I'll start by saying I pretty much Never eat falafel cause I find it kind of weird, BUT this place was able to change my perspective on it a bit.  

     All the people working were really nice.  It's always comforting to be greeted with a patient smile, especially when you have no idea what to order.  My boy ended up ordering me the most basic thing possible cause I had to start somewhere.  All in all, it was pretty good and all the flavors seemed to go well together.
  I must mention, we'd be more inclined to go back if they weren't all wearing matching shirts, aprons, and hats.  The uniforms made everyone seem less like real people and more like fast food employees to us.   It's not really the kind of feel some people want when they're in Rittenhouse, but of course I don't speak for everybody.
    
I'll definitely give it another shot next time I'm feelin' ballsy in that hood.  I hear the specialty falafel sandwiches are quite good.  They sound 'interesting'.

Came here tonight was seated within 2 minutes of my arrival. The waitress was very helpful and made suggestions on which foods she thought were really good. I ordered for appetizer crab bites, my sister ordered bbq wings and my other sister ordered mild wings the appetizers were really tasty. For entree i ordered the crab leg and shrimp boil with a extra lb of crab legs with old bay seasoning. The boil came with corn and potato it was seasoned to perfection delicious i will definetly order this again when i come back here to eat. For dessert i ordered the creme brulee but it wasnt what I imagined it was not good at all, my sister ordered a boil also with crawfish and shrimp she really enjoyed her broil also. The only complaint was the desserts need to be upgraded to something better. She tried the gluten free dessert it wasn good either . I think if they change their desserts this place would be even better.  The decor in the restaurant was really nice i felt like i was on the beach.

This place is amazing! The sushi is delicious and fresh, the menu has something for everyone, the restaurant is clean, hip and inviting and the owner is super friendly and kind. After we ordered, the owner walked up to our table and introduced himself. He shared his story about his wonderful restaurant and later gave us free dessert, which was a tasty Japanese ice cream. I highly recommend this place if you are in the mood for Japanese and/or sushi.

Food was okay, normal quality Chinese food. It was quick and close, so I'm a little biased to give it three stars.

Interesting note, while I was there a woman was sleeping on the counter. Literately.
Sleeping beauty finally woke up when I was leaving...

Yup, same as everyone below.  This place has ultra friendly staff, but when I'm there the music is pretty much standard issue club without much more to offer, and you hear the same music every week.  

The smoke is ALOT.  I'm a smoker and it even gets to me after awhile.  If they're going to keep smoking there, I feel like they need to amp up the ventilation or limit it to certain areas - it's just a bit intense to drink, dance and talk with friends when you can hardly see them through the smoky haze.

The crowd is typically pretty nice.  I never noticed mad Asians but I'm also usually pretty well into the night when I end up here. 

Overall, I wouldn't recommend it, but I'm sure I will return some night in Old City.

I've been going to Michael for 5+ years now. It's the longest I've stayed with a Stylist ever.  A few years ago, I'd decided to grow my hair out from boy-short to long. Michael helped me through it all, and, except for a few weeks that I couldn't get to the salon, my hair never looked like it was going through a growing out phase. Michael always kept the cut looking fresh and stylish. 

I also go to Michael for color and keratin treatments. Salon Thalia always meets my expectations.

Easily one of the best barbershops in Philly and would even recommend it as a tourist stop if you could us a haircut as it is on South Streer. The crew is cool, they have wifi, and time always flies if you have to wait. Being new to the area, I appreciate a truly local spot and think you will too. The clientele is diverse and you can get a Phillies logo on your head or a perfect professional clean cut.

Joey is your man if you are looking for a perfect fade and good conversation.

Came on a slow Sunday and got the pork tacos... as a food lover I enjoy new tastes to add to my pallet. These tacos had a delicious pineapple salsa and a nice mildly spicy sauce among the other ingredients. Just the owner working but he brought it out to us and was very nice. Try it out if you're into great tastes!

Definitely a hole in the wall place. It's tiny but they pack a punch of flavor within their food. I always get the Mexican style corn, chips with guacamole, and any taco! They ALL taste great. Also, the drinks are super bomb!
Average prices and selection. Decent customer service. Terrible delivery, Comcast is more reliable. If you are getting your item delivered block out half a day.

If you're like me, you have trouble deciding what flavor milk shake-lassi-shaved ice to order when you go to your local icee eatery. Fortunate for us, Hassan has solved the dilemma: you can mix and match flavors at the uber-delicious and refreshing Mood Cafe. 

Want to branch out from the thick, rich, mango lassi but can't decide whether to try mint or honey? Why not get both! I ordered a shaved ice and was tempted yet scared by the idea of rose or sandalwood, though mango and strawberry are options as well. What to do? Shaved ice suicide! All the flavors together, double-rainbow style with fresh fruit layered in! Being from the south, shaved ices are a summer staple, but this is the best shaved ice I have ever (and likely will ever) have. Usually, once it gets all melty I toss out the syrupy liquid. But in Mood Cafe's case, I look around for a straw! Yum!

There are also plenty of vegan options-Hassan said he can make a coconut milk lassi or milk shake. I'm not a vegan, but that sounds so darn good, I may have to try it...along with about a hundred other things.

Delicious food, unique brews, period decor. City Tavern is a must stop for those looking to experience historical Philadelphia at it's finest. I appreciate that the wait staff, dressed in period garb, don't act as though they're in a different century, they answer questions about the menu and make great recommendations. Try the Benjamin Franklin brew or House porter. Interesting side note, you can purchase the beer for take out. And purchase the pewter serving ware, too. Had delicious pepper pot soup, beef pie, rabbit and duck, served with various veg, such as barley, cabbage and egg noodles. Delish!

great use of an otherwise unused area after work hours. It's perfect because it's a large outdoor area and Chops across the street and Marathon a block down get soo packed during Sips. I came here on the 4th, and there was plenty of seating, and plenty of standing room. The music was being played from a bartenders phone, but it was still popular and upbeat music which was fun. Not sure if they have a DJ during Sips when people are expecting a lot more. 

Main reason for the -1 star is the service/drink prices. I got a sangria which was premade and clearly with cheap wine and like one cut up apple in the whole batch. $9..... like you can get a really well made cocktail full price for that in nice restaurants closeby. But it's a beer garden so I should have just opted for some of the $6 drafts I suppose. Despite it being chill/quiet on the 4th, the bartenders were super slow and overwhelmed.. hope they staff more people/more experienced people during busier times cause waiting for drinks was annoying.

All of the ingredients were there but it just didn't work for me at all. Shrimp tacos had way to much onion & cilantro. Easy enough to deal with when silverware is provided. Filling and good just nothing special. 
Music was great. Salsa bottles on the tables should be wiped clean.
I can't deny the great reviews from others.  There's just better Mexican food experiences to be had.

love this place, came here pre-COVID and can't wait to come again. There's a cute little bakery in the front, but you can also order the pastries when you dine. We got the pastry basket to split -- probably not the best way to start the meal but it was fantastic! 

Also would love to raaave about the chai tea. I regret not ordering one for myself! So so good. For actual food, we got hummus and baba ganoush -- both so good & light. We also got the kafta kebab and zaatar flatbread. All super good - and very full by the end. Awesome food for a fantastic price!

I hadn't been happy with my hair in a few years, I wasn't sure if it was due to my thyroid condition or bad hair cuts (or a combo of both).  I decided to take advantage of the $35 Tuesday new client special and scheduled a cut with Rachel, hoping that a new cut would make me love my hair again.  

Everyone at MOKO was amazing, Rachel talked with me about my hair for a good 10 minutes before I was shampooed.  She understood that I wanted to make the most of my natural wave.  She took lots of time showing me how to style my hair and didn't push me to buy lots of products to recreate the look she gave me.  Of course I loved the products she used (Intelligent nutrients), I bought one that day and will be back to buy more.  I love the cut, I can style it myself with ease and it even looks great when I decide to straighten it.  I am so happy to have found MOKO and Rachel.  

My only complaint: I wish someone had mentioned I could save $10 on my next cut if I had booked it that day, as a poor graduate student every penny I can save helps!

Hello, beautiful. 

Visited this lil bar last night with a friend. We were confused about the actual name since there's currently no sign out front but we felt welcomed by the big painting of Kenzinger on the side of the bar. 

We walked in around 4:30 and figured they must have just opened since their only patrons were a dad and his little kid eating pierogies at the bar. We grabbed one of the few tables toward the back and were greeted by the bartender, Nicole. 

We noticed the draft list was only Philly Brewing company beer. She gave us the scoop that the bar and brewing company had a great relationship with each other so they only feature PBC beer on tap. "They're good to us, so we're good to them." 

We weren't complaining about that! I ordered the dry cider while my buddy ordered the Wunder beer. The bartender asked me if I ever had the cider and warned me that it was super dry. She then told me that her trick is to put a drop of raspberry schnapps inside to sweeten it up a bit. She was right, it was absolutely delicious. 

We both ordered food, the sirloin burger and the BBQ pork Sammy. Luckily, both of our Sammies came with hand cut, homemade french fries. I love fries more than most other foods so I was delighted to munch of them while my BBQ pork cooled. The sandwich was even better than the fries. Mounds of pulled pork cooked in a sweet, slightly tangy BBQ sauce. You can tell that they don't just plop some sauce on the bun. They marinate the shreds of pork in the sauce so they fully absorb the flavor. 

A couple hours and a slew of hipsters later, I was on my 4th dry cider and my buddy had switched to good ol' Kenzinger. We tipped out waitress about 30% and let her know we'd definitely be back with friends. She was so knowledgeable and this hidden gem hosted a great time for my friend and me. This place makes me almost wish I lived in Port Richmond. 

A couple of tips:

This place is cash only. There is an ATM in the bar and Nicole let us know that the one across the street is surcharge free. 

There are other bottled beers available. Local and domestic if you're not into PBC  beers but why the hell wouldn't you be?

Hipster heaven. But it's more entertaining than annoying.

Parking! There are a few spaces you can park for free right beside the bar out front. 

*Bonus: Nicole let us know that at the end of Feb they have a pierogie fest. 21 different kinds of pierogie deliciousness that she makes herself. Count. Me. In.

I only come here because my primary Doctor is located in the building
This Hospital seems to be well kept. It appears to be clean and tidy to me. It is very easy to navigate. There is plenty of parking. They seem to have plenty of wheelchair accessibility.

Do not use this moving service!  They are rookies in the moving game and do not do any of the services they claim.  The movers they sent me were terrible.  They immediately got their rental truck stuck in my yard and then they began loading without using any sort of protective moving materials.  From there the rest is downhill....

They ripped couches jamming them through doorways and they also sweat all over our items since they didn't use any sort of protective material while moving.  I would look anywhere else and pay a bit more since this is a waste of money for the service you receive.  They are con artists who leverage a bait and switch technique to make your feel comfortable and then your movers show up and you realize you got what you paid for.  

After they ripped our largest couch the movers denied it and the company still has not followed up with my about reimbursement.  Look elsewhere!
Great food, great prices, kind of bistro atmosphere but friendly and prompt service .

Philly International is an average airport. Nothing special. My only issue is the poor food choices in each terminal. It doesn't seem like they have many choices. At least in Terminal A. 

Other than that it is pretty clean and nice.

I always have a soft spot for Greek food, and when I heard the return of Kanella as Kanella Grill, I was overjoyed- and of course I had to give it a try. I couldn't have picked a better place, and once you enjoy it, you'll wholeheartedly agree with me. 
I've tried a platter of chicken kabob, with home-made pita, incredible hummus, fresh salad and rice on the side. My kebab platter was a platter of perfection, and it maybe the best kebab I've ever had in Philly.

What you need to know about Kanella Grill:
- Everything on the simple menu is simply delicious.
- Try the cucumber juice with mint and Tanella; it's more of a smoothie. 
- Casual BYOB.
- Cash only.
- 11am-9pm ( Sundays till 4pm) and close on Mondays.

Welcome back Kanella 
Five Yelps.

Came here with a friend for brunch around 11am on a Sunday and the wait was only 10 minutes for a table for 2. The larger the group, the longer the wait  of course. Service was not exactly warm and bubbly, but efficient and on point. Our food came out within 10-15 minutes. 

I ordered the scrambled eggs with lox and a side of home fries and rye toast. My friend had the breakfast quesadilla with chorizo. She really enjoyed her meal. My scrambled egg was a little sweet because of the onions they used, but well cooked. The lox added a nice salty kick. Home fries well flavored and went great with some hot sauce. 

Overall, good brunch food at reasonable prices right in Philly. Nothing fancy smancy or unique, but good breakfast classics. Great for vegetarians as well since a lot of the dishes can be made with or without meat.

We have gotten take out from here 3x's and every time it is delicious! The shrimp fried rice is fresh and homemade with big pieces of shrimp! The fried California roll is yummy! Every thing is good that we have gotten so far!

Refused to honor the 20% off discount they run on certain days. Dropped off on Tuesday morning, they said that it was an express service when I picked up on Saturday.  Horrible customer service, the guy laughed in my face when I said that this would be the last time I'd go to their business.  Multiple other cleaners in Northern Liberties, I recommend you go to any one but Liberties Walk.

delicious donuts. i came here specifically for the phish themed fishman donut and it did not disappoint. good coffee, and nice outdoor seating area too. not to mention, the employees were super friendly!

We went to Pesto on a Friday night around 7:30 with a reservation for 2 and we're basically immediately sat after arriving. We were greeted by our server offering to open our bottle of wine and gave us time to settle in. Pesto had so many great specials not featured on the menu and our server was ever so patient to repeat the specials to me 3x and answered all my questions before I was ready to order. The eggplant appetizer was amazing! My husband ordered a seafood special which was a really big portion and so tasty! I ordered the Veal Saltimbocca and I could not get enough of the sauce! Our server even gave us a complimentary Brushetta appetizer, which was fantastic. We finished off our delicious meal with the Oreo ice cream cake! It was incredible! We will definitely be back to Pesto. The restaurant is located right on Broad Street in south Philly, but it has such a neighborhood, family oriented feel. Great for romantic dinner or a fun group dinner!

I absolutely love this restaurant. My husband and I have ordered the hot and sour soup, won ton soup, fried rice, and chicken lo mein. The food is full of flavor and comforting. Definitely our go to place when we have a craving for Chinese food.

In the depths of a cold, gray winter, one can find refuge at the Meridiene's hotel bar and restaurant, Amuse. The hotel is chic place, done in a mod-hippster style, but without the snobbishness. You enter through a pair of pink, vertically zebra-striped doors into the bar. Reception for the hotel is tucked around to the left. Your first thought is, "Now THIS is hospitality." 

They offer a good selection of premium liquors and beers. Their cocktail list is terrific. I had a Boulevardier (for the bourbon lover) and and their Amuse Old Fashioned. Wonderful. For snacks, the sweet potato croquettes were delicious. You should not leave without trying the pretzel bites. They are served with a butter-mustard sauce that has an addictive ingredient in it. 

A note on the service. Efficient. Friendly. Full of bonhomie. Not afraid (or irritated) to make a suggestion. 

A terrific spot for a cold day pick-me-up. 

The experience sold me on the hotel. I would come back and stay here, based on my time in the bar/lobby.

Great chicken! If you go to Rotisseur, you MUST get the chicken and then the yummy beets and goat cheese and roasted potato wedges. I love the concept and how fast it is yet still yummy and healthy. Not too much salt which is up my alley. The owner is super pleasant and friendly and loves to educate his customers on the food.  Great spot for quick & yummy protein best for post work out!

Im not going to go on and on and on about the great things that you can get at the reading terminal market.  I could bore you and do that, but i won't.   What i am going to do is to tell you where you can get the number one, unquestionably the best sandwich in the entire tri state region, and quite possibly the known universe.
 I myself have also been to all the classics and then some in this area; Johns Roast Pork, Tony Luke's, Geno's, Pats, Jims, Steves, and all over south jersey. Without hesitation Tommy Dinic's has the best sandwich in Philly... but its not the roast pork which i love and is now famous or infamous for winning certain prizes on TV.  Dinic's Roast Pork is great and ive had it a bunch, but what ive had many more times is the best sandwich of all time. The Goat sandwich if you will. (Goat, sports talk for Greatest Of All Time). 
I will maintain till the day i die and cease to exist on this beautiful planet that this is indeed the number one sandwich in the philly area.  The fact that u have to go to heart of the Reading Terminal Market to get it only cements its legacy. 
 This Goat is of course the Brisket of Beef wih long hots on their classic Sarcones roll dressed in beef gravy.The simplicity is beautiful but the flavors bring me to tears.  These boys have taken Braising the perfect brisket to an art level here.  I have had this sandwich an insane number of times and every single time i have had it, its just as good as the last.  There is NO CHEESE on this sandwich, repeat, do not put cheese on this sandwich.  Thank you.   Just order it, with the hot peppers and sit down in the market and eat it.         
Then begin to reevaluate your standards for every sandwich tha ever came before and every one tha will come after. I bow to you Dinic Family, i would gladly kiss your hand and call you godfather.

I stopped in a few weeks ago for some cinnamon buns for next mornings breakfast. They don't make them, I left w/o buying anything. I always feel a little funny doing that so I stopped in last week and bought a half dozen cookies for evening dessert. 
The cookies a mix of  3 raisin and 3 peanut were Excellent,,,BUT I must protest....$18.00 for 6 cookies.When I heard the price I was a little shocked,but didn't want to cancel the order sine they were already boxed and the counterman  was  courteous.
I will not return...we will stick  w/ Pepperidge Farms (sp) and serve them on a fancy plate.
The food is delicious.  The bartenders and hosts are friendly.  Shout out to Yusef the head cook!  Food is fire!
Great small bakery.  Good selection of bread and pastries.  Tasty muffins and cakes.  The line is always short and the staff is very helpful.

I just spent the day in the city with friends from out of town. Neighbors and friends have been constantly suggesting I try this place considering my love and passion for pizza. I was not disappointed, nor were my friends. Nick, the owner, was very pleasant, attentive, and down to earth. The pie was absolutely perfect! And mind you, I'm extremely picky when it comes to good pies. Sauce and cheese had a perfect balance. I hate crusty too doughy or too over cooked, but I was surprised as to how precise it was cooked at a timely manner as well. 10/10! Definitely recommend and will be back!

Here from Florida, stopped in China Town for some Thai food. Healthy portions and the cabbage salad was tasty. The service was good despite minor language barriers and the restaurant was clean. 
The prices were reasonable

An excerpt from the Institute's website claims, "We brought better beer to a sadly under served area of Philly".  They could not be anymore correct.  The bar sits on the corner of 12th and Green, which is a totally bar-desolate area for about a half-mile to mile radius in all directions (aside than the Prohibition Taproom, which is only about two blocks away).  The area appears to be growing in terms of younger renters moving in, as the area directly around this bar is sandwiched by Fishtown, NoLibs, Spring Garden and Fairmount.

Inside features a very small bar, probably seats up to six at the most.  There are high tables scattered around and a long church pew thing with low tables.  It still smells like fresh lumber in here.  The second floor features another bar that's actually bigger and can accommodate more people because of its U-shape, however, the second floor doesn't feature as many draft handles as the first floor.  It is, rather, a whiskey oriented bar.  So it's beer below, whiskey up top.

The draft list is always rotating depending on how fast things get kicked.  Bars automatically score points for me when it rotates its beer list.  The price is very reasonable as most drafts are 4-5 dollars, mostly 5.  There's usually something like Yuengling or Pabst on draft for the value-beer drinkers or those whose tastebuds have failed hours ago.  They also just started serving food as recent as July 24th.  I found that to be very strange, the bar has been open for a little over a year and just started with the eats.  It seemed clear to me why the Institute didn't pull a decent amount of clientel on a nightly basis, it was empty most of the time.  No drink munchies.  Exacerbating that fact was that Prohibition taproom was only a stone's throw away, serving up decent beer and food, snatching up any hungry and potential patrons.

So I went again when it finally opened up its kitchen.  New-hired waitstaff, kitchen, cooks, and bartenders.  The Puerto Rican fusion looked good, but decided to play it safe while the kinks were worked out; I just got the burger.  Similar to Good Dog's swollen hockey puck, a large donut sized burger with tall, hard chewing buns.  It was fine, the meat was medium like I asked and tasted seasoned so it wasn't bland.  I really like the fried salt-cod, it tastes like a fried Vietnamese snack I like that accompanies beer-drinking sessions.  So far so good, but the waitstaff showed that they were new.  In a bar that prides itself on beer, you need to know what's on tap, the style, and most importantly, how to serve it.  Over the course of the night, I'd confuse the hell out of the staff just by simply asking, "Can I get a He'Brew Rejewvinator?", to the point where I'd have to locate the proper tap handle for the befuddled bartender.  Then sometimes we'd receive beer in the wrong glass.  Things that were low in ABV would come in goblets at times.

In all fairness, the whole "restaurant" part of the equation is still raw.  I'm sure things will get worked out in time.  Meanwhile, more people were there that night than any other night I've visited and that's promising.  Also, sitting outside on a warm night on the sidewalk is really chill, even if there isn't much to look at around the neighborhood.  I really like the fact that there is good beer very close to where I live, and that it rotates its list when the chance comes.  I hope this place blossoms even further, I'll be sure to follow-up with another review in the future.  Yes there are lots of imperfections, but I don't think it'll deter me from the beer.

After 2 and a half years in my unit, I will be moving out and across the street to Fairmount at Brewerytown. "Why such a close move?" you might ask and the answer would be because while I absoluely LOVE this neighborhood the pure lack of professionalism on behalf of owners/management, absence of amenties and poor communication have become TOO MUCH. Over the course of my time here the management has changed three times, each progressively getting worse!  The owners of this property continue to offer nothing, but a unit and rising prices while building around them create actual communities with amenities for equal or lesser amounts. It's insanity. Most recently, the call box for visiting guests has been ringing busy for two weeks. After THREE calls to maintenance my I was told that "it's a software issue and there's nothing that management can do." Their solution? "tell your guest to use the access code that we give the post office to get it." 1) COMPLETE security breach for a complex that's had multiple breakins 2) how would that even work for delivery companies such as DHL (which wouldn't be an issue if they did package collection like EVERY OTHER "LUXURY" APARTMENT)? I would NEVER recommend this property for anyone looking in the Brewerytown area because there are far better options for your money.

Delicious. Good price and happy hour. All profits go to homeless..like old fashioned diner with pie case and all. Breakfast is good too. My favorite is wine in can they serve is good actually. I'll be a regular for sure. Cauliflower soup and blue plate special are my favorites

Fantastic Indian-fusion restaurant down in center city.  While they do offer your normal sabzi/shak dishes, there are also some unique and exquisite dishes.

Upon walking in, it didn't feel like a traditional Indian restaurant, but more like a lounge, with a bustling bar on the left and tables along the right wall and in the back.

The drink list is pretty neat - their mixologist does a good job of creating some pretty cool themed cocktails that are slightly different than what you'd expect.  I ordered a gin martini-esque drink that was a bit too sweet for me and a whiskey one that had a nice bite.

For appetizer, we ordered the crispy spinach chaat.  Based on other Yelpers, I was pretty excited to try this dish and it did not disappoint.  Each piece of spinach was fried, making it crispy and crunchy, mixed with your normal chaat toppings.  The spinach was packed with a lot of flavor and it just the right amount of sauce and spice.

For our main dishes, we split the paneer tikka naan pizza and the jalfrezi with mint paratha.  The pizza was okay - nothing special.  It was nice to have the paneer on it, but the taste itself was a bit dry.  The jalfrezi with mint paratha was delicious!  It was the perfect amount of veggies in a tasty tomato-based sauce.  The mushrooms were cooked and seasoned perfectly, and the paratha were grilled and thin.

Overall a solid place for dinner and not crazy expensive.  I've heard they have a decent brunch, which I'll have to try.

I was asked to write a good review to get a cheap protector over my iPhone which was repaired not 3 days ago. The same day it shattered in my purse into pieces. The next day my phone cracked in my purse with nothing but wallet lipstick and tissue in it. I called and was told my so called warranty does not cover crack in two days. Thais place is a rip off. Do not use!!!!!! If I could give no stars I would

huge seats, no lines, and assigned seating. i know alot of people hate assigned seating but the huge plus to it is that you can choose seats in an area where no one else is sitting (assuming its not crowded). the power recliners are awesome. theres a crazy amount of leg room as well. my one gripe is placement of the restrooms. they decided to split them apart and have one on each end of the hallway where the actual theaters are. this sucks bcuz if u just wanna run out to relieve yourself u may very well have to walk all the way to the far end of the hallway to do so hence missing more of the film that necessary. this happened to me and i found it pretty annoying. the satff was cool. prices were normal. overall a good experience. ill be back.

So I just had the worst experience here and I swear on my dead grandpa, I couldn't even make this up. So I called there on a Saturday night at 520pm to see if they would take anymore customers and they said yes. I arrived at 535 and was tasting and asking about the cheese so I would know which ones to buy. I asked the girl behind the counter if she had any imported camembert and she showed me the one that I tasted before and didn't like it and told her I wasn't a fan of that brand. Then someone else behind the counter said something that I wasn't able to hear and this girl laughed and I felt like it was about me but I ignored it because I wasn't sure. Then I asked her if she had something sweet, nutty, and hard and she suggested me a gouda. I tasted it and liked it and told her to give me a quarter pound. Then I asked for a stinky soft cheese. She was annoyed but I'm like idc cause I want and need my cheese and her job is to assist me. She suggested me a cheese and I tried it and didn't like it. She suggested me another cheese and I tried it and didn't like it. Then she said idk what kind of cheese you're looking for. I said soft and stinky and something similar to von trapp oma, if you've ever had that before. She said no but some man behind the counter would probably know. Then he said to me well aint no cheese in this world that is alike so dont come in here talking about a similar cheese to von trapp oma. I'm like this old white man is probably having a bad day and just nodded my head. Then he said we got 200 plus cheeses and you need to know what you like ok? Now it was 550 and they close at 6. He said to me you need to hurry up because we all trying to go home. I'm like flabbergasted. I mean I understand everyone hates a last minute customer but we all been there before and not only that, I called to make sure it wasn't a problem and whoever was on the phone said it wasn't a problem. One minute later, he proceeded to put the curtains down on me like I was nonexistent. I didn't realize he was trying to be a dickhead until afterwards when everything added up. He over here acting like his wife slept with his best friend or something but anyways I said can I try Ossau irraty? The girl behind the counter got me the cheese and he said look here, this is your last cheese, we all got stuff to do. Do it look like we want to fucking be here forever since you wanna ask a lot of questions, why don't you come in the after noon? He rolled his eyes at me and his face was getting so red. I responded well people fucking work 9-5 and people get off at 5 so what is you saying. This old white miserable man needs a better attitude. I will never come there again because the service was fucking shit and why would I wanna go there when there's Dibruno lmao. If I do come to the Downtown Cheese Shop then I'll come to TASTE the cheese lmao. I'll make sure I'll come every single time I'm craving cheese hehe. Then if I like the cheese, I'll head to Dibrunos to buy the cheese. Every cheese that the downtown cheese shop have, Dibruno has! Also, I had no choice to go to the downtown cheese shop because Dibruno was closed at 5pm. Dibruno has wayyyy better cheese.  More varieties. And they sell baguettes!! ANYONE THAT LOVES CHEESE NEEDS TO TRY DIBRUNO. BETTER SERVICE, CHEAPER CHEESE, AND BETTER CHEESE. THERE'S ONE ON 9TH AND WASHINGTON AND THERE'S ONE IN CENTER CITY! **IF I MADE ANY OF THIS UP THEN PLEASE GOD TAKE MY LIFE. DIBRUNO BROS, DIBRUNO BROS, DIBRUNO BROS, DIBRUNO BROS, DIBRUNO BROS, DIBRUNO BROS!!!!! I LOVE DIBRUNOS!!!!!
Amazing.  Food was unbelievable. Fresh seafood and absolutely fantastic sauces..  I really cannot say enough about how awesome this place was....  I cant wait to go back.

Informative tour.  How wonderful that we are able to view the place where our founding fathers met and made the decision to split from Great Britain and where the Declaration of Independence was signed.  Tour is about 20 minutes and free. We were able to do walk up tickets on day of.

I am currently on a year-long membership, and I honestly like the classes - the instructors are great.  The personal trainers are hit or miss.  You'll either end up with someone who understands your personal goals/needs, or someone who enjoys pressing your buttons unnecessarily. 

My problem lies with their customer service.  For a gym that tries to be one of the best in the city, these people can honestly do a lot better.  There are maybe 3-4 people who know what's going on, and the rest are clueless.  I recently called about a billing question (I just wanted to know what I paid for upfront) and I got transferred 3 times, each time to a different person.  One of them had no clue how to even look up my billing/payment history, and cut me off to give me the same non-responsive answer for the upteenth time.  

Seriously - for what you pay, these people can honestly do A LOT better.  Not planning on renewing once this is all over.

I have been going to Francis (the man, not the salon) for 20+ years now for my hair color and style.  I've been going to Francis (the salon) since its inception.  Those two statements themselves should tell you what I think of Francis Salon.  But let me elaborate ... 

Besides giving fabulous haircuts and awesome color and highlights, Francis is a treat himself.  He never stops talking, well almost, and everyone knows everyone else.  We all talk about our lives and families...our ups and downs.  Francis is frequently quoted in The Philadelphia Inquirer as he feels deeply about certain things and isn't afraid to voice his opinions (part of what I love about him).  The best part about going to Francis isn't the great haircut and style you'll certainly receive.  It isn't even the price, which is MUCHO lower than what most in-town salons charge.  It's being part of the Francis family who frequent the salon.  

Recently, because Francis was behind schedule, I volunteered to have Charles do my hair.  Charles is a recent addition to the salon and the best person Francis has ever hired!  Charles performs magic with his hands, giving the BEST head massage along with the shampoo I've ever had.  It's worth going just for that alone!  Charles recently changed my color and style, and I can't tell you how many people have already asked me where I have my hair done.  The salon has an updated look now (thanks to Charles) and has 2 shampoo sinks and 4 stations.  Neither one overbooks so your wait time is just about nil.   Can you tell I really, really like this place?

Pretty awesome restaurant. The decor inside and outside definitely amplifies the revitalization of North Broad Street. The food was great. I ordered two Po'Boy sandwiches and they were delicious. I love that they put your sandwiches in little boxes which prevents it from getting smashed up. The staffmembers were very nice and very attentive

It's best to have a reservation for this place if you are going for dinner. The location is relatively close to the theaters so it will bring in that type of crowd during the dinner time from 4-8. They have very fast and attentive service. I would highly recommend the mac and cheese and the wake and bacon burger. Both are AMAZING. Their menu had a wide range of items from comfort food to seafood dishes. They also have an extensive wine and liquor list. The stairs up to this place is a killer but worth it.
I called for service and they came out the next day and fixed the pipe within an hour.  Very professional and the price was very reasonable.  I will use them again.

Despite being fifteen paces from the titan of all coffee shops (you know who you are), Philadelphia Java Company holds its own. It's like walking into a cliche when you push open the glass door-- and that's not a bad thing. Tiny tables surrounded by mismatched chairs clutter the front of the shop and chalkboards neatly display the menu of coffee and sandwiches. When I was unemployed, I would sit in the quiet back room of Philadelphia Java Co. for hours 'looking for a job'. In reality, I was enjoying my insanely delicious vanilla latte (made with only the finest La Colombe beans) and using the free wi-fi to catch up on celebrity gossip and occasionally, current events too.
