Their coffee is phenomenal (or try the chai for a fabulous coffee alternative) and their sandwiches are tasty and affordable. 

I love to support the little local places and I get happy whenever I see a crowd inside Philadelphia Java Co. Next time you're standing at 4th and South, torn between coffee shops, go for the little guy, Starbucks will be fine without you.

UPDATE: The other day when I got my latte from Philly Java Co, the adorable barista asked me my name and somehow formed an "L" in the foam on top of my bev. I love this place.

BE AWARE... THIS PLACE IS CASH ONLY!!!
They conveniently have an ATM located in their little vestibule that charges an arm and a leg on top of your bank fees :(

Disappointed because they call themselves "Dim Sum Garden" but have a measly 20 "dim sum" items on their menu with 12 of them being "dumplings"... 5 of them being "cakes".  None of your traditional dim sum options. What dim sum places doesn't offer steamed pork riblets or shu mai... or a verity of buns.  

We were told this is the place to come for the sought after "Shanghai Soup Dumpling". Honestly... I've had better.  The dumpling skin was thick and rubbery. The only saving grace was that the broth inside was good.

Honestly... save your money... if you are going to call yourself "Dim Sum Garden" you better have a verity of know dim sum to offer.  It's like me calling my place a "waffle garden" and I'm only offering you three options.  Again save your money there has to be better places in  Philly.

I would be remiss to not mention... the place is dirty and the staff is rude.  The waitress knew I was not happy with how she was treating us.  She was snatching empty plates from our table that she hit the brim of my husband baseball cap and didn't even apologize. 

The reason for the one star was the over all experience.

There are so many businesses in the Reading Terminal it would probably take you 20 years to taste them all lol just kidding.

I was originally brought to Shanghai by a friend and I ordered Shrimp & Brocolli in garlic sauce. It was so tasty and spicy I was begging for water. However, it didnt stop me from scarfing the food down.

I was there yesterday again I just ordered Chinese veggies because I'm on a diet and these were awesome at well.

Service is quick and staff is friendly. 

Average meal is $6.95

I bought a tree and was very pleased with the freshness and shape. It was full and had a good taper. The delivery was within the estimated time window and the tree trunk was cut right in my driveway. I recommend them heartily.

Came here a couple times since I moved to Philly as a grad student. I always get a great cut! I don't come too often as it's a little pricey. But, if I know I need to look my best, I definitely look to get my haircut from here.

This terminal is impossible to get to, and they don't hold your flight if you're trying. Missed so many flights because of this hell hole, and I'm always the first off my last plane.  Take the shuttle.... You won't make it in time. Walk... You won't make it in time. And the staff actually a LOVES it when you are upset... It's sick, and this place is un-American.  I'm a fair person, and I'd give it negative stars if I could.  I despise flying though here, but it's often the only way to see my family.  I just can't do it any more.

Mercato is the kind of place 5 yelp star reviews should be reserved for. This Italian BYOB has some of the best food I've ever tasted and I can't believe it took me so long to try it. 

I went here for restaurant week and was blown away with all three courses. The famous artichoke lived up to the hype, I would gladly bathe in that sauce, but the calamari stole the appetizer show. The meat was so tender yet smokey on the outside which just went perfect with all the other components (especially the different kinds of olives). It was served like a salad and a bite with a little bit of everything was to die for. 

I tried both the short rib ragu and the pyramid pasta, both very rich and delicious. The gnocchi was shaped differently than I'm used to but those little ricotta balls were some of the best I've ever had. Amazing sauce that I was sure to soak up 100% with the table side served bread. You can't go wrong with the combination of brown butter and lobster/shrimp, I could eat those pyramids every meal. 

Dessert was the perfect ending to such a great meal. Both the cheesecake and chocolate cake won't win originality points, but the taste speaks for itself. The chocolate cake was warm and moist and served over ice cream, completing the knockout punch for my food coma. 

Our reservation was for 7:30 and we were seated promptly. We were on the somewhat rushed side but I was quick to forgive them as the combination of restaurant week chaos and torrential downpour shook things up as the outdoor tables were out of commission. Nonetheless, the service improvised and did a great job considering how small this place is. 

Can't wait to go back and try everything else.

PHL is at the bottom of my Airport list!

With all the Airports I've visited since posting on Yelp, this is the bottom. Taxied into Terminal 4 on Southwest, deboarded, and immediately noticed this airport is in dire straits starting with the disgusting mens bathrooms and lack of sanitary conditions. After a marathon walk through the airport with several moving walkways under repair, we caught the car rental shuttle from a disgusting diesel bus. I'm sure this was not the impression the city was hoping to attract tourism!

I ordered delivery through the app caviar. It was a smooth process to order from the app, they have their menu but no pictures so you have to know what you like to order. It's kind of annoying to use the app caviar since they aren't listed on uber eats or grub hub and you have to download a whole new app just to eat their delicious viet food. The food was fresh and delicious as eating in the restaurant but it was also quite pricey so it's really a treat to eat their food when you're on your budget. What's nice is they give you the food in reuseable containers so after eating you can save and reuse the containers which is nice! I'm Vietnamese and from SoCal and this is the best viet food you can find in west philly.

I eat sushi every week. I like to explore new places and try almost everything on the menu.  If you want a great sushi go to Fat Salmon or Roe. This is the 2nd time I have been here. I thought I would give them another chance, but never again.  and yes just like other review said  GROUPON BEWARE!!!!

On the groupon you supposed to get 2 of any type of soup or salad up to $9. They wont let you. You have to get only miso soup and house salad which come to abou $7.  For sushi you allowed only certain sushi not what it says on groupon deal. 

This place is BYOB so I brought a bottle of wine with me. After  45 mins and 5 times asking for wine glasses and wine opener, the waitress who has no emotion at all finally came with our requests.  Oh before that I had to take a wine bucket from another table that left to keep my white wine cold.  Seriously I dont know what is wrong with this waitress.  There were 4 other tables and about 10 people including us.

The soy sauce was dilute with water.   it's very clear. no it's not low sodium soy sauce.  BEWARE of sushi pictures.   Some of the pictures are fault advertisement.  Yes it used to look like that when I came last year but not anymore.  For example, Crazy tuna roll.  There used to be a thick piece of tuna on top of  each piece of sushi.  NOt anymore.  it's a very thin tuna slice on it instead.  VERY DISAPPOINTED!!! 

We also ordered takoyaki appetizers.  it comes after we are done eating dinner and it burnt.  

It seems that they reduced the quality of everything here.  Horrible food, horrible service.
Please dont come here if you want to enjoy your meal. I usually dont leave bad review or this bad but this place is beyond horrible.

What could be better than some fresh-made warm cookies after a night of eating or drinking? Not much. 

We walked past this place on our way to Bourbon Blue and immediately decided we were going to hit the spot on our way back to the car. So that is precisely what we did. After having some appetizers and drinks at Bourbon Blue we walked back and stopped in at Insomnia at around midnight on Friday night.

Insomnia offers both regular size and larger Deluxe size cookies. I decided to get an ice cream cookie sandwich. So I went with a double chocolate chunk cookie on the bottom and a regular chocolate chunk cookie on the top with vanilla ice cream. The cookies were warm and gooey and the ice cream was excellent. It was a fine ending to a fun night.

Boss Plumbing provided excellent service from start to finish, and they gave me good advice as a new home owner. I originally called them to move my laundry sink, but they also installed new water lines, shut off valves, and they corrected the laundry room piping. I called them in the morning and they were able to come out the same day to assess the situation with the work occurring the next day. From the beginning, they were able to identify that I needed new shut off valves for the water supply and that the existing piping configuration was not providing proper drainage. Warren and DJ completed the work, and they were knowledgeable, friendly, and efficient. I will definitely call them again for servicing my radiator and for any plumbing needs.

This was one of the best dining choices I've made in quite a while. The entire experience was fantastic. I had the PB and Bacon burger, and I couldn't believe how delicious it was. House made grape and berry jelly as a dipping sauce! Yum! Jalepeno and cheddar tots as a side. I am so full and proud of it! The only thing that could give the food a run for its money, was the staff. Kelsey is super personable and welcoming. From the moment we sat down we felt at home. Chris helped to keep that feeling going, saying hello and  chatting with us about our experience. Not only will we be back to try every burger and every hot dog, but also to enjoy the warmth of the great people who work there. Thanks!

My whole experience at this restaurant went from bad to worse, leading me to conclude that the only reason this place is so highly rated is the lack of competition in the neighborhood. I came by myself for a casual lunch, and was seated in the back corner at a dirty table, when there were plenty of clean tables of the same size, including the one right next to mine. I then sat there for several minutes while other tables around me were cleaned, before someone stopped to ask for my drink order, still not noticing the table was covered in water and crumbs. 

I ordered an iced coffee, which turned out to be a can of la Colombe served with a glass of ice and cream. It was delicious, obviously, but not what I sit down at a restaurant for. For lunch I ordered the vegan chorizo burrito, which additionally promised avocado, house made salsa, black beans, and rice. The actual filling was 75% plain white rice, with a smattering of black beans, three pieces of chopped avocado, and a blotch of sauce here and there. No chorizo to be found. Oh, and there was also a dry side salad.

While paying at the counter I noticed some tasty looking home baked treats behind the counter. Maybe that's what all the rave reviews for, but after spending $20 on a pile of white rice and a coffee from CVS, I'm in no rush to go back.
Maybe they were off this day, no bagels tried to give me the last bagel. The coffee was like water, weak and flavorless.

Writing this review from our table. We ordered crab Mac & cheese that had no crab in it.  The cowboy steak I ordered medium was well done in parts medium in others, and even rare in some sections.   The bread we ordered was literally burnt on the bottom. Only reason this isn't 1 star is because believe it or not I've experienced worse than this *edit* no this place is the worst
From North NJ native and NYC dweller: Talia's has the best bagels in Philly.  They would fit right in and be competitive in Manhattan.  The whitefish on a plain is my personal favorite here.

This was my first experience at the Walnut Street Theater and I can't wait for many more to come. We saw Legally Blonde and it was fantastic! Highly recommend!! The theater was cute and not cramped. Very clean. Ticket prices are amazing compared to what you would pay for Broadway.

Went there yesterday 2xs once for breakfast n dinner not only were my hash browns hard as a rock I was able to drop it in my table n here the bang I tryed to call but no one answers I finback at dinner bother my woooers were over cooked fries were stale onion rings oldso good to say yet again they got me for another $26 if imyou want BK I suggest taking a ride to any other but this one I have video I sent to the health department yesterday so they can see this place is ripping my ppl off by serving food made hours ago
This place is amazing!!! If you have several members of the family that are picky, this is the place for you! It's got such a wide variety of foods, and deserts! Everyone was very nice!

I absolutely love this place. My favorite in Philadelphia. 
They're sandwiches are amazing.

Absolutely the best comic shop in Philadelphia. The owners know their stuff, and stock a wide variety of books from almost every area of the medium. Factor in events like the annual Locust Moon Comics Festival, art shows, support for local creators and their frankly jaw-dropping ambition as a publishing imprint (take a look at who they've rounded up for their upcoming Little Nemo anthology, you will, and I do not say this lightly, plotz) and there's no question what shop I'd recommend visitors to Philly check out first.

My boyfriend and I were in Philly last month, and it being my first time there I did a lot of research on the best places to eat with vegan options. Everyone seemed to rave about Front Street, and they did not disappoint! The atmosphere was great, the service was fantastic (thank you, Tirrell!) and the food was to die for. We loved it so much that we tried to go back a couple days later but there was an hour wait, which would have been totally worth it, however didn't work with our schedule. Would recommend to anyone vegan or not, as there are plenty of delicious options for everyone!

Really easy to get to by PT and a friend and I did this today. Took the trolley from west Philly and used free interchange at 15th street to transfer to the Broad st train. Take the train southbound and get off Snyder. Very easy. And close to the subway train station!

Yup, my friend has been here before and I have only been to the Roxborough location.
Same menu, lots of Vegetarian choices: (Seitan, Veggies, Tofu choices).

Ordered my old familiar (and fave entree): Seitan Enchiladas. Yum!  Very flavorful and filling with black beans and veggies. My friend also had a Skillet Seared Seitan Tips, which is actually under Appetizer and she said was very filling in itself. 

Very mellow, since we had a late lunch. We will be back. Again and again!

So, I must update my review, but not my stars they stay a 5!  This visit was full of color and just as cutting edge as my last one.  Because of my underestimation of the time needed to achieve the color I wanted Abbey, Dierdra, and Dena all helped to make my pink perfect!  Let me tell you, these ladies know their stuff.  Just from a picture on my phone they nailed it!  I would definitely recommend any one of these stylists, they all rock! Color or cut, this is the place to go!

Absolutely fabulous!  We went for brunch before the Flower Show.  Our server was Sarah and she was amazing; honestly, the entire staff was friendly and attentive.  

We sat outside under the pergola and it was beautiful.  The food was great- if you go for brunch you must get the cruller!  We shared as an appetizer LOL but it could be a dessert.  The chicken and biscuit was to die for.  I had the asparagus & mushroom gazette which was light and perfectly delicious.  The cocktails were also fantastic.  I mean- they juice everything themselves- including a grilled pineapple for a mock-tail (I asked to add tequila and it was A-mazing!)

We loved it so much we tried to get in for dinner but they were booked.  We live in Jersey and are trying to think of reasons to go back to Philly so we could go there again.  If I was local, I'd be there all the time.

I was one of those troublesome customers. I needed the man working the register to break a hundred and he did without complaint. When I wanted a cup for water. He actually opened a fresh gallon of water for me. Even though it was troublesome he kinda just cared that I was happy. These little things just made my day. The foods great! But more than that the staff is just pleasant to be around. Which is hard to find in the city. If you're in the area and need a snack definitely come here (:

Awhile back, I had a volunteer gig helping to run a fairly big-deal fundraiser, and finally had to buy a professional looking suit. Since I could basically use a fundraiser myself, getting a good looking suit for cheap was really important. I found a decent choice at Daffy's for about $200, but it looked exactly like how much I paid for it. 

I took the suit over to Royal Tailor to see if anything could be done to make it a little more professional looking.  When I walked in, there was a guy in the corner furiously sewing away, and a man behind the counter looking over receipts or something. As soon as I walked in, he looked at the suit, nodded, and pointed me over to the "dressing area." Without really any conversation at all (seriously, almost no words were exchanged, which i found worrisome at first) he held up my jacket and helped me slide it on.  In an instant he was pinning things and pulling things and after about 30 seconds, asked me what I thought about his suggested sleeve length. It was perfect.  All of it. Exactly what I was looking for, and didn't have to say a word. I was amazed.

When it came to the pants, it was just a quick discussion of cuffs versus not, and a tangent on amounts of break, but in this case as well he pretty much just told me what looked best on me and I agreed.

I picked up my suit three days later, payed what I consider an incredibly reasonable amount for the work (you can probably find cheaper, but not much, and definitely not maintaining the quality level) and three hours later was at my event.  

All night long, people with a LOT more money than me kept stopping me to tell me my suit looked amazing and asking me what label it was.  I was a little embarrassed to admit that it was a cheap suit from Daffys, so I relied on the half truth of "it was custom tailored" which seemed equally impressive to these people.  

I highly recommend this shop to anyone looking to improve their appearance.  They can do wonders with cheap materials (I also believe they can make custom suits but I have no experience there) and know enough about their craft to tell you exactly what will look best on you.  Trust them, you'll be glad you did.

Came here for lunch with coworkers. I loved his place. The fries are amazing and I wish I had followed my gut and got a side of them with my salad. My coworker was nice enough to share, but I will remember to order my own next time. We visited this place on a breezy summer day. I couldn't have asked for a more perfect day for outdoor seating.  The monkey court is a fantastic idea! The waitress was very sweet but towards the end of our meal it was hard to get her attention. And we dined there after 130p for lunch.

I gotta say...Talula's Daily is the shiz. Friends, if you don't know this already, if you just check-in at Talula's with Yelp you get a free cookie. No questions, no requirements to buy anything. Just a free giant delightful cookie. Now how many places do that these days? And their cookies are baked fresh everyday. I always get their double chocolate chip and buy a snickerdoodle one and I leave immensely satisfied. 

Food here has always been great! Lunchtime can be quite expensive if you're just looking for something under $10 but it does feel like you're treating yourself when you're here. Now where Talula's shines is at their Secret Supper Club Dinner. For $55/person you have a set 5 course meal which changes every month. And the food is divine, up there on the most delicious meals we've had. It's inventive, eye catching, and palliative pleasing. You get your money's worth and you walk away stuffed! The portions are big and they give you granola they made on site to take home. You can also watch the food being made right in front of you since the kitchen is open to view. And since it's a smaller restaurant, the service is intimate and attentive.

Service is always incredible and the sushi is the best.  Hands down my favorite sushi restaurant in the city - and it's BYOB! 

Would recommend this spot to anyone.

Remember back in the 1990s when John Madden had his All Madden Team specials? He would find crazy things and label them "All-Madden." He said the team would travel by being "hauled" and they would practice outside in the snow with reindeer watching.

I have a feeling that if John Madden had a show on Food Network, he would most definitely declare Nomad Pizza Truck "All-Madden." I can hear him now:

"Pizza is all about a truck. And if you're going to have a truck, it has to come with a wood fired brick over. You take your wood and your fire and your truck and that's what pizza is all about."

My encounter with the Nomad Pizza Truck happened at a wonderful Yelp Elite Event. I represent with pride so I immediately asked, "Can you make a vegan one?" Their response, "It will take about three minutes."

And three minutes later, I had a slice with no cheese. Three minutes! That sounds like GOB Bluth building a house in two weeks but it's so hot in that oven that three minutes is all they need. A brick oven on a vehicle that uses gasoline. How on Earth does it not explode?

The pizza with simply sauce and basil was fantastic. You've heard a lot about Osteria. This is better. I later returned and they made me one with mushrooms. Their mushrooms are high quality but I actually liked the plain one a little better. I guess because the sauce is so good and I prefer to focus on it with no distraction.

If I see that this truck will be at an event in the future it will be a huge asset in drawing me to said hypothetical event.

South Street Bagels is convenient to the Queen Village area. The bagels are good, but very small compared to other Philly/NJ/NY bagel spots. They have a great variety of cream cheeses and spreads, and even a rainbow bagel for kiddies! They also had tofu "cream cheese"... perfect for our dairy free/Vegan friends. I opted for the whole wheat everything bagel with pesto cream cheese, and while well flavored, it was VERY SMALL. I liked the cream cheese though, it was a fun and different option that you don't typically see. 

I did thoroughly enjoy the iced coffee! It was strong and bold, which I appreciated. The service was also pretty quick and friendly. I wish the bagels were a little more robust.

Celebrated my daughters 30th birthday this weekend at Pub and Kitchen Pabbit room and I can't thank Laura and the P&K chef staff enough. Food was plentiful and delicious and the staff friendly and courteous.  The Pabbit room provided a bright and attractive venue for a warm and festive gathering while snow flew outside.  Thank you all for a special day.
One of my favorite Chinese take out places. Lo mein isn't as greasy as other places. Portions are large and food taste so fresh. Also, delivery here is extremely fast!

This place was exactly what we were looking for. A little pricey but well worth it. My wife and I both had a smoothie that was delicious, we also had a shot with ginger in it that basically was like having an all natural 5hr energy shot. Great for breakfast or lunch. I would definitely try this place out again

Very nice atmosphere and great service.  But....food was not good.  We were a party of 5 and was able to try a large portion of the menu.  Lobster ravioli - salty, pork chop and salmon - ok, appetizers were ...meh.  We thought the tuna app was the best dish.  We do not plan a trip back :(

The Dandelion is one of my favorite restaurants. I have been going for a couple years after a recommendation to go and order the fish and chips. While I still order the fish and chips regularly, I recommend getting the Pickled Beetroot Jar to start and the Scotch Honeysuckle drink. I haven't really had anything that I disliked from The Dandelion, so you're pretty much set if you see something else on the menu that looks interesting. They change their menu around depending on the season. Oh, and the desserts are always delicious, as well.
Good food, reasonably priced. Our delivery woman was awesome. She realized our drink was missing and went back to get it for us!

ClassPass review 

Great experience but facilities is okay compared to other studios. The storage section and actual studio is nice but the bathrooms are just okay, older. Booked the class through classpass, came in and sign in a waiver and headed in to my first hot yoga experience. I recommend you drink a lot of fluids and do things at your pace otherwise you'll end up feeling really tired. Instructor spoke the whole time and demonstrated some of the more challenging poses which was fine since you can just look around the room to know what to do. You'll definitely get a sweat on if that's what you wanted.

I've been here twice now for happy hour and found the food and drinks to be quite good. Here is what I have had so far: 

1. The "Tri Tips" with tiger sauce, which are chicken fried steak tips, were weird. I don't know if I really liked them, but they were one of those things that stick in your mind because, while the flavors are good, you don't know if they are necessarily good together. The breading on the tips were delicious, but I don't think I liked them in the steak per se. The flavors of the breading and the meat of the steak didn't seem to go together well and they competed. The sauce was also very good, but not with the dish. All of the flavors just seemed to be fighting one another for attention, instead of complimenting each other. 

2. The pork sliders. Small but good sauce. A good starter. 

3. Shrimp po boy. Elegantly buttered baguette. Perfectly coated fried shrimp. Delicious picked tomatoes. Complimentary and well proportioned lettuce and sauce. 

4. Small cheese plate. This rotates, but when I was there it was raspberries, walnuts, toast points, and some type of Gorgonzola-like cheese. Great portion for the price and really fresh. See picture

The only thing is that I wish they had regular happy hour drinks. They only have a happy hour drink of the day, and no others. Although $3 Newbold IPAs all day every day is pretty great. 

Overall, really friendly staff, appealing decor and atmosphere (they had fresh lilies in the bathroom that smelled heavenly), and versatile ever-changing menu. I will absolutely be back!

Before you start wondering why I didn't give this place 5 stars, it's ONLY because of the prices, and no they weren't bad, just...over $10 for a burger? I don't know but I just hate handing over that much cash for a good old fashioned burger.

With that only weird thing out of the way, the burgers were AMAZING. Probably the best burger I have had in the city so far, and that's including Five Guys and the normal bar-type places. It's simple, that's what makes it so good. It's just good quality, thick, tasty meat, that's all there is to it. Really thick burgers, really good toasted bun, and huge portions of sweet potato and regular shoestring fries cooked to perfection. This food is really tremendous.

I ordered the Good Dog Burger which came stuffed with cheese and topped with sautéed onions. Wasn't much onions on it but the flavor was all there. Cheese just leaked out as I bit into it, oh my god it was so good. They serve you a sauce that is kind of like a mix between mayonnaise and ketchup, and it was perfect for both the burger topping and to dip the fries into, so good. My girlfriend ordered the regular burger and that was fantastic too, topped with cheese and lettuce, and the same sauce. Here, it doesn't matter what burger you get, it's always going to be good because of their meat, it's just incredible.

Next, the beer. They have plenty of micro-brews on tap as well as plenty of bottles and cans for you to choose from. I had a couple beers that were both pretty good. Philadelphia Pale Ale and a Flying Fish Grand Cru. I really love Pale Ale's and the Grand Cru had a unique sweet taste to it that really did go well with my meal. It was also during the 4-6 weekend happy hour where each drink is $1 off, so that was awesome. My girlfriend had a couple cans of the Strongbow Cider, imported from England. It's like a spiked apple cider, it's actually really good. They were pounders too and with happy hour just $5 each...awesome.

Overall awesome meal and awesome beers. The bar itself is nice and dim and the crowd seemed real cool, people were real friendly and attentive, no d-bags here. Bartenders were real nice and so was the server, plus she seemed like she knew a lot about the beer which was cool, that's always cool.

Apparently they have more than one story but the guy said that they were having a private party upstairs and we only could choose from the downstairs tables to eat (or eat at the bar). At first we weren't sure how much this was going to affect us but it only took about 10 minutes to grab a table and the waitress noticed right away and gave us the seat, that was real cool. They really don't have a set hostess however so finding a seat (unless you let someone know on your own) could probably become a war if it's real crowded, I guess that's another small minus.

Overall, good bar, good beer, GREAT food, and decent prices. Will definitely be back with some friends to grab a burger and a good draft, maybe one of those private parties can become my birthday?!

This is the worst TD bank location in the city. There is always a line because they only have 2 people working at any time of the day. The debt machine is always out of order, and the employees are SO SLOW. Go to the branch on 11th and chestnut for actual service if you have to.

I would not recommend this salon for those that have natural hair. 

Everything was going well at first. I was greeted very warmly, taken even before my appointment time, was offered a beverage, and the staff seemed pleasant. I asked a lot of questions about the products they were using on my hair given my past experiences with hair salons, and they were very forthcoming and happy to answer it all. The problem started when I was getting my hair blown out. As a person with 4C hair and given I had some tangling, my hair should be detangled and blown out in sections. The person barely combed and detangled my hair and just went for it with the brush/blow dry and it was painful. She was apologetic but still, if you say you service natural hair, then you have to REALLY service natural hair. When the owner took over, she was way gentler but made a snide comment. When I came in I stated that all I wanted was a shampoo and blow out and yet when she was done blowing out my hair she asked several times if I wanted additional services (trim, curl) and when I said no she said she felt "slighted," which I thought was very unprofessional. She ended up just pulling my hair back into a ponytail. She gave me a first-time client discount and charged me for the service and I gave an additional tip. When I looked at my receipt it said $25 for a ponytail and $15 for trim. I called the studio to clarify and she stated she's knows that I didn't get a trim but that is how she got the price where it should be and pretty much hung up on me. ?????????? I'm confused as to how a shampoo and blowout ended up being $65 when the website says differently (priced less). This salon ended up being the total opposite of what I thought it was going to be. I am very disappointed in my overall experience. This salon does not cater to natural hair, she had poor customer service and clearly overcharged me and was aware of it. I don't mind paying for quality service, especially to a black-owned business but this studio was not that. I was the one that actually got slighted.

Horrible customer service. Completely screwed up my appointment and claimed that it was "cancelled" even though it was not. The office manager was incredibly rude and lacked any sort of compassion or understanding. Do yourself a favor and DO NOT go to this dentist's office.

I really like this place and order take out from here often.  The woman who works at the register when you walk in is so nice and helpful.  The food is very good and well priced.  There is a medium sized seating area if you want to dine in and there generally isn't a wait.  I did dine in once with 5 people.  It was Sat night, i called and said we'd like to come right over.  The nice lady sounded a little concerned but she made it happen!  I always get the beef lo mein.  Good food, good prices, and good service!

Make sure to get the ginger syrup with your sweet tofu with red bean.

Oh, what a synergistic dessert it becomes. Warm, slightly sweet silky tofu, red bean, and a sweet gingery syrup that has that spicy bite to it. 

Great cheap start to my day...

*not a lot of English going on here, so it can be a little intimidating for the non-Chinese speaking, but if I can convey my order, so can you.
Messed up my eyebrows. I told her to clean up and instead she changed the shape completely. My eyebrows are also uneven, in both shape and thickness. I will never come back here.
Great place and really good food. The service was ok and our waitress got us our meal pretty quick. I had the roasted duck. Highly recommended.

This place, which is the sister restaurant to 20 Manning, is a cute BYOB with good food.  I usually eat the tuna, but have also enjoyed lamb or salmon here.  It's a great place to bring a date.  Larger groups will have diificulty with seating.  Bring beer or wine.  Come early to avoid the wait.

Today is a day full of rain. I ordered lunch at just after noon through grub hub. The oder took so long to get here because of all of the rain... when they finally did deliver it, they gave me extra food and even refunded me. These things made me grateful because they didnt have to do that. I just want to thank you Dim Sum Noodle for your courtesy!
Dawn

I walked by this place a hundred times before I stopped in. I was surprised how crowded it was and such a nice vibe from the crowd. Menu was hard to choose - all hearty and great ingredients. I was trying to be good after the holidays so I got a Miles salad with chicken. My wife got a Cobb and my son got the avocado tuna melt. Really great lunch. If you are thinking about a great place with no pretension and just great food, don't even think about it - Miles is your place.

Came here with a bunch of co-workers.  We ordered a bunch of different apps, beers, and entrees with different sides as well.  Everything was just awesome - it's the type of food that you stop caring if you look like a slob because you'd rather loose your dignity than avoid eating the delicious items in front of you at a scarfing pace.  Amazing beer selection to tantalize the tastebuds and keep get you there.

A cool spot to be a grown kid lol. They have classic arcade games and an okay menu (the chicken tacos were really good). I just wished that they served a better variety of mixed drinks, but a great spot nonetheless

This place was super cute inside! We got here right before they closed. They had "$6 flights on Thursday" which consisted of 6 scoops. This was perfect for me + my friend we each got to try a few flavors and the scoops were big enough to get a good taste but small enough that it wasn't overwhelming amount of ice cream. 

I really loved the chocolate almond crunch and my friend loved the maple toffee crunch. 

Will definitely go back here !

This place is really poor on the service. I don't think they know how to handle the rush of people that come in from South St. The tables get cleaned hap-haphazardly and they don't bother wiping down the seats so when we got our spots I had to brush off a few french fries from the previous meal. 2 Stars because they apparently still know how to make a good Malt, even if it was a little expensive.

This was our second attempt to like this restaurant but alas it was not in the cards.  We came here several months ago and were scared off because the food was soooo salty.  Well tonight the food was much better but the service was not.  Our server was indifferent at best and even surly.  He was clearly overwhelmed as they were very busy.  That does not excuse them for ignoring us or having such an indifferent persona when they remembered we were here, even though we were only a party of two.  We watched several parties show up, wait patiently, get ignored, then leave.  We will not put this on our
 favorite restaurant list.

I am a NJ native now living on the west coast and in for a short visit, so getting a pork roll egg and cheese was a must. 

I found this place disappointing. The bagel was pretty good. Nice texture, crust outside, chewy inside. But the rest was not great. The egg was not scrambled at all so you'd get a bite of all yolk and a bite of all egg white. The most disappointing though was the pork roll. I don't know what that was but it didn't taste like the pork roll I know! It was thick cut and not at all pan fried. It barely looked or tasted cooked at all. And the flavor was off. Definitely not Taylor Ham brand. I ended up taking it out and eating just an egg and cheese. Oh well! I have one more morning to try!

I could sum up my review in three words, "Tasty margaritas!  Squeee!"  I love the add-your-own-tequila pitchers of "margarita mixer" they sell for around $10.  The flavors vary, but all of the ones I have tried have been fantastic.  I think my favorite was the black cherry.  The blood orange is also very tasty.  

While having a pitcher or margarita before the food arrived possibly influenced my objective opinion of the food, I also loved the food.  Especially the tamal appetizer (and I hadn't had much margarita at that point, so my opinion is probably a little objective).  I think that was my favorite thing on the menu.  

My husband is picky about steak.  He always thinks he wants to order steak.  And then he does.  And then he complains because it isn't as good as what he could grill himself.  But he loved the carne asada here.  In fact, he said "This is better than what I could make myself." And I've never heard him say that before about a steak.  

I can't really think of anything bad to say about Lolita except that they are cash only and they don't take reservations which makes them somewhat difficult to deal with (but they are totally worth it!)

It's hard to figure Darling's out. It;s a nicely designed place in a hot restaurant district with nice service, but someone who makes culinary decisions in the place doesn't seem to have any respect food. 

Every open-faced hot turkey sandwich I've had in a Philly diner has used turkey slices with a thickness that only comes from a carved bird. Even if they didn't cook it in-house, the whole idea behind that sandwich is that it resembles that filling comfort food you have the day after Thanksgiving. Darling's turkey resembles thinly sliced packaged lunch meat with gravy. Seriously. Maybe 5 slices. I'm pretty sure it's not just a resemblance.

The white chocolate raspberry cheesecake was plated in a way that I guess was supposed to look fancy. Picture a large slice, surrounded and topped with 5 huge dollops of whipped cream - more whipped cream than cake by volume. It was drizzled with what tasted like the bottled liquid chocolate you thin out with milk to make a drink. 

Tell me if I'm wrong, but I'm pretty sure most people opt for white chocolate because they're opting against dark chocolate. Well, I do at least. And that stuff is concentrated. Put it on something with a delicate flavor balance (say, something like white chocolate and raspberry) and you're dropping the curtain on the show before it begins. I gave it a try, but the only audible taste was Bosco.  After brushing away as much of the dark stuff as I could, there was a nice tasting cheesecake below. 

The harshest part of the meal was in realizing that I could have had a really nice meal in many other places in the neighborhood for the same price ($22 including tip, for the entree and dessert, no drink) 

So many lost opportunities Darling. You've got looks, and nice people on staff, but what really counts, just doesn't. 

Two stars because the food was edible (I save one star for places that have food poisoned me.)

A fantastic location for badminton and table tennis. Club 28 has more than 10 proper badminton courts, very well lit, spaced out, for a reasonable price. Individuals can buy 3 hour sessions for $15 each, or reserve a court for $45/hour. Individual sessions allows you to play on multiple courts. 

They also do re-stringing of racquets. Their front desk shop has a variety of items, racquets, shoes, shuttles, strings, clothing, accessories, mostly all from Yonex. 

They also have a corner with few ping pong tables. The tables are new and properly setup.
I got the non-vegetarian combo with chicken tiika masala and lamb pepper fry -- Surprisingly good for being in a mall food court

The cream filled donuts are unique as far as texture is concerned.  They're a bit airier than your typical donut and have a nice crisp.  The flavors in a few select varieties are what really make Beiler's stand out, to me, though; in particular, the maple and caramel.  If you're going to order one donut, I would go for the salty caramel or the caramel apple.  If you're getting a half dozen, try the maple bacon and the Elvis delight, both of which would be amazing even without the bacon.  The key lime was another personal favorite.

The line is usually not very long on weekday mornings.

Waitress was decent, but not very attentive or friendly. Our two favorite menu items are no longer on the menu, which is sad! Rice and beans with my wife's meal were cold and the beans with my meal were very cold (like they just pulled them from the fridge)! We complained to our waitress and received an insincere apology and said she'd go tell the cook. There was no compensation for cold food. We used to love chili's, but will definitely not return to this location and will think twice before going to any Chili's again

Foodies version of the Pearly Gates

If you're in Philly and you bypass this place, your making one HELL of a mistake! I loved this place so much that I'm flying across the Pacific ocean and the continent just to sit in this marketplace to enjoy an amazing Dutch breakfast prepared by an Amish family, have an amazing roast pork with broccoli rabe sandwich for lunch from Dinnick's, and go crazy with trying to figure out where to have your final meal of the day from the plethora of food booths before they close the market for the day.

...And yes Lisa, you were right. It's St. Peter, not St. Gabriel, that guards the gates into heaven. Forgive me.

for these prices, this place is pretty good! i mean, you have to understand that this isn't gourmet, mindblowing, celebration sushi. this is everyday, im-too-lazy-to-cook takeout sushi that is fast (most of the time the wait is under 20 minutes unless its fri or sat night), cheap, and for all intensive purposes, pretty good!

I go here a lot because it's super close to my house, and am disappointed almost every single time. I understand that working sucks and dealing with people all day is a drag but you gotta suck it up and give your customers more than a look of hatred. It's not that bad, kid. 

They get my sandwich order wrong about half the time and look angry when I ask them to include items I've paid for. Otherwise the food is fine.

The happy hour food menu here has great options ranging from lamb meatballs to empanadas and priced under $5. The happy hour drinks were either beer, wine or vodka based and I would have liked to see another base alcohol option, but otherwise those offered were good. We had a party of 6 and were able to find a large booth/table in the basement without a wait. The decor and ambience in this place is really interesting and visually stimulating which I think was definitely a big part of the experience. The basement was really dark and made reading the menu difficult and it was pretty loud with both music and talking which might not be ideal for some circumstances or groups, but it worked well for our group. The restaurant is really large and lots of different nooks to sit in rather than a large open space. This was the first restaurant I've been to that server roasted cauliflower as a whole or half head as opposed to chopped up which made for an interesting presentation. The pizza options were affordable and very filling.

Burgers were delicious and unique. Tried the Cali, Juicy Philly, and the Cuban with the group I was with. Enjoyed all three, but loved the Cuban! Fried pickles are delicious. Can't wait to try a milkshake when the weather warms up a bit!

disclaimer: we are not from the area. the decor was very intimate and upscale, but the food didn't wow me. it was priced higher than what i have spent normally and the portions weren't huge. it wasn't bad but the flavors weren't my favorite. the service was good

Modern look, cozy feeling, parking on the street. 

I enjoyed my brunch at this place. Food was fresh, tasty and not too overly creative. Portions are quite generous.

Gluten Free & Vegetarian friendly!!! 
They offer bread and buns that are gluten free, as well vegan protein substitutes.

I cannot believe it's taken me countless games here before I wrote a review. I LOVE this stadium. I grew up with Kaufman Stadium and when we moved to NJ, we spent our summers at Yankee Stadium, and as beautiful as those stadiums are, there's something very special about CBP. 
Unlike Yankee, there's not a bad seat at CBP. You can buy the cheap seats and still have an unobstructed, great view. Not so at Yankee Field. There's also plenty of places on the concourse to sit or stand to take in the game as well. Lots of great food options, too. Very vegetarian friendly. We've learned to get the Power Tix. Spend an extra $10 when you buy the ticket and get a $12 food credit. Prices are steep, as they are in any stadium, but I think they are more reasonable than other places we've been.
Anyway, staff is super helpful and friendly, the fans create an exciting atmosphere and Gabe has built a contending team. This is just a great place to take in a game.

We stopped in here on a Wednesday night after hitting up the Art Museum. At first, I was apprehensive because there was quite a line BUT the free beer samples and streamlined system made the "wait" non-existent. The staff here is accommodating, friendly and  fast. They answered all of our questions and were very helpful.
The food is amazing. We had the marlin mini tacos and the salsa & chips as appetizers and the frozen prickly pear margaritas. Delicious!! For the tacos we had the house special Mahi Mahi, the fish of the day (which was perch), a pollo and a carne. All four of them were incredible. The carne taco was one of the best tacos I've ever had, but all of them were amzing. Not a loser in the bunch.
We will definitely be back to Buena Onda!

I stopped in for lunch yesterday after, admittedly, trying Marathon first but discovering a long line.  Continental had caught my eye many times in the past, given its bright red exterior, but I had never stopped in until now.

Upon arriving, the staff was very attentive and let us choose our seating preference.  It was a bit loud throughout the restaurant but I felt that the upstairs would be a bit quieter, so we went with that.

The basket chairs that were hung from the ceiling were interesting, and actually quite comfortable.  The upstairs was loud due to the open layout, but just quiet enough to have a conversation.

I had the continental burger, with bacon.  It was very juicy and the bacon and other toppings were flavorful and fresh.  Service was prompt and friendly.

best italian in philadelphia. 
never had a single bad thing here. 
period. 
end of story.

Thank you Yelp for helping me find Philadelphia Eyeglass Labs! This place is pretty awesome. I've gone through about three optometrist (ophthalmology?) offices in the past 5 years, and PEL is definitely my favorite. The doctor and his staff (I'm terrible with names, sorry for not catching any!) were all excellent. I just came in for a pupil dilation and to get new eyeglass frames and lenses, (I got a full eye exam a few months ago, just didn't have time for those things), and got really great service on both. The doctor is really cool and nice, and went into a lot of detail in explaining my eye health situation (I'm basically blind without corrective lenses) and in his recommendations for my next set of frames. One of the opticians at the front desk was super helpful in finding me a great pair of frames (which I've never done before by myself, so I was really grateful for that) from the extensive collection they offered. She was also very patient about going through my complicated insurance process to make sure I got the best deal on the entire service. Overall, really happy with the treatment I got here, and can't wait to pick up my new glasses in a week.

So glad we came here this past weekend.  The place was packed, but we were seated right away.  Everything we tried from raw oysters to the mussels were delicious, but the true standout for me were the peel & eat shrimp...wow!  Service was friendly as well.  Would love to go back next time I'm in Philly!

I've patronized this establishment a handful of times but this last experience was absolutely horrible! And they will never get another dollar out of me. I called in my order (chicken steak and a lamb) and I had to pay for it over the phone. When I arrived the cashier couldn't fine the paid slip. So I waited a few. Got my food and went home. When I placed my order I asked for my hot peppers and sweets on the side and they were not on the side... the put them on the sandwich. And Instead of just laying them on top, they cooked it  in the actual meat! It was toooo much and over powering. All I can taste is hot peppers and sweets on each sandwich. Secondly my chicken was EXTREMELY rubbery and had some pale pinkness to it.(husband had a stomachache at this point) Which leads me to believe that it was undercooked. So I called them and asked to speck to a manager and the same cashier that took my order identified herself as a manager(oh and I had to ask for her name 3 times).  When I told her my issue she said hold please.. and I could hear her in the background talking to order people.. saying I was lying.. and don't it take 24hrs .....blah blah blah. She got back on the phone and said all our chicken is fully cooked... as she was talking to me I could hear people in the background talking SHIT. So I asked her what is the problem.. it was extremely inappropriate for your coworkers to be talking trash in the background and she offered up that they are a team and that's how they basically do things. At this point I'm Absolutely livid with their unprofessionalism. Then someone else gets on the phone and he tells me hes the manager. WHAT KIND OF GAMES ARE YALL PLAYING I said. He told me that girl I was talking to was just the cashier (but she identified herself as a manager) then them assholes hung up on me!!!!!! What the FUCK. How does anybody have these kinds of people working for them. They lack Professionalism and are not courteous. It's very unfortunate they lost me and any of my family as customers. The owners need to look at the people they got working for them and Reconsider.

This place is awesome! Chef and servers are nice and respectful, portion sizes are great. Definitely recommend the oxtail platter- they hold a lot of meat. My boyfriend had the jerk chicken and that was pretty tasty as well. Would definitely come here every time we hit up south street!
Small but delicious food. Lots of food for the money. Everyone in the neighborhood knows about this place.

You win whopper bowl you win!

I got a recommendation to go here due to the new Love for ramen. After reading the reviews and eating at all the ramen places like furious spoon I had to try this out.

My wife got the Terakawa Ramen who's broth was hands down the best I've ever had. Silky smooth and the pork was super tender.

I wanted to challenge myself so I went with the kyushu danji ramen and Boy was it filling! Lots of meat and veggies and the egg was perfectly cooked! High recommend to any ramen lovers out there!

BYO! We brought a bottle of Nigori and they chilled it on ice for us. Saved us some cash, thank goodness.

Call ahead for reservations. It gets uber cramped in here. While you're on the phone placing a reservation, get in your order of oyster shooters. An order is 3 shot glasses with oyster & sea urchin, with a side sake glass filled with salmon roe and quail egg. You dump the roe & egg into the shot glass of raw oyster and urchin and stir it up. (see the picture I added) Shoot it back and chew... omigawd... IT'S SO GOOD! (preparation takes forever, hence pre-ordering them over the phone)

Nothing is cheap here. Between 3 of us we shared a $50 chef choice sushi plate. The pieces were small and many of the types of fish were strange. Stuff I'd never heard of! Only one piece of hamachi. Some of it was really good. Some of it wasn't. My friend ate the last piece that no one wanted (white with a black stripe down the middle) and she regretted it. Ew! From our collective West Coast perspective, it was waaaaay overpriced.

If you're ordering rolls individually, don't miss out on the Dragon Roll (?) with strawberries on top. It sounds stupid, I know it. But... omigawd... brilliance to my taste buds.
Poor service and not friendly. Food was ok but very uninviting. Felt like they didn't want to serve me. It's a shame because the place is close to me but will never go back.

Came here for dinner. Really nice building the restaurant is located in but sort of crammed seating. Waiters were excellent throughout the night. For appetizers we started with Shanghai calamari, the cheese steak dumplings and the tuna tartare. All 3 were excellent. I think my favorite was the calamari but I'm biased since I love calamari :p it had a tiny bit of sweet and spicy taste to the sauce. The biggest surprise was the dumplings. They were surprisingly tasty and the spicy ketchup worked well! Tuna tartare was pretty typical and my least fav of the three.

For entree I had the 45 days dry aged ribeye. I asked for medium rare and they did a good job cooking it. Portion was great and the steak was tasty although not sure if worth price tag other than the portion. Definitely had better steaks. We order as a side lobster Mac n cheese, spinach and onion rings. Onion rings were just large onion rings lol not worth the price. Mac and cheese and spinach were tasty and would recommend!

Ordered a non "broth" meal and it was very mediocre. The "broth" meals were very good and would definitley recomened them with the shaved noodles!

Tip: DON'T order the spicy for extra $2. Just add the spicy sauce on the table and it tastes exactly the same. Made the mistake the 1st time, but not again.

This was the worst customer service I've ever experienced. Men's warehouse was more helpful than these guys! My fiance and I walked in and were never greeted. Two gentlemen that were behind the counter continued their conversation as if no one had walked in. I initiated an awkward hello and felt as if I was interrupting something important. After looking around and asking a question about gray tuxes, we were told "gray isn't in right now". I will never go back or recommend this awful place.

This is a cool bar they have many beers and lots of room, the interior design is perfect, the people are nice. 
The food is not good, I've eaten here three times to give it a chance, its not good food. It tries to be high end bar food I guess, but just kinda misses the mark by overplaying to the fatty gluttonous palate of the neighborhood, maybe people like it, I don't.

Had an early dinner with my mom and sister here today. I've been curious to try this place and finally got around to it. Glad I did. Great meal! Clean eating that tastes amazing and doesn't leave you feeling crappy. Lots of healthy options popping up in the city but none that feel this wholesome....I will be back often forsure!
Great atmosphere especially during COVID times. The BBQ jackfruit sandwich was okay. The crab cake sandwich was more to our liking
I felt like I just walked out of the streets of philly and into Firenze. Gorgeous flavors. Gorgeous. The Vanilla had a hint of bourbon--perfection.

Great location to try a new beer and hangout with some good friends. The place offers many bottled and canned options as well as some on tap along with a limited menu. The casual atmosphere and ability to choose from such a wide selection is top notch.
Food was OK (I only had fries) but the service was garbage. Typical place where the tenders care more to complain about being at work and hanging out with their friends who come into the bar.

It's been 2 days since our recent dinner and I'm still full! My favorite specialty cocktail was their Love Triangle and my favorite appetizer was the Foie Gras (we also had the beef tartare and crab cake). For entrees I ordered their infamous cheesesteak and my husband ordered the 24oz dry aged porterhouse. They were both cooked incredibly well. I love being able to choose our own steak knives. My husband chose the German one (the name escapes me right now) since he said the tip of the knife would help him de-bone the porterhouse and I chose the samurai throwing one for no specific reason. Haha. The fried artichoke side was different (but not bad) and the truffle mac&cheese was good. For dessert we went with the toasted chocolate smores and the coconut cream pie. The smores is an indulgently rich dessert and the coconut cream pie was the perfectly lighter pair of the two. Celebrating many special occasions here, this is certainly our favorite restaurant around the Philadelphia/South Jersey area.

I went here because I needed an emergency tooth extraction. They got me in same day, which was a plus. However there are a few things about this practice that leave me less than satisfied. 

1) they are super weird and aggressive about payment. Once the doctor discussed the treatment plan with me, a man in a suit came into the exam room to explain the pricing to me. It just felt very ...invasive? Confrontational? They wouldn't do the treatment until I paid and I had to leave the exam room to do that. That's not usually how dental practices do things, you usually pay at the end for services performed. For some reason my insurance did not cover most of what they were doing so it was a big sum of money. 

2) The doctor and dental assistant seemed to be annoyed with me asking a lot of questions before and after my procedure. I left not really knowing what my full recovery time would look like, and just a sheet of paper with instructions that were not that helpful, especially because they were for wisdom tooth extraction which is not what I had done.

3) I am in a great deal of pain and don't feel like they warned me at all about what to expect. They said if I had the procedure done yesterday I'd be able to get on my flight the next day (work trip) but am in so much pain I had to postpone the flight. I might have decided on a different course of treatment had I understood how much pain and swelling I would have. This goes back to not taking the time to really explain the recovery process to me. Now I have to get on a flight tomorrow for a work trip and spend a conference looking like a chipmunk and slurring my words. 

4) This is supposed to be a 24 hour dentist. They have someone on call. I called around 8pm because I was in excruciating pain and wanted to know if that was normal. No one answered. I left a message. It's now 9am the next day.. the practice is now open, even and I haven't received a call back. Unacceptable. 

I haven't recovered yet from the procedure so I can't yet tell if they did a good job or if I'm going to have any complications. So, I'll update this review with that outcome.
Delicious!!!!!!! Maxing food and amazing service the apple French toast are supper the bacon and eggs out of this world

I have heard nothing but great things about this place so I had to go.   I went during the week around 1pm and the line was out the door.  I was genuinely surprised at how quickly the staff was able to get through the line, but they managed.  The wait was about 15-25 minutes long. I decided to get a cheese steak hoagie with banana peppers. The steak was chopped up more than I like, but the hoagie was pretty good.  I have experienced better,however, it was still good. Although I wouldn't go out of my way, but if I was in the area...I'm stopping by.

Loved that this place closes at midnight which gave me enough time to stop by and pick up some delicious desert! I got the morning roast which was really good! The portions are big enough to share if you'd like. I liked that they had a lot of seating and some games (jenga and connect four) out if you wanted to stick around and play a little. I would definitely come back again if I'm ever in the area.

Atmosphere: A young and vibrant cafe! We went pre-COVID lockdowns. Mango Mango exudes youthful and fun energy perfect for casual group gatherings or a fresh place to study.

Service: We were a bit confused at first as to whether we would be waited for order or if we had to go up to a counter to order our food, so we initially sat down at a table close to the door. Our waiter was kind enough to move us to an upstairs seating area though!

Food: We ordered the strawberry mango juice sago & lychee jelly with mango ice cream. Although aesthetically pleasing, Mango Mango unfortunately did not meet our expectations in terms of food quality, and we didn't think our dessert was worth the price. The mango tasted super artificial, and the ice cream was a bit too sweet. We were disappointed because as mango lovers, we were super excited about the idea of a mango-themed dessert place. 

Overall: Mango Mango is a place you go for the experience rather than the desserts itself. The people and environment is very friendly and casual, but the dessert didn't live up to the hype the rest of the cafe had to offer.

How could you be closed on mother's day, it's the biggest day for going out to eat, even if it's for pizza...not just any pizza, great pizza...I dont get it????oh well I guess we will have to wait till Friday night

Ya really cant go wrong @ Penang.  The food is incredibly fresh and made to order and pretty damn cheap and fast.

It's totally casual + relaxed, I always try taste something new, they have a broad menu.  Watch the spice levels, it can get hot, but they'll gladly tone it down for you by request.

I had lunch there on the solo tip the other day, I had Thai Basil Noodle and Malaysian Coffee and the bill came to $12.  That's pretty hard to beat. 

Check this place out as an alternative to your run of the mill Chinese place.

I've been here twice and loved it both times!

The first time, the boyfriend and I split the polpettini pizza (mini veal meatballs) and the sausage pizza.  Both were very tasty with the perfect amount of cheese/sauce/meat ratio.  Crust was very crispy. 

The second time we went, we shared the simple but delicious margherita pizza, and 2 pasta dishes.  One was parm, ramps, and speck angel hair, and the other was a bacon chorizo gnocchi of sorts.  Both pastas were amazing and I wished the dishes were bigger so I could really stuff my face!

Zavino also has a great draft beer and wine selection.  The servers are friendly and knowledgeable.  The only downside is that the place is very small so on a busy night there is a wait.  The bonus is that they have outdoor seating!  It's a must-try pizza place if you like tasty, thin, and simple pies coupled with good drinks.

So myself and a group of friends decided to check out The Corner last night for their first ever Rooftop BBQ.  Being that we were the guinea pigs for the event we had no idea going into the night what the dinner would be like.  Let me tell you, it could not have been any more fun.  Not to mention the delicious food served from chef John Taus including barbecue baby back ribs, corn bread, collard greens, black bean and avocado salad, succotash and strawberry pound cake all for just $30.  The endless buckets of ponies ( small beers for you non beer drinkers) that the owner Tony kept supplying the tables with did not hurt either.  Overall the staff made our party feel like family and that is one of the most important aspects to having a good time with friends. I would definetly check this event out again.  We will definetly be doing so next Saturday when Chef John will be smoking and than roasting the 50 lb suckling pig that he has been salt curing all week.  Cant wait to eat that swine.

Goodness gracious, great balls of fire. This most easily explains the travel experience you will most likely have when you arrive at PHL. This time I had just gotten off an international flight and had to go through customs and rechecking my bags, etc. That wasn't the bad part. The bad part was going back through security. Luckily I had rechecked my bags quickly and made it to security fairly ahead of the rest of my flight. There were 3 people checking plane tickets/passports. Yet only 1 person was working at a time because the metal detection part was so backed up that no more people could fit in line. So my question was, why couldn't you move at least one person  to help with the other portion? The management of the floor was terrible. 

Now, I'm a fairly seasoned traveler so I know what to take out of my bag but one of my bags got pulled to the side after going through X-ray. It seemed that at least one bag from every group got pulled to the side. They had a designated person to bring the "suspicious" bags to be reinspected on EACH station. Normally there are only 2-3 for the whole security station. Anyways, I waited about 10 minutes to get my bag reinspected. The guy who was in front of me had apparently packed his toiletries too well and the X-ray wasn't able to see them. Then it was discovered that my bag was pulled aside because of baby wipes. Ughhhh. All in all, security was poorly managed and extremely inefficient. Not to mention, it was blazing hot and probably 40 degrees outside. 

They have added tablets everywhere which is nice I guess. People can play games and charge their phones.  In terms of their employees, I feel like PHL needs to crack down. This has always been the airport that has the most employees just roaming around, on their phones, or just doing nothing. All of their time on clock could be managed so much better and that's all this place needs.

makes you proud to be a philadelphian to have this kind of venue in your city. the show was fantastic. the artchitecture is breathtaking. the $8 mini glass of wine should have been cheaper. but oh well.

Everything,  EVERYTHING, is good! The costumer service excellent .  When I say excellent I mean like the old fashion way, how have you been how are the kids friendliness  type of service. The baked goods are OMG good, South Philly good.   They even have milk and coffee.  By the get the tea biscuits 6 for $3.00.

I just moved to Philly after 7 years in San Francisco, so my soup dumpling standards are high.  Dim Sum Garden has phenomenal soup dumplings.  They call them pork buns or something like that (top of the menu).  Pork soup dumplings, crab soup dumplings, you can't go wrong.  They are out of this world.  Also, the food is super cheap and the staff is very nice.  Go there. Now.

We had dim sum there last weekend. Quality of the food is better than a lot of other dim sum in China town. Inside the restaurant is kinda dark because there are no windows. That's a small negative. Price is reasonable also.

The chicken sandwich on foccacio was very tasty.  Good bread, good piece of chicken, and zesty bread.   The chicken-apple sausage sandwich not as good.  Kinda boring.

Nice, wide and deep beer selection. 

Nice atmosphere too.  Can't remember the last time I saw a real wood burning fireplace.  Which certainly warmed up the bar on the afternoon I was there.

Back in October, I went to my friend Ross's charity happy hour.  I, feeling lucky, bought an arm length of raffle tickets.  Ross, feeling lucky, asked a girl out who he met there.  Ross is now well on his way to happily ever after, and I won a $50 gift cert to Twenty Manning... much better than a lousy t-shirt.

Anyway, fast forward and 20 Manning is gone... replaced by its grill friend.  My friend Lauren and I were meeting up for dinner and this seemed like a good place to try.  They accepted the gift cert despite the name change and we went about ordering.  I decided on a turkey burger and a beet salad, Lauren on a salad with chicken (the Chop Chop).  Everything was good, with the beet salad as the standout (the freshness of the veggies was really great). 

We also went with the toll house cookie for dessert, which is pretty sinful.  That said, given this is a classic recipe, you can kind of do this yourself with store-bought cookie dough, in which case there are other good options. 

Service was spotty but friendly -- it took a while to find a waiter at first and the staff seemed a little confused despite the restaurant not being to crowded.  

The decor is great.  Light, airy, fun, unpretentious, Parisienne.  Kind of like Parc without the sass.  

In all, this felt very much like an episode of Kitchen Nightmares gone right -- everything is fresh fresh fresh, with white walls and chalkboards.  But I still think Gordon would find something frustrating around how close they are to being a great neighborhood find and yet how far off they seem to be as well.

I absolutely love this lounge. I was able to spend a few hours here on both the frontend and backend to my trip to Philadelphia.

The Centurion Lounge is a great place to unwind and get recharged.  The space is gorgeous with plenty of workstations, sitting areas where you can relax, and plug ins galore.  The restrooms are clean, spacious, and private. They even have a shower if you feel the need to freshen up.

The food is delicious with a Mediterranean flair.  I had both breakfast and lunch here and they were both delicious.  They also have a full bar and plenty of televisions.  All of the folks working here are wonderful!

Never again! Both the manager and waitress's actions towards customers last night (9/24/09) were horrible. I have never experienced such rude and disrespectful service in an establishment in my life. If I could have given this place negative stars I would have!
Canned here from way uptown just for the cheesesteak--- best in the city!!! You won't regret it!
Jasmin is the sweetest! Took me even though I was late and took her time glamming me up. She listened to what I wanted which was natural lashes but with some volume and she did exactly just that.

Not impressed with the staff. I was in the store for 15 minutes and not once did the three women in the back take one moment  from their conversation to look over at me and see if I needed anything or even say hello -- even after trying to initiate a conversation asking about a product. Their inventory was interesting - but I wasn't able to get any information about anything because of the poor service. They lost a sale and a customer, because they seemed more interested in what happened last night, than doing business.

always so freaking genuinely friendly!
I'm somone who incredibly specific about friendly but genuinely bar service. I'm always impressed when I come here and I haven't been here for 6months!  thanks again Mac's. great people working here and fantastic beers and food. order the calamari for happy hour eh? a little more like fishnchips batter but delicious.

I love Sweetgreen delicious healthy salads with a great variety! There's two locations in Center City Philadelphia, one on Market Street, one on Chestnut. If you want a free salad go to the one on Market Street during the weekday later in the afternoon. Went there twice after 2pm and whoever was working didn't know how to work the register and I got a free salad not once but twice! However the people on Chestnut are a bit nicer and more professional. Either way, great salads!

Happy Birthday to....me!!! Had the opportunity to come for bday and eat with a special lady with great food, drinks, and conversation. Here is how I saw this special night for turning a year older...

Positives: Nights out on the town in Philadelphia is a regular, weekly thing. Each restaurasnt, dive I go to in the city never disappoints. This was the same thing that was true when I dined here. Beautiful restaurant located right on Market st. The bar and restaurant looked absolutely modern and amazing. Great decor and color inside. Reservations helped with getting seated wuicker and upon arriving we were immediately taken to our table that we had to go up a small stairway that held another area for seating similar to the level below. The waitress was very kind and personable and allowed my guest and I time to look at and decide what we wanted to select from the menu. We decided to go with a meat and cheese chucuteri board, sauteed cauliflower and brussel sprouts, and spicy flavored duck wings. Loved the chucuteri board, different cheeses, meats, sausage, and spreads. The sauteed cauliflower and brussel sprouts were the absolute . How could something be that good?!?? It was the highlight dish of the night.


Negatives: i liked the duck wings, they just wre a lil more spicy than I preferred. It was still good, but noticeable how it wasnt that much meat on the bone in comparison to chicken. Overall, I was still happy with the choice to dibe here for my special day. Looking forward to coming back here very soon with my dining partner.
Muito Bom, boa comida, atendimento excelente. Very good. One of the best Mexican restaurants.

If I could give this 3.5 stars, I would. It was above 3 stars, but not *quite* 4 stars.

An older lady took my husband and me to Ernesto's for dinner, and we were not disappointed in the least; for a Thursday night, it did seem kind of bare though.

Forgive me, but I cannot recall the actual names of the dishes we tried (and I can't seem to find their menu online). However, the crab cake dish with mushroom risotto and zucchini was perfect. There were large chunks of crab and lots of mild chives throughout; the sauce on the side was light and a perfect compliment. The chicken penne with red sauce, capers and olives was above average, and we probably wouldn't order it again; I would definitely get the crab cake if I returned.

We ended with the hazelnut gelato, which had a chocolate shell coating and strawberry sauce drizzled over it. Overall, so-so.

The service was impeccable; our waiter was attentive and made recommendations throughout. He opened our BYOB wine for us and poured the first round (though he did not pour any additional rounds). 

In short, we would consider returning if we had a week or so in Philadelphia, but probably not if we had less time.

Boyfriend and I had a great meal here yesterday! My boyfriend told me he's sick of the "hipster crap" I force him to eat, so he picked this joint. The menu appealed to him since it is very straight forward and predictable, but still interesting. 
The vibe was cooler than I expected. It seemed more like a cool bar than a steak house, which I appreciated. It was nice for a romantic, trendy night out. 
We started by sharing a crab cake and crab bisque.  Even though the crab cake was fried it was delicious, and served with a really interesting salad that I loved. The bisque was perfect.
Boyfriend got a ribeye that was like 22 ounces I think. He absolutely loved it and finished every bite. I ordered a New York strip with cheese, mushrooms, and a demi glaze sauce which was on special. It was absolutely fabulous. I ordered veggie kabobs on the side which I highly recommend as well. 
As far as drinks go, I highly recommend the smartini. It tasted like candy and went down very smooth, but was still wonderfully potent. My boyfriend got a drink called the evil genius which tasted very strongly of whiskey. He liked it but there's no way I could have drank one. 
For dessert we shared creme brûlée and bread pudding. The bread pudding was the stand out for sure. It featured raisins and a delicious caramel sauce.  Highly recommended!
I should mention as well that our waitress was on point the whole night.  She was very friendly and efficient.
All of this deliciousness definitely cost us...before a tip the bill was $193. But we got a lot for our money and loved every bite of it. We both highly recommend is spot for a nice date night, and we'll definitely be back.

I really like Deke's but I'm not a fanboy for the place like many people seem to be in this area. 

This location is exclusively take out, which is ok because it's about 2 blocks away from me. It is a positive mark on the neighborhood as everything around it smells like hickory and applewood smoke.

I give all of the sides 5 stars. A very loud shout out to the corn bread. Holy crap, this stuff is priceless, while being super inexpensive. It's more like corn cake. It's very heavy, soaked in molasses and super moist. I sincerely believe I am a return customer just for this stuff. But the potatoe wedges and mac n' cheese are great too.

The ribs are good, but I wouldn't call them world class. My biggest disappointment was the half chicken. I understand how smoking works but the skin was too tough to chew through. I almost wish they'd put it in the salamander just to crisp it up.

This is a great neighborhood spot and for the price I definitely recommend it. The quality is there and the family deals are a good value. Check it out!

FYI. There is a very small (4 car) parking lot for you to park and run in but it can get congested very easily and you can get stuck by morons parking everyone in. It's best to leave someone in the car making sure this doesn't happen, or walk if you can.

YOU KNOW YOU'RE JUST HERE TO TAKE YOUR ROCKY PICTURE...

But do yourself a favor and actually spend some time to enjoy the museum's collection.  It's a bit steep at $20 (The Art Institute of Chicago is $12, The Lourve is $14), but it's a nice way to spend the afternoon and the ticket is good for two days and also grants you access to the Rodin museum.  

The impressionist collection contains a version of Van Gogh's Sunflowers, Monet's Japanese garden, and some great pieces from Cezanne and Renoir. I also really enjoyed their collection of armor and weaponry.
