As for our entrees, I had the pulled berkshire pork sandwich and she had the koch farm turkey burger.  She enjoyed her burger and surprisingly I enjoyed the pork sandwich as I ordered it out on the limb since I had too many cheeseburgers within the week before.  This restaurant also has an atmosphere like a house.  Overall, I would definitely recommend this place to anyone looking for a good meal.

Installation is nice, fancy, happy and attractive.  Drinks are good.  Food is good and plentiful.  Waiters are friendly and helpful.  We were a large party and ordered an array of dishes, all were excellent.  Portions are large, you might like to consider sharing. I definitely recommend it.

I'm pretty sure there are some health code violations up in this place. This place really needs a face lift as well. 

The service was really good, and the food was fine, but towards the end of our meal I started to notice things. The server brought us a carafe of water which was nice. Then I noticed that there was a shelf of these carafes prefilled with water. They were without lids completely.

I saw another one of the servers touch her hair with both hands just before making her table's drinks. 

I got the chicken and Belgium waffle. The waffle was pretty amazing, but the second piece of chicken had a weird taste kind of like it had been frozen for awhile. I just remembered that there was a strand of hair on the page of my menu with the lunch specials. 

I don't really know how this place has 4 stars. Maybe when it was more new? Or maybe it's because they serve alcohol.... That was honestly why we went.
I order from this place all the time. They have the best Mexican food and the pizza al pastor is to die for!!!
Overpriced, glorified convenience store. Dirty, smelly, and unwelcoming.

When my best friend told me we were going to have some of the best Italian food at an Irish pub, I couldn't believe her. I looked up the menu and it had a lot of great options, so I was excited! 

We got there around 7 pm on a Friday night so it was pretty crowded, she recommended to go towards the earlier hours. They told us 20 minutes for inside or immediate outside, so the weather wasn't too awful to just sit outside. They have a cargo shipping container converted into a outside seating which only has 3 tables and than 2 picnic benches outside of that. The inside is VERY tight seating. To comment back to the 20 minutes there was several couples who were also waiting for inside seating and only one got seated by the time we finished our dinner, so the wait time was not too accurate from what they told people, but it's busy, so I understand. 

I started with a hot cider from their winter drinks, which were amazing! We shared the fried burrata, you read that right, FRIED. BURRATA. Than I ordered the pesto gnocchi and she got the Bolognese. The fried burrata was next level, cannot pass up on that one. The pesto gnocchi was to die for, the gnocchi was perfectly cooked and pillowy. The friends pasta was a little under for her liking, but she said she really comes for the burrata at the end of the day! 

This is such a small business hidden gem in Fishtown and definitely you cannot miss it if you ever are in the area!

This was our first time in Philadelphia and went to Bocconcini's on advice from our friends. What a great start to a vacation. The food was incredible and lots of it. Our reservations were for 7:30 on a busy Friday night and we were seated promptly at 7:30. Our server Jenny was so friendly and knowledgeable about the menu and believe me, we had plenty of questions. She never once rushed us to make up our minds. We started out with risotto balls for an appetizer. They were so good. Those, along with the garlic knots, sold me. Then came our entrees. They were huge and absolutely incredibly wonderful. We ordered linguini and meatballs, carbonara, and an artichoke pie. No complaints from our table. We had plenty of left overs to take back to our hotel and enjoy later. This was defiantly our favorite place to eat in Philadelphia by far. Kudos to the chief, Jenny, and Shelia for a great dining experience.

I have to say this is the best Chinese buffet in the area for reasons you would not expect. I do have to say their "traditional/generic" Chinese dishes SUCK. No offense, but the lo mein, fried rice and general tso's needs to be drastically improved but what i like about this place is the variety. The sushi bar is average, I've seen others like it in the area but what sets this place is apart is the Mongolian Grill. You can get beef, chicken sewers, shrilled all grilled, you can make your own Mongolian hot pots and make your own stir fried noodle station. Also the amount of seafood at this buffet is pretty impressive. They have snow crabs, blue grabs, crawfish and shrimp and they all look decent! I would definitely come back here on a hungover Sunday once again

Saw this place on TikTok and I had to try it out. Came here for happy hour with one of my friends. Highly recommend making a reservation if you want to sit in a heated tent since happy hour is strictly outdoors. There was an hour and 15 time limit but everything came out quickly so that wasn't an issue. We tried a lot of things out, but my favorites were the crab rangoons, potstickers, and kobe sliders. The pork bao wasn't as good as we thought it would be. The tofu tacos also had the same sauce as the pork boa that we weren't fans of. I also tried the edamame dumplings and I wasnt a huge fan of the texture. My friend got the shrimp tempura roll and she loved it. The last thing I wasn't really a fan of was crispy brussels sprouts. Crispy is supposed to mean crispy. Not soggy and burnt.  We also got the tequila scorpion bowl to share and that tasted amazing. Didn't love the place but didn't hate it either. Drinks were the best part. Order what you know you like if you want a good experience. Also just a side note. The music in the tent was really loud. I don't know if that's the case everyday, but I could hardly hear myself talk.

I ordered delivery via GrubHub -- their page denotes no minimum and no delivery fee. I ordered a sandwich and a two liter soda. I can understand that maybe that isn't worth the delivery for a small establishment...but I don't think that the resolution for their predicament is to call me up and beg me to order something more, then move into more harassing me into buying something more, and then when I said that I am really not interested in a pound of french fries to myself, I was told that my business wasn't worth it. I haven't eaten in more than a day, and was sitting around waiting for the sandwich that was supposed to arrive in ten minutes. Great. 
Basically, I can't review their food because they deemed me unworthy, but from the looks of the other reviews, I'm not missing out on much.
I just get upset when people think it's ok to blame others for their own mistakes (IE no delivery minimum or fee)...because now I've had to have a confrontation with someone today that I really would have preferred to avoid.
Sadly, there are probably enough obese people in South Philly to keep them afloat.

I was in the area running errands and decided to stop in and see what all the hype is about. O.M.G this place did not disappoint!! Definitely my new favorite cheesesteak. I got it with onions and American cheese. The meat was tender, cheese creamy, & bread soft and substantial. They are well known for their roast pork but they killed it with their cheesesteak as well! I went on a Sunday afternoon and I waited in line about 15 min, not too bad. It does get hot in the small building and it was packed to the brim so prepare the get a little sweaty in summer months. They def have it running so smoothly & although they are working their butts off everyone seemed very happy and nice. Already planning when I'm going to go back for another cheesesteak.

Sadly my first Philly in Philly was a cold brick. If it was fresh it would have been great I could taste remnants of deliciousness. But had to let it wait as I watched the entire crew bicker and argue. What could have been awesome slipped away with the heat of my sandwich as it sat there while the team fight.

Always walk by this place and it looks cute but tiny on the outside, but its actually three floors. This place isn't closed. We went there Thursday night and ordered the d'angelos salad to shAre as an app, which was delicious and they split it up on two plates for us. The waiter seemed a little annoyed by all the questions I asked but he was able to answer everything. I ordered the tortellini and added sweet sausage, which was delicious but a little al dente. My fiancé got a fish place which I didn't try but he really enjoyed it. Wine by the bottle starts at $50 so the bill will add up, especially if you go for a meat option, but I like this place. I would probably give it 3.5 stars f I could because the tables are in sort of weird positions and it feels a little tight. Good food though.

When I first came in here, I was already given a small good impression that this cafe was well distinguished from the rest in Chinatown. Modern it looked, it would attract the many teenagers around the area. Calm background music with wifi, it would make a fine social area. The drinks there ranges from a moderate to slightly over priced. From many of my friends, the drinks are not bad, however, to my view, it is inconsistent. Their smoothies are watery , not exactly "smooth". On good days however, the smoothies are fine. In a way, it depends who works there. One drink I favor the most however, is their thai bubble tea. Cold, creamy, with the pure tea taste, one of the finest Thai bubble teas I've tried. Overall, the place was fine, but I'd prefer other cafes.
Food was great, the atmosphere was unique. We loved the lobster mac and cheese and would come back just for that!!!

Great spot for a quick snack or lunch. Belgian waffles made custom with the toppings of your choice- fruits, nuts and chocolates. Crispy outside, gooey center... yum! They also have great, filling sandwiches (I love the Hummus) and fresh salads (with an awesome balsamic dressing). But be sure to get there early because they go fast! O, and the staff is always super friendly. Definitely one of my favorite lunch spots on the go.

After Randolph's at the bottom of Chestnut Hill closed, I took my jewelry to the "new" Randolph's at the top of the Hill. It was a whole new business, no longer truly affiliated with Randy. When I asked them to renew my engagement ring, instead of just replacing the emerald as we discussed, they made a whole new ring, made a new wedding band to fit it. When I asked where the engraving inside had gone, the lady lied and told me it had disappeared when they stretched the ring. Huh? 
One of the new diamonds fell out in 3 months, and I took the ring to Chestnut Hill Jewelers. They told me it had been done cheaply: only one prong was used to hold the stones instead of the customary two. When I went back to "Randolph's," they refused to refund my money.  
CH Jewelers redid the ring so it was secure, for a very reasonable price. 
I have since taken all my jewelry to the lovely ladies at CH Jewelers. They are honest, knowledgeable, and friendly. I highly recommend them. Be safe and sure; don't use Bijoux (the new place the Randolph people opened). Take all your jewelry needs and desires to Chestnut Hill Jewelers and be assured of fine service and beautiful products.

Mad Mex has great margaritas and great wings! Their happy hour specials are really good (half off drafts and wings, $7 late margaritas and $3/$5 12/16oz margaritas). Although the food and drinks are great the service here is absolutely terrible (David). It took more than 20 minutes for our drinks to come. Then we didn't see our waiter again for a half an hour! Once we finally ordered our food it took forever to come out and then our waiter never came back to refill our drinks. Although we really enjoyed our food and drinks we probably won't return due to the poor service.

Very average and over-priced.
I would not come back here...but Green Leaf is right across from them, and their food looks delicious.

Edit: Sick 30 minutes after eating here.

Great new decor with food and prices just as good as the original location. Most people go for the xiao long bao, but I would recommend trying the shengjian bao

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shengjian_mantou

My only caveat here is that the menu is slightly different. Some of noodle soups I enjoy from the original location are not there.

Honestly, I've been in and out of all of the Drinker's pubs over the past few years but the center city spot is by far the best location. 

Great location, good prices, and friendly, good looking service (Alright, with the exception of the toolbag guy with the bell tat. Rude and unhelpful - steer clear of him and snag another of the bartenders to take care of you). 

Check out their happy hour for sure. Just pick your bartender carefully!

Before Kasumi came along, "fresh fish" and "sushi" in South Philly are not terms that go together. Even so, I happened to be in the area and ya know, when ya gotta craving it has gotta be satisfied! Turned on google maps and looked for a sushi place. Always knew this strip was the brand spanking shiny addition to the area but did not realize it contained this hidden gem. Was only expecting mediocre gas station food and mentally prepped myself to get sick but when I opened my little boxes of food, I was pleasantly surprised at the freshness and quality.
First up came the sashimi sampler. Again, was expecting just thin slices of fish(South Philly remember?) but it came with 5 robust pieces of sushi. And all the fish were fresh! 5 pieces is just enough to wet the palette.  
Next was the main attraction-the spider roll- it was nice and filling, had the satisfying taste of a fried soft shell crab and the sweet umami glaze.

Much better!  Food and service amazing! 
Ordered salads and kebabs and loved everything along with the wine I brought.

Pint night is the only reason I ever have/ever will come here.  And unfortunately, my last pint night experience wasn't that great.

Maybe because it was the last week of college before everyone went down the shore for the summer or because I hadn't been there on a Tuesday in about six months, the place was unusually packed from what I remember.  Filled with Temple bros and hoes, everyone was particularly loud and annoying because they had to scream over the awful club music.  We're surrounded by huge TV's with tons of good games on and I have to listen to Usher for the hundredth time?

Anyway, we were able to wrangle a table for our large group and the server (God bless him) was so frazzled by how busy it was and kept apologizing for how long the drinks were taking.  At first, it was understandable and we remained patient.  But around 12:30 when we ordered our drinks and still didn't receive them 45 minutes later, I got fed up and decided to just go up to the bar.  Bad decision.  I've been to bars where you have to make your face/arm/twenty dollar bill apparent to the bartender and usually it works after about five or ten minutes of waiting.  After about 35 minutes, the entire side of the bar I was standing on still hadn't been paid attention to by the neglectful, clearly annoyed bartenders and I just gave up and returned to the table where thankfully, our drinks from the waiter were sitting.

Again, a wide selection of $2 domestics and imports is hard to pass up in these economic times, but seriously? THAT long of a wait?  Hopefully I can go back mid-July when all the Temple students are gone and maybe I can get a beer within two minutes of ordering it.

Also, the chips and salsa were stale and bland.

I just love everything I ordered here! Everything is always good. Fast and never poor service everytime I come in. Bird nest, pad thai, duck noodle soup always my favorite! Place is always pack and you do have to wait a couple of minutes for a table. But it's worth it.

All around class-act place. Really sharp looking bar and restaurant. We were just in for drinks but the food looked good. Casual modern and upscale place in a historic setting. 

From the hostess to the concierge everybody was very friendly. The bearded bartender was the star of the show. Nice to find a place with bartenders with personality.

Ordered food for a party with some Korean friends and the results were outstanding.  The delivery arrived on time, hot, fresh, and delicious.  The driver was polite and we had a great time enjoying fried chicken in various flavors (original, extra crispy, extra spicy) along with corn salad, radish pickles, ttock in spicy sauce, and salads.
Chris was excellent very helpful made it easy for me I highly recommend him and elite media
Agree with others. The food is ok. The amount of time it takes to eat at or order from this restaurant is mind blowing. An hour to cook a breakfast sandwich is ridiculous.

Cranky bike store owners usually usually exhibit similar characteristics as cranky record store owners: Generally unhelpful disposition unless you know exactly what you want. 

Via Bikes was in this category for the majority of the last decade, but I think it's been eclipsed by Jay's. It seems like it should be a friendly local bike shop, but I found it way more condescending then the most "hipster" run fixie shop. Maybe I caught them on a bad day, but I don't plan on going back.

There are a lot of really great bike shops in philly with good attitudes: Firehouse, Bicycle Stable, Bicycle Therapy, Trophy Bikes. I've had great interactions with all of staff at these shops over the years (and through 6+ project bikes) 

What sets them appart is attitude. I don't always know what I'm looking for, but the shops that help me find it are the ones that get my money, and usually more then either of us intended.

For a coffeeshop, the tables are pretty spaced apart which makes it a great place to get some work done without being distracted by other people's (sometimes obnoxiously loud) conversations going on on the side. The place is well-lit with good ambience.

That being said, depending on who's working, the music can be way too loud. Also, the tea selection is okay but not extensive, though the london fog is pretty good. I wouldn't say anything here is worth paying 4-5 bucks, which is how much most beverages seem to cost. I really only come here when I  want a change of pace while still being productive. There are definitely better places to go if you're looking for a quality drink.

Great mom and pop find. My pan fried flat noodles (N8) were really tasty and filling. My friend had never had beef rendang and enjoyed his rice platter. I thought everything on the plate was served too cold and the beef, although it had good flavor, was so dry and tough to eat. I'll give it the benefit of the doubt and hope it was a fluke. Prices are awesome for real, authentic indonesian/singapore style dishes.
Just moved to the neighborhood, food is good and hot, the lady at the counter was friendly and our order was ready in less than 10 minutes!
Favorite place to get my haircut in the city. All the reviews speak the truth. Friendly guys that do sensational work. And you can't beat a $15 haircut. I won't get my haircut anywhere else.

We've known about Hawthorne's for years but never had the motivation to walk to the east side of Broad for breakfast. This morning, we were up early enough to get there before the brunch crowds so we went and arrived shortly after they opened. The space is really cute and cozy. It was wet outside though and the floors were insanely slippery. Between three of us, we ordered the Hawthorne omelette, the 11th Street omelette, and the special mocha chocolate pancakes. Unfortunately, everyone was disappointed. The omelettes were prepared French style, which is fine in theory, but they didn't taste like eggs at all. The filling for each omelette was tasty, but only one bite had cheddar cheese in it and the omelette itself was essentially wrapped around the filling almost like they were two entirely different components to the dish. The cheese failed to meld the egg and the filling together...perhaps because there was hardly any of it. The pancakes that weren't burnt were decent, but we were surprised that they served them given that the bottom was completely black. Not just overcooked. Burnt. The chef should have known better. In all fairness, the waitress, who was super sweet, appropriately removed it from our bill. Maybe the regular chef took some time off for the holidays.

Philly Cupcake cupcakes are among the best I've ever had (red velvet and chocolate with vanilla buttercream frosting are my favorites). The cupcakes are absolutely delicious--they are moist and the frostings are light. They are not limited to cupcakes; they have a very diverse menu of confections including: dark, milk, or white chocolate covered rice crispy treats, marshmallows, Swedish Fish and Oreos. They also have coffee, Bassetts ice cream (!!!), cookies, brownies and even doggie treats!! Not only are the cupcakes and confections incredible, but the building itself is aesthetically pleasing. I worked in the area during undergrad and I was appreciative of the White Building and the cafés that were there, but the corner of 12th and Chestnut--for lack of a better phrase--was a hot mess. The vibrant pink and blue that embosses the Philly Cupcake logo that hangs on the front of the building is inviting. Even the interior is delicious. Honestly, I could rave about the awesomeness that is Philly Cupcake, but I will end with this: You will not be disappointed.  So, check out Philly Cupcake and feel free to indulge and satisfy your confectionary curiosity.

i made a haiku about this pizza: 

crunch like an earthquake
slurpable cheese (tang and grease)
the only pizza

I can't stop thinking about this pizza and it's coming out in weird ways, clearly. I am literally obsessed with this pizza. The only reason i go on instagram is to check the account for this place to see their posts/stories. Then this leads me to think about the pizza throughout the day. At least once a week, my willpower to resist the magic pizza caves, and I end up stopping in for two (huge) slices. 

Some people complain that 4 dollars for pizza is too much. First of all, these pizzas are cut into six slices so they are significantly larger than your average slice. Second, I'm not coming in here because I have a desire for just some pizza. It's because this is now the ONLY pizza i want, the only pizza that satisfies my stomach (soul). It is THE pizza and I'd pay 5 dollars a slice and i'm not ashamed to admit it. 

Thank you to these supreme pizza magicians. I love you a lot.

I loved the vibe and energy of this place. I went with two friends and ran into another. We ordered the buttermilk pancakes, and then got two orders of the fried chicken sandwich, one with fries and one with a salad (because healthy balance), and lastly a few rounds of the grapefruit mimosas. I really enjoyed how kind the wait staff is, and the food was pretty great. I would say the only reason I didn't get 5 stars is because of the time it took to get some of our items, and the fried chicken kind of gives more of a baked chicken vibe or maybe that is just the chefs take on it, but either I definitely recommend this place for brunch. I am very eager to go back for dinner and/or happy hour! 

I did want to add that they do have outdoor seating, as well as a bottle shop inside. So I would definitely advise checking that out.

Really?

Earl Grey Sriracha ice cream: the flavors did NOT go.

Was part of the first tour this season and, like most people on the tour, did so through groupon. It was a really quirky fun random date night! Especially since both of us would count ourselves pretty versed in the city, it was fun to be a tourist in our own city. Joe runs the tour like he's out at the bar with you: there's no script, no formulaic "on your left you'll see...", he peppers facts with f bombs and phrases like "the brits saw us as goddamned traitors, you know?" The conversational tone made the tour approachable, inviting, and entertaining. While a lot of his information, especially in Washington Square Park, is readily available (signage in the square tells a lot of the same info) it's the way it's presented that makes this work. 

His information is seedy and not ghost story heavy, so if you're looking for a "haunted philly" tour look elsewhere. If you're looking for a silly way to spend 1.5 hrs (and get half off to his blood and beer tour and money off food/beer at Triumph) grab a friend (or 2. or 2.5. whatever makes you happy cupcake) and book a Grim Philly Tour. At the very least you'll get a chuckle and a different picture of Philly

Best vietnamese hoagie outside of down town . Plenty of meat , fresh veggie , tasty vietnamese mayo and  bread is soft on the inside , but yet crispy  on the outside .Call ahead and order for pick up is the way to roll . Cash only .

Excellent spot. We were looking for a late lunch and found this place. The bacon cheeseburger fries and a shake were terrific. The burger has one huge puck shaped patty. The new spots are doing the smaller thinner patties and while I like that better this did not disappoint. 

The fries were good and the shake was really good. 

Lost a star due to the crazy long 20 min wait with no one in the place. Indifferent teenager at the register needs to be replaced by a robot. Finally the floors could use a washing as they were sticky.
The greatest little spot on Passyunk. Beloved by, seemingly, a small group of devoted regulars. Great for a date.

I am not Gluten-free, Vegan, or Vegetarian.  

However, my gf is gf, and I don't mind eating at healthy vegan/veggie restaurants. We happened upon this place, and with the decor, warmth, and vibe decided to get dinner.  Our waiter, Andrew, was great! Down to earth, attentive, and had a knowledge of the food. (my gf is good at asking for specifics of certain dishes for dietary reasons) 

We ordered chips and guacamole. YUM!
I ordered the "Chick'n" enchiladas.  It's not real chicken, but it was delicious and filling. SO GOOD. 

I'd say this place is dietary restriction friendly, and regardless of that, it's cool, casual, and has great food.  Two thumbs up!

Small BYO sushi restaurant. A large group of us trued about 12 rolls - I'm not going into the list, but they were all decent. They have a good 3 for $30.95 deal that includes most rolls. Service was friendly. It's a small space that isn't impressive, but the service is friendly.

i took one of my chihuahuas here ...really for a second opinion .........i was really very impressed ...this place is wonderful...the entire staff was fantastic and friendly and very professional .....i love the vet in charge ....she is fantastic ..knowledgeable and compassionate .........i will be using this place for my rescues and pets in the future .......a good vet is difficult to come by ..............her name is dr. aimee berman ......wow was i impressed .........her energy was friendly and welcoming ..and very knowledgeable and professional at the same time .....when you speak to her she actually listens ( unlike many vets ) and takes the time to respond ...and educate me as a client ........the facility is state of the art ......the is part of the national greyhound rescue .............ngap .............but they medical center there is also a regular vet practice and they see all kinds of dogs and cats .........i highly recommend this ....so well worth the trip ...........when you use this vet ..it also benefits ...the rescue its associated with ..............what a gem of a place ..........a well hidden away secret of the Philadelphia's parkwood neighborhood ...............
Omg silky, creamy, tongue tantalizing heavenly goodness!

This food truck does not get as much attention as it deserves!

I've eaten 3 different things at iPho: Pho (of course!), fried shrimps (forgot the actual name of the dish), an Pork Banh Mi, all of which were great! Coming from a food truck, I did not have high expectations for the Pho, but they pulled it off. The only thing is that some of the noodles were a bit clumped together. The fried shrimps were great for a snack or shared appetized between 2 people. And finally the banh mi. The flavor is totally there. It was reaaally spicy, which I liked, although they did not ask me if I wanted it spicy (I guess that makes it more authentic??). The obligatory veggies, cilantro, and sauce were present, and the pork was perfectly cooked  I only wish it was a little bit bigger! :'(   

...But overall great stuff.

I don't know why anyone who lives in South Philly would bother to drive all the way to Chinatown, search 40+ mins for a parking spot, and pay for said parking spot when Wokano is right there on Washington Ave with free parking and in my opinion, pretty damn good dimsum and dinner.

To give you an idea of how great I think this place is, I was only home visiting for a few days and ate here twice (almost three times if mom dukes didn't oppose).  First time was for dinner.  This place has the best beijing spareribs (king to spareribs), ever.  The sauce wasn't too thin or thick, not too sour or sweet.  The pieces of pig were fried perfectly and had a good balance of fat and lean parts.  (Mouth is watering right now.)  If you're up for it, do try their fried pork intestines served with picked veggies.  Yeah, it sounds pretty gross but oh so tasty in reality.

Dimsum had a very good variety along with the typical shrimp dumplings and siu mai.  There was this one fishball with cilantro type dish (lang yue quao) that was perfect taste and texture-wise.  The mini egg tarts were good too.

Too bad Wokano is like 3000+ miles away from me, but it'll def be my go-to spot whenever I'm in town!

Had a great Prix Fixe business  lunch at Fork (2 courses for $25). A few bottles of Veltliner, oysters, skate, great peanut butter chocolate layered dessert. What a great value for food this good, get there and take advantage as soon as possible.

Service was friendly, knowledgeable, and good.

I went with a friend from out of town for Sunday brunch. The live performers were an unexpected treat and immediately elevated the experience. We opted to share a couple appetizers and the non-vegetarian thali and our server was really helpful with suggestions to make it varied and enough to share. My friend got a beautifully presented mango lassi with pistachio sprinkled on top. My chai sangria was also well made. Our appetizers were the reshmi kabob and crispy spinach chaat. Both looked visually engaging, were tasty, and were good portions. The thali was in general a little bit milder than other Indian food I've had but definitely not lacking in taste, and I'm used to spicy food.

Food is yummy ! I only got the vegan falafel "sandwich" but it was basically falafel wrap. The falafel were so good, not the super best around Philly, but as a small mom and pop spot on Passyunk Ave, it's great. Nice and crunchy, super flavorful and a huge generous amount of everything from the cabbage cucumbers and slaw to the size of the bread they put it on. It was only about $7-$8 so def a nice treat for when you're at Vin Cafe or just looking for a good vegan spot on the go.. they fixed my food in only about 10-15 mins while I was sipping away at vin cafe across the street. Convenient! Craving them now lol.

as stated before, i have a high bar for Thai food. Circles very nearly met it (that bar  shall forever be set by a little seaside Thai joint in Fairhaven, Mass).

Circles is a small, comfy BYOB in NorLibs with tasty, fresh food, nice, not overbearing decor, with attentive staff that serve drink and food with a lot of attention to detail.

we got thai ice tea, which came in two glasses with little carafes of simple syrup and cream, so you could sweeten and cream your tea to your preference, instead being served to you already mixed together, which is how it usually comes to the table. how charming and utterly brilliant!

the Tom Kha soup had a lovely, subtle, warming broth, although it was more oniony than i've ever had, and the mushrooms in it didn't seem cooked at all - almost like they were just dropped in the hot broth. 

my partner got red curry with brown rice (which had an amazing texture!), with the perfect amount of heat and crispy vegetables. the same could be said for my pad thai - i ordered it medium spicy, and it was pretty perfect (then again, i love spicy food, so it may be a little much for the average person). all the veggies and sprouts in the dish were crisp and al dente, and the noodles, while saucy, were not drowning in grease. five generous-sized shrimp. elegantly plated. love!

I really hope no one at Federal Donuts sees this, but Wah-Gi-Wah is the best chicken in the city, hands down.  It is the only thing that can motivate me enough to make the trek to West Philly.  I'm never not slightly in the mood for the Lahori Chicken.
I live nearby and go often, sometimes with my husband or friends and sometimes alone. Food is always delicious, staff is warm and friendly, atmosphere is quiet and comfortable.

I came here specifically for the chairs/ambience, and that didn't disappoint. The swing chairs on the second floor were pretty cool, and the chandelier was an artsy touch. The whole place sort of reminds you of childhood with the colors and the layout, but in a good, classy way.

The food, however, was below par. My boyfriend and I had the french onion soup dumplings, Korean tacos, and lobster mac and cheese to share. The dumplings were unique and tasty and the tacos were pretty standard fusion, but the lobster mac and cheese was unbelievably salty. I think I drank six glasses of water that night. 

Overall, come here for the ambience and the chairs, but don't expect to be blown away by the food.

This place is such a hit or miss.. The Happy hour is pretty good and it's totally worth it for the $5 flatbreads (try the BBQ pork, trust me on that one) and the outdoor sitting area. The service, especially upstairs is not the best and if you don't already know, no one will tell you that there are no waiters and you have to order at the bar. The bartenders vary and some are really great while others will ignore you in a completely empty bar.. If it wasn't so close to work and the flatbreads weren't that tampering I would probably never come back.

Excellent service!  I stopped in to MidCity early this morning. because I had  low pressure in my tire. They found the nail right away, patched my tire from the inside, and guaranteed it for the life of the tire. The owner was  professional- work was fast and his staff so courteous and friendly. but the best part of the experience was  the ambassadors that greeted me  at the door -and stayed beside me until I left  the two most adorable playful dogs, I have ever seen, Luna and Mona.

It's got a good selection of classes, but several have been cancelled recently at the last minute for no apparent reason. The weekend hours are also limited and that's when I have the most time to work out. Showers in the locker room have almost no privacy and elderly swimmers leave puddles of water all over benches/anywhere to change. Weight room is usually crowded and the tight space packed with guys makes women feel uncomfortable. There are lots of better options for the money.

My friends and I just got brunch here and the menu was so extravagant. I ordered the petit steak with sunny side eggs and it came with an awesome sauce mixed in, which included mangos and diced apples!! One of the best brunches I've had. 

My friend felt the Lamb chops were a bit small, but the owner was nice enough to give him a few extra chops with his meal. An awesome owner and host! Would definitely come back soon!! :)

We attended what should have been a beautiful & elegant milestone birthday party. Needless to say I am sad for my friend, whose birthday we celebrated & the spouse that paid for it.
First things first...when the temperature outside is 100+ it is a requirement for guests to feel comfortable. Hallways leading to the reception were painfully stuffy, but tolerable if the main space was cool... No. There were old window AC units doing very little to combat the heat. To make matters worse there were several fans that remained off for the entire evening, despite the owner making several appearances (dressed very poorly walking through a black tie event) and seeing guest trying to stay cool with makeshift fans. One of the drapes was wrapped around a fan, instead of fixing it we just continued to sweat while sitting still, not able to fully enjoy the party. I'm not sure how the open bar was provided but again 100+ day and there was no water in sight; it had to be purchased from another party. The bathrooms were very cramped and HOT! Spanx are already the enemy, in a sauna they are worse. There was no freshening up in the restrooms that's for certain. Men are apparently not concerned about their appearance as there were no mirrors in the men's room. My fiancé had to sneak into the ladies room to adjust his tie! And the icing on the birthday cake, I can only assume it was a relative (son) of the owner but there was a young boy, again dressed very casually for a black tie event making frequent walk through a and at one point even helped himself to food. 
I actually had this place on a short list of future venues. NO. In the dark you can see that there is potential for this place to be nice, however it falls short in many ways and is definitely a no go for a summer event. Glad I was a guest so I will never be a patron.

Our dinner order arrived quickly, and the food was still warm. Everything was delicious. As a large group, we were able to sample several dishes. The starters: the Guac is great, so are the chips. Make sure you also order the tasty ceviche-like shrimp dip, which is amazing. As main dishes we had a little bit of everything. The salmon and the sauce it comes with are outstanding. So is the shrimp taco, which I particularly enjoyed. I also loved the Tinga burrito. Really, all dishes tasted great. And all this at an absolutely moderate price. Now I have to try items from the brunch menu... Highly recommend this place.

Used them for a last minute Christmas Eve event for 50 people. I called up and told them what tables I had and they knew exactly what I needed. It was awesome. The linens where clean and pristine and so was the service and very cheap. I will never be using party rental again for my catering! Thank you so much.

I've been going to the family practice at Locust Walk for the past few years and the care I've received here is top notch. I've had extensive work done- Invisalign, dental implant, crown- and every doctor and assistant who have treated me have been amazing. I am so happy with the results of my treatment- my smile has been completely transformed. The entire staff, including the receptionists, are friendly, professional, and knowledgeable.

Decided to try the Big 'N Toasty this morning: a great concept, featuring a whole, fried egg instead of the scrambled kind.  Execution: bad.  The egg and the bacon was dry and stale, presumably pre-cooked and re-heated.  Yet, it still took them over five minutes to prepare the sandwich.  Apparently, they botched the order of execution: many people after me in line got their orders first before they finally got to mine.  I'm really getting disillusioned about Dunkin' Donuts in general (as their donuts are actually smaller now for the same price), and specifically this store.  I don't think I'm going to return....

Cheap and skimpy portions equal crappier pizza.  Do not get this pizza unless you want to overpay for an ordinary plain pizza.  I don't care how many football players he gets to help him peddle his crappy pizza.  It just sucks

Always one of my favorites, have been going here for 20yrs now. The food is unlike anything you will find anywhere else and will not disappoint. Start with appetizer sampler. Kadu, a turnover filled w/pumpkin is the best and make sure to also get Sambosa Goushti stuffed with meat and chick pea. I can taste the lamb Kabuli Palaw rite now. It is chucks of stewed lamb in a mound of rice with pistachios, carrots, raisins, almonds with some of the sauce from the lamb on top. Family always love the Lamb Chop Kabobs and Aush which is a veggie soup w/meat a bit of mint and yogurt.
I love this place and have recommended it to so many people thru the years. I have yet to hear anything but positive things. Ask for Walid - a great guy.

I stay in Hilton properties all over the world upwards of 90 nights per year and I found really nothing wrong with this location. The staff was very friendly and knew their jobs well. My room was clean with two big flat screens and a nice comfy bed. The heat/AC worked fine and so did the wifi. I left too early to take advantage of their breakfast deal. I will stay here again.

What a cute place! First off any place where you can easily reserve a table for ten during Sunday brunch online is amazing. Downside is we had to wait for the previous party if 16 to clear out so we waited about ten minutes past our reservation time before we sat. The high chairs for the two small kids were ready but not cleaned well enough. 

Our server was friendly, patient and attentive. We started with the included chips and salsa.   I also ordered queso fundido and a pitcher of margaritas El Corazon. Whoa! That was amazing with blood orange, pomegranate and passion fruit plus a hit of Serrano pepper. (Alert - we ended up ordering a second one to go with the food.)

The menu had solid brunch options. Our vegetarian friends ordered pancakes. Most had tacos or burritos. We loved ours with tender and flavorful goat. We also got a small side of greens with carrots and radish. I'm still pretty sad I didn't get churros but there was that second pitcher. Oh I tried their michelada since I'm on a current kick. It was nice and spicy. 

The bill wasn't too bad $200 for seven adults and three  kids. No drama to split in four ways on credit cards and no automatic gratuity. I'll definitely return withh a large party.

Good place to hang out, have some coffee, use your laptop and do some work without being bothered. I'm not sure what coffee they brew, but it is strong and well made and so are their espresso based drinks. 3 stars only because the space is kind of cramped... whenever I walk up or down the stairs, I feel like I'm about to fall off of it.

Living in Center City we go out a lot lol..... but I am a foodie who unfortunately has to be Gluten Free due to Celiac's Disease so it gets pretty depressing sometimes. More often than not I have to sit there and watch my fiance try new beers on tap or the special sandwich of the day, while I'm left with the choice of a mixed drink and salad or fries (because I am a vegetarian also). 

POPE I want to say thank you for carrying a good gluten free beer and also thank you for my AMAZING Gluten Free Grilled Cheese Sandwich! The bread was so fresh and delicious!! I am so grateful for all of your Gluten Free, Vegetarian and Vegan Options!

We had a great time last night 9-14 for my sisters birthday. Our server was very nice and answered all our questions. We ordered a charcuterie board with 3 cheeses, salmon, skirt steak, and spaghetti bolognese. The spaghetti tasted really fresh and my steak was cooked perfectly.  The salmon had a sweet fruit sauce which was different than anything I've tried before.

I really loved this barber and I would travel about an hour to get my haircut here because I saw their quality. But going from a $40 beard and haircut trim to $60 during the pandemic did it for me. No person can cut a man's hair good enough to pay $60 every three weeks.
So yummy!! I got the elk burger with sweet potato fries and onion rings. The onion rings were AMAZING!! The elk was super lean and tasty too. Can't wait to come back
I love this restaurant. The food, atmosphere and staff are the best. One of my favorite places to get oysters!!

Went here on a Friday night after the new guy said you couldn't beat the prices. I will never doubt this man again.
I will never begrudge a Dive Bar for what it is or how it looks. You have to judge a dive bar on its specials and its food. On both points, Pitchers is easily one of the best Dive Bars in Manayunk. Happy Hours starting at 1pm(trust me there was a guy waiting for them to open up that was still there at 5) and 10cent wings? You can't do better than that. And did I forget to mention HOW GOOD the wings are? The Teriyaki wings with Wassabi dipping sauce were phenomenal. I don't even want to share what my total was at the end of a couple of hours(i had two sets of friends arriving at different times, don't judge me) because they HAD to have cut me a break. Like the 5 stars indicate, As good as it gets!

I love this place. Certainly in my top 3 brunch places in Philly. It's a little pricy, but worth every bit. I cannot day enough good things about the food. Con would be the size, because they do like to pack people in.

What a letdown!!! :( 

I was really looking forward to trying this place for my birthday and looking back, I wish we had walked out immediately.

Upon entering, I was told that the upstairs dining room was closed, (which was recommended by others) so that was an initial bummer. As the waitress walked us back to our table, I immediately felt like we were in a diner (slightly nicer tho with their addition of mirrors and twinkle lights). The crowd was looked primarily over the age of at 60 and the decor of the restaurant (wooden tables and chairs), as well as staff's very casual attire, further enhanced the diner-esq atmosphere.

Why didn't we turn around and leave just then? Let's just call it birthday optimism. 

They offer you a 3-course $30 pre fixe menu as an option, but of course only selected entrees are offered. I opted otherwise and ordered cream of mushroom soup and the special that night, which was soft shell crabs with a corn-mash cake and roasted red pepper cream sauce on the side. My date chose the pre fixe menu, and ordered the mushroom soup and the lobster ravioli. The soup was pretty good; the mushrooms were blended into small pieces and the broth was creamy and flavorful. 

The entrees? Major disappointment. The soft-shell crabs did not have much flavor or seasoning. They consistency was mushy and pretty unappetizing. The corn cake they were layed on was mediocre as well as the spinach, but overall the dish was not good. Needless to say I didn't eat much of it.  The lobster ravioli had some chunks of lobster which was good, but the sauce on top not only looked like cheese-whiz, but tasted like it. If cheese-whiz belongs on anything at all, it's a cheese steak.. NOT ravioli. Tisk tisk.  When the waiter noticed my meal had barely been touched, he offered me the menu to chose another entree. I went for the rack of lamb. The meat was cooked medium rare as I requested, but the meat lacked seasoning and the potato gratin that accompanied it was just O.K. I regret choosing another entree, as we should have just had the waiter remove my initial dish from the check and left. 

Since dessert was included in the pre fixe menu, we chose the chocolate-chip cheesecake. It must have been the pastry chef's first cheesecake ever-- because if it wasn't, they should be ashamed to serve that. The top layer of the cake looked and tasted like solid cream cheese. At that point I wished that I had a bagel in my bag to smear it on so I could have had something of substance from the meal.

Overall, this place is exactly what it looked like when we first walked in-- diner quality at best. I suggest they lower their outrageous prices if they continue to serve dishes of such low quality.

Wow!! This was an amazing. I came here with my fiancé at night and all the lights were hanging and looked beautiful. There were also hammocks where you can lay and look at the trees glowing with the lights. I wish there was more going on. There was only one place to get food. I wish there was like a variety of food trucks here but it would probably be hard with the limited space.

This was a cool place to hang out but we did not stay long. We just walked around to see the cool views and see what the hype was all about. It was a beautiful night and most likely will come back.

I booked my rental online and when i arrived they offered me a 16 passenger vehicle. Im a female are you kidding me? I declined it they gave me another car that smelled like cigarettes and i told them i am NOT driving off with that car it stinks. The lady brielle at the counter gave me another car and might i add they never did a walk around nor advised me the trunk doesnt close fully even when locked. So all my belongings were stolen in my vehicle and to make matters worse the car would not start for HOURS and i missrd my flight. I was to return the car 11am and was not able to get there until 530pm because it would not start.. I came in to return it and explained what happened and they still charged me another day. Horrible service. I missed my flight and stuck here another day because their faulty vehicle on top of my things stolen out my trunk. The counter guy provided me a measly business card for complaints... DO NOT RENT HERE!!!!! I WILL be contacting their corporate office and BBB.

I find PECO to be next to useless. I wonder what the hell you people do 90% of your day. If you would do routine maintenance on power lines, we wouldn't lose it every single time it spits rain, there's a rumble of thunder or the wind blows. Outage reporting is a joke, you never ever have the correct times which can ultimately be off by DAYS. 

I could probably learn how to become an electrician on YouTube by the time you come to fix my power that's been out for 12+ hours with no restoration time. 

If I were to begin paying you for days of working service per month, I'm sure you'd magically begin to get things done otherwise you'd be losing 5-7 days of pay each month.

Edit: you don't plan on fixing the power here until Saturday by 11PM. How is that even acceptable?

I mean are you kidding?
I went though many motorcycle web sites including Amazon and I purchased here and there a couple of things and what have you (since after taking 16 years of break riding; I'm back) ... and then I found Revzilla ...
so I checked out the videos which were detailed and then placed my order.
Then I received my order and tried what was received and I was not sure ...
so I called Revzilla and spoke with the coolest dude ... down to earth and honest opinioned and asked some questions ...
What a great help I received from behind the phone ... as if I was sitting in front of this honest dude and he was assisting like I'm his brother ...
Andy, in customer service ... Dude! you Rock! 
thank you ...
Awesome Service! simply Awesome ... thank you Andy, Thank you Revzilla!
you folks take care,

This is NOT a review of their roast pork with broccoli rabe...
---
...Because that sandwich rocks. 
This IS a review of their chicken cutlet with sharp provolone, and curly fries. 
Setting: frigid
Some guy doing 5 pound cheesesteak challenge (I think he paid $40, and if he finished, the prize was the $40, a Polaroid, and a T-shirt; one hour later he gave up)

Food: The chicken was dry as hell, and the curly fries sucked

Verdict: Would probably be back, but stick with ribeye-based (or roast pork-based) sandwiches instead
Excellent food but the menu is limited. Make sure you are in the mood for a French cafe before visiting.

We discovered Beck's cafe at the Reading Terminal Market last weekend. Boy am I glad we did. My wife's family is from Louisiana and Mississippi, and have a long history of cooking cajun cuisine. We had the muffaletta sandwich and shrimp/crawfish etouffee. The etouffee was amazing and as good as what my father-in-law makes. The chef was knowledgeable about the holy trinity (celery, bell pepper, onion) and gave us a good tip on where to buy gumbo file powder. The bread pudding with pears and raisins was the best I've had since visiting New Orleans. We will have to go back for there breakfast beignets. They also have alligator on a stick!

Dan is awesome! Although he wasn't able to fix my problem (the part I damaged cost more than buying a whole new computer), he was great to work with and he let me bring my computer over right away. I would definitely go back to him with any computer issues!

LOVED Terakawa ramen. We noticed the huge line outside Terakawa for dinner and we knew we had to eat there. We were given a 15-20 minute wait time, so we walked around Chinatown and came back in the 15 minute time frame. We were called in and seated right away at the ramen bar. I ordered the spicy ramen, which is a spicy seafood broth with kimchi and my SO ordered the signature Terakawa ramen. He really enjoyed his, as did I. We also ordered the tempura shumai to share. It's not like your traditional Chinese shumai, but nonetheless it was delicious and the sauce that it was drenched in was tasty. Terakawa is cheaply priced and delicious. For two of us we ordered two ramen bowls and shared an appetizer for less than $30!

Cozy ambiance, complimentary cup of hot tea, amazing sushi. The sushi/sashimi is high quality and super fresh, which is why it is a bit pricey but it's worth it. Honestly probably some of the best sushi I've had in the city. Haven't done the omakase yet but very excited to try it at some point because I'm sure it will be spectacular. I've only tried one of their ramen dishes (I think they've added a couple more varieties) and it was delicious - the toppings are fresh and generous.

I came expecting a good scare, and I wasn't disappointed. I don't want to spoil the experience for anyone, but the first portion was by far the scariest for me. There was a hallway with actors lining either side dressed in hoods and it was the creepiest experience walking through. I'm not doing it justice by explaining it... just go and find out.

Tips:
Get there early to avoid an insane line.
Wear a glow necklace for extra scares- if you're adventurous.
Make sure to grab a $5 off coupon after the tour (good for daytime admission).
Great experience with Loonstyn. They were responsive, answered all my questions and were fast in scheduling me. And they sent before and after pics of the roof without me asking.
I have been a loyal customer for over 2 years but will never go there again cause I was treated unfairly !  All I was doing was cheering for my respected team

The place where franklin's stands now, has been playing russian roulette as a lot of earlier tenants have come and gone, but i hope franklin's is here to stay ! It's quite a laid back neighborhood  place with bunch of screens where u can even catch ur favorite teams play.

I don't know how long the deal is gonna last , but franklin's has the best happy hour deals on this side of the Schuylkill ! A lot of juicy 0.25 cent wings we gorged upon ferociously whilst trying to pay attention to the bingo numbers being called out in the background , on once a Wednesday. 

With a tonne of seating and an uber friendly staff  that goes along with a solid beer choices and a bunch of well drinks on the cheap , Franklin's certainly makes for one helluva night out in the burbs of east falls ...

I am a vegetarian looking for a great place to celebrate a graduation -if you want a place to rush you through a dinner service, this is the place to go. Honestly, the service is absolutely terrible. I have never, I mean NEVER had a server tell me that I need to order faster because there is another reservation after me at 7pm, (which we had a reservation at 5:00pm and we ordered at 5:30pm). If you want to spend quality time with loved ones and and enjoy a nice meal, DO NOT GO TO VEDGE! They will rush you out the door to make an extra few bucks. Please, go to Veltri for a quality, great service veggie menu. I went to Vedge for my brother's graduation dinner, and I could not be more disgusted. I went to Veltri for my graduation, and I was over the moon with the food and the service. I am TOTALY disgusted with this restaurant and how they treated us. I WILL NEVER GO HERE AGAIN!!!!!!!!!!!! As a vegetarian, I got better service at Morton's steakhouse than I did at Vedge.
Fresh and tasty bagels.  Try the Veggie Sammy - one of the better sandwiches I have had in a while.
Love this place. Never a wait. It's not too fancy, not too shabby, not too expensive, not too cheap. Everything is just right and exactly what you are expecting. Perfect casual meal
Friendly neighborhood business. Always efficient and service with a smile. I print stuff here often and drop boxes.

Great staff and service. This is my first pedicure experience and they really did a good job making it enjoyable. They serve you wine while they take care you which is extremely relaxing. Definitely recommended.

I was really looking forward to trying this place out since it is so close to my house and the reviews were pretty decent. I've been eating pho for many years but I am not picky by any means. I'm not vegetarian or vegan but I wanted a pho with a lot of veggies so I asked the waiter if they had that and he said yes. They brought me a bowl of noodles with ICEBERG LETTUCE. The lettuce was the only vegetable. The broth was very bland and the soup was missing the normal scallion/onion/cilantro mix. My boyfriend got a flank pho which was also very mediocre. I asked them for more vegetables and they brought me a small plate of greens. I was so disappointed I felt like crying. I didn't complain anymore but I also didn't have another bite of the soup. I paid the bill and left a 20% tip but I will definitely never go back.

I have to to agree with Mindy's review below- My experience was pretty much dead on with hers. I went Friday for Restaurant week lunch. I loved the decor and ambiance. The barternder was very entertaining as our group of 8 waiting at the bar for our table. We started with pitchers of the Lemonana (can't remember the exact name)- Typically made with bourbon, half of us opted to have it made with Rum instead which was much better- was like a mojito/lemonade- I'm not a fan of bourbon so I am biased- the others seemed to like it. I was also very impressed with the fresh ingredients that were used for most of their original concoctions

The hummus lived up to it's hype- It was my favorite part of the meal. In addition to that I had the sweet potato starter and the branzino for my entree. Both were good but didn't have me jumping out of my seat.  The portions were very small- satisifying enough for me since there were 4 courses and we only paid $20- but If I was getting that for the regular prices, I don't know if I'd be that satisfied.

Overall we had a nice experience at Zahav- it won't be added to the top of my list but I could definitely go back if with a friend or even for some hummus and drinks at the bar

Amazing amazing amazinggggggggg fooooood. IM NOT EVEN EXAGERATING. THIS PLACE IS SO COMFY/COZY. You guys should try the potatoes with mushrooms , the fresh salad, and the Branzino!!!! The waitresses are always checking to see if you need anything and take care of you very well. Another thing is that the place has an indoor and outdoor seating. In the winter time, there's a portable heater that makes you feel so comfortable and super warm outside. Once again, Love love love this restaurant.

Have not tried the ramen here, but the true winner in my mind is the snow ice. My friend and I have gotten snow ice at other ice cream spots in Ctown and after trying the snow ice here, determined they were "garbage!!". We got matcha and taro to share, and I've heard they also have mango, though it was not available when we went. Matcha came with fried mochi, red bean, grass jelly, and strawberries, while taro came with the same but with crushed oreos instead of strawberries. Texture was light, flavor was good, do yourself a favor and come here often!

Their broths are so good! It was a fun albeit pricey experience, but would go again. The service was also amazing :)

Would recommend the quail eggs and anything of their wagyu beef!!

Quality of food was sub-par for price. Avocado sundae was terrible & inedible. Deviled eggs were tasteless. High table chair was basically like sitting on two wooden planks. Only good thing ordered was the salmon. Service was decent. Just so many better places to choose from to eat at this price scale.

Accident can be either good or bad. This one was actually a good accident. I meant to go to Nan Zhou noodle house. When you first walk in. You are greeted by one of the waiting staff. You get seated, you get a menu, and you get water of course. I ordered dumplings and the house special noodle soup. Theres really no presentation to playing dumplings so it looked standard. When I first ate the dumplings, it tasted pretty good. You can tell that this is freshly made dumplings. By the way it was made and tasted. The sauce was great too. The noodles itself was pretty good. Had a nice bite to it. Like med rare steak. Just right. I'm not an expert on their broth but I felt like it was missing that "wow factor". The meat was very tender and had a nice fat content. The service was pretty standard for an Asian restaurant. They take your order, they bring your food, and fuck off. Which I like. Overall rating 4/5 stars.
Overall great experience! Carlos is very responsive, his cleaners did a great job deep cleaning my home, they were prompt and friendly. I've already recommended their cleaning services to friends.

I really wanted to like this place but it was so empty that it made it a little uncomfortable walking through there.  I was the only customer walking around that joint.  I didn't get to choose my own produce - more like Italian Market style where you tell the guy what you want and how much.  He weighs it and you pay him.  Selection wasn't great.  They did have a good selection of bulk nuts, pastas and dried herbs.  Also has an amish baker (who also sells REALLY good dairy products).  The florist was empty.

All in all, I think it could have some great potential, but pretty disappointing for now.

This visit was good! Service was friendly and fast.  I asked for an entree that they didn't have enough of so they made me some in about 5 minutes and bought the food over to our table when it was done.  

There was plenty of seating, the area was very clean as well as the bathrooms.
food was ok, service was damn slow

Not so great. If I was Mr. Hilton, I wouldn't want my name on this place.
The location can't be beat, but the rooms feel like a hospital.

ZERO WASTERS, BEWARE! 
The food here is great, but they bring you a water with a straw without asking.

My experience was poor. I went to this dealer because they had decent prices and reviews. I went to pick out a car with my fiance. However while entering her information into the computer, the salesman makes a "joke" that her last name was the "worst he'd ever heard". He proceeded to say to her, "it is a good thing you're getting married" to get rid of the name.  

The basic expectation to be treated with decency by a mature adult was not met. The Faulkner salesman insulted my fiance and her family. 

No purchase.

Good food. We got the pulled pork, Mac n cheese bites, Mac n cheese stuffed burger, and ranch corn.  Everything was really really good.  They need to iron some things out still...they ran out of some food and air conditioner wasn't working.  The shopping center looks a little sketchy, but usually you find the best places in those!  I would suggest order and pick up your food. Overall food was great and I would totally go back!

Good food, though I felt like the portions were smaller than I was expecting for the price. I'm surprised that other Yelper's rated this two dollar signs. I also wish they'd had gluten free soy sauce. Other than that, there is really nothing to complain about and I still was able to enjoy my meal.

I was so excited to run into Christopher at the atm (thank you Philly and random run ins!!) and find out at which salon he was working now! He did an amazing job as always and I'm so grateful to him for restoring my super curly hair to a non-crazy state.

The salon is a really beautiful, open and airy, minimal space on the third floor with a view of the shot tower out of the window. It was such a pleasant, chill experience, and the prices are great.
A rare gem of quality and kindness in Philly. Owners are interested in repeat business, treat everyone with respect, and ensure quality every time.

This is an airport. Planes fly out and planes fly in. Sometimes you can get into the airport with a ticket purchased by you or a loved one or your work in order to get onto one of the airplanes. Airplanes take you places where cars would take too long. 

You have one job, Airport. And you did it. Good Job Airport.

I don't know what I was expecting. I guess a 4 star restaurant based on the ratings. However, what I found was a hole in the wall pizza place with staff less than eager to be working their jobs. Little enthusiasm is noted by restaurant goers. The food was just alright - and that's why I don't understand the overall rating. I ordered the pulled chicken salad and buff chicken pizza. Glad I ordered a small and a slice because they were only fair. I usually never doubt restaurants with a four star rating on yelp - especially when they have so many reviews - but this place would make me question that in the future.

They were awesome at first when they opened, The food, atmosphere was perfect, especially Sunday brunch. You didn't mind waiting because you know that you were going to enjoy your food and the atmosphere. THEN THEY FELL OFF. After my first bad experience it wasn't an big deal. Then I went back numerous times, each and every time I went after my bad experience  it became worse and worse . I don't know if they changed the management and cook however the food became nasty and burnt or their stuffed French toast with cream cheese was horrid it came out like slop, I asked for over medium eggs they came out cold and well done. I really wanted to love this place.. kinda sad about it.

One of the best places in philly-a hidden gem for sure! We have been coming here for 4-5 years and every time we eat here it is delicious. The owner recognizes us when we walk in and we only dine in about 3-4 times per year. Everything is fresh and their specials are always excellent. Overall, I highly recommend Giorgio's on pine!
Extra for being BYOB!

Superb pasta dishes, and a solid craft beer list. The portion sizes are exactly what you would anticipate for a restaurant of this quality, although if you are extremely hungry, I recommend ordering an appetizer as well.

I haven't been there for dinner yet, but after my first brunch visit I'm eager to go. The bread, which they obviously put a lot of stock in, is excellent - soft and airy on the inside, but grilled to give it a nice outer crunch. The open-faced braised pork & gouda sandwich with eggs was hearty and satisfying, and they had some thoughtful specials that really expanded the normal menu (the buttercup squash one was well done). The pickled salad did get me on a couple of bites, though  - I enjoyed it for the most part but the vinegar was occasionally overwhelming. That aside, I wasn't complaining about the bottomless french press, and the space and presentation likewise get thumbs up.

When I think of Center City happy hour - especially in the warmer months of the year - I think of sipping cocktails, champagne, and bitter beers with friends at high tops near open windows, while slugging back plates of oysters, here on Sansom Street. 

Happy hours were always bustling and busy. Never a dull night, it was always good to a bit early. The oyster crackers were somehow always a highlight for me, filling an oversized goblet on the table, and refilled as often as you ask them to (I always did.)

Because it was busy, service could often be slow, but they are definitely training their servers pretty well; I never recall being left underserved, though it's best to arrive with a laid-back attitude and schedule following, because it might take a while to get seated and get your drinks & snacks. 

The food here is fresh and high-quality, and when it comes to seafood, that is exactly what you want!

Food was good, but service was really bad. It took about 15 minutes just to get a drink menu after being seated, and we saw others seated by the host but then walk because no one stopped at their table to take their order. At the end, we waited for a check for so long to the point that we had to check with management-- our waiter's shift must have ended and they never brought a check to us prior! All in all, great space and menu, but they have a FOH problem.

I wish I could give this boutique a 10 star review. Being only 30, recently diagnosed with breast cancer, losing my hair and getting married in less then 7 months I was beyond stressed trying to find the perfect wig that felt like me. Merissa is an angel! She was so accommodating and made me feel so comfortable! Ontop of this the owner Jamie is truly so kind and helps anyway she can. I'm so thankful I found this place. I highly recommend this shop to any woman experiencing hair loss of any kind!

Stopped by late last night for a steak sandwich and I must say I really enjoyed every bite. Bread was super soft which made my sub even more enjoyable! Only thing I did not like was having to pay an atm to take cash out since they don't accept cards.... that is my reason for the 3 star rating. Overall service was fast! Deff recommend BUT don't forget the cash.

As someone who is originally from England I Approve of this restaurant! I have been here for happy hour, lunch, cocktails, and dinner and every experience has been pretty decent. 

Their happy hour is extremely cheap..if you can get a seat at the bar. I LOVE their curry deviled eggs and their Welsh rarebit is alright but both great for their happy hour price. 

For lunch I have had their roast beef sandwich which was phenomenal, although I think almost 15 for a sandwich is a little ridiculous. The person I was with got the bangers and mash-they were decent. With this meal I got the cask flight which was a great way to try all their beers they have to offer. 

Dinner was a little lack luster. To start we got the English cheese board which was paired well with its different crackers and jams. I got the pork belly which was a huge portion but I think they could of put more black pudding on it (the reason why I chose the dish). 

The ambience is what I love here, a great place to cozy up in the wintertime. My only complaint is that each time I have had a cocktail from here they haven't been good at all.

I went here for beer fest.  There was a great selection of beers, no lines, and having the indoor and outdoor option was nice.  It was also cool that you could get the beers that were on draft.  Not cool was starting to run out of beer with 1.5 hours to go.  The food was okay, although there was a line for that, and it also ran out early.

I love Red Sage!  Been going there for over a year and I'm always pleased with the service I receive.  The space is open and pretty with hair styling in the first 2 rooms then manis and pedis in the back near the shampoo stations.  Upstairs you can receive body treatments and facials.  I highly recommend Red Sage for anyone looking for a great salon in the Northeast.

"Inoffensive Mexican food" is the perfect way to describe it.

It's fine in a pinch, inexpensive and generous portions. Salsa is a bit salty. Nothing is very spicy. But still, not terrible. It might even be worthy of 3 stars if you're drunk.
Are you looking for a one-stop-shop that's packed nuts to butts?  Well, you found the place. Avoid between 11 AM-1 PM or you'll feel like cattle trying to traverse the place.

Went for dinner and a drink. It was very crowded due to the Flyers game at 7pm. Noisy with chatter and loud music. Plenty of tvs to watch various sporting events. 
Very basic food menu. The pretzels are amazing tho. $15 for the Pizza Pretzel sounds a lot but it was HUGE and smothered in cheese pepperoni and tomato sauce. Delicious. Very average sports bar with few menu options and specialty drinks. 
Cafeteria style shared seating. Friendly staff was a plus. Our pretzel took too long to arrive so manager came over and apologized and gave it to us for free.
Great music venue. Food is fantastic and cheap! They don't have any bottled beer which is not good.

I've only been to this location once (I've been to the Baltimore location a few times), but as a general note, Green Line has good coffee and AMAZING muffins. Actually, I've only gotten the apple cinnamon muffins here, so I can't really say I've had a wide sampling of the food they have, but these muffins are probably one of the best I've had. The perfect blend of crunchy, soft, sweet, and tart. Need to eventually try the other items on the menu, but I just can't stop myself from getting what I know is good here.

As for the location itself, I really like the atmosphere here. There's a lot of space, but Green Line still manages to stay cozy. There are outlets in one of the corners, as well as some on the floor, so if you need your laptop to get some studying done, you'll have no problem doing it here. Surprisingly, this place is less crowded than I'd expect it to be (which is a plus for me). Free Wifi (just ask for the password) and no credit minimum!

Nice little spot to grab some coffee or get some studying done. Even though it's pretty close to Penn's campus, it's not swarming with Penn students (which can be nice sometimes).

If your looking for yoga pre·tense or better than you Instructors...this is not the place. I have been here a month and all of my class I stir goes have been wondderful. Sarah and Bree are amazing...gentle souls that are honestly concerned with my practice, beginners that I am
 I feel co portable I  any class. The best#sarah lockers rooms EVER. Clean spacious and real showers...mouthwash and q-tips...details mean a lot. They care.  #Bree #Sarah

The best Kilbesa in the city!!! I learned of this late in my life, and I won't eat any other Kilbesa. Besides the Kilbesa they have many other sausages to select from. Also a large variety of hand made fresh perogies, also the best I ever had. The man running the joint is the happiest person I ever met in a butcher shop. Family business that makes you feel like family when you're there.

The couch tomato cafe is wonderful. I especially enjoy the house crafted salads or wraps! You can either create your own or pick from the various varieties they have created! It also has a great outside seating area where you can enjoy your food in the good weather! I would definitely recommend

And so, after much time has passed, I bring you all an updated review.

My glowing review has stuck out like a sore thumb for long enough. 3 years ago when I wrote that review, I felt Alden Park deserved 4 stars. 

Management and ownership changed about a year ago now. In December of last year, I needed to make an easy move, so I moved into another building at Alden Park. If it was as bad as it is now, I probably would have tried harder to find a place elsewhere. Renovations are taking place, though I have not seen any beneficial changes to date.

Lets start with the pool. It was closed for four months, and now it is open after renovations, or in other words, a bunch of suave outdoor couches now flank the pool. It used to be a fairly relaxing time there. There's been big parties there every weekend - even on Sunday afternoon - groups of people drinking and playing loud music. Part of the renovation plans include an outdoor pool by the Cambridge building.

While the building was undergoing work to the exterior, my car was showered with stones and dust from the building. If you value your car, you will still feel some anxiety parking here, even though it is not on the street.

When I moved to the Cambridge building, I couldn't properly make coffee. I have lived in Philadelphia for 9 years and I have never had this issue. I tried everything until I realized it was the water! I bought a ProPur filtration system and my coffee now tastes perfect. I didn't have this problem when I lived in Kenilworth. Another note about the water: it is shut off pretty often in my building. Sometimes when I work from home, it gets shut off without notice, so I run out of water to drink. 

The management/leasing/front office is terribly disorganized, but this can be a plus or a minus. 

I've likely forgot a few things, but I'll update this review when I remember.

The only good things about this place: 
- maintenance staff (not the concierge, not the office staff) are courteous and helpful
- it is an interesting and historic place to live
- proximity to Wissahickon trails
- proximity to SEPTA regional rail

Alden Park is a beautiful and storied property, and current management does not value it, and soon, I worry that its tenants will follow suit. 

If my satisfaction with living here were put into a graph, it would shoot up my first year, plateau from 2012 to mid 2015, and then steadily decline to where I am now. I won't be renewing my lease.

Probably the best fish tacos (grilled sea bass, butternut squash, fried jalapeño peppers on corn tortilla) I have ever tasted. Blood orange margaritas also rank among the best I have ever had. Chips, salsa and guacamole were all good...not great. Cool vibe. Solid service.

It was a cute ambience for black millennials but the service could have been better. I had chicken and waffles and that was really good but they messed up our order the first time. I would come back on my own but not with a large group of people. We literally waited for a 1/2 hour for a server.
Update: I just swung by to try to grab a 6 pack. They are closed. It's 5:30 on a Friday evening. How are these people even in business?

I've been to one shot a few times, and I have mixed feelings. I really believe that I should be able to order a cup of coffee for less than two dollars. I understand that they offer Stumptown, but the prices for drinks here still seem high. That being said, they do brew some delicious coffee, and I'd rather overpay for something I like then get a good deal on something that barely passes as coffee.

One Shot recently moved to a new location on George Street , and that whole place is beautiful. I met with my good friend Bryana and we sat outside and had coffee and breakfast. Charming 1940s music played inside and out, although Bryana told me this wasn't common. Whether inside or out, the atmosphere is great. Some people send hours in a coffee shop working on papers or other work, and one shot is the kind of place where you will almost enjoy yourself while typing away.

The food menu here is small and hit or miss, but there are a few items that really shine. Bryana ordered the bagel topped with creme cheese, crushed nuts, and drizzled with honey. One look brings on a doling mouth and a bit of envy. I ordered a weird sandwich: bacon egg and mac-n-cheese. This sounds odd, but makes the perfect meal, whether breakfast, lunch or dinner.
