I used to love this place years ago when they gave you fuller portions and the staff was energetic and fun with the customers.

Just popped in here on my way home from work whilst craving mozzarella sticks (lol)... did a quick yelp search and discovered it was the nearest spot. I was pleasantly surprised. It gets 5 stars for its genre - corner store/deli. This tiny spot has a little bit of everything. Deli sandwiches, paninis, a grill, fried food, a seating area, some fresh produce, etc. Most spots of its kind do not do any hot food so this sets them apart. Staff are also cheery and competent.

Similar to Dalessandro's, this joint runs a tight operation where the line goes so fast, I went in and came out with a sandwich and not sure what happened in-between. Staff was very nice though is what I remember.

I got the classic beef with some sweet peppers. Not sure why they're sweet because they didn't taste like anything. Would have been better with banana peppers or pickles.

Lots of beef. It was spilling everywhere and the bread was very white and very fluffy. A little bit bland so I added my own ingredients like pickles and spinach and onions. Very filling and just as long. It was like a solid foot of pure carbohydrates and protein (sprinkled with a heart attack and high cholesterol yayyyy).

I also learned later that the line was to-go and you can actually sit down like a human being. Very strange concept for a Californian.

Absolutely horrible. Prepare for understaffed delays, and further incompetent service. I was afraid to complain bc they seemed like the type to spit in my food and it would have accomplished nothing. Great fries.

Great customer service and wide selection of hardware. I was looking for a new set of locks for my house, and the guy behind the register was extremely helpful. He directed me to what I needed, and he didn't seem to mind answering all my questions. I have no idea what I'm doing when it comes to fixing things, but I feel like I can come here and get all my questions answered. I'll definitely be back!

This place is a must when you go to the terminal market. 

You wait in a long line but it's worth the wait. I really don't intend to overly hype this place up. Because I know when I expect food to be the best thing I have ever tasted in my life or when I expect food to take me from earth and to the moon and back based on raving reviews, I get disappointed. So I will say this, the donuts are probably one of the best you will get in your lifetime... Not to hype you up. 

When you get a hot and fresh one right out of the fryer, you will die and go to heaven and back to the reality of your donut. My favorite is the apple fritter. When I say my favorite, i mean I can eat their Apple fritter for the rest of my life. 

My least favorite is the key lime donut. I would not recommend that flavor. But hey, for less than a dollar a donut, I think it's worth eating every flavor at least once. 

Now the lines are extremely long. So be prepared to wait in a very very long line. 

On a final note, their donuts are very moist and spongy. What I mean is the donut has a bouncy texture. So, these donuts are still pretty decent after a whole day of laying in their box.
This place is great. They fixed my iPhone and iPad right away!

Highly recommend these folks! We got locked out of one of our bathrooms accidentally and they literally came over within 20 mins! So friendly, so professional and very easy process overall. They are so wonderfully kind and helpful! Thanks again!!

Jake from Bredenbeck's was a pleasure to work with. The bakery was able to ensure almond extract was not part of any of our wedding spread to accommodate a family member's allergy. Moreover, they were able to accommodate a gluten allergy with some special items that our GF guests loved. Wedding guests from all over the country are still raving months later about the outstanding quality of the pies, cakes, cookies and other treats. Our only mistake was not sending everyone home with a care package from the bakery. They were simply the best.

Was just there for the first time, the egg rolls are transcendent, the buffalo chicken cheese fries were the best I've ever had, can't wait to go back, if you're in the area check it out, you'll be glad you did

I dropped in here looking for a quick last meal in Philadelphia and wow was I impressed :). It looks a little bit fancy, but we went in casual with no problem.  We were a little bit offput at first - we couldn't sit outside, we got a squished up table, they only had pepsi products, and they wouldn't let us make any changes to any of the signature pizzas... We also were really looking for a nice side salad to have with lunch, but they didn't have any side salads so we had to split a "small" Romano (Caesar) salad off of the dinner menu (the lunch menu was a little limiting for my taste so we got our pizza off the dinner menu too - but there wasn't that much of a price difference anyway). Luckily we really enjoyed the salad but it was plenty enough for a minimum of three people as a starter salad. It was nice and light, and had delicious thick slices of Parmesan on it. We decided to make our own pizza and chose a wheat crust with pepperoni (every personalized pizza comes with basil, cheese, and tomato sauce).  I swear this was the best pizza I have ever had! Perfectly cooked delicious thin crust - and it wasn't too wheat-flavored so it actually added to the pizza instead of distracting from it. I also wish we could have tried the pasta too - every plate that went by us looked scrumptious! I honestly can't recommend this place enough - wish we had one in San Antonio!

Interesting name combination, which means it is a place really for Chinese food and with sushi as an afterthought? Anyway ordered their Shanghainese soup dumpling that everyone raved about, Sichuan spicy fish filet, Cantonese-style beef with tofu casserole, and Taiwanese beef soup noodle. That covered four types of Chinese regional cuisines and have to say the first three are all above average.

I must be living under some kind of stone or something because I had never heard of Yelp until just recently...

I came across Snip and fell in love with the atmosphere, the owner Sean,  manager Sharon and the stylists that are employed here.  Erin was suggested to me by Sharon and I fell in love.  She gives a fabulous hair cut and color.  Thank you so much Sharon for referring me to Erin.  I love my new color and style

This place is literally a hole in a wall. Walk downstairs to the underground Asia Supermarket, past the display of pig heads on ice, past the freshly gutted fish, past the mountains of Asian snack foods, and you will find a delicious smell. Follow it. As soon as you sit down, order the salt baked chicken wings (they can take a while, and they burst at the seams with MSG, but it is so freakin' worth it). You can order the rest later. You're probably safe ordering just about anything else off the menu. The rice dishes and noodle soups are all solid, and it's hard to find food of this caliber for dirt cheap.

I typically have a rule of thumb for Chinese restaurants like these. The quality and authenticity of the food is directly proportional to how dirty the bathroom is. In this case, that property still holds.

This place is easily one of the wonder's of the east coast.

From food to goods, to skin and hair care, the market yields natural care inside and out of all kinds. My personal favorite of the market, is food of course, but the legendary roast pork sandwich, to the rib sandwich I swear my father and law drove down for. We even had our fair share of garlic bacon we had the opportunity to taste many times before walking away with a whole pound of it and an amish butter.

This place overall is amazing, and we need more places like it EVERYWHERE!

An all around wonderful dining experience.  Lively atmosphere, outstanding service and some of the best food in Philadelphia.

The menu is large, and we didn't know how to narrow it down to a reasonable amount of food, so we opted for the tasting menu.  $55 per person isn't bad for 15+ plates of food.  

Must try: Lamb meatballs and flourless chocolate cake.

Picked up the shuttle from the terminal and headed over to the Alamo rental center. Good service and quick stop at the counter before the gentleman led me outside to pick my car. I got a 2016 Chevy Malibu. Clean car and it had Apple CarPlay which was pretty cool! That was fun to play around with in traffic. The car did this weird thing if you were stopped for too long at a light. When you pressed on the gas, it sounded like the car was starting again and hiccuped a little before going.

When I returned the car, I filled it up at a gas station about 8 miles from the airport. The guy checking me in said I didn't fill the car all the way (the line WAS on full) but checked the car in as full anyway. That was a little annoying.

owned by the same people as mercato, valanni seems to try a little too hard.  they have some very good drinks and a lot of interesting items on the menu.  i would have to say that one of my favorites is a basic app, the trio of spreads...the smooth feta spread with grilled pita is outstanding.  i cant say that i have ever disliked anything that i have had their they are just victims of their own success found across the street at mercato.

My husband and I came here for breakfast on a Monday morning while visiting Philadelphia. We were immediately greeted by the hostesses and were seated right away. We opted to sit inside. There is limited seating inside and outside. The atmosphere is welcoming and there is local art on the wall available for purchase. 

We ordered iced coffee and freshly squeezed lemonade which was good.  For breakfast we shared an avocado on toast which was delicious! The bread was perfectly toasted and the avocado was very flavorful. My husband ordered the biscuits and gravy with a side of eggs with cheese. He raved over the flavors. I ordered the breakfast burrito which was delicious! There were so many delicious options and their specials looked amazing. Overall, we would come back again and try something else!

Sonny's slogan is "Philadelphia, the city of brotherly and cheesesteaks" appropriately. The hubby and I love cheesesteaks, so when we found ourselves a mere 15 minutes outside of Philly on a Saturday morning, we knew we had to drive in for lunch.
After a quick search of Yelp, we decided on Sonny's. 

Totally. Worth. It.

The cons: apparently there is a perpetual line and only ten or so tables in the entire place.
The pros: absolutely every thing else.
The food was delicious and the portions were huge. (We could have easily shared one sandwich and fries). You can pick your meat, pick your cheese and pick your toppings. The hubby got a beef cheesesteak with lettuce and fries; I got a chicken deluxe cheesesteak with fries. They have Coke products (score one for this southern belle!). Despite the long and never ending line, they were very friendly and no one behind the counter seemed stressed. The food came out quickly and I scored a table before my food was even ready.

Pro tip #1: Pay cash for a 5% discount
Pro tip #2: Apple Maps will NOT get you here correctly. Use google maps instead!
Pro tip #3: Sonny's is a mere steps from the Liberty Bell, Liberty Hall and the Ben Franklin Museum, so why not have a little history with your lunch?

My boyfriend took me to Talula's Garden to celebrate my birthday. It was absolutely enchanting. The ambiance was just perfect and the staff was amazing. Our waitress took her time with us because we were out of towners seeing the menu for the first time. The wait time for the food was almost none. It was very quick and absolutely amazing. You don't pick your sides with your entree and i think it's for the best. The chef knows best and did wonderful.

We were able to get a reservation last minute which was great! The food and service was okay - nothing to complain about, but definitely did not meet expectations for Philadelphia Italian dining. Pasta was very salty and the portions were surprisingly small. Chicken was unseasoned. However, the dinner specials were excellent.

Service was rushed - seemed to be understaffed but waiters were overall friendly and provided great service.

By far my favorite thai food in the city!! 
Their curries are to diiiiieeee for!
They're packed with flavor and pure coconut milk, none of that diluted, thinned out, lack-luster curry you get at most other thai restaurants in the city.
Listed from my favorite down: PUMPKIN CURRY (salivating as i think about it), green curry (two words: thai eggplant) and the panang (but don't love the veggies in this one).
I have multiple fans of the Curry Duck in my life as well, though can't attest to that one.
Another favorite: vegetable dumplings, they are stuffed to the brim with leeks and wrapped in this thin layer of indescribable, semi-gooey in the most delicious way, soft dough : more leek, less dough, that's how I like it! I could go on about the papaya salad too but I'm getting way too hungry (as I wait for my bi-weekly delivery). The only thing I wouldn't give 5 stars are the noodle dishes,  but that's b/c the take-out factor always ruins thai noodle dishes...I'd give the pad see ew noodles 4 stars and they're my favorite. Word to the spice addicts: order anything/everything 'thai hot' and they will deliver....perfectly extra hot without diminishing the other flavors!
Rose did an awesome job on my nails and eyebrows!!! Found my new nail technician!!! She's a perfectionist therefore my nails came out perfect!!!

Decent selection of pet supplies however the staff here leave a lot to be desired. First an employee was throwing boxes with live fish in them on the ground aggressively. This is totally inappropriate conduct for any pet store. Lastly an employee made an attempt to sell me and my friends illegal drugs. This store has a few employees that are a disgrace to the brand.

I have always loved Wawa. My parents used to send me there for milk, because apparently, Wawa has the best milk. Now, until I started frequenting this particular location, I lumped all Wawas together, generically, considering all Wawas are created equal. This is just not true.

I HATE THIS WAWA. It takes forever for the ladies behind the counter to make a sandwich, but I know a lot about their personal lives, who their brother is dating and what they did the night before. I don't really care about their gossip, I care about my sandwich! Also, they are always out of something, but don't tell you until after you order your food. I might not have gotten a salad had I known there would be no shredded cheese or tomatoes!

I think I have mentioned pickles in a couple of my reviews. I love pickles. I could eat pickles for days. So I always ask for pickles on the side, and almost always get an attitude when I check to make sure they've included them. Eye rolls? They're just pickles, but they're important to me!

Even when the line is long, there's never much of a wait, but usually the shortis are sloppy, the staff is rude and the store is understocked. There are many other better places to go in the neighborhood. I really dread going to this Wawa.

And I guess this is true of many Center City businesses, but there is always someone outside begging for money or asking me to buy them something inside. I hate that crap.
Wasn't near what I expected. Everything from beer list, food, and service was all poor. It seems the regulars are treated much better. Traveled pretty far to be this disappointed!

My two pizza friends and I walked to Pizza Brain on a sunny pizza afternoon to get some pizza pizza (ew, definitely not Little Caesars). Our pizza feet were tired but we eventually walked into the cute pizza place and ordered pizza slices of pizza. They don't have many pizza options for pizza by the slice, but the ones that they do have do pizza alright. 

I got the pizza slice involving pizza pepperoni, and pizza basil. This more than filled up my pizza stomach, as the pizza slice was pizza huge. The pizza flavor was pizza incredible, and even though my pizza body couldn't take another pizza bite, I still did and loved every pizza minute of it. Outdoor seating for all you pizza al fresco pizza diners. Ethan is awesome at being pizza personable and pizza knowledge.

The six cackling women, millennials of course, celebrating one's birthday, sitting next to us, and the big mouth couples to the left who talk louder to get their point across, because as we all know, the louder you speak the more validity to your conversation, couldn't bother me enough to disturb or disrupt my dinner at this solid 4.25 star Italian BYOB in South Philly.

Service was fantastic, I felt bad I had to ask our server to repeat almost everything she said because of the noise level.  But as I once said, if you want quiet stay home.  

For appetizer I started with the Burrata, prosciutto, and roasted peppers.  The burrata was soooo smooth and creamy, everything else a perfect accompaniment.  5 stars.  I tasted the Seafood Stufato my dining partner (for what used to be date night) ordered, the arrabbiata sauce (tomato base with 10 times more flavor) was to die for.  In addition, the calamari was so tender. 5 stars

Entrees were very good, but lacked pizzazz, excitement, punch, something to bring them up to 5 star level.  The pan seared branzino special was a perfect piece of tender, fresh white fish over a bed of empty tasting squid ink risotto.  The squid ink adds color, but does nothing for flavor in my opinion.  My main dish was the lamb shank, a so melt-in-your-mouth as you ever had, fall-off-the-bone tender lamb was again surrounded by not-full-flavored mashed with a few asparagus spears buried in the bed of potato. 4 stars for both

My friend had the apple cake.  Fabulous and back to 5 stars.  The gelato was still too frozen, not creamy and nothing special, the lowest rated dish I had all evening.  I've had better almost anywhere. 2 stars.

I am looking forward to having Burrata as another go-to in South Philadelphia.
This is without a doubt, the worst, most disgusting hotel I have ever been to. I should have trusted my gut and ate the cost of the prepaid room, and went to a different place.
Nick came out within hours after we called (non-emergency) and re-keyed 4 locks for $90. I highly recommend this company.

Unfortunately I couldn't complete the 4-day cleanse I paid more than I could afford because they expired 1/2 way through.  I knew they tasted a little off, but all the bottles began to expand except cayenne/lemon and cashew milk.  

I drink vegetable juice often and these tasted way off by day two.  

Too bad.  Lemon/cayenne was fine though.

I have stayed here several times when it was the Holiday Inn.  We recently stayed there 12/25/13, in a renovated room. Nice firm bed and extra points for the full sized pillows! (I hate those mini pillows that so many hotels are using!). The new room was very comfortable, when I stay there again in April, I am looking forward to the renovated lobby. The location is brilliant, walking distance to both the restaurants in Old City and Chinatown.  I always recommend this hotel to people who are traveling to Philly.

I've had lunch once and brunch twice. Always amazing and fresh authentic food. Mimosas are a must as well. Would recommend to anyone looking for Cuban food. Also very helpful staff if you don't know what you're doing.

Double Knot is not only beautiful but the food is delicious! I enjoyed the creative menu, variety, and plate sizes. A great mix of hot and cold dishes, meats, veggies, rolls, and sushi/sashimi. My favorite was the Duck Scrapple Bao Bun! I did drop one star due to the lackluster cocktails. Our group each ordered something different from the menu and nothing had wow factor, the Eldridge Tie and Re-Animator Tie tasted almost identical (tasted like watered down orange juice) and the only decent one was the Singapore Slip (very fresh and tropical). We opted for a delicious bottle of Grenache Blanc for the remainder of our meal. The service was pleasant. The downstairs area is beautifully decorated! I felt like I was in Singapore again. Great place for a group of friends or a date.

Bad restaurant- both food and customer service quality wise. There was a hair in the food and when this was communicated to the manager, he said you can take it out as it's on the outer side of the food but if you are not comfortable then I can prepare the dish again. Obviously why will someone be ok with eating food with someone else's hairs. And once it got ready, he didn't even come to the table to serve it, instead asked us to come and pick it, and this restaurant is not self service. The quality of food is also not good, not that flavorful for an indian restaurant. Neither I will go there nor will suggest anyone.

If I ask any of my Israeli or Middle Eastern friends, they'll say Zahav is "aight" and that Suraya brunch is a lot more affordable and true to their culture. Better watch out Zahav! I had to of course, check out Zahav so I could cross it off my Philly restaurant list. Sadly, I don't think I'll be back.

I wanted to make my first experience at Zahav right so I opted for not just the regular tasting menu ($48/pp), but the chef's tasting menu ($65/pp) to try this lamb shoulder that people rave about. Note that I've previously not liked lamb shoulder because it reminds me too much of pulled pork, but with less flavor so I was expecting to be mind-blown because of "how great their lamb is". However, by the end of the night, I concluded -- I still don't like lamb shoulder.

The desserts and roasted cauliflower were the highlights of my night which was disappointing. I feel a little jipped to be honest because we had 3 mezze, 1 al ha'esh and 2 desserts but they were all from the menu (except the lamb shoulder). So the only benefit of chef's tasting menu is that you don't have to pick what you get but I was expecting being served things that we couldn't find on the menu..

It actually would've been cheaper to get what we got a la carte. Our bill for the chef's tasting menu for 2 people + $10 drink was $140 before tax/tip. If we had ordered the same thing a la carte..

Salatim ($16)
Turkish hummus ($9)
Fried Cauliflower ($10)
Grilled Duck Hearts ($10)
Haloumi in Brik ($10)
Lamb Shoulder (idk how much this costs)
2 Desserts ($18 @ $9 each)
Total: $73 without the lamb shoulder so you're telling me the lamb shoulder costs $57 ($130-73)? Maybe it actually does but for the small portion size (2 people share 1 bowl of lamb shoulder? seriously?) I wouldn't have paid more than $30-40 for it.

So overall what did I learn? Chef's tasting menu isn't that special. Stick to a la carte, stick to what you like and don't succumb to the hype. I think I would've appreciated this dinner a lot more had I was fully explained that the chef's tasting menu didn't stray from the menu too far and that they wouldn't stick out their necks for me even though I tried to convince them to do lamb chops instead of lamb shoulder for me.

The only good thing about this restaurant was its location in our hotel. Since it's the restaurant that fulfills the room service we expected the same menu but the kids menu in the room doesn't exist in the restaurant. The waiter dictated things we could have for kids including chicken fingers and Mac and cheese so that's what we got for each. The Mac and cheese was most likely from a box not surprisingly but one portion looked chunky and dry like it was left over and the other was very creamy. The chicken fingers were completely dried out and tough. My husband got the chipotle burger and what he got was a, not awful burger with just a generic barbecue sauce on it with absolutely no chipotle. When we asked for the desserts the room service menu said came with the kids meals the waiter told us they don't do that in the restaurant. Confirmed by his manager. My crab cake sandwich was fine and the drinks were good. We paid almost $100 for two drinks two kids meals a burger and a sandwich and most of it was disappointing or inedible. I would not recommend this place to anyone.

My husband and I live close to this pizzeria so we decide to order from here when we are in the mood for wings sandwiches pizza
But the people taking orders there are rude they never get the order correct it has happen more then 3 times then when you call and tell them about there mistake they quickly get aggravated and rude

Everything from the atmosphere, to the service, to the food (most important) was outstanding. Nina's really is the classic South Philly Italian BYOB experience. Wonderful place for a date. 

We had the chef's tasting menu and received two different appetizers (homestyle meatballs and a really nice marinated tomato salad), two pastas (a delicious penne bolognese and a gorgonzola gnocchi), and we split a tiramisu for dessert. 

All in all, I was thrilled with the experience and will be back again soon!

Went here with my roommate for a spur of the moment dinner...and was really nervous with all of the bad reviews. But I was definitely pleasantly surprised! Started out with the crab Mac and cheese (which was okay). My roommate got a steak that was cooked perfectly. I got the crispy salmon with a sesame ginger glaze and it was AWESOME! Been craving it ever since. My white whiskey lemonade was super good as well. Place was pretty empty but got fast, friendly service!!
Food is very delicious. Layout is very nice compare to Pho place in the area.  It's only a few block from my house so no need to drive down to South Philly.

Had dinner here the other night. After being so excited about finally having sushi in Fairmount, I remembered I was in Fairmount and that so many places are over-priced. 

On the up-side, it's BYO, nice atmosphere and great quality: we had the dumplings, eel roll, Philadelphia roll, Ping Pong roll (fancier spicy tuna roll), and Godzilla roll (eel, tempura shrimp, avocado, topped with strawberries and honey). The rolls had more fish than rice, were very fresh, and creative. The Godzilla was tasty but odd - it was pretty sweet and not my favorite but worth trying. The staff were polite and helpful.

On the down side, it was pretty pricey for small portions (especially the dumplings), as compared with other places in the city with great quality and reasonable prices. So overall, I'll probably go elsewhere.

I really enjoyed my stay at the Embassy Suites in Philly this weekend. It was like staying in an ecotarium with comfy beds, big TVs and free breakfast every morning!

Trust my review, because I've stayed in MANY ecotariums. Not really. (Or have I?) Moving on.

Big plusses included free parking ("F" you $40 a night parking at hotels. "F" you in the "A".), and FREE breakfast that was chock full of goodness far beyond continental.

Price? We got the room for $85 a night via Priceline.com. So, we were pleased.

Location? Meh. It's right near the airport. You're about a 10 minute walk from the nearest commuter rail (or ask if the shuttle can take you if you're lazy... like me) into the city. Maybe a 5 minute drive from Citizens Bank Park, which was our final destination in Philadelphia. 

Negatives? No free internet, and iffy cell phone service. Sometimes I was wandering the halls, looking for service.

My wife is originally from a FSU country so we go here about once a month and blow about $200 on all kinds of goodies. It's generally more expensive than Bell's but is located in a nicer area and the premises are well maintained. If you like trying out new foods I highly recommend this place. I always get a good laugh when they have euro techno playing ...... unh tiss unh tiss....

Salted Caramel Cupcakes. Enough said. This is the spot I go whenever I need anything sweet for a birthday gathering, etc. The best part is, I was out shopping for my wife's birthday and needed a treat for her...and I found it through Yelp. Thank you all for leading me here. The internet was right about this one...
I love the coffee and food, but the Plenty Café on spruce & 16th has a barista who has made our order incorrect on three occasions.

Came here for a graduation celebratory dinner with my friend and her family.  We had a fairly large group - 7 people.  We had a reservation, but we weren't able to be seated right away.  No problem, we'll just grab a drink at the bar while we wait.  

I ordered a mojito, given the rum and mojito reputation of the place.  Honestly, I'm a cheap date - but I didn't feel a thing (or taste any booze) in this drink.  Don't get me wrong, it tasted good - but I was drinking an alcoholic beverage for a reason.  And I did not achieve my desired result.

The food was good - we tried several different small plates, however for their main entrée menu, I was disappointed that mufungo wasn't on there...I asked the waiter, and he said they usually have it, but the menu changes pretty frequently.  What a bummer.  The service was attentive, and it was fun to watch when the salsa dancing started downstairs.  Our server did an excellent job of handling us as a larger party, including one child.

My advice - give this place a try, the food is flavorful.  They handle groups well, but make a reservation if you can because they seem to get pretty busy.  I just wish they kept mufungo on the menu year-round.  I would also advise to skip the mojito if you want to drink a cocktail that feels and tastes slightly boozy.  If you are simply looking for a good tasting refreshing beverage, then sure - give one of the mojitos a try, they have many different fruit flavors.  While I'm not a salsa dancer, I would consider coming back to watch the dancing and *hopefully* enjoy some mufungo.

From what I have seen, they sell everything at the same price as other budget-friendly places (Amazon, manufacturer website, Walmart, etc.).  In addition, the shopkeeper is super friendly.  I am not used to super-friendly stores.. maybe it is my neurotic southern California mentality but I tend to feel like shop keepers are suspicious of me if they approach me.  He was just very nice.  Great Bodum selection!!!  And when you walk in, it immediately smells of sweet, delicious coffee.

Good coffee; nothing superb or out of the ordinary. At one point, someone put a lot of time into making this a coffee shop with warm colors and all the basic trappings any cliche' coffee house would want; double sofa seating area with coffee table, board games on hand, warm 'coffee house' colors, emo barista with a rasta-style knit cap... But what left the biggest impression on me is the obvious need this place has for a good cleaning and tidy-up job.

Leaning, empty bookshelves, barren sugar station, tables askew and seemingly not cleaned for days.. This wasn't a busy Sunday afternoon when we dropped in and yes, the barista was working alone, (the sign of cheap management perhaps?)... But it's clear this cozy little coffee shop is in desperate need of some TLC and a major bathroom cleaning.

This is one of those places which has decided along the way that all that really matters is the cash you walk in with, and not the customer experience it gives.  Too bad.

Just so you know this location is on a side street, IN THE HOOD. 

As a Chicagoan I wanted to see what the fuss was about. I didn't see anything special the philly steak meat was plain and no season. I like the ones from great steak better in you local mall.

I also got an Italian hogie, doesn't compare to any sandwich in Chicago.

Maybe im spoiled.

Fantastic food. Their wings might be my favorite in the city! Really good quality. Also their cheese steak and Lodge Tot Poutine is delicious! I highly recommend a visit. I actually order Uber/Eats there for delivery.

While my husband and I were walking around Chinatown, we decided we wanted boba and saw Vivi's. We never tried this place before, so we went in!

Plenty of seating area. Can accommodate big and small parties. They do have restrooms for customers, which was nice :) we were welcomed right away, but I did felt a little rushed to order.. 

I order the Tiramisu Milk Tea and my husband ordered the Coconut Milk Tea. They were quick! Probably took like two minutes.

Tiramisu Milk Tea did not taste like Tiramisu, but it was good! Kinda reminded me of Vietnamese iced coffee, which I didn't mind at all!! My husband did not like the Coconut Milk Tea. It kinda had a weird aftertaste that I didn't mind so much. So I traded with him because he enjoyed mine more. We both like the Tiramisu! We probably wouldn't order coconut again.

Overall, this place was clean and the milk teas were okays. Not bad.

My second time here and it didn't disappoint. The downstairs lounge and bar area is perfectly dark and chic. I had the lamb meatballs and tuna tar tar and both were amazing. Great cocktails and the bartenders are friendly and very attentive. Will be back for sure.

Fantastic tattoo studio in philly. Awesome space with tons of talented artists. The staff is extremely friendly and creative with their design work. Hours are great and plenty of eateries and coffee shops nearby.  Highly recommend!!! 
Ps- John Tarrao has been my artist for the past year and he's sensational- full of inspiration and creativity   I highly recommend John

Our concierge at the Ritz Carlton set us up for an incredible meal. It was our anniversary and they treated us like royalty. This was the ultimate Cozy eatery but it was staff and the food and the drinks that stood out. Amazing oysters,  filet with marrow Demi glacé (liquid gold) - amazing craft  cocktails and our waiter and the entire staff were attentive  and cheerful.

DISAPPOINTED. the last two visits i have been annoyed. i said i wasnt going to go back but i had a last minute family gathering. we got family style everything. No salad dressing at all, no grated cheese, not eve salt and pepper. And I do NOT live close to there. Im done never again. And we freaking tipped for pick up.

I normally get the roast duck and roast pork to go, which never disappoint. Seasoned well, rich in flavor. Their sauce for the duck is similiar to Siu Kee. Its marinated very well into the bones. Delicioussss..

First time dining in since their second renovation (which has been a good few months), with all new owner and staff.

- bland soup.
- soggy noodle.
- shrimp wonton only, and the wonton size has been cut in half.
- slow wait staff.

I think I'll stick to their duck and roast pork. To go.

In few words: Overpriced hot-dogs, with a twist. 

Destination Dogs has opened less than two weeks, and I've been there few times already. Not because the dogs were marvelous, but I was curious. Why a hot dog on a bun cost could cost $12?!
Destination Dogs is inspired, obviously, by different destinations. Like a melting pot; domestic and international -hence the name. All hot dogs here are creative, some were pretty good, some were meh. 

A highlight of what I've tasted so far: 
What I've tasted so far:
- Kansas City Beefs: All beef sausage, topped with beef BBQ short ribs. Pretty amazing. 
- Completo: All beef hot dog, dressed up with avocado. Good, so GOOD.
- The Nicky Newarker: hot dog w/ potatoes, onions, peppers, yellow mustard. Probably the only dog without a twist. 
- Cyclone: All beef hot dog w/ chili. Hot dog with interesting twist. 
- Cleo McDowell: All beef hot dog, with cheese sauce and pickle, was my least favorite one.
- Honorable mention: Grilled Chicken sandwich. Huge sandwich, but nothing special. 

Tip: Truffle fries not worth the calories. 

Three and Half Yelps

Friend and I came here after a concert in Fishtown, desperate for a quick and filling bite. Philadelphia's a heaven and hell for foodies- heaven because there's SO MUCH GOOD FOOD, and hell because there's SO MUCH to choose from and sometimes you can end up at a place that's below average. Kostas is definitely part of the heavens of Philadelphia. Kostas is a casual Greek bar with DELICIOUS food and a great and rather broad selection of beers. It doesn't look like much from the outside, but it really epitomizes the saying "what's on the inside is what really matters." 
I'm a huge fan of Greek food, it's really hard to go wrong with it. But what blew me away about Kostas was the service. Oh man, in spite of being so busy, the attentiveness of the staff is equivalent to authentic Greek hospitality I experienced this past summer. My friend and I were immediately greeted, seated, and continually checked upon by Vacily (hopefully I spelled his name correctly). He was a FANTASTIC server: swift, attentive, kind and enthusiastic. This place was bustling yet my friend and I never felt like we were kept waiting for anything. It's honestly the most efficient service I've experienced at any bar and restaurant.
I ordered the pork souvlaki and my friend got the veggie gyro. Of course, both orders were delicious and the pork was cooked to perfection- tender, a little juicy, and spiced perfectly to complement the tzaziki and gyro. 
Vacily was so fantastic he hugged me and my friend at the end of our meal as a goodbye and invited us to the bar any time for great food and drinks. Without a doubt, if I or any of my friends are ever in the Fishtown area again, Kostas is going to be the go-to.
The best pizza crust.  The tartufo pizza is our favorite!!! The truffles and poached egg make it really yummy

Don't go here if you want to get better. I took my sister and niece on Tuesday because they were sick. We waited 3 1/2 hours and it wasn't crowded at all.  Only one of them got a prescription and it was for an over-the-counter antihistamine. My sister got worse with no actual treatment. She called back a few days later and asked if they could prescribe what he mentioned when she was there but he mentioned it wasn't necessary because her mucus was not that bad, but now it is and they told her basically their job is done unless she comes back in for another copay and waits another 3 1/2 hours to be seen again. These doctors today ain't worth a damn. Hopefully we won't need the ER. Thanks for nothing!

We came here recently for the Air Sex Championship (yes, we did) and paid $10 cover each. Definitely a divey/hipster bar but we knew what to expect. We sat at the bar and ordered a few drinks. I liked their beer selection but every draft beer we got came in a room temperature glass. And the glass smelled funky so every time I took a sip, it smelled a little like dirty dishwater. I spotted a big variety box of chips behind the bar and purchased one. The expiration date was back in June. I shrugged my shoulders and still tried to eat some but they tasted like cardboard. So gross!!! 45 minutes after show time and it still hadn't started....we were really over this bar by then so we bounced. So disappointing overall.

A very healthy and reasonable priced restaurant. The portions were a nice size. The only gripe I have about the place is that they were sweeping the floors in the direction of my table while I was trying to eat my food. Dust particles were flying all over the place. Granite it was towards the end of the night but still - wait until people are done cleaning to do your nightly cleaning.

Great tiny Greek place near the penitentary and art
Museum. Fresh, yum Greek salad and the lemon soup was really good too. The place is tiny so you can dine in quiet and the people who run the place are nice and friendly too. Would return. get the baklava !

Food is amazing, but bar service can be challenging. Nice folks but no attention to detail, and low on personality (tonight anyway) no offering of cheese on a pasta dish, no asking what kind of wine I would like, Eric. If you like it hot, try the diablo sauce! Spicy!!! Great with a chicken parm! Plus old 70's rock music on sound, TV's have sports and people are try to have a classy Italian meal, a little confusing! Come for the food!

Five stars every time.  The food is deloishus and the service is so good that it makes me want to BE a waitress again, which is a past I swore a blood oath I'd never look back to.

A warning to the vegans out there. The coconut club.  You WILL initially want to send it back to the kitchen, thinking you accidentally got something with meat in it.  But don't panic! Don't cause a scene. This is a safe space.  Just keep chewing and you'll soon realize: it isn't flesh.  It's just coconut.

Great beer selection for the recreational beer connoisseur, nice outdoor seating. Stopped in for beers and pretzel bites, which everyone loved. Dirty bathroom, and there's only one for everyone to share if that is an issue for you. Staff was friendly and attentive.

This is an intimate restaurant perfect for date night.  The food did not disappoint.  It was excellent!  Every course from appetizers to dessert was prepared with precision and thoughtfulness.  Would highly recommend Friday Saturday Sunday.

I don't know, I wasn't expecting this place to be as low key as it was...but I'm new to the area. 

My husband happened upon their instagram and the burgers and sandwiches looked to die for....and the pop-Clair also caught his eye..errr his sweet tooth. We bookmarked it and decided it was an appropriate place for our 1 month anniversary (hahahaha). 

We made reservations for Saturday evening but that was totally unnecessary, when we walked in the place was almost empty. There was no hostess or anyone but I remembered from a Yelp review that it's basically self seating so that's what we did. Eventually someone came over and I ordered the sweet heat mule (loved it, definitely packed a punch) and the coconut whiskey mojito (not the best mojito I've ever had, not really refreshing but was ok). Each was $10. 

Ordering drinks was the easy part, food was the hard part. We had seen so many things on their instagram that looked tasty. We asked the waitress some questions comparing the IG to the menu (I knew some of the pictures were off menu, those were clearly stated but some weren't). For the most part she recognized the picture even if off menu but there was one burger in particular she said "hmmm, I don't know if we do that here", that was for a fairly recent post. Ask SEVERAL items are off menu. I think it might be better to just update the menu. 

We decided to go with the brisket mac and cheese as an appetizer. Was ok. 
My husband asked for the off menu corned beef special. He was a little disappointed because what they brought him didn't have cole slaw like the IG picture. He said it tasted great regardless though. 
I went with the Kraken. I LOVED the spicy calamari and honey sriracha...like I want to put it on everything I cook at home. Was so good. 

We finished off by sharing a pop-Clair. It wasn't what we expected at all. It was actually a pretty dry outer batter with a hint of sweetness filled with hard ice cream...I did not enjoy my first bite but it got better as the ice cream softened a little but I still don't think I would recommend it.  

Overall, this place is super chill, casual vibe, very spacious. I'd come again for the burgers (when I'm cheating on my diet).

The other night I got some super solid American style Chinese food from Okawa at a great price. They even called me when my order was ready for pick up! Inside the restaurant is very clean and new. I live down the street and this place will probably be my go to for take-out Chinese food. I don't often write reviews on Yelp, but I noticed that this restaurant had only one review that is 1-star and written by someone who appears to be a troll (look at his other reviews).

This is a brunch review only. I've been wanting to go here for a while and it did not disappoint. 

I ordered the English Breakfast because I wanted to try the black pudding. It reminded me of scrapple and now I'm a huge fan! I was expecting more of irony taste to it but didnt really get much. Also came with eggs, bacon, sausage, fried bread, tomato, mushroom and baked beans. Bacon was melt in your mouth crispy with a slight smokiness to it. Never thought I would enjoy baked beans for breakfast but I can't knock it. Fiance ordered the Royale and he was very happy with it.

Bloody Mary fans will love the Dandelion's bloody. Super fresh and you can get half or full. I went with the full because it's basically two bloodys for $14. This is a steal compared to most other restaurants charging $10+ for one.

Service was excellent and attentive. Loved the quirky decor that created a homey feel. Nice outside area right in the sun for a lovely brunch. Next time I'll bring my dog!

What??!!  I don't understand the great reviews and how this place is ranked so highly here and all other best of lists.  My food was mediocre right from start to finish.  The salad was overdressed, not seasoned enough (borderline too sweet) and lacked acid; it's a salad for christ's sake, where's the damn acid? I also, had the lobster bomba rice (came lukewarm and too sweet - does amada despise salt and pepper) and the special of the night, elk.  The elk was great, cooked to med-rare, however, a bit on the salty side; the micro arugula salad that accompanied it tasted like I ate a salt shaker!  And dessert, ugh, just skip it, awful execution (sloppy) and awful flavor/balance of the different components.  There's clearly not a pastry chef here, but they charge pastry chef prices...however, the complimentary tuile was tasty though - JUST EAT THIS.  

I would recommend skipping this joint altogether - the chefs look uninspired and bored.  I was falling asleep watching them.  Also, another fault - why do they sit all the single diners at the bar when there are plenty of open tables (for 2 and under) in the centre of the restaurant where it's cozy and full of ambiance.  I felt like I was the bad kid wearing the dunce cap.  BOO AMADA, BOO YOU!

I've followed this truck religiously for a year already. Every morning starts with a Rival Bros large coffee. A day cannot begin without one. I'm not really a coffee snob, but I know what I like. I can differentiate from a cup of coffee I like, from a cup that I don't really enjoy, and usually Starbucks coffee makes me beg for other selections in about a week's time, but Rival Bros has kept me their fan for the past year. That says something!

Mmmmmmmm....foodgasm. Got the pulled pork sandwich as suggested by many friends, and was not let down. The pork was so juicy and dripped down my hands when I ate the sandwich, I was tempted to link my palsm but had to refrain so that my fiance wasn't completely embarassed to be with me.

I absolutely loved my first experience at Blue Nail. I am new to the area and was looking for a place to get my nails done and tried this place out after a couple others. This is a really good value place - service and quality is excellent, at any price, but a total steal if you factor in the great rates. They are very nice, attentive, and methodical. I did not feel like they were trying to rush my nail service to move on to the next customer - they took the time needed to make it perfect without me even having to ask. They include a lot of perks and extras in their regular mani pedi (eg hot stone leg massage) that other places do not. I will be a devoted customer from here on out!

This just in: bouncers at 32 Degrees are still friendly, and still not douchey. Cover's still free too.

There's now a connection to Cuba Libre next door, in case you want to get your salsa dancing on, or have a mojito, but it isn't always open. Not sure why that is, and haven't investigated yet.

Place is still small, but the back seems larger. That could be due to the flatscreen? 32 Degrees isn't perfect if you roll deep, but it's still a fine place to start the night.

I used the word "still" a lot, didn't I?

This place is HORRIBLE!!! Machine took 30 minutes to fill with water and then never began a wash cycle. The ladies were rude and not helpful. I had to pay for another machine. Initially, they told me I could buy detergent in the store.  After a long and frustrating discussion, they did give me detergent for the two loads I had to a start over. It is also expensive and sucks you of your time!

My friend took me here for the first time for a birthday dinner. Although it was pretty crowded and it took a while for us to be seated even though we had a reservation, the food was great. We went during happy hours and everything was a decent price. I loved their shrimp kimchi fried rice and their wings! The drinks were good too! Just keep in mind if you want the happy hour menu you have to be seated outside or at the bar
Nice clean and relaxing environment. Best mani & pedi in Chestnut Hill. Has been my go to spot for some time now.

Update, it's been well over a month now, but multiple attempts to get It Can Fit to come fix/replace the couch have failed. They haven't even bothered responding to my review and even if I get them on the phone, I can't rely on them to stick to any appointments they offer.

If you are looking to move soon, my advice would be to find a more professional mover way ahead of time. These guys (ICF) were available last minute and I decided to bite with the lower rate that they quoted, but do yourself a favor and plan your move ahead of time and don't think too much about any slight price difference. It would be worth it to go with a professional moving company.

You won't get better pizza!  They can give you any topping with cheese that is rich and flavorful, and buttery crust.  Subs and pastas are great for lunch or dinner.  WP Pizza is in an accessible place and everyone there is friendly and helpful.  Lots of experience, lots of character, lots of good people!

I've got to give a shout-out to West Point Pizza - I used to work down the street and they were the best place to get lunch!  Keep up the good work!

Went during restaurant week for dinner and had my best rest experience yet. While the entree (fried chicken) was okay, the other elements stole the show. The Nashville hot buns and cheese curds appetizers were both amazing. I would simply go back for just them. The dessert I chose was the apple slab pie which was very good as well. My boyfriend enjoyed his peanut butter mouse. 

My favorite thing was actually the frozen slushy drink "the winter slush" I believe it was called. So delicious and their was actually alcohol in it!

Excited to give this place another try!!

Picked just the right spot for a late lunch/brunch passing through Philly. Ensconced in the cool Bella Vista neighborhood this funky tavern offers fine and unique choices for food and wine. I loved the pork and tomato ragout with excellent slightly crisp and warm baguette spears topped with a boiled egg. My husband's burger was perfectly medium rare and accompanied by wonderful fries. The wines offered by the glass were out of the ordinary and delicious. The service was helpful and professional.

Bulletproof-glass Chinese places are a category all their own.  As some of the earliest commercial pioneers in the worst neighborhoods in American cities, BPG Chinese restaurants  have to do more than make good food to stay afloat... these folks have SEEN things...

Like your go-to roach coach or diamond in the rough Jamaican place, you never forget your favorite BPG Chinese, and this is my new favorite in Point Breeze. They accept credit cards, participate in GrubHub and are priced very reasonably.

The portions were enormous, the egg rolls were crunchy and hot, and the Orange Chicken was the perfect balance of gooey and crispy. The Pork Lo Mein was a monstrous portion albeit a little bland, and the vegetable stir fry entree was an awesome accompaniment to carry us well into fourth meal and tomorrow's lunch.

I felt great (and regular!) the next day, and I'm happy to say that I'll be ordering from here again the next time the craving hits.

* I write reviews and give ratings based on my experience with what the restaurant was trying to be and what segment they occupy.  if you blindly walk into places because of a star-rating and don't take into account what a place really is at their roots, you're gonna have a bad time.

As an employee of Penn for the past 16 years and one of the first patients to be operated on by the wonderful Dr Scheie at the Scheie Eye Institute, I am disgusted by the treatment I received at Penn Optical at Scheie today. There were 2 men working (barely) there today when I went. When I tried to get assistance from the first guy, he blatantly ignored me and fussed around with a display case, instead of helping me. It was very obvious he was ignoring me on purpose. When I asked the 2nd guy if he had any frames for a smaller face, he said there was no point in showing me any frames because I hadn't yet scheduled an eye exam appt and they were switching out some of their inventory. Excuse me but how hard is it to show me the frames and then let me figure out if I like them or not and proceed from there. I will NEVER go back there again, even though I get a bigger discount there with the Penn insurance. I'd rather pay more money for better service! Rudest service, or lack there of, I have ever received. Dr Scheie would be so disappointed.

Here we have another location for a pretty popular matcha chain. So far I've been here a couple of times for the soft opening and once after they officially opened. The space itself is clean, bright, and inviting. The employees are nice and were more than happy to answer any questions I had.

I'll start off with the soft serve since it's the main selling point and is in all the other desserts. The matcha soft serve is nicely balanced. It's not too bitter and slightly sweet. You get some earthy vegetal notes from the matcha itself. In terms of texture, it's very slightly icy, but it's kind of expected. The vanilla is also very solid. Nice, creamy, rich, with good vanilla flavor. I think I prefer the mixed since the vanilla helps balance the texture of the matcha and the matcha helps keep things from reading too sweet. The waffle cone is crisp and tasty as well.

The matcha latte similarly was very solid. It wasn't too sweet and you were able to taste the matcha in spite of the ice and milk. No complaints here.

The Maiko Special is a parfait with soft serve, red bean, agar jelly, chestnut, mochi, corn flakes, and matcha chiffon cake. The individual components are good, but my main gripe is how the dessert itself is layered. We talked about how the matcha isn't too sweet. The mochi isn't sweet either so it kinda made the top half relatively bland (hard to mix up since there's no room). Meanwhile the sweet red bean and the corn flakes are at the bottom without soft serve to eat with them! If I could change it, I would have a layer of soft serve at the bottom or in the middle at the expense of having a thicker layer of soft serve at the top.

The matcha kuromitsu kinako float was another victim of an awkward eating experience for the sake of photogenicity. The soft serve sits on a bed of ice and I just didn't know how to consume it?? I just ended up eating most of the soft serve while trying to sip the drink. I'm not sure about why there's ice when the soft serve is there to chill the drink itself. I really liked the kinako flavor mixed in with the drink though. I personally thought it was a bit sweet, then I realized there was a good amount of the kuromitsu syrup sitting at the bottom of the cup. If you don't have a big sweet tooth, I'd probably avoid this drink. As someone that gets their boba at 25-50% sweetness, I'd probably stick to the non kuromitsu floats from now on.

Overall, if you like matcha, I would definitely recommend this place. It lives up to the hype. Next time I want to try the plain matcha to really get a better idea of how good the matcha is. Other than that, I'm coming back for a soft serve cone after a meal in Chinatown.

Great spot for Restaurant Week.  Every one of the 10 plates I had a bite of was delicious.  Highlights included the ham wrapped duck, the gazpacho, and the creme brulee style dessert.  Service was quick and efficient.  Pricey no doubt, and the portions were small, but it is a tapas style place so that's to be expected.  Definitely worth a trip for some of the best food in the city.

i used to love this place, authentic mexican food they deliver. but something happened the food quality went way down, they hired new cooks who don't care and you're lucky if you get a good meal.... oh and the worst customer service I've ever experienced. NEVER AGAIN
Not much to say here. Pat's was better. Better tasting meat, better bread and better fries. This place, like Pat's, was very average and didn't live up to the expectations.

This is never the place that you want to go... it's the place for when you just can't bear to have the night end!  It's in Center City, so if you're caught there and McGlinchey's closed this is where you go!  The drinks are overpriced and it has the vibe of a hotel bar in hell... but it's OPEN!  One of the strangest, but comforting parts of this bar is that it has a memorial plaque to a past regular customer.  It says that he spent many nights here trying to figure out how to get off of the night shift.  I don't know why I like that plaque but I do.

I'm from the Middle East and this is the closest authentic shawarma I could get in Philadelphia! Really cool owners, nice place, awesome food! Try the chicken shawarma on pita with roast pepper hummus and no rice! It's awesome! And guess what, they're hot sauce is out of the world, best I've had in The U.S!

let's just say I know two things in lifea god bbq brisket sandwich and cupcakes.

brisket sandwich is back on the menu this week....get your tuckus in there before it's off to Boca with Bubbe!

This is a really pretty church.  Usually I end up in the Chapel, but was in the main part for Christmas mass.  It was surprisingly empty for Christmas.  The music was really nice, but I was sooo cold the entire time.  I guess they don't have any heat?

I wouldn't even give this place a star if that were an option. The food was gross. The stir fry mushrooms were overly sweet. The black bean mussels/ baby clams were TOO salty. The chef is too heavy handed with the seasoning. The crab meat in the sushi was still frozen and stuck together. When the check was already paid, I left the tip in the check book and I was ready to go. As soon as I closed the check book, the server walked over leaned into the table to grab the check book and she just stayed there! She was blocking my way from leaving my seat as if she wanted to lock me in there to make sure her tip was there. I said excuse me but she didn't move. I had to shift the booth that I was sitting in to get out. Terrible food, terrible service. I will never return.

The fried chicken dish at Sbraga was my favorite fried chicken in Philly.  Unfortunately, as of late, it appears to be on hiatus.  I was thrilled when Fat Ham opened and a similar, but spicier fried chicken dish was on the menu.  When I was finally able to wrangle a friend to go with me, I was thrilled.  

She drove into the city to meet me.  Luckily, even at 6 pm on a Friday, parking on the Walnut Street bridge is abundant.  You only have to pay until 8.

The first thing that struck me about the Fat Ham was how tiny it was, like only seats probably 30 people tiny.  We did not know this before our arrival.  We went 30 minutes before our reservation to grab a drink at the bar, but I probably would not do that again--at least on a Friday.  The bar was packed, but the drinks were flowing, so after our first drinks (she had the Sauvignon Blanc on tap, I had the gin and aperol cocktail which was delicious and well made by friendly bartenders--I highly recommend it, if you like gin and aperol, of course).

We were seated promptly at reservation time and were quickly approached by our server.  We had spent the past 30 minutes at the bar salivating over everyone's dishes, so we were ready to order.  Our server explained that the food was served tapas style (smaller plates) and recommended three plates per person.  My friend and I both ordered three plates of our own and waited for the food to arrive.  I would recommend, if possible ordering plates for the table to share.  Our plates were all served at different times, which resulted in my friend eating my appetizer b/c she thought it was a bread dish for the table and us staring at each other while we ate our plates.  

The FOOD:

Southern Hummus--this is a peanut-infused hummus topped with bits of whole peanuts.  I thought it was a nice twist and the grilled bread it was served with was delicious--both crunchy and airy.  There was just enough bread for the amount of hummus served (I hate the dreaded out of bread, cup of hummus scenario).  

Baked Mac and Cheese--The "mac" was actually shells of pasta that were well cooked, slightly al dente.  The cheese was a creamy mix that included a smoked cheese, perhaps gouda, which went very well with the crumbled BBQ potato chips that topped the dish.  It's hard to go wrong with mac and cheese and this dish does not disappoint.

Hot Chicken--This dish is very similar to the Sbraga dish, with the addition of a spicy hot sauce coating the chicken.  The large piece of chicken tops a dill-ranch soaked thin piece of white bread and a few pickles.  The ranch cools some of the heat and really helps you taste the chicken and the flavors of the hot sauce.  Make no mistake, this is a hot dish, so if you do not like spicy food, stay away.  The chicken and skin are incredibly crispy.  The breading is not too thick.  

Hot Sauce--every table is topped with a bottle of homemade(?) hot sauce that is different than the hot sauce on the hot chicken.  It is mild and garlicky, and perhaps includes tomatoes.  It was delicious.  I used it to accompany my mac and cheese and it added delicious garlicky flavor.

All the food was delicious, the cocktails were creative, the servers, hosts and bartenders were friendly, helpful, and knowledgeable--which was super impressive so soon after the opening.  The Fat Ham is another gem from Sbraga that I will add to my rotation.  My friends on the other side of the Schuylkill will be excited to know that I definitely found an excuse to take more trips to University City in the near future.

While waiting for my room at the Philadelphia Marriott Downtown, I stopped into Thirteen for a bite to eat. 

Since it was after 11am, my choices were the breakfast buffet or the lunch menu. I wanted breakfast, but I'm not the biggest fan of buffet breakfasts (other than freshly made omelettes or waffles, is anything really ever hot or fresh or not picked over especially after 11am).  Therefore, I opted for the lunch menu. 

Thirteen offers standard lunch fare with salads (Caesar with optional chicken or shrimp) pizza, spaghetti and meatballs, burgers, and turkey club to name a few. However, one item grabbed my attention which was the chicken and waffles since I was originally in a breakfast mood. 

The chicken (3 good sized pieces of breaded white meat) was golden brown, crispy and steaming hot. The waffle (in 4 separated sections) was also golden brown and crispy. I didn't care for the herb and peppered butter, plus there was enough on top of the waffle to clog multiple arteries (I guess that's Philly style). Note to self; next time request the butter on the side. The maple syrup had a hint of bourbon which for my palate was good; the last establishment I ordered chicken and waffles I found the bourbon too strong. 

For $21 the meal is steep, but you pay for convenience in a hotel restaurant. You could do better, but also far, far worse hence my 3 star rating. Enjoy Philly!!
The Mac N Cheese is to die for! Great staff and a staple in the downtown Philly community.  The garlic truffle wings are pretty good, need a little more flavor though.

Jeff is about as good as you can find in Philly (far better than Hahnemann! Kinder than HUP).

My father went here for an outpatient procedure and it was handled well.
Administratively (as with most large hospitals) it's kind of a nightmare.

Allow ample time - your 8 AM (or 9 AM) appointment - when you show up at 7:30 AM may not start until past 10 AM!!!

Lots of hurry-up-and-wait.

Inconsistent taste each visit and it doesn't have the hint of ginger flavor in their signature kungfu MilkTea, you have to taste the NY locations to notice the difference. Honest opinion here, their service is extremely slow compared to other tea houses in Chinatown, TeaTalk, TeaDo ...T back etc.

I thought it was good, but still thought Han Dynasty was better. However, to be fair, we went to Han Dynasty with a huge group so I was able to sample much, much more than when I came to E Mei with the hubby.

The beef with long peppers was, hands down, the best dish we tried. Dumplings in hot chili oil was also amazing. I actually wasn't a huge fan of the dan dan noodles - I thought the noodles at Han was better, although the hubby liked E Mei's version better.

Mai Lai Wah is one of my favorite Chinatown joints. Also, it's open real late, which makes it a great option after a night out. I usually get items off their 'noodle menu' - which you might have to ask them to see. I love their steamed dumplings which comes with an unusual and delicious ginger sauce. Other favs of mine are: - Pan-fried noddles w/ Seafood - Roast duck noodle soup - Chinese greens w/ garlic sauce

So after my negative pizza delivery experience, the owner of Slice actually contacted me via Yelp.  She offered a credit for another pizza, which I went to pick up.  I called about an hour in advance and actually got there earlier than I expected and it was ready in 10minutes (before when I said I would pick it up).  

I got the clams casino pizza and shared it with two of my friends.  We all liked it a lot and had at least 2 slices - the crust was thin with a good chewy texture and a lingering sweetness and there was ample amounts of clam.  I personally would have liked a higher ratio of bacon to clam, but I understand if the clam was supposed to take the forefront on taste.

So since my second experience was very positive I am changing my average rating to a 3.

Let me give you some background of where I'm coming from here. I haven't been to an actual beer distributor down here. I've only been to a few in the northeast, the suburbs, and New Jersey. I usually only buy 6 packs or individuals from delis closer to my apartment (in Wash West). 

In particular, I was looking for the Neshaminy Creek Croydon Cream Ale. I couldn't find it at any of the aforementioned delis or even the Bottle Shop on EPA (I hangout down there sometimes), but they had it here!

A lot of the shelves were bare (due to renovations?), but they had all of the usual crafts (Dark Horse, Yards, Neshaminy Creek, Yards, Victory, etc.) and of course all of the regular beers like Bud Light. I was actually surprised at their selection of Dark Horse. A lot of smaller places that sell beer (six packs and individuals) don't carry a lot of Dark Horse, but they had it here. They had all of what I usually buy (from those delis) like Victory and Neshaminy Creek, so Bella Vista Beer is great for me.

I guess I should mention that I didn't see any prices on this six packs (of Neshaminy Creek Cream Ale that I bought), but I was surprised to find that it was below $10. I was expecting it to be around $12 because that's what the delis charge for lower abv craft beers, so I'm pretty happy with this place.

The dude at the checkout was cool, and there's a cat.

This review is for FreshDirect- Washington, DC as there is no page specifically for their WDC delivery. 
This review is also specifically for FreshDirect's Fresh Entrees, Fresh Sides, Produce and Bakery Desserts.  

FreshDirect is equally compatible to Whole Paycheck if they delivered. When it comes to prices, you're paying high, top dollar. Fresh Pasta Salads, Egg Salads, Cole Slaw, Broccoli Salads etc. Entree meals, Pasta Bolognese dishes are absolute deliciousness, and absolutely expensive. 

Refrigerated. 
Clearly FreshDirect keeps all of their Fresh Entrees and produce refrigerated on-premise. 
Tomatoes arrive having been refrigerated. Everyone knows you don't refrigerate tomatoes. Upon arrival your tomatoes are rock hard and have already begun that stale faze. Your shelf life is miniscule. Eat 'em quick! If you can tolerate a hard tomato that won't ripen. 
All in all FreshDirect's produce is light years better than Peapod's. Peapod's produce is dismal. 
Fresh Rotisserie Chicken and Fried Chicken all refrigerated, tough and soggy. Rotisserie was wet and leathery. Fried was wet and soggy. Both (and tomatoes) equal not so fresh.

Bakery Desserts are decadent, fresh and delicious with cakes, pies, fresh cookies, buns and such ready to walk through your door. 

Those that tip your delivery employees well, don't over tip here. All they do is walk in and set your boxes down and leave.  

We all have choices. I choose to often pay ridiculously for convenience. 
Splurging on all the Fresh Entrees, Fresh Sides and bakery desserts that Peapod doesn't carry means I treat myself to FreshDirect periodically. 
Value vs. Quantity for everyday items and staples means I'm sticking to Peapod. 
 
Oh, unlike Peapod when something is wrong with your order, you're not going to get it resolved on your invoice the error was made on, you get a credit on your next purchase at FreshDirect. BOO! 
Reeling you back for more.
I like that this is a healthy treat. It's also conveniently located across from Rittenhouse square...Take your yogo while strolling through the park!

Under impressed. Too start with, the food is over priced. I don't hide paying for good food, but this was just average. Pleasant looking decor, but not much else as a positive. The Caesar Salad was tasty as was the Ahi Tuna. However, everything else was just ok, or less. The Creme Brule was soupy and our Char was bland. Nothing inspiring.

The worst for me was the lack of impressive service. Nothing was served to the table together. One dessert was served a full 5 minutes before the other two got to the table. Seriously? And we order soup so a spoon was brought to the table, but the spoon was filthy. The server could have easily checked the spoon first. 

We will not be going back. Too many other good options in Philly to make this lackluster effort worth another dime.

I work right down the street from Moshi Moshi and usually get take out from there when I am on my break. I am always happy with the sushi, I normally get a Tuna Roll and a Philly Roll, with a small salad. The staff are usually really nice and work very quickly to bring me my food. It's also pretty cheap too for being in the Rittenhouse area.

I came here once for an actual sit-down dinner with my roommate and we ate in an area where you took your shoes off and sat indian style on the floor while you ate. It was a fun experience!
Normally when I go it is not very crowded either which is kind of nice because its not a very big place, so I would feel overwhelmed if there were a lot of people in there.

I don't even know where to begin, I had a great time here till Saturday when I was rushed by ambulance. I want to take the time to thank you thank you Jamal and Chris for going above and beyond comforting me in this real bad time. Especially Jamal how funny your wife has the same bday as me to the year.. I feel really blessed that you guys were the ones who went out your way to take care of me and made sure I was ok as well as safe and helping me breathe, till the ambulance came. To Shakria man o man I feel like I made a homie in philly, she made the stay so pleasant and was beyond helpful. I definitely will be making this my hotel as well for all my company's business trips and events when ever in philly. Thank you for all your hospitality you guys are the true meaning of a great home away from home. Angie B from Cali

This review is DECADES overdue! There are two groups of people that I hope read this. Tourists who are visiting our city and the directors for Fox Sports. Every time I watch an Eagles game I want to scream from the rafters REAL PHILADELPHIANS DON'T EAT HERE!!!! For reasons that completely escape me, people are led to believe that Philly people frequent this place. It's even mentioned in a few tourist pamphlets that the city puts out. The guy who used to own the place was a blatant racist.  The radio spots he'd run were nothing short of hate propaganda and the infamous "English Only" sign just screams spend your tourist dollars here.  On top of that the food is mediocre  at best. So for the love of god dp not patronize this place. Instead send your tourist money to Jim's steaks on South St or just follow the real philly people to Tony Luke's

I usually go to Sulimay's in Fairmount and LOVE it. But, out of convenience for location, I went here. I am growing out my pixie cut and very clearly stated that I wanted the back and sides trimmed, and for the stylist to thin out the bulk using thinning shears (as they have done in Fairmount). The stylist basically just gave me a full trim and now, I'm set back in terms of growing out my pixie. She took way too much off the top, and left the back too long (the opposite of what I asked for). Furthermore , she never took out the thinning shears. I am very displeased with my hair.

Being stuck on the isle of Northern Liberties isn't too bad. I have pretty much everything I need around here. Pizza, dry cleaner, eye care...

If I need something extraordinary, I can always venture downtown for it. It's usually a hit and run trip. But my craving for gelato could only be staved by Capogiro, and it seems a but silly to go downtown just for a gelato...but I did it anyway.

Now with Nana Petrillo's, my Capogiro gelato needs are met. They have all the best flavors and they get fresh Capogiro gelato daily. I have also made Nana my go to spot for my coffee. Really good coffee and iced coffee. Is it excessive that I go through 20-30 coffees a week? Maybe. But that's besides the point. 

I have made friends with the owners mom. She bakes all the tasty goods herself in the back and she is really funny. Makes me feel at home:)

The staff here is also aces. Very sweet and fun to talk to. Wonderful addition to the libs life.

We were looking for a new place to try this weekend and decided to go to The Tavern. Set in the basement with low ceiling, it has a warm and cozy feel. Dress code is casual too, which is cool.
They have a good selection of cocktails and wines. My husband ordered one of the tequila based cocktails and I got the house Cabernet. We got kale chips with the "secret sauce", deviled eggs and roasted veggies-mushroom with poached egg for appetizers, sliders with edamame-mushroom-black bean burger for entrees. Everything was great!!! I must add that the house wine was impressive. We think the secret sauce is mayo+sriracha+garlic, whatever it is it went perfectly well with those crispy kale chips..yumm! I am a vegetarian and it is pretty hard to find a GOOD veggie burger right but the slider with black bean burger here is probably the BEST I have had so far.
Will definitely go back for more with friends and family! :)
Chicken Mole Tacos were very good. Loved the squeeze of orange they're served with. Chicken Burrito was so-so. Super yummy watermelon sangria. Would definitely go back and try salsas and guacamole.

I love Varga bar! Truffled cheese fries with crab meat? Delishhush. They have really nice beers and the wait staff is pretty friendly. It's a little tight and when it's crowded it can be a tad uncomfortable. Not a good first date spot but great otherwise.

For $5 and being all ages, the show that we saw (Get In) was pretty good. Some people are not funny, but they were only on for like 15 minutes so it was manageable to get through. A few were super funny and it was a great time. Me and my husband had a really great night here!

Where to start?  First, after reading the positive reviews on Yelp we decided to order the mozzarella sticks.  All I can say is, UGH.  The mozzarella sticks we got CLEARLY came out of a box from the freezer. They were congealed cheese that wasn't even hot enough to be stringy.  They arrived warm, which was more than I can say for the pizza we ordered (more on that in a moment), but the positive attributes of these mozzarella sticks end there.

Now on to the pizza.  We decided to go for the classic Margarita pizza since my friend is a vegetarian and I wasn't a fan of some of the toppings listed on the veggie pizza.  I don't know what others expect when they order a Margarita pizza, but I expect a good amount of fresh mozzarella, fresh basil and slices of tomato.  What we got was a mostly sauce pie, with some small slices of mozzarella that only covered about 1/2 the sauce, and a barely visible sprinkling of dried basil and oregano.  While I accept some blame for not having read the menu more closely, I have to say that when a restaurant uses a well known name for a classic dish you expect to get what the name means.  While this was a serviceable mozzarella pizza, it WAS NOT a margarita pizza.  And to add insult to injury the pie arrived barely warm even though the pizza joint was less than 2 blocks from the apartment. If I wanted to microwave my pizza I would've bought a lean cuisine.

The sauce was delicious, something that usually makes or breaks a pizza for me, and the crust was good, but I those are the only good things I can say about my Zio's experience.

I purchased a Groupon deal a few months back for a gel manicure and a regular pedicure.  I went and they did a good job and no issues with the Groupon redemption.  Today I went back for just a gel manicure which was a second Groupon deal that I bought for this place.  The cost was $19. however when I arrived the lady at the front desk said I couldn't use a Groupon deal today.  She said their policy is one Groupon deal a year per person.  OK I guess I didn't see that when I purchased the Groupon.  She said I could pay her one dollar and the Groupon and she would honor it. I said why would I pay you a dollar more over what the Groupon deal was and she had no explanation?  I then asked how I could get the Groupon refunded to me if they weren't going to honor it.  She then called someone and spoke in a language that I did not understand and then handed me her phone.  She said they want to talk to you.  I got on the phone and that lady said we will honor the Groupon if you give us $6. which would bring my out of pocket up to $25.  She said that is what they charge for a gel manicure.  I declined and I will now pursue a refund from Groupon.  Bottom line, know this place said one Groupon a year.  Hoping this info saves you time and any unnecessary aggregation.

I never write yelp reviews; I made an account just so I can warn you people that this is the worst place ever! For some very average Chinese food,  my plate ended up costing $20!! Should have cost no more than $10. I could have gone to a nice sit down restaurant for this price. The cashiers were rude, and unfriendly. I would recommend going ANYWHERE but this place...

One night my hubby and I were in the neighborhood, and wanted to have a few slices, as we were quite hungry at that time. He  passes by this place daily and has stopped several times for a good and quick bite to eat. He said how good their pizza is and asked if I wanted to stop.
