We did, and we each had a pepperoni and plain slice. Wow, truly a "Taste of Italy" as posted on their store sign! The pizza was nice and hot, the crust had the perfect balance of chew and crunch, the sauce was delicious and the cheese was very nicely melted and distributed and was the perfect golden color, and the pepperoni was awesome!! 5 !! Will continue to come there when the pizza craves set in or when I'm in the neighborhood again! Thank you for making such good pizza!!

Pretty decent! 

Went for a little reunion dinner/drink session with some old friends. We ordered the guacamole/sangria for the table. Guacamole was pretty yummy, although I wish the sangria had tasted a little better. 

food-wise, it was yummy. large portions, and the quality of our food was really great. A couple of us went for a drink or two beforehand, bartenders were very accommodating and the drinks were pretty good. 

A little over-priced for type of food, but atmosphere and fun/funky vibe of the restaurant makes up for it. We had a great time reuniting with everyone, and the decent "food/atmosphere/loud/fun" -ness of the place definitely helped. 

Will be returning!

Caught a concert here and its a decent venue.  There is a large parking garage across the street, although $14 isn't a bargain.  Nor was the $4 bottle of water.  However, the place appeared clean and well run.  Biggest negative though is the absence of after show bar options within walking distance.

You make it so very hard to love this
Bar...I really really want to. The concept is great. I want to be able to watch football (soccer) at 9am, have a nice authentic Irish breakfast and a morning bloody Mary or vodka and oj...as I sit at your bar writing this, I am told the kitchen MIGHT open at 11ish... You stare at my empty bottle and wait for me to ask for a beer? It's sitting on the ledge, unless you just began bartending and have never been in a bar a day in your life YOU KNOW WHAT THIS MEANS...I WANT to spend my money, HERE in this economy, WHY DON'T YOU WANT ME TOO? We used to goto tir na nog, but they don't want me to love then either. Please let me know a good soccer bar with a AWESOME staff who takes pride in their work, are personable and WANT TO TAKE MY MONEY

Just wanted to give some updated love to my all time favorite restaurant in Philadelphia- my main man Han. Your food is what dreams are seriously made of- easily about my 25th time there and I have converted about over 20 people to follow. Recent discoveries is the soft shell crab- spicy and absolutely amazing; eggplant side mushy but garlicky; and the double cooked pork belly. Faves still stand as Dan Dan (duh); wontons in chili oil (could literally eat a bucket); double cooked fish; sesame noodles; pea leaves; cucumbers- it never ends. Go there NOW!

On Friday, our bartender Paul provided us an awesome night in preparation for the holiday. Whether it be great conversation with theories on Rey's lineage in Star Wars: Force Awakens, or blunt suggestions on menu items, we both left asking "when do we go back?" and firmly stating "he better be working there." 

Sure enough, his suggestions of the Buffalo Cauliflower and Lobster Mac and Cheese were on point. We spent much of the night chatting up this friendly bartender and taking in the pleasant scenery. We will definitely be going back soon! Thanks, Paul!

This is how you do it!
Yes!
Love love love.
And also, much respect to the guy who is ALWAYS ALWAYS ALWAYS working here.  He is super-nice and very efficient.
I've seen him training many a new employee, but I do not think they last long.  It is mostly this one guy, who manages not to look stressed out no matter how long the line is, or how many suburban punk wannabe teenagers are going back for endless refills from the salad bar.

(Note: The Walnut St location has Diet Orange Fanta, which is surprisingly delicious - it seems to be absent from this location, which, you know, is probably good since that stuff cannot be healthy)

Ok, so clearly I am out of the main stream in terms of my adoration of this fine establishment.  In truth, I would give it 3.5 stars, but that is mostly due to my preference for dining styles.  I have always been a fan of - the more flavors in life the better, and when you dine w/ friends at Vetri, you certainly get your share of variety in sharing tastes.  So where's the beef as they used to say?  And I don't mean in the hoofs and horns kind of way.  I think my issue is with the Gatling gun approach to rolling through the courses.  Although I wouldn't say we were 'rushed' per se it didn't feel at all leisurely or particularly elegant, although we were there for a good 2.5 hrs. 
 
The flavors were indeed very good.  Some were excellent, but most were just so so considering the level they should be in this league.  The wait staff was extremely knowledgeable about the food and especially about the wines.  But they seemed really rushed to drop the plates, explain the offerings and run away.  

So after a $155 tasting fee, and $135 for the premium wines, I'm not feeling the sense of $360 per person value (after tip).  And might I add the pours for the wines could not have been much more than 2 ounces, although some may have been 3 oz, but not a drop more.  I have NOT looked up the prices for the bottles, but this seems awfully steep.  If you are pouring 2.5 oz from a 750 ml bottle you are getting 10 pours per bottle.  With 7 pours for 7 courses you are paying about $20.00 per glass at $135.  Are each of these bottles valued at $200 or more?

So at the end of the day -  glad we went to check this place off.  But wouldn't pick it a second time around.  I would however rush back to Osteria, another of Mark Vetri's establishments.  Felt the service and food there were above par compared to Vetri.

The sushi and sashimi were excellent, with extremely fresh high-quality fish.  Todd, the chef, has also put together a lot of creative looking rolls, and the ambiance is great, with lots of natural light.

My wife and I didn't realize you have to order ahead for the omakase tasting menu, so I'll have to update after we go back next time.  Highly recommended.
I come here atleast once a week, usually for breakfast. It's always very good. Their coffee is the best! And I highly recommend ordering creamed chipped beef with rye bread from Le Bus.

The Adirondack Sandwich in a nutshell..
Great bun.
Great sauce.
Good meat. (filet) 
Good sandwich.

The fries and wings were delicious and they had a decent selection of beers. Their lemonade was that fake sugary crap and therefore terrible. I would definitely go back for the Adirondack but would probably try one of their burgers.

I love seeing the creative creations Dizengoff comes up with to make their unbelievably delicious hummus even more delicious. The pickled veggies are the best compliment to the creamy hummus. If you are a hummus lover DEFINITELY try Dizengoff.

I recently spent a week in Philly and had dinner at many wonderful restaurants including Zahav, Vedge, and Talula's Garden. My meals and experiences at all of these restaurants were absolute perfection but Talula's was my favorite which says a lot! Beautiful earthy decor both indoors and outdoors, wonderful cheese selection, excellent/knowledgeable service.

The Hotel itself is amazing. We really liked the room. 

We arrived from a 10 hour road trip at 11pm and there was no parking available. Drove around for 30 minutes looking for an available lot. I was spent. 

When booking, we requested a late check out. They said that there was no record of this request and they could not offer it. Manager and desk person were quite rude. 

Not our best experience. 

Again, the hotels location and room design are awesome. Maybe we came on a bad day.

Great little restaurant and craft brewery.  Service is a little slow for lunch... and the servers are jerkish about seating you if you're not eating (which is funny... because I'll buy way more in beer in an hour than most people will buy in food) - but it's still a favorite haunt of mine in the city.  

Being a brewer, the beer available are pretty much whatever they just kegged.  By far and away my favorite is the Grog - an earthy dark brown ale - which is what I reach for if I'm looking to set and talk for a bit.  If darker beers aren't your thing, I recommend 60 Shilling... an amber Scotish-style ale.  If you're a beer wuss and are wondering where the Miller Light is on the menu, order a BPA.  Oh - and if you're like me and love a good wheat beer, pray O-Tay is on the menu.  It's a rare appearance, but an awesome beer.

I don't go to Nodding Head for the food... although they have a descent burger.  Lately I've been getting the Jamaican Chicken sandwich off the bar menu... or the Brandini burger.  Only thing I'd warn against would be the french dip - which if is is actually famous I don't know what for.

I'm giving it four stars overall because of the beer.  It's be a two for food though - so aim in when you're looking for a happy hour or a late night drink.

I am struggling with what to give this place. We ordered here last night and it was a rainy night so I am sure they were busy. They told us it would take 1.5 hours to get delivery. They took under an hour which we were happy about. We ordered two large pizzas:

- red dawn: roasted red potato, roasted red pepper, smoked shallots, kalamata olive tapenade, flat leaf parsley
- haymaker: seitan sausage, red onions, garlic butter, tomato sauce, daiya cheese, fresh oregano 

The large pizzas are very large and we definitely didn't need two but we love leftovers and pizza for breakfast. Neither of us are vegans or vegetarians. I used to be a vegetarian years ago and have attempted veganism twice. There is no doubt that cheese pizza taste much better. We both liked the haymaker better than the red dawn but you can still tell it isn't real cheese. I ended up just eating the potatoes off of the red dawn because I thought the potatoes were too much on the dough without cheese and much else. We usually gorge when eating pizza and end up being ridiculously stuffed because we will go back an hour later for a 3rd or 4th slice. This time, we both ate 1 1/2 to 2 slices and didn't want to go back for more. Maybe it is the non dairy that keeps us fuller or perhaps veggies and fake cheese just aren't the same as the real thing. 

I need to order again and try things other than just pizza.

If you are vegan or vegetarian, then I can see this being a 4 or a 5 in your book. For all others, skip this place for pizza as you can get a way tastier pizza elsewhere.

I was all set to love this place--so conveniently located to my apartment & so many vegan options on the menu that I had trouble narrowing down. There was a 15 minute wait, no problem, but when the very hungover looking hostess took another Kristin who was there after us (I was with Kristin H) when they called our name, that was the 1st red flag.

The service was OK, our waiter was friendly, but at times, nowhere to be found. It's not that I wanted to be rushed out of there or anything, but knowing there was a huge line outside, you'd think they'd pay attention to when we wanted our check.

The food was also just OK. I was really not impressed with the vegan chicken cheese steak, definitely not so flavorful, but the Sarcone's roll was good. I'll give them a second chance--there are 2 brunch items I'd still like to try--but my expectations are low.

Also, on the way out, a busboy, a waiter, & a host all (separately) said, "Bye girls." We're grown women, how about you don't infantilize us, BOYS?! The appropriate phrase would be, "Bye, ladies."

Wow. Now that I've written it, the 3 stars seems generous. I'll keep it 3 stars, because at least they TRIED with the vegan options, but sorry, Green Eggs, I'm not really a fan.

I can't believe I'm forced to give Pep Boys any stars - they don't even deserve one. It has always taken a ridiculously long time to get a simple oil change at Pep Boys, but I can deal. After my most recent trip, though, I can decidedly no longer deal.

I work long hours and go to school. The only reason I continue to put up with Pep Boys is because of their hours. (Fun fact: Frankford Auto has similar hours, and way better customer service! Go there instead!) On this most recent trip, I made an appointment to get an oil change at 6pm, which I thought was reasonable considering they accept appointments until 8pm. I receive e-mail confirmation of my appointment, all is well.

I walk in at 5:45pm and let the man at the desk know I had an appointment. He cringes a bit and says "Oh, did nobody call you?" No, nobody called me. "There are no techs working tonight," he tells me.

I'm pretty much fed up at this point, because my long waits in the past made me question whether making an appointment actually *did* anything. Turns out that no, no it doesn't. They literally do not give a fuck if you have an appointment, and have no problem allowing you to drive all the way there between work and school just to turn you away because no one could be bothered to call you and let you know that they had to cancel your appointment.

I asked him if it mattered at all that I made an appointment -- after all, if no one else was working, who the fuck is supposed to call me aside from the only guy on duty? He pretty much said no, they don't pay attention to appointments and "the earlier the better -- you should get here at like 8am."

So, yeah, bye forever Pep Boys. I'm done wasting my time.
Bad service and food.  And manager kicked us out of Karoke bar since we complained about poor service and food

If Dizengoff's hummus isn't the best hummus in the world, I don't know what else would be. Okay, I might be exaggerating a little, but their hummus is seriously the smoothest, creamiest hummus EVER! The hummus comes out warm, often topped with toppings of your choice, which I am yet to be disappointed with, and a side of pita bread. If you are ever in Philly, or even better on Walnut street somewhere in Center city, this place is a must try! My only complaint is the lack of plentiful seating due to small space and their hours (why must you close at 7PM!!) Otherwise, a fantastic place, definitely a hidden gem in Philadelphia.
Great food for the family and big groups. Very authentic chinese food. Tasty and delicious! Great duck and wonton noodle soup! Hot and sour soup is one of the best in my opinion.

8:45pm on a Sunday. 4 empty tables outside. See the hostess about a table. Her response "we really don't like to seat tables outside unless you're eating. Are you eating?"

Me - "margaritas and chips/salsa."

Hostess - "I'm sorry, you just need to go up to the bar."

Me - "oh, ok"

4 empty tables and 845 on a Sunday night....have fun but not with me. Piss poor service if I've ever seen it. Coming from a 17 year hospitality business veteran.
Great first impression! Jack (the owner) was friendly and courteous. I'll definitely be back.

Skill of nail techs range from amazing to subpar. The one amazing tech no longer works there, and the other techs I've tried did a sloppy and slow job of cutting my cuticles and polishing my nails. They carry a good selection by OPI, Essie, and others, but not the latest colors. Pricing is fair, salon is impeccably clean, and they use yummy smelling Bath & Body Works lotions. I'd go back in a pinch, but defintely not my favorite salon of the bunch.

OMG. This place is the best brunch spot EVER. Bring your friends from out of town here to blow their mind completely. Whether you have a sweet tooth or you're in the mood for something savory, this spot is perfect. I had the vegan pecan pie French toast and it was SO GOOD I was amazeddd. The display is beautiful and definitely an Instagram worthy post.

These kind of quick grab places are always tough to review.  It's just a hot dog, really.

Are the dogs themselves anything special?  No.

Are the topping options awesome?  Yes.

Will I come here again?  Probably.

There's always nights where you need to grab something quick on the way to a show, don't feel like making or sitting down for dinner, whatever.  Underdogs is worth throwing into that Shake Shack/HipCity/Honeygrow/etc. mix every now and then.

I've been to Raw for both lunch and dinner.  Two very different experiences and atmospheres.  Both were fantastic meals.

My wife and I went there for a last minute dinner on a late Spring Saturday night and sat at the bar (there is a bar and a sushi bar that are connected - if you sit at the bar, sit at the non-sushi part of the bar with more room and easier drink access).  We ordered some fantastic and creative sushi rolls, sensational sashimi, soup, sushi, a bunch of beers, and more fun rolls.  We love it all.  The lighting was perfectly dim, the music was very cool and loud enough to be "lounge-like" but not too loud to talk.  The bar tender gave us great advice for my beers and the rolls.  We had a fantastic dinner, we were not rushed, and we felt like we did very well for approximately $120.  We would go back there in a minute over Morimoto.

When I went there for lunch with a colleague of mine, we ordered a bunch of the things each of us liked best - whether it be sushi, sashimi, rolls, soup, salad, whatever.  All was very good and fresh.  For lunch, the service was fast, and the server was very helpful.  The mood of the place is not at all like dinner.  It is a great place to go for a business lunch as it is a bit expensive for just a lunch.

Recently used Z Dog Walkers to care for our cat, Professor Bad@ss while we were away for 6 days.  We never had to hire a cat sitter before, but since we are new to the area, we had to hire someone.  Natalie came to our apartment at a time that was very convenient for us to meet our cat, discuss her care requirements, look over vet records, and to get our keys.    Natalie is clearly very good with animals, and Professor came out to greet her and flopped right down for belly rubs.  
We paid the $20 administration fee in cash and wrote a check for the cat sitting services at our initial meeting, although full payment was not required upfront.  Daily rates were in line with other cat sitters that we checked out online.
We came home from our trip and Professor was happy and healthy.  She definitely had a fun time with Natalie.  I had to call Natalie from Florida because we needed a copy of our key back as soon as we got back to town and she left it in the apartment for us, just as we asked her to.  We already booked Z Dog Walkers for another trip in January.  We would definitely recommend this company to anyone looking for a reliable, friendly, capable cat sitter.

So, Ive been a member for the last 4 years but heard they were allowing day passes with limited detail to go along with it without having to buy a membership. 

Well this place has literally turned into a joke. 1000 dollar cabanas??? Do they think this place is the Wynn encore Day Club? 1000. Dollars?  I can get a flight to vegas and back with hotel and pool acess for less than that... 

No chairs for day pass members even if you offer to pay for one. And there is 50 availbale all stuffed in a corner. Good thing i didnt drop 400 on a membership. Awful. Towels are 10 dollars to rent. No drink specials/buckets... food and drinks expensive... 

I thought this place couldnt get any worse until I walked into the new Monarch. Total money pit nightmare. Instead of dropping a 1000 here go to St. Barts. Much nicer...

This past weekend I had to get my bike fixed on the spot. Community bikes was recommended to me by an employee of another bike shop that didn't have time to fix it for me that day. Everyone at community was really friendly and Brian (who fixed my wheel) even stayed after hours so I would have my bike for work the next day. Best of all, I only paid $40 when I was originally looking to spend around $100, while still feeling like I'm riding a brand new bike. He even topped off my tires.  I'll definitely be back here.

I'm so glad they're here!

Palm Tree has been a blessing to the neighborhood.  They have so many interesting foods!  The deli is great - quick, clean, delicious, and offers many different options for wraps, paninis, hoagies, and salads.

The store has a lot of different offerings for food as well.  I usually start cooking at home and then think to myself  "this dinner would be better with.... (insert random ingredient here)..." and pop over to the deli and find exactly what I'm looking for - assorted cheeses, spices, veggies, fruits, soups, sushi, yogurt, hummus, chips, crackers, ice cream, cookies, etc.  They also carry a lot of organic products, healthier chips like lentil chips, pop corners, etc.  A really, really big variety of food for a small corner shop and deli.  

The staff is super friendly and recognize me, my husband, and my room mate and great us by our first name when we walk in (and we never go together).  They go out of their way to make everyone feel welcome.

Great French onion soup and a well-curated wine list. The menu selection isn't tremendous but everything is well-prepared and there's something for everyone. We had amazing service--just the right level of touch.

Wow - this place is amazing. We didn't have a reservation but we went in as soon as it opened. There was a few of us that did the same exact thing and it was actually somewhat packed for the restaurant just opening. 

We got seated at the bar, and had their Hummus with Daily Topping. Wow. This is literally the best hummus that I have had. It was so creamy and smooth, and the pita bread that it came with was delicious. After the appetizer, they actually gave us a complimentary cauliflower which was pretty good. We then transitioned to the Kibbe Naya (raw lamb wrapped up in lettuce), which was good, but I wish there was more meat or maybe less lettuce. The crowning moment of this experience was the lamb merguez - which was perfectly made. It was tender, smokey, and made me wish I ordered another one of it.

I was headed down to Vintage for a couple glasses of wine after work one night and passed the vague + logo outside of Apothecary.  That was intriguing enough to get me and my friend inside.
The garb of the doorman was very appropriate to the bohemian europe feel of the place, although I'm not sure if he was dressed like that because it was his uniform or because it was the day after halloween.
But I digress.
The setup of the lounge was very nice.  Long couches ran along the wall with tiny tables and tea lights.  There is an outside deck with space heaters to make it more comfortable in the colder weather.  Everything had a nice matte, almost art-deco feel to it.  The downstairs had a bar and dining area, but we didn't see much of it as we went upstairs.
The signature cocktail list is very exciting and refreshing, with non-traditional ingredients such as elderberry, yohimbe, fresh ginger, cayenne, green tea infused gin, valerian root , gingko, aloe, and things with the words 'liniment' and 'tincture' attached to them.
I tried the French Buck, which was a spicy ginger-based cocktail made with absinthe, spicy and delicious.  I ended up with two of these by the time the night was over.  I also had the Immunity Idol and the Dermis Fantasticus.  The former was very light and fruity and the latter features muddled cucumber, which makes it cool and refreshing.  My friend had the Booty Collins, which featured the aforementioned tea infused gin, cayenne, passion fruit, and agave.  Also very spicy and delicious.  Every cocktail I tasted was outstanding.
For a light snack, we tried the miso-marinated chicken lettuce wraps, which were fresh and tasty served with an excellent ponzu sauce.

Some minor complaints:
-There was a dj playing which I'm fine with, but his music seemed a little generic, although I did hear Radiohead once in his rotation.
-My friend went to the bartender to ask for a quick round of shots after last call was made and he turned her down then served other patrons a few moments later, which was pretty lame.  In retrospect, at that point we probably didn't need shots anyhow.

Overall, I'm a sucker for a clever menu and a cool looking place to chill and this was definitely it.  Be warned though that the average price for a cocktail is around 11 or 12 dollars, but their beers are all around 3 or 4 dollars per draft so there's a good balance.
I really enjoyed myself and look forward to going back soon.

For $8 a pop I'd expected more than 90% noodles. I could barely even tell if there's sauce in this because i dont see any red coconut sauce....now that i think about it, they probably forgot it and the coconut taste was from the chicken...How many thai basil did i get in here? I saw probably 2 leaves. Sure i wasn't expecting a truly authentic stir fry but they were pure stingy with the ingredients.

I could walk into any asian place and get something more for my buck than this place, except okay, this place is 'healthier' in the sense that there's less oil. All in all, im paying way more for way less. Not a fan.

The food was very disappointing.  We ordered the nachos as an appetizer - they came out at the same time as the rest of the meal and were ok.  The pork tacos were inedible.  Lots of fat and grease.  The chicken tacos were only slightly better, just very poor flavor for the chicken.  The burritos were pretty bad too, an odd mix of rich, very little chicken and lots of rice and refined beans.  The chocolate brownie for dessert was ok, but the caramel cream accompanying it was just not very good.  Overall, very disappointing.

Where do I start! The staff there are super friendly, we didn't have a reservation but we're able to seat us with only a 15-20 minute wait. The food was great. The appetizer chicken wing was delicious shared it between 3 and was filling. It's BOYO!! So bring your favorite bottle and indulge on some great food

Words can't express how excited I was when I saw a new restaurant was opening up a mere two blocks away from my front door.  "HORRAY!" was thrown around more than a few times.  However, I'm ashamed to say that after all of that excitement, I just finally made it down to Alla Spina.  

I had heard mixed reviews of their food, but fellow Yelper Shannon P. has eaten there more than a few times so I figured, if she likes it there--I will too.  The man friend and I decided to try out this local spot before heading out to see a play.  To start, we ordered the beef fat fries.  These came with a side of ketchup and mayo.  They were crispy and delicious, and despite being fried in beef fat, weren't overly greasy.  I also ordered the Birmosa (peroni and mango juice)... which was a pretty tasty mix.  

For our main dishes, the man friend ordered the pork tenderloin with seafood fritto misto
and chimichurri mayo, and I had the potato gnocchi with gorgonzola fonduta.  Both tasted great, but we were definitely underwhelmed by the portion sizes.  Not to be a fat kid or anything but... I felt like my gnocchi could have been a side dish.  I was making a conscious effort to slowly eat the pasta one by one putting my fork down between bites so that I wouldn't be finished too soon. It gets pretty awk when you're done your food and the other person is still chowing down.  The pork tenderloin was perfectly cooked, and the fried seafood surrounding it was delicious... but again, the portions were tiny.  Gotta give a shoutout to the chimichurri mayo though... I just wish I had some bread to dip in it.  Seriously, it was da bomb.

We plan on coming back to try Alla Spina again for happy hour... maybe when we aren't working off of Erin Express hangover hunger?

I have frequented this restaurant multiple times with my coworkers for lunch during restaurant week. The staff is always pleasant and appear eager to please. We have never had to wait long for reservations and always have a large party of 6-7 ppl (large for lunch time). 

The decor is decorative and bold and actually have wonderful art which is quite reminiscent of Italy. Having enjoyed Venice, Rome, Florence, and Pisa I think the decor is perfect and a true depiction of the various coast along the shores of Italy :).

The food is always served hot and fresh. The seafood is fresh and the food always have bold and aromatic flavors that tantalize your pallet before the food reaches your mouth. The veal is moist, tender and bold with flavor. The side accompaniments are paired perfectly. 

The dessert is sweet, filling and enjoyable. You must stop in for a good meal, with great indoor scenery and flavors.

I made it to a show here before it closes as a venue! I am grateful for this as this place was definitely an experience in itself. As everyone else has mentioned the church is hot as b***s, its all high schoolers, and its smelly, but its definitely one of a kind. 

My boyfriend and I got here pretty early for Com Truise last week, stupidly thought we could buy beers at the venue and realized we went to a show in literally a church basement. We then walked around the corner grabbed a six pack and walked back into the church. I did not know you aren't supposed to bring beer in? Oh well. 

Once the show started, we were right in the front, the crowd was mostly 15 year olds, but everyone was really polite and not pushy. We gradually got sweatier and sweatier and then luckily the show was over, I thought the sound was decent no complaints and the bathrooms were definitely better than some other Philly venues (Kung Fu Necktie I'm looking @ you). 

All in all the opportunity to see a show here reminded me of the time I was 13 and went into CBGB to get a t-shirt before it closed. This place is a Philly legend and its amazing to think that Arcade Fire played in this very church basement many years before they closed out Coachella.

Turns into a dance club at night on Saturdays and they keep it oddly very very dark. They really do not have the space for a dance club and it is very crowded and loud. If you want to see and have some space go to McGillians across the ally.
I've been here a few times but I tried the vegan beef tacos and boy were they amazing! Tastes just like the real thing!

Passed this place on my way back to my hotel. I love Mac and cheese so I thought I'd have to stop in before I left. I ordered the Barclay Basil Chicken Mac and cheese and I thought it was really good! It could have been better if it was hot and not 'warm.' t could have used more chicken though. I think I only had a few tiny tiny pieces in mine. The crunchy potato chip bits on top were nice and the basil was good. Most of the time basil pesto can be gross unless you make it from scratch with fresh ingredients. If there was more it was finely chopped and hidden. Since they only have Mac and cheese it felt more like a guilty snack rather than a meal. I was starving and had a very early breakfast (granola bar) and ate this at 230 and I'm still a little hungry. Also, it may have been $9.75 and a lot of other reviewers are saying it's ridiculously overpriced but the price didn't really influence my decision. It's not like I'm stopping there every day. For a guilty snack go for it!

after getting a post-exam craving for spaghetti and meatballs I ordered some from Allegro's. It comes with choice of soup or salad--I got the minestrone--and garlic bread. A couple of things: 

1. The spaghetti was a HUGE portion--could have fed 2 hungry people or 3 peckish people. Literally. I'm a big eater, so this means something. 

2. The minestrone was really lame, but I thought it would be safer than a wilty iceberg lettuce side salad (but that's just speculation) 

3. the meatballs were meh. they were those squishy kinda cafeteria tasting meatballs.

4. They didn't put enough tomato sauce, but if you have some on hand in your kitchen, no harm no foul, I say. 

5. It came with 2 garlic bread twisty-type rolls. They were aight, but i was pleasantly surprised that there were 2. 

Anyways. Filled me up and I still have a lot left for tomorrow's lunch and dinner. Wasn't bad, wasn't great, but I didn't have to lift a finger, so that's the world we live in!

Location, location, location. Perch Pub is in the middle of everything. It's on a second story overlooking Broad Street, catty-cornered from the beautiful Academy. The experience and setting saves this place. I went for an early bite to eat on a Friday night before catching a show at the Academy and was pleasantly surprised by the food and draft selection. The grilled cheese was a highlight: nothing fancy, but hit the spot. The sandwiches all looked creative but not too creative, a fine line to walk for any restaurant. It was late Fall and they had a seasonal, local pumpkin ale. Overall, that drink was my favorite part of the meal. 

I walked away from Perch confident in a 4-star rating, so confident that I wanted to bring my girlfriend back after a show at PTC a few months later. It was a Saturday night so we were braced for a large crowd. And it was definitely hopping - which great for them. As we waited for either a table or a spot at the bar, we pressed ourselves up against the wall to be out of the way. Unfortunately, that didn't stop a harried waitress from saying, "Um, you're in the way," as she rudely brushed past my shoulder. We moved next to the bar and as she passed back through again, she visibly rolled her eyes at us.

I'd like to give it a third try. I'm hoping for a best out of 3 experience, in which case I'd restore the 4-star rating. But honestly, no matter if there are 2 or 200 people in the place, your attitude toward your customers should be welcoming. They have a great location, a good menu, and a fun vibe; but if they continue to act like they want my business, I'll be more than happy to go elsewhere.

Most of the food here is decent. It'll scratch the itch if you need Mexican food. But the chicken green chile enchiladas are EXCELLENT. Like truly phenomenal, crave when i dont have them, would go out of my way for them excellent. The chicken soup is good too, especially when youre feeling under the weather.  Also everyone who works there is so so nice, they gave me the wrong thing in a takeout order once and they were so incredibly kind and helpful in correcting the error. Love this place!!

A little pub type restaurant on the outskirts of Old City.  Very reasonably priced and a wide variety of items on the menu.  Portions were huge.  Waitstaff was not particularly friendly, but got the job done.  The Roast beef itself was kind of dry on a hoagie (a grinder or sub depending on where you're from), but my friend had the pork and loved it.

It's a nice and romantic restaurant for a date. The staff are friendly and helpful. Their desert is amazing! Highly recommended the salted caramel budino! You won't regret! The only problem is that the portion size is a tab small.
Great venue for wedding. Food was great which is unusual for a wedding. Decorated beautifully.

Falafel sandwich with the grape leaves as a side was not only delicious and plentiful, but very affordable. This was my first time trying falafel and I was pleasantly surprised how good it was. I will be back with the same order next time I'm on Market St!
The food was bland and terrible, it is over priced and the customer service is lacking.

This is your stereotypical Chinese restaurant -- very loud and not so great service. But the dim sum is solid and the wait isn't too terrible. The only thing that peeves me is that they don't use a number system. You just wait in line.

Food is mediocre and the prices are high. I enjoyed the oysters, but the fish and potatoes were not well prepared.

Also this location has been cited with numerous health code violations.

http://www.philly.com/philly/health/special_reports/clean-plates/Philly-puts-Cheesecake-Factory-on-notice-drops-suits-against-McCormick-and-Schmicks-and-Joy-Tsin-Lau-Dirty-Dining-Clean-Plates.html
Hidden gem in Center City . First experienced it in DC and I'm so happy it's here in Philly . Fresh ingredients and open late

Philly is full of surprises. Indeblue was a great surprise in the middle of downtown.  Atmosphere was warm and intimate with low lighting and Earth toned walls.  Service was on point, helpful, quick and polite. The courses moved easily in and out of our table. We are the  chef's tasting and got to try five items plus dessert for $45.  Every single thing tasted authentically Indian (my Indian wife was with me) and perfectly made.  
My only point of disappointment was the cocktails. We tried the Anjeer and the Bombay Muraba and both recipes were hollow, too sweet and lacked alcohol.  I recommend sticking with your trusted cocktail instead.
Overall, extremy good experience and high recommendation.

Three of us had dinner at Will BYOB last week and we all left hungry, including a friend that literally eats like a bird. The portions are artfully arranged but don't eat here if you have a normal appetite. As for the food, it's like so many other new trendy expensive(for what you get) places that try to intimidate you with silly descriptions on the menu that make you wish you had Julia Child along to intrepret. Meanwhile, it's mostly cheap ingredients with fancy (in this case French) names. And when did "Skate" become something expensive and worthy of a $27 price tag? 
        The waiter we had was so over-the-top pretentious that we almost walked out immediately but it was raining and we had nothing else reserved on a Sat. night so we drank our wine and tried our best to tune him out. Parking anywhere near this place is a needle-in a haystack deal at best.
         We all agreed it was the last time we'd eat there. I'd say don't have the "wool" (or skate) pulled over your eyes. Save your dollars($$$$) for something better; much, much better, almost anywhere else.This is Philadelphia. Shame on Will!

This literally was the worst cheese steak I've ever had. Chinese take-out cheese steaks are way beyond better than this place. I don't understand why there was so much hype with travel tv shows and youtubers. The meat taste like boiled meat no flavor and the bread was stale, cheese wiz taste just like cheese wiz, not much more to add but extremely disappointing for driving hours to philly to try "authentic" cheese steaks.

Soba noodles good, but soy sauce was too much, too salty.

Had to rinse the noodles with water and then re-heat them.

Reasonable price, good portion with a decent amount of vegetables.

Got my nose pierced here. It was a bit expensive ($50 for the stainless steel and $140ish for the gold) but they were thorough and clear. Friendly staff and clean place.
One concern was the half an hour wait time which turned into 45 minutes. Anyway, I was satisfied overall.

Oh Anthropologie, how I loath to love you. I can't even allow myself to go into this store unless I'm prepared to empty my wallet. The last time I went, I accompanied two of my friends, and left my wallet at home...good thing, because I fell in love with a dress that I surely would've bought, if I had my trusty credit card on me.

I read in Philadelphia Magazine a while ago, that the man who created this store understood what women want to experience when they're shopping. They want it to be an experience, and be made to feel special...well, he nailed it on the head! Everything in that store is awesome and SPECIAL. I want EVERYTHING in there. The clothes are cool, stylish, and unique, and all of the home accessories are so warm and inviting....

I've actually considered trying to get a part time job there to feed my Anthropologie addiction. Working there may allow me to wear pretty dresses, but being in the store that long might place me in a cardboard box on the side of the street....might be worth it if I have their bedding though :)

One of my favorite lunch spots across from my job. They have a great selection of smoothies and bubble tea as well as diet dishes. Very cute, modern tech decor with comfy seating. 

My only negative comments are that they charge convenience fee for card services under $10, and during the lunch rush, service can get pretty busy.

they are open later than most places in chinatown

they have beer

the extra large bowl of pho is literally the same size as my bathroom sink, filled halfway with meat, noodles, and a broth that i wish would have just that much more pazzazz = this costs $8.49 and is the size that would feed a family in rural vietnam...

Came here for my birthday dinner with a bunch of friends and had an enjoyable time, as I've been meaning to try this place!

Overall, a very upscale, elegant vibe to the place.  You walk in immediately to the host/hostess stand, with a bar that wraps around the front and a larger dining area in the back.  Our table wasn't ready, so we hung at the bar for about 30min and had some drinks while waiting.

Their drink menu is certainly innovative, and I give them props for trying, but the inconsistency and some of the flavors do not mix well together. The best drink I had was the Veda G+T, a standard G+T, with large juniper bitters to give it some zest.  The other gin drinks (Fall Thyme in India and Royal Tea) did not taste good.

We were fortunate enough to try a lot of different dishes since there were many of us, and here's a quick recap.
The crispy spinach chaat was amazing - the crisp was real and it was super tasty.  The lassoni gobi was also awesome - deep fried cauliflower in a garlicy/tomato sauce...you can't go wrong.  The broccoli kebab was a complete miss - bland and had no flavors.  The hummus platter was solid - nothing crazy, but had a nice flavor/spice profile and the accompaniments were a great addition.

We shared a bunch of different entrees:
Paneer Makhani - was a bit surprised that they only have two paneer dishes on the menu, but this one was decent - nothing special or fancy, but hit the spot (as most paneer dishes do)
Chole (Chana Masala) - stay away from this...one of the saltiest dishes I've ever had...not sure if the chef messed up or if this is how it always is, but this was too salty for everyone
Baigan ka Salan - the eggplant was cooked nicely and the curry was tasty

The bread basket was a delicious assortment and they were all thoroughly and properly cooked - not too burnt or soft.  And their rice...wow!  I know it sounds weird/silly, but their rice is so good - perfect texture, steamed, not lumpy, and melt in your mouth good.

Overall, a decent place and definitely a place I'll come back to, especially since I know what to order (and not order).

Hubby and I stayed here last Saturday night on a whim.  We had to go to Philadelphia and since we didn't have the kid, we figured we'd spend the night if we could find a room.  

Overall, this isn't the nicest Marriott that I've ever stayed at, but it isn't bad either.  In fact I'd place it above the one in Georgetown in DC.  The lobby is very large and nicely appointed.  The check in staff was nice and very accommodating, he switched our room for us twice based on requests.  

We were allowed to check in early, which was another nice touch and we were able to get a room on the top floor.  The view was amazing.  We either had an accessible room, or the rooms are just very spacious but in any event, we had plenty of room.  I like that you needed to use your room key in order to operate the elevator which made me feel safer.  
                           
The location is amazing in terms of convenience but if you aren't used to east coast cities, you might find it a little, shall we say, scary.  Living in Baltimore, however, has taught me how to duck and weave, so to speak!  The bums in Philly are quite aggressive though, so be prepared to be cursed at if you ignore them or move around them when they walk up to you (one of them called me ignorant as f* when I moved away from him as he zombie walked toward me to ask me for money).  

We didn't try the bar, restaurant or room service so no comment there.  I did appreciate that there was a Starbucks on site.  

The bad: The fricking $48 parking fee!  That sucks!  Also, I personally feel that the days of charging for wifi in a hotel this expensive are long over.  

Side note: The Paralyzed Veterans of America used this hotel as the host hotel for their annual National Veterans Wheelchair Games.  There were parazyled veterans everywhere!  It was really nice to see how accommodating the hotel, staff and even guests were to them all.  It was truly spirit warming to see all of them and to chat with them.
Good food, nice cozy setting. Great for lunch in center city. Very well priced too. I had the turkey burger. It was a bit messy but still good!

I've made two recent stops in here and I've been surprised to realize that the quality of their pizza has gone up. I always thought it was pretty good but it's up there among my favorite pizza places in Philly now for sure.

The slices are thin, delicious and the different types of available pizza toppings are also very creative. I tend to stick with more basic stuff, as in, I'll always grab a slice of pepperoni and then try whatever else. This has ranged from buffalo chicken, barbecue chicken, pepper steak and onion - all of which have have been fantastic. They've even got some special Sicilian slices too. When I'm craving carbs I've definitely gotta try a slice of mac 'n cheese which I spotted on my last visit...

Must stop while you are in Philly. Tons of amazing vendors all in one place. Definitely stop by DiNic's for the roast pork with broccoli rabe. Beiler's deserves all it's fame and glory, too. Every donut is incredible. These shops won't disappoint!

This is one of the biggest hidden gems in University City. I went for the first time today and was in love. The owners, Kay and George, are the most lovely people I have ever met. Kay learned our names and treated us like family. The organic coffee was excellent (normally I need a LOT more sugar to like coffee) and the atmosphere feels like home. 

Not the place to go if you want a quick cup, but a place to go if you want to break the Penn bubble for awhile and do some work or just read a book. Highly, highly recommend.
Drinks are alright, but the aesthetic is pure bliss. Love the retro look and the Bocci Ball outside was a hit. Bowling was fun. Will definitely come back again

I'd say don't ever come here, but if you like being treated like a piece of garbage, then this is the place for you! We got to garage at 5:00 for our reserved table. Our first friend to show up was bringing her baby since she had no other choice and was only staying for one drink. She was turned around immediately at the door, in a way that definitely could've been handled better. You would've thought she was walking in with a bomb strapped to her chest. The law in the state of PA is after 9:00 no kids, but I guess they can just make up whatever rules they like, whatever. So after that, I went over to play ski ball. We got 2 games in and on our 3rd, the ski ball machine ate our dollar. "No biggie! I'll just tell the bar real quick I'm sure it happens all the time." Wrong again. Although it does happen "all the time", according to the MANAGER (yeah this is the same manager that screamed at a customer to "say fucking excuse me next time." Real nice. And he did say excuse me.), but they can't give any money back that the machine has eaten since they don't own the machines. Absurd. The company that I work for also doesn't own the machines, but when that happens, we give the customer their dollar back! Just make a note of it for the vending company. So we were now out a dollar and having a terrible time. So I suggest maybe we try playing pool. I go to the bar with a $20 bill looking to break into smaller bills so I can get change. The bartender could not have acted any more inconvenienced. She said "there's literally a change machine right there." I said "I don't need $20 in quarters, I need smaller bills." So she slams my smaller bills on the bar and goes back to texting on her phone. Real nice. Finally, when our whole party had gotten there, I asked if I could push the two tables (that we had called ahead and gotten reserved for us) together because everyone wanted to talk to each other. Guess what the answer to that was? You got it! "Nah. Can't do that." Wow. God forbid you have to move the tables back 2 feet when we leave. This was the worst experience I've probably ever had at any establishment in the city, and I definitely have no problem never giving money to the entitled children that work here again. Bummer.

Quite possibly one of the worst delivery experiences I have ever had with a Philadelphia eatery. I signed up for yelp just to express my dissatisfaction and frustration (I look desperate huh? Lol)

11:15 - My girlfriend and I order food for delivery (we are hungry because partied for st patties day!)
12:30 - we call and the lady who answered the phone said that they had been busy and the food was on it's way to us. 
1:00 - we still hadn't received our food. We offered to come pick the food up (how nice of us lol) and they insisted that it was on the way. 
1:30 - we call again and said we can pick up and still nothing
2:00 - we cancel
2:45 - we get a call from the delivery guy (wtf!?!?)

I'm sure they were busy and I understand but please be honest when dealing with customers. Do not tell me it's out for delivery when it isn't. If it takes 3 and a half hours for delivery you shouldn't be in the delivery game. Ok my thumbs are tired of typing

I came by myself a bit after 9 the Friday after Thanksgiving. Hard to find the place -- look for the 112 sign, nothing says Franklin. Little but of a wait to get in and get a seat at the bar, but I'd guess YMMV depending day and time of visit. I found the bar tenders friendly but but gregarious. There are only 4 seats at the bar and the setup isn't so good for watching the drinks get made either; so if you are not by yourself, you actually want a table or booth. Drinks are tasty, but only about medium strength. Reasonable value at $15. I had Train Dreams, 220 Million Used Car Salesman, and Autumn Milk Punch. Punch pictured -- that one was really something special, one of the best cocktails I've ever had!

I contacted Josh about getting a deadbolt installed in a door and he got back to me within 20 minutes with a quoted price. He kept me in the loop while he was on his way to my house and got the job done in a quick matter! I would definitely recommend and I will keep him in mind for the next time I need some work done at my house!

This place was awesome. I got the tuna stone bowl meal and my daughters got sushi. It was amazing!! So good! They had a groupon that they honored without any stipulations other than sir gong forty dollars minimum. It was well worth the effort to get there on vacation.

Went here once for dinner and loved it!  I thought the food was very flavorful.  It was good for a group where we could order a bunch of stuff and try everything.  I recommend sitting upstairs in the bar area.  Drinks were good too.
really really good.  all home made stuff.  expensive yes but delicious.  yum yum yum

I went here back in 2016 and got my first chemical peel. I didn't know much about them and the Aesthetician Jamye was very informative. My face was very soft and felt less clogged. She took her time and did a lot of extraction on my nose and the surrounding area. The results lasted a long time. I started doing light peels at home myself. I had wanted to go again some months ago and unfortunately she no longer works here. At the time the receptionist told me they only had one aesthetician. I needed an appointment within a certain time frame so I had to pass it up.

Ok, so I placed my order on 02/24/18. The confirmation email said I shipping would take 3-5 business days. I knew it would take longer because of their Facebook fame so I waited. After a few days, 7 business days,  I thought it was strange that I never got a tracking number or an email saying my package has shipped. So I called Yadain, and spoke with a young lady.This is the only time I have ever spoken with her, all of my other dealings were with another male rep. So I called on March 6 to check on my order.  I called the young lady gave me a tracking number and said my order will ship that day. She said the tracking number may not work the same day we spoke because it hasn't entered into the system yet. I said ok, I waited some more.

 The tracking number still hadn't worked after A WEEK. In short, the tracking number NEVER WORKED. I spoke with the male rep MULTIPLE TIMES. I told him my tracking number didn't work. I told him that its important that I'm able to track this package because I live in an apartment, they ship through FedEx, and FedEx Likes to just leave your package at your door, where anyone can walk by and take it. Instead of leaving it at the rental office. 

There was one time I called and the guy told me that the ingredients  had been on back order so it hadn't shipped. This is AFTER they told me it shipped already. HOW?? So then in our next convo he was shipping it as we spoke. Another few days, nothing. He told me the tracking number didn't change I can use the same one. The tracking number never worked. By this time, its late March and I placed the order in February. I still TO THIS DAY, have not received my package. I spent good money on everything I bought. In the end I'm empty handed. They lied about the timeline of shipping my order, they eventually told me they had two different tracking numbers on file and NEVER gave me the other tracking number. They told me that my order may be coming in two different boxes, I got neither. I tried to support black businesses and this is what happened.

We went for Fishtown restaurant week.  We chose our meals from the three-course menu ($25 per person).  

The atmosphere is great with a friendly family running the business.  We were welcomed upon entering, even though the place was already crowded.  After ordering drinks, we were given bread and a home-made pesto sauce.  Both were delicious and great to hold us over until our appetizers arrived.  Also, Joe, the owner/bartender offered to take our order before a large party to ensure we wouldn't have to wait even longer for our meals.

We got mussels and clams to start.  They were so fresh, we could still taste the salt water!  They were cooked with garlic and herbs that helped us finish the bread we had leftover.  The portions were generous, and it was nice to share.

I got the stuffed cabbage for my main.  There were three large stuffed cabbages with spinach mashed potatoes, sweet corn, carrots, and green beans.  It looked and tasted home cooked.  There were no pretenses here - what you ask for is what you get.  I also came home with a portion for lunch tomorrow!  For anyone looking for a traditional Eastern European meal, I would suggest the stuffed cabbage.

My husband had the Yugoslavian burger.  It is served without a bun.  The meat was grilled perfectly, and it was served with big slices of tomato with diced raw onion and some sort of goat cheese.  It also was delicious.  Although it sounds so simple, it was done just right so that thenatural flavors worked together perfectly.

The third course was a walnut cake with chocolate syrup.  There was no choice for dessert, but that only proves that they only serve what they make themselves.  Simple cake, but very nice after a big meal.

Before we left, Joe told us to stay just a big longer, and he served us each a shot of home-made pear liqueur.  It was incredibly sweet and so good!  It was a nice touch.  I didn't get the feeling that Joe serves this after every meal, but there was a big crowd for restaurant week, and I think he had a few new customers in the dining room.  

I would definitely suggest Jovan's for a long, home-cooked meal experience.  It has a feeling of eating at your grandmom's house on a Sunday night.  I will definitely go back.  No complaints, just compliments.

Ambiance and service were amazing. We weren't blown away by the food but the place was packed so what is the incentive to change? We did feel bad for the server who had to recite a special menu that featured  about 9 specials, at least. At that point mide as well type them on a sheet.

The smoke meat was delicious just without the barbecue sauce. I am a fan. The sides selection was ok but needed more varieties. I did not care for their German potato salad. I realized I don't care for vinegar in my potato salad. Collard greens was awesome. Almost as good as mine.  Will definitely be back for more smoke meat.

Libertine recently opened up in my neighborhood. When I found out that Chef Derek was the owner I was very keen to give it a try. I remember enjoying loads of meals at his former establishment Derek's in Manayunk. We decided to try out happy hour and were pleasantly surprised to see an extensive drinks list (beer, cocktails, and wine)--something for everyone. For nibblers, we opted for something light and went with the crab toast and hand-cut fries, both were delicious! 

Side note: The establishment was dog-friendly. The hostess brought out a dog bowl and several treats for our little furball.

Don't be fooled by the staged photos, the decor is decent, but the space is extremely small, limited seating, tables/chairs reserved for customers whom never make an appearance. In addition, there's a cover charge just for entry. 

In addition, the ventilation is basically non-existent, in short, been there no need to go back...

I have been wanting to stop in to the new Zavino on Drexel's campus for a while, and came by for dinner on a (really) quiet Saturday night.

We started with the ricotta of the day (it was a cow's milk ricotta with a citrus honey oil) and the rosemary flatbread. The bread was salty and flaky, and the ricotta (especially paired with the citrus honey) was incredibly flavorful and silky smooth. 

We also ordered the ricotta-stuffed meatballs, which were moist and tender and dressed in a delicious tomato sauce.

For our entree, we split "The Joey" which had pork sausage, mozzarella, and chili flakes. The crust was perfectly crispy, event charred in spots, which added a nice smoky flavor, adding to the flavor of the sausage. 

Service was very good, although I'd be curious to see how it is on a busy night.

Amazing! I was a little skeptical after reading the other reviews about the service, but I had a great experience ordering pick up. I was told my food would be ready in 20 minutes and it was already done when I arrived (but was still hot!). When I went to pay, the cashier noticed that one of my items had a price written on it that was a dollar higher than it was supposed to be, so they fixed it accordingly. Not to mention the food was delicious!!!
I don't know why this place has so many bad reviews about service- the employees were all attentive and respectful, and I didn't have to wait for my food at all.
My favorite part was all the vegetarian options on the menu! There were veggie burgers, veggie cheesesteaks, and lots of falafel. There were a bunch of meat alternatives in the freezer section, too. Love this place!

All I can say is wow. We came here last minute bc I saw meatballs and housemate pasta for our almost 5 yr old. I had the royale and it was perfect the fries oh my god. To die for. My son ended up with the chicken sausage and ate every piece! My husband also had the rigatoni and thought it was very good although he wants to go back when he can actually eat meAt. The service was great and food amazing. We will def be back.

There's (obviously) a lot of great Farmers' Markets in the city (we're so frickin lucky!), but this place is beyond my favorite. The setting is lovely, the variety is amazing, and the people are really great. Favorites of the moment: 
Betty's Tasty Buttons graham crackers
Griggstown Quail Farm chicken 
 Talula's Table sausages 
-- plus early spring produce.

We went as a party of two for restaurant week. We were seated in the back which was as tight as a can of sardines. The waiter told us that they split tables to accommodate more guest, and that it's normally not that tight. The food was amazing !!! We ordered the lamb sliders ( my fav ), the spicy chicken empanadas, the seafood paella, and the Oreo beignet. Great food !!!

Yes, Stephen Starr restaurants can be overhyped, but I left this colorful one feeling pretty content:

First of all, let's start with the decor.
+1 for red Pee Wee Herman-ish bike with bling on full display at bar (see picture).
-1 for bar top that looks like gross fruit cake

Next, let's move onto the food offerings (I am biased since I just tried dessert here and may have rated this place better or worse if I had the full fledged appetizer and entree):
+1 for excellent spiced rice milk! Yum!
+1 for yummy sopapillas (think puffy, doughy, slightly chewy donuts with cinnamon sugar on top) and chocolate dip
-1 for there being a little too much dulche de leche (caramel) within aforementioned sopapillas that would be better without. 

Last but not least:
+1 for fun drink menu with a cartoon guy with a sombrero stumbling around drunk labeled "boracho" (translating to drunk)
+1 for having a novelty black and white photo booth whose picture set of four only cost $4 buckeroos! (Other places charge more). We squeezed in 4 people! 

Also, though I'm glad they let us sit at the bar right away, I thought it was stupid that they would not let us sit at one of the PLENTIFUL empty tables because we were not ordering a full meal. But if you can overlook this nuisance, you should stop by and say "hola" to El Vez!

I like this place a lot. The interiors are neat, the bar looks cool, and the constant anime (or daft punk videos) playing on the screen behind the sushi bar is awesome. Great place for a date if you don't mind the noise.

Drinks-wise, the sake mojitos are bangin. Remember to ask for a double.

As for the food, pretty much everything I've eaten here has been ridiculously tasty. My favorite by far is the duck two ways. Roasted duck breast cooked perfectly, accompanied by duck served in a bao bun? All about it. The sauce is great, too.

Their special sushi rolls are creative and delicious; I love the shiro-aoi - spicy white tuna and avocado topped with hamachi and jalapeño. Other menu standouts include the braised beef short rib over udon noodles (meat is unbelievably tender), and the lettuce wraps.

Definitely a good place to try if you're down with S. Starr and you like Asian fusion.

Kim's is the best food truck to go to if you want something quick. Many items are already premade so all they do is just serve. If you are in a hurry, no matter how long the line is, you only need to wait about five to ten minutes tops.  However the bad part is that since the premade food has been marinating in the same pot for the whole day, different ingredients have the same texture. It sort of brothers me if I chew and feel the same texture again and again even if I can eating different vegetables. Yue kee's is made to order which is why I am pointing this out even if Kim's is a food cart.

This place does hit the spot if you are feeling cheap msg Chinese food. 

If you dont like fried tofu, do not get dishes with tofu in them. Go to yue kee's for soft tofu. The only dish to have soft tofu is their mapotofu.

Also they use the same.sauce for multiple dishes so do not be surprised if things taste the same even if there are different vegetables and ingredients in the dish.

Comcast 
/Xfinity is the biggest cable service rip-off ever! My current package is only supposed to cost me $189. per month.  Somehow, even though I make regular payments on my bill, it always seems to exceed the expected monthly payment amount. My bill seems to jump way up to the amount of $500 to $700. I have been making regular monthly payments on my bill; and whenever I asked why this keeps happening, no one at Comcast/Xfinity can give me a reasonable or acceptableanswer. 
On top of all of this, I keep trying to explain to them that there must be a mistake. I suggested that there must be some type of mistake; and maybe my new account is crossed with my old account, within the same apartment complex just a different apartment number,
 but no one is willing to check deeper into the matter. I have also complained about not being able to use my landline phone. I have a bundle package which includes internet, cable, and phone service. I have not been able to properly use my landline phone since the day they connected me, at my most recent location.  Every time I attempt to answer the phone or make a call, the call is dropped within minutes. When I ask them about crediting my account, they were only willing to credit me $12.97. Really?! After over a year of having this problem!?
Needless to say, I have decided never to use their services again. A friend of mine suggested that I get a fire stick, and that is just what I am going to do.  The one thing that Comcast / Xfinity is good for making sure they get their money!

Our favorite BYO, anywhere. 

Go early, because if you're there on the weekends, there will most likely be a wait. If you go during the week, I'm pretty sure they take reservations so call ahead!

This place is upscale casual...you can wear nice jeans or a dress/suit and still feel like you fit in. It is warm and cozy in the winter, and with the windows open in the spring and summer, it has the perfect breeze and feel. 

Mercato is LOUD. Don't bring anyone hard of hearing or uptight, because you can see/hear everything that is going on in the open kitchen, and the size of the place makes for a lively, loud atmosphere. None of which we mind! 

The food... we dream about. We have trouble going anywhere else, because a trip to the city deserves a trip to Mercato. Among our favorites are the Beemster cheese with caramel and chocolate (OMG)...the lobster ravioli (which makes you want to lick the plate clean if that would be acceptable), and the Parmesan crusted Veal Medallions. If you get there on a day when the tomato bisque is on the menu, get it! Excellent and will make you dream of a second helping. For dessert, get the Molten Chocolate Cake. You can be full, have room for nothing else, and still finish it because you just can't stop. 

Again, Mercato is our favorite BYO, anywhere. Check it out next time you are in Philly!

Nice place with an extensive collection of artwork from around the world. We came to see the Picasso exhibit, and toured the permanent collection as well. I'm not much of an art person, but we still spent better than 3 hours walking around, looking at everything. Very interesting place. 
Small coffee shop and gift shop on the lower level.

Not sure what to say...how about the BEST BREAKFAST EVER..the pancakes were amazing and my daughter said the french toast was the best she ever had...I can't say enough about the staff our waitress was awesome with our 4 kids....I can't forget how cool Redding is to walk around..
Super professional. Fast and efficient. The guys took me in as soon as I stepped foot in the shop - car was done in less than an hour. Clean shop with comfortable waiting area! Thank you
Their veggie miso ramen is awesome! Like it especially before long flight. One ramen bowl with gyoza, egg, bamboo shoot and tofu, amazing.

I cannot say a enough great things about my experiences with Sarah!  I have fibromyalgia and also long standing injuries.  I have spent years looking for the right people to help me and Sarah is one of those people!!   The first time I saw her I could not even stand up straight.  After 90 minutes with her, not only was I upright and mobile I had a large decrease in the pain was feeling.   She then took the time to show me some stretches that I could do to help maintain the body work she has done.  Outside of the body work to help reduce pain and increase my quality of life, last week my allergies were out of control, she did some lymphatic system work that that left me breathing and lifted the sinus fog I was feeling.  Again when I was leaving she gave me some techniques to help maintain the work she did.  Sarah has a vast knowledge of how the body works and uses a combination of techniques that addresses the issues you are having.  She listens and explains everything.  I am so glad I have met Sarah!

I've came 5 times in the afternoon and usually sit at the bar where I've never had a problem with service. They are gluten free friendly with their burgers and have never messed up my order. 
Earlier this week however I decided sit outside for lunch with my classmate. When we arrived it was pretty much empty and orders were taken promptly. Our burgers came out 20 mins later but both were missing side orders of avocado and fried egg. As the place began to fill up we waited for our waitress to come for 15mins before going inside and flagging someone else down. It took another 30 mins to get our sides out and by then our burgers were cold. 
We were told there was a problem with the waitress and that they'd accommodate our bill however. Originally they failed to do so but when we complained they promptly comped the side orders. While that was appreciated, overall we were only served water once while waiting for my card to be charged. Probably never coming back again during lunch time since service can't handle the load of people.

Great place for brunch! The portion sizes are great! The price is great too! You can get a full breakfast for under $10. It doesn't feel like you're in a diner. The atmosphere is modern. They provided great customer service! I have no complaints except they should have a speaker on the first floor that plays different genres of music as well. I will definitely come back!

Vernick is EASILY my favorite restaurant in the city, I've lived here for 4 years, been to easily over 100 restaurants, and Vernick is it for me. I've been here between 5 and 10 times and I've always be more than satisfied. We ptsd for the tuna poke, beef tartar on toast (my personal favorite), peas and bacon on toast, potatoes, the grilled octopus and romaine hearts with cheddar. All of which were amazing, but we did agree next time we might just get two beef tartar because its absolutely to die for. For desert we had key lime pie and their dark chocolate cake, which again, blew us both away. In the past we've tried almost everything else on the menu and I've never been disappointed with a single dish. 

If I had to recommend anything, I would say the beef tartar and the tuna poke. They are by far the best dishes I've had in the city. ***Also, tapas is the way to go here, they have so many great dishes, I wouldn't want to limit myself to one large dish. Service was also great, as always. We will return again and again!

I really love my apartment and the facilities. The apartment has great personality, the rent is reasonable and leasing staff are great. The sauna and adjoining restrooms could be better maintained. Handrails should be added to outdoor steps. But overall very nice and I have enjoyed my 1 1/2 years here and I plan to stay.
   My only disappointment is managements lack of responsiveness to the COVID pandemic. Some employees and the majority of residents don't wear masks. The elevators are 3 ft X 4 ft. I've been told it would be unreasonable to request another resident without a mask to wait while I ride the elevator alone. I am in a high risk population so I want to follow CDC and public health guidelines. 
   I have been able to ride the freight elevator but have to walk through the garbage room to access the elevator. They have accommodated by keeping the garbage room cleaner and even painted the inside of the elevator.

First time trying Yiro Yiro after a recommendation. I got the chicken Yiro on a pita which was really good. It was seasoned well. I misinterpreted the pita with tzatziki side as pita chips so the side ended up just being extra for me. Platter is well worth it, added an extra skewer to my order. Will definitely be back again.

The food is always OK, but I never find it to be worth the wait. The last time I went I had a huge hair baked into my biscuit. Safe to say I'll be trying other places (without 2 hour waits) for a while.
This place is a RIP OFF. Never buy the New Years Eve tickets. They are a RIP OFF. We bought tickets and waited outside for 45 minutes EVEN AFTER BUYING TICKETS. NEVER GO HERE

Great authentic Haitian/Caribbean food at a decent price. Try their "fritay" (fried food) platters on Friday and Saturday which feature plantains with choice of turkey, pork, goat, or oxtail. You won't be disappointed.

Pro's:  Chilean Sea Bass, Seaweed Salad, Vegetable Tempura, great service from waitstaff. 

Con's: Very small seating area, inconsistent food, mandatory gratuity if you use online discounts.

THE GOOD: Great food and service. Staff is always friendly and attentive. The lychee mojitos are to die for!!!  Although the menu is really small, the food is almost always amazing. The sea bass is phenomenal! The menu caters to those who love sushi. 

THE BAD: If you don't like sushi,  it's slim pickings. I am not a sushi eater, but the entrees were so good, I decided to try a sushi entree. I was a bit disappointed with the amount of food I received. For the price, I was hoping for a bowl of rice or soup to accompany the "sushi entree". The arrangement was great, there just wasn't enough food on the plate for the price. It looked like an appitizer, not a meal. Also, they are inconsistant with the way they serve their entrees. I ordered the sea bass in the past, and received grilled esparagus with it.  The combination was delicious. When my friend and I returned, we both requested grilled asparagus to come with the sea bass like the last time. We were told that wasn't a problem. When the food arrived, there was no asparagus on either plates.. Again, asparagus was requested. The asparagus that was eventually served was steamed, not grilled as requested. Also, it had no flavor, as if they didn't bother to season it. The Wagyu beef is way overpriced. The beef portion was small, tough, full of fat and gristle. Totally not worth $37. A complete disappointment, especially since half of it was inedible due to the reasons described.

We have found, if you use a groupon, they charge a mandatory 15% gratuity. If you ask me, it totally cancels the online discount. What is the point of offering a special if your going to make up fees to compensate for it? You (the patron) don't save a dime. You end up spending full price in the end. Don't bother purchasing online coupons for this restaurant. 

Otherwise,  the food is great. A lot of people come for sushi. I love this place and they have excellent drinks. Just wish they had more consistent food preparations. Also, that mandatory gratuity for patrons using an online voucher is obsurd and bad for business. JMO
Amazing food great prices nice culture, quiet setting Amazing waiter.

I'm never one to write reviews but I felt compelled as the service rendered here exceeded my expectations upon reading some of the reviews. I got gel manicure done, and what typically averages 45 mins-1 hour from other nail salons took only 25 mins (not rushed at all) and I'm very happy with the result!

To top it off, although I didn't have any cash to tip, the staff was so nice about it and didn't make me feel uncomfortable in any way. I will definitely be frequenting this place moving forward!

Got into town late after a long cross country trek. The person at the front desk recommended this place and it was only a couple minute walk. I should have walked right out. The bartender was swatting fruit flies the whole time. I won't even get into the meal only to say I have regretted it all day today.
It was awesome. Foie gras toast and duck salad were highlights but everything was excellent.

First time there. Food was good, crisp spring roll, vegetarian wonton soup was really good. I had vegetarian garlic chicken made with Seitan, it was ok, a Lil bland for me tho, even tho I asked for them to make it spicy, wish it had more of a garlic taste.  But i will try a few other dishes next time I'm in Philly, n they didnt have my fav...vegetarian hot n sour soup, I really wanted to try it.

I don't understand all of these positive reviews. I have eaten Rita's water ice several times and it was okay, if nothing compared to ice cream. Finally, I had the opportunity to stop by Rita's in Center City. We ordered two Blendinis and sat down to enjoy. The first bite, to my dismay, was sickly sweet with little flavor. I'm usually the kind of person who's never met a dessert he couldn't finish. But my Blendini was just awful. It reminded me of cheap ice cream mixed with water and a lot of extra sugar. I ate several more bites because I hadn't eaten dinner but it wasn't long before I couldn't stomach any more. I sampled my companion's as well which was no better. We tossed both cups and never looked back.

Stay away, just stay away. Your taste buds will thank you.

So I've lived a block away from this nail place for over 7 months and I finally went. I am mad at myself for waiting so long. The inside is really clean and super fancy looking. All of the employees are SUPER nice and friendly- even if you aren't getting something done by them. After looking at their IG and seeing all the amazing talent these guys have I don't think I'll have any need to go anywhere else. Quality work- great prices- clean spa- friendly people. Why go anywhere else in Mayfair and the surrounding areas!!!!

Im here about once a week and the volunteers at the Liberty USO are beyond phenomenal! Food is always great and Im always greeted with a smile no matter what the time of day.

Hard to believe this is a volunteer organization!

I hate to have to do this 2 nights in a row, but here we go again:

Does anyone bother to read ANYTHING other than what they see posted on a backlit screen on one social or anti-social media platform ANYMORE?!?!

SERIOUSLY!!!!

Below are my verbatim standard delivery instructions in recent months, with my phone number x'd out for obvious reasons:

Special Instructions:
"CALL  xxx.xxx.xxxx WHEN YOU GET HERE.... BRING PLASTICWARE AND NAPKINS - DEF! -:) DRIVER MUST BE WEARING FACE MASK OR ORDER WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED!!! NO EXCEPTIONS - PERIOD!!! THANK YOU!!!"

Does anyone reading this review have any problem understanding the basic, simple and direct language in these instructions?

Right, I didn't think so!

Would you think that at least ONE part of these simple requests/instructions might have be honored?

Not by Quikfixx!

There are reasons why I prefer to tip in cash, which was how this order was going to go if the driver had "bothered" to call me and show his face with a mask on it.

1: I used to work as a waiter for 8 years and I know what it's like to make a living from tips

2. And much more important in today's world, I need to see the driver with a face mask on.

Like it says in my instructions, "NO EXCEPTIONS - PERIOD!!!"

Frankly, I couldn't give a FLYING F*CK about what ANYONE thinks about face masks anymore.

But when it's my life and health at stake, the lives, health and well being of people I love and care about at stake, THEN WAKE THE F*CK UP, PAY ATTENTION AND SHOW SOME F*CKING RESPECT if you expect to get PAID!!!

The food?

Don't know, don't care, except that it was cold after being out on the sidewalk for g_d knows how long long until I finally called this so-called "restaurant."
