It's in the freezer now, and will be OUT ON THE STREET with next week's TRASH, EXACTLY WHERE THE DISRESPECTFUL BONEHEADS FROM QUICKFIXX BELONG!!!

I ordered from Domino's. They don't have the same kind of selection, but they never let me down. And I can pay for the whole thing in cash if I want to, which means- no show with face mask, no deal at all.

End of story.

Figure it out, Quikfixx, because you need a QUICK FIX new way of doing things in today's 

End of story...

________________________________

Being that it is in a food court, you can't expect much. Zaffron is OK though. It's fairly cheap and the food is passable. 

Chicken, steak, etc with rice, veggies an hummus is kind of tough to mess up that badly. Not somewhere I'd go out of my way for, but if you are accidentally stuck in that cookie-cutter, bland food court at Liberty Place, Zaffron is probably one of the better options.

Brunch 7 days a week! Do I need to say more. I went with my daughter (19) who can be a picky eater. We settled on the banana acai fruit bowl and the lean green pancakes. Both plates were delicious. Casey, our server was very pleasant. She was excited that the restaurant was finally open and anxious for what the future holds there. The restaurant was very spacious with beautiful counter tops, exposed brick and reclaimed wood everywhere. I loved how the bathroom stalls were made of old doors.... Brilliant! I look forward to returning and trying more of the menu items.

Welcome to the neighborhood!
It's a very friendly placeThe food is good and the service is always excellent. Their draft beer is great ! This  place has various types of chicken and they are all delicious.

Sushi rolls are pretty big, so you might want to order one less than you normally do to start. A lot of options for sushi and bubble tea. I'd pass on the taco appetizers, they were really sweet and a lot of lettuce. The place was pretty busy throughout the night and a lot of take out orders being placed. 

The music could be dialed down a bit, it was like eating sushi in a night club.

It's an old diner and has all the classics and great prices.  I don't live nearby but I love to drive here on quiet Sunday mornings and get a good cheap breakfast and a great cup of coffee.  Their breakfast specials are a good deal because they come with coffee AND juice included.

Rushed to VSEC for emergency care when I found a cat seemingly near death under my deck on a Sunday evening.  They revived him with fluids, kept him for 4 hours, and pronounced him otherwise "normal."  Only when I got him to the regular vet for follow-up did I realize how much acute and treatable suffering VSEC had missed -- despite my specific questions that pointed to the problem.    

I mentioned to a VSEC tech on intake that I had noticed brownish blood spots on the paper lining the bottom of my cat carrier.  When Dr. Fortney later checked in with me I asked her whether they had identified the source; she said no, he wasn't bleeding -- so I assumed that what I thought was blood was merely mud.  

VSEC's reports expressly said that his ambulation and heart were "normal."

On Tuesday we saw the regular vet.  (Cat had been in a crate, mostly sleeping, until then.)  

As soon as she took him out of his carrier she noticed instantly that he could not walk normally. *His paw pads were burned and sloughing off (entirely gone on one of his hinds), and all four paws were coated in gunk and badly infected.*  She soaked and debrided them (removed dead tissue), and started him immediately on both painkillers and antibiotics.  With persistent infection and pain he needed more debridement and medications a week later.  

Not until he was into the second course of medications did his ambulation become anything like "normal."  He continued to leave brownish spots of blood on the sheets and blankets I put down for him for a couple of weeks.  Yet VSEC not only missed this despite my questions about the blood, they told me his walking was "normal."

The regular vet also heard, on a routine listen (and confirmed at later appointments), a grade 2 heart murmur -- yet Dr. Fortney had said that his heart was "normal."  

Even for emergency care, this fell far short.  If nothing else, VSEC should have told me to get this little guy onto antibiotics and painkillers right away.  

Also terrible:  I have been trying to discuss this with VSEC customer service (Nina Cohen) for a full month, and after leaving me one voice message she has stopped responding.  I have sent her records, photos, and multiple follow-up emails, and left voice mails - asking to talk about what happened and perhaps get a partial refund (I paid more than $300 for this substandard care!).  I told Nina that I hate writing bad reviews but would have no choice if she would not respond to me ... and she didn't respond.   

So ... here's my review.  :-(  First, and last, trip to VSEC!  

(P.S.  still huge props to Crystal at the front desk for being pleasant, supportive, and professional during a long wait!)

We're in an exploratory mode lately when it comes to Chinatown, so Xi'an Sizzling Woks was a fun way to break out of our normal spots.

It's a tiny place on the edge of Chinatown (park a block south if you don't want to drive around all night).  We found the service to be extremely friendly, if not a little awkward at times, but I'll take it compared to other spots we've been to.  Everything looked pretty clean, and the smell of cumin and chili oil was wafting in the air.

We ordered the Biang Biang noodles with ginger, and they were great.  Chewey noodles, a little spice, a bit of oil, and some ginger....just wish there was a little more veg in there.

The Liang Pi is an interesting dish that I would never order if it weren't for the not-so-gentle prodding from Yelp.  It's a cold dish with a lot of odd textures, but I found it to be oddly addictive as I kept eating.  It was advertised as spicy, but I found it to be medium, and that's my only complaint:  a little heat would have perfected it.  

The dumplings with chili oil were very good, but not in the same realm as David's with ginger sauce, and the lamb skewers were good, but not worth the cash.  I agree with the other reviewers who say you need to be a serious lover of cumin to love these skewers.  

The Chinese burger was my least favorite item.  I found it to be dry and lacking spice.  It has potential to be very good, but some sauce and spice is necessary.

The best of the best.  Charge by the pound and these are dense small plate sized cookies so be careful.  I love that they sell milk and have multiple times had "milk and cookies" meetings with clients here.  Best of just one of the things from their menu.
Probably our best tasting menu in the past year.  Relaxed but refined service.  Mellow and enjoyable atmosphere.  Courses were off the charts - delish!

Took a shot in the dark and ordered Real Food Eatery on Caviar tonight and WOW! My meal was absolutely delicious. I was so full, but I kept eating and eating because it was that great.

In summary, this s**t is FIRE!

Danny and his team are top notch. Besides being subject matter experts on topics of automotive repair and aftermarket/specialty equipment installation, they demonstrate passion and integrity on everything they do. I've been going to Danny for tint and stereo work for my past three vehicle and will be doing so for my current vehicle shortly. I've been going and will continue to go to CK because the team there always stands behind their work and are keen on ensuring I am 100% satisfied with everything. The guys at CK are extremely proficient and technically adept. To give you an order of magnitude for comparison, I was quoted at least 5 hours for an iPod integration (using OEM parts) for my wife's LR3 by a LR mechanic. The team at CK was able to complete the job in less than 1.5hr and I couldn't be happier with the outstanding job they performed. Dovetailed with their domain expertise and client focus, CK's rate is very reasonable, which further bolsters my decision in being a customer at CK Auto Image.
Was nice. Much better than their competitors across the street! Excellent amount of cheese wiz along with good steak.

This guys are the BEST. My experience with Sunshine Movers was on 26th and it did exceed my expectation. Danny was very sweet and did give me explanation  of every little detail and charge  regards my move and kept me inform about arriving of the guys.......There was NO extra fees or surprises ... Actually it was ...The surprise to me was how sweet , professional, polite, hard working David and Tre was . I never had movers before taking such a good care of my belongings the way they did. They constantly ask me question about my preference and guidance and communicated with the whole entire time. Talking about dedication for excellent  service. Im soooooooo glad I used Sunshine mover. This guys worth every penny and even more. The other moving company should learn thing or two from them....seriously!!!!!!!!!!!!!! 
If you looking for stress-free experience , well spend money and outstanding service I HIGHLY RECOMMEND Sunshine movers

I just moved to Philly and thought I should try the Geno's vs. Pat's thing. I happened to order from Geno's first simply because it was closer. I ordered a "provolone with", but what I got was a mediocre cheesesteak with a side of racism. The bread was good, but the steak was overcooked and the onions were too big and had too little flavor. Even the provolone was flavorless and just kind of became indistinguishable from the bread. I didn't try the "freedom fries," but I assume they would be just as bland as the steaks, albeit with more freedom. Whatever that means.

The "speak English" signs were delightfully ironic; as there were only a handful of white people eating at Geno's while Pat's was bustling with a much more multi-ethnic crowd.

Anyway, for the best cheesesteaks made in "Murika," give Geno's a try. For the best cheesesteak in Philly, you'd be better off pretty much anywhere else.

Hit this place after the broad street run. They were a bit crazy as to be expected. The staff got us a table with minimal wait and we had a party of 6.

The drinks were pretty good. I am a bit picky with my Bloody Mary mix and I just didn't enjoy this too much. Beer selection was nice and would have been a better option for me. 

Food was pretty good too! I had the meatloaf sandwich, and many others got the burgers. I wish I had gotten a burger. Nachos were tasty too. 

Environment is hipster meets bar food. And I mean that in the best way possible. It was what we were looking for after a 10 mile run. Beer, greasy food and no hype.

I don't mean to oversell it, but this is the best f'in restaurant with the friendliest staff on the planet. I've brought people like Steve Wozniak and Felicia Day to this location as part of a conference for a pre-event speakers' dinner, and they've always knocked it out of the park. Double Lobster, are you kidding me?! The drinks are mixed strong (try the cocktail specials!), so be sure to have your party boots on.

Try the szechuan style french fries even though they're not on the menu - simply amazing!

Most dishes also have vegetarian alternatives.

I stopped here during a drive down to DC for lunch with my boyfriend. You can immediately tell this is a locals only kind of spot. It took me awhile to figure out what and where to order. I got the roast pork, spinach, and sharp provolone sandwich and bf got a regular cheesesteak. The service is pretty quick and the prices were a little higher than i expected but still fine. The sandwich was warm and toasty and loaded with pork. I wish it had more spinach and less meat though. The sharp provolone adds a great dimension to the sandwich but def makes it a little saltier. The cheesesteak was good, i liked that it was on a sesame seed roll.

Iron Hill Brewery & Restaurant is an exceptional, and I strongly recommend it. This place is unbelievable. Every single thing was incredibly flavorful and delicious. I became very good friends with the staff, who are all fantastic and super helpful. We will definitely come back to Iron Hill Brewery & Restaurant again.
Solid brunch spot. The specials keep things interesting, and they have a delicious grilled cheese sandwich if none of them catch your attention.

A burger place with the ala carte up-charge mentality of a steakhouse. Burger was good, not "I must go back" good. And with their prices I simply won't. No drink specials---ever. And on a Tuesday night you should have at least one drink or draft beer on special. Nope. And the bartender had a cute canned response that "everything is special" which means, "No, we're going to screw you for $7 a beer all night long and you should feel honored!" My wife got the veggie burger and while the flavor was good, the consistency was so mushy it barely held its form and squished in your hands when biting into it. A total disaster and needs to be rethought---I mean, make the thing a little crispy or something, but don't have it oozing into my hands when I eat it. And last, the duck fries. Fries never suck, generally speaking, but my wife and I both thought they were unremarkable and actually didn't finish them.  I'm not sure what other people see in this place. It does have a good atmosphere but they lost me after that. I can think of several places where the burgers are much better for less, so the choice is yours: decent burger with bacon and mushrooms ($20), two beers ($14), and fries ($5) or a killer burger (try St Stephens Green, seriously, $12) and better fries and slight cheaper drinks (not much though). Sorry Village Whiskey---I can afford to eat at your restaurant but you don't deliver.

Came for an anniversary dinner and was really impressed! Service was impeccable. The nightingale cocktail was refreshing. Very easy for vegetarians! Got the labne, baba ganoush, fatteh, battata harra, and fatar meshwi.  Apples and ashta for dessert.

Definitely a place to come back!

Underwhelming experience. 

True - this place is in a prime location, has the allure of a BYOB, and looks "fancy" but I felt tricked. Detach the food from the setting and it'd be mediocre. Portions were also tiny for the price.

This place is way nicer than i remember it from my college days.  i guess it turns more bar/drinking scene after 11 pm on the weekends? we were there a bit earlier for dinner. their menu is more extensive than i expected, and portion sizes were large.  warning: it gets reallllly loud when the band starts playing. 

i thought the pizzas looked a little weird/ not normal but no complaints on taste from the people who got pizzas. i got sushi (yes, they have sushi. its odd.) and it was good, decent sushi. very fresh.   apps were good (calamari was the best i have had anywhere, zucchini fries were a little too thick cut for my preference). The granny apple sandwich was also enjoyed by members of our party.  

Our server was AWESOME!! her name was Lindsay. she was accommodating and very pleasant. even though we may have been a pain in the butt. hopefully she thought we were entertaining.

The atmosphere is charming, good lighting and wall decorations. I really enjoyed the meatballs. They were cheese stuffed and full of cheese. The sauce and the cheese complimented each other nicely. 

My gal pal had the gnocchi. It was a great portion size, with an irresistible sauce.

I ordered the veal parm and I will offer my constructive criticism of this dish. It was overdone and oversized. 14 oz is too big of a portion for a Parmesan dish. It was so big that the pasta served with it was in a separated. It was over fried which caused the meat to be dry and tough, and the breading separated from the meat. The spaghetti, and the red sauce with this dish were VERY good, I took that part of the dish home.

Our server, and even the hostess, who I didn't tell about this, were very sympathetic.  

This was my first time at this restaurant, I would return again if I was local to the area!

I was a little surprised Eulogy was on Open Table because when we walked in, it definitely looked more like a bar than a restaurant. We headed upstairs and were greeted and seated immediately.

The beer list was extensive, and they had some rare ones on tap and in bottles. We started with a cheese plate, which felt a little snooty since the place looks like such a dive bar, but it was deeelicious! Three cheeses, strawberries, apple slices, and bread. Then we moved onto mussels (the fella) and a trio of sausages with mashed potatoes and carrots (me). Mine was fantastic, and the fella's mussels were solid.

Again, it filled up pretty quickly, and we were pleasantly surprised with the food and the beer.

Let it be for the record that my initial incentive to register for a yelp account was to present a positive rating and review for Wissahickon Cyclery (WC) because I feel that strongly. And, truly, if you'd ask my wife, she would tell you that I'm a pretty harsh critic.

Price: I've shopped in at least a handful of other bike and cyclery shops in the Philadelphia area. WC's prices, for the basic merchandise I've purchased, were average to well below average. I'm not a racer, so I'm not a good judge for the prices of the more extreme cycling items, but for basic bike servicing and items, my money was well spent.

Service: Simply excellent! I have always been greeted when walking in, whether or not someone is already being served, surpassing by far the other locations where I may not even get a look, much less a greeting, until standing at the counter staring for help after several long moments.
I've rarely been disappointed with the time or outcome of their service, and for these very (very) few times, WC was quick to remediate the issue, and thus further resolve my already growing patronage. Their service quality is absolutely worth repeating.

Expertise: In both my own experience and associates who've joined me here, WC's staff are not only well knowledgeable about all aspects of their industry, they are generous and patient in sharing and communicating them with their customers. I'm grateful for this because I have felt, at other locations, to be made the fool for asking questions or not knowing some nomenclature within the cycling world as if I ought to be among the elite in order to step over the threshold of their storefront. At WC I feel welcome to inquire about the things that both interest and worry me, and the informative responses I receive are helpful and even caring.

Product: My bike has been long lasting and durable. I've used it almost daily, 10 months out of the year, for about 8 years. I can most assuredly attest that WC's product quality is super. Admittedly, their location and stock is smaller than you're going to see at other Philly area locations but I haven't found that to be a problem. Besides, why carry more variety when what you already provide is great and inexpensive?

Overall: Wissahickon Cyclery has had my strong patronage for many years simply because they have many times taken good care of me... and my bike. As a moderate cyclist, and a non-motorized-vehicle owner, I would definitely refer them to any friend or stranger looking to find a place for cycling parts, service, and/or information.

Frustrated that my elderly black parents were told that a fly infested apartment with a water leak was "move in" ready. They were no able to see the inside of the apartment prior to moving in. The complex only shows you the model apartment. There were flies everywhere. On their face, on their food, all over the walls and furniture. Still waiting for management group to fully rectify the situation.
Got takeout here a while back. Looked nothing like the photos on Uber eat. Practically inedible.

Service is spotty, the margaritas have too much sour mix, and the menu has a shitload of typos.
They do have a nice, large outdoor patio.  I enjoyed feeding my stale chips to the squirrels and birdies last night.

I was very disappointed with my experience. We had a birthday dinner reservation for 9pm and we were not seated until around 10pm. One of the glasses that was given to us had clearly not been washed- there was a lipstick stain on it. The service was fine, but it was nothing special considering how long we waited for the table. To make up for this, they gave us a plate of lettuce with dressing, nothing else. 

The food was spectacular, however it did not make up for the negative experience we had. I've been twice before and had no complaints, so I'm hoping this was just an off-night. The two stars is mainly for the service. The food makes it worth giving it another shot

Have always wanted to try City Diner and finally got the opportunity to this weekend. My friend and I came on Saturday afternoon around 1/2 pm and it was empty enough that we could be seated immediately. 
Between the two of us we ordered French onion soup, a cheese omelet with home fries and toast and French toast. 
The food came out so fast that I was only able to take two bites of my soup before my food camp which definitely was a downfall because while I was eating my soup, my omelet got cold as I wasn't eating touching it. 
The French toast was absolutely delicious. It was so fluffy and well cooked. The home fries were also delicious. 
I would definitely come back here again and again. So wonderful that they are open 24/7 and have an alcohol license!

Had the pulled pork sandwich which was quite a surprise! I didn't except the cilantro, basil, and carrots to be in there---reminded me of a banh mi. Very delicious. The skillet cornbread was interesting because it had jalepenos in it, but i think Baby Blues BBQ had much better cornbread. The mac n cheese was ordinary, nothing special. 

The meat is def the way to go here.

Loved this place, been wanting to stop in for the longest because I always pass it on my way to other restaurants.  It was my first time having Malaysian food and it was really good. Some of the dishes reminded me of Thai meets Indian cuisine.  Myself and a coworker went on lunch and were seated right away I looked through yelp to help decide what I wanted and got the Thai basil noodles which were great! Everything I ordered was good and my coworker really liked what he order too.  Only draw back with this place as with a lot of restaurants in China town the staff isn't really attentive to you...it's like once you order they don't really pay you any attention until it's time to pay. I would definitely go back and have some out of town friends who thing would really love this place

We LOVED The Love!!! The food was delicious... The potatoes were a lil dry but the charred onion remoulade that comes with them SO GOOD!!! 12-31-21 was our reservation date, 57 degrees that day. We were comfortable with the outdoor seating and the service was excellent.

If you are ever in Philly make sure you visit Warm Daddy's. The atmosphere is laid back with great people. They have a live band, on the night I visit Acoustic Band was playing. They sounded great from signing Bobbie Brown to Brain McKnight to many more. I had the short beef ribs, red rice and collard greens with Turkey necks. It was very good. Our waiter had a couple small mishaps only cause he was new, but I still tiped him well. Overall you too should patronize this great establishment.

You can't go wrong with anything on the menu at cheu. The ingredients are fresh and thoughtfully added to make each dish well rounded. I do suggest the brisket ramen. Also the dumplings are always a good choice. Service is very quick and friendly. Good craft beer selection as well. One of my favorite places to eat in Philly.

Amazing airline!  Great Flight Crew!!  Top notch and seasoned pilots!! 
We have had the best experiences as a family flying on Frontier. It is disappointing to see bad reviews. Flying can be stressful, and no airline is immune to the difficulties that arise.  The crew has ALWAYS been professional and helpful.  Most recently our plane was diverted to Philadelphia because of dangerous weather in Providence, RI...  the crew was completely on top of the needs of the passengers and calmly and professionally answered all questions and concerns from anyone who was nervous. 
Frontier Airlines has proven themselves to me and my family and we will all continue to be loyal customers for years to come.
Eating the green curry with mock duck right now. ITS THE BOMB!!!! WOWOWOWOWOW!!!! so good.
Right next to where I live. Very convenient. The staff are very nice and the food is delicious. Also, from 11am to 2pm, a full meal is 4.99. I eat here during those hours all the time.

I called for pickup while driving, they said "we don't do that anymore, you have to come in." When I get there I hear him calling out orders for pickup to customers waiting. Ok?.....after waiting 25 mins for a cheesesteak, I open it in the car and there's basically NO CHEESE WIZ. (I asked for a Steak WITH WIZ) not only that but they were so cheap with the meat...CUSTOMER SERVICE IS ALSO HOTTT GARBAGGEE. This place has been on the decline for a while now. Would be surprised if they stay open much longer. Won't spend another dime here.

Love!

Free chips + salsa with every meal, fresh ingredients, and super legit food.  

The only thing I didn't like at first was that my tacos weren't the normal indistinguishable Taco Bell fare that I'm used to, but I quickly realized that's my fault, not theirs.

This place is a gem.

For the price, you can't beat it.  It's well located, with all the amenities an average Joan needs.  Free wifi in your room.  The staff was very friendly and helpful (something I wasn't expecting from Philly... they throw snowballs at Santa here! http://uk.video.yahoo.com/watch/4182474 )

And you're walking distance to great bars, restaurants, and parks.

One star off because my refrigerator was unplugged and I woke up to room temperature leftovers... yick.

Buck-a-shuck oysters!!! it was fast and the oysters were awesome- and the place wasnt packed like oyster house is... so we loved it! We only had oysters though- so we cant speak for any of the other food.

Ekta had decent Indian food def worth a try. The food seemed healthy when compared to the other Indian places in town. The Indian food here tastes like home cooked food as it is less greasy. I went here for buffet and loved their salad section; their Chana salad and the veg soup was good. Also, they serve fresh naan bread on the table for buffet which is definitely a plus. Def worth a try!

I was in a pickle on the afternoon of September 16th and the bartender helped me out tremendously.  My phone was dead and she kindly lent me her phone so I could get out of my jam.  Words can't express how much I appreciate what she did for me.  Super great staff, very friendly, and great beer.  Can't wait to go back!!

I came up to this area of town, specifically so I could shop at this Blue Mercury, the other one was undergoing renovations.  Wow, what a beautiful, big, bright airy beauty supply store!  I was given a gift card for my birthday, I was excited to use it and I left with an Oribe hair masque, I love Oribe and Blue Mercury carries a lot of it!  I'm going to start checking out all of their other brands too, but I go there specifically for the Oribe haircare.

The sales ladies who helped me were friendly, knowledgeable and courteous.  I was very pleased with my shopping experience here and I checked out a few other local stores while in University City.  It was definitely worth the trip from Center City and I would definitely come back again!

This place is really loungey and blasts hip hop and R&B. It's super dark in there at night. Overall, the sushi is really solid. Tried the usual staples and a Chef Special roll: spicy tuna, pepper tuna, Alaska roll, and Blushing Lava roll. Cuts of the fish tasted fresh. We also got a tofu udon to try it. The broth is super watery and has no flavor. Overall, this was a good experience, but hard to distinguish with the many sushi spots that are available in the area.

What the F did I just try and eat??

The beef on a stick is literally unedible. I could not chew parts of it and had to throw it out. Without a doubt the worst beef on a stick I ever ordered.

General Tso's Shrimp - the most doughly covering I have ever had and this is not a good thing at all. Glabs of sauce sporadically spread on the doughly pieces and I got a headache from I guess MSG before I was done the meal.

Wonton Soup - Dish water broth. We have all had bad wonton soup broth and this place unforunately has the "dish water broth wonton soup".

Thai Shrimp Roll - two for $4.75 and they leave the tails on them. I mean this is the one item you try and give a good presentation on?? It is not that bad but everything else I have tried here is extremely hit or miss.

I honestly feel like they get subpar products from their vendors to save money on food cost. 

I will not be coming back.

Ugh, if I didn't live outside Philly, I'd go out of my way to never fly into/out of this airport again.  The security lines are always a nightmare (frequently due to the fact that they have parts of them closed) and the staff are so impersonal.  Add to that the incredibly invasive pat-down I once received for no reason (during which they nearly dumped the very expensive contents of my camera bag onto the floor) and the lack of wifi and I'm just so NOT a fan of this airport.  The only good things about PHL are the variety of food/bar/shop options available to you after you go through security and the regional rail line that will take you right to the airport.

My 9 year old and 7 year old loved this place, and so did we.  The butterfly pavilion was a surprise hit.  Butterflies swoop around you and sometimes land on your clothes.  The kids can even pick up butterflies, once they are taught.  I highy recommend this place.

Even though I don't live in the neighborhood, Fleur is my go-to for ordering flowers! After my boyfriend had a spectacular bouquet delivered to my desk at work for my birthday last year, I've gotten my whole office into ordering from Fleur as well. The owner is a sweet guy who really cares about his customers and takes pride in his art.

Great place- highly recommend 
Very nice staff and last minute apptmt
I'm picky with my nails and they were great

A truly awful experience with this place. I will never go back. I had a manicure that I wasn't totally happy with - polish was thick and streaky. Not a huge deal, but I had an event and wanted them re-painted. I went back an hour later and politely asked for a quick polish change. The man up front (I assumed he was the owner) said "What's your problem?? There's nothing wrong. This looks fine. You probably messed it up." I was offended but tried to remain polite in my explanation. He got EXTREMELY confrontational and said "No! You are asking for a free service we only have time for paying customers." ...as though I hadn't already (over)paid this place for a regular manicure just an hour earlier. With so many other great nail salons in Rittenhouse, this place is not worth your business.
There are so many cars you can choose from. These guys are truly awesome. Good service, professional people. Highly recommend. Five stars!

Ordered Al Pastor burrito, Elite Asado and the Horchata. 
Burrito was duhlicious. Pork was moist and tasty.  Burrito was filled with rice, onions, pineapple, some corn, peas, pork and cheese. For lunch is was the perfect size if not too much after eating the Elote first. A hot, crisp corn treat roasted on the grill with lime, mexi mayo cheese and chile. Really tasty. 
The Horchata to wash things down was very refreshing.

This restaurant is the coolest! We loved the vibes of the basement dining area. The waiters were knowledgeable and truly helped to navigate the extensive menu! The food was beyond delicious. We loved the edamame dumplings, tofu, and crispy Brussels sprouts. Can't wait to go back!
Delicious blue lemonade and carrot cake. Can't wait to try more from their menu...Will be a regular here!

Went to see the lego special exzibit..  Was an awesome exzibit but walked through it in about 45 minutes,  assumed it would come with regular admission as well since the exzibit seemed short but it did not. I felt it was a little over priced for what was given...  Left wanting more.

Solid brunch place in NoLibs, it's a little bit off the beaten path which I like because it keeps it a little hidden gem. There are about 20 seats maybe inside and about that many outside. It was pretty hot the day we went so we decided to sit inside. The servers were knowledgeable and nice and the service was attentive. For food we went with the cinnamon pull aparts, the brunch burger, and the egg sandwich. Everything was really, the burger blend they use is really good, the burger had a good beefy flavor and the bacon is just awesome, I would just get a side of that. The potato roll it was on held up well and the breakfast sandwich was also very good. Definitely a good place to hit for brunch we will have to wander in here one night have a dinner.
house special #1: it was an okay sandwich. i agree with what some reviewers stated about the price being a lil high and a lil skimpy on portions.

Drinks upstairs is nice esp when it's nice out.  From the outside, it doesn't look like a famous restaurant or anything fancy.  I thought it was a diner!  But the food is decent but the drinks are pretty bad and overpriced.  It's an interesting decor though.

Be aware of this practice. The doctor would put in wrong code to get more insurance money. I went in for an annual physical. The doctor asked routine questions about my health. I brought up that I felt a bit fatigue thinking this is part of the routine annual physical checkup. Lord, was I wrong. It turns out the doctor entered this visit as a sick visit so months later I received a bill of 92 dollars for it. My insurance company would have covered the whole cost for an annual physical. But because they entered in as a "new patient sick visit" just because I brought up being fatigue during the check-up, I'm now on the hook for this amount.
The coffee's fine, but the actual reason i swing by daily is due to the delightful individuals who are paid to run this place.  God bless them all.

The roast pork hoagie was fantastic. But is it better then the Philly cheese steak? Absolutely not. It is definitely up there in my favorite edibles, but they just don't compare to each other. It's like apples to oranges.  The pork was juicy and everything came together with the addition of the gravy. I've never had broccoli rabe before and my wife was worried that I wouldn't like it, turns out on this sandwich it is a welcome addition that worked with the flavor of the sharp provolone. Did I enjoy this? Very much. Will I be abandoning my Tony Lukes' Cheese steak? No. But it was damn tasty.

So-so ... I went there when they had an exotics vet.. And she was awesome!  But I took my other pets there a couple of times and always was disappointed.  Dr Michelle is very disinterested and automatic about care, and while Dr Geffin is nice enough, he is a bit of an alarmist and has never given me accurate information.  I have consistantly found out that what he told me was inaccurate and often times to his financial benefit.

Last year I took a kitten there I had found.  She had Cerebellar Hypoplasia and walked with a wobbly limp.  He told me she may be rabid, and have panleukopenia AND distemper.  Those are the same disease.  That was the last time he did or ever will see me.

Horrible customer service. Asked the female working behind the counter a question and she just showed attitude and didn't respond. Absolute garbage attitude. Overheard them talking about customers while they where getting food. Garbage standards and would tell everyone to stay away from this specific one. Management team should definitely read these reviews because it does not live up to the Chick-Fil-A standard of customer service and the employees are extremely disrespectful and Ignorant. Was definitely not " my pleasure "
Delicious meal. Lime soda made from scratch. Best masala dosa I've had. Staff is friendly and quick to ask if you need anything. I recommend this restaurant.

As an avid jewelry lover, this place is absolutely my favorite go-to place to buy unique pieces! Absolutely in love with their studs, rings, and pendants, which are all at a reasonable price, judging from their quality. Need a new pair of earrings for your new outfit? Perfect place to stop by. My favorite are the delicate, cubic stone small hoop earrings, that sparkle like diamonds.. you can tell I'd be going back soon to swoop these up.

Additionally, these pieces make great gifts to loved ones and family members, because they will package your item in a pretty box! They offer UNBEATABLE prices/services to have your watch battery repaired. I'll always choose this store to go to because the owner has a great eye/skill in repair and taste in jewelry. 10/10 stars!

I been to Del Frisco's Double Eagle in New York. This restaurant was comfort, more causal then Del Frisco's Double Eagle, with TVs and music. The food was tasty and well cooked. This is a Very good place to eat and I recommend it.

We still love Giorgio's. Unfortunately (although unsurprisingly), they recently increased the price on most of their menu. The food, service, and atmosphere are still wonderful, but the slight bump in cost was just enough to make Giorgio's good for a more occasional special-ish night out instead of a standby.

Great place! All the tacos were excellent especially the fish tacos and pork ones.The taco of the day was shrimp and chorizo which kind of doesn't work. Not a fan of the chicken tacos.$4 for the fish and 3.75 for the rest.

If you are looking for great Mexican in West philly - this is your spot! Just plan on double checking if you have any food allergies or preferences and don't expect to go in an hour before closing and feel welcome! I truly can't say anything bad about the food though, it's spot on but it has been better! It definitely used to live up to 4+ stars but it must have changed owners. Was disappointed to see the menu has been reduced to about 10 items and the churros were discontinued. They were the BEST! The "old" guacamole had great selection, service, and taste. They still have the taste down but are lacking in selection and service. 
 
My girlfriend and I got to Guacamole around 8pm last night and from the start the girl working the register was pushing us to get it to go (they close at 9). We ate in and shared the Nachos and Tacos de casa (both veggie). While some menu items listed cilantro, these did not. Of course, both dishes came with cilantro which my girlfriend cannot stand! She was able to eat around it on the nachos but it was mixed in on the tacos and she couldn't take it. I told the girl that the tacos have cilantro and she doesn't like it and asked if they would be willing to make one (it came with 3) without cilantro for her. She said of course so I say back down and then she said that will be $3.25. Oh - did I mention I had already tipped her a generous $4 on our $19 meal PRIOR to getting our food or service? My girlfriend tried to explain to her that it wasn't listed on the menu and had it been (like some other items) that we would have requested no cilantro. Neither of us were feeling terribly confrontational so we paid. Then she asked us about three times if we wanted the taco to go. Now, I am not typically someone that makes an issue of nothing but when I know something is wrong it bothers me on a core level. And this did. When her cilantro free taco was delivered to the table I said to the girl - it's okay but you really should not have charged her for the taco. She was very defensive and insisted there was nothing wrong with the taco and that she had to charge her. THEN she started removing all of the unused silverware and putting the stools on the table (around 8:30). We left no later than 8:45pm and they practically locked the door behind us. My girlfriend was extremely disappointed because I told her about Guacamole and raved about it. She has been asking to go for months and unfortunately she will not be returning. 

I was very disappointed because I also know the owners to be very sweet and I cannot imagine they would have handled the situation this way.

Touristy tourist, tour groups, but no tours. My friend (who's lived here for about 7 years,) had never been here when I first dragged her touring Old City. 
It was a September day, a bit windy from Penn Landing probably and the kiddies were not around. There were many hipsters and elderly groups milling about so we took our coffee and went outside to the area that has some tables and chairs looking out the better side of this "shopping mall". You can watch the horse and carts clop along the road here. The other side is depressing, people plop down on the stairs and look kind of homeless and depressed. Needless to say we didn't have lunch here. The place we got coffee (and a bagel and muffin which were good,) last Autumn wasn't too bad but there was no privacy inside.
Last week it hadn't changed. Not that I'm surprised about that. I didn't go to the Bourse for the history this time. If it didn't have it's own website touting it and wasn't included on many of the historical sites, you'd have no clue there was anything important about it. It inspires anxiety and crushing humanity in anyone that is looking for a unique experience. And I don't mean in the Grand Canyon sense. More along the Penn Station in NY sense. So why did I go last week? I knew I would get some kind of souvenir. I went to what I thought might be a family type place. It was in the middle of the concourse and started with a "V" (BTW whoever deals with their website should really update the names of the shops.) I got "some souvenirs" for sure. Shame on me for thinking I'd get something of value. Two of the 7 items I got were broken. Cheap-@ssed cr@p. It wouldn't have made me so mad except that I thought the owners were very accommodating. Yeah, to get me to buy some over priced teddies that had nothing to do with Philadelphia. Anyway I won't be doing that again. For less than the price of a broken laser (yeah it was for a freaking cat but the screw top that kept the batteries in was broken,) I got a reusable coloring mat and special crayons at the Fireman's Hall Museum. 
Whatever. Lesson learned.
I should have left the minute I saw at least 300 teeny-boppers, 100 girl scouts, 200 cub scouts and various young adults and bewildered elderly people packed in the lower level. It was a nasty show of humanity and consumption that only politicians love to see. You can even see an example of what I'm talking about at their website.
Do yourself a favor; buy from the Franklin Court, browse the Franklin Institute, or just go to the Dollar Value and fake it. DO NOT BOTHER with the Bourse. Somebody tell the city that this place really doesn't represent history! (Hmmm, maybe it really does?)
P.S. My friend was spared the ugliness by waiting for me outside on the steps as she had a smoke.

http://www.bourse-pa.com/index.html

Very busy place and took a while to get sandwiches, but it was so worth the wait. I had the Phogie (like a Baja min) which was part of their regular menu, and my husband had the McArby, which was a special posted on a small note on the menu board. One of the best I have ever had, my husband's, too. Looking forward to visiting my daughter again so I can go back. Be sure to add the Zappos chips to your order.

On the one year anniversary of visiting Barcelona, P. and I decided to try Barcelona Wine Bar for some Spanish wine and tapas. There was a little bit of a wait to get a table, even after we called ahead to see if we could get a reservation and were told just come on over! Not a big deal. We grabbed a drink at the bar and by the time we got a few sips in our table was ready. P. Used to live near the Barcelona in West Hartford Connecticut and loved going there so he was in charge of what to order. We chose 3 items from the cheese and charcuterie section: the manchego (a must have when enjoying Spanish food IMO), the Serrano ham (yum!) and the San Simon (a little smokier than I prefer in a cheese). Then we chose the Patatas Bravas (crispy and delicious), the hangar steak (really tender and flavorful, but a bit smaller than I thought the price warranted), and the gambas a ajillo (yummy shrimp). After enjoying my first glass of red Spanish wine, I asked for a recommendation of another red to try and was offered a tasting of 3 glasses of wine that fit my criteria, which I thought was a nice touch. I can't remember which one it was that tried but it was delish! Finally we saved room for desert. P. Had the crepes and I went with the hazelnut chocolate cake, which was basically a chocolate lava cake with ice cream. Usually a safe bet, although I think my cake was slightly overdone making it a little hard on the outside. I enjoyed a nice cappuccino with my desert while P. Opted for the sherry flight. He really enjoyed that! For me, on a Tuesday night, this was such a nice treat. The service was really friendly and I'd like to come back again soon.

Really nice workers.  Went there today to pick up a good road bike and got what I wanted in price range (~300 dollars).  The only issue that appeared was finding a bike that fit me well(I'm 5'3", with all the height in my torso), but even with that I got a bike that worked.  They let me test drive and I have a 30 day warranty for any issues that might appear.

I'm a fan.

I had a really busy week and procrastinated on getting my dry cleaning done. It came to the point where I needed it for the next day by 2. I went around to at least two other places that could only promise returns by 5, but Spruce Hill cleaners did what no other dry cleaning service would do and had the clothes well cleaned by 2 the next day. Would definitely recommend! They are the fastest dry cleaning place I've seen and really go out of their way for their customers.

I had to get 4 seat belts replaced, thanks to my dog with separation anxiety and sharp teeth. The guys at Ren's found me the best cost on belts and the installation fee was very reasonable. I ordered the belts and then had to go out of town for a few months before they came in. Ren's kept them in a safe place and replaced my chewed up belts as soon as I got back. They even cleaned my car as a consolation for having to wait! I now have unchewed seat belts and shiny tires!

Ali is the best bar tender ever. so genuine and great to be around !!! if you get the chance spark up a conversation with her she has lots of great, nice things to say and is very knowledgeable about the city and different places to go!

Unfortunately the quality continues to go down. The employees are always eating while at the register. The interior 
of the store is dirty. The students who work there and  parents do not do a good job of monitoring the small children who add the toppings to the yogurt. Little fingers are everywhere. Last time we stopped and we walked in and walked out. So sad it used to be such a nice place.

Great spot to delve into an array of beers with selection that is sure to have something fit for any palate. I myself love darker porters and stouts. And they've got that for sure. Food is pretty tasty as well on a consistent basis. Gotta recommend the tap burger or a pizza if you want food. Nachos are CRAAAAZY as well. Please do share those hahaha trust me.

Almost forgot. WHEN you go...DO.NOT.LEAVE without going to Howie at the bar. My man does it well in the well haha. But seriously. One of the best bartenders in Philly and you can take my word he'll treat you right. Tell em I said he better!

Awesome!

Albert is really great at what he does. Fast, affordable, and attentive to detail. Have gotten my hair cut which turned out great on his advice and go regularly for a beard trim. Highly recommend.
The atmosphere was what I liked best about this place. I just got off he plane and was starving. Short walk from the hotel. Nice beer selection and good music playing. Will be back!

I've heard a lot about this place but just recently went.  It seemed all that it's been hyped up to be.

This is the coolest bowling alley I've ever been to.  Awesome music, attentive staff, good bar.  Upon walking in and waiting for the rest of our party to arrive, we got some drinks at the side bar on the first floor and got a table.  Soon after we got our shoes and were assigned a lane.  There are extensive snack-food menus at each of the lane tables which was perfect for how drunk/hungry we were.  It took quite a while for our food to arrive, but we did order a lot and it was a Friday night.  The waitress was very patient with our large group and got all of the orders right.  It wasn't horribly expensive either, and they have all of the best drunken munchies - cheese tots, burger sliders, chicken quesadillas, chips and salsa - everythaang.  The bowling was fun too, although there were a few times the lane got jammed and we had to wait for 15 minutes for the attendant to come fix it.  

Also, it has a black and white photo booth!

I would come back here again definitely, just for the food and the fun atmosphere.

Upgrading my review based on my visit last night, was even better than my last two visits and Morimoto has made it's way to my top 10 favorite restaurants in Philly, for sure.

I returned last night to celebrate my anniversary with my honey and we had a fabulous time. Ambiance, service, and food were outstanding 5/5 on all accounts, that's hard to come by.

I wanted to do the Omakase again so we could sample most of the menu, along with ordering some things a-la-carte. Our server was FABULOUS, so knowledgable, easy to talk to, great recommendations. He originally recommended the $150 Seasonal, but after discussing what type of items we wanted to sample, and that we don't eat red meat -- he changed his recommendations to accommodate us. He was very honest and said the highlight high value entree in the seasonal, was the wagyu beef, so we wouldn't really benefit off that option. He helped us come up with a dinner option perfect for us -- the regular $100 omakase, and told us which items would come out in what order, and helped us choose our other a la carte items based on what we were interested in, and also as to not repeat any dishes based on our omakase. One of the best servers I've ever had the pleasure of serving me, thank you! 

Honestly, everything was fabulous, I have nothing I didn't enjoy. But I'll outline my favorite highlights: BEST TUNA PIZZA IN THE CITY, there I said it, but I'm not kidding. I've had a lot, and I don't think I can eat it anywhere else ever again. The tuna is so fresh, the jalapeno flavor is perfect, it's so light, flavorful and just.. GET IT. Yosedofu is pretty fun, as it's made fresh table side -- a nice dish if you enjoy tofu. Carpaccio is delicious, the hot oil makes such a nice smokey flavor it almost tastes like you're eating red meat like a thinly sliced seared steak.Duck is rendered perfectly, crispy skin , juicy well seasoned on the inside. 

Overall, I am certain I will return for the next special occasion or craving..  the age old question of " is it worth it?" YES. I vouch that the omakase, or the restaurant in general is 100% worth it. The quality is there, the service is there, and for those of you with a bottomless pit stomach like me -- the quantity is there. This is not one of those high end restuarants with tiny portions, you will not leave hungry. I already want to go back... drooling as I write this review

I remember my friend recommending this place to me last year when i went to Philly for the 1/2 marathon. I finally got to check it out again this year when I went back to participate in the 1/2 marathon. I went at an odd time of 5:30 PM. The place is small; there are only a few tables. The menu is basic, it is less than 1 page long. I ordered the lamb noodle soup with the thin hand pulled noodles, instead of the thick flat noodles. 

The soup dish came out almost instantly. They also provide hot tea. They give you a lot of noodles in the soup. The bowl is pretty massive and they also had a good amount of lamb meat. The broth was sooo good. I don't know what kind of soup base it was, but it was so clean and refreshing. It even had a hint of lamb from the lamb bones and lamb meat. The lamb was tender and fatty. The noodles were pliable and had a great chew to them. The herbs were fresh. Wow the soup was just warm and hearty, and my only wish was that it was spicy. However, i just used the hot sauce that they leave on the table to make the broth spicy. This dish was cheap too!

Great bang for your buck. I wish they had this place in NYC Chinatown. Def need to go back next year to carbo load.

If there are words to describe how good a crab risotto can be, I cannot image ones strong enough to convey how much I loved the crab risotto entree at Dandelion. The house Dandelion salad was also great and light - despite being much larger than expected.

The ambiance was great, although it was a little loud and the tables were on the tighter side. The wait staff was more than exceptional. Loved it and can't wait to return on my next trip to Philly.

A friend told me about this spot so decided to give it a try. Its a small bar/restaurant and I arrived during HH. Thursday's are live music night and a gentleman with a cello was playing in the corner. 

I ordered the burger with fried onion, cheese, tomato, lettuce with a side salad. The burger was medium-well and was so juicy. 

We were sat by the bar along the wall where high tops are and it was nice way to see the entire bar and listen to the music while we ate. I was intrigued to try the pirogies but they were sold out. I'll have to come back to try them out as this place apparently is known for their great pirogies!

This was a nice spot for breakfast when I was in town. They had a few outside tables and were being very cautious with COVID around. I enjoyed my wrap very much and they have a clothing store inside as well.

It's really nice to have a fabric shop in the neighborhood.  But it ends there.  The staff is not friendly, not interested in creating a community.  The fabric is all overpriced funky prints. If you have more traditional taste forget it.   I took one class there, was working on a project and wanted help.  Asked if i could bring it in for advice and i was told no.  The class was very ridged. no flexibility in the way it was taught.  Got the impression the owner was not really a seamstress, she knew enough to teach what she was teaching and that's it.  I found the staff snotty.  Actually tried to get in for other classes but apparently the interesting ones fill up quickly.  That goes to show people are starved for creativity.  Will go to an overpriced, limited, unfriendly shop for lessons.  Its too bad the only other place is out in the northeast.  Doesn't anyone in CC want to teach sewing garments???  Please open a store if you do, you'd make a killing.   If you want to make a funky quilt or pillow using overpriced prints and an unfriendly staff with limited knowledge - this place is for you.

Service by Leslie was good on a Wednesday evening, competent and attentive. My Bullfrog Old Gold Kriek was a bit bizarre - never had a kriek before, pink sweet-sour slightly fizzy and a little alarming - but after a couple of sips, I enjoyed the heck out of it. Except pink. Never got used to the pink drink in a tulip glass. The menu warned me about the tulip glass. Screamed frou-frou, and I don't do frou-frou. Should have ordered it in a dirty tulip glass. That would have been less frou-frou.

The gumbo wasn't so great, to me. I spent about a year in New Orleans on an expense account. I didn't grow up there, so my only benchmark is restaurant gumbo. Had a lot of gumbo I loved, some I didn't. Khyber's gumbo doesn't taste like any gumbo I've had, and it falls into the "I didn't" on the like scale for me. Some reviewers say it's authentic and delicious, so maybe their mammas made gumbo like this. You, dear reader, are just going to have to decide for yourself.

It was a little expensive, though the (yummy) frou-frou beer was a good part of my bill. Can't tell you go there, but won't tell you to stay away. (But try the kriek. Ask for it in something besides a tulip glass.)
Delicious! I've been here twice and both times, my experience was great - food, service, drinks. It's pricey, but worth every penny. Their octopus is the best I've ever had.

We were starving after walking the city for hours. This was such a great find and the classic cheesesteak was the perfect tender meat and gooey cheese. It was so good, definitely still dreaming about it months later.
Came here with some friends. Two of us didn't order anything. One of the waitresses filled the half of the waters at the table and ignored the other guests. Quite rude.

This place has a really nice atmosphere- comfy sofas, bicycle stools, etc. I got a grilled cheese with avocado once and it was really good. I'm not a fan of their coffee at all- couldn't even drink it honestly and some of their snack items are grossly overpriced- it's $3.99 for like 15 grapes. But it's a really nice option for commuters!

Loved my curly cut from Lauren! She was really kind throughout the entire process and talked me through everything she was doing/ explained what products she was using. Definitely will be returning again.

The pain between my shoulder blades and neck had gotten so bad I actually threw up.  Enter Dr Silverman - a few treatments later, I felt wonderful!!!! It was amazing to have the pressure and pain gone in my neck.  Without the pain, I was able to start running again. His staff is the best! Amy rules!!!!

Came back for breakfast and was not disappointed. I ordered the apple cinnamon French toast and my friend ordered the blueberry pancakes. On Saturday's, those two items have a special for $8.99 each. I preferred the pancakes over the French toast. I could taste actual blueberries in the pancakes and it was so good. Two pancakes can feed 2 people. They are also very generous with the amount of butter. The French toast was good, but I wish I tasted the apple. It was good French toast, but I wouldn't order it again. My special came with fresh squeezed orange juice and hands down the freshest orange juice I ever had! I want to come back and try lunch next time...and get the famous apple dumplings again!

Pizza is great, fresh and tasty. Service is fine. BUT, buffalo wings and salmon salad is terrible. Worst ever. My salmon is burnt, BURNT!! Actually the whole surface is charcoaled. Why would they bring something like this out?! I have no idea. The lettuce, it feels like they are dragged thru car wash. The whole place smells weird. Order pizza to go instead of sitting down.

Perfect place for a great quick bite on a Saturday night. Pizzas slices are huge and delicious. You must see the size of these slices. Been here many thanks time and never disappointed. Took my 2 & 5 year old niece and nephew there this past weekend, very kid friendly. A must try in Old City!

This is the closest location to my neighborhood, but not close enough. Even though it's a bit more off the beaten path, it may be the best location to visit if you're driving,as they have a parking lot. It's also might be the biggest, so there's room to sit down.

Other than that, it's the same fantastic donuts, 6 fancies and 3 fresh powdered at a time, that we've come to love, rotating through their menu. The chicken is just as good.

Maybe it's better they don't open in Fishtown, I'd visit far too often.

I am incredibly lucky I am to have Little Babies ice cream within walking distance of my dwelling!

The flavors at L.B. are not for the the 'vanilla crowd', if you get my drift.  If your palate is advanced and/or quirky or you have ever been told by a 'meat & potatoes only-type' that you "eat weird food", then L.B. is your place!

Little Babies offers a variety of Philadelphia style ice cream (dairy) as well as fantastically creamy, non-dairy ice cream. 

 The flavors I have tried are:   (and I love them all)

Banana Chip
Box Of Chocolates
Chipotle Chocolate
Chocolate Milano
Chocolate Salt Malt
Coconut Chai
Coconut Tea
Earl Grey Sriracha
Goldenberg's® Original Dark Peanut Chews® Vanilla Molasses
Goldenberg's® Original Dark Peanut Chews® Chocolate Molasses
Lime Toasted Coconut
Lychee Lemonade
Mint Cookie
Reanimator Coffee Red Eye
Smoked Cinnamon
Speculoos
Thai Iced Tea
Blueberry Lemonade
Chocolate Pomegranate Swirl
Coconut Coffee Kamora
Strawberry Pinkpeppercorn
Sweet Potato Burnt Marshmallow
Peppermint Hot Chocolate
White Russian

Warm welcome, quiet evening.  Basket brought to the table included lightly toasted bread and slices of delicious matzo, made in-house. 

My arugula and shaved parmesan salad didn't include the promised artichoke, but was fresh and perfectly dressed. 

The orechiette and tuna in light tomato and olive sauce  could have fed two of me. There were just enough capers to add bright notes to this dish. 

My companion got ahead of himself and ordered the tartufo for dessert, which did not disappoint. 

This is a fine place for a relaxed dinner.

amazing food and service! the waiter here was so sweet and recommended us some great dishes- we tried the keftah kabob, beef kabob, chicken shawarma, and lamb chops. He even gave us some baklava on the house :) ! I would definitely come back.

headed down there a few weeks ago with my sister and brother in law. i had heard this place had an extensive bar, and my brother in law is a whiskey and rye aficionado- so i thought i'd bring him there and try to impress him. and he was impressed! we had drinks and dinner. the drinks menu is extensive- and really cool! 
the food as delicious. well...my food was. we all shared the farmhouse platter meats, cheeses, fruit, olives) and a spinach salad. i had a special- sweet potato and lamb groton. it was seriously mouth watering. my brother in law wasn't as impressed with the pork he ordered. my sister had quail- which was great and definitely different.
we ended the night with a homemade ice cream sandwich.

i would definitely recommend- although i wish the food menu was a little larger.

I waited in anticipation for Kermit's to open after first seeing life in the building a few months ago while walking my dogs. Knowing the Sidecar people were in on it, I expected good things. After last night's pizza dinner (cheesecake slice shared for dessert), I can say the wait was well worth it. The meal and establishment appeared to be everything I have all come to expect from the folks behind our favorite local watering hole (excellent ingredients, friendly/professional staff, and a relaxed environment). Well done.

This location opened in September however I just noticed it last week! 

I would give it 3.5 stars if I could. 

After the Mummer's parade on New Year's Day, my fiancé and I were looking for a bite to eat. Open Table didn't have many casual dining choices at that time...mostly fine dining. That being said, we figured we would try out this new Iron Hill location since I am such a fan of the Media location. 

I like how it looks from outside. It is very large and the signage beckons you to come in. 

The hostess was nice and seated us immediately for our Open Table reservation.  Our server was nice and provided good service. 

The actual dining area is very large and open. I never really care for that floor plan because it feels like a college dining hall to me but it is what it is. The bar area was nice. The TVs were adequate. Not too many but more than a few. They can be seen from the dining room but there are some obstructions depending on where you sit. 

We stuck to items we typically order at Iron Hill. We split the nachos which seem to be getting smaller and smaller and smaller. It's getting difficult to justify the price for the small amount that you get. I miss the old school large shared plate of nachos. The guacamole on top was good. We got sweet potato fries and I asked for them to be cooked well done which was a good choice because they were perfectly. Recently they have gotten larger and they are always too thick and floppy. I also miss the habanero lime and horseradish sauces. Now they only give you the garlic paprika and the vanilla. We also got the cheesesteak eggrolls which I did not eat but my fiancé enjoyed. They were served with ketchup. Again, the portion size is quite small compared to other restaurants that serve cheesesteak eggrolls. Finally, my fiancé got the roast beef sandwich which he said was flavorless and he would never get it again. He said it couldn't even be saved by the bland horseradish sauce. I normally like it but I didn't try it so I can't comment on the comparison to other times. 

Got the flourless chocolate torte for dessert with raspberries and salted caramel sauce which I thought was an odd combo. 

There is an open kitchen. 

The bathrooms were clean. 

Another table had a hair in their food which is always disgusting to see or hear. 

It's located right by the train station which is convenient. Parking seemed limited right around that area as far as street parking and super close lots.

My office ordered lunch for about 40 people and I was sent to pick it up. The secretary is super organized, and had sent the fax over twice and confirmed the order, all a week in advance. When i got there they had no idea what i was talking about. and no, she didnt accidentally send it to the wrong location. they did manage to make the sandwitches in about a half hour, even on top of thier lunch rush, and they charged me half price for the major screw up, but i wouldnt order catering from them again.

Today was my first but not last visit to this new spot. I have never had chaat before and the very friendly man behind the counter was very helpful.  Delicious and full of different flavors and textures. I look forward to trying all the items on the menu. I found the spice level to be perfect.

A thriving corner store in the age of Wawa!

Clean shop, fresh meats, great hoagies & friendly staff.

Love the local favorites: Amoroso rolls, Stocks pound cake, chrusciki.  How about adding Holmesburg vanilla cream doughnuts?

Not crazy about the fries, otherwise perfect.
The Peanut Chews Vanilla Molasses flavor was AMAZING. It was so creamy. The Peanut Chews were a little hard, but still delicious. And I loved the molasses flavor in the ice cream.

We have been waiting patiently for the restaurant to open after we found out that it was going to be another one of Chip Roman's restaurants. We finally had a chance to go in for a quick bite last night and it was as delicious as expected. We had the gnocchi and beef short ribs. For dessert, we had the rhubarb & strawberry with coconut mousse. Everything was cooked to perfection and service was amicable. The restaurant had a very nice ambience. Would have stayed longer for a few more drinks but was exhausted after a long week. Will definitely return. Highly recommended and you will not be disappointed!

So terrible. I can't remember when's the last time I had a such un-sanitized seafood. I ordered take out and they said it's only gonna take 7 mins, and ended up 30 mins. Don't go here, and food quality is bad and it's too over priced. I literally had a stomach ache afterwards. I went in on a Saturday night, during rush hour (7pm) and guess what THEY HAVE NO CUSTOMERS. What a scam. Very disappointed.

Silk city bitch, silk, silk city bitch.  Dance, dance, dance, dance.....  DJ night is legit.  They keep the crowd pumping.  Staff needs to pay more attention to the fire occupancy levels.  It's less dancing and more fighting for personal space.  Not the type of place I'd go to find a date, but it seems that's what the rest of the crowd is into.  Let me rephrase that: the crowd loves friction-welcomed or unwelcomed.  

Music is 5 stars, everything else is 3.

I admit, I came here because my hair doesn't have a lot of options: it is either long or short... the tragedy of fine, thin hair.  After my last place charged me $65.oo for a haircut that legitimately took 3 minutes, (wet it and cut off two inches before they saw my color) I decided to find new place that was affordable, and hopefully decent.

Deluxe is in my 'hood and the rave reviews (+the 10$ off your first haircut) sold me.  I had an appointment late in the afternoon and was scheduled with Maria.  I waited for almost a 1/2 hour and then because she was so behind, she had someone else wash my hair.

Granted, I do watch a lot of Tabitha's Salon Takeover but still- wash and wet my hair before even talking to me or seeing what my hair looked like? I thought that was bad news. How did she know my hair texture or the damage level?

Once I actually got in the chair, she gave me a decent haircut; again, nothing abnormal but nothing phenomenal.  Since most of my haircuts look the same, the personal time is usually what puts me over the edge or makes me a fan.    Admittedly, I will return because the haircut was fine and the cheapest around.  Hopefully, my trip back will be better and I can rave like the other yelpers here.

I went there twice first to get a sew-in which was okay,and the second time I went to get the "Tree Braiding" and I have to say my hair was nice the person who did my hair name is alice, but there are a couple of flaws that will make me not return anymore. The flaws were it seems like she's the only one there 90% of the time, which means your wait time is way longer or she'll ask you to reshedule because she's by herself. It took her 9 hours to do my hair on a 4 hour job, because she kept stopping working on other customers, I made an appointment twice to get a touch up and twice she told me to reshedule. I paid $250 for the tree braiding which is i think is was over charged but it does last for about 3 to 4 months, but she doesn't offer a free or discounted first touch up which i think she should since you paying $250 dollars. Lela's charges the touch up based on the time spent doing the touched up and the type of braid. So there's no set price. So my touch-up ended up costing me 60 dollars which she wrote down on a piece of paper to tell me, Maybe she didn't want another customer to hear what she was charging me. I thought that was weird when she could have just told me, that looked suspicious to me.

Sara was so easy to work with and did an amazing job on my wedding gown. I wanted a neckline customization that she was not certain would work and she did everything to make the customization work. She has a great attention to detail and did a beautiful bustle as well. She even stitched my Spanx into my dress! I did not even know that was an option! As a nice bonus, her shop is welcoming and adorable and she carries a selection of jewelry (my husband even got me one of the necklaces from her shop as a surprise on the wedding day because I told him how much I loved it when I came home from my first visit!). I would highly recommend Sara and the Tailory!

I have been to Lolita twice, and walked out twice because of the horrid attitude of the tall male host.  Walked over to other other mexi-themed restaurant across the street where I always get great service.  Will never go back.

Great little Italian spot. The spaghetti dish is a generous side with giant meat balls that are stuffed with cheese. Low lighting and pleasant music makes it the ideal place for a romantic date or a pleasant night out with friends!
pretty good, came here when I was full and I ate even more because it reminded me of the ethiopian food I had back in Little Ethiopia in LA, CA.
I got a facial from Cindy. She was good, skin felt great after! The environment want very "spa like", meaning the room wasn't totally dark and you could hear bustling center city so just be aware.

Han Dynasty's food is generally flavourful, although often very oily (i.e., swimming in oil, not in a good way.)  Decent beer selection.  Good service.  

I'll review the food roughly in (descending) order of preference:

1. The highlight of the meal for me was the SESAME NOODLES, which were delicious and more deeply flavoured than versions served elsewhere.  Definitely want those noodles again.

2.  I also enjoyed the Cucumbers in Chili Oil, Garlic sauce tofu, as well as some fairly standard though well-prepared Pea leaves, Bok Choy w/ Black mushrooms, and Eggplant.

3.  Hot sauce scallops were breaded and deep-fried, and silken in texture inside -- the core of the scallop was still raw and soft.  Only problem was that the dish was so oily that I couldn't pick up any pieces of cabbage or other food wo/ picking up tons of oil with it.

4. Thinly sliced Double cooked pork belly is flavourful with leeks.  Texture could be improved -- pork seemed a bit overcooked.

5. Many dishes are dominated by the smoky-spicy flavours of Szechuan peppercorns.  It's worth a try if you like spicy & haven't had them before.  These dishes (Vegetarian ma po tofu without pork, Mung bean noodle) were not my personal favourites although they were definitely flavourful.

6. Fried veggie dumplings were disappointing:  the dumpling skin was much too thick & the filling was on the bland side.  I would avoid these.  My dining companions also apparently felt the same way; nobody seemed to want more dumplings after they had tried their first.

All in all, it was pretty good (especially by Philly standards) & I'd definitely return for Sesame noodles and some of the other dishes.

I was kind of disappointed after all the hype. 

Returned to Philly for a girls weekend and waited an hour and a half for a table for three. Unnecessary. The atmosphere is very nice; the space is pretty. The service was good, but the food, unmemorable.

I had the Hawthorne omelette which was nothing special. The potatoes were quite good, though.

The table split the acclaimed funnel cake french toast which was fine, but the funnel cake was lukewarm and soggy, not hot and crisp like a true carnival funnel cake. It did not turn out to be the fatty, sugary, delicacy I had expected. 

I will stick to other Philly brunch spots like Ants Pants Cafe, and Sabrina's.

This is a tough place to rate. i like the food. it''s good and by food alone i would give it 4 stars. the reason for taking off another star is their phone rings incessantly and obnoxiously, while everyone walks by and ignores it. very very very annoying! After the second time i saw someone walk by and ignore it, i asked if they noticed the phone was ringing. He said "too busy here to answer." Some other tables laughed but he was serious. I asked if he could just mute it, or turn the phone off. ...nope. 

On to the food, I got the eggplant w/ garlic sauce. It's good. rice isn't included but definitely order rice with it. it's supposed to be spicy but wasn't at all. next time i 'll just ask if they can make it really spicy. 

looking forward to trying more menu items.

It seems almost pointless to review Honey's since everyone knows it is the best brunch place in Philly (except those who just want to be contrary and deny themselves the goodness). 

Brunch is phenomenal and worth a 45 minute wait (if it is longer than that, go back another day). Their tofu scramble and enfriolatas are basically heaven and their biscuits are unrivaled in Philadelphia. 

But I did finally go for "lunch" and was properly impressed. The vegetarian chicken fried steak was extremely good and came with macaroni and cheese and green bean casserole - two standards that they do very well. 

It's pretty much impossible to go wrong at Honey's. They attempt to use local ingredients where possible and free-range eggs. The decor is homey and the staff friendly and knowledgeable.

Decided to give the happy hour a shot and so glad I did! I arrived around 5:30 on a Friday and was placed at a booth with no wait, as the restaurant wasn't yet crowded. I tried the BLT which came with kettle chips. The sandwich itself was delicious. I was pleasantly surprised by the portion size, considering it was a $5 happy hour menu option. I later tried the guacamole, which I didn't enjoy as much, but it could be that I was full after eating the BLT! 

The bar area became more crowded as time passed, so the noise level did increase quite a bit. That didn't bother me any, since I was there for the happy hour. 

Overall, I really enjoyed the ambiance, the food was well priced, and the service was great. We even got treated to fried oreos, which were amazing!!

I followed what the other reviewer said and had Lisa tailor my white coat a few months ago. Originally the sleeves were so long that I had to fold them over several times to regain use of my hands. Lisa did an excellent job of measuring me and shortening the sleeves on my coat, and all in a very timely manner. Will definitely go back next time I need anything else hemmed.
Roti Canai is good for App. others are good as the price. Not really clean though
My chicken sandwich literally tasted rotten. Slow service and the cashier had a terrible attitude.

After we got our wedding flowers here, i told my husband not to send me flowers from anywhere else. Today for my 30th birthday I was surprised at work with a beautiful arrangement from Almeida's. My husband asked if there could be tulips even though they are out of season, and there are 2 in the arrangement along with Lillies, orchids, gerbera daisies, and so many others that i do not know the names of (but equally as beautiful!). Thanks again, Artur!
GREAT place for breakfast!! The wait can be long, so we tend to call in and pick up. But the atmosphere is awesome there! Get there early....or late.
Delicious and extremely flavorful. My favorite spot for Mexican. I typically get chorizo tacos and my boyfriend the panchos torta. Both amazing. Great service, super kind and quick. Love it

Been here twice. Both times I spent several hours there doing work, drinking coffee, and enjoying a snack or lunch. The place is comfortable and quiet. The staff is nice, welcoming, and gracious to all of the customers whether they get a cup of coffee or a meal. There were two bathrooms, fast internet speed, and a variety of places to sit and work. It's a nice place to hold a meeting or an interview. Great neighborhood place. Highly recommend it!

@MSP for my sister's funeral I'd never heard of Sixt, but they alone had economy cars cheapest, and the best reviews. Only hitch was courtesy phone at unoccupied desk didn't work, and I'd never been provided the number of the off-airport lot. Quick google search found it here on yelp of all places. 

PLEASE POST THE NUMBER AT THE DESK AT MSP. 

Returning was a breeze, they show you where to refill gas cheapest nearby when you're arriving, and actual return of the car takes seconds. Friendly staff all around. I left my hat in the car & a quick call and they sent a shuttle back to me with the hat. 
Five stars but for the phone snafu.

I think the photos speak loudly. Never have I ever had Biryani with peas. This isn't even considered chicken fried rice, it's just flavorless rice with dry chicken and peas.
