Italian pizza is not the thick crust Pizza Hut-esque junk one gets here in the US.  In Italy the pizza is crispy (and, yes, occasionally a little burnt) at the edge and moist in the middle.  You have to eat it with a fork and knife (because that's what civilized people do) and having your pizza with a tasty glass of wine is normal (because what's better with a garlicky tomato sauce than a rich red), not pretentious (says the girl talking about pizza in Italy.  Moving on).

We had the arancini, caesar salad (REAL ANCHOVIES IN THE DRESSING!!  Hallelujah.) and the sausage pizza.  Service was GREAT.  Many of Stephen Starr's restaurants leave me with a bad taste in my mouth, but not this place.  We will definitely be back.
Generically tasty food and drinks. Typically perfect lighting, acoustics, decor, service, etc. Consistent. Reliable. Soulless. It reminds me of McDonald's somehow.

I moved to the area in January and have been patiently waiting for them to open. It was well worth the wait! I went the Saturday before Easter and wow were they busy! I made an appointment for later in the day instead of waiting. I have a feeling they're going to be busy often so I'd definitely recommend making an appointment.
I got a pedicure, gel manicure, eyebrow wax and lip wax and was extremely happy with all of my services. Extremely clean, very professional, but also warm and welcoming at the same time. It's a family owned business and they very much exemplify that welcoming neighborhood feel that I love so much about Roxborough!
I'm looking forward to making this my regular spot!

I give Soulfedphilly 3 stars.

The sauce is awesome but the shrimp are not deveined and the mussels are not cleaned well. I had a few hairy mussels.
nice place to go for a date night! very peaceful and tasty food . good selection of wines

I honestly wish I could give this salon more than 5 stars. The space, the staff, and the work is absolutely beautiful. Christopher is hands down the most talented stylist who has ever worked on my hair and his down to earth and welcoming personality made the experience even better. He completely transformed my hair and I have never left a salon feeling this confident until now. I can't say enough about this salon, it is really a game changer. Thank you a million times over!!!

We booked this hotel through the Park, Stay & Fly program, as we had a very early flight and needed a place to park the car for our 6 day trip.   The other hotels were already booked for the night, or over $150/night.  But we decided the Microtel for $100 would be suffice.  Next time, pay the money for another hotel!  

The room was so small that one of us had to sit on the bed in order for the other to walk through.  And that's with only 2 suitcases in the room.  The bed was horrible.  I would have actually gotten more sleep if I would have slept on the floor, but I didn't even think about doing this here.   Shuttle service was very reliable and about the only good thing from our stay.

Omg. Food here is AMAZING! This was my first time visiting Sister Muhammad's at the recommendation of a friend. They told me to try the fish hoagie and lamb cheesesteak and both were delicious. The fish was cooked to perfection--  fried crisply outside but incredibly tender inside (get it with hot sauce). The lamb cheesesteak was my favorite. The lamb tasted distinctly like lamb and was seasoned to perfection. I savored each and every bite. 
The cashier was a bit clueless. I asked for a takeout menu and she told me to take a picture of the dine in menu. I asked if there was a menu online and she said she thought there was. I asked what the website was and she looked at me blankly and said she had no idea. Regardless, the food was amazing and I'll definitely be back. I love supporting local and black owned businesses and this place definitely deserves community support.

Sunday morning in University City/West Philadelphia.  I was hungry and happy I ended up in front of Cedar Park Cafe.  I was really happy I kept reading the menu until I found the Danwich section.   A breakfast cheesesteak with eggs and hash browns. Everybody's on the good traditional roll together. I do wish everybody got mixed together on the grill but little bite discipline takes care of that.  $7.50, you know I love that.

Hot Diggity leads with their hot dogs, but the well stand out are the fries. And not only do have have some of the best fries in town, they have these amazing dipping sauces. And there are dozens of them to try.

The hot dogs can be meaty or vegan. And they all have a creative name that is illustrated by amazing comic book quality art. They also have house made sodas. My favorite is the Lime Cilantro Jalapeno.

Try their roast pork and duck rice plate! Super yummy 
Their soups are tasty as well..
The only soup I thought was mediocre was the chicken curry soup
The broth was very bland compared to their other soups.
Their fried dumplings are delicious..
 Overall you can't go wrong

I have been trying to get to Max Brenner's for a couple of years.  I was pleasantly surprised at how delicious the food was. We started with a hummus trio that was served with toasted flat bread and the wings. Two of us had burgers with the chocolate powder dusted fries. Best burger I've had in awhile. Of course we were there for CHOCOLATE so we shared two desserts. We had a fondue and something that looked like a deconstructed affagato. I wish I could remember the proper names. The quality of these desserts was above par. The reason I said I was surprised at the quality and gave four stars was the ambience. There was a slightly neglected feel to the restaurant. The wall next to our table has screws and picture hangers that were painted over and nothing else. The servers were all sitting at one table  and when I went to the bathroom I had to step over a pair of shoes one of them had slipped off and left in the walkway.  The service was prompt and friendly.  It's not an inexpensive night out.

These tacos are legit. The portions are generous - there is a lot of meat in each! Honestly, 2 tacos would be enough for me but I was very hungry and I just went for 3. Also, I was with 2 friends to share - which brings me to why I docked a star. No mixing and matching? Who wants 3 of the same taco? The beauty of having so many varieties is being able to try them! 

I shared the Korean short rib, brisket, and chorizo tacos. I think the chorizo was my favorite, followed close by the brisket. The Korean short rib was good too but I think the sauce was a bit sweet. 

There are 4 salsa options. However 2 were labeled hot and 2 were very mild... Maybe some bottles were missing but I found it strange there was nothing in between. 

Overall, delicious tacos. Definitely try it (with friends to mix and share)!

Great casual Italian fare. I went for dinner a few nights ago and I ordered the cacio e pepe, which was delicious. The portions are generous and all of the pasta dishes come with focaccia. Service was quick and friendly.

I had a very negative experience at this hotel. I realize it is not a 5-star hotel, but it is not cheap either. And for the amount I spent to stay there I expected excellent guest services and accommodations. The front desk staff was rude to me on arrival. When I entered my room there was trash on the floor left over from the housekeeping service, as well as dresser drawers pulled open. I did not mention this to anyone as mistakes can happen. However, the next day when I returned to my room after housekeeping had cleaned it, there was a toilet brush left on my dresser. I felt that to be disgusting. When I approached the housekeeping staff on the floor I was met with an attitude. When I called down to the front desk to speak to housekeeping management, I was told I would receive a call back shortly which never happened. I called management the next day who was apologetic and accommodated my inconvenience, but I felt the need to request no one enter my room further. I did not feel comfortable staying there with such negative back and forth. Although the room and interior were adequate and the facility is located in a great spot, I would not ever spend money to stay there again.

4 stars 4 days in... I'd say they're doing very well for still being a newbie.

So far, so good. The menu is pretty big and has delicious, fresh offerings. As well as excellent drinks--both of the alcoholic and mon-alcoholic variety.

We had the grilled corn and asparagus salad with homemade buttermilk herb dressing and chicken salad with grapes sandwich on thick, toasted, grainy bread.  Both were fantastic.,The shoestring fries also rocked.

It's a beautiful airy space with a nice bar and soon to have outdoor space... I hope it excels.  And I hope to try it again soon.

Granted I just moved from Texas so I have high standards for my Mexican food, but these tacos were not the best. They are absolutely huge in comparison to any other taco place I've been to. I felt like they needed a bit more garnish and flavor.

Watched 5 of every other rental bus drive by before I was picked up from the airport. Bus was packed. As we drove past the rest of the stops without stopping I saw many waving, laughing, and flailing for attention. Clearly a bad day for hertz pickup at the Philly airport.

I called Mark at 4p on a Friday and he was just finishing up a job with A.J. a few blocks away. They were at my home within minutes, quoted the job and finished at 5:15p. Great guys, great service, fair rates, and a pleasure all around to work with. Recommend to anyone looking for responsible guys to help with your junk.

We have been coming here just about weekly for a few years now. The menu just gets better and better and I love all the gluten free options. It is super kid friendly and a wonderful mainstay of the neighborhood.

The layout is your typical Philly BYOB - small storefront setting with 15 or so tables pretty close together, and a rather short, pricey menu.  My friend and I each ordered the sirloin medium-rare, which was done to perfection.  I could have used a little more of the potatoes and greens, honestly - they were very tasty - but I know how chefs are about presentation.  The waiter was attentive and the food came out surprisingly quickly.  It was actually an older, classier crowd than I was expecting, although more young folks seemed to come in as the evening wore on.  Overall, an excellent meal

First to mention, I am not a big Brunch person so my opinion is that the food is fair in taste. It seems like any other normal breakfast type food. It was my first meal of the day so luckily I had an appetite to finish the portions. They give a sizeable amount of food per order. I got the Huevos Rancheros and it was a good blend of starchy potatoes and eggs with veggies. As a non fan of brunch, i would not go here unless other friends are down to go.

It is a BYOB location so get your drink on if you have a solid block in the morning or early afternoon to catch up and enjoy your time with your friends.

A family friend had got us some long hots, mozarella cheese and stromboli from this place and I thought they were all very good. I immediately checked out the reviews here and came here last week. I wanted to get a chicken meatball sandwich. I did not realize that they just sold meatballs individually. However, the gentleman at the counter called someone inside and he agreed to make a chicken meatball sandwich with their homemade gravy. When the sandwich came out, I immediately realized that this was going to be my lunch and dinner. The first bite and I could tell this was the best meatball sandich I had eaten in my life. My search for the best meatballs in Philly have come to a grinding halt at this place. I don't think anyone can make better meatballs than these guys. Absolutely the best.

There's breakfast is really good.  My sister had the ribs and liked them.  I got the pork sausage and it was bomb! So glad this place opened up close to my place.  What we had was great. They said they are still in the soft opening stage so they didn't have any of their korean bbq.  Will definitely come back for more.  The service is great and the waitress was so nice and friendly.

Went to this place a while back with a friend. I had been searching for an Ethiopian place since moving here from the DC metro area. After doing a bit of searching on Yelp, I decided to try it.

We ended up going on a Saturday afternoon in mid November. When we got there, we were the only people in the restaurant and it was pretty chilly inside. Part of that was because the space is pretty small, so any time someone opened the door, cold air rushed in and filled the space. However, I'm not even sure the heat was even on. It was so cold, we ended up leaving our jackets on the entire time. 

The service was fine. We were greeted once we walked in and our orders were taken shortly after we sat down. As for the food, it was nothing to write home about. We the had the Combination for Two, which included dohro wet, kitfo ground beef, and wet lamb. Of the three dishes, my favorite was probably the ground beef. They provide plenty of injera to scoop the meat and sides. Overall the meal was good, but I was a bit let down. The glowing reviews I had read for this place had gotten me really excited and my experience didn't really match them. I've had better at several other places in DC. There are a few other Ethiopian places nearby that I'm looking to check out in the near future. And I may come back to Meskerem just to see if I have a different experience the next time.

It's all about the view, folks.  We ate lunch at Jean-George last weekend.  I had a craving for a burger, so why not?  For $24, it's gotta be good, right?  Just OK.  Nothing great.  The restaurant was filled with wait staff, but everyone seemed a bit confused.  So, I sat without any sugar for my iced tea for quite a while.  Go for the view, have a cocktail, and skip the lunch.

There are a lot of reasons to check out Rice and Spice. You can pick up all your essential Indian cooking supplies. (I loaded up on bricks of paneer and pre-mixed spice packets with which to cook the paneer.) But, more important, you can impress your date with your knowledge of local culinary culture by taking him/her to the great little restaurant hidden from view in the back of store. The food tastes like home-cooking and is reasonably priced. If you have the time, you should eat in and enjoy your food while watching Bollywood movies on the MASSIVE flat-screen TV.
they don't even deserve 1 star. Rude and the food was awful.
I rented in Graduate Hospital for two years and am overall satisfied with the value, responsiveness of the landlord, and ease of resolving issues. Would recommend!

I have been going to Cooperage since they first opened and I have to say that I've enjoyed every visit. They started out on a great foot with boiled peanuts and sweet cornbread being served instead of the normal bread and butter; however, recent visits have discovered they no longer offer that- in fact, they don't seem to offer anything in it's place just yet, but I'm sure that will change. 

The food here is always great. Think classic Southern dishes (pulled BBQ, meaty chili, fried chicken) with nice twists (fruity sauces). The cheese plates are also amazing and well-priced and I heard the waiter talking about the lady who picks the cheese out- she actually goes to the farms and tastes the cow's milk?! I don't know how true that is, but kudos for an interesting story! 

The whiskey is also great, but that's not Cooperage's doing really. I mean, they do carry my local fave- Root, so they get points for that, but the prices are a little rich for my blood. That being said, it didn't stop my friends from going overboard one night and racking up a huge bill...of drunken fun. The happy hour crowd is fun and it gets packed fast, so get their early to grab a seat at the bar if you're trying to watch any game on tv!

Bonus: There's outdoor seating, but it's right on the sidewalk/next to 7th Street. 

Special Note: It's in the Curtis Center, home of one of the most beautiful Tiffany mosaics I've ever seen and also a really cool marble fountain. Take a walk right through the Center after dinner to check it out!

Found this place as I walked around Philly looking for a good bar for a beer on St. Patty's and found a beer Mecca. I was so blown away by this place. As a matter of fact it may be Philly's only redeming quality... oh and of course the whole Declaration of Independence thing...

If I ever happen to find myself in Philly again I will get my hotel as close to this place as possible. With Chimay and the Duvel in my gullet along with some amazing frittes... I would prefer to be in crawling distance. Cheers!

My toddler doesn't have a Yelp account so I'll post on his behalf! This is the first place he ever tried pizza. He loved it! Not only does he love it, he has learned the route from our house to Spuntinos! If we walk nearby and attempt to go a different direction he will get out of his stroller and walk in the direction of Spuntinos. God forbid their not open! What I love is that Spuntinos uses good clean ingredients so I don't feel bad feeding my kid pizza. It's delicious real food, not greasy, not heavy, just delicious!

This place is pretty good. Ordered a turkey burger which was awesome, and sweet potato fries which were gross, but had potential. They were hand cut fries (fresh). The issue is they covered them in a sweet syrupy sauce which made the fries mushy and disgusting. I would totally get the jawbreaker again, but the can keep those weird french fries.

Pure Design created the most beautiful bouquets for my August wedding, and on short notice! I called the shop a few days before a major holiday and less than two months before my wedding, and Michael was able to meet with me within 48 hours. After showing him a few photos from Pinterest, he was able to put together a look that was exactly what I was going for, and (most importantly) within my budget. I'm so pleased with how our bouquets turned out, and I would highly recommend Pure to other brides.

One of my new favorite happy hour spot! The bartenders were amazing and always attentive. The food was delicious and for the price it was unbeatable. My favorite dish was the tuna poke. I would definitely come here again.

Quick stop in Philly today for business and figured: when in Philly, gotta have a cheesesteak. Ordered a cheesesteak to go with provolone minus the mushrooms. I was parked illegally and the girl at the counter offered to run it out to my car even though it was 6 degrees with -15 wind chill. Gave her a tip of course and she met me at my car in less than 3 minutes. Food was excellent and i will definitely be back next time i'm in town!

Bill came over after my outside lock stopped working- the key went in and turned, but wouldn't un-lock.   It wasn't an emergency so he came a couple days later, when he said, and on time.  I was so embarrassed when it worked!  He explained what happened- it was really hot, and suggested I hose it down if it happens again when it's really hot an humid, and that would contract the metal a bit. And when I asked how much, because he did fulfill his obligation by coming, he said "no charge".  He was so nice.  I put his number in my phone in case I need him!

I love this hotel.  This staff are friendly. The rooms are clean and neat.  The room decor is relaxing and the floors are quiet.  The bed is the most comfortable hotel bed I've ever slept in! The bathroom is roomy, though the shower temp could be hotter and it could use an exhaust fan. The lighting in the room is very nice. I found one lamp was enough light to cover the entire room.

Inflation is hitting the falafel guy hard - his prices increased 20% from $8.00 in 2012 to $10.00 in 2013.  Sometimes I think there's a fine line between genius and insanity.  Our Philadelphia falafel truck owner, Konstantinos, is definitely is quite bright and an excellent cook.  I met the falafel guy (I did not ask him his name my first time there) and I think it's safe to say I liked him instantly.  I like hard-working self-employed immigrants. He was blasting good 80's music from his satellite radio.  This guy has no inhibitions or hangups interacting with his customers despite his social oddities.  I love it.  I had to try this place myself after having the food compared to my much beloved Hined and Nabil at the King of Falafel cart located at 16th and JFK.  If I am going to eat out, I like to order food that I would not be able to cook at home myself.  This guy fits the bill perfectly.  I think he has a charcoal grill running in his truck.  I could smell it from a block or two away.  I had some kind of grilled chicken and fig dish that had a wonderful flavor.  His hummus was really good with a sweet taste to it.  He had fresh alfalfa sprouts that went great in the large platter.  I think his falafel tastes really good, but it is too greasy for my tastes for regular consumption.  I had enough food for 2 people, but I ate it all.  It was so good.  Konstantinos said he could be working at a restaurant in New York City, but he loves doing his own thing.  He takes 1 order at a time and interacts with you while cooking.  He joked with me calling me "Mr. Professional Professional" by the the time my food was done.  He proudly says he says he uses "quality food; quality ingredients" and I believe him.  He takes his work seriously, serving 1 quality dish per day.  I have now visited twice and it seems to be consistently good.  As long as you're prepared to wait in line for awhile, you'll have a deliciously spiced, though slightly greasy meal with fresh vegetables, falafel and meat.  Who could ask for more?

Walking by one day on Main St. Manayunk and I saw this place. I've heard good things about this restaurant so I decided to try it out. 

This place is gorgeous. 

I had the Sushi and Sashimi for 2. My lady never really had it, but she was all for it when the plate appeared right in front of us. It was a chef's choice and it was a great decision.

Though a little pricey, the quality and care is there. 

If you enjoy japanese fare, go to this place because its very hard to find good a good sushi place around philly!

We recently ordered takeout from Laser Wolf for my birthday and it was ah-maz-ing! The portions were huge, granted we ordered an extra entrée, but we had food for 3 days. It's located near Fishtown and offers both outside dining and takeout. We ordered earlier in the day since we weren't sure if they would sell out, but they had everything ready for pickup at our designated time. I would highly recommend ordering early if you're looking to have a meal over the weekend. I believe you can order ahead and set the day/time that you are looking to pick up. They are currently open from Tuesday thru Saturdays.  If we could have ordered everything on the menu, I think we would have. It is a bit pricy, but 100% worth every single bite. It was definitely one of the better meals I've ever had.

Each takeout option comes with your entree, hummus and pita, salatim (Israeli salads), and dessert. We ordered the options of skewers- you can pick any 2 (beef, chicken, lamb, and vegan options), the branzino as well as the Tuna Crudo. We opted for more food instead of adding on cocktails since we have plenty of wine at home. Also, my boyfriend mentioned that it was my birthday on the reasons to celebrate/order and we received a free lamb chop. My only complaint is that we needed more pitas for all the delicious food that we got. I did recently see that you can now add 2 pitas. Their menu is always changing so I can't wait to return and try all the other items that we didn't try already.

Highlights: 
Hummus and pita: fluffy, pillowy pitas served with ultra-creamy hummus. I have no idea how they make it so soft and delicious. Please teach me your ways. I would highly recommend ordering more pita since we barely had enough for 1/4 of the hummus. It would have been nice to have pita for the remainder of the meal. It looks like it was a ton of olive oil, but it was only on top. The rest of the container was deliciously smooth hummus.

My favorite salatim: 
Turkish Tomato Salad - definitely my favorite salatim that we had. It was super fresh, good herb note, and balanced with salt.
Ajvar - I had to google what this was. It was a mixture of smoky eggplant and red pepper. It had a slow heat to it and paired really well with the pita. I would eat it with a spoon and bathe in it if I could. It reminded me of Israeli pico (salsa).
Israel Pickles with Shipkas - tart pickles with spicy hot peppers. Warning: these peppers are really really spicy so be prepared to dive into the pita or other salatim to cool your mouth down. We ended up not eating most of the peppers due to the heat.
Cucumbers with dill - classic cucumber and dill dish. Fresh and definitely palate cleansing. Much needed after eating those spicy shipkas.

Tuna Tartare - served with Schug (spicy jalapeno sauce), ginger, and cucumber. It was light, fresh with a creeper heat from the Schug sauce. The pickles and ginger added a nice crunch. If you like tuna or want to try a tartare, I recommend adding this to your order.

Lamb Chop- it had a good spice level, subtle. The meat was super tender, almost buttery, and smooth. It was cooked to perfection. It was so nice of Laser wolf to add it to our order. I'm so glad we got to try it.

Chicken skewer with harissa- the chicken itself wasn't that spicy which was surprising since most of my experiences with harissa have been mouth-numbing. It was really juicy and flavorful. I highly recommend that you eat it with the remaining salatim for an extra delicious bite.

Bulgarian Beef kebab - the kebab had a smoky flavor with a slight paprika spice. It reminded me of a lumpy hotdog but way more flavorful.

Branzino - a whole branzino with tomato and ginger. It honestly fell right off the bone and was so tender. It was meaty and not fishy at all. There was a slight heat to it and towards the center of the fish, there were hints of ginger. The branzino was definitely the best fish I ever ate. Highly recommend ordering this if you like seafood. Probably the best thing we ate all night.

Key lime bars - black lemon, amaretti crust with a white chocolate ganache, and labneh whip. I don't typically like key lime pie but damn, this was out of this work. It was a perfect balance of sweet and tart and completely balanced. The creamy whip on top was heavenly.

All in all, Laser Wolf was amazing. Everything we had was cooked and prepared to perfection. The food was incredible, especially after transporting about 20ish minutes to home. I highly recommend ordering from Laser Wolf if you have the chance.
Went here for lunch today. Burger was still a bit squishy/messy but good. Fries were much better and the cheese dipping sauce was rad. Wish the seating inside was more accommodating for groups of 3+

Brittany was a great realtor and advisor for us. She was very responsive to our questions and concerns. She went above and beyond to explain documentation, how the home buying process works, etc. She was patient and understanding as we took our time during the home search and in the end we found the perfect home. Highly recommend working with her. Thank you Brittany!

Went on a Friday during lunch and was in and out before my actual appointment at 12:45. The technician was very good. Would give 5 starts but i prefer to lay down as I often become faint while getting blood drawn and they only had one semi-recliner chair option available. Other than that, quick, clean and friendly.

Basketball Court is Unleveled. The Neighborhood is ghetto and very dirty. Trash is all over the place, they have workers everyday picking up trash just because people in the neighborhood are dirty and on top of that there are drugs being sold on every corner. This playground receives only one star based on the fact that Will Smith recorded his intro to Fresh Prince of Bel-Air on this playground. These are real facts.
It took over an hour and a half before finally being seen by the doctor. All the staff including Dr Ryan Offer were really great! Unfortunately I probably won't go back due to the long wait time

Simply Wow! I've heard so much about this place but never had access to a car to go into the heart of South Philly (10 and Wolf). I heard all about how great the tacos were but when I got to Los Gallos I was over whelmed by the amount of choices. I ordered three tacos; chorizo, al pastor and the steak. They were all very tasty. I also had an order of quesadillas which was the best mexican meal I've ever had. I have no idea what kind of cheese was inside the Dilla but oh geez I couldn't get enough of it. 

Go there and enjoy real Mexican. No point wasting your time on silly Center City Mexican restaurants.

D'Angelo's is a little off the beaten path to where I would normally go for lunch location wise, but I am so happy I did! The hostess wished me a happy birthday as soon as we arrived and the owner also sang me happy birthday, talk about great service!

We each had the signature D'Angelo's salad, it was so interesting... in a good way (think egg, bacon, shrimp, and an awesome house dressing). I had the lobster ravioli, which was in the most amazing sauce. My guest had the chicken parm and seemed very pleased with it. Dessert included, of course, delicious canolis!

We were very pleased and will both be back in the future.

I never had draft latte in my life until now. It's really flavorful and a treat. Feels like an iced coffee but it's far better. The atmosphere in La Colombe is so cool and neat, but simple vibe. I wish that they at least filled it up to the top, because there was close to an inch of room in my cup left... paying $3.50, that little bit matters since it tastes so yummy.

This was my first race and I absolutely loved it.  It was a well thought out race and had so many details that were well executed.
I did the 5k, but I signed up for the fast corral because I have been training.  It was the perfect corral because I ran the entire time and I didn't have to run around too many people walking.
When I was feeling thirsty the water and Gatorade stations were at the perfect place.  They definitely kept me hydrated.  
During the race I was hungry and the hot chocolate mug was perfect at the end. The large mug was more like a plate that came with hot chocolate,  plenty of chocolate fondue and snacks to dip with.  Since it was a chilly day it was a perfect snack.  They also had stations with tissues and wipes because the hot chocolate fondue can get messy.  
As an added bonus they gave us a swag bag when we picked up our race bibs prior to the race.  My favorite swag item had to be the 1/2 zip jacket/pullover.  It came with a hood, thumb holes and zip up pocket.  I was not expecting such a good jacket/pullover and I will wear it a lot in the future.

We used a half price groupon and still the bill was way too high for what we got. The food is good but portions are very small, not worth the price. I have had just as good food in order and sit-down or take out type of place for a lot cheaper and more generous portions. Mint tea was very good though :)

I mean, it's a cheesesteak. As far as Philly cheesteaks go, this place has a pretty great variety with a friendly staff. It was a pretty good cheesesteak. VERY large serving size which is always wonderful. I would say a bit above average. Plenty of seating , easy to find. I would go back.

I saw this place on a few blogs and compared to Jim's on South Street, I am a bit underwhelmed but still - pretty good. That's all I got.

I got here and was seated promptly upstairs with 2 other occupied tables.  I placed my lunch order for the chicken and proceeded to wait.  Another couple was brought up to the seating area about 10 minutes after me and also placed the same exact order.  

30 minutes into waiting, the couple that was seated after me gets their entire order while the table before me and I looked at each other like "Wtf just happened?"

I waited 45 minutes in total, no one came to tell me what was going on and even if I were to fill in the blanks myself and say the chicken needed to be cooked, that still doesn't explain how the couple after me got their order first.  

I've been here several times and this is the worst experience I've ever had.

Such a great place to have a fun night out with friends! Checked in with yelp and received a free shoe rental. Service was great and responsive would definitely recommend for a fun night out. 

The food portions are average but there are plenty of options to chose from. I prefer this location over the other bowling options in the city. :)

I can't attest to the authenticity of this place,  but as a sandwich lover I have to recommend the train wreck po' boy. It struck me as a cajun flavored schmitter. Steak, salami, andouille sausage, cheese, and onions on a nice roll. It was awesome. The cajun fries are good as well. Everything else looked pretty good, so I will have to go back and check it out.

Delicious steaks, horrible parking. Worth getting a ticket for illegal parking. Tender, flavorful steak with whatever toppings you like (just Wiz for me) and they let you take a moment to decide what you want - unlike 2 other famous Philly cheesesteak places. We got takeout but would have preferred to eat in if we had been able to park legally. Clean restaurant and friendly staff. Next time I will be getting mine with fried onions and mushrooms! The aromas were definitely mouthwatering coming from the grill. And of course what makes every great cheesesteak is a delicious roll. And the fries looked and smelled heavenly even though we didn't get any. Next time!

The girl that helped me with a passport photo here today was awesome!  I didnt catch her name and sadly for this store, she is leaving for bigger and better things.  I was in and out in less than 15 minutes and the attitude here was great.

Came in for brunch on a Saturday afternoon, around 1-1:30 PM.  It was not crowded at all, but don't let that deter you.  I was tempted by the cereal bar.  While someone below said, "why pay $6 for capn crunch plus tax and tip?", I say "why not pay $6 for cap'n crunch and every other amazing childhood cereal, all you can eat!"  It seems like a kitschy thing for Supper to do.

But alas, I resisted.  We had the deviled eggs for an app.  I just do not like deviled eggs I think, but my boyfriend looooved them.  You can definitely taste the difference among all the types.  My fave was the one with bacon.

Then we shared the pumpkin french toast and the red velvet waffles.  Pumpkin french toast was amazing.  You could taste pumpkin flavors and spices in the bread.  It wasn't a huge serving at first glance, but plenty to keep you full.  The red velvet waffles, in contrast, were a really huge serving.  The cream cheese creme or whatever it was on top was so rich and delightful.  The waffles themselves were pretty ordinary in taste, despite being that deep red color.  I never really thought red velvet tasted that different though.  

Service was excellent; super attentive server, refilled water at all times.

Definitely want to try the other items on the brunch menu sometime!

I ordered a custom cake for my daughters 2nd birthday party. It was a 3D replica of a septa bus, inspired by the bus that goes by our house. All the kids loved it, and adults remarked on how tasty it was.

My yoga history is ample.  Despite my lack of sheer flexibility, I have done vinyasas, downdogs, sweat, mats, big classes, little classes, hatha, and bikram with the best of em.  I found Raja Yoga about 2 months ago and cherish it.  It's a quaint little studio with quirks to boot.  When you enter into the small room, Wes, the owner/operator, is there to greet you and remember you forevermore.  

I really feel like I'm taken care of at Raja Yoga.  Wes, as well as the other instructors, pay attention to your needs and will give you various poses to work with whatever creaky (or non creaky) body you came with.  

Wes is also very attuned to the environment.  I call this TEC: total environment control.  Too hot? He's opening a window.  Too cold? Here's a blanket. 

I always leave in a yoga blissed state.  Hop on board! Namaste.

We stopped by for brunch before we left and man I wish I could re-eat my meal 1000000x because it was so good. We started off with the Mango Mimosas by the pitcher and an order of the Hot Chicken sliders. These blew my mind! I almost canceled my real order just to order 5 more of these because they were so tasty. But instead we got the breakfast burrito and veggie omelette adding bacon to it. Both were great, delicious choices. Everything was so good!!! The aesthetics, music, food, drinks, service 12/10! And this spot is black owned! Definitely will be coming back to this spot anytime I am in the area. The also sell chicken and waffles and French toast as I know those are popular dishes as well. Indoor and outdoor seating available.

Went here to get microblade,
The stylist who did mine was fired a day before my second appointment.
I was told to wait for her call to work it out with her..
I found that to be  ridiculous since I took off work for this and paid 600$ to the salon.
I thought they should respectively give me an appointment with another stylist.
They did but not long after called me again to try move my appointment.
I'm not going back. 
Plus the atmosphere here was uncomfortable.

I came her based on a recommendation from an acquaintance and was completely happy in every way. My brother and law stopped here before a show at the Trocadero and were able to get our order in time to see the opening band. The food is delicous -the rolls are soft yet not too soft with meat cooked perfectly and slight crunch of onions not overdone like some others. The cheese was melted into the steak and mushrooms were delicous -just exceeded my expectations and they even have a nice small cooler full of local and national craft beer which is a plus.
4 stars for Legals Seafood restaurant. The rest was just an airport like any other.

Stopped here for lunch after wife and I visited the PHS Flower Show.  A proper British Gastropub indeed.  Had possibly the best fish and chips I've ever had (state side or in England).  Paired with a Yards IPA, it couldn't have been better.  Ambiance was excellent and service/bartenders were superb, what a fun place to be!  I'm definitely a fan and it's about as good as it gets.

Picture this absurdist comedy, if you will: 

Felicia arrives at the United departure gate in what her sleep-deprived brain considers to be a timely fashion. After receiving her boarding pass from the electronic ticket kiosk (no bags to check = no line), she ascends the escalator to be stopped short by what can only be described as an apocalyptic TSA line. 

Trudging along in the stream of disgruntled humanity, our heroine realizes with growing alarm that she is edging dangerously close to her West Coast-bound plane's departure time of 6:22 a.m.

Furiously hurling sweater, sandals, laptop and carry-on in the bin before finally waved through as a NON-terrorist, observe Felicia fling everything indiscriminately into her carry-on and race, pell-mell, towards that impossibly distant Gate D9. 

Sprinting barefoot through the terminal in naught but a flimsy camisole and cut-off shorts, her roller suitcase cornering like a late-model Lotus, Felicia skids sideways down the jetway as the last passenger to make the flight. 

Though their newly-arrived guest is now panting like an elderly canine in heat, United's flight attendants cheerfully provide Felicia with her own entire can of Diet Coke and look discreetly away when she tears up (repeatedly) over the in-flight Miley Cyrus movie.  Those tears give way to loony giggles when The Simpsons follows some lame-o family comedy on the screen. 

We close the curtain on this wee slice of life with these parting thoughts: United, if you gave us a food choice when we bought our tickets, why did you serve no food?  Also, god bless assigned seating.

I'm visiting from LA, got The Block sandwich and a Sterling Pig Brewery "The Snuffler" on recommendation from the bar tender. Both were awesome. It also happened to be happy hour and I got a great blueberry margarita for just $6.50. Cool location, welcoming service, good food, good drinks, highly recommend!

I hope this place reopens soon - two stories of goodness! The space is airy and very well lit with tons of natural light. Came for brunch and we were seated immediately and service was quick. My daughter loved her pancakes and I had a really delicious tomato soup. Super convenient location close to museums and sights.

Super fast! Came in at 11:30 out by 12. 
Staff was super friendly and dr was great. Was also able to get my prescription there instead of going to the pharmacy

Seaweed salad was good. It wasn't drenched in dressing. Although I could stand to lose the lettuce and tomatos. 
I'm not the craziest about Japanese fusion, but if your looking for some good comfort sushi at a reasonable price this your place. They serve very large chief specials, and half sized normal rolls. Their shitaki roll was wonderfully marinated. And my server, Min, was wonderfully helpful. They only thing I didn't appreciate was my scallop sashimi was seared.

I'm glad I finally found a replacement for the NYC barbershop I used to go to.  I will be coming here every time I need a haircut and I would recommend it to everybody.  I had tried some other establishments in the city, and it was hard to pre-judge them because they all seem to have 4-5 stars on yelp.  Therefore I had tried ones which were trying too hard to be "old school" but were more like groups of men dressed like undertakers who gave sub-par haircuts.  

Today my experience was great. I would recommend asking for Lou if he's available.  He got out a book with pictures and had me choose the ones most similar to what I wanted.  He didn't even ask about clippers, he used scissors only.  That was really what I was looking for in this haircut, and I felt very comfortable in this store.  I enjoyed the conversation and will certainly be back.

This is the first place I've visited since moving to Philly where the words gourmet and affordability coexist.  The small shop atmosphere on the corner of 19th and Pine seems like a staple to the local community.  I go here for three main reasons:

1. The deli - it can't be better!  Top-notch sandwiches, wraps, and salads for a very reasonable price.  Subway's Five Dollar Foot-long? Nah, would much rather go here!  Fresh product for the health-conscious individual.  Can't go wrong with Boar's Head deli, fresh veggies, and various dressings, cheeses, and breads.  Best advice I can give: stop here for the deli!

2. Small grocery shopping.  Sure, it's a small store, and it fits all my non-major shopping needs.  Snacks tend to be more reasonably priced here than in surrounding grocery stores.  Small fruits and vegetables section which prices are either comparable or better than others.  Coffee and (for the occasional cheat perhaps) pastries are available.

3.  Freshly Squeezed Orange Juice.  Okay, so this has been an odd discovery for me since coming here, but I just can't go back to boxed OJ!  Call this a personal preference, but this is where I get my freshly-squeezed orange juice fix.

Actually, there is a fourth reason - the employees are friendly, and the place is clean!  I'm always looking for a "reason" to stop by!
I got my flowers at Bee's for my wedding and Kelly was very helpfull and the flowers were fresh and beautifull!

This is a new sushi restaurant in the Target shopping center... They have done a good job decorating the interior. Their specials all day everyday is 50% off selected sushi and hand rolls. It's actually a large list of items to choose from at 50%.. I've been in a couple times since they opened a few weeks ago and think this place is definitely worth a try. It's also a byob with the Beer Center just two doors to the left. 
It'll be one of my late lunch spots from now on..

My favorite vintage store in the city!  This boutique boasts unique finds at reasonable prices.  Sure, you could find something cheaper on your own, but this store knocks out hours and hours of searching for creative and unique pieces.  

I love to wear vintage pieces that are special, but not over the top.  At Vintage Connection, I can find that middle ground, and still search through some really funky clothing.  An added bonus to enjoying this store is the weekly updates on their website.  The only downside?....You do have to try on all prospective buys because you cannot return items, and sizing is always up-in-the-air with the older clothing.

As a contractor I eat out a lot for lunch. I saw the reviews and decided to try it out. As I sat down at the table I noticed how clean and fresh the dining looked. Then came the waitress Tawnya. All business and very efficient. She answered all the questions took our order and brought the food back so fast I thought she had roller skates on. The third time I went there I actually got her to laugh. I the food is excellent and the service was superb. A friend that was with me said she would being her grandchildren back for Sunday breakfast. Great find. Check it out.

The waitress that told me the cheesy crab fries were fire needs to be slap. These fries are disrespectful and F'n trash. No seasoning, like 5 childish pieces if of crab meat, the cheese barely tastes like cheese. I'm PISSED. Not worth $10 AT ALL

Love , love, love this place! The macarons are little pieces of heaven. The flavors change every time, so my tip is: if the flavor sounds good to you, you'll love it! We always love the milk taro and caramel sea salt. I love anything that is coffee flavored, but my boyfriend who doesn't drink coffee...not so much. Don't be afraid to try different flavors though. We have made it a habit of getting one of each flavor when we're there and we're always happy. We recently tried their pudding too. There was a carrot cake creme brulee and the guy torched it in front of us (very exciting)! There was a caramel sea salt pudding too, which was amazing!
I'm addicted to anything they make sweet with sea salt! Sad they were out of ice cream sandwiches--will have to try to catch these next time I'm there. My only negative is that they have weird hours...wish they had a more typical schedule.

I love the technology based approach to ordering and service. Very cool. My Philly cheesesteak omelette was good, but created a little strange. At first I thought they forgot the cheesesteak but it turned out all the veggies were at one end and meet at the other. Was half way through before I realized. Could have used more provolone but otherwise a good omelette.

I can't criticize anything about this hotel. Our room was very pleasant, nicely designed, good size, great view, extremely comfy bed and excellent linens. All the things you would expect from a luxury hotel. On top of this, their restaurant, The Fountain, was the best, highest level restaurant we've ever been lucky enough to lick our plates in! As I understand it, Comcast is building a skyscraper in Philly, and the Four Seasons will be moving to their top floors. That will undoubtedly be over the moon!

Second time coming here for take out and was disappointed. I got the exact same thing about a month ago and it was better.  Not sure if the chef changed. Not sure if I'll be back to give them another try. 

Beef Ho fun- the noodles and beef were really bland. Had to add soy sauce for flavor. 

Egg plant, chicken and salted fish- also very bland. The sauce tasted watered down.

The staff is nice. 
Centrally located in Chinatown. 
Typical quick bite Chinese restaurant in Chinatown.

Update:

After going here for four years, they served me a plate of shit and billed me for it after I complained and wouldn't eat it.

I ordered the fajitas mixtas.  It came out on a sizzling iron plate with tortillas and beans.  Ok, looking good.  Then, I ate a shrimp.  Everything was so overcooked, salt must have spilled and, the worst part...  the mixed vegetables came out of a freezer bag and the had water chestnuts in there - like you get at Chinese joints.  Like LaChoy shit.  No joke.

I pointed this out the old lady/cook.  She asks "no good?"  I say "No good.  Take it away."  She says "You want something else?"  I say "No. And I don't to pay for it.  Take it off the check."  She says "Ok."  She brings the check and there it is.  I point it out and she wouldn't take it off.  I told her we've been going there for four years.  She said I had to pay for it.  So I did and said goodbye.  

Looking for a new favorite Mexican place.

We have been planning for this restaurant for a while, but eventually made to it last weekend. I was along with my wife, my friend and his wife. We were warmly welcomed by the manager.  They have an open kitchen, so that the aroma reaches the nose before the food reaches the mouth :) 

We ordered Vegetable Pakoras and Samosas for appetizer. They were very well cooked, and come with 2 kinds of sauces, sweet and mint. For the main course we ordered Chicken Tikka Masala, Kadhai Paneer, Chicken Biryani, Garlic Naan Bread, and Mango Lassi. My wife loved Kadhai Paneer, and we all enjoyed the Chiken Biryani as the chicken was perfectly cooked and spices were well captured in every grain of rice. For the Dessert, we ordered Lahori Kheer.  They usually serve this cold, but you can ask them to heat it, if you like eating it warm.

The service is impeccable which makes this place a perfect destination for any kind of celebration or a regular meal with friends and family. The food overall was outstanding. The total check came to about $15-$20 per head.

The location is little challenging to reach, but worth the efforts!!

I also get a chance to meet the owner and learned that he is in to the restaurant industry for 20+ years and also owns 3 additional restaurants in Philadelphia area, along with Mood Café at Cedar Park, a 5 star yelp rating.

I have been on a BBQ kick as of late and have even taken to doing my own BBQ at home.  So it is hard for me to resist when I find a new BBQ spot. I happened to be in the area and saw this place behind a barbed wire fence, so I was a bit hesitant to try it. 

First the good....the meats are excellent.  I had the Brisket Sandwich and my wife had the 2 meat combo.  She got the sausage and the ribs.  All were good.  The Brisket was succulent and the BBQ sauce was tasty.  The ribs were tender and the sausage was juicy.

Second....they have a condiment bar.  Except it is a BBQ condiment bar!!!!  This is  the first time I have seen anything like it, with everything you could possibly want to put on a BBQ Samich.  They even had an Eastern Carolina style vinegar BBQ sauce.  It was good.  I actually wish I had that on my Samich instead of the regular sauce.

Third....the Meh.  Their side dishes were a whole lot of just OK.  I had the Mac and Cheese and it was nothing to write home about.  My wife with her combo got three veggie sides and was less than thrilled and barely touched them. Although she did like the biscuits. 

So I wouldn't go out of my way to come here, but if you are in the area....

I'm a regular - the food here is so delicious. I've eaten in a few times, but usually just order delivery. All of the noodle dishes are great! It's like a "homier" and more low maintenance (not to mention cheaper) version of Han Dynasty.

Thinking about sipping a cold Belgian beer at Eulogy while sitting in front of a steaming bowl of mussels and fries almost makes me wish I lived in Philly again. And then I remember that I don't like Philly that much and I can't stand mussels, but when you're at Eulogy you seem to forget all that nonsense. It's been 3 years since I ate here and it'll be at least one more before I get to go back so I'll just sit here on the left coast counting the days..
New to this area in Philly.  Needed my name on my work jacket found aces and they did a good job.  If I need any else  embroidered I will be going back to aces Design.
为什么每次去 刷卡才能贴标签 现金就手写在杯子上 手写在被子上经常发生没给topping或者就是说了不要冰还加冰 只要用现金都是手写不贴标签 刷卡就有贴白色标签 为什么....

I went here the night before Halloween with a friend. We drank a reasonable amount but then I got sad and decided to drink an unreasonable amount. She was busy having fun (not upset about it, glad I didn't ruin her night) and I went to sit in the little gated outside area because I was so severely fucked up. Everyone kept coming up and asking if I was okay, people got me water, the security offered to call an uber to get me home. It was very nice to be supported when I was a mess. So even though I had a terrible time, the community there is great. Only reason I took off a star is because a single shot cost the same amount of a mixed drink and that was kinda bullshit.

I live in this area and I have seen this location go through different make-overs, but it seems that In Riva is sticking and staying & why you may ask - because the Food and Service are Top Notch!!  It's a great place to check out when you can sit outside and take advantage of the scenery on Kelly Drive.  The food is tasty and the Pizza's are one of the best items on the menu.  They are cooked in a wood fire oven.  The have good beer on tap and the wine offerings are pretty good as well.  The speciality cocktails are named after cyclists...nice touch.

The staff are friendly and courteous and I like the prompt attention of the servers and the knowledge of the menu items.  

My Tip:  Happy Hour is cool and 1/2 off beers.

Great place for a party, group outing and mainly bachelorette parties. Food is okay, and I like their "bucket" drinks. The atmosphere makes this place. Not a place I'd go for a drink with co-workers for happy hour, but for a fun night out with a group this is a great place to go!

Friday was my first time at Tierra Colombiana and I really enjoyed it. I went with 5 other friends. It was pretty busy so we were glad we'd made a reservation. I ordered the shrimp and rice, others had chicken and rice; tilapia, shredded beef dish and salads. I can only comment on my own meal which I thoroughly enjoyed. It was very tasty and the portion was large enough that I have  had 2 additional meals from the left overs. They did not skimp on the shrimp - plenty to go around. 
The fried plantains were loved by all as were the guava milkshakes. Yummy!  Two people had the cerviche (seafood) which they said was too overwhelmed with cilantro to be good.  Only one person had space for desert and ordered the cake submerged in various sweet milks/cream? She loved it. Service was super friendly and attentive.

All round thumbs up for an authentic, mostly tasty meal in a restaurant with a great ambiance. I'd love to go back and try other dishes - did I mention that the menu is vast and comprises many different types of Latin cuisines?

I'd eaten here a couple of months ago and had to get around leaving a review
Muslim fish sandwich-excellent and different, I never would think raisins would work on a fish sandwich, but they did.
Fish and rice, not quite as good, I think I'm just spoiled by Indian cuisine and rice, this batch had good flavor but was on the dry side.
Great local place and the locals in the area know it, massive line for a weekday but very efficient workers

Disappointing experience for the cost.  Our bill was about $600 for 4 people.  The decor is amazing but that is all I can say that was worth it for this place.  We noticed besides the booths the tables are in such close proximity - you can hear everyone's conversations.  Its a steakhouse and they didn't get a single temp right!!!  They messed up the sides and service was just ok.  I would expected better for the price.  Overpriced - will wear out

I've tried this chain back when I lived in nyc. I had just returned from Yunnan and the soup didn't taste authentic. Then I moved out of nyc and my food options became limited. I've been here many times and though it's no comparison to noodles in Yunnan, I've came to accept and like the offerings here. The only thing is the spicy soups taste almost identical... cabbage and 金汤 were almost the same... and I didn't see any picked cabbage... but overall I like the food here, not a lot of msg and I don't get super thirsty afterwards.

I am a regular here at Terakawa. I love it here and I'm never disappointed with the ramen. They always come out consistent with taste and it's very delicious! I usually get the Spicy Ramen but the other ramens are just as good. The restaurant interior is small but it's very homely, which I like. The waitresses are also all very kind. Highly recommend!
This is a great place to go if you want a nice, quiet dinner and a large, tasty pile of meat. (The pitas/hummus/tzatziki are, of course, also delicious).
Had a blast dancing upstairs.  Beer was a surprisingly fair price.  Bartenders upstairs and down were friendly and prompt.

Overall the restaurant was a big disappointment. The last time I was here is when it was just LeBus and it was excellent, but it has gone downhill since then. There was real problems with the kitchen, it took over 45 minutes for our main courses to be served and it wasn't busy. The waiter was also inexperienced as a person in our party ordered a salad and it was brought out with her main meal.

If you were a vegetarian, your meal was top notch. My wife's brown rice nut salad and my friends crisp pear salad were great. Another in our party had the special lobster ravioli which was also ok.
Unfortunately the meatatarians in our group had Moms meatloaf which was a disaster. Described as "prime black angus ground beef, mashed potatoes, fresh vegetables & homemade mushroom gravy" the result was over cooked and dry beef, mediocre potatoes and canned mushrooms. My friends meatloaf meal was the same. The manager approached us near the end of our meals and asked how they were, but I did not make a fuss as the rest of my party's meals were ok and I was not in a confrontational mood. 

The only reason this restaurant didn't get one star was due to their nacho appetizer my friend had. This was one of the best I have ever tasted as they make the chips in house and the cheese was great. So if you come here, go for the nachos and veggie dishes, Your cravings for meat would best satisfied elsewhere.

i waited until my 4th visit to confirm what i felt on the first visit- this place is banging.
practically around the corner from me, i dont know how we didnt know about it until recently-probably because we rarely cut down that block of fitzwater.  we normally cycle through el jarocho, la veracruzana, and a few others- all good spots, but this spot impressed the hell out of us in comparison.   barbacoa burrito was my favorite burrito so far- the lamb is downright buttery.  we even ordered delivery twice and the quality was just as great via delivery, which isnt always the case.
go try this place ASAP if you havent yet!!

nice resturant nice staff.
that's were it ends.
went there for Sunday brunch oh my.
25 dollar for cheese omelet and 3 pieces of sausage. Are you frigging kidding me with this breakfast.
small meals and high prices.
not again trust me.
can't recommend.

On my way home a few nights back, I had a sudden urge for a great slice of pizza.  I'm a huge fan of Lorenzo's on South, but it was out of my way and I was feeling some toppings.  I stopped by the NYPD pizza shop on the outskirts of the Gayborhood to try their slices. 

I walked in an met face to face with a schizophrenic pizza shop.  The name should have tipped me off.  There was Italian music playing and other "motherland" decorations.  So you're trying to be a pizza shop with an Italian identity named after NYC located in Philly?  What's the deal?  Further, this is just a stop-by joint.  Sure, there are a few tables in the back, but no one is hanging out here.  It's a grab and go.  There's no problem with that, it's just not charming. 

Despite the atmosphere, I was impressed with the shop.  There were several different types of pizzas half cooked and sliced, just waiting for a hungry patron to say the word have have the slices whisked into the oven for the final minutes of baking.   

I opted to go with the veggie slice, which was covered with diced tomatoes, green peppers and garlic.  The ratios of the toppings, cheese, crust, and sauce were impeccable.  The crust was perfectly thick and crispy.  There are so many pizza shops in town that just don't have this kind of skill.  Everything tasted great.  Honestly, the taste of the pizza was enough to make up for any of the short fallings of NYPD Pizza.  

Bottom Line:  I generally don't expect to shell out $3.24 per slice.  I also don't generally expect stop and go joints to have excellent pizza.  Although I may not stop by again anytime soon, this is surely the best bet for Center City area pizza.

mercato has quickly become a regular spot.  i live right next door and you could not ask for better neighborhood cuisine.  the antipasti is not to be missed and you cannot go wrong with with any of the entree's.  dessert is hit or miss but id always rather have another steak anyway.

The tour was mostly fine but there were a few issues. First, the website said the buses would come every 20 minutes but it ended being every hour. The covering over the top deck was so scratched you could barely see through it. And overall, the tour was too short.

The food is good with a lot of side dishes, prices are reasonable.  The restaurant is clean and service is good, just remember to push the button when your ready to order.  Tips:  don't help out the waitress or move the dishes around and bring a Korean friend if you want to get free Korean pancakes.

Alamo rental car company is the worst!  I had a rental car for 30 minutes before it broke down with a flat tire. Waited over an hour in a strange city for someone to pick up the phone for roadside assistance. Once I spoke to them they told me the tow was coming but that it would be another hour. My sister has to come an hour away to get me (something I wanted to avoid by renting the car in the first place). Needless to say they cancelled the tow and the gas station where I left it had to get the car towed 4 days later because no one picked it up, even though when I spoke to someone at Alamo the next day they informed me the car was towed. Over the last 2 1/2 weeks I have contacted them almost daily to find out the status of my refund and each time they say "we are sorry" we will fix it and get back to you. And each time I call it's the same song and dance and nothing ever happens. For those of you who travel I would not recommend Alamo as your go to rental car company. Worst customer service ever!  Still waiting for a call back!
The Parmesan-crusted pork chop is a favorite. The pastas are wonderful.  One of my favorite casual Italian restaurants.

The food is average,  served is always pretty good,  but Willie the owner is just horrible with customer service. Forget saying something isn't good,  he will throw a temper tantrum like a infant,  and you can't understand him... Just a bad dude!

Save yourself a headache and tons of time and purchase your furniture anywhere else but at Bob's. Online knockdown furniture (think Sauder) is far better quality. I ordered a desk and a nightstand to use as a printer stand. After the delivery guys left, I noticed the corner of the desk was smashed and the nightstand had knicks and scratches in the finish. We called Bob's and they said they would send replacements. Great. Now we have to sit around waiting on another Saturday. A few weeks later the replacements came. Only they brought the desk and no nightstand. At least the desk seemed to be fine this time so I signed off and the guys left. When I tried to open the keyboard drawer, it wouldn't open. I looked underneath to see what it was stuck on and the track was rigged with 2 pieces of flaking wood chips and was hanging off! I had to fix it myself and the drawer will not even line up correctly and is crooked and uneven. In general, the drawers on both pieces stick and do not glide easily. This furniture is pure and total junk and probably won't last a month with normal use. They want to replace it again, wasting my time, AGAIN. No thanks. I am so disappointed that I got taken by Bob's but will never purchase another thing from them again. You were warned.

So the plane wasn't here yet and they were still saying "on time" 30 mins past our boarding time. Said nothing to the full flight of people and right at the departure time switched the screen to "delayed". No one said anything the entire time. Never mentioned why or what time the new boarding was or acknowledged the inconvenience they inflicted on everyone.

Again another horrible experience here. Waited over 30 mins at the main bar for a rail cocktail. Securitity is rude as hell. It's like a jail here. You'll be treated like crap. Go some place  else. Philly is a great city with a great "gayborhood"! Spend your money where it will be appreciated and you'll get a good product!!!

My favorite cheesesteak spot. Yes, it's a tourist trap, and yes the lines are ridiculously long, but it's still one of the best original cheesesteaks. Plus, there's a reason why its touristy - because its famous - and a eatery is always famous because the food is good. 

Cheese whiz with onions. Get the cheese fries and drinks at the other window. BEST cheese fries, you gotta try em. They have small containers of peppers on the the side you can help yourself too. Seating is outdoor on benches and is limited, but you never have to wait more than a few minutes to grab a seat. Much better than their rival geno's across the street. Staff is friendlier too.
Best mediterranean/Turkish restaurant in the region. Everything on the menu is delicious and they never disappoint.

How does this place only have two reviews?
I went here with two friends for a late dinner but we were seated right away. I had the bacon-wrapped scallops and they were quite possibly the best scallops I have ever had. Only downside was that there were four of them and they were rather small.
We attempted to then have drinks at the lounge towards the front but there were no more seats left and it was only about 9pm.
Small spot nestled in the shopping district.  Service was quick and attentive. Definitely get the falafel sammie! We did and so did all the other tables around us. Great prices!

Had a great time here.

The Sunday night $35 menu is a really awesome deal. The snail crostini was the highlight of the night, but the calamari salad was awesome as well. The meatball appetizer was just alright.

The entrees... are surprisingly enormous for a nice italian place. The pork chop is delicious and absolutely enormous, and the papardelle was great too. I had the steak which was pretty good, but I would probably order something else next time.

Worst venue in Philly. Bad line ups, expensive everything, and the sound is only decent from the middle of the floor. Go to the Union Transfer or the Trocedero. Although they both suffer from the sound issue too.

My boyfriend and I were so excited to eat here - me mostly. We've never been here before, nor had we heard of it, but the yelp reviews and pictures really had me amped about eating here. Though it was a graduation weekend with tons of people in the city the wait time was minimal and the service was excellent. We had the Cremé brûlée French toast & Chicken and Waffles Benedict. This meal did not disappoint. I had a side of bacon and a side of Bacon & Jalapeno Mac & Cheese (it could've been much cheesier) and I ordered the fresh squeezed lemonade with berry compote. I was in heaven. I will definitely be returning here on my next visit to Philly. #NOM

I recently took my baby, a 2015 Can Am Spyder motorcycle to High End Vine Street Car Stereo for custom LED Ground lighting, strategically placed carbon vinyl wrap, to protect my paint from scratching in vulnerable places and a marine grade USB Power Port. What I got from them was the best customer service, best workmanship, best products and the best damn pricing anywhere in the tri-state area. All this without compromising quality. I'm a new customer who will definitely return for more of what this wonderful family run business has to offer. One other thing, their technicians are top notch professionals (especially Mark). Special thanks to Anthony! Warning ... I'll be back!

Love this gym. Ample amount of space for cardio and weights. Never an issue with wait times or searching for a particular weight. Staff is friendly and helpful. I haven't participated in any classes yet so don't reference me yet for those opinions. Four stars

Tried Moctezuma for lunch a little while ago on a Sunday, partly due to so many positive reviews on Yelp! (Holla...!)  

As others have noted, the dining room at Moctezuma is sparse, but I give them major points for cleanliness, and my boyfriend liked the huge painting depicting Aztec life on the wall above us. We were greeted by a friendly host (perhaps the owner?) and promptly given chips (warm) with two different types of salsa, both delicious.

My boyfriend and I were in search of their lamb tacos, which we'd heard Moctezuma served, and serve them they did! We both ordered the lamb tacos, which were $10, but come with a small bowl of soup in a lamb broth with carrots, chick peas and rice.  The food was tasty. 

I can't say it is quite my new El Jarocho, but it was definitely worth it!

I come to Philly every so often to visit my family. So when it comes to eating out, I only want to waste my time at great quality restaurant. It was an abnormally warm winter night so that means those who would usually be hibernating at this time are out and about enjoying the mild weather. Every byo we wanted to try was jammed pack. My sister recommended El Rey because she had a great dining experience there before. My bf and I arrived at the door and I was immediately stopped in my tracks. "What is this place? a ghetto diner??" Needless to say, I was startled by the exterior of the restaurant because I was expecting a trendy Starr restaurant. 

Rule # 1, I failed to follow the ultimate rule of "never judge a book by its cover."

We waited for 50 mins or so and enjoyed cocktails at the bar. The restaurant retained a genuine vintage look. Didn't look like it there was an effort on the decor, more of a tribute to what it used to be like many years ago. 

I must say, the food blew me away. I'm a huge skeptical when it comes to mexican food. A lot of places that are "supposed" to be good, tend to disappoint me. There's no lie to be made. The food was authentic, super fresh and reFreshing! We shared a pitcher of guava margaritas which I was super excited for. Sadly, I did not taste any hint of guava. Tasted more like bitter orange or grapefruit. I mentioned it to our server who simply replied there was no such orange or grapefruit margaritas. Whatever is was, it wasn't guava, or maybe it was a really wrong tasting guava. 

My table of 5 shared guacamole, 2 types of ceviche (blue crab ceviche is a must must must!), corn on the cob (must) and plato fiesta (also a must for a large group). Dessert truly was the home run. Churros and mango and mexican chocolate ice cream. 

Besides the fake guava margaritas, the meal was a knock out. Would love to come back.

I don't know how I missed reviewing this place! Their 10 dollar lunch special got me through school lunches. I used to eat here twice a week.

The salmon, eel, and yellowtail rolls are all great, however anything with tuna leaves something to be desired.

I haven't been in since the renovations, but I just ordered a groupon so I will be visiting again soon. This time with wine.

Not a bad place to go and hang out at in Manayunk. They have a stellar wing special on Thursday (35 cents/each) and the wings are pretty big and are definitely delicious. Drink specials aren't too shabby either, and they give you free chips and salsa when you go in!!!!

Chubby's is a pretty cool place. We made the trek from our hotel by the airport to get our favorite cheesesteak for the second time, Dalessandro's, but when we got there the line was out the door and it was packed in there so we said screw it, let's try Chubby's. 

The cheesesteak was pretty good, I got American without onions. The beef had good flavor but it could have used some more cheese, next time I'd probably order extra. The cheese fries were also pretty good. The seating area was really nice, we sat at the bar but there was also room at the booths, which seemed nice. 

Overall the cheesesteak wasn't on the same level as Dalessandro's, and the cheese fries weren't as good as Ishkabibble's, but I'd go back again because of the nice seating area if Dalessandro's is crazy packed and I wasn't absolutely dying for my favorite cheesesteak.

My current rankings:

1. Dalessandro's 
2. John's Roast Pork
3. Gooey Looie's
4. Cosmi's Deli
5. Joe's Steak & Soda Shop
6. Ishkabibble's 
7. Chubby's Steaks
8. Jim's Steaks South Street
9. Pat's King of Steaks
10. Geno's Steaks

We had Sunday brunch at James and I thought it was A-OK. This is a relatively new New American restaurant and bar (opened September 2016) that is located just two blocks south of Logan Circle (a.k.a. Logan Square). The name is fitting because it's named after James Logan, the 14th mayor of Philadelphia. It's a modern take on American fare with an elevated tavern atmosphere.

This is a nice-looking, spacious restaurant with a sizable bar and both indoor and outdoor seating. Outdoor seating consists of umbrella-covered tables on the sidewalk. The interior has an assortment of booths, tables, high-tops, and TVs. This place works just as well for groups as it does one-on-one dates. Refined yet casual is a good way to describe the environment.

Brunch is served Saturday and Sunday, 11am-3pm. A bar menu (food) is offered daily, 3-5pm. Most brunch plates run $13-20 each. Expect to spend about $9-13 each for soups and salads and $16-18 each on sandwiches and burgers, all day. Dinner entrees will set you back $22-27 each. So, this isn't a cheap restaurant, but it's not very expensive either.

We were part of a large brunch party (about 20-30 of us) and a special preset menu was prepared for us. We took up three tables in the dining room. The menu was three courses:

First Course
* Individual fruit salads - seasonal fruit, berries, mint

Second Course (choice of)
* Frittata - spinach, mozzarella, artichokes
* James French toast - vanilla, cinnamon, whipped cream, maple syrup
* Brunch burger - ground short rib and brisket, Taylor pork roll, Cabot cheddar, sunny-side-up egg

Dessert (choice of)
* Apple pie
* Chocolate cake
(Perhaps more choices, I forget...)

In general, I thought the food was fine. No complaints. Everything was plated and presented nicely.

The "seasonal" fruit in the individual fruit salads consisted of watermelon, cantaloupe, honeydew, pineapple, strawberry, and blackberry. All of the fruits were ripe and fresh, comparable to a fruit salad one can get at a grocery store. I liked this light and healthy option. However, from a sit-down restaurant, I expected some creativity or something unique to set it apart from being essentially a plain fruit salad.

Of the second course options, I tried two: the frittata and James French toast. Both were good. These dishes were a little on the heavier side, particularly the French toast which was like a dessert. The French toast was prepared with challah bread, I believe. The consistency was somewhat dense. Not among the best French toast I've had, but not bad either. I liked the frittata better, but it could have used an extra ingredient like meat or something. Again, not bad. The frittata came with a side of chopped skillet potatoes, peppers, and onions. We asked for a side of ketchup to go with those.

For dessert, I tried both the apple pie and chocolate cake. The desserts looked wonderful. The apple pie was yummy, but it was fairly dense and cold, like it had just been removed from the fridge. Same with the chocolate cake. The chocolate cake was super rich. While tasty, I could only take a few bites of it. Same for my friends.

For drinks, I had both an OJ and iced coffee. The glass of OJ came with a slice of orange which was a nice little touch. The drinks were par for the course.

Service was great. There were at least a couple servers working our large group and they were polite and attentive.

In the end, I found our meal here to be satisfactory. While the food wasn't amazing nor memorable, I still thought it was decent and sufficed for what it was (brunch). I liked the design of the restaurant, inside and out, and the service was on point. Restrooms were clean too.

I'm getting hooked on this self-serve tart yogurt craze. Kiwi is the third tart frozen yogurt shop I've tried and my only complaint is that the location is dangerously close to my office building in University City. 

It's way too easy to run out for a few minutes, fill a rather large paper container full of chocolate hazelnut and plain tart frozen yogurt, sprinkle on some coconut and mochi topping, and be on my way back to my cubicle. If you haven't tried the mochi topping, give it a shot. It's a pretty fun chewy consistency and it doesn't get all frozen and hard. There's a long list of toppings from the healthy fruit toppings like strawberries and raspberries to the less healthy Butterfinger dust and Oreo pieces. 

The Kiwi website claims that they have live probiotics in all of their yogurts. Probiotics are pretty important to have every day especially in our Western diet, so I'm hoping this is true. Even if the probiotics aren't that great, the calorie count is low. I've never regretted a trip to Kiwi at the end of my lunch hour.

Be sure to ask for a couple of sample cups. That way you won't end up filling your actual cup with a mystery flavor like banana bread. If you want to try something other than the plain tart yogurt, I highly recommend the chocolate hazelnut. It's similar to Nutella but contains far fewer calories.

For a bar that has a website that preaches normality and attempts to rebound any type of 'hype', passing it off as press excitement, boy, does this place smell of blue stilton.

Was I salivating like a rabid dog at the thought of this place opening? No. Was I interested in checking it out, getting a drink, and potentially expanding my bar list from 3 to 4? Yes.

"Lee", or whatever your real name is, chill the heck out man. 

1. Please do not have your bouncer ask us what we are looking for when we turn at an address YOU listed on the internet, and then try to claim he doesn't know what we are talking about.

2. Please do not make us recite the name of the bar to said bouncer as if this is some bar dictatorship (I'm actually surprised we weren't required to give a blood sample or at minimum, a song and a dance about how much we hope to love the place).

3. No more 'briefs'. This isn't flight school. A simple sign, 'no phones, no loud talking, etc' should suffice. If that's good enough for Soho house, arguably one of the best private clubs in the world with locations all around the world, its good enough for your 1 door, non-private new bar.
