4. Strengthen your drinks. $10 is only bettering NY prices on cocktails if you use as much liquor. I've had a better high off a Tiramisu from Whole Foods.

Yes, we 'got in' (can't believe i'm even writing that about an open to the public bar), but had to leave after 1 drink because the pretentiousness of Hop Sing was making my head hurt. We left, went to Dandelion and had a damn good night.

I hope this place re-evaluates what it wants to become, because if one thing is certain in the 'Philly Scene', something better ALWAYS come along. When the novelty wears off, those that (somehow) did manage to have a good time might just be over it too.

Happy Birthday Howard!

I gotta say that the the cheese-steak was good, but not, "get in the car and drive past 3 other places to get there" , good like some previous reviews would insinuate. The portions are huge and after finishing my small I was glad I hadn't gone for the regular sized steak. The place was doing a steady turnover and people practically needed sherpas to carry out their sandwiches. 

To know: This place is tiny. Only three little (smaller than the amount of paper they wrap the sandwiches in small) tables and very tight space. Don't count on sitting. 

The real reason I'm giving 4 stars is because of the staff. These guys and gals (did I really just write 'gals'?) were a lot of fun. Going out of their way to be helpful, friendly, etc. Their spirit carried over to the patrons waiting. Everyone was smiling and talking to each other like old friends. The kicker was when an older gentleman mentioned to someone his birthday was the previous day. The guy behind the register overheard that and yelled it to the guy on the grill and before you knew it he's yelling out, "Hey everybody, let's sing Howard happy birthday!" and we did. You should have seen Howard's face light up... 

3 stars for a good steak and 1 big star for making everyone smile on a miserably cold day.

Oh Gia. I lived very close to here for over two years, and gave it many more chances than it deserved.

I've tried the salads, the breakfast sandwiches, the coffee. It's all fine, but it's all about 30% more expensive than it's worth. The wait time for Gia is really long, however, and I've spent (not even kidding) upwards of 15 minutes to get a non-toasted bagel. Process that for a second. A *non* toasted bagel. They used to have a delicious cinnamon sugar bagel that when toasted, was pure heaven I admit. Before they got rid of it, they burned it the last two times I got it, making me almost cry when I opened up my takeout bag. 

They've also tried to rip me off a couple of times-make sure to count your change as it's often been $1-2 full dollars short. When called out on it, I'm immediately given back the money, making me believe it was less than accidental. Also, watch out for any popular meal times, as Gia will be CROWDED.

Wow  delicious demand more times of MEE EATING here lol !! Just passing thru on a Wednesday or Thursday saw rock statue and got funny  picture of me and Katie (back together sorry for dormancy) doing a "winning stance punch" right there ! I got lasagna ! Yum . Sauce strong  like rocky. Katie got butter Alfredo beany noodles  ya it tasted like the Tuscan sun lol. Fairmount Italians here we go . Just watched myself outside on a tv screen eating the leftovers .., they held up! Still don't think I'll be back unless Katie's mom aka Godiva 22 inches invited me back to stay awake for a week lol. So GOOOOD

GOOD CHEAP SUSHI - The atmosphere leaves much to be desired... you feel like you are in a pizza shop. They do serve Japanese beer (along with Heinekein) and saki -- The beers wer $3.50 and the sake for a large pitcher was 6$! The sushi was large and super cheap and delicious! This is a great VALUE!
Food was excellent but a bit pricy. Service was overall not great. There are better places in Fairmount to eat!

We went to Relish yesterday to try what all the hype was about but I wasn't that impressed. The atmosphere was nice with the live jazz playing but the food wasn't as great as I thought it would be. Fried chicken was mediocre. The chicken corn chowder came out cold and had a sour taste to it. The lamb chop as pretty good but not the greatest. Me and my GF would probably went to ms tootsie instead. Overall was good but not worth the drive.
Nice varieties of donuts but I actually love their fried chicken more. The chicken had just the right amount of flavor and crisp.

came here on a thursday afternoon bar crawl and accually came across it by accident....i was impressed!  its a old, old bar with neat decor...upscale divey but clean....didn't eat here but the beer was cold and a good selection....the female bartender wasn't the friendlyest but maybe she was having a bad day....kinda got ignored.....toilet was clean but the coolest smallest restroom i have ever seen in philly...i will def. give it another try and get something to eat next time!!

Spirit Airlines has my vote as the worst airline on the planet.  I was with a group of 11 people that was held up in airport security.  Spirit Airlines changed our itinerary to leave 20 minutes early, causing myself and 5 other people to miss our flight.  The rest of our group made it on the flight because they ran as fast as they could to the gate with young kids in the tow.  But that's just one of the disappointing processes Spirit Airlines employs through their seemingly unhappy and disgruntled employees.  First off, I could not add a carry on through their website.  Their website never works.  So when I got to the airport terminal I went to their kiosk to purchase an additional carry on only to find that the check in bag was the only option.  I purchased the check in bag option for $50 and brought it to the ticket counter only to find there was no one.  No one at the counter.  Mind you this is Spirit Airlines at Newark NJ airport.  Which is also decidedly the worst airport on the planet.  Time was ticking and it was an hour before my departure so with no one in sight another lady from another ticket counter advised I take my bag through and have them check it at the boarding line.  But with the itinerary change, and security holding us up, plus searching a personal item bag, I got stuck with two personal item bags, and $50 in the hole.  Needless to say the rebooking process was also a nightmare.  A terribly long wait and 3 different counters finally got me a flight from an airport 2 hours away (another $70 in the hole for the train ride) and a 7 hour wait, plus I would have to spend the night in another city due to the transfer layover being 9 hours.  And that was their best option because they oversell their flights banking that a certain amount of people will miss the flight.  On top of that I had the extra carry on bag (which was different that the original bag I wanted to check because this bag was held in security and contained fragile items) Spirit Airlines would not apply my $50 towards the $65 carry on charge.  Simply put they have zero customer service skills and this entire debacle has lost them at least 10 customers.  I want to feel certain my family and I are going to be taken care of, treated like humans, and our future patronage taken seriously.  I couldn't be more disappointed in their service.  Don't fly with them.

The smoothies are well priced and very delicious. I mostly go for either the green smoothies (super kale or go green) or the protein power and am never disappointed. Having all the fruits and vegetables on display helps you feel like you're making a good choice regardless of what you end up ordering.

The acai bowls are good but are very frozen, so give them about ten minutes to thaw if you choose to order one.

I went to Royal Boucherie with a large group of 14 people this week (early October). We were lucky to grab a reservation for that many people only a few hours before we arrived. 

We started with some cheese and charcuterie boards. Since we were a big group, they asked for minimal substitutions for our orders. Knowing the boards had some nuts mixed in, I opted not to have anything to keep things simple, but I secretly wished they could have made one board with no nuts or with nuts on the side. Even when the boards were brought out, and the servers were explaining the different selections, there were inconsistencies in what contained nuts, so I knew to stay away.

I was starving by the time dinner came and ready to enjoy my steak au poivre. My steak was perfectly cooked to MR and was delicious, but a bit fatty. I do wish there was a bit more Pomme de Terre or more simply stated as mashed potatoes since I was still a little hungry after I finished my meal.

Despite being a large group, the service was good, and it didn't take too long for our meals to come out. The entire space is dimly lit and would be the perfect place for an intimate dinner.

We didn't have desserts, so I opted to have one additional glass of wine as dessert when I got back to my hotel.

My favorite restaurant in the entire world .

Moved back to California and can't find anything even close to being as good. My dish of choice is Indian mee goreng
I love the welcoming environment, the great service and  the convenience of making  my appointments online. Gia is amazing and I'm always happy with the end result!!

This is my go-to when I want to grab a little snack downtown, that isn't too pricey.

The customer service is always excellent and it's never crowded when I go around lunchtime.

A trip to Philly is never complete without having a cheese steak.  While people will argue who has the better cheese steak Pat's or Geno's I'm going to have to go with Pat for the simple reason that this is where my friend took me and this is where I go when I'm in Philly.  I would also like to add this is the only time where I believe adding Cheeze Whiz is acceptable.

We've had good Mediterranean food, and then we had GREAT Mediterranean food from Apricot Stone. Easy to order over the phone and the service was super quick. Phone to pick up, to mouth in under 25 minutes. Tucked into an ally in Northern Liberties, Apricot Stone is your go to place for fresh ANYTHING on their menu. 

What we snagged: 
-Falafel Plate
-Filet Plate 

Both plates come with rice pilaf and your choice of salad, pita (white/wheat), and sauce. SO much food we had enough for lunch the next day. Looking forward to post Covid to join them outside or inside. They're BYOB too. Even better. 100% will be ordering again during Covid and you should too.

After reading these reviews, I was really looking forward to this experience but it ended up being a complete letdown. 
I did my due diligence and read all the Yelp reviews for the various parking authorities in Philadelphia so I could comparison-shop and find the parking authority that was right for me. Considering that this particular one has the highest rating in all of Philadelphia, I was expecting greater service than what I received! 

I parked illegally and went shopping for a few minutes, hoping to return to at least SOME kind of customer service. But no, the PPA discourteously left a ticket on my windshield. There was no friendly note, no chocolate mint wafer, and not so much as a smiling hello from the (presumably lovely) meter maid. 

It's almost as if they don't care at all what their customers think of them. Next time I will make sure to choose an entirely different parking authority to be ticketed by. They have lost my business forever.

Full disclosure, I did not eat HERE. However, they have one of the best happy hours around this area... And believe me I did a tour. They currently offer $5 off cocktails, $3 off beer, and half off glasses of wine. Being a wine drinker, this is a great deal on good wine. Other places may offer $4 wines but they're house brand. I got a name brand $10 glass for $5 and it was amazing. My partner got a signature cocktail for $5 off and loved it! The bartender was fantastic! Bar is small but seems like you can order and pack the small high tops around the bar. Flatbreads are $9...we didn't try any because we had other plans but it's a pretty great deal.
If you're in the area stop in from 5 to 7 pm for HH!

I'm one of those people who hates going to the mall because everything is mass produced (I normally end up at places like nordstroms rack anyway) - I love the eclectic mix in here. It's not cheap but not super expensive considering the area. I'm definitely going back.

Went to Morgan's Pier last weekend and can say that it's good to be back! Just what my boyfriend needed after months of quarantine *boo hiss* the food was GOOD and the drinks. were yummy. Frank at the front was so polite and accommodating, since we didnt have a reservation! We be back soon! :)

If zero stars was an option, that would be the selection here.
Every slice they had in the place was obviously a pathetic attempt at creating something resembling pizza. The dough wasn't entirely cooked, the cheese wasn't melted at all. $3.00 for a buffalo chicken slice would be fine, but not when it tastes like the cheapest ingredients that were, as I mentioned earlier, obviously not entirely cooked. I ate a few pieces of chicken and maybe 1/3 of the cheese, crust and "dough", then tossed the rest.
 Completely unclean, every table was covered in grease. There was a fly INSIDE the oregano shaker on the counter, there were flies in the bathroom. The soap dispenser in the bathroom had no soap in it. So, how then would employees wash their hands? Well, I doubt they would given the general state of everything else I saw in the place.
Let's also point out the fact that "Tower Style" is pathetic attempt to reference New York City style Pizza as a selling point for their sad excuse for "pizza". Besides which, THE tower(s), yes there were two, no longer exist. Kind of a dumb thing to keep your business named after something that remind people of the worst act of terrorism that our country has ever experienced. On that point, is the "real" Philadelphia Pizza so lacking that someone would think they couldn't just make a fake Italian family name or something, instead of "Tower Style". Ridiculous!
Furthermore, no prices were listed on the above-head menu boards.
I have never in my life stopped eating a slice of pizza, this place take the crown for worst pizza I have ever paid for. Awful, Awful, Awful, someone should call the board of health on this vile establishment. I would go get the thesaurus to find a fun list of adjectives to toss in here, but this Sh*thole isn't worth another moment of my time.
A smart business person might open a decent pizzeria across the street and put this foul abomination out of business.

I took a chance on this place, and am I glad I did!  I got the special, and it was money very well spent.  The guys did a great job, both inside the car and out.  It was more like a "mini-detailing" than a car wash; made my 2003 Acura look like new.  They even rubbed out some paint on my fender for a nominal fee.  My only remark is that there is no place to sit while waiting for the service --even an outside bench would be good.  Other than that, great place.  I will be returning!  And remember to tip these  guys well...they are exceptional and deserve it!

Most of their employees don't know anything about auto parts.  In my dealings with them, they have given me the wrong size brake rotors, told me they didn't have vacuum hose(while I was looking at it), told me parts were out of service (was able to order at a different shop), told me a part number didn't exist (ordered part from different shop), and were unable to diagnose a check engine light.  As big as this shop is, they don't even carry the type of engine oil I need for my car!  I can't speak about their service department really, they've never done work on my car.  But their parts department is horrendous.  Go somewhere else.

I've had this loose sounding noise for over a year and it started after I got a few things done at Mavis Discount Tire in Bala Cynwood. A lot of things went wrong after that. Made me even more cautious  and selective of auto shops. I went to a few mechanics who couldn't seem to diagnose and fix the noise. My brother recommended this shop and I'm glad I finally gave them a try. The noise is gone and my car feels pretty new again. The staffs were very friendly, attentive and helpful. Would definitely be a regular here and highly recommend!

My wife recently had some furniture reupholstered at  Ms Bak's Upholstery.
The work is done on premises.  She found the workmanship to be excellent.
It was completed when they said it would. They have material to choose from or you could purchase your own. They have pick up and delivery .She is so glad she
found this business.

I will echo others who have claimed Gleaner's to be cute - it really is. The staff is friendly and there's always traffic in and out of this place, so clearly they're doing something right.

If you're looking to grab a coffee to-go, Gleaner's is just fine. However, due to the size, I'm not sure it's conducive for working. Space is cramped and it's rather loud, so if you do come to work, perhaps bring some headphones.

The menu boasts a variety of coffee drinks and sandwiches, but I found it to be on the pricier side. Also, cash only, which may deter those of us who rarely carry the green stuff.

We had my son's surprise birthday party and it was a private party arranged by daughter in law.  I cannot say enough about the fantastic food.  Everything that served was excellent and a very large selection.  The servers were wonderful.  They were so kind, friendly and could not do enough for us.  What great bunch of people working for Roy-Pitz Barrel House.   The party was just this past Saturday and several of my friends and myself too have recommended Roy-Pitz Barrel House to many of our friends.   We would definitely go to this restaurant again.  Just loved it!!!!

It was an extremely warm (seasonally) late October evening when I was at Stratus, so it was extremely enjoyable to be outdoors while ordering drinks. I would have returned but, I was expecting the perfect rooftop bar and Philadelphia really has completely missed the mark. The view was nonexistent when there was so much potential.

The decor and ambiance are amazing! My group sat in one of the private lounges located to the left once you passed through the initial bar area. It was dimly lit and set the perfect tone for the more casual evening we wanted. As for the prices, the guys covered them all so I honestly can't say.

No doubt these crepes are 5 stars. The pizza app was delicious - most delicious sauce ever but the whole thing was wondrous.  The beverages are lovely - the tequila whatever tasted like straight tequila but without the kick (would not do this again, though once was ok) BUT the blueberry whatever was amazing and could have had a pitcher of those if that was available. The waitress was easy going and accommodating. The dinner crepes were perfection and you could even "create your own."  My coworker and I got 2 and split them so our waitress had the kitchen cut them for us and served separately. The dessert crepe was awesome - an apple pie one. The roasted pepper soup was delicious. It had some sort of liquid bacon on top. A bacon creme of sorts. For entree crepes I don't even know what they were anymore it was just blissful. One was chicken and one was sausage and our other coworker got a beef bourguignon. All amazing. Did I say that yet?  Just. Go. Now.
Sour cream doughnut... burnt and probably worst ever. Not sure how they even put out or sell crispy blackened donuts,  but in the airport setting I was unable to notice in time. Bummed

Clean and more organized than the usual Walgreens. This is the biggest Walgreens I've ever been in. It is spacious with three levels. Ground level offers mostly grocery items and food. The second level is like a mini food court offering frozen yogurt and other lunch items. The third floor offers beauty products. The third floor also houses the pharmacy.

If you're looking for a chill and cute coffee shop that is kid-friendly, then this is your place.  I popped in on a weekday morning for some coffee and a bite to eat.  I settled on a croissant, mainly because I didn't see any breakfast items listed on the wall menu, but I saw several other patrons enjoying bacon/egg/cheese bagels, so perhaps I overlooked this part of the menu.

The ambiance is cozy and I'd be lying if I said I didn't get any work completed.  There is a cute table full of crayons and legos for kids, which I'm sure makes many parents happy.  During my visit, one little girl said to her dad, "I really like that table with the crayons."  She repeated this statement twice and finally, dad gave in and let her sit there.  It was the cutest.

When the weather is warmer, I'd like to try and sit outside, as there is some cute outdoor seating in the back.  I'll be back, especially once I have kids of my own, as I know they will be welcome (and occupied!) here!

I am all for my servers wearing pants and my hookahs having flavor without much effort.

Neither happened at Infusion last night. I didn't understand the servers' uniforms of provocative leotards and Hot Topicy stockings. I mean, it's not a strip club, right? Actually, would strippers even wear leotards?

I also didn't understand why they didn't have a drink menu. Our server was a "walking menu," and provided me with a Tom Collins-esque watered down cocktail when I asked to be surprised with a gin drink.

We ordered the Pirate Cove hookah ($25), which was relaxing and delicious when it could be tasted. There were issues with the bowl a couple times, and it didn't last as long as the hookahs at B-Side and Byblos.

When it came time to pay, I asked for a separate check from the couple I was with -- and the server put $13 of the hookah on mine, plus she gave herself a very generous mandatory $7 tip on my tab, which made no sense to me considering that my bill didn't warrant more than a $5 tip. Why would a 25% tip be mandatory, girl? I paid, but I wasn't happy about it.

Aside from the cost, the strange server costumes and the somewhat lacking products, the place was pretty neat. Lots of potential, hence the 3-star rating. It's dark and flashy -- located above Nick's Roast Beef in Old City -- and has comfy couches for groups, on which we sat. The bar area looked pretty nice, too.

OH DAMN

I had heard great things about Urban Farmer from colleagues beforehand. Even so, I was at the Logan Hotel for an event, starving, and just wanted to grab something quick. It could've been anything and I would've been happy. That being said, I loved this place.

I got a burger and it's unique in the sense that it was served on a house made English Muffin. That part made me curious. But it was AWESOME. Cooked perfectly at the temperature requested and it just worked, and it was different.

Service was also top notch. Also loved the ambiance.

Highly recommend - if it's in your budget.

Big fan of this place---just doesn't get 5 stars because I don't think it's the best burger out there but I've never left disappointed and I've always left full and happy.  Dallas burger is the way to go...I almost always get this one but I also like the Napa Valley burger.  My only thing with the Napa Valley burger is that sometimes they go overboard with the goat cheese.  First time I had it, it was a dream...second time, goat cheese overload---so much so that I couldn't really finish it all.  

Milkshakes are an essential part of the meal----big fan of the coconut, even bigger fan of the pistachio.  Always thick, creamy and they taste exactly like the flavor advertised, instead of just lots of vanilla ice cream and a hint of flavor.  I've had the black and white also but honestly, don't bother...it's nothing special.  Staff is awesome---seems like they actually like working here.  Will go here over and over and over again.

Had a wonderful date night here with my boyfriend. Was impressed with the quantity of sushi we got, and the kimchi fries were delicious. I was also pleasantly surprised by the number of vegetarian rolls available. Loved the Lombard roll the most, but was not quite as impressed with the Delaware roll. My boyfriend's Philly roll could have used a bit more salmon. However, overall it was very enjoyable and I will definitely come here again.

This place is every thing you want it to be. Perfect, cozy corner bar, with an outstanding beer selection. The staff and owners are super friendly and fun to talk to. Wednesday night Quizzo with Kevin is by far my favorite quizzo in the city - but don't tell too many people! I want to keep it competitive for my team! Beers are reasonably priced for craft selections in this city, and their food is too. This would be five stars if the food were a notch better - it's not bad, just basic.

I'm really curious on how their chicken breast is cooked. Is it boiled and seasoned in the same broth as the pho or just boiled, removed from the broth, then broth gets seasoned? The chicken was quite bland in my "Good Deal" soup and was cold as well. I wish the soup was hot enough to warm up the chicken, but because it was in a chunk and not thin sliced like beef, it didn't quite heat it up. It was the same with my kids chicken pho too.

The noodles - a tad overcooked. I like the chewyness of rice noodles and I felt this placed served it more on the mushy side. Had to eat it quite fast so it didn't get more soft.

The broth - it lacked a lot of herbs. Asian cooking cooks with tons of herbs and also tops with herbs. All I had were the topping and the broth wasn't quite there.

They have a great location in Fishtown, however, I think for a bowl of Pho, it's too pricey. I get needing to pay rent. I do not think I will be back though.

The food is generally good, though I felt the quality varied a bit each time I've been here. However, this place has hands down the best naan in Philly - definitely better than the naan in the other Pakistani places nearby. It also has a great selection of paratha that are also very tasty (potato, cauliflower, or paneer, etc...). 

The service may not be existent, but it is, after all, an eatery in the back of a grocery store - where the aisles end and the magic begins. You basically go to the back of the store, write down your order and slip it through the kitchen window, establish yourself at one of the school cafeteria-style tables, and then "ta da!" your food arrives, usually within reasonable time. It's very much a bare-bone dining experience beneath fluorescent lighting, embellished by flashy Bollywood dance tunes coming from the corner TV. There is also a sink on the wall nearby, which is very convenient if you just feel like washing your hands during your meal.

The food options are solid, prices are reasonable, and I've never had a bad experience here. The downside for them is that I tend to buy a lot less groceries, once full.

Come here for Restaurant Week! It's the most amazing deal -- my boyfriend and I shared 7 courses for $35pp. While I was surprised that we had to share the 7 courses (I was definitely under the impression that we would each get 7 tiny courses), we took home at least 1/3 of the meal! 

The Pork Bao Bun and Crab Wonton Taco were the two items I was least looking forward to, but ended up being the best! The Salmon was amazing, and the Chicken Satay and Beef Short Rib were tasty. There weren't copious amounts of kobe beef in the Kobe Beef Fried Rice, which I was a little sad about but not surprised considering how expensive kobe beef is. I expected the Sesame Udon to be a small soup, but it came out to be a humongous bowl of grapefruit-flavored udon, so be prepared!

They even give you two ice cream cones at the end -- we had thin mint and chocolate caramel, I believe. I told them I didn't eat caramel, and they were sweet enough to give me 2 thin mints instead.

Service was great and pretty attentive! Food came out much, much faster than expected. I also thought the interior was cool with the blue lights, though Sampan was hard to find from the outside (for me at least!).

The staff here was great! We arrived at the very beginning of happy hour so we had our choice of seats and the attention of the hostess who, after we told her we were from out of town, proceeded to help us out with making plans for the next day / evening. She was so friendly and helpful, we followed her advice all weekend!

Starters: Guac and chips were good, I typically like mine a little spicier, but it was well made! The spicy margaritas were very good!

The tacos are served in 3's (which was perfect for the three of us!) - we had al pastor, carnitas, and I don't remember the name of the last one, but it was lamb tongue. Al pastor all the way! The carnita was also good. As for the tongue... the sauce on it was very good, but the meat texture just wasn't for me. The staff highly recommended it, but I think that's for the more adventurous types!

The space is very open and everyone there seemed to be enjoying themselves. They have a gender neutral bathroom setup (nice!) and they have seating at the bar, large tables, hightops, and a window seat (where we picked). There was also a foosball table that wasn't being used, but I'm sure could be a nice added treat!

We wanted to try one more drink so they directed us downstairs to Oyster House - again, the staff is so friendly! 

If I'm back in Philly for happy hour, I'd be happy to visit again!

City Tap House (CTH), perched on the Radian's second floor, is an exciting new take on American dining.  While it takes cues from the trendy, hip Marbar nearby, CTH acknowledges that it's more of a college bar, although it's much nicer than your standard co-ed dive.  The decor is warm and earthy, with rich wood paneling and a bright open dining room in the back.  They both lead to an expansive outdoor patio overlooking Walnut Street with firepits and outdoor seating.  The bar needs to find its niche, but aside from that, it's a great place both for dinner and for a night out.

The food is pretty standard, nothing too adventurous or out of the ordinary, and their burger is far superior to the celebrity competitor down the street.  Daily specials keep the menu interesting, and they also do a terrific weekend brunch.  But odds are if you end up at CTH, you'll be pouring over their extensive list of drafts, representing beers from around the region, nation, and world.  The meticulously curated list categorizes the beers by type and changes daily.

CTH also features a weekly line-up of live entertainment and events.  Tuesdays are Quizzo with Johnny Goodtimes, and other nights of the week live performers play at the bar (although they're not always very good).  Overall, it's a good place to grab a beer or dinner with friends; definitely a good pre-movie or post-movie spot if you have tickets to a film at the Rave.

I really want to give this place a great review. They are clean,  modern and friendly.  They speak English and they actually talk to you. More than just asking what you want done, they have full on conversations with you. The last two times I got this older lady. She's very friendly but sucks at doing nails. First time I got her I thought maybe she was just having an off day. Second time my nails were jacked up and she cut up my fingers. I can't give a good review if they think that the quality of her work is good enough to keep her there.

The service is great. Most of the serve staff are Philadelphia natives who have worked at little Petes for a while. 

This is one of the only places to get food late night around rittenhouse square so it's always filled with drunk Penn students. 

The food is mediocre and they don't take credit card.

This was my 3rd choice unfortunately but glad I decided to pick up food here. I was craving meat and this placed carried lots of it!!!

I decided to forgo any and all bread so I can just have delciousness so I ordered the 3 meat combo which came with pastrami, turkey and corned beef with a side of the potato salad.

The corned beef was my favorite. The other 2 meats were a bit more on the drier side. They should have side sauces like au ju to put on the meats or to dip the meats.

The entree was filling and hearty for $10. A great selection if your craving meat!!!

Was in Friday evening with my family. Had a great meal and overall wonderful experience. The staff was on point and manager was friendly and checked in on us during our meal. Ribs were delish and sides were excellent.  We will be back.

The bf and I stopped here for a bite to eat right before picking up a keg from Yards. Having their food truck right outside of the brewery on a Saturday afternoon is both brilliant and convenient! 

We split the pulled pork grilled cheese and the pesto/ricotta/asparagus sandwiches. The pulled pork was the better of the two. It has a mix if cheddar and American cheeses, but I wish I had a little more cheese taste while eating it. Maybe a sharper cheddar or substituting the American for another cheese? 

The pesto/ricotta had delicious bread that was toasted perfectly. But I feel like the flavor was missing something--the ricotta was light and fluffy but I hardly tasted the pesto. In fact I forgot it was even on there until I started writing this! Add more pesto to it, or add something like cracked pepper and this would also be delicious overall.

I wasn't gonna write this review but I feel like it needed to be addressed. Signed up for this gym went for almost a year. And decided to move to a closer gym with parking space right in front of the gym. Called about a month ago about cancelling my membership, but was told that I need to have the membership for a year in order to cancel. I thought okay that's fine, make sense enough. Called in this week and told them about cancelling and they said just come in and we'll take care of them. 
I thought great no problem tell them I will come within this week. Came in today to cancel my membership. The person supposedly in charge there, from the get go is giving me attitude.... okay. Being condescending the whole canceling process, asking for my membership barcode, I had saved it on my phone so it's easy for me to scan, there's a scanner right in front of me that I could scan my barcode in easily, but he decided to ask for my phone, I though okay not a problem..... as he scanned it banged the bottom side of my phone to the counter.... at this point I was kinda annoyed, but I thought he might have a bad day... let it go, and he goes on talking about there's a month ahead notice to cancel! 
So he said I have to pay for another whole month for me to be able to cancel my membership..... Told him I called a month earlier, but he just wouldn't take no for an answer.... determined to charge me another whole month. Look if this is how you want to make your money fine...  I wasn't gonna argue. Saved my breath and I was on my way. If you're not happy with your life.... at least be courteous to your customer so they don't have to experience your miserable life, rolling your eyes and being rude to your customers... 
Sorry, but that's not how business is being done. That's fine take that one month membership fee, and know I will take my business elsewhere and would never consider retro fitness ever again. 
Be kind to others man, that was just disgusting. And I'm sorry to the lady that was working next to him, she was there the whole time didn't know how to act, looking at how he treated a customers.
Get off your high horse and be humble. Treating your customer like that, who do you really think you are?
Again I don't like being negative but I feel like this is not the first time this is done to a customers. 
Hopefully there are repercussion with this review so nobody else experienced what I went through today.
Awful...

Ugh this one is so jank, just walk 3 blocks and go the the dope ass one on 20 and market. They're not open on Sundays, like helloo trying get some bubbly for my boozy brunch! 

And there's always the top shelf stuff behind this counter but if you even go close to it to check out prices, etc, they freak the f out! Like screammm at everyone. Well, then, how about you come over here and be normal and ask me if I need any help. The people here are MEAN. 

And, one time I came with a friend who didn't bring her ID and they wouldn't let me buy alcohol and were super rude/annoying about it. Ok fine. But then the subsequent 100 times I've been here I've never been ID-ed. Am I getting old!?
wish i could get in here. unfortunately every time ive ever tried tickets have been sold out and the box office has been flooded.

The coffee was good as the previous yelper mentioned despite the server not even offering me a description or an option between the two choices. I would consider the service far below average. It's a rainy day, I don't want to be buzzkilled walking into a nearly empty establishment with a poor customer service experience. I delt with a lady with shorter bangs (not the red hair). Baked goods- overpriced and limited. Bagels, I wondered why they weren't properly displayed in the case, when I left I understood why. They were stored in plastic bins, which is a weak attempt at maintaining freshness. Thankfully (so I never order it again) the girl cut the bagel unevenly. The top half was hidden by the toasting but the bottom half tasted like it was two days old easily. It wasn't fresh, and though I'm not a fan of a lot of cream cheese, I'd rather have more than less. It's a one-time lesson for me. I won't return for eating and realistically I see no reason to go there for coffee in the future with so many better options close by. I understand their rent is high in a fine new building, but they need to focus on a stronger customer service experience and better, fresh products.

Wonderful and fun girls stay in Philadelphia on the way to the beach... very clean with solid covid safety measures all in place. Felt safe at this fun and trendy hotel.. The rotary phone and radio were a hit with this crew

Great bar to just hang out and watch a game.  Cheap beers and never painfully crowded.  You can actually have a conversation with people, so its busy but not overwhelming.  I always love a bar that serves tater tots.  Best bar food ever!  Also have delicious chicken fingers. Yum!

DELICIOUS. I went here to celebrate a friend's birthday and we ordered the edamame dumplings, tuna rice cracker, ceviche, shrimp pad thai, brussel sprouts, and calamari. The edamame dumplings melted in your mouth (although they are pretty small) and the ceviche was fresh and tasty. No one was a huge fan of the tuna rice cracker - the tuna was great but the rice cracker was too thick and tasted kind of like cardboard. The shrimp pad thai was a game changer among all the small plates and I love how they cooked their brussel sprouts. Calamari wasn't anything special but it was good and the sauce it was served with was great. The waitress ended our meal with complimentary ice cream cones (she said there was coconut in them) for my friend's birthday and they were delicious (I usually don't like coconut). Great meal and well priced for all that food and 2 bottles of wine.

I ordered a cheesesteak Stromboli and fries.  It was very good, and the sauce was amazing!!  I tried Santucci's when I first moved to Mayfair 9 years ago and didn't like it but they have definitely improved!!  Definitely recommend!!

Not very good.... soups aren't good, protein choice are questionable.. nothing here seems like it has quality. The price want bad for an ayce experience but I don't think it's worth it. Our party of three just ordered the hot pot ayce on a gloomy drizzly day... they gave us comp papaya salad and spring rolls.. those were good and a nice addition to the meal. The first plate of protein came and we were honestly apprehensive about some choices... fish..  beef.. shellfish. Veggies came and those seemed fine. Then a plate of beef and lamb came and it didn't turned us off. We ordered another plate of steak and called it a night. The soup wasn't good, the sauce bar was clean but had a stale feeling.. just ehh. We stopped by to grab dinner before our trip back to md. All three of us definitely regretted stopping in after we finished.
The Broad Street Bully is unreal. There is no other spot outside of South Philly that you can get such great Italian sandwiches that are this delicious.

Stopped in for dinner and drinks a couple weekends ago with my girlfriend.

Since we arrived at 6pm on a Saturday, the 45 minute wait seemed reasonable and we made our way over to the front bar in hopes of sampling that legendary beer list. After nearly a half hour of awkwardly motioning towards the bar (the place was packed and the area surrounding the bar is quite narrow), we finally snagged two seats and anxiously opened the menu.

Needless to say, their reputation wasn't all hype. This beer list was massive, featuring a ton of American micros, almost everything Belgium has to offer, obscurities from France and Switzerland, Russian River on tap (a rarity for Philly pubs) and vintage Thomas Hardy old ales.

I grabbed the Rosee d'Hibiscus (which was intriguing) and my girlfriend had a La Fin Du Monde. Although the bartender was attentive and knowledgable, he was way too busy to get us a second drink as we waited for nearly another hour for a table.

Finally, around 7:30pm, we were seated at a small table near the door. We immediately ordered more drinks and I got a Cuvee de Alex Rouge and my girlfriend got another La Fin Du Monde (she's a little less adventurous than I). For food, we got the Flemish Grilled Scallops as an appetizer and the Flemish Mussels and Salmon Framboise as our main courses.

Around 8:30, our scallops arrived (sadly, there were only 3) and our dishes came at nearly the same time. My mussels were passable, but the salmon was coldly and slimy. Although the Salmon was taken off the bill, the damage was done and my girlfriend and I left thoroughly disappointed after a nearly 3 hour trip that only got us 2 beers a piece and empty stomachs.
Not the place to be per my check in comment.  Food was ok.  Good atmosphere.  Ok wine selection.  Maybe they were off tonight.   I've had several people recommend this place.

As someone who doesn't necessarily have a highly fine-tuned palate for the quality of pho broth but has a lot of Asian friends who are relatively particular about broth caliber, I tend to go to whatever pho place that my friends prefer... which often ends up being Pho Ha! 

Personally, it's not really my favorite for many reasons: it's cash only, they don't bring you the bill so everyone has to pay at the counter (call me spoiled), decor is very bare boned, and the smell of pho seems extra thick in the atmosphere somehow (tip: leave your nice jackets in your car to avoid carrying the smell with you and save yourself some dry cleaning money). Also, it doesn't make sense to me that the larger pho bowl size is like 70 cents more expensive than the small bowl; in my opinion, the small bowl is plenty filling enough for the average person but you kind of feel like you're not getting your money's worth because the price difference is so small - marketing tricks, mind you! And I have to say, on two of my visits here, there have been different mix ups with the bill where they claimed that we hadn't paid for items that we were very sure that we did. None of it was badly intended but it seemed like they sometimes have trouble keeping track of the payments.

But hey, the pho is delicious at bargain prices, and it's great for accommodating large groups, which is always a huge plus in my book. Their service is fast and efficient, and there's plenty of parking so that's something else not to worry about. Definitely worth the visit, especially in the cold winter time.

I should have read the prior reviews but needed a quick manicure and stopped here.  The entry after reaching the top of the stairs smelled musty and almost like sweaty socks.  Yuck.  The manicurist barely massaged my hands, more like a pat - very sad when you are seeking a little relaxation during your lunch hour.  I felt so strongly about the lax, unwelcoming experience as well by the male owner at the desk, I had to write this review.  Too many other nail salons do it right in the city.  Skip this one!

This place is awful. We had an appt today for our wedding flowers. It snowed last night.. We dig out car out and get all ready to discuss our wedding.. Chrissy is not at the shop. We call her and she is snowed in.. What!!! Everyone is snowed in.. We checked our email our phone and nothing.. She never called to reschedule.She just needed to call...we would have been happy to be warm in our house then get up and drive through the snow .. Just so unprofessional !

Hands down the best restaurant in Philly. Each dish's flavors are so complementary, don't be surprised if it's like reliving your first sushi roll. The food is THAT good. It's also a fun dining experience, because...hey, it's tapas. And don't be fooled--it's not overpriced or outside anyone's price range. Make a reservation if planning on dinner, as it gets packed to the gills very quickly. Request a table in the back room if you can, because it's less crowded and noisy and more intimate.

I was really close to getting pizza somewhere else after reading the reviews of Key Pizza, but the fact that it was really close and had a great deal on pizzas made me change my mind. It appears that they are really trying to change their image, because I didn't have nearly the bad experience that others have had.

I was greeted by friendly people as I walked in, and they had my order ready to go. When I found out that I was a little short on change, one of the guys behind the counter told me to forget about it. Not bad on the service side, I'd say!

As for the pizza - it was pretty good! I wouldn't say it's the best pizza that I've had in the City, but it was definitely not terrible. The crust is crispy, and there is plenty of grease to go around. It's well balanced by the zesty sauce and well-portioned mozzarella. There are a ton of different toppings to choose from (but I prefer simple pepperoni). 

I'd definitely consider returning to Key Pizza when the mood for an affordable, tasty, (and maybe greasy) Italian pie strikes again.
I ordered 2 jackets and still haven't received them and I can't get in contact with anyone this is crazy

I fell in love with Franklin Fountain when I walked in and they were playing the song Hello Dolly.  The ambiance is very quaint and reminiscent of the early years in Pennsylvania.  It also doesn't hurt that this old time fountain makes an awesome Cherry Bomb (sweet tangy cherry soda with a scoop of rich chocolate ice cream), and the root beer in the root beer float is spicy, assertive, and robust.  

Other helpful things to know...
-They only have outdoor seating which can get a bit crowded
-Don't forget to bring cash 
-Located in a quaint area near Penn's Landing
Got the remote and played r Kelly ignition. Place is off the chains. Give me that toot toot, give me that beep beep! This is the remix to ignition popping fresh out the kitchen!

Spent a beautiful Saturday evening patio dining at this wonderful restaurant. My partner had a very nice house Chardonnay and I enjoyed their signature mojito. The waiter suggested the Mac and cheese appy which was decadently delicious and something  we both enjoyed. For the main meal she had the tuna burger and house salad which she enjoyed. I had the fried chicken and was somewhat disappointed as my expectations were raised due to reviews I read. It did have a nice spicy kick to it but that was the scrawniest chicken I had even seen.wouldn't eat that again. Service was good. The patio is a marvelous spot to eat and enjoy the company of those you care about. Highly recommend this spot!

I was looking for a dress to wear to a friend's birthday dinner, so I had a purpose when I went in and I think that's where I went wrong.  Marshalls is a great place to just browse with no clear intent because you'll find a ton of stuff that you love.  But if you have something that you need and you go looking for it here, you'll be disappointed.  

Even though it's spread out over two levels, it feels very cramped and claustrophobic with just enough room for one person between racks of clothes.  Try to go during the day when it's not crowded and not right after work like I did when everyone decides to go shopping.  The store felt very clean, though, unlike a lot of stores right on Market Street but that may be because it's new.
The taste is not what I expected. The crawfish that I ordered was not fresh and I kind of have stomachache after eating at that restaurant. I would never go again.

This salon is formerly known as Lucky Dog. One of the Lucky Dog groomers purchased the business and created Suds and Swagger. So if you loved Lucky Dog, fear not because Suds and Swagger is still the same wonderful groomers. 

I love the girls who work here. I'm very picky about who grooms my dogs and have had some bad experiences in the past. My dogs always look great when they are done here and most importantly they don't seem stressed out.

Ok I will try to never fly with them again. From the first impression I got was at the curbside check in and this was at Philly airport the guy that was there could care less on helping with the check in he stood there talking to the other guy. After 10 mins. He looked at us then told us to go inside we really don't want to use the curbside it cost tips that was his words. Well we have used curd side many of times and we always tip. But to tell us we have to tip was like what. Well after a few more mins that gone by. He started to help by it was too late for our flight to do curbside but if he would have helped right way we could have used it. He told us we missed it by 5 mins. Like I said try to use another airline.
So close to home but yet I feel so far away in the country and leave the stresses of the city to seek the beauty of the woods. Best place to walk the trails and also the path with a stroller.

It's overrated! And some people there are nasty with attitude " of course they see these people waiting forever"  .. " the guy in the pick up window "  , pay and pick up system is not that great .. u have to wait forever .. it's just cheesesteak ..any other restaurants have same quality....

Came here with a group of 7 after having made a reservation for 5 and it was super busy so had to wait a while but was worth it!! Inexpensive and delicious brunch fare. I split the omelette with avocado and the pumpkin french toast. Would definitely recommend the omelette. The potatoes that came with it were super good and crispy. The french toast wasn't super pumpkin-y which was a little disappointing but was still delicious so ¯\_(ツ)_/¯ will be coming here again!

We were really excited to try this but im really not sure why it is getting rave reviews. I've had Jollof rice made by a Nigerian woman and it tasted nothing like this. We got red sauce and it just tastes to much like tomato. Chicken is something I could have made at home. Plaintains weren't fresh. Love African food, but this isn't anything special.

Manakeesh is my new go-to for lunch. The delivery is always quick and the food is so good! The falafel platter is AMAZING. Hands down the best falafel in the city... yes I just said that. The fattoush salad is delicious and the pita is to die for. Hummus is good, but I've had better. Will definitely be back to try more things!

my wife was here for an event - what a hidden treasure!

vast, and in the heart of Philly!

great food, great spaces, and parking.

And it looks like a castle.

Cool stuff inside.

You can book this spot.
This place is a hidden gem!! EVERYTHING is homemade, even down to the salad dressings. The Crab Tots maybe the best thing I've ever put in my mouth. Excellent service. Amazing food.

i love love this place. what a cool place to just hang out, good vibes, good music, great service. owner is the nicest guy you could ever meet. next time i'm in town, i will definetly be visiting Kif's.

I got the chicken and rice today. The man working asked if I wanted salad, I asked for just lettuce and onions, and he obliged. I also asked for extra white sauce, again, no problem- not even an extra charge. The meal also came with a can of soda. It was only $6. I had literally $7 until tomorrow when I get paid, so this was fantastic. I got home, and ate. Everything was delicious! I will definitely be back!

The truck is located just outside the Dunkin Donuts at Ridge and Leverington.

This cafe was one of the reasons I moved to Philly. Cozy, great coffee, stellar sandwiches and soups - really friendly staff. Plus: later than your average-cup-of-joe-place hours! Anything coconut is excellent. Colonel Mustard (sandwich) is a must try. I've also gone on date here before so, you know, it's a good meeting place too

I went to Lo Spiedo with hopes of experiencing a memorable meal.  I was having dinner with a few work colleagues.  The server was attentive and provided good service.  To start we ordered the polenta cornbread, chicken wings and fried mozzarella.  Nothing stood out, needed more flavor.  The server over heard us making a comment about getting six small pieces of fried mozz for $8 and sent us another plate on the house.  This was not necessary but a nice gesture.  For dinner I ordered the pork shoulder which was disappointing.  The outside portions of the pork were dry from being over cooked and needed seasoning.  One of my colleagues ordered the Bucatini which he said was over salted and not impressive.  I will say the creamed kale was excellent, cream and butter make everything taste great.  The mac and cheese was also good.  The server told me the restaurant was all about simple seasonings and they want the wood oven to stand out.  I wanted to like this place but I was not impressed at all.
My favorite bar in the world. Dopest staff, best music. This is a bro free zone. Definitely steer clear if you suck, you're not wanted here.

Food was ok but customer service leaves a lot to be desired. I reserved a party of 10 for Fathers Day weekend and 3 got into a car accident entourage and didn't make it. The manager charged me $55 per each of the 3 for a total of $110. Bulls!&!!
That was unreasonable. Highway robbery!!. She wouldn't listen to me and added a tip on top of the no show fees. Smh. We ordered appetizers food and drinks for the entire evening for a nice tab of over $300. They do ask you to make reservations with a credit in case of a total no show, but most of our people did attend. Customer service is king. 
I won't be attending Rouge again.
Great service to start, the food is prepare to perfection, Mónica is great she is always ready to help out, coming back for lunch.

My colleague and I decided to have lunch.. And we were thinking of something we haven't tried yet, he suggested hand drawn noodles.. Glad we found this place! It didn't disappointment! Prices were great and the portions are big! Great customer service, manager was there taking orders and helping out which was a plus! She really cares about the customer service..

We got the chicken wings with thai sauce and that was really crispy and flavorful, I had to add some siracha because I want it a little spicy.  I got the Beef noodle and added wontons, it didn't disappointment.. I've tried a lot of noodles all my life and this is different.. The aroma itself is amazing.. The hand drawn noodle is perfect! There's a mushroom that they put and that was delicious! I'll definitely try different dish nextime.

A few tips:

1. They have Chinese pickles on the table, when I was about to take some my colleague saw from another table that the they were using there own chopsticks....... Oh boy. I suggest to management to take that vegetable out of the table and just offer it when customer is waiting for their food.

2. They asked if we want our broth spicy we said yes.. But we didn't know it cost a little more.. It taste great so I'm not really bashing but just a suggestion, notify the customer it's a little extra.


Will be coming back to try more dishes

My girlfriend and I passed by this restaurant. There's no fancy logo outside but we saw a big group left the restaurant, so we decided to give it a try.

First thing I noticed was the photographs  at entrance. The restaurant hosted banquet for Chinese chairman before, that increased my confident to this restaurant.

We sit down await for waitress show up. She came to us very quick with a very warm smile. She kindly introduced many dishes she recommend, then we realize most of seafood here are MP, and the price are little bit above standard. ($28 for 1 lb lobster, $26 for sea bass.)

I ordered a lobster. Later on she brought a pot contained a live lobster to me. I was surprised. Since most restaurants may choose lobster by themselves, the way they did was very honest but also unique. I did not satisfied to the lobster she picked. So she led me to their water tank to select new lobster. She picked another lobster about 1.5 pound priced $38. The price was over my budget so I decided to change my order to steam sea bass.

The story was not yet come to the end. 3 mins later she came to me said they ran out of normal size sea bass. The sea bass they had were too small to the price they cost, so they ask me to chose other kind of fish. I agreed to the deal. Then they sent staff to buy another fish immediately from market.

Therefore I finally had my steam fish after 15 minutes. It's very fresh, and we could test it was live caught before it cooked. So my girlfriend and I were very thankful for the fish's sacrifice for our dinner (We normally don't eat live caught.) But the entire experience in the restaurant from ordering to dining we're very friendly and satisfied. I would recommend people come here for Chinese food or dim sum.

But I think their best deal is dim sum for sure.

I went to Nan Zhou and ordered fried chicken noodles and they were amazing- The noodles were thin, not greasy, and were blended nicely with the chicken and veggies. My food came out very quickly and I had a very attentive waitress. I will probably take my parents here next time they come to town!

"We're known for our food" the waiter told us... later noting there was no qualitative adjective in that statement. It was one of those meals that makes you angry people can do that to food. The specials: sushi-grade ahi tuna sandwich was fishy. When sent back the waiter said "it IS fish you know." The fried chicken salad had heavily breaded bland chicken on warm lettuce. Regular menu items: ugh. Pork in the cubano tasted old, funky, and otherwise flavorless - dousing it in mustard didn't help. The vegetable spaetzle was steamed vegetables over pasta - completely free of flavor or seasoning - though the baby liked it, making it the favorite item on the table.
Otherwise: the beer is good, as are the french fries... so there's that. 

The sticky table with a bug on it and loud screechy music rounded out the experience... damn this place makes me sound like my grandmother... I never complain about music.

Sugarcube is basically the closet away from home of my BFF, and she always looks amazing. But she is 6 feet tall, model slim and incredibly striking. I am, on the other hand, 5'5, a size 6 and (aside from the whole black/blue hair & multiple tattoo thing), constantly get told that I look just like someone's sister / cousin / ex-girlfriend. That said, both me and my bestest pal share a love of Sugarcube, and the two of us always find things that make us look terrific even though we couldn't be more physically different.

But alas, I am not made of money, nor do I have a sugardaddy who throws dolla dolla bills my way in the pursuit of dressing me like the awesome chick that I am. If that were the case, though, I would shop here every single day of my whole entire life. Elisa (the owner) just gets what brilliant fashion is about, and she's the kind of person who not only makes you feel comfortable from the second you walk into the store (it's a gorgeous, open space, btw) but she has an crazy ability to find what looks great on you but won't go out of style five minutes from now (ahh, the old "timeless fashion" thing is cliche but true). 

So even if you're on the budget plan, go. Look around and get ideas. Drool over the artistry and simplicity of the clothing and accessories (but not actually ON the merchandise - that's gross). And then get a second job so you can come back here and shop to your heart's content. Sugarcube is totally worth it.
Great service. Movers were extremely professional! I'd highly recommend them for any move. They were on time and completed the job well ahead of schedule.

Get the rabbit pot pie. You will not regret it.

Everything our group had at dinner was wonderful - from the pitcher of dark and stormy to the crab risotto. The staff was really friendly, and we didn't have to wait very long (we arrived around 9pm on a Saturday) to get seated. Also, the decor was perfect.

Go here if you're looking for a great dinner place (could be good for a date or fun with a group)!

i've never eaten in, only got take out from the buffet during lunch.  for $10 they give you 2 containers to fill to the brim.  i am able to get 2 - 3 meals out of it.  i LOVE their food! and the gentleman that greets you is all smiles.
Dope convenience store and really good quality food and drinks. Also open 24/7 which is great being a physician working at Penn!

The food was fabulous.  However, I think something needs to be done about the service.  It was very courteous, but I was unable to obtain very ordinary things.

When I got cornbread, I asked for butter to go with it and was told there was none.  So far so bad, but not terrible.  However, later I asked for salt.  I was told "I'll check" or something like that.  The waiter came back after 5 or 10 minutes and told me they didn't have it.  I told him that as this was a restaurant, they really had to have salt, and that I was really not asking for anything exotic.  He then asked how much salt I wanted, and I told him just a little.  So he said he'd check.  He went away again and came back with an empty little plastic container saying they only had seasoned salt.  I told him never mind.

I do not expect any answer but "yes" when I ask for some salt.  I sincerely doubt that the cook uses just seasoned salt and no regular salt in the cooking, so I do think the restaurant had salt.

Again, the service was very courteous and pleasant in manner, but this is just ridiculous.  I'm torn because I want to go back because of the food, but do not want to have to deal with such ridiculous responses to what ought to be routine requests.

Beers are a little limited and not that varied in type, mixed drinks tasted a little weak (and flat carbonation). But we really liked the food!  We just shared a couple apps and definitely approved.  The burger is pretty good!

A couple different servers walking around.  One of them seemed a little unattentive and spacey, but the other guy was really nice and helpful.  On another visit, the wait staff was nice and helpful.  So I would gladly come back here.

Barbuzzo is decent, but we arrived for our first visit with heightened expectations due to our friends talking up the experience quite a bit. I guess that's a key factor in why we left feeling underwhelmed.

Granted, this is coming during the post-pandemic era, so we continually tried to convince ourselves that this was simply a result of understaffing, etc., but still...at nearly $300 with a tip, our table walked away disappointed (including our friends who were fans and had convinced us to try Barbuzzo). In a city full of excellent restaurants, any subpar meal is going to easily standout.

Anyway, our server really didn't bring her A game, but looking around, it seemed like they may have only had two servers for all of the outdoor tables, so she was clearly overwhelmed. Still, there was no personality, no excitement, and really very little service. Things like water weren't refilled, and for a table of 4, a single bottle disappears quickly. We're okay with a bit less attentiveness, but this swung way too far to the inattentive side of the spectrum. I'll still try to give her the benefit of the doubt, though, but it's a shame they can't staff enough to handle the volume they're willing to seat.

Dining outside at this intersection is always going to be interesting, so you'll definitely need to keep that in mind. If you know, you know, but if you want to dine in a less chaotic environment, sit inside. We can't knock them for the environment since we know what we're getting into when dining on 13th Street, but when nothing else seems to be hitting the spot, not being able to have a casual conversation with people at your table because of the surroundings is magnified.

All of that aside, we really felt like the food fell flat, overall. The meal started out well enough. The ricotta is excellent, as well as the beet salad. I could've been happy just ending the meal there! The pizza doughs were bland and thicker than we'd expect for wood-fired/Neapolitan-style. The meatballs were pretty good, but also quite pricey for 2 meatballs. They were fennel bombs, and I love fennel, so that was a win. The sauce with meatballs was the best red sauce at the table, as well. They should really be listed under their snacks section, though. The pastas were hit or miss. The actual squid ink pasta was excellent and perfectly al dente, but the overall dish ended up being a bit bland. The pan seared gnocchi was quite good, great texture, and lots of flavor, but the Tufoli was a completely flavorless disappointment. It was surprising, as we've enjoyed some of the other establishments from this group, but despite some good dishes (especially the beet salad), nothing stood out as something that had us thinking, "we can't wait to return!"

The dessert was okay. We had heard all about the Budino, but the caramel was quite bitter. Perhaps that's the level of caramelization they're chasing, and it was partially balanced with the full spoonful of flavors, but it wasn't enough to bring us back. The tiramisu, served in a plastic cup, also didn't leave us excited. It was okay. A fine texture, but mostly an ordinary tiramisu that my tiramisu-obsessed self could've gone without.

So, overall, I'm sad to not be able to give Barbuzzo a higher rating. Our expectations were high, and obviously the industry is struggling to maintain it's highest highs of pre-pandemic, but considering how many restaurants are still crushing it consistently, Barbuzzo still needed to provide a better first impression. Perhaps we'll return when the world gets back to normal and the industry is back to its best. The ratings and reputation that preceded our visit has me optimistic that we simply came on an off night, but it'll be a while before we gamble again. Hopefully next time it's as good as all of these great reviews I'm seeing. Cheers!

Ordered breakfast and asked twice for scrambled eggs to be soft.  Eggs came way over cooked, brown, and dry.  I complained.  Couldn't eat more than two bites.  Zero sympathy from restaurant and no offer to redo.  Breakfast was not cheap.

This has got to be my favorite gourmet grocery store in the country. I love this place so much that every time I am in Philly, I walk away with no less than 2 bags filled with groceries. I love their variety of options at the fresh food counter. I am growing more and more disenchanted with Whole Foods because of their unwillingness to spice things up a little and offer different food items; every week they have the same tired looking burnt, ridiculously fattening veggie burgers! WF is so lame! I never know what surprise Di Bruno Brothers will have in store and I am never disappointed. I take that back, I am disappointed by one thing: they used to have the most incredible Quattro Formaggio spread and for some reason it has been missing from the shelves for the past year. When I inquired about the possibility of it returning, nobody seems to know anything, which is weird, because I am positive that I am not the only person that has requested this very much missed cheese spread. Please get my beloved cheese spread back and open a location in Chicago!!
Penrose is a much better Diner than Melrose.  The food is better, the staff is better and the overall atmosphere is better. Come here for a better diner experience and avoid Melrose at all cost!

All I'm going to say is Excellent!!! Also after you eat there you will be a King & Queen! My brother, his GF, his son, my two kids and nephew and I ate there and it when down!! We didn't even care about our BILL lol! We are from west Africa, Liberia & Lebanese from Beruit Lebanon and my brother GF is Philippine . But you would have thought we was all Spanish ppl with a Philippine eating African food with HOT PEPPER LOL. 

We all LOVE HOT PEPPER! But we love MAMA AFRICA!! Our country, nothing like a good African food! Upper Darby, PA, Kings & Queens African Cuisine is the place to be 100%! EXCELLENT CUSTOMER SERVICE, Very Sweet the staff, EXCELLENT FOOD PRESENTATION, 
Family Owe, shout out to the owner Young King @Sahmed. Will visit again and highly recommended! We drove 2 hours to support! We had 2 Cassava Leaf, 1 Potato Green with vegetable oil, fried fish & dry rice, grilled lamb & fried rice! Dame that dry rice is to die for & grill Lamb lol! That checkout bill I didn't want to see Lmfaooo thank god my brother took care of it. RIP to those dishes!

Dameeeee  I know what you guys are thinking lol yes it's that good 7 ppl deep! We also had 2 Potato Greens with Palm oil to go LOL!  Also the portion of food is HUGE!!!! check out my pic!
Life to short for average FOOD!

5 stars for the bazooka limon guacamole!! 

The atmosphere is great and really fun. The decor is very trendy and hip. I love the overall feel. The food and drinks are great.

It is a little overpriced, but that's my only complaint.

I wish I had known about Pumpkin Cafe earlier - but then again, maybe its a good thing, at least where my wallet is concerned. I live so close to it that I could very easily end up having all my meals there. 

My obsession probably has to do with the fact that they use fresh, local, seasonal produce in their menu. The salads are definitely my favorite since I loves me some veggies - the Roasted Beet is always a winner in my book, as is the Chopped Greek Salad, but the Cafe Salad is also pretty addicting. Sandwiches are also great. Everything just tastes fantastic and fresh - I have to try more of their prepared foods, and soon. Pumpkin Cafe is a great lunch spot, but they're also open later for dinner if you just want a casual meal out. The place itself is small, though, so don't come with a large group and expect to find seating. But since you can easily get things to go, you can choose the venue for your amazing meal. The staff there is really nice and friendly. You've got to try it!

After all the accolades, I was expecting something at the very least on par with Pizzeria Vetri, a benchmark for an above average pizza in the city. No such luck. While the sauce lacked acidity, it also lacked flavor. Same goes for the cheese, which was quite frankly shocking given its appearance. The flavor of the crust is the one bright side but it was soggy on the vegetable pizza (better on the plain cheese pie). 

I can count on more than two hands the number of pizzerias within the city and immediate area that put out a better product and at a reduced cost than Beddia's $22+ price tag.

Went to Lolita for Restaurant Week and it was super average. I was disappointed after having such great experiences at Barbuzzo and Bud & Marilyns. Maybe I picked the wrong menu items (for some reason I happened to pick all vegetarian stuff, even though I'm not vegetarian). I got the veggie burrito and it was kind of bland and boring. The description made it sound like it was packed with lots of flavor, but it really just tasted like rice and beans wrapped up in a tortilla with some curried cauliflower thrown in. Maybe it was just the items I ordered, but nothing really stuck out to me here.

I love this bakery and it's the one that I will go to if I need a cake nearby. They are the only bakery that I know that can do number cakes and picture cakes, which I always do because I am a very humorous person and I do those for my bday peeps! They always did my cakes and I never had a problem with them. It's a very reasonable price and very good. It is more on a sweeter side to it, but it's still very good. The only thing they don't do is fondant cakes. 

They do a lot of already made cakes with like kids designs and stuff for them, and they like cute design cupcakes for kids as well. They sell all types of different cakes as well, which is why I really like going there and ordering it and it is not too far from me as well. The only thing I hate when going there is that there's no parking. I usually pre order my stuff and go pick it up. They even have candles there to buy. Great bakery and I am going to use it soon as a matter of fact for a picture cake! :D

Great French Dip and tasty fries.  Met my cousin after getting off Amtrak and before going to a Penn wrestling match.  I had no idea what to expect.  The food was fantastic.  It's too bad I live so far away or I'd be going there at least once a week

Before I found this place I used to settle for Solo, but this little gem definitely topped them. I usually order their beef, lamb, and fish tofu along with my hotpot bowls. All of the skewers are always perfectly cooked and juicy. Never disappointed. 
Been here multiple times and could never resist leaving without at least $30-$40 worth of goodies. Give it a try, you'll enjoy it.

The only problem with these self-serve yogurt places is that you can easily overdo it. Thankfully if you stick to the non-fat or low-cal flavors, you aren't doing much. Don't bother with the cones. Coconut & blueberries FTW!

I've been here a lot. The reason I keep coming back is because I'm a glutton for their massages. And massage they do!

Mani-Pedis will set you back $42-$45 - a bit pricey - but you get an amazing leg and foot massage, and then again about two or three shoulder/neck/back massages while you get your mani and while your nails dry. I'm willing to pay a little extra for that lovely perk.

The place is small - they have five pedi chairs, and about five mani stations - so when it's packed, it's super crowded. Try to go early in the morning if time affords.

The mani/pedi's themselves are really great. My pedi I got lasted forever, my mani not as long.  I've been there over six or seven times, and the duration of my mani has depended on what technician I got.  The quality, however, of the mani is always flawless, they pay attention to detail and they get the work done beautifully.

I did however, notice, like the other poster said that the employees do visibly gossip about the customers now and then. (although find me a nail salon in the city that doesn't!) However, I've always been treated with smiles, kindness and patience - so that has not turned me away yet - 

Like, I said, the massages keep me making me come back!

British food has a pretty bad reputation.  Of all the things the Brits do right (tea, MINI Coopers, Downton Abbey, Princess Kate), food is just not their thing.  So if you had told me I'd give a British meat pie shop 5 stars, I'd say BULLOCKS!

But alas, there is a killer British place a few blocks from my house and I'm very excited about this.  I visited to take out at lunchtime on a Saturday.  Keep in mind, this spot will close when they sell out- check their facebook for updates.  Also, the shop itself is tiny- maybe 10 chairs? So I'd plan for this to be a visit for lunch to-go.

I ordered a bacon and egg pie, a mushroom/escarole/fontina pie, and two orders of mashed potatoes and parsley oil, which came to about $17 with tax.  Before I go any further, you may ask, What is parsley oil?  I really have no clue what is in it, but it tastes like a light herb-y gravy.  And was super good.

So how was the food?  First of all, all of this was enough for both of our lunches, as well as my lunch the next day.  (FWIW, we are fairly light eaters).  The pies were delicious.  Flakey buttery crust and the fillings were amazing.  The mashed potatoes and parsley oil were super delicious as well.  Perfectly warm and filling for a fall day. 

Overall, I LOVE that there is an interesting new food option on the Avenue, that is not only incredibly delicious but also super affordable. I'm really hoping this spot takes off and is open at dinner time and has a larger seating area.  In the meantime, however, I will definitely be stopping in whenever I have a chance.

After reading the long list of Yelp! reviews & food network accolades, we went to Paesano's located at Italian market, 9th street, Philadelphia, PA. First off, this is a really small eatery located in an old neighborhood. So, if you are looking for ambience, this is not the place for you. Secondly, the food is OK but is not great. We had panelle and Gustaio sandwiches. They  were ready in 15 minutes or so .  Both sandwiches were wrapped in a thin tortilla type thin crisp bread and came packed with ingredients & sauce. Because of the heavy ingredients, the think bread was falling apart and this made a  messy eating.  Panelle was kind of spicy due to the hot sauce but was otherwise ok. All in all, we wouldn't go here again as it is not worth the long drive. If you live in the area, it is probably good place for a quick take-out.

We had an amazing time at this spot. Our waiter (Harry) was so friendly and helpful. He suggested his favorites from the menu, which we tried and did not regret! If you like lamb, definitely go for any of the lamb dishes here. 
Our appetizer was filled with flavor and so enjoyable. 
Our waiter kept the pita flowing! (There's nothing quite as terrible as a place that's sting with bread). 
Our meals were satisfying and the portion sizes were good. 
We will be returning!

Can I just say I think this may be the best bakery in the world? I had their chocolate raspberry cupcake which was amazing. Please go on the cupcake wars show. And the vanilla cupcake - perfectly dense perfect sweetness . I almost don't want to write an awesome reeves because I am scared the next time I drive up there from Dover Delaware everything will be sold out. My husband ( most picky- doesn't like sweets)  and I devoured the banana pudding.i also got mini cupcakes and haven't tasted those yet....

I want to like Hawthorne's, truly I do. I like the  concept ("bier cafe"), the decor (and who doesn't like oj in a mason jar?), the staff is all seemingly friendly however the overall experience is inconsistent. Hawthorne's is a bit larger than other brunch spots in the city and they also have outdoor seating but you will still have a wait (Average 10-20 minutes for a 2 top).

 If you haven't already been to Hawthorne's, check out their online menu - their selection all sounds drool worthy however what is actually served is not. The final straw being brunch this past weekend in which I ordered the steak & eggs (served with a mozzarella salad & balsamic vinaigrette).
