The steak was well and tender but the over easy egg was over cooked around the edges and under cooked near the yolk, practically inedible. Sorry but I'll stick to a brunch spot that can cook an egg.

I highly recommend this location to anyone looking for great pizza and excellent employees . I had a problem with my doordash delivery, the delivery driver failed to collect my pizza from this location and left my pizzas sitting at this location for over two hours.  

I ended up having to go to this location to collect my pizzas. Jonathan and Monet (employees at this location) and their manager were awesome about the situation. They were understanding and their excellent customer service was very much appreciated.

I can't thank them enough. This location and pizza is fantastic!!!!!!!

I took my car here for four times last year (state inspection, tire change, and minor repairs), but I was too lazy to write a review until today.

Jim (owner) is a nice guy. He only does the job you asks and the charge is fair. The only thing that bothers me is that he's not good at picking up your call...I guess that's because this is a one-man shop and he's always working in the garage.
Huge bar. Lot more smokers than originally assumed and beer was good.

I love this place.  The 3 roll special is an incredible deal and you'd be hard pressed to find better sushi for the price anywhere in the city.  You might think the portions will match the price but I always end up quite sated.  There's a good selection on the menu and there are always interesting special rolls up on the chalkboard.  The staff is always friendly too.  Space is limited so it's not the kind of place to go with a group of friends.   I've never actually eaten in but like to grab some sushi to go and feast in Rittenhouse Square when the weather is nice.  I work 3 blocks from Izumi in South Philly but when I have the time I'll always choose to make the trek to grab lunch from Vic instead.

Absolute heaven!!!

This has to be one of the best hoagie shops I've been to and I've live in a few different parts of the country.

Every offering is made with care, the bread is always fresh and the meat combinations they offer are out of this world. I literally start salivating thinking about it and like a zombie I find myself walking there to get one!

...

Ok back to review with sandwich in other hand. :)

My personal favorite is the "Soprano" which includes genoa salami, capicola, cotigene, prosciutto, mortadella, soppressata, grated locatelli & provolone cheese. Now that's a hoagie!!!

They have several other offerings some simple like an Italiano which is your standard Italian sub, to much more complex sandwiches. They even have a "kick up a notch" menu with several selections for those who like a spicy hoagie!

If you are ever in the Roxborough area, this is a must stop! You will not be disappointed. 

Warning: These hoagies can be addictive and may lead to zombie like walks where you suddenly find yourself with a hoagie in your hand, and may lead to need a new wardrobe due to increases in waist size.

Chun Hing was closed this evening when I got there at 7 pm, big sign on door. Sign said closed by order of Health Dept.  This is second or third time it has closed since February.  Maybe its time for complete rebuild.  Been there several times in last 8 wks, never the crowds it used to get.  Closure of most stores in shopping center, incl Pathmark, might be part of reason.
Best music and sushi in the city. Good service, still BYOB as of the writing of this review.

Reviews came up good for oysters, but the blue points from Connecticut they had were disappointing. The sauce for the grouper was good, but the fish had a metallic taste. There was a sign outside the door for a strict dress code but it was not active....people wearing track suits and hoodies....too bad this classy restaurant is such a tourist trap

After a very disappointing trip to a cheesesteak place in center city, I was told to give Dalessandro's a try. Holy. Cannoli. I won't go anywhere else. They are the BEST! Quick service, even when there's a line. And GREAT steaks!

Fantastic dinner last night at Double Knot. Beautiful inside-- loved the bar area. We were seated at the bar area in front of the kitchen. The most popular choice on the menu is the prix fix. For 60 per person, you each get 12 courses! Great deal if you can eat that much, although I am glad we chose to just pick everything out ourselves. We ended up with 14 different plates, and the portions were a bit larger than I expected. It was enough to make me feel stuffed upon finishing. We ordered the pork gyoza, asparagus, Tokyo scallion, pork belly, skirt steak, short rib, some type of crab/ramen soup, ceviche, roasted squash salad (most greens-- one of our favs), swordfish meatball, the lotus pork sausage, shishito peppers, baked sea bass sushi roll, and chicken thigh. Perfectly done. We'll be back.

I had always wanted to go to the Eastern State Penitentiary Historic Site since the time they first opened up to the public. With my niece in town for her college break I finally had the perfect opportunity to get to ESP. 

The price of admission includes a self guided audio tour with earphones and a player. Steve Buscemi narrates the audio tour. (I forgot he was in the Big Lebowsksi) It is a very easy to follow audio tour and contains a lot of history about the prison and its former residents. On our visit, the weather was beautiful so we sat out side in the yard and relaxed for a bit. It is nice to just walk around for a couple of hours and enjoy this historic landmark.

Of course we checked out Capone's cushy digs during his short stint at ESP. The information on the incarceration rates in USA compared to other countries was both revealing and shocking. The modern prison industry has sadly become a money making enterprise. I really enjoyed the visit to ESP and would go back again. It was entertaining, interesting and educational. Go figure?

4.5 Yelp stars

This place is amazing if you are at all a foodie, or just like to experience and taste a city! This place has something for everyone here! My suggestion is bring your wallet, appetite, and make sure you have plenty of time! To many places to try and cram in a hurry!

I'm disappointed this place average is 3 stars. I won't give this place a 5 just because I don't come here enough, it's a treat to me.

The pizza might be the best pizza in University City that is open late, delivers, has BEER!, and is not that expensive. The selection is always great for those who get slices. The service is better than any other fast food place in Philly, they're fast, efficient and ARE NOT RUDE!!! just for that maybe it should be 5 stars... 

They have outdoor seating, always a plus... BUT you can't take beer outside... booo! I understand why it's just sad. The food tastes pretty damn good and their cheesesteaks are more than filling... don't order a side of fries unless you're starving. I forgot how big they are once, ordered fries and could not finish all my food. 

How could i forget to mention: free soda refills!

Great brunch spot. Everyone said the crepes were delicious but filling. Mom had packed a bag of green peas for me, so I'll have to check out the crepes later.  Grandpa had the ragatoullie, and Granny Li the shrimp. Dad some onion soup and Mom went for ham & cheese. All except Dad waddled it off with a river walk. Prices - expect about $20 per head + drinks to exit.

I usually go to Nam Phung but parking in that plaza requires a miracle on Sundays, and so I came to Pho Saigon. The portions are bigger. I believe their smaller bowl is almost equivalent to other pho places' large bowl. The broth had too much cilantro that covered its true taste, so it makes me think they put MSG. 

I have to say the service was super fast and we were attended to right away and received our food within 10 minutes. The price and potions are great! I would come back here again if I were in a hurry and hungry!

I'd previously never done one of these escape rooms before (and I wasn't really sure what to expect), but I was impressed with the setup and execution of this one. Tyler did a great job of managing our Titanic-themed game, giving us hints when we asked for them, and just overall giving our group a good experience.

Craig labullshit is sour fucking grapes.  Super knowledgeable bar staff.  The bar manager is stellar as well. He doesn't balk at complex questions  but rather has more information, for the snooty bar patron like myself, that enhances the experience.  It's all the knowledge with none of the pretentious bullshit of Ranstead room.  The owner and his wife are wonderful people.  The interior decor is thoughtful and eclectic.  10 of 10.

I am not a cheesesteak person. Usually I'll get one after a night out or when tourists come into town. But I can honestly say Dalessandro's is the first place to make me crave a cheesesteak. It's that good. Just go and see for yourself!

Perfect for a late night snack. I like going there for some coffee and pie - they have a variety of desserts from a local bakery and the fresh squeezed orange juice is nice too. 

The lunchtime staff is usually good, but unfortunately at night the service goes down hill sometimes.  It's worth the trip for typical no frills diner food at a reasonable price.

* Used the yelp check-in so I paid no cover!*
Expect what you would expect from a place called the G Lounge. If that's what you are looking for (which I always am) then you'll love it. Drinks are ok priced for a club, there are go-go dancers and men are obviously there not to talk to friends and kick back a few. Music was good and I at least was dancing.

Korean Ribs taco meat was sub par but the crowd and beers were good.  

Perhaps stick to the beer and veggies

This place was terrible. The food was not as pictured. You have no opportunity to make adjustments or ask questions as the concept is serverless.  You order on a screen, pay on a screen, get charged 18% gratuity on a screen....wait what?! The servers understand the concept well too! They barely speak to you, do not make eye contact, and stay as far away as possible as not to be bothered by your needs or wants.  
I ordered a bartenders choice beer sampler and when it showed up I was told the 4 beer names in 4 seconds flat so I asked the server to please repeat them.  He seemed irritated.  He must not get paid well enough to talk to the customers. 
My fish and chips were not the chunky hand battered pieces of cod pictured on the screen but instead two triangular fish pieces. Not what I expected for $22.  I wasn't asked if I needed anything else, or if my meal was okay, or how I liked the beer sampler.  I left frustrated and irritated by not only the service but the concept in general.  This was a nice bar/restaurant not a fast food place. Honestly I understand the idea of this serverless concept in a busy airport situation.  But it is in dire need of a call your server button.
Literally the best. Always. Never too salty, always flavorful, my fave takeout ever. Loveeee su xing!!!

The museum stayed open late during Pittcon so I stopped in to see some old lab equipment. The displays are lovely and the people all very courteous and enthusiastic. I'm a fan if the old alchemist genre paintings upstairs in the Fisher collection, which you can view with an appointment.  Great place for science history or general instrumentation buffs.

What can I say.  My family has been going to Cosimos for years.
I moved away and when my grandpa was sick in hospice, I would come home to visit and Cosimos was my spot.  

Now that I'm back in town, it hasn't changed at all.  Their ready made slices and other doughy stuff is plain old GOOD.

Love this place!!!  A terrific selection of coffee beans, roasted in-house.  Delicious pour-overs and espresso drinks.  Friendly and knowledgeable workers.  And a large space with a range of benches, chairs, couches, and stools for kicking back, chatting or working, and enjoying your coffee.

I want to reiterate the comments about awful customer service.  It was around lunchtime, they probably had 3 tables of people and some people getting carry-out periodically with three people working.  The service was very slow but I can forgive that.  But with too many workers one person was using a potato slicer even though I could see a huge bucket of sliced potatoes.  Anyway, the slicer was louder than the music and the harsh sound made the girl at the table behind me jump from being startled.  The pounding of the slicer overpowered my ability to have a conversation with the person across the table from me and was starting to give me a headache.  I got up and asked the worker if she could perhaps wait until the lunch crowd exited to finish using the slicer given the disruption and the abundance of potatoes.  Instead of thinking customer-first, she said it doesn't bother her and that's what she does all day.  At that point, I asked then if I could get a to-go bag as it was so disruptive to my lunch conversation and pointed out that other patrons were also bothered.  Not only did she not get me a bag, she went back to the machine and appeared to purposefully be using it louder and more frequently.  I ended up throwing out the rest of my lunch and leaving.  I will not be going back. 

As to the food, I shared the classic poutine, the truffle poutine, and a classic slider.  The poutines were both above average but certainly not as good as you would get in Canada.  The slider is on par with a Wendy's Jr. classic cheeseburger, so stick to the poutine if you go there.

Bartenders are not particularly personable,  at least not with everyone. When i sat down it was just before the cut off time of food being served, but i wasn't given a heads up. That would have been nice. I have visited this bar on one other occasion and had the mussels, and they were good.  Next time I will try to show up sooner. It's a nice bar/restaurant. While I am staying at the hotel this time, I didn't even know it was connected to a hotel last time. Not your average hotel lobby bar.

JeeZ people calm down with all the negativity. It's a UPS store. Yes they charge for things, and yes it gets busy given it is smack dab in the middle of the city. Be patient. Never had a problem at this store. Trust their packing process bc it is a hell eva lot better than we can do. I promise it's a solid job. Breathe people

This place is a five in regards to food. If you're looking for good quality Vietnamese food, this place won't let you down!! I got the shrimp and garlic dumplings and the beef and brisket Pho - Both were absolutely delicious. I can tell the dishes were homemade. For dessert, I got the pineapple sorbet and it was so yummy!! I can't wait to come back to try the coconut sorbet. The space is a little tight and the seating is a bit uncomfortable. They have mini stools as seats so I had to keep adjusting myself. Other than that, great spot!

Pretty good bubble tea. Prices are ok, but it's conveniently located near China Gourmet, so grab some dim sum and some bubble tea. It might have been the choice of flavors I made, but I wasn't a huge fan of the tea I got when I was there (brown sugar tea). Definitely going to give it another shot.

Wow. This pizza is simply amazing. 
Duhlicious!
I ordered a half plain (mozz) and half roasted onion because my 6 yr old daughter does just plain mostly. Sans the princess I'll be more adventurous and try some of their finer add ons like roasted peppers, salame and pickled peppers.  

It is thin crusted pizza cooked to perfection with a little bit of burnt crust along the edges. 
I felt guilty adding crushed red peppers not wanting to disturb all the great flavors in my mouth but it only adds to the great taste. 

For those of you not wanting to wait for a great pie, you might want to try on wed or thurs nights as Joe Beddia describes it best "it's a bit more mellow on those nights."
I ordered tonight (sat.) a lil after 7pm and was given an estimate pickup time of 8:55pm. Holy smokes, lucky I live close by. 
Returned at 7:48 and my pie was ready at 8:53. How's that for timing. 

As you might have read, there are only 2 tables and zero chairs and zero phones. 
So long as Joe keeps pumping out pies that taste this good we'll have to follow his rules; like it or not. 
Personally, I don't mind the minimalist decor and procedures because the pizza kicks ass. 
Who am I to recommend Pizzeria Beddia?
Well for comparisons sake and to be fair, I have tried some of the best pizza this city and others have to offer. I've been all over Italy and my cousin owns a pizzeria in Reggio Emilia, Italy. Even his pizza isn't this good and that's saying a lot. 
Other pizza in the city that certainly deserves a mention along side Beddia would include Vetri's Osteria, Starr's Stella, Tacconelli's and even neighboring Pizza Brain. 
I think Best of Philly will be well deserved.

Oh and about the price/cost criticisms I've heard, I don't think $18-$28 for a great pizza is anything out of the ordinary in a big city like Philly.

Wonderful new addition to Philly; Solmonov & Cook at it again! Bright cafe with delicious breakfast/lunch options (salads, toasts) and desserts.

Atmosphere: Cool vibe with high ceilings, and great lighting. Be expected to wait ~15-20 minutes (sometimes 30 minutes) during busy times (weekends, noon). Generally quite loud inside, and seating may be difficult to find if you go during prime lunch times, or over the weekend. The music is... eclectic - at one point, Superstition by Stevie Wonder, then 30 minutes later, shift to hard core EDM. For less wait times, go around 2pm, when the cafe is quite empty.

Food: Recommend trying the Jerusalem bagels (very well toasted), or the quinoa & avocado salad! Their toasts do contain both sweet and savory - if you're looking for dessert, their date & molasses toast is a good option. Coffee is incredible, and they use reusable straws as well! However, because they are still new, many food options are unavailable later in the afternoon, which is unfortunate - go early if you're looking for specific Jerusalem bagels or pastries, as they will run out. 

Overall: 4.5/5 - great atmosphere and delicious food options! Slightly on the louder side though, depending on whether or not they're playing EDM. My only real complaint is that their food options run out, but I imagine this will happen less as they are open for longer.

Sunday school was a great finish to a perfect beer Sunday.

Headed to Trias late Sunday after recovering from the awesome experince at Monks cafe. It was hopping for a Sunday night, but Philly isn't sleepy town CT. Managed to get a seat at the bar between to couples.

The Sunday school was a great deal, I passed on the wine special but tried the cheese and beer specials.

The spicy crab stuffed Picadilla peppers were excellent. 

Bartenders were pretty busy but friendly.

I ordered off of EatStreet tonight. I live in an apartment building and waited 1 hour for my food. I told my doorman to let him up to my apartment. He called when he got there and said he wouldn't come up bc my tip was only $2. I proceed to go down to get my food and ask him basically why he wouldn't come up and then he continued to go off that the restaurant is far and that he spent money on gas to travel here. He also said that I didn't get enough food or leave enough tip for him to come up. He was just rude overall. I said ok I won't order from you again and he said that he didn't want to see my face again. Also after he left other residents in my building stated he was showing and ranting to other people in my complex prior to me coming downstairs that I only ordered $8 worth of food and only gave him a 15% tip. 
I usually don't write bad reviews but watch out he may be mad at you for ordering the minimum and not providing a sufficient tip. Delivery man I didn't set your radius up on EatStreet so don't blame me!

Pretty disappointing... if you want a better tasting gyro just grab one from Zestos right next door- taste is much better then Yiro's beef/ lamb mix of a gyro. The chicken was ok and I tried here 3 times just to make sure they were not having an off night. I really wanted to like this place. The Baklava is amazing, there is one plus. The lamb beef mix has got to go- 0 flavor. Nice try, totally underwhelming wait for them to open.

Reviews be damned I went down to check this place out for myself and I'll have to say I think a lot of you yelpers are either too harsh or need to head back there.

I ordered a cheese burger with fries and a large drink which came out to less than $7 which was pretty cheap in my opinion. The staff was a little disorganized but I think that's to be expected when you're just starting up. There was a group in front of me so it did take about 10 minutes or so to get my order. They were very friendly though and aware of the wait. The head chef gave the guy in front of me an order of free fries for having to wait a bit.

The burger was really good, juicy and not overcooked. If for some reason you like your burger cooked well done, I'd probably let them know. Good roll and lots of toppings. The burger was smaller in circumference but thicker than I expected so it was of adequate size in my opinion. The fries were very good as well. Thin cut and crispy, no need for ketchup, the true sign of a good fry. There were plenty for me so I'd have to imagine the reviewer that thought there weren't enough probably orders the super size at McDonald's.

If you're eating at the picnic tables, beware of the birds. There was this little one that wouldn't leave me alone trying to get at my fries. They know no fear and are very persistent. It came so close I could probably have picked it up and it took so much for me to shoo them away you might have to do just that to keep them from stealing your food.
Enjoyable airport bar + good food. Reasonable prices. Try the "cold shrimp". Good value @ $11. Solid IPA selection, $7 each.

Used them for a special occassion (wedding).  They made our table centerpieces.  After a trial centerpiece and changes were made per our request, they were able to come to something we liked.  However, when centerpieces were delivered, they were different than the agreed upon/trial centerpiece.  The bride and mother of the bride are allergic to a specific flower that they used.  We told them several times not to include this flower, AND had them take them out of the trial centerpiece as such.  Yet, there they were on the tables at the wedding reception.....   Further, the groom's boutonniere was not what we had ordered either!!
Disappointing.  Plus we paid more than we should have paid.....

The food is delicious and cheap and plentiful, which is why I wanted to give it four stars. (The menu is overwhelming, and it's all so delicious that I usually do a "pick three" option so I can bring home leftovers!) The refried beans are the stuff of dreams. And if you go there for lunch, it's definitely four stars.

But I made it three stars because, for dinner, it's extremely crowded and noisy inside (the tables are very close together, and the booths are pretty cramped), and a wait for a table can be really, really long. Normally I'm not that picky, but I find myself choosing to go elsewhere because it can be very unpleasant to wait an hour for a table and then be a foot away from a very loud and obnoxious group of people. Plus, the service is pretty average and can be incredibly slow. But the refried beans and the margarita pitchers nearly make it worth it.

Just ordered on Grubhub 2 platters(oxtails & rasta pasta) $69.00 . The amount of oxtails I was given would've have been a portion for a child and I added shrimp to the pasta only 3 . They should be ashamed of themselves for taking peoples money and giving them these  type of portions . if You don't treat your customers correctly you shouldn't have a business

This place deserves one star.  

Horrible produce section.  If it isn't already spoiled/overripe it will be within 24hours of bringing it home.  

Once I brought home a gallon of milk only to immediately open it and find it was spoiled.  When I brought it back 30 min later the manager fought me over whether it was really spoiled.  It was only after making him drink some that he believed me that it was spoiled. 

The prices are ridiculously high, which I understand is for the convenience factor.  However for that price they should at least have fresh, unspoiled food.  You can pay the same amount at whole foods and at least get quality in return.

Why am I giving it two stars, then?  The customer service counter will let you buy rolls of quarters.  This is very convenient when you're trying to do laundry and the banks are closed.  That is the only reason they get two stars.

Came here on a recommendation while in town for business.  For an appetizer we got the sauteed calamari.  The calimari was a little different, as I've only had the fried style before.  It was very tender, not rubbery, in a tomato based sauce, which was great for dipping bread. My entree was the veal franchise, which was cooked perfectly.  The sauce was rich but not overpowering.  I got 3 pieces of veal, which was consummate with the price of the meal, and also came with a side of pasta in a simple tomato sauce (I felt the sauce was a little bland, needed more basil or something).  My co worker got the linguini with seafood.   She said everything was excellent.  I negotiated for a mussel, and it was cooked well, and I enjoyed the sauce it was in.  For dessert, we split a cannoli, which was serious stuff.  House wines were $7 I believe, and had a decent variety.

Yummm! This food is tasty! One of the very few places that opens for dinner seating before six. Nice dining room. Pleasant Latin rhythms in the background. Service is a nice tempo. I love those little buns and the dipping sauce! The entre' is fresh and well prepared. The Chocolate Cigar is unique and good! I'll come back here again...and again. Highly recommend you try it!

This place is fucking awesome.  I went to law school here 15 years ago and I still crave an Abner's Cheesesteak once in a while. They used to have a great peppers bar, and pickles. Yum. I hope they still let you throw a dollar by the grill to tip, its totally disgusting and unsanitary, but fuck it! It's Filthadelphia, who cares?  Honestly, I truly despised the City of Philadelphia and the time I spent there.  It really is a Class A shithole, with no redeeming qualities.  Abner's was one of the few positive perks of living in a cold, downtrodden, miserable, East coast City. I was happy to see it is still alive and thriving.  Btw... Geno's and Pats are totally overrated and taste like shit.  Seriously, Abner's is so underrated and the people were actually nice there.  Which is rare in Philadelphia.

Last night, I came here for dinner, and this morning I'm feeling the after effects of the food I ate.  I ordered the bibim nang myun, which tasted good at the time, but it had to have been loaded with salt or possibly with MSG because I woke up at 5am feeling disgusting.  I'm at work now and still feeling it.  And only now do I remember 2 different friends telling me that they always feel sick after eating their nang myun.  I know that some people go here just for that, but I will definitely avoid it next time I come here.

THE OWNER DOES NOT LIKE TO BE YELPED! She's a very charming and kind lady and I ask that everyone respects her wish of keeping her cafe a yelp-free zone. I know there's already a lot on here that has been said but I leave this here in hopes that future visitors will not yelp her. Thank you!

I give five stars when I can't find anything to complain about, and our first trip to Goat Hollow was perfect. We sat at the bar, and ordered three drinks between the two of us over the course of the evening: the Gold Fashioned, Clover Club, and Negroni. Each of the cocktails was well-balanced and made with skill and accuracy. Our bartenders were friendly and attentive, taking the time to banter with us while also providing efficient service to us and the others at the bar. Our food came out quickly. I had the Sedgewick Mussels (in a garlic-herb-tomato broth), and my companion ate one of the burgers which was cooked exactly as she requested it. Both of our meals were excellent. I thought the place seemed stylish and upscale without being pretentious. AND the price was right: three cocktails and two entrees came to $53.
My daughters took me there for Mother's Day   The food was fresh and delicious the service was impeccable. Great ambiance.  I would love to go back again and again soon

Their croissants are out of this world!! Of course the first time I try it is the day before the owners leave for a two month vacation! I'm having an intense craving right now and they will not be back until March. What is a girl to do!?!? I seriously cannot believe I took this long to come here and I'm glad that I finally did. It is seriously that good. It's like buttery deliciousness. I also ordered a tofu banh mi and which also pretty amazing. 

March can't come soon enough...

A big thank you to yelp for hooking use up with tickets for a  Elite event that allowed us to enjoy the Game Masters video game exhibit. If your a Gamer you are going to love this exhibit!!! It opened with real arcade games that you can play such as, Donkey Kong Pacman Defender tempest Asteroids etc. As you move through the exhibit you are introduced and led through the full array of video games each categorized by format and system. they also did a great job of explaining the various types of game out there and gave a great introduction to the founders of each type of game. As if that were not enough, you have an opportunity to play various games too. We both fully enjoyed this total experience, and we're not into gaming at all.

The rest of the Franklin institute was stellar !!!! some exhibits were being repaired, while most of the displayed were fully functional and blast to interact with. The museum is clean and well maintained too. Spending an afternoon here is a must for anyone visiting the city !!!
Amazing spa! I loved my time here! I will recommend this spa to all of my friends! I will be coming back here for my massages.

Overpriced. Food not so special. Fair service at best. 

Location is very small (not a bad thing).

I love Shake Shack, but this is definitely thee best location!! (20th and Sansom) Mike and his staff are professional and courteous to their guests. It is always clean and the food is always cooked to perfection.

Great for free wifi
A touch on the pricey side
Good chicken salad

Cafe Mocha is a great location if you're trying to get any sort of work accomplished. There's a ton of seating, it's never crowded and the wifi is free all day. It's mostly table seating, there's only a few 'comfy' chairs, so the book clubs and discussion groups tend to stay away.

You can't see it from the street, but there's a really delightful back patio with a few tables and chairs, a really nice place to get away from the street but still be outside.

The downside of the place is it's sort of pricey, especially for regular coffee. I'm a big fan of the home made soups (vegetable) and sandwiches (chicken salad.) It's kind of a small thing, but I love getting bagels here because they actually spread the cream cheese for you instead of handing you some despicable little plastic packet.

Heard so much about their bun bo hue so I had to try. 

Street parking only which is very hard to find. Might need to walk a block or two but this place is definitely worth trying. The restaurant is not that big since its a small mom and pop store. 

They don't have a lot on the menu but this is why their food is delicious because they put their time on making the broth which is on point. I got the bun bo hue less spicy since I'm a chicken to heat. My fiancé got their pho. The service was fast , My food was delicious with lots of meat, soup was spicy even if I told  them not to make it too spicy so be mindful of their level of heat. 

Overall their soup was very good. Something worth going back too.

We had a great dinner there last night.
We tried to go once before but they were unexpectedly closed.
The owners were very classy in encouraging us to try again.
Well, we did and it was great.
Service and mgt were very attentive.
We had 3 of the dumplings...beet, shrimp, and soup.
And, the Cheesesteak thing was a nice surprise.
Wonton soup was excellent and the spicy salad was very unique.
Thanks for a great experience.

This is my favorite spot for authentic Mexican food. I order once or twice a week and never have had bad food. I've tried a lot on their menu and the mole style enchiladas and chicken tinga quesadillas are top notch. Food is really fresh. Stay away from the burritos, or get a substitute for the French fries - this combo was the least flavorful dish I've had here. I always do take out, and haven't had any bad ordering experiences. You also get a lot of food for the price.

We tried Veggie Lovers a couple of years ago but I forgot to post the review back then.

The scallion pan-fried tofu was good. Solid, home style cooking. 3.5 stars

YES! A delicious banh mi sandwich, walking distance from my place! For $5.5 - 6.5, you too can savor this vietnamese classic. 

Warm, toasted french bread, still soft in the middle, filled with sliced cucumber, pickled shredded carrot, cilantro, jalapeño, and your choice of meat or tofu. I opted for the lemongrass tofu and my bf got the #1 house special, which is a combination of bbq pork, ham, and paté. While the lemongrass tofu was good, I would probably go with the house special next time, it was an excellent flavor combination. The fresh, toasted french bread alone is enough for another visit.

Service was friendly and fast. The restaurant is quite small though, so plan on taking your order to-go if you're with a group. Cash only!

I'm kind of surprised I haven't reviewed BBP considering I've lived across the street from it for 2.5 years. Maybe because it's just mediocre.

Your experience probably depends a lot on your service. Bobby's usually takes their time with your food, meaning you could have just waited in that horrendous line at Chipotle and probably still be done at the same time as Bobby's with no line. I'm going to run an experiment next time.

On top of the normally slow service, I've noticed recently that sometimes your burger will be done and no employees bother to get it for you (even if you give them the stinkeye). This latest time, I saw a bag sitting at the counter when no one else was waiting for their order but me. THREE employees walked by without bothering to check the bag. I asked the cashier about it, and he went to look and then decided to not report back to me even though I was standing right there. I got in line at the cash register and asked him again. I finally got my food. While I was eating, I noticed that not one, but TWO employees stole fries out of customers' bags sitting at the counter (instead of getting the meals to the customers). Are you kidding me?! That's not just rude; it's UNSANITARY and disgusting. Terrible, terrible service.

The burgers are pretty good most of the time, though they can be cool sometimes. This latest time, they messed up probably the easiest order ever: burger with lettuce and tomato cooked medium well. It came out medium rare with no tomato. Thanks a lot.

Medusa is one seriously weird joint.  On the surface it definitely looks like a place I'd like (I dig the whole divey punk-rock aesthetic in a bar) but there's just something off about the whole venue.  I stumbled upon it when I was at a show at the Church (being an all-ages non/alcoholic venue, I was interested in scrounging up a beer and a shot of Jameson before returning to the kids and being made to feel OLD!)  The shot was more than I like paying for my whiskey (but I'm spoiled by my various bartender buddies) but the beer was a cheap microbrew (big plus).  I don't know, maybe the place could grow on me.  It's definitely an anomoly in the 'hood.

Because of the home imprisonment, we have been trying some local spots with takeout for the first time. Loved Nonna's! Very nice people. The veggie pizza was out of this world The square Margherita pizza was a little doughy but still delicious. The chicken wings were wonderful, just the right amount of spice. Prices were very reasonable. We will definitely be back. The indoors looked beautiful, lots of mahogany, so we really want to eat indoors when things return to normal (if they ever do). Remember to tip generously in these very, very difficult days for those restaurant employees who are still working and hanging on by a thread.

Excellent friendly, family based all around Vietnamese restaurant, with very good service. Fast and hits the spot for my pho fix. My friend brought me here and said this is the place to be if you are in the area. 

I had the chicken pho and it was delicious. The broth is good and the dipping sauce on the side is made of a very unique blend of lemon grass and other herbs that brings out the flavor of the chicken.

Come and try it out for yourself.

Well. Where does one start. We sat outside and then asked what beer was on tap and our server mentioned yards and dogfishhead. So we asked which ones and she wasn't sure, we figured she's not a beer drinker, it's ok, so we walked up to the bar to ask the bartender, who proceeded to look around for an answer, look at the actual tap and said she didn't know either. so they know what breweries they have beer from, but not what types. We went for yuengling. We ordered some food and two members of our party ordered a gin and tonic and a whiskey sour. after a 10-15min wait we got the beer and a red drink that instead of a gin and tonic was what we deduced was a tonic water and grenadine. when our other member finally got his whiskey sour it was mostly sour with a splash of whiskey. however the kicker that made us get up and leave was when my friend who received the incorrect gin and tonic got a replacement of a neat whiskey and got back to the table with it she realized it had bugs in it. not one bug. not something flew into her drink, several little beetles (like you might find in with your rice or pasta). We got up to leave and brought the drink up to another server who was very kind and offered to right the situation, but we were done at that point. not sure if they had a bad day or they are normally that bad. but we will definitely not be returning. p.s. sorry to the waitress, you were also very nice and we regret not leaving you a tip for your service. but the bugs. I have never seen that. We could not stay.

I purchased a custom charm from Bel Jewelers for over 400 dollars.  The charm had some imperfections that we requested be fixed.  After 4 attempts the charm was slightly discolored and we were told that it would not get any better.  We were also made aware of the "no refund" policy.  When my husband expressed his displeasure about the piece, Bel Jewelers complained about him to his employer since he had stopped in on his way home from work. We have since decided to have the charm redone by another jeweler.  When that jeweler tested the gold it wasn't even 14k, as stated on our receipt and stamped on the back.  I would never recommend this jeweler to anyone.

**I received a message from the owner to give more facts about the behavior of my husband so here is the full story behind that.  

When my husband went to pick up the charm the last time, the girls behind the counter started giggling at his displeasure.  He stated "this is bullshit" and got upset. He was told there was no refund policy and after spending so much money on something that meant so much to me he got visibly upset and told them they would never receive his business or any of his fellow firefighters.  The store claims that he was threatening, however, when we requested the security video (my husband was fired based on their word) we were told that while their security cameras worked the day before and the day after he was there, they did not work the day he was.  During our appeal of his discipline action, I called the store and request that they write a letter to the department saying that the situation had been blown out of proportion.  The owner stated he was too busy too deal with this, that my husband should have called and apologized for cursing in front of the women in the store.  After crying and begging for him to help, he said he would make a phone call to the department.  I do not know either way if that phone call was ever made.  

While this incident would make me never use them again, the reason I wrote this review is because after all of this when I gave the charm to a second jeweler to melt down for a new we were told that the gold wasn't even the karat it was supposed to be.  It was 10-11karat not 14.

My 2nd time trying fuel, it's  not bad at all.  a nice place to eat and chill. I had TBM with chicken and a green tea matcha, the sandwhich was good but had little flavoring when I tried it but I'll definitely try something that's new to me on the menu next time.

Owner take tips. Food is delicious; not overly salty. Place seemed clean. Very nice older gentleman served us with a smile. Considering that he cannot take the tips, he was very nice and served us well. He gave us everything we requested. Missing one star because the place looks plain and has holes in wall and pink paint all around.

Food here is sooooo good. I have to come more often. Service is...interesting. They all work together so it's nice that you can get help from anyone but sometimes they all think that someone else has taken your order or checked on you when no one has. There's indoor and outdoor seating which is perfect for summer months. My friend had a waffle, eggs, and some of the best turkey sausages I've ever tasted. They were SO savory and flavorful. I really wanted to order some for myself but I already had too much food. I tried the hanger steak & eggs with grits. It was wonderful. The steak was cooked and seasoned perfectly. It came with a huge portion of grits and the presentation of the entire dish was very nice. Someone near me has the chicken and waffles which looked phenomenal. I'll definitely be back.

I went here the other day and forgot to leave my review. I was hoping to snag some different fruits that I haven't tried before. The major turn down from this place is that it seems to have many flies that land on all the produce and fresh meats and fish.  I did get some different fruits and a few other things to try. The prices are great including on the meats and seafood but unfortunately I can't stand bugs on food so I wouldn't buy anything like that there.

After walking across the Walnut Street bridge twice a day for six months now, I finally popped into the Walnut Bridge Coffee House.  If this place was closer to my starting point (also known as "home"), I'd probably hit it up more, but by the time I reach it, I'm almost at work, so it's just not convenient for me.  Anyway, that's not weighing in on my rating, it's just the reason it took me so long to stop in.

I don't know whether to judge the Walnut Bridge for selling $5+ lattes, or if I just need to accept that the times, they are a changin, and that's how much it costs for a small latte from an independent cafe.  It's probably the latter, and that's probably why I'm not addicted to caffeine.  

This place is way cute, and I could definitely see myself camping out to read or write or whatever.  It's cozy.  Their homemade foods and sweets looks superb, although I did not indulge myself after the sticker shock of the $5 chai.  But if I ever need or really want a homemade truffle, this will be the first place I go.

please don't throw any stones, but seriously i just don't get it.  i love chinese food, but i don't understand the unapologetic "authentic" cuisine of han dynasty.  the dan dan noodles were bathed in oil and the noodles themselves were tasteless.  the pork belly was also just a fatty and greasy mess.  making food more greasy and spicy is usually an amateur cover up for flavor and freshness shortcomings.  i can't even bring myself to give han dynasty a second chance despite the fact that i only live two block away.

I have been searching for a pizza joint that has similar flavor profile as back home and this place is great! The spicy chicken/paneer pizza/cheesesteak is amazing at this place! The masala fries are also highly recommended.

I brought my girlfriend here for brunch, the wait wasn't very long for a table for two. We were seated downstairs, but I felt like I was a paced in like sardines which made it hard to enjoy my brunch. Other than that the food was good and I enjoyed it much, my girlfriend and I split the Yogurt that had pumpkin seeds and honey in it, we ordered a few Bloody Marys, but I was unhappy that they have a secondary drink called a Blood Hound with all the fixingings of a Bloody Mary, that was pretyy upsetting. When I go to a place that serves Bloody Mary's, we tend to get the pickle and olives and all the goods with it, here you had to pay extra for it. We ordered our brunch which was a simple Stuffed French Toast and a Smoked Chicken and Apple Sausage Sandwich. Our food took the longest time to get to our table, and by the time we got to eat we had to leave because our meter ran out. Now, we set the meter for an hour and a half so it took roughly fourty five minutes to get our Brunch, thats not very acceptable. When we received our food my syrup had french fries in it that must have fell while they were carrying the food, gross, so it wasn't an entirely enjoyable time, I expected much more. I will quite possibly come back and hope to have a better time.

I went to the 10pm Saturday night show last weekend, and it was really a wonderful performance -- great people (off stage, too), hilarious routines, interactive sets, and an intimate feeling in the small three-sided theatre. 

The New Jersey Turnpikes VS the Pennsylvania Fighting Amish were really hysterical. At every turn, the audience had a chance to shout out answers, vote on moves, and participate in various ways. I made a huge mistake by pointing out my friend to jump on stage to take the lead in one act... and instead, I was pulled up on stage! I was the main character in a "Yes/No" game, and got my picture taken with the crew in the end. 

This place is BYOB and the tickets are fairly cheap. I'd definitely encourage anyone to attend to check it out.

Food was OK, though neither my sandwich nor my husband's salad came out with all the right stuff. The service was horrible--took 20 minutes and was just a mess behind the counter. Like no one was actually in charge or thinking about how to run the thing. Will definitely not go back.

I was not expecting much from a taco place that looks like hipster heaven and has a white dude's name on it, but I was so, so pleasantly wrong.

I had a bean and rice burrito and the mushroom quesadilla; both were super delish. The chips and guacamole were also a hit at our table. Good prices, great vegetarian options. Pretty decent service.

I wish we had one of these in the city where I live.

Came in here while waiting for a seat for breakfast.  Met JD and staff and everyone is extremely friendly! Couple of T-shirts caught my eye and decided to buy them! If you're looking a well maintained and well informed shop for all of your nerdy needs, please stop by this shop!  Located right next to Greg's Kitchen street parking is available!  Say hello to the shop's dog, Parker! cutest puppy ever!

The food here is very basic. We approached the counter and the way everyone was cramped into a tiny counter just didn't appeal to me. We opted for the apple dumpling, to-go, with cream on the side. We saw the girl pop in the apple dumpling that was sitting in a foam takeout container into the microwave. Oh, is what I said to myself. The dumpling didn't look too good. The pastry that was supposed to be around the apple filling was broken up into what seemed like a million pieces and everything was just a gloppy mess in the foam container. The presentation made me not want to look at it. We tried it and it was pretty good tasting. I just wish the dumpling didn't look like a gloppy mess in a little foam container. Unappetizing looking.

Amazing!  We worked with Vito to plan our wedding at Ballroom at the Ben.  He was very enthusiastic, fun, and knowledgeable.  We brought a few pictures of what we wanted to the initial meeting but did not have strong opinions or ideas.  Fortunately, this was not a problem at all.  Vito had incredible ideas and helped guide us towards developing our "vision".  Ultimately, we put a lot of trust in Vito and were not disappointed!  The bouquets were gorgeous and the Ballroom looked stunning.  We truly enjoyed our meetings with Vito and the entire experience with Carl Alan Floral Designs.  Thank you!
Love this little spot what more can I say? Food always fresh, friendly employees and on the spot with delicious delicious sweets and more ahhhhhh

Full disclosure, this review is ONLY for their single slices. I've been on a bit of a quest lately to find the best slice in Philly; well, the NorthEast part of it anyway, and I've come across WAY more pretenders than contenders. But, Droughies is far and away leading the pack at the time of this writing. (By the by; how the hell do you pronounce the name of this place? "Druggies?" If so, thats unfortunate.) Anywho, I stopped in here to grab the 2 slice and a soda, $3.50 special (whoo hoo!). The slices came out quick and are even jazzed up with this circular sauce pattern on the pizza. The dough was "toothsome" (I love that word), which is a big checkmark for me. I hate dough that is either flaccid or crunchy. If I wanted a wet tortilla or a cracker, I would've ordered that, but Droughies gets the crust perfect. The sauce is the, "delicious with just a hint of sweetness" kind, and the cheese tastes like actual cheese. AND, no orange grease dripping off the slice! 

I can't give 5 stars for the entire establishment because I've only had the slices, but if their other offerings are as good as their pies, you could probably do a lot worse. Highly recommended.

Any place that serves a huge selection of wine, beer, cheese, AND chocolate is amazing in my book.  

Two things differentiate Beneluxx from Tria.  The first thing is: you can get a tasting sized glass of wine so you can try a ton of stuff there.  Ok that description sucked - I guess the closest thing I can think of to make it clearer is that it's kind of like a flight of beer but wine.  It's a lot of fun.  Each table has a glass washer on it to clear out your glass before a refill.  It's kind of fun.  The second thing that differentiates Beneluxx from Tria is: they serve CHOCOLATE.  

It's a great date place!  Very cozy, nice mood lighting.  I'm not sure when it gets really crowded but I went once during happy hour and it was almost empty.

Definitely a must for those on the beer and wine bar circuit.

Lifesport is a family-owned gym, which gives them total autonomy to be flexible with their prices and what not. I only had two weeks in Fairmount, but desperately needed a gym to go to that wasn't too much of a hike away. With Lifesport just down the road, I couldn't help but check it out. 

I managed to snag some sort of custom membership that only cost me $3-$4 (instead of $10) for everyday that I went for 10 days. This is typical of Lifesport: they always invite you over to their gym to discuss goals, commitment duration and programs so you can get your time and money's worth. Price wise, i thought this was super reasonable. Not many gyms are able or willing to make this sort of compromise. 

In terms of facilities, Lifesport (don't let their size fool you) has a wide variety of machines. In addition to the standard selection of free weights, they even have niche sports machines; my favorite being the dragon boat paddle erg. If anything, this just shows how different a gym they are compared to the franchised ones.  

Overall, the place looks great, and the staff is super friendly. The gym is extremely well-maintained, and surprisingly spacious given the sheer amount of workout machines they have. They have a dance-esque studio, a designated room for weights, and a cardio area.

This was by far our favorite attraction on our recent trip to Philadelphia.  It was a little spooky and it was extremely cold in January (there are only few spot with heaters available) but we really enjoyed it.   We liked the audio tour, it just made it so much more interesting and helped not having to read so much (which always gives me a headache after long periods at museums).  I would definitely recommend a visit to this place while in town.  My daughter liked it so much we may come back for the Halloween tour in the fall!  The only disappointment was that we missed the Al Capone cell by about a week since it was closed for maintenance.
First time eating Korean bbq and it was awesome! My favorite was the rib tips and the original Chinese hot pot!!!!! So good

I dig Lucky 13, thanks to Jackie G. and Jason F. Clark is their friend and now he is mine too. He is a great bartender.  Also, Victoria is a great bartender too.  The food at Lucky 13 is also great. Because I'm trying to become anorexic these days, I had to split a salad with my friend, but it was really really good. That salad was the "Lucky 13" salad, and what a salad it is. Anyway, we watched most of the World Series here, and sadly, Clark is a Yankees fan. At least he wasn't a d*&k about it.

Meh... this is a suburban bar parked on a city block.  In a lot of suburban bars, people are looking for one thing: a place that serves alcohol.  If you can do that, you have a successful business model.  One thing I prefer about bars in the city is that most need to do more to retain their clientele, which leads to a better bar experience for the patrons.  I think bars are better when personalized, you can find something that is exactly what you are looking for, rather than the same music played incredibly loud at a place without anyone dancing or seemingly listening to the music with the same drinks, the same... everything.

This is a lowest common denominator bar designed with one purpose in mind: get as many people to come in as possible.   This results in a lack of personality and soul for the bar.  There isn't really anything special about it.

This place is FANTASTIC!  My boyfriend and I were very excited to head to Nina's Trattoria for our date night, and we were not at all disappointed.  We found out about it on Groupon--I purchased a deal for a seasonal three-course tasting for two people.  The best part?  It was only $35!  It's a BYOB, so we brought a bottle of red wine and were ready to be surprised. The catch with the Groupon is that the courses are chef selected, so you have no idea what will be brought to you.  (They do ask about any dietary restrictions right away.)

When we first arrived, we were greeting by an incredibly friendly staff, who really made our night feel special.  They started with homemade bread, and quite frankly based off of that alone, we knew the actual meal would be delicious.  Our appetizer was the Nina's Salad and homemade meatballs to share.  I gotta tell ya, these were unlike any meatballs I'd ever had! Amazing!  For dinner they brought us spaghetti in a blue cheese sauce with walnuts on top and gnocci with sausage.  I'm not one to order pasta when I'm out to dinner, but these dishes were both wonderful.  For dessert, we were brought homemade tiramisu and a strawberry banana cheesecake.  They were both the perfect way to end the perfect date.  Thanks, Nina's Trattoria!  We will absolutely be back!

I enjoyed coming here for drinks after lunch with my boyfriend and his mom and brother. This place is very nice and spacious with a lot of natural lighting. We ordered all three flights (one of them wasn't on the menu but it was like an IPA flight or something like that). My favorite out of everything was definitely the Yards Philly Ale.

The service was great as well and I loved that they had a pool table for us to play with.

I was treated very poorly by some of the staff a few weeks ago, but I figured it might be a one off.  However, what they did to the loyal contingent of Bills Backers who had an agreement with the bar and waited 2 decades for the moment that was denied to them is disgraceful.  They filled your bar every Sunday, and that's how you return the favor?

Just tried Just Salad and ordered the roasted turkey wrap ($10.29) and kale smoothie ($6.49).Unfortunately the wrap came with no turkey and the smoothie is a pre-made blend (not fresh). Definitely disappointed.

I love wandering into Open House, there is something for everyone in there.  
At first glance, the shop can be overwhelming, there is an absorbent amount of things here, from books to cards to scented soaps and candles, however everything is thoughtfully arranged where it is manageable. I could get lost in there for quite some time just perusing the stocked shelves. There is always a ton of Philadelphia themed knick knacks,  which are my favorite, being that I live in Center City! There is honestly something for everyone.  I always find unique gifts for any occasion here.  

The jewelry is really well made and the price point is not horrendous either.  I have several pieces that I wear all the time, they are well made and I always get complimented on them everytime I wear them.

If you are ever stuck with a gift idea, this is the place to go, or just a place to go and kill sometime. I'm confident that you will find somethign to capture your attention here.

Where ELSE would one--COULD one-- go in Center City for tastefully-curated lingerie? Mona's store has been there forever, and would be easily overlooked if not for her eye-catching window displays. I first came into Coeur as a teenager, with a poor understanding of what a proper bra should do, or even what size I was. The first fitting was fraught (should they be that high? that shape? that direction?) but Mona's got incredibly sharp eyes for individuals' body shapes and sizes. Her great taste in flirty colors and supple textures is just icing on the cake. I've brought friends and boyfriends here, ranted at sports-bra-wearing-acquaintances to visit, and most recently brought my older sister by for her first-ever fitting. She went from a quadruple-boobed, self-proclaimed C-cup to being more supported than a suspension bridge in a beautiful, F-cup Chantelle hand-picked by Mona and fitted with precision and care. The two bras we picked out (one daytime, one boyfriend-time) ran $130, and for the comfort and confidence that a good bra gives you, it was worth every penny AND the money we spent on celebratory wine afterwards. Thanks, Mona!

I had a really good meal here. The buffalo sweetbreads were delicious, the beer list was excellent, and the service was great too. I also love that they have outdoor seating because there is not nearly enough of that in South Philly. This place is a great addition to the neighborhood.
SO awful. They are so rude on the phone, not worth my time. Call elsewhere!

This New England location appears to be permanently closed due to structural damage that the owners refused to fix. That's what I gathered from a City of Philadelphia Department of Licenses and Inspections sign on the front door several months ago. Sorry, folks, but we liked it too.

A great place to relax between visits to the many historical sites in this area! Had a nice inviting feel to it!
 We started with the Arancini (Fried risotto balls)! They were excellent! Crispy on the outside with a gooey cheesy center served with a delicious marinara sauce! Our fried chicken sandwich was also very good! Could have been the secret spices and fried pickle!
 The Bavarian Hefeweizen(My Better Hef) was perfect for a warm humid day in Philadelphia!
 Our waitress,Julia,was friendly and engaging!
 One note on the bathrooms! It's several small individual rooms,not stalls,so don't worry about someone of the opposite sex asking if they can borrow some toilet paper
 Would definitely come back!
Excellent!!! Went for restaurant week with the whole family. Tried almost everything on restaurant week menu. Love everything except the crispy rice. The service is awesome. Will definitely go back.

This place is NUTTY but it's my local beer distributor so I've got to represent. The owners are really nice, and we want to convince them to get a punch card...every time you get something dry cleaned you get a punch, five punches get you a free case! 
I doubt they'll do it. But I can dream.

Great beer selection but can tend to be a little pricey.

I've ordered from here several times, and each time the delivery driver has called me to say how shitty the traffic is getting to my place. Today takes the cake, as he just left my order on the first floor of my building after calling to tell me the drive was "awful."  Hey guess what? Don't deliver to places you don't want to drive. I'm less than 15 minutes from this place. The food is great but this particular driver is completely intolerable. Never again.  So bad that I registered for Yelp to post this.

DRINKS: 

Stopped by for some summer early evening cocktails on a Saturday.  First impressions, awesome decor... I felt like I was in a different era.  Wide selection of cocktails, typical mixology prices ($10 - $13), and a SUPER knowledgeable bartender who really knew his shit.  The Conquest of Gaul was great as was the Old Fashioned (up to my standards).  Maybe one of my new Philly haunts, and that says somethin.

I've been coming to Xfinity Live since it opened a few years ago. It's a great concept at a perfect location. Really, why did it take Philly so long to open up something like this? The best thing about Xfinity is that you have so many options for food and drinks. On weekends there is usually live music on the outdoor stage and inside there are sometimes live bands and djs mixing it up. Why only 3 stars? This place tends to get really crowded and sometimes overrun with 21 year olds who can't hold their liquor. The bathrooms tend to be on the gross side. I suggest stopping by Xfinity before or after hitting up a game or concert at one of the venues. If you show your concert ticket/game stub they usually don't charge you for cover.

I was in the mood for Korean food and needed to meet up with a friend downtown, so we decided to try Miga, a fairly newish Korean restaurant in Center City. We had to wait briefly for a table, and then were taken to a semi-private room off of a raucous main room. In another situation I might have felt exiled, but we had a lot of catching up to do and the private room was nice. We started with vegetable vegetable tempura, presented in a tall fried vegetable tower of a thicker-than-average cut. I especially love the zucchini tempura. 

Before our main course arrived, the waiter brought this amazing selection of pickles. I'm still thinking about the hot pickled garlic scapes. mmmm. I know I should get more creative, but I had a craving for Bim Bim Bop, and so that's what I ordered, in a steaming bowl. The soft fried egg arrived on the side, with chili sauce, which I promptly dumped all over everything. I loved the mushrooms, and such pickles with each different bite.

Miga is not BYOB, and more expensive that some other Korean restaurants in the neighborhood with similar food quality. But if you'd like to be waited on and feel a little fancier, give it a try.

Reliable local pub.  Comfy neighborhood feel, HD TV's and respectable food (thumbs up for the sweet frittes and Charlie's salad).  Beer selection is pretty meh - they consider Guinness and Hoegaarden "premium" - but overall a good choice to watch a game or for a laid back night with friends.

I have been to many studios in Philadelphia and this is one of the best.  They have a great mix of classes, are always helpful in class, but most importantly it is extremely welcoming for all experience levels.  The owners are very involved and created a culture where they try to get to know the students and have created a great community.

Blue Corn is just the best. We always take guests from out of town  here because it's so consistently good and the staff is so welcoming. They always have interesting specials if you want to try something new. The spicy margarita with the cilantro and jalapeños is phenomenal. Get one of everything.

One of my go-to options when I'm at HUP, CHOP or Abramson.  It's a million times better than hospital food and it's inside of Abramson, so location can't be beat and it's better than the old Potbelly's across the street (RIP), but that's about it.  With the new(ish) cafeteria 1 floor up and the HUP cafeteria, it's hard to say this place is worth it, but in a pinch, it works.

Pros:

The sandwiches and salads are solid and fairly filling.  Similar to other Gia Pronto locations.  They're hearty and sustainable and I've found that they have a wide enough selection depending on how you feel and the reason for your visit.

Food is served hot and made-to-order (sort of - paninis are premade but grilled-to-order).  

Seating is spacious and plentiful most of the time but I wish they had more regular tables and less bench seating and other sort of "gathering" space.  

The menu is probably the most expansive of all hospital-based options except maybe the HUP Cafeteria. 

Cons:

Prices are a little high for lunch, but a captive audience helps.  If you're going for something cheaper, the plentiful food trucks less than a block (or 2) away are a good alternative.

After a rush, this place can use a good cleaning.  It's not dirty, but it ends up being a little messy.  

Other notes:  Service is fine and standard for this type of establishment.

Conclusion:  It works for why you're there.  The many stations are a little annoying at first, but for a quick bite or salad it does the trick at an okay price.

After having a tough week at work- I decided to make an appointment for a facial. Still had some credit left on a gift cert so I figured why not? I had a very easy time scheduling an appointment online for a revive facial with Victoria. And boy was I glad I did! From the time I was greeted on the second floor and given my robe and locker til the time my appointment was over- it was such a pleasurable experience. Everyone was so nice and Victoria was amazing. Not only was I able to relax during the facial- but my skin was glowing for days afterward. I highly recommend.

This Holiday Inn is great.  Great location and friendly staff.  The bed was comfortable and the room was clean.  The breakfast is good as well.  Good value for the price.  I recommend staying here when visiting Philadelphia!

3.5 stars. I'll round up.

I work at DU library, and saw this truck numerous times. Me and my coworker were debating when we should try it out. It was a late summer day we ended up going, there was only 1 guy working. We walked by other times and saw 2 people... so not sure what the normal number of workers are. 

There was one person ahead of us. We took the time to review the menu. Decided to go 2 beef pho, 1 chicken banh mi, 1 beef banh mi. The issue i found was that it took a really long time for our order to come up. We both were freaking out a bit because we only take 30 minute lunch breaks... and we already waited 15 minutes for food (most likely because only one worker). Pho broth should essentially be simmering non-stop during lunch hours, and boil water ready for pho noodles to be cooked. But that's just me...He did have to cook the meats for the banh mi which (chicken) takes longer since it is fresher and "to order". 

The prices were reasonable for what we got. The pho was surprisingly pretty good. The broth had some nice flavors and there was adequate amount of beef. I had more meat balls and brisket than raw beef (tai) shown in other people's picture. Perhaps they changed it up a bit? It came in a big gulp-esque styrofoam cup which made it easier to eat. The banh mi was a miss for me. It wasn't a true vietnamese hoagie. It was a fusion of korean bbq sauce marinated meat---which is a bit too sweet. The other parts of the banh mi were pretty good though. I'd suggest trying a lemon grass vietnamese inspired marinade. My friend also agreed that the banh mi meat were sweet. But she also agreed that the pho was pretty good. 

Flash forward another few weeks, my friend went again for the pho--and it seemed they switched the pho bowl to a smaller but wider styrofoam cup. Unfortunately, she said the quality went down a bit. 

I'd really love for this truck to succeed. It gives a better option than the other grease trucks in the area.

I don't work at Drexel anymore, but when i'm in the area anytime soon in this colder winter, i'll be sure to check out the pho.

I got a membership 3 months ago, my first massage was with Steve and it was great. I wanted to try a few therapists to see who I liked best. The second month the massage was mediocre at best, she didn't seem to care about what she was doing at all. The third month was a very strange experience, the therapist was making strange noises that made me very uncomfortable, I had to tell her she was hurting me 3 times, I didn't ask for a deep tissue but that's what I got. I actually couldn't wait for it to be over, not at all relaxing. That was actually the last straw so I want to cancel my membership but I can't find any info on how to do so on the site.

The room was similar to the room in the Westin DC City Center and Westin Grand.  So this must be the Westin's new theme, which I approve.

The room was a perfect size, casual and modern and in really good condition.  The dcor was soothing.  There was a HD LCD flat panel with tons of HD channels.  The room was well lit.  The work are had a nice sized desk with plenty of outlets for charging laptops, mobile phones, cameras, etc.    

The Bathroom was large and roomy enough for two and came with the standard Westin Heavenly bath products.  There was a small boo boo.  There wasn't the full compliment of toiletries when I arrived, but a note for the very nice housekeeper fixed that.

There is high speed wireless for $13 but it was inconsistent.

This Westin does not have a Club Lounge or a pool but there a nice sized gym with newer equipment.  There is a business center somewhere on site.  The hotel valet parks, but there is the chance that you can find street parking or a surface lot or self parking within  the Liberty Place complex.

There is a restaurant on site, but it didn't appear to be to inspiring.

Since my last stay here, this property has greatly improved and is definitely my number one choice for hotels in Philly.

I hate to say this, but it would appear that Stiletto is no more. A recent glimpse at its windows while walking by revealed the sad news as an 'available space' sign now hangs prominently in front of the blinds. I will fondly remember the day when, after just recently moving into CC I stumbled into their shoe boutique, the SALE sign prompting me onwards, to find myself with a pair of Brian Atwood wedges in hand, a steal at $70 in cash. After a mad dash back home for the required amount (only four blocks away!), I walked away gleefully with my spoils. *sigh* I can only pray to the fashion gods that it will somehow remain a designer shoe store in the end.

Best lamb and pork tacos in Philly (and beyond), no contest.  And the soup (rice, drippings broth, chickpeas) is divine. Horchata is terrific too.  But I only go when they are not slammed. Otherwise, the place is packed, and not in a pleasant way, the tables and chairs are too close together, and the smell of table disinfectant is intense.  But when there is time and space to enjoy, it's the best.
I've been to Rally Coffee a few times and loved it. Nice people and reallu good coffee and drinks!!

To hell with this place, doesn't deserve any stars. My wife bought me a 2014 Dodge Charger from this place as a Christmas present in 2016. On June, 12, 2017 I noticed excessive noise when accelerating so I took it into the dealership. Come to find out that noise I heard was due to there being a hole in the exhaust due to rust. How does a car 3 years old with 25,000 miles on it rust to the point a hole is formed in the exhaust. Especially after the vehicle was inspected and certified by their "master technicians" and sold as being a top of the line vehicle "barley driven" and "nearly brand new". Would you believe they wanted to charge me $1300 dollars to replace my exhaust after me having purchased the vehicle not even 6 months ago, so I decided to speak to the owner,Tom hesson, in regards to the complete ripoff, whom politely told me to go fuck myself, and refused to swap out my vehicle or offer any assistance or conflict resolution. This business is filled with scammers and greedy individuals who will try their best to leech money and happiness out of your life. Please don't do any business with barbera. You will regret it!

La Viola was recommended by someone who's a Philly local. He said that many of his colleagues enjoy coming here, especially since it's BYOB. Be aware that this place is also cash-only, so make sure you have some cash in your wallet when you walk in. 

I liked the cozy atmosphere of this place. We were seated immediately. I saw that there were other large parties there, and they all seemed to be enjoying their dinner. 

We were given some complementary bread. It was really good! Warm and fresh. The bread dip (butter and some sort of soy sauce at the bottom) was pretty good. I'm a carbs person, so I had to stop myself from eating so much bread before the appetizers even came out. Okay, now onto the food. 

Grigliata Di Calamari: I was expecting fried calamari, not grilled calamari. I guess that's my fault, because I didn't read the menu clearly enough. Anyway, I didn't like this appetizer, because the calamari wasn't very tender...it was hard to chew. Not a fan. 

Antipasta Mista: We got a selection of cheese and meat. I didn't like this appetizer that much, either. The meat was too salty, and the cheese was a little too salty too. Not to my taste. 

Gnocchi Al Filetto Di Pomodoro: I usually love gnocchi, but this one was pretty bland. It was covered in tomato basil sauce, which I wasn't a fan of either. Not much to say here, other than I wouldn't order this again. 

Risotto Al Frutti Di Mare: This was okay. I liked the texture of the risotto, but wasn't a fan of the marinara sauce...too salty for me. 

Overall, I think the service was good. The waiters were nice, and responded to our needs. However, I won't be coming back here, because of the food. Wasn't a fan.

This hotel was very nice in the lobby, staff was super friendly. Room wasn't the cleanest, bathroom was a little dirty.The bed was very comfortable and the room felt cozy. The biggest benefit was the location and having a place for my car to stay. Overall had a nice stay for the weekend here, we would return.
Went here with my friends and I had a wonderful experience. I also loved my nail tech and he understood what I wanted and made sure I walked away happy!

I gave pats 1 star yesterday and today I tried genos, and I like pats slightly better, so genos gets half a star. Their meat is too thick and chewy, I didn't like it. Bread was hard, didn't have much flavor. So far only John's Roast Pork is the good one

Kite & Key is best enjoyed while sitting at one of the outdoor tables. I don't make it there as often as I should, considering their beer list (so nice to see a not-that-accessible Ninkasi!), but it's awesome to have them a short walk away in the neighborhood.

I've only had dinner here once. I stopped in for my brother's birthday last month with my mom and grandmother. While most of us enjoyed our meals, my grandmother wasn't a fan of her roast beef. She later opted to switch to a burger (also what I was eating) and liked that much better. Only thing to know: service was a little laggy, but otherwise, it was great.

Gotta make it back soon!

There is something about enjoying a product no more than 20 feet away from where it is being produced. Yards Brews are awesome anywhere, and even more awesome here at the brewery.  Take the free tour. Enjoy the free samples. Stay for some beers before. Stay for some beers after.  Shoot some pool.  Sit back, relax and enjoy the fruits of Yards' labor. Beer me.

I used to come here on occasion years ago and never really enjoyed the food. The drinks are good and the staff was nice but the food was always sub par for various reasons e.g. Not fresh, over/undercooked, etc. sad to say things have not changed   Honestly not sure how they stay in business.

We got to the hotel at approximately 5:30.  It is know 7:55 and I have not got a room yet.  The room was paid for with a reservation.  I went to the restaurant in the hotel to eat and told them I would be there. I came out of the restaurant a lot of people that were in the lobby waiting for a room weren't there.  I asked about the room again and said it appears that people who came in after me weren't here.  He told me that a lot of people left and went to the concert.  Also one of the elevators is broken.   I have stayed here previously and did not have any problems.   This is the worst hotel experience I have ever had.  I have never had to wait until after 8 am for a hotel room.

Brought my laptop in to get the screen replaced right before the holidays. They had the items in stock, and were able to take care of it in a matter of hours.  They were professional and their prices were reasonable, I would definitely recommend them.

Today I had one of the worst dining experiences of my life.  Upon waking up in my new apartment, my friend and I decided to explore the area and find a place to eat.  What a first seemed like a charming outdoor seating area soon became the place for a most dismal meal.  After waiting 20 minutes for anyone to come over and ask us if we wanted drinks, we ordered the eggplant pizza and the vegetable panini. After another 20-30 minutes we received our food.  The pizza was tasteless and the crust was inedible and a panini is NOT supposed to be served on a hoagie role.  The bread was almost too tough to chew through. Our waitress never checked in on us and we never so much as got a refill of water.  The man with the water pitcher passed us four times before we stopped him, then he acted surprised as a wasp flew out of the  pitcher and into my friends glass.  When it flew away he did not offer her a different glass and merely walked away.  I will not be returning and urge you not to either.

The food was amazing! 
Also, guys, Adel, the owner and chef was cooking for Pr. Nixon and Pr.Dwight D. Eisenhower! 
I enjoyed food a lot! Recommend!

I honestly wish I could make it to Fairmount more often for breakfast, especially since it has places like Rybread. Actually, it's probably a good thing that my neighborhood doesn't have an equivalent, because this is where I'd be spending my money every morning.
