In my case I'd be buying the OBX and a coffee every day. One is a delightful breakfast sandwich that's more satisfying than most full brunches in the area. The other is freshly brewed and rich, the way a bagel and sandwich shop should make it.

Good for happy hour, only okay for everything else.

I've been here for brunch twice, and the highlight of each brunch were the lump fries-- so so so good. They don't skimp on the sauce or the crab. Also their house made ketchup is to die for.
Otherwise, the crab omelette was so creamy that I could barely take more than one bite, and the steak and eggs were over cooked. The eggs Benedict was the best brunch item that I've tasted here, but it still doesn't compare to other brunch places around center city. 

Draft beers are half off during happy hour. That in addition to the lump fries make this place worth at least a quick visit. (But not for brunch)

Had a really excellent weekday lunch here. The short rib sandwich and truffle/mushroom pizza are both highly recommended. Love their beer selection as well. It seems that many of the negative reviews of this place come from City Tap House being really busy. I can't speak to what the place is like on a Saturday night, but Wednesday at 13:00 was great. My only complaint is that we paid more than expected on the beer. If you're just ordering by looking at the taps, or putting together a custom beer flight, the prices may not be clearly advertised. Our beer flight was $15 for four 6-oz pours, and one of the unlisted beers on tap ended up being $10 a glass. I can't really hold it against them though, since we didn't ask what they would cost.

Milkboy always has good happy hour specials.  I especially like their drink The Pharmacist.  

I've been there on different nights with friends and more recently through my bookclub.  It gets pretty packed between 5-6:30 on weeknights, but it has been pretty empty around 7 once the happy hour specials are over.  

Their drinks are good, I always find something tasty on their menu, and they have great service.  It gets pretty loud during happy hour, but that's nothing new for Philly.  Definitely recommend MilkBoy for drinks or happy hour!
Absolutely incredible collections! We spent almost four hours in here looking through all of the displays. Very unique and informative.

I was going to wait for another session or two to write a review but I couldn't wait to tell everyone how satisfied I am already with jus one session! It's been 3 weeks since I went to my first laser appointment for my underarms. I'm italian and always had thick underarm hair that would grow back a day after shaving, so I was very excited to get laser. After 3 weeks I noticed it's barely growing back, I haven't had to shave in one week and whatever does grow is very thin and light and unnoticeable, and this is with only one session!  I'm excited to see what it'll look after my 6th. Also I purchased the groupon so anyone who thinks you'll get a crap service, well you're wrong. I took my time researching companies to get my laser from and very happy I chose bare med spa. I am very happy, satisfied customer and now thinking about getting the brazilian!! Also this place is very Professional, clean, and you'll be in and out within 10 minutes!
Nice looking 20-30somethings crowd, but tap list is short and lacks character. Nothing that you wouldn't find at Applebee's. Disappointed...

Wanting to love this place but disappointed! The carne asada burrito - the meat was so charred it was inedible! The burrito so dry for $15 -chipotle is better!!! The empanada was ok - the crust had no flavor. I feel like I lost a lot of money on this food since it wasn't edible.
The Foodery in NoLibs puts prices in front of its beer.  Why can't you do the same?

Not a fan of Cafe Soho. 
There are flies buzzing around your head the entire meal. 
The bowls and cups need to be updated. It's just cheap stuff. The double fried chicken was all skin. Talk about overrated. 

I will not be going back.
Crepes are so damn easy and cheap to make yet I still come here on a regular basis to give them $8 in exchange for a satisfied craving. It's a problem.

I've been to all the heavyweights that Philly has to offer in the steak sandwich market.  Geno's ranks near the bottom among them.  Apart from its curbside tourist appeal and experiencing a side-by-side comparison with the rival Pat's, my advice is to skip this mediocre steak and indulge in some Jim's or Dalessandro's.

I really think the workers here are magicians because they are so incredibly fast.  We ordered 2 roti canai for apps & immediately the waitress turned around & it appeared.  Then we ordered our entrees - Mee Siam & Captain's Noodles - & before I even had a moment's rest, they came running out with our entrees.  So quick!  Some reviews talked about their terse service but I did not find it overbearing.  If anything I really liked that my water was kept constantly full.

1. Roti Canai = super yummy
2. Noodle dishes = cheap & huge (yay for leftovers that will be my dins tmr!!)
3. complimentary red bean soup dessert = yay!  a bit watered down but I still gulped it down

Come here hungry & ready for tons of malay food.  There's sushi but with so many choices -- I dont see why you would order off that menu.

I have been meaning to write this review for so long because that's how sweet they are! I went there for my manager's birthday and because I had been dying to try byreks. When I got there, the owner (I'm so sorry I don't know if I ever asked what your name was) asked me about my manager and I told him it was a surprise for his birthday. Once he heard that, he threw in aryan in the house! I thought it was just milk LOL (I was close)! Anyway, my manager Chim was so happy and even thought I made the byreks hahahaha I wish. Shumë falemimderit!!! :p

I'm only staying at this hotel because of a business trip nearby and this was the cheapest option. When walking in, I was slightly confused on where to check in, they layout is poor and I was told I couldn't check in until 3 PM. I wandered around the city until check-in, until having to re-check in and grab my key. 

This whole hotel looks like it has not been updated since the early 2000's. Everything smells old, feels old and is very dated. When trying to take a shower, the water was freezing cold for a good 5 minutes before barely warming up. Also, don't bring any food or drinks, you don't have a fridge. There also has been a cleaning cart outside my door all day, I have left and come back - still the cart in front of my door. Yet, they do not clean your room unless requested.... There also has been two bar glasses from the previous guest outside my door for two days. 

When trying to use the Wi-Fi for business, you are prompted to pay per hour or day -  it is an outrageous fee. When I reached out for why I have to pay for Wi-Fi, in 2018 - I was told I could pay to become a premium member to have Wi-Fi. That is ridiculous, even motel super 8 has free Wi-Fi. 

This place is fine for a night, but they do not go out of their way to make it a nice stay. I would avoid this place like the plague.

As the photos will tell you, both the space and the presentation of food are beautiful. And after the amazing meals I've had at Tinto, Garces Trading Co, etc., my expectations were admittedly high.  Unfortunately, JGD didn't live up to those expectations. 

The dishes are tapas style, so we ordered several small plates: the pecans, the crab croquettes, the lamb belly ravioli (a special), the potato cassoulet, and the pekin duck (their spelling, not mine).  The crab croquettes were the prettiest, several tiny little marble-sized balls of crab sitting on individual dollops of avocado sauce. Heck, at this place, even the bread and butter were pretty... but while the bread and butter were exceptional, none of the dishes wowed me. They were all good, just not great. Compared with my experience at Tinto, where each dish elicited oohs and ahs, these were more... ohs.  And while both places are expensive, I spent a lot more here, and not with the satisfying "it was worth every penny" feeling of a luxurious night of dining. 

To end on a happy note, we finished with two desserts. The first was a bourbon-themed one involving beignets (yummm), mousseline (never heard of this before but it was fantastic), and Makers Mark sauce to drizzle over both (loved it). The second was a chocolate-themed dessert including a Valrhona chocolate mousse (heavenly), a tiny chocolate cake, a cylinder of ice cream, and various other tasty accoutrements. :)

If I'm ever looking for a place to just have dessert in a gorgeous setting, I will not hesitate to return to JG Domestic!  But for a great meal, I'll stick to other Garces restaurants.

Oh how I love Ken Shin! 
I am never disappointed when I eat here. The service is great and the waiters are cheerful. I had the beef bulgogi and my boyfriend had the honey chicken (I think). Both were amazing. The food tasted fresh, as soon as I ordered I could here the beef sizzling in the back. 
Ken Shin is a cute small place for dinner or lunch when you don't want to deal with center city parking. The ice tea is also very tasty and homemade. 
Food is a little pricey for semi-typical Chinese food but it is worth it.

I was traveling to see a friend in Feasterville when I came upon this shop, nestled in a little cozy town away from the hustle of downtown Philly. Lisa was an amazing host, very bright and fun with the spunk I like from yarn store owners. Her selection of Quince & Co is impressive, and as I told her I haven't seen that much Quince outside of my shopping area in a very long time, not even at Looped in DC! I was hoping they would have more Madelinetosh but she said that Tosh was a new acquisition for the shop, so I'm hoping that will grow before I come back in the spring. I ended up buying enough of the Quince & Co in Lark for a stitch-block cowl for roughly $36, which is amazing. Overall I'd recommend this shop to any of my knitter friends traveling to the Philly area!

Maybe I've been spoiled lately, but I found Irish Pub just an ok place to go for lunch. 

The decor is a bit shabby in the day light (although maybe it looks better in the evening after a few drinks).  While others I was with seemed to enjoy their Shepard's pie, which was certainly a huge portion, the turkey breast sandwich I had was nothing special, and the fries that accompanied it seemed like they were dumped straight from the freezer to the frier.    

While it was not an expensive place to eat,  the value seemed to fit the quality of the experience. I do not see any reason for a return visit.

Three words:Corned beef special :-) I used to go here at least once a week when I lived in West oak lane and although I have tried other things and they are all just beyond delicious the CBS  Is the best of the best :-) I don't get here often anymore but when I get that hankering for a great hoagie I stop here. Thanks guys for always getting better and better.

Absolutely awful. Booked 2 rooms that were supposed to have two beds each. Called 4 times to ensure this because I know hotel booking employees can be incompetent. When we check in they say sorry only one bedroom available unless you upgrade to a garbage dirty suite that had to hot water for $75. No manager on duty, left messages with two managers and got no calls back. Horrible. Cannot believe this place !

These ingredients are so pure
They're Medicinal! No one explains how good u feel after eating at Gus's Stand!! Never disappointed, always Fresh off
The Charcoal Grill Chicken 
(Best in Center City) veggies homemade Falafel & hummus,
And Much Much more! Come
Try it- hiiiiiiiighly recommended-
its a Must! 
Especially Visiting Philly!!

Been to this location twice. Two horrible experiences with rude workers. The fact that their prices are criminal and their ice cream is PACKED with unhealthy ingredients is just the tip of the iceberg. Would go 0 stars if possible!

I went here with a party of 4 for restaurant week because the menu looked great. Though I'd been to Tavern on Camac a few times I'd never noticed this little restaurant tucked away underneath it. The atmosphere and decor are impressive. The bartender/waiter was very helpful and attentive. The food was fine, but nothing I'd brag about. I found it to be inconsistent in seasoning. I got the arugula salad for starters and it was very salty. The chicken satay looked good, but seemed to be lacking good seasoning - I was looking around for a salt shaker. Then I had the fried catfish with grits and squash. The fish portions were plentiful and perfectly cooked, but again I could have used a little more seasoning on the entire dish. For dessert I had the chocolate cake with bacon and peanut butter sauce. The peanut butter sauce made the dessert worth it! Don't get me wrong, the clearly homemade cake was good, but the peanut butter and bacon made it great! 

Others in my party were pleased with some courses and disappointed with others as well. Most of us decided we probably wouldn't go there again.

I'm right around the corner from the refuge and I love this place. It's a comforting departure from city life and it opens up a new world of wildlife and nature. The staff and contributors to the refuge have really done a great job with making the refuge an amazing experience. This place has a lovely variety of birds, reptiles and amphibians and some mammals. I can't say enough how much I enjoy visiting here.

This is a throwback review because I'm missing my favorite bar. 

I have such fond memories from this establishment. Their doggie decor, photos submitted over the years from actual good dogs, warms my heart. Of course only good dogs are allowed on the walls. Which, of course, is a trick question because all dogs are good dogs. If you think your special fur baby would add to the overall great vibe, entries are usually accepted in the fall and winners announced in Dec. I know I'll be anxiously waiting for my dog to look photogenic this year, in hopes she can earn her place. 

Drinks here are great. I really like their in-house cocktail creations. The bartenders (at least the few I've had) really knows their stuff and one even knows his hockey! Great conversations to be had at the bottom floor bar. There are three levels so explore them all! Top floor has games and is full of fun. 

Foodwise, honestly they have a really solid burger and delicious mac&cheese (best mac always uses gruyere. Martha Stewart said so). 

Ahhh nostalgia. Good Dog Bar, I miss you so and will be back.

Came here from recommendation from my boss. Inside this old historic city lies this modern hip wine bar restaurant like the ones you see in Hollywood.  After looking at the menu, I knew we should try it.  It was restaurant week so we opted for the 4course prix fix menu. I had the salad frizz, grilled shrimp, sea urchin pasta and cream brule. I was in heaven. The course was off the top awesome. I ordered white wine to go with the pasta. It was light and the uni was fresh and went really well with the squid ink pasta and crab meat.  I'll definetely have to come back here when I'm in phyli.
What a wonderful crew works here. Creative restaurant week offerings, beautiful AND delicious. Kudos to the owner and the staff for putting it out there for all to enjoy.

I visited Vedge for happy hour on a Friday night.  I border on vegan, but have a hard time giving up cheese (oops) though I am in the process - I first tried the Maitake steak wit which essentially to me was my first vegan Philly cheesesteak - very exciting - mushrooms with some sort of rutabaga puree for cheese which I will soon be googling.  I also had the fried Kimchee potato salad which was very tasty and the Korean seitan taco which was very spicy.  For dessert, I had the Yuzo custard/bruled coconut-miso pocky which had a lime flavor and was very good.  I will soon be returning to try other menu options but would highly recommend happy hour.  Also, I have a tree nut allergy and though I am sure I annoyed the bar staff, they assured me they take allergies very seriously.  Awesome place.

Had a nice dinner last night. Service was great, super friendly and knowledgeable about the menu and the inside dining was nicely done. Was a little bummed we could not sit outside, they have the outdoor area set up but all the tables were closed for some reason. Sushi was nice and fresh. Cocktails were strong. Would go back and check out karaoke too!

Sat at the bar and
Tried to order for a while.
Was ignored.  I left.

We love Cajun and gumbo and so decided to try this place for delivery today. For reference it took and 1 hr and 10 minutes to arrive, food was still relatively warm. 

As a personal preference we prefer pickle chips over spears, so we were excited to see them on the menu. 
Unfortunately they were soggy and so were fries, but the rest of the seafood platter was enjoyable! 

Gumbo was missing a kick, but we prefer things spicy - It did have plenty of okra and half a blue crab. The 
Cajun rice was good, and the corn which states (2) means you'll get one piece of corn cut into 2 pieces, no flavor really. The provided sauces helped. 

My partner doesn't care to order again, but I would.

Love the Terminal Market. Whenever I come to Philly on the weekend, I make sure to stop buy for a sandwich or some sweets. Great options for food- read to eat or buy to prepare at home. It is a must do in Philly.

This place was my first introduction to Philadelphia. Without knowing I would ever move to the city of brotherly love, I stayed here for a couple nights. Obviously, they have an eye for design. It's like a classier Jonathan Adler designed the place- multicolored busts of Ben Franklin in the lobby, a giant roaring fireplace, and kitschy designs on the inside of the elevators. They also provide animal print bathrobes, free yoga mats in the rooms, and maps with suggested running routes. Way to make this Colorado girl feel right at home! 

The lobby happy hour is such a great idea, and the bed was the comfiest hotel bed I may have ever slept in. We also got a pretty good deal on the room for the amenities. However, the next time I came out, the rate was much, much higher due to big city events that weekend, so keep your eye out! 

I now live in Philly, but suggested that my boyfriends family stay there when they visit, something they decided to do this week. I went with his mom to check in, with my Great Dane in tow. I knew this place was dog friendly, but they were amazing with him. He was up on his back legs hugging the valet and eating treats from numerous employees. It was so great! If you want to get extra points in my book, be dog friendly. Way to go, Hotel Palomar- I will continue to recommend your hotel to any out of town guests!

Philly Pet Care is great. Our dog is always happy to see Dave when he comes to pick her up and when we come back from our holidays she is always a happy girl. They are easy to work with and help accommodate ur schedule as required and within reason off course. I could not think of anyone else to leave our dog with. Finally the fact that she stays in a family environment is really important for us. They are always my first choice to leave our furry child with. Fully recommend it  to anyone.

One of my favorite hangouts!  Lovely corner spot, lots of windows (opened during beautiful days), and the staff loves my dog. :)

Good:
- Great beer (rotating elixirs)
- Great food (jumbo lump crab fries!)
- Happy Hour M-Sat 4-6p (half off drafts!)
- Outdoor seating available (dog friendly)
- Nice staff (even Rich - he just acts tough - I hope!)

Eh:
- Can get very loud inside if there's a party!
- Growler fills a touch pricey ($25)

Brace yourself. Beer Week is here.
The Classicos tacos are my favorite (hard shell, ground beef)

Another UPenn favorite. This small shoe store has more shoes than you think could fit in it. Their selection is amazing. Their prices are also reasonably priced. Where else can you find a $10 pair of shoes and a $100 pair in the same store?

They also keep up with the trends for us college folk. But they do have a good selection of walking and orthopedic shoes for the older generation. So if you are ever in the University City area and need a pair of shoes, The Natural Shoe Store is the place to go.

Fresh sushi; we had spicy tuna, salmon & avocado & special roll of the day which consisted of blackened tuna. Sadly, there is no substitution for brown rice.  Pricey. Drinks are good - can recommend the kono hito. The outdoor space and unisex bathroom sink both pleasant surprises.

This is without question the best BYO in the Passyunk neighborhood and one of the best in Philadelphia. It's been open for less than half a year and already it been a home run from day one! The food is amazing and super constant, it feels like you've adopted a Greek grandmother and she's cooking just for you. The manager/owner is extremely personable and is there every day and is amazing at what he does. The wait staff is also great, not a single complaint! It's in a great spot and I can't wait for the weather to warm up so they can open those huge windows. It's the kind of place that makes you want to go eat and hang out there everyday

Not only is Philadephia rich with history, but they make some awesome beers. We learned while hanging at the Irish Pol that the Yards Brewing Company has recreated beers made from the recipes of Thomas Jefferson and George Washington.

You can find Thomas Jefferson's Tavern Ale on tap at the Irish Pol, along with about 20 other fine beers on tap including many local brews. It has an APV of almost 8.0%, which is not only kicks a punch - but was perfectly smooth. 

No wonder the man was inspired to draft the Declaration of Independance, with a nice strong draft in hand. What better way to experience history than first hand, right? 

The bartenders here know a lot about beer, it's relatively inexpensive, not crowded and feels like a fun local bar.

I only used them a couple of times when I needed a part for my car and I know every mechanic in south Philly uses them they are good for the special parts that you need that pep boys and the others can't get you and can get it for you within a couple of hours the same day.
Came to replace an iPhone 5 screen.  Paid a little more but it seemed less shady than another place I've used in the past.  The guys were nice and the pricing was reasonable.  I will use them again.

Dr. Biscardi is amazing

She works hard to resolve your problems 

i get my new glasses and i am confident it will help me. 

Great service great Staff

I recommend her highly 

make your appointment today

g

There is nothing worse than being hungry 25 minutes before a flight starts to board.  

Open seating at this location, with the menus arriving with our server moments later after we sat down.  My partner ordered clam chowder and I ordered some sort of cheesy potato soup with lots of seafood in it.  About 5 to 8 minutes later the soup arrived.  The clam chowder my partner ordered was delicious but it was obvious it had been microwaved due to the signs of burnt soup residue around the edges of the soup bowl.  My potato cheese seafood thing was tasty, but luke warm and the potatoes were kind of hard and crunchy.  When our server came back to check on us, I asked her if she wouldn't mind microwaving my soup for another 30 seconds. She took my bowl and ten minutes later it came back so hot that she could barely hold it with her potholder. 

With my stomach growling and only 10 minutes left to eat, I poured my icewater into it, which brought it back to the lukewarm temperature that it was when I got it.  It feels  to me that the person in charge of warming my soup was being passive aggressive.  Can't prove it, don't know that for sure, but my intuition tells me that my sending my soup back to be microwaved for an additional 30 seconds was unacceptable. 

I felt disregarded, frustrated and don't think I would ever step foot in this location again.

Staff were friendly clean  attentive service was fast food was pretty good prices were good. I would definitely recommend this place. Wish there was a little more parking. But hey it was a nice experience anyway and that comes from a family of 5
Found a fish scale in my spicy tuna roll and a small piece of black plastic. Not quite sure how that got in there and I don't really want to know. I don't think I'll be going back there...

Stood in line for over one hour to try my first authentic philly cheesesteak and I'm sure glad I did!  SOOOO good that I wished I had ordered two!  Don't know if I would do it again but definitely a place to try while you're in town.
The fish hoagies are very good. I doffing like any of the other foods. I thought they were bland. I tried the fried rice and their vegetables.

Great spot! Best cheese as well! Lady at the front was so helpful and let us try so many different kinds of cheese. She was so friendly and kind. The smoked Gouda is amazing. So was the 7 sisters and shellbark. A must go for cheese lovers.

Had a really great time here. Our waitress was super helpful as we explored all the delicious items on the menu. My date and I both went with the rigatoni alla vodka and it was AMAZING. Topped off with a really nice red blend. Definitely worth a visit and I'll be returning soon to order the exact same thing.

I've been going here for 8 months.  The staff is great and I like the classes I've gone to.  It's never crowded which is awesome and the equipment is in good condition.  Overall I've been pleased.  

HOWEVER, now that I need to cancel my membership to move away, it's clear that it's going to cost me at least an extra month of membership because even though the CONTRACT says that you give notice to the gym itself, they hand you a card for an outside billing company to handle cancellations.  So, buyer beware.

We had a good first experience at Pizzeria Vetri. The staff was really attentive and pleasant, as well as super nice to our little dog (bonus points for having dog-friendly outdoor seating!).

The pizzas were good. Their crust was very thin and crispier, so if you like your pizza with more elasticity, I'm not sure if their pizza will hit the spot as far as texture is concerned. That's probably my only real "complaint." The sauce was good and the mozzarella was quite salty (not a downside at all) on the margherita pizza. We tried the seasonal polpo pizza which was loaded with octopus. Overall an interesting pizza that I wouldn't be opposed to ordering again. The meatballs were a little too charred on the outside and dry on the inside, but their sweet red sauce was tasty enough to power me through. 

Overall, Pizzeria Vetri was a positive experience. We were excited to find a pizza place with a dog-friendly outdoor area since we were traveling with our pup. The staff was wonderful, and I enjoyed their pizzas. We'll definitely be back at some point. Especially if we're craving some pizza and are staying in the area!

I've never been a Starbucks fan but had to settle for it this morning. I waited in line for 10 minutes to place my order, placed it paid for it then waited for another 15 minutes to be made. When I saw people who were in line behind me getting their order I asked about it and was told they're out of the skinny mocha.  
This is horrible customer service!

As everyone calls it "Whole Paycheck".
Ridiculously over priced.  
Waited 25 minutes to pick up an on line order, but apparantly the person in charge of that was on break, then was told to call Amazon for a refund, which I still haven't received, and spent over an hour on the phone trying to get it issued.
An absolute nightmare.
Turned me off of Whole Foods and Amazon for good.

Ordered my food at 800pm didn't get it untill 1045 worst service ever bunch of people walking in and out of the kitchen that I don't even think work there never and I mean never will I ever go back to this place EVEChicken Wings Thai WingsR!!!!

The only real problem with this place is their beer (not happy hour specials) is a bit overpriced. You can get almost the identical beer for a dollar cheaper at Devils Den. I love P.O.P.E. but I'm going to have to keep my patronage limited to when they have their happy hour specials. They should really have a few decent beers in the $5-$6 price range, last time I went I think every single beer was $6 and up. Waitstaff is always awesome, never had a problem. Great for a beer but not a place most people can afford to drink at for a couple of hours with friends. Still worth a stop in and try the burger- one of the better ones I've had in this city.

*Update: I really hate to do this to you POPE but the last two times we've been there, the service was lacking. It was the same server (short-hair blond) and while not downright rude, she's not friendly and last time we had to go out of our way to flag her down for water. I ordered the Cheasapeake Fries, which are usually on point but the fries came out so overseasoned that I could only eat the crabmeat off the top. I should have had them sent back If just to change out the fries but I figured if getting water refills is such a hassle then forget it. Their burger is always a great go to but after the lackluster service and subpar Cheasepeake fries, my friends and I are going to avoid this place for awhile. Hopefully they get back to the great spot they were very soon.

Very friendly sales and service staff can answer any questions you have. These guys have taken great care of me and my bikes on several occasions and even remember me by name when I come in. I have dealt with other shops in the past but will use BB going forward. If they don't have something in stock they will bring it in for you to try, check this place out
Absolutely delicious! Every dish was extremely tasty, drinks were great, service is exceptional! Highly recommended!

This place just opened and we wanted to give it a shot! We are glad we did! A great Indo-Chinese spot in center city. 

We ordered the paneer 65, which is absolutely phenomenal. It's to die for! A great dish.. we wish we got one each instead of sharing! 

For entrees we did the Singapore noodles and the vegetable Manchurian. 

The noodles were ok, maybe just not our cup of tea.. but the Manchurian was out of this world. Finally a place in the city that does this dish well! Everything about it was perfect! Great flavors! 

Finally to wrap things up we got the fried ice cream and this was very good!

All in all a great meal where we would definitely return. We would get the paneer again for sure and the Manchurian, but would try a new dish besides the noodles! 

Our server (Kevin) was the greatest. Was funny and informative. His way of taking care of customers should be the industry standard. Very nice and always smiling. He made the experience even better and made great suggestions!

I came here with my family just for dessert and drinks but I was very impressed! We ate the vanilla cake and it was so delicious. Seriously, we ended up ordering a second one after devouring the first one in seconds. Safe to say I'd highly recommend. The drinks were also great too. My whisky came with a nice large piece of clear ice that's both practical for the drink and aesthetically appealing. The atmosphere of the restaurant is very chic and has an awesome view of the city. I would definitely want to come back and actually have dinner here some time.

A little over priced but I say the SHAKES are DELICIOUS and worth the visit!! 
I'm generally not a fan of milk shakes.  They are too heavy for me and think the taste does not justify the calories.  But the Peanut Butter Shake here,   YUMMY YUMMY YUMMY.  Hits just the spot.  In and Out shakes move out of the way!
Burger are good but it is an extra dollar or two than I'd want to spend.  But I'll bite the bullet.  Let that "Milkshake bring all the boys to the yard," shack or the corner of samson and 20th.

Bruh... when I tell you... this food SLAPPED... it slappeD. Best Caribbean food I've had here. My friend and I shared two small platters (curried goat + jerk chicken with rice and peas, Mac & cheese, and collard greens) and it was delicious. Good portions, great flavor, and such good service!

We brought a big group here for my friend's birthday and had a blast.  The food was excellent!  Not huge portions but really high quality food, which is how I'd prefer it.  I had the penne vodka and tiramisu.  The service was very friendly and helpful, but rather slow (that's why it got bumped to 4 stars).  In there defense though, we were a group of like 16 people.

Note: It is BYOB which is awesome and saved our group a lot of money.

Delicious philly cheesesteaks.  YUM!  My coworker brought me to Tony Luke Jr's so I could have a true philly cheesesteak experience and it was well worth the short walk from the office.  Like most awesome food places - it is a no-nonsense, small restaurant.  Big menu up top, make your choices and order at the cashier.  I got the philly cheesesteak with sharp provolone.  Delicious.  Their onion rings are fantastic too!  Melt in your mouth goodness.

Eh. They have a good amount of super-cheap furniture in here, but a lot of it is severely broken or stained. Why accept this stuff as donations? I guess someone's buying it, but you would do better checking the "free" listings on Craigslist than actually paying money for this stuff.

Somehow on my birthday I always wind up in very ordinary, touristy, overpriced places that I would never go any other night of the year.  This year, the Dark Horse hosted me and a dozen friends.  When we were wandering the streets trying to figure out where to go, my only criteria was that I didn't want us to all be smooshed into one or two booths or spread out all over the place in a small room full of 100 people.  i wanted to be able to mingle with everyone.  Someone mentioned that the Dark Horse had a lot of space, so we headed there.  And what space they had!  We wound up with an entire room pretty much to ourselves all night.  And in that room was:  A bar with a bartender, dart boards, a big fancy TV, windows, plenty of seats, and room to breathe.  It was perfect.  I don't think I'll be hanging out there on a regular basis, but I've filed the Dark Horse under "Roomy" in my mental card catalog of bars.  It is pricey, though.  Paying more than $3 for a Lager is criminal, and this place charges $5.  Eek.  $7 for a shot of Jim Beam?  I happen to know that you can buy a whole bottle for $15.  Anyway, this labyrinth-esque bar may come in handy in the future, and I may chalk up those $5 Lagers to equaling $3 beer + $2 personal space sustainability.  I can live with that.

I have a history of infidelity..... when it comes to my stylists.  For a very long time, I would not stay faithful.  I was convinced there was always someone out there who was better.  Any you know what???  I was RIGHT!  Finally...  I found Cynthia.  She gets me.  She gets my hair and what to do with it.  She knows how to restore my red locks to their original and true fiery nature and she knew to talk me out of it when I was foolishly considering bangs.  Cynthia is what a stylist should be: talented, creative, forever-learning, honest, and a good listener.  And she's just a fun and fantastic person to boot.  So, to say that I am thankful to have found Cynthia would be an understatement.  Since my first appointment with her, I have been truly satisfied and I have never felt the need to look anywhere else.

North Third is the place to go to if you're looking to have a couple drinks and grab dinner in Northern Liberties.  It has an identity crisis.  The restaurant is decorated with Mexican and Americana art.  It doesn't really go together, but that's the beauty of this joint.  There is always something new to see on their walls.

The drinks and food menus are fantastic.  There have a nice selection of bottles and rotating drafts.  They have lots of different options for vegetarians and meat eaters.  I had the Asian Spiced Tuna Burger.  It had greens, wasabi mayo and ginger dressing on it, which really complimented the tuna.  The fries that came with the burger were spiced just how i like them.  My entire meal tasted super fresh.  I'm very happy this bar is in my neighborhood :)

AMAZING!!!!!  This is in my top ten favorite restaurants I have ever tried.  Every dish was PERFECT.  The combination of flavors in each entree/appetizer was divine.  while the menu does not have a laundry list of items, the items that are on the menu are more than enough....you just cannot go wrong!  

I really like the ambiance of the restaurant.  It has a cozy feel to it, and definitely a place you can go for a romantic dinner or with friends and family.  the service was very good.  

I cannot wait to come here again!  You must try it!  (make sure you bring cash- they do not take credit card)
Service was poor, food was decent. Fun vegan option, but not worth the waiting. My food was served ten minutes before my friends food. Hopefully it was an off day.

Got $20 and tryna have a good old fashioned time? Well this little gem in Fishtown is the place to go! Just about 2 blocks above the El train, Barcade looks like it would be a hole in the wall night club but as soon as you step through the courtyard you're in for a treat. It's got beer and food options but like the name suggests it has a delightfully nostalgic arcade full of games from the 80s and 90s. There's two token machines (one in the front and one in the back) so you need not worry about filling your fanny pack with quarters! There's a 4 player pac man table in the front of the bar and needless to say that and Ms. Pac Man have the longest line but all and all it's worth it. Definitely going to be a frequent visitor

Excellent food truck. Definitely a nice change from the ubiquitous bacon egg and cheese trucks and halal carts. The food is fresh, reasonably priced, and cooked to order. Big portions too. I've tried the orange peel chicken and the chili garlic chicken, both over jasmine rice. Both are delicious and highly recommended.

Always loved The Village. Moved out of the area about 5 years ago, met up with an old friend this morning and decided to stop in for breakfast. It hasn't changed - nice atmosphere, comfy booths, friendly staff, good coffee and food. Price is right, too. $14 for the two of us for breakfast - can't beat it. Left with full bellies and didn't break the bank.

My friend and I both got the hot chocolate. We were charged $4 each for what was just cheap hot chocolate mix (probably Swiss Miss) in frothed milk. What a rip-off. The only reason I'm giving 2 stars is that this place had nice ambiance.

Very cool speakeasy with a great vibe and one of the best drinks I've ever had (portmanteau (sp)).  The only slight con is that the bar tends to get crowded and wait times between drinks may be slightly longer than expected.

Nice and cozy interior, and it usually doesn't get too loud unless it's super busy. The coffee is a bit more acidic for my tastes, but I enjoy the chai lattes. The pastries and bagels are also pretty good. My only complaint is that their items are a little overpriced, in my opinion.

A complete waste of time. If you have simple taxes and can follow directions I would use tax software. They will charge you $200+ to fill out your tax's. The staff is unfriendly, unskilled, and they can't even keep an appointment or book the tax preparer you were assigned. If they can't manage an appointment or staff, do you really want them doing your taxes? 

I had an appointment with someone on a Thursday night. When I arrived 15 mins early, they told me he wasn't there that day. So they can bait and switch with no notification..what can you do. I was assigned someone else but that was fine. As I was waiting someone come in and asked to see a tax preparer but had no appointment. I decided to get a quick bite to eat and came back at 7pm.  After about 15 minutes, the walk-in got someone to look at her taxes. I waited 30 minutes more. A total of 45 minutes before I decided to reschedule for the next available appointment with who I originally had my appointment. So 30 minutes later I get a email confirmation and it's not who I scheduled my appointment with. I have two days to do my own taxes. Thank H & R block for making it so frustrating to use your service I am going to do it myself. You saved me lots of money. 

If they can't even managed your appointment or staff, do you really want them doing your taxes?!

I will never purchase your tax software too. So done with H&R Block.

AMAZING. EAT HERE, NOW!

We saw this place on yelp and decided to give it a try. Fantastic experience. Friendly, prompt service. The food was perfect. Imaginative, well spiced, generous portions and all around excellent flavor. We will definitely come here again!

Oh god I'm so fat right now.  Hours after eating at Tony Luke's and I'm still full. We stopped in before a trip to Target because I was ravenous and shopping on an empty stomach makes me bitchy. 

The atmosphere & service were ok.  It kind of smelled like chemicals in there, I'm assuming from cleaning supplies. The food was really good though.  We started out with the Tony Luke's fries. The fries themselves were just average, but were smothered in cheese and bacon (2 things that will make ANYthing taste good) - decadent fatness. I then had the Italian Roast Pork sandwich: thinly sliced pork, sharp provolone, deliciously bitter broccoli rabe...YUM.  I ate the whole thing since I wouldn't be going straight home afterwards & couldn't take it with me.  I was painfully full.

Expect great sandwiches, not great atmosphere and you won't be disappointed.

Weird you can't get a cheesesteak at philly airport in timely manner 
Normally if you're anywhere in the city they have they steak going at all times and the cook puts it on the roll and out it comes (pats Genos anywhere even Tony Luke's at ball park)
The franchise at airport has two or three people moving at a snails pace and make each steak to order in about 16 minutes
Really silly
While people wait in line the cashier stares at the cook who stares at her

This Stephen Starr restaurant is not eye catching in South Street hustle and bustle. Upon entering we were greeted by very friendly and courteous staff. This place is dark at the center area but there are lamps on the booth seatings also by the kitchen. I saw a diner using the flashlight from the phone to read the menu.

Service was great. Our waiter Mike was very friendly and knowledgeable about the food and drink and helpful in accommodating my gluten free friend.

Scallops: you must get this. Beautiful presentation, looks like an art. It's thinly sliced swimming in buttermilk . Topped with a dot of soy. The soy is salty so after I removed it it's more enjoyable.

Complimentary rice crackers is served with butter and bean spread. Light and addicting 

Japanese eggplant: perfectly cooked, not mushy at all served with crunchy pistachio. It's very good and well seasoned.

 Peekytoe crab with poached pollock : very good. It's a light entree, perfectly cooked fish. Highly recommended.

Crispy Pork belly: not too fattening, tender meat, melts in your mouth.

Dessert: definitely get the rocky road, it's a cold pudding with sweet marshmallow. The squash panna cotta is served with pepitas and cocoa nibs, not very sweet.

I had an AMAZING experience at Andre Richard Salon today... SO amazing that I couldn't wait to write a review!  Like other reviewers mentioned, the atmosphere is spot on.  Attractive and clean, but not overly ritzy or pretentious.  I had a cut and color with LB, who was very sweet and pleasant to talk to.  It was my first time coloring my hair, and she did a great job explaining every step to me and recommending products that would keep it looking great.  Oh yeah, and the color is beautiful!  During my shampoo and haircut, two other stylists mentioned how great the color turned out, which made me feel like a rockstar.  =)  She also gave me a nice blowout -- Unfortunately it was raining profusely when I left, so it didn't last long, but I have to admit that my hair has never looked so good after running through a storm.

I usually hate getting my hair cut (it just takes up such a big chunk of the day), but I really look forward to going back!

Claire and Ari made my shoot an absolutely amazing experience! I had the time of my life and I left feeling 10000x more confident in myself. I am so happy I decided to do this not only for a gift for my fiance, but also for myself. I will definitely be doing another shoot soon!

My fiancé and I had a great experience here picking out wedding invites! Jessica was beyond helpful, patient , and really made the process enjoyable. She emailed/ spoke with us frequently, answering every question promptly. They have a great selection and will work with you to fit your budget. The quality of the invites is beautiful, we were both pleased with the final print. I highly recommend The Papery!

Some folks are wary of trying new Chinese restaurants for fear that the food will not agree with them; however, whenever the opportunity presents itself, I will scan a menu and if the offerings do not suit my preferences, I don't bother.  Since Green Garden was within decent distance to the train station, one evening I decided to give them a chance because I noticed they also offered an extensive selection of sushi.  From the looks of it (and the number of lab coats I saw walk in), their clientele consists mostly of Jefferson students and employees.  Although I do not fall into that category, I did make conversation with some students who were waiting for their take-out.  I settled on an egg drop soup and sesame chicken, both of which met my expectations.  My only beef, however, is with the flightiness of the staff--they seemed a tad overwhelmed with all the calls coming in while trying to serve the customers who are eating in.  That being said, I'd be willing to come back and try their sushi even though I wasn't in the mood for it that particular day.

Last week I got two flat tires on 95. After waiting nearly an hour for my roadside assistance company to come tow me, a Center City truck pulled behind me and offered their help. They took me to their shop @ 2nd and Callowhill where within an hour, ordered me 2 new tires. While I was waiting, Tony gave me a rental car to pick my kids up from school. By the time I got back, my Jeep was ready. They saved me from a total nightmare. Also, the receptionist Valerie was so nice. I'd recommend this place because of the  convenient location and helpful staff.

I LOVE Mack and the Artist Haus.  First of all, just awesome haircuts; great welcoming beautiful environment full of plants and art.  What I particularly love is that I've had hair EDUCATION over time: Mack has taught me how to take care of my hair in a way that jives with my minimalist and frugal lifestyle, and now my hair has been brought to a level of health and vitality that I have never had before.  I also so appreciate how Mack cares about being a proactive member of the East Falls community, actively supporting the vitalization of our nascent business corridor in our neighborhood.  Lifetime customer here!

There's a pizza place connected that I'd like to come back to next time I'm in the city. Walking in it was packed and we had to squeeze through but that's the way it should be if it's a good spot! We started with the prosciutto app with seasonal fruit and it was delicious! (A little more would have been perfect). For my entree I got the pesto pasta with shrimp and tomatoes, it was amazing! My friends and I had a great night and I can't wait to come back! 

*BYOB*
*Gluten Free options available!*

This running trail is near and dear to my heart. 

Many openings to this trail from the center city area and the trail leads to Kelly Drive so you can train with any distance without getting bored. The views are great and a ton of people out on a nice day -- walkers, runners, bikers, or just people lounging about soaking up nature.

Friendly staff, but terribly slow service. We sat for 20 minutes in an empty restaurant before anyone gave us menus. Standard American fare; as a vegetarian the only real option was an oily vegetable pasta. Vegans would be limited to the salad bar. I imagine steak-lovers would be better suited.

Before I review the food I need to mention how wonderful the folks who work here are. One Saturday morning the bf and I went to get breakfast from Hot Bagel (next door). I had the dog with us so I stayed outside and sat on Hostaria's front step. I was carrying a wristlet that day and when I got up to leave I left it there. I called everywhere I was that day and could not find it. In a last ditch effort before I gave up on my search I called the restaurant. They had it and it turns out they were searching everywhere to get in touch with me. So to return the good karma I decided to have dinner here last weekend. It's much bigger than it looks from outside and has an adorable back patio. It very much feels like you are eating in your own backyard because of the ambiance and the comfortable servers. The food is delicious as well. The bread they bring out would be fantastic if it were from Sarcone's but oh well. I had the pesto shrimp linguine and it was so good. BF had the sweet potato gnocchi, also good. For an app we had mussels. The menu itself is small but the list of specials every day is impressive. We had to have our server repeat it twice! The place is fantastic. I highly recommend it!

Looking at the reviews of the past year for this restaurant makes me suspicious that they have been written by competitors vs. ordinary folks!  The food has always, always been decent.  It's a buffet so you cannot expect a plate cooked JUST for you!  The options are amazing with enough choices to feed a "picky" family.  It is true they  have had to cut back on some of the selections - that's to keep the cost in check.  Prices for everything, everywhere have risen [markets, gas, appliances, doctors, etc., etc.] so of course they have had to make difficult choices to keep their prices reasonable.  Sushi selections are more than plentiful at dinner as is the overall choices of food from fish to meat to vegetarian.  Having written this, I plan to go there again tonight - yum yum!

My friends and I used to go to the South Street Diner constantly, but it's changed a lot since they've changed owners over the past year or so. The food's still pretty good and they have some fun breakfast specials (stuffed French toast, mmm) but overall it feels less like a diner and it's too expensive now. At least they kept the same waitresses.

I've been living in a Premier Access property in Northern Liberties since the end of January with 2 friends. It is far and away the best rental experience that I have ever had.  The properties are beautiful:  3 bed, 3 full bath, 1 car garage, roof deck, finished basement, contemporary design... the list goes on.  The employees in the leasing office have been very good to us as well.  Ariel and Amy were amazing to work with during the process of signing the lease.  They answered every ridiculous question that I had and always responded in a timely manner.  The property manager, Mike, spent over an hour with us at the walk-through when we moved in. The rent is more than reasonable considering how nice the properties are, and I have no doubts that we will be extending our lease before it expires.  6 stars!!!!!!!

Dear lord what an awful milkshake, and fries and a burger to match.

The shake was like drinking chocolate milk, thin and watery. Fries were lukewarm and soggy.  And the burger is TINY.  This isn't any better than any fast food chain, and three or four times the price.

Portions here are VERY meager for the price, and they are WAY overstaffed and STILL slow.  My friend and I were the only people in there and it still took forever for them to get around to talking to us.  And they had no idea what kind of oil the fries are cooked in.  Be careful if you have allergies!

Only plus here is that it's open late, but we'll see if that lasts.  But during normal hours go to Five Guys if you want a sizeable, reliable burger and fries at a reasonable price.  It's further from my house, but worth the extra block of walking.

I stopped going here a few years ago because I would be quoted one thing, and the final cost would be MUCH different. I went back recently for the convenience (it's nearby) and won't make that mistake again.

First let me say that currently, my car is locked in Lei's Auto even though I paid in advance, left my spare key, and told everyone I spoke to I would pick it up on Sunday. I am missing work and losing money because of this situation.

I went to have my inspection done a week ago and was told that my reverse lights weren't coming on. They couldn't find the problem and would call me back. After googling for 30 seconds, I figured out the problem was probably the reverse light switch, but I'm not the "hovering" type or a mechanic, so I assumed they would figure it out eventually. 

I had told them I needed my car by Sunday, so when I hadn't heard from them a half hour before close, I walked down (about a mile) to pay and see what was up. That was when they told me the problem was the reverse light switch and they would have to order it. "Come back on Tuesday." I understood that they don't have every part for every car, and had no problem bringing it back.

On Tuesday I called to let them know that I wouldn't be able to make it in, but that I would drop my car off first thing on Saturday. "No problem!"

I come in first thing on Saturday and again, I tell them I need my car on Sunday, pay for everything in advance, and leave my spare key. The associate there had to search for the part ("I know I ordered it..."), but eventually found it. A few hours later I get a call - "It turns out we have the wrong part and won't be able to do the work today. You can leave the car here until Tuesday, but if you need it this weekend you can still come and pick it up." I said (after making them confirm they ordered the correct part this time, which should be their job, not mine), "Ok, I'll pick it up tomorrow."

I showed up today to pick up my car and it was not left out for me. I have left messages and sent emails. No reply. Because Lei's didn't leave my car out, as they said they would, I am missing $80 worth of work today, and had to cancel on two small children who are always REALLY excited about their guitar lesson.

I will update this review if they do the right thing.

Not sure what happened, they used to have the best margaritas in Northern Libs. Margaritas are weak. Taste like juice. Had 1 drink with a friend and we're moving on to another restaurant. Very disappointed.

The food was very underwhelming, the space and atmosphere were terrible. There are many other delicious  brunch spots in No Libs to choose from, so I will not be back to Green Eggs anytime soon.  oh- and it's cash only, wtf.

This was a really fun place with interesting drink combos. My favorite was the poprocks and champagne (the pop rocks really pop in your mouth when chased with champagne). I also liked the games they had out and their overall music choices were fun and dance-worthy. Good times!

I've been to Ralph's several times and I am glad that I liked it...as I get the feeling that a negative review would invite a crowbar to the knee while I walk the streets one night (I kid, sort of).  This latest time spurred me to write a review because I really liked it more than previous visits...the food this time was delicious (rigatoni with chicken) and the spinach gnocchi was also solid.  The vibe is old school italian in a way that is inviting as opposed to intimidating.  Our waiter was dating the daughter or granddaughter of Ralph and he was entertaining as hell.  Listen...the food isn't going to blow you away, but take it all in and observe what is going on around you and you will like this place.  My only complaint is the bread...it really isn't that good.  I am sure that sounds sac-religious, but I have spent a ton of time living next to King Arthur Flour so my standards are high.

Anyways if you live in Philly and haven't tried it, you need to cross it off your bucket list.  If you are visiting from out of town, you should step out of the cozy restaurants in center city and try this authentic italian in south philly.

Amazing, cheap. Pick what you want. This is fast food that won't make you feel guilty about loading your face with junk. And it's cheap. The fixins bar is a great way to customize your treat. 

Both veggies and non veggies love this place. Waffle fries appeal to all.

gen g you are crazy their food is great and their staff is awesome never a problem i think you are a miserable person. i eat their cream chip beef once a week and it is always great their pizza is awesome try their coo coo bird pizza its awesome
Very nice place.  People are very helpful and friendly.   Very glad I chose to come here.

Went here for the dim sum experience.  Walked in at around 4pm and it was empty. A waitress folding napkins looked at us and didn't say a word. I asked if we could sit anywhere and she responded with a grumpy "yea". After some time another woman brought menus and started to walk away. I asked if it was too early for Dim sum  and she replied, no, dim sum closed. No dim sum. So we walked around a couple of corners to Dim Sum Garden and had a great time.
This place is great! I tried the shrimp pad Thai, and it was so much food for the price! And the gentleman that cook my food was a sweetheart, I would totally come back!

So some of the things at this place are absolutely tasty like the Tandoori Chicken Sandwich. I love that you can walk in, grab what you want, pay and leave (or use their wifi and sit). It makes health food quick and easy instead of waiting for them to make it on the spot

The con is that this place is ridiculously expensive and I was not impressed by their smoothies. The prices are out of control for some of the items especially the vege-only sandwiches. I tried two different varieties and it was literally a thin spread of vege humus and a bunch of veges for $7.50 - ridiculously disappointing for the amount of food you receive.

The down and low is that I will stop there if I have a craving for something healthy or something sweet that is sort of healthy, but not often since I'm a student and their prices are ludicrous.

Great concept, perhaps not 100% greatly executed in terms of value. The products do seem fresh, however, and that is a big bonus.
Had an issue with flyers around South Philly.  Owner addressed issue.  Thanks for getting back to me!

French comment : vous aimez le chocolat ? Alors c'est le restaurant qu'il vous faut pour plonger dans cet univers! En entrant, vous avez déjà les odeurs de chocolats. La carte est très intéressante. Cocktail à base de chocolat sympa. Certain plats ne sont pas extra, mais la plupart sont originaux...et très réussi !

Buffalo Billiards is a good place to go with friends to enjoy their 6-8 happy hour during the week, which offers $3 drafts, $4 wells and half off apps. The wings and buffalo chicken dip are quite good here. There are multiple TVs throughout, several pool tables, 3 dart boards, and a few other games including skeeball. The service is usually pretty good. The girl behind the bar with glasses (not sure her name) in the evenings is very nice and helpful and provides great service even when it's crowded. The middle-aged man who is often working behind the bar, who I believe is a manager, doesn't have the friendliest personality and has actually been fairly rude to some of my friends before which is disappointing. Some negatives, I've actually ordered drinks near the end of happy hour before and the receipt had 7:58 on it  for when the order was placed and they charged full price and said it was 8:00 already when I ordered (even with that argument, 8:00 is technically still happy hour in all their advertisements; but oh well). It's not a huge deal and just a few dollars difference, but something to point out which can give you a idea of their customer service style. I've actually held events in the upstairs area before which they did a good job of having all the food we ordered out and on time for the event and it was fairly painless setting up everything and communicating with the events person, not sure about future events being held there but it is a good space for private events. Overall, it's a decent, large place for grabbing a drink and taking advantage of happy hour.

I've tried on numerous occasions to give this place a chance. Every time I've been there the service is HORRIBLE!!! 
I was in the area with a friend and wanted to get a nice meal and possibly listen to some jazz. Since I've had numerous bad experiences here, I informed my friend about this. 
We proceed to get a table in the basement where live jazz is played. It's a quaint little space and gives you a bit of nostalgic feeling. Our waiter came and took our drink orders. 5 minutes. 10 minutes. 15 minutes. 20 minutes pass. The waiter never came back to the table. Didn't even try to come to the table and apologize for the wait. I proceeded to get up and flagged down the waiter and asked how do I pay? I was referring the $10 per person cover charge for the live entertainment. He said wait a minute. 
This is where it got interesting and the main reason why I'm writing this review. The manager comes out and I ask how much do I owe. He says nothing and don't worry about it. I replied I'm leaving because waiting over 20 minutes for a drink is unacceptable. He response was, it's a little busy. I just looked at him and walked away. 
This was poor customer service on soooo many levels. Why didn't the manager apologize? Why didn't he say he wants to make this right? Why didn't the waiter come and explain the long wait? Why did the manager just let me leave?
I'm a yelper and foodie. I frequent many restaurants in the surrounding areas. It make me wonder if I was being treated bad because I'm not a regular? Not wearing a button down shirt and shoes?

Found a closer place for fresh chicken pho!  My daughter and I enjoyed this place so much we came back the next week.  This time I tried the combination banh mi and goat curry. The banh mi was very good with crispy French bread. Goat curry was ok would have enjoyed it with chicken more. The chicken pho came with the salt and pepper and lime dip, one of my faves! Will return real soon!

Nicole salad was the best I've had. And the cherry desert. Also the soups were amazing.
Ambiance excellent along with maintaining covid regulations.

Best in Philly? I'll have to try John's first before giving them the crown. I got the pepper Cheesesteak with wiz, fried onions and hot crushed peppers on the side. The meat was flavorful and juicy, the bread however didn't really hold up, got a bit soggy, wish it was toasted or had more of a crunch to it. The wait was about 25 minutes.

For some reason I had it in mind to look up all my old college hangouts on Yelp for kicks tonight....

Well, El Azteca. It was so awful that six years later this place still sticks in my mind as the single worst "Mexican" restaurant I've ever had the misfortune to patronize. I swear that the only reason people come here is for the BYOB option on margarita pitchers, but the rumor was when I was in college that they'd switch your alcohol for bottom shelf tequila whenever you'd do this. I hate tequila so I can't comment on the veracity of this.

No, really, this place was so awful that even through the drunken stupor I was usually in whenever I came here I was aware that the "food" in front of me barely qualified as such. I remember one time that I (for reasons unknown to me then or now) asked the waiter pack up my vegetable enchilada or whatever brown, mushy, overcooked grease bomb I had ordered, and he looked at me like I'd grown a second head and done a backflip. "You'd actually want to eat this again?! SOBER?!" his baffled expression seemed to say. 

The weirdest part of this restaurant is the women's restroom, though. If they haven't remodeled since the last time I was there, for some inexplicable reason they have two toilets in a single room with no partition. What? Yeah, okay. 

Being transplanted to San Diego now, I'm actually offended that this restaurant is classified as Mexican. Never in my tenure in Philadelphia did I see anything at El Azteca that qualified as "Mexican," let alone as "food."

First time here on a Friday night after work. It was easy to get a reservation for 4 using OpenTable. Service was better than chain restaurants, they were very friendly. I had the Crab and Linguini Rossa. The flavor was good, but thinner slices would have blended the garlic flavor better in the dish.

I hear they are known for their pizzas, so I will try one next time. Overall not a bad little spot nearby to grab a quick meal. I hope the foot traffic and/or some aggressive marketing will keep their doors open.

This place knows what they are doing! Started up here this winter when the cold shut down my running regimen. This southern girl can't hang. Flywheel kept me in shape all winter in the bumping darkness of a basement studio in Rittenhouse.

Call me antisocial, but I loved being able to sign up online (or on the app), pick out my bike, show up layered in all my winter accoutrements looking beaten down by wind/snow/sleet, and NOT TALK TO ANYONE. You walk down the stairs, check in on one of the three tablets, and find your shoes right under the check in station in a cubby with the number corresponding to your bike number. Throw your stuff in a locker and get into the studio to slog it out in the dark with some killer jams. Easy peasy and I don't have to be embarrassed about my haggard appearance or winter grumpies! If you're a first-timer, the folks at the desk are super helpful though!

Many of the lockers have non-functioning locks, but I didn't mind that too much (one star off for that). There are always employees at the desk during/between classes so I felt like my stuff was safe. Take your wallet/phone into the studio if you're really worried about it. 

The class itself is always a great workout. What I also love about Flywheel is the digital readout (so you know exactly how fast you're going and how much resistance is on, plus your total power output - no "quarter turn to your left, quarter turn to your right" business like at some studios). They also keep track of your visits/stats so you can track your progress - I love competing with myself each time I go! You can also sign up for the optional Torq board so you can look up at the screens during class and see how you rank against other riders. 

Each teacher has their own style and intensity, but I enjoyed trying out several before figuring out my favorites. Amandah really seems to motivate me the most and I leave feeling like I left it all on the bike. I also really enjoy the arm workout 2/3 through the class. It's a nice leg break and I can always use some arm work!

Other stuff: 
Very clean
Water refilling station
Free towels
Student discount
Nice private bathrooms with ample space to change if you need it
First class free

Stopped by on a whim when I saw the truck open. I am so glad I did. The meat is so well seasoned and cooked perfectly. It was tender and very juicy. I had the top sirloin sandwich and it had lettuce and tomato. The simplicity of it was great because the meat was delicious. The roll was also soft and fresh. I will definitely stopping by more often. 10/10 recommend.
Cafe Roma is a yummy bakery with lots of great items but my favorite is the Italian Rum cake - plus, the rumsoaked leftovers get better and better in your refrigerator - heavenly.

Good Indian cuisine, with a cool vibe. We stuck to small plates; Curried Butternut Squash Soup (OK), Calcutta Pork Ribs (OK), Lassoni Gob (Awesome) and Vegetable Samosas (Very Good). While I don't feel the need to run right back there, we'll definitely be back eventually.

Freaking good. Don't miss on the app of tomato pie. Comes in a slice so don't expect a big ol' pie. I had the angry pizza which was satisfyingly spicy with the Thai peppers in the sauce for a lil kick. Gotta stop in and try, get there when they open and hang at the bar to avoid waits!

I took a Wednesday night class with Marla and it was awesome-- really challenging but also incredibly rewarding. It was my first attempt at Pure Barre and I was terrified, but Marla quietly helped me with a few adjustments (without drawing attention to me or away from the class, which was key). I would definitely recommend Pure Barre and Marla's class, and plan on returning soon!

we are serious patrons of Zavinos, like for years. BUT, the black and white TV must go. please! it's arcane and distracting because we can't tell what's going on! today's sports and TV is in color. please bring back the 21st century!!

Great Mexican food!  I just discovered this place even though I live only a few blocks away, and I'm really glad I did. The selection of different Mexican dishes is large, and the food is really scrumptious! There are many good taquerias in South Philadelphia. This one is now one of my favorites.

Glancing down at my receipt from yesterday's café au lait from Shot Tower, I notice the wifi password is typed along the bottom, clearly visible. That, my friends, is the sign of a business that thinks about their customers. If only I would have noticed it yesterday when my wireless card kept crapping out on me, I would have saved myself a ton of frustration! Next time. 

The open space with minimal table seating (only one community table is available) and a ledge with stools that line the windowed walls is very inviting. So was the barista on my visit, who seemed to know every customer who walked through the doors. The stools became a bit uncomfortable after a while, which is why I eventually decided to move on, but I could definitely see myself hanging at one of the outdoor tables on a breezy fall day. 

My café au lait was made with care, but I had to procure my own pink packet from my purse since my sweetener of choice was not available. I'll certainly return!

Denise created a perfect floral arrangement -- I called her from out of town and described one of the arrangements already posted on Yelp.  She modified, improved it and delivered the arrangement to my local destination --  the result was perfect!

After going to Zahav the night before, we did not know how Abe Fisher could compare. Incredible food, service, everything!!!
Started with the small assortment of sushi inspired by things you se in a deli - incredible. Chopped liver mousse - so light and creamy! Then chicken with Swiss chard and schmaltzy potatoes and challah crusted cod. Both delicious. Finished with bacon and egg creme and chocolate babka - fantastic!! Service was great - everyone was so nice and we got to thank the chef before we left!!!

Beijing is really underrated. It's byob and it's simple quick Chinese food. You can takeout or eat with a group of people. It's your typical Chinese food but friend rice lo mean that chow fun is pretty good and the portions are large as opposed to other places. Staff are friendly and quick as well. They also have bubble tea here which is nicd

Great food. Good service. Amazing veggie options. I have been to 4 or 5 Ethiopian restaurant and this would be probably be 1 or 2(the other one is in Chicago)

Only negative. Poor restrooms

When I moved to Port Richmond about a year ago, this was the first neighborhood spot I tried. I was hooked. This has become the #1 place I take people to when they come to visit, and is the place I will always order from when I "don't know what I want." I will admit, I have probably been to Nemi 10+ times now and I am never disappointed. 

I'm probably a bit biased since I live a few blocks away... But it is so easy to walk over and pickup food during the quarantine. My boyfriend and I always call and place our order, asking for extra salsa verde because it is literally one of the best things we have ever eaten. 

Guac is always top notch, the salsa verde is a must-get, and the hand-made tortillas are addicting. I can't say enough good things about this place. If you are in Philly, you gotta try it.

Cantina Los Caballitos is a pretty good place to hang out with some pitchers of margaritas.  Guacamole and chips aren't bad.  Nachos were just okay.  It can get pretty busy.  All in all, it's not bad if it's what you're in the mood for.

This was the first time I called ask do you take walk in yes as soon  as I came I  the young lady greet me with a smile  I explained what I wanted  she suggested to me the gel I told Jenny I have my own color she found it in gel we chatted for a few did a great job I paid told her I will see you in two weeks  very accommodating friendly polite.

So I am giving Bump as good as it gets for drink specials...otherwise I would just be a fan.

I have been going to bump for about 4 years now, and not much has changed except the happy hour is now a dollar more. I did notice this past time that the crowd wasn't as diverse as I like it but times change :)

I can handle the 4 as opposed to 3 dollar martini rate seeing as they still manage to get me nicely tipsy after three or 4(12 dollars for 4 drinks...yes please). Happy hour is 5-7, even on Saturdays. Speaking of Saturdays, I was pleasantly surprised the last time I went on a Saturday. Their entrees are half off every Saturday. I am pretty sure that they might have switched chefs, because the food was much better than I remembered. 

I had Tequila glazed salmon with garlicy leafy spinach and yummy rice. 

It is hard to find a yucky martini but I will recommend a few that aren't on the menu currently that they will still make. 
-The Gay Grasshoper (Tastes like an andes mint)
-Key Lime Pie

There was a new one that I want to say is mocha? It is amazing. 

Be careful not to get too many with champagne, you will pay for it in the morning.

So if you are planning a night in the gayborhood or the surrounding area, bump is a great place to get tipsy and proceed onward!
Really they were a zero. Bought a bunch. Threw a bunch away. Hate to do it, but Conshohocken (who supplies the Reading, PA Fairgrounds Farmers Market) is AT LEAST 1,000,000X better...

I wish I could give this place 0 stars and am surprised by the amount of positive reviews there are for this place. Food was decent but the service was horrible. Our waitress was not very welcoming and also did not do a good job of going over how tapas ordering worked. She gave the table a weird look when my dad asked how he should order for all of us since my dad has never been to a tapas restaurant before and since tapas are relatively small, let alone shareable. The price of the food was not worth it considering the portions as well. She continued the unfulfilling experience by spilling water all over our table and claimed that she was "out of it" and carried on with her bad attitude for the rest of the evening. They also mention in incredibly small print that an automatic 20% gratuity is added to the bill when over 5 people but failed to mention it to us before we ordered/paid. A total mood killer & was probably one of my top horrible dining experiences....will not be coming back again.

I love sky cafe, their noodles are amazing, the value is just about unmatched in the city, and I'm a big fan of the spice level. I do have one complaint though. Don't talk your customers out of trying new things on your menu! You guys have loyal customers who won't stop patronizing your business if they try something that's a little funkier than they're used to. On multiple occasions I've tried to order something only to be told "you won't like that" or "that's not popular." Well your customers won't know if they like it or not if we can't taste it! I wouldn't even bring this up if I didn't see other people who have had the same experience. If it's on the menu, let us order it. Otherwise I can't recommend Sky Cafe highly enough!

AMAZING!!! LOVED EVERY MINUTE OF IT!!! the only downside, i wish there was infant feeding room where i could go cuz bathroom stalls are uncomfy to do but the zoo is clean, pleasant atmosphere, baby loved the carousel and everything else. i was there for full day n didn't finish it. very entertaining.

The Khyber Pass Pub is a solid beer bar.  I've had no problem walking in and finding something really great on tap.  Some bars have a nice selection of mediocre brews, on the occasions I've been here, Khyber seems to step it up a bit.  I've always had outstanding service at the bar too.  I don't know what it is but the bartenders are just amazing.  Even when they're swamped, they always turn to me with a smile and seem happy to fill my glass. 

Although I've been to Khyber a few times since they opened for special drinking events, it took me a few months to actually sit down and eat there.  The food is very good.  My first bite in to their pulled pork sandwich was filled with smokey delicious pork bark.  Really delicious.  As a meat eater, I was still very impressed with their non-meat options.  I'm sensitive to the needs of others and recognize that this could be a great vegetarian destination.  At the same time though, I felt that took away from some more delicious meat that could have been on the menu.  The server we had was amazing.  Very friendly and well attentive to our needs.  Something was forgotten on one of our dishes but she realized it before we even had a chance to bring it up.  She made the fix and comp'd part of our meal for that.  

I'll go back to Khyber more for the beer selection and awesome staff than for the food.  I myself have to be in the mood for southern barbecue fare.  Although the food is delicious, I'd like to see just a bit more depth.  I always have a great time at the "new" Khyber even when I'm not expecting to.  There's just a really great vibe in there and I think it's fueled by all the awesome people that own it and work there.

Mr. Lin is a sweet old man who takes pride in his meticulous work. I needed a dress hemmed last minute and he was willing to make the alteration immediately as long as I sat there with him. He told me stories and make Chinese pun jokes the whole time. Prices definitely can't be beat and when I tried to tip him, he wasn't having it. Please support him!
So happy to have this in the neighborhood! They are so friendly and accommodating. Plus they do a great job for a decent price. They work so hard and I am so grateful to have them close by!!

The restaurant is located at the really beautiful Logan Circle. With the garden and the water, it is so much better than the place in DC with the same name. Anyway, I like the interior and the decorations. It is trendy and comfortable. What I could not understand is the space in the back where the bathrooms were. It seems some remodeling were going on. 

I was there for brunch with two native philadelphians. I had grapefruit juice, salad, an over easy egg and fennel chicken sausages. The presentations were pretty good. The food themselves were decent. The salad is a little on the bland side and the sausages were extremely salty. I could not taste any fennel.
