I thought the sausages would be a little charred to add on the flavor. But in stead, they seem to be steamed or boiled in water. But I do like the over easy egg a lot. It was perfectly done. Sometimes, you would be surprised how unusual to get the egg right. 

The waiter we got was somewhat awkward. He would interrupt conversations to check on us. 

With the great location and the decorations, I think I would not mind going back. But just for the food, I think it was just OK.

I tried sitting outside to drink & wait for my to-go food instead of sitting inside at the bar & it was a whole different experience. Our waitress was sweet & attentive & just generally good at being a waitress. The food we ate at home later & it was wonderful as usual. I've never had any problems with the food or service at Gunners Run, just the ambience inside. So going on a nice night & sitting at a table out in the piazza makes it a whole different experience. Sitting outside it's kind of a peaceful & nice. You notice less that there are very few customers besides a few regulars at the bar & you don't get that vaguely depressed icky feeling. They really need to do something about the inside of the restaurant, so I have two different ratings for this place. Nice weather & outdoor table: 3 stars.  Weather forcing you to sit inside: 2 stars.

Quiet possibly the best meal I have ever had!  We came here for my girlfriend's birthday, and we truly couldn't be happier.  Great service, impossibly delicious food and great ambiance.  Bring an extra bottle and make sure to get the dessert!

Free yoga on the pier on wkends! Loved it and it was hard moves ... But great for new yogis and advanced ones.  Free and if you feel charitable that day, they do collect donations.  It can be bit noisy from patch train, construction and duck boats but free yoga! Come on! Go! Nameste !

My wife and I ate hotpot in Ochatto yesterday night. Let me start with telling you the good things about Ochatto. The venue is nicely decorated. Due to the high ceiling, it does not feel cramped. The food is also up to expectations. We enjoyed the soup bases in particular. We order three of them: the tomato soup, the Tom Yum soup, and mushroom soup with pork broth.

That brings us to the negative parts of the Ochatto experience. The waiters are blunt. It was quiet when we came in, but we had to wait for ten minutes for a waiter to order something. We waved at waiters that were just standing, but they were unwilling to help us because another waiter was apparently responsible for our table. I can understand that the restaurant assigns tables to waiters, but in quiet times, the personnel should be more flexible. What makes it even more ridiculous is that the assignment was suddenly ignored when it was time for us to pay. Another waiter than our assigned easier presented us the check. It was also strange that the pork broth was mentioned as vegetarian soup on the check. 

Another annoyance is that the restaurant does not have air suction above the hotpot like other restaurants. I agree that it does not look pretty, but lack thereof makes it hard for people with glasses to operate the hotpot at times as glasses tend to fog up when the steam flows in their direction. Moreover, all your clothes smell like soup after a visit. Finally, a decent hotpot restaurant should provide tongs for the raw meat. Expecting the customers to use their chopsticks to handle the meat is unhygienic. It took several times of asking to get just one tong. Well, I'm not shy to reduce my tip for lousy service. 

All in all, I have mixed feelings about this place. I might come back for the food, but not for the service.

Today I received a microdermabrasion facial from Joanna! It was an amazing relaxing, informative and brightening (literally, my skin looked so much brighter when I left) experience. Staff were super friendly, offered water or tea (I got a green), and the waiting room was beautifully relaxing. The moment you step off of the elevator it's clear that there is a thoughtful design, which I so appreciated. Joanna explained every step of the process, as it was my first time getting a facial let alone microdermabrasion. First she cleansed my face and chest, massaged my arms, wiped it off work a hot towel. Then she extracted buildup on my face - uncomfortable and brief but clearly necessary. She talked to me about how melanin functions in the skin and how that resulted in hyperpigmentation in my skin, especially after a breakout. As a dark skinned black woman this was informative. Joanna helped me recommit to a skin regimen and I purchased P50. Definitely coming back! Thanks Rescue Spa. 

1/27 - Today I had my second facial with Naomi - a Biolift. On both occasions she was informative, warm, and so down to earth. She took the time to let me know what she was doing and how it would serve my skin. After I saw her in December for a Fix-It-All facial I saw amazing improvements in my skin-- significantly less breakouts and my dark blemishes also brightened. She recommended that I incorporate the Biologique Creme Demopurifiante and use my P50 day and night. I also utilized the Masque as an overnight spot treatment for blemishes, and they healed so much faster than usual. I'm a believer and can't wait to see Naomi again!

The stuffed challah French toast is amazing! We went on a Saturday afternoon so of course it was super crowded. Make sure to join the waitlist before heading over... we made the mistake of showing up without a reservation and ended up waiting about 45 minutes for a table for 2.

Really really good pork dumpling in oil and spicy grilled beef. Addictive Szechuan pepper burn. I want to keep going but I must stop. They brought tea (complimentary) and delicious pickled vegetables that helped cool off my mouth between bites.

To be fair we went to this restaurant for first time on Night Market night, so it was very busy.  The good:  1) bartender rocks the drinks - very yummy, 2) the shrimp/feta salad was delicious.  The bad: 1) shrimp/fish ceviche was not good.  Tasted like they simply put cocktail sauce over it & lavished it heavily to try disguising fish that was chewy & shrimp that didn't    make any impact at all.  2) fried chicken with spinach & corn bread salad was 50/50.  The corn bread salad was definitely a plus but the fried chicken was under-seasoned & the spinach overcooked.

thanks MC for bringing me (as per Grub Hub special) a wonderful Easter dinner.  My best friends sent it to me through a special 'Invite to Easter Dinner.'  I had the Chicken Parm over a zesty pasta with marinara. Came with a salad, Italian bread.  I've had 2 leftover meals since and much more left.  The mozzarella sticks were also generous and arrived with a special sauce. The ice cream dessert was enough for another several servings. Everything was hot (cept ice cream) and very tasty.
Delivery was exactly on time and safe!
I'd recommend this place highly.  They have attention to detail and conscientious customer service.  I will definitely order from there or go again.  I ate there once before and the place is immaculate.

We came to Jasmine Rice because we were craving a spicy BYOB dinner. We were pleasantly surprised by how quaint, clean, and welcoming the restaurant is, and the location cannot be beat.

We were very happy with the meatball appetizer (although it was very small for $8), and the green curry beef dish, which had an aromatic coconut and lemongrass flavor. I also ordered the green papaya salad, and it was a bit water-logged and had lost its crunch. I don't know if this dish was prepared fresh, but I have had much crunchier papaya salad at other Thai restaurants.

In general, the portions were small, and we had to ask for extra rice in order to fill up. After paying $40 for two people, we almost left hungry, which was pretty disappointing. Overall, the quality of the food was pretty good, the service was nice, but I am not sure that I would come here again due to the poor value, in my opinion.

IN PHILADELPHIA.I KNOW WHAT IM WRITING ABOUT.MAIN OFFICE 40TH WALNUT.see the pics attached to company on main page of company that look digusting.well me and another neighbor living over it.i was told it will be cleaned years ago and it wasn't,instead money was wasted on interior painting and a shiny floor around area.no one should live over anything like that.bugs,mice in that area.they dont care as long as its not in apartment is their response. i complained about front doors can easily be accessed most times because latch locks stay stuck in both doors when turning key if it happen to be locked while opening so anyone can just pull open door and they in building to rob,rape or kill someone which almost happened before.wd40 spray not doing the job.the doors and frames need replacing.outside of building on other side as seen in pics is boarded up like a crackhouse and bugs and rodents and cold air can easily come in building.dont bother with bullshit reply like you care .you had years to fix issues.when fixed then i remove comment.a loyal tenant who called about many issues in building over the years that no other tenant bothered to do gets treated like shit.karma come back in various ways

After a few visits to Village Whiskey, I like it. There is nothing really to love about this place unless you are really into waiting for a table only to be rushed out of it. 

The food is good. The burgers are really good. The duck fat fries are ridiculous. I happen to believe that potatoes are not worth $6 no matter what kind of trendy fat you are frying them in. 

The service leaves a lot to be desired as well. The hubs and I went to start his birthday celebration with drinks there. It was probably 5 in the afternoon. It took a while to get served, but when we did get served the drinks are fantastic! That's where the three stars come in. The drinks are amazing. I had an aviation and John had an Old Fashioned. 

They also let me order my personal favorite prosecco and st. germaine without a hassle öf  "whats that?" like i get at so many other places in this city.

Two of the worst cheesesteaks and pizzas I have ever had. They put a cheesesteak on a wheat seeded roll. Needless to say it was horrible. 

The pepperoni pizza I got had no flavor whatsoever and tasted more like the pizza I would get at 3am after a night of drinking.

I am embarrassed to have this place making food in Philadelphia, especially if someone from out of town accidentally stopped in.
Service was quick, atmosphere genuine. The food portions are enough to feed two people with one dish. The SanteFe Burrito bowl is packed with flavor , get the steak for sure.

On opening weekend, a certain amount of confusion and back-up should be expected. My boyfriend and I heard the news that entry was delayed by a couple hours. We were bummed, but accepting of the fact that we would be able to receive free tickets to attend at a later date. Since we were in the area anyway, we decided to walk over just to see if anything had changed from the earlier reports. Luckily, we picked the right time because we were able to get in only a half hour after our original appointment time. While we waited, a glass of wine and some live jazz helped pass the time. 

Once inside the collection, it was clear that we wouldn't be able to take everything in during the designated hour. But what we did see was absolutely incredible. And I'm pretty sure my buzz from the wine (and okay, the beers we had at a bar nearby before that) added to our enjoyment. We'll definitely return once the initial excitement wears off so that we can enjoy the works with less of a crowd. But in the meantime, a walk around the gorgeous grounds (that are actually still in-process) isn't out of the question.

Came here for the first time after reading all the positive reviews on Yelp, and they were all correct.  Although the place is small, the food and service there are exceptional!  We got the bun bo hue, pho, braised chicken, and banh mi, and they were all good.  Glad to have found this little gem in South Philly.  Price is quite reasonable as well.  Will definitely be back!  Thank you.

This zoo is a joke.  I feel like the positive reviews are from people that haven't been to any other zoo.  This place is a money grab.  High parking fee of $16, high ticket price ($80-$90 for family of 4), and then everything In the park costs more money, including some of the exhibits. Plus whole areas are dedicated to "hey buy this keychain or buy this photo."  I wish the focus was more on the animals And education of the community, but there would probably be a fee for that here.

I really do love Stir. I love that we have a gay bar on this side of Broad street. I love that you can come here any time of the week, any time of the night and it is still...not that crowded. 

They make a damn good drink here as well and I love that the plasma always has like E! or the Style network on, because there is nothing that I like better than watching John Travolta's True Hollywood Story while getting a little wastys. 

Also, this place used to be the ugly stepsister of the gay bars in Philadelphia: The Post. And although I was never  in the post as a legal adult, this place was crazy. Things found only in dreams happened at The Post.

Sometimes at Stir, you can look around the bar and pick out the odd few people that were probably regulars at The Post...but the majority of the cliente are young and kindasorta yuppie. 

But if you squint your eyes and think thoughts of life before pink martinis inhabited this space, I can still see the leather and chains coming in and out of the bathroom with no abandon. 

Ahhh, I love Stir. 
xo

The place was ok, nothing special and way overpriced compared to neighborhood shops. Also had to wait like 30 min for my food with hardly anyone there. I would still rather go to Central Pizza down the block and get a steak. Better priced and better quality.

Wow! The steak was absolutely incredible!  I had a 12oz filet mignon with 3 super humongous asparagus spears. Best steak I've ever had. Meat melted in my mouth and cooked to perfection. I highly recommend this place for the steak lover. They won't be disappointed.

Tried Hawthornes for brunch for the first time this weekend.  While I did not partake, the beer selection was as ample as I had anticipated based on word of mouth.  Surprisingly absent today was anything from my favorite semi-local brewer from DE however.  But beyond that, vast and varied!  Will definitely need to come back.

As far as food, the brunch was quite good.  I had an omelet  with sharp provolone and ham.  The provolone was indeed sharp, the ham delicious.  The side of homefries were some of the better I have had in some time, herby and combined with onions and bell peppers.  

My girlfriend went for the banana foster waffle, which was just decadent, dripping with syrupy sweet sauce and luscious chunks of moist banana, atop a perfectly cooked waffle.

So, good beer, good food...  why three stars?  I'm afraid that rests upon the service.  While our waitstaff was reasonably attentive and the biercafe was fairly vacant,  this meal for two with only coffee to start still managed to take considerably too long, approaching two hours.    So, keep that in mind when you visit.

I will go back, but perhaps for beverages alone next time.

My favorite cheesesteak place in Philly. Hands down, best, most fulfilling cheesesteak is the belly filler. You get a foot long (that honestly is 2 ft in reality) filled with good quality meat, veggies, and yummiest cheese. Personally, this order I usually share with two of my other friends so it can be finished. 
I've never really dined in, I order pick up because delivery takes a long while. If you're ever in Philly and you have time visit this place, you can thank me later.

Doesn't do a damned thing for me.  But it is CHEAP and you can get sammies, burgers and FRIED pub grub here for a quick lunch or a beer after work.

Maybe I am too snooty-snotty for this, but I am not a fan.
This is your typical Chinese takeout, but with a twist. The vegetarian fare here is outstanding and a rarity for the area. My daughter loves the sweet and sour tofu. I prefer the general tso's tofu.

The food is good.  The 4 dollar delivery charge is very steep but it is good authentic Jamaican. 

Unfortunately, the customer service was beyond terrible.  We ordered via Grubhub at 8pm and were told our food would be here at 9:11pm. At 9:15 we began calling and the phone was busy each time until 9:30.  At that point we were given an attitude upon calling and then told our order was cancelled.  After arguing this as we never cancelled our order and never received a notification of the cancellation we were put on the phone with someone else, who told us they didn't send the food because they stopped serving at 8:30 (despite us ordering at 8pm).  She was no help in getting us our 54 dollars back (yes thats for two people, it is relatively expensive) and basically just said she would figure it out and get back to us. 

Finally grubhub called and gave us the truth, which was one of their employees deleted the order and they decided not to tell us. 

Overall, the customer service was so bad and we were so blatantly lied to that we will never order from here again.

The felafel burger/sandwich is AMAZING!  I added avocado which really made it perfection (including the fries! yum!).  The fresh squeezed juices are amazing - orange and grapefruit.  I want to try everything else on the menu now!

The only downside to our visit was that they ran out of bagels for our whitefish platter - but the brioche was equally good and it just shows how popular they are - it was a busy morning there!
Left smelling like the bottom of a deep fryer. Recommend changing your oil...sandwich was burnt

City Sports used to be my go to place for athletic gear.  The location is convenient for me and they usually have what I'm looking for from triathlon gear,  and running gear, to cycling accessories.  

However, the last time I was there the woman who checked me out was extremely rude.  I will be finding another store to get my athletic gear from, or ordering online (not from city sports).  The only reason I didn't give them one star is because they have a large selection.

Gorgeous hotel. A view like no other and pristine rooms. The gym was such a treat - could stay all day with that view. Excellent location. Could not have been happier. Frederick at the front desk went out of his way to ensure our stay was pleasant. Highly recommend.

I love this post office. Sometimes there's a long wait, but the workers can't help that some customers are unprepared/whatever. Speaking of the workers, they're awesome. This location is my favorite USPS in Center City.

It was aiite... nothing too amazing.
Have had better mexican food at Qdoba and so on
The chicken lacked flavor... If I wanted to douse my food with hot sauce for flavor I would have gone to tacobell
Amazing friendly service. Very kind manager and attentive waitstaff. Great French toast and chicken and waffles! Wish I lived near here to come regularly.
Yummy. Tasty. Perfect occasional treat and worth the cheat meal! Great team, quick preparation and friendly service. Definitely a staple in the neighborhood - a Brewerytown "must do".

Friendly warm staff  and they will do exactly what you ask for. 
variety of flavors and cupcakes designs. They even have puppies treats, how cute!
my fav is Reese's Chocolate Peanut Butter Massacre even though I am not a cupcake fan. It is a cash only unless if you have a large order they might take your card from my experience.  For my friend's birthday I ordered a giant Cupcake and it was amazing. then i ordered a mickey mouse head for my coworker baby shower who is obsessed with disneyland, not disappointed.
Four Yelps!

darn wish i was more impressed after trying this place twice. coming from the bay area, california --so i am spoiled and pretty picky i'm realizing when it comes to chinese food and dim sum. for a place being named dim sum garden, it's really limited in actual dim sum options. i think my rating would change if it were actually called dumpling garden or something.

this restaurant has a lot of little tables squeezed into it so it seemed packed with lots of happy hungry people. we ordered a variety of things including:
*the pork soup dumplings: skin was way too thick, good amount of well balanced tasty soup that wasn't overpoweringly gingery as some places can be (DTF)
*crab soup dumplings: preferred the pork dumplings over there.. it wasn't as crab filled as i was hoping which i'm learning the usual case when ordering these 
*scallion pancakes: pretty good and great as leftovers to heat up on the pan later
some type of stir fry round noodle: kinda bland and greasy 

i was really excited for some good soup dumplings after hearing a lot about the place but i think i may just have to venture to joe shanghais still to get my cravings satisfied. the soup dumplings at the other place down at the corner of this same street was tastier with better dumpling skin

Wow! Best BBQ pork and fries I have ever had. I don't even post Yelp reviews so that is saying a lot! Great sandwich flavor, freshest bread, fries could tell were hand cut real potatoes. A MUST try if you're in Philly!!

Best bike shop in Philly, with the best personality.
Jeff always has me laughing at his awesome stories.

He is a gem, and Philly bike owners are blessed to have him around all these years.

Way to go Frankenstein's!!

YUM. Had a humongous and tasty brunch here. I opted for the eggs benedict, which was perfect. The hollandaise was very creamy and complemented the hearty slab on Canadian bacon atop my muffin. My plate was heaped with breakfast potatoes, onions, and peppers which made for  a really filling dish. My mom had the breakfast burrito which she found fresh, tasty, and extremely filling. BF had steak and eggs which were prepared well, but a little cold. All of our food was slightly less warm than breakfast food ever should be. 

Two stars off for service and space. Green Eggs is pretty cramped, but I would expect wait staff to be able to navigate the room without slamming into purses, chairbacks, and elbows. These bumps happened several times during our meal and made it a lot less enjoyable. Add that to the snooty, cold waitress who ostensibly felt that she had much more important things to do than wait on us- AKA DO HER JOB. Additionally, we saw a party of 8 or so come in and ask if tables could be joined so they could all eat together. They were refused- what the heck?! It just seemed unreasonable and petty. And cash only? Come on. Green Eggs seems like the type of place that has a firmly established customer base and could not care less about visitors/new patrons.

I had a graduation photo and am woefully unable to do my own hair.  They managed to squeeze me in last minute and early in the day.  I was very grateful!  I got a wonderful student who was very kind.  I scheduled a blow out.

I don't care too much about ambiance, and Jean Madeline has a very factory feel to it.  There are many rows of chairs, and it was very busy when I went.  There were some teachers hanging out at the end of the row, but very little to no supervision during my actual blow out.  It took a long time, including wait time I was there for almost two hours.  (I waited for maybe half an hour).  HOWEVER, I was running half an hour late, and I called ahead by about an hour to let them know.  

My hair initially looked wonderful but fell flat within an hour.  At least there were no fly aways in my photo.  I would come back for something basic such as a blow out if I have another time crunch because the prices are good, but I don't know if I would try anything too complicated.

Fantastic food. You can't find a better deal on Italian in the city, and this is high end, authentic stuff. Lovely service as well - we had a big party of 20 and the servers didn't miss a beat. We will be back!

I stopped by Hatch & Coop for a late-night dinner. After looking at the menu, I decided on the chicken & waffle sandwich with fries. I also asked for the chicken to be spicy. The wait for my food was not long. After getting back to my hotel, I started to eat.

Here are my thoughts:
Chicken: It was fine. It was flavorful but not spicy. The chicken was in the form of patty which was a bit of surprise.

Waffle: Delicious!

Fries: It was okay. I would probably order the tater tots.

Overall, I would say a good experience.

Can I give it no stars?  If I could, I would.  
Went there for my husband to buy me a necklace.  
Felt like we were in a sleazy used car place.  
The salesperson was going back & forth with the manager about prices.  When he realized that I liked the least expensive option, he raised the price!  
Also, had a promotional gift card that they had sent out; it was worth $50.  When I took that out, salesperson said he had already given us enough of a discount & we couldn't use it.
WILL NEVER GO BACK!

Introduced to this place by a coworker, and so glad she shared! During the lunch hours they have a steam table where a 2 choice combo cost $6.00. Includes white or fried rice. I always go for the white rice, red curry chicken and spicy tilapia route. Haven't been disappointed yet. 

This place is pretty small, there is a counter up aginst the window with some stools but its pretty much a take and go joint.  What I really love about this place is the people who work there are so friendly, always making sure they say hi and bye to you. 

So if you get a chance to go in, say hi and bye to the people at Thai Singha House.

I tried the tuna dumplings and they were every bit as good as I imagined. Expensive, but definitely worth it. I may have liked for them to make it as maybe three smaller ones instead of two, since they were pretty large. The presentation was also beautiful.

We ordered Brisket, Pulled Pork, St. Louis Ribs, and Chicken, along with sides of Half Sour Pickles, Burnt End Baked Beans, Coleslaw, Sauerkraut, and Dinner Rolls.  While nothing was bad, nothing really stood out either.  Brisket seemed to be the fave of the group; however, some of found the ribs to be a bit on the dry side.  Of the 3 sauces (vinegar, spicy, and sweet), my favorite was mixing the sweet and spicy together.  Vinegar tasted like vinegar.  Sides were good but I missed sides I'm used to having with BBQ, such as mac-n-cheese, greens, mashed potatoes.

A good place for big groups.  Quick service.  Nice, laid-back atmosphere.  Reasonably priced.

A bit overpriced for what you get. Some of the portions are microscopic, which would be okay if it were cheaper,  more impressive or innovative.  However,  it is neither of the 3. There are several tapas places in the Ironbound of Newark NJ which can run rings around this place. I renember going to Valanni pre-tapas, and it was a much better restaurant as it was a lot less gimmicky. Good tapas is not easy, in the same way cupcakes invariably taste worse than a slice of a larger cake. It takes grounding in a long tradition of small plates, not just some tricks executed in cut down entrees.
Not a bad place to drink beer, watch  TV, and ogle.  Decor is a bit Hooters-esque (memories of a North Florida fishing hangout). Crowd is college bar meets neighborhood bar.

What can I say? Well, for starters whoever does the hiring has a good eye for helpful and generally friendly people. I think that's important to note because every visit I've paid to Pizzeria Stella has been pleasant.

They have an amazing sparkling Lambrusco in house that I HAVE to have every time I go. Not saying you should drink that one but they got several tasty wines and drinks so don't bother BYO.

This place is also good for keeping a couple pizza specials that are usually on par for the season, a few weeks back when we went they had a corn pizza for the veg heads that my friend and I tore up with no regrets and the pepperoni is always a favorite. 

Somethings you won't find described in the menu or online are the nice clean restrooms and the bar top seating area where you can watch the pizzas being created and cooked in the wood fired oven. It's a cool thing for the kiddos to see... how real pizza is made. They also have nice desserts but I didn't have any. Was too stuffed with pizza!

This place is a true South Philly lunch counter.  They call themselves "South Philly's Best Kept Secret" and I am reluctant to blow up their spot.

Come in here for breakfast or lunch and let Sue cook it up like your surrogate Italian mother.  Nothing is fancy, but she takes pride in what she does and keeps things interesting with specials.

All the South Philly staples are good here.  Just soak in the flavor as the locals banter and Sue's tiny elderly father struggles with the deli slicer.

Thrilled to have a fantastic nail salon so close to home!

I messaged Beautique through Yelp for an appointment (it was actually pretty late at night), and Tuyen got back to me within the hour to confirm. Showed up a few minutes before the salon opened for my 10am appointment, and they took me right away. The salon is clean and beautiful--plus, there's off-street parking! Gotta love it.

I forget the name of the manicurist I had (ugh), but she was so sweet and did an amazing job! Really took her time, which is always appreciated. And I don't know what music channel/playlist they had going, but I was very much digging it :)

Everyone was so nice and accommodating and I will definitely be back.
A great 3 star place. Im sure thats what the place aspires to be, so its standing good for what it wants to achieve. Order drunken noodles and massaman curry. Food was decent. Cute small place. Byob.

fun place for drinks and apps! i love the fire place area in the restaurant and the decor is adorable. i find myself always getting the same dishes every time we go there. mac and cheese, fish and chips and Shepards pie are all delicious.
I'm changing my review a little due to being grumpy when I filed my last review. This is a goodspot but the coffee bar does need to pick it up a little. The cheese guys are the tops.
Been going to John since 1977. Can't ask for a better recommendation than that. Great guy and a wonderful barber!

Great atmosphere paired with a quick and friendly staff makes this spot a must visit. Their unique flavors definitely set them apart from the other spots in the area!Just had dinner in Chinatown? Definitely stop in. Pregaming before a night out? Yeah you need some of this stuff. Heading back home after a long day at work? Idk man, just come in and try that good good. So... why're you still reading this review? Get your a$$ over to Kuriimii!!

I got a hair cut by Kodi and he did such a good job! He went above and beyond to make me feel as comfortable as possible given the covid circumstances. I would 10/10 go here again for any hair needs I have. Book with Kodi and you will leave happy !

Dirty glasses, covered with lipstick, unacceptable.

New glasses do not make up for this shortcoming.

5 stars for this Popeye's! I'd say I'm a regular here, averaging about 3 visits per month. For a grab n go meal, I do appreciate the early-week (Mon/Tues/Wed) $5 meal special (a choice of 2 pieces light or dark, spicy or mild + a side + a biscuit + a drink). The staff has gotten our order right each time, and I've never had a bad meal here!

This was my first and only experience with The Beer Store. I went in to purchase a gift card. Upon entering, there was no one at the register at the front of the store. I waited around for a little, walked up and down an aisle, and still, no one had come to the register. I then went to the bar area in the back, where no more than 8 people were seated around the bar. There was one bartender, with whom I made eye contact as I stood behind a bar stool. She was conversing with a couple about personal matters - this conversation continued, as I stood there, obviously waiting for initial service. The bartender, after making eye contact with me (seeing I was there, clearly), did not take a moment to step away from her personal conversation to see if I needed anything. After standing there, waiting around for more than a minute, I walked out. There was still no one at the front register area, nor did the bartender ever make any effort to serve me. I went to another craft beer store in the area instead, and received excellent and immediate customer service. I would not recommend The Beer Store to others, solely for the fact that I value customer service, and feel that this establishment is severely lacking in that department.

Ordered their octopus salad and "fresh" margherita pizza. Both misses - they should relabel the salad as "Chickpea with (some small pieces of) octopus salad". Seriously? And the 'fresh' pie crust was anything but fresh. And the tomato sauce was seriously bland - as if the sauce was misdirected from being delivered to a senior living home.

Popped in this past Sunday afternoon at around 2 pm-got a table for 3 with no wait. Server was friendly and helpful in terms of rearranging tables for our needs. 

We ordered quickly, and food came out beautifully presented (and very tasty!!) in about 20 minutes. I had the Provence salad ($11, lemony dressing and grated pecorino over shaved fennel, artichokes, roasted red peppers, and mixed greens) + tuna nicoise and the soup of the day ($8), a tasty mushroom barley blend with lots of other veggies and chili. My salad was pleasantly tangy and dressed just the right amount. I loved the way the tuna was finely shaved on top. The soup seemed a little small for being $8 but was hot and hearty. Would not have been enough for a meal alone.

My dining companions got the Tel Aviv salad ($12, cumin-orange vinaigrette over toasted pistachios, kale, arugula, farro, pickled radish, and beets) + chicken and the grilled cheese (gruyere!) and cup of tomato bisque ($14). They both got coffee (there was an interesting and varied selection of options, including nitro cold brew) and were happy. 

Seemed like a good place for a casual lunch or brunch...including a casual lunch or brunch date! Place had a nice hum to it. I'd go back.
Nice atmosphere, definite knowledge of herbs. But be warned, no empathy!!  Did not feel nurtured or cared for. There are several people who practice there, i am referring to Cara Frank the owner.

wow, im unpleasantly surprised that this place hasnt been reviewed more! it is such a little hidden gem. i know that in the 40th and chestnut intersection has your usuals - Distrito, New Delhi, and Papaya -  all which offer convenient, pretty tasty dining (for the most part).  It is pretty easy to overlook this place, but for lunch, its a great deal!

i came here with grace s. and jade p. a while back and we shared the Spaghettini w/ jumbo lump crab meat, pad thai, and a curry. all the dishes exceeded my expectations. most of the selection was under $10 an entree, which is an excellent value for the quality and service of nan. i highly recommend anyone in the area to give this place a try.  be adventurous and try out this little neighborhood gem :)

Welcome to the neighborhood, Pizzeria Vetri. As a Fairmount/Art Museum resident, I am happy to have you here. 

My friend and I were lucky enough to snag a spot on the official opening weekend. I've heard that they don't take reservations so we really did luck out getting a seat. Our next stop was Wanderlust down at the Piazza and the fact that we were able to casually dine in our yoga gear wasn't a problem. 

We both ordered the Arugula salad, although I was originally leaning towards the Italian Caesar Salad. Based on the recommendation from the couple next to us and affirmation that the Arugula salad could be prepared without olives, I was sold on the Arugula. Upon bringing out my friend's salad, they had forgotten to prepare mine without the olives. The olives seemed pretty big and unobtrusive so I said it would be okay to leave the olives. Our server was on top of it and already sent mine back. The error was handled seamlessly and with grace. The salad was delicious and I was happy it was prepared to-order after all. I never would have thought that potatoes would go well in a salad, but they worked out quite well. The pesto was more oil based- as in not clumpy as a pesto can be- and made for an excellent dressing. Although not listed on the menu, the small chunks of Parmesan cheese made for a great addition. 

On to the pizza...

My friend and I had ordered one Margherita pie to split since we didn't want to fill up too much before yoga. She had originally debated on getting the Tonno pie however decided against it. This must have confused our server and we were a little confused ourselves when a Tonno pie showed up at our table. As this was an error on their end, we were able to keep the Tonno pie at no charge- score! Another issue handled with grace and professionalism. Ultimately, I did like the Tonno pizza a little better than the Margherita. I figured it would be best to start with the "basic" pizza and expand my repertoire from there. The Margherita was a little too simple. Don't get me wrong, simple is not a bad thing. The crust was light, flaky and baked to perfection, the basil was fresh and the cheese was melted perfectly. It just wasn't a life changing experience. The Tonno had that same great crust, but was just a little more exciting. 

As many have already pointed out, the space is small and you are sitting at communal tables. Since this is a pizza joint with a casual atmosphere, I'm okay with that. Next time I'd love to sit by the window seats, complete with a view of Philadelphia skyline. 

The service was fab and we even got to see Mr. Marc Vetri himself hard at work. I've never seen him before so I was quite star struck. 

I can't wait for my next visit. They have an awesome beer list and there are other pizzas I'm dying to try- based on the menu descriptions and seeing our "table neighbors" enjoying them.

I've been going to this studio for awhile now and I have to say that it is by far my favorite place to get my hair colored, cut and styled! Alanna is my hair colorist and she has always gone above and beyond what my expectations were, especially in dealing with my constant desire for more and more highlights! Going from blonde, to an ashy medium brown, back to blonde, Alanna has managed to carry me through it all, and very gracefully. She always takes the time to sit and discuss my hair before we begin, which makes me so much more comfortable with the process and helps me understand how to keep my hair healthy through it all. It's very encouraging to have a colorist that cares so much about the health of my hair and not just how it looks!

Everyone at the studio is super warm and welcoming, which makes the whole experience much more enjoyable! I always recommend this place to my friends!

When you walk in don't let the initial smell of the joint fool you. It's the building. And yes - that needed to be clarified by the bartender (Fran - who was awesome!). The food is really tasty. They bring you rice (choice of green or regular - I chose green) and refried beans (the most flavorful refried black beans I've ever had!!!) and it was all fantastic. 

They have a great selection of beers. Victory Golden Monkey bottles? You're a winner in my eyes. 

I tried their chicken and carne asada soft tortilla taco, and the ground beef hard shell taco. In my opinion, the chicken was the winner. Great spice, tender shredded chicken, and a corn tortilla that stood the test of juiceiness - very impressive.

Also tried the Monte Carlo Margarita and it was spicyyyyy. But I loved the color of it, and it didn't  water down as much as you'd expect as I baby-sitted a little bit. Great when you've had a hard day and you neeeeed a margarita to bring you back to your happy place. 

Three stars for the initial response to the atmosphere, but 3.5 for the service, clientele, and the attention to flavor in their meals and drinks.
Delicious food!! You're able to bring your own bottle of alcoholic beverages. Clean and lively atmosphere.

This clean and beautiful spa is professional to it's core. Owner Cara Soloman is skilled in deep tissue massage and waxing with a wonderful style that speaks to her experience. I have had the best and she is the best. No complaints here. This is the real thing with a clean contemporary environment that makes you feel relaxed. This spa is not trying to be anything it's not yet it hits the mark if what you want is a real service and somewhere beautiful convenient and clean and real to get it. Congradulations to Cara Soloman for creating the real McCoy in a spa - not an easy thing to do or others would also be able to do it as well..

I'm not sure what happened to this place, I used to LOVE Yogorino but it seems like the quality of ingredients has decreased since my last review.  The yogurt tastes just like any plain yogurt at PinkBerry or any other yogurt place.
Channi is awesome. I like this one much better than the others in Philly. She nurtures your shape takes her time in every customer.

I'm not much of a steak and eggs kinda guy but I was at a butcher so I got it.  The food was so good I did not care about anything else.  Hand's down the best steak and eggs I've ever had.

As far as carry out goes I got some eggs.  $7 is a little steep for eggs in my opinion but I was eager to try them.  If you buy store bought eggs you'll be blown away!  However I have a farm share where I get my eggs at Greensgrow and they are equal to one another at a better price.

Good gourmet sandwiches, prepared with care.  Similar quality & prices to Picnic on Walnut Street.  Tried a turkey +avocado sandwich and a chicken + roasted red pepper sandwich, both were tasty but a bit expensive.

It is very disheartening when you expect your paid transportation to arrive and leave on time and they do not! I understand there are delays at times however for a bus company 50 minis late is unacceptable !!! The delay of the bus has chipped away at the cushion of time I left for myself. 

I arrived for my 6:45am departure at 6:00 am. It must be standard for this company or route to have delays because people were arriving at 7:00am as if is normal for a bus that should have departed 15 minutes before, they still had to wait. 

Will I use them again? Maybe but if I do I'll make my plans based an hour or more delay!

I remembered this place because my friend got his wife's engagement ring here a few years ago.  I came here to get my rings polished/redipped after the steven singer snobs told me they only do that for jewelry that has been purchased there.  Their lost in any future business. 
Alicia was still there and as helpful and kind as ever.  My rings were done in under an hour and she even called me to let me know I could come pick them up.  When I got back we had a lovely discussion about emeralds since my husband is trying to think of future gifts..  I will be sending him here!
the food and drinks here are great.  The seating is very cramped though.  My favorite item here would definitely be the budino.

i finally tried the Porcos & Small Oven Bakery Washington Ave.. and im still figuring did i like it enough to reorder.. and think not but i'd rate it very good plus! i'd say its a more yuppie kind of pork sandwich..lol dont laugh.. but it's on a toasty crunchy toasty ciabotta type roll.. i asked for extra juice but with this type bread drinks it up & still makes it seem dry.. they put some bit of crunches on it..the skin of the pork.. and it is nicely spiced!! again it might be fantastic to someone but i like my pork sandwiches like momma use to like..long seeded sarcone type Italian bread with provolone & spinach etc. I ordered  bf the meatball ..i see him pulling the bread apart.. feeding a few pieces meatball to 2 dogs..not good sign..lol he said the meatball was tiny bit pink in center and couldnt identify the cheese (he is not a philly guy)  i think it the fresh mozzarella (not his thing). so more a thumbs down from him... anyhow they also have other things that look pretty great.. and having a close by bakery is great.. these delivery services though really add up.. the total for 2 sandwiches was 31 bucks. with tip and added charges.  But think you must check this out if you love Roast Pork and for a small square roll it's pretty fulling..

While in rehearsal at the Adrienne for a show, a number of my cast-mates raved about the sushi from Vic, and since it was across the street from the theater, I stopped in to grab a quick bite to go one evening before heading to rehearsal.  As I scanned the menu, I spotted a 3-roll special for $10.95--an amazing deal!  Plus each roll = 8 pieces so you really get the most sushi for your buck.  It's a small place and it looked to be a bit crowded for the dinner hour, but there are a few small tables and seating at the sushi bar, so stop by and sit in or take-out!

I love this place! Very local and chill vibe. Staff does it all, host, serve, bartend, bus etc. I love the summer time here with the patio chairs, give you a little more space and awesome people watching. The vegan burger is delicious and the draft list and bottle selection is more than you could ask for!

Tried it twice so far - once for brunch and once for dinner. Both times, the kitchen messed up big time. Wait staff sort of apoligized, but not really. Certainly wasn't the waitstaffs fault, and certainly they shouldn't blame the kitchen, but they could have checked on us a few more times and apoligized a bit more for the kitchen's mistakes.

Both times, first course came right away, and then the wait began. We waited, and waited, and waited - told more than once that our food was on it's way - and then waited some more. We watched three tables around us get their food - all of whom were seated after we were. The most amazing part was that I could see the kitchen from where I was sitting and they were standing around doing nothing!

It really is ashame, because the food is rather good. But the mis-steps from the kitchen and the less than attentive service are reasons enough not to return. Wish it  wasn't that way since the nieghborhood needs some good reliable places - this just isn't going to be one of them.

A good choice, with an excellent location a block away from the City Hall. The rooms are big, and the amenities really complete (an excellent gym, spacious laundry facilities and business rooms). There is a Starbucks located within the hotel to have cheap breakfasts, and the cleaning was excellent.

I feel the need to echo the statements on here. This is, without question, the best vet clinic I have ever visited. These people care. I mean they really, truly care.

When I adopted my two fur babies from PAWS, both cats originally went to the Mount Airy Animal Hospital clinic, and I was very displeased with the rushed service each cat received. The vet didn't seem to really care or take the time to ask me any questions or ask me if I had any questions for her. The space was loud with frantic, barking dogs, and the facilities seemed dirty and outdated.

Fast forward a year later, and both of my cats were in need of their yearly checkups.  After seeing the numerous 5-star reviews on Yelp, I decided to give Caring for Cats a try. I was shocked when the polite and extremely knowledgeable receptionist asked me more questions about my cats than the previous vet had during my visit to the other veterinary clinic. The appointment was scheduled for the next day without issues.

The offices are clean and relaxed, the ceilings high and the sounds muffled. There are no barking dogs or people screaming behind counters. This is an incredibly professional and kind space. The veterinary technicians ask many questions and genuinely care. Dr. Ruth is simply phenomenal and has the kindest, most caring demeanor one could hope a vet to possess. She even rubbed her hands together to create warmth before placing them on my recently groomed long-haired cat, fully aware that his short fur coat could make him sensitive to her cold hands. And she asked questions and really listened to my questions and concerns. Just amazing.

There are no hints of upselling or pushing services on animals in the hopes of inflating a vet bill, either. When a tooth cleaning was recommend for my 5-year-old cat with tarter buildup, an appointment was instantly scheduled because I believe and trust in these people. 

I took both my cats here during two separate visits and walked away so impressed each time. 

Above all, the prices are reasonable, cheaper than my previous vet for service that's even better. 

I have found a lifelong vet. Thank you, Caring for Cats.
Honestly one of the grimiest/dirtiest places I've been to. Ordered a beer and left. Was my first time there and will be my last time there.

Ordered take-out and they forgot my side of lobster mac and cheese that was supposed to go with my meal. Had to return to the restaurant, which is always annoying. Food was cold by the time it got home. There was a long wait for pickup because you had to go to the bar and see one of the bartenders, who were also making drinks and taking care of other dine-in customers. Food was mediocre and definitely not worth the price.

I've only been in here once when it didn't involve dropping off my magazine. Let's just say it's one of those bars that you end up in when your either pleasantly buzzed, or your looking to get that way. And for what one I was at the time let's just say I plead the fifth (it was a good First Friday and I was with my friend Slim who looks and talks like Steve Buscemi. So you can imagine.).

It sure is small, it doesn't have much in music or entertainment, and it doesn't have the noteworthy alcohol selection that most bars have. but like several people have alluded to in previous reviews this is a true neighborhood bar. believe it and get ready to walk into some elaborate and entertaining drunken  conversation. and now I really want to see that muttish black dog sitting on the barstool that Will was talking about.
They made my car look brand new. They came to my job, paid attention to detail and the guys were very nice. Would definitely recommend.

We went there last Friday night for a romantic dinner. Was the first time for us there. The service is impeccable, our waiter was knowledgable and introduced the concept to us which is phenomenal by the way. Food is authentic from various regions of Italy. Try the agnolotti!

The young lady who works the desk is prompt and professional. We had title transferred in under 5 minutes. 
The office is clean and nice and there is some parking out front.

Went here on Christmas Eve for my bday and it was pretty dead, as expected. There isn't a sign outside so just in case you've never been there before--it's the door to the right, upstairs. Once I walked up I realized I had been there before years ago and had a Greg Kinnear sighting (he was filming a movie in Philly at the time. Note:he's really short). Anyway, they had some really great silhouette artwork on the walls and the bar--looked like ink drawings of famous people (the Jackson's were well represented). I ordered a Cosmo and the bartender gave it to me in a whiskey glass with way too many line particles in it. I gave it back and told them I ordered a cosmo, not a whiskey and that I'd like a proper glass please. The bartender seemed annoyed but come on! After that my boyfriend bought a bottle of champagne and the bartender was all smiles from that point on. He sort of looked like he could have been Common's younger brother, so I told him and he chuckled a bit. By the time we were getting ready to leave it actually started filling up quite nicely! I was surprised and if it wasn't for an early wakeup the next morning I would have put on my dancing shoes! I think this place would probably be a lot better on a non-holiday, however I wouldn't pay a cover to get in. It's pretty small and I think I'd get annoyed if there were too many people in there. Overall, customer service was ok, the drinks got better, and the dj was good.
Hubby and I went to see Lydia at the Adrienne and the show was phenomenal! We look forward to catching more plays here in the future.

Got the taste of maine tonight. the lobster roll was great, however, the shimp and crab rolls were extra salty almost unbearable. Had to was them both down with root beer. will definitely go back but just for the lobster roll.

I been dying to come here for some time now and when I realized I was only staying 3 blocks away I made my way here. The entrance its a bit hard to find but there was an employee outside smoking and he just nodded when we looked confused. 
It was about 7pm when we arrived and the place was only half full so we didn't have to wait which was awesome. We got a booth and right away I couldn't stop looking around since this place its so cool and just full of history. 
Our waitress came by and explain the menu once we told her we never been here. I decide to go with the bartenders special and she asked me some quick questions about my drink preferences and then headed off to get our drinks. Don't recall what the name of my drink was but it was delicious and strong.
Unfortunately I had dinner reservations somewhere else and couldn't stay here longer but I really loved this place super intimidate.

I tried their original fried chicken and garlic soy sauce fried chicken.  It's flavorful and nice crisp outside, juicy inside!  Really satisfied my and my husband craving for nice fresh fried chicken!  I'll try some other dishes next time.  Back soon!

I've been going to Lee's for years for various needs - especially to get keys made.  Every time I've been in here, everyone has been super nice.  They have a variety of overpriced and normally priced objects, some of which I'd definitely invest in if I lived closer by or didn't have a car to drive to a place with a more reasonably priced item.

Recently had to take my car there after being sideswiped. It's not too far and it was Allstate approved so I figured it would not be a hassle. These guys were great. I had updates twice a week on the progress of the repair and they listened when I told them something wasn't right with the alignment. It took longer than what I had expected but overall the work seems to be too notch.

My husband and I married here a year ago and everything was perfect! The views are amazing and they work with your budget. All of our guests raved about the views and the food. We did the stations and this is NOT your average catered wedding food. The food and cake were phenomenal. All of that being said, what really set our wedding apart was the service we received. As a bride, they totally eased any stresses and anxieties. On the wedding day, the bride/ bridesmaids and groom/ groomsmen each have a room to get ready in with light refreshments. The bride and groom each had a wedding planner with them to take care of anything. I literally did not have to worry about anything on my wedding day. They make sure everything is set, they make sure you are where you need to be when you need to be there. Ali and Charmaine helped with my wedding and after being to other weddings, it is obvious how much smoother things run with their help. I definitely recommend getting married at top of the tower!

I was told that Dalessandro's was one of the best places to eat an authentic,delicious, cheesesteak. I have to say that the sandwich was amazing, I ordered it with provolone and grilled onions with pepper mix on the side. The experience of ordering and talking to locals was fun since I'm from NC and establishment reminds me of places I go to that serves pork barbecue in the south. I'm sure Dalessandro's has a lot of reviews similar to this one but a good ol'boy from the south gives y'all two high fives

Oh My goodness.  Do NOT rent from this company.  They literally raised my rent at the height of the pandemic, then, this month, overcharged me by over $7,000, then asked me to basically deal with it- I got an "Oops, sorry that happened but it was not on purpose- why don't you go call your bank? "  They said IF I couldn't get a stop payment, they couldn't reimburse me until at least a week later.
This company will not take care of you and also will charge you as tenants if they deem certain issues to be your fault.  Absolutely avoid these people!!

3.5/5

Nice friendly staff, store was clean, superb location right off the SEPTA, and this location had everything in my size. The BRs by me in Buffalo NY never carry my size. 

I'd go here again.

I first tried Underdogs when they made an appearance and provided some of their specialties at a Yelp event.  From that point on, I schemed about going to the full restaurant.  After talking about it for several months, I went into the city specifically to go to Underdogs.  Took a 50-minute train ride for hot dogs.  But they're really so much more.  They're more of an adventure than just a hot dog.  You can go anywhere for a regular old hot dog, but these are huge, delicious hot dogs with such a creative array of toppings.  Prices are reasonable and the fries that accompany the hot dogs in the combo meal are great as well.

I've tried four of their dogs so far and am determined to try them all so that I can get my name on the wall.  It's going to take me a while (23 hot dog flavors in total with regular specials to accompany those), but it's going to be awesome every bite of the way.

THE WORST EXPERIENCE OF MY LIFE. Took my car in to replace the ball joints and revived a call that the mechanic broke off a piece from my car making it inoperable. I tried to explain to them that I was moving out of state in a few days and needed my car to drive there. After dragging their feet they finally found the part that was missing and attempted to charge me an additional 1100 to fix it. There was no accountability all the way to the top because the service manager actively avoided speaking to me to resolve the issue. Now I'm currently displaced, without a car and they haven't done anything to make the situation right. It's apparent this particular pep boys doesn't care about its customers.

These people are extremely unprofessional and have no idea what is going on at there properties, nor do they care unless some sort of calamity brings them from behind their desks.  If by any chance you have the unfortunate experience of ever coming in contact with Debra you'll see why her own workers refer to her as a "bitch".  If you want to live in the shittiest place in the neighborhood Solo is the place to go; mice, cockroaches, horribly maintained buildings and the smuggest  management in town.

I enjoyed this bar because 1) there are several photos of dogs on the walls and 2) the bartender is originally from a town near my hometown.

It was a bit crowded on a Friday night, which is to be expected.  There was a long wait for a table but I also watched the hostesses take their time with giving empty tables to people who were waiting.  They have a pretty good beer selection to choose from, I just wish we had been able to try the food.
No. Just no. irked to where i dont even want to explain.

A great location and relaxed atmosphere. The coffee is good and the food is decent but the tables are extremely sticky.

Do yourself a favor and grab a Philadelphia weekly paper and spread it over the table top.

I've been going to them for years, they're honest (even when they know you're not going to like what they say), their prices are reasonable, and most importantly I trust their work. They get the job done, these are the guys I always go to for bike work in West Philly.

Also - they were the cheapest and fastest tube fix, back before I learned to patch my own (which they even taught me how to do!)

At first I was only going to give 3 stars. But after seeing the pic of the Star Wars sushi... 4 stars is a must (I'm a big fan)!

Anywho, the service was excellent and speedy; I didn't have to ask for water refills once (I drink a lot of H2O)!

As for the sushi, YUM. I was very pleased with my Spider Roll... the price was fairly decent too! can't wait to go back :)

I have experienced satisfying visits to this bar.  There is more seating than some other local "Belgian beer bars", including some outside tables.  Beer list is good, food was good.  Not my favorite in Philly, but a SOLID choice.  Go for it!

Love this local neighborhood spot! A lot of options to choose from, the cute hipster vibe is inviting and the staff is super friendly! Loved the atmosphere and all the little cute plants around. Will definitely be back soon!

I started coming here as soon as it opened and I've been to so many nail salons in the city and this is by far the best. It is so sanitary in here especially during Covid, the staff is so friendly and accommodating and they use the best ingredients. It is also very luxurious and while they do charge a little more than others in the city it is by far worth it. My go to forever!
Tony is extremely reliable and a very trustworthy mechanic!! He's also nice, easy to deal with and affordable. And the garage is always so neat and clean!
Great prices to make tasty treats! About $1.00 for a bun/bread. Light fluffy and creamy. So far the almond bread bun is my favorite. :)

While a student at Drexel Unviersity New Number one was my go to place for take out chinese food and is usually where I get Chinese when in the area.The food is good, nothing special, but good, and the delivery time is very quick. The price is very reasonable and if you order more than $15 you get a free egg roll which is always nice, especially if you're ordering as a group and they aren't aware of the complimentary treat (don't tell my friends I took advantage of them for years, please). My usual order is always chicken and broccoli with brown rice and if you order this dish during lunch the "lunch special" price is about $3 cheaper than the dinner price, so that's a plus.

In the end New Number One is your run of the mill Chinese take out joint with a bit too much sodium and a bit too much enthusiasm from the order taker, but the price is just right and the delivery time is super quick so give it a shot!

Around 7 on Monday I came in to talk about memberships because I just moved to the area , there was a blonde girl at the front (she told me her name was Brooke), she looked extremely annoyed when I asked her for help , she rolled her eyes and huffed and puffed when I asked her for a tour and asked her why I should come to giant ! I was appalled , then a very nice dark haired girl Morgan came over and asked me if I needed any help I guess she could tell that I was annoyed , she was so helpful , she answered all of my questions she was so nice and not annoyed with the million questions that I asked. I ended up not getting a membership at giant just because of the girl Brooke.

Great authentic Chinese food, even as a vegetarian. When you first sit down, you are provided some complimentary light tea, salted peanuts, and pickled vegetables. The tea was quite light and refreshing, while the peanuts were a bit too salty, but still good, while the pickled vegetables had very strong ginger tones that were a bit off-putting. For the main course, we ordered the Home Style Tofu as well as the vegetable lo mein. Both dishes were incredible! The home style tofu is actually the same as their braised tofu in brown sauce, but apparently it is a bit spicier. The spice levels in the dish were perfect, not requiring any additional hot sauce nor glasses of water to relieve the heat. The tofu itself was cooked wonderfully with a firm outer texture and soft inner texture. The dish also contained some vegetables like mushrooms and onions. The brown sauce in the dish was also perfect, both in quantity and in depth of flavor, coating the tofu and vegetables. Definitely will order this dish again.
Onto the vegetable lo mein, which you may mentally be underselling to yourself. With the Americanization of many Chinese foods, such as lo mein, people may undersell this to themselves mentally as some sort of lower quality take-out dish. Fortunately, E Mei has come to demonstrate the authenticity and deliciousness that can be obtained from these egg noodles. While you can still definitely tell these are not the most authentic Cantonese lo mein noodles, they definitely take a step up from the bland American-style lo mein. This was an excellent choice to complete the tofu dish served with rice providing a culinary that will definitely bring me back again next time.
An additional plus is the great price for both of these dishes!
This restaurant could definitely work on their organization a bit more though as they lacked enough menus for our table and probably other tables too. For people coming to a restaurant for the first time together, that was pretty disappointing.

DO NOT MOVE IN ADELPHIA HOUSE 
If you know what's good for you, and actually read the reviews they are all true. I have lived here for 3 years now. I even have referred people to live here. (Which was a major regret) if you like not having proper running water which they will make up excuse as to why the water shut off, but to me if things get fixed the proper way the first time than the problem should not continue another 10-15  times more. Or the fire alarms constantly going off forcing you out of your apt for sometimes for an hour. No heat on the coldest days of the winter. Or a cracked window that they make excuse after excuse as to why they can't fix it. Better yet if you like being woke up before 6 am because they are remodeling empty apts so they can keep screwing more people over. Than this is the right place for you! This building is so clearly in bad shape and should be shut down. The property management is a joke. Kathy Basilo is a joke to talk to she acts like everything is a hassle for her to do, because why should she have to move from her desk to do anything. She wakes up at her house and showers before work. Why should she care about her residents. Since she is such an issue to talk you can only really talk to one property manger Christina. Which she is not much better. I came on yelp to see if any residents felt the same way I do, and clearly they do. I also emailed the lady who "responds to the emails" Kate which she is just as unhelpful as my property management. She will just try to compensate the money you spend on heat and water to make up for it. Which is what should happen regardless since you are paying for something that does not work. I didn't really care about the 50$ they took off my rent when I had no heat on the coldest day of winter and was snowing. While property management just apologizes through an email. PMC is honestly a disgrace. The only helpful person and reason I signed my lease was because of Patti Gorman. To any one who signs a lease to this place after all these reviews good luck. The only reason I gave one star is because you can't post unless you give something, but let me be clear this building deserves no stars.

I cannot say more wonderful things about Ariel Morgenstein, who was our realtor during our home buying process. She's responsive, patient, knowledgeable, beyond helpful for a new homebuyer, and every other positive adjective you would want to use on your realtor. She made our homebuying process so easy and actually enjoyable. The owner of my previous home (we rented from a friend) used JG for property management as well, and they were also incredibly responsive and efficient on the rental side. Could not recommend JG more highly!
I stopped at the Big Ass pizza place. Got a slice, took one bite, and tossed it. They have some nerve to charge $3 a slice

My girlfriend made us pop in here when she saw it while we were passing by and it turned out to be the perfect treat for a hot, summer day.  All of the mini popsicles are fun, fruity flavors and because they are relatively small and made with fruit, they are a guiltless treat.  We tried the raspberry lemonade and minty watermelon pops and both were great, but I felt that the raspberry popsicle had the most flavor.  Lastly, they popsicles were very reasonably priced and to help them out in the colder months, they also serve coffee and some nice baked goods.
CT provided delicious, thoughful and creative dishes. Every plate contained multiple flavors and textures. His presentations were simply beautiful. CT is a true pro. I would recommend him to anyone.
Delicious beers & really enjoyed the outdoor seating and the area across the street from the brewery.

I had a tough time deciding whether to give Farmicia 3 or 4 stars. I wouldn't say I'm stingy with my stars, and I definitely don't have a set rubric for every establishment...hmm...I give it 3 stars for the food, 4 for the atmosphere. 

The location is very convenient with lots of parking options nearby. ( I personally prefer the garage on 2nd just Norht of Chestnut)  I really like the layout and feel of the restaurant. I also like the eclectic decor and loved seasonal decorations that were still up. 
Bathrooms were modern and clean. My only issue was that the dining room was very bright (brighter than most restaurants for dinner service) and the bathrooms and the hallway leading there were very dark. It was sort of disorienting... Otherwise, loved it.
I love the selection of "Tonics" ... I went with the Tuscan, which was 
uber lemony, and tried the Sextini for Her which had a nice mild taste.

The food was on the better side of just ok. I got the crabcake appetizer, and  although it wasn't "jumbo lump" like the menu says, it wasn't bad. It was tasty, there wasn't a lot of bread type filler inside like lots of crabcakes out there, but it was a little too dense and rich, and the crabmeat was quite stringy. I wasn't thrilled with my choice, but I wasn't left wishing I had gotten something else.
Friends who got the pasta with tofu and grain salad both enjoyed their selections. 
For the main course I had the duck with "crispy skin and cherry glaze" ... The meat itself was tasty but the skin was super soggy and the cherry glaze was more like liquid cherry pie - way too sweet for me! 
Everyone else at the table went with the 10oz rib eye which was a very generous 10 ounces. The steaks looked huge and were served over mashed potatoes and spinach. The steak was very tender and pretty tasty.
Dessert kind of fell flat for me. I ordered the vanilla bean creme brulee... It was pretty bland and wasn't well caramelized. The best part of ordering creme brulee is cracking the top! I did however enjoy the lemon shortbread cookie that accompanied it! I tried the chocolate torte which kind of tasted like a generic brownie to me, but I'm not a big chocolate fan, so you might love it! The fruit crisp was probably the best selection, but the streusel topping had kind of turned to oatmeal by the time it made it out to the table. 

So,  the food didn't wow me. But I really did enjoy the atmosphere. I don't think I'd go back for dinner, but I would love to try brunch or just go for drinks.

Obviously whenever I'm craving a delicious Philly cheesesteak I make my way to Philly from NJ once in a blue moon. This time I hit up Oh Brother and I was not disappointed. They are a little pricey but they have more than just Philly's on the menu. The rib-eye cheesesteaks are delicious, the bread is fantastic and cheeeeese! Oh, I also got a milkshake which was also delicious. They didn't get 5 stars because for the price, the sandwich can be a bit bigger.

Honestly, this is just a hype. It wasn't anything that I expected. It's nothing special. I am visiting and expected Pat's to be known for some good Philly cheese steak. I would recommend going somewhere else. This and Gino's is a tourist attraction, don't get fooled.

Great food comes with a really long wait! The prices are manageable. 

I enjoy the friend chicken, Mac and cheese, and the candy yams from this spot. I always stop by when I am in town which is usually three times a year. 

The last time I went we waited 1hour and 30minutes. The food was good but the service felt rushed and the waiter only checked in once.

This is a fantastic option for a lunch under $10. "Asian Chipotle"/ "sushi burrito" pretty much nails the description on its head, though I usually think "raw" when I hear "sushi" and my favorite options here are cooked. It is totally customizable, so you can switch up your order to match your cravings.  The recommended combinations are also fine choices with the best IMO being the Hai Street Chicken. Their sweet green tea is DELICIOUS and a perfect balance to your meal if you're like me and enjoy generous pours of spicy gochujang. It is worth noting that their staff is extremely friendly. There's always a positive energy in the place!

If you're torn between roll or bowl, I would go bowl because the nori tears easily and you run the risk of spilt saucy rice, which is never a fun clean-up. My favorite combo is bowl, chicken katsu, cucumber, red onion, red cabbage, papaya salad, yucca chips, and spicy gochujang. Yum!

This place is GREAT!!!! I went there for my birthday and it was a very enjoyable experience. The prices are affordable, the waitresses are attentive and best of all the food is AWESOME!!! I definitely recommend this place for a good time in the city!

We came to Philly for our anniversary and decided to try this place based on a number of rave reviews.  We were not disappointed.  We came on a Sunday night and had the pre-fix menu.  The staff was very attentive and knowledgeable.  They treated us like royalty and graciously let us try things we've never had (like snails - which were so unbelievably tender and delicious - I shall sup on them again!).  The food was excellent - so much attention to detail and flavor.  we both opted for the duck as our main course and found it to be the best we've ever had by far - charred to perfection, yet bloody inside.  It was as good as many good steaks as I have had.  The desserts were so tasty too - not too sweet, subtle in their decadence.  Lastly, we were lucky to meet the Chef and owner, Chris.  He was so gracious and humble.  He gave us a number of recommendations that really helped make our weekend special.  we truly hope to make it bad for their anniversary party.

These people are worthless.  They ruined a nice Hugo Boss dress shirt and overcharged me for dry cleaning several sweaters and refused to take responsibility for either and basically told me they were going out of business anyway so they didn't care.

It was ok. I was expecting more I guess from all of the hype. After I had already eaten, I read about the "this is America, speak English" thing. If I would have known that ahead of time, I would have went across the street. I expect that kind of stuff in Alabama, not in a place as diverse as Philly

I have been going to Joe for months.  I always leave with a marvelous hair cut.  He is thorough and a perfectionist.  He is a pleasure to chat with.  I enjoy the scalp massage and high quality hair products he applies.  I'd give Joe more than 5 stars if it was an option.

It is nearly impossible to make an appointment. I have called for weeks now only to reach their voice mail. I left a message and have not received a call back...... If it wasn't for the fact that I have already purchased a Daily Candy coupon I would give up.

I only give this place two stars because Joe, the bar manager, is such a sweetheart. However, this place is SO FUCKING GHETTO!! I have been a regular to the place going on two years now. With that, the bartender, Chantel, is so fucking ghetto that she literally tried to fight me tonight. I brang my co-worker there for the first time because she wanted good food and a relaxing atmosphere. When Chantel proceeded to get in my face, i was so confused, i asked my co-workers if there was anything i missed and my co-worker said no. I did not do or say anything disrespectful. When i told Joe, he said "she's just drunk!" Needless to say, i will no longer be returning here. I'm not okay with working places where the bartender tried to fight the guest.

EDIT- on my way out the door, she screamed, "FUCK OUTTA HERE!!"
Everything was literally amazing the server , drinks , food and outside dinning . Just AMAZING

I have been trying for over two months to schedule an appointment after purchasing a groupon. Communication has been abysmal... EXCEPT once I finally admitted defeat and asked them how to return my Groupon because I had been trying to schedule for so long they got back to me immediately.
Authentic, delicious, reasonable priced Mexican food. Try it, simple enough. Directions are on the top right, bring ten dollars.

The BBQ riblets (with the sauce) were really tasty! I ordered them for lunch and the server recommended I order a side dish. I chose the flourless corn cake...ewww. The portion was huge but it was for naught since it was inedible. It was flavorless and the bottom was downright soggy. The server asked if I liked it, and when I told her no, she told me the corn cake was supposed to be soft. Uh, what? Soft, yes, mushy, no. This server and another that we briefly interacted with were a little standoffish.

Skip Pat's & Geno's, this place makes the BEST sandwiches, full to bursting with flavor and goodness.  Or if you want, go ahead and get some cheesesteaks from Pat's or Geno's or both, and compare them with any of the sandwiches from Paesano's, and I suspect you will, like me, never go back.

Customer service here sucks. There's absolutely no Organization in the way they service customers. No lines, no numbers no anything. If they don't like your face they won't wait on you period. Ample amount of reps just poor customer service. I should've went to the one on Island Ave. Do better people please.

SO GLAD I TRIED THIS PLACE! 

The place is very spacious and there's outdoor seating as well. The staff was really nice and the food was delicious! I had an egg sandwich, and with their sandwiches, you get to build it yourself. So I chose cheddar with chicken sausage on an everything bagel and I added spinach to my sandwich; I also had a side of rosemary potatoes. The food was great! Good portion size. 

I also got a nutella mocha, and I was a little disappointed in it because there wasn't much nutella taste/flavor at all, if any. Overall the drink was a good mocha!  

I will definitely be back to try the other breakfast/brunch entrees and drinks. They have a matcha latte, which I would like to try. They serve lunch food and have pastries as well. They also make fresh fruit smoothies! I really wanted to get one to-go, but the line was getting long, so I decided to get it next time.

Definitely try this place out if you're looking for a light brunch place!

The croissants, pastries, and baguettes are as good as people say (if their bread being used in the much-missed Pif doesn't mean something to you, it should).  You're as likely to hear the friendly staff conversing with a customer in French, English, or  Cambodian.  They serve mean cups of Counter Culture coffee.

The space, while pleasant, isn't spectacular: it could do with being a bit more homey.  But with baking this good, who cares?
Very friendly and courteous folks... Just landed from Texas and had to make a quick stop to try the cheesesteaks! Can't wait to try them!!
