Nice spot in town for a casual night! Its cozy and comfy in there and the food is great. They have a pretty wide choice of drinks to start a long chatty dinner! 

Tips: 
Ask for plantain bread with their special dipping sauce. You will not regret it! 

For a light dinner, i recommend to try their combo appetizer that you can choose 3,5 or 7 small plates for less than $60 then you can taste all of them. 

Highly recommend to try their appetizer taste menu. We ordered just appetizers and we got so full. 

I'll definitely come back for entrees. I will try vaca frita next time for sure!

On 2-7-19 my First visit to Thomasena's. I was excited to order especially after reading the reviews. Upon my arrival the place nice & clean and did not have a long line and I was thankful. The reason I decided to try Thomasena's was from the reviews and too test the food. My job was also looking to have food catered for a funeral service. Please note I was rather hunger after not eating anything until 3:00 PM that day. I order the fried fish, jerk chicken , Mac& cheese and string beans. Please note the food was indeed plentiful. However after 20 minutes of eating a small  portions of the food my stomach began to ache, and bubble up, two minutes later diarrhea. I am not saying this to hurt anyone, I thought it was truly just me with a bad stomach however, I shared my food purchased with my co-worker and yes you guessed it. Diarrhea as well.  So it wasn't just me!! Eat at you own RISK.

I hosted a late night university happy hour here with 98 attendees. The Staff was excellent to work with for planning and our group was very happy with the event. Since it was a late night event, we stayed at the bar and out on the patio deck.  The fire pits were a huge hit. The place is gorgeous, great beer selection, and the service is great.  i highly recommend for a large corporate party or happy hour.

$10 for a haircut plus a very pleasant owner and his wife. Not exactly a great ambiance but the place is clean and they are good at cutting hair. There are only 2 chairs though so wait times could be longer.

If your looking for great drinks, food, and service, Mifflin is the spot! Between the awesome rotating draft list featuring the best local breweries to the staples of the food menu like the brisket cheesesteak, it is a place that is sure to please.

I have to say the feeling is mixed.. Did they meet my expectation, food wise... Not really, but then price wise, we are not looking at anywhere near NYC prices... $55/00 is like a steal for me, hence my expectations.. With lowering the bars, the food is very good to the least.. I also love to thoughtfulness of the chef making the 2 dishes of the tasting menu different, therefore promotes sharing. Service is wonderful and very attentive.. 5 stars.. Food is where the discounting is... Good but not great. But definitely worth the price..

I came here during the day on a beautiful Saturday morning to grab some brunch and see what it was all about. First I suppose I should mention that I've never been here, so it is possible I was just caught up in the modernness of it all. I love the look of the place, kind of like a planned neighborhood (like suburbs) but prettier. I'm sure the stores are expensive, but when I was there I really just wanted to see what it was all about. 

I went to Darling's for food and had a pretty good experience. Afterwards we walked through the farmer's market and thought it was interesting that there weren't many 'farmers' there. There was a nut and dried fruit guy, a guy selling local meats and cheeses, a cheese/pie person, and a bread guy, but no fruits and vegetables, which I'm pretty sure is a requirement for a farmer's market. 

Anyhow there was also a fashion/craft show going on with lots of stalls with different products. I bought an awesome frilly headband from one vendor and am very excited to wear it out (it's a bit much for everyday wear). We walked around and talked to some of the vendors, and basically had a brilliant time. We ended our visit with a trip to Euphoria smoothies and coffee and grabbed some most excellent bubble teas. 

Truly, I thought it was a great time, and a very interesting space to hang out in. I like the idea that if feels so different from Philadelphia, it basically made me want to move to the neighborhood (probably not actually in the Piazza, but a house nearby could be pretty great.) I will definitely be returning in the future to see what it's like at different times and to visit more of the shops.

Professional. Prompt. Polite. Hard working. Diligent. Careful. Amiable. Shane and his crew of Joshua and Joe worked their magic in moving my entire life across the city in 4 hours. I was recommended to broad street movers by a close friend and will eagerly pass along my own endorsement to anyone looking for a trustworthy, dependable moving company. Well done, guys!

We finally got back to Thanal last night for dinner. Yes they have a great takeaway service but just stopping, sitting out in the evening air and talking food with the staff makes it all seem better. And no mess to clean up! I maybe over stating, but the food brought to your table right from the kitchen is so much better than from a paper bag. Thank you Thanal for the effort to serve us!

All of be benefits of "outside" without the downsides of cold and rain. Linked to the back of Sampan, Graffiti Bar serves the great Asian food from Sampan under heat lamps at long high top tables. They serve relatively cheap beer. Definitely worth a trip.
I live approximately 8 blocks away they told me I was out of the delivery zone...no I'm n an urban area that's y....ignorance ....
Pretty cool place with Karaoke (public) and huge cocktail selections!

The service was polite and good enough, but the food was just slightly underwhelming. I got the Benedict special which was what I would expect from an Eggs Benedict, but there was barely any hollandaise sauce that was supposed to be the special truffle hollandaise. Also friend got cannoli French toast that was just dry. Altogether it just wasn't that great, but maybe if we had brought a drink to this byo place we would have had a better time.

A bit pricey, but good food and drinks!

We got a couple of plates to share. Highlights were the potato croquettes (can't go wrong with fried potato and aioli sauce!) and mama's meatballs (my friends were so shocked it wasn't meat).

Make sure to make an indoor res, I'd accidentally made an outdoor one and it was cold even in November + there were some smoke smells from nearby.
Best gelato and sorbet in Philly-hands down!  Actually, best outside of Italy !!!  So sad they are closing down.  Crossing fingers this will not the last of this amazing gelato!!!

GF and I have been enjoying a weekend in Philly, and this place was absolutely the highlight, stopped by on a Friday for just drinks, and came again the night after to enjoy dinner

From the second I made a reservation over the phone we were treated incredibly. Felt like the red carpet was being rolled out by a close friend. We got there a little earlier than I had made the reservation for, and while we were more than comfortable to wait by the side for our table, one of our servers Bennett went out of his way to make us feel comfortable and welcome, just like he did on the phone, even getting us a glass of champagne while we waited(did I mention we were super early to our reservation? He was so accommodating) . 

Once we got seated, we had some great cocktails and the vegetable samosas. I can't specifically recommend a drink because every single drink we had was awesome. Balanced and creative, just delicious. 

Nitesh served us for most of the remainder of the night. Equally hospitable and kind, we could tell he took a lot of pride in where he worked. Between the two of them, some of the best service I've ever received. 

On top of all that, the food we got was delicious. Tender lamb, juicy shrimp, not over cooked. Raita and naan were perfect.  Big nod to the kitchen. 

Seriously can't speak highly enough about this place, might come back to Philly for this place alone. Huge shoutout to Bennett and Nitesh, awesome dudes!

The toppings are unlimited, which is nice, and they have gluten free cheese, which is a plus for those who don't eat dairy. Dough is freshly made, and for each pizza, you decide how many toppings you want. The sky is the limit! 

Added bonus! Management is very nice and helpful! I had a situation, which they were able to assist. Thank you!! I appreciated that very much!! 

Parking can be tough, and it is not directly on Walnut St..You have to make a left of 15th and Walnut, and you'll find it down the street. Address is 1430 Walnut, but it's off Walnut St...Enjoy!!!

Rookie mistake ordering the steak with mushrooms and peppers.  However, the mushrooms were from a can the the peppers were pickled.  Should have probably ordered the original with wiz or gone to Pats.
Did not dig this place ...i felt like asses were all over my food.  I dont know if ut was because it was happy hour or what.

Believe the hype on this place!  The food and service was excellent.  Since they change the menu every season, I won't get into what we ate because I'm sure a million other people already did.  It is a pri fix menu for I believe $75 a person.  What should be noted is that I do not eat anything funky..aka no snails, no baby animals of any kind, no frog legs etc.  When I called ahead to see what would be on the menu, there were a couple of items on my no bueno list.  I almost cancelled the reze because who wants to starve when they go out?  Luckily, they said they could easily accommodate my restrictions.  They have a pescatarian and vegetarian option for each dish.  I would recommend calling in advance to let them know.  It should also be noted that although they have a full bar, it is also a BYOB and they do not charge a corking fee so yeah it may be pricy, you don't have to pay for booze.  I highly recommend!

From the bright exciting exterior sign to the great service and the yummy food, I'm definitely a supporter of Taqueria Feliz. Impressive happy hour deals! We tried fish tacos (as good as other Yelpers said, and a steal at happy hour price), chili margarita (magnificent), carnitas tacos (amazeballs), and chicken tacos (underwhelming but only compared to everything else.) My only complaint is that you have to pay for chips & salsa but they keep 'em coming so it's not a deterrent from visiting again. Oh, and the homemade ice cream is a great way to end your meal!

i'm not sure i understand the hype. 

the line looks long but it actually moves fast so that's good. would have been pretty angry if i wasted a ton of time on these just-alright donuts. 

we got the following:
- chocolate covered cake donut, husband enjoyed and said it was pretty good 
- maple bacon donut, donut seemed slightly undercooked and just deflated as soon as i took a bite. the maple buttercream was delicious but too much on the donut. 
- old fashioned, i'm a sucker for super plain donuts. this was very yellow and straight up looked undercooked. it was only ok. again, deflated as soon as i took a bite.
- boston cream pie, husband said it was good but again the donut deflated and there was way too much filling that the donut did not hold up. 
- glazed cake, pretty good but nothing special or amazing 
- blueberry fritter, husband really enjoyed this. but i did not take a bite. this is because i watched them making a fresher batch and i noticed that the same spoon they used for apple filling was used for the blueberry filling in the fritter donut. i'm allergic to apple, and i didn't want to risk it. if you have food allergies, i'd be careful here. kind of surprised at how careless they were with mixing their utensils and tools. 

if there's no line, i guess it's worth trying. and maybe their other baked goods are great, but i thought they were known for their donuts. and if that's the case, then i really don't understand the hype and would skip next time at reading market. there's so many other delicious things to eat there!

I'm a frequent Sampan go-er, especially for happy hour. Last week was no exception, when I came here with two of my girlfriends at 530pm on a Friday. The one downfall of this place is the extremely small bar area and the fact that they will not seat you unless you are eating dinner (which usually requires a reservation as they are insanely busy). However, I can look past this since their happy hour specials are just so friggen good. I literally dream of their kimchee fried rice and edamame dumplings and this visit didn't disappoint. We patiently eye-stalked the bar until we saw a few people signing their check to leave and jumped on the seats. We each ordered the house wine since we were in a wine mood. Their happy hour cocktails are usually always good but wasn't feeling it this time. The white wine was pretty standard but went down smooth and wasn't too sweet. 

Then we ordered food to split: edamame dumplings and kimchee fried rice of course, brussel sprouts, pork potstickers, cantonese spring rolls, and kobe sliders. Everything was delicious, as to be expected. I didn't actually try the kobe sliders but my friend devoured them so I take that as a good sign. It was my first time trying the pork potstickers and cantonese spring rolls. The pork potstickers were delicious, came with 4 so it was a decent portion since they are bigger than the edamame dumplings. The cantonese spring rolls came with 2 in the portion; they were pretty good but nothing special. The brussel sprouts were amazing as always. Three glasses of wine and a full belly later, we headed out. Can't wait to be back, probably in the next few weeks for my next fill!

If you want a breakfast of dou jiang (soymilk) and you tiao (fried dough), this is the place to go. The you tiao didn't taste very fresh, but it did taste very fried. Oh well. The place also serves cold drinks and smoothies, as well as douhua (tofu pudding). It's relatively inexpensive, and can be fun for a quick bite.

Since this place offers sushi, Shanghai, AND Szechuan cuisine, you can expect a hugeee menu with almost anything to appease your appetite at any hour. Whenever my mom wants to grab food in Chinatown, she only wants to come here. Also BYOB! For sure, during dinner time at least.

Most items are mediocre (great value since the prices are fair, and the portion sizes Asian mom-approved) but certain dishes are quite outstanding and authentic.

- Pork soup dumplings ($5.50 for 6) , comparable to Dim Sum Garden ($6.25 for 8) but with a much shorter wait and quieter ambiance. However, price does come out to $0.91 here vs. $0.78 at DSG for those interested in $/dumpling haha.

- Rice ball in sweet wine ($5.75), a dessert. Hard to find in other Chinese places. Basically small, chewy glutinous rice balls in a sweet syrup. CLASSIC. Makes me think of my grandma.

- Spicy Stir-Fry Pot, a mouth watering feast large enough for 3 servings. (starting at $32). I say starting because the base price is $8 and then $5.95 for additional ingredients (you have to choose 4). This is not offered in the full menu book, it's on a small piece of paper on the table. If it's not there, ask for it cause this dish is amazing and way better than Han Dynasty IMO. The base itself comes with delicious, authentic ingredients like mushrooms, kelp, lotus root etc. For the ingredients, I had lamb, fish filet, beef, and jumbo shrimp. Ordered medium spicy with numbing but wish I got mild because I was blowing my nose and downing water every 2 bites. It comes mixed in this huge bowl but NO RICE. Must order rice separately for $0.75/bowl - which is annoying BUT REFILLS ARE FREE **happy dance**. 

Service is 5 stars if you speak Chinese LOL. Not all the waiter/waitresses speak great English, so I can see how the quality of service can be lost in translation but hey, that's part of the authentic experience.

Came in with a friend and walked into a warm comfy space.   Immediately greeted by the owner and waitress ready to take our order.  Our host recommended the truely original Dan Dan noodles and the lemonade to start.  The noodles had just the right amount of heat and flavor without being overly oily and greasy.  Then we had the most delicious spicy fried chicken bites. They were super addicting. We had a few other dishes that flew out of the kitchen still hot.  I will absolutely come back...and with friends.

Vivi Bubble Tea was a cute find in the middle of Philly's Chinatown. The place was pretty empty when we went, and they had many health and safety protocols implemented due to the pandemic - we felt very safe inside. I got a taro tea which was quite large, with plenty of boba. The taste of the tea itself wasn't the best, but I would definitely order it again.

Excellently prepared, fresh coffee.  My cappuccino looked as beautiful as it tasted smooth.  A good cafe should have great vibes--a place you actually WANT to sit and do work.  The smell of fresh coffee beans and the purr of quality grinders is set on a background of awesome artwork-lined walls and hip tunes.  The baristas know their stuff and are not afraid to recommend other cafes in town.  Located in a great part of town, too. Thumbs up.

I was excited to try this place since I live only a couple blocks away. The food wasn't bad as it is hard to mess up Mexican but it wasn't delicious. Servings were a nice size but there was nothing special about anything we ordered. The food was NOT authentic.. We ordered the shrimp fajitas, a chicken burrito and beef enchiladas. They were all just ok. Honestly, I think taco bell has more taste. There are much more authentic mexican restaurants in the neighborhood. 

One thing that was really annoying was that they charged us $2.50 for extra salsa but never said that extra salsa was going to be an extra fee. There was not enough salsa for the amount of chips they served so I thought it was wrong for them to charge extra for the salsa.

Maybe this place is better for tacos.

This small but totally cute place is worth checking out. The food was on point and the price wasn't bad at all. I loved the attention details here. From the lanterns on the ceiling to the toppings on the crab California roll. Again the food was on point. Atmosphere was hip, young and definitely fun. The reason why I'm not giving a 5 stars is the drink size. The beer size is as big as a cup of coffee and we had to keep ordering more due to the small glass that it's served in. Maybe this was intentional to increase the bill but I rather you them charge more and give me a bigger glass. #babybeer

After seeing all the positive reviews, I decided to try this place.  I went for a Gel Manicure, and arrived on a Saturday afternoon.  The manicure did take a very long time, but it wasn't really because I was getting extra pampering.  It was partially bc the manicurist was polishing the gel polish all the way to the edges of my cuticles, and then cleaning the excess off with a brush + remover.  I only noticed once I arrived at home that there were amounts of polish at my cuticle that weren't cleaned up, so I ended up having to pick off the messiness at home so it woudlnt look awful as it grows out.  For a 1.5 hour long gel manicure with a bad polish job, I paid $50.  I normally pay $25 for the same service at my salon in NJ....and I would never come back here.

I started taking the Blue Line when I started working in the city after graduation. Frankford Transportation Center is standard and has the different Septa buses that will take you wherever you need to go. Since it's the first and last stop in the Northeast, they have 2 sides to take the Blue Line down South, so the wait isn't long. I would always drive and park near the station since I don't like taking the bus unless I have to. To save money, I would park in the neighborhood behind or on the same road as Walgreens instead of paying $2 daily to park in their lot (a free tip for my fellow Yelpers). This transportation center is located conveniently in the center of convenience stores, fast food restaurants, and grocery stores. They even have it within the station, which is convenient if you're in a rush. The only thing I don't like is when I get off late at night. The whole feel of the station changes. You'll hear police or ambulance sirens, but I suppose that's quite normal in Philly right? Overall, this transportation center is standard and convenient.
I love Boston Market i go all the time and I've never been let down by the food i always come hungry and leave full

This is the best new restaurant in Philly. Period.

Instantly one of the best restaurants in the city. High praise, I know, just go on over and see for yourself. The food is sublime, the service is perfect (attentive and knowledgable, not overbearing), and the cocktails and bar selection are top shelf (literally and figuratively). 

All things considered, the prices are very reasonable. You will gladly pay your bill at Vernick Food and Drink. 

Put it on your list and I guarantee it will remain at or near the top. Get in while you can. They opened very quietly, not a peep, not even a tweet. But trust me, the hordes are coming. This is going to be a hot ticket once the reviews start rolling off.

Oh, the peas and bacon toast, the house made mozzarella, the entire raw menu, the fish, any fish, ... just go. eat.

Got the paneer Masala Kati roll today for delivery and a mango lassi. It was really good! I'm indian so I didn't expect it to taste that authentic but it was beyond my expectations and the lassi has the perfect amount  of sweetness! I'll order again!

Extremely disappointed!  With the rating they have on here, I did have high expectations.   Apparently where I went wrong, I did not order the standard items that are recommended on the reviews.  But if it's a great restaurant, there should be more than a few great items.

Buddha vege spring rolls - bland and burned
Steamed vege dumplings - bland, damn near tasteless
Beef and scallions - bland
Chicken and broccoli - bland

Needless to say I wont be back, there are far too many great restaurants in Philly to give this one another try.

Dr. Glen is fantastic - it is so hard to find a good, trustworthy, thorough dentist and I am glad I am finally fixing my dental issues. I haven't had a lot of good luck with my teeth in the past but Dr. Glen is getting me back on track. The office is friendly and timely for appointments and I can walk to his office, which you can't beat. Even if you are traveling from out of west Philly, it is definitely worth the drive. I am not dreading my future work as much now that I have found a great dentist!

This place is great.  I work at 20th and Market and go hear at least once a week.  Friendly service and good quality ingredients.  Prices are very reasonable and always a consistent product.  May go to items are the Tuna Melt, Tuscan Chicken Sandwich and Turkey Avocado.

I have never browsed the store in person, but I recently had a great experience placing a special order. I called on a Wednesday to see if they had a specific book, and then placed a special order for it over the phone since they were out of stock. I received an email saying it had arrived on Saturday (you can also give a phone number to contact). I forgot to ask how much it cost on the phone, but I am happy to pay a little bit more to support a great local business, especially right now. The employees were friendly and knowledgeable, and it was overall an excellent experience! I will definitely go back in the future, and can't wait to eventually shop there in person.

I can only speak for the take-out since I prefer getting down and dirty with my hands in the comfort of my own home. And it's pretty popular so it gets pretty packed for dine-in. 

Food is great. Flavor is on point and I'll continue to get my seafood fix here. 

I do want to mention that the takeout bags are always clean and secure. There's nothing oily on it and I really appreciate that. Nothing sucks more than having half your food spill out before you can eat it.
I ordered lamb souvlaki and expected a skewer with the meat like the picture on their menu. It came as a gyro in a pita and tasted like beef and was extremely gristley. Gross. I couldn't eat it.

We finally made the time to stop by and try Tandoor. It's a pretty nice spot, clean and big.
We actually ate outside since the weather was amazing. Very pleasant interaction with servers and manager was very helpful.
Delicious food and great size portion as we tried 4 main courses. Chicken Malai Kebab, Tandoor Mixed Grill, Tikka Masala and Chicken Biryani. Garlic Naan is like not other.
Highly recommend this place as we have tried other spot in the area. Great precises and excellent service.

I actually really liked Ms. Lena.  My friend and I walked in and were greeted by her.  She's very friendly and my reading was very engaging.  Regardless whether or not she's "legit" the experience helped me learn more about myself and my wants.  I highly recommend her if you want a quick reading.  She's worth her price as well which was nice to see.

We were intrigued to see what was different about Myanmar food since we are generally fans of South Asian cuisines.  It wasn't particularly surprising that it is similar to Thai.  One dish would have been enough for both of us because the portions are huge.  Service was good.  It was busy but not overcrowded.

we are big brunch people.  have been to all of the philly favs but this is the best.

went on a saturday at about 11 and there was not wait (win!)

recs:

the huevos rancheros is probably the best such entree in the city.  the chorizo is well seasoned and flavorful, the eggs are perfectly done.  it comes with a spicy red chili salsa.  fantastic.

the ricotta french toast is a must get.  the ricotta topping, which is cold and sweet but not too sweet, is a perfect contrast to the hot crispy french toast.  there are plenty of bananas in there to add even more texture.   everything is garnished with chocolate shavings and pistachio bits.  

the coffee is bottomless too.

will definitely be back.

Definitely a warm neighborhood hang out. They really made me feel welcomed as they would even open a little early to give me a cup of coffee. Very friendly staff although there are a few newbies that aren't very outgoing. Food is always good and the wellness elixir is my solid go to comfort drink when I'm sick. Price is the only reason I don't give them five stars.
Solid Arab Cuisine. We got hummus, falafel wrap and kofta kebab platter. Everything was great. Generous portions. Staff was friendly. The space is very simple and clean. Overall great experience!

SEPTA is a completely unreliable form of public transportation. The 507 to Malvern is constantly late, but how late is unpredictable. I can leave my house thinking it's 5 minutes late only to find that it's 20 minutes late by the time I've reached my station. As a commuter, this is unacceptable. I use the train to get to work, some kids on the train are going to school. Why should we all be stressed about being late?

Also, the trains are dirty. Always trash everywhere. And there are never enough cars open. During rush hour, you need to be one of the first stops to get a seat or you're standing.

I wish there was a better form of transportation or that someone at the organization took enough pride in their job to push for real change.

It an ok place to order if your looking for cheap food. This place is super cheap. The pizza is ok, their fries selection is pretty good. Sandwiches are decent. I rarely order from here unless I have a lot of mouths to feed and everyone is drunk enough that pretty much anything they eat will taste good.

I find it amusing that the Gershman Y is home to Jewish high-holiday services and Gay Bingo, but I guess that's a positive quality for a good multi-purpose room.

While I've only attended Gay Bingo, I had a good experience on the 3rd floor of the Gershman Y. Multi-purpose in nature, the tables & chairs set up for Gay Bingo seemed like they could easily be moved or rearranged for another event. The 4th floor had balcony seating that overlooked the 3rd floor, and I believe were more like stadium-style seats. 

The building is a little older, but every nook I encountered seemed functional. There was an option of elevators or stairs, and the areas I was in seemed like they could hold a crowd without actually being crowded. The areas I was in for Gay Bingo weren't too aesthetically pleasing, but again I stress the functional part. 

Three stars to this practical, multi-purpose facility.

they aren't trying to be ruth's chris. perfect little stop for a slice and soda. super clean, pleasant atmosphere and great staff. glad i stopped, hadn't planned on it. if i find myself nearby again, would absolutely return.

I dropped by car off last night because I needed a new battery. The store was closed so Thx to reading reviews here I made an online appt and showed up at the appointed time. I had no complaints- appt was for 9am and the car was ready by 11:20. EXCEPT when I got in the car outside after paying. Just as Catherine C. posted 4 months ago there was a left over paper floor mat protector on the drivers side floor covered w greasy footprints for me to discard AND all my radio station presets had been re-done! Is this a crazy radio obsessed auto mechanic working there who NEEDS to change the radio presets OR is this what happens when a car battery is replaced? I'm kinda suspicious....

Here is the thing: I love to bowl, and have patience for long lines on Friday nights because I understand that other people love to bowl too. What I don't have patience for, is waiting 2+ hours for a lane, then finally getting to bowl only to be told 20 minutes later that there was a private party booked for the space and so I had to vacate. I have even less patience for going downstairs to the desk, only to be told that there were no other lanes available but that I could get a voucher for another night. "I don't live here" I said through gritted teeth. I asked to see the 16 year old manager who reluctantly gave me a refund without making eye contact of any kind.

I'll stay in Brooklyn to bowl. Thanks.
Really nice coffee shop with cool vibes, good (low key) music, friendly barista. Coffee is tasty and the environment is great de studying with plenty of room.

My review of this place is split into two.

For car washes the place is excellent (4.5 stars)

For detailing services not good at all (2 stars)

I took my 6 month old car in with bird poop etching in various spots and a few minor scratches.  The manager took a look at the etching and said that the car would need to be compounded and waxed.  He then said that because the bird etching will require extra effort from his men that the price would be $135 plus tax.  I was fine paying this amount but asked him repeatedly if the compounding would remove the etching.  Each time he reassured me that it would.  2 hours later, my car was finished.  BUT to my amazement at least 50% of the bird etching remained on the car.  The small scratched were also still there because the guys compounding simply did not pay attention and try and remove them.  They went back over the scratches and they came out in 20 seconds.  But this is something they should be looking for and not something that I should have them redo.  I asked why the bird etching came off of some spots but not others and the manager told me it ate too much into the clear coat and he could not get it out without destroying the paint.  We argued a little about it but ultimately I was forced to pay $150 for a compound job that was mediocre at best.

I later found out that products such as McGuire's Ultra Cut compound used by true professionals are perfectly capable of removing the bird etching.  Sadly, I did not know this when I paid for a terrible compound job here.  

The car wash is great but stay away if you want a proper detail.
This place is a joke, overcharged my credit card. Refused to credit me back. Stay away from this store. Fraud! Had to dispute it with my credit card company to get the money back.

This is a upscale restaurant located in the University City area. Our waiter J was great he was very funny and had a lot of personality. I visited this place for dinner and may need to come back as it is known for its brunch & happy hour. I order the catch of the night and it was amazing, super fresh and filling, the other entree was  their house burger which was great!
Had the Crabby Mac on my first ever visit to Mac Mart. Would compare the experience to a blind man regaining his sight.

Nice experience, food and service are good. Their seasonal specials r good. 
Wait is a bit long.

This is hands down the best froyo I've probably had, sure there are other frozen yogurt places that are "good" but if you want the best than look no further to yogorino. There yogurt is amazing and fresh and once you go there the first time you would want to go again and again. So whether you want froyo or gelato as they have that to. Simply stop what you are doing and go get some yogorino whether it's your first time or 100th time you simply can't go wrong.

Also every Tuesday they have double punch Tuesday's pretty much means that you will receive two stamps on your punch card instead of one so go on four consecutive Tuesday's and you have your self a FREE froyo, and as I always say you can't beat Free

Yummy first time here for this California native. Well recommended by a philly friend with excellent customer service and tasty food. Check in for a free huge portion of calamari. Seared tuna is bomb.com

I have been going to Lolita's for many years now. I go their for casual weekend dates or to celebrate other significant events in my life. The ambience and atmosphere is very nice. I used to love Lolita's when it was a BYOB. The quality of the food is the same since it reopened but the recipes that I fell in love with 7 years ago when I first moved to Philadelphia have changed. My favorite is the Enchiladas verde. Before they were served with tofu but with the reopening, they are now served with mushrooms. I love mushrooms so I don't mind it but I do miss the way they prepped the tofu. It was amazing. The BYOB Lolita used to have fresh fruit margarita pitcher mixers and you bring your own Tequilla. That has changed now but luckily they still offer some fresh fruit margarita pitchers that aren't loaded with sugar and you can actually taste the fruit. Lolita's will always be one of my favorite philly restaurants.

Tried the moussa and soy garlic flavor fried chicken. Decided to try this place finally after hearing so much about it. Maybe part of the problem was walking in and expecting a little too much.  Maybe another mistake was not getting one of their specialty doughnuts. 

The flavor combinations for the chicken was definitely interesting. The soy garlic was crispy and sweet and delicious. The moussa flavor was interesting. The cashier described it as a falafel - like taste. I had gotten that sort of feel while I was eating the chicken, but it had more of a cumin flavor. Although the chicken was a bit dry, it was still pretty good. This meal also came with a complimentary plain doughnut. It was sweet, but a bit greasy. It kind of reminded me a little bit like the fried dough stick that would be served with a bowl of congee at a Chinese restaurant. 

However, I know I haven't had the full opportunity to have what this establishment has to offer. Will update this review once a doughnut is had!
My favorite Yunk restaurant! Food is absolutely fantastic. Great vibe, great staff, and great live music from time to time. I try to hit this spot at least once a month! Nothing bad to say!

My first visit was exciting... a friend referred me so i thought I'd give it a try. My masseuse was Mike, who did an awesome job at listening to my needs and helping me free up some of the stress points in my back.... so i decded to book again today and while i was getting undressed, I happened to look over and tske a look at the fan that was blowing around the room--and I'm glad i did! This thing was filthy as you can see in the picture---it was circulating around the room ( I'm sure just dust as no air could possibly be getting through) if you suffer from bad allergies like I do..this maybe you should find another place that does more than empty the trash in each room. Seeing this fan makes me question how many other corners they are cutting, are the sheets clean, are the towels clean....i think I'm going to cancel my membership because it's a total contradiction to health and wellness. How can one relax in filth. BUT ON THE BRIGHT side they did charge my credit card without a hitch.

I am in support of this low cost clinic, but you get what you pay for. I have been to this location multiple times, have had numerous problems and will not be back. First, my puppy had stomach issues. The vet could not figure out what was going on after several visits and put the dog on antibiotics and stated "this is what we give dogs with chronic diarrhea so hopefully it should help." After one visit at a different vet, they knew exactly what was going on and cured my dog. 

****That would not cause me to write a review, my main issue is they messed up vaccinating my puppy.**** I followed their vaccination schedule and had my puppy vaccinated for rabies at 12 weeks which is required by law. For some reason, when I went back at 16 weeks for the last DHPP shot the lady at the desk thought she knew what she was doing and circled my puppy needed the rabies shot and the DHPP shot and handed it to the tech. We went into the exam room, the tech scooped my puppy up, and the other tech quickly injected 2 shots before I could even question. After I asked her why she got two, she said she gives what is circled. 

MY PUPPY ENDED UP GETTING TWO RABIES VACCINES WITHIN 4 WEEKS AND ENDED UP WITH A SEVERE ALLERGIC REACTION AND HAD BE RUSHED TO AN EMERGENCY VET. 

I understand being low cost but that should not impact the safety there. There was no communication on what was being done when we walked into the exam room. If you do go there, question EVERYTHING.

Fun times on Friday nights! Came with some friends on First Friday for dinner and the place was packed with quite a crowd, most of whom where there for the live Irish music.  My friends and I snagged a table upstairs so we didn't have to shout over the music whilst in conversation with each other, but so that we could still hear it somewhat.   My friend Gwen and I each settled on the crab and vegetable spring rolls, which were good, but not nearly enough to fill me up.  The strip steak sure looked enticing, so I guess I'll have to come back another time and try it!

What do you do when you sacrifice grabbing a decent meal in favor of going to a killer show at The Starlight Ballroom? Go to Chinatown immediately afterwards since your so damn close to it. And that's exactly what I did after I saw Tings Tings tear up the stage.

Rather than go for my usual pick of House Of Chen or David's Mai Lai Wah I decided to go with here since it was my first time noticing it. Since food was so on my brain to the point that I couldn't bring myself to put much effort into deciding I went with my traditional pick Of General Tso's Chicken and some Spring Rolls. And both definitely managed to satisfy the hungry beast that was growing within me to the point that I won't go into any lurid detail. The Spring Rolls where crisp exactly where they needed to be and had a filling center (mine had meat but you can go veggie). They also cooked up the perfect portion of Tso's and had a sauce that was tangier than what I'm used . It was also big enough to hook up lunch the next day.

While I can't comment on whether or not it's as late night as House of Chen or David's, it at least comes pretty close to it. And it has a delectable menu that hooks up plenty of cocktails and veggie options. So it looks like this tide has enough to rise high.

Went for lunch with my Son and Daughter-in-Law who came here from Busan when she was 6 and speaks fluent Korean. I had the signature Galbi, my Son had Kimchi Jjigae, and DIL had the Chicken Buldak. WARNING: Even if you're a Spicy food fan, the Buldak will be a challenge!! Everything was authentic and tasty, but what I really liked was the fact that they're not stingy with refills or Ban Chan like some other Korean BBQ places are. This place is a find in Center City!!!

I like this place because you can go with your non vegan friends and they won't be like ewww. I go here every single time I visit philly and it's definitely my go to if I have a sweet tooth or am missing the flavor of chicken!

I don't really understand why it's usually so dead inside!  This has to be one of the largest bars/nightclubs in the city...  or maybe it only seems that way because you're the only one there? 
I don't know...  multi-leveled... 3? I've moved away from Philly a few years ago and don't completely remember. 

Things I've loved: they had a night of free hot dogs & $5 pitchers of yeungling.  I've witnessed the most hilarious hot dog eating contest (which now makes me eerily wonder.... where do they get all those hot dogs, anyway, and why are they just giving them away?) and also a best stripper contest (not allowed to strip down past their underwear, though, apparently you need a permit for that).   In the summer on beautiful, warm nights their outdoor upper deck is very awesome and refreshing..  cute little terrace with city views.

Things I've not loved: The creepy single people with glazed over eyes sitting at an empty bar that stare at you while you're there. The lack of other people!  I guess this isn't their fault? I don't know.. I don't know why it's always so empty when I think it shouldn't be since it's right in the heart of everything.  Makes me wonder there's something shady happening.  It doesn't seem welcoming, I've felt like I've received glares from being in there and being female. Maybe they were just queens jealous of my stunning good looks and perky natural boobs. 

I'd go back... but probably not until I pre-gamed somewhere else beforehand.

This place gave me my Chinese-food fix, but I probably wouldn't venture out this way just to have lunch or dinner here, as it is a further hike from my starting points. The food was decent, slightly above-average Chinese food. 

One server was running the whole place, which consisted of quite a lot of take-out, as well as the sit-down patrons, so she was doing as good a job as only one server can do given the circumstances!

A charming place where the only thing better than the beer selection is the delightful conversation with it's knowledgeable staff (Does that sound cheesy?). The beer selection is excellent. I stopped in for a birthday gift for my boyfriend and left with an extremely rare bottle of Dogfish Head. If you don't see what you want, ask! Beers are usually hidden behind other six/four-packs in the cooler. Unsure of what you want? Let them know and I guarantee you will be drinking happy.

Anastasia is the best -- I'm convinced that she is my wiser, older self - like a personal Yoda - coming back from the future to steer me clear of sh*t beer and poor life decisions. If you have time to spare, there are a few booths in the back of the store which I highly recommend for people watching/eavesdropping.

I love their "Crab Fries" AKA Fries + Old Bay...I don't love their gigantic portions...

I wish there was something healthier on the menu, but then again...you're not going to CPs for "healthy food" or a real cheesesteak...

Even if you've been to Architeqt on 10th, it's definitely work checking out Architeqt North! The north location maintains its trendy but quaint style and provides services just as high quality as the original location. My color is spot on: EXACTLY what I was looking for and I couldn't be happier! I usually see Pawel, but would trust just about anyone in there with s color or cut if he was booked (and many times he is!) I admit I bounced around from salon to salon when I first moved to Phily a year ago, but I won't go anywhere else now!

For what it's worth... not worth it.

I bear no ill will toward Fork. I've been on many culinary adventures and I had always heard good things. Finally the lady and I went last night.

While we had a really nice and romantic table up front, the service was so-so, from hostess to waitress. The hostess had her back to us for a good few minutes while we stood there awkwardly, and the waitress took an eternity to get to our table in the first place, much less for each return. But hey, no biggie.

When the food came, I was underwhelmed to say the least. I had the filet mignon with braised short rib. The short rib seemed as though it hadn't been cooked long enough to get really juicy and tender the way short rib should be. The Filet was nowhere near medium rare as ordered, and was just OK. My girlfriend's rissoto was so-sotto (hahaha).

Look, I'm no great chef but I've made both filet and rissoto that blew this out of the water. 

While Fork wasn't terrible, I can think of plenty of places where I'd feel good about blowing $200 or so on dinner. Fork simply wasn't one of them.

I love Pat's...their  fries are way better than the competition! There are no indoor seatings so if you go during winter, like I did ( took a cab)  you will be eating that cheese-steak out in the cold ....but it's worth it!!!!!!!!

I hate having to deal with car maintenance just like everyone else but I have no clue what everyone is complaining about! This place was great and quick. The service manager was really nice as well as the guys that worked on my car.

I stopped in last week for new wiper blades and because my tire pressure light was on. I had been having trouble with the back drivers side tire but two weeks ago I just put air in it and went on. When the light came back on I decided to have it looked at. 

They kindly put the wiper blades on my car for me and then I made an appointment to bring the car in today to have the tire fixed. My tire was patched within 10mins and since I needed an oil change too I let them do that. I was out of there in 45Mins! 

Just a reality check for those bashing these guys below- they work really hard to fix the car and have great customer service. With as many makes and models there are out there some things take time. Car maintenance is a part of being a car owner! If you don't want to do it then take the bus. We wont judge you for it but don't take your frustrations out on the guys trying to fix your car when it is YOUR car that is the problem (you wouldn't be there if your car was perfect). 

Great Job Boys! I am very happy with my experience and will be back.

Very young crowd. We went there on Saturday for the 80's -90's music and expected a few older folks, like us, but all there was were newly minted 21 yr olds. With the exception of 2-3 songs, the music was NOT 80's -90's. The dance floor was pretty small and very crowded. We ended up going upstairs for drinks. The drinks were expensive and very watered down. We had to have the bartender open a new bottle in front of us because it was obvious that they water down the alcohol in the bottles. With the exception of a hot dog cart, there is no food. So plan accordingly. The floor was incredibly sticky. I almost left a shoe behind! Bathroom lines moved along well because they had Bathroom Monitors moving the drunken teenagers along and keeping them from lingering, taking bathroom selfies. We signed up for the ball pit as soon as we arrived and were given the option to wait or pay $5 and get in line now. We waited 1.5 hours. Finally we got in line. There was no one really monitoring the line and we had a group of 6 cut in to join a couple who were ahead of us. No one said anything to them. It took about 20 minutes to get into the pit. Prior to going in, you take off your shoes, they give you disposable socks, and a phone lanyard. You get 5 minutes of ball pit. While it looked cool and was kinda fun, it smelled of feet and sweat. Pretty gross. And most of the kids spent their time selfie-ing. There is hand sanitizer at the pit exit. Use it! I drenched my arms, hands and neck with it, then immediately went to the bathroom to wash up. The music was crap and with the obligatory ball pit trip out of the way, there was nothing more to do but leave and get a bite to eat. In conclusion, I'm sorry I went. It was not 80's -90's music and the kids were obnoxious and rude. Definitely not worth the $ we spent on parking.

Ah, a nice, dark, neighborhoody place in a hood that gets dodgy quickly to the south, and expensive and ritzy to the North. This place is a great bridge between the two and not just because they carry La Fin du Monde (translates to: the end of the world) here. This is a great spot for a sausage sandwich and a few beers. Which can become 4. No, make that 6, in the course of a great night or weekend afternoon. Staff is awesome, and the other patrons are unassuming and fun in a way that reminds me of a Bukowski story. I like it there, and I wonder why I don't end up there more often.

Dinner here was certainly tasty, and it was fun to share plates with my two friends.  We enjoyed it, and I appreciated the creativity with vegetables.  But personally, I thought the food was pretty oily.

In order to save some money for my recent wedding, I decided I was going to make all of our bouquets and boutonnieres myself.  Ha.  About a month before the wedding I realized "what the heck was I thinking" and contacted Pure to see if they could help.  

In less than a month, going off of only pinteret photos I sent them, PURE created the most beautiful bouquets, boutonnieres and corsages I have ever seen.  This is seriously not hyperbole.  They were incredible.  More than I expected and just gorgeous.  I have now dis-assembled them (2 weeks later) and have the heartier pieces of the bouquets in bud vases around my house.  They keep on giving and making my world more beautiful! 

I would seriously recommend Pure to anyone and will 100% use them again for future flower needs.  I couldn't have been happier working with them.
The chocolate chip banana bread is gold. Eating it make's me thing of the Gwen Stefani song Hollaback Girl because it is bananas !

I tried Amalias Cafe during the week for a light dinner.  We had it delivered but I am planning on returning next week for a light dinner with a few friends.  I ordered the hummus and falafel, which came with Naan bread.   My son raved abou the chicken Kabob sandwich.  Delivery was quick and the food arrived hot and the price point was very reasonable.  I will definitely be ordering again.

I have been to Jake's in the past.  I came back with friends for Manuyunk Restaurant Week.  I arrived earlier than my friends, so I sat at the bar & it was Happy Hour...so I inquired about specials & glad that I did!! Wines are $5 & they had great picks.   Once my friends arrived..we were seated & the service was attentive.  I ordered the Spicy Meatballs & Parmaseañ Fries...Both Delicious!!  My friends enjoyed the Pre-Fix Menu item.  

I will be back for sure.
they're super nice there and its a great office. they are as professional as they come !

A nice little grocery store. Some fresh produce, spices, and Asian packaged groceries. I'm not sure about their fresh cheeses, dairy etc bc I havent tried them but their produce is pretty good. Prices are not all great, so wouldn't say it's necessarily the best value all the time

The artwork on display is beautiful, the most famous being the glasswork of Dale Chihuly, probably. He has a huge piece called "Flame of Liberty" that spans several floors and is visible from the gift shop. Staff on-hand are polite and helpful.

This museum covers everything liberty and related topics, including diversity, religious and political freedoms, violence - at times the exhibits were hokey (with sometimes simplistic verbiage), but always illustrative (through visual media, games, audio, you name it) and sometimes POWERFUL. I was moved at times during my afternoon there, and recommend it for a visit at least once.

excellent food! We had the charcuterie plate as the appetizer, a small plate/bowl of mussels ( if small is this big, how big is the large?), the duck confit, and the pumpkin cheesecake. Oh my! Everything was delicious. the mussels we're amazing ( we got the genthi version and the combination of blue cheese, bacon, leeks and mussels was to die for). The cheese on the charcuterie plate was not extraordinary but the sausages were very good. I am sure that we would enjoy duck confit much more if we were not already too full, but despite that, it was very very good. I don't have sweet tooth but oh my, that pumpkin  cheesecake was the best! We also had very good beer (even though we are mainly wine people). oh yes, we will be back!

DO NOT UNDER ANY CIRCUMSTANCES ORDER FROM HERE VIA GRUBHUB OR ANY MOBLE ORDERING DEVICE. They never get the orders and then never answer their phones and play it off to the moble delivery service like you're the one who is nuts. I had better customer service from a colonoscopy than from this place. Take your business somewhere else where it is well deserved.

We're here from Michigan for a quilt show at the Expo and a friend of ours who grew up in Philadelphia and moved to Michigan some years ago and she told us about the Philly Cheese Steak here and how great they were so we just had to try one well we found one at the King of Prussia Mall. We bought the steak and cheese with with wiz on it and the sandwich was excellent what 
 a great meal also got the fries that were great even cold we have a steak onion and cheese sandwich on Flint Michigan called Big Johns which is great also I want to thank the people who waited on us and the person who cooked it again Thanks for a great sandwich and experience. We will be back.

I had to go into work on a Saturday in order to revive my servers after a planned power outage. That's the life of a sysadmin, giving up a weekend day here and there. All of my servers came up fine and I had some time to kill, so I drove deeper in West Philly to find a little Indian takeout restaurant I was curious about, a place called Desi Chaat House, which is reputed to serve authentic Indian fast food and a dish that few Indian restaurants seem to offer: pav bhaji. Pav bhaji is a Marathi fast food dish that consists of bhaji, a thick potato-based curry garnished with coriander, chopped onion, and a dash of lemon, and baked pav. The pav is usually buttered on all sides. Service was painfully slow at Desi Chaat. There was only one person behind the counter and he took his time taking and preparing the orders. All the spices were stored in big candy jars and sauces were in convenient squeeze bottles. Unfortunately, a lot of the food was pre-prepared and refrigerated. Pav bhaji is best enjoyed fresh off the grill. Still, Desi Chaat's rendition of pav bhaji gets a thumbs up from me. It's chunky and spicy with lots of potatoes, peas, and cauliflower. Think of a good, warm, absolutely delicious veggie salsa that you eat with butter toasted White Castle burger buns. The other five customers in line were all Indian, which is a great sign. The menu is completely Indian, unlike some ethnic places that try include fried chicken on their menus to cater to the locals. "How spicy?" the server behind the counter asked. "Spicy," I replied. I had been to India and had the real deal, so I expected it here. The price was right at $5.99. Most every dish was $5.99 or less. Chaat update: Since my first review, I've had the pleasure of experiencing many different chaats from Desi Chaat House. The chicken and lamb chaats are very nice, perfect for meat-eaters. The kashmiri chaat is my favorite of the veggie ones. I personally find not much difference in many of the different chaats as they all seem to have as a base some mix of potatoes and chickpeas with different cereals and nuts. The Dahi Bhalla (black gram dumplings) are good. It's like eating a doughball that absorbs the flavors of the sauces. The mango lassi is awesome. I've been getting one almost every time now. The paan ice cream is surprisingly delicious. The first taste was really weird, but it grew on me with the second, third, fourth scoops. Whoa, this is really good! A taste of India!!!

Their pumpkin latte is delicious. Their cold brew is wonderfully intense  
And, the people that work there are so nice.

Ambiance:  It can be pretty difficult to find a parking spot for this Rita's, but of course it will be worth it.  The inside is a little cramped, but not ridiculous.  The place seems a clean, but certainly worn around the edges.

Service:  The two ladies working when I visited were both upbeat and quick to ask if we were being helped.  The lady who took our order and rang us up was a breath of fresh air, very cheerful.

Food:  Tastes like all other Rita's....delicious!  Gelatos, blendinis and plain custard in a cake cone are favorites.

Overall:  I would certainly come back to this Rita's location again.  The staff was great and I love Rita's!

Great sushi and phenomenal atmosphere...on your bucket list, then tick it off!  The menu is explained to you in great detail with suggestions of the drink menus for accompaniment. There is also a seasonal sushi list for the latest and freshest sushi selections. Though one would naturally believe that your dress would need to. E high end also, it's not the case. Attire was casual by most of the guest.
Good food at a great price! Love their $1 skewers deal, don't ever stop this deal please!

$15 for a 10-20 minute head/neck/back massage, wash, and cut

I went to Kevin's for 4 years until I just moved to CA. I am coming back in October and going to Kevin's. I was poor and in school when I lived here and stumbled onto someone else's yelp review looking for a reasonable haircut. This place is a gem

First, the best part of a haircut is obviously when they shampoo your hair right?? Throw a 10-20 minute scalp, neck, and shoulder massage on that. I've actually timed it b/c I was amazed. This is how I'd treat myself after big exams and stressful times haha. 

I would have paid just for the massage. After that massage, you could give me a mediocre haircut and I'd still be happy. But this place gives a tremendous haircut!! I've come away impressed every time. Also, no one talks to you during. There's no forced awkward conversations. I'm a social person but when I'm getting a haircut, I just want the haircut ya know??

Highly highly recommend this place. I've sent friends, they've all been fans!

I had my first appointment yesterday with Dr. Rowbotham and she was great. Super attentive, listening to my ramblings and concerns as well as asking pertinent questions. Since it was a full appointment with physical and I hadn't had blood work done in a couple of years she had the tech take blood and not even 24 hrs later she called me with the results! I mean the level of service so far from this practice is amazing and a full 180 from my previous doctor & office. I also like that they are very technology forward with the patient portal and they have convenient evening and even Saturday hours so I don't have to worry about burning all my personal or vacation time at work for routine doctor visits.

I went here for Saturday brunch with a large group. The staff was very accommodating to our party and helpful answering questions about the menu. The poor waiter probably had to answer every question 3 times for each area of the long table. And each time he did so pleasantly and excitedly about the menu. You could tell that he was passionate about the food, which got our party excited to try just about everything! Everything was delicious, from the pastry basket, to the coffee, to the fabulous flatbreads. Our group was there for quite some time, and able to enjoy every bite while having great conversations. Not once did we every feel rushed out or asked if we were ready for the check. It felt like a such a welcoming place where not only did they serve great food, but also a let the meal be such a great shared experience among close friends.

R2L is the most magnificent restaurant in the city of Philadelphia and one of the finest in the country. Between the food, service, spectacular views, and beautiful interior, the overall experience is unparalleled. Perfect for a sit-down celebratory dinner, yet edgy enough for cocktails on any given weeknight, R2L is dynamic and as versatile as any restaurant you'll find in the country.

We went several times. We went in and there was like 10-15 people. The last time we went there was at most 30 people. No one was dancing. There only like 2 places to sit. Not really a friendly staff. They made rude comments when we left early. I dont think i would be coming back to this place.

I used to love this place, always was good food we would come in about once a month. But the last two times I've been there i was very disappointed, they must of got a new chef and hes terrible. you need to bring back who ever was here prior to about 2 months ago!

Stopped in for a quick pre-Lowes sandwich and was impressed by the freshness of the ingredients and friendly service. Also shocked there aren't any reviews for this place, which seems strange.

The menu is huge and Joe himself is in kitchen making orders. He asked how everything was and offered to wrap up my leftovers for me. The hoagies are HUGE, and lasted two meals...not too bad for $7!

Will definitely return and try out other menu choices. Those egg and cheeses sure looked delish...

This is a great discount mall.

You can really get the best bargains ever.  Try  Banana Republic, Ann Taylor, Gap, Saks, 9 West...the possibilities are endless.  The ride is not too bad either. It's only about 30 minutes from the Main Line and so worth it. 

Pros: Worth the drive! Quality clothing at half the price! 

Cons: Bathrooms could be cleaner.

My bf and I have this thing about trying coffee in different places we visit - and we were super surprised with philadelphia, esp this place!  Loved the decor - every table was taken up by someone with a laptop. I would definitely be a regular here if they opened a place back in nyc!

Went to dinner here during a sightseeing trip to philly. So quaint and filled with history! Expected it to be a tourist trap with crappy food but I was pleasantly surprised!  The pepper soup was great as was the porkchop. But be prepared, the portions are huge! The sweet potato biscuits (a Thomas Jefferson recipe!) will definitely be on my thanksgiving table. Oh yeah, I'm getting the cookbooks!

As a waiter myself, I despises the idea of "gratitude" that is in use in most restaurants. It allows the waiter to do nothing while expecting a tip from the customers. Tip is earned, not a given fact. If a waiter such as myself would like to get a decent tip, we must work hard to make sure everything is well with the customers. Refilling water, talking orders, clearing plates etc. That's why I used to go to Hippot Shabu Shabu. However, when I went today, I was shocked to see them adopting this method of "gratitude" as well. I decided to ask the waiter about when this happened and the reply left me boiling with anger. The waiter told me "it's because you didn't leave any tip last time" which I do each time I eat here. It angers me even more since this is the first time I actually decided on NOT to tip due to how bad the service was. I was out of drinks, food, plates on my table, hotpot water drying up, the food I did order didn't come and only after I asked for some more did someone come to do something about it... this happened multiple times as I was eating there. Yet she has the nerves to ask for a tip, a gift of actual gratitude from the customer to the waiter for giving them a good experience, that of which she did not deserve at all. If you are looking for a place to eat with good service and hard working waiters looking to make your experience special, this is NOT it. 

Not going back again after this, not with being forced to pay for god awful service.
This is the quintessential Philly pretzel bakery. Hot and fresh out the dang oven YEEEEEEE

I'm enjoying my first ever cup of La Colombe this morning and am so pleased that I felt compelled to immediately write a review!  After the first sip, I am addicted!  Fresh and simple are two words that come to mind... My husband and I have been doing taste tests the past couple weeks pitting cheap store brands (I HAD to know is there really is a difference, I know... Silly me!) against more expensive brands, but the tests are now complete. We have found our new coffee! #LACOLOMBEADDICT

Awesomely amazing store! Such a great variety of gifts especially for the Philadelphia enthusiast. From the beautiful jewelry to the cool novelty-ish gifts (whiskey stones anyone?) this store could be a one stop holiday shopping experience. The only thing negative I have to say about this house and their other store, Verde (which also has great finds, including an abundance of cool jewelry), is the sales staff. For a lack of a better word I find them both snobby and unwelcoming. They looked at me, going so far as to almost follow me around as though I was going to swipe some merch. It's a very uncomfortable feeling to have when seeking some retail therapy. Oh well, the unpleasant staff cannot keep me away from the cool merchandise. A must for anyone searching for a gift they can't quite put their finger on.

Gato is always amazing with service and makes booking appointments super easy. Erica is hands down my favorite stylist. She pays attention to your preferences and is always down to switch up your style based on what is best for you. Definitely the best gem I've found in fishtown. They always offer coffee or drinks and make everything super comfortable.

This is a nice, clean, small, art house theater. Only four stars because I can't help but compare it to my beloved E Street Cinema in DC. While both are Landmark theaters, E Street serves booze and has much higher-end snacks (wasabi peas, Tim Tams, etc.) than does Ritz East. I understand why they can't serve alcohol here, due to the cracked out alcohol laws in this city, but I was disappointed that they don't have fancy snacks, as I thought that was the norm at Landmark Theaters. They do, however, have a leg up on E Street because they actually have stadium seating! That is a huge plus for me. 

Word to the wise, though - wait to visit Ritz East until something other than Tree of Life is playing. I want my $10 back. Seriously, if you told Tina Fey and Steve Carell to make an independent film as a joke, this is what they would make. I've ever seen a "film" so far up it's own ass.

The best view of Philly I have found, gorgeous interior and design, and decent enough drinks. 

Was at the bar for cocktails, so did not try the food. Went around 6PM on a Monday. Crowded but eventually found some space at the bar. Only 2 bartenders and the people after us literally waited 20min for two iced teas. Drinks are pricey but you're obviously paying for the decor and view. Relatively small list of cocktails available at the bar. Also took a while to come out. I had the comme si and it was good. I like the Raki which I've seen in a drink before. Nothing to write home about. 

Now why would you go here? Because of everything else. The 60floor elevator ride gives you an amazing view right from the get go. The glass on the high ceilings, the flower arrangements, everything just makes the ambiance amazing. You can get a great view of sunset, the art museum district, north Philly, all the way out to the Betsy Ross Bridge. 

One other negative was that it was confusing as to how getting drinks and seating works. There is a restaurant and bar down the stairs (which have water fixtures on both sides and offers a great view once again) which we presumed we can't go to, but there were no instructions. Then there is a bar on the top with lots of couches and small tables as well. It was unclear if those were up for grabs, or if anyone would come around to take your drink order if you sat at one of the high tops. Took us a while to realize we had to go to the bar. 

In a nutshell, you absolutely have to go just to check out the ambiance and view. Pretty sure there is nothing like this in Philly. You can take or leave the drinks.
I have never once had a positive experience with the meat at this Chipotle. It is constantly repulsive. It is either always burnt or just chunks of fat. I will never return.

Visiting Philly and couldnt wait to try Pats. What a mistake! The meat was so dry and tasteless like an old shoe. The person who waited on me was obnoxious and rude. I wish I could rate it zero stars . Ive had betyer at Subway! Massive disappointment!

The drinks are good but not the best things you've ever had, the employees in there always think they're better than everyone kinda hilarious, over all it's an ok place totally over rated, just keep in mind there are plenty of bubble tea places in Chinatown.

I stopped in for inspection and a question about a knocking noise coming from my wheel well. Everything was done very quickly and professionally, no shenanigans. Very friendly fellas here, the guy at the front desk happened to be a fellow Honda fan and AP1 owner making for some nice conversation.
$10 for two fish tacos. flavors were okay but the fish was over fried and kind of dry and hard to chew. great for post 2am chow but probably not otherwise. open till 3am

I took our dog here for grooming and it was just okay. I did not really love the style he came out with- too poufy looking from the blow drying. But the main reason I'm only giving 2 stars and will not go back any time soon is that it is so dirty. Hair everywhere, 6 inches deep  and smelly too. I don't think they ever clean or sweep. I was worried my baby could have  got fleas!

1. Bussssyyyyyy
2. Tight space
3. Saucy

Consumed:
Spicy Fried squid dish $10.50
Bulgogi $13.50

I really enjoyed the squid dish. The squid was the perfect texture and flavorful with the sauce. 

The bulgigi was a little dry, but still decent.

Give Utown a try!

We really liked their wraps.  They are very delicious.  Veggies fresh, sauce just done right, chicken tender and juicy.  

Although I just wanna mention two things - 1. The wraps tend to have the skin burnt quite often.   2. When I pick up in store, I noticed twice the cashier had her mask off.   I am just super sensitive with masking.  


I would probably order more often if the above two can be adjusted.   Again, thanks for making awesome wraps!

The "new" Walnut Street Supper Clubwas the chosen location for a friends birthday dinner. I had been here several years ago, when it was Portofino Italian Restaurant . This place is hit or miss. The food is good, but not acceptional . The waitstaff also performs with a live band , which is a great idea , however, the music is so loud that it was difficult to hear my dining companions . In addition, our waitress was a great singer, but no so stellar in the waitress department . We arrived on time, and had to wait over an hour for our food to arrive. We noticed that not much food was leaving the kitchen , so it seems that everyone was hungarily waiting. Our waitress did not come over even once in that time to apologize, explain the situation, or bring us any additional bread. By the time the food came, we were all ravenous - which is not the best way to enjoy a meal . In a foody town like Philadelphia, you've got to do better in terms of food and especially, customer service.

The pizza topping wasn't bad, but the base was frankly disappointing (we went for the wholewheat option).  Too thick, and really difficult to chew.  It was almost like they'd reheated the pizza or something....  We would have also liked to have seen some evidence of tomatoes on our pizza.  But maybe that's being a bit picky.
Not sure we'd go back. There are much cheaper, tastier options in the neighborhood.
Good food great service. Small portions

Excellent food and service 
Had the Burrata pizza , it was perfect from the crust to the burrata

If your in the area you gotta try them

Limited but solid cigar selection.  Outstanding bar and service.  Not really into cocktails but if you know your liquor, rum, tequila, bourbon, et al you'll be happy here.  

Thankful it's here and is where I hang my hat when visiting Philly.  Didn't give it 5 stars as there is always  room for improvement.....

I'm not one to order dumpsters regularly. However after I ordered my dumpster speaking to friends there may be cheaper options out there. 
What I can say though from my dealings with Eagle Dumpster is their customer service is everything you want from a company and I leave feeling ok about the transaction. When ordering the dumpster the response time by email and phone from the company was always prompt and helpful. I ordered at the last minute and over a weekend and even though it was Saturday and Sunday they would replied to me. They took the time and helped me pick a dumpster that best fit my needs. 

The man dropping off the dumpster was very friendly and skilled. He dropped the dumpster on the 2x10s my town insists on if you put the dumpster in the street. I called a day before my scheduled pickup date and asked to extend a week longer. They didn't charge me anything for extra time because they didn't have a shortage of dumpsters. 

I on the scheduled day of pickup I assumed they would be around mid day and had not moved my truck that was parked less then a foot from the dumpster. However I heard the pickup drivers truck early in the morning and by the time I scrambled to get dressed and out to move it and give the driver a hand with the tarp he was gone. My tarp was folded neatly on my porch with the bungee cords piled on top. 

In today's world dealing with any business it is rare to see the extra effort and was really surprising and greatly appreciated. Often after dealing with a business now a days you leave feeling somewhat shafted and annoyed. Meanwhile accepting of that's just how life is now. 

However if you need a dumpster I know of one company that actually makes an effort to treat the customer well. So if I need a dumpster again I'll have no issue calling Eagle Dumpster for my business.

Super cute and soothing atmosphere. Food was decent too. Great place to come on a date or with friends for talk over tea with a mellow soundtrack and comfy chairs. Got the hot milk tea that comes in a teapot, very cute but even at 30 a little too sweet for me. The matcha creme brulee was super tasty. Good service.

Hired this company to come out and do my front steps and I am so pleased. The weather has been really sunny so I figured now would be a good time to get it done. Of course the day they came to do it, it rains!!! They were so professional though. They scheduled me for later in the week (Friday) and came back on time as scheduled. Now that everything has dried up there aren't any cracks or chipping and everything looks amazing. I should also mention that Im a new homeowner who doesn't have a lot of experience with contractors. This company has set the bar really high though and I will continue to use them.

I saw a good review on google and decided to try this place. I was a bit hesitant to go inside but I saw all the people buying their lunch. Even the police officer bought his lunch here! 
I even asked a lady if she's a frequent buyer. She told me they have the best wings. 
I tried the General tso and it is one of the best I tasted for only 5 dollars! 
I give this 4 stars because of the location.

I don't like big corporations like this, but target is by far my favorite. Comparing them to walmart is like comparing a chickfila to a McDonald's. Both will kill you, out other places out of business, pay employees next to nothing and look tacky as hell, but this is the better option.

This store is very clean, well stocked and the staff are very nice. I also heard they donated a new library to a local school - very cool! 

Keep it up!

Reno is AMAZING. I have long thick hair that he cuts into amazing layers and I constantly receive compliments. I can't get there as often as I'd like because it's a bit pricey for my budget but when I do, it's worth every penny. Just be aware - he cuts off a lot when he does my hair so if you're nervous about losing length, be sure to say something!

When I last left North Third, my opinion was mixed to say the least - I love their menu, but felt the service was lackluster, culminating in a maddening show of negligence by our waiter. For exactly one year, North Third was on our "Oh No, Not There..." list.

But what if things were different? What if my memory was purged of everything that was frustrating about North Third? What if we finally had the server to match the quality of the food, making it the solid NoLibs attractions I always wanted it to be?

I'm happy to report that North Third is my friend again. I greatly regret not getting the name of our server last Thursday, as she was helpful, friendly and attentive.

I can finally give proper respect to the food and drink selections, all of which still manage to impress. The wings are perfection, the sandwiches always worth it, and the pirogis are still choice. They always keep interesting beer choices from the best breweries around, so if you're a beer fan this place doesn't disappoint.

Mmm... I would have to agree with the first reviewer. I really felt like I wasted money and that I should've tried a different shop inside the Bourse.. I ordered the pork belly with ramen and spicy miso broth. The pork belly was good but it was very uncomfortable to eat.. it should be in smaller pieces, so that I don't have to chow down on a big and thick piece of meat. I was really disappointed in the broth.. it literally had no depth. Just tasted like some miso paste in water with black pepper. It was not spicy at all. I like the concept but I don't think it works. I can't imagine it tasting any better with udon or pho noodles.. also the "salad" that goes inside the soup does not taste good inside the soup. I thought it'd be on the side. The meal overall didn't taste cohesive. idk I would not recommend this place. I'd rather wait in line at terakawa or go to hiro than spend $15 here. I really wish I could've liked this place..

What a great area locale. the crew, food, and brews are all top notch. great happy hour specials on both drinks and pub fare. come for the watermelon double IPA, stay for the Mac and cheese or buffalo chicken dip. look for Paul or Jill behind the bar and leave with a smile on your face.
Really good pizza! It's not Gusto's but a solid runner up. I took off a star bc it's a little pricy but otherwise no concerns. Will be back :)

It's rare that Yelp lets me down, but it happened here. Stopped on the way back to my hotel and picked up the Taste of Main sampler to go. When I opened the box, I was immediately surprised by how little food was actually in it. What was supposed to be 3 half-rolls looked more like 3 quarter-rolls. The two crab claws were stuck in with one of the mini-rolls. Started with them. Wow. Worst, most inedible crab claws I've ever had. The meat was stuck on them like it was glued. Zero taste.
