It's shocking to me that a place with the reviews this one has could have served something that poor. 

Moved onto the rolls and all three were dry and had minimal taste. I couldn't have been more disappointed in this experience. 

To give them a slight benefit of the doubt, I stopped in at 8:30 pm, 20 min before they closed. As I think about it, the food tasted and looked like it had been sitting around all day. Or was leftover from the day before. Maybe the guys working had gotten lazy and were serving old food. 

Hopefully, this was a one-off bad experience. I certainly won't be going back to see.
Rude and dishonest. I'll never let another Pep Boys anywhere near one of my cars again.

I am fortunate enough to live half way between Rybread and its sister establishment Rybrew.  Not only is the food consistently excellent, but the owner and staff are friendly and accommodating.  My "go-to" place for a great lunch.

I attended this establishment yesterday with family and friends. It was the worst service I've ever received on top of our bill being almost $400. We all literally ordered a appetizer, a drink and a meal. The owner was dressed with booty shorts and her cleavage was out. We waited over a hour to receive our food despite us being there way before some of the owners friends coming in and receiving their food and drinks before us. The owner sister and friends were at the table next to us drinking their lives away and being very disruptive with their loud and obviously drunk conversations. She never acknowledged us except when she needed a chair from our table to give to someone else. She also was sitting and chilling with friends smoking hookah. When we addressed that we were unsatisfied with our service she gave nothing but excuses. If you're not a familiar face to them prepare yourself to be treated like an unwanted stepchild. And to top it off I made a post on Instagram about my experience and someone on her team from her restaurant's page left a very unprofessional comment that lacked proper grammar... DINE AT YOUR OWN RISK
Delicious food, great atmosphere. Service was a little slow but we went on a Sunday so that was to be expected. Great vegetarian and vegan options as well.

Great and affordable authentic Chinese cuisine. Limited menu which makes ordering quick and easy. Food is always hot and fresh. Most times food is prepped right there in front of you! Proof it's not frozen.

First let me start by saying this is definitely a College bar. Another yelper below hit the nail on the head, "Filled with D-bags and creepy old dudes at times..." With that out of the way, I am not a fan of this place, especially since they have a cover charge. MEDIOCRE AT BEST. Normally, I would be fair and rate this place with three stars, because there really is NOTHING SPECIAL about this place. To be fair, I've only gone out here on a Friday/Saturday night to hang out. The reason I feel the need to review this now, is due to the bad experience my friends and I recently had. Closer towards 1am, a friend and I were at the bar getting a round of waters for our party. I ordered 4 waters and vodka club. I handed my friend two waters and asked him to take them over to our party. I missed his drink order, so I moved out of the way so he could order from the bar. Out of nowhere, a bouncer came up to him and asked him to follow him downstairs; no rhyme or reason. With my back to them at first I was about to deliver the waters and noticed the escorting going on. I quickly made my way to drop off the waters and go see what was going on. Apparently he was being bounced because the bouncer Tyler said so. Mind you, my friend did nothing to deserve to be kicked out for the remainder of the night. He was not combative, was not drunk, was not falling asleep, was not being lewd or loud, I mean, of all things you could be bounced out of a bar for, he did none of those things. I tried talking with the several different bouncers all who said that if he was ok, to wait back in line and he might be able to get back in. So we did that. still no luck. I asked to speak to the acting security manager named Pete. While Pete was professional, he did not give us the time of day to make a case or even evaluate my friend's current state to see if he could be allowed back in. We frankly were denied and at that point, after spending a decent amount of money for a group of 11 people, we as a group were pretty unhappy with the security service and we all decided to leave. We will not be returning to this establishment nor will we recommend anyone to visit Ladder 15. STAY AWAY, THERE ARE BETTER BARS IN THE AREA.

This place has some potential, the space is good, the service is great, but there are just too many things that make it not worth the cost or trouble. First off the cover charge is ridiculous, especially because once you get inside the costs of drinks are on the high side! Then the music, while there were some jams, the dj either played too short a snippet or followed it with a song that had a completely different type of vibe which was jarring and people would literally just stop moving for a few minutes. I like all types of music but I found this really annoying.  The dance floor is also pack and weirdly laid out so it was hard to move around and mingle, however the AC on blast directly overhead the dance floor was GREAT overall I don't think I'd go back unless someone else requested to for a bday or something just because there are plenty of other clubs that are way cheaper and you can still bust a good groove!
Incredible, flavorful pho. Cozy, small spot with a great menu - so many more things I want to try, if it weren't out of the way for me.

We got burgers from Lucky's and was really pleased. I went for the Cubano burger - which was a burger patty with pickles, mustard, and pulled pork. It was unique and delicious. 

We ordered fries and tater tots as sides - the fries were average, but the tater tots were excellent. 

The staff was friendly and helpful and I look forward to trying more of their menu.

Whetstone is one of my favorite neighborhood places because parents and their guests can have a real meal, and my kids love their kids menu. Good for brunch, lunch, and early dinner for families, and I happily return to their bar with friends or my spouse after bedtime. Thank you for recognizing that parents like to eat well, too!

Move over, Dunkin' Donuts! Beiler's does NOT mess around when it comes to donuts.  Every so often a former co-worker at one of my previous jobs would bring in a box of Beiler's donuts for all the employees to share, so I became more excited and eager to discover what they had to offer on my own time.  While I more frequently visit their location in University City at 39th and Chestnut, their location in Reading Terminal Market has its own unique selections, includes an additional bakery portion that produces delicious pastries, breads, and much more on the daily, and even features combo deals! 2 donuts and a coffee for just $3.25? Sign me up! Don't be deterred by the long line that often wraps around the kiosk; Beiler's is most definitely worth the wait, and you will not regret it, nor will you find donuts as delicious as theirs anywhere else!

I had a spectacular dinner tonight at Buddakan. To start, my waiter, Anthony, was a delight. So friendly and personable (even behind a mask). I'm so glad I was seated in his section.

The food was phenomenal. I started with the edamame dumplings, which were so smooth and creamy. They were a little one note for me, but they were still good. The General Tso's dumplings however, were an absolute religious experience. They came out piping hot and I wasn't quite sure what to expect, but the filling is savory with a sweet and tangy broth. I would've liked a proper soup dumpling spoon, but I did fine just putting the whole thing in my mouth. My entree was the char grilled beef, which I actually picked after multiple reviews on here. It was so good. The beef was tender and flavorful, and the shoestring potatoes had a spicy sauce on them that all really blended together well. I didn't eat much of the greens, because they had raw red onion in them. I would've liked to have seen this mentioned on the menu, but it's my own fault for not asking.

For dessert I had the Buddha's Temple. It was fine. It was probably the low point of the meal for me. If you're a big chocolate fan, you'll probably like it more than I did, but it felt like something you can get anywhere. I also had two drinks: the Mindfulness cocktail and the passion fruit lemonade mocktail. I preferred the lemonade, but it might be too sweet for some.

The restaurant is beautiful, though I will caution that it's quite loud. I was there at 5:45 on a Sunday and the music was pumping, which of course, causes everyone to speak loudly. The Buddha in the center is a fun focal point. I also had a fun conversation with one of the managers who told me that all the dumplings are made by hand daily by a woman they call Mama Han(?) who has been with them for 24 years. She estimated that she makes 1,000 dumplings every day. In all: I'd definitely return and if you're going: get the General Tso's Soup dumplings!!

I lost my daughter at 20 weeks, I was calling to ask for information about cremation services. At first the woman was fine, then I told her I was only calling as to get the information for the cremation service and she offered to have me come down to go over paperwork which I could not do since I was on bedrest to which she replied she could come down to my house to discuss. With all my medical bills and hospital bills I was only looking for a certain budget to which she had not mentioned yet and I didn't want to waste her time by having her come all the way to my house and possibly be out of my budget which is why I wanted to clarify everything over the phone first. She got very irritated once I said this and said "well we offer it for free but if you don't want it feel free to keep shopping around" I tried to very politely explain I wasn't trying to be rude I just wanted to clarify things because some places were charging $300+. She told me to have a good day and hung up. 
Immediately after another employee called (at the time I wasn't aware they were from the same place) , and I told her my story and she said "oh well we offer those services for free, don't worry we can go pick the body up tomorrow for you and I'll call you in the morning" so I call the hospital morgue to tell them thinking that everything was worked out. Since I didn't hear anything that morning I called in the afternoon, she basically said "oh well the owner told me you didn't want our services and you wanted to shop around" I explained to her the misunderstanding and said I still wanted to go through with the cremation and she said she'd be in contact with me. Fast forward over the next 2 days I called both ladies back as well as the main number and heard nothing back, at least answer and tell me that you wanted to refuse the service instead of avoiding me altogether.
No grieving family/parents should be talked to or treated like that. It made my loss so much more stressful to just have that complete lack of communication. 
One very bright side of this is that I went to Brian W. Donnelly funeral home and Brian is so compassionate and professional the experience was EFFORTLESS. So I would definitely recommend everyone go there instead.

When I think of a classic Philly cheesesteak, I think of a sub that is overloaded with meet and dripping with cheese and grease. This is not that. It is MUCH better! The steak on this is not finely chopped but rather thinly sliced. I got mine with American cheese and onions. Once I folded the sub in half, the cheese got gooey and melty and WOW was it amazing. The bread is soft yet perfectly crusty on the outside, like French bread! I'm so happy I came to this spot while I was visiting because it did not disappoint. My chocolate shake was delicious, and the service was awesome!

Excellent veggie burger: freshly made and a generous size. Servers seemed a bit unwelcoming, but perhaps that was related to the time of day (before happy hour) or to the fact that I was getting take-out. I did sit at the bar with a soft drink while waiting, and others around me seemed pleased with their experiences.

The Xi'an food was great，but the awful service ruined everything. There was a rude female server who chase the costumer outside and force costumer to give over 15% as the tip for lunch！This is a bad behavior and my friends said they won't come again！
Amazing, everything about this place is outstanding, you can't beat the prices, the hamburgers are are the best I ever had, and you will go nuts over the pancakes

Best steak I've had in Philly. Nice cozy decor. Not too cramped or loud. My server Brandon was awesome, super knowledgeable about the menu and very attentive. He even laughed at my shitty jokes. You're a bro, Brandon. The steak is a little pricey but if you can stomach it I highly recommend it. Otherwise get the burger.

I was here for a City Fit Girls event for a sports bra fitting (I got an amazing new hot pink sports bra that I'm thrilled with) and I also looked at sneakers. Of course, I hate sneakers if they're not cute (and totally unsupportive) like my Vans or my Converse, but the staff here was super nice to me, despite the fact that I'm a total pain in the ass.

Takia checked my gait and recommended some sneakers. She was busy with the bras, so she connected me with Wylie, who was also incredibly helpful. But then I felt bad about wasting everyone's time because I was really not going to buy sneakers.

True story--the shoes I tried on were super supportive and comfortable and would probably be great for my back, hip, and knee pain. Takia and Wylie clearly know what they're talking about. But they're all (ALL--like across ALL brands of fitness shoe) just too flashy and would look terrible with every outfit I own. Can't these companies make something that looks PLAIN? Or, you know, like Vans or Converse? Anyway, instead of acting totally annoyed by me (I'd be totally annoyed by me), Wylie sold me some really great insoles for my Vans, which are totally life changing.

So I highly recommend Philadelphia Runner. Great staff. And great sneakers, if that's your thing.

Went to Headhouse yesterday for the first time. I had been there years ago when it was Tori's and loved it then. I was surprised that the decor hadn't changed at all in so many years but was hopeful that the food would be good. I wasn't sure what to expect and initially I wasn't thrilled. The host was a bit awkward in greeting us, and the large fan in the main dining room was a little off putting. Our table had a great view of the TV, but I had no interest in watching TV and wondered what it was even doing there. Basically, the ambiance was quite strange. Then we met our server,  and my opinion of the experience did a full 180. Jerry was AWESOME. He was so sweet and kind,  had a very welcoming personality and was knowledgeable about the menu. He gave us great recommendations and we thoroughly enjoyed our meal. My boyfriend and I shared the calamari, crab soup, lobster grilled cheese, and clams & linguini. All in all the food was pretty good. We enjoyed everything, and the food clearly consisted of fresh,  quality ingredients. The truffle and asiago fries were pretty great too. Bottom line: go for the food, not the ambiance, and hope Jerry is your server.  He was the bomb.

Never had the pizza here even though it seems like it's what they're known for
This is my GO TO for buffalo chicken cheesesteaks! Definitely recommend
At long last, I've found my salon. Architeqt is great! All the staff are super friendly and knowledgeable and Sequoya, my stylist, is amazing with balayage and cuts. I even just brought my mom in.
Bad service, the waitress/ cashier is mean and keep asking for tip. Didn't even ask me if I want water and never came to refill my glass. Don't come here

I've been to the Mann a few times and have found it a fine place to catch a show or a concert, but I wanted to specifically shout out a wonderful usher. 

My friend and I arrived at the Robyn show last night JUST as a crazy storm hit and everyone was brought under the roof. We were soaked, confused as to what was going on and where we should go and an usher named Marilyn was extremely helpful, courteous, and pretty much made our night better. I know staff don't often get praised when they deserve it, so I really wanted to highlight her here. 

Again, the venue is great, but kudos to a terrific staffer who is VERY good at her job!

When Birra first opened, I was unsure of another pizza place on Passyunk.  However, I am happy to say that I was quite wrong.  They have excelled as the new, hip eatery on the Avenue!  

The atmosphere helps to differentiate the place from the rest of the eateries, or even Italian-inspired eateries in the area.  It is trendy, but the music they play is Motown which adds to the urban-cool vibe that seems to bring this place down-to-earth and mellow.  The crowd ranges in age and culture, which is great to see as the diversity adds to the atmosphere.  

They have a great selection of microbrews on tap, and their pizza and menu is exquisite, and the staff is very knowledgeable about their beverages and menu options. Some of my menu favorites?  The Wild Mushroom Salad that has a unique assortment of exotic mushrooms blended with melted fontina cheese and a hint of lemon.  Their pizza ranges from traditional to creative with their Mac and Cheese Pizza (ohsogood).  Dessert tends to be seasonal.  At Thanksgiving, they had a Pumpkin Tiramisu which was so delicious, we ordered a huge cake for our Thanksgiving dinner!

My only critique is that the indoor seating tends to be a little cramped and awkward.  This place usually gets crowded, especially on weekend nights.

Service is everything and this place was lacking in this department.  Our server seemed to be intoxicated but maybe it was her personality until she begun to confuse things about our bill and order.   Manager comped out dessert grudgingly but when we told him of the huge Roach that decided to die next to our table,  he said it was normal and at least it was dead.  Hmmm food was delicious but service left a bad taste in my mouth.   Will not be returning... very disappointed in how this was handled and don't want to eat where roaches are the norm.

Everything was great at Zahav. The ambience in this place is so welcoming. Such a marvelous menu, it was very hard to choose, but I am really happy that I got Crispy Haloumi. Let me not forget the servers, who were on top of their game and provided attentive service. Definitely will come back to Zahav always.

LOVE this place!  Moved into a place around the corner a little over a year ago and was so stoked when this place opened!  The bartenders are super knowledgeable about their beers on tap that change pretty frequently.  The food is also stellar!  If you are hung over on a Sunday (as we all experience sometimes) you MUST go and have the breakfast fries.  Fries smothered in Tabasco, gravy, and topped with an egg.  I also had a truffle cheesesteak once which was absolutely wonderful.  If you havent yet discovered this place make the trek out to point breeze and you will not be disappointed!

Ive been here a few times, but this time, i tried their "cookie-which". I got the eggnog ice cream and S'mores cookies. It was so good! So filling! It was so huge as well! It was about $7, but, It was worth the price.

Super unique and a fun art museum. The mosaics are very beautiful and impressive, however, I felt it was overpriced. We only spent about 20-30 minutes in the museum so, it wasn't really worth the money. Plus, considering the entire area has pottery/ceramic work all over and the front of the entrance is also decorated, you could save yourself some $$ and just admire from the outside. It also seemed a little grungy and dirty -- like the art could use some cleaning.

What a great location. I always have gone to springboard media for all my apple needs. Staff is very knowledgeable and couldn't be more helpful. Free loaners with service if you buy your Mac there. Now a location closer to where I live and work.
Zahav's baby brother.  I love Zahav, and love this place!  Fantastic hummus + amazingly chewy, warm, fresh-made-in-front-of-you pita + salad and pickle accompaniments = awesome lunch.

Just what we were looking for on our family visit to historic Philadelphia.  Enjoyed the atmosphere and the food was good.  Honestly, though, we've eaten at similar colonial-era restaurants where the food seemed more authentic.

Ehh. I had a falafel bowl salad and was underwhelmed. I asked for the spicy sauce my husband had on his pita falafel, which was way better, but they wouldn't give it to me. Oh well. Next time I'd get the pita falafel.

We really enjoy Lucky 13. From the outside it may look like a dive or tattoo parlor, but the inside is really cool. Neat Schmidt's of phila. Memorabilia & other old school stuff lines the shelves. Head banger music posters line the walls. Excellent Juke box. Always a good selection of craft beers on tap. The most surprising thing about this bar is the menu. The food is awesome, as good as any gastro-pub anywhere. The food selections are diverse
 Enough to satisfy almost any taste. They have everything from Meatloaf to really good vegan dishes. Check out the Ace Of Spades sandwich, quite possibly the best vegan sandwich in Philly. The staff is also great, Clarke who owns the place and also bartends at times is always a pleasure. My wife and I always make a point to stop in on Saturdays in the late afternoon to see Katharine who bartends Sat afternoons. She too is always a pleasure to visit. A really fun neighborhood pub.

Great take out location, so excited it opened up in the neighborhood. Their delivery and service is such a plus during the pandemic shutdown. I have already tried the sushi and the chinese take out on the menu and not disappointed! The quality is good, the portion is good, and the taste is great! I am impressed by their spider roll, dragon roll and the hand rolls. Honestly, the price is such a plus. I will be ordering from here often and excited to try other items on a lazy night!

First off, the girl that manages appointments and arrivals is super on top of it, professional, and friendly. CJ pierced me (nips and ears). He was also super professional yet relaxed/friendly, and made me feel at ease. Can't suggest this place enough.

This place is charming and well stocked with interesting things-- they sell acaii juice and cocunut juice here.  I also love that you can buy produce with out going to the supermarket and the bananas are so inexpensive, just the other day they were selling for 50 cents a pound.  They also have a great selection of peanut butters and nutella, and products like them.   I bought a turkish nutella-like product that was a mix of regular hazelnut spread with a swirl of a milk-hazelnut spread.  I like to think that because it's turkish it is somehow healthier and not as fattening. (i know, i know...) Anyway great little convenience store and moderately priced!

Excellent food, portion size is just right.  I like the creativity of the chef, and the serving staff is very attentive and will give you a recommendation.  The fact that this is a BYOB is an added bonus.  Keep up the great work in this hidden gem.

THE most amazing food! The service was great, the atmosphere was a romantics dream.  I sort of liked the BYOT, makes the whole event a little more personal. Cant wait to return... Ohh and the Mahi Mahi is Unbelievable!!

I often dont love when Indian food is dressed up and become fancy. I have been to several such establishments in DC and NY and usually just leave feeling like though I ate good food I would love some more of the more traditional dishes or at least takes on them. IndeBlue manages to provide good service charming decor while cooking up some traditional (with slight twists) indian dishes. The Naan is executed perfectly with tremendous fluff and flavor. One of the most popular dishes seemed to be the fried cauliflower which was a huge portion though in my opinion not flavorful enough. Overall a great indian restaurant in Philadelphia, possibly my favorite.

Most of the time, the pho here is pretty good. It's never been bad.

Ordering over the phone is super quick, and they never verify my info. I just shout my order into the phone, they confirm what I shouted, and then the phone call is over. They know that their pho is so good, a no-show is unthinkable.

Sometimes, I take longer to get to Pho 75 than I intended, either due to traffic or something stupid. I start to wonder if they'll call me because I'm taking too long. Once, I didn't show up until 40 minutes after I'd placed my order. They did not call. You have to respect that level of confidence.

After several visits here I can say that my favorite thing to do here is get drinks with friends.  They have a good-sized selection of beers and wines, as well as liquors, so there's certain to be something new to try or an old favorite to comfortably imbibe.  The ambiance and decor - especially upstairs - is of relaxed elegance, which is conducive to quaffing and chatting.

There's a wide array of foods, both savory and sweet.  I found the savory dishes I've tried so far generally unremarkable; others have them liked them more.  The sweets, however, certainly satisfy.

Service has been consistently excellent, with knowledgeable and well-trained staff.  Prices are reasonable considering what you get.

A nice mixture of high top and regular tables, Founding Fathers has a riff on poutine (so ironic, because we'd literally just been discussing poutine and wondering where to get it in Philadelphia, when we decided we wanted a drink and stopped in): what more could you ask for?

As often as I get food at Temple, I've only ever eaten at the crepe truck once.  It has nothing to do with the food, but rather has to do with the fact that the truck isn't near any of the buildings I go to, and I'm too lazy to walk the extra block or two.

Anyway, the one time I went there, the guys in the truck were really nice, and the crepe was very good.  I got the chicken Ceasar crepe.  If I ever rouse myself to walk over there again, I think I'd like to try one of their fruit crepes next time.  Definitely try this place.

I was pleasantly surprised at how good the bean burger was, but if you're looking for something vegetarian you should let them know because the bun was waaay too tasty to not have been toasted on the same grill as meat. The salad that came with them was colorful and fresh.
The popular duck fries were small, crispy and topped with gooey duck and gravy. The portion was perfect for two people to share, but requires a fork to avoid goo soaked fingers. I thought they were just ok, typical bar food, but my boyfriend thought that they were great. 
On a separate visit for brunch I had a benedict that I found to be bland.
On both visits staff took a long time to give us a bill, but were polite.
In the winter the place smells heavily of it's fireplace to the point where I left smelling like the fireplace.
As a Manyunk resident, Ban Bamboo is my favorite Thai spot in Philly. All the curry dishes are superb, tom yum soup is delicious, and the spicy thai basil dish is to die for. Love this place.

Truly a 5 star experience working with the Bachelor Boy's Band! From the booking process, to scheduling the actual set list and logistics, to the performances the night of the wedding, everything was a dream! Special shoutout to John who made the process seamless as it should be and was always available for questions and advice. He understood my vision and perfectly executed. Everyone in the band is incredibly talented and passionate for what they do. Our guests are still raving about the band! If you want a fantastic vendor and some of the most talented musicians around, book Bachelor Boy's Band!

This place was hands down one of the best places that I have ever dined at!! It deserves five stars for it's eclectic décor, wonderful customer service, good music and of course good food! I'm from Southwest Philly and it's funny cause I never knew this place existed, was it a hidden jewel??

When I arrived, I was impressed by the outdoor seating and the large open windows, which allows for natural sunlight to come thru. I came with a party of three and we decided to eat outdoors. They also offered a nice bar and it was also BYOB.

Our waiter was very friendly and attentive to our dining experience. I ordered the Moroccan chicken and waffles while two of my friends ordered the salmon omelette and potatoes and my other friend ordered the shrimp and grits which also came with potatoes and pita bread.

Ok, so my chicken and waffles was oh  so good, the chicken breast was crispy, flavorful and had a sweet glaze on top. They also had cilantro on top of the chicken. I could tell that they probably soak the chicken before cooking it because it was so moist. The dish also had a spicy sauce on the side to compliment the crispy chicken. My waffles was buttery, crisp and also flavorful. My meal was the perfect balance of sweet and savory and it was served on this pretty wooden plate. My friend tried my chicken and he even said it was the best chicken he has ever tasted! Btw my other friends also enjoyed their meals. We decided to get dessert so we all shared the bread pudding and the Oreo dessert both were delicious and offered a pretty presentation. Kudos to the chef that not only cares about the quality of their food but also the presentation. This restaurant prepares their food with precision!

Overall this was a great dining experience, we all sat and talked for hours cause it was just so comfy and we listened to the jazz music in the background and Frank Sinatra. My friend loved this place she already made plans for us to come back in October for dinner and she said she was going to try their lamb entrée. I recommend all to visit Aksum.

The perfect dentist experience.  They are professional, kind, and accommodating. Sit back relax and enjoy a movie or show on Netflix while your getting your teeth cleaned. Best dentist I have ever been to.

Lines were a little shorter than Genos but there was still a line. I had the cheese steak with mushroom, peppers, wit provolone cheese. The bread was saggy, there was little meat. Nevertheless, the steak and the onions were well season. Very flavorful indeed compare to Genos.

National Mechanics is probably the easiest-to-spot bar in old city. It's exterior gives off the vibe of a gothic mausoleum, with it's marble pillars, lit torches and ye olde time script. Some might find it intimidating. Don't. You might miss out on one of the few bars in that area not taken over by citizens of New Jersey who smell like Tag body spray.

As grandiose as it appears on the outside, the interior is inviting, yet quirky. The crowd is a decent mix of company yes-men with rolled-up sleeves, art students, mods, reformed hipsters, ladies out on a girls night and guys doing the same. I say decent mix, instead of eclectic mix, because it's the kind of place where anyone can come and not feel like they're invading someone else's Scene. Everybody seems pretty positive there.

Come early to avoid the crowd. Stay late on the weekends, because they have a DJ who spins some dance-worthy tunes. My only issue is that sometimes it's TOO loud, like for some reason people aren't dancing because the music isn't loud enough. Memo: Sometimes people just want to drink and talk to their friends. Give it up, already.

Service is good, bartenders are attentive and quick. Private unisex bathrooms are relatively clean. It seems like they only have one server running around, so table service takes a while.  Your best bet is to just go to the bar yourself and bring back a pitcher for your friends.

Quick bites like the popcorn chicken, fish & chips, or their thick pub fries are more than enough to absorb the alcohol. Excellent veggie burger.

There's usually something to do there every day. Karaoke on Tuesday is fun, like a special-needs support group. Quizzo is on Wednesdays. Fridays and Saturdays are good for people-watching or if you like to dance but hate "clubs."

Pros: Cheap-ish beer, fun non-pretentious crowd, good service.

Cons: Loud, tables fill up by 10:00, DJ who doesn't know when to quit.
Ordered flowers for my friend's birthday. Did the designer's choice. Loved the results! Called to confirm. Person on the phone was great. Flowers arrived on time. Would use them again.

Great music venue. Certainly among the best in Philly. It's a music venue with a bar, and not the other way around. People come to hear great musicians play great music. I know many people out in the Philly suburbs who go to the Tin Angel regularly.

I have been lucky enough to perform there twice, once under my own name, and once while backing up musician Bill O'Meara. Both tiimes I found the sound to be stellar. The nights I was there, Barb was running sound, and she's great. They have a little back room for artists to sit and gather themselves before the show. The room itself is very long and not very wide (like many city venues and apartments). You enter a quarter of the way up the room. Behind you is the bar, in front is the seating, then the stage.

The only downside to the venue at all is that the bathrooms are located behind the stage. This creates some unwanted movement to the side of the stage as people walk to the bathroom, which can be a distraction to all while music is going on. But that is a small drawback.

There is a back alley for musicians to stop while unloading, and then a very harrowing, steep, stairway to bring equipment in. I hate that part, but again, it's a small price to pay for an otherwise terrific venue.

Parking in that part of Philly is awful. Make sure you park some where that allows parking after 10pm! Philly has decided to mark many of their street parking spots in that area as "2 hour parking until 10pm, tow zone after 10pm". So watch out for that.

My wife and I had a late breakfast here in early August 2017. The food was superb, we had cheese omelettes with home fries and wheat toast. The food was well prepared and delicious, nothing fancy, just very good food. I highly recommend this place!

UPDATE: I guess this place shut down? Haven't been able to order from here since my last order. I don't know if the restaurant or Yelp has to do this, but shut down the page or something. Super disappointed, the food was pretty great. :(




SOOOOOO GOOD! I got the empanadas, my co worker had a burrito, and my boyfriend had the Cuban sandwich. The portions were huge and inexpensive. My empanadas were flaky and crunchy with a moist beef and cheese mixture in the inside which was well seasoned. It came with a side of "queso" which seemed more like nacho cheese, but it was pretty good. Went well with the empanadas. My co workers burrito came with a side of rice and refried beans. The burrito looked pretty good. He did mention that the cheese wasn't really present until the end of the burrito which he asked for extra of. There also wasn't much sour cream. I ordered a side of sour cream for my empanadas as well which I didn't get. My boyfriends order was the Cuban and it was HUGE. The seasoning was perfect and had a nice spicy kick to it. He loved it and I thought it was good as well. Overall everything was decently priced and very appetizing. Our order even came fifteen minutes earlier than what Eat24 had estimated. I do think that they should add a "sides" section to the menu though for stuff like extra cheese, sour cream, hot sauce, etc. that's the reason I can only give 4 stars. Will definitely order again though.

So disappointed tonight. I have been to this place many times and I really love the sushi! However, I came in tonight with my boyfriend and a friend at 9:10 pm on a Thursday. We promptly ordered and got great sushi, noodles and maki. At 10:10 I asked for some tea and it was promptly served. The music stopped and some lights went off. The waitress apologizes and informs us we need to leave in five minutes. I understand she needed to get home but we got no warning upon arrival. I had to basically chug my tea and we cut our dinner very abruptly short on the night before my birthday. I was a vocal supporter of this neighborhood gem but now I am not so sure. We spent a good amount and gave a good tip out of decency.

If I could give no stars I would. 1 I received the wrong meat on my sandwich - sausage instead of bacon with zero warning 2. The delivery man was exceptionally creepy and had his eyes closed the entire exchange.

This place is freaking amazingggg. I've lived a block away from this building and many restaurants have come and gone but Mike's is the real deal. Goodness. I've been there several times now and had the following things: brisket, pulled pork, cornbread, and collards. It's all good. The brisket is out of this world tender and the corn bread is perfect. Also Mike's BBQ sauce is fanfreakingtaatic, too. I can't say enough good things. 

Update: I've developed a bit of an addiction to this place. I honestly go once a week. Try mixing the mac and cheese with some pulled pork! Another pro tip, at the end of the transaction enter your email address and sign up for the rewards program. 

Another update: I freaking love this place. I love the food and I love the people. Mike and all of the staff are lovely and make a wonderful and valued addition to the neighborhood. Go support Mike's, eat some delicious bbq.

Closed when their hours online state open. It is 10:50am on a Monday and the doors are locked. There are no signs on the doors or windows to display correct hours of operation. I would like to opt out of rating, as I certainly can't rate properly. This doesn't merit one star, but I don't feel anything higher than two is fair.

As a first time visitor to City Tap House, I have to admit... I was impressed.  

I met a friend for dinner on Monday night, and the bar was hopping with professionals (both young and seasoned) with a sprinkling of college kids.  The dining room was nearly empty when we sat down for dinner, but quickly filled up as our evening went on.

The beer menu was extensive and they have a special called Flight Night on Monday nights.  $10 for 4, 6 ounce tastes of almost any beer on the menu (only bumping off a star since the one beer I was really in the mood for, they ran out of).  For dinner, my friend ordered the mussels (with a red spicy sauce) and I ordered the Hanger Steak with parmesan fries with truffle aioli.  Both were delicious. 

The wait staff was courteous and quick.  Between the food and the service, I'm sure we'll be back.

My father always told me how good their Prosciutto and Sharp Provolone sandwich was.  He said he would take it over any sandwich in Primo's any day.  I thought the man was crazy.  

Needless to say, I will be going to Mi Pal's Deli instead of Primo's.  I had one last week and it was unbelievable.  Like usual, my dad was right.  When will I learn?

If you haven't tried Mi Pal's, please do yourself and you taste buds a favor and do so right away.  They might just be South Philly's most underrated deli!

My husband and I both love music, so a band was a must for our wedding, and the Bachelor Boys Band was absolutely perfect! Everyone at the wedding couldn't stop talking about how awesome they thought the band was. Adam was our main contact and he was extremely helpful and responsive through the whole process. Matt and Najwa were amazing singers, and the whole band did a wonderful job playing exactly what we wanted to hear! They got everyone (young and old) up and dancing and the whole place was rocking to the end of the night. We also had Matt do our MCing, and it was well worth the extra fee, as he kept everything on schedule so we didn't have to worry about a thing. We couldn't have asked for a better soundtrack for our wedding day!
Soft, tasty, chewy, chocolatey cookies, what more could you ask for!  Get the chocolate chip cookie dipped in chocolate chip!! Oh I wish I had one right now!!

This is BY FAR my favorite record store in Philadelphia. They carry lots of new reissues on vinyl, especially lesser known folk, jazz, and experimental artists. Today I purchased LPs by Cornelius Cardew, Albert Ayler, Bridget St John, and Anne Briggs. They also have a great selection of used records of course, with a healthy influx of new arrivals. I've also traded them my old LPs and CDs and they gave me a very generous amount in trade credit for them.  The bins are always stocked with Philly artists like Sun Ra, Purling Hiss, and Jack Rose. The clerk had great recommendations when I visited today.  He saw what I was buying and knew just what else to steer me towards. A delightful spot! 
Also, they wear masks, have a plastic barrier around the counter, and only allow a few people in at a time, so it feels very Covid safe.

i have a small business in philadelphia and tow decision towed one of our vehicles........boy what an experience.  how about having our car less than 24 hours and the bill was 2340.58 ripoff fraud everything going on and the police can do nothing about these crooks.  we are a car rental company so they totally take advantage will only take cash, never have the bill ready, will call you back when its convenient for another day of storage and the list goes on.........admin lot fee labor clean up wench gate fee storage transport tear down special equipment hazardous handling worst part about it they got two of our cars in 2 days

i wish there were zero stars or negative, how they ever get 5 stars is beyond me rude ignorant could care less 27 minutes on hold and told me they were busy meanwhile my driver was standing in their office no one else there..............................add liars to the list

avoid at all cost

I usually don't mind the vibe here and they are usually pretty good. But I don't understand the need to schedule an appointment if the barber isn't ready or still working on a client? Like why am I waiting an Extra 30 minutes after my appointed time? I blocked off my schedule for you, meet me with the same courtesy.

Dined here multiple times for brunch. This review is based solely on the breakfast aspect. I've had the "Kitchen Sink" entree which was pretty good - just super heavy for breakfast if you're looking for a lighter/healthier option. Today I had the veggie Benedict which was also good - came just as expected not amazing but not bad. They serve you an abundance of potatoes with your dish. They have healthy options and vegetarian/vegan options as well. Service is great - servers are always enthusiastic.
LOVE, LOVE,LOVE. Prices are great and baked good ever better. I currently live in Maryland and introduced Stocks to some neighbors, they now request cakes when I go visit family in Philly!
The waffle sandwich was great! Customer service was good our waiter was nice. The wait time was a little longer than I would have liked.

What a great new addition to Chinatown. This cool modern food court has something for everyone. You can even get Middle Eastern Street food here.  I really enjoyed my lunch at Philly Poke but really need to come back to try the goodies at Coreanos a Mexican Korean place. 
       What could be better than Bulgogi Tacos? The double fried chicken also looked good. Kitty Korner also looked interesting. Want to try the Japanese curry pork Katsu. For dessert have to come back on s warmer day to get some of that Thai ice cream roll at I CE NY.

Had a pretty terrible dining experience at Han Dynasty. After waiting an eternity for the host to seat us at a table that had actually been empty the entire time, our meal proved to be a big disappointment. We could barely hear ourselves think because the TV was broadcasting a Food Network segment about this restaurant on continuous loop at full blast. The food was hardly impressive. The copious amounts of garlic in the tofu dish we ordered actually overpowered the spicy red peppers. Dandan noodles were mushy and overcooked. The stir-fried four season beans (ganbian sijidou) were just regular green beans and they were COATED in so much sugar that they were inedible. When the owner came by we told him there was a little bit too much sugar in the dish. He grabbed a green bean right off the plate with his hand, tasted it, told us it was "fine" to him, and walked away without apologizing. On the positive side, his unhygienic sampling technique and cavalier attitude made us forget about how mediocre the food tasted.

My girlfriend loves to stick to places she knows. I know tons of people like her. They stick to the knowns, to things they can predict. The Latest Dish is predictably great, but it's genius lies in its specials. Trout with leaks and goat cheese? Fucking incredible. Lamb shank braised in red wine? Brilliant, just the perfect amount of gamey-ness. As far as drinks, the gf keeps coming back for the raspberry zinger and I love the oberon. The pesto-evo for the bread is great; every time.

The service staff is professional. Not warm and engaging every time  but their food is that good that they don't necessarily have to be. 

Seriously. If this place doesn't confirm your belief in being a foodie, or make you want to be a foodie, than nothing will. Try everything. The original lobster mac and cheese. I'm sorry every special sounds great. The sides are indispensable. Sorry. Good luck.

I went to Sulimay's Urban Salon for the first time yesterday and saw Ashlyn. I have dirty blonde/light ash brown hair and wanted to lighten it up a bit. I came in a little unsure of how exactly I wanted to have it done, but after showing her a few pictures she immediately knew what to do and nailed it. 

I have had my hair colored, highlighted and balayaged (is that a word?) several times in the past and I've always been satisfied, but this time I was thrilled. Ashlyn gave me a perfect cut and the most natural-looking balayage I've ever had.

Also- I am used to being in a salon for 3+ hours whenever I get a cut and color, but she did a flawless job in under 2 hours! Made me wonder why it's taken others so much longer.

If you're anxious about trusting someone new with your hair, I recommend Sulimay's and highly recommend Ashlyn!

hands down, some of the BEST Chinese food I've ever had! service is phenomenal, great food, great prices! doesn't accept credit cards, but other than that...amazing. Great menu and definitely one of my faves

My family and I had dinner last night at the White Dog Cafe. 3 of the people in our party of 7 are vegan. The food was fantabulous. It's a made up word but describes my dining experience.  The garlic hummus was incredible as was the arepas. I had the udon noodle salad-delicious. Everyone one of the carnivores at our table enjoyed their meal.  Giving props to Andre. He had so much patience dealing with us and he was an excellent server.

Well I was told to try Isgro's above all else...  I am from NY area so I am comparing to Veneiro's...  Anyway, it took 40 minutes to get served with only 8 people in line...  14 cannolis and $49 later I was out the door.  Got home with much excitement to try these famous cannolis... Bleeeccccchhhhh, what a disappointment.  And it wasn't only me.  I had four separate boxes for four separate parties.  They all said the same thing, the cannolis were terrible. One of the parties had a box of six...  all thrown into the trash.

Well, I guess if you've never been to Veneiro's then you will never know what a great cannoli tastes like.

Oh, and by the way, the cannolis that I took home look nothing like the photos from Isgro's.  They were barely filled with ricotta cream...  and looked to half the size of the photographed cannolis.
Great spot I love there beef and chicken cheesesteaks. You can't go wrong with Dalessandro's. The only thing is it's always a line.

I am offering an update to my previous review some time ago. I visited Al Sham in recent months and have been pleasantly surprised and impressed with the massive improvements and overhauls. They are now offering a very very very large selection, some might say to a detriment, but I can not speak to that since I have not sampled much of the new menu. They are even offering various varieties of lassi. I never thought that was really in their wheel house but... 
The food that I did order however was better than it had ever been here. The salad seems much fresher and the portions were good. I ordered falafel salad with side of yellow rice. Was very good. 
The prices have significantly risen since I visited and reviewed last though. I am willing to pay for this sometimes if they remain consistent. 

My one gripe is that their customer service is not always stunning. Its still hit or miss when it comes to friendliness and communication. 
 I'll visit again and review again to see that they are keeping to new standards.

I absolutely love this place and I used to drop in twice a week when I lived in the neighborhood. Kay and George are the sweetest people ever, you feel like family when you're there. Service is a bit slow, and the coffee is no-frills, but if you want to get in and out in a hurry with a fancy espresso drink, there are other options for that. This is a cozy place for coffee, community, and a homemade slice of pie.

Cafe Lift is one of my go-to brunch places.  They're a great little industrial-style joint with a daily changing specials menu as well as some great brunch staples.  If you have a sweet tooth like me, you have to try the Cannoli French Toast.  It's so delicious and everything you want french toast to be.  When I'm in a savory mood, they always have great tacos on special as well as tasty pizzas and sandwiches on their regular menu.

They're cash only, so make sure you bring some.

My roommate and I happen to both be big into Club Monaco.  Unfortunately, we also happen to be big into not having high-paying jobs, so our opportunities to drop $125+ on a pair of pants are few and far between.  That being said, the few times I have visited the Club Monaco store on Walnut were all great experiences.

The store is a decent size in my opinion, because I'm not one for large overwhelming retail spaces.  Instead, it's a narrow, but deep store that uses it's space effectively.  It's not packed to the brim with merchandise, which makes the shopping experience more enjoyable.  I can, however, see it becoming overcrowded on a Saturday afternoon, but I've been wise to avoid that.

The staff that I've met were all helpful and kind.  They were also careful to not be "hovers," and I can't explain how happy that made me.  You know what I'm talking about.  You walk into a store, you're not looking for anything specific, you tell the shopgirl that, yet every time you turn around she's still up your butt trying to sell you something you know your boobs would look terrible in.  Pet peeve!

The last time I was there I fell in love with a little, strapless, black dress.  It was the only one left in the store, but it was a 4 and just too big for me.  The shopguy was nice enough to check out other stores to see if they had a 2 hanging around anywhere.  He actually tracked one down and had it sent to my house.  I appreciate and remember that kind of service.

Also, Club Monaco's basic design palette is always black and white, sometimes venturing into navy blues and creams.  It's not just a symptom of this specific store.

place is great - o sole mio pizza is my go to but their white/reg pizza is great too.  just like any other pizza place though i usually have to order well done (esp on busy days) so they don't get tempted to pull it before crust gets crispy.  haven't had a bad thing here, even their monster chix salads when feeling like eating bit healthy.  owner (or i think its the owner b/c he's there basically 24/7) is a great guy too.

Awesome pedicure - great experience. Came here with my mom on a Tuesday and had an appointment. Appointment only on Mondays and Tuesdays which is nice! The best pedicure I've ever had I'll be back every month now. Great salon very nice and clean and the owner Tien is fantastic. Highly recommend.

My sister ordered an arrangement for my birthday. We were not home so they didn't leave the flowers, however they did notify my sister that the flowers were delivered. It wasn't until she asked me if I received the flowers that we figured out that they had not been delivered. I was out of town a couple of days and they finally ended up delivering the flowers to me at work. It was the saddest looking flower arrangement I have ever seen. Clearly it was the same arrangement that they had tried to deliver almost a week prior. It was a combination of lilies and some purple daisy-like flowers. The lilies were crumpled with brown spots on them and the other flowers were wilted. That said, I think even if they had been delivered fresh, it would've been a very sad looking arrangement. In part because there was no actual arrangement to it. The flowers look like they had been jammed in a vase by a five-year-old. I would never, ever order flowers from this company.

The bakery was suggested to me by a coworker.  I found myself in the area for work and stopped in.  I bought a vanilla loaf pound cake and a chocolate frosted marbled pound cake.  I gave half of each to my mother and took my halves home.  I sliced them down thin and had to hide them from my kids pestering me for slices the rest of the night.  Family members sent me text messages asking me where I got the cake from after they had a taste of it while visiting my mom.  A whole loaf was about $10.  I don't carry cash so I did have to slip next door to get cash from the ATM (nice neighborhood variety store too!).  When I came back my order was bagged and ready to go.  A lovely community jewel.  I will tell others.  I did have to maneuver to find a place to park so be warned.
Absolutely awesome!!!!   Love the hole in the wall type places.  Guy serving was kinda rude so that rounded it out.  Perfect.

This is a beautiful restaurant. The staff is great, and the food is good. It's easy enough to find free parking nearby, and the prices are nice.  I am surprised that more people don't talk about this place; maybe it is a hidden gem.  I'd like to see more vegan options.

Yelp saves the day again! I was looking for a new place to grab brunch on Saturday and stumbled upon the reviews for Cafe Lift. I was immediately smitten and jumped in the car right away to get a table. Luckily, we didn't even have to wait! There were so many yummy things on the menu that it took me about 10 minutes to decide (I ended up going with the Crispelle with crabmeat, spinach, tomatoes and cheese - DELISH! I decided savory over sweet, but damn, those pancakes with homemade whipped cream looked amazing). The service was great, and they kept the La Columbe coffee and fresh squeezed orange juice flowing. Just a few helpful tips for you: 1) You need a key to go to the single serve bathroom (which is very nice, btw) and 2) It's cash only, so be prepared.

The owners are opening a new bar/restaurant on the next block called Prohibition - I'm looking forward to checking it out. In the meantime, I'll be  coming back to Cafe Lift to brunch it up on a regular basis.

Through a surprise birthday party for my fiance and invited 10 of our friends, her son, and his girlfriend. Her son and his girlfriend said it was the best time they have ever had together, and they were surprised that they had so much fun with older people "son is 26". Our friends traveled in from upstate Pennsylvania and the Jersey shore. They all agreed it was well worth the trip and everyone had a blast. To quote one of my friends "best night ever". Anna is amazing! Also would like to note, one of my guests owns an Italian restaurant. He totally enjoyed himself. 
Thank-you La Cucina!
Shavon gives the best eyebrow waxes EVER!! And i've had my share of waxes and threading believe me. Mine are sparse but look fantastic after each visit :) the 12 is totally worth it!!

United sucks and your flight is late.  Seriously, it's the only reason you're here. The food - and wait staff - is cold, greasy and super salty.  Welcome to Philly.  Good news is the beer - like the wait staff and those awful fries - is cold, and there's a good selection of beers.  Good luck catching your flight back home; hope the rest of your day/night is better for you.
Service good, food was fresh and tasted great. I had the salmon eggs Benedict; really good.

i've been pierced at Infinite ever since I was legally allowed to. I have never had a negative experience. Professional, warm, knowledgeable staff. Skilled and clean piercers - i've seen clean technique that could rival my nurse practitioner! Love Love Love this place. There is nowhere else to get pierced in the tri-state area.

Oh gosh! After reviewing menu for black restaurant week I saw they had bread pudding, my pregnancy craving for like a month and can't find it ANYWHERE (except Famous Dave's). Went ahead to place order. Didn't realize all the detours and road closures in route (coming from Roxborough) which lead my 25 min trip to take almost 45-55mins! As I approach there was parking in Baltimore Ave (great when you are overdue in your pregnancy and can't walk far) cute exterior and interior. Greeted by waitresses and then someone goes to explain that I order a really long time ago and the ic cream melted...ok no problem there was traffic and detours I'll still take it (even tho the ice cream should've been placed in a separate container to begin with). Go back to my commute back home (35 mins) and get ready to dig in (first "meal" of the day at 2:30pm) bring my fork to a rather dark piece of bread pudding and it's HARD AS A ROCK!!! Wtf!!! I just spent $17 on two orders of BURNT & HARD bread pudding floating in melted ice cream!! Me and the baby are clearly disappointed and If I decide to return in the future I DEFINITELY WONT get bread pudding!! Like how the hell can you serve burnt food? Never again!

We hit Dotties as part of our summer donut tour, and really wanted to love them. But vegan donuts just aren't our thing. The flavors were just a little to unique for us. The store was clean and quiet. The staff was polite. 
Parking was a challenge for us, we ended up having to walk a couple blocks.

I used Payless and had a very nice experience, the young lady that waited on me was very friendly and outgoing.  I got a very nice 2015 Nissan Sentra fully loaded I was impressed it only had 273 miles on it.  I saw some negative reviews but their prices were the best around by far.  They were busy but the line moved quickly and I was on my way.  The return process very simple in and out.................will use again and recommend to others 5 stars.......good job!

A few years later decided to give it another shot. I need to save others from having the experience I had. My pedicure was horrible. I asked the woman to cut my toe nails but she refused because they were "too short." She somehow managed to cut my toe anyway, which then started bleeding. The polish was so smudged. I had her repaint them but it still looks sloppy. I went home after and had to cut my own toe nails because the woman refused. 

My nails were equally as bad as the pedicure. I got an acrylic refill. I asked her to cut them down a little and she chopped my entire nail off. They were filed crooked and not square at all, like I had asked. The painting was so sloppy; there was polish all over my fingers. I was so upset but decided to be polite and leave a small tip anyway. When I asked for change, the nail tech took my money and walked away with all of it, not giving me any change back. I was too exhausted at that point to even argue. 

On top of all of this, I was rushed out at 7pm. I was sitting by the dryers and they told me I had to leave because they were closing. Really disappointed in this place. Please save yourself and go somewhere else.

How on earth have I lived in Bella Vista for 4 years and never eaten at Chickies before a few months ago??? Thankfully, a friend of ours got hoagies to bring down to a Phils game from Chickie's.  We've been hooked ever since.  Heaven.  

Reasons why I love Chickies:
1.  It makes me sad to think of all the Italian hoagies I have eaten from other delis in the city.  What a waste when these hoagies are around!  
2.  Chicken cutlet sandwiches are also tasty
3.  Sarcone's bread.  Need I say more?
4.  The owner came out to ask us how our hoagies were as we munched at the sidewalk tables.  Seriously - when was the last time a deli owner took so much pride in sandwiches? 

This is the real deal!

*Edited 02/14/2012*

I had a problem with this laundromat the first time I used them. A couple of my things were stained and I had some trouble getting it worked out. 

A little while ago, however, the owner found out what happened and made things right. I thought she would want me to amend my bad review, but when I mentioned it she told me to leave it up, and said "The world is a competitive place. We strive to do good work."
Great little spot in Fishtown comfortable friendly staff good food. The Bagella Fitzgerald was Amazing.

I had a great experience here. They were slow midday so I walked right in and had my hair cut by BJ. She was friendly, professional, and a really good barber. From the outset we spent a couple of minutes talking about my hair-- both how I wanted it to look and, more importantly, what its like when allowed to sit naturally. It's that sort of personal treatment that justifies going for a haircut more often. After a few minutes I had a great cut and a few styling tips. In the time that I spent in the chair I felt welcomed and comfortable...the staff was friendly and accommodating. I'll be back, without a doubt.

umm, the waffles are gross. way too sweet with big bits of "rock" sugar (for lack of a better term) and i don't know if the thing is to not clean the griddle, but they should because there was big burned chunks of shit.

Ugh. I was so excited about a new salon in the neighborhood but I am SO disappointed . The pedicure I got was awful and not to mention the polish job was a disaster. I literally could have painted my own nails better. Maybe it was an off day or I had someone newer doing my nails but in my opinion a waste of $30 (not including tip!).

Considering the limitations of a takeout place that doesn't have indoor seating, Johnny Hot's offers a great product, for an amazing price, with unbelievable fast service. Recommended for breakfast and lunch. It's great to take your sausage to the park across the street and enjoy.

I work close to the area and stopped in for a quick refill and a gel pedicure. Not only did I wait 25 minutes to be serviced, when the nail tech did my refill, she nicked my cuticle bed and left my finger bleeding while she used the tool on my other nails. When I drew to her attention my finger was bleeding, she wiped it with a cotton ball and applied a blueish green liquid that burned.
The shape of my nails were butchered. I simply asked that she cut my nails down- somehow she couldn't redo the coffin shape and they're terrible. The filing was also done poorly. Just overall very ugly - I was so upset because my nails were previously shaped so beautifully- they had just grown too long.
I ended up paying $67 for extremely poor quality. It took her 2.5 hours to play all up in my face because these nails are a mess.

This is purely for the bubble tea review.

Although they are not " known" for their bubble tea, I'm a firm believer that if you put it on your menu, LET IT BE GOOD. Otherwise, don't put it on your menu. 

$4.50 for a bubble tea. I go the Thai Iced Tea bubble tea. She gave me the Thai Iced Coffee. 
I had already left, so whatever it's okay, I like coffee.

Coffee I can tell is mixed with instant creamer, sugar and it may or may not be real coffee. It wasn't BAD, but let's say it was a 2 out of 5 stars for coffee.

The bubbles /tapioca was terrible. Not cooked thru in the middle (still white) and they were hard. 1/10 for the tapioca. Will not return ever for bubble tea

What an exceptional house and tour.  The tour guide was wonderful.  He was so educated on the house and Philadelphia history.  The house and content were gorgeous.  Price is low. The Fairmount park is beautiful.   Tour are hourly starting at 10am Thursday to Sunday.  $5 for seniors.   There are gardens to see in the spring.   Highly recommended visiting.

I rented from Centra for two years. I had a wonderful experience. My apartment was very reasonably priced, spacious, and exactly what I needed. Whenever I had problems and needed maintenance, Centra responded and typically had someone out within 24 hours. Their maintenance technicians were friendly, efficient, and respectful (my cat also loved them, and like most cats she likes nobody). Kimberly McCloskey is fantastic and always followed up after a maintenance visit to be sure that everything had worked out well. I was overall very pleased with my experience. I have rented from several management companies and Centra has been by far the best.

We've been living in Philly for about five months now and a few places have emerged as neighborhood favorites and The Sidecar Bar & Grille tops the list. Mainly, we have been going for their fabulous Sunday brunch, but we have also visited once for dinner and it was equally enjoyable.

Recently, they have opened the second floor which is a comfortable, sun-filled room that is ideal for brunch. 

The one time we were there for dinner, I have a wonderful special of mussels and homemade sausage over cannellini beans. For brunch, I tend to gravitate to the Crabby Benny or the Huevos Rancheros. Whatever you do, get the bread basket. It changes weekly but has always been delicious whether it's the Chocolate Chip Pumpkin Bread or the Crumb Cake or whatever.

And the beer list is to die for. They always have a nice variety of styles from a wide-assortment of breweries, usually featuring local brews. When we were last there, we were thrilled to find they still had Stone Vertical Epic 11/11/11.

We were out of town one weekend recently and were totally having Sidecar brunch withdraw.

STAFF IN KITCHEN ARE NOT WEARING FACE MASKS.
STAFF IN KITCHEN ARE NOT WEARING FACE MASKS.

I am sorry to report that the kitchen/staff at Alyan's Middle Eastern Cuisine on 4th between South and Bainbridge (603 S. 4th) are NOT wearing face masks while handling and cooking food. 

I walked by about 20 minutes ago and decided to stop in and order some food to go. There was an older man standing behind the register, sort of half in and half out of the kitchen area (if you're not familiar, the kitchen is up front and they cook the food out in the open basically.) He was on his phone and not wearing a mask.

A younger man came over, no mask - I think he may have been waiting tables as well as taking orders at the register - and he took my order. He then went into the fridge and began pulling out ingredients for what I'd ordered, all without a mask. He conveyed my order to the man who was on the phone, who began heading into the kitchen area, presumably to start cooking.

I told them to cancel the order and that I couldn't believe they were back there without masks.

I'm not looking for an argument about mask wearing or your "rights". I am conveying this factual information, and everyone can make their own informed decisions moving forward.

VERY disappointed.
Love the bagels!! Get the everything bagel with a mix of the veggie and Jalepeno cream cheese. The tiramisu is amazing also
3.5 Sonny's is not a bad cheesesteak, if you are in the area and you want to satisfy your craving hop on in. Pickup was fine and prompt in these Covid times.
South Philly in somerton  outstanding service and food our waiter John was exceptional we will definitely be back

A friend recommended this salon and I was in desperate need of a pedicure for an upcoming wedding. I went before the lunch rush on a Friday and was taken right away though the salon was already busy. The staff is friendly, my toes look great and they take credit cards! I will definitely be returning if I have to get a pedicure done during the work week.

This place has it all. Great food, atmosphere and service. Moderately priced with a 10% discount if you pay in cash! 

My favorite, "Veal Bracciole (2)
Traditional veal cutlet rolled & stuffed with proscuitto, provolone & peccorino romano cheeses with celery, egg  & a touch of long hot peppers.  Braised in Italian gravy" I shoulda got about a dozen to take home!

Marras used to be my favorite pizza in Philadelphia, the last time I ordered pizza  it tasted like cardboard no flavor whatsoever. Tonight I ordered white pizza with Ricotta cheese ( my favorite) and I could barely eat a slice. The ricotta cheese smelled and looked like it was over a year old. It was yellowish green color and you could tell it was not fresh whatsoever. Very disappointed.
Used to be my go to pizza spot and now may never order again:/

Center Philly has a lot of really large restaurants.  This isn't one of them.  Cozy, dark, with a few tables and an intimate bar.  The bartender knows his drinks and the cocktail menu has something for everyone.  If you tell him your booze of choice - he'll make you something special.  

Southern cooking with an upscale twist.  The smoked wings were excellent bar food with a tangy Carolina sauce.  The rainbow trout was rich, with velvety sweet potatoes and sautéed broccoli rabe.  

Unexpected...special...delicious!!  Sit at the bar!!

I frequent this SF simply for its convenient location and the hours are superb. It's first downfall is the price. I know it's the hip neighborhood to live in but sweet Jesus, this isn't Monaco! Secondly, although the store looks terrific, the produce is horrible, and on occasion I've gotten an already-sour carton of milk. I prefer to do actual shopping across town at TJ - for 2/3 the price and better food. 

Lastly, the bike rack on the upper level is installed incorrectly, rendering it useless with the exception of two bikes longways.

I'm giving them that elusive 5th star!

I've been going here for the past 2 years it looks like, and today they earned that 5th star. There was a billing problem with my credit card in that a simple accident resulted in my getting charged $110.78 instead of $10.78. 

Shit happens, so why do they get the 5th star for making the mistake? Because they solved the problem quickly, with respect, and a sincere apology. I didn't get any perk/extra in the process, I would have refused (hey it's a small business, be nice). 

I felt good walking out of there, need I say more? Good people here.

This is my favorite Chinese restaurant in the area, hands down!

First of all, they have a vegetarian soup. Not a soup with veggies that is made with chicken broth. No, an actual vegetarian soup. This is a big deal to me because I grew up eating soup as the first course at Chinese restaurants and now that I'm a vegetarian I can never get soup anywhere we go. Try the vegetarian noodle soup. It's hot, filled with thin noodles, and stocked with real vegetables.

Try the mock general tso's chicken. It's delicious and vegetarian. Vegetable lo mein is greasy in all the ways you want lo mein to be.

So the good stuff first.  Obviously the chef is back at his old tricks and delivering the same high quality brunch many of us used to enjoy at Café Estelle.  If you enjoyed his old spot, you will recognize a couple of dishes on this menu.  The majority of our food was fantastic.  The breakfast burger was outstanding although the bun on the bottom could have been a little thicker to support the meat.

Also totally loved the outdoor space.  Very comfortable with no intrusions from the street.  Highly recommend sitting outside if available.  Also, bonus points for having vegan options.  Would be nice to see vegan pancakes on the menu.

Why only 3 stars?  Well, both orders of homefries were completely undercooked.  I'm pretty sure one of the potatoes was raw.  Service was also average.  Nothing specific to complain about but nothing I would remember.  I would like my coffee kept a little fuller and hotter during brunch.

That being said, we'll be back for certain and I hope that I will have the chance to upgrade this review.

Came here on a Saturday night after a long day of museums. Reservations are highly recommended! We made reservations for 630pm and got seated right away. Good thing we did because I saw at least 4 parties get turned away at the door because they were booked up. There was a huge party of 12 at the corner so they took up a majority of the seats. The place is very small but cozy. I never felt like I was elbow to elbow with the table next to us.
My boyfriend and I took advantage of the BYOB policy. In fact it seemed like every table did. There's no corkage fee and our waitress was happy to open bottles with her corkscrew for some of the older clientele who were having a hard time. Water was refilled regularly and the wait service was expedient but never rushed or intrusive.
My boyfriend and I did 4 appetizers (Baked Brie with honey and almonds, the Mediterranean tapas plate with Hummus, Baba Ghannoush and Feta cheese and warm pita bread, the Seared Scallops with a spinach cream sauce and Moroccan style mussels) and 2 desserts (Apple cobbler and Peach Bread Pudding, both served with homemade vanilla ice cream) and the bill was a measly $48. What a Deal!
I recommend this cute place for anyone looking to have an affordable and delicious dinner when in the Fairmont area of Philadelphia.

I like it here. More low key not as crowded as the other places I always go to. I like it because it's a little more mellow. The food has been good every time and the girls are really nice every time I go in. Check it out if you've never been. They always have good salads. Oh and I like the music too.

EXTREMELY disappointed.
Purchased a Key Lime Pie for dinner at friends.
Our guests had requested key lime or lemon meringue - so when I saw this Keylime with Meringue - I purchased it - even though it was small and and had no height over the pan.

Cutting into it, I could tell it was old. The meringue was sticky and hard. As well as runny where the meringue met the key lime custard.

Even worse, the entire pie was entirely TOO sweet. The meringue had a granulated sugar texture and the key lime didn't have the slightest hint of tartness.
At $26 for an 8 inch pie that was one inch high .... never again.
Sorry, Night Kitchen, you lost my business.

Took my dog here for the first time and it was a great experience. The staff are very friendly and helpful. My first impression was the office is very clean and organized despite the location they are in. Secondly, the wait was very short. Thirdly, I did not fill out any paper work at all. They use a very good EMR system and I was very impressed by it. I work in a doctor's office and use EMR systems everyday and this office really put theirs to use to the max. Dr. Thomas and the vet tech was very nice and gentle to my dog. I have taken her to the VCA and she shakes severely every time. But here, she did so well they complimented how well behaved she was. I also like the fact that Dr. Thomas explains what the problem is and what the prognosis is. Then, he gives you a layout of all the treatment options and put them in order from priority first. The tech then comes in and gives you a list of the treatment plans and the prices (again, on their awesome EMR system) and then you just basically pick and choose what you want done. I didn't like the VCA because the vet there ALWAYS try to persuade or convince me to do a million things that are so pricy and unnecessary. Dr. Thomas gave me a rough estimate for a procedure but unfortunately my dog ended up needing more things done. The price ended up being higher than what he expected but he was kind enough to give me a discount to make it more reasonable. Now, most people may think it's a scam or they're trying to appear as if they are giving a discount but working in the medical field myself, I know what it's like to run an office and the expenses. So, I appreciated the discount. I did spend quite a big chunk of money but then again, I adopted my dog recently and she was not properly taken cared of as far as her health. It was expected that I would have to spend a lot to get her on track. So far, my dog is doing fantastic and I am very satisfied and happy with the care. They called the next day to check on her progress and have her scheduled for a follow up as well. And......their hours are great!! I work two jobs, 6 days a week and don't have to take any time off from work to get her seen.

Though a little overpriced, DiBruno Bros has all of the specialty foods that anyone could ask for. The prices are a bit steep, but it's natural to pay for quality. If you have specific diet needs (Kosher, vegan, gluten free, etc) DiBruno Bros will definitely have something great to offer you.

I love the wide variety of food to choose from on the menu..So far I've had the pizza (duh) and a gyro from here..Both were good..And the owner/whoever it is I see every time I'm in there..Is a really nice guy and always friendly..And my top two fave things about this place..That its two blocks from my place and open real late..Which is great for someone like me who finds herself drunk and starving at 1am with no food in the fridge..Sweeeeet!

Edit: Recently came here with my better half for dinner..we got 2 free slices of cake because the owner said he liked us!..Thanks! And u get an extra star just for that!

Even when it's packed (which it usually is) this place is good. Very quick and clean which is hard to find. They're gentle but effective. 

I've gotten eyebrows and a manicure here. Check out the cute little nail decals because they always turn out super well! 

The only legit criticism I have for C&C is their eyebrow care. I have bled twice, but I've been here loads of times. I don't like when they do my brows too thin which happens quite often. You really have to say you only want your brows cleaned up if you don't want 2/3rds ripped off. 

That's what makes me give them a three. Watch your brows!

1 star is being kind to a restaurant with a snotty hostess and manager who treat guests with open table reservations as second class citizens if they are 2 older people who didn't reserve specific tables. Otherwise you are relegated to loud cramped 2 seaters and told to take it or leave it. We left it! First restaurant I've walked out of over poor treatment before I sat down. Never coming back. Ever!

Stephanie has been absolutely fantastic in the 6+months we've been using her as a walker/pet sitter for our 2 pugs.  She absolutely adores animals and I always know our pups are in good hands when they're with Stephanie.
