Her prices are very reasonable and I know for a fact that she  fulfills her time commitments for walks (we've had other walkers in the past who definitely skimp on the duration of walks)  She is super flexible with last minute requests if something comes up and has never not been available when we need her (for last minute walks or weekend pet sitting) She always goes above and beyond when we are away whether it's taking our dogs to her house to play with hers or making special trips to the park on nice days.   It's been so great to have a very reliable dog walker who loves our dogs as if they were her own.  I would absolutely recommend Stephanie and Wag the Dog if you have pet needs in the area.

I have been to this place a couple times and I think the last time may have been the last.  It was the first time I received a pedicure there and it was horrible!  They really didn't do anything at all to my feet to get them softer.  They even charged me extra for foot scrub, isn't that generally just included!? Even after the "special" foot scrub, my feet still have dry skin and are rough.  
I also received a gel manicure.  The lady (who I believe is the owner) that took my previous polish off filed my nails/fingers so much that I had a scab on my finger from her doing it so hard and rough.  
They really need to get some professional nail people in this place who know what they are actually doing.  There are too many good places in the area for me to go back.

WTF,

Stopped in last night and had a great time being serenaded by two piano players on the second floor; first floor has one piano player.  Found out that they just opened a week ago and they are fully booked Saturday night.

Gotta make reservations and get there again to enjoy this fun music spot in Old City.

Anyone else been there yet?

A shout out to the wonderful people at South Philly Italian Kitchen.  I recently ordered and picked up some of their homemade fresh mozzarella and made a dish for a gathering of friends.  It was ready when they said it would be, and it was wonderful.  Thank you to all of the staff and especially to Joe the Boss, you guys rock!!!!

I've spent a pretty embarrassing amount of money on my kitty over the past few years, most of it at The Cat Doctor, and all of it worth it. The Cat Doctor has been my cat's vet for several years now. They've seen him through fleas (yuck), kidney trouble (scary), summer haircuts (hilarious), and regular checkups. He's also boarded there on one occasion.

You can tell that they really think of their cats as their patients - lots of warmth and respect. When I had to take my cat in for an emergency hospitalization, they not only kept me in the loop on all the decisions they were making, but *encouraged* me to call and check on him over the few days he was there. Pretty rare, in my experience.

As other reviewers have mentioned, they have a nice homey atmosphere, with lots of other cats wandering around. They also keep a good stock of various prescription diets and other cat paraphernalia (combs, treats, toys, etc). 

The parking around the neighborhood is decent - it's street/metered parking, but I've always been able to find a spot close by. 

Two important caveats: 
1) They're pretty expensive. I might've looked for a new vet by now if my experiences hadn't been quite so positive. They can usually give you an estimate if you ask for one, though.
2) They close after 7pm weekdays, 1pm Saturdays, and aren't open at all on Sunday. Completely understandable, but I'd love to know that they'd be there for a middle-of-the-night or weekend emergency if I needed them.

Definitely a new experience for Frites. I loved the addition of meats on top of the fries. This is exactly what I was looking for as a late night snack or even a great mid day meal. I got the Mikey like wingy when I went, highly recommend for anyone who likes buffalo chicken and blue cheese. 

The servers are pleasant and the food came out quickly. Less than 10 minutes from ordering to table. I went around 12 am on a Saturday and it was not crowded at all just to give everyone an idea of what to expect. 

Overall this is a quick stop to get all of your late night or mid day cravings for frites. They also have shakes so they have the sweet and salty thing nailed for those who are into it. Lastly the sizes range from snacks to meals so there is undoubtedly  something for everyone.
Good food. Been many times. You do get your money worth. Large platter can feed you for two days. I always enjoy it.

Cozy restaurant with excellent service and a fun and sexy vibe. The outdoor seating isn't truly outdoors because of the clear plastic overhang, but it's decorated tastefully and feels like you're in another country which is nice. The indoor area is cozy as well and dimly lit. Both areas are great for dates, though the night we dined here it was really hot and humid outside so the indoor dining area may be a better choice for some who don't like it hot. The food is DELICIOUS. I ordered the broccoli rabe twice because it was so good, and the eggplant parmesan was exceptional also. The main portions are substantial and of all the dishes we ordered the hands down favorite was the Rigatoni Alla Vodka. The sausage was seasoned delightfully, the pasta was cooked perfectly and the cheese was generously grated. Very well cared for comfort food and great overall dining experience. I only took points off for some aesthetic reasons and the fact that there is only a single bathroom, but the food most certainly makes up for any deficiencies. Try it!

This spray tan was literally the greatest spray tan in history.  If you are questioning trying to airbrush spray tan and usually go to a booth, this is so much better.  It is worth every dime and lasts for well over a week.  I can't wait to go back again and will realistically probably be going back every 2 weeks!

Ordered via Grubhub. Food is tasty, but damn...no hummus or sauces on falafel and shawarma sandwiches? Dry.as.a.bone. I suppose when you order at the restaurant you can specify what you want, so that would help.

I ended up at Moshi Moshi because the four year old I was traveling with had a melt down so I wasn't gonna risk taking him to Morimoto.  What a pleasant surprise to end up here.  I took the six year old for a walk while mom tried to help the four year old put himself back together again.  We discovered Moshi on our walk.  When she finally called and told us the coast was clear i relayed the good news about another possible sushi option.  Since sushi is the cuisine the hole family is happy with we had big hopes.  No disappointment here we all left very full and satisfied.  The fish was fresh and the service was excellent.   We came in at 5pm on a Friday night so the place was empty - we were the first customers.  This was of great advantage to us it allowed us all to sit at the bar and watch the "sushi show."  If your looking for good quality sushi without all the frills this is the place for you.  I enjoyed their green dragon roll and a sashimi plate.  The kids ate there usual and mom enjoyed some sushi and saki.  I would return for sure next time I travel to philly!

You realize what your favorite restaurants are when you don't get to eat at them for months. During lockdown, we heard large companies declaring bankruptcy and closing their doors -- the only thing I could think of was: is Entree going to be okay? When they posted they were doing their classic 3 course meal for $30 for takeout, my husband and I immediately ordered. And I can assure you, the frutti di mare tastes just as good at home as it does in the restaurant. Supporting our favorite businesses has never been more important than now, and I have never been more thankful for Entree BYOB.

I finally made it to Audabon after trying for several months.  They now opened a retail store in Chinatown.  I only tried one lavender macaroon, which had a creamy center and was delicious!  However, I tried all 4 puddings!  The carmel and banana are rich and addictive.  The sesame and buttermilk are more subtle and less rich, but just as good.  The quality of everything is evident, as well as fresh.  I am hooked, and planning my next visit.  The owner Audrey is a friendly and enthusiastic woman.  I hope they do well in their new location.

[ENGLISH BELOW]

Kalo lagi lapar dan siap makan enak, jangan melewati restoran luar biasa ini.  Pada HUT saya, kami bertiga mampir dan pesan cukup untuk 6.  Nasi Rendang komplit dgn ayam, kale, kentang, sambal hijo, udang.  Bihun goreng luar biasa.  Tahu dan tempe goreng dgn sambal merah (habis), bakso tahu goreng (Batagor), ikan bakar.  Belum selesai nih!  Soda gembira , es teler, dan juice sirsak.  Perut kenyang dan lingkar pinggang nambah dekit

OK.... if you are hungry and ready for something delicious don't skip this amazing Indonesian restaurant. For my birthday three of us came and ordered enough for six people. The Rendang rice dish includes a beef steeped in coconut and curry, chicken, kale, potato, Green chili sauce, and shrimp.  Yummy stir fried noodles (bihun goreng), fried tofu and tempe, grilled fish.  Also soursop juice.  Full stomach and full hearts!  Having lived in Indonesia for 15 years, I can tell you that this is about as good as you can expect in the USA. Don't pass up a chance to try fantastic Indonesian food.

Awful. Went here 3 years ago and cut by a girl who was very good. The guy who owns it was there tonight and cut my hair. Didn't ask what I wanted and assumed just a fade which I was fine with... he cut half of the hair on top and missed the rest...
Fade was awful. Didn't realize until o got home..
Also I asked if they took card. When haircut done he told me the card machine was not working... I very eeasily could have just left that's how dissatisfied I was but I decided to pAy the man and leave.
Awful will never come bAck

This Canadian was very happy to see a smokes open in Philly, my new home. Poutine is the perfect post drinking food. The Toronto downtown locations will see 100's of people lined up after 2am when the bars close even in the snow and cold. 

This location largely tastes like the Canadian locations. I'm a traditionalist, I only get the the classic poutine made with fries, gravy and cheese curds. This is how poutine is made in the majority of Canada. These three ingredients are perfect together and adding pork, chicken, cheesesteak or anything is blasphemy. Why mess with perfection? But that's just my opinion so try all the different poutines and come to your own conclusion.

I've been here 3-4 times since they opened and service was friendly with each visit and the food was consistently good. I do find the poutine slightly saltier than it would be served in Canada but that appears to be true of many foods in the U.S. 

Philly now has a few Poutine options in the city but I think Smokes is the best of the group. Well worth a visit if you enjoy poutine and more importantly if you never had poutine... what are you waiting for!!!! 

PS: poutine is good anytime of the day, not just after drinks...

When in Philly....!! to be fair I'm not a fan of Philly cheesesteaks but my husband really wanted to try them in Philly, so we ended up here based on the reviews.

There was a line coming out of the restaurant but it moved pretty fast. I ordered the hamburger and my husband had the cheesesteak. The hamburger was pretty disappointing, lacked any flavor, the buns weren't hot, and it didn't come with any condiments, but I guess I'm probably the only person that orders hamburgers in a Philly cheesesteak place. 

My husband wasn't raving about his sandwich either, so it was an ok lunch. 

The place is pretty small and crowded so getting a table can be very tricky, so plan accordingly!

We enjoyed shopping through this Whole Foods. The people here were really nice.  However, there were some products that seemed like it had been sitting out unrefrigerated as the color of the meat had changed.  We let our cashier know about this and she told us she would inform someone immediately. We appreciated that she cared about our concerns.

Something different here that I had not seen in my local Whole Foods in Chicago were baked pound cakes. I ended up purchasing one just to try and they tasted really great. It even included fresh blueberries. Yum! It was on sale too that made it very affordable. Location is good too. It was only a mile away from where we stayed downtown. We had no problems walking here.

I love the expressive hand. I have gone here on dates with my husband. Had a mini bridal shower with 2 of my closest girlfriends and spent a day painting with my sister in law for my soon to arrive child.  Each time has been fun, relaxing, and creative. The people who work there are so helpful and we always sit with new people and get to spend the afternoon getting to meet new friends.

A decent place to eat, our waitress got the food mixed up with another table, he also did not charge us for the food. The food was OK, nothing to the extraordinary Overall, I would recommend it, but I wouldn't come back because it's not my scene.

Just received our order, after we found out that they had forgot part of order I called them back and was told by the girl on the phone that it wasn't her fault and they're not going to send the driver back. So here's one  customer who won't be ordering from this place again

Love this coffee shop!  This place made studying for my CPA exams (marginally) less painful.  They have great drinks and good food.  They also have very cute outdoor seating in the back of the cafe.  

If you're staying at the cafe for awhile, remember that refills are 10% off!

Excellent experience, but as a diner, Sbraga breaks one cardinal rule.

Last night, my wife and I went to Sbraga, which is Top Chef's Kevin Sbraga's new restaurant that opened last October.  It has a loud, open kitchen atmosphere, with a lively vibe, and well lit by the massive windows that fill the restaurant with natural light.  Service is attentive, and on par with a four star establishment, so you won't feel forgotten.

But lets cut right to it, shall we?  The food is excellent, and in nearly all dishes, they were inventive, flavorful, and even whimsical (try the scallop with pressed watermelon, hinted with jalapeño and mint).  I had a wedge salad (which seemed like a step down for a spot like this), a sliver of sea bass with pine nuts, and quail with pork belly.  Now, all these dishes had excellent flavors (though its hard to screw up a wedge salad).

But I left hungry.  I get it, its fine dining, dishes are typically small, but even at most fine dining restaurants, your third or fourth course has something a little more substantial on the plate than what was delivered.  My wife had one, beautifully cooked scallop, but just one.  My branzino, though delicious, looked like it was put through a pasta press.  The quail had excellent flavors, but struggled to satisfy, and I found myself picking at the bones, trying to get one more morsel.  

So my wife and I left Sbraga with a smile, and grumbling tummies.  Maybe next time, I'll have a snack beforehand.

This is a very low-key restaurant that does much of its business as takeout. It has a dozen tables or so for in house eating. It looks like a family run business. The service is friendly fast. I had the lamb kebab. It was just just fine! I would go back.

Philly Self Defense is run by a great group of people dedicated to helping people of all skills and experiences gain the tools necessary to feel confident and safe. I really enjoyed the foundations course, which provided a great level of instruction as well as a bit of a workout, in addition to gaining practical skills. I'm looking forward to trying a functional fitness class next.

Their good is good when you actually get what you asked for.  The last two times I went I couldn't even identify what they gave me.  I love the BBQ pork sandwich.  When they get it right, best sandwich ever. I just want what I order.  Five stars if they get it right.  Zero for the   salami-type meat product today they gave me today.  Lesson learned.  Check your order before you leave!!

Added more stars because I've been getting what I ask for.  And they redecorated!!

Pleasingly, my friend and I were able to get a seat here immediately during the crazy downtown happy hour rush.

The chefs' counter made for an entertaining seat, the pizza was delicious and perfectly sized for 2 to share.

Our server seemed to have some trouble navigating the very narrow and cramped space to get back to us, and because she was taking our order from behind us, had a lot of trouble hearing and understanding our order. Indeed it took 3 tries before we were actually able to successfully express that we wanted to order beers.

The restaurant really could have used one more server to improve the service time and attentiveness, but in such a tiny space so chock full of tables, I simply don't think there was room.

Matcha and Nutella are my favorites from this place. They are not too sweet and the matcha has even a hint of bitterness with I LOVE. 

The service is always on point and you can always ask to try the flavors first! 

Quality of the ice cream is really nice! Very creamy and smooth! Definitely my go to ice cream place in Chinatown
So many fees if u try to quit, plus fees just for the sake of fees while you are a member. Sad stuff.

This review is based on lunch only. 
When I try some place for the first time, I order the basics. If a restaurant can't do the basics well, I won't order anything else. 
I got the regular mac n cheese which comes with "bacon bits."  The bits were actually large cubes of bacon. Looked great on paper. Not so great tasting. I thought it was a bit too smokey and the bits were chewy and very unappetizing. Would order again without bacon as crazy as it sounds. 
Ordered a burger. Bun was really cold for whatever reason. Burger patty was decent enough. Fries were delicious. 
Friendly service, but had a very long wait time for appetizers. 
Like I mentioned earlier, this is a review for lunch only.  Two stars as it is less than it claims to be.
 I will try dinner.

The food is GREAT!!   

The service is BAD.

For a take out/delivery spot, I don't expect much as far as service goes, but WOW was I disappointed when I had ordered delivery, on a Thursday night which shouldn't be that busy, and my estimated delivery time was 60 minutes.  I figured I'd buckle down and just wait. 

And wait.  and WAIT. 

After an hour and 10 minutes, I called.  I was very polite and asked if maybe my order had been confused as a pick up....no.  It'll be out in just a minute.  WOW.  So it hasnt left the restaurant yet.  I simply said thank you, I'm just glad now it's on the way!  

It has now been an HOUR and 30 MINUTES!! food still not here. BUMMER.
My nails after 3 days, there is some weird dark bubbles in the paint.  Over all friendly enough staff though they don't like to be corrected if you don't like your nail shape...
Amazing and friendly staff, beautiful decor and ambience and the drinks are perfectly crafted by Nick!!!

Walking to the corner pizzeria in south philly to pick up some pizza for my friends, I had not known what my wife ordered for us. I was immediately blasted in the face with a beautiful odor of a plethora of cultural toppings- pepperoni, sausage, beef, salsa, peppers and spices. What I thought was just a pizzeria looked like something way more. I walked away with a large pepperoni and a chicken mole pizza. I had no idea that they had mexican style pizzas and served breakfast! Ahh! The New York Style pep was solid but I craved over the chicken mole. With red onions, mole sauce, and mozarella?? Supa yum. This place is intriguing to my brain and was suggested to try the cheese fries and the al pastor pizza which I will definitely be trying SOON.

I love this place. The coffee is awesome, the customer service is always pleasant (which is surprisingly hard to find) and the cafe itself is a super comfortable place to get work done!

Go here!

Following other Yelpers' reviews, I realized that the old Tweed spot has converted into an American seafood restaurant. And what's made me excited was the offering of $1 oysters. Well, Summer is coming and there's nothing say Summer more than seafood. 

The place itself is lofty, decorated in an old world hotel bar room scheme but happy hour here still attracts a large crowd. Once upon entering you'll be greeted by a very friendly hostess, since we're having dinner, we were led upstairs. 

The menu itself is quite exciting. It's more adventurous than your regular traditional American fare. Raw bar, crudo, carpaccio, intriguing ingredients here and there, flourishes; it's full of things that I really want to eat. 

First off, raw oysters. When I first asked the waiter whether we can participate in the Happy Hour festivities, he said no, but that doesn't mean we can't order the $1 oysters. They are sold by 5, so we had 10. It comes in a bed of ice with lemons, cocktail/horseradish sauce, mignonette and 2 miniature bottles of Tabasco. They are not exceptionally huge but they are still a good size, fresh, clean and tasty. I know a couple friends that would go gaga for these and I'll bring them next time and each get at least 10.

The crudo sampler is cute, This day we had kampachi, fluke and salmon, The fluke being our favorite. The curry oil and sea bean went surprisingly well with raw fluke. The portion is a bit small but it's fun to try something different. I've long had pig ears, it's popular as street foods in Asia and the ones here are crispy fried and came with hot sauce. It's almost like a meatier bacon, surprisingly not too greasy and the portion is good. The idea of having roasted bone marrow is awesome but the problem is that there wasn't a lot of it in the bone, definitely not enough to spread over the four buttered toasts it comes with. I wish there's a lot more of it because at the end I don't even know if I knew how it tasted. They also offer a bourbon and use the bone as a luge, but it doesn't hold a lot of appeal for me.

Keeping on with the unusual we ordered the pork jowl, it comes highly recommended by the waiter. He explains that the jowl is usually very tough but the jowl was cooked for 9 hours and comes out tender and juicy. He's not very far off. The dish comes with a slightly spicy cheesy grits that is very delicious, a few whole spring onions, slightly bitter and cute and crunchy breakfast radishes. Thinking that it's not quite enough, we also had a roasted baby beet salad, multi colored roasted baby beets with a creamy goat cheese with a side of arugula. It's one of the best things of the night.

I'm coming back next week with new friends to try new things.

Supreme has lost themselves a star. And it's not just because they're no longer the only game in town for Haagen-Dazs chocolate chocolate chip ice cream. (Although that doesn't help.)

After shopping there a few more times, I've realized that they actually are pretty overpriced compared to most supermarkets. The layout is actually pretty unintuitive, too. You want tupperware? It's next to the drink mixes, across from the Virgin Mary candles.  Duh.  The checkout process is also pretty unpleasant. The lines are usually long (20 minutes+ for a non-express lane), several of the lanes are cash-only, and the checkout folks are usually chatting with each other and/or snacking, which is not conducive to my getting out the door quickly.  Finally, the place has a kind of grimy feel to it.  I don't think it's actually unclean, but if I'm going to buy my food from someplace, I want to be able to literally smell the Pine-Sol on the floors. Freshly-waxed and squeaky-clean it is not.

This is my favorite restaurant. Everything on the menu is fabulous. It never gets boring since they change the menu up often.  The owner and staff are always friendly, attentive and a pleasure to deal with. I would give it 10 stars if possible. 

Carol U.

Sandwich was pretty big and filling but for a cheesesteak it hardly had any. Prices weren't
 bad..for 3 sandwiches paid $30. The cheese fries were GREAT!!! Tons of cheese and they were just great! 

Would I take that drive from Jersey for just that? No, but if I were here..i sure would come again.

We spent a day in Philly on our way home to Syracuse. Cheesesteaks are our favorite sandwich, and we've had the best of what Syracuse has to offer. (Spoiler alert: None of them are in the same league.) Through our visit, we had marked five places to get cheesesteaks while in town. Genos and Pats were more for their history and relevance than anything else, but it was nice starting here and working our way up.

We had stopped by Geno's the night before since all the other places were closed. The next morning we came to Pats, since none of the places were open yet. I love that they have the food ready less than 20 seconds after you've placed the order. Wrapped and everything, but still assembled fresh. We also appreciated that they broke down the cheesesteak jargon on posters for us ignorant outsiders. We got our cheesesteak "wiz wit". Wiz for the cheese, which we had never heard of until coming to Philly. "Wit" meaning "wit onions", as opposed to "wit out". We loved it in Philly.

The bread was soft, and the onions were cooked all the way. The meat was in full, thin chewy slices. They don't cut the sandwiches in half, which was unfortunate for us because we ate this one in the car outside of Swann Memorial Fountain. The sandwich was messy to the touch, it took some serious doing to get this thing into two pieces and eat it without cheesing up the whole car. But we managed, and we'd do it again.

We voted Genos over Pats, and all other places over both. There's a lot of Kitsch surrounding both "original" locations, and it's made them both soft. Still though, absolutely no complaint coming from out of town. My rating of 3/5 is based on context. I'd go out of my way to eat at Pat's any day if it were in Syracuse, but I'll never come back now that I know what else is available in Philadelphia.

When I shop here, my inner hippie twirls and buzzes with delight.  Granted, I start feeling a bit self-righteous, like Kyle's dad on South Park when he buys a Prius, moves to San Fran, and starts smelling his you-know-whats, but STILL!  The produce is local and unique.  I like that you know what farm you're buying from.  And they sometimes have baby bok choy, which is great for a quick stir-fry.

I haven't tried any of the frozen meats yet (ground bison, anyone?) but I've heard their products are delicious...and after watching Food Inc., buying meat here would set my mind at ease.  I did pick up some of the raw, vegan macaroons on Sunday because they were on sale (past expiration).  Aside from being a touch stale, I really enjoyed the lemon poppyseed flavor.

My only complaint about this place would be that it's a bit pricey, but that's to be expected until one day, in a dream world, buying local, seasonal produce becomes the norm.

This place is one of my favorite tea places in Philly. They have a little bit of everything for everyone. I enjoy their black- or green tea-based drinks the most. 

Some of my favorites include - First Love (Red Guava, Passion Fruit, Aloe Jelly), Passion Fruit Black Tea with Mango Jelly, Honey Jazz (Honey, Jasmine, Lemon, Aloe Jelly), and Sunset (Kumquat, Lemon, Aloe Jelly). I do recommend reducing the amount of sugar and ice in your drink, as I am sugar-sensitive and like a less ice/drink ratio. However, I do not prefer the milk bubble tea here. I would much rather go else where for those, but I know their Thai Tea is quite popular.

Additionally, I really like their onigiri and fishballs as a quick snack or light meal. Typically, I can order two onigiri and be satisfied for a while. They offer onigiri filled with avocado, kani salad, spam, seaweed, salmon, shrimp tempura, or Kenshin (kani & seaweed). They're all good and come with spicy mayo on the side.

At first.. I was ecstatic about the doggie day care.. Friendly staff, large facilities.. Etc. Etc. Everything on their website seemed fantastic ! But what I loved the most was the intense detail they put into your dog, meaning assessment sheets and things like incident reports, who they played with and so on. I left my puppy there Memorial Day weekend and she came home a happy pup! Couple of scratches but we didn't think anything of it being we have a pitbull puppy and she's very hyper at times. Plus we loved the owner Portia.

We recently left our dog with them on Saturday and retrieved her Sunday morning. When we picked her up and brought her home we noticed her face was extremely scratched up as well as her stomach. We called and called the facility only after numerous phone calls did someone answer. when we told her of the incident she became extremely passive aggressive and told us it was a result of our puppy ramming her face full force into the crate constantly. ?!?!. Complete nonsense. Our dog has been crated outside and at home, with other dogs and never has done that.

The owner Portia returned my phone call and was very nice and asked me to email pictures of our dogs face and stomach and we did. Today my girlfriend was returning back their phone call and the husband or other owner answered and became very rude and aggressive telling her she needs to stop going on and on! 

We just wanted them to acknowledge the fact that maybe something happened while they weren't paying attention to the dogs. 

Very disappointed with the outcome, we were very hopeful we finally found a good doggie day care!

This place is sucks, me and my gf we went to this place and the waitress wasnt nice at all, she dont even smile or talk to us about the food but she did talk to other customers, why? Bcause we are asians and the other customers are americans? She seems so racist and the owner and the cook too, we feel like they are just so racist to asian people becausethey greet happy new year to the other caucasian customers. This is the second time and last time i gonna come.same treatment on first visit to us.
Really love Aldos pizza steaks wing dings best place in the neighborhood. I would recommend dining in or picking up. Delicious A+++

Hands-down the most overrated pizza in the city. I lose respect every time anyone touts this place to be anything near-good. I used to go here quite frequently actually but that's when I realized it just wasn't good pizza.

Most Philadelphians honestly have no clue what the hell good pizza even looks or tastes like, so don't trust the locals on this one.

For almost the current price on a gallon of regular gasoline, you can purchase yourself a gigantic hot mess of slop. The crust is consistently sloppy and chewy, the cheese is over-abundant if you're into that sort of thing, and what little sauce there happens to be is void of any real saucy taste.

Also, if you get there at the right time you can experience the best, worst drunk crowd imaginable. Expect the service from the establishment to be pretty close to any "Soup Nazi" comparisons.

It's really not even worth it if you're drunk at 3am. Drunkenness lowers inhibitions, it shouldn't make crap like this taste that much better. Double up on your money and buy yourself a delicious falafel sandwich from Maoz on the other corner of 3rd & South or just walk down to Wawa for whatever your heart desires.

Their food is tastier than Evans (also doesn't hurt my stomach in an hour), and their beer is cheaper. Plenty of room to sit, plenty of beer and wine selections. Never had a bad experience. Pasqually's is good stuff. And the people at the counter are always super nice.

I had really high expectations for Cry Baby and everything was good but didn't quite meet my high expectations. We got a bunch of pastas and the eggplant parm and ate family style (note: the place is not really a "family style" place but we just share food). The eggplant parm was really good. The potatoes (a side order) were probably the best thing we had. All the pastas were fine, not amazing. The cacio y Pepe pasta was a bit startchy in particular. 

The service was very great. We celebrated a birthday and they made a nice fuss over the birthday girl. We were the first to arrive for dinner right when they opened on a weekday so it wasn't crowded at all.

The  service and rudeness was unacceptable.  They insult Gulf Arabs. It took about 30 mins for the food and when it arrived , it was cold. They didn't want to heat it and they did not want to clean the tables. Absolutely rude and  unclean.

This was my favorite late night go-to when I'm hangry and need a good drink. It's never overly crowded and it's reasonably priced. 

However, my last experience was quite a disappoint. It was the food but the service ... 

Imagine ... it's 10pm and you just finished working a 12 hour shift on your feet all day with no break. You friend wants to meet up to chat. Why not pick a place like Frankie's to do so. I arrive 10 minutes before my friend and ask the gentleman for a table of two. He walks to the waitress and talks to her for a moment and then seats me. He tells me that she will be right over. Well that right over never came. In fact, there were several employees who walked by without any acknowledgement.

So, finally after 15 minutes, I stop a gentleman as he's walking. He seems annoyed. I asked If I could place an order for a drink.  He tells me that I have to order it from the bar. (That's when I should've asked for the check ). I told him that I requested a table for a reason and it wasn't to run back and forth to the bar to place orders. Can you please get my waitress? 

He walks over to her. She comes over about 20 mins later. I'm annoyed but giving the benefit of the doubt. I order a drink and I never see her again. Oh and my friend arrived about 10 minutes ago and no offer of anything from the staff. I am furious at this point. And that never happens. It takes a lot for me to get to that point. 

Finally, the bartender comes over and tries to explain. Miscommunication ... she had clocked out ... blah blah blah. And yes, I was listening. My point about all of this is that I didn't know any of this. It is not a good feeling to be ignored by staff that see you but don't want to make eye contact. 

This situation should never have happened. In fact, I consider this customer service 101. A simple apology from the waitress (who had clocked out but was still there when I walked out) would've gone a long way. But anyway, before this, I had been there several times. Since this happened, I haven't been back. You lost one. But ...Hopefully the service gets better.  It only takes one time for me. Sorry. 

 But if anyone does go, the mac n cheese is to die for.

Cold pizza, and I live a block away from them. This place used to be pretty good about their food, however their quality has  definitely gone down A LOT.

Conveniently placed, but still not worth it. The wings are good, but then again it's hard to mess up chicken wings.

We have been using Boulevard Animal Hospital ever since Dr J Geffen started his practice. We have had 4 dogs and 4 cats and have always recieved excellent care for our pets.  The staff is just great and knowledge is superb.   I would never think of changing to a different vet. We started using Dr J Geffen over 30 years ago when he only made housecalls.  We now use Dr Brett for our 2 Siberian Huskies and when he notices a potnetial problem he reacts to make sure that there is not a serious problem.I have full confidence in these vets and I would never think of changing.

Wow! I was in shock at how amazing this local spot is. I'm
Already back from vacation and still dreaming of this pizza. I would say if you're a pizza lover and vacationing in Philly you HAVE to check this out! 

As vacationers we wanted to check out the local spots and not so much the tourist spots. Luckily we fell into this restaurant

When we first got there we grabbed the only available table outside. The place was packed! Our waitress was such a sweetie! She recommended a ton of attractions and local food spots for my boyfriend and I to check out! It was really really kind of her to take the time to share that information with us. If you are reading this - we appreciate you!! 

We ended up going inside and sitting at the bar for quicker service. It was kind of late already and we were starving! We were able to sample a couple of phillys beers which was a nice touch. My boyfriend ordered one and a chicken parm pizza and I ordered (I forget the name) the second option on the menu. It's just red sauce, cheese, and herbs but that is authentic pizza! If you can appreciate that and the taste of real Marzano tomatos you'll love it here! 

The restaurant isn't huge in space but that's what makes it so special. It's also in walking distance of most hotels and local bars if you're interested in checking those out. We did not check the bars out but we did walk from the Loews hotel. Not a long walk at all. 

If and when I go back to Philly, I am definitely coming back for more pizza!!

This place was able to accommodate our very large group of approximately 60 people for two years in a row now on our senior trip. We've made it a part of our repertoire to stop by because of the kind people, the quick and friendly support/service and large space. With several options, from a buffet to sandwiches, there is something you can find for all diners. 

The students also raved about the food, so it is very kid friendly!

Outstandingly fresh sushi.  I have been going to Osaka since it started a quite few years ago.  So much better than any other sushi place I know of in the area.   Expensive even by NY sushi prices but always amazing and they charge $2.00 for a green tea.  (In NYC tea is always free.) The lunch specials are very good.  Spicy tuna, yellowtail scallion, spicy yellowtail and north shore rolls are amazing. Sashimi 100% fresh. Also recommend the chef's choice for one or two.  Nice choice of wines and some creative cocktails.  Very nice atmosphere. Nice place to go to celebrate birthdays etc. or just for a treat. Friendly helpful waiters.  Best to go on days it is less crowded.  However note:  There is NO happy hour here and there has not been one for years.

If I could give The Victor Cafe 10 stars, I would do it without hesitation. What a fabulous experience on every level!!!

Hard to know where to begin, but food first. 

My main dish was stellar, could not possibly have been one smidge more succulent. It was a special of the day, the Frutti de Mar, and it brimmed with mussels, calamari, scallops, clams, oysters and fish. The broth was heavenly. They prepared mine over risotto rather than linguini as I don't do wheat, and it was a wonderful combination. Several gluten free options and they are happy to switch up most noodle items as well to accommodate. 

Our table of six shared a Caesar salad and the smelts appetizer. I have never experienced a better Caesar, and it could easily serve as an entire meal. Smelts are not my favorite in general, but I liked them and my father loved them (he's the anchovy/smelt guy). 

Oh! And the desserts! To die for. 

The history is well worth the read (on the menu or website). Really neat. And of course every 20 minutes or so an operatic piece and singer are announced and then the place swells with magnificent song! 

It was my father's ninety first birthday and he was serenaded by an operatic rendition of happy birthday! He loved it!!! A great place to celebrate an occasion. 

The atmosphere is friendly and energized (albeit pretty loud, my parents are older and don't hear well so were not able to participate in a group table convo - but they didn't complain, as the experience itself made up for it). 
The upstairs is a fun place to have a drink. Very open and friendly, with beautiful old brick walls and arches. Worth checking out even if you are wining and dining at the table only. 

So. To recap - Food. Beyond excellent. Atmosphere:   Fantastic. Service: spectacular.
they forgot our guac, they forgot to put lettuce, and some of the chicken in my burrito was inedible, never had a bad experience here before so it was a disappointment

Some of the best mashed potatoes in the city! This may not be a major menu item but definitely worth the addition to anything you order. 

I recently visited for their brunch and was a little overwhelmed! The blueberry French toast was way too sweet, I love a sweet brunch item but this one was hard to finish. 

Service is always great but this can be caused by the seemingly low amount of tables they've had on my prior visits. Great spot to stop into if your in Center City and need a bite but don't have reservations elsewhere...they always seem to have room or a wait that's less than 15 minutes!

So glad to have this in the neighborhood! Finally made it in today and it was a delightful treat. I had the Briezzi crepe which included my favorite items: cheese (Brie), fruit (sweet figs), Provençal herbs, and balsamic glaze. Perfect combo and one I've never had in a crepe. Savory and sweet! It made me so content. I also had some fancy pants soy chai made with lots of love that warmed my soul. Please go here. Please patron this place and keep it open forever. I can't wait to try the shakes, waffles, and smoothies... As well as the other crepes!! So excited to go back.

Always have a great time when I visit!  Side-bar: don't bother making a reservation-they will have no idea what you are talking about-lol. Great Caribbean food-strong drinks (the liquid weed is awesome). Good vibe. Enjoy!

I know that Riverwest generally gets good reviews, but I've been here for less than 2 months and can't wait to leave. It may be the case that I was spoiled in 2601 Parkway, but I'm of the mind that people should do their jobs and buildings should be managed by intelligent humans.

Where to start. For one, the elevators are horrible. Add at least 5 minutes to any travel time. Recently 3 out of the 4 elevators were broken and this was seriously unpleasant. I've also heard stories of people getting stuck, and I've experienced dogs peeing in them.

The hallways are pretty much not taken care of and are all dirty as hell. I've been tempted to go clean the walls and carpet.

I could deal with all of this if the staff were at all functioning. But they aren't. I have never had any of my questions answered, and I'm usually yelled at. Recently I've been having issues with my apartment, stemming from the contractor that the building recommended I use. The building maintenance was not "equipped" to help (ie. hang up a curtain rod) and so I had to hire someone else. I've learned through this situation that the manager of the building is pretty much unattainable and it took me a call to the management company to actually get him on the phone. You normally have to leave him a voicemail and hope that he calls you back. When I finally talked to him I asked if he could recommend another contractor since both the original one is MIA and the building maintenance is not "equipped" to do maintenance. He told me he had a card somewhere but that he's really busy with classes and probably won't have time to look. Right. Thanks. I proceeded to call the manager at my old building who was able to give me an organized document of people they recommend...contractors, plumbers, electricians, etc. etc. Glad they had time to help despite the fact that I DON'T EVEN LIVE THERE. I also find this funny, because they are managed by the same umbrella company. How Riverwest has gone so terribly wrong is beyond me.

At least the location and price are great, or else I'd be a VERY unhappy camper. My apartment has also been very nicely redone by my landlord. Wish the rest of the buildings management was up to par.
don't see what all the fuss is about. ate there today, got gristle in my steak, paid $9.00 for it. and threw half away! ps- cash only!

Another one from the annals of McThrill's forgotten to review list. I didn't forget about it because it was bad, but because I went during the one game of the World Series that the Phillies did horribly in. So that blacked out the memory a little. 

But bad gaming watching blemish aside McCrossen's was a decent bar with friendly and humorous servers, Pumpkin Ale on tap (that's probably phased out since it was Fall, but hopefully there's a good Winter Ale taking it's place) And awesome specials like a full rack of ribs. It's not a place that I would go out of my way to go to. But it's a consistent place that's perfect for hanging out with my Fairmount friends.

Fantastic!!!! For my daughter's pre-wedding day bridal nail party at their salon, they had champagne, cupcakes, and a bouquet for her. And they did the best nails to boot! If you are celebrating your wedding or engagement, please go to these people. They will make the most of your celebration!

We went to this place thinking it had nothing but yogurt, they have 4 yogurt flavors only, the rest was all ice cream, I got cookies and cream ice cream, it had absolutely no flavor, I got chocolate chips, weird old freezer burnt flavor, I will definitely not be returning its a shame because its around the corner from my house!

Everything was perfect.  Our server was extremely accommodating and allowed my friend to drink his own beer (we had originally planned on going to a BYO)-- I wouldn't plan on taking advantage of their kindness on this one occasion, but I think it's certainly to their credit that they'd let it slide that one time.

Our server was attentive, quick, and friendly without being in our face too much-- perfect balance.

The food was also really great- between the 3 of us we had the buffalo burger, salmon, ahi tuna sashimi, and tomato bisque.  Everyone enjoyed their food, everything tasted fresh and was cooked perfectly.  Especially the salmon- everyone has a salmon dish, and salmon is almost always fine, but this salmon was perfectly done.  It made me realize how mediocre all the other salmon dishes I've ever had really were.

There were no leftovers.  It was a great dinner out- we felt like we had our privacy in a fun, bustling setting and we had great service-- the kind that's so good that you almost don't notice it.  I have no complaints!

I haven't had much good Mexican in Philly. I discount this as a mexican place because it's too pricey and mexican food should be cheap. But, they have great drinks, the food is good, it's a fun atmosphere and my dates have always been happy. All and all it's a good spot.

We just found this restaurant. Cause we always around washington ave for get spanish food. On sunday this place already busy.we still have to wait.after we get our food.the food so delicious. We love this place.
Nice spot for hangout with big screen tv for watching sports if that matters to you. The wings are good, I had jalapeño cilantro, very interesting flavor.

Manakeesh! I'd been stalking you on Facebook and Yelp, ogling your flatbread sandwiches and exotic sweets. Yesterday afternoon, when I saw a "now deal" on Groupon, I decided that the day had finally come to taste your Lebanese wonders. So I grabbed the groupon and a good friend, and I headed across the river to West Philly.

They have two outdoor seating areas: one in the front, as well as a patio on the side. The interior is open and airy (the building was formerly a bank), with Arabic designs decorating the exposed brick walls. They really did a great job with the space. It's lovely and welcoming.

From the equally welcoming staff, I ordered the following:

-Veggie manakeesh: possibly my favorite of the manakeesh...es.

-Lahm bajeen manakeesh: ground beef with tomato; second favorite. This one and the veggie would be good ones to try first if you're new to this type of food, because they have familiar flavors.

-Kafta manakeesh: ground beef with lamb and parsley. For me, this one fell under the "glad I tried it, but wouldn't necessarily order it again" category.

-Honey manakeesh: this, presumably because of the liquid-y nature of the toppings, came wrapped up instead of flat. It's basically a hoagie-shaped wrap of fluffy bread filled with honey and "sweet cream" (tasted similar to ricotta) that you eat like a giant sandwich, while getting honey freakin' everywhere, like a slob. (Or that might just be me.) My Iraqi friend tells me that back home, this was a standard breakfast for him. I can confirm that it makes a lovely accompaniment to one's morning tea, because I had my leftover half this morning. The honey provides just enough sweetness without pushing it all the way into dessert territory. (They use raw honey, as well.) Love!

-Sfeeha: mini meat pie, which you can order individually or by the half-dozen/dozen. Adorable and delicious.

-Fatayer: mini spinach pie. Same deal as above; almost as adorable and delicious.

-Some type of sweet cake that I cannot remember the name of.

-pot of tea, which is served in a stainless steel footed teapot on a tray with two small glasses, one mint leaf in each. (Completely irrelevant aside regarding handle-less teacups: After burning my hand on many a piping-hot cup of tea in my day, I often absentmindedly wondered why the Asians and Arabs, who drink so much tea, never embraced the whole "teacup with a handle" idea. But now it occurs to me... if it's too hot to touch, it's probably too hot to drink. Hm. Makes a lot of sense, actually.)

So I had quite the mid-east feast on my first visit, but there is still a ton of stuff I want to try. (Nutella manakeesh with banana and hazelnut shavings?! Want/need.) I've made it a personal mission to sample the entire menu.

If I lived in the neighborhood, I could easily spend all day here studying (they have wifi), sipping tea, and snacking on savory sfeehas.

Brad Richman is the worst dentist I ever encountered. He's pompous arrogant and running a disgraceful practice. Two years ago I had my first surgery of any kind in 64 years on earth. I needed a wisdom tooth pulled. He left part of my root intact and severed my nerves leaving me with permanent damage. The pain every second of every minute of every hour each day is excruciating .He ruined my life . He seems to care more about the money than his profession and the patients. He referred me to another dentist at the Jefferson because he knew he screwed up. I called his office to get reimbursed for his major mistake and he didn't even have any human decency or compassion for the way he ruined my life. I since had to be operated again ( Not by Richman Never)and unfortunately my problem can't be fixed. It's costs me thousands of dollars out of my pocket because of the grave mistake he made on my lip gums and jaw and left me in  excruciating pain,,Shame on you.

Such a cool museum, lots of displays (body parts, wet glass, info lol). 
They have lockers for you to put your stuff in(purses/backpacks). 
Just a heads up- they don't allow you to take pictures in the museum.

Horrible experience with Botox. I had a wedding and I went there what a mistake. There is no symmetry in my face. My right eye had ptosis. It looked like I was hit in the face. First time EVER Botox made my face look worse than when I went. I have been getting these injections for over 10 years. I know what great Botox looks like. My mistake was trying look great. I have a strict skin care regiment to keep my skin is good condition this treatment literally stripped me of all the work I put in. First I was told it would be 12.50 a unit to fix my problem on top of the 360 I had already spent. Then it was come in and we'll look at it. Either way I'd never go back. I get better results from my jade roller. And FYI at the wedding my eye looked horrible you could see it in most pictures!!!!

Speedy, excellent, professional, courteous, knowledgeable service! Hard to find that if you tried. If you got a high performance import or Mitsubishi, what jo ass waiting for?! Bring your car here for service!

Got the need for speed? These guys too! Get your installs done here. Enough said. Call and make an appointment.

Traveled to this hotel from NYC to stay at for labor day weekend. Check in was at 04:00 PM. We arrived at 06:45 PM to check in to ensure it would be ready. Was told it was still being cleaned and would take a couple minutes. Finally given room at 7:25 PM. Our room should've been ready at 04:00 PM. MISSED OUR DINNER RESERVATIONS AS WE DIDNT EXPECT A 30+ MINUTE CHECKIN. NO CUSTOMER SERVICE HELP AND NO APOLOGIES FOR THE DELAY. DO NOT BOOK AT THIS HILTON AT ANY COSTS. 

Will contact corporate to talk about their concierge.
Almost too many incredible choices. Minus one for the absolute mad house that it always is. If you're visiting Philly and don't mind crowded places, this is a must-go.

Over the weekend, I stayed here again at an amazing rate for a girlfriend's night away. It was marvelous! Our room had a great view, the beds were super comfy, and the entire staff was professional and courteous. The entire hotel was booked but you couldn't tell because there were no elevator waits, no mass confusion, lines at checkin or checkout, etc. 

One of my girlfriends got bumped into a suite and we all pre-gamed there. The hotel is beautiful and so convenient to great shopping, restaurants, and nightlife. I will definitely be back...again.

Pretty great food! Haven't tried their pizza yet but they do a fantastic job on a chicken quesadilla it's finely chopped chicken breast cheese onion all STUFFED in between a golden brown grilled tortilla. I have ordered delivery from here two times and it has been really fast both times. Looking forward to trying out their pizza and updating the review. Welcome to the neighborhood and thanks for banging out tasty food

What else can I say that hasn't already been said?  I guess all I can do is share what was on the menu on my first visit.

We rolled in just after noon on a Sunday, following a late night or rather an early morning out on the town.  We were informed there would be a 45 minute wait.  Fully expecting this I was prepared to wait, but the rest of the crew weren't so enthusiastic about it.  After a little reasoning and persuasion (we're just going to stand around waiting someplace else), we put our name in and killed some time on 9th st.  

When we returned we were promptly seated in a tight little table by the front door.  After pouring a round of mud our server gave us a quick rundown on the specials of the day.  They included savory baked bean and smoked cheese pancakes or belgium waffles paired with andouille turkey sausage, challah french toast topped with cantaloupe, raspberries and fig served with lavender/lemon zest syrup, an omelette packed with lima beans, jersey tomato, onions and the like with breakfast potatoes and an option of bacon on the side, and a london broil with onions and peppers with potatoes.  

My roommate and I opted for numero uno, pancakes for him and waffles for myself and our lady friends both chose the omelette.  My roommate was a bit wary of the unorthodox batter additions and our friend Sarah quipped how she wasn't fond of lima beans, but I assured everyone they were in good hands.  

Eyes wandering in between coffee curls we became aware of the phallic centric theme about, interspersed with mammary knick knacks and brick-a-brack procured from Carman's travels.

After a few chuckles our waiter came over with our orders.  Each plate was as satisfying as anyone could have hoped for.  Maple syrup linked up perfectly with the baked beans in my waffles, which were crisp on the outside and fluffy in between.  The sausage was pleasantly spicy and flavorful.  The omelettes rivaled the waffles in fluff factor and were bulging with big fat chunks of ripe tomatoes, lima beans and slop sinks, and the potatoes were among the best I've ever had.  Portions are itis inducing but It wasn't too much to ask of us to clean our plates considering how delicious everything was.

I suppose the only reasonable excuse you could provide for still reading this is Carman's ain't open right now.  Because if it is you should already have your name down for a table.

Although I never actually ordered from here, I will say this: They are so friendly and professional. The employees are more than helpful. I believe the turnaround time was two weeks for T-shirts and I needed them sooner than that which is why I went to another business. But after my experiences with other screen printers, I will always send people here instead. I know for my bridal party shirts, we will definitely get them from here. I like that the fact they do not charge you a bunch of little "file conversion" and other BS fees like other places on 5th street.

Dry steak, had to clean our own tables, 4 customers there. Great bun, ruined by the steak, fruit flys by the cherry pepper display. Uber driver said only tourists go there, that was us. Be out of business without us.

This is my favorite seafood restaurant in Philly bar none.  The food is consistently outstanding, no matter what you order. The oysters are absolutely the best in the city--ask your server for advice, but you really can't go wrong.  The lobster roll reminds me of Maine.  Seafood stew is delicious.  Perfect atmosphere to boot.  And on top of it all, the service is always great--attentive, knowledgeable and supremely polite.
Oh, and the butterscotch pudding for dessert is incredible!
Can't miss here!!!!

Rude staff, broken checkout machines, long lines at the Pharmacy. They are well located & stocked, even an optician on-site but I try to avoid this shithole as much as possible
And while we are on the subject of CVS, why do they insist on littering s phil w/ their unwanted coupon sleeves? You'd think a company who went as far as to eliminate nicotine products would not despoil the environment with plastic bags (and yes, I have multiple of the worthless "no flyer" stickers on my house

I grew up flying in and out of Philly.  I remember when it was delapidated, brown and ugly.  I remember when the food options were limited and somewhat disgusting.  I remember confusing baggage claim and horrible check in lines.

Since their rennovation several years ago, there has been marked improvement on the development of PHL and I give my stamp of approval.

I've never had a bad experience here and I come through PHL usually once a year.  I arrive from SFO, sometimes I have baggage to collect, sometimes I don't.  When I do, I make my way to baggage claim and pick it up with relative ease.  When I make my way back to SFO, I arrive 2 hours early, never usually have an issue with check in (sometimes there's a sports team of sorts (30 girls in tennis gear, 25 boys in soccer jerseys) checking in at the same time as me, but the 2 hour advance arrival usually is enough to get me in and through security on time.  Depending on your terminal, security check in can be confusing.  Some terminals, security wraps the lines around in an annoying way.  Some are pretty straightforward.

If I'm early and in Terminal C-D, I hit the food court for one last Chik-fil-a fix before I head back to San Francisco Proper, which is chik-fil-a-less.

Dr. Beatriz Mirabal Garces has been my dentist for over five years. She's hands down the best dentist I've ever had!!!  A friend recommended her practice, Garces Dental Group, and I'm soooo happy I scheduled that first appointment.  The entire staff is friendly, professional, well qualified and patient-always willing to answer questions, explain things, and provide options if they can. I've always been greeted with a smile. I've never felt rushed or made to feel badly for not flossing more. They provide a number of in-house procedures which is great so you don't have to go elsewhere. Dr. Garces even provides in-house Invisalign orthodontics.  The office atmosphere is light and airy and relaxed, and the music is good. If you need a great dental practice, look no further!!!!
The meal we had was okay, not great, and their biscuits are really strange. We won't be going back. There are other, better places.

Not sure why this spa gets such great reviews, perhaps this type of quality is considered top notch for Philadelphia...?

Unfortunately in my case the service was lacking. Front desk staff is great-very friendly; the atmosphere is very luxe, clean and relaxing. I had a wax with Linda for the first and last time. Despite her somewhat strange attitude (I couldn't tell if she was being extra sassy, arrogant or just aloof) her work was lacking. I usually go to Bliss Spa and I am out of there in half the time, with the actual waxing done properly. If you're going in for a wax, I would not recommend Linda.

Lots of really good pizza here in Suburban Station.  There are always customers in the place, and everyone seems to be working hard to keep things moving.

Mostly thin crust NY style but lots of topping choices.  They also have specialty pizzas, Stromboli, salads, a grill station for steaks, fries, etc.  Lots of bottled sodas and fountain soda, too.

I really liked this pizza!  Reasonable prices, too - just over $15 for a whole plain pie.

Should also mention that large plain is an 18" pie which is really big!  My office mates and I were full with two slices each.  Cheesy!

you got a star for old times sake! The pork sandwich was smaller and tasteless
I do so much better on my stovetop. People were mediocre, nice enough cashier, back help were playful. Can't believe I took my family there. Ever hear of fried hots? You are going to fade tony as all businesses that push the envelope to do. Better make some changes and be like1999

The food at Nam Phoung was OK. I found the food was quickly prepared and not a lot of heart and soul put into it. The food was bland and mediocre. I gave it a second try and experienced the same. The restaurant was a nice size, good for  parties of 10 and up.
So so good, with great staff and wonderful vide. Reason for 4 star is that lunch time is a bloody hell with the line right out the door, but that's what to expect from such a great place
Okay the pizza was really good! BUT the manager (old guy who's always there) is very rude. They need to take plastic.

This place is insane the flavors of vietnam intermingle
With delight here as you savor the sweet beer pho and the soft piano play in the background! The a ala carte Menu offers any coMbination you can think of ! Do you aren't limited to just the broken rIce platters or the soup I like that a lot ! The staff are pleasant and very attentive to anyone who comes in. They fed a homeless man first time I was here I thought that was a rare qualtity  to see nOw a days but great to see in an establishment so close to home! I give them 5 stars if you are shopping in Germantown stop by this place ! It won't disappoint

The one I got (La Playa) has beet, carrot, apple, ginger, lime...in smallest size. Is juice supposed to be not that cold? Cause mine wasn't cold? But I enjoyed it throughly. Wish I got a medium. Under $5. Worth it. Ain't nobody got time to juice on your own. The next time I got Green #1 which has a ton of good stuff in it, but I took out romaine and replaced ginger. They did it for me for free and kindly. I got a weird feeling in my stomach cause all I had was the juice and yogurt with granola so I had to eat half a donut to make my stomach not feel weird. But I also then had ramen, dumplings, ice cream, and beer...so we're all good here. Decor in here is tacky...music is nice tho! And great lighting for doing work and feeling healthy
Don't eat the food. Do talk to everyone. Karaoke your heart out. Have the time of your life. Don't eat the food.

We came with a party of 14 people and we were very disappointed by the service. The food itself was decent (entrees were alright but maybe overpriced) and the desserts were very good. 

The main issue was that our server did not have it together AT ALL. Our entrees came out to us BEFORE our appetizers! And we didn't even get half of the appetizers since they were cancelled without our knowing. Also our server was very inappropriately touching almost everyone in our party and we were not comfortable with that at all. She was rubbing our shoulders and leaning in next to our faces and it caught us all off guard by how unprofessional it was. At least it gave us a good inside joke to go home with.

Really nice! It's such a cute place. Went for Saturday brunch and sat outside. Although I only got the basic eggs, sausage and potatoes it was one of best basic breakfasts I've had!! Everything was seasoned so well and the hot chocolate was heavenly. I will definitely be going back. Great customer service as well!
Horrible recommendations of beer. The selection leans towards sour or super bitter ipas.  Yuck.

I am a little bit of a snob when it comes to food.  Since moving to Philadelphia from NYC its been a bit of an adjustment.  Although there are many quality establishments in Philly, comparable restaurants in NY always blew me out of the water...until I ate are Garces Trading Co. 

Atmosphere was great, service was great, wine was great, the food was fantastic.  My fiancee and I found it to be the best dinner we have had since we moved to Philly (we moved here 16 months ago and we have been to a lot of restaurants in the city).  Highly recommend the Pasta e fagioli, pappedelle with lamb ragu, and stake frites.

For any Texans who live in the area, Percy Street will serve as a beacon for those craving something familiar like the bbq back home.  I had read some press that mentioned Lockhart, TX (a town near Luling) was home to one of their favorite bbq places so I knew they had done their homework.  The menu is simple -- meats by 1/2 and full pound, sides available in both small and large, and big jugs o' beer.  

A quick glance at the menu showed that all of the meats came with pickles, onion and white bread!  Oh yeah!  When we told the waiter that we were from Texas, he asked us if the menu looked like appealing -- to which my husband replied, "Well, does it come out on butcher paper?" And when our waiter said yes, we knew we were in for a good night.

My husband had the Yards root beer (tasty!) and I ordered the Manhattan, TX (recommended by our waiter) and it was great - whiskey, bitters, and they rinse the inside of the glass with Campari.  We ordered a 1/2 pound each of brisket (moist or lean, we went with the moist) and pork belly.  Sauce on the side so you can actually TASTE the meat.  For sides, we chose the mashed potatoes, collard greens and green beans.  All were delicious and needed no additional seasoning (and I make some mean collard greens!).   The only side I might add is some jalapeno cornbread (but that's not a biggie, I just miss it).

Desserts are simple and homemade -- like apple crisp and pecan pie - and we decided to sample the banana pudding.  Fresh bananas, couple of 'Nilla wafers and our meal came to a wonderful close. 

The staff was super attentive and checked on us throughout the evening.  High ceilings, hardwood floors, spacious tables, a solid jukebox, and a good bar area make this a fantastic addition to South Street.  I still smell like I've been roasting over a pit -- which is exactly what I should smell like when I leave a BBQ place! Okay, it's not as good as back home but hang a Texas state flag, put some Jerry Jeff Walker on the jukebox, and serve me a Shiner bock and I might not remember that I'm in Philly.

I have eaten here about 5 times now for breakfast.  Love the food.  The creme brulee French toast is soooooooo good.  Portions are large so you can share.  Service is quick and cash only.  Also tried the salted caramel latte the last time I went and loved it. If it's the weekend get there before 11 for breakfast or be prepared to wait.

Had high hopes for Via Locusta - perhaps too high. I had several happy hour items and was left feeling underwhelmed. Bartender and service were fantastic. Might come back to try more pasta plates, but portions and flavors just weren't there to justify the price.
Good place to grab a slice. Consistently good and always has hit slices ready. Beware- this restaurant has super weird hours that don't correlate with Google or their own website.

Excellent menu and nice people. Loved the tuna salad and meatball appetizers. Excellent flavors. And the entrees were fabulous - gnocchi with wild boar ragu and pappardelle with mint and lamb ragu. Then topped the night with olive oil cake and mixed gelato. The best!

Love coming here for my pedis and dip manicures. Haven't had a consistent nail tech I see yet but each girl I've had so far (5 visits) has been great! Everyone is very friendly and does a great job. They just expanded so the place is bigger to host all the customers.

I have only been here a handful of times, as it's *that* kind of place. The kind that makes your wallet cry as you walk out. Each time I've gone, it has been pretty late at night, but I never waited more than five minutes to get in.

I'm not a big drinker overall, but Franklin Mortgage's drinks are more like works of art than alcohol, so I love them. I can't remember ever having a bad drink there and the atmosphere is just great -- even down to the bathroom.

My recommendation is to finish a night here. It's relaxing and sophisticated while guaranteeing a phenomenal drink.

I always came here to get a bottle of something on my way home from work.. Then I noticed they had a door of craft brew . So on payday I came by to scoop up a bottle I enjoy only to hear how "disgusting" they think my choice was amongst themselves. Which is a shame because I don't mind paying premium for lukewarm beer if it means I'm supporting a store I like.

Really fun to go out to at night (I'm 24 so the crowd is typically around my age). Music is always great and they have the big screen TV with any big games etc. on.

I also really like their food and drinks. My roommates and I are huge fans of the Buffalo Chicken Tenders (so much that we ordered them for our Super Bowl meal.) I've also had the Fish & Chips and they were great -- not too greasy, good white fish inside. Everyone raves about the Shepard's Pie but I've never had it.

The seabass had a watery taste and the steak was dry. It's completely overrated. 
One star for ambiance

I have several "off-the-rack" BB jackets/suits. They have a distinctive style I like, and I am a perfect size 42.   I also have several custom made suits from England. I was sent an invitation to a special "made-to-measure" show - then a follow up call to tell me of the very nice discount at the show.  A made to measure suit should be a suit made to very detailed measurements taken at ordering time. What I got was suit made to off the rack specifications with a different fabrics and lining that is too big, for a lot more money. I will take it to a real tailor and see if he can salvage it. BOTTOM Line: if off the rack doesn't fit go somewhere else!! You father's Brooks Brother's is gone.

Ordered a plain large pizza on seamless. Driver called, I came outside.  See driver walking back to car and driving away.  Call driver, told to "come to F*&king store" to get my pizza.  Call store, talk to manager, get hung up on.  Never again. 

Terrible Cheesesteaks as well, but that is a separate review.

The end result on my wedding day was great. My hair and makeup was what I wanted and lasted all day and night.  Becca knows her stuff and made adjustments when I said I wanted more gold or pink or a piece of my hair was not parted the way I wanted.  Becca is very talented. She was great at individual fake eyelash application. 

My first trial was a disaster. The makeup did not last after 2 hours and actually peeled away from by face.  The foundation or concealer did not sit well with my skin.  I was very nervous about the wedding day so Becca used different foundation (airbrush +$20)  and offered a re-do of my trial makeup which worked out better.

Victoria was ok. Don't expect to ask a lot of questions.  I wanted to communicate the needs of my MOH and MOB but they were not interested so I was a little nervous of how they were going to turn out and if the artist would have the right supplies.  It worked out fine but again, do not expect a lot of custom information.  

Also I asked what brand makeup they used and got a response of professional brands like Bobbi Brown,  Mac, Urban Decay, Not the case. Mascara was $4 Maybelline Great Lash (the Pink and green bottle) and the rest was also CVS sold NYX. Be specific about the brand of makeup you want or bring your own.  

Again the end result looked great and lasted. The process to get there was a bit bumpy for someone who has never gotten married or was nervous about what to expect.

Absolutely the worst emergency room experience I've ever experienced.  Went in fir extreme stomach issues at 2am.  1 other patient.  Saw the nurse twice and the resident twice.  If I didn't have the IV in my arm, I would have left.  He said this is for life threatening conditions.  I guess I didn't qualify.  I will never go there again.  And during this time of the pandemic, no mask.  Horrid hospital.  Oh. One more thing.  I heard them laughing about me, while I was in the room.  I was alone, so my husband couldn't help me.  My mom is in Bensalem and I live in Virginia.

Brown Betty now has small square cakes for sale at this location. I tried the vanilla pound cake with the chocolate icing. It cost $24 and was worth every penny and then some. If you like the cupcakes and you need a cake for a party (or you want to treat yourself) then you will love Brown Betty's cakes.

This place is amazing!!!!! The food is fantastic, consistent and unforgettable. The chef is so nice and always takes his time out to come say hi and remembers us! Such a great place. Live music on fridays and saturdays. Definitely a staple on passyunk. A+!!

Went to the Juice Merchant for the first time on yesterday. I got the protein power smoothie, which contains a soy or hemp protein powder, chocolate, peanut butter, & banana as the stars of the show. It tasted great and had a nice texture, but honestly I'm giving them three stars simply because of how overpriced it is. With tax, I paid around $9.30. It's a refreshing alternative on main street for healthy food especially when you have a baby strapped to you and you're hungry on a walk, but I think I will stick to making my own protein concoctions at home as usual.

Decent fulfillment of your neighborhood chinese needs! I had a neighbor who did work on their kitchen and told us it was by far the cleanest chinese food kitchen he'd worked on. 

My delivery took a little over an hour, but it is a Friday night during COVID. If memory serves, delivery has been speedier in the past. 

I've had many chinese food staples from this place in the past and they've all been very good. Order included vegetable lo mein, shumai, and a Philly roll (just can't resist a Philly roll). The good was no longer hot but still very satisfying. The lo mein was onion heavy, if that's your thing. Noodles were cooked well and had good flavor. It was a little light on other vegetables, but overall good. I haven't had shumai before, if you're in the same boat they were like mini dumplings. I think they may have included meat but I'm not entirely positive and the menu didn't specify. They were tasty and a nice added app without being overwhelming. I've been hesitant to try their sushi, and though it's not the most complicated, the Philly roll was solid!

This may not wow you, but it is a staple in neighborhood go tos. The word of mouth compliment on their cleanliness, and the friendly family I've encountered when picking up will always keep me coming back. Maybe next time I will explore more of their sushi offerings!

Americas most convenient bank can get crowded quickly and sometimes they only have one to two tellers. Drive thru can be slow as well but employees r nice and always apologize for the wait.  They are attentive to your needs once you get to them but the line can be frustrating, especially if you are running in on your lunch break or before a quick trip to Target.

The parking lot is kind of small and there is hardly ever a line at the ATM.  Also if you looking to see a customer service rep about your account, it's never really a wait.

Everyone here is kind and professional. I've gotten multiple services done here. It's an inclusive, non judgmental environment. Everything is clean and organized. 

I've worked with almost everyone at the office - Dr. Ringpfeil, Dr. Bird, Dr. Dorfman, and also Bao Tran. Everyone is so well-versed in all of your dermatological concerns, and will address the issue to the best of their ability.

Pizza City is a great neighborhood pizza shop. I grew up in the neighborhood and this is where we always purchased our pizza from on a Friday night. The prices are reasonable and the staff is friendly. In all the years we've been ordering from Pizza City (20+ years), I've never had a negative experience there.
Best burgers in philly, great staff, and homey environment :) - bring your dolla bills, cash only

Because of it's location right on a busy corner on South Street, I initially avoided trying Yoga Garden because I assumed that the street noise would be audible in class.  FALSE.  The spacious, immaculate space is so quiet and peaceful that you can actually forget that you are on South Street as class progresses.  I've been to classes taught by several different teachers and all have been good.  Some instructors have given different cues than what I have typically heard in other studios; these cues have been very useful in finding appropriate ways to figure out how to get into various postures.  There are plenty of props available and the studio offers free mat rental.  Also, the price points for class cards and monthly memberships are very good.  In addition, Yoga Garden currently offers two 7AM classes (Tues/Thurs) and has said that it will be adding more, potentially starting at 6:30 AM.  So people with crazy jobs who struggle to find time to make it to a yoga class at other studios can do yoga in the morning and then scoot over to work (on time).

So yourself a favor and remember the save room for dessert! The chocolate bao buns are AMAZING. I came here on a Wednesday night and there was about a half hour wait. My favorite thing on the menu are the soup dumplings, the Pac Man buns mainly because they have scrapple on them and the Pastrami Bao Buns. If your on East Passyunk Ave, this is a great addition to the street. If there is a long wait at Cantina, I definitely recommend venturing across the street and checking out this restaurant as an option!
Stopped by on a whim, just had drinks, but service was great, especially for a busy Saturday, and the drinks were great!

I have a pixie cut which requires monthly maintenance and my regular salon seems to be undergoing some kind of scheduling crisis (a story for another day). This led to me frantically Yelping quality salons that would see me the same day. Perfect Cut seemed like a good fit, but I was skeptical because this place doesn't look like much from the outside. 
I called and was told I could pick a time slot or be seen as a walk in. I scheduled for the same day. All of the staff were friendly and efficient. My appointment included a wash/scalp and mini back massage, the haircut, and an after cut rinse and style. I asked for a hairdresser skilled with short cuts and was assigned Nick.
