I gave him very little information and he was able to perfectly execute the cut I wanted. Nick works very quickly and exhibits impressive precision.
Best of all the haircut was $16.

This establishment was EXACTLY what I needed. Good hotdog, good beer, good prices. 
Vibes were 100% chill and we will definitely be back. 
All day $5.50 pitchers?? Sign me up. 
Staff was friendly and helpful.
Decor is funky and inviting!

My experiences with this company are terrible. AVOID AT ALL COSTS. Do not hire Marcus. UPDATE: Marcus, your response to my review in no way reflects our interaction. You are clearly confusing me with another unhappy customer. (There was no discussion of a $90 or $400 job, nor was there any escalation of the situation. Everything was cordial. I really just wanted you to do the job!) I am not aware of the job you are describing in your comment. You obviously have numerous unhappy customers.

Something magical happens when a warm, soft on the inside, buttered and toasted on the outside, JJ Nissen bun contrasts with the cool sweetness of lobster, with just a touch of celery for crunch. In my humble opinion, Oyster House's lobster roll is "good", very good in fact, and possibly the closest you'll get to a "true" Maine lobster roll in Philadelphia. 

Oh, how I miss Maine..
Great staff and yummy steaks! Just wish they were a lil more meaty. (that could just be my inner fat girl talking...)

We came for a quick bite and for our cravings as well. The service and food were outstanding. The burgers were just as ordered and delicious but the service really made our visit stellar!  I had the Bacon Cheddar Burger and Onion Strings was really delicious and so good, it is better. The onion rings, fried pickles, and fries were all good too. Will return and recommend!

Amazing program and preschool  to put your children in, I love the fact that this is not just a regular preschool but its a boutique upscale facility , the program is unique and the facilities is over the top clean. I would never trust my children in another place, if you are looking for somewhere that isn't just a daycare then this is the place for you. Most importantly they have staff you can trust, an amazing curriculum and the food and play area for the children is unlike anything you can find. 10/10 recommended. My children come home everyday and tell me all the amazing things they are learning and how excited they are to go back. My One year old is already trying to read, he can count and memorizes songs. My four year old is writing, reading and drawing the most beautiful artwork. Thank you again Right Steps Education, you were the perfect choice and the best place to have my children I am happy I found a place I can trust with my life.l

i am so glad, that when i went there was a group of kids split up between pats and genos, most went to genos so there was actually some place to sit. so we picked pats to try first. 
thankfully no line so we ordered like total rookies and messed it up and the guy just laughed at us and smiled. 3 words.... it was like mind blowing lol. but he did tell us to 'enjoy' after thanking him when we got the food ((unlike genos))
the meat, small but it had flavor, wasnt dry but wasnt overly moist, and i just wanted a touch more cheese seemed like the middle of my cheese steak was perfect while the ends dry. 
and it travels well !!!!!!!!!!!! went to ny and the bread wasnt even soggy, while genos went across the street to the car and the bun was soaking wet and we had to toss it before finishing.

i pick pats!
peppermint smells good, but overwhelming at times

Came in on a Friday night for dinner with friends. Even though the place was hopping, we were seated quickly and our waitress was fantastic.

There were a couple of ciders and the waitress was more than happy to give me a sample so I could decide. I had one of the flatbreads with my cider and it was delicious.

We also had a soft pretzel for the table, which was awesome... loved the dipping sauces. Perfect casual pre-race dinner with friends!

My son loves this place.  Personally, I'm not a Mexican food lover since spicy and me don't agree.  But my grown, adult children who have spent many an hour eating at restaurants swear by this place.  That's probably the reason every time you drive by there's no parking in their lot.  So there's nothing i can state about a particular menu item only to say that knowing how much my kids love this place it has to be a winner.....

I wasn't sure quite what to expect.  You walk in + it just looks so modern, overly touristly... yet the majority of diners look like locals + alot of college students.  This surprised me because this place is a little pricey + seems to exceed a typical student budget.

I was pleasantly surprised at every dish I tried.  Everything was so good.  And what you think was generic... they added their own flavor to it + just made it that much better.

- The Chicken Potstickers are good, but the taste nothing out of the ordinary.  If anything, the quality of ingredients is really good.
- The Crispy Chicken + Seafood Spring Rolls are fried perfectly, not too oily, and presentation is pretty.
- The Crab Fried Rice is to die for.  Honestly.  So delicious.  And they do not skimp on the crabmeat, no sir.
- The Vegetable Fried Rice was surprisingly really flavorful.  I say this because there is no real main ingredient/protein here like there is for the Crab Fried Rice.  Probably the best veggie fried rice I've ever had.

The delicious food comes at a price, though.  Drinks are also a little expensive.  You can definitely order things + stay within budget.  It's just kind of hard to when everything is so good.  I am definitely coming back here on my next trip to Philadelphia.  And this time, I will request a reservation to sit in a pod!

Delivery sushi that is actually good! I order from this place at least once every 2 weeks - and I'm someone who usually cooks and eats at home. The sushi is always fresh, the rolls are interesting, and I love the guy who usually answers the phone. Yes, it takes a while for the food to arrive, but let's be realistic - it's sushi. Sushi takes a while even when you are in the restaurant. I love this place. It's a gem.

A great sandwich place. You get a warm toasty sandwich. Way better than Subway or Quiznos. Open late hours if you have cravings or just hungry. Lots of sandwiches to choose from, my favorite is anything turkey. Quick, friendly service.

Pics on Instagram @amanda.n_
Cheesecake Factory? Pfffffttttt .... More like Macaroni House! Am i rite??? Dont like it fight me
Not a fan of the food. The flavors are a bit off and each dish is way over priced. It would be a fun date spot though since you can get dim sum themed food with some strong drinks.
Absolutely love this place. They never fail. The fried onions the crushed pepper flakes. Uh it it's too die for honestly.
Bo Wah never disappoints and I always receive a great size portion. This restaurant deserves better reviews. SO GOOD!!!!!!
Delicious banh mi (I ordered #12, the charbroiled pork). Tasted great and fresh. No dine in, cash only right now (covid times)

Love. The. Concept. Stopped in for a casual day date after wanting to try it out since they opened, but didn't think the kiddos would go for it. Finally made it, just the two of us, and we're so glad we did!! 

What a fantastic idea ...dogs offered in many varieties including all beef, pork, bratwursts, chorizo, lamb, duck, Cantonese sausage, chicken, even wild boar, alligator, kangaroo and python, topped with ingredients inspired by domestic and international travels! 

Each dog is so insanely clever, with names just the same. They also list the corresponding airport codes to each locale on the menu. Each dog represented their respective destination so perfectly and had me wanderlusting quite a bit! I bet it was a blast creating this menu...The Hebrew hammer which has latkes, the Louisiana Dog which has a combo gator and shrimp sausage and included jalapeño remoulade and so on....

I chose "The Veg Dog Millionaire", (the most popular vegetarian option...they informed me they're working on adding more vegan options) which gave me a chuckle and had me wanting to rewatch Slum Dog Millionaire ASAP! This dog was obviously Mumbai inspired and had a samosa filling topping! Oh yessssss!! Delicious!!! It contained all of those warming spices I love in Indian food! So, so good! I asked for the spicy aioli on the side which hubs gladly added to his dog, the "Salchica Vergara", inspired by the cuisine in Bogota. It was deep fried, topped with bacon, yellow mustard, mayo, ketchup, pineapple relish, coleslaw and crushed potato chips.

 While I had a difficult time finishing what was on my silver tray which included a small side of potato chips, he had no issue finishing his dog and chips, and wanted to chow down three more! Say what?! We did both agree the bread was a bit much however. The dogs are served on a bun shaped like a New England style lobster roll, but we think it was more of a Texas Toast type deal which was a tad too thick on the bottom for our personal taste. I can imagine some folks nix the bread towards the end of their meal as the dogs themselves are rather long and filling. 

The staff is uber friendly, knowledgeable of the menu and they're willing to substitute any protein to any dog combo. They are also open to replacements and/or additions to the toppings. Very laid back and they cater well to picky eater such as myself. Thank you! There's even an option to completely create your own dog, but with ones so carefully crafted it seemed silly for us first timers. 

Brews are pretty great and varied, with about 16 on tap. We decided to taste the Mardi Gras, a lighter lager for our first go in honor of Fat Tuesday. Delish! They also have some pretty rad cocktails. 

I loved the simple decor with nods to traveling found in the vintage inspired posters and on the flight arrival/departure board which I absolutely loved! Nice bar with flatscreens too. We enjoyed catching some of the Winter Olympics while we dined. There's also a few video games in the back! How fun! 

We will definitely be back and after seeing what it's all about. I think the kiddos would actually dig it too. I mean, kids love anything 'make your own' and the promise of crushed potato chips as a topping sounds right up their alley. Add games...sold. 

I really think it's a must try! It's all just so fun and such a novel idea! It allows your tastebuds to travel the world or revisit destinations you love in one restaurant. You can come a bunch times and still taste a different combo each visit.

Solid meal! We came with a group of 5 and were seated promptly (it was a late dinner).  The cushioned bench seating was a favorite.  The Aya's taster platter was a solid starter to try multiple things, but to it fill up too much before entrees.  
I don't think one entree at the table wasn't enjoyed!  Good serving size without the gluttonous sizes of some chain restaurants.  The vegetables in the sautéed veggie entree were very freehand delicious.
The staff was very accommodating and friendly.  Turkish coffee was explained to us and tasted! 
The ambiance with candles and ample seating good for communication was just what we were looking for!
You cannot beat it being a BYOB!!!!! Sweetens the deal!!
We will be back!

I stumbled into El Vez last week, as I was searching for somewhere cool and "local" on my first trip to Philly.

Yo quiero El Vez!

El Vez is a swanky little Mexican joint, that offers their hip clientele Mexican food and drinks and with some extra cohones! 

I only sampled the El Vez bar on this trip, as I was rushed to meet some work colleagues. Regardless, I was impressed enough to give El Vez a Yelp Shout Out!

If you're cool, you'll like El Vez.

Smaller venues always win my heart over crazy large stadium-like places where I'm not even exactly sure which blob on the stage is the singer. The TLA is a great standing room only venue. You can get really up close to the stage, always a plus and the acoustics are great. Beer is pretty pricey, but there's a 24 oz option so you can make fewer trips back and forth to the bar. The bathroom was rough, but what do you expect from a music venue? Votive candles?

Had the meatballs with pine nuts - AMAZING! The vegetarian ravioli with squash and greens was scrumptious and the Cuban sandwich was a very good size. Our server, Malise, made me a virgin coconut majito which was out of this world.  The ambience is quaint and exotic and the staff delightful. Definitely going back there.

I have been going to another nail place nearby since I moved here and have officially found my new go-to spot. I got a pedicure, a gel manicure and my eyebrows done and it all cost $77. I'm going to Jamaica and was a little reluctant to try somewhere new, but I am so glad I did!

When I came in, I was told to pick a color, so I picked a color for my toes (an OPI color) I asked if she had a "similar" color in the gel for my nails (usually selections of gels are weak). I was happy to find out they have a whole selection of OPI gels and even the same color to match!

The pedicure was great, she was very thorough and they give you great long massages with hot stones! The chairs are super comfortable and the girls make sure that it never stops massaging you.
I sat down to do my gel manicure next, she turned on a little fan by my feet so my toes would dry at the same time...I love that kind of efficiency! She did an awesome job on my nails, no polish on my skin, no hangnails, no cuts, perfectly even filing!  After my manicure she gave me a long delicious shoulder massage which apparently comes with the pedicure.

Another girl took me to the back to do my eyebrows...this is sort of a task because I have very straight brows and I want them to be arched so badly but not thin...She did an amazing job, practically painless and she took the time to tweeze out all the little babies you can't get with the wax.

I'm very impressed with this salon, all the girls were so friendly, the salon itself was extremely clean and very reasonably priced for the way you get treated. 

Oh and you get a discount if you pay in cash..AND they have rewards cards!

I seriously can not wait to come back!

I ate here last night and am currently experiencing what I think is food poisoning.  I ate the salad around 7:00, woke up at 1:00am vomiting and having bowel movements.  Today I felt nauseous, vomited, diarrhea again, and general dry mouth and not feeling well.  I thought the grilled chicken I asked for maybe tasted a little off.  It had to be from Saladworks because I felt fine when I went to bed and woke up feeling horrendous.  I don't know what to do

My favorite coffee shop when I travel to Philly.  Very tasty espresso, perfect cappuccinos.  When I have nothing to do, I often walk from my hotel on Society Hill to this location on nice days.  Their beans are also what are served at my favorite bagel place back home outside DC, but its not quite done as well as here in Philly.

Great Studio, Great Classes!

Went to Tuck Barre & Yoga for the first time last night for Buti Yoga with Megan & could not be more pleased with the energy, intensity & overall fun of the class! Megan was amazing & completely down to earth! You know when you go to a studio for the first time and you feel nervous & uncomfortable? Not here - Meg was interacting with the class the entire time and really got a feel for when we were getting fatigued & pushed us to finish.

Feeling it today, but will definitely be back soon!

Ordered a pizza and a couple other things. Took almost 2 hours when I was told 35-40 minutes. Service is really good and I feel bad for the people working up front, but not sure if short on staff or what in the back but food took way, way too long.

These Federal doughnuts in Philly might just be the best ones I've ever had! And as a New Yorker, that says a lot! These perfect cake doughnuts are made fresh and are a hot, sugary piece of heaven! The cinnamon brown sugar and the strawberry are my faves! The staff is super friendly and takes pride in their product. A special shout out to Kevin who was so helpful and got me to taste various different flavors. Federal Donuts is a MUST and I can't wait to go back!
Amazing experience, I rented a 1br while I was in Philly.  My expectations were blown away!  The place was amazing, I highly recommend it to anyone in the area.

Love this place. My son has had an amazing experience here. The teachers make sure the kids are active, have fun, stay safe, and learn a lot. They're encouraging, providing individualized assistance and lots of recognition. And their class schedule is so flexible - for a reasonably monthly fee you can go to class as many times a week as you'd like.

A busy bubble tea place nestled in the corner of two street names I don't recall. While driving through Philly, I, as usual, had a bubble tea craving post-lunch and stumbled upon this place. With quite a line to boot, I figured it would be worth a try, and yes, I was right. 

The boba was good - soft, and not very sweet. I tend to prefer sweeter boba, but I must say it was objectively good.
The tea was a bit strong - while I do like strong tea, I typically prefer a lighter milk tea, so it was strong for me, but my friend quite enjoyed the tea. It was brewed well, just not quiet my style.
The grass jelly was quite good.

Overall, a good place with a unique offering of drinks, despite my choice to go with the classic. I'd like to try other things on the menu to get a better feel for the place - it was cute, busy, and seemed good, and while I didn't quite find my "cup of tea" the first time around, it's an objectively good boba place.

"Would you like us to valet your bike?"

No joke. I biked here for a half-day workshop and was about to lock up my bike in front of the hotel when the hotel staff offered to hold my bike inside. It was a first for me, so I thought it was worth a 5-star thank you. I've heard complaints of the hotel aggressively trying to keep people from locking their bike up in front of the hotel, but that wasn't the case for me.

I love taking people here so they can experience the over the top and quirky style of Continental. Almost every time I have gone here their has been a wait, but they have a great lounge area to order drinks on the second floor. Their cocktails are delicious and they have decent food. I always enjoy the lobster mac&cheese and the shrimp quessadilla.

Due to the pandemic they allowed 1 at a time or if you came with someone they can enter with you. Everyone was wearing masks and gloves. The staff was friendly.

The air was  perfumed with notes of vanilla, chocolate and other savory and sweet notes of yumminess. Everyone. I tried 1 of every cupcake. I shared with my family of course. The icing was my favorite. The cake cake wasn't as moist as I like. Overall nice experience and beautiful location.
Amazing breakfast sandwiches especially on a long roll. Great presentation of food with the flavor and satisfaction to go along with it

If you like the Rocky series then this is the place for you. You get to clime the steps to the Art Museum and have a good look around the area.  Every tour bus that goes buy plays the Rocky theme.  There is a statue just to the side of the steps so you can get a picture of Rocky or with so many people around there are plenty of people who will take a photo of you and Rocky.
Delicious food, excellent and friendly service! Would definitely come again. Love the spicy guac!!

I came to the salon, asking for ombre highlights and a trim.  The girl who started dyeing my hair was wiping snot from her nose while doing my hair, then when she was drying my hair, SHE BURNT MY SCALP WITH THE BLOW DRYER.  I was really upset because there was hair on the ground at my hair dresser's station that she didn't have time to clean up, yet she had time to try on new sneakers during my appointment. 

After being burnt, I went into panic mode, afraid that my hair would be a disaster for work the next day.  The only redeeming part of my visit was Kevin cutting and styling my hair.  He is very talented but the girl I had who worked on me first was quite the opposite.

I left regretting all $20 of the tip that I reluctantly gave, especially because instead of getting ombre highlights, I just got extremely subtle normal highlights and a sore scalp.


**IF YOU'RE GOING TO USE THE 20% OFF "FIRST TIME CLIENT DEAL," MAKE SURE THAT YOU PRINT OUT THAT WEB PAGE (ALTHOUGH IT DOESN'T SAY THAT ANYWHERE ON THE WEB PAGE)

Horrible.  Found an apt listing on Craigslist, so I called Philly Apartment Company.  First, the guy I spoke with on the phone couldn't find which apartment I was interested in.  After figuring it out, I made an appointment to see the place the next day.  As advertised on the posting, I made it clear that I did not want to look at the apartment if parking was not included,as that would be a waste of everyone's time. He told me he would double check and call me back -- which he did.  He "confirmed" to me that parking was definitely included in the amenities.   I go to see the apt the next day (viewing with a different person than I spoke to on the phone).  The apartment was NOT available Nov. 15th as I had been told on the phone, but Oct. 15. I knew then this wouldn't work out -- as I was not about to pay double rent for a month.  Next, I was told AGAIN that parking was included, and the woman would check if a November move-in was possible instead of Oct.  I followed up with her the next day, and she promised to be in touch.  NEVER HEARD FROM ANYONE AGAIN.
I see the posting on Craigslist has since been changed to NOT include parking --- so after being "confirmed" 3 times that was clearly wrong.  Wish this company would get your information and dates straight.... 
I would never recommend this incompetent company to anyone and have found a new apartment (in the same building!!) on my own through Craiglist. Thanks for nothing Philly Apartment Company.

Welcoming atmosphere with people of all different backgrounds offering professional assistance or a kind word. 

Highly recommend.

So we were In town for my birthday. I decided i wanted dumplings. We looked around and found good reviews here. They were correct. This is a great little hole in the wall. It's a tight fit but the food is awesome.
Hands down the BEST roast beef sandwich.   On the outs combo is amazing.   Trust me.   So delish!
I would crawl on my knees through shards of glass just to bask in the aroma of those sweet potato fries.

Would not recommend as a coffeeshop or for food. Can't speak for the beer.

Came here for to work during the day as a coffeeshop. Great space and friendly staff but the WiFi was not working for my laptop and the food left a lot to be desired. The staff tried to reset the router twice but did not seem to care about the lack of WiFi outside of that.

Second time I came in, their sound system was not working so they had the TV on very loudly instead in the morning which was distracting. Even though I was the only customer in the space and told them it was loud, they did not try to change the volume. Also the coffee was room temperature at 8am. 

Both of these experiences left me feeling that they don't care about their customers that much. Know they are new and still figuring things out so hopefully both their tech and their service improves over time!

My cheese steak brings all the boys to the yard, and they're like, it's better than yours.

Damn right, it's better than yours. I could teach you, but I'd have to charge.

That's what I was singing through bites of my awesome cheese steak (with provolone and onions) while at the ballpark last week.  My expectations of a ballpark sandwich were not high, but I was delighted by the freshness of the roll, the tenderness of the meat and the sharpness of the provolone.  Add in the curly fries and you have a winner.

Generous portions at a fair price give this a fourth star.

I generally am not the type to set out and write a review about a restaurant at all given my husband has worked in the industry and I have a lot of patience. Though the experience I had at BM&B was incredibly disappointing. I have been super stoked about this place since I heard of its opening and decided to give it a shot. After being seated promptly, everything after that took awfully long. Our appetizers weren't too bad, but awaiting for our bahn mis was close to an hour wait. It's something I would not have minded, though there was no communication between us and the servers. We were constantly overlooked or brushed off. Waiting to get our check took another half hour after being ignored over and over my servers who were right next to us. 

The food itself was subpar. The brisket bao buns were a great highlight, though. The bahn mi I could have done without. No rich flavors or textures you get with a great bahn mi. We skipped on drinks, given the menu is already pricey for what you are given. 

The most disappointing was the lack of communication and service in general. I may come back. I know friends who have had great times here. May have been an off night. I'd stick to the appetizers and drinks.

I had heard good things about the salon so I decided to check it out instead of going to my usual salon. Overall great experience. All technicians were super sweet and accommodating upon arrival. I went around 2 pm on a Tuesday and it wasn't super busy so I was able to get in right away. The salmon was clean and had a comfortable atmosphere. 

I had Linda. She was So professional, kind, and fast. Not the "rushed" kind of fast, just efficient. I was in and out with a gel mani in under an hour. 

They also offer a mini massage at the end- always a bonus! 

Highly recommend and will be going back!

Everytime I'm in West Philly, around breakfast time I must stop here!!! It's a small corner spot but they have great attitude for their customers it's a family environment and I love their breakfast!!!

I've been coming here for years, since I got my driver's license at 16.  It's one of the top places to me and I love the entire experience.  There is usually a large line, regardless of when you go.  This place reminds me of the Soup Nazi from Seinfeld.  The line queue is a small maze, but one you get up front, everyone gets quiet and starts sliding to the left.  A large man looking down at the counter and not at you will yell "next" and you better order.  Don't make him say it a second time!  Regulars know how to order, but you see tourists having conversations and pissing this man off.  Don't get Whiz ever, but it's the easiest for them.  Provolone with will get you the great provolone cheese and the "with" will get you nice fried onions.  After you order, get out of the way immediately and let the next person order their meal.  Someone else will ask if it's to go and take your drink order.  Joke around with these people, they have personality, unlike the big guy making your sandwich.  I've gotten a yoo-hoo ever since my first visit and I love the combination.  On occasion, I'll get a beer and a yoo-hoo too, but mainly just a yoo-hoo.  

It's greasy, good and I love how some of the meat's flavor comes from being a little burnt.  You could fight over who has the best cheese steak all you want, but this is definitely my favorite and should be in your conversation.

My husband and I are in Philly celebrating our anniversary and we hit this place on the way out after a friend recommended it. Honestly the best brunch I've had in my life. So good. You must visit. We got the French toast and the shrimp and grits.
I love the crawfish and shrimp here. The sauces are amazing and the food here is definitely worth it. I prefer takeout but eating in here is good too. I definitely recommend this place.

I'm going to offer a disclaimer on my two stars in that this is a review after a Caviar delivery. So it's absent the atmosphere of the restaurant, plus the food isn't served on nice plates, etc... nor is it straight out of the kitchen.

But that disclaimer aside, the food is still the food, and one would assume the most important part of the experience of a restaurant. (I think folks in Philly don't have a ton of great options for Indian food, and the bar is simply lower.)

Suffice it to say we were thoroughly disappointed last week. We ordered quite a bit of food, assuming leftovers and wanting to try a lot of different things based on how good the reviews are like here. 

-The bread was oily and tasted flat
-The chicken wings were way over fried and breaded
-The chicken pieces in the chicken madras were dry and thin
-The "tandoori mixed grill" really should be listed in quotes, because it's not a tandoori mixed grill. One way to know is that all of the meat was dripping in some kind of sauce or juice.
-The raita was ok, at least
-They forgot one of our items, but I guess it's just as well...

The pictures on the website look amazing. But this was a real bait and switch. We definitely won't be visiting the restaurant any time soon...

If you've been to Philadelphia and you have not had a cheesesteak, then you have not truly experienced Philadelphia. There are many cheeesteak shops, with different names on them, but one of the most popular and famous is Tony Luke's. I rate the cheesesteak a 3.5, I give the extra 1/2 for all that he has accomplished.

This one man's dream started seventeen years ago and is slowly growing into an empire. His original shop in still on Oregon Ave. and Tony Luke's Beef and Beer Sports Bar is right across the street. http://www.tonylukes.com/menu.htm 

Tony now has a restaurant at the Borgata Hotel & Casino, in Atlantic City and in Trenton, NJ. He is opening up restaurants in Las Vegas, New York City, Los Angeles, Fort Lauderdale, Chicago, Dubai UAE and many more. Nice huh? 

He has rubbed elbows with some of the best, like long time friend Bobby Flay and Guy Fieri. He recently was in a Bobby Flay sponsored food & music fest, with other great businesses like Katz's Deli, Pink's Hot Dogs and Burgermeister. The food is the best of the best from around the country, as curated by Ed Levine, founder of Seriouseats.com. author and New York Times and Gourmet Magazine food writer: Nine were chosen from across the country and Tony Luke's was one of them! 

Tony Luke's has won many awards for his old style Philly sandwiches, from the Food Network's 2007 award, to GQ, Gourmet Magazine, Zagats and so many more. He has been featured in Philadelphia Magazine so many times that they have honored him into the "Best of Philly HALL OF FAME." 

Okay, okay, I'm getting there! Now to Tony Luke's Ashburn Alley, inside Citizen's Bank Park. Plan to stand in long roped off lines. When I finally got close enough, I could read the menu and what I really wanted was a cheesesteak hoagie with provolone. Lots of cheesesteaks on this small menu, but I see no hoagies. So, I start asking my fellow stand in line patrons and they all say, oh no, you can not get the hoagie here. Bummer, lines were long, I needed to get back to my seat and so I did not ask, after glancing around and not seeing lettuce and tomato. 

My cheesesteak was good, wit onions added, of course. Tony uses rib eye steaks for his cheesesteaks. After viewing his awards and menu for his Sports bar, I really need to pay this a visit. They grind their burger meat daily, their pork sandwiches and chicken cutlets are winning numerous awards. 

Tony Luke's can now come to your home with Pronto. http://www.tonylukespronto.com/ 

Tony Luke's proudly supports the brave Americans 
who defend our freedom across the globe every day. 

Have you had your Tony Luke's sandwich today?  I owe Sam L a cheesesteak!

Great night!! This comfy theatre with a friendly staff gave us a night to remember. No need to say is that the play was fantastic (Hamilton) but the staff made it even that much more better. Temperature in the theater was great, concession stands were quick and they contributed to a-thoroughly great night at the Forrest theater.

Such a cool, upscale, nice place in Northern Liberties. Awesome drinks, amazing food and some of the most comfortable and welcoming combination of ambiance, bartenders, and music playlists in Philly. Everyone can enjoy, period. Amazing location and building too.

Bar bien caché, avec un ensemble de codes à suivre à la règle. Enfin surtout une : tu sors ton téléphone, tu dégages.
Pour le reste : cash only, dress code (pas de baskets. Jeans acceptés)

Ambiance prohibition donc, avec une entrée discrète, un gorille à l'entrée qui ne rigole pas, et qui prend tes papiers d'identité pour :
1/ vérifier que tu as plus de 21 ans
2/ tu n'es pas sur la blacklist (si lors de ta dernière visite tu as, disons, utilisé ton téléphone)

Et mise à part le moment de stress où ton passeport disparait derrière une mystérieuse porte grillagée, l'expérience est fantastique.
Lumières tamisées, déco wtf, serveurs en costume aux petits soins, et les cocktails... À se damner. Ces mélanges sont rafraîchissants et plein de subtilité. Parfaits ! ( et bien chargés )
J'ai essayé un cocktail à base de rhum : hotel nacional. Excellent.

This place is fantastic! Everything I've had from here has been so good and I love how nice the staff is. The food is authentic and they have some cool stuff that I've never tried before. I'm going to be a regular here!

Came here with some friends while visiting Philly. The Reading Terminal market was pretty crowded and we had a decently large party, so we opted to come here to eat and have some drinks. MM has a pretty decent drinks menu so we all got a mix of speciality cocktails, beer, and wine. The chicken pot pie sounded pretty good so I ordered that, along with some snacks. The pot pie is huge, so be prepared to share unless you came with two stomachs.

Overall, food and drinks were enjoyable, and service was great from beginning to end. Thanks for a great time Molly Malloy's!

Really superb sandwiches, the crunch of the plantains and savory chicken is a taste combination that I will definitely back for. The homemade sauces are also nicely put together and highly recommend trying them all.

R Falones a S.W. institution for fine steaks and Hogies for as long as I can remember. They offer other fine menu items but their cheese steaks and hogies are the stars. I've been eating both for a long time and have never been disappointed. Falones also has a fine selection of breakfast items. The stand outs are the bacon egg and cheese on a long roll. They are also available with sausage and crispy scrapple. Everything they make is great do yourself a favor and call ahead place your order and pick it up without having to wait. Most orders are available 15 or 20 minutes later. Treat yourself and go that extra mile for food that will have you coming back for more. Falones a sandwich shop you will love. Also a great selection of beer. Your one stop shop that has it all.   Dan J.

I happened to pass by while I was looking for breakfast. I walked in and was not sure what I want to drink. Another girl walked in and ordered a hummus, Arugula, tomato on an everything bagel. It sounded amazing. So I ordered the same thing the gentleman asked if I wanted onion I thought that was a great idea,. And I had a Cortado. I have to say it was the best breakfast I've had in Philadelphia. I also got a vegan banana bread for the road. It was so good. Everyone was so nice there. The gentleman that ring me up even remembered I was going on a road trip and wished me luck. I will definitely come here again my next trip to Philly.

My wife and I really love going to the Trading Company.  Nina is the greatest server!  She is Always So Helpful, even if she is completely busy. The food never ceases to please & satisfy. A very welcome to the Garces Empire!

The food here is soooo good! We got the pork chops and Delang shrimp. Fried rice on the side. We murdered EVERYTHING then ordered 2 extra platters to go! Such great service and so worth trip. It was also priced really really well. Girls lunch was a major success.

re APEX that i took from NY - DC last week.  

there are more important things that the cost.  cheap is great but most americans (OK maybe not all) can afford to pony up an extra $10 for quality service on a RT ticket.

APEX is frigging dirty.   i wonder what the bacteria count is on that thing -- people regularly put their dirty shoes on seats, all the wipe-able surfaces are super dirty and the bathroom belongs in hell.  forget toilet paper and expect to gag from the smell.  

sorry, i've not travelled to a third-world country (except NOLA after katrina) so, while i would never expect a 4 or 5 star hotel or need it, i don't want the opposite either.

from now on, i'll be looking for BOLT or VAMOOSE and gladly pony up a little more cash for a vastly improved classier ride.

is it because i am a woman??   maybe.  maybe i like nicer things.  i really don't know.  but a flushable toilet where i pee where  the previous persons odors aren't lingering, people who can tell me my answer in english (just so i know i am on the right bus and other info i might ask), and safer, maintained buses.  

i'd much rather arrive alive and well than earlier than expected.  

anyway, the CHINATOWN buses are an experience.  i've boarded them 4 times in my life.  but as long as there is a better choice, i will pay more and take the higher road.  like a shady boyfriend, sometimes it's better just to avoid what you know could end badly.  n'est pas??
Let's face it, the only thing this place has going for it, is the Milk Shake, but from what I'm reading below, even that is sometimes a struggle. Despise being dragged here, saturday after saturday.

My first experience here was PERFECTION. I got a same day appointment for a much needed haircut ( I hadn't had one in years and had major dead ends). I feel like a new person after this appointment. Brianne was amazing and was professional , enthusiastic, and made sure I was happy with everything. I will certainly become a regular here. The salon was clean, trendy, and welcoming. The whole staff ( front desk, stylist, and style assistant) we super friendly and they even offer complimentary beverages which was an unexpected treat !

Everything's amazing. 

The bread. I literally come here just for the bread. 

Everything I have eaten is delicious: 
Steak frites delicious. Tomato tarte. Quiche. Salmon. Their mashed potatoes are superb. The salad w warm shrimp. The lobster cocktail. Vietnamese coffee icecream. Macaroons are amazing too (they come six in a pack but they're tiny. Bite size. 1/2 the size of an average macaroon) 

Service is great.

Going here was the most pleasant experience I've had in a while. As soon as I walked in the door I received a warm welcome from the woman up front (who I absolutely adore). The exam was quick, accurate, efficient, professional... their inventory of frames is outstanding with very reasonable prices if you are working with insurance,... it was just a super person-centered experience. Just a great little shop with amazing staff. AND IT IS LOCALLY OWNED.

I have only visited Morgan's Pier once, but like others have said before me, is indeed a gem. I really enjoyed their beer-based cocktails, and my friend was loving the array of specialty beers. The openness and roominess of the outdoor bar is also a refreshing change, where you have great views of the Delaware river, Ben Franklin bridge (looks especially cool lit up at night), and Center City. The varying seating arrangements allows for comfortable accommodation for both large groups as well as couples.  The DJs that night were excellent, spinning really trippy, bass-heavy electronic music that put you in this hypnotic zone initially, and giving way to more frantic beats and remixes of popular songs as the night went on. My friend and I asked if we could bring hula hoops in to use on the dance floor, and as it was deserted at the time, the staff allowed it and was even encouraging. We had a great time hooping (the music and fog machines, along with a mild beer buzz, was a great combo). Once things started to pick up at 11:30, we were asked to stop using the hoops, lest we accidentally hit someone on the dance floor. Ah well. :-) I really enjoyed the atmosphere and drink menu- will definitely be returning next summer!

Sabrina's is a great place to eat breakfast. On a Saturday though, get there prior to 9 if you don't want to wait. Advise, bring along your own spoon. They never give you a spoon or a stirrer for your coffee, odd in a breakfast place. After our third visit we asked if they could PLEASE bring us spoons for our coffee.  He returned with 3 and informed us, "they are the only ones we have."  I'm not sure whether he was being funny or not. They have an inventive special list. In September there was a Simpsons theme. We went there twice in September. Two different times two different servers and I quoted the name of the special right from the menu, both times the waitron looked confused. The first time the waitron said, "I don't know the specials, they just changed them." Two weeks later, the same guy who told us that they only own 3 spoons, gave me a blank stare and then asked me whether I wanted a short stack or a large stack. I told him, "just bring me what's in the special page."  Another blank stare. There was only one size option and price on the page.  I will come back because the food is really great and I live in the neighborhood. But I wish the staff would get their act a little more together.

I go here sometimes if I need button up shirts for work and they usually have a good selection. The clothes are well organized, but household stuff is kind of cluttered and thrown wherever. They also will have NES and Genesis games there at times, so that is always a plus.

Overcrowded and not a place to go if you're looking for a spot to study or write or read. When I was there, there would be like 10 people gathered around these teeny tiny table clusters... it was crazy. 

The drinks here are killer. I was really impressed by the quality of their drinks. Had a Nutella crepe as well and that was pretty good. 

I would go back, but only to get something to go.
Albeit from the insufficient supply of pecan rolls from Vegan Treats, this place is the source of many things delicious!

The dress I recently bought at Grasshopper fits perfectly, looks awesome and wait for it...was seriously marked down. As the Kool-Aid man would say, "Ohhhh Yeahhhh!"

The staff is quite helpful and always looks amazing. In fact, it's probably worth a visit just to get fashion inspiration from what they're wearing. They have great sales periodically (see above) so if you're not into dropping three figures on a dress, check their website and mark your calendars so you, too, can get a sweet, sweet deal like I did. Just be careful that you don't step on Tiny, the shop dog. She lives up to her name, so you might mistake her for a toy if you're not paying attention.

What can I say about this company.  They came out to correct work on two different jobs that two different WELL KNOWN plumbers did at my elderly mother's home. 

Their fast and courteous  and STAR QUALITY SERVICE  is  A1 in her book.  Bill is very knowledgeable in his craft.  We will DEFINITELY be using them again.  GOD BLESS GOODMAN PLUMBING!!!!! A WORK OF ART!!!

I love their food, I really do. But 2+ hour wait times on delivery to less than a mile away? Ridiculous. If I order for pickup, it's ready in under 20 minutes. Why does it take so long to shuttle a sandwich down the street?

US Airways has a direct flight from Charleston, NC. The airport is larger than Charleston so it takes some walking from the fate to the baggage claim. It also take some time to retrieve the bags. The rental car facility is outside the airport, so you will have to take the bus to go there. The baggage claim does not list the arriving flight, so you have to wait and look for your bag.

I'm going to start this review off by echoing what I think I'm reading in many of the other reviews... I really want Le Cochon Noir to survive and thrive.  Philly needs this place.  It's a great space - plenty of room & plenty of vibe, with owners and servers who care and really seem determined to make sure everyone who walks through the door has a great time.  There is no cover to hear great music - we were really impressed with Jesse Teich and her band  - and you can't help but enjoy yourself with good food, good art (large pieces of art, with artists rotated on a monthly basis), and good music.  

That said, I do want to try to be objective and review the good and the bad of this place.  The food is all very good - but, it's also expensive.  You'll wind up spending $100 for 2 for dinner.  I expected this going in & figure it's worth a couple of extra bucks since they have a no cover charge policy.  And they recently got their liquor license... but for now, they only serve wine & liquor.  It may be just me - but, if ever there was a place that needed to serve beer, it's a BBQ place that features live music.  I hope they put in a nice selection of beers by the time I return again.  

We loved the Char-Grilled Colossal Shrimp appetizer (athough it really should be 4 shrimp instead of 3, so you don't have to fight your date over the last shrimp!), quesadilla (the best deal on the menu) and the Hickory Smoked Rotisserie Chicken.  I'd recommend all of the above and would get them again next time.   We wanted to try their crabcakes - but, oddly they had just run out... oddly because there were only about a dozen people there all night.  They clearly take pride in the food and seem to want to make the food be the star of your experience.

If Le Cochon Noir was downtown, the sights, sounds & smells would be drawing in passersby and it would be filled to capacity - but, this location draws no foot traffic (on Parkside, behind the Mann Music Center) , so it's more of a destination spot.

OK, I don't usually post 5 stars for anything - especially restaurants, but something about this homey feeling diner with the cute fall decorations in the Logan Circle area of the city made be just go for it.  I was there for an early morning Sunday breakfast and the place was full.  We didn't have to wait and sat down right away.  The portions of the Barking Chihuahua was huge and I couldn't finish it all.  So the food was good -- actually better than the average diner.  But I have to say that what gives this 5 stars is the service.  So it's not Le Bec Fin or Zahav's or the Fork but for a diner, it's one of the absolute best I've been to.  We did have an issue with an exploding ketchup bottle but it was handled very quickly.
Just as good as the location in reading terminal! My favorite is the walnut chocolate chip cookies! I'll definitely be back!

Pro: DELICIOUS, authentic Mexican food. 

Con: the waitress we had was not as attentive as she needed to be (e.g. Forgot to give us menus, silverware, water refills, checkup on us throughout our visit)

Luckily everyone else there was supportive, and made it more of a team effort to take care of our table. Also, the food... serious props to the chef.

Yaaas. I had some sort of hot buttery whisky drink recommended by the gentleman taking my order, which was the perfect concoction for a cold day lunch date with a friend. I paired that with a grilled cheese and felt like I could hang in there for a good couple of hours with the size of the sand which. Across from me in my friend's sizzling skillet was a chicken curry dish, which was also warm and seasoned to perfection. Warm and savory was the perfect combo for our lunch date and I loved the coziness inside this place. It's perfect for journaling or doing work, or as we were, catching with a friend. Not the cheapest option around, but you get what you pay for‍

Highly recommend this place for drinks and light eating tapas. The small sharing plates makes this place ideal for a date, especially if you want a little more adventurous eating. We got the lamb gyro, butternut squash pita, and village salad. All I highly recommend. We also had the dolmades, which were okay. I come from Greek decent so I am a tough critic when it comes to Greek food, which is why this place isn't 5 stars in my book. The service was superb and the decorations are beautiful with hanging lights and candlelit atmosphere.

Martha was the final stop on our nice Saturday of brewery hopping in Fishtown. Martha is your quintessential neighborhood bar, dedicated to all things local. They have an extensive list of local brews. I was most impressed, however, with the servers who were very knowledgeable, passionate, and did an excellent job of making recommendations.

The food was ok although a bit pricey but convenient for the orchestra. That said, my rain coat was stolen from their coat rack and the manager said "too bad, we have a sign saying that they are not responsible"! They will not do anything! So be forewarned, Estia doesn't care about their customers; I will dine else where in the future.
Tried the brisket sandwich this time. Sooooo good.  Side of collard greens. Damn!

Came here as part of a Manayunk food tour, so we didn't get to order for ourselves, but the concept for the place is awesome, and the ice cream that we got on the tour was really high quality stuff. 

We were each given a little cup of their dark chocolate ice cream, with a dollop of whipped cream, which was described as their best seller. Very tasty, but exactly what you would expect from a dark chocolate ice cream with dark chocolate bits in it. As I said though, very high quality! 

I can't wait to go back and treat the 'ol tasters to a more exciting order, as some of their flavors and sundaes looked really interesting and delicious.

Also, the coolest and most unique aspect of the shop is an open wall with stations where you can play classic arcade games, which are projected on the wall of the adjacent building. So fun!

Buddakan came highly recommended from a friend & i now owe him. Fantastic restaurant with low noise & very calm vibe. The food was adventurous & i loved everything served. Wehad the dim sum sampler, kung pow mong fish, duck breast. Dessert was the highlight, lemon tart & Chinese donuts. Definitely check out this place in Philly. Book early, we could only get 545 friday night 24 hrs in advance

I have only been to this particular CVS twice and both times were horrible experiences.  Their stock is lacking and the employees are unfriendly. The 2nd time I was there, I spoke to the manager (man? woman? who knows???) . It was extremely rude and unprofessional.  If  you need to go to CVS, go to the one in Roxborough or Andorra instead.

This is my favorite Chinese takeout during my time at school...I still try to drive by once in a while... but its a lil scary at that corner.. I love their selection of mock-items, as in vegetarian choices...the Mock General Tso's Chicken is sooo delicious... I've tried ordering this at other places but they can't seem to get it right... an order of Mock General Tso' Chicken is pretty large and can be shared with 3-4 others...yummy!!

Participated in the Philly Cheesesteak Tour while visiting the city.  Sonny's was the first of five tour stops and my favorite.  The bread was chewy, but soft.  The meat was great, but I really enjoyed the cheez whiz. Using cheez whiz rather than American or provolone seemed to make all the difference.  Will definitely return on our next trip.

Came here for a free happy hour special last Friday night that my friend set up. 

We got "free appetizers," which were just tater tots, lol. 

The drinks were strong and actually pretty good, but the bar was extremely crowded. Well drinks for 4$. The atmosphere was pretty fun, but I'm not the biggest fan of piano bars. The crowd was a bit rowdy, which was strange- because it was a piano bar. 

I'd give it another shot, but agreeing with another poster- there weren't enough places to sit.

This place is AWESOME!   I have been here several times, and they have the most amazing Gelato ever.  I honestly don't know another place like this in the Philadelphia area.  Nobody if it's cold outside, or a wonderful spring day, you will enjoy this!

They have many different options, and they even have lactose free options which I hear are really good as well.  I have tried multiple different options including the tiramisu, banana, kiwi, thai coconut milk, but the chocolate is the best.  Well, the Nutella is really good too.  

This is a go to place, and actually a decent date spot after going out to dinner.  The staff is always very nice, and I have never had any issues.  

Now I am craving this place.....

I love the location and variety of equipment at this gym, however the hours are extremely restrictive -- the gym closes far too early, especially on weekends, making it nearly impossible to fit in a workout if you have any plans during the day. Even during the week the gym closes as early as 10 pm some weekdays, making it difficult to finish a workout if you have a busy workday, and want to avoid peak hours right after work.

For me, the most important part of a gym is that you can use it -- given the restricted hours, and the high price, I have to give this gym 2 stars.

I liked this place a lot, and came to try it after seeing it is ranked one of the top ten diners in America, on this food series I saw on netflix.

However, Philly does breakfast and brunch well, and I must say I think I like Honeys and the West Ave grill better. 

Not that the food was bad, but some of it just wasnt memorable or was forgettable...except for two things which will be the reason I return: MONKEY FRENCH TOAST and their BISCUITS. It's probably some of the best French toast I've ever had. Mango and banana and caramel.....to die for. The biscuits were definitely made in house and spectacular.

Other than that, I had the eggs with Turkey bacon and potatoes.....meh. potatoes weren't seasoned in any way....Turkey bacon was just bacon, not bad or good...

One of my friends ordered one of the frittatas and said it was amazing...but another ordered the pancakes and said they weren't good, and another had a vegetarian dish that she also said wasn't that good.

Another interesting thing....they make their own in house ketchup, served in old wine bottles! Pretty cool, except it tasted like marinara sauce lol. They don't have Heinz, so bring your own if you need true ketchup with your eggs and potatoes!!!

Other things to note: the place is extremely tiny, expect a wait,...and that means in the cold because there is no lobby area...when you are seated, it's cramped...for ppl to a 2-3 person booth. 

Ambiance: definitely a dive spot...hand written receipts, and don't expect restaurant service...it's a diner!! :-)

My husband and I used to be big fans, but some portion sizes have decreased (the prices have stayed the same or gone up). That wouldn't matter so much if the staff were nicer. Twice in person and now just last week over the phone we've received some of the rudest treatment we've ever had in food service, just for calling in with questions about buying a hazelnut dessert for someone with an allergy. The woman who answered the phone should lose her job immediately - disgusting behavior. It's also obvious that not all the staff are clear about what is and isn't gluten/allergen free. We let her know she'd lost Swiss Haus two long term customers and we will not be back.

I came to Lulu's for a study break, and was greeted pleasantly by the owners. They have a large blackboard that lists the menu. Their specials were described to me as nonalcoholic cocktails. I ordered the Charm, which was a lavender lemonade. Not too sweet, with a nice kick. My friend ordered the Summer, which was a mango tea and the best drink of the bunch for sure. Another friend ordered the taro milk tea with bubbles, which was a little disappointing; it tasted very artificial. We also shared a popcorn chicken; seasoned perfectly, it was a great snack to go along with our drinks. The service was quick, the staff was friendly, and the prices are great. Definitely check it out if you're in the area!
Fair Chinese food. The hot pot here is acceptable compare to other hot pots in other restaurants. Anyway, it is just an acceptable restaurant.

Best ramen I've had to date. Can't beat the combo deal either. I always get the spicy tuna as the combo add on. Sushi is fresh. My friend got the fried rice as an add on and loved it. Whenever I'm in the for ramen this is my go to, wish they had delivery! Maybe they will soon. Spicy ramen is my favorite here, not too spicy or overpowering of the flavoring. Just perfect!

I don't think there is much to say about this place that hasn't already been said. Its awesome, top to bottom. I love the Barking Chihuahua when I'm in the mood for breakfast, and the meat-loaf is always a crowd pleaser. Also, this is an EXCELLENT date destination. So if you're trying to impress someone, take them here, they will love the food, love the atmosphere, and love you for it (hubba hubba!)
I really loved the hot cocoa and the atmosphere was nice. The service was fine. I didn't have anything amazing happen, but nothing awful. A decent place.

You have many choices for Polish cuisine if you are in Port Richmond.  But it's probably not the best idea to start knocking on the doorposts of random residences on Thompson Ave.

I have a better way.  Let me help you.

The best Polish restaurant in Port Richmond is The Dinner House.  Inside it's clean and cozy with little touches from Poland that they probably don't even notice (e.g., the ceramic ware).

There's a nice weekday lunch special for like $7.

What to order?  I would strongly recommend the Chlodni Litewski (a cold borscht served with a hard-boiled egg) and the Golobki (stuffed cabbage filled w/meat).

I ordered the pierogi once--mine were filled with meat--and they were tasty but not out-of-the-ordinary enough to justify that I would order them again.

This place isn't necessarily for the vegetarians:  MittelEuropean cuisine stems from a tradition of eagle-eyed hunters and hardy farmers, not the picky Portlandians of our sad modern age

Pros - So unique with the decorations/small pub feel/Dali paintings all over. Awesome food - love their Buffalo Chicken Wrap. LOVE the food and my boyfriend said their burger was amazing. Very popular place - always crowded when I went. There's an outdoor bar and patio for in the summer when they have live music. Right next to Torresdale Train Station so its good to stop in and have a brew while waiting for the train. Good prices for food. The monkey bread is basically a glorified cinnabun.

Cons- The Horny Monkey Ale or whatever they call their local brew is a dark beer so YECH. Did not care for it. Pricey for drinks. The staff isnt the friendliest although the service isnt bad. The chips dont have any flavor but its still awesome that they are made fresh.

Will go here again!

This is a place with a split personality as far as cigar bars go. It for sure has the price and the air of a high class cigar joint but small touches can really ruin that facade. Drinks are a tad higher mark-up than even a NYC bar besides the fact they have some super-premium stuff and a very vast selection. The cigars they carry while towards the smaller end than what I'd expect are very top notch (Sand Sharks, Padron Family Reserve.)  This all came crashing down towards the end of my girlfriend and my evening when they went from swanky to full blast pop/rap music. It was so loud I could barely enjoy my cigar and my girlfriend wanted to leave asap (ruining the nicely marked up Opus X I was enjoying as well as her Ashton). I'd be fine with either an upscale feel or a more down-to-earth variety (Cappelli Brothers), but I don't jive very well with the split personality. I feel like they are trying to be both Club Pache and Carnegie Club at the same time. I'd possibly go again but I see myself wandering over to the Cappelli brothers to save some serious cash as well as the headache from the loud music.

This was a great find!!  We were looking for somewhere we could eat with great food but in the neighborhood instead of having to travel down south philly.  We found this place on the internet and never knew we were passing it everyday!  The place was decorated alittle weird but the food was awesome.  The pasta was fresh and the sauce was so good!  There must be alot of regulars because the chef was out at the tables chatting and checking on everyone!  Definitely going back!  BYOB

For the most part, Circles has good food.  The prices,  however, are insane.   My sister and I got delivery. ..Curry with tofu, Pad Thai, and one soup - after taxes and delivery fee it was almost $38!!  Seriously???  Also, you can order online, which is nice, and even place orders for advance delivery. ....which is apparently a struggle for them.  The first time I did this, I placed the order around 2 PM asking for a 7:30 PM delivery....they showed up 40 mins later. The second time was tonight.   They don't open until 5 PM on Mondays,  so I placed the order in the early afternoon and asked for a 5 PM delivery (knowing it wouldn't be exactly at 5).  I waited patiently until 6:30 PM before calling and asking for status....The girl on the phone looked up my order and said they must have not seen the order since they weren't open at the time,  and ensured me it would be on its way in 15-20 mins.  Not so much.  Finally at 7:45 pm, my food came (and it wasn't hot).  You would think that they would offer to take some money off the bill, or throw in a free appetizer.....nope.  That's just bad service in my book.  I'm not sure why they use the advance order option if they can't figure it out.

There's a lot of weird things in the bowels of Suburban Station... the wig store, PC's restaurant, a dollar store, the sushi place that looks too nice, the strange ticket scalper store, the other wig store. None of these places really peak my appetite. Orewa deli is pretty ok though. I have a few coworkers that swear by it, so I gave it a try. I got a chicken caesar sandwich on focaccia bread. The bread was pretty good. The chicken was a little iffy, a real dark meat and left-over scraps affair. The lettuce and tomato and other stuff in the sandwich seemed pretty fresh. Its been about 3 hours since I've eaten it and I don't think I have food poisoning which might be more than I could say for many of the establishments in Suburban Station. Service was fast and there was ample drink selection. Definitely the best choice in those grim, low-ceilinged surroundings.

Oh yeah, they also take credit and my drink and sandwich was slightly less than $10.
I ordered from Uber eats and I'm so disappointed!!! I ordered a spicy salmon roll and a salmon avocado roll. Awful! No flavor at all! Such a waste of money

Just opened next to parc, there was a wait there so they accommodated us right away at the bar. Beautiful place and clean. Our bartender Chris was excellent and made strong cocktails. Bathroom
Was behind the wine bar which was a little odd. The foaccacia with honey butter I highly suggest. We got the snapper which was a little too pickled but the cheese/cauliflower small plate was excellent. The past was decent size for price and delicious. Good atmosphere and service for such a new place would recommend even for just drinks.

I usually have decent length reviews, but I'll keep this one short because all you need to know that this place is WORTH THE HYPE. Good old Dave and all the other reviewers got this place right. 

You order ahead by phone and it's cash only. Both the pizza and sandwiches are top notch, but I was more impressed with the pizza. This place is a must-try in Philly.

Driving in from upstate NY we used Yelp to find a place for some sweet treats and we were not disappointed! I called ahead and talk to the sweetest person who told me if she needed to she would pull cupcakes aside for me since we were going to be arriving later in the evening. We didn't want them to runout and she gave me every bit of confidence they would have the goods when we arrived. Now when you walk into a bakery/cupcakery you want to be enveloped in that wonderful fresh baked cupcake smell and boy are you ever!! I mentioned I had called ahead and she said they are sooooo grateful when people call ahead. They strongly encourage it. She remembered talking to me instantly and was quick to help me. The service is phenomenal! The cupcakes even more phenomenal! Please go check this place out!! Your tastebuds will thank you! And I want to thank this place for making girls weekend even better.

1star...not many old italian staples like villa d roma..or santuccis across the street...the waiter did not address the table at all..until one of our party showed up due to being late..he refused...I will say he was so full of himself..farm raised small shrimp in the grilled app...the braciole was very dry and not tasty or enjoyable....we had to ask for water and for him to uncork our wine ..several times...really..just look at the strange menu..and go across the street..or to villa d roma....they really all acted like you were just
,VERY LUCKY"...TO EVEN BE EATING HERE....
None of our party was thrilled...and would not return.......AMAZING......ACCURATE REVIEW FOR OUR EXPERIENCE..

Good. You get a lot of portions for about $10. My friends and I ordered the jerk chicken, Mac & cheese, whiting, salmon, and rice. The price is right. The meat-fish was well seasoned no complaints there. However I don't recommend the Mac and cheese. At first bite you'll go "wow this is delicious" and then soon after you get a unpleasant taste. I don't know how to describe it, but the Mac and cheese is meh. It tastes like it was purely made with one cheese and no seasoning? So a hard cheese taste that wasn't very good.

This place is the best for a late night Chinese food in Chinatown. I've been going here for over 20 years and it never disappoints. The staff is friendly the food is great I highly recommend the fried dumplings they're huge.
Charging for toppings? GFY That's scamtacular. For what they charge, lettuce and onions should be included.

Do yourselves a favor and go to the Popeyes down the street for fried chicken. Our chicken was obviously reheated in the oven. The thigh was soooo dry -- how do you even make a piece of thigh dry? The spice blend for the coating wasn't good either. Somehow it gives a very strong herbal flavor. 

Other things we ordered were fine. Their house made hot sauce was good.

what's not to like...quick and attentive service, surprisingly good salad bar, and your choice of meats and fish.  i was partial to the lamb (especially the chops), ribeye, and both fishes. and keep the fried bananas coming.

Just got my haircut and colored, Spot on terrific! Over ten years and planning for another 10 years, excellent service, clean, wonderful people.

Price is definitely right...still travel here to get the best cut and color by Tara. It is laid back and friendly, like being at home.

I was just there this afternoon.  After beibg sested we were never served.  We watched 3 associates walk by and make eye contact with us, multiple times, but no one came over to our table.  After 15-20 minutes of waiting and finishing the glasses of water that dropped off when we arrived, we got up and left.  We let the host know that we were never seen by a waitor/waitress.  She said sorry about that.  

It's kind of sad because we visit here often.  I guess our patronage isn't needed.

the food was pretty good. the bf got a cheese plate that he LOVED. it was nicely varied and the accompanying honey, pecans, sliced pears, and crustini were really great. unlike some other places, this was a robust helping and artfully plated. i had the tomato soup which was poured over a wedge of grilled cheese. also quite good. for the entree, he had the scallop dish, which was AMAZING and i had the salmon, which was asian-inspired, and good, but not great.

as for ambience, i was expecting something classier. i'm used to fine dining involving someone taking my coat and a dress code. there were people in sweat suits and things just seemed a notch under elegant all around. 

it WAS an experience, though, and we both enjoyed ourselves. certain elements were just a little disconcerting. moshulu is close to being a decadent night out, but THAT night, it was just a nice night out.

i forgot to add that part of what put a damper on things was the 15$ parking fee we weren't aware of until pulling up that night.

The lemongrass scallops and shrimp are worth the visit.  It's a quiet place.  Do ot sit by the door in the winter' you will freeze your tatas every time the door opens.  The bartender was very polite.

Worst delivery experience.  Have been a loyal patron of Pagoda for over four years.  It used to be consistent.  Tonight I received inedible food that I can't imagine they would ever serve in-house.  Spare ribs that were the burnt ends, not even cut, and scraggly (picture proof posted).  The seafood stir fried noodles were a mystery in two parts.  One part seafood drowning in a mysterious white sauce, and the second part consisting of noodles alone in another container.  Not really "stir fried," is that.  I called Pagoda right away and was told over the phone they'd just remember to give me free spare ribs whenever I order the next time.  Highly doubt this is a reliable system.  Not acceptable for a $30 meal.  Will never go to Pagoda again.

UPDATE:  Grubhub got Pagoda to issue me a partial refund.  I'm afraid my stomach ache will not let me absolve Pagoda.
The quintessential neighborhood cafe! Cozy and bright interior, coffee and computer charger essentials. Great place to do some work or meet a friend.
This place was amazing. I ordered the jerk chikn salad and it was great my husband ordered the veggie burger which he raved for as well. Great food great service!

We knew we had to stop here while in Philly, but it was by far our least favorite steak of the five places we went to. The portion was underwhelming, the bread was soggy and the cheese was hard to find. I'm glad we went just to say we went, but it's the name that keeps people coming back!

The line was short because it was raining when we arrived, but it did move very quickly.

I was in such a rush to get a pedicure that I decided to try here after being extremely unhappy with them in the past. Tina used to take care of me and she was excellent, but when she went back home for a while, the place is the worst.
My first pedicure after the quarantine and boy am I not happy. I called and someone answered and said to text my request to that number and she would confirm right away. A half hour later, I tried calling again, no answer. Then I called a few minutes later and she answered and said I could come in 20 minutes. I did just that. They seated me in the chair quickly, but there is no shield between the chairs. The girl next to me had a mask on so I decided to just relax. They left me sitting with my feet in the water for 20 minutes. My feet were getting all wrinkled up. Finally a guy comes out with his lunch bag, then comes to work on me. He was nice. He did the best he could, I guess. I don't know who trained him. No lotion at all. One toe was messed up and I couldn't see it until I got home. Then I notice he only did one coat on a few toes, and he didn't paint all the way down to the end.  There was space between the polish and the cuticle. Then I went to pay and the price went up $6.  Ok, maybe they needed the money with the pandemic. So now they don't take a debit card, the machine is broken, but they have 2 new ATM machines that were never there before. I heard her saying they are opening a new shop across the street and making that a massage spa at the current location. Good luck. Next time, if I ever feel the need to go back in there, I hope they run a better shop. I stopped going in there because as soon as Tina wasn't there, they were rude to me and when I asked for a specific person, they gave me a lady that spoke no English at all, and skipped half the steps in doing my nails. When other people leave, they get a big goodbye, and thanks and come again. When I leave, I say very loudly, thanks and a have a nice day. They barely even look up. Whatever, I just hope I don't get desperate enough to go back. 
P.s. I am a very good tipper as well, so maybe they just flat out don't like me.

The size of this place was never meant to be a restaurant - the moment you walk in you feel a little claustrophobic. On top of that they insist on playing jazz music that's way too bass heavy for me to be relaxed and focused on my food that's trying to fight for space on my minuscule table. I think the food was okay - I couldn't tell, nor did I care. I left as soon as I could and never looking back.

Do yourself a favor and buy a breaded chicken wawa hoagie and sit on the park bench at fitler square instead.

We had another great dining experience at Osteria the other night. The food and service were great as always. My wife forgot her purse. One of the employees hopped in a car real quick to chase us down while we were walking home to return her purse. We were blown away and super appreciative for the effort. We will be back real soon.

Paper Source is the paper source.  Ha!  I crack myself up!

No seriously though, it's great in there.

I needed some unique Thank You cards for some of my staff at work and a friend/ colleague suggested Paper Source in Rittenhouse.

Perhaps it's my love of all-things-paper that makes this an obvious one for me, but I had so much fun just perusing the inventory.  From funny cards, quirky gifts, silly books and beautiful decorative paper, I can really spend some cash in this place!  

Which actually leads to my only quibble- it's kinda pricey.  But I'm not sure that's really even a quibble.  I mean, if you're shopping in Rittenhouse you should expect to pay- this isn't the Italian Market or the Springfield Mall.  Paper Source is amidst the many higher-end stores in Philly.  But I really didn't mind paying a little more, since I knew I was getting quality product that would be hard to find elsewhere.

My purchases included some erasers with quotes on them like, "Shit" and "Holy Crap", a bundle of Smencils (smelly pencils), two packs of cute and unique Thank You cards and one card for my husband that had a sirloin steak illustration on the front and said, "Well Done!"

Added bonus?  The staff was really accommodating and kind.  They were also a little form of mid-day entertainment, as they were dancing and singing to Depeche Mode as they charged my card and wrapped the goods up for me.

I will definitely be back again since this is the perfect place to buy a gift that will guarantee a smile.
Went to mass here when I visited Philadelphia and was blown away by the architecture! What a stunner of a cathedral! I had to come back with my camera so I can take some pictures!

This may be one of my favorite places, in any city.  It has an excellent, fun and descriptive wine menu. The light food - paninis & bruschetta -is tasty and the ambience is casual and easy.  A couple of tables outdoors lets you enjoy your wine al fresco.  And "sunday school" can't be beat for a half price glass of wine they want you to try out.  I wish they'd open one in NYC...
simpley the best seafood restaurant there.  my hat off to ya. tooooo costly though. but u gotta try when in phili at least once.

Pretty disappointed in the food quality. The drinks were awesome and tasty! But, they used flour tortillas for their tacos (where's the corn??), and between my husband and me, we had 4 different tacos ... none of which tasted very good. The guacamole was definitely the best food item, but it also was just okay. Super disappointed!

Absolutely terrible 
They do not protect, they do not serve!
Lazy, uneducated, unhelpful. Every time I call it's a problem, every time I file a report it's disregarded. SHAME ON THE PPD

I really like falafel.  Like REALLY like it. In fact, I'm going to be heading to the Middle East soon, and I plan on subsisting on it for about 5 days.  But I digress.  

I headed to Bitars one Saturday afternoon with my mother in tow, as we needed some lunch to go before we hit up a the mountains for a short hike.  We were excited about the prospect of falafel, as we both share a deep seeded love for them.  We got to Bitars, and ordered a few different falafel's, and some baklava.  We got the falafel's with hummus and tahini, and had them a few hours later.  They kept well - they were definitely NOT soggy when we got to eating them - which is a testament to how they were made.  They came wrapped in a well grilled pita, with tomato and lettuce.  The falafel itself was well made - not great, but well made.  It was a tad bit on the salty side, and didn't quite have that amazing chickpea taste I was looking for, but it was good.  Overall, pita bread was good - falafel was salty and a bit odd.  

The baklava was another story - I enjoyed that a LOT. I found it to be perfectly well made, and really delicious.  

Bitar's space itself seemed causal and airy, and the service was definitely good / fast.  I'd go back, but only if I couldn't get myself to Mama's (my fav falafel place in this city).

The food is pretty good but the waitstaff leaves a bit to be desired. 
Stopped in at 8pm on a Tuesday-there were 5 of us, requested a booth and was told that the booths were closed. 
They're were only 2 other people in the restaurant, didn't seem like a big deal to be seated at a booth. 
Besides the fact that they wouldn't give us a booth, she was extremely cocky on top of it. 
Won't be back anytime soon.
They were packed and their service was actually pretty good. If you need a quick slice or a decent cheesesteak, this is not a bad option.

One of the few places in Philly that actually does sushi right, especially the more traditional edomae style.   This restaurant really shines due to the quality of the fish, where you can taste the freshness and subtle flavors of the fish without the need to cover it up with sauces or a ton of soy sauce.  The texture is solid and firm, not the soft bordering on mushy as seen in lower quality fish. They also provide a diversity of pieces that goes beyond your typical salmon and tuna (you can forget about spicy tuna rolls here - you'd want to actually taste the tuna!)

I had the mini omakase and added a number of pieces afterwards.  I'm definitely looking forward to going back for the full omakase along with trying out their Izakaya as well. 

Definitely 5 stars from me!

Hey! Fellow ramen fans and more. 

I'm back to change my review. This place has done a 360! I'm going to take sole responsibility for the turn around of this business.  Let's start with the fact that they have CONSISTENTLY had "veg of the day". C O N S I S T E N T L Y! ! I'm frickin ecstatic to say the least. Also eggs have been on deck at full force, what's ramen without eggs anyway??? 

The broth is really savory and rich. I love eating here now. 

Give this place another try guys!!! 





PRIOR REVIEW BELOW!

They need to get their business together. They NEVER have veg of the day. They have run out of eggs more than enough times. The one in center city has waitstaff service.
