WHO IS RUNNING THIS PLACE?! who are you and why are you absent! yes your food is cheap and the quality is decent, but I do not appreciate spending my $12 hard earned dollars on inconsistency. 

I am so glad I'm going to Tokyo in March. There I can overdose on real ramen where they more than likely won't run out of eggs! what the hell! 

y'all had one job!

Good addition to the up and coming East South Philly area.    I haven't eaten here yet, but everyone I know who has says it's really good.  Especially the brunch selections. 

Had a few Harpoon Raspberry UFOs the other night when I was in the mood for a girly beer.   Basically a Dirty Hoe (wheat beer and framboise) premixed in a bottle, this was very tasty, light and not too sweet. 

They also had Dogfish Head 90 minute IPA on draft, which was killer, and only five dollars, for a sort-of rare and high alcohol beer.  Loved it with their bar snack, a very spicy popcorn with peanuts and caramelly toffee- careful, the heat builds and leaves you sucking down your beer!

All around very good vibes, bartenders were attentive and cool, can't wait to go for brunch!

Best of Philly.

We were in Philly this past weekend for restaurant week and The Barnes Foundation.

What a great food city.  So many choices and such GREAT service.
Everyone doing the sustainable, farm to table, good stuff.
Love all the interesting cocktails and beer selections.............we had some great meals including Vernick, Vetri & Fork, however, let me say that ZAHAV is the winner.

What a memorable meal, and I can't wait to go back!  Next time we are sitting at the chef's table.

The mezze was incredible, as was everything we tried, including the WONDERFUL off the charts desserts.

The GM came over and chatted us with us for a while.  Service excellent, they had to wheel us out, we ate so much food.

One of my favorites, even better than Manhattan. 

GO, if you can get in, we were lucky there was a cancellation that Saturday evening.

xo

My favorite part about this place is just how seamless the experience is, and all the additional amenities. There really isn't anything THAT much different with the teaching style other than you have special shoes/you do a weighted arm workout about ¾ of the way through. 

This morning I walked in, checked-in on the iPads, it told me my bike number, and in the cubbies below, based on bike number, my shoes were waiting for me. They remembered my shoe # from  my first visit! I then walked over to the lockers which you enter your own code from, and threw my stuff in. I walked into class, and a helper told me where my seat was and helped me adjust my bike.

The class is dimly lit, high intensity, and well structured. It feels like you're in a club with the music and lights which is awesome. The handles on the bikes also force you to have good posture/structure which is critical. 

There's also a "torq"board which def sounds like twerk board (in which case I would dominate obvi) and you can compete with other people in the class because there's a screen in the front with your scores. You can use usernames to keep it anon aka bring back your 2004 AIM screename. 

The bathrooms area amazing which I know is a weird thing to notice about a studio. They have Bliss products (shampoo/conditioner/body butter/body wash) which is great! Hairties, bobby pins, spray on deodorant, little baggies for your sweaty clothes. The shower looks like a hotel shower. It was just super easy like I said! 

After class you can jump back on the iPads and it will tell you your calorie count and other stats and compare it to your other visits. Super cool! 

If you have the $, this is definitely the place to be. I'm sure Philly will be getting a SoulCycle soon enough, but until then, this is the "trendy" spin studio.

I absolutely love Island Design Natural Hair Salon. I have been a regular customer since February 2017. Ms. Lorraine started my locs with two strand twists. I went to the salon because a series of blowouts left my hair damaged in some sections. Although I was natural, I couldn't even tell what type of texture my hair had. Ms. Lorraine was very honest with me about the condition of my hair. She has a very no-nonsense delivery (in her way of talking) that put me at ease immediately. She gave me a lot of information about my hair and about how to maintain healthy hair. It is very clear to me that healthy natural hair is her passion. Over the last year, going to the salon every three weeks, I have seen her do all kinds of hair styles. I've seen loc starts, coloring services, loc extensions, loc maintenance, and so much more. They can do any natural style that you can think of. There are also a couple of very nice young ladies who work in the salon. They are all very hard working and talented. I've seen them do lots of great hairstyles including weaves, blowouts, braids, colors, etc. I have seen people walk in with hair in terrible condition and walk about with fabulous styles.
The reasons I go back in no order:
1. My locs are beautifully maintained. I get many compliments every time I get my hair done. My scalp is clean and my locs are strong. I always let Ms. Lorraine pick my styles. I always get updo's and they look extremely professional. 
2. I never wait! I make my appointments, show up on time, and they do not make me wait for service. Make no mistake the salon is busy. I appreciate that they don't book 4 & 5 appointments at the same time and make me wait on the stylist. I don't have all day to wait at the salon.
3. The vibe of the salon is really good. It is comfortable and even has a secluded area for customers who need to be serviced in private. The young ladies who work in the salon are very professional and really treat the customers well.
4. I really respect the owner as a business woman. This is absolutely her business and she knows it. She knows how to treat the clients. I have seen her decline to service clients - because their requests would have been damaging to their hair. (This is important to me because I have been to salons that will take the money and leave your hair wrecked). 
5. Prices are comparable to other natural hair salons in Philadelphia. 
6. The salon is in a good location. Free street parking, easily accessible to buses, takes credit cards, lets you book appointments online.
7. This is a great place to come get educated about your hair and natural haircare in general. The stylists (and owner) are always refreshing their knowledge of products, hair treatments, and styling techniques. 

-- I have no problem recommending this salon and/or any of the ladies that work there. I brought my daughter (very tender-headed with lots of hair) for natural hair services, blowouts (without damage to her thick hair), trims without taking every inch of her hair. I continue to go every three weeks. However, I am sure to book my appointments in advance. This is a well established salon. I've never seen it (NOT) busy.

Check out this salon. You will not be sorry. (Disclaimer: I am in no way affiliated with this salon. I just really like this salon, the people, and the way they take care of my hair).

I live a block away and have always wanted a good bar to move into this space. Unfortunately, for one reason or another, they never work out and I won't be surprised if Bar One follows suit. 

The drink menu does not have prices. That should have been the biggest indicator. We ordered Kenzingers and Philly Pale Ales and the prices were absolutely ridiculous. Almost $7 for a single Kenzinger is unheard of, even in the most pretentious Center City bars, and this is South Philly. A shot of Jim Beam- $8. 

I'd rather walk a few blocks and go to literally any other bar then give this place my business again. 

Two stars go towards the half-way decent music and I can't deny that the food smelled pretty good. Can't imagine how overpriced that must be, though...

I don't know what happened, but something changed over the past few years since I first reviewed.

The #2 (pork banh mi) has changed for the worse. The sauce they marinate it in is different and it seems like a fattier cut of pork than it used to be from the last few times that I've had it.

It's pretty tragic considering how great the #2 used to be. Cafe Cuong makes a better regular banh mi and Lee's makes a better pork banh mi.

I loved it. We had brisket, beef short ribs and pork ribs. Every bite was awesome. Their food had a perfect bark, beautiful smoke ring and juicy taste you can't beat.  I can't wait to go back.

They don't serve beer but P2P brewery was there so we grabbed a couple of beers.

If you live around there they offer breakfast. I think I saw a sign for cream chipped brisket.

I purchased a Honda Fit last June. I have been to their service center 3 times since. I hardly post reviews but in this instance I think I should. The service dept at Honda Philadelphia are awful. They have no customer service skills, they don't even acknowledge you when you walk in, no one looks up to help you. The day I went in, only one person & myself were waiting & 5 people were behind the desk. Still no acknowledgement. Instead they leave you standing there. I finally said something to get someone to help me.  It's a new car, they said inspection would take 2 hours, I got a ride home only to have them call me 20 min later to say it was done. I like my Honda vehicle but the service dept SUX!

Great live food.  I like to check out my food before I eat it, and this place is perfect for that.  The chickens are always tasty and make the best bone broth, and the rabbits made an amazing stew.  Very healthy animals, kept in conditions better than the ones that are pre-processed and in your supermarket.  Ignore the vegan reviews of this place, you can't judge a book by it's cover, especially if you have a general distaste for that genre.
Great food and service is always fast. Atmosphere has always been chill every time I've been here and there's always plenty of seats available.

Buzz Cafe has the most delicious bacon, egg & cheese croissant I've had the pleasure of encountering in Philly. Cute neighborhood shop, great food, coffee, service, relaxed/cozy ambience. The hours seem to change sometimes depending on whether it's busy or not. Thank you for operating an awesome cafe.

Another well known vintage sweet shop in Old Town Philly is Shane Confectionery, which dates back to the 1860's!  Now this place has been around for much longer than its neighbor, Franklin Fountain, and their extensive history includes a move to a different location and back, as well as a reopening in the spot it is in today.  You can read about their extensive history here.

If you haven't read my other posts, I state outright that I'm not the biggest sweet-tooth ever, especially when it comes to candy, so I went to Shane with the purpose of candy-gazing.  And candy-gazing was what I got.  Shane Confectionery is the quintessential vintage candy shop and a dream for any kid, but on top of that, the variety of candies and chocolate they carry is phenomenal for a quite the tiny storefront.  I was flabbergasted by their numerous offerings of lollies, chocolates, truffles, fudges, and even candied popcorn.  The choices I could pick for were a bit overwhelming...

There were so many items I could pick for, I ended up just getting some chocolate.  I probably should have tried their salt water taffy, but I guess there's always a next time!  My only concern is that some of the items look somewhat stale and old, maybe due to the lighting, or maybe due to the large variety they carry, but either way, this place is a must-visit for children and adult alike!

Altho the waitress was super nice, it was a slow service lunch... and my food was borderline terrible... grilled veg open faced sandwich (in theory, soooo sooo good) but even the salad it came with was icky.  Not a lot of veg options here.  However 3 other people had excellent lunches there (ex: pulled pork). They do have a lot of TVs, all sports, if you like.   
So in conclusion, my food was blah & the service was sloow, but it was a cute clean cut bar, so I'd usually give 2.5 stars but.... they have the elusive exclusive chocolate brewed Yards porter on tap! So 3 stars it is.

These guys were amazing. They were so fast and efficient. Would use them for every move. Worth every penny to take the hassle out of moving. They were each taking 2-3 boxes at a time, huge boxes too. I was in shock. Use these dudes if you want a carefree and quick move.

If you think this review is long, just wait until you're standing there waiting for the elevator. 

I wanted to like this hotel, I really did. The location is great. But when most of the reviews gush about the free water, you know something is wrong. 

The first room I got didn't have any windows. At first I was taken aback. I'm on the 12th floor, but don't get a view?? What hotel room doesn't have windows?? I was going to ask for a different room, but decided to give it a night and see, maybe I was just making a big deal out of nothing. Well, the second day I wound up asking to be switched. The lack of windows combined with the TINY size of the room made me feel like I was in some sort of semi-cushy solitary confinement, and I felt like I couldn't breathe in there. Dramatic? Yea, maybe. But it's how I felt. Also, almost none of the outlets worked in the room, and none of the tv apps worked. 

Second room I got, was apparently a "deluxe" room. I don't know what made it deluxe, because it was even smaller than the first. But it had a window! And the outlets and tv worked!

So I'll give the pros first:
1. Location! This is right smack in the middle of center city, Philly and close to SO MANY amazing things-restaurants, shopping, sightseeing, etc. Awesome!
2. The water. Yes, I know I made a snarky comment earlier, but it really was convenient. Also, the drinks and snacks in the lobby, nice touch. 
3. Daniel, the employee who helped me with the room change. He was SO nice and accommodating. I absolutely hate asking to change rooms and feel like such a problem child, but he worked with me and personally brought the key to the new room up to me. Then he walked me to the new room to make sure I liked it and greeted me by name every time I saw him in the lobby for the rest of my stay. Seriously, excellent customer service from him. He's actually the reason I gave two stars instead of one. 

Now the cons:
1. Parking is literally nonexistent. Not limited, there literally is NONE. Now, there's a parking garage a block down, but since there isn't even a place to pull in to unload your luggage, that means lugging it through a garage and down a city block. If you're a heavy packer, you've been warned. Not only that, but the parking is expensive, though not more than other places in the area. The hotel validates it if you park at the garage a block down, so you do get a discount. Still kinda sucks, though. 
2. The elevators. Dear god, the elevators. These were the bane of my existence while staying here. You walk in the front door and there's an elevator, which you take up to the lobby. There are stairs which would save seriously at least 2 minutes, but they're blocked off so you can't use them. Then you take a different elevator to your floor. The problem is that the first elevator is TINY, with four people making it feel cramped. Add to that the fact that both are the slowest elevators I've ever been in, and it really dampens the whole stay. I literally timed it one morning and waited over 4 minutes for the elevator to take me to the lobby, only to be greeted with a line of people waiting for the second elevator to get to the ground floor. And then you get to take an elevator to your parking floor. Overall, add 10-15 minutes in the morning just to get to your car. I'm not kidding. 
3. The shower in the second room took almost ten minutes to get hot and the water pressure was terrible. I would get up, turn it on, brush my teeth and stuff, and then be able to shower. The first room wasn't like that at all, so it seems hit or miss. 
4. The cleanliness leaves a bit to be desired. As you can see from numerous pictures in previous reviews, comforters are stained and rooms are dusty and/or dirty. No lie, I had an overwhelming urge to wear flip flops in the shower and did so for my entire stay. No idea why I had the feeling, but I went with my instinct.  

Overall, honestly I'd stay somewhere else. There are a ton of other places to choose from, and even if they're more expensive it's probably worth it.
The good dog burger with that aioli sauce is perfection!  The service is great and the dive-bar atmosphere is home-y and quite charming.

Snagged a rez for a Wednesday evening date and had high hopes. Arrived to find the place super packed, pretty cute, and staff very nice and attentive. 

I had the Rabbit & Foie Terrine  to start, and while beautifully plated, found it to have the consistency of canned tuna and almost cracked my teeth on the small bones that were ground up in it. I tried to forget about the appetizer and look forward to the entrée, but I had a bad taste in my mouth literally. For an entrée, I had the Suckling Pork Shoulder. It was a small beautiful portion that tasted just okay. I was so disappointed with the flavor and portion size for the money that we skipped dessert and wont be back. 

$110 (including tip) for 2 apps and 2 entrees, no drinks and no dessert = unsatisfied and feeling ripped off.

This has been my go to cheese steak spot for 20 years. The bread is always fresh and the meat is always tender. I get my steak with American cheese, fried onions and mayo. Delicious. They get very busy on the weekends so you should come early. They don't have anything but steaks and hoagies so be aware. Nice beer selection if that's your preference.

Can I pick 6 stars if I turn my iPhone to landscape mode?
-----------
Make no mistake, I could dock a star for their service. But if you think about it, that's simply a product of slow table turnover.  They don't rush you out once you finally get seated, and trust me - getting called for a seat feels like winning a small lottery!

A summary of our experience:
- An extensive beer list, with highlights including Delirium Tremens & all types of Chimay (I particularly like the Blue Chimay - smooth, like Colt 45!)  I think I've tried half of one percent of all their beers.
- Spanish flies - somewhat out of place in a Belgian brew pub, but a great addition. These wings were was ridiculously hot...MUCH hotter than those you find at your average wing joint!  The best way to describe this degree of heat is the Szechuan concept of "ma la."  Trust me, if you like heat, this is your kind of dish!
The accompanying Belgian fries really helped put out the 5-alarm fire.  (Embarrasingly, she edged me by one wing, without a sweat!)
- Mussels: the highlight of our evening.  I ordered the Provençe mussels; she, the Ghent mussels.   A great balance of light cream, leeks, and BACON!  I caught myself using the shells as a makeshift spoon, until I realized the bread roll does a much better job. 
I've had most of their mussels, and you pretty much can't go wrong, except the Thai curry mussles (it was a bit too gritty).

- Needless to say, we will be back.  In fact, since the waits are so long, we should put our name down now...

I was very pleasantly surprised by my lunch here. I ordered a vegetarian Greek wrap, and it was full of fresh crunchy vegetables, a light dressing, and fresh feta. Only con was the typical high price of airport food ($12), but the wrap was very filling. I would definitely come here again.

Stumbled across this new cigar and hookah bar after a night out with some friends and thoroughly enjoyed ourselves. We got a mint and orange hookah and they set up an awesome table contraption with 4 pipes. The hookah was fresh and tasted great.. We went downstairs to a comfy living room with couches and a tv, hooked an iPhone up the speakers and had an awesome time relaxing and hanging out! It's byo so you can bring whatever alcohol you want and they will mix it with fresh fruit/herbs/whatever you want... I highly recommend the fresh daily!! The owner Andrew is super friendly and made us feel right at home. We had so much fun and I can't wait to come back! 

Bonus: it's next to gay pizza!

Solid food bit delivery takes 1.5 HOURS even on a slow night. Grub hub says they have to fulfill refunds constantly.  Mine was just another tonight - never got food.

They offered a free $3 desert but the driver would have to go back and get it while we continue to wait. Sad managing skills,  hire another driver.

Consider Govinda's a lesson in how meat is a completely useless food group.  

OK so a little backstory:  I was raised eating meat, but at about 14 years old (and after disecting a baby pig for school) I began a life-long journey into cruelty-free living.  Now, as a vegetarian for a really really long time, I've bought and made tons of over-priced meat substitutes of various permutations of tofu, seitan, tempeh, and textured vegetable protein.  I can safely say that I have NO IDEA what the people at Govinda's do to make their fake meat so freakin delicious.  Who knows, maybe they were blessed by the yogic gods due to their extreme non-violence practices and given the modern day version of manna to serve to well-faring Philadelphians.

Oh. and did I mention that this is a Jain restaurant?  I don't have official confirmation of this, but if you look at the menu there is a marked lack of garlic and onions, and they use spring mix instead of whole lettuce heads.  Talk about ahimsa all the way!  So badass!

Other than the fact that you'll probably have to wait for what feels like a hungry eternity to get any kind of food (I mean seriously, 15 minutes for samosa? I can see it right there and you don't even have to heat it up!), what you get in the end is totally worth the wait.  

You have to try the classic chicken-cheesesteak (I like it in a wrap with tomato and spring mix) or the kofta sub (veggie meatballs with marinara).  And save room for their Vegan Treats cakes and cookies.  You really haven't lived until you've had a vegan whoopie pie and a slice of peanut butter bomb cake.

In the United States, 7 billion chickens are killed per year.  Seven billion.  That's 19 million per day.  And people eat their carcasses.  So make the choice and go forth and eat, the non-violent way!

I like most of the beers that I've had. Fries were good and plentiful. Issues: the place is a bit dim for my tastes. I like the high ceilings though. The place lacks a soul from what I've experienced so far, but maybe that is me (lacking a soul).

Great selection of independent and alternative rock music located right on South Street.  I could spend (and have spent) hours browsing through the store's CD and record collection.  I have found rare stuff here that is out of print or that you can't order from Amazon.  Definitely worth checking out if you're an indie music fan and visiting Philly.

You walk into a very small space. In fact, its so cozy that there are no secrets in this place.  The pastas are made and cooked well, however, the sauces could use some creative inspiration. The servers were nice enough and the food came out quickly.  I thought Porcini was very cute and certainly very affordable, nonetheless, it would not be my first choice for pasta.

I recently visited the constitution center with a friend. It was not what I expected, but was very enjoyable nonetheless. The best part was the beginning where they file you into a circular theater to listen to an actor, accompanied by a video, speak about what it is to be an American. It was very moving and well-done. A must-see when visiting Philadelphia.

This was my first time here and I enjoyed the food. Has a strawberry Bellini that was yummy. We ate at the bar since there was an opening. They now take reservations per signs up in the window and inside the restaurant. My friend and I both had the chicken and waffle dish. It was a whole waffle and 2 pieces of chicken. Chicken was fried well and moist. I would have it again. The muffins were a special appetizer that I could not pass up - they arrive hot and you could smell the chocolate inside them.

There is absolutely nothing wrong with this restaurant, and I quite like the decor.  But for the price, I want outstanding food and impeccable service and this has neither. The atmosphere is also a bit zoo like.  It feels to me like a place where people from NJ and out of town go to when they want to feel like they are eating in a classy joint in the city.

My Lyonnaise had delicious dressing.  But the egg was really small and the yolk not runny or big enough to saturate the tiny hidden cubes of potatoes, so it didn't really come together the way a Lyonnaise should.

The skate wing was perfectly fine.  It came with lovely bits of artichoke heart that were fresh and hearty tasting.  I wish the brown butter were more delectable and something I wanted to sop up with my pieces of skate, but not the case.

Also, our waiter was very hesitant, standing at the side of the table never asking if we want to order.  He cleared our silverware after the first course and our second course arrived presumably hot but with no silverware for us to eat it.  We had to flag him down and when he brought silverware it was only for three settings not four, so again we had to wait to all start digging in.  It really is the small things that matter and this place lacked attention to detail.

 I would be happy with this quality of food were it in a small corner restaurant that didn't charge as much.  But with all the fuss and attitude about this place, they need to step up the quality.

I went there twice to get a couple of Reigning Champs pieces.
I didn't really want to buy the display hoodie so the guy went out of his way to go into the back and get another one. 
Every single employee went out of their way to make sure I got everything I needed and made a lot of conversation with me. They even introduced themselves to me personally. 
Totally love the new UBIQ and they have a great selection of stuff.

So glad that I finally got to come here. I am a huge fan of Yards beer and always wanted to drop by the brewery for a tour, some beer, and a possible growler fill. Today my wish was fulfilled. 

We arrived shortly before 2PM. Parking can be a pain, right next to the brewery, but you are able to park across the street on Delaware Avenue, where we easily found a spot. 

As soon as we walked through the door, we were greeted by a sweet hostess who poured a sample of a special release beer that was also available for purchase (I'm still kicking myself for not getting it). We then proceeded into the brewery where a table welcomed us with several cups of both Yards Brawler and IPA, two very delicious beers. 

I've been on quite a few brewery tours and you hear the old saying "if you've been on one, then you've been on all of them." True to an extent but I learned quite a few interesting facts on this tour that I didn't know before. Our tour guide was super jolly with a great sense of humor and kept us interested the whole time (and even let us have a few extra samples). I can say with confidence that this was one of my favorite brewery tours that I've ever been on. 

Needless to say, by the end of the tour our small tour group of 6 was starting to become a lot closer as the free samples did their job. We left the brewery tour feeling great and proceeded to check out the brewpub area, which is filled with memorabilia, growlers to go, and packaged beer for sale. 

One final noteworthy thing about Yards that totally scored points in my book was the fact that they don't make you buy one of their growlers, which a lot of brewpubs and microbreweries will do, if you decide to purchase their beer to go. And for only $10 for session beers and $12 for signature beers, that is a damn good deal! Can't speak for the food, but that's just all the more reason to come back.

Philly classic can't go wrong.  I always bring out of town visitors here and they are always so satisfied.  Prices are good and locals equally love it which is why it's one of the most authentic cheesesteaks in the city.

Only thing is they are cash only

Got free tickets to the advance screening of Knives Out last night through Elites Go Free. Thanks Yelp! This was a murder mystery movie, but was funny and had a stellar cast. My husband and I really enjoyed it, and definitely recommend it. As for reviewing the theater, this was our first time at Ritz East, and we had a hard time finding the entrance. The signs around weren't very helpful but with the help of some other people who have been there before, we finally arrived. This theater is small, the seats aren't very comfortable (I found myself wiggling a lot trying to get comfortable) and the movie screen isn't very large either. If you're looking for an IMAX or 3D experience, this is probably not the place to come to. I gotta to say though, location wise, this place is very convenient. There is a parking garage right above the theater, or street parking within walking distance. Wouldn't mind just coming here once in a while to catch a movie.

While in Philly recently, I saw a rental sign on a place that looked promising so I quickly copied down the number and left a message for Todd Levinson. The next morning he called me back, and so began a great experience. After asking me a bunch of questions, Todd put me in touch with his rental specialist, Chris Coughlin. Chris began sending me recommendations via daily emails. By then I was back in my Tucson AZ home, and looking at places solely online. When I sold my house, we had narrowed the market to around 6 or 8 finalists. I had no time to come see them in person, so I empowered my brother and sister in law to make the decision for me. Chris took them around during a busy holiday time and helped pick my new home. He took video and chatted with me on FaceTime while showing the properties. I can't say enough good things about this team. They were responsive, honest and truly helpful. I recommend them highly!!

Waste of money. This restaurant is new to the neighborhood so I thought I would check it out. Definitely not going back, had to literally hustle home because my stomach went sour. The seafood is not fresh and is priced as if it were. This guy must be making a killing with canned/freezer seafood. Lobster mac and cheese tasted/looked like Anne's white cheddar shells with a few pieces of canned lobster. Dungeonous crabs were rubbery and had an off color/taste. The u peel shrimp was awful, some pieces were over cooked while others were raw. Our drinks were the only thing that was good. If your not going to serve fresh seafood then don't market your business as a seafood restaurant. Skip this spot, there's too many other restaurants in Philly worth trying.

Holy crap. The most delicious food I've had in a really long time. I had the creme brulee french toast, which took me forever to decide I wanted because EVERYTHING sounded so delicious. I also had a side of the homestyle hash browns and the bacon. I did have to send the bacon back to be cooked a little longer, but that's just because I like my bacon crispy, but when it came back, it was delicious. Nice and thick, and the portions with everything were very generous. The staff is friendly and prompt, the music that was playing was a touch on the louder side, but it was good. I live three hours away, and I'd never leave town without visiting this place.

We had a problem with the spigot on our rooftop deck and called My Plumber. Mike showed up in 30 minutes and diagnosed our problem. The spigot was not working at all, but the spigot on our outside patio was working. Mike stayed with us and helped us solve the problem within 15 minutes. We had accidentally turned off the knob in the basement three years ago and it cut off the flow to the spigot on the roof. Mike didn't charge us for the visit and he was a great guy to talk to about our plumbing issues. We highly recommend him for your plumbing service. 

Thank you

People LOVE this restaurant. It was hard to get a reservation, but we managed to. Can you think a place is overhyped but still consider it a 5 star Yelp restaurant? Because that's how I feel about Barbuzzo. Everything was great. I have zero complaints. But I'm also not sure why it's SO popular. Overall, come here if you want good food, but don't fall into the depths of despair if you can't get in.

I LOVE Fuel.  It's tough to find a place that serves light, healthy food that doesn't weigh you down the rest of the day.  My boyfriend ordered the veggie wrap and I had the Shrimp and Avocado salad.  The chilled shrimp on top was fresh and marinated perfectly.  The drinks are great too-he loved his Green Tea Matcha and I had the Give me a Tan which was delicious.  The only thing I might change is the Fuel TV which has weird videos and club music on.  Not my fave.  Otherwise, awesome place!
The best! I always order the triple delight and pork Ramen and sake... It's the only place I stop when flying in Philly!

Went to dagwoods again the other night. Service was great and the food was excellent. Ordered clams casino, fried calamari, and the roast beef. The clams casino was one of the best I ever had. Excellent flavors with a perfect balance of clam meat and stuffing that they use 10 out of 10. Fried calamari was cooked perfectly. It had a nice crisp to them. The hot roast beef was packed with meat. Service was great the waitress was on point with refilling our drinks and recommending dishes. Thanks again!

i've sold and bought here. tables are a bit expensive but it's for a good cause so i guess i can't really bitch. And the few times i did sell i made out fairly well. 

But those days are over, i think i'll stick to the buying part for now. Records, there are millions. If you look hard enough you can find some great stuff for decent prices. Like the Ramones 'Leave Home' for 10 bux, and not some reissue. Whether it's show posters or slow roasters, you're likely to walk away with some good finds.  Lots of good diy stuff as well.  Plus a kitchen full of Tecate. 

The downside, it can get packed. If you get there when it starts it isn't too bad.  But at the end of the day you can get deals, i remember selling spray painted shirts for a meager 3 dollars when the shirt itself cost 2.

I just had my first (of many) Sampan experiences tonight, and this review might be a little inflated because I'm still on a food high, but I'm gonna write it anyway.

First off, we got here for their amazing Happy Hour (4-7 every day) and had some cocktails. The cocktail was their special for tonight, pear juice with bacardi limon, sour mix and some grenadine. I gave this a try and immediately decided I needed to stick with that because their special was indeed very special.

It was cool to enjoy cocktails while watching Michael Schulson and the staff in the kitchen prepping for the dinner rush.

The food was all amazing. Very inspired. We sat at the kitchen bar, so we got to see a very busy kitchen making some very delicious-looking dishes. Everything we had was fantastic. I had Brussels sprouts for the second time in my life (and in the past two weeks, for that matter) and they were definitely one of the most delicious things I can remember having.

I had a Kobe beef slider which was fantastic. I had mine rare (the way to go) while the server neglected to ask my fiance how she wanted hers cooked which resulted in a medium burger that she did not enjoy. Aside from that snafu and disappearing at times, our waitress was friendly, knowledgeable and very helpful

I also had the Philly Cheesesteak- fantastic.

The only thing I had the whole night (there was a lot of trying of other people's dishes going on) that I wasn't really blown away by was the duck.  For a place with such unique dishes, their duck entree (one of the pricier options) was a bit bland.

Watching the kitchen, I found so many things I want to try, though and will definitely be back here soon. If, for nothing else, their amazing happy hour!

Just got off the buss station and desperately needed coffere and deff wanted to avoid any main stream starbucks orver priced termail coffee . Turned to yelp and finally found this place . Lopez is AMAZING and very sweet plus he makes a killer mocha! You can tell ny your 1st sip hes a pro. All the flavors are perfectly and deff great with a homemade pastry  . Fyi To find this small amazing cafe can be tricky since its a building and they dont really have signs. Seating is small but intimate and BRING CASH !!
love brian!! he is amazing! my hair has never looked better and im very very picky! im so happy he is so close to my house !!!

Always skeptical about a takeout asian place that does japanese and thai too. I haven't tried the thai but chinese is eh, and sushi is eh. We've gotten take out from here 3 times now and have never been too impressed. Think that was the last time we'll give it a go. 

We got chinese last night and the wonton soup had nothing in it besides wonton..where the pork at guys? My kung pao was a bit bland. I would say the fried rice is better than average take out though. My boyfriend got general tsao's chicken that was pretty typical and just goop.

Sushi was really not as bad as I was expecting. Nothing great, but still fresh and hit the sushi crave spot. 

They are fast and friendly though so I'll give them a thumbs up to service and decent prices.
Needed a souvenir T-shirt to bring home to my 3 daughters. Very affordable and lots of items to choosw

After moving back to Philly, I tried out a lot of salons including many on Phillymag's best of  list and was inevitably disappointed. But then I found Chroma, which is a fabulous  salon. Amazing owner, Carmelo, expert staff (Adam & Jaime and Karlie are all highly talented) personalized service, lovely friendly atmosphere and fair prices. Truly the best in the city!

I happened upon this place when I got a flat tire in South Philly, and what a happy accident it was.  I immediately loved the vibe-large photos of delapitated buildings and rowhimes in Philly made for a lovely backdrop.  I am a sucker for craft beer & microbrew places.  The beer menu here is ridiculous.  Beer flights ($9 each) are a great way to sample a few beers-and increase the likelihood that you will like one.  I had a great strawberry wheat brew on draft that was recommended by the waitress-who happened to be lovely and attentive.  The food was out of this world too-my friend & I got burgers which were great.  Can't wait to go back!

I ordered the strawberry banana sundae and it was just ok. Maybe I'm disappointed that they also used whipped cream from a can instead of making their own too but the texture of my crepe was too much on the mushy side. Next time I think I will try a savory crepe instead.

This is a small two screen theatre in Center City! A real rarity in this day of high tech everything, The Roxy operates with vintage projectors from the 1950's and even has plenty of vintage seats to prove its authenticity.
I spoke to the owner at length today about the projectors and he was explaining how they change out the old leather drive bands- pretty cool.

While vintage has its charms this may translate to testing out several seats to find the right one for you, too springy, too firm, off kilter, or extra recline.
I prefer to know that my money is going to real people who live and work here in Philly not just some corporate multiplex.
 The guy maintaining the herd of folks in line to see Harry Potter is more than just the door guy- he is actually the projectionist, and no he wont start the film before everyone has a seat! The older woman at the ticket counter always has perfect red lipstick and flashy earrings on, why? Because its the movies and it is an EVENT! 
Real buttered popcorn and candy galore at prices that wont leave you feeling robbed. I love the Roxy and so should you.

They recently added an all-you-can-eat option that is a dollar less compared to nine ting. The selection has a good amount of items and sauces to keep every person happy. In my opinion, the broth tastes better than nine ting. 
The only cons I can think is that when it's busy, the service isn't the best It could be.
So good! Biff tacos are amazing ... crazy what you can do with walnuts. Love that they use the Berkey water filter.

mmm. Fresh pasta.

There are few things in life that are as enjoyable as fresh pasta. Add in that I don't have to make it myself AND it's fairly priced and I'm in love. 

For something like $4 a pound (or $5 for specialty pasta -- spinach, garlic, lemon herb, etc), By George offers delicious fresh pasta that you can cook at home. It can be cut as spaghetti, fettuccine, lasagna sheets, etc. 

The other products at this stand look fantastic,  but I seriously can't get past the pasta. Why did it take me so long to know about this???

I happened to stumble here on my way back from the liberty bell and the first thing that got my attention was that how relaxed they were on selling fireworks and while walking around this place I realized that place was so empty that I felt I was the only one here or maybe its because im having firework withdrawals from when i was young. However our mayor banned that too like the sizes of soda over 16 oz. :( Anyways

What a great little treasure of a shop. Vintage clothes from 70's through the present. Also great jewelry, scarves, shoes, obviously hand selected because each item has it's own charm. The prices seem fine to me, this is not like a typical second hand shop - everything has been selected for it's uniqueness, and I expect to pay for that kind of attention. You could easily buy a dress for $10 - $60 here.

Excellent coffee. The pastries are terrific and the owner is a joy to deal with. A truly neighborhood type of place. 

Did I mention the coffee was great! I'll be back. No pretense here.
Just stunning!! Amazing!!! And for room service, expect huge portions of food...

The pedicure here was good - very precise, looks good. However, if you're looking for the full pedicure experience, this isn't the place. The guy doing my nails was trying to chat me up more than anything else... So much so that when it came to the foot/calf massage he pretty much skipped it. I'd go back, but I wouldn't see him again.

Beef pho was good, although next time I might order one with more variety of toppings. The herbs and beansprouts that came with it were super fresh. Broth was decent - added some srriacha and the sauce that came with it to spice it up and add more flavor.

I became a member at Weston right at the beginning of the year 2012.  After evaluating Sweat, Philly Sports Club, and the Weston, I decided the Weston was the best fit for me.  There are so many class options which is really necessary in my world.  Particularly, the location is wonderful and there are so many more classes at the Weston than PSC on Market Street.  Even Sweat doesn't have as many options, and while I can appreciate the additional facilities that Sweat has among the various gym locations, I know myself well enough to know that I won't be going very far out of my way to work out.  The lack of weekend classes at the Weston is a downside, but I have found a routine that is working for me with other activities during the weekends and classes during the weeks.  

Just walking into the Weston, I had a better feeling than any other gym I've found in Philadelphia.  I guess if I had been given the impression it was some fancy schmancy  health club, I would be disappointed too, but the Weston is plenty big with lots of equipment.  It isn't state of the art, but I dont want to pay state of the art prices either and the Weston's membership fees are quite reasonable.  The classes really are top notch.  I had a schedule for working out in mind when I joined and as I tried more classes, I found the classes that I love the most and switched my routine.  I found so many I liked that I am sad to miss the classes I can't attend, but the ones I go to are my most favorite and get me excited to even be at the gym.  

The classes alone make the Weston deserve a five-star rating, but the one thing lacking, and the thing that originally made me give them only 4-stars, is the shower facilities.  The women's dressing rooms don't have any sort of ledge or chair even on which to put your shower stuff down.  It bothers me immensely.  And I hear the men's locker room has a communal shower; that would be a dealbreaker for me, so hoooray for being female.  The showers are such a minor detail to such an otherwise wonderful gym.  

Redo the showers, Weston!  

So, if you're considering working out here, definitely look around and take note of the things you will use or appreciate.  Personally, the Weston's priorities align with my own and I've been extremely happy with my decision to join!

Went to Eatalia for dinner last night.  Arrived around 5:30 and was seated promptly and politely.  From the hot seeded bread, delicious antipasto, stuffed chicken breast, garlic mash, vegetables and unbelievalbe tiramisu and cream puffs, it was a wonderful experience.  I too enjoyed Bistro Juliani and I am pleased to have again in the neighborhood a delicious establishment to frequent.
Stopped in here after the Philadelphia Art Museum. Amazing food!! Friendly staff. We will definitely be back

Cool place, good food, good service, good music. Had a 5:30 reservation, brought the kids along and they enjoyed it as well. My son had a plain cheeseburger and really liked it. Cheese board was decent, the chicken was good and the lamb chops were very tasty.
The Chocolate Debauchery for desert was awesome, and enough for the four of us to share.
Gave it 4 stars despite having to wait a crazy amount of time for our meal. Our awesome server apologized and took care of us.

Because it's a tourist attraction, you get the out of towners (like myself) who don't know how to order and slow down the line. While the cheesesteaks are above average, the extras like the drink and fries line are below average. Because of the necessity for speed, the service here is horrible. When you reach the corner with Geno's and Pat's across the street, it's fair to just go to the one with a shorter line. The taste isn't that much different, and both are worth trying. This one just fell flat with me.

BEST BREAKFAST/LUNCH IN PHILLY, seriously. I love this place, I love so many of the dishes that I can't even choose a favorite. Rich, sophisticated French-Lebanese cuisine. The plates are big, you won't leave hungry and they serve La Colombe coffee, too. Friendly and helpful staff. I would eat there every day if I could. 
A few of my favorites: Brisket huevos rancheros, Lebanese club, Cobb beefsteak salad, Saumon fume a cheval, El bait omelet. and more...

Great sushi! The Uni is fantastic if they have it when you're there! The servers are very professional and the chef is very personable and accommodating! We ordered the sushi-sashimi platter and it was fantastic! (Half eaten in the pic sorry)

Ok well I've never been a huge fan of Starbucks, I'm a Wawa guy, but due to construction and a job location change Ive had use Starbucks for my coffee addiction. There are two Starbucks by my job, I've named them Good Bucks and Bad Bucks...if you didn't guess by he 1 star rating, this location is Bad Bucks.

Oh where do I start??? The 15 minute wait for coffee or the fact that while your waiting 15 minutes you are watching 4 employees do as followed: 1 cashier, 1 asks you what you want and writes your name on a cup, 1 makes the coffee, and 1 stands and watches and chats...I'm guessing that is the manager. 

Wow... What a waste. This place give Starbucks a horrible name and a general manager really needs to investigate why this location is so poorly run while a location a few blocks away on 16th and Market is run so well (that's the location of what we call Good Bucks).

If your in the area, walk the extra 2 blocks, you'll save more time and sanity than you know.

As a millennial, I love brunch and it's ingrained in my lifestyle. It's standard issue. I went to Little Spoon with a couple of friends and thoroughly-enjoyed my time there. 

I ordered two scrambled eggs with cheese and two chocolate pancakes. First - the eggs tasted amazing. Perfecto! Second - the chocolate pancakes were fresh and fluffy. Amazing! Third - GET THE ROASTED POTATOES. I know, they're potatoes but it was very good. 

Do yourself a favor and visit Little Spoon!

Holy Pig. I mean this place really knows how to get down with the swine. First off the outside of the restaurant has a very bistro and welcoming feel, the inside is cozy and dim with lots of small tables all close to one another. 

I started off with the scallops, they were perfectly seared and really melted in my mouth. They came with a nice lightly dressed salad that went perfectly. My companion started off with the boudin blanc which was basically a pork sausage in a lentil and mustard seed puree. 

For dinner I went with the slow roasted pork belly, and Companion went with one of the specials for the evening, the bone-in pork shoulder. Both of us couldn't get enough of our meals. 

The pork belly was so incredibly tender that you could slice through it with your fork. Even though it appeared a little fatty, once it was in your mouth it didnt even matter. The skin was crisp and the meat was soft and tender. The pork belly came with a side dish of beans that didn't even compare to the wonderful flavor of the pork. I could've done without it, but I understand that they wanted a little more on the plate. 

The pork shoulder  was perfectly browned and crispy and the meat was tender. My only complaint would be that there wasn't a ton of flavor to the meat. The shoulder came with roasted root vegetables which were cooked perfectly. 

For dessert we split the short-bread pudding with pecan ice cream. It was tasty and the perfect portion for each of us to get a taste without feeling stuffed after. 

The waitstaff was nice and helpful. I've said it before in reviews, but I love when they aren't afraid to make a real recommendation. My companion was first going to try the rabbit dish, but the waitress instead recommended the special which she said was a real crowd favorite.

Made the treck up to Wajoe for a little DIY bbq. We arrived around 6:30 on a Wednesday and there were only a few other tables full, but by the time we left the entire place was packed!

We ordered the kimchi pancake (gigantic and DELISCIOUS!) and 2 different meats to bbq. Each table has a vent and a cooktop for you to manage the cooking of your meat. (The waitress will help you if you don't know what to do, and there are definitely plenty of items on the menu that come out fully cooked with no diy needed) Our entrées came with rice, vegetables, and sauces. Definitely a different, fun, and interactive experience.

A very mediocre restaurant that is great when you're too lazy to seek out better options.  They have plenty of outdoor seatings, great happy hour and brunch deals, and decent prices and portions on their food.

Just don't come here with a lot of expectations.  Not every day can be a winning foodie day.  Sometimes a tie is ok.
These guys were quick and professional. Already referred a few friends here. My car looks beautiful !!

The best South indian Restuarant in NE Phila , If you do not eat beef this is not for you ! The best beef biryani , beef cutlets , beef chilly and many more , real home made taste , like in India ! the people are good , This place is a take out only !quantity is Awesome , Low prices .

Placed a Door Dash order last night and after waiting over an hour for my food they said they were going to  cancel my order because they ran out of salmon. The Door Dash Driver took it upon herself to call me and let me know this because she was surprised they didn't tell her this sooner. She stated that she had already been waiting for my food for a while. She then let me speak to someone that worked at the restaurant so that I could get some clarity on what was going on. The person I spoke with said word for word " We ran out of salmon like 2 mintues ago." She also said I could replace it with something else. So I chose the Lemon pepper wings and thai wings. So it was my understanding that my order was complete and would be sent to me. Only for me to wait another 20 minutes and receive an email that my order was canceled due to them not having all the food items. This store is completely unprofessional and they have no kind of Customer service skills. This is not my first incident with Tasties. It's just my first time speaking up about it smh.

Went to Fork & Barrel shortly after it opened for the Wednesday cask ale and hot dog special. I've been wanting to go back ever since!

I only hung out upstairs. It has an old timey saloon feel and they were playing music to match it, which I loved. They have peanuts at the bar and you can throw the shells on the floor. I REALLY love that. The cask ale was good but I thought it was a little pricey. No matter - the special was well worth it. I forget the name of my hot dog, but it definitely had a fried egg on it. It was huge and delicious, and was enough for me for dinner.

I've heard the actual food is quite good and will certainly return to try it. The bartender was friendly and the food/beer was good, and I really love the ambiance. This place is a total gem in East Falls.

Le Sigh.

My boyfriend and I came for dinner originally for Restaurant Week. After reviewing the menu, we both decided their RW just wasn't worth the $35. After such an amazing experience at Yelp Elite's event a few months back, I really wanted to give this place a shot.

We opted to share the $55 clambake for two with mussels, corn, sausage, lobster and clam. I give this dish 3 stars at most.. almost a 2.5 although I wouldn't go as far as 2 stars. Lobster was cooked well, that was probably my favorite part. The mussels, I've seen better. And the clams -- well take a look at my photo. I eat a lot of seafood, and I was honestly thinking really hard before posting this. But I've never had clam at a restaurant or at home, that didn't have that piece (the brown/gray part) removed. I don't get it. Please correct me if I'm wrong. But this is the first time I've ever been served with that piece attached and that super turned me and my boyfriend off.
The corn was almost undercooked, just almost. It barely made it. But it was quite standard.
Sausage: EW. 0 stars for that. I really disliked it. I honestly took 1 bite, and threw the rest in the garbage bowl with the lobster shells. 
Potatoes: had no seasoning.. it felt like it was just cooked and thrown in with the clam bake, hoping to soak up the flavors from that. 

I tried one of their raw oysters priced at $2.5 for 1, and I'll give them that; it was really fresh and good.

As for service, I'd say it was standard. Not substandard or over the top amazing. Just standard.

Overall would I return? Most likely not. Based on this experience, and Route 6 being a seafood restaurant. I expect just a tad for my buck. Because looking back, it looks like the only thing I enjoyed was the company of my boyfriend, the lobster, and raw oyster. Everything else in the clambake, was meh.
Ordered manayunk burger , fries and buff chick salad it was excellent !

The happy hour fries are the best I've ever had - melted feta, peppers, onions, and some other spices and seasoning made my friends and I nickname them "crack fries" and they've become a staple ever since I moved to Philly a few months ago.

When it's warm out, there's an outdoor area/beer garden in he back where you can play various games such as connect 4, a ping pong table, and a generally awesome vibe. 

I have yet to have dinner here yet but based on the happy hour food and appetizers, it will be amazing and I can't wait to try it! 

Staff is also friendly and polite, checking in frequently and delivering quickly!

I went to HH on the top deck of the boat and had a wonderful time. 

The staff was great and informative on the menu. Everyone seemed really happy from workers to guests. 

We got the red sangria and it was a perfect thing to cheers to the sunset on the water. 

The location is awesome! Not far from Spruce Park!

While the soup dumplings are definitely worth getting, this place doesn't have a lot of the dim sum dishes I was looking for. I would skip on the shrimp dumplings, which were oversteamed, and also skip on the very ordinary scallion pancakes. This place also has a $20 minimum for credit cards do bring cash or plan to get 4 dishes. 

I thought the waitstaff was fine however I did hear them say quite a few nasty things about customers in mandarin.

This (mainly) cheesesteak joint in Reading Terminal Market is run just like the jawns in South Philly i.e. someone who reminds you of your curmudgeon Italian uncle takes your order and his buddies churn out steaks by the dozens.   One area where this spot is much better than South Philly (in my opinion), is variety.  They have both chicken and vegetarian cheesesteaks.  The chicken cheesesteak is always moist and delicious and is one of my favorites in the city. Downsides are they charge you extra for hot peppers and the portion sizes of the additions (peppers, onions, etc.) are not gigantic.  Still worth it, just buy a jar of hot peppers yourself at the gourmet grocer up the row from Carmen's.

This was just perfect from the moment you walk in and order 

Steak was ..PERFECT 

I ordered a steak with and wiz.
Shamrock shake 
Fries 

Was one of the best cheese steak I ever had 
Im From philly ...my spots are abners and Steve prince ...I am stick to say this cheese steak was just amazing ....and we'll crafted...

To be fair, I've only visited the knit with once. That being said, the reason is because it was difficult to locate and I walked past it 3x. Also, once I found it, the inside was so cramped and packed with materials, I didn't want to venture beyond the front door. I usually frequent the tangled web, but they didn't have a hook I needed so I decided to give the knit with a try.  If they have great yarns I wouldn't know because I got my hook at the front counter and never went back

Hands down one of the best chicken cheesesteaks right here! Thee best. The steaks are loaded with meat and fried onions. The best part of it is the chopped cherry peppers that they put on. One of my favorite cheesesteak places!

I had been on a quest for this elusive falafel truck for many months. The exotic locale and precious few hours open make it quite difficult for me to reach, but I had read so much about this enigmatic vehicle that I waited patiently for a chance to escape the laboratory for the express purpose of discovering this fabled cuisine. 

During my first attempt, I arrived at 1:15, which is not such an unreasonable hour of the afternoon. There were approximately 5 falafel seekers ahead of me, but alas, I was told he would not be serving any more customers that day. Bollocks! My plans were foiled, and the mythical falafel had evaded me. 

I anxiously awaited another opportunity to seek this supposed holy grail of middle eastern food. The second time I arrived at 11:30, and was forced to wait interminably. But wait I did, suffering through fathomless prattle from the proprietor, to which I politely smiled and nodded in response. Finally, my toils (and currency) were justly rewarded with a heavy platter of delicious-smelling sustenance. 

- end dramatization - 

It was a lot of food, but also kind of expensive at $10 a platter. As the guy said, you could pay a lot more at a restaurant for food like that. True, but this is a food truck and not a restaurant. The chicken and rice truck in New York is about half the price and twice the deliciousness. 

Most of it tasted good, even really good, but nothing was particularly outstanding except for maybe the grape-and-other-unidentifiable-things-salad, which was very interesting in flavor. Everything else was a little too garlicky even for my taste, and I'm the kind of person who could snort garlic powder. Also, the chicken and falafel were both too burnt, and I generally like a little charred flavor. 

It may be worth noting that my friend politely asked several times if it would be possible to bring another platter back for someone else. He began to babble about a liquor license and something about drinks. I'm not sure if he legitimately doesn't have a firm grasp of the English language, or if he was consciously choosing to ignore her question by rudely changing the subject and feigning incomprehension. 

Was it good? Sure. Was it worth all that time, effort, and money on my part? Maybe not. Still, the falafel nazi is worth trying at least once, as long as it's not too out of the way for you.

Its a no frills bike store/repair shop. They've done work on my bike a few times and I've never had any complaints. I had to replace my tires a few times from riding so much and I love that Via carries gumwall tires (the old school black and brown tires). The service is quick and reasonably priced. And I've never had any "attitude" from the staff that you sometimes get at bike shops. 

But FYI they are CASH ONLY so just keep that in mind before heading over there otherwise you will be making a trip to an ATM

Every time I'm in Philadelphia, I try to get to Pearl's. Great selection of raw oysters. And equally delicious oyster stew. After filling my belly at Pearls, then get the pleasure of wandering the market.

The food here is actually very good, shareable plates that are different from things I would  normally eat. The Mac and Cheese is wonderful, the grits are some of the best I have had in Philly and the fried chicken is out of this world, and the Tilapia would almost be enough to bring me back.... 
However I cannot give this place more than two stars because of the experience we had there. I tried to make a reservation and was told they do not take them. OK, no problem. We came on a Wednesday night, and put our name in with the hostess, who stands outside. She took our name and told us it would be an hour wait. We asked if we could sit at the bar and have a drink, which she was very strange about. So we told her we would go somewhere else if she would call us when our table was ready, she agreed. Over an hour went by so I called to see if our name was up, she asked me to hold on and promptly hung up on me. At this point we wouldn't have gone back but we had a living social deal that was up that day. I told her my name and said we had been on the list and I had been hung up on, she said it would be another hour wait, we then saw our name was scratched out on the paper (weird?) We could see MANY open tables through the window, and pointed that out, she told us they were all reserved... We got a little angry at this point, and informed her we were told they did not take reservations. She said hold on, went inside for about 10 minutes came back and finally sat us with a very rude manner. The waiter was very friendly and great, but I'm very put off by what happened and will not be returning.

I finally have found my Italian pastry shop. I grew up with neighborhoods filled to the brim with Italian pastry cornerstores and eating my way through every display case goodie. Having moved down here not too long ago, my quest to find nostalgia and my go-to pastry shop has been achieved. 

Isgro is a local staple with the feeling of a classic Italian-American pastry shop.  Unlike the ones I grew up on, Isgro only carries sweets and not bread or cold cuts. You will find a wide range of classic cookies from different types of biscotti, butter cookies, and Italian must-haves like pizzelle. The pastries are top-notch with the cannolis being in the top 3 that I have ever had. My personal favorites here are the mascarpone ones. The tiramisu is simply phenomenal as are the lobster tails, rum cakes, and more! 

The staff are all very friendly and take covid precautions very seriously by having glass covering them and the food along with wearing masks and gloves. The store is a little narrow and there can be long lineups so be sure to prepare to stand outside. Also, hunting for parking is miserable but doable!

I totally love the vibe and the servers. My waitress was super nice and gave out free dessert to us because of the delay of the pizza. Their pizza is super good, I love the most! In their basement, they have happy hours so that you can enjoy food with some afford prices. But if you don't do happy hours, the dinner will be a little bit pricy.

The sides were all fresh and the tomato broth was very good. The pork chop was a little dry but not the worst. It would be nice if they gave larger portions of vegetables and less meat but thats just me personally. I like how everything was separated so I could choose what I like or dislike. 

The szechuan soup base was a little bland but I will be coming back to try the other flavors.

The servers didn't really care to refill water.

I've only been to Companion Pets for one visit, but the service was amazing. I also live the area, which is a plus.

My cat was scratching her ears for months so I finally decided to take her to Companion Pets to have her examined and to update her shots. It was easy to make an appointment and they have later hours to accommodate my 9-5ish work schedule. I arrived for my appointment and the vet saw me right away. The service was attentive and thorough. They examined my cat's ears and determined the cause of the issue. They also took a picture of her, which they later sent me an email with a detailed invoice. The invoice was great because it not only included the services provided with the costs, but minutes from the conversation I had with the vet. I loved this aspect of the service. And the prices are very reasonable, for her shots, exam, and ear culture and medication. Another important thing to note is that the vet was very patient with my cat, who gets scared and hissy in a new environment. All in all, amazing service and I highly recommend coming here. 

One more thing: Companion Pets also provides services for ferrets which is a definite plus. My boyfriend took his ferret here and was very pleased with the services as well.

Based on the reviews for R2L, I get the feeling that it's a love it or hate it place. Here are my thoughts on why I love R2L, and my guesses to why some people might hate it.

FOOD - 4/5

As a caveat, my girlfriend and I came Restaurant Week, so the menu is different from the a la carte. Regardless, we enjoyed both the taste and the set-up. We started off with shrimp cocktail and crispy risotto balls as appetizers. I thought both were simple but delicious - a great way to start off dinner. Our entrees were salmon and beef short rib + butcher's filet combination. I thought the meat combo was a bit overwhelming  (it's made for the die-hard meatlovers) but the salmon was enjoyable though slightly overcooked. So here is my first guess on why R2L has received disappointing reviews in the past. However, the food altogether was still quite enjoyable - especially when we got to dessert - the hazelnut treat was wonderful!

AMBIANCE - 5/5

This must be what people come for, the coveted "Window Seats." If you want a window seat, reserve at least a month in advance and follow-up with a call to the restaurant to confirm availability. R2L is a relatively spacious restaurant but there are limited number of window seats, so while I understand that many of the 3 star reviews on Yelp are written to express frustration over the lack of availability of window seats and related matters, it's not fair to punish R2L if the fault isn't on the restaurant. In the end. 

Note - there are some window seats better than others, so be as specific as possible. Some window seats have columns that block the view, so it's recommended to clarify you don't prefer those seats. 

The view is worth it, though. My girlfriend and I came for an early dinner to catch the sunset and then a nice evening view of Philadelphia. With the addition of privacy as the window seat has you face the outside without any tables blocking the view, it's definitely a worthwhile experience. Ultimately, while we were lucky to have window seats for our evening, looking around the restaurant, the view would be nice throughout the place. And don't forget to look inside the restaurant too. The decor is wonderful and so is the view of the kitchen.

SERVICE - 5/5

Earlier I noted that the food is not fantastic, but I think service really makes up for it. We would get checked on frequently, and everyone seemed very friendly and receptive. Throughout the night, we really felt we were being catered to.

TL;DR: Don't let the 3.5 average review be a deterrent. Make plans in advance to come here for the Window Seat and expect a treat with great service. Definitely a great place for a romantic date if planned well!

Worst Bath&Body works ever.
Nobody helped me while shopping and they rejected to give me extra one more bag,although these were for gifts for 2 people and spent almost $50. 
To be honest, I have never seen any retails stores to reject to give extra one more bag(not fancy bag, just a cheap quality bag). Cashers were extremely unkind And said "we never give customers extra more bags.". I will never come back and won't recommend this location.
Great spot for brunch.  Quality beer selection, attentive staff, and one of my favorite chicken and waffles in Philly

I really had a fun night at Vesper. The staff is fantastic and welcoming and the food is tasty, well portioned and well priced. I had the scallops, the pork belly and the fingerling potatoes. All had me licking my fork clean. The drinks were great as well! I will definitely go back! Oh there was a wedding party there when we were. It is an amazing space for parties! Even has a speakeasy vibe to it.

Food was delicious. Large portions. Great service. Apparently, George Washington's inaugural ball was held in the upstairs dining area. This turned into an inebriated discussion about balls in the hall with our friendly and witty server. Marta Washington's chocolate cake is delicious and not too sweet. Everyone enjoyed their meals; not one complaint.
Delicious authentic food! Definitely try their Mac and cheese. Their jerk is my favorite

It might as well be a third world country in there. The manager, Tim, is probably one of the most inept people I have ever dealt with. My friend and his wife stopped in for lunch on my friends birthday. I called the restaurant and explained to the person that answered that I would like to pay for my friends lunch and send over a bottle of champagne for my friends birthday. I didn't think this was a very complicated request and I've done this many times before at other restaurants. They put me on hold for about 10 minutes so they could get the manager- Tim. After holding for ten minutes, Tim picked up and I again explained to him what I would like to do. I could immediately tell that Tim didn't really care about my request and wasn't going to try very hard to accommodate. Literally, all he had to do was take down my credit card and it would of taken about 30 seconds. Instead, he insist on emailing me a form for me to fill out and email back knowing full well my friends are at the restaurant and time if of the essence. Of course, he doesn't even listen when I give him my email, because he didn't even care about helping me, so he sends it to the wrong email. By the time I get the form, fill it out, and send back it's been over 45 minutes. When I call to confirm they fulfilled my request they just go "oh yah your friends left". A little common sense, courtesy, and desire to accommodate would go a long way- especially in the service industry. Thanks for your help Tim, you really nailed it! I'll tell my friend you said happy birthday.

Update: my friends were at Farmicia for a full 45 minutes after Tim said they had left.

Our office tried this place for delivery today. We ordered 2 pizzas, a salad and some wings. We placed an order at 9am today for a noon/12:10 delivery. Not only was the food delivered 30 minutes late....but it was delivered to the WRONG OFFICE. Food was ok but the inability to handle a rush and get an order delivered to the correct address is just sad....BE WARNED about ordering delivery here

We came in after a long day of walking the streets of Philadelphia and enjoyed a few pints of quality craft beer. The restaurant was very inviting and also accommodating to our three year old. They made her buttered noodles which wasn't a menu option and she devoured them.

This place was decent.  The food tasted fresh; however, when I asked for the sandwich and pizza slice to be heated, the food was barely above room temp.  I was kinda disappointed with it.  I also got the tortellini salad, which tasted like a caprese salad, but was missing something...it more than likely would have been a bit better if they had added some balsamic vinegar.
Probably one of my favorite Vetri restaurant in Philly. Everything I had here was delicious. Reasonably priced as well. I prefer this restaurant over Osteria.

While searching for something new to do this Christmas season my wife and I came upon the light show at the zoo. We're so glad we decided to make the trip down. While it can be a bit pricey, we believed it was worth it. There are many attractions and 2 times light shows. Total cost with parking for 2 adults and 2 children was $86.00. They have people dressed up as well in Christmas lights. From peacocks to lions! It was pretty cool. Some concessions were open as well as food trucks. No animals though. We were hoping to see some but didn't. The staff were extremely friendly and helpful. We would return.

Seriously surly service (except for the owner perhaps, who tried to get his folks to be nice), and seriously yucky cheesesteaks.  I am not a fan of the fact that the menu says what the cheesesteak price includes, but you don't actually get those things unless you specifically ask for them.  How does that make any sense at all?
I wanted to update, since my last review I have been back more times than I can count and have only had good service.
Really great tomato pie, & really great prices. Definitely a go to quick lunch for me.

I had the opportunity to check out Sabrina's Cafe the last time I visited Philadelphia and was pleasantly surprised with how amazing their brunch was. I used the Yelp option to put my name on the wait list and Uber'd over. Upon arrival, the entrance of the restaurant was PACKED. There's no decent waiting area and the restaurant is small but all was good since there were two empty seats at the bar. Score!

OK so as soon as my friend and I sit down the server brings us water, a pitcher of orange juice and opens our wine for us. Yes, you heard right. You can bring your own alcohol, as this place is strictly BYOB and they do not serve alcohol. Wish I would have brought two bottles but it's cool. Next time.

So I ordered a half stack of pancakes (2) and a side of turkey bacon. I added chocolate chips, marshmallows, and bananas (oh my!) to my buttermilk pancakes (I was going to start my diet for the new year the following day so don't judge, ok? and bananas are healthy lol) When I realized how good my food was going to be (because everyone else's food looked amaze) I wished I would have ordered a full stack.

I'm glad I didn't because I barely got through the two pancakes. So delicious and filling. The bananas and chocolate chips were cooked IN the pancakes and the marshmallows on top and it was just absolutely perfect.

The service was impeccable. The eclectic staff was super friendly and the atmosphere was great. I got to relive high school a bit with the music they played *cue Ignition remix*

This place is horrible. I ordered a jerk chicken cheesesteak. They gave me old chicken fingers chopped up on a roll with cheese , literally chicken fingers. I live 30 min away from the restaurant, so I was not driving back to return it. Waste of money !!! Check your food before leaving if you dare to try the restaurant because they might scam you, like they did me.

This is a seriously amazing gift store in downtown Philly.  I have been here multiple times and can't seem to leave empty handed.  They have a ton of Philly themed merchandise as well as a huge selection of beautiful jewelry.  They have a large greeting card selection, as well as a ton of baby shower gifts.  You won't be able to just browse, so make sure you bring lots of money!

Doma is my neighborhood sushi spot and i could not be happier! The sushi is incredibly fresh and service is really quick. I normally only order take out (since it is only a block away) and they usually only take about 5-10 minutes to complete an order. My go to options are salmon avocado rolls and salmon sashimi (i am slightly addicted to salmon). This restaurant always delivers on quality and the price is really reasonable too.

Looking to try a new byob, I decided Dmitri's would be a good pick based on reviews and a recommendation from a coworker. We arrived early on Friday, right around 6, and were seated right away. The restaurant is small, but cute. We were told the specials, given water and wine glasses promptly, and soon our waitress came over to take our order. 
We decided on the hummus and the grilled octopus as appetizers, and I ordered the seafood combo for my entree based on a recommendation from the waitress, Ben ordered one of the specials, a bluefish pasta dish. To be honest, I was slightly disappointed in the hummus, it was not very flavorful. The grilled octopus on the other hand far exceeded my expectations and was a large portion. The bread and dipping sauce was also tasty.
Our entrees came out and both were very large portions - the seafood combo was a heaping, hot, bowl of clams, mussels, calamari, etc in a red broth, served with rice and greens as a side. Ben's pasta dish was also quite robust. We took our time drinking our wine and enjoying our dinner, not once did we feel rushed, and our water glasses were kept full throughout the entire meal. It did get a bit loud despite not being terribly crowded, but it wasn't unbearable. Overall a very good meal at a very reasonable price in a casual setting - will certainly return to try their other offerings.

Great spot for a nightcap. The cocktails have NYC prices, but the service and conversation with the bartender made it worth it. He picked up on the little things and made conversation with each of the guests that made them - me - feel welcome. I had a drink called "the muse." I'm not normally a gin fan, but it was perfect. 

Overall, great spot for a casual drink. The open atmosphere - both the  physical and intangible - are perfect. Highly recommended.

Word on the street is, the Quinoa Porridge is the cat's pajamas. Too bad I got the Eggs Benedict. Undercooked eggs were basically ectoplasm on Canadian bacon, and the potatoes were, for reasons this reviewer could not discern, slathered in a healthy coating of slime. Half-expected Peter Venkman to be the bus boy and Egon Spengler to be slinging hash.

The peanut butter cup latte, while quite tasty, does not really fit in with the Ghostbusters allusions above.
