Pretty solid happy hour in a nice environment. From 3-6PM M-F, they have $5 select classic cocktails, select glasses of wine and $3 select beer. The classic cocktail when I went was a Gold Rush (whiskey, lemon shrub, and honey). It was tasty but a little on the sweet side, so I had a glass of cava next, which I enjoyed. They have food specials as well. I had the charcuterie board ($9) which included a seasonal pate (I think it had cranberries in it, which made it delicious), pickled vegetables and andouille sausage. I wish it came with a little more bread (only 2 slices, and even after loading them the toppings, I still had meat left after finishing my bread). Service was good.

Wouldn't come back here again. Staffs with nasty attitude with their arms crossed the whole time. Dirty restroom. Sweeping floor next to us while dining in. Go eat somewhere else. This is not the kind of service you want to pay for

I've been wanting to come to Craftsman's Row for the longest now...and to be honest I was expecting something a little better. I feel like the vibe of the restaurant doesn't match their Instagram and the kind of food they serve. We got a cheesesteak burger (I think that's what it was called), fries and Mac and cheese. The burger and fries were great but the Mac and cheese was lacking a little bit in flavor. What you really have to come here for is the milkshakes...TO DIE FOR! I'm not joking. If you get anything on the menu, get a milkshake. You won't regret it. If it wasn't for the delicious milkshakes, I would give this place a 3, but they definitely boost the rating!!!!

This review is for the "To Go" business: They have a good selection of tasty vegetarian sandwiches (in particular the chicken and pepper cheesesteak, but the service is quite iffy.  We called to order ahead several times and they have a habit of putting you on hold for ten minutes and then hanging up on you.  And then when you call back they do it again.  After that last hang up we've given up on them.
Good ricotta cannoli, but that's about it. Back in the 90s Potitos was awesome. Everything good goes away.....simple fact. Find the new best.

Oh Neeeeeeeeew Waaaaaaave..... what happened to that little Queens Village gem from 7-10 years ago. Where is the 3 bell review Laban gave you....? This used be that "go to" neighborhood bar where you would not have a second thought about here to hangout if you needed to fallback on an excuse to get out of house.... 

I ventured back to the old neighborhood and check in on you...... you are sick, you are old, outdated...what happened to you? Where is the flair, where is the chicken cutlet with rabe and sharp provolone before it was popular, where are the waitresses that are above 13 yrs old and cute, what happened to.......you get the picture,

We had the buffet and it was quite nice and reasonable too. Less options for the vegetarian but good for non-vegetarians. 

Tandoori chicken is highly recommended and chicken tikka masala is also good.

Definitely try out for good indian buffet food.

You will be overwhelmed, you will be very overwhelmed.  In a good way though!  It's like we took spanish tapas markets like the botequin and the san miguel market and americanized them.  Little shops sell their specialties and everything is SO fresh.  Goods are also sold here (doorbells, nick nacks).  Reading terminal is very close to the bus terminal and city center, so this is a convenient location at the beginning, middle or end of your trips.  I went to the Dutch Eating Place, which is amazing and affordable.  However, there were lines to dine at many other places so I would look around and choose wisely!
Friendly and efficient service at a very good price. My team definitely knew their stuff, and made this move the easiest I've ever had!

Location is convenient on Main Street and it's great they have their own parking lot for customers. If it weren't for the parking garage, I wouldn't come here as getting a space on Main Street would be difficult. When I first started coming here, there were two employees that were very helpful and pleasant. Lately the person who's been working the counters is lazy and rude. Please bring back the hardworking employees who cared about giving people good customer service.

I did not have a positive experience with the dRemodeling home estimate process. I paid the $145 fee on 1/23/18. I had the site visit/home assessment on 2/1/18. I visited the design showroom on 2/2/18. I then waited three weeks to receive the final proposal on 2/26/18. When I declined to move forward on 3/5/18, I requested the dimensions and photos taken be sent to me. I thought that is what I had paid for in part. I never received a response. I have a small, partially finished basement. I was very surprised to receive a proposal in the $67K-80K range to complete what I had intended to be a modest renovation. From the very beginning, I requested the estimate be broken down by the three rooms (main area, bathroom and laundry/utility room) and this was not done. If I wanted to tailor the project to fit my budget, I wouldn't know where to begin as nothing was itemized on the estimate. The estimate was vague and contained mostly boilerplate language that was not specific to my project. In fact, my husband and I wondered if we had even received the correct estimate. I don't feel dRemodeling delivered on what was promised, which did not give me confidence in hiring them. On 5/21/18, I replied to a follow-up email sent by dRemodeling with these same comments and never received a response.

The reason that this gets 2 stars is because the barista was very nice. The tea and coffe, on the other hand, not so much.

I asked for earl grey tea and what i got was brown water. I asked for them to remake it, which they did happily, and it came out the same way. 

I decided, well maybe they had old tea, or a bad brand, so i tried a "Thai Coffee." Please understand, espresso with lots of cream and sugar is not Thai Coffee.

The bagel was good, and the barista super nice, but I probably won't be back again. There are plenty of other Coffee/Tea places in the area I can hit up!

My crepe was so good that I ended up not even being freaked out by the fact that all of the waitresses looked like bizarro versions of me.

We got the cheese plate before our crepes - it was so-so, with one amazing cheese (a goat cheese with truffle) and one not-so-great cheese (the hard cheese was too hard and too bland to be of much interest). I wish we had skipped the cheese plate, and saved up room for dessert crepes! Next time. 

I found that it was a little crowded for my tastes. It was obvious that they were going for a Parisian street cafe ambiance, but I felt like I had to constantly check to make sure I wasn't going to bump elbows with the nice people to either side of me - and I'm a pretty small person. The great food and friendly service made it totally worthwhile, though. We'll definitely be returning!

Was in the Italian Market for the festival... after the crowds died down, went to this place for dinner. The food was excellent. Great flavors, inspired menu. We had a couple of different plates, and everything was delicious. Ambiance was relaxed. Staff was super friendly. I would gladly drive the 2 hours from NYC to Philly just to eat here.

CAUTION--all these wonderful Yelp reviews would make you think you are in good hands--CAUTION. 

I am not speaking to the work because never got that far due to:
1) WAY overpriced quote
2) misinformation telling me the leak was just in the tub and there wasn't one in toilet when there WERE issues in both places

JB quoted $975. Because it was not an intensive job (couple hours at most), and the fact I told him my partner said there was a leak also in toilet which he said there was not, I got another quote. This plumber repaired both tub and toilet for $500. Double the price and not even a correct diagnosis!!! Go elsewhere!

In my opinion, Dr. Cohen and his staff are the best in Philly. We used to take our dog to Queen Village Animal Hospital but we were not getting the attention that our dog needed (she has skin allergies). Dr. Choen not only helped us in the short term to make sure our dog had what she needed, but he takes the time to sit with you, learn about your pet, and help find a long term solution.
He is knowledgeable, kind, friendly, honest, and unassuming. I seriously cannot say enough positive things about CCVH!

This place is awesome. I definitely recommend starting your meal with the marinaded olives and a pitcher of their sangria, which changes depending on the season. All of the pizzas are delicious - a standout being the Brussels Sprout. I've never had a bad experience with the service here either. The staff is friendly and obviously well trained.

Really nice decor, laid back ambiance, good music.  Great staff.  They were really knowledgeable about the food preparation, drinks, and gave great recommendations.  We had the Strip Steak with Pasilla sauce, and the Blackened chicken with mango salsa (which wasn't too sweet like most places and had just the right amount of spiciness to compliment the chicken.  If you like margaritas, or tequila, they have a good selection. Everything tasted delicious, and I can't wait to go back.

Man, people are all over the map about this place... but I love it. 

One caveat for this review - I almost always sit at the bar, and almost always at or around Happy Hour, which for my money is the best in the city: a great, regularly-rotated selection of beers, all at half-price. And even the regular prices ain't that bad; for $4-5 you can get a rock-solid craft beer, about the same price I'd expect to pay for a lager at your typical non-dive joint.

Food is ok, that's the only reason I knock off a star - haven't been blown away by any of my experiences with the regular dinner menu, although I do like the tapas: chickpea fries and the patatas bravas make for for some good finger food to soak up all that sweet, sweet half-price beer. 

Haven't tried brunch.

Never had bad service here - all the bartenders seem pretty friendly to me, and there's only once I really had to wait long to eat (and I got comped for it). Came here once with a biggish party for a friend's birthday, and service was still solid and nobody went thirsty. 

I know this place gets compared to POPE a lot, but:

a) it's a few blocks closer to where I live, and I really am that lazy

b) POPE happy hour goes from 4 - 6, and just because I'm a freelancer with alcoholic tendencies doesn't mean I can drop everything to get loaded that early. I do have some standards.

Sure, they don't have crab fries - but life is full of compromises...

This review is for people with food allergies. If you have them, be careful ordering food and drinks here. I'm super allergic to tree nuts and sunflower seeds/oil so I always check whether something is safe to eat before ordering. There were multiple times I've ordered something here only to have it come out with nuts on it -- these were usually cheese plates so at least the nuts were visible. I've stopped ordering any food here after this happened multiple times. The drinks can be a problem too - one time I got handed an almond milk latte instead of a regular milk latte. Now I just stick with Americanos or pour overs. I like the space and come here often but unfortunately can't trust most of the things they sell because of their food safety problems!

I'm Gonna make this review quick because I hated everything about this place although unlikE a lot of other people I didnt wait in much of a line so I'm just gonna break it down

Bun--McDonald's 
Meat--cheaper than McDonald's 
Toppings--had the hot?? And it was a bunch of red crap that we will call peppers I also had a concrete?? And it was nothing special what makes me most upset is the bill came to 12 bucks and Wendy's is much better and it would've only cost me 2.00

We are thrilled that a Franzones opened near us! The pizza tastes just like the Conshohocken spot and the guys are great there. We ordered a chicken cheesesteak too in our most recent order and it was even excellent. Highly recommended. However, if you order using Doordash you do have to specify in a note under their cheese steak wrap that you just want to sub a regular or chicken cheese steak on a roll and they will do it. For some reason the doordash menu doesn't list their regular cheese steak options, only the wrap option. So you just need to leave a note asking to sub it out for a regular steak is all.

Unlike other early players in the Philly Craft Beer Bar space, Standard Tap has managed to remain at the top of the heap despite a changing landscape and an onslaught of newcomers.

The beer selection remains great,  the food menu is inventive but still satisfies your basic needs, and even if you're not a drinker it's a great place to simply go for dinner.  The atmosphere is excellent, making the most out of its re-purposed row house to keep things interesting yet home-y.

Keep on keepin' on, Standard Tap.

You get what you pay for! First the two pair for $69 deal is for frames with plastic lenses only and only for single vision. If you want bifocals or progressive lenses you will be charged extra.  If you want glass lenses it's extra. If you need progressive lenses it's extra. If you want glare or scratch protection it's extra and is not covered by their warranty. I felt like they were trying to get me to purchase all those extras so the $69 deal went out the window! Don't be fooled. Their deal is to get you to walk in the door and then try to upsell you! I will say that their staff was pleasant and helpful and just doing their job to get you to spend more money. More importantly, their optometrists do not do an extensive eye exam. There too they try to get you to pay more for certain services that are automatically included in fees from other optometrists. Once you pay all the extra fees you end up paying the same at other optometric establishments that are not chains. Don't waste your time. Go somewhere else. You get what you pay for!
Good carnitas burrito. VERY MESSY, but delicious. Good drink selections.

Bland food. Bad service.

They have every incentive to get you to come back (punch cards, raffles, etc.) except food that you actually want to eat. No wonder the place is dead at prime lunch hours.

I suspect that they're secretly trying to get on Kitchen Nightmares.

Tried SET the other night.  The chicken 'skewer' was more of a kabob with raw chicken and vegetables.  The Mexican corn salad could have been good but the corn was flavorless, probably used canned corn.  The wings were ok, but not worth a repeat.  I liked the fries and drinks were decent.
Nicest dudes ever! Really go out of their way to explain everything and are super friendly and honest about what will work best for what you want. Best place on South Street for sure!

Definitely not a foodie by any means, but the food & experience here = amazing!

We sat at the Chef's Table, which was awesome. It was pretty cool to see orders being prepped, then inspected by Chef Landau before being served. 

The Peruvian Fries and Korean Tempeh were delicious. Never have had chilled red wine before, but the Bonarda was excellent. 

A little pricey overall, but well worth it. 

Will be coming back, and definitely recommend to others.

They don't have fries here. I was sad. I'm from Arizona and I wanted to eat up a Philly cheesestake with some fries. Bummed. 

Keep in mind this place is a cash only place. 

I got a cheesestake with onions and wiz. It was good. The bread was BOMB. It was a messy sandwich cause of the wiz. I got my children a turkey sandwich and they liked the meat. 

It's tiny inside. So, you gotta eat and bounce. Even tho it's a 3 star rating for me, I'd come back and maybe add more to my sandwich or try a different cheesestake. I just wasn't like, WOW THIS IS LIFE CHANGING.

Rude staff and incredibly loud music.

It says restaurant, right? Okay, because this ain't no disco-tech or club.

The biggest problem is some of the staff is SUPER rude; to the point where you get one drink, pay, leave, and go somewhere else for dinner.

I think my last waiter was surprised I tipped him at all; he was terrible.

The drink was good, though. The bartenders know what they are doing.

Also, the music... it's at a 10 when it needs to be at a 5. It is so loud in this restaurant (from the music, not the patrons or staff) that you cannot - CANNOT - have a conversation with a person sitting 2-3 feet from you.

I do not recommend for a date night; unless to get 1 drink... or to get completely shitfaced and scream as loud as you want; ain't nobody gonna hear ya'.

Animo is the perfect healthy lunch spot for people who don't like artificial preservatives, processed foods, empty calories, deep fryers, GMO's or microwave ovens. Everything served here is organic and whole, thus DELICIOUS and energizing. Animo offers a wide variety of juices, ginger and lemon shots, burritos, burrito bowls, frozen banana custard, and acai bowls. Although I've only been for their great lunch, they do offer breakfast as well.

Sometimes there's a long line at peak lunch hour but they do get the food out relatively quickly. The food is really fresh and they have a nice selection of kombucha which is why I keep coming back. They're a little low on seating and the tables really only accommodate parties of 2. Their staff is really pleasant and friendly. This is a great location, right across from the Comcast Center which makes it almost impossible to find parking nearby. The only major downfall to Animo is the cost. I got a burrito bowl with guacamole and kombucha and it came out to $17. Regardless, this is still a go-to for me and I recommend to anyone who wants a healthy yet filling meal. I'm teetering on the 4 stars vs. 5 stars line here.

This place has a chef with the gift of culinary creativity that's pretty rare.  

I'm talking about the gift of being able to combine flavors that is hard to even imagine, let alone execute and create a dish that works together in a way that Jazz does - a much better ensemble together than its individual components.

To add to the superb quality and taste of the dishes, they provides us with perfect service and a great atmosphere.

A true gem.

This place was a great spot for breakfast. We went on a Sunday morning and the place wasn't packed at all and we had a great experience. There's a spot to just get a pastry and take it to go or you can go and sit down as well. The menu had some great items on it and I ended up going with a waffle with berries and cream. It was quite delicious even though it was a bit small. The price wasn't bad for the waffle as well as the other menu items. I highly recommend this place for breakfast or if you want a good pastry at any time throughout the day.

Ok.. so it was restaurant week when we first tried Buddakan and I totally had in my mind that we probably would have a nice meal, but to not get our hopes up too much... 
As we arrived we were greeted by extremely friendly host letting us know that our table would be ready in a few minutes and showed us to the bar.  We got to the bar and as the bartender set down the bev naps the host returned to us and said our table was ready...
We got to the table and the server was too very friendly and talked to us about his favorites menu items. We ordered the rock shrimp buns (not on restaurant week menu) to start b/c they sounded great and OMG they were amazing!! Spicy, sweet, warm delicious!! I WANT MORE!!! Next was our meal.. Apps consisted of the shrimp & scallop spring rolls, yum and the beef satay, good but not my fav.  Next were the entrees... I ordered the Mongolian beef short ribs, YUM and my husband ordered the Chilean sea bass WOW THIS WAS GREAT!! Dessert was the "dip some" donuts, fantastic and the crying chocolate cake, good but not as good as the donuts... 

So needless to say this place totally blew our minds even with our preconceived notions of restaurant week... we will definitely return soon for another great experience!
Good food, customer service was ok. Definitely seemed a lil overwhelmed. I was at my cousins and wasn't sure of any places to eat. Good food, long wait

Beautiful setting, spacious and great for a large group. The buffalo mozzarella salad was really good and the crab cake was beautiful. It was a pretty big serving of crab. The homemade rigatoni with its light tomatoes sauce was nice and light for a pasta dish.

The good news: I have had multiple physicians here over the years and have loved them all.
The bad news: I have had multiple physicians here but they keep leaving.

Other than physician turnover, I have found this place to be a great medical practice.  This includes the office staff.  I do have to say that too often they don't answer the phone, but otherwise I have found them to be nothing but friendly and helpful.  I'm surprised by the negative review.

I have been a Mitchell and Ness Jersey collector for a number of years, but no longer. 

A little while back I was gifted an Allen Iverson Gold jersey for my birthday by my aunt who passed away from breast cancer a few months ago. I loved it, however when I went to clean it for the first time, following the instructions on the label.

AND THIS IS WHAT HAPPENED!!!!



I go to the store to exchange my product for a similar one and I'm immediately greeted with hostility!

I tell the cashier my situation and that I don't have a receipt because it was a gift and she says there's nothing that can be done because the jersey is no longer in stock. DESPITE THE CLEAR DEFECT IN THE JERSEY!

On top of that, the fat old security guard UNBUTTONS HIS GUN HOLSTER AS IF IM TRYING TO ROB THE PLACE!!!

All I wanted to do was exchange a jersey an I was treated like a criminal!

Mitchell & Ness used to be a place of high quality jerseys and excellent customer service. 


Now it's turned into a store of rude customer service and CHEAP CHINESE JERSEYS!!

if you want to buy the best jerseys, I suggest going elsewhere, because the only thing that this store cares about is your money. 

And if you want to get a better jersey I suggest going to Alibaba, same quality at a fraction of the price.

These are life changing donuts! I had a strawberry rhubarb donut, which till this day may be the best donut I've ever had. I also had a hot brown sugar and cinnamon donut which is heated up fresh and unbelievable. The only thing bad that can be said about this place is that the donuts have holes which made me sad because it was less donut for me to eat and that's obviously not their fault. Federal Donuts gets put at the top of the donut pedestal...the donuts themselves get put in my stomach.

This location will scam you.  They claimed there was a scratch and charged my credit card $600 and $228 two separate charges.  The girl working there with glasses also questioned why I came in half hour late for my reservation, like she needed a reason.  I'm trying to pay your company don't question me about anything.  Stay away from this place there are better options in South Philly.

Fast and easy delivery, they called twice to ensure they had right street locations.  The feed is inexepnsive but of high quality.  I had the enchiladas with rice and beans - and it was perfect.  Service was fast, delivery that is, and the food was hot and looked great.  This is about to become a take-out/ delivery habit of mine, I can tell.

Been coming here for years now and somehow I'm still not sick of the strawberry lavender donut . The fancy donuts are a hit or miss. My all time fave fancy donut was the milk and cookies, please bring that back 

Apparently the concept of hot dusted donuts doesn't sit well with everybody and I sincerely feel bad for those people who don't like it because these donuts literally melt in your mouth like what more could you ask for lol. 

The people that work here are super sweet and friendly, and they always throw in a couple extra donuts which I really appreciate! 

Also half my camera roll is federal donut pics so enjoy :)))
Best sushi! Friendly service and a great spot when you're in University city or visiting International House!

I love the Smak Parlour for their crazy and totally awesome gifts and accessories. Always one of my first stops in Old City when looking for a unique birthday gift.
P.s: They have the cutest display window!

Shopping for 2 new cars- de Simone Kia- one of the most unprofessional dealers I have EVER experienced- insulting and rude! Wow! I'm not done- I am very professional and nice - John the #s guy- he was fake , disrespectful- he has to be a relative! Needless to say - no kia....they're tin cans

First time trying this place and really enjoyed it. It is a relatively small space with a small bar. The hh wine offerings are nice and they do a generous pour. We had the escargot, French cheese puffs and the cheese board. Escargot were great, cheese puffs just ok I'm my opinion and the cheese board was large and very tasty. We actually were unable to finish it. Would definitely go back.

Since my original review was written they have changed some things.
For example the smell of burning oil is gone and it's nicer to be there in general due to a T.V that is either showing HBO or Sportsnet.

Quite simply, one of the best dining experiences ever. Because we went on a weeknight before 7:00, we each were comped a happy hour beer. And, it was a really good local brew and not something they needed to get out of there. I had the lamb sirloin which was seasoned and cooked perfectly. It was also the most tender piece of lamb I've had in years. My wife had the skate wing - you can hardly find that anywhere. Like the lamb, it was amazing!  Service was top notch, and the ambience was comfortable and classy.
Easily the best Huevos Rancheros I've ever had. The service was excellent. Attentive and friendly. I will definitely be back next time I'm in Philly.

I have been here a bunch of times and each time I like it less than the last time. It is awesome that you can bring one bottle of wine per table, but they also have a full bar so you can order drinks. (hybrid byo)  I also like the organic focus and they have vegetarian options for our vegetarian friends.  It is a good place for a big group.  I brought my parents there once and they didn't really enjoy it.  Went for brunch once and thought it was okay.  With so many great dining options, this is not a top choice.

The sky is blue.  Winter is cold.  Lou Dobbs is an idiot.  Philly has no good pizza. 

Yep, some things never change.

Went to grab some take out here because apparently the six block hike to 23rd is too far for delivery.  Whaaaa???  Anyway, picked up two medium pies for $24.  The price proved to be the best feature of the experience.  Service was friendly and competent.  Pizza was, "pizza that could have come from anywhere" pizza.  Blah crust.  Blah sauce.  Canned mushrooms (Gah!).  The spitfire pizza was decent at least, but insufficient to justify a return trip.  Honestly, I think Round Table does a better job.  Clearly I'm alone here.. but there it is.

Omg I keep daydreaming about this place. 

I went on a Sat night and there was a wait. But it is soooo worth it and it is a BYOB--lovely! The servers are fast and efficient so the wait for a small party (2) isnt long. We weren't really sure as to what to order-- so we asked the server for suggestion.  We ordered the soup dumplings (yummy); slice pepper beef and general tso chicken. Sooooooo good ! 

Check it out! The crowd is a little young but the food is well worth it.
Shanti Yoga Shala is a beautiful new studio with yoga classes for all levels! The teachers are very kind and knowledgeable.  I am so happy they opened up in Old City!
who doesn't love Rita's! Mango is my favorite!

This is definitely a cool spot to hang out on a hot summer day! Mainly because they have AC, but also because it's pretty dope.

I've been here twice, and in the four years between visits, not much has changed. I was hoping for a few new exhibits or new medical oddities displayed proudly in a jar, but it seemed as though it was all the same things I remembered from last time. 

You can easily spend 2 hours here just checking out everything. And there's also a gift shop once you're finished looking at gangrene covered body parts or face tumors.

Discount tickets for students, seniors and military! Yay!

Best pizza crust in Philly!!! Jose is hands down the best server! Carmela pizza is the greatest addition to the menu, highlights the flavors of the city ! Please keep it on the menu!!! And everyone, add the long hots!!!

It was not a good experience! While waiting for a table, a young man took all the chop sticks and spoons dumped them in one plastic container and filled it with water. He then moved them around with his hand, then he removed them and started placing them on our table( in other words please ask for a fork)! 3 out of the 5 dishes were good, but 2 were just Blah!!! It was so tightly pack, it seamed ever waiter as them were walking past some part of there musty body rubbed across my back. The kicker was the Hair that was Fried into one of the dumplings! It didn't look like a hair from your face

WOW. Mirra, Natasha & Andrea took such great care of us at the bar. Food was awesome. Service was incredible 10/10 would recommend to a friend. Come here if you want to enjoy good food and great company.

It's a dive bar.  Plain and simple.  It doesn't pretend to be anything else.

My friends and I walked in on a Saturday afternoon and there was a motley assembly of patrons.  One guy was combing his goatee with a brush/comb, one guy was yelling something about Chinese food and the others were just hanging out smoking.  (Yes, you can smoke in this bar).

There's really not much to say about this bar other than cheap drinks and a friendly bartender.  My friend would probably call her too friendly since the bartender called her "mommy."

I have visited their 19th street location many times, but because of the rain today I thought this would be a better option. I am so happy that I did decide to! The food was great but what really did it was the service. The cashier that took my order did not take it properly and my sandwich had mayo on it, which I asked to have it removed. When I returned my sandwich after my first bite, I spoke with the manager and he quickly apologized, had a new one made, with a fresh soup, and came to check on me and the rest of my party to make sure we were ok. Mistakes happen, but thank you so much for the quick and seamless recovery and turning the experience around. That is what hospitality and the service industry is about. Thank you!

I just took a tour of the Mural Mile with Tjai and it was awesome!  We met at the Independence Visitors' Center and walked all over downtown looking at murals.  Despite the incredible heat, Tijai made the tour fun and informative.  She's both a tour guide and an artist, and very knowledgeable about mural techniques.  I would definitely take another tour with this group!

The customer service was excellent. Being black and walking into a restaurant where you are very different but you was still treated the same was amazing!!!! Our server was nice and answered any questions we had. My husband and I shared the brisket noodle soup and is was amazing with flavor and the price!!!! Please go support!!!

I happened upon Tea Magic while taking a stroll through Chinatown and was drawn in by the pictures on the menu in the window. I ordered the cucumber, celery, apple, honey juice and it was amazing! I also ordered some BBQ lamb on a stick, which was pretty good and only $1.50! I have since been back several times with mixed results, so I will sort of jumble the visits into one review. First of all....check your order when it's done! They messed up my order on three visits, by forgetting an item or two. Once, they forgot an item, then said they would make it for me, but totally forgot about it and after waiting over a half hour for it, I gave up and left without it. Plus, their juices are very inconsistent. You can order the same drink, but it's different everytime, both in taste and texture. They are, also, very slow at getting your food and drinks ready, even when it's not busy. Their octopus balls are out of this world and because of them, I overlook the bad service!
The banana pudding is absolutely amazing! This is a place that I have dragged many friends into who have become fans!

He is a hair magician! I am from out of state and could not find a stylist in my state that knew how/was comfortable with giving me a perfect ashy blonde on Asian hair. Kevin and his stylists are experts at this. They were very hospitable, even offered me cookies and water after being in chair for 7+ hours, and did a conditioning treatment to offset any damage from my previous heat styling. Go to a specialist and go to this salon!

Tip: one thing to be aware of is that it won't be Kevin who works on your hair the whole time. There was another talented stylist who did most of the coloring and foils while Kevin oversaw the work. He did the cut and styling. I did not know this cause I thought it would be Kevin the entire time. I did not mind as they all did a good job but just something to keep in mind.

This place has been a staple of our Chinese food fix, not because it's the greatest tasting but because it's in walking distance from my house and has awesome prices. 

The hot and sour soup....average.  The General Tso's....average.   Beef and Broccoli...average.  But it's just enough to satisfy a Chinese fix on the days no other food will cut it.  When I'm hungry for hot, consistently descent Chinese in the afternoon, I'm calling China House and ordering the L.32.
Terrible service, food is OK, over priced, waiters were rude and acted like I was on another planet when I asked for an itemized receipt. Do go here.

After finding Standard Tap on the Philly Magazine top 50 list, we decided this was on our to-do list.  And you should stop by also.  The menu is not extensive but what we had was fantastic.  There is a large beer selection.  We started with the octopus appetizer that we wished was available as an entree.  The burger was very good but I have had better.  The swordfish and shrimp was very tasty and not a morsel was left behind.  The biggest (and only) problem is the acoustics.  Even on an early Saturday evening, the background noise was bothersome and this restaurant is better for a group than an intimate meal.
Popped in solo on spur of the moment last night. Had the veggie gyro.  Very good.  Food appears to be of good quality and freshness.   Nice, friendly service.  Good addition to the neighborhood.

Great lunch by the office.  Walked over to this clean, big and well organized operation.  Ordered and was on my way in less than ten minutes for take out.  The Pho Chicken was delicious.  Filled with tons of meet and veggies with a tasty broth.  Cilantro, jalapeños and bean sprouts added great flavors.  Noodles were plentiful and hit the spot on a cool spring day.  Worth a try.

how could you not love this place? They've really turned this large, urban, historic indoor public back around after some down years. It's been thriving with lots of tourists, conventioneers, and locals. Most everything you'll get here is delicious. 

They've previously had a cheesteak venue that was horrible get replaced, which made me feel good because out-of-towners shouldn't have a horrible first cheese steak. Great selection of foods in separated stalls. There's also some diners (Amish one is my fav), produce markets, deserts, fresh meats and cheese shops, some specialty and foreign goods. It's also very close to Chinatown and it's markets. I've seen Food Network special events shot here and culinary exhibitions. 

A few of the shops and diners have small seating areas, mostly at counters. There are also a good number of public tables in the middle with a high turnover rate. This place has people in it all day long but really is packed around lunch time. 

There is some metered street parking and nearby expensive lots, some reasonable a little ways away, but lots of foot traffic here from center city business district, convention center, and hotels. 

You should have no problem finding something delicious to eat or cook here.

Ordered takeout. Took them over 30 minutes to make pork potstickers?? 
Everything was VERY overpriced. Lamb skewers were over $10 for six small skewers with barely any meet on each. Chinese chives pie was SO SO SO bland, absolutely flavorless. They were also poorly made and were falling apart.
The pork potstickers were pretty good. However, definitely not worth the 30 minute wait after all other items were ready.

We wandered past this place during a stroll on Passyunk and thought it'd be a nice stop for some drinks. Sure enough the drinks were delicious. Unfortunately the service was god awful, severely rude, and amateur. I haven't left a place with such a bad taste in my mouth in quite a while. It is apparently a crime to order drinks and not food. The punishment for this crime includes being patronized sarcastically, being ignored, and having your bill thrown in your face. I'll tell you, there are a hell of a lot of places to eat and drink in Philly and I definitely do not recommend this one!

It's nice to find another quick stop place for bfast sandwich and coffee or small soup and salad. Food is good with lots of variety for Vegetarians and Gluten Free. 

You can sit indoors or outdoors or just order to go. It's located on a pretty little street.

I've never gotten an actual meal here, I pretty much come here for the pastries (which are solid, the same ones that a lot of coffee places use for their pastries) and nothing else. It is a bit ridiculous to pay 2.70 for a pastry you finish in 4 or 5 bites, but it's really good. The coffee here is (apparently) good (I don't drink the stuff) and it looks like their actual food is really overpriced. But whatever, the experience that I've had here is pretty solid. Always nice to stop by in the morning for a treat.

Some complaints: their hours are terrible, especially over the summer. Basically whenever there aren't annoying penn undergrads around (any of the holidays) the place is closed any time you'd want to go. Including weekends and mornings till 9 am.
My favorite ramen house! Highly recommend their signature ramen soup. Very flavorful.

Love this place! Staff was friendly. Food was delicious. Way better than your average airport food. After a long flight, this was a welcome addition to my layover. Food is prepared fresh, onsite, unlike many airport food stops. Pork sandwich was on point. You can tell they make everything FRESH to order. Will definitely stop here again.

Recently I went to Philly to go on a first date with a fantastic gentleman. He told me the burgers here were super and he wasn't lying. I played it safe and got a bacon cheeseburger and it was so yummy. I wasn't a huge fan of the fries but they were still alright. The service was stellar too. Our waitress was personable and awesome. I would drive back to Philly for this place!

This would be another go-to place for me. This is one of the cheaper places in the area, and the food is good. As far as I'm concerned their cheese steaks are just as good as any place else on South Street, if not better.
I've been going here since I was a kid and I *almost* refuse to eat pho anywhere else except my grandma's! Amazing staff/owner & the best pho.

This is my favorite brunch spot in Philly. The menu is always exciting because they add specials with novelty ingredients (for example, captain crunch French toast). The restaurant has a hipster-y feel but everyone is welcome. The only downfall is the long wait to get in because they don't take reservations. I would love it if they could add some sort of waiting area with seating. However, this place is worth the wait once you get inside. The red velvet pancakes are awesome and massive; they could probably feed at least three people

Great roast pork since I been going here. Honestly this last visit was a lil dry,, maybe they are not making them wet like they normally are cause there doing only pickup cause of the pandemic but this will not stop me from going there

I went here for brunch this past weekend and had a great experience. I ordered the quiche Lorraine which was incredible and perfect in every way. It came with delicious crispy potatoes and I also ordered a side of candied bacon. Highly recommend getting candied bacon while you're here- it's incredibly delicious. 

Service was good. I think the wait can be long if you don't have a reservation (we did so that wasn't an issue). The outside seating is limited but cute. Indoors is bigger than you think. 

I don't live locally but I would definitely come here often if I did. They're also byob and have full coffeeshop capabilities. I had a delightful soy latte with my meal!

Its always funny for me (as a meaty) to partake in a veggie friendly eatery since I have the fortunate (or unfortunate) knowledge of what meat tastes like.  With that being said, I do like to indulge in the fake meats occasionally.  

I split a cheese steak with my buddy Blake and then followed it up with a slice of the Yukon pie.  The cheese steak was good and I could tell the bread to "meat" ratio was heavy on the bread side, but as a carb lover, it did not bother me.  It is as solid of a veggie cheese steak as you will find in this city.  I was a little annoyed that the description did not make mention that it came with green peppers (and mushrooms and onions), but no worries as I just passed them along to Blake.

The Yukon pie is the shining star of this place.  Carbs on top of carbs....yes please!  The crispiness of the thin sliced potatoes on top of the crispy crust was perfect and the rosemary and salt seasoning was delish.  It was cold outside, and unfortunately inside too, but luckily this pie is meant to bulk you up for the winter months.  Good thing I don't live close.  

I would like to say that I would go back for other slices (they did look tasty), but somehow, I think I am in a monogamous relationship with the Yukon Slice.  Oh well, at least it is love.

I was nervous about waiting  a while-from all of the reviews, but we had burgers in front of us very quickly on this thursday night. The staff made it easy to get into the flow of the place. The burger was mighty tasty and cooked correctly for myself and my date. I love the toned down casual atmosphere of Mr. Flay's place, great concept. Kind of a hike for me, but definitely worth it.

If you book a room on the inside remember that if you open the shades, those in the courtyard can see in.  10th floor room with a window view are completely silent!  Love the smell of this hotel.  We are Sheraton owners and Le Meridien is a favorable choice.  Textures on the elevator walls, the lobby architecture and the staff are amazing.
Came here for the first time just to get a bottle of water, of which of course they didn't have, aside from their 'rejuvenated water' for $5. So I bought it and it was (sorry to say) disgusting.

Eh. Not a big fan of this airport. Security is incredibly slow - I understand that a lot of people go through here, but Chicago Midway and Chicago O'Hare are able to keep the wait down to a reasonable amount of time. Same for DFW. Take a cue?

The people who work here aren't the most chipper, either. I know airports aren't notorious for their friendly personnel, but PHL left a bit of an unpleasant taste. That being said, if you want to fly in to Philly, what other option do you have?

This little shop is great for unique gifts for friends and family and even yourself. My husband bought a liberty bell and bolt tshirt and loved it so much we went back and bought him another one in a different color. He always gets compliments on it and it's so soft I can see why he wears it all the time. I've gotten baby onesies that have cute patterns for my friends kids too and I known they're not going to be like any other gift they'd get from anyone else. The prices are a little high, especially for pillows and housewares, but I've yet to find designs that rival theirs so I'll probably back for some Philly themed pillows sooner or later.
This restaurant is excellent. The food is of good quality, great flavors all around and they even have a wonderful staff. Will be stoping by here again for sure.

Giorgio's is a wonderful little BYO located a bit off the beaten path in Center City. When I say little, I really mean that. There's only a handful of tables, so you're best off trying to make reservations in advance.

Having been there several times, I'm a huge fan, and it's my favorite Italian place in the city, by far. They always start you off with some bread and olive oil with garlic cloves, which (ignoring the breath effect later) is divine. Something very simple, yet oh-so-elegant and awesome. I've become a hands down fan of their spaghetti primavera with the white wine garlic sauce. To be honest, I don't think that it get's better for a vegetarian in an Italian restaurant than that dish. If you're a rabbit like me, get it. I promise you'll love it. Really.

Haven't really tried their desserts, to be honest, since I'm usually quite full by the time I've gotten to that point in the meal, but I've heard that they're all wonderful homemade-style treats, and the tiramisu is especially good.

The manager/owner (I'm assuming Giorgio himself) is always walking around in a non-obtrusive way and ensuring that everything is going well. You can really see the pride and passion he's got for his place, which is really warranted given the quality of what he's putting out on the tables. The servers themselves are fantastic. There's only a few of them, since the place isn't too big to begin with, and they're always on top of their game, ensuring that no glass ever gets to the bottom and that everyone is well taken care of. We've been to Giorgio's some 5-6 times, and they've recognized us every subsequent visit, which really gives you that cozy, family-run establishment feeling.

Tip: If you're planning on taking Septa to 13th/15th and then walking, that's a great option to walk through some vibrant areas of Center City and enjoy Philly, but be aware that the walk will run you 10-15 minutes easy, so factor that in!

Party of two made reservation via OpenTable. The first unfortunate thing to happen was that find Pau apparently change the reservation from 2 to1. Who does that?

We also made the reservation to sit at a high table seating. When we arrived we were seated not in what we asked for.

Service was prompt to come to our table to let us know about the happy hour specials and the menu options for the evening. We ordered two drinks that came relatively fast, and tasted the same. We ordered a vodka cranberry and the seasonal margarita.

Now the food... Choices are a bit odd including the "Japanese pizza", which apparently is far from what it is supposed to be. We also ordered the pad Thai which is not the typical noodles used but had the standard flavoring. Would not spend $14 on this pad Thai, which does not even include meat or shrimp which is an added expense. I would like to add that we ordered pad Thai with chicken, but we were brought a platter of that I without chicken. The other problem with our food is that we ordered a second entrée that did not appear until about 45 minutes into our meal, and only have occurred when I asked for it. Although the house staff gave us free truffle shavings, it does not make up for the lack of attention to our dinner.

At the end of the night when we asked for the check, they were various mistakes with the order total that the staff was nice enough to make the changes for. Overall would highly not recommend coming here because there is not even air-conditioning on a hot evening. Even the 10% yup checking offer is not enough to tempt even the hungriest of yelp.

It's really nice of them to let you taste as many flavors as you want before selecting. 

While a tad on the pricy side, I like getting my chocolate hazelnut and pistachio fixes here. 
Not many places in the city where I can find pistachio ice cream or gelato so I was pretty happy about that. Lol

Omg.  Great meal.  Amazing food. The cheese plate was too die for.  

Our waiter wade was amazing.

What a great race! I participated 9/15/13 and plan to again.  The weather conditions were perfect, there was a crowd of spectators supporting everyone along the run, and great goodies at the end of the race, even a beer tent...how could you go wrong!!??

The race course itself was fantastic...scenic through the streets of Philadelphia then along the river where it was mostly shaded and cool.  The people were courteous and fun.  Time went by quickly and before I knew it I was in the home stretch being greeted and cheered on by masses of people...what a crowd!!
Quiet BYO on the edge of philly. Food is always good and consistent​. The place is small but worth it. Inexpensive also
I've been to el vez a few times over the years. while the drinks and the ambiance are amazing, the food is standard at best. that being said, i will head back for the drinks on a weekend!

I haven't been here for food yet, but this is solely rating the bar and night life. The service was good, although the bartenders just seem miserable. It was pretty slow for a Friday night, which I expected in the summer. It was just a boring bar with a mediocre DJ. I'm not saying I won't ever go back, and I'm sure I just went on a bad night. Definitely worth a shot to check it out.

Great bike shop with fast and very good quality service that is reliable. I have never had to wait for more than an hour for simple fixes such as flats, break adjustments, new handlebars, etc. but I did have to leave my bike overnight for more complicated stuff, and had to wait a week to get the 'appointment', but they didn't make me leave my bike in the shop for that week, which is a big plus. The prices are very reasonable for the service. The staff can be a little bit snobby but mostly they are friendly and helpful. Also a good place to buy Jamis or Specialized bikes.

Chris and his crew did a wonderful job moving our daughter and her roommate!  They were on time, observed COVID-19 precautions, and completed the move early.  It was an in-city move, with the apartment having 3 stories and located on a city street where there was no available parking.  They handled it like pros.  We would use them again if needed and would certainly recommend them to others.  Thanks again guys!

We found this place by accident when I was reading the map wrong. It ended up being our favorite meal of our get away weekend in Philadelphia. My husband had the salmon and loved it. I had a salad with added grilled shrimp. Delicious!!

One of the biggest advantages to living in South Philly is the ability to savor the grocery overload that comes as a result of living within such close proximity to so many different kinds of markets. And while I typically just go to Hung Vuong to appease my rooster sauce vice (just $3 for the HUGE bottles of sriracha!), its also my go to whenever I feel the need to peruse ingredients of the Asian persuasion.

The one pratfall to it is that the meat and seafood section usually ends up having at least 6 items that either terrify and stare right back at me (or both!). But the produce section is decent, there's a good selection of condiments that go well beyond rooster sauce, ramen gets well elevated beyond what you had during your college power meals, and the tea and spice selections are pretty damn impressive. And everything is pretty inexpensive so you usually get those secret ingredients on the cheap.
Tasty creative dishes and wonderfully attentive staff. Loved the scallops, coconut rice, and crispy calamari salad.

I was out wondering around on a layover looking for a place to eat during COVID. Saw the lights of the tent and people while walking. I stopped and grabbed a seat outside yet inside. All I can say is FANTASTIC PLACE!  The owner was out and about chatting with the guests. Wait staff was very stand fast. Food was awesome. I will be back to visit with Giorgio again. Thank you!

We got 5 maki rolls and some mei fun delivered. It was good. Nothing fantastic but decent. The fish seemed pretty fresh and the only gripe I had was the seaweed in the miso soup had a weird texture to it. Prices seemed very reasonable for the area, too.

Wow.  I should have read these reviews first.  I went to H&P years ago on the recommendation of my PCP and saw one of their therapists.  After a while with them, I didn't feel like I enjoyed my visits so I moved on.  About 8 months ago, my regular therapist asked me to see a Psychiatrist so that we could ensure the medication I was on was the right one.  No one takes insurance down here, so I ended up having to go back to H&P to see one of their Psychiatrists.  I filled out the online form, and called and left a message and no one got back to me.  I emailed again, and they apologized saying they had an influx of people calling.  I was told the initial visit was $149, and the 15 minute follow ups were $59.  I was also told that they charge your card for $59 the day before your appointment in case you don't show.  I went to the initial visit an was charged $149.  Six weeks later I went to my 15 minute follow up, and they charged me twice.  Once for Hornstein @ $60, and once in the doctors name for $60.  I went for another 15 minute follow up and they charged my card for $73.  Then I had an appointment one day, and that morning I had terrible pain and I went to the ER.  I called the office Manager immediately to tell her, and I left a message.  Do you know they still charged me and didn't give me the money back?  I was in the ER for Christ Sake. Did I screw up your schedule for missing my 15 minute meeting???  Then 2 more visits where I was double charged. I have had so much going on lately, that I only recently looked at my EOB's.  They were submitting 2 codes, 2 line items the whole time!!!!!  One of the visits, I saw another Psych doctor because mine was away, and she ate lunch while I was with her.  My last appointment with my Psych doc, she asked me what my name was about 5 minutes into our meeting.  So, they were charging me for the outpatient visit @ $150. AND for an Evaluation @ $225!!!!!!!!!  I emailed the Office mgr, Barbara, who called me back instead of putting the response in writing, telling me that I actually owed them more money  because the $59 were only estimates!!!!   I PURPOSELY ASKED UPFRONT WHAT THE RATES WERE.  Their billing rep called my insurance company and told me the rates based on the fact that my deductible had not been fulfilled yet.  When I called the Office Mgr to complain, she said that there are sometimes additional charges, and the $59 was based on an estimate because they don't know what my deductible is.  UM, HELLO!!!! THE BILLING REP CALLED THE INSURANCE COMPANY AND TOLD ME WHAT MY RATE WAS BASED ON MY DEDUCTIBLE!!!!!!.  I am reporting them to the APA, I am filing a complaint with IBX, and I will take this as far as it can go.  Do not trust them.  Maybe the owner will write back saying she is responding while on vacation and that I should be lucky.  Sooo disappointed at being taken advantaged of and lied to by a place you should feel safe.

I love this place.  Deserves a 4.5.

I've been here 3 times, twice to buy beer from carry-out, and once for dinner.
The carry-out beer is great, they have a large selection of many craft beers that are hard to find by the bottle.  Russian River, yes!  

Once I had dinner here, we bought beer from inside carry out, and opened the bottles at our table sitting outside.  We also had draft beer too.  The server was great, so friendly and wonderful (I wish I remembered her name! Short brown hair)   I wanted raviolis, but she said it was spicy so I had to decline.  For dinner I had Salmon, was delicious in a pesto sauce.  My boyfriend had crabcake... being from Maryland I never order a crabcake anywhere, but I tasted his and it was actually pretty good!  For dessert we had some specialty items, I had some chocolate pudding that was to die for, and my boyfriend had banana creme brulee, that was pretty good, but it had some kind of chunky rice pudding in it too.  

Overall, highly recommend this place.  I heard the brunch here is the best, so I plan to come back soon for breakfast food!

Came here on a Saturday night.  Waited an hour.  Started with a sampler of a dozen oysters.  They were so-so.  The Arugala and Endive salad was large and quite good.  My companions both ordered the grouper with a chimichurri sauce.  This was delicious and expertly prepared.  I ordered the Fishermans stew.  I generally order this whenever it's on the menu.  The flavor of the broth was terrific - loads of fresh fennel.  There was not a lot of seafood in the stew.  Three shrimp, four muscles, a few calimari rings, and a small piece of fish.  It was mostly tomato and fennel.  Stews are hearty and this was more of a soup.  I pointed this out to the waitress and they brought out additional seafood - more shrimp and calimari.  I was full after this and was appreciative of that gesture.  Maybe this is just how the chef prepares this dish and if so, I would not order it again.  Ambiance is festive and service was excellent.  I would definitely go back.

I went to Anthony for a bikini waxing.  He was super friendly and made me feel comfortable.  The appointment was quick, but I don't mind quick because he got the job done.  Never once did he have to re-wax an area, which I think it the worst.  His price in Center City is unbeatable.  I will be visiting him again.

Sooo good! 

Service was quick and friendly. I had the Mama's falafel platter ($11) and it easily could've fed two people. The falafel is delicious--crispy outside and smooth inside. The cucumber/tomato salad, slaw, and hummus sides are also good, but don't really justify the price tag. You're better off just getting the falafel sandwich.

I chose my words carefully as I wish to fully express how kind and attentive the workers at VSE were to my concerns and my animal. Anyone who goes here must be in a bundle of nerves, and I am very grateful for their kind patience with me. Furthermore, they did nothing but shower my puppy with love and attention. They were all attentive, ingratiating, and knowledgeable about what steps to take. 
Such wonderful people made my day! Thank you Symphony, Megan, and Erica!

M and I were happy campers in our Delancey apartment. Oh so fancy on delancey. It's very fancy on old delancey! However, then our apartment was bought by AMC Delancey and Steve wanted to make our apartment even fancier. 

He was pushy and thought just because he gave me a ride to 30th street station once that we were best friends. Then we started to have problems with our internet. M was not so happy if he came home from work and could not play bejeweled. This went on. Well I thought I'd call my buddy Steve. I texted him because we were buddies right? Then he was like lets talk on the phone about this like adults. Oh Steve I don't want to talk I just want wifi! They finally got it figured out. 

We went from being able to pay our rent online to one time me having to run over to 8th and Arch and slipping it under the door. Not convenient especially when I used to have to commute on regional rail past the airport to work. 

M still lives in the apartment with his friend. Wade is still the manager for the property and still pretty inconsistent. I'm happy I don't have to deal with them anymore!

The BEST Pizza!  Wow, I am so glad I made the trip.

The red sauce had such an amazing flavor.  We got the Fungi mushroom pizza (no cheese) and wished we would have gotten it with the red sauce that was on the Haymaker slice but the garlic sauce was delic too.

I wasn't sure about the wings but the habanero citrus sauce hit the spot.  It wasn't super hot and of course it is not a wing but it was perfect for "chicken.

There were so many things on the menu I wish I would have had more time to go back.

"Delivery" is brought on a pedal bike so I can see why we were out of the delivery area.

The people were friendly and helpful.  The place is small and a nice hole in the all.  Is that an OXY?

It was warm without A/C but the night wasn't too bad.

The lemon vegan cupcake also hit the spot.

We lucked out and found a parking spot down street.  Best date night!

Would love to go back.

My boyfriend was like a child everytime we passed Bliss. He always points and states that "we have got to go there". We finally went...not really impressed. I had the lobster risotto-it was so dry. Certainly not what I had in mind for risotto. My boyfriend had the duck and I enjoyed it much more than my own meal. The service was good, but the later it got the more anxious our waiter got. We left at 10pm, which is a full hour before they close, but maybe our waiter had somewhere to be because he kept looking at us as we chatted after the meal. I did not hate Bliss, but I would not recommend it to friends either. In a city full of wonderful restaurants I can think of many more places to send them. For the price ($100+ for two people) I was disappointed.

The best spice shop anywhere. Enormous selection, excellent quality, great prices, and none of the pretentiousness found at other "gourmet" spice shops. They closed for a few months in 2010 thanks to a catch-22 battle with the city's Licenses & Inspections department, but thankfully they are now back. Whew! If you live in the area and have ever spent $5 on a crappy little bottle of supermarket rosemary, go here once and you will wonder how you could have ever been so duped.

The staff at this clinic are very calm and pleasant, and handle my nervous dog really well. Dr LaMon is so pleasant and honest, and she has appointments after 5 on weekdays which is helpful. We really enjoyed this clinic, so much that we keep coming back even though we moved to the other side of town and could find someone closer.

It's a Radio Shack.  None of the employees know anything about electronics, which is typical.  Fortunately, I can usually find what I need without their (lack of) help.  But the service here is particularly poor.  If you like listening to Spanish music blasting while the clerk sits on the phone with her friends, this is the place to go.  The only saving grace is that it's within walking distance.  Otherwise, I would go to another RS where the employees gave a semblance of caring.

This place definitely has it's act together.  I was window shopping and the sales people gave great customer service and let me test ride their bicycles around outside.  That was pretty impressive to me.  I was really happy with my experience.  Prices seemed standard but not too high.  Would definitely recommend.

I just went to Masaya for the first time.  Shay did an awesome job cutting my hair. I'm always really vague and unsure of what I want done and she was cool and just asked some questions to decide on the best style.  She took her time and made sure I was happy.  

The place itself is hip and modern and very comfortable without being insanely priced.

Skip. R2L is stuffy and overpriced. Food was great- but so is almost all food in Philly. Service was friendly but did not compensate for rude hostess and bartender. Enjoy Brotherly Love sights from somewhere else.

Perfect place to come in for a beer. Great brunch and happy hour spot. Very quaint feel. Love the ambiance. Must order the baked Mac n cheese! It's incredible. Definitely stop in if you want a tasty treat!

Restaurant week is your opportunity to try the best of the best, and that's exactly what I did! Italian food is generally not high on my list of restaurants however, when there is a special event or special price it's an opportunity to dine outside of your food comfort zone. Today Sunday 3/3/19 after enjoying a August Wilson play 'Gem of the Ocean' at the Arden Theater we were off to Paradiso during East Passyunk Restaurant Week.

This is a beautifully decorated restaurant with soft eggshell to deep brownish tones. Each table was set nicely with the appropriate settings. We had a window and bar 'view' and could see everything even as far as the open kitchen.

After placing our order the server brought us bread with a hint of garlic and dipping oil, I prefer warm bread this wasn't but, no problem. We both ordered Buratta, Parma prosciutto, roasted peppers, tomato plated over a light spread of pesto, absolutely delicious. Next Black Fettuccine (squid ink), Rock shrimp, lump crab meat tossed in a spicy tomato sauce AH MAY ZING!!! I ended with Warm Apple Crostata with Cinnamon Gelato it was  very good except the Crostata was not warm. The only sight negative was after eating bread etc., our table was not freed of crumbs. If they have a crumber tool it was not used.

All portions were fair in size and delicious in flavor.  We had a prompt, attentive server. Combined it's more than enough reason to stop in again at any time!

Went here for a birthday hibachi lunch on new years day. The food was good, the prices were fair and the chef was the most entertaining and funny that I ever experienced with hibachi! The decor in the main restaurant was nice and modern but the hibachi room would be nicer if it was similar style and did not have 100s of photos on the walls. Kudos for the ambient chill music they were playing. I will be back and plan to try their sushi.

Ordered lamb seekh kabab and chicken tikka masala. The portion sizes were good and rice accompanying the meal was great. The lamb was great. The chicken tikka masala was average. The restaurant should mention that the chicken tikka masala entree comes with peppers and onions mixed in. I would have considered another entree had I known. Would go back again, but ask for no peppers and onions.

What a great experience. We ate at Victor Cafe last night for my wife's birthday. She was happy with the choice. We arrived early and went and sat in their upstairs lounge. My wife and I had a glass of super Tuscan. The decor is a unique tribute to RCA records and Rocky. This is the restaurant that Rocky bought for Adrian in the movie. Very relaxing sipping a nice wine and listening to Sinatra in the background. Steven is the bartender upstairs And is friendly and knowledgeable. 

We were called promptly for our eight o'clock reservation. We were seated downstairs for dinner. Downstairs the decor is a mix tribute of black and white photos of the DeStefano family (owners founder of the restaurant), and Italian opera singers. Yes the novelty of this restaurant is that every twenty minutes a bell is rung. Then one of the wait staff tells a story of a particular opera song, and then sings it. Boy do they sing it. Beautiful voices that add to a great experience. 

The food is good. For appetizers we had the calamari and eggplant croquettes. These croquettes were amazing. I had the fish of the day, Branzino, and sautéed spinach. It was good. The fish had a crispy skin, moist on the inside, and well seasoned. My daughter had the fradiablo. She said it was delicious. My wife had a stuffed chicken with creamy polenta. Sadly she did not care for it. For dessert wife got the creme brûlée's, and I had a cannoli. We all had cappuccinos. The staff brought my wife her dessert with a candle and singing happy birthday in opera fashion. She stated afterwards she only wants to be sung to in opera for now on.

What a great experience. If your in south Philadelphia check out Victor Cafe. You want be disappointed.

The worst bar I've ever experienced. Service was horrible. Felt like a burden to the place when trying to place a drink order. Bartenders walked passed my husband and me at least a dozen times without acknowledging our presence. Do not waste your time if you expect decent service.

After eternally searching for my car with the right spec, I luckily found it on carvana. It is a 2019 Dodge Charger scat pack with 5k miles. I was at first skeptical of buying from carvana as they were good and bad reviews. But the deal was too good not to give it a shot. So we ordered it to this location. Everything went over smoothly in the ordering process, but the one thing I will say is that a lot of cars get "reserved" on the website due to people clicking the get started button, which initiated a lockout for other people attempting to buy the car. But besides that, the people on the carvana help line were very helpful with the whole process. We arrived to the lot in philly and I must have just been so used to being swarmed by car salesmen that I felt we weren't being attended to. But a minute later a carvana employee sat us down and explained how the process worked and we were able to retrieve the car for the machine. The whole process from us arriving to driving off was only about 50 minutes which was so relieving. 

In all I highly recommend carvana and the easy and simple car buying process the provide.
Small, limited menu, but everything on the menu looked great and our food was delicious! Quaint and charming restaurant. Not sure if I'd go back.

Wow!  After the night I had at Tashan, I had to write a review. I recently bought the Living Social deal for the 5-course tasting menu for two. It was a great deal, and having been there one other time I figured why not.  My boyfriend and I took our neighbor -  we had the deal for two, and he ended up buying the chef tasting menu for $60 off of the menu to make things easy.  

This was the BEST deal I have ever gotten. The food just kept coming.  Even the menu price of $60 is more than fair for everything you get.  They gave us 3 courses of small plates to share for the table, but honestly we thought we got enough for 5 courses with just that. Then we had 2 large entrees to split, and to finish things off we each received a trio dessert sampler.  AMAZING. Everything was very tasty - the scallops were the biggest we've ever seen.  The menu was slightly different then the last time we were there, and it can be a tad intimidating... another reason to get this chef tasting, they plan the meal out for you. You will not leave hungry or dissatisfied! 

We had the NICEST waiter.  He told us he was new, but it didn't matter, as far as a service standard goes, he was great.

This place was awful. The service was terrible, the food was bad. The bread they brought was totally burnt. The "veal medallion" was extremely thin, maybe it wasn't veal? The fresh mozzarella was definitely store bought. The pasta was all bland. The worst part - the servers took forever and were totally unapologetic about the bad service. But, to be fair, the water is excellent. We were scared of ordering dessert for fear of getting a pineapple or something! The manager gave us a hard time about getting separate checks? He is lucky we even paid.... Gordon Ramsay where are you?

Visiting Philly for a few days and looking for a good cheesesteak. Yes this is a pizza joint. But it was close to the hotel and it was raining so thought I'd give them a try. Considering this is not a Proper cheesesteak place I was pleasantly surprised. Although the meat was not plentiful it tasted decent. Their fries were nice and crispy. Service was great. There are better cheesesteak places but for what it is I would not pass it up in a pinch.
This place is amazing! It's a diamond in the south Philly area. The drinks are dynamic and delicious. The food is on another level! Try the tortas or the empanadas :)

Great beer, good burgers. The perfect spot for a casual dinner when the goal is to drink a lot and eat American food.

The beer list is really impressive. I have enjoyed trying new beers here. When I ask for recommendations, I nearly always end up with a beer I have never tried before but end up liking. Be adventurous.

Be warned, the wait on the weekend is absurd. They don't take reservations, and you can easily wait an hour for a table on a busy Friday or Saturday night. Then again, waiting while drinking some exotic beer is not exactly a terrible thing.

Mmm pizza. Forbes was really good. The basil and smoked pepperoni gave a good depth of flavor.
Henrietta was delicious. I love sausage on pizza. Nice crispy crust.
Such friendly service!!!!
Probably the best pizza in that area. But I live in center city and I'm not sure it's worth the hike after the first try.
Definitely must go at least once to check out all the pizza memorabilia and hipster setting.

They put REAL cheese on your Vegan pizza! DO NOT GO HERE IF YOU ARE VEGAN!

I have been vegan for about 1 year. I cannot compete with the owner's track record of nearly 30 years vegan, though. That is what makes this so confusing. Why would he sell pizza with real cheese claiming it is daiya?? 

For those of you unaware, daiya is a plant-based cheese that is really high quality and tastes amazingly like real cheese. Though the taste is decieving, the appearance is not. It does not melt or brown. 

I have made my fair share pizza and mac n' cheese using daiya.., never, not once has the cheese browned like real cheese when it has been baked. Not when i've baked it 20 minutes, not when i've baked it for an hour. My boyfriend and I ordered our pizza, waited 20 minutes for it to cook, and then picked it up. 

We sat down and began to eat. I looked down and realized that the cheese I was eating was stretching, and was crisped brown on the top. Plant based cheese does not brown or stretch, as it is not real cheese. I started to feel very nervous and scared I was going to get sick, since I have not eaten animal products for a long time. 

We took it back inside, and asked the owner about it. He flat out lied to us and said they use daiya. That was absoultely not daiya. The fact he is vegan and is claiming to have vegan pizza there is flat out disgusting. Luckily, I am not allergic to dairy and felt pretty normal after the few bites I had. 

I am very upset that someone would do this... the owner is probably passing off real cheese as vegan to save money, since daiya is expensive, running around $6.00 a bag. 

I'm not even mad from a financial POV, what had happened was just disrespectful and confusing. This man is a total fraud. It is hard enough being vegan; not having many options to eat or  dine out, on top of being subject to ridicule from non-vegans... The fact that this was even an issue in a community of like-minded people who are supposed to be supporting each other is really hurtful. I have never felt so disrespected  Please do not buy from this man, his only objective is financial gain.

Chey's Kitchen, Center City, Philly. My favorite thing about Chey's is Kurt the owner-operator putting his 27 years culinary skills and love into everything that goes across his counter. Next time I am at Chey's I will be getting something with sauce (Kurt made 6 hr slow cooked), cole slaw (looking at it you can see Kurt's love), Mushrooms (too die for) and a smoothie.

I love Amtrak for it's reliability and flexibility.  As a frequent traveller of the northeast corridor, I love the option of taking the Amtrak even if it is sometimes more costly and time consuming than the alternative plane or bus or car. The quiet car is quite relaxing and condusive to reading, sleeping, working, or enjoying the  beauty of the connecticut shoreline to the industrial marks of the mid atlantic states. My favorite trip is DC to Philly because it is so quick.
Great atmosphere, great service and delicious food! The maki rolls were great! Must visit!

Worst service ever. Waited for two and half hours for the food to come. Once it came it wasn't even worth wait. Wouldn't come back due to food and service. I understand if you can't handle a large crowd but then other wait staff should step up and help so that guests are taken care of. Not to mention there is no AC so you are sweating during your 2 hours of wait time.

I tried out the Khyber Pass Pub last week with the boyfriend before seeing a movie at the Ritz. We went on a Monday night around happy hour ($2 off drafts!! :D) and the place was understandably pretty quiet except for a few groups eating and a small crowd at the bar. 

I was very pleased with the music, which was a good selection of classic rock-- quite a few songs I heard at KPB are somewhat obscure and don't get played on the radio much, so that was a plus. I was only unhappy with the volume on the music; at times, it was too loud and I could hardly hear myself or my boyfriend speak.

The beer: large and varied list, with many less well-known brewers. I ordered the Green Flash Grand Cru (mostly because of the name) and I was not disappointed. It was a little darker than Flying Fish's version, but it went really well with my dinner.

The food: my BBQ brisket sandwich with a side of fries is the reason why this pub gets five stars. I am no BBQ connoisseur, but this sandwich was the BBQ I've ever had. The meat was tender, juicy, covered in the smoky BBQ sauce, and was piled in four layers on my bun. The bun itself was buttered (huge plus) and there was a glorious amount of horseradish on the side that I could spread on my sandwich. The fries were quite good-- nice and crispy without being hard shells, and with the right amount of fluff. But the most amazing thing on the table was the boyfriend's side of mac-and-cheese. It was heavenly, I tell you. We asked the waiter what cheese was in it and he returned to tell us that they used a bechamel sauce. Oh God. I could die. I've had the best pub mac-and-cheese on this earth. Seriously. Go try it.

Overall, this place was really cool, with great food and beer and friendly service to boot. I hope to come back soon for more and in the meantime, this is definitely going on my favorite bars list!

This place was really nice. The staff was very friendly. They gave a nice neck massage towards the end, which felt amazing. The only downside I found was that the nails chipped after a week and I didn't like that. I went back to my go-to nail salon and got a new set of gel mani. I wish it would have lasted longer than a week though.

The best hand drawn noodles! So fresh and delicious. The restaurant is very busy but turns table quickly. I think it was worth the wait. They only accept cash. They give very good portions for the noodle soups. I felt the noodle soup was simple yet flavorful. You didn't need to add any sauces and could just enjoy it as it came. Price is very reasonable. Only $7-$8 for a bowl.

Love this spot! Definitely looking forward to returning once we're all allowed to leave our houses again. Booking is easy and John has always done a great job with my hair. Great down to earth and casual atmosphere to the place -- feels easy to get into a conversation, or not, depending on your mood.

Went for the first time and I can 100% tell you this place doesn't even deserve one star. Total disaster, from dirty plates, forks , stincky carperts and to the most rude service ever. My server was one of the worst I have ever met in my entire life. She was putting whipe cream on the plate and some of it was slipping outside the plate and what she does is starting getting the whipe cream with her fingers and licking it. Seriously?????
