Didn't even wash hands afterwards. Didn't even try to say sorry. Keep in mind this is my first review and usually don't even bother writting one but my experience was so bad I couldn't resist.  They need to close this place asap!  Do yourself a favor and don't go, trust me!
three cup chicken and cold sesame noodles were amazing. I don't know what the other reviewers are talking about because the service was great!

Gunna make a slight update on my previous review for this place:

Donuts are still fantastic and I've returned multiple times since the first time I came here in the summer. I've kind of went through a full circle of all their flavors at this point and I think I've decided that my recommendations go like this: if you're going to be eating them right away always go for the hot and fresh flavors over the fancy. Of the hot flavors, I think my favorite is by far the strawberry lavender. There's just something about the hot donuts here they do so well from the fry and sugar in the outside to the warm fluffy interior it's literally the perfect compliment to a hot cup of coffee on a cold winters day. 

I've recently tried their chicken for the first time and can safely say I've been a fool for sleepin' on the chicken options here. The fried chicken sandwich gonna make a grown man cry over a sandwich that's the extent of the flavor profile on this suuuper simple sandwich. Like literally just bread, chicken, pickles, and some good good sauce they call rooster sauce. 

Anyways, you don't need me to tell you how good FedNuts is, just ready the thousands of reviews on this place! If you haven't gone yet and you're in the Philly area I would make a beeline for this place asap.

Superb pork tacos!!  Okay, it's not fine dining but 5 stars for food truck food.  They were at Love Park today and I wolfed them down like nothing.  Love the food trucks, we're so lucky in Philadelphia...
Been here numerous times. Buck a Shuck Mondays is the best.

Overall, great sushi for the price.  The service left a lot to be desired though. We were there at 6pm on a Saturday night so I get that they are busy. We had to ask like 6 times for lemons and it took a very long time for our sushi to come out- almost an hour.

Also, we got the ninja donuts as an appetizer. There was no description but I thought they were sweet potato and chicken based on a review I saw here. Most of them were sweet potato and either chicken or fish but one was a scallop. I can get really sick eating scallops so I am very lucky I wasn't the one who bit into them. 

The rolls were very tasty though. They aren't huge rolls by any means but for the price, they were great.

Very impressed by Holistic Home and their work on cleaning our house! We wanted an initial "deep clean" of our house as a spring cleaning. They went above and beyond, we keep noticing small details of what they cleaned. Our house smells natural and amazing. Will have to check out their products. And will definitely be having them come on a more routine basis, so worth it!

I had previously looked around JS and was impressed with the selection of avant-garde designers. Recently, I purchased a pair of Margiela boots. After arriving home, I noticed they were supposed to be discounted by $200 but I was charged full price. I figured it was an honest mistake.

I returned to the boutique two days later and explained the situation. The manager immediately acknowledged that they were indeed supposed to be discounted. She promptly refunded the amount and apologized.

I would definitely recommend this boutique for lovers of fashion, particularly those with a more experimental taste. The employees I've encountered were all friendly and helpful, and even when a mistake was made it was quickly resolved without any problems. Great selection that's carefully curated with helpful staff.

I must say I'm impressed with my ability to choose restaurants during restaurant week.  I'm never disappointed! This was true with Audrey Claire.

Appetizer: I had the pear and Gorgonzola flat bread and it was amazing. AND HUGE! My friend had the arugula salad which was good but nothing extraordinary

Entrees: We chose the lemon sold and the ahi tuna and shared some of each. They were really good.  We even enjoyed the sides which included couscous and green beans.  

Dessert: My friend and I both agreed that dessert was the BEST part.  We had the bread pudding and the chocolate cake! YUMMM

We were way to full at the end but it was totally worth it.  And the BYOB status is a definite plus!

When I am visiting Philadelphia at least 4 times a month, this WAWA is my go-to location.  The people working in this location behind the deli/sandwich department are incredibly accommodating.  There was an employee (I will update his name) that tried to help me order something that was an alteration to the menu.  We both could not figure it out but, he hung in there and made it happen.  His demeanor, kindness, intent, were beyond words.
When we were finally able to figure out the order Dee was behind the counter and made it happen.  All I wanted was instead of Tuna salad, Chicken salad.  She was so accomodating and apologetic about the order not being perfect.
These are the type of people you want working for you when dealing with the public.  This appears to be a difficult location but right up to the security guards I have to rate this 5 stars.  Well done!

I ordered on Uber eats so no review on service....The food was ready pretty fast. Wings we're OK...I would eat there again...My only real negative is that my fries tasted fishy like it was time to change the oil in the fryers...Other than that I would say not my favorite but I'll eat there again I'm sure.

Really good Thai. loved, loved, loved, the coconut soup. Pad See Ew was very good, and our favorite were the Thai springrolls....Going to try the cheesesteak rolls next time. Staff was friendly and courteous. Go to the building with the brown awnings and interior, that is the restaurant.

To start out with, I love their other location up in Fishtown.  And was super stoked to find that they were opening up another joint, and even better, it was less than a block from my place.  So we head out there opening weekend super excited and sadly were very much let down.   Service was slow, in getting us drinks, refilling said coffee, and bringing out food.  We were there for a good half a hour before we got food.  And again sadly, food was a let down, much less than what I expected based on previous experiences in fishtown.  So bad that couldn't finish my omelet and homefries.  

Totally understand that it was opening week and they were working out the bugs, but I've gone back a few times over the last few weeks and it is just not consistent.  Service, while friendly is hit or miss on efficiency, and the food is again sometimes good, sometimes not so much.  Soggy breakfast sandwiches, burnt toast, you get the idea.

I wish I could rave about it, and I hope that they work out their restaurant growing pains, but unfortunately, probably won't be back until then.
Just had my first visit to Ovations and I loved it! Kim did my hair. I showed her a picture of what I wanted and I'm very pleased with how my hair turned out!  I would highly recommended!

one of the best rolled ice cream places i've tried around here. unlimited toppings?!?!!? can't go wrong with that. the staff is friendly and there are a lot of options here. definitely recommend if looking for a good ice cream or bubble tea place in chinatown.
La veranda really is very good. My family has been going there for years and I never really appreciated how good it is. Lagostinos, Pasta, Steak. Had the best steak I ever had at La Veranda.
Amazing service AND food! The grand opening deal was also awesome! And they even have bubble tea. All my favorite things in one place, definitely coming back :)

This museum is beyond amazing...make sure you bring enough quarters cause this isn't one of those quick skip through type of museums. ( Yea to cover everything ya gonna need about a good 3 hours..stack ya quarters lol) I swear I felt like I was in the middle of an episode of Mysteries at the Museum and for only $15 all the stuff they have there the view and experience is crazy...I'm definitely going back without a doubt..

OK I know that there are already a thousand positive reviews for DSG and the Pork Soup Dumplings... but here's one more.

I've been a fan of those amazing salty little babies from the time that Sally [and her mom] starting rolling them at the original ghetto location next to the bus stop - ahhh, does anyone miss the days of having to step over the street urchins to get inside?!

Everything here is good - the brisket noodle soup rocks and will feed two normal people - but let's face it this place is all about the soup dumplings.  Not sure what's in them, but it is as addictive as crack - and there's enough salt to keep you thirsty for a day or two... but hell it's so worth it.

The new location is nice, but these days I'd avoid it on the weekend if possible - or the wait is considerable.  When you do get seated, order at least two order of soup dumplings, but not too much as they are not the same when they get cold - and most likely will stick to the bottom and rip open - and you will cry.
Total jerks.  I could fill this review with obscenities.  Their trucks need to be shut down.
I'm on a roll! Another cheesesteak spot that is official and well worth the money they're knocking on the door for top five for me. salute to this beautiful Cooper sharp cheesesteak

Food: good but not great. Soup of the day was awful. No flavor- could not describe the ingredients. Server offered to take it off the bill but did not.   Brought our entrees while we were eating apps.  
Service: waiter was cordial but very rushed. Our empty glasses were never removed until we asked. Never asked how our entrees were.  
Overall, not impressed.  I hate the way restaurants let you know what time you have to leave! I realize that some people will sit there all night but I don't like the way the check and dessert come together.  We pay over $100 for dinner and feel like we have to inhale our food! No wonder fewer people are going out to eat!  Also, if you are bothered by noise it can get very loud here

One of the most consistent places downtown with top notch food. Ok, the table was a bit of a wait, but the food was worth it.
I dont care what you order , be it fish or game or pasta, their food is in the top ten in Philly. Since we waited for the table they were nice enough to offer free dessert.
Wasn't sure with the change of chef's if the food would be still be as good as before , but no problems.
Not really a place for kids, like another Yelper wrote, but for adults it's wonderful.

I wish I could write Zero stars. Make sure next time you go here, you go to your monopoly game and get the get out of jail free card, bring that with you to the four seasons and that might help you get past all of the insane charges they throw at you on top of the terrible service.  I've come here a few times recently for a late dinner after getting back from a long flight. Last time, we order a quesadilla for an app, and my boyfriend gets a burger platter and I get a chicken finger platter. They put a 5.99 sharing charge on there for getting an appetizer to share ? Are you f--kin serious ??? I was tired, didn't want to make a scene so we just paid the bill and rolled out and even gave a tip because we are not monsters, it might not have been the servers fault, maybe that's just the shitty policy, but I'm serious that is just messed up. 

We will absolutely 100% never come back to the Four seasons diner again. What a piece of crap diner. 

It actually used to be so much better. I used to be pleasantly surprised at how friendly the servers and staff were. They made me feel positive and happy to be there and eating at their establishment. These last few times I've basically felt like I was being treated like a piece of trash that was abusing the staff. My boyfriend and I Were extremely nice and polite despite this offense with the 5.99 sharing charge ! How are we going to order three $10 plates of food and you basically just throw an extra 5.99 on just for the service we are providing of eating the food off the plates ??? You should be paying us that 5.99 if anything, what the hell? I can't even understand this business practice what is the purpose of it??? 

Don't go here. The food is sub-par (how do you mess up a quesadilla??). It's generally pretty dirty. The music they play is seriously awful, the lighting is weird. The service has gone extremely downhill over previous years. Everything sucks AND ON TOP OF ALL THAT they charge you an extra 5.99 just to eat your food that you ALREADY are paying for.

*mic drop*

Quick and efficient, smart guy, straightforward. Had a prepurchase inspection done for a car that I was working a deal on. John was able to find a few small issues and an overall opinion to move forward with in order to decide if I should purchase the vehicle. Easy to work with and honest. If I was in the area I'd definitely stop by for car work in the future. Easily a place to recommend!

This is God's sandwich. 

The pastrami is to die for. With cheese. On rye. With mustard. 

I go there once a week

Ok. I travel all over the country from NY to Los Angeles and everything in between.  I have been a vegan for almost 10 years now.  I didnt have a reservation and was a bit worried but got super lucky and a seat at the bar opened right as I got in.  This is hands down, undeniably the best vegan meal I have ever eaten in my life from appetizer to desert.  Not only that, but EVERYONE on the staff was so nice and so cool and so attentive to me- even the wait persons that weren't serving me!  It was really a great experience to eat there.  I can't not say how blown away by everything I was.  The food and the service were like nothing I ever experienced.  No pretentious anything here.  Down to earth and so appreciative to have me as a customer.  Plus the bar is amazing.  Definitely lived up to its reputation as the best vegan restaurant in the United States.  If and when I am back in Philly it's the go to.  The food is superb and so creative and cooked to perfection.  Wow.  Just wow.  Thanks Vedge.  You guys are the best!

I have never been in this place when it is packed. I was here Saturday for lunch and it had been awhile since I was here last. Service was good. This is a good place for burgers. I had a lamb burger which was delicious. My friend had a turkey burger which looked really good and she liked it. I also like that they give you the option of either side salad or fries with your burger. Don't get the coffee- it was rather bitter and cost $3.50. But then again, I didn't see coffee on the menu and this isn't the place that comes to mind when thinking of good coffee. But they have a decent drink menu. Good place for burger and drinks. I like the lunch here better than some of the other places in the neighborhood- while I love to party at Urban Saloon, Rembrandts is better for food. Also tastier and cheaper than Jack's Firehouse.
Made my move so easy! The booking was easy, the movers were efficient and careful with all of my items and the price was great! This was my second time hiring them and I highly recommend them.

After a special Yelp event here a few weeks ago, I stopped by to revisit my favorite food offering that night - a grilled cheese sandwich at the MIghty Melt.

But first, the Bourse.  I walked over here from South Street, which is a pleasant 20 minute wander.  For a late lunch, there was plenty of tables, the bathrooms are nice and clean and there were no lines. Can't vouch for prime lunch hours though.

I also thought it would be hard to hear anything, but it wasn't too bad in that big open space.

Glad the Yelp event reminded me that this is a great place for a meal.

Appearances are deceiving. Within the little pink ramshackle building of Tartes, the pastries created are the finest pieces of culinary art I've had the opportunity to savor. 
I went to Tartes on a whim; a coworker of mine got his board certification, and I wanted something nice to offer in celebration. On the word of a friend, I went to Tartes. Asking for a dozen a la carte tarts without prior notice is quite a task for any bakery. Luckily, they not only accommodated my request with speed and grace, but answered my questions about the flavors and packaged them as beautifully as if I arranged the order far in advance.
The flavors were varied, and equally phenomenal. The creamy decadence of the chocolate creme brulee, the warmly nostalgic pumpkin, the perfectly balanced apple-raspberry ensconced in a flaky, buttery crust pulled harmonies of 'ooh's and 'ah's from my coworkers. 
I ordered the lavender pound cake for myself based on the recommendation of other Yelpers and was not disappointed. The cake was delightfully light and heavenly perfumed throughout.
Overall, wonderful service and an impeccable pastry. Though I'm sure they are not be able to accommodate to every extraordinary circumstance, I'm thankful they were able to help me. Ask politely, and respect their decision.

Fogo de Chão Brazilian Steakhouse is truly quite awesome. Overall, great atmosphere, ambiance, cleanliness and customer service. Every thing in the menu looked wonderful, but I had to go with Braised Beef Rib Sliders, it was an amazing meal. The staff was really friendly and service is awesome. I can recommend that place enough, it's so relaxing and amazing vibes.

The environment is beautiful and all of the employees were extremely pleasant. I came in for a Moroccan oil treatment/blow dry and it came out great! I recommend Bryanna because she is quick and does an amazing job with even long tedious curls like mine. It was an amazing first experience and I will definitely return, a little pricey but worth it! They even offered me a glass of wine upon entering! So lovely. I will be going back soon as time allots. ASK FOR BRYANNA. 

One more tip- if you have really curly hair BUY THE WET BRUSH :-)

Opted for the interior detailing. It was a little pricey, but I needed a great cleaning. Car had dozens of stains all along the interior, smelled terrible, and had gunk jammed into all of the crevasses. They came to me and spent several hours deep cleaning the car. Looks brand new and smells great. Was definitely worth the price.

I wanted to splurge a little for my pre-flight meal. I had a Handsome Ginger cocktail, beef yaki soba and crunch roll. All very good; especially for airport food. As a I said, it was a splurge, but worth $57 + tip for one person. Service was excellent.

Update: I initially left a review that stated my frustration with a long wait we experienced on New Year's despite having a reservation. 

We went back tonight and we had a fantastic  time. Their management team reached out to us to offer us a dinner on the house to come back. We took them up on their offer and we were thrilled with the service. Our waitress Nichole did a wonderful job, and everyone was most kind. The food was delicious and we made sure to take good care of our wait staff. Will be back again and we appreciate the excellent service we received. Thank you!

Ok I went back for lunch today, and I have to say, I was quite impressed. We got the seafood pancake appetizer, and I had the Galbi (short ribs), and for $11, you got a healthy does of short ribs served over peppers and onions, a 3 piece Cali roll, 2 piece Kimbob (Korean roll), piece of egg custard, chilled noodle salad, fried rice, and two dumplings plus miso and all of the usual side dishes. It was A TON of food. WOW. It was also tastier and the sidedishes were more pronounced then last time. It was basically the same price as Giwa, but much more authentic,and much more food. Yes you have to tip, but most do at Giwa anyway. I am a believer. I think it is now on par with Pastoral.

Undergoing renovations and with cramped space, it was nevertheless easy to grab a seat at the window with enough room to spread out. 

First of all, I found the prices a bit high, especially because you only have two choices of size, 10 and 18 inch. Buying two personal pizzas would work out to cost more than one large, but the large is WAY too much pizza for two. (Around $20 for two personal pizzas as opposed to an average $18 for a large.) Toppings on the create your own can also add up quickly if you go for the less basic and more "gourmet". I wouldn't mind the prices as much if there was a middle option--even a 12" to split among two people--but there isn't. 

However, it is worth it, because the pizza is delicious and came out super fast--barely a wait at all. The crust is perfect, with crispy black bubbles, and the sauce was flavorful. Also they are really generous with the toppings, so the prices aren't as bad considering. The staff seemed pleasant.

It's not exactly close by but I'd say it's worth the occasional trip.

I ordered pizza from here through GrubHub, and I thought it was the best pizza I've had in Philly so far. I enjoyed having the option of wheat crust, and the sauce was sweet, which I prefer. The next day, I decided to stop by to order some food in person and to also compliment them on the pizza. The person at the counter seemed annoyed, and, when I mentioned the pizza being the best I had had so far in the city, I was told that I hadn't tried enough yet. Yikes! I order a special sandwich to go, and I waited for quite some time before it was ready. I promptly left with my order to eat it somewhere outside. It was delicious!

My advice would be to order delivery from here, either through GrubHub or their website. That way, you can enjoy the delicious food without having to deal with some questionable customer service.

Great place! Everyone was very friendly from the moment I walked in. Gianna did an amazing job with my hair - I'll definitely be going back to her. She even took the extra time she had to curl my hair instead of just getting me out early.

One thing to note: they don't do tips on a credit card so make sure you have cash!

most important thing to know about north bowl:

THEY HAVE THE MOST RIDICULOUSLY AMAZING AND REALISTIC VEGGIE WINGS I HAVE EVER EVER EVER TASTED. 

after craving chicken wings for the past 3 years ive been veggie, i have finally been able to get something so close to the real thing that i no longer have to crave chicken wings. amazing. they are well known for their amazing tots (i like the buffalo kind), but they should be known for these wings, too.

ok. wednesday's here - $2 tots and $2 bowling (after 10). there's always a  waitl ist though so you might wanna go early and put your name in. early as in before 9. there's a bar downstairs and upstairs while you wait.

the decor is nice, sleek, and modern. there's a big lebowski screen shot on the wall. great seating at the actual alleys.

and my second favorite thing after veggie wings: an oldschool photobooth for those cute date photos.
Amazing fish tacos and margaritas. The restaurant has a lounge atmosphere and is the perfect date spot. They have lots of specials. If you are looking for something different, this is a great place.
Best cheesetakes I've had so far in Philly. A little lengthy on the wait time but it sure is worth it. They give you a massive sandwich. WizWit Please!

Carnitas tacos were the highlight of my meal.  The meat was crispy and moist.  Also had a fish taco and a "tinga" taco, spicy pulled chicken.  All the tacos were topped with shredded iceberg lettuce and some sour cream.  This worked on the chicken and pork, but gave the sour cream gave the fish tacos an unpleasant flavor.  The fish was also not as fresh as it could have been.  I ordered all tacos "hot" but the only one that was more than slightly spicy was the carnitas.  The tinga could have been spicier.  Adding some salsa verde remedied this situation.

Chips, guacamole and salsa verde tided us over while we waited for our meal.  The salsa was on point, nicely spicy but not too much so.  Guac was tasty and satisfying and chips were fine.

Looking forward to going back and trying some more menu items.

We were visiting some friends in Philly and we all craved sushi and went here since our friends were regulars there. 

The restaurant has a cute feel to it and the staff was so polite. 

We ordered the Sakura, another salmon combination, and one additional roll; there were 5 of us and we were stuffed afterwards. 

The rolls were really good. There was one with tuna and avocado, I don't remember the name but it was my favorite of the rolls. 

The sashimi was very fresh and the pieces were nice and big, which is nice when compared to other sushi places. The Sakura had I think 8 or 10 different kinds, so lots of variety. 

I also ordered a drink, I forget the name, but it had raspberries and champagne or something to that nature in it. It was really good. We also split a large bottle of sake which was very smooth. 

The atmosphere is great for groups or dates. Overall, we enjoyed the restaurant.
I ordered a wiz wit and it was good, but I expected more from the flavor. I'd say it was an average cheese steak. I liked the options on the menu though.

Nice hole in the wall near the Magic Garden. Food and service was excellent, the only complaint I had was the atmosphere. Other than that I can't recommend this place enough. Go for lunch special combo, you won't be disappointed.
Sooo good! Definitely would recommend the red snapper and fried plantains. No photo because I ate it too fast!

I came here on the 2nd night it opened and it was amazing!! First off, the interior design was unique and beautiful. The restaurant is spacious and I loved how everything was set up. The food was great too - tasty and authentic! You also get good sized portions too (I ordered way too much food), which is rare in Old City. I cannot wait to go back and have my friends try it out!
Jaizelle is a gem. Not only did she do a beautiful job on my dress, but she went above and beyond to accomodate me. So talented, personable, and easy to work with; look no further!
Excellent diner and very friendly service. Took my family here for breakfast on Christmas and couldn't ask for better service. Eggs Benedict was delicious.

Definitely recommend!!! My girlfriend and I had a wonderful dinner last night. The food was absolutely delicious and the prices are fair. The owners were both in the restaurant when we were eating and helped us decide on what to order. They were both very kind and soft spoken. The restaurant is very small and cozy giving us a very personal experience. We ordered the rice balls as appetizers and could not believe how delicious they were. Our meals came out quick and were cooked to perfection. After wards we shared a nice conversation with the owners. I highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a peaceful delicious meal with friends or even alone. Cannot wait to go back!!!  BYOB

Love it! Beautiful lay out, amazing work that you can meander and see so many stages and phases of Rodin's development. Granted I am biased considering he is one of my favorite artists, but this spot has become my happy place. There are spots to just sit and absorb the work that are actually comfy, and you are welcome to sketch and there are books you can look at regarding his time and work. Its small, but very impressive still!

This is my favorite bar in Philly thus far. Dirty Frank's is everything you hope it will be. An amazing dive bar with amazing patrons. I found that the drinks were good and the people enjoying them were so willing to engage one another. I love a bar with that homey feeling that allows everyone to just enjoy themselves so much that they interact like old friends. This bar reminds me so much of my fave spots in SF. Yay Dirty Frank's.

I was really looking forward to trying federal donuts after hearing so much about how great they are. The donuts here are just ok. I didn't find them to be as good as what I can find back in NYC. 

Yes, they are made fresh and come served to you hot. They do melt in your mouth with each bite but in terms of taste they fell short. They have special donut flavors that change daily and they run our pretty quick. We were able to get in just before they closed and ordered a strawberry donut and an oreo donut. The strawberry donut had this really strong artificial flavor  that I really did not enjoy and it was covered in so much sugar. It was not good and one bite was all I could take of that one. The Oreo donut was a bite better. It had a good vanilla glaze with chunks of crumbled cookie so in a way the oreo cookie itself made the donut taste better. 

Overall, I think one experience here is enough for me.

I really hate bashing places, but this joint sucks.

Service was sooooooooo slow.  Like 25 minutes before take our order.  35 minutes before food gets there.  Outrageous.  Two people.  Come on.  My dining mate said same thing happened a week or two before and he left after waiting 20minutes without someone coming to his table.

The food was so sub-par, almost in just plain gross territory.

The nachos are worse than the ones I made as a kid.

The burger is absolutely horrible.  A pre-formed patty totally made from frozen.  It was grey/brown.  Way overcooked. 

The fries sucked.

The "parmesan nuggets" was literally chunks of parmesan on a dish.

And you may be thinking, "this guy ordered burger, nachos, parmesan, I shouldn't take his word anyway."  But trust me, it was the most exciting thing on this extremely limited menu.

I'll give two stars because the place was decent looking inside and I just feel bad for them since they're going to go out of business (because they suck).

Geno's... is the place good? It is a decent cheesesteak at best. Are their better cheese steaks out there and cheaper? Yes. If you are visiting Philly it is an iconic establishment that serves an iconic cheesesteak. As many folks have pointed out, lines are long, cash only. The meat on the cheesesteak is strangely cold. Not sure if this is just how it is or what. In order to go 4 stars or higher, the cheesesteak needs to be served hot! The hot peppers in the condiment tub actually adds the flavor needed to make this taste good. More or less a historical site worth visiting...once. Also a tip- this isn't located in a good side of town. While we were eating this lady in line was talking about kids throwing rocks at cars. Sure enough, we walked back to her car and her windshield had a foot long crack along with a few other cars parked on the street. Be safe- take an Uber :/
The rabbit nachos weren't nachos, or really that good. But the service was. Great lighting. Big bar and big bar lounge both have sweet views, not a bad place to come and have a few expensive drinks.

I went here to get my natural hair pressed and curled and I wasn't happy with the results. It turned out extra flat and the curls were really droopy. The stylist was also extremely rough with my hair and seemed to not know how to properly detangle. Also I waited a long time to be washed and also to be styled after my deep conditioner.

Incredible venue ! Tried many of the dishes and was not disappointed in any way . The menu is varied enough to please everyone . Service was outstanding . Save room for dessert ... You'll be glad you did ... cannoli was incredible !

My partner and I ordered from here tonight since jyoti was already closed.
In hindsight this was a terrible mistake.
The delivery was on time and the food was hot. However, this is where the positives end. 
We ordered 1. Vegetable samosas- which were fine and, considering the remainder of the meal, the highlight of our dinner 
2. Chana Masala- it was extremely watered down and lacked flavor. 
3. Naan- we have no idea how the naan might have tasted since we were given paratha with potato stuffing. The paratha was warm but somehow rubbery in texture.

At the end of the night, we were hungry so we settled on eating the samosas. 

I would not eat here again and would not recommend it to anyone.


Update- I was refunded for the naan and Chana masala

This is my new go to dessert spot!

I passed by Capogiro for years and just tried it last night. All those wasted years without gelato but now, I cant stop craving it! The Irish Coffee and Dark Chocolate are a scrumptiously delicious combo. I am gonna struggle with trying new flavors when my first cup was so good. I didn't realize the difference between froyo and gelato but for those who dont know gelato is much richer, creamier. 

I had dinner at Spice 28 and Capogiro being right around the block was too perfect. One small con, it seems this must to be a couple's joint. Kind of like Madame Puddifoot? They mostly have tables for 2. I recommend you come with an even number of people.
The ambiance is to die for ! Felt like I was back in Savannah. The omelet was amazing but the salad tasted like grass. Great for brunch with the girls

I don't understand all the low-star reviews of El Azteca. I guess some people love being so negative and picky, or they're just the typical complainy Republican party of No. Anyway, I have been coming to this place for about 10-12 years and it has never been anything but really good. My favorite is lunch special #6 which (if it's still the same now) is the beef burrito with melted white cheese, refried beans and side salad with guacamole. Everything else is awesome too. The San Jose Special enchiladas are exceptional, and so are the yummy fajitas with extra tortillas. The spices are just right and the flavor of the food pleasurably explodes in your mouth. Yes I agree with one reviewer that the Mexican decor is chintzy and paperific. That's why dishes are not $20 each. You go here to eat because you love Mexican food and this joint really keeps me anticipating my next visit and craving for more.

The amount of meat is sparse compared to the bread. The onion was bland. Know what you want before you step up to order and be specific about it. They asked my fiance if he wanted a drink to go with the meal and he said yes without thinking too much into it and feeling rushed. They didn't ask what kind and hurried him along to pick up. By the time we got to pick up, I asked what kind of drink he got and to which he answered I didn't even tell them but at this point we were just handed anything that they felt like giving us. Honestly I thought this was hilarious more than anything because I have heard about this "soup nazi" treatment from others. The one star is because the food just wasn't good. I've had way better cheesesteak from my college cafeteria.

Khyber is a gem in Old City. I've been here for brunch, lunch, dinner, drinks, And it's never disappointed! Their New Orleans style grub is fantastic- the PoBoys are a must have for new guests. They always have great specials and the beer selection is expansive. They are one of the few bars that carries my personal favorite beer- Mad Elf - during the holiday season. 

The divey atmosphere is comfortable and relaxed, not pretentious at all. The staff is always friendly! 

I highly recommend the shrimp POBoy, beignets, and any beers on the specials list!
This is what you get when you accept and order and then cancel it because you decided to close 3 hours before your stated closing time. Honor your commitments.

Came here after a not so great customer service experience at another spot nearby and was pleasantly granted a table without reservation for a group of 8.

I really enjoyed the mixed seafood + bean curd soup and dry-fried beef chow fun. Also, can't go wrong with fried rice, duck, and Chinese broccoli at a spot like this.

It's your typical family styled Chinese dining with the glass spinning centerpiece table. Service was quick as we came just before the dinner service picked up. Overall had a great experience. Would come again!

Awesome food and great decor. Old tavern style restaurant with great art and antiques. Waitresses look. Like models and have the personalities of them also. I swear she rolled her eyes every time we asked for something.  Really killed the experience

Amazing experience. :-)  We waited for an hour since it was a busy time.  We didn't mind waiting.  As it turned out it was really worth the wait.  I wish I can stay and try dessert.  The main meal took a long time and it was getting late.

Fascinated with the BYOB concept and obsessed with Mediterranean cuisine, I had been dying to try Audrey Claire ever since I moved to Philly. My boyfriend and I went on Saturday night and treated ourselves to the 3 course meal. We got the beet salad, brussel sprouts, soup, salmon and trout. It was all outstanding - rich, tasty and decadent. Definitely ant to go back as soon as I can!

I took my first Flywheel class last weekend (FYI-first class is free!) and had a great time. I'm still relatively new to spin, so it was tough, but also a lot of fun. The 45 minutes goes by pretty quickly and was definitely a good workout. No one leaves there without a good sweat.

Jackie played great music during class and was really helpful getting me started figuring out the right seat height, etc. She gave very clear instructions on when to adjust torque and how fast we should be going.

Classes are a bit pricey, but I will probably be back at some point!
This place is way over-rated. Tasteless soup and salad. Mediocre steak.

Prior to arriving I had no idea the restaurant was totally vegan which was exciting to discover since I'm lactose intolerant! My boyfriend and I ordered a falafel sandwich each with a side of shwarma fries and both things were amazing. The falafel was super fresh and the seasoning on the fries was so great that we had to get another order to go! Matt got a banana tahini shake and loved it. Would definitely go back again!
$34.51 for regular oil change   They are honest and very reasonable! Super nice too! Totally recommend them

Saad's has the BEST lamb shwarma. The meat is tender and flavorful. It melts in our mouth. WARNING: you will dream about this sandwich leading to unshakable cravings. 

I have yet to have their chicken maroush, but their hummus, shwarma, and falafel are some of the best I have had. 

The owner and staff are very friendly and always willing to accommodate. They are usually busy so allow for about 30 minutes to get your food. 

Go here. You won't regret it!

Finally. I've been hearing about this place since I moved here. In my heart I want to give it a five-star review. But as is the trend with most restaurants I've been to within their first month or two of opening, they're always kinks that need to be worked out. The only kink I noticed here was the food; nothing really stood out. Don't get me wrong there were excellent dishes, but nothing you haven't seen or tasted before. 

The ambiance is fantastic. The Japanese television entertainment projected onto the wall a-la-Trestle Inn is a nice touch. Service was excellent.

The real star is the beverage selection. Do you have a great draft beer list, and their sake selection is fantastic. It's not often that you find dining venues that offer Nigori sake let alone more than one kind of Nigori sake.

Food: 3.75 (Tuna Guac, Hamachi Kama, Karaage Wings, Shogayaki, Pork Belly Skewers; sushi isn't the draw here. Go for everything inside the menu.)
Drink: 5.00

Go check this place out for yourself, it's fun. Looking forward to visiting again and exploring the drink action more.
Meat on philly cheese steak was really dry. Came here because the place across the street was packed, should have waited.

This little place on pine looks super hipster with kind of a Mediterranean/Middle Eastern-esque feel. The restaurant was filled with colorful couches. A couple of my friends stopped here on Sunday to try out their happy hour - the list isn't very extensive including $1 oysters, clams (which they were out of) and you-peel shrimp. They also had $5 buffalo tempura shrimp and $5 pork sliders. The buffalo shrimp was pretty good, but my friend who ordered the sliders said they were fairly dry and needed more sauce. After we quickly ate our food, we left and decided to finish happy hour elsewhere.

When we asked to try a flavor, I was told that they don't give out samples to taste. It's because they are common flavors and we know what they taste like. If that's the case I don't understand why there are so many ice cream shops carrying same flavors and some do well and some don't. Guess Bassetts is not unique

The vibe was immaculate. So cozy, yet classy. Got a window seat on the second floor and it felt like we were eating in my grandmother's breakfast nook. We started with the Parker House Rolls & Cheeseboard, very diverse cheeses and sides paired well with the bread. The Brisket Pasta was a home run according to my girlfriend. She housed it! I had the cioppino, it was good not great. Sauce was a tad bit on the watery side. That is my only complaint though, the rest of the experience was fantastic. They even treated us with champagne and a free dessert since we were celebrating a birthday too. Definitely coming back for another special occasion

I've been raving about the Lil' Pop Shop/Lil' Pie Shop to all my friends.

"I had THE BEST s'mores pie the other day," I say. "The graham cracker crust, the dark chocolate ganache, and the perfectly caramelized marshmallows. Soooooo good."

Yes. It's true. The s'mores pie is so good. But there's s'more than that. Har har. 

The popsicles are pretty good too as you'd expect, but it's also a surprisingly cozy and nice cafe to work at. You've got wifi, outlets, warmth, and usually enough space that you aren't fighting for it (although it does get packed every once in awhile). Oh! And they're open late, which makes it my new winter working cafe :)

Check out Lil' Pop Shop when you get a chance!

Definitely one of the best manicures I've received.  They use a brush to help wipe off any excess that gets on your fingers, which is the first time I've seen that (granted I haven't done THAT many manicures.).  I didn't notice any mistakes, and after a whole week, still no chipping at all (not gel).

They also give you an arm message before they do your nails and a neck massage while your nails are drying.

I was first introduced to El Camino when I met a group of friends there one evening. The food is exceptional for a bar, and I like the large open seating area and low lighting. There is also a bunch of outside seating, but I can't comment on it as I've never eaten outside here.

My favorite menu items here are the fried pickle chips (corn-meal batter gives it a really nice texture), the brisket sandwhich (on buttered texas toast/can sometimes be fatty), and the ribs! The ribs are done in a Texas style, great dry rub, smoked in-house, and no sauce. The bark on these suckers is delicious, smoky, and a little spicy, and the meat falls right off the bone. It was so good, I don't even remember if it came with a side, who cares? 

Also, if you are having a late weeknight, the margaritas are half-price on weekdays after 10PM. That's a pitcher (about 4 glasses) for 10 dollars. They are done in a traditional style, non-blended, and served on the rocks. Yum! I actually prefer them this way. You can get salt or sugar on your glass, but I like it straight up, nothing on the glass.

If you're going to visit El Camino I suggest a weekday as it seems to get very crowded on the weekends. I can't say for sure, but usually crowded places in this area get obnoxious really fast.

I live outside of the city but look forward to lunch time at work because they deliver to the hospital in university city. I never know what to order because everything is so good! "Pete's summer time drink" is amazing too! Especially now with outdoor seating I can't wait until I get a chance to go for dinner and drinks :)

Best pizza in Tacony!! Our favorite: New Station Extra Special Pizza!!
Delicious Greek salad too

I visited Jim's during my trip to Philly in July. Everyone says to try a quality Philly cheese steak. And so I did...

My first time there, I wasn't that hugnry (Haha). So i shared one with a friend. A must: get the steak with CHEESE WHIZ and onions. Very good combo. There was a line (like always) but it was good time chillin' with friends while waiting in line. It made the line/time go by faster. 

Its interesting to see how they cook the steaks while you wait in line. I don't know how those guys do it on the summer days...and fast! They must be sweating bullets. Props to them. 

There's seating upstairs...and plenty of it, which is a plus. People go in and out so fast, mostly likely you'll find seats. 

The cheese steak literally melts into your mouth. It was so good. The cheese whiz, cooked onions, and steak...went so good together. 

I actually went back for a second time later cause I had a craving for it. This time I had one for myself while a friend of mine had three (and finished it in less than 10 minutes. He's a monster.). Try the box ice tea with it. Real good and refreshing. 

I have a craving right now as I type about it...but I live too far and have no car. :( I will definitely go back when I visit Philly again!

Can't get enough of this place. Great beer (no tap and bottles / cans). Great food to go with the beer in a old school bar. Had the Good Dog Bar and it was magnificent.

Get the Good Dog fries as well (half regular/half sweet potato)

I came here twice in one week - once for the kick off of Philadelphia Science Week, and then another day just to see the Pompeii exhibit before it ended. 

To start off with, I used to LOVE coming here for field trips in elementary school. It was always a lot of fun. Now that I'm an adult, yes, it's sort of lost its magical appeal - there's nothing here that I didn't really know already (I'm a huge science geek and I watch a lot of documentaries on Netflix)...but it's still a nice way to spend the afternoon. Except...EXCEPT...it is really expensive! Just to see an IMAX film it was super expensive, ouch! And the movies are about 45 minutes long. 

We watched Titans of the Ice Age...a film about mammoths. It was alright, I guess. The Pompeii exhibit was nice, but they packed us in like sardines and I have extremely bad anxiety issues so that was not fun for me. Evidently that's how they run all their special exhibits? They pack people in? If this is true then I guess I won't be checking out more in the future, which is a shame. The exhibit was neat, but not as interesting as I was hoping. Lots of old tables, vases, paintings. Interesting, but not as interesting as the plaster citizens. There was a pregnant woman - sad! And a few children - also sad!

Loved the movie they showed before we entered a particular area of the exhibit, depicting the city within a span of 48 hours as Mount Vesuvius rained down ash. They even had a fog machine going, which was pretty cool. 

The Science Festival party was cool. They had Yards Brewery supply a special brew, which was good, and some wine for purchase. The wine was good, too. I enjoyed the different stands that were set up - I learned from a nurse how to give CPR to an infant, learned about keeping bees, how to extract DNA from a strawberry using Bacardi. All in all, a pretty good time. 

Despite the fact that it's expensive and not as exciting as it used to be when I was a kid, I'd still like to go back sometime.

Last night was the last time I'll go to this Cava location.  I have ordered Cava from multiple locations before, including this one on Chestnut. 

The food station was messy and the staff, although quick, were not friendly or helpful. It was my boyfriends first time there and no one at the counter even attempted to help him order or describe his options on what he could get.  

We went at 7PM, which to me seems like a good time to have everything stocked up because many people eat at that time.  However, they were out of falafel and many of the salad green options were out.  Usually, this would not be a reason to keep me from coming back... 

When I got home and started to eat, I realized that the black lentils that I ordered were not fully cooked. They were crunchy and unpleasant to eat.  Felt like eating small rocks.  Lentils are small, so it was impossible to pick them out of my bowl. I wasn't able to eat any of it. 

Chestnut street Cava has lost a customer!

See pic attached for my disappointing dinner :-(
Found a place with great coffee, a great view, and great staff!!! On the plus they had almond milk. It always frustrates me when I go to get a coffee and they don't have milk alternatives.

Waiting for our table...at the bar.  Italo the bartender is such a sweetheart and makes a great superfruit lemonade cocktail.  We are excited to try their food, but first I had to mention how skilled and kind he has been!  Great first impression for this place.
Excellent ingredients. Staff were extremely efficient and knowledgable. Food was delicious. Nice dining room. Reasonable prices and BYOB. Will be back soon.

Been coming here over 20 years since college time when I was still a poor fob student from Hong Kong and it has always been great Chinese food if you know what to order. Sure it has to cater non-authentic food for the general public, but look for what rice/noodle patter loved by Hong Kong blue collar people you will find gem. 

Two excellent examples attached - braised beef curry with onion rice platter and beef and tomato with an extra silky egg on rice. Same great taste for over two decades. 

4ish star with some old time sake round up to five for support.

As an update, BCI is amazing for catering. We stopped at maybe 5pm on the day of our party to order a mixture of vegetarian and meat dishes. They promised delivery promptly at 9:30pm. 9:30 on the dot, and they were at the door with 6 full trays of piping hot food, plus a bag of fortune cookies and lots of (free) 2 liters of soda. We fed over 60 hungry people, plus had some leftovers for under $200. Yeah, it ain't a plated dinner service, but who doesn't want takeout Chinese when they've had a drink or three?
Free movie nights outside! Bring some wine and snacks and this is the perfect date night. Right alone the river. The ambiance is perfection.

Was totally disappointed in everything. We were seated right away. Server took our order. Roast Beef sandwiches, took at least 40 minutes to get them and we were missing our appetizer and the sandwiches were missing things and the roast beef was dry. Never coming back.
This is our favorite place. We went here many time already. The food was great ans the service as well. I will recommended to try. Give them a shot! You will love this place.

Huge portion, low price and fast service. Perfect for a quick bite at night. I ordered a lamb sandwich with tzatikki sauce. Bread could be cooked crunchier. They should also put more sauce because it was quite bland. I end up putting lots of ketchup on it. Lamb itself was great.

Went to Sweet Lucy's for the first time on a Monday night, during which they have an all you can eat bbq buffet. I didn't know what all that would entail, but they had everything available! Pulled pork and chicken in their signature bbq sauce, brisket, ribs, and a wide variety of sides including a very creamy mac and cheese. They even had dessert - a seasonal fruit cobbler, ice cream to make it a la mode, cookies and brownies (also offering gluten free options). The apple cobbler was the perfect end to this great sampling of bbq for dinner. I would recommend going on a Monday to try what they have and see what you like, it's well worth it!

If you look at the apartment ratings here, you'll notice they all have one thing in common: TERRIBLE REVIEWS

Since there are SO many negatives about this building i'll list them in order of annoyance:

1. Construction 24/7 from either the Comcast building or Sterling Apartment renovations
2. At least 1-2 elevators are down EVERY week meaning that the elevator takes a minimum of 10 minutes in the morning/evenings
2. Rent always increases by  $70-100 every time you renew your lease
4. Fire alarm goes off every week. I once woke up to the alarm going off at 4am and had to wait outside in the winter cold for 6 hours before they let us back in the building
5. The property manager (Tanner Sheehan) doesn't give a damn about the residents' concerns
6. Utilities are split according to apartment size so even if you don't use any Heating/AC you will still pay over $100 in utilities
6. When you move out you'll receive literally less than 10% of your security deposit back due to "damages" and "stains"
6. I've founds cockroaches in my bathroom numerous times

As a resident of this building for 4 years I can honestly tell you that this was the worst living experience imaginable. The only thing nice about this building is the location and honestly had i known what a nightmare I was walking into I would've gladly paid an extra $200 to live somewhere else. Living here is a HUGE mistake and every resident who has lived here will tell you that. Personally, I think all the leasing agents should be required to hit every potential resident with a baseball bat so they can truly understand how painful it will be to live here!!
The staff is super nice and very helpful. I will only go there from now on. Had an issue with getting my title from my finance company and they were helpful and patient.

I've gotten takeout from here at least 6 times this year and THAT'S saying a lot because it's just march. I've gotten spring rolls, lemongrass soup, pad thai with tofu, red curry with tofu, etc. The great thing about this place is that you can choose your PROTEIN. You can add or take away veggies if you don't like certain ones or if you want more sauces. So it doesn't matter if you're vegetarian or non vegetarian, you can get whatever floats your boat. I get takeout through GrubHub. The service is so fast and easy! They're also very accommodating if you forget to order something through GrubHub, if you call the restaurant they add the item on without an issue. App + Entree doesn't cost me more than $25 which is wonderful! Plus I get plenty of leftovers. They pack everything very nicely for takeout too!

The atmosphere of this place -- chalkboard with the menu specials, sunflowers, green apples, light, open, and airy reminds one of the feel-good, save-the-earth vibe of Ikea.
Inside it gets very crowded and noisy. Very hard to have a conversation without shouting and trying not to listen to your neighbors'.
The best bet is to sit outside, and look at the lovely Spruce St. brownstones, while dining on flat breads and the like. 
The food is pretty good, interesting (have had ostrich as a daily special) and the waiters attentive.

Kinda cheap, and not in the good way. No rice with delivery orders. Be warned. Employee at restaurant was not helpful when I called to ask where is the rice. Only Indian restaurant I've ever seen do this... And again,, not a good thing.

Easily in my top three (if not #1) favorite breakfasts, ever.  Excellent sweet and savory options.

I loved the pecan pie French toast and the family all gave rave reviews for the red velvet pancakes, cookie dough French toast, and short rib eggs Benedict.  

We didn't skimp on the calories, but there were healthy options, too.  Only in town long enough for one breakfast here -- boo hoo!

I stayed at this hotel for three days. The fire alarm went off around 9:00 a.m. on September 24th and the loud speaker message directed us to the stair wells. No further information was shared and some people in the stairwell we were in were upset, crying, etc. We proceeded to walk from the 21st floor down to the 1st floor.  When we got to the lobby we saw people upset, crying, etc. Guests did not know what was going on and some peoples family members had not made it down to the 1st floor and/or they had not heard from their family members. Then they called an all clear so people started heading back to their rooms. Then about 10 minutes the fire alarm went off again. Crazy!!!!!!!!

I had had two very disappointing visits to the T.G.I. Fridays which is connected to the hotel.  Two days in a row the same issue occurred at the T.G.I. Fridays. On the Friday of our stay, every time my mom and I wanted something we had to wait 10-15 minutes. We waited about 15 minutes for the waitress to make her initial table visit. We inquired about drink prices twice and waited 10-15 minutes each time for the waitress to come back with the drink pricings. Once we placed our order, we had to wait about 10-15 minutes for our drinks. Then we asked if we could have our check and had to wait 10-15 minutes for her to return. Then we had to wait another 10-15 minutes for our change. 

On Saturday of our stay, my mom and I had to wait about 15 minutes for every time we asked the waitress for something. We waited about 15 minutes for the waitress to make her initial table visit. Once we placed our order, we had to wait about 10-15 minutes for our drinks. Our food came out in time. Then we asked for our check and we had to wait 10-15 minutes. Then we waited 10-15 for our change. Other tables had the same wait time so they shared the same experiences that we did. We overheard one table say that we're not tipping our waitress because they are to slow. Not sure if that table had the same waitress or not. 

Two days of dealing with the same exact experience is not good. Both waitresses were nice but they were VERY SLOW. Having to wait 10-15 minutes for each item that you ask for is unacceptable and frustrating. I'm not sure if this T.G.I. Fridays is short staffed, not trained well, etc because I've been to several T.G.I. Fridays around the United States and this is the first time I've ever experienced this. 

To make matters even worse, T.G.I. Fridays is in charge of the complementary breakfast daily for this Embassy Suites location. All three days, the breakfast foods which were located in the hot foods area's were cold. Why would cold food be served? No one wants to eat cold nor should cold food be served. Were the heating trays on?

Won't stay at this Embassy Suites location again. The hotel staff were very nice people.
Being from Colombia, the taste of this restaurant is amazing!! and the prices are great for the excellent quality.

We will sorely miss Mr. Grey's hospitality and his unimitable ribs.

They have been a mainstay in Germantown for decades. Our enjoyment started long ago. I do believe I have a numbered ticket in my wallet because we never knew when Mr. Grey would ask us for our number.
This spot is great!! The Hand Drawn Noodle Soup is amazing. Also, GREAT prices. Takes cash only or venmo.

I used to come here a lot for brunch but since the re-opening of Hawthorne's I have opted to come for dinner instead since its less crowded. I love the new draft beer list they have for half off at happy hour. The shandy is homemade, light and refreshing! I definitely recommend it for a first drink at dinner. The cheese and meat board is worth it and it comes with 3 cheeses, 3 homemade dips and 2 meat. Next Scott and I opted to both get the pasta dishes for dinner. I usually never opt for pasta but I saw gnocchi on the menu in a brown butter sauce which I knew I would enjoy. I didn't just enjoy it, I LOVED it. The gnocchi was crisp and fluffy just like a great piece of Italian bread. It also came with cranberries, parm cheese and squash. A perfect blend of sweet and savory for dinner.

My friend and I enjoyed a tasty lunch at Rooster Soup and both plan to return frequently. Philosophy is meaningful, food is great, and service is friendly, professional , and attentive. The large portion of cauliflower soup was more than plenty, also delicious. Banana cream pie is as great as Grandma's. I encourage all to enjoy and support this great, little diner. Very clean also.

I didn't expect this to be so good! The chicken was flavored to the T! Nice and juicy, exactly how fried chicken should be. I just felt like the chicken was too thick.... Maybe if they flattened it out a little bit more it would've made it worthwhile to me. My jaws were getting tight less than half way through LOL. The customer service was lovely, and the good was quick. Oh yeah, the fries are freaking great!

100% this company & its employees are outstanding!  I had fleas 2 weeks ago and was unable to hire a company because they were all booked up.  When I called Evans Pest Control, Tiffany was so sympathetic & apologetic she was unable to schedule me for 3 weeks.  However, she took the time to tell me & offer their professional product, how to apply it and what steps to perform before & after!  Who does that! Miraculously, probably due to a cancellation (I was too grateful & excited to ask) Ryan came out & sprayed my entire house, basement & yard!!  What a relief! FYI:  I had The WORST experience with Terminix who scheduled my appointment, charged my credit card & then proceeded to re-schedule me 3 times!!!  Typical bait & switch fore knowing they too were fully booked!
Thank you Tiffany, Ryan and everyone at Evans Pest Control - you are the most ethical, professional, friendly & helpful people I've encountered- YOU ARE ALL THE BEST!!
Grateful in Fishtown :)

There are tons of good things to say about this place.  Genuinely helpful and friendly staff.  The ambiance, happy hour, and morning coffee made me feel at home.  I appreciated the fitness room, sustainable design features, and the healthy snacks in the room.

One of the best brunch spots in Philly! The way they cook avocado and pancake is just incredible! The owner is stylish, he always greeting the customer and make the atmosphere even better! Go there on weekdays, or you may want to wait 25 minutes to get a table. Highly recommend!

I haven't been this disappointed since the last Eagles NFC Championship game. I have been looking forward to checking this place out for quite some time. When we first sat down, we asked the waiter for his drink recommendations. His response was cocky, condescending, and simply disrespectful. It was as if how dare we even ask what cocktails are good since every cocktail will be the best you have ever tried and consider yourself worthy of even being here to try it. I don't mind a waiter being proud of their place of employment. Actually I respect that quite a bit and have written about this being a very positive attribute of a good restaurant in previous reviews. But this guy was as cocky and condescending as you can be. This type of negative, hostile behavior carried forward for the rest of the evening. I have written over 180 yelp reviews. The number of times I have criticized a waiter is less than or equal to one. But this guy was atrocious.

Now, on to the drinks and food, what the focus should actually be. Both were subpar. The old fashioned tasted like cheap whiskey on the rocks. Very poorly made. The trout was bland and lacked any sense of creativity. The only positive was the oysters. They were very flavorful with no addition of sauce required.

Very unique and very different the only dessert place like this in this part of Philadelphia. The desserts are beyond excellent but the service was slow I went with a party of 5 and it seems like that was too big of a group because they took forever to serve us.
Good service, beer, and cocktails, but literally price gouging for buying beers to-go.

Great food, Great service! We were seated immediately and our server was wonderful. I had the fried green tomatoes, smoked wings and lion burger. Great! We went on a Saturday afternoon and my 8 year old daughter was comfortable and loved the window booth. Great atmosphere. We will be back.
BBQ pork was way too salty.  Really wanted to like this, but was disappointed.

I found myself in the area a few days ago and stopped into Nick's for a sandwich and a few rounds.  Suzanne S. is spot-on about the decor of the place -- "dated and gloomy."  Still, there's something kinda charming about it.  It feels very much like the neighborhood it's in.  I sat at the bar and the staff were cheery and quick with a beer.

Food-wise, I had the roast beef with provolone and long hots.  It was good, though I found that the long hots didn't really add anything to the equation.  What was most remarkable about the sandwich, and what stands in such stark and curious opposition to nearly every other roast beef sandwich I've ever eaten, is its neatness.  This sandwich was a self-contained unit, and there weren't hunks of roast beef falling out all over the place.  I liked that about it.

I forget the name of the appetizer I tried (Hot Nachos, maybe?) but it wasn't worth remembering.  It consisted of a baking dish of waffle fries covered in hot sauce, then processed cheese.  Even reading the menu description, it didn't sound that great, so I got the "Wow" sauce, in hopes that it could redeem an otherwise bland dish.  Now, I didn't try their lesser hot sauces, but the "Wow" tasted just a few scoville units shy of Tabasco.  Disappointing.

Overall, though, if you find yourself in the north east, you can definitely do worse than a delicious roast beef sandwich and a few cheap budweisers.  If you're searching for anything more than that, though, keep looking.

Being a local business owner, I've been to kings oak numerous times, going back to their soft opening comedy night. Probably make my way there for atleast a beer twice a month and have sampled a good part of the menu. I've never had a problem, til today. Our group got split up finishing some corn hole at the piazza, we walk up, 3 guy 3 girls, security tells us guys have to wait. 15 minutes later the rest of our group shows up 4 guys 5 girls and the girls walk in, security tells the guys they can go in. I ask why we didn't get in with the ladies we came with, security gets in my face and says, "you got a problem we can do this, we're a private security company we don't care". Seriously walked away insulted, I only expect that at clubs at night, aggressive security on a beautiful Sunday afternoon ruins everything. I feel bad about leaving this review based off this one bad experience, especially for a neighbor, but this needs to be fixed.

I purchased an item from Robert online and WOW, WOW WOW WOW, Robert blew me away with the price, quality and amazing customer service. There needs to be a special place for people like Robert who knock it out of the part. I can't wait to do business with him in the future, there is no way I will work with anyone else. Even though I don't live in the area I can tell he is one of the best (if not the) best in the business. I was expecting an 8 or 9 out of 10 and Robert gave me a 10+. Thank you Robert, I have never met you or visited your shop but you made me so happy - you rock!

I really don't care for chains. This was decent. The Black Beans? Yes, please. I will go back ( to the DC location) and try several other dishes. Came here with a group of about 12. Customer service-impeccable.

YES, as good as all the reviewers said. Tremendous wait already building up early Saturday for dinner. Thank heavens I read the Yelp reviews and had a rsvp!

Had the Dan Dan noodles (i still dream about you and your spicy love) and a fish dish (tasty goodness). The only reason why i am not rating a 5 is because of the mapo tofu dish I got. My date and I could not eat it. I don;t know what season/herb/etc was in it, but it tasted funky. I think maybe we just got a bad batch. We literally had 2 bites and left it aside. 

I didn't feel that guilty after eating because I was sure I burned off all my calories sweating. The heat is intense, but sooo worth it. I've never had this heat in any dish I've eaten in Philadelphia until here :)

As someone who frequents coffee shops a ton, this is one of my favorite spots for the following reasons.

1. Delicious coffee
2. Large space, but not a ton of seating so it's fairly quiet and good for working and spending an afternoon.

This spot is lovely. The only drawback is the location for me, but I'm still willing to drive out here from Graduate Hospital so that's saying something. Highly recommend!

It's times like these where I wish I were really a cartoon.  Then I could just make a funny face (mouth open to seventeen times the normal size, eyes bugged out with little springs boing-boinging them right out) and have the word, "ARRRGHHHH!!!" in big print above my head in a quote bubble, and that would indeed say it all.   Unfortunately, I can't really make that face (without a lot of plastic surgery and going blind) and have to do a little better than "ARRRGHHHH!!!"  Darn it that I'm not a cartoon.

Two colleagues and I went to this location for lunch and seated ourselves (as the sign indicated).  The place was already quite crowded so we took the big shared table in the small room in the front.  Almost immediately, a few other parties came and sat down with us at the table.  After about ten minutes, two of those parties walked out due to bad service/non-responsive service.

We browsed the menus as well as the specials on the board.  It was just a few minutes after noon, so none of us expected that any of the lunch specials had already run out, but such was the case with half of them.  Everything we ordered had an issue right from the beginning:  stuff ran out, ingredients were not available, things like that.  One of us ordered an item that she usually gets for take-out.  She was told that she could not have that item at the table, that it was a take-out item only and ready-made.  She was told she could get the closest thing on the menu to it, but it had half the ingredients and was way more expensive.  (And no, our server would not let her purchase the item as take-out and then bring it to the table.)  The three of us continued to scour the menu to find lunches that were available.  This was not easy, as it seemed that everything we happened to pick was already gone, or not what we thought.  

We finally place our orders and we think there's a light at the end of the tunnel (as in, we were starving at this point and couldn't wait to eat!)  A lot of time goes by and our server barely made eye contact with us as he tended to the folks around us.  Refills on our waters were very hard to come by, and we had to ask for bread three times.  Then the bread came with one of the jams completely empty.  We asked for the refill on jam four times and it never came.  Frustrating, because I was really looking forward to trying the raspberry jam.

After about 15 minutes with no food, someone from the kitchen came over to the table and asked who ordered the cobb salad (me).  I was informed that they ran out of eggs, and was asked if I wanted a substitution.  I basically had ordered the salad because of the egg, so I told her I would have to look at the menu for a new item.  A minute later, she came back and said she found an egg after all.

After another 15 minutes (still with no water refills and none of that elusive jam!) my salad came (with no egg) and only one of the other orders.  Finally, my other colleague's entree came and it appeared to have an ingredient on it that was not advertised (mayonnaise).  She asked our server to check and see if there was mayo on there and he acted like she was insane, and took his time coming back with the answer, which delayed things considerably for her (she doesn't eat mayo, and that's totally her right to want to check. I would have done the same thing, as a fellow mayo-hater.)

We finally were able to all start eating our meals when the server came over with two hard-boiled eggs on a plate for me.  At this point I just had to laugh.  I was thankful they did bring the eggs (guess they found two hiding somewhere!) because the salad was pretty bland without it (there was hardly any chicken and the avocado was mostly browned and inedible).  I've never been given hard boiled eggs with a salad that I had to cut myself (except at a salad bar, and even then, most times it's cut up for you), but I wasn't going to complain.  I got the last eggs!  

The most ridiculous part of the experience though was the attitude of our server.  We had to ask questions because things were not available, including daily specials (who would think they'd run out by noon?), and because items were not given to us (like water refills and bread and jam).  Not only did he seemingly forget a lot of things, but he put on quite an act when he was talking to us (spoke to us in a very sing-song voice, smothering us in niceness) and then making faces (rolling eyes, etc.) when he thought we weren't looking.   Sadly, he did this to our table neighbors, too.  (One woman's entree came drenched in dressing and she had specifically asked for no dressing, as she is diabetic.  He actually argued with her and said that the dressing will be on the side, but that what she saw on her plate was part of the "pre-made slaw" which happens to be dressed.  She then reiterated that she specifically asked for no dressing on the salad at all and he said he'd be right back with the side of dressing.)

Food good. Service not so much.

Since starting school in university city i've been looking for a good Greek place to get food. And Greek lady did not disappoint. The lamb gyro are tasty the only thing is that I didn't expect fries in my gyro it was a nice surprise. It's a good place for broke college students. And it doesn't help if you mention yelp you get a free drink. I will be back soon probably today.

don't really need to come back here again. The sea food is just OK. I ordered the calamari and it came with lots of fried carrots and other veggies as well. Was hoping to get more seafood though. And the lobster Mac was just OK, nothing special. Great service, cocktails and biscuits though!

I was between rating 2 or 3 stars, but the waitress was really nice so I gave it three. First of all the place is adorable and inviting and it's BYOB and that's great. Me and my girlfriend started off with the calamari and the muscles appetizer. The muscles were AMAZING, big and fresh and the white sauce was DELISH! The calamari was okay, we mixed it in the white sauce the mussels came in and that really improved them. The menu is VERY SMALL! I got the veal marsala and the veal was good but the marsala sauce was strange, like sweet basalmicy, and not enough mushrooms. My friends filet over Gorgonzola gnocchi was good, creme  brûlée and tiramisu AMAZING, but we had originally ordered a piece of hazelnut cake that came frozen solid.... Maybe I'll go back. Maybe not. They need more selection

I cant believe with all my check-ins on fb I've never reviewed this place! I've been to every Han Dynasty in Philly including 3 separate tasting menus. Needless to say I am a huge fan of this food. Real szechuan style. I love my food spicy ("10"!) & this place serves it up in spades. Han is also a laugh riot and fun loving guy. My favorites are dry pot style, usually the lamb. Also Mapo: its spicy tofu with minced pork. The wonton soup is also very good. Orange Chicken is awesome but only served in the Montgomery county restaurant.  And of course everybody loves the Dan dan noodles about a "6" on the spicy scale so pretty much everyone can handle the heat. Chinese are not known for their pastries so skip desert.  Byob at this reastaunt but west philly and old city have their liquor license. I deserve a free t-shirt for this review!

Only tried their buffalo chicken pizza--their special for Vegan Pizza Day--and it was just a delightful experience. The chicken was nicely crispy on the outside, and the amount of sauce on the slice was well-balanced with the other ingredients. It was a little hard to maneuver with the big chunks of chicken on the pizza, but well worth it.

The sodas are also really great with interesting flavors--my friend's tried them all and he likes all of them. When I was there, he had the lavender while I got the tangerine. Refreshing and unique.

Zero Stars. This place is absolutely the worst pretzel establishment ever. I walked on a nice fall even to grab a fresh hot pretzel. I ordered a cinnamon sugar pretzel and it was hard as a rock. I asked the clerk if she had other fresh pretzel available and she said no. Then said gave me the "wtf do you want me to do?" and brushed me off so I just left with the hardest pretzel ever in my hand. 
I should have asked for my $1.25 back. Tried to give this hole in a wall a chance but I'll stick to Auntie Annie's.

Edited 1/5/11

I went back to Ten Stone a couple months ago for the tuna wrap... AND GOT SALMONELLA.  Buyer beware. I'm taking away a star for that torture..

-----

I have to agree with the reviews that have been posted - Ten Stone is a bit pricy, but it's a nice/casual place to get a drink and some bar food.

The hummus comes with a pesto sauce which was a nice touch.  The nachos are pretty standard.  The server seemed to know a lot about the beers on tap, which was helpful. 

I ordered the tuna wrap which was stuffed full. It was pretty delicious, and I would go back just for this item.

However, I give Ten Stone three stars because I am not thrilled with the prices.  While it is consistent and you get what you are expecting (tasty bar food), I'm just not totally sold that it's worth it.

The sushi is good but everytime I order for lunch (45 mins ahead of time) and I'm only ordering sushi, it is never done when they say. So I have to wait 10 mins (totaling 55 mins) for 3 rolls of sushi. Dont say my order is ready for me to pick up if it is not. This is unacceptable!

It's rare for a place to be amazing for different types of meals, but Mixto is one of them. You'll have such a great time whether you come for brunch, dinner, or even happy hour.

For dinner, first off, there is something about the bread that is just amazing. We always ask for for refills. Our favorite dish there is to split the paella. Totally worth it! Absolutely amazing. Their mojitas and margaritas are both delicious.

Although drinks-wise, nothing compares to their sangrias! I've come with girlfriends just to split a pitcher and hang out. They offer white, red and sparkling pitchers, full of fruit and tasty goodness. 

Finally, after a crazy night out, come here for brunch and get their colombian frittata. Amazingness in a pan. Eggs, chorizo (I get substitute with turkey sausage), cheese and fried plantains. Make sure and get your smoothie too. I love the guanabana :) 

Philly is full of good eats but make sure you check out this one!

This is one of my favorite restaurants to go to when I'm in Philly. I would go with my roommates before and my friends when they visit. I normally would dine in at Sang Kee but due to COVID, I ordered pick up. 

I was very happy when they finally opened up. I ordered the salted chicken fried rice, vegetables, beef chow fun, Peking duck, roast pork, HK style duck, and white rice. Everything was very good but my favorite dishes would be the duck and the salted fish fried rice. I would sometime order the noodle soup and other specialty dinner dishes.

The food is always fast but the service I have to say it's not the best.
