Amazing restaurant.. delicious food.. subway was running late due to "medical emergency", i called to let them know and they could not have been more sincere and thanked me for calling.. upon arrival two people asked me how our commute was... service=amazing.. there was not one time when my water was not full or dirty places were sitting in front of me.. best italian food i had in the city (closest to food in italy)....our waiter couldn't have been more helpful.. explained dishes, offered suggestions.. also appreciated that they informed me when making a resi that it was cash only establishment..food was delicious.. i had the torta, gnocchi, eggplant, and tiramisu (tourista option).... atmosphere is very laid back, almost like you at your grandmother house for sunday dinner...

The service,  atmosphere and food is EVERYTHING!! 
Cescily is sweet, attentive and thorough. Honestly, I was surprised by her ability to meet my needs. The place was pretty full and the music was playing yet she heard me every time I called for her. 

The DJ plays every single 90's

Felt like a sardine in a can too packed in.
 The service and food was ok but  also felt rusted so they could
turn the table again a couple more times. It's a place I would not go back too.

This place has either the best or second-best gelato I've ever had (the other candidate is hundreds of miles away and I haven't been there in years, so it's impossible to compare). Creamy, smooth, delicious, intense and creative flavors, friendly service -- can't beat it! I like that they rotate through local and seasonal ingredients as well. They make a respectable cappuccino too if you're not in the mood for gelato.

This is one of my least positive experiences in Philly. We had 6 for dinner each dinner was dreadful. Drab back room, poor quality service, and bad food.
Stay away

I really don't think this store is very expensive- I bought a really cute vintage dress there for $40, and a 40's blouse for $26. The majority of the items are true vintage (as opposed to a bunch of stuff from the 80's you truly CAN find in real thrift stores) and while I might not pay that much for just anything there, they often get unusual stuff I don't mind shelling out a bit more for (though far less than you know, something new at The Gap.)

As for people getting so uptight about people reselling stuff from thrift stores, hey, that's what used goods collectors do! They get up early to go through estate sales and paw through tons of junk to find good stuff. It's a lot of work. Record store owners and book store owners and antiques dealers all do it. I don't see how it's a raquet of any kind-I love going to real thrift stores but I can't pretend the vintage selection will be like the one at Decades- as much as I'd love it if every Goodwill was brimming over with 50's dresses!

Not really authentic Asian food, but its a good place for lunch. We got mostly Thai food and chicken satay. Food was fresh and servers were friendly. Salad was also fresh with vinegar. We enjoyed the Satay. Outdoor sitting is really nice. Thai fried rice were more like chinese fried rice.

Wow, this place rocks. First time in Philly and now I know where to go when I come back. Everything from the cold salami and cheese plate to the free appetizer to the dishes to the wine were great. GET THE LAMB SHOT! Yes, its lamb on a stick in a shot glass dipped in all kinds of goodness. It was probably the best thing I had all week. I also had a duck dish which was good but I don't remember the name.

Although it looks a little more like a sushi restaurant than a tapas bar it is still a nice atmosphere. No problems with the service either.

 I went in on a Tuesday and even though it was full, we got seated right away. They have a downstairs that probably opens on the weekend for when I assume they are much busier. With food this good, I don't know how they aren't busier. I wish this place was in Chicago!
Had a very good roast pork sandwich and my girlfriend had a cheeseburger, which was basic but well made.
Good food- only a few vegetarian options though.  I always get a fish and tofu hot pot which is very tasty.  Good bubble tea too!

I was here for a wedding -- an amazing, glamorous wedding.  The food was amazing.  The cocktail hour featured a true international cheese display, brie en croute, a sushi station, a Mexican station, and much, much more.  The dinner was just as impressive -- everyone at my table was raving about the food.  As a vegetarian, I was especially impressed!  Instead of the typical pasta dish they serve to vegetarians at events, they have an actual entree prepared:  samosas (kinda... even better, really!) with grilled vegetables.  It was, hands down, the most delicious vegetarian entree I've ever had at a group event.  The service was top notch, and the building is gorgeous.
This place is great! They have a beautiful waiting area with coffee and water with a great view! Also it was quick and reasonable and a job well done! I highly recommend Phone Repair Philly

I think that Jim's on 4th and South is slightly better, but I actually think that Lickety Split may be better than both!

I went here yesterday on my way out of Philadelphia.  Earlier in the day, I went to Jim's.  Jim's is slightly better (I rated it 3-stars) and their meat is more seasoned and minced/chopped.  

SUMMARY:  Philly Cheesesteaks need an update.  Anything that has CheezeWhizz as an option is bad news.  It's better to eat NYC pizza instead.

I rate my experience here five stars, because I had a blast with a group of friends for a birthday party. After thinking about my experience however, the restaurant itself is somewhere between 3-4 stars. Let me explain.

First of all I was impressed and quite shocked honestly that this place was PACKED like can't even stand in the entrance to wait for a table on a Tuesday night. Good for them! Clearly the word has gotten out that there is something to be desired at Bud and Marylin's. The restaurant is decorated very appropriately with 60s decor including a very ugly, though very comfy, floral couch along the wall for bench seating. Be sure to make a reservation, or try your luck at the bar. We waited a short time and were seated promptly at our reservation time.
I was surprised to find that the menu was not very vegetarian friendly. Most places in Philadelphia these days have at least one vege friendly entree, or something desirable that isn't a salad. Pretty much all you got here were several salads and a tofu "slider" which seemed a bit of an afterthought and was by far the worst thing I ate that night. The cheese curds are a must, definitely my favorite bite. We also shared skilled bread, which came with bacon topped cream. The bread alone was way over salted so I can't imagine adding salty cured meat on top would enhance that flavor, but it is bacon, so who knows. I also had a kale and parsnip salad with hush puppies that was OK, but honestly kind of forgettable. 
We drank a LOT-cocktails were well crafted and our waiter had good recommendations for my lush friends and I. We had some of the specialty cocktails but also did partake in simple classics-a good gin martini, a boulevardier, and old fashions galore. We enjoyed the desserts but note to self, they were HUGE. I saw some mixed emotions about the funfetti cake on this website, but I did indeed enjoy it, but by that time I think I was three cocktails in so it may very well have been terrible... woe.
Overall, if you are a carnivore, there will probably be more things on this menu that you would enjoy, but sadly it fell short for a vegetarian. It would take a menu change to get me to come back quickly, though I can't not recommend this place for a nice celebration dinner with a fabulous group of friends.

A friend of mine recommended I go here as he previously took his car to get fixed. My AC was not working properly and I've had so many different mechanic throw different prices at me that were astronomical. Jon ( one of the shop operators) looked at my car and after a series a questions, determined what my car needed. He even talked me thru what the problem was in layman's term so that I could understand what the heck was happening. I just needed a recharge and did not need any additional work done like these other guys had told me. Jon didn't take Advantage of me not knowing anything about cars and straight up helped me. On top of that, he didn't charge the crazy prices either. It was afforded and fair for the work he did. Now I bring my car to Jon if I have any issues! You'd be a fool not to!

I do like the 'unusual' concept of a dog first place, has many dog pictures and dog oriented materials, pillows, statues etc. but that's not my review its all about service & food. The waitress we had did a decent job however in cases where we had the mushroom soup it was a purée type of soup. If you like puréed soup (I don't as well as my wife) this wouldn't be an issue, but although fairly tasty it was not because of the consistency that was standoffish. We ordered the fried pickles (full dill) not bad but had better, the saffron spaghetti and the filet mignon. The filet mignon was the best of the four items that we were served which was done correctly and had a ginger base which luckily did not overpower the meat. I give it a B (had better again). The saffron spaghetti was a combo of small shrimp, really small scallops m&m size or smaller, & mussels (we took the calamari out). I don't know if it was the heavy saturated spaghetti (cheese infused) or the mussels, but I got sick pretty quick. Maybe it was the too rich combination of everything, or maybe the shellfish that wasn't cleaned or ? but I'm not one to get sick easily. My wife stopped quicker than I did she did not have the mussels so I think that was the culprit. The bill for this with three drinks (2 beers & vodka/OJ) was $109 so not too cheap. After getting home and recouped a bit, I called to speak to the manager about the experience. All she said sorry but we'll make a note if any else gets sick. I'm not looking for a free meal but maybe some off the bill for at least the item that did it. Poor business to the paying public. And the final note the noise level was close to unbearable and we weren't that close to the bar. And if you don't like very close seating to your neighbor, then this is not the place for you. We won't be back and I wouldn't recommend this place for no more than a beer or drink at the bar.

I went here during center city sips for dinner because of the 15% discount of dinner if you order something from the sips menu. Altogether my experience was excellent. I had the mocha madness cocktail which was delicious, like drinking fancy chocolate milk! My boyfriend and I shared the wings as an appetizer, which were hands down some of the most delicious wings I've ever eaten, and I do love me a good BBQ chicken wing! I had the veggie burger (with additional cheese) and my boyfriend had the asparagus crepe. Both were absolutely delicious!! The only thing I have to say negatively about my veggie burger was taht it was wayyyy to big. It fell apart and honestly half the size of it was enough to fit nicely in the bun with everything else, but then again burgers from anywhere are always too big, so at least you cant say I didnt warn you! For dessert we shared the apple streusel thingy, and it was deeeelicious. Our server was super friendly and the staff atmosphere in general was a great one! The decor details were great, even down to our dessert plate which looked like a ceramic piece of graph paper (very unique and humorous!). Altogether my experience was great, just not 5 star worthy because it takes a lot for me to give something 5 stars :)

The sweet potato gnocchi special was  amazing! Can't go wrong with pasta here, all homemade, light & flavorful dishes. Have dined a few times with groups of 4-6 & all raved...Delicious!   Service & manager/owner are great. The space is very small so it can be loud.  BYO. Reservations highly recommended but don't arrive to early as there's no where to wait.

Came here for a girls' night and fell in love with this place. It was absolutely amazing. We tried a trio of different pastas.....someone got pasta with seafood and pesto, another person got a meaty red-sauced pasta and I got calamari pasta. Absolutely fresh and to die for. They also serve fresh bread with garlic oil which includes huge chunks of roasted garlic...I could have eaten the whole thing to myself. Our waitress was knowledgeable and friendly throughout our stay and gave us her personal suggestions, which we appreciated. I'm a bit of a crème brulee snob, so my expectations are always set high when ordering dessert. It was one of the best crème's I've ever had. It was torched perfectly and the serving size was perfect. I love the concept of BYOB places in Philly, and we brought several bottles, which were chilled and opened for us, complimentarily. Our entire bill with 2 desserts included came to 50-some dollars. I couldn't believe it. The only downfall was there was a bit of confusion at the beginning of the night. We called for reservations at 7 and when they got there, they had us down for 9pm. So they sent us next door to the pizza shop (which is owned by the same people) to wait. We were starving at this point, so we ended up ordering an app-- as soon as we got it, of course the owner came over to tell us our table was ready, around 7:45--so we had to scarf down our app (which was amazing!) and gulp our wine (which wasn't a problem ;) to head back over to the restaurant. It kind of got off to a frustrating start, but believe me when I say it was worth the inconvenience to partake of this experience. Can't wait to go back!
Expensive gas but you can't get work done for cheaper or quicker in the city.

"A" for elegant, slick decor with classic overtones reminiscent of the Oyster Bar in Grand Central Station, the Grand Daddy of the concept.  "A" for the fun vivacious Happy Hour crown and value drinks/oysters.  Oyster crackers, excellent, fresh, plentiful.  Drinks created from a rich array of quality booze.  Peel and eat shrimp great value.  Soups, underwhelming but NOT bad...I just prefer a heartier stock in my Snapper Soup (dating myself with the Bookbinders comparison). Bud had New England Clam Chowder, same comment: underwhelming.  So food= "B".  That said, I go out for the whole package, and I think it's a great time.  Sweet hostess, professional yet engaging barkeep. I like that the age skews younger to a 20's-30's professional crowd. For architectural purists, floor to ceiling white subway tile.  Classic white marble aged bar-top.  Wide plank wood floors. Exposed ductwork and track lighting provides a nice juxtaposition between old/new, done in a monochromatic pallet of taupes, whites, creams, browns and back.  Very nice.

Three floor seating of great ambiance! A traditional Italian cuisine in the heart of  South Philly. A frequented spot for holiday parties for us. The food is exceptional and the service is on point. Always accommodating and attentive. 


To name a few favorites...
Eggplant Parmesan is to die for! The braciole is superb and the fettuccine is a delight. Homemade Gnocchi will keep you full and saving some for home. Delicious dessert menu as well, but one would not expect anything less from an Italian restaurant in the heart of South Philly. 

Applause to the chefs!!! As well as all the staff!
Very nice spot!!! Steak AMAZING- Scallops AMAZING!!! Cocktails were AMAZING!!! I throughly enjoyed this spot!! Food and service was great!!!
Very nice park but can be hard to get into at times. Figuring out the west and east side of the park was a bit troubling at first but once your there it's great.

The only time I would come here is for the breakfast. Big portions. You know how diner food is. Wouldn't come here any other time tho. Food is smothered in grease and oil. And the veggies are not fresh at all. I took my burger home once and it had mold on it after a day of being in the fridge. No fresh ingredients and scared to come here. I'd rather stay healthy

One of my favorite "fast food" veggie spots in Philadelphia. The falafel is always consistently well-prepared, salad bar always fresh, and can't beat the price for what you're getting on the go. Service is quick and mindful, only thing is the placement of the bathroom is pretty odd as it is behind the counter. Overall, tasty and rich with a nice a respectful staff!

Whoops! Realized I meant to post this review on the NEW Farm and Fisherman Tavern site.  

We were excited to try this place after having a wonderful meal in Philly at the original Farm and Fisherman.  I must say that I'm a little biased because I think SJ has some amazing BYOBs so going anyplace where I have to pay $60 for a bottle wine sets me back a bit, but I will try not to let that distract me from this review.  Loved the bar with the true tavern feel.  We opted to sit in the dining area which was comfy but I wasn't crazy about the overhead lighting. Felt like we were in a spotlight.  Ordered the calamari which was delish- light and airy.  For our entrees we had the rigatoni bolognese with short ribs and the tavern steak. Both were tasty - especially the tavern steak. Highly recommend.  Glad we came but to be honest, this place didn't have anything special over the wonderful restaurant (BYOBs) in neighboring Collingswood.

Now THIS is what I expect beer gardens to look like! Dozens of picnic tables for large groups or to share with strangers.  Free jenga sets for everyone to use.  These kept us entertained for hours.

Interesting beer selection although not varied enough for my taste (too many IPA's and pale ales when I wasn't in the mood for hops).  Bartenders were not terribly friendly, but they were also pretty busy.  I think I'm spoiled by really friendly bartenders up here in Buffalo.

Only 3 stars because the crowd became a little annoying as the evening wore on (and patrons became increasingly intoxicated).  Too many "bros" hitting on the seemingly available girls.

OK place to shop. I have found a few things there though most of the time it all looks very humdrum. TERRIBLE place to consign. I have been consigning for years and would never consign with them again. They act like they are doing you a huge favor, they reject perfectly good and stylish items, and you wind up getting pennies back. I sold about seven items and got a $20 check. It was not worth the time of taking it there, I would have been happier donating. Next time I'm taking my stuff to AIDS Thrift.

Finally stopped by the new site of Yards on Spring Garden Street for some food and drinks! Yards is an acclaimed local craft beer company in Philly and I find their new restaurant & bar worth visiting!

When we walked in the entrance at 5th st & Spring Garden on a Friday evening, the staff warmly checked our ID and got us stamped. The nice about Yards is that you can either request a table for a more formal dining experience, or just order your food & drink at a line and hang out in the huge cafeteria-style seating area. The interior is simple yet unique, as you can see the brewing facility behind the glass. They also have a beer shop near the entrance if you feel like grabbing a pack of beer to go.

Yards has a wider selection of beer than I expected, as I've only tried 2-3 types of Yards at other local bars. The 20 oz draft beer is $6 or $7 and the beer flight is $7. My friends ordered beer of their liking and I sampled two beers from the revolution flight. Really like love stout!

The food is on the upper spectrum of bar food in Philly. Our group ordered Philly wings, saffron arancino, green salad, Yards BBQ platter (for two). The wings were pretty nice and I liked the extra spiciness of the jalapeno power. the green salad was refreshing and light, with simple honey-lemon dressing. I haven't tried the saffron arancino. Finally, the BBQ platter was such a great choice for meat lovers like us! It included cheddar bratwurst, brisket, ribs with red cabbage slaw, potato salad and cornbread. The cheddar bratwurst was so delicious and cheesy, I'm sure I'll come back for more. The brisket and ribs were okay, definitely can't match those tender or off-the-bone perfection in Texas or St. Louis, but were flavorful and satisfying.

The new Yards is definitely a trendy place to visit in 2018. Bottom line: great bar food and freshly-brewed beer, friendly yet somewhat slow service. Price-wise, more expensive than neighborhood bars (it seems they don't have happy hour rates).

Stopped by to visit this gem of a location while visiting my girlfriend.  Unfortunately the upstairs levels were packed but we did find a comfortable seat downstairs.  Our bartender Jason was awesome! (We did look around the second floor and I have to say the decor and ambiance were on point). I highly recommend checking this place out!!!

The City Tavern is Awesome for a one or two time thing... But I would not make a habit of going. Being as though this lovely experience does come with a high price tag‍ my tag being almost $200 for a dinner for 2. Which consisted of one drink each, two appetizers to share, and 1 entree a piece and 1 dessert(shared). But being as though it was a special occasion I was ok with it... 

So I decided to take my boyfriend here for his Birthday ... I always like to try and do something different. And this was on the list. 

We went on a weds. With reservations made for 5:45. I must say we got there a little before 5:45 and was seated immediately, which was great! However, when making my reservation I asked for "a window seat". And the seat we were seated at was for sure not a window seat. So I don't even think they really read any requests you ask for when making reservations. So don't waste your time 

The place was pretty packed for a weds if you ask me. But that's a good thing!! And the beautiful weather added to an even more beautiful day. 

When we walked into where we would be seated. Everything was beautifully decorated as in the colonial days, looked very cool!! The waiters were all dressed up like in the colonial days and looked great!!! 

The waiter lit the candle at our table which made the ambience even more beautiful. And filled out metal goblets up with water (so cool)... 

The food I must say was very tasty!! As appetizers we had the crab cakes and asparagus plate. The crab cakes were delicious!!! However, we could have probably went without the asparagus plate. It wasn't bad... I just felt like it was a whole bunch or nothing.. 

But both entrees were delicious!! I had the duckling which was very good. I was iffy at first because I have never had duck before. But it was really good!!! 

My boyfriend had the New York strip, that came with two jumbo shrimp, mashed potatoes and vegetables.  He really enjoyed it!!! He said "that was the biggest strip he ever saw"... I had a little piece and it was nice and tender. He likes his medium rare and said it was cooked to perfection  

For desert we had the cheesecake and it was pretty good. However very rich!! I'm not a fancy cheesecake eater so it was to much for me. But he loved it!! So all in and all in it was a great meal... 

It is pretty expensive so it might be a while before we go back. But we will keep it on the menu for whenever we want to splurge again... 

Side note..... we were pretty bummed our server wasn't able to give us the history of the building.(which I feel for the price that should be a must) but he did give us a pamphlet. And we didn't hold it against him because he was pretty nice. But I just we would have got the info ver

Ok so this was the first stop during my recent divorce party. That's right, my divorce party. My sister and friend decided I needed a celebration for closure. So we did a little bar crawl, with Devils Den being our first stop. 

We went inside to talk to the hostess about sitting outside. They happily put two tables together for us and it was a lovely day out. They had an excellent beer selection and everyone was happy with their selections. They had a few beer flights on the menu but I'm not sure if that was just because it was Philly Beer Week. Beers were well priced. 

We looked at the food menu, but no one was really hungry at that point. I would like to go back sometime   As a side note:  my sis and brother in law love it here for brunch so I'm only assuming the food is good!  They are good judges of food!

Inside there was a fireplace separating the dining area from the bar area - I'm also assuming that is pretty kick-butt on a cold Philly day. 

Our server was nice too!  

So 4 stars for now based on our experience - to be adjusted once I have food here!

Such a great spot to gift shop! The selection is always changing. Every time I walk by, I have to stop to see what's new. And. Make sure to get an Art Star punch card. Each time you spend $20, you get a punch to earn $20 free on anything! Maybe even something with a bird on it!

I've taken my car here for service a couple of times.  They completed the oil change, rotated tires, etc. quite competently.  I do, however, have an issue with a practice that I find borderline questionable.

The last time I took my car in for basic service, they recommended that I have two issues that needed attention.  Given the expected outlay of cash, I decided to call later for a service appointment.  As I thought about it, I called them back to explain what the issues were.  It turns out that there isn't necessarily anything wrong with the part, but based on the mileage on the car they suggested it.  

I will continue to go there, but for very specific and minor maintenance.  This situation just highlights the fact that you have to ask questions, or get a second opinion.  Don't just blindly accept their suggestions.  They really typify the stereotype of a shady car salesman.
I was here with a large group and everyone was really pleased with their meal. The staff was super helpful and the salsa was great with the chips (honestly, my favorite part).

The Bar Method Rittenhouse is fantastic! I have been going to the studio since it opened in February 2018. I have increased my flexibility, lost weight and met great people! Gina and Kristen, the owners, have done such a fantastic job welcoming all the members and getting to know to us individually. They built such a great community! All the instructors Mary Carmen, Sarah W, Sarah M and Amanda are all so dedicated to their teaching and making sure all the students have the best class each and every time!

Reading terminal is a huge space with a plethora of choices to the point of being slightly overwhelmed. Coming here on a weekend might not have been the best decision, due to the large crowds. You can find pretty much anything you want to eat in this place, along with great items that would make a great present or souvenir. There are things to suit every taste and diet. I will definitely be going back before I return home. But it will definitely be on a week day.

Not too impressed, as a former vegetarian and mostly veg now. I see the excitement about the faux-meat fast food concept, but...it's something I would have gotten excited about in high school, not now as an educated foodie.

I ordered the curry tofu wrap (I used to order curry tofu at the shack..nostalgia!), but the tofu/curry sauce was not very curry-like and extremely salty. Extremely salty. I kept having to dip into the mayo-like sauce they gave with it (which was bland, thank goodness).  Which means that my meal may have been vegan, but was not healthy. too much hydrogenated oil to make it like mayo/cream sauce... not worth it.  

I might go back to try the udon salad. But I am not getting that tofu wrap again; gross. I only ate it all because I was so hungry, but I've got eaters' remorse.

I would say that this is great for the vegan/vegetarian who also likes compact-vegan-frozen-meals-for-one, but perhaps not someone who is looking for healthful, simply nutritious food.

I live around the corner from this place, which is located somewhat on the outskirts of Center City's foodie nexus. Finally gave it a try when, around 9:30 on a Saturday night, we got the munchies and realized nearly everyone else was closed or closing.

There were a couple of full tables in the restaurant, but it was definitely on the empty side considering the time of night (I would have been expecting more tables lingering over dessert etc). I usually see that as a warning bell, but hey, we were here and hungry, might as well give it a shot. We were seated promptly by a quirky and charming waiter, who wasn't trying too hard to be either. Being greeted with smiles and a "don't worry about it" when strolling in around the time their kitchen closes was certainly a relief--you all know the looks when you're given a table but you also know you're about to be seriously rushed.

No rush here. We ordered our appetizers (mussels and grilled Romaine) and the management, distraught that we were sitting down to a meal without wine, brought us some from the back room on the house. That's just *nice*. Appetizers were delicious: I liked my Romaine way more than I liked my beau's mussels, but she liked them just fine. The crispy-charred Romaine came with a perfect balsamic reduction and soft blue cheese--to. die. for.

For mains, we both opted for the specials: she got a catfish-like non-catfish (can't remember the name of it) over shrimp ravioli, and I got a seafood mixed grill (salmon, calamari, shrimp). Our food arrived in a timely fashion, with the manager swinging by for a chat, and again absolutely no rush or pressure. This time I liked her main better than mine: the un-catfish was delicious and tender, and the home-made shrimp ravioli had a thick, toothsome dough that wasn't overcooked. My mixed grill was good--the calamari was perfectly cooked and not at all rubbery, as were the shrimp--but a touch heavy on the salt, and the salmon was definitely overcooked.

We got coffee and more company instead of dessert (neither of us had room, though the tiramisu that we saw going past looked delicious). Our waiter had home-made limoncello (limes rather than lemons) to share. I think we ended up getting home after 11pm, and our bill came to under $70. Fabulous.

Final verdict? The few slips in the meal were overwhelmingly forgivable considering the execution of the rest of it (clean, assertive flavors), the fantastic service (the best I've experienced in Philly, hands down), and the value. Will definitely be coming back.
Love this place. AMAZING Food!!! Their Philly cheesesteaks are really good, their prices are a little on the high side, but compared to other restaurants in Philadelphia, their inexpensive.

Obv you can't go wrong with Pat's! Pretty good cheesesteak, (though Steve's is still better in my opinion) Wiz without!!! Love the sliced beef and the wiz is obviously amazing. A bit over priced, but there's something to be said that you're going to the most well known cheesesteak spot in Philadelphia. Go when it's freezing out or at off hours so that you don't have to wait in an insanely long line.. Also, make sure you get the Cherry Soda, goes nice with that delish sandwich.

I had to change my wedding plans due to COVID and we did a backyard wedding.  Adrienne and her staff were amazing!  Adrienne went above and beyond to help coordinate my vision.  On the day of the wedding her staff showed up and turned my backyard into the most beautiful wedding.  My guests raved about the food and about how pleasant everyone was, and I couldn't agree more.  Not only that but my oven broke in the middle of the chef preparing our dinner and they traveled with an extra oven.  I didn't know anything happened until the end of the night.  They also went above and beyond with safety during this pandemic.  You guys were amazing, thank you!

Loved it. We had an excellent first time here. Friendly, attentive, prompt service. Terrific martini along with a spider roll appetizer and two delicious entrees.  We eat lots of Asian food so we tend to be picky; we'll definitely be going back.

This is a great bakery to stop by. The buns all look really fresh. Many are wrapped in plastic for easy transportation. The lady at the counter was rather friendly as well. I ordered a cream bun which was pleasantly less cream-saturated than usual. It was also dusted with coconut, which seemed like a nice touch. The one thing that is bad about this place is that it has far less seating than its competitors. So, while it wouldn't be the first place I would go to sit and eat, for the times when I just need a bun on the run, this is a decent place. One odd thing about the place is that many prices aren't posted. However, the prices seem to be in line with Chinatown norms.
Place is good for drinks.Kieran is clueless by best.Wouldn`t come back to this place again.

I had a great experience at Philly Bride! I worked with Bethany and she maid my future MOH and I feel comfortable and like we were in great hands. As this was my first time trying on gowns, she allowed me to try on all different styles and hone in on what I want to wear on my wedding day. Although I left without a dress, I am not counting Philly Bride out as I found 2 dresses that are definitely still in the running!

I came in with severe lower back pain I'd had for over a week, so bad that I was unable to sleep and literally every movement or position hurt. 

Dr. Gao applied the needles and some mild electrical currents to a few key ones. The resting period was very relaxing and I felt pleasantly heavy. Afterwards I was taken to another room (was surprised to find much more ease of movement almost immediately) for an acupressure massage by Lisa. Holy crap, was she amazing. 

I've had plenty of satisfying Chinatown massages but proper acupressure is a whole different ballgame. She spent a lot of time on my ankles and feet, with very intense pressure in specific places that hurt in a good way and didn't tickle, and I have very ticklish feet. I was grunting and groaning the whole time because it was VERY intense but it really releases pressure in areas I didn't realize were connected to my problem area on my lower right back. These ladies are incredible pros who know the human body amazingly well. Lisa did keep checking in with me to make sure it wasn't too painful, and she showed me stretches and gave me follow up instructions to use heat and avoid cold. When I stepped down I was amazed to find almost zero pain remained. I was truly in damn near constant agony for a full a week and one visit almost completely removed it. 

Make an appointment to be safe, and know that late in the day (after 5) it's tough to see Dr. Gao.

My boyfriend and I are always looking to try new places to eat, and we especially like to seek out non-American food.  So we tried Konak, a charming Turkish restaurant tucked away at 2nd and Vine.  It was a cold winter night, and Konak was warm and inviting.  The dining area is large, with cathedral ceilings, which befits the place's name because Konak (pronounced co-naak) means "mansion" in Turkish.

My boyfriend and I cozied up at an intimate, candle-lit table across from a party of older folks who seemed to be celebrating someone's anniversary or retirement.  They were a lively bunch, and they made us feel right at home, since they were regulars.  Our server, too, was very friendly and on-the-ball.

The food was even better than the atmosphere.  My boyfriend and I got a flight of Turkish wine samples, all of which were very very good.  I especially liked the red wine because it is dry and smooth.  Turkish food is similar to other Mediterranean cuisine and is just as tasty.  We started off with the skewered mussels and the spicy ezme (a spicy onion, pepper, tomato, and garlic sauce) with flatbread.  My boyfriend then got the Lamb Shish Kebab, which was so tender it was like eating filet mignon (I stole a bite of his dinner), while I dug into a stuffed eggplant.  We ended our meal with Turkish coffee, which is bitter and sweet at the same time, and a dessert special of baked pear stuffed with walnuts and cinnamon.  The dinner was truly worth venturing out into the cold, arctic night.

What made our experience even more delightful was that we happened to go on a night when they had a Turkish band and a belly dancer, which apparently is every Friday.  I was a little embarrassed when the dancer tried to get me to dance with her, but the old men at the table next to us seemed to really enjoy her gyrations.  All in all, this is a classy joint with a genuine Mediterranean flair that is difficult to find in the city.

They also have $2 dishes on Tuesday nights for people seeking to sample Turkish cuisine.  Check out their website for more weekly specials.

There is parking at a lot halfway down the street, and we didn't need reservations the night we went because it was the dead of winter and hardly anyone else was crazy enough to venture outside.  I'd recommend making a reservation if you plan on going at prime dinner hours on the weekend.  I'd also recommend checking out the menu online beforehand, and remember to bring a couple of $1 bills in case the belly dancer is there...

Lovely place; late for our reservation we sat outside, which turned out for the best -- it was a lovely evening and less stuffy than sitting inside.  Service was great; food came quickly.  Ordered the pitcher of Mojitoes -- the bar staff forgot to put alcohol in the drinks --  $36 for what amounted to Shirley Temples.  

We had the pork shoulder and the glazed rib steak.  The rib steak was served sliced, and was done very nicely. It was accompanied by perfectly seasoned mashed potatoes and seven fresh green beans, which were also perfectly prepared.  It was a very good dish.  The pork shoulder dish was another story.   The pork was properly cooked but rather listless; it was accompanied by a side of flavourless horse corn and a baked puree of potato that was was non-seasoned to the point of being inedible.  

It was Restaurant week, so hopefuly for Mixto it was only a case of their having the cook staff distracted by the demands of the special menu.  But there are too may other reliable options downtown to have dinner than to take a chance on dining there again.

We've been having a great experience working with Lindsay on in-home trainings. As first-time dog owners (aside from family dogs) she's been an invaluable resource of advice and guidance. We rescued a 4-yr-old pit bull mix who definitely has some baggage in terms of anxiety likely in relation to her abandonment and Lindsay is giving us all the tools to help our dog. Behavioral training is a long journey which can be frustrating and discouraging at times, but Lindsay does a fantastic job of keeping us positive and celebrating small achievements. We feel confident having her in our corner as we work to get our dog as comfortable and confident as possible in her new life.

Disclaimer: I am English. Therefore, I had a little different expectations about this place than other may have.

Went on a Thursday - We made a reservation, but walked in totally the wrong direction which made us 20 minutes late, the staff were extremely friendly and found a seat for us. Ambiance was traditional and felt very cozy. 

Food: I was hoping for a few more TRUE British options. Of course I was expecting Bangers and Mash and Fish and Chips, but I hoped for something else like Sheppards Pie or my favorite - Scotch Eggs on the appetizer menu. It might be because the restaurant is focusing on a smaller menu, but I think these items could be added.

We ordered: Home-made Ricotta, Fish and Chips and Bangers and Mash 
Ricotta - Came with very thick delicious crusty bread. I thought it was a bowl of butter! It was so creamy and mixed with olive oil. Very delicious, but not typically English.
Fish and Chips - the fish was very salty - and this was coming from my fiance who could eat a plate full of salt.... The chips on the other hand were delicious and thick cut.
Bangers and Mash - Didn't loveeee the sausage meat, but it's never going to be 100% English. The gravy was pretty good, but again a tad salty.

I had great company, so it always makes any dining experience good, but I wouldn't rate this as top-quality English food. If you're looking for friendly staff and a fun ambiance, you will have a great time.

What's not to love about this Di Bruno Bros.? Its location in the Italian Market dates back 75 years. Though small, this may be my favorite location of all - the curated selection is top quality and allows you to get in and get out with everything you need. The cheesemongers are the best. I could walk through that store and not even look at the cheese and they will still offer a slice of whatever is being featured that day. (Note: When in Di Bruno, I always purchase cheese.)

Last year my fiancé and I took our families to Di Bruno After Hours, which is when the shop closes and you have the entire place to yourselves. You bring the wine and beer, Di Bruno hooks it up with cheese galore and various snacks. Even my stepdad, a man whose personal constitution involves something about pledging allegiance only to American cheese, found things to enjoy. 

Here's to 75 more years!

As a Mexican native i was extremely pleased and surprised by the outstanding and authentic Mexican food at Las Buganbilias. What a delicious treat.  They also have many vegetarian options.  Staff was very friendly and helpful.

Never got to try it because we showed up for a 2pm group brunch reservation at 2:10 and the kitchen "couldn't accommodate us," despite more than half the tables being empty and a 15 minute grace period. Very disappointed in the unapologetic way it was handled. Definitely triple check that they can honor previously made reservations before booking a birthday group!

Came here for breakfast on a Saturday morning and saw toward the back dining area as it was a little busier up front. Seems like a spot with a lot of regulars from the neighborhood, and everyone who worked here seemed very friendly!

We had some breakfast sandwiches that were pretty good, nothing special, but good nonetheless. Plus, the prices will be hard to beat, especially in this area. I will definitely be back to grab a quick and cheap bite in the near future.

My first ever taste of chicken & waffles and I'm a fan! Chicken was not dried out like fried chicken often is, and the chili-honey glaze gave it a nice sweet bite. The cabbage slaw gave it some nice crunch and freshness and the soft waffle tied it all together! All in all and A for Foolish Waffles!

Clave isn't the best coffeehouse in the area--that would easily belong to Kaffa Crossing on 44th and Chestnut--but it's got an awesome vibe, great music, and friendly people working there. I love that there are free refills for  in-house coffee and discounts on the rest. My only complaints are that the food is a little too pricey and that it can be damn near impossible to function--let alone read or study--when the barrista starts playing the bongos. I mean, it's unbelievable loud even if it sounds ok, and always makes everyone get a little awkward until he stops.

Went there for lunch on a Sunday. Hostess and waitress were nice/competent. Clientele was a mix of families along with some younger folks. Decor is kitschy but deliberately so.

The food was OK but the waitress pushed the guacamole sampler on us. Don't get it -- it is overpriced. We were charged a total of $30 for the sampler -- which all together was probably smaller than a single serving of guac (which costs anywhere between $10-15).

As a note - restaurant reached out, apologized for any miss in perceived value, and said they were going to reconsider the plating.

Just ok...

Adult frappé was delish.  I was asked how I'd like my burger and I ordered it "medium".  It came out  well done.  The taste was good but sadly nothing spectacular.  I would come back if someone else suggested it but it wouldn't be my suggestion for a fantastic burger.  Sorry Wahlburgers you didn't knock my socks off.

Beers are very pricy. The veggie burger sucks, but the fries were really good. The draft list is varied enough but they don't show you what kind of beer each one is. We had to look it up on google every time. The lighting sucks and the chairs are uncomfortable.

This is an extremely convenient Vitamin Shoppe location that I was very happy to find existed. I love ordering online and picking up at this location. One time they ran out of a small bottle of magnesium and gave me the larger size for no extra charge. 

I really wish that the employees/"health enthusiasts" wouldn't try to sell customers on their  subscription services. I've been asked to do this one too many times.

I placed an order for pickup, showed up, I was locked out and had to call inside and then wait in the cold for them to bring it to me which was annoying to start.

Then I got home and what the actual fuck?!? What are these abject morons doing to food?

I ordered a toasted bagel, I got home and they had smeared it in nasty butter. You don't fucking put butter on food without someone asking you for fucking butter! Straight into the trash.

I ordered a blueberry muffin, they sliced it in half and burned it on a griddle. I wanted a fucking muffin, not some rock hard burned piece of garbage.

Don't waste your money here, it's run by idiots.

DO NOT RENT HERE
I have had nothing but issues since I moved in.  First, my car got towed.  No-one told me there was assigned parking.  Then, when the pool finally opened, found out you couldn't use it with out passes that you had to pay extra for, no-one told me this either.  Contacted the numbers given to me to have the gas/electric put in my name.  Showed gas bills every month on my rental bill, now PGW says I owe them 400$ because my gas hasn't been paid since I moved in.    Boyfriend comes to visit on the weekends, emailed and told my "occupant" needs to vacate the premises within 24 hrs or I would be referred to legal.  People here are nosy and constantly in other peoples business.  My apartment has been entered without prior authorization.  People warned me before I moved in, I really wish I had listened.

I had such a nice experience here! The staff was so friendly and very kindly accommodated my dietary restrictions. The food and coffee were excellent and the space was very cute and clean. Definitely recommend!

The sports memorabilia and nostalgic gear and merchandise Mecca in the states! Mitchell and ness had become the premiere manufacture or quality and throwback jersey, hats, jackets and all fan apparel. Let me say there one and only flag ship store does not disappoint they have something for everyone and for every sport and every team!! This place is stuffed and stocked with great apparel  for whatever and whoever your a fan of!! Located in the heart of philly it takes on the old American feel of the city fits in perfectly with the vibes and feel of the people and city. The staff there is very polite and curious and always talking sports. There set up and decor are on par and it's a experience just walking in to shop. Great store great staff a must stop in the city of brotherly love for any sports and fan of any team.

I booked the room for $200 per night because it was the last available room in the city. When I went to check in the girl told me that the owner had cancelled my reservation because he couldn't process my card (which is a corporate AMEX, so I don't believe that). They never notified me that they couldn't process my card, nor did they notify me that they cancelled my reservation. She then told me that they had a room available for $400 per night. As I started to get upset (because there were no other rooms available in the city, and wasn't sure where I could go) she told me that she felt intimidated, and threatened to call the cops on me. I asked to speak to the manager, and was told that there was no way to reach him until the morning. I felt like they were trying to scam me for the higher rate. Avoid this place if at all possible.

Everything about this place is spot on from the service to the food. Did I mention the ambiance? That too was perfect from the exposed brick walls to the great looking bar. Servers knowledgeable and helpful. The kitchen knows their stuff. All the accolades are well deserved. Go!

I've had many pumpkin spice coffees since it started in the past month or so. I drive around the city a lot so I've probably stopped at 6 or 7 different dunkin donuts.   

This particular one has the worst, water downed pumpkin coffee. The first time I didn't taste it until I got home.  Took 2 or 3 sips and couldn't do no more.  It tasted like semisweet water. I've also had it prepared a lot of different ways. Black, just sugar and regular.  Nothing tastes like this. So I chalked it up to a bad brew. 

Today, I tried this same spot again. This time I tasted it before I left. Equally horrible. Last time I stopped in this dunkin was about 2 weeks ago around the same time. (9am) Returned it for a regular plain coffee. 

Heed my warning.  Try it before you buy it.

clean rooms
cheap parking
helpful staff
free breakfast 
affordable....what more could you want?

Okay, the only reason I'm giving this place 2 stars is because I've had worse service and [I realized at the end of the meal] this is a hotel restaurant.

The hostess seemed confused by my boyfriend and I entering, and decided what table to place us at after visually scanning the restaurant twice (rather than any seating chart or table:waitress ratio). The waitress came to get our drink order before we even finished getting our winter gear off.  Additionally, she asked if we knew what we wanted before we looked at the food menu; and then, came back a second time while we were clearly still discussing the menu.  I'm not sure if I'm being overly critical; however, I was a server for two years, and I know it's not that difficult to pick up on body language for appropriate timing.

The appetizers were the best thing about the meal: the onion soup (really good with a variety of cheese) and the bruschetta (lotsa tomato). I got the bacon & blue cheese burger (which came with a pickle. really?) well done, which was so awfully charred I had a few bites.  My boyfriend (who is biased because he's had real English shepherd's pie) said his shepherd's pie tasted somewhat similar to pulled pork and his salad was clearly a near-expired bagged mix without dressing.

I won't give one star because our waitress was nice and the appetizers weren't bad.

It doesn't get any better then this!
The staff is AMAZING.  Everyone is always so friendly and helpful.
A great variety of classes offering something for everyone along with all new equipment in the weight room.
 This place is exceptionally clean..you always see someone cleaning.  
 If i could give the Christian Street YMCA 10 stars I would!!!!
Their three roll combo deal is unbeatable. I've been here a couple times. One of my favorites in the city.

UPDATE: Thanks to Rocco for letting me know Fuel To Go actually DOES serve breakfast--whoops, I must have misread that somewhere. I stopped by this morning around 7:15, but I guess they don't open til a little later, so I'll try another time. It's great that Rocco has a presence on here and seems really committed to making this business great, so I'll definitely be giving them another try soon.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A little overpriced, but the food is good. I got the South Philly wrap here once, though I wish they had labeled it so I knew what was inside (whatever it was, it was good, but I still have no idea!) I love that they give calorie counts on everything--that's definitely useful. Lunchtime service is very busy and sometimes slow as a result. I also wish they had more dessert items in the to-go section because they have run out a couple times that I was there (at 11:30..or maybe they weren't out yet?) It would also be nice if they were open for breakfast so I could grab something there for breakfast or lunch. I definitely like coming here for a change when I need a quick(ish) healthy(ish) lunch.

Ordered 3 cheese steaks and Italian hogie for a lil over $30.00. Being from California I took Yelps recommendation and I'm sure glad I did. Everything I envisioned, rude smartass guy taking the order. Cash only and a line of people waiting to get into the tiny lil spot.
New favorite cafe to sit and work.  Lots of natural light, plenty of plugs, good wifi, and music isn't too loud.

I can't really figure this place out. I've been coming on and off since they opened but it's unclear to me what they're going for - gastropub? Too snotty to be a gastropub. Wine bar? Not enough explanation or guidance for it to be a wine place. It could be best described as a hipster beer place with food, and that's most likely what you're going to get. Service is sporadic and at times patronizing. I came in for dinner on a 8pm on Wednesday and was told I had approximately one hour to eat and leave (yes those words were used). I felt like I was a burden by asking for a simple dish of shishito peppers and a wheat beer based on the reaction I received, and when the food came, it seemed like they couldn't wait to get rid of me. I was one of two people at the bar! 
2 stars bc the peppers were delicious.

I will continue to say this about this specific establishment over and over - this is the most incompetent chipotle facility I have ever in my life gone to. Constantly running out of food, have workers left and right playing with their thumbs not knowing wtf to do and now saying no to customers who want a quesadilla because "it's too inconvenient and only offered online" - a bunch of clowns work here

Now, to experience absolutely delicious Cambodian food, you don't have to spend $100+ bucks at some high end asian fusion restaurant. We can all just head over to Khmer Kitchen's and dine like royalty.

First, they operate with only one server and there were times when I felt rushed with some of the dishes arriving later than expected. Portions were surprisingly humongous. I can't guarantee you'll like Cambodian food but you'll definitely be filled here. With limited seating, max capacity here is also probably around 25 people. Being a renovated corner store in the heart of South Philadelphia, KK tries it's best to accommodate it's ever-growing patrons. 

Second, everything is homemade from top quality ingredients. The authenticity cannot be beat. The following dishes rocked our socks off. 

GRILLED SEASONED MEATBALL( $5) 
I've never eaten such amazing pork meatballs in my life. Perfectly cooked. Each meatball bursted with flavors. The size was rather large as well. $5 for 12 meatballs is what you call a deal in any language. 

BOK LA-HONG ($7)
Home-made Cambodian style papaya salad packed a kick. It was made with fresh julienne cut papaya along with fresh tomatoes, fresh basils, and fresh vegetables. Did I mention the word fresh? 

PRAH-HOK KAHTEEH ($8)
If you're reading this review, get this dish! It's so good. It's essentially caramelized ground pork cooked with prahok (crushed, salted and fermented fish paste) and platted with various vegetables for dipping. 

South Philly now boasts several amazing authentic Southeast Asian places that range from Sky Cafe to Nam Phuong. You can add Khmer Kitchen to that list. You are gonna absolutely love this BYOB. No wonder discerning Yelpers' reviews average out to a full 5 star rating here!

CASH ONLY. Whatever, I'd pay them in gold if I had to. 

Be there or be square. Khmer Kitchen is the best.

A hidden nail salon with great service. I went there for a pedicure and wasn't disappointed. They have a free complimentary drink which is always good when you waiting for your nails to be done. Their dip powder prices were a little pricey but the service is good.
Can't stop going here. Turkey BLT so addicting. Add an amazing Grilled Cheese to the mix now. Please stop being so good so I can start my diet!

While visiting Philly, my boyfriend and I were dead set on getting some good seafood. Being from the midwest, we don't get fresh fish often. I chose this place based on Yelp reviews, and boy am I glad I did.

It was Sunday I believe and they were doing a chef's menus (basically using up all the ingredients they needed to get rid of) but it was still amazing. One of the best meals I've had it my life from start to finish. I will definitely be back next time I'm in town.

I am so glad that this Borders is closing because now instead of saying "Once, I was dumped by that guy Jeff the Chef outside of the Borders on Chestnut" I will be able to say I was dumped outside of "what used to be the Borders on Chestnut." 

It will make for a better story because people "in the know" will feel a sense of connection-- like they know about something that is no-longer there-- that other people (young, suburban, tourist) have no idea what we're talking about.

Also Jeff the Chef you are a jerk for dumping me on Chestnut Street.  You could have at least picked Walnut.  I know that was like five years ago but REALLY DUDE.
Went for my first experience of Korean BBQ and this restaurant did not disappoint. Food was delicious will definitely come back again

This may be my new favorite steak restaurant in Philly! My husband and I both got the bone-in aged ribeye. My steak was a fattier cut. I love steak with good marbling so I was definitely a happy camper that night. The knife literally went through the steak like butter! We also had their mashed potatoes, creamed spinach and mushrooms. The mashed potatoes were so incredibly creamy and amazing, the spinach is unlike the kind I'm used to (it still had a bite to it and not mushy like other restaurants and not as creamy), and the mushrooms had a tartness that helped cut the grease which I appreciated. The only thing is, I wished they served our steaks with more roasted garlic and pickled onions! We ended up flagging the waitress down for more mid-meal because it was such a magical pairing. Sadly, I did not have room for dessert. Maybe next time?

Coffee and fresh juices are great in the morning before work. Lunch during work. Apps after work. It can be pricey so not a routine thing; but the options are right there attached to 30th Street Station by a bridge. There are no words to describe the burger. It's AMAZING. The chicken panini has this great pesto sauce. Salads are seasonal so be sure to ask cause it might not be the one you got last time.  Good selection for when you want an alternative to the train station fare.
It is with deep and sincere sadness that I have to report that Jesse no longer works at this pharmacy. Enough said.
The manager is awful !!! Some good styles but a very cold place to be ... It's a same giving the other ZARAS such a BAD name .

Talk about bait and  switch.
I am moving to a new  location. 
1st customer service  promised everything including  rebates.  2nd customer service  rep stated that no rebates would be  offered since I am moving to an area that  has  Verizon Fios service. Asked for a  supervisor. Still waiting. I could  cancel service, pay termination fee, sign up for new service and get better pricing and debit card. I guess  this is one way for optimum  to gain market share.

Tacos: literally salty and shaped like tacobell. Not even joking.

Drinks are OK but overpriced.

I shouldn't spend $80 on Mexican food. Ever. 

(Disclaimer: I'm a Mexican from the south, so I know my shit.)

Simply excellent. This was our first time here and we were celebrating our anniversary. We sat outside facing the park and it was so nice! The bread basket is exceptional. I had the West Coast oysters (so good!) and my husband had the ravioli (loved it!) for our apps. For my meal I had the special for the day which was prime rib and it was outrageous. Cooked perfectly and melted in my mouth. My husband had a burger and was very happy as well. They even surprised us with 4 tiny macarons and a candle on a plate that said happy anniversary! The ambiance, service and food here is stellar. This place is a must.

Came specifically for the music  and paid the cover charge to be seated closer to the action.  We came 2 nights in a row because we were in town on vacation and had nothing planned other than to enjoy all that this city has to offer.  The music was great and the drinks weren't expensive at all.  The service was fast and friendly.  We absolutely had a great time.

I'm kinda sad...  :(  while in philadelphia i only ate at one cheesesteak place, and it was sonny's.  don't get me wrong, there's nothing wrong with the food.  it was pretty good, but i wanted to check out more places that specialize in the infamous cheesesteaks.  

i think we got the classic.  which is a cheesesteak with cheese whiz.  it took about 5-7 mins for it to cook, and when we got it, it was smaller than i imagined.  which is ok for me, cuz that allows me to try more items on my trip.  however, i wished they used more cheese.  i guess i should've asked them for extra cheesewhiz.  i for one like fake cheese, (think nacho cheese) and for my first real cheesesteak, sonny's was good enough for me!

On a tourist kick we went to the bar from "It's Always Sunny"  and was expecting a experience like the Bull and Finch Pub   but was greeted with a regular  close to dive bar. 
  Great Beer selection of locals and micro-brews from all over the US, not as many as other brewpubs, but a great selection across flavor profiles. Seemed like mostly college kids/ hipsters. It was quizo night, but we saw the quizo girls stop by a few time before a waitress showed up.   Food and Apps arrived.  Hummus was ok, didn't taste like it was made there.  Fish Tacos had some great heat and nice taste.   I'd rate it a little better that regular bar food, and their fries were great!

Not really great. Maybe Green Garden was so bad that in comparison Evergreen was looking worthy of a lot of praise (??) / I don't know what I was thinking last time I reviewed Evergreen so I wanted to update.
I used to like to order the mixed vegetables combo platter, but most times I ordered it, the mixed vegetables smelled kind of funky (rotten funky) and that kind of freaked me out so I haven't ordered from here in a long time. It smells so good whenever I walk on that block that I am occasionally tempted to stop in, but then I remember the rotten vegetables and keep walking.

In a word...AMAZING!!!

I've been eating here for years and even though I'm now 2 states away, anytime I find myself in Philly I find myself in Sister Muhammad's kitchen. Have you ever tasted love in food? Eaten a meal where you know the person picked out all the best ingredients and served to you what they would serve to themselves and their family? That's the magic of this place, not to mention the innovative dishes that they've created like the Fish Fried rice. I recommend you eat in, take some orders to go and never, ever forget dessert like the banana pudding and butternut squash pie, which I like to take home and warm in the oven. Also, the place is sparkling clean and the staff friendly and helpful. It's not fast food, it's good food!

Very good bagels- hot out of the wood fired oven. Love the texture and full flavor! 
Next time in Philly I'm going to enjoy them in the shop.

Let's start by saying Philly bar prices will pretty much earn any place an extra star. I found this place on Yelp just looking for somewhere simple to get beers. It was freezing out so we took a cab and said to the driver "You know where McGillin's is?" he said "Yea" I said "Alllright" 
No cover charge. Sweet.
Walk in ... PACKED! Shoulder to shoulder couldn't move an inch practically. 
I walk up to the bar... "Pitcher of Yuengling" ... $9... ah Philadelphia how I love your dirt cheap prices. $9 Pitchers on a Saturday night in the city center. Ah McGillin's how I wish you would stop letting people in at some point. I asked the door man about the cut off and he said "We'll never stop letting people in." 
We ended up standing for about an hour before I could snag some seats. 
I remember the music being a pretty decent mix for a college bar + some country. Whenever I'm in Philly I forget that it's part of Pennsylvania and the the rest of the state is...country. It's just odd to me to be in a bar and everyone pop off when some country song I've never heard of comes on. All in all it was a pretty good time. 

They serve food?

I chose this doctor because of the positive reviews, so I owe it to Yelp to add my opinion.  Unexpected charges and poorly managed insurance billing made things very frustrating for me, just do your homework first.

I don't eat in hotel Restaurants or bars very often, but I tried this place out and the food was really good and I didn't think it was too badly priced either. I loved the drunken mushroom starter. The staff were also very nice and our drinks never went empty. What more can you ask for.

Let's start off with the roti canai (Indian Pancake) with chicken and potato curry. It is a must get. Maybe two or three.. $3.95, for one piece of roti is a bit pricey considering how thin it is, you will not get any fulfillment for this.But it is so good!

I've passed by here many time and thought it would be like a similar place I've been to do so I've only avoided it until the boyfriend brought me here the other night. I was pretty impressed and is still craving that roti canai.

I wasn't sure what to order so I went through the Yelp pictures until I found something that caught my eye. The Indian Mee Goreng (Indian style stir-fried egg noodles in an authentic mild dried squid sauce with tofu, potatoes, shrimp, egg, grounded peanuts, and bean sprouts.) This was just tasty enough with the shrimp shells crispy enough for me to just bite and eat through.

I like how fast the food some out and it's HOT. I will definitely come back here for the two dishes that I got to try. How about two pieces of roti next time though?

Going through the menu, I also wanted to try the roti telur because it is filled with eggs and onions but the boyfriend and I did not like it as much as the roti canai. There is a lot of onions if you like that though.

BAP is a cute little hole in the wall place that I wouldn't have known about if I hadn't stumbled by it while hungry at 4pm. Service was quick, since I was the only one there, and the prices are pretty low, but I was disappointed to receive the smallest portion of bibim bap I've ever had. Despite the fact that the beef bibim bap costs $2 more than the vegetable version, there was very little meat in the dish. The food was ok, but I prefer Giwa, and it didn't come with any sides. The rice also comes pre-coated in spicy sauce, which I didn't mind but may be a problem for people who prefer their food totally mild or want to customize the spice level. Altogether a perfectly fine Korean joint, but next time I'll probably walk the extra few blocks to go to Giwa.

I went to Jamonera excited because a good friend recommended the place. I was not impressed with the menu or ambiance. The menu was small and very limited. The restaurant seemed overcrowded due to the limited space. The tables were too close together and there was no sense of privacy. 

Tapas and small plates can be filling and rewarding but that wasn't the case her. The wait staff encouraged ordering 6-8 small plates to share, now I know why. We tried the papas fritas, skirt steak, manchego stuffed meatballs, grilled spare ribs and a paella dish. The papas fritas were the best thing we ordered, they were crisp and flavorful. The steak was tough, the meatballs were a little salty and the ribs were too dry. 

While this was a new experience, I knew that I would not go through the doors here again, for this was not appealing to my taste buds.

First time having a grilled cheese and I might have set the bar too high! I got the Brielle which has Brie, cranberry chutney, caramelized onions and roasted pine nuts. It's the #1 favorite of the cashier who helped me. 

TIP: he suggested adding chicken, truffle oil, and roasted garlic. OMG so worth it! Brielle on steroid.

Everything about it was delicious. The bread they used has a little hint of sweetness, grilled to perfection with a buttery flavor. The cranberry chutney has real pieces of cranberries, adding a nice touch to the savory sandwich without overwhelming the main flavor of Brie. Combining with onions and pine nuts and you're in heaven. I wish I had one right now...

While I was waiting for my food, I saw them preparing the Valley Thunder sandwiches in bulk, probably for a catering order. Wow even looking at them being made me question my choice of Brielle haha. I honestly think you can't go wrong with any of the grilled cheese sandwiches here.

Staff was so genuinely friendly. Can't wait to come back!

I came here with two friends on a Saturday night. We tried going to other restaurants, but they were all packed. Surprisingly, there was no one in this restaurant, so we decided to stop in. The interior was tiny, but cute. The service was friendly, but inattentive. She seemed more worried about takeout orders than our table. The food was average at best. Nothing special, except for their cheap prices. I probably won't be coming back unless I'm stuck with nowhere to go.

Came here for dinner Thursday night. Having been away from the city for 2 years i forgot that even at 8pm the good places are still packed. so we ate in the lounge upstairs.  The service was fantastic. We had a few sushi rolls which were fantastic and very fresh. Probably the best basic sushi i have ever had. They have a huge sashami menu which I also had the swordfish which was amazing as well.. Price was a bit high but for center city sushi it is about what we expected to pay. Cant wait to come back.

I have tried spin at a number of places in the city and this is one of the finest places I have been.  The bikes were a little unusual but easy to adjust and actually easier than most to use. The staff was warm and friendly. The teacher was excellent, and her music (unlike most places) was not too loud or too offensive.  The price is competitive, but you can do one class for free. I was lucky to get a bike where I could see in a mirror (I love to watch my form), but there were a lot of bikes that did not have mirrors nearby.  There is a bathroom with a shower but I am not sure the shower is for spinners as opposed to employees.  They give you towels and have water for purchase at the usual way-too-high-how-does-water-cost-this-much price.

We just went back to Franklin's for the first time since shortly after they opened. They now have a very respectable bottled craft beer selection (mostly local), and a large number of fairly run-of-the mill taps. Price for beers was very reasonable. We had an order of wings and poutine (fries with gravy and cheese), both of which were great. The staff was friendly and attentive, and we enjoyed sitting out on the deck with our dog. The host brought out a bowl of water and some biscuits for her.

It's nice to see that Franklin's is doing well, and I'm sure we will be returning throughout the summer.

Great selection of beers, and servers are super attentive.  Nice vibe in South philly and roating menu. Check out the mac in cheese it is to die for. Also they have a happy hour option which is pretty decent.

They did an amazing job creating a custom made wedding band for my engagement ring. I looked everywhere for a matching band for my ring but could not find anything. They suggested a custom made wedding band, explained the process to me, and gave me a very reasonable price. A few weeks later I had a beautiful ring that I can't wait to wear for the rest of my life!

More like 3.5 I would say. I am rating up because of the convenience they offer.

I have to admit that I am really lucky to have so many resources in my new neighborhood. This UPS store being one of them. While there are post offices also nearby, sometimes I head to this store to ship packages out. I have stopped by several times now and the employees are always nice. 

They only allow 2 customers in the store at a time and you have to wait in line outside but the times I've gone, the line moved pretty quickly. They also enforce social distancing when inside the store. 

They also offer mail box services which is kind of cool.

3.5 Stars for all round experience at this new Philly brewpub. 

Quick takeaways include:

- Great large, open, modern loft style space

- Lots of seating options - Bar, tables, High-top tables and large outdoor patio seating

- 25 plus taps many with Goose Island's better beers (Lolita, Sofia, Juliet, Matilda and Halia)

- Full bottle selection including some of the glorious Bourbon barrel stouts 

- Food was OK for bar style menu. I liked the charcuterie plate but it was a bit pricey for what you get

- Lots of GI merchandise including the actual goose tap handles, many T-shirts and a full set of branded glass wear 

From a "needs work" perspective:

- The in house brewed beers were not very good at all. Stick with the GI classics and you will be fine. Funny, I found the same at the Toronto, Canada and Chicago GI brewpubs as well. Most of my party came to this conclusion as well

- The staff were not as knowledgeable as they should be when it comes to beer. This may just be growing pains of a new location

- Location is not ideal but great if your attending a concert at the Fillmore. 

In the end, the trend of new weaker in house brewed beers keeps the brewpub rating at 3.5 stars. That said, I absolutely love their stouts which I will return for whenever I'm in the area.

BK

For a moment I am in far away land as I walk the bridge. Both sides have water lilies wildly growing in the ponds in the deeper part of summer. I am not in Philly anymore... exhale. Where's my beach chair!?

I was looking for somewhere to eat while running errands downtown.  I got a sandwich from Herschel's East Side deli.  I forgot how thick the sandwich was.  I ate it down with a fork to make it easier.

It was loud and touristy, but I was expecting that.  Whatever you order get it "to go" as finding a seat is not fast, even if you have someone scouting ahead for you.

a.bar is one of my favorite places for creative and quality cocktails. Also fun to sit outside and watch the foot traffic at the corner of 18th and walnut just outside of the square. The staff is very nice and friendly and the burgers are so good, too!!

Ordered dinner for my wife and myself
From the soup to the main course everything was hot and delicious
Been going there for years never disapointed

Easy 5 star experience. Food, service, and ambiance all shine here. Placed a takeout order and picked up in 20 mins - super easy. The food was piping hot, well packaged, and delicious. Great option in the neighborhood...and also worth a drive in if you're craving good Thai food!

Flavors here are really on point! The vegetarian combo comes with a great variety of lentils, greens, and veg that are all flavored differently and all a very good representation of ethiopian cuisine! The injera here is so fluffy and light, and the portion was the best deal I've come across for the vegetarian option! Also tried the special kitfo, which was spiced well, just over cooked. Will try it again and specify that I like it rare, and try again, because this little place is really sweet!
Rude and nasty staff,  dismissive of you unless you intend to buy something!!!  Horribly run store!

This restaurant is  like a small oasis among the crowded madness that is Reading Terminal. A nice place for a meal or a drink, without having to fight for a seat at a public table that may, or may not, be clean.

The sloppy joe is the standout for lunch at Molly Malloy's. It's so delicious. It seems like I inhale it in less than a minute. And the owner is so nice an accommodating. She immediately handled a minor mishap with my girlfriend's meal, making sure we left thinking about what a great time we had there as opposed to the mishap.

I can add Molly Malloy's to the list of top notch food spots in Reading Terminal. Don't even think about grabbing a burger next door to Molly's at Hunger Burger. Get the burger at Molly's instead!
Contract paid in full, June, 2017.  Work still uncompleted as of March, 2019.  Repeated phone calls go unanswered.  Stay away from this business.

Went there today with my boyfriend for Sunday brunch. I got the chicken and waffles. It was a little spicier then most chicken and waffle dishes but still very good! The service was good, and there was no wait. ( Hard to find on a Sunday at 1:00pm)

Admittedly I'm biased, as a dear friend of mine worked here for years while attending college at UArts, but nonetheless, Le Castagna is a great italian restaurant in philly. Quality food, fresh ingredients, and a wonderfully friendly staff all help add to the charm of this neighborhoody restaurant. 

Be sure to try the pastas- they are made in house and are scrumptious! Also, they often have inventive ice cream flavors to enjoy... have a food adventure and experiment! you can't go wrong here!

I got into Philly and came straight here. After standing in line for 20 minutes, I ordered the sandwich and before I could finish, the guy slathered a layer of cheddar cheese sauce on there. I said but I wanted Provolone. The guy who puts the sandwich together says in a bored voice "it's already done" and the cashier just says it's too late. So I had to eat a Philly, not the way I wanted. But in the end the sandwich was just "meh"

Charlie's Steakery chain is even better.

I went to The Victoria Freehouse for lunch the other day, and I really enjoyed it. I liked the layout of the place and loved the Harry Potter banners. What I'm assuming was the owner was really nice. We ordered the crab on toast, curry chips, pickled beetroot deviled eggs, and chicken and chips. It was all very good. Even my fiancé enjoyed it, and he's usually really picky.

They have a really nice beer menu. Pages and pages and pages of good brew. Their drafts were very pleasing as well, although not nearly extensive (obviously).  Staff were Ahhhhiiittteeee.... Pretty down to Earth and not too full of their own Shite. The food was Uhhhhh.... Alright.  I mean its a pub. Good munchies.

Kevin was awesome!  He arrived a little early, which was a pleasant surprise and big relief as it was a weekday morning and I had to get to work right after our appointment.  We had a couch and chair cleaned and he did a great job- they look wonderful and smell great!  Not to mention, he was very personable and professional and the price can't be beat.  Will definitely use again!

My favorite BYOB in Philly! I must have eaten here 20 times now. It's unpretentious and just perfect. I hope they never change a thing. 

Favorites: grilled octopus, pil pil shrimp, soft shell crab, grilled whole fish, bluefish with pasta, almond cake, and really anything else on the menu.

Worst service ever! When we picked up our cat there wasn't one on the spot we were told to go. When we dropped it off they didn't have curb check in because we had the wrong car. Horrible service will not use again.

I'm not a fan of the Eagles or any professional football team, but I do enjoy going to the games with my Eagles fans/friends. Eagles fans are passionate.
