I love the atmosphere of a crowd that loves (and hates) their team. I've heard all the cheers and all the jeers. A lot of alcohol fueled salty language is always present. Warning: DO NOT wear the opposing team's jersey. You will be taunted and tormented to no end. It is funny until the fists get thrown.

The ice cream tastes like nothing.

N-o-t-h-i-n-g.

This place is all about toppings and manages to completely ignore the brick and mortar of the ice cream cone. It's kind of like being a butterface. Or a hot person with a crappy personality. The toppings are great, but come on. Amp up the vanilla flavor, will ya?

Went here for this first time about a week after it opened.

Food was phenomenal. The papaya salad was amazing - spicy and unlike just about anything I've eaten. The chicken curry also not to be missed. And cheap with plenty to take home for tomorrow.

Clearly, a casual family run place. The owner seems super nice and chatted with us about the food and opening a restaurant in south philly.

Can't wait to go back!

Federal Donuts, you do not need another glowing 5 star review but what the heck, I am going to give it to you anyways.  You see, I recently went to a "friendsgiving" where in place of turkey, Federal Donuts chicken was served in all of its glorious flavor options.  As always, it was knock your socks off phenomenal.  Actual turkey thanksgiving would be ashamed of itself.

Your donuts are awesome (even my dog eats them when I stupidly leave them on the table and turn my head for just one second).  Your chicken is awesome.  Your coffee is awesome. Your staff is awesome. 

So happy holidays to you, Federal Donuts.  You are worth every lb. I gain each time I indulge in your heavenly fried wonderfulness.

This is what we look for when we want a nice dinner.  And it was surprisingly inexpensive even with drinks, an appetizer and dessert!  Service was awesome from greeting through to the end, though it did take a long time to clear our dishes.  We started with their cask flight, a Bombardier, and the marinated olives.  The cask flight was great and the DuClaw both smelled and tasted delicious.  The marinated olives were good, but I think I would have preferred just plain brined olives.  It was a good mix and was great on their bread.  We had the rabbit pie and macaroni and cheese.  The rabbit pie was easily one of the best meals I've ever had - super flakey pastry on top and delicious filling with nicely sized pieces of rabbit.  Macaroni and cheese was also really good and we loved the cheese they used.  For dessert, we had the banoffee split and it exceeded our expectations.  Every piece of it was delicious from the pastry base to the browned butter ice cream and the candied hazelnuts and brûlée banana slices.  The overall restaurant was nice as well, comfortable seating that was broken into several rooms and we weren't too close to other parties.
Service is excellent, my waitress Twanda was wonderful and my food was everything !! I would recommend this place to everybody, just make sure Twanda is your waitress...

I recently moved back into the area from New England and while I was away I finally made the jump to blonde. One thing that worked amazing for me during the lightening process was a product called Olaplex. Since I was moving back here to Philly it was a must that the salon I went to used this treatment on my hair. I scouted olaplex's website and got a long list of salons in the Philly area that carry this treatment. With the help of Yelp I began to research. I came across Ozzies Salons reviews and pictures and immediately called to schedule a consultation. (This is something he doesn't say you have to do, but he was nice enough to take my request!) I like to schedule them because I have very curly hair and a lot of it! So it sometime scares hairdressers away from wanting to work with it. Ozzie however said it wouldn't be a problem. He gave me one task which was to get in touch with my previous hairdresser to see the formula she used on me. The day of I was excited to finally have my roots done, little did I know Ozzie was going finally achieve the lightness I've wanted and that so many others have told me that it couldn't happen! It looks awesome and I love it! Afterwards I got a lot of complements for random people about how nice it looked and that it suited my complexion. The tint to my hair is a bit bluer than normal because I asked him to add more blue tint to the toner since I like having the color stick around for a little bit longer.  It was a fun experience! He is really nice and takes his time to make sure everything is great with your hair. I for sure will be back to see him!

Me and some bros hit up this jawn after the Sixers game. The 76suckers lost but BonChon made up for it. 

We got in at around 10pm on a Tuesday and the place was still lively with customers. We were able to get a table on the second floor overlooking the bar. Korean MV's were playing on the big screen as we waited for our 20/20 wings and drums. The finger lickins came out hot and fresh and relatively quickly. The waiter dropped a drum stick on the table but nbd, 5 second rule I guess. I let my bro have that one anyway. 

So tender, so crispy, umami oooo mami! It's really not fair that I have to drive 1.5 hours for Bonchon cuz it's a craving that no other chicken can satisfy. Not KFC, not BWW not Poopeyes. Ohh Bonchon I miss u already...til we meat again.

*Please do something about those grezzy slippy floors btw.

We just moved to the area and went on a Friday night. I thought the service was great that night. Their beer was tasty and the food was good. It wasn't the best in the world but pretty good and the place is a nice place to take friends when they come to visit. Its a decent hang out place and I would go again because I had fun  that night but I think it had more to do with the company I kept than giving praise all to the place...

WOW, this was a great omakase experience! It was not my first time having omakase, but it was my first time having it in Philly. After talking to the owner during my experience, I think he has an even cooler story. He used to work at a three Michelin star restaurant in NYC doing omakase for around hundreds of dollars per person, and he wanted to bring the omakase experience to more people for a budget friendly price--which led to his opening Sakana in Philly. Philly is in need of a much larger Omakase scene. I feel the chef's pain in that many people don't know what omakase is or what it should be, so they judge it incorrectly or don't even want to try it.  (Please look up what omakase is beforehand, so you're not completely surprised! It's similar to a tasting menu, in that you are not ordering a la carte.) That being said, I appreciate the chef's value in bringing the dining experience to Philly and trying his best to share it with more people at a better price. Like, what more could you ask for! 

I went with a small group of people (3), and we all ordered the sakana omakase, which is $108 per person and around 21 courses. It includes a kaiseki, at least ten to twelve pieces of fish and a wagyu dish at the end. For the price, it's pretty amazing what we got. It's also byob, and they open and chill the wine for you! The fish was amazing!!! My favorites included the Peking duck taco, which was part of the kaiseki and something the chef said he made at Masa in NYC, the scallop ngiri piece, the tiger shrimp piece and the double toro ngiri. At a certain point, you lose track of what and how much you've eaten, but it was really quite an experience. I will definitely be back!

I don't know what the hype is here. It's an ok bar but nothing too great or memorable. 

The food was ok, it was bad but it wasn't mind blowing either. 

The service was good I give them that but it's also because it was a Sunday evening and not so packed. 

The atmosphere of the place is what I really loved. It was a great and friendly place to be at. They had karaoke, the bartenders and waitresses were singing along to the DJ, it was a lot if fun. It makes for a great hang out spot. 

The alcohol is why I'm only giving it 3 stars. I ordered a Long Island ice tea and it was horrible. My wife got a Fuzzy Navel and I swear it was missing the alcohol. Thinking it was maybe a mistake we have them another shot at it. We got another round and nope, same thing. IDK if its maybe because I'm not from around here and in California they make things stronger but these guys need to up the alcohol content in the drinks.

I came in on a Sunday afternoon and sat at the counter. Though the counter was filled with servers, I did not receive an acknowledgement from any of the staff. I sat there for 10 minutes before I went and got my own menu. I asked a server if she was the server for the counter and she rudely informed me that she only took care of the right side of the counter. I had to wait for my server who apparently was no where to be found. Normally I understand a busy restaurant, but usually a server would go grab the server who's station it is or at least grab the customer a cup of water. I left and will never go to this place again. As others mentioned, there are way better restaurants in the area. This place does not deserve your money.

This place was a total gem. I went in thinking they only took cash, because that's what one reviewer said so I stopped in WAWA to withdrawal cash for no reason. The restaurant had a touch screen ordering system which was super customizable and it took credit cards. I ordered roasted the miso cod and salmon bao. Both were delicious. The chef and staff was super friendly. The chef even came out to ask everyone how their food was.

I ate at Del Frisco's last night for the second time in three weeks.  If I could give them a zero I would.  On my first trip with 4 people they served two people at our table and the other two of us had to wait an additional 20 minutes for our dinner.  Last night our steak and king crab came out cold, they forgot our salads, the foie gras appetizer was cold when it was supposed to be served hot out of the pan.  How do you screw up this bad this many times.  And the services was slow and very inconsistent.

Horrible experience! Went on a Sunday around 7 nd we ordered our food..after a while waiter comes back nd says they don't have any more French Fries!! Because we order 5 meals and they only had one order of fries. So we're like okay that's fine.. so he goes back nd comes back saying another item on the menu was out too. So we asked for something else..guess what they ran out ! So we finally re ordered our whole meal making sure they had everything. Now the moment of truth..food comes we're all excited nd take our first bite nd guess what !!? Food is COLD ! Specially the only order of fries they had! After that took a bite of my Mac n cheese and almost immediately puked in my mouth! It tastes like expired dairy. So you tell me what kind of business runs out of good nd serves cold nd expired food!? Definitely will not recommend this place. However the vibe is chill decorations are nice but the fooood is garbage!

This place is an absolute gem. I don't think it's possible to get a dish here that isn't superb. 

We've been several times now and while the service can be a tad slow in actually getting you to order, the servers are always fast and attentive in removing plates, checking on your meal, etc. 

The food is -- well, it's amazing, frankly. The foie gras is fantastic and the ouef de pecheur tastes way better than any poached egg dish has a right too. (I literally could not stop exclaiming about how good it was -- even as I shoveled it in my face. Just order it. Trust me.) 

Entrees were all excellent as well, though in my experience, they tend to do fish slightly better than red meats. We've always gotten an amuse bouche to start and a small desert tasting comped, which is a nice touch -- especially since we never have room to actually order desserts (which look awesome).

The space itself is very nice. Inside dining room is classic Paris bistro, and the outdoor "garden" is lovely in the evening. Can't recommend this place enough.
It was a good place to sit in for beer with friends. I loved the vibe and it was entertaining to watch people sing on the stage.

I am updating my review on WCL. Despite my last experience I returned to WCL because First Person Arts performances are held there (and I love First Person Arts programs!). Remembering my last eating experience, I stuffed oranges and apples in my bag - honestly -- to my husband's horror.  At WCL, I asked my waitress if the chef would mind steaming me a plate of vegetables.  She said yes; he did it; and I enjoyed a great plate of deliciously prepared vegetables  Best of all, I did not have to embarrass my hubby by eating my stash!

Seating was fast and the staff was honestly super friendly from the start. We came on Saturday night and it didn't look busy at all. Once we were seated our server informed us that there was no clean silverware for whatever reason so we had to eat with plastic forks and knives of what I'm guessing was part of the "to-go" utensils. Once we ordered and received our food we noticed that the plate that had preserved sweet potato fries instead of regular fries had about 14 fries while my friends that ordered regular fries got a decent portion of fries.  The serving size of that grilled lemon chicken was terrible and not worth the money. Given that this is chain restaurant I expect more of it. Joseph our waiter was super friendly, outgoing and just overall good vibes and was quick to fix the fries issue, I know the things that we really dislike was not his fault. The staff and our server are the only reasons I didn't give this Applebee's 1 star.
Kept me on hold over seventeen mins to tell me there are NO appointments to see a psychiatrist until August (this is June). This is Penn MEDICINE I'm talking about. No joke.

When asked to choose between Pat's and Genoe's I'll take Pat's 100 out of 100 and I judge a person's character who likes Genoe's (not even kidding). With that said Pat's is in the bottom half of my top 10 steaks in the city for a number of reasons but one of the biggest is its size. If you're hungry get two but it is a great 3am snack after a night of boozing. OG Jim's in West Philly (not South St) is hands down the best steak you will ever have.

Stopped in here last night for a couple of drinks and some apps. Love O'Neals Happy Hour specials. Never a bad idea.
GREAT prices for sips and bites and friendly staff to top it off? Yes.
Nothing fancy or too crazy. Just a great place to come and hang out, relax, watch the game, play darts, cards, Quizzo...whatever. Great time.

Their  philly cheesesteak is more tasty than the original cheesestake.I am fan of their smoothies and sandwhichs.This a takeout place and does not have seating arrangement to accomodate dine in customers.

Love the food, ambiance, service! Everything was perfect! It was our 10 th anniversary and we were supposed to fly out but with COVID we couldn't! This was our stay local dinner plan and I can't say how amazing they made us feel! 

Absolutely recommend and would love to go back

I had to wait until the next week for an appointment.  Received two automated confirmations.  Contractor was a no show with no notice.  Called office, got called back by Junior who gave me several weak excuses and absolutely no guarantee as to when the estimator was going to show and no explanation as to why I did not get a call regarding the no show.  Estimator calls me and states the earliest he can get over is in the afternoon (it was a 9:30 am appt).  Waste of my time.

TERRIBLE SERVICE
I had out of town guests and took them to Bareburger. We were a party of 4. When we entered, we were behind a party of 2. The party of 2 was immediately seated at a 4-person booth, while there were multiple tables for 2 available. Host told us they were just clearing another booth (and pointed to it) and said we would be seated there shortly. In the meantime a party of 5 came in after us. It took 10 minutes to clear the table and then another hostess came up to the original host to ask who was waiting for a table. He told her our party of 4 and a party of 5. She proceeded to seat the party of 5 at the booth, adding a chair to the end of the table. There were no other tables, let alone booths, available for 4. When we told her we were supposed to be seated first, she said there was no other place for the party of 5 and it was just logical to seat them there!?? In what world is that logical?  We walked out and will not go back. Went down the block to Barra Rossa, seated within 5 minutes, had excellent service!

Another annoyance at Bareburger, while we were waiting to not be seated, a hostess came and shouted at the several groups of people waiting to MOVE TO THE BACK OF THE RESTAURANT SO PEOPLE CAN GET THROUGH! Has management never heard of customer service training?

Great escape room experience. Booked the escape room for my staff for a team building experience. The escape room staff was very attentive with emails and phone calls prior to booking. I appreciated their level of professionalism. When we got there (dressed in appropriate 80s garb which I highly recommend to get people in the spirit of the game), the escape room staff oriented us to the escape room and outlined the expectations. This escape room is comprised of four rooms which is one of the reasons why I picked it over others with fewer rooms. The staff were very helpful and had great energy. The rooms were well taken care of and the theming is just great! We had a great time, lots of laughs, and lots of teamwork. I felt the escape room accomplished exactly what intended - a fun, bonding experience.

Okay, this should really be about 4 1/2 stars because this has been my nail salon since I've moved downtown. As most people have mentioned, you can probably pay less other places, but what has me coming back is that the manicurists are super detail oriented to the point of being anal (in a good way)! 

I am by nature, "low maintenance," but when I need to get high maintenance ready, read: special event or just to feel pampered, I can count on these ladies to fix months of abuse. Haven't had a pedicure since last summer? No problem. You'll walk out floating on air. Bite your fingernails and your cuticles are a wreck? You'll be hand model ready by the time they are done.

I always leave with a tingly perfect 10 (or 20) and I have no qualms recommending this salon to anyone, including men! (You know who you are). The best part is that even if you put your nails through the grinder, you can expect your mani or pedi to last a good 2 weeks or more. Let's just say you'll get tired of the color before you need to come back. Rest assured, you're in good hands here.

A bakery with amazing bubble tea that I think often gets overlooked.

Bread Top is my favorite Chinatown bakery. I find that the different buns that are available here are much fresher than some other places in Chinatown. This is also my favorite place to get egg tarts, which have a nice and flaky crust with a soft sweet egg filling in the middle. I think part of the reason people skip over this place is because it's not located on the "central" street of the Philadelphia Chinatown (10th). 

The only reason Bread Top is so great is their bubble tea (which I think is their hidden gem). While their tapioca isn't the best I've had (it's a little soft, but still very chewy which is good), this place is the only place I know of that makes their drinks with real fruit. In the fridge they have near the counter, you can see tubs of fresh fruit (watermelon, honeydew, strawberries, etc...), and when you order one of the drinks, they'll scoop the fruit right out of the container into a blender. My favorite is the taro. 

The store owners are also very friendly. If you haven't been here, you should definitely try it out. Otherwise, you're missing out!

I love this place!! Cheap ($35) brazilian wax and in and out in 15-20 mins. If you're used to the cushy "hi, hello, how are you today? Would you like some coffee or tea?" b.s., then, sooooo not the place for you. These women, do a great job with a flair of, well, arrogance. And I LOVE that!! Underneath, they are a bunch of softies, especially Emma. She'll even give you recommendations on what to do and NOT to do after your 'special' wax. Did I mention they're cheap AND quick?

It simply doesn't get any better than this!

Great selection of lamps for sale, and incredible deals on repairs--even of antique items!
In addition, there is no pretension, no hype, among the staff.  It's a no-frills operation,
but if you're looking for quality and sane prices, who cares?

I

I had amy jo today for my first experience with hard wax for my brazilian. After having a bad experience at another salon with paper strips i was nervous. She was so friendly and personable. She explained the entire process and all the products she was using throughly. I was in and out in a half hour. Nice and easy and painless. I highly recommend amy and also if it's your first time you get half off :)

We normally go to the Trolley Car CAFE for breakfast, but traffic was being diverted last Sunday due to some kind of marathon. We decided to try the DINER instead.  

The menu has tons of stuff on it, and it all sounded good. basically, it's everything the CAFE offers, and a whole lot more. Finally, I decided on the crab omelette, potatos and pancakes.  I enjoyed my meal, but my bf thought it was just OK.  He had the Eggs Benedict. The pancakes were absolutely TERRIBLE... really gritty and dry.  

We both agreed that although the diner's interior has CLEARLY seen better days, it was cozy.  Our waitress was nice, so overall, I enjoyed the experience and would return.
Food is usually pretty good but the service is really horrendous. Many of the servers are snarly and expect to wait a long, long time for your food. Drinks are really expensive as well, just FYI.

I frequent World Cafe Live, both upstairs and downstairs, often. They usually have great music. The sound is better downstairs, but that's to be expected, since that's where the bigger acts play. The ambience is nice. The servers are pretty attentive for a music venue; I have yet to have a truly bad experience with the waitstaff.

Their beer selection is all right. They have a rotating Victory tap and usually a Dogfish Head tap, so I'm pretty happy. They even had Hop Wallop on tap, which is a fine, fine beer. The beer prices are pretty standard for Philly. However, the fancy drinks are pretty ridiculously priced. I once got a Strawberry Shortcake drink, and although it was delicious, it wasn't quite worth $10. Also, the well drinks are $7 or thereabouts, which is pretty outlandish. I think they use middle-shelf liquor, at least, and not Vladimir or Banker's Club. The food is okay, but a little expensive for what they offer. The veggie chili was okay, if maybe a little thick. But $8 for a grilled cheese seems like highway robbery to me. I know I'll be back here, but probably just for beer and music.

Ate a wonderful lunch after running the Hot Chocolate 15K on 4/1/17. Service was friendly, quick and helpful. We were greeted with a pot of excellent tea and a smile. The eggplant with chicken in garlic sauce was extraordinary. We also ordered a house special of shrimp, scallops and chicken with fresh mango and veggies in a sweet and sour sauce. The dish was well prepared but a bit too sweet for me...I think it would have been outstanding if done in a ginger or black bean sauce instead. All in all, an excellent destination for dining in Philly.
Two Wongs definitely make it right!

as a contractor i have installed many kitchens that my customers bought from here, they are low quality and do not last,they look good in the catalog and showroom floor but buyer beware ! the furniture is also pretty low in quality.

I've stopped going to this D&D cause the service was slow in the morning and they would get bombarded by the high school students when the performing arts school is in session. A positive is that they have off street parking - but no drive thru. The lot is small and is often filled with taxis. Not my first choice as there are other/better options.

I am torn between 4 and 5 stars.  I love to get fresh dairy cheeses here and also Grana Padano and other Grating cheeses.  The line can get lengthy as they serve each person as an individual (which is nice.)  Worth the wait for yummy cheese!!!!

If I could give zero stars i would.  I went there Friday for severe abdominal pain i was given morphine sent home told to see gastro Dr. Threw up for a day. Nursing staff was the worst.  The doctor was clueless!
Love this Shabu shabu! The meat is really good and the seafood is really fresh! If you never have Shabu Shabu before, this is the place for you!
If foosgoonewild had a restaurant..and the food is mad good also. Come for the gram, stay for the food. Not sure which will end up more infamous but blessed to have em
Came here on a random suggestion during an outing. Loved the atmosphere and the service was great. The food was also fantastic. Definitely a good place for good grub and good atmosphere.

I got the crispy wings plain with sauces in the side. I did this so I could try some variety. They were meaty and nicely cooked. I made a mistake with one of the sauces thinking it was something else. I think the next time I'll try the wings not crispy and plain. And then I will get the sauce I originally wanted. 
Nice staff and very quick!

This is my first time flying American Airlines in probably over 10 years, and the only reason I flew at this time was because they had a direct flight from Philadelphia to Key West, if I could have found another airline with a direct flight I would've taking that. It was very nice that they did give me the option a few days before my flight to upgrade to first class which was a pleasure going to Key West, we get to the Key West airport and they only had one baggage carousel working when in the airport they have to so everyone on the flight stood there for over 45 minutes waiting for their luggage to come off. The flight home out of Key West international airport was a disaster the flight did not leave until 3 PM and it was supposed to leave at 2:48 PM and then we get into Philly and they never tell us what baggage carousel to go to to pick up our luggage. I don't think I will be flying AmericanAirlines anytime in the near future. The customer service is not great at all and on a three hour flight home I saw the flight attendant one time throughout the whole trip that's when she brought us a bag of pretzels and a soda never came up to check make sure everything was OK.

Nice restaurant in Manayunk! They are usually pretty booked for dinner, so make a reservation if you can. The drink selection is pretty good! In terms of food, I'd highly recommend the cheese plate and steak Mac n cheese! The service was a little slow, but we also first tried this place on the 4th of July. We will definitely be back :)

When it first opened up, BWS was the place to pull some heavy-hitters for ridiculous prices. Many pink fingers held up private press PCP biker lps, stoned loners, etc. from the cheap bins, grinning like weasels. It was a great place to still possibly score some hits in town. Over the years the owner, staff or whoever have certainly gotten more hip to the gems, but they still price fair and solid. The staff is great, by the way!

I must say that I am a fan of Spruce Street Harbor Park and I have already been twice since it reopened for the season two weeks ago. It has improved a lot since last year, but I think could get even better with each passing year.

It gets pretty packed on the weekend, so my recommendation is to try for a weeknight. Don't even bother trying to get on a hammock on the weekend, but if you do happen to get lucky, they are tons of fun. Maybe a little dirty though (I heard bums sleep there at night!).

My favorite thing to do is grab a beer and just hang out in the beer garden area on the sand. There a number of chairs and benches, but honestly for how much foot traffic this place gets, I'm surprised they didnt make the beer garden much, MUCH, bigger this year. For what it's work, it's nice to sit on the water and just chill. 

For food, so far this year, I've seen the return of CHIFA, federal doughnuts, and distrito. For ice cream, Franklin Fountain!!!!!

My recommendations would be, a yeungling summer wheat, the chicken sandwich from federal, and a cone of your fave franklin flavor! If you get a place to sit, don't let it go!!!!!! 

Oh and park in old city in the slanted spots and just walk down!!

What a phenomenal experience.  It was our first time taking a Segway tour and they made sure were safe and comfortable before starting.  The guide was awesome! He was responsible, respectful and helpful. We did the 2 hours tour and made a huge square around all the areas.  I really felt like we were able to see a lot during the tour.  I liked being on the road and moving a little faster. I highly recommend this tour.  Make sure you leave 10-15 min for walking from parking to Segway rental.

Love love love the tehini shake it was sooo good not sweet just right.. will try a different flavor next time.. maybe the one with the coffee... love the falafel balls..crispy on outside but soft on the inside... a little salty but its ok.. i dont mind the saltiness especially after dipping it in the tahini sauce that comes with the bag of balls... im not a fan of amba sauce.. good things theres the tahini sauce... but that shake is a must!!! i got mine delivered and scheduled it the day before coz they get really busy..and on the day of the delivery, they also moved the time one hour earlier..good thing i was available to receive the order..

0 star.

They are sent by American Home Shield to fix our washer/dryer. They came to our place with no tools or gadgets to diagnosis the issue, pushed couple buttons around on the washer panel, then left in no time. The in next one and half months, they just completely disappeared from the mother earth. 
Once we were able to reach them via AHS in Feb, they just ordered a new part via AHS, but still washer is not working, then they suggested to AHS to replace the washer, and finally they got fired by AHS. 

Then AHS sent out another contractor called Universal 1 Repair, which blamed the power outlet issue. It turns out this is what this company did to all its customers in last 3 years, blaming the power outlet. 

It has been 6 months, our washer is still broken.
Ordered meat ball it was decent for a corner store !Shockerrrrr ! My buddy went to order pork no pork so he says give me a meatball only 1 meatball left . I dunno I tried twice now ! Good luck

When I moved to Manayunk, this was the place that seemed like the best to go.  It was a huge let-down.  I tried to give it a chance- the first time I went, it was awesome.  I went for about 4 or 5 visits, once every other month.  I had the same stylist each time, and he never seemed to have any idea who I was, which was disappointing.  The first time I went, I got an all over dark brown, with red tips at the end.  The second time, he asked if I wanted my hair the same as last time, to which I said yes.  I got a medium brown all over color, and that's it.  When I said something about it, I was informed that he did not have time to do anything else.  When I went back the third time, I wanted some dark caramel highlighting, just a bit, not all over lightness and definitely not blonde (which is how it turned out).  Because they book themselves so full, they never have time to fix what they don't do well.  Also, extremely pricey for the poor service that they provide.  Received a facial here once, and there was no improvement to anything that I had told the woman I wanted help with on my face.  The entire facial, the woman tried to sell me the products that she is a rep for outside Salon E'toile.  Super unprofessional and not the relaxing experience that I was picturing.  Very disappointed with this experience, and have since took my business elsewhere (where I really like).

I would have given 0 stars if possible. If your in need of a nice suit and great experience: Boyds

Now back to my time with Suit Supply: there customer service is absolutely horrendous. I own over 10 pieces from Suit supply and was a fan. 

I recently went in to return a pair of suit pants that had a few rips after only wearing them twice. The first sales associate I spoke with insisted the rips were moth bites. After disputing that claim with him - knowing my new apartment doesn't have moths (and reconfirming this after the fact) - he called over his coworker who also immediately and matter of factly claimed that they were moth bites, having only glanced at them for a moment. They were short and rude. I would suggest you take a page from Nordstrom or Boyd's and learn respect for your customers. 

Train your sales associates to be humble (see other reviews).

I enjoy networking with many others in the professional industry who need suits. I will not recommend Suit Supply to them or other friends in need of a suit for any occasion.

I reached out to corporate to express my displeasure with this store. They said I would have to prove it in order to get my $200+ pants replaced. They said they would get back to me and I haven't heard from them since. 

Lost a customer.

Four of us went here recently for dinner on the late side. The place was very busy and we ended up being seated upstairs, after a drink at the bar. The atmosphere and the food were outstanding. Their appetizer-sized "toasts" are really wonderful and creative, as were the various small plates. Our only slight criticism was that the wait staff in general seemed very young and inexperienced. One of us asked our waiter about an entree and the response was basically to say that "if it's on the menu, it's great, do you think I'd lie to you? " Whether or not he was correct, it certainly wasn't a response you'd expect from an experienced waiter in an up-scale place like Vernick's. Luckily, the food more than makes up for this issue. We would all gladly return.

Friends of mine come here quite regularly and so I finally went to check it out.

First -the good:

Nice beer list. 
Pizza was delicious!  Tried the pepperoni and the Buffalo Chicken. Perfection. As another reviewer noted, they don't try to get too crazy experimental as other trendy pizza places do, but that is really okay. I'm often disappointed by that stuff anyway.
Bartender- Greg, was attentive and friendly.

Con - it was packed. I was at the bar and I felt bad because it was a squeeze for people to walk by because the aisle is so small between the bar and the tables on the one side . It took away for the experience a little bit. I'd like to go back on A weeknight when it is a little less crazy.

Overall, great joint!

DELICIOUS!!! 
The cajun fries are a must! They have a ton of beers to choose from and a great menu that brings together just about enough random food items to make it work! Love it!

As someone who loves burgers and has eaten at a lot of different joints, the ones here are a notch above the rest! They are juicy and delicious! I always order two double cheeseburgers. I can't do this place too often because of the calories and the price, but when I do, it's a treat and worth it!

I've been partying in NoLibs for years and I must confess, I never really had any desire to go to North Bowl. I was invited to a gathering there a few weeks back, so I went. I'm very glad I did. 

As a lounge alone, this place rocks. It's spacious, clean and bright. Two bars, plenty of seating/leaning space. The waitresses and bartenders in my experience were all kind, helpful and friendly. Add a full bowling alley and decent bar snacking food to that and you essentially have North Bowl. (I only picked on some small things that we ordered as a group, I can't elaborate on the food.)

I have bowled. I know how to bowl. I am familiar with the dynamics and rules of bowling. However, I do not bowl. You do not need to bowl in order to enjoy yourself at North Bowl. I've been back a few times now just to drink and hang out and it's always been a good time. 

It attracts one of the most diverse crowds I've seen in a long time. All races, ages, blazers, ties, heels, t-shirts, jeans, etc. It's a real cool vibe and jamming on the weekend.

There's a kick ass air hockey table here and a Rolling Stones pinball machine. 

If you've never been here before and want to try someplace new, grab a friend or three and just go here for a few drinks to kick off your night. There's a good chance you'll leave thinking, "That was cool. I'll come back sometime!" 

I know I did.

Was stuck at hotel near airport without transportation so we decided to order through Grubhub. Got the food and didn't send the baked potato, which was my dinner. My wife called and they rudely told her that she should have answered her phone (or phones were both by our sides the entire time and never rang). They credited the amount back but I went to bed hungry.

This is an awesome place!  The food is fantastic! The service is excellent! The location is prime!  Its located in the up & coming Point Breeze neighborhood, on what will eventually be the main drag.  We've been there for brunch and dinner!  I don't have a bad thing to say about this place. Everything on the menu is delicious.  My most recent trip, we had the Farmer's omelet and the Cheesteak Omelet.  Both were incredible and a great value. Paid $25 and I threw in a 25% tip. My only suggestion would be that they should dim the lights during dinner time because its very bright inside and the owners should invest in some acoustical panels or softer surfaces to dampen the conversation noise.  You should definitely pick this as your next brunch or dinner.  Oh and forgot to mention its BYOB!!!!

Literally some of the best food you can have in Philly. Might I dare even say, America. This small little BYOB serves phenomenal Italian cuisine in 4 courses for $37. The pasta and entree course we're my best courses by far. The lasagna topped with an over easy egg is to die for. And the ribeye steak topped with an egg and lemon caper butter is unbelievable. The flavored are so complex and have sooo many layers. I don't think I've ever had Italian food like this. I ate here twice in one week, and plan on going back when guests visit me from out of town. The service is also very quick, and don't get me started on the bread. Best bread I think I've ever eaten in my life. If you want a slam dunk meal everytime, visit this BYOB.

In the interest of full disclosure, I actually won a dinner for 2 at Public House through a drawing on another web site. That said, I'm pretty sure I found out why they are giving this stuff away.

First off, here's a quote from their website, "Parking: Corner of 18th and arch...from 5pm on is $5".  Actually, there are 2 garages and 1 parking lot on 3 of the block's 4 corners and not one of them is $5. They are anywhere from $9-$11. There is a lot about a block and a half away that was $7 from 4pm on, so, that's where we parked.

Our reservations were for 7, we walked in the door at 6:55. There was a hostess at the stand and she seemed to be dealing with a customer (i think) over some sort of happy hour situation that was going on. I stepped up to the stand and she asked that we give her a minute. Well, we had to give her 5 and that was after the gentleman  that she was "helping" had walked away. By the way, we were the only ones waiting for a table. When she was finally ready to seat us, we watched her use the touch screen computer to seat us in a booth that appeared to be empty, according to the screen, I guess. Well, it wasn't, which led to us being sat right next to the restrooms. No biggie, but whatever. 

The dinner for 2 that I'd won included 2 beverages 2 entrees and an appetizer. For the appetizer, we ordered the loaded nachos. They weren't all that loaded with much, other than cheese and the chips had spent a little too much time in the oven. 

For the entrees, I ordered the rib eye and my fiancé ordered the fish and chips, only to find out that they had just sold the last serving. So, she had to go with the penne with crab. My fiancé's entree showed up and the server/runner looked surprised when I wondered where my entree was. I then watched that runner run out 5 more dishes, none were mine. On one of the few times that our waitress stopped by our table, she asked where my dish was and I told her it hadn't been served yet. We're pretty sure that this is when it finally got fired. So, my dinner showed up about 10 minutes after my fiancé's did.

As for the quality of the entrees, my fiancé's penne with crab came out with a sauce that was so runny, there was never any sauce on the noodles by the time the bite made it to her mouth. She was also none too pleased with the weak amount of crab meat. My rib eye on the other hand was cooked exactly how I wanted it, a perfect medium. I was confused however by the drizzles of hot wing sauce on the meat and plate. I guess the color looked cool on the plate, but that sauce belongs on chicken wings, not a rib eye.

Finally, I'm kind of a beer snob, so, if a restaurant has food that tastes like God, himself just blessed my taste buds, I don't care, if all the beers that I have to choose from are 4 different kinds of watery corn sodas. Public House is not in the business of delivering the best in craft brews, but for a corporate chain, they have a diverse selection of imports, domestics and craft brews. I went with a Victory Hop Devil and wanted to finish with a Blue Moon, since I haven't had one in a very long time. It was the only item that we were charged for and it was $6. That's about $2-$3 over priced.

I can't say that I'll ever go back to the Public House. It's just not my type of crowd and I don't think that they totally have their stuff together there.

Place was cool. Service sucked. And waited 20 mins at least for each round of drinks. Manager was nice. Got us a free round and offered to dry clean my shirt cause the wall I was sitting next to stained my shirt. As u see in the picture those are my three drink glasses. Same drink. 3 dif glasses get all still on the table. Weren't taken away. Place is ehh. But cool concept.  Vr was cool

I had two plumbing issues, a leaky toilet and a water heater that started throwing rust water and staining my laundry.  I got the telephone number for Goodman Plumbing from my next-door neighbors.  I called an made an appointment, and Maurice was here the next day to asses the issues and give me a price.  He returned on Monday, replaced the water heater, but because of another problem with the toilet, was unable to complete that job that day.  He came back with the necessary parts the following day, and the toilet issue was resolved.  The price was a couple of hundred dollars more due to the problem with the toilet, but it was definitely worth it to not have a leak into my kitchen.

After the job was completed, Maurice cleaned up the area and made sure everything was back in place.

Goodman Plumbing also handles heating issues, and I will be contacting them again to check my heater and repair a couple of radiators that haven't worked in a couple of years.  Again, the price is very reasonable.

I would highly recommend Goodman Plumbing to anyone!

Kathy and the entire team are friendly, knowledgeable, and helpful. They are the perfect balance of a friendly local shop on the hill paired with the great connections within the jewelry industry. 

We recently took a couple of items in for repair. The work was done beautifully, the turnaround was under a week, and the price was great. 

Overall, friendly staff, quick repairs, and reasonable prices. We definitely found our jeweler on the Hill.

This is by far, hands down, the best paint your own establishment ever.  What a great way to bring out your creative side and relax at the same time.  Marcie, the owner, was warm and extremely helpful.  
You can sit and paint until your heart's content.  No one is shoving you out the door.  This makes a wonderful gift for someone !!
being there once, a small eatery. A lot ofe stuff I have no idea they are. It is kind of hard for me to order. But the GIANT sweet bread spoke to me. It tastes good.I like it !
It's ok. Not bad but not really good either. Priced high for what was delivered. The food arrived quickly and was accurate.

My husband I went here for lunch and not only was the service really slow (think: took 20 minutes to get refills, and Diet Coke obviously needed to be changed) when our food finally arrived the chicken on my Caesar salad was either really underdone or a terrible cut of meat. Gross. Will not be returning.

A perfect brunch spot for two. 

We ordered the smoke salmon, roasted artichokes and poached eggs crepe and the crepe with scrambled eggs, poblano peppers and avocado salsa. Both crepes were made with buckwheat and they were crisp, light and very flavorful.

We ended the meal with crepe with bananas and others with a scoop of vanilla ice cream and OH BOY. One of the best desserts I've had.

Tips: 
- Let them know you're sharing and they divide the plate into two and didn't charge me anything additional. 
- There's outdoor seating available.
- Street parking on South St. is for up to 2 hours.

This place is FANTASTIC. Highly recommend and would be delighted to come back.

Fresh tasting Maine-style lobster rolls, tasty sides, friensly staff.  The high quality food is worth the price.  I can't afford to go here every day for lunch, but when I feel like an upgrade that's still quick and casual, this is one of my go-to spots.
This park provides something for everyone of all ages. Plenty of photo opportunities, food, and activities. The live music and hammock set up makes for a great relaxing environment.

Delicious!!!
I visited Philadelphia for a few days around Valentine's day weekend and was pleasantly surprised to find Sabrina's Cafe. On a Friday afternoon my partner and I were looking for a place to have brunch. We were both very happy to stroll into Sabrina's. For a Friday afternoon the place was packed and for good reason. Everything we had was great. The service was spot on and our breakfast was delicious. We Teo different orders of pancakes with eggs, avocado & either bacon or sausage. The portions were more than enough for two people and the OJ was freshly squeezed. 
Would love to go back there.

Love thIs place! Everytime I go I am never disappointed with the good. They stopped being byob which is really disappointing.  I would say it's comparable to Han dynasty but closer and with out the wait for a table.

Big venue and lots of food vendors.  There was a lot of people when I came here to watch WWE Money in the Bank, but  people were surprisingly not on top of each other. 

The seats, however, was a different story.  They were way too close next to each other and it was a bit uncomfortable even though I'm short 5'1.  I can't even imagine how it feels if your tall and sitting on these benches for an extended amount of hours.  With so many people next to each other, it was bound to happen.  I always end up next to people with really bad body odor, maybe that's just my luck.  Needless to say, it was not a very appealing time for my olfactory perception.

The BEST BBQ I've had north of the Mason-Dixon Line. Why only two stars? Cash only. I haven't carried cash for years.  No one carries cash. The ATM and its charges don't make the lack of accepting credit OK. A food truck that serves tacos takes credit cards. This is a clean, down-to-earth, cool garage with a big bar in the middle that serves incredible BBQ near downtown Philly. Our awesomely freckled waitress suggested a beer I've never had before that I'll definitely seek out when I get home. The three sauce options were unique and definitely added to the perfectly smoked ribs & brisket. The fritters were a great appetizer. Only that one complaint about this place...get a Square or something guys! You're almost perfect. First world problems.

The current employees are very nice compared to some of them you had before. The people working the booths where you pay are nicer too. The drivers are better too. Before you needed a translator to understand what they were saying. If you could fix the shocks on some of the busses that would be great. I sometimes feel like I'm driving in a military vehicle during World War 2. The bottles of water when you leave area great.

Elvis has definitely not left the building....He's holed up in a small corner bar a few blocks from the Oregon Ave station in South Philly.  I was there last night because I heard from some friends that they were having Rock Band/ Guitar hero on Thursdays...nice mix of a few regulars(Me) and people who stumbled in to hear what was going on.....sick time, not to mention this hot bartender named Renee, who will spoil other bartenders for you as she pours the next one as you're finishing the one you're drinking...(Ok Princess you owe me one for that) seriously great service.  Take the train down....it's worth it..

Multiple visits to this great little restaurant confirm that it's among Philadelphia's finest.  And, what's even better - this place is open from breakfast through dinner, unusual for a restaurant with, yep, "High" standards for each dish it sends out from the kitchen.  Most recently went for breakfast:  the ginger yogurt with strawberries and pistacios was super!  The bowl could have fed two (but I ate it all myself, slowly), and the cup of Rival Brothers coffee was a treat too.  Breakfast is a nice opportunity to explore great food, and Chef Eli Kulp and team get it right.  Peruse the small bakery display when you walk in for you'll want to try one of the unusual pastries or the less unusual but very tasty bagels.  Get yourself up when the sun rises and stake out a spot at High Street on Market, for breakfast...

Wendys is wendys. A cheap, fast food chain with a just "okay" selection for food on the go. The reason I gave THIS wendys such a high rating is because I live around the corner from this one. I get done work late most nights and every time I go to this particular wendy's i am always greeted with such great attitudes and even though the food is just "okay" i would so rather go here for the great energy, rather then mcdonalds or burger king any day.

I'm writing this review because I recently had a dream about the breakfast sandwich at Middle Child: perfectly pillowy eggs, sharp gooey cheese, peppery arugula to break up the richness, and buttery crispy bread surrounding it all. I can almost taste it as I'm writing this.

I've had the breakfast sandwich here too many times to count, and each time has been as good as the last. The base sandwich is, as I have just described, absolutely perfect. But if you're craving something extra you can add sausage, hash browns, avocado....anything the heart desires. 

Don't worry if you're not into breakfast though. The other sandwiches are also amazing. So far, I've had the Phoagie (yummy fusion vegetarian sandwich in a delicious toasted sesame bread) and the Bye Felicia (a tasty vegetarian special with cauliflower), and loved them both. 

It is also worth mentioning the atmosphere of the place. It's like an old-timey diner, but bright and lively. The people who work there are super friendly and inviting, and you can watch them in action as they prepare your food (I love people-watching!) The place is kind of small, so be prepared to either wait for a seat, or be willing to take your food to go!

this place is amazing the only bad part is i cant have them everyday. The food is amazing their service is hands down amazing . this place it great for the whole family. never had anything bad from here.

Frankford Tax Professionals has now done my tax filings for three straight years and I've got nothing but great things to say! Even with added complexity (foreign income, w2 & 1099, LLC, FBAR), they attack the job with great detail and they find things I would have never noticed. Looking forward to working with them into the future.

I live less than a block from this cute little produce store. It has what I need and then some. The husband and wife are very sweet and accommodating. It feels very home-y and welcoming. Lots of produce in a somewhat small place but it doesn't feel crowded. Definitely my go to store!

I come here almost once a week for dinner with two of my friends and I love it! It's a small, casual space, perfect for a quick bite when I'm on Penn campus. I usually get a variation of one of their special salads, which they're more than happy to accommodate, or I'll get a salmon salad sandwich! I've yet to try their other food, but everything I've had thus far has been great, and their autumn lasagna sounds amazing!

Really love this place, authentic Italian meats, cheeses, bread and condiments combine for some great inventive sandwiches. This time had the Jackson Street, breaded chicken, sharp provolone, roasted peppers and a spicy dressing on a great Sarcones roll. Vesuvius is also great if you like long hots. Owner and staff are very friendly, great neighbors place.

Hmmm. Stuck between 2 and 3. The dining area is very attractive, spacious and has a fun and inviting set up. there are tables, high tops, booths and even "bar" type seating. I had an order of fries and a milkshake..$10. Seems a few dollars overpriced, but the food was tasty and plenty. I used the bathroom while I waited and.. yikes. The first one I opened was very messy. I can look past one dirty bathroom in the city, so I continued to the next bathroom and when I opened that door it was more of the same. The "trashcan" can only fit about 3 paper towels. Many were scattered on the ground. There was even a workers apron left on the changing table. Lastly, this was my second visit and during each of them the wait was much longer than it should be. My fries and a milkshake took at least 10 minutes. They were fresh and I wasn't in a rush so it was ok, but several grubhub and Uber eats drivers came in to pick up orders and were frustrated to find out that they hadn't even been started. Seems like this might be a regular issue.

With industrial decor, acoustics are terrible, like being in an oil drum. I had to cup my ears to order. But with a small streamlined menu it gives them focus to provide a competitively priced artisanal pizza.

This is probably the single worst real estate agency with whom I have ever worked. The agents we used were Lisa and Maria Quattrone. I know the housing market is tough so I won't comment on their sales skills. However, their organizational skills and customer service are appalling. 

Completing a transaction remotely requires patience, organization and communication. I asked that whenever an email was sent my way, they call to make sure I received it. Never happened. I asked for a clear list of closing instructions and timelines. Never happened. 

If there is any complexity involved in your deal, I would look elsewhere.
Very good and authentic sandwiches. It is a cash only restaurant however, prices are extremely reasonable. Do not pass this cool little spot by without checking it out.

I come here pretty often and really like it. I get the original açaí bowl without coconut. You can add peanut butter, almond butter, etc., but I don't. There's also smoothies and other bowl options. I've never gone in and there weren't people there. For $9 it's pretty filling in my opinion, but my coworkers don't think so. I get it for breakfast or lunch, and I'm satisfied!

First, side from being horribly over priced, the food or most of it, is just not good.

Here's the rundown. We got Mexican corn. Now I've made this myself when I'm not lazy and it's good. Theirs wasn't bad and I stress not bad but it was $8.00 for 4 3" pieces. Then there was the guacamole. You know the tiny cups that you get say apple sauce in? Bigger than a ketchup or soy sauce cup but I'm talking little. Yeah, well that was 6 bucks and the queso of the same size was 4. The barbacoa steak and grilled shrimp quesadilla are the only reason for the second star because they were good but they were respectively $14 and $16 dollars.

These may not seem like steep prices but trust me, these were tiny portions but the worst part of this order were the tortilla chips. Stale and foul tasting. There's no excuse for that.
Delicious food at reasonable prices.  There are lots of vegetarian options.  Cozy atmosphere.

I don't usually write reviews for beauty services since I'm a guy that only does pretty basic grooming--haircuts every 3 weeks and that's it.  However, my wife and I came here for spray tans  before we went on holiday last week, and everything turned out great. I had never had a spray tan in my life and had no idea what to expect. The girl walked me through everything and put me at ease--standing half naked in front of someone you don't know can be awkward, but she had me talking the whole time and before I knew it she was done.  We followed the after care instructions to a tee and the color looked completely natural and lasted for the duration of our holiday.  It also faded very naturally. I believe Anita sprayed me and Jessica sprayed my wife. They were both great.

The atmosphere was really nice.
The foods was about average
The service was pretty awful. 
Friday night about 7pm

They didn't have their liquor license up to date so they're was no 'craft beer'
Their soda machines were broken so they're was no drinking at all. 
The veggie burger was decent. But there was too much thousand island dressing 
(Save you r money walk to trader Joe's and buy their veggie burger and tomato chutney)
The brisky business was okay. Actually it was over done and didn't leave an impression; this is a burger place needs to leave an impression.

I did appreciate that they had a meat alternative!!!

Wanted to update my review of this salon. The owner Vicky was unaware of this encounter and contacted me personally to apologize about the one employee I dealt with. I was always pleased with everyone that has taken care of me and the technicians are exceptional both personally and professionally. I am once again a happy, satisfied customer.

Absolutely great. I'm very judgmental when it comes to food. Food alone gets more than 5 star here, adding the ambient, staffs, decor with everything its best in its class. Ask me about the price? 'Worth it'
I am from nyc, went this Buddakan when we went to visit PA, I know there is one in Manhattan too, I have yet to find out how is that one.

I've lived here for my sophomore year and as a person new to college, this was definitely a good option in not needing to find new furniture or go through an extensive leasing process. There are downsides like the printer never working or always showing your key fob when you walk in, but they're definitely minor!

This bar/restaurant sits in the Mechanics' Bank building, home to a few Philadelphia historical firsts: the first prominent laborer-run banking institutions (uh, don't quote me on that) and the first local Coyote Ugly (and last; this franchise location has since closed). The interior is well-lit and has an electric feel, from the Christmas lights, to the dog on the wall (complete with neon trimmings), to the weird gadgets lying around. Don't forget to check out the bathroom sinks.

Our waitress (Amber) served us quickly and with a friendly attitude, which was pretty amazing since she looked like the only server on the floor that evening. The place was packed with a happy hour crowd. (And they were indeed happy. I talked with three fairly inebriated young women and - in character, of course - took a photo with one.)

Tried a few bites of the nachos, but mostly drank. (Irish car bombs are the way to go!) I asked Amber for an alien secretion shot and gave her the recipe when she told me the bartender didn't know how to make it. I was thrilled when she brought it over. Thankfully there are still bartenders patient enough to listen, remembering that customers = money. To top the evening off, our party was rewarded with a free round of shots, courtesy of Amber and/or National Mechanics. I was too happy to remember from whom, exactly.

Definitely a fun place, and recommended for a good time!

Had a 14 ounce new york steak and twiced baked potato tart.  Both food items were really delicious.  Came with wife and son.  They served my 2 yr old first with pizza and fruit, and gave us bernaise sauce with the steak.  

Cant wait to eat here again... not even for the excellent food but for the very accomodating staff.  It was so humid today and they really made us comfortable before we ate and calmed my son and us, and brought us drinks and pizza lightning fast. Check came after we declined dessert, and they give you corn seeds when you leave and my wife liked that too.  Great air conditioning!
No hyperbole- some of the yummiest sandwiches I've ever eaten!! We also got the iced turmeric, honey latte which was delicious as well! Counting down the days until I go back

Mamma Maria's is my favorite Italian restaurant in South Philly.  It's on my "must eat" list for any friends and family who fly in to visit Philadelphia and want top quality, authentic Italian food.

The owner, and also cook, is "Mamma".  She is from Tuscany and came to Philadelphia about 5 years ago.

There is no menu - waiter will announce the dishes, which change frequently depending upon what ingredients there are.  I have never had a meal that was not EXCELLENT.

The house wine is free flowing.  It's all you can drink - they will keep refilling your glass until you can't stand if you let them.

And, the best part of every meal for me at Mamma Maria's is the dessert.  At the end of your meal, the waiter will bring out many different desserts along with homemade Limoncello.

If you are looking for a good time with family and friends, or if you are out to impress some clients, this is as good a choice as you can make in Philadelphia.
Their pizzas are very inconsistent. On a whole they are dry and lack sauce and toppings. The crust tastes like cardboard. For the price it is definitely worth skipping.
This is a good spot to watch the game and enjoy happy hour with friends. Nothing fancy, what you see is what you get.

I have come here a couple of times and not sure why there is so much hype about this place. Food is ok depending on what you order and not particularly memorable, service is hit or miss, space is cramped.

Positives: 

Clean, tasty and you can actually see the food being prepared and not behind glass. Plenty of vegan and vegetarian choices .  Gravy is thick and spicy . Original drinks 

Need work: time for food to get prepared, in dinning area is small and congested
My favorite bar in philly. The drinks are cold, the food hot, and the karaoke passionate

Simply amazing hearts and excellent service. I came as a first-time customer without an appointment (by accident) and they squeezed me into their schedule despite having zero obligation to. Not only that, but they drove me home after I left my car! I swear I have not met anyone so friendly and genuinely kind since growing up in my small neighborhood in Brooklyn in the 90s.

They changed owners and are always understaffed. Guy who did my nails did such a bad job I had to return the next day to have them fixed... he also overcharged me $10 and they only refunded me $5 sounds petty but when it comes to something cosmetic it should be done right... or at least to your liking. He also wasn't wearing a mask

What an amazing restaurant! I came to Philly for a weekend getaway and decided to go on a foodie adventure. I stopped at pretty girl cook after seeing a plethora of delicious photos. There was little to no wait time for a party of four! Which is unbelievable for a New Yorker looking for a Sunday brunch with no reservations. I ordered the shrimp and grits and I was even more amazed . I will definitely be returning next time I make my way to Philly! Also BYOB!

Used a Groupon to get a gel manicure and express pedicure and this place gets 5 stars! The women who did my nails were very professional and friendly as well. Very clean and good color selection for both regular nail polish and gel. Both my toes and gel did not chips at all so far and I went on a weeks vacation! I'm very impressed. Great job ladies!!

I actually never realized this place was there until a couple years back when there was that hoopla about its name being racist towards Chinese folks. My parents lived near Chink's and we thought it was hilarious (we're Chinese) and so we decided to show our support by getting some steaks there. Oh man it was awesome. The steaks were great (the best we had up 'til that point since my parents only recently moved to Philly) and the folks were real nice. So totally worth it!

THREE YEARS LATER: My dad still loves the place and goes there for lunch whenever he's in the area for work. Good job, people who campaigned against the name: you won Chink's a long term Chinese customer.

By far my favorite breakfast sandwich spot. Their kaiser roll sandwiches are amazing, but what really changed my life was their toasted everything bagels with cream cheese and bacon. Yes, cream cheese and bacon. It sounds kinda funky, but good god it is delicious. So delicious, in fact, that I am regretting mentioning it for fear that more people may start ordering and it would no longer just be "my thing." 

I was totally eating that sandwich before it was cool. Just sayin. 

Eat at Jean's.
Ordered delivery from Evo last night. It arrived quickly and the pizza was excellent.

walking around at the Reading Terminal market, deciding what to have for my meal of the day! then I saw a sign, "best voted philadelphia" n it was said, the best sandwich (po boy) so I gave it a try! I picked the fried oyster sandwich! It was good! the mixed pickles and the creole mayo complement each other! the one thing I'm complaining about, it was messy! I probably used 5-6 napkins! but I wouldn't just stop for just the sandwich. I will definitely come back again, n probably I will try the platter, n probably if they could give me the creole mayo on the side! that way, it would be less messy! :)

The restaurant serves Shanghai styled food which is kind of a rare find in Philly Chinatown. I really enjoyed the food during my visit (the food has certain authenticity to it) and am glad to see it has expanded to accommodate the demand! 

Recommend to try the steamed juicy buns. Definitely a signature dish there. It may be a good idea to call ahead for a table or just avoid the lunch crowd. The waiting line certainly doesn't stop growing. (Seems like everyone knows where to find good food.)

The Foodery rules! They have an amazing beer selection. Last night I purchased a 6 pack of Ballast Point Grapefruit sculpin and Abita purple haze, it came to around $23 so it's a little pricey, but totally worth it! They give 10% off each bottle in a six mix of your choice. 

The food is also awesome! Great for breakfast and lunch. You can order a sandwich and get a beer, sit down and enjoy-- this place is a really unique spot with a delicious selection of beer and food, I highly recommend stopping by!

We visited the night before a very stressful surgery for my husband and had a fabulous dinner at a. Kitchen. The staff was amazing!! They were very accommodating, as I was walking with a cane and made every effort to accommodate my gluten sensitivity. The atmosphere was funky, hot  and fun and the vibe was extremely relaxing. I highly recommend it for any and all occasions.

I really liked the vibe when we first arrived, and then reality set in. After seating ourselves we had the pleasure of waiting fifteen minutes for two cups of coffee (it wasn't wildly busy). We placed our breakfast orders and awaited their arrival by watching one of the servers play on his phone. Our food was brought to us in to-go containers, and we made the mistake of asking for our meals to be placed on plates. Ten minutes later our waitress returned with cold food. I returned mine and decided I didn't want to bother with anything else, and my friend had hers remade. The waitress was very unapologetic and didn't seem overly concerned about the situation.
Prices can't be beat and the response time as well. These guys were so friendly and got the job done. I recommend them to anyone

The food was good, but  we waited 1 1/2 hours for our food.  Giovanni has a terrible attitude, he was impatient, brash with people in the aisles and at the tables; yeling and just down right nasty. This place can not handle a big crowd and there was an event being held about phenomenal women.  

I won't be returning and I don't recommend this place to anyone!!

Great sandwiches and coffee. I like the solid effort to support locals so it's expected their groceries are a bit pricey.  It's not the best for tea though but it is a coffee place.  Great, great sandwiches and crazy tasty canelles.

I did a silly thing and got a scarf stuck in my 2-year-old HP laserjet B&W printer.  It responded with large ugly streaks and shadows and vengeful clicking.  I didn't want to recycle it and wanted to get it fixed.  The fix cost me $75 labor plus cost of parts ($200).

Now, aside from the questionable wisdom of paying as much to fix my printer as I probably would have spent on a new printer, my experience with the Computer Fixer was overall positive.

I'm currently a student at Drexel so I found them through the Drexel IT website.  I think the value in their service is that they help individual customers and price accordingly.  Yes, it's expensive, in my pricing research for fixing this print, it would have been more expensive with a corporate fixing company geared towards corporate businesses.

The location of their office was easy to find.  The technician was courteous and got back to me promptly (within 3 business days) with an estimate of what the total cost of repair would be.  The fix was completed two days after I okayed the ordering of the fuser.  My printer now works just like new.  In addition, there is a 90-day warranty of the fix.

Although I do think that the cost of fixing might for some people go for just buying a new machine (computer or printer), I think that there is ecological value in fixing what you've already got.

One of my favorites Indian cuisine - fresh, fast and friendly! Now they have moved a couple of feet over to a new spot and although the new spot is more modern (I prefer quaint and authentic ) the food is the best I have had !!! I will be happy when they have dine in services!
Taste is over average, but extremely understaffed. Full house but only two people were working. We stood there for several mins until being seated. Then waited for the water, waited for the menu...

This place is simply amazing. Koch's is the best deli in Philadelphia, in my humble opinion. Please, please, PLEASE!!!!....if you're new to Koch's, understand that part of the "experience" is the long wait. We used to joke when I went to college nearby that if you call ahead, add 30 minutes to the wait time and then when you finally get there, be ready to wait 10 more minutes. 

Worry not though, the staff is friendly and will frequently stop what they're doing to pass out free samples to the waiting customers, anything from their famous corned beef to their delicious honey turkey to their juicy pickles. 

They have a huge menu but they'll still make practically any kind of sandwich you ask for. Top picks, for me, are their corned beef sandwich and the Restaurant School Special. Be sure to add a Doc Brown's Cream Soda to the order! 

I urge all my friends to try this legendary West Philly tradition. The wait is long, but once you take a bite of one of their sandwiches, you'll be glad they took the time to make your meal just right!

I don't think I have ever brought someone here and had them leave  saying anything less than "wow, that was awesome". Actually, I can say with 96% certainty that that's the case. I hadn't been in awhile and when a friend was visiting from out of town, decided that a little dive bar crawl was in order...this was our first stop. Like any other night this place provided premium people watching, on this particular evening there was a gentleman across the bar who looked like the resurrected Richard Pryor with dance moves that rivaled MJ and to our immediate left was another kind sir clad head to toe in black rocking the sunglasses indoors at night (because the sun never sets on a BAMF). He was listening to his own beats on his iPod even though the music chosen by the other patrons was stellar including a crowd pleasing ACDC Big Balls. 

Sheila is almost always my bartender and she is just the sweetest thing. Upon ordering shots of tequila she asked if we would like "house tequila", not knowing what it was but allowing the night to take us on a wild adventure said "sure, why not?", this was a terrible decision....it....was....crap. I stared up at the beer of shame ledge and wholeheartedly contemplated ordering a Milwaukee's Best for old times sake, but remembered back to my college days and how nights of Beast almost always ended over a toilet, I decided to walk away. Before leaving, Sheila invited my friend and I to the Dirty Frank's summer picnic, she even said I could bring my dog. That's love, this place really makes you feel like family.

On my most recent visit to DF, I was with a fellow yelper and three of his other friends. I was equally torn this night between continuing on with my gay entourage frolicking through the gayborhood, or taking a turn and heading for Franks. Franks won and I found myself chat-chitting in a sticky booth having TouchTunes A.D.D. yet again as each song played was better than the last. Again there was people watching, again there was cheap beer, and the moment that sold me forever...the entire bar singing to The Monkees Daydream Believer, a song that throws me back to my childhood when my parents insisted I listen to the oldies. With a tear in my eye and a lump in my throat I said to myself "Thank you myriad of Franks, you have touched my soul in an inexplicable way and I promise that I will love you forever".

Salesperson was unfriendly, uninformative,
And was not interested in answering my questions or making a sale. Went to Lowe's and had a wonderful salesman and purchased and washer and dryer. Will not be going back to Gerhard's. Obviously they don't appreciate their customers!!!!

Luna is amazing! So great they are local esp with st bens leaving!! They help support the community. My only wish is a super flavorful, colorful vegan/plant based option. I feel like in a more conscious hood might be ideal. 

The staff and owners are so kind and helpful!

They have coffee go visit check them out. Also their succulent collection will leave you so envious!

I like that you are: 
BYOT
Call yourself "modern mexican"
On 13th Street and run by my favorite ladies.

I haven't been in years (as well as my other two guests) and it was only fitting we went back for National Margarita Day (look through other reviews and you will find who the other members of my party are). 

We walked in on National Margarita Day (a Friday) at 7:30pm and seated as soon as the whole party was there. I was very surprised... but heck, I'll take it. 

We decided to order a whole slew of apps and take it from there. I really think this is the best way to go.  Each dish was super tasty and we ordered two Margarita pitchers for our bottle.

Super delicious tacos! Crazy lunch crowd so plan accordingly but definitively worth waiting for.  Portions are quite sizable to keep that in mind when ordering. Love the variety of sauces to compliment the main courses.

I ate at Johnny Mananas yesterday afternoon.  Because the weather was so nice, my friend and I opted to sit outside.  Our waitress was very friendly and attentive.  The chips and salsa that we snacked on while we waited for our food were delicious!  I ordered the taco salad with beef, and it was very tasty.  The portion size was just right, as was the price.  I'd definitely go back.

On a cold winter day, the bf and I decided to try this french restaurant, mostly because it was french and I have a soft spot for foie gras. I really like the atmosphere of this bustling bistro: cozy, kinda dark, but inviting at the same time. After placing our order we were served yummy bread to snack on. The bf tried the spiced hot apple cider w/ brandy. It was rather delicious and totally warmed me up. Next came the foie gras and the tuna carpacio. The foie gas did not disappoint - It was a little awkward to put on your spoon, but I savored every fatty morsel. The combination of the sweet pear and the salty meat gave a wonderful combination in my mouth. The tuna was great too, very fresh... and the citrus that's added to it just livens up the taste buds. Next came our entrees. Our waiter raved about the lamb shank, so I decided to give it a try. I'm a fan of lamb in general (lamb lolipops and rack of lamb), but this lamb shank was just too gamy for me. I just ate my bf's duck entree which, albeit a little of the fatty side, was so good! The meat was moist and flavorful. I will be back here again, just pass on the lamb.

MOJITO MOJITO MOJITO!!! Once you had a mojito here at Cuba Libre, you will not find any better ones anywhere. When I go to any bars or restaurants and I order a Mojito, I compare it to Cuba Libres, and none have even come close to being half as good as CL's. The place itself looks amazing, it feels as if you stepped into a outdoor restaurant someplace in Cuba! But you know what was better then all the rest? The food, seriously, if you had yet try Cuban food, this is the place for your first. It was my girlfriend and my first time trying Cuban food, and we absolutely loved it. I can't wait to come back again!

This is a classic, old-school pizza parlor that has supposedly been around for quite some time.  You can watch the workers hand-make everything in front of you as you order at the counter.  The place can get busy, but there always seems to be hot, fresh pizzas waiting to be sliced up (no pizza sitting for hours under Plexiglas here).  If the 'za has been sitting out for a while, the workers will throw your slice(s) back in the oven.

Choices are limited.  You can get a slice of cheese ($2.00) or sausage ($2.25).  Rumor has it that if you order a full pie, you can get pepperoni, but I haven't tried it out to know if it's true.  Slices are the perfect size and have a perfect amount of grease--enough to make it tasty but not so much that it turns you off.  They also carry the perfect soda to wash everything down with--Fanta Wild Cherry.

The staff is friendly and seems to care about their pizza.

I definitely recommend King of Pizza if you find yourself on this exciting stretch of Germantown Ave.

Not satisfied with the service at the outlet AT ALL.  purchased dining room chairs November of 2017.  Since then - less than one year the screws which attach the seat to frame are constantly falling out!   It appears that the screws are either too thin or the wood is dry rot.
