For this reason I will continue my furniture purchases from Raymour and Flannigan!    Such a waste of money at this store.  NEVER AGAIN!   Customer service leaves a lot to be desired as well -- the staff  lacks knowledge of pricing and items which are advertised.  Wasted time and money.

The Abbaye is dishing out consistently good food and beer. We just moved to Northern Liberties and we've been back a few times already. If you can catch their specials menu with the chicken cheesesteak (with smoked cheddar and apples) then you MUST TRY IT!!! They make everything in-house. Try the sweet potato fries! There are about 2 lbs of them... so make some hash with the leftovers ;)

Rotating local beers on tap- can't beat it. Will be back soon! I just wish they had a little bit bigger menu... but I also appreciate the time they put into the few items they list.

Ok so let me start by saying that the girls I had do my hair were absolutely great. Cate did my color and Nicolette did my cut. They were very personable and did an excellent job with my hair. I love it a lot. The color and cut is great. My complaint is that my hair feels extremely dry and overly processed and still does. I've used a lot of conditioner and my hair still feels disgusting. Not sure if this will eventually fade. It's been 3 days and it hasn't faded yet. I've never had this issue anywhere else before. The waiting area is extremely uncomfortable. I went early because it didn't make sense to go home after dropping my daughter off at preschool. My back was so painful after sitting in those chairs. The greeting I received when I walked in left a lot to be desired. The lady acted like she didn't want to be there. She was a little better when I left so I chalked this up to her not having her coffee yet. When I went to pay I almost fell over. $160 for color and cut!!!! The most I've paid was $120-$130 for master colorists and stylists to do it at other salons so that seemed a little much. I was then told that I couldn't put the tip on my card!!! What?!?!? Why wouldn't you tell clients this on the phone when scheduling appointments? Especially new clients. Most places you can and the ones you can't either tell you when scheduling or have ATM's inside their salon. I was told there was one at the Giant a couple doors down. I went to get cash. I was charged a ridiculous transaction fee of $3.50. I wasn't able to tip the girls as much because I was planning on using my credit card. So because of the practices of this business the girls lost out a little on the tip. Which is a shame. I was going to give them double of what I ended up giving. 
They do a great job so I recommend Cate and Nicolette a lot. Just be prepared with cash for a tip and be prepared to pay more than you would at most places. My mum got a hair cut and color very similar to mine and only paid $60. Granted her hair is not as thick as mine. I would go back but only when I have an extra buck to spend and I'll remember to bring cash.

Forget Pat's and Geno's.  Jim's is where it's at.  Loved the upstairs dining area.  Something about the steak that I liked more than the other two.  

http://www.nadsbakery.com/2010/05/day-3-philly-cheesesteak-wars-ritas.html
Best place Ever. Period. soup dumplings are Fu*cking amazing!

It was a godawful sweltering afternoon when I made my way from the train station to the Double Knot to meet friends for dinner.  Slightly disheveled and sweating, we descended to the quietly lit downstairs portion of the restaurant where our mood and comfort instantly changed for the better.  Black wrought iron fencing, Japanese wall hangings and little dining alcoves scattered here and there gave the feeling that we had stumbled into a very secret, very comfortable and very hip pirate's lair somewhere deep beneath the city.  This is a place that invites you to never leave and to share whispered gossipy confidences or conspire with friends to plot your next crazy adventure.  I plan on going back just to hang out more downstairs and soak up the atmosphere.         

   The downstairs menu of the Double Knot is all about small plates and sharing although they do have some dinner-sized offerings.  Sashimi, sushi and robatayaki choices are sprinkled across the menu and everything we ordered overflowed with flavor dancing delicately on our palates.  The Yellow Tail and Hamachi were so fresh and light I thought I was dining near the ocean on the coast of Japan.  The black cod fried rice was just the right mix with an earthiness to it offset by the tender bits of cod.  We also tried the edamame dumpling (bursting with flavor) plus a couple of sushi rolls.  The glazed salmon was very tender and the sea bass melted on the tongue.  I strongly suspect that everything else on the menu is just as delectable as what we enjoyed that evening.  Next time, we plan on giving the Chef's Tasting menu a try.  If you get there before we do, let me know how it goes!

   My bottom line on the Double Knot is that it's new, it's hip, the menu is fresh and flavorful and the service outstanding.  Dine downstairs and I promise that you will feel like you've been given VIP  access to dine in one the city's most secret underground enclaves.  This is my new favorite place in Center City.

All I can say is woooowwww!!! I went here for the first time yesterday and ordered the banana split in a cup and it was like a creamy sweet, delicious explosion in your mouth (get your head out of the gutter! Shame on you!) I have to admit I stood and looked at the menu for about 15 minutes because there was so much to choose from! And they have a drive through, but everything is made in house so the ice cream wasn't too sweet. The guy at the window was super helpful and nice he answered all of the questions I had. I got soft serve vanilla and hard strawberry I inhaled it! So so so good. I'm planning on heading over there after my workout at the gym lol my trainer would have my head! But hey you scream we scream we all scream for ICE CREAM!! Yummy yummy!

The service here just makes you lose faith in humanity in general. We didn't even get to the food part. It was a bummer cuz we were so much looking forward to it. Ya know what's better? Get a Philly Cheesesteak near the Macy's instead!

This hotpot place is unique for its food conveyor belt - cool concept! Coconut drink is really good. Tried the wagyu beef, delicious! Server, Erika, is nice and fast service. Came here on a Sunday night, wasn't a long wait.

They are a customer service nightmare.  They sent me an authorized piece of furniture and I have made five phone calls to return it and been told different things each time.  I had paid for a piece of furniture in August and when delivery time came in October they told me it was no longer available.

Had a nice birthday dinner Saturday night.  Very clubby atmosphere.  Great service.  Large prices, but this is the high rent district and you expect $$$$$.  Heck it cost $32 just to park for dinner.  Overall the steak was good, not the greatest, but it was an enjoyable outing.  The wine list is huge, and the $52 bottle we had would have cost about $10 at our local store.  Would I go back? Probably not, because we have great steak houses here in Wilmington,,,, and the parking is free!   (of course they did throw in a single scoop of vanilla ice cream for my wife's birthday)  lol

The eye doctor is excellent. Really takes his time. They staff has always been very helpful when I have been here. I have been wearing glasses & contacts for years. I always here not to go here because they are very slow. The amount of foot traffic that comes is the reason why they are slow. They don't have much of an eyeglass selection but they are affordable. The contact lenses are inexpensive also. I'm a college student & I don't have vision in my Plan. So affordable is what I need & the doctor is very thorough.i don't mind the wait it's well worth it

My girlfriend took me here for my birthday and I couldn't have been happier with how everything turned out. We got the wagyu carpaccio, burgundy snails, foie gras pate, pumpkin gnocchi and wagyu short rib. Honestly everything we got was incredible in terms of balancing flavors and texture within the dish. I never knew you could serve snails with potato aligot but that with the pistachio butter and black truffle was awesome. My favorite dish was easily the wagyu short rib. So amazingly tender and had such depth of flavor due to the smoked mushrooms and perfectly cooked meat. Definitely don't skip out on dessert and make sure you get the dulce de leche! 

Eric the waiter was so incredibly friendly and well informed about the food! After the dinner he even offered us to step into the kitchen to meet the chef and get a look behind the scenes. Definitely will be back soon!

From the moment we arrived, we were greeted with a warm smile and attentive staff. The outdoor seating is colorful and inviting. The restaurant is BYOB, so we brought whiskey and pinot noir. We asked for a glass of ice which they brought out quickly and refilled when it melted. 

We skipped the appetizers and got three mains to share family style. We tried the Mint Pappardelle Lamb sausage Ragu whipped Lioni Ricotta, the Squid Ink Tonnarelli Tuna Nduja Turnip Green muddica' herb bread crumb, and the Grilled Branzino Calamari & Shrimp saute' Escarole. 

For dessert, we shared the Dark Chocolate Almond cake Fiordilatte gelato Amaretto crema. 

There was so much attention throughout the meal. We asked for our pasta dishes first, and the dishes were promptly removed when we were done eating. Extra plates and nee utensils were brough tout as we changed courses. 

Our wonderful waiter noticed our table was wobbly, and brought over a table wedge without us having to ask. We didn't feel rushed at all throughout the meal. Ice buckets were available for those who brought chilled wine, and they had trending pop music playing.

Overall, this was a very fun restaurant that I'd recommend for fun weekday dinner for others visiting Philly!

Thoroughly enjoyed and satiated after a happy hour. Clean bright comfortable place.  I had the deviled eggs, salmon/trout pate and the fish and chips (not all of it).
Really good, great taps and specials. Not a cheap place but worth it! Service was friendly, efficient, and pro.

Having worked as a pizza delivery driver for a few years out of high school, I developed a love for traditional, thin, hand tossed, Italian pizza.  I don't mean to disrespect the Italians, but this Greek style pizza is right up there with my other favorites.  I love getting sauce on top because there is SO much of it, and I love a lot of sauce.  Not only that, but their sauce is delicious; nice combination of sweet and fresh tomato.  I also find the sauce on top pizza to be better after it gets cold.  You don't end up with the sauce dried into the crust, and the cheese all hard, but instead a nice, moist, saucy pizza.  The other favorite of mine is the BBQ chicken pizza.  This pizza is one that a lot of places can't seem to get right, but Aramingo's Best hits the nail on the head.  Nicely cooked pieces of grilled chicken, with a nice BBQ sauce...it's pretty much perfect.  Broccoli bites and mozzarella sticks are also delish.  They deliver quickly, and are always friendly over the phone.  5 Stars!

I attended a brunch here on Sunday for my fiance's birthday.

Pros:
1. Rooftop is cute
2. Food was good and reasonable.  We had the shrimp and grits (shrimps a little salty) and the fried fish and grits.
3. A lot of staff was on hand
4. The hookah staff was dope and helpful
5. The music was good
6. Directly across from the casino

Cons:
1. With all the wait staff on hand it was impossible to get prompt service (weirdest thing ever). I have never in my life witnessed so many people walking around doing nothing. The hookah staff was way more helpful than the waitstaff. 
2. Our reservation was not in the system. Thank goodness I had a confirmation email. They overbooked and did not have tables ready.  We sat for 20 minutes at our table before anybody came over to ask us about an order.  We had hookah though. Thank goodness for hookah or we would have gotten up and left.
3.  We were told we could not go up to the roof because it was a private event. Come to find out it was no private event. Once seated on roof with our hookah we were content.
4. The shots of Hennessy were more like a drops of Hennessy.
5. While it is called Golf & Social we could not play golf. There was no tech on staff. Go figure!!!!

This place caters to a younger crowd so I do not think the staff or mgmt. cares about customer service. My advice, train your staff to interact and ensure that all staff members have the same information.

I went with my girlfriend to Philadelphia to get her passport. I didn't want to go, but she convinced me to. We got to the office early so it wasn't open so we decided to look for a place close by to eat. There was nothing but this restaurant that called our attention since its called "Cuba Libre" and the owner of the restaurant we work for is Cuban and his always talking about owning a Cuban restaurant instead of a French restaurant. We decided to give it a try. We had to wait 30 minutes for them to open the doors but it was worth waiting. The staff was really friendly. The decoration is really interesting and it goes with the name of the restaurant. We ordered our food, we didn't expect to be as exquisite as it was. We were in a rush because we had to get back to New York and we needed to catch our train, we would have loved to stay for a little longer but we couldn't. We have plans of going back this summer just to eat at Cuba Libre. Thank you for an amazing experience,
Dinner was delicious. Table was kind of small made enjoying multiple plates at a time difficult. Otherwise decent services. Would come back with group of coworkers and sit at the bar

Long overdue review for this place, but these guys work their ass off and do a great job. I don't think the carpets and dashboard of my car have ever been cleaner without me having to do it myself. I like that they clean all the windows and hand dry the car after it comes out, and I like that you can watch them wash the car from the waiting area (you can see how much more thorough the hand washing is compared to your run of the mill automated drive-through car wash). It seems the $10 Sunday Special coupons that used to always be floating around the neighborhood may have ended, but I still think what they charge for hand washing a car is reasonable.

I'm an avid fan of Japanese cuisine and this place was a disappointment. To begin my rant, the place was hard to find. For those of you who are wondering, it's behind the parking lot of D&B. In addition, we had to pay $11 dollars just to park inside the lot and find this place. The place is pretty nice as it was being renovated. They have a great view (especially at night time, not sure how it is when the sun is out) you can see the bridge light up. 

As for the food, it was a complete disappointment. We ordered sushi, maki and sashimi, and it was as bland as cardboard. The special rolls we ordered were pretty much drenched in spicy mayo sauce that my appetite went away after a couple of pieces.The hibachi food was almost the same as everything else - bland. As someone stated before they use the same sauce for everything that I just lost it after a couple of bites. The hibachi show that the chef did was pretty amazing, it was probably the only thing worth paying for. 

Conclusion:
-food sucks and very pricey!!! You are basically paying $30+ dollars for a dish you can make yourself at home and maybe even better!!

I've been getting my hair cut and colored by Anne at Ovations for almost five years now, and the results are always fantastic - but my reason for this review is their on-site wedding day service. The Ovations team came to the bridal suite to do hair and makeup for my bridal party and family, and they were such a pleasure to work with. The hairstyles were stunning and the makeup was absolutely flawless!  Guests were commenting all night about how beautiful the bridesmaids and our mothers looked. I felt incredible all evening and everyone looks amazing in the photos. Anne, Katie, and Dee completely exceeded my expectations and made it a dream wedding day.

The absolute worst service I have ever had in my life. Rude ignorant mean horrible people do not go to this place. Two issues back to back. First time get their order in the drive-through pull up to the window they tell me I did not order and they do not have my order. The person actually told me that I can leave if I don't like something. Figured you know what F them I will not even bother leaving a negative feedback.  About four months later which is today I pull up and remember the situation but wanted my coffee. So instead of ordering a drive-through I pull up to the drive-through window and tell them hey can I get a coffee she looks at me and says you have to order a drive through the same person that serviced me the first time. I said remember me obviously she didn't last time you told me I didn't order so there's no point for me to stand and order at a drive-through here's my order she tells me to go F myself right to my face. I hope whoever reads this ignores this location.

We took this tour as a fun day out for our office department. We could not have had a better time. All the shops were great...plenty of chocolate for anyone to try or buy, and our tour guide Angie, was just phenomenal!

I visited L'Etage last night for an event. The space is cozy with fabric covered walls and dark, leather furniture. Given a large crowd, though, this performance space could get uncomfortable quite quickly. I honestly didn't find the staff to be that welcoming or friendly and the table service during the performance was distracting because it's hard to avoid walking in front of the stage. Also, $7 for gin and tonic? Really? I'll just bring a thermos next time. I'm interested to check out the story slams here, though I don't think they could even hold a candle to The Moth's productions.

They just opened a bar in a bigger space. Personally I think a bar that serves pizza is genius. The pizza is good and the drinks are even better. The people I've met here are great. Maybe it's not the best in the world, but it's really good and it's a bar. That serves pizza. You can get a giant slice of pizza with your drink. That alone should be the selling point. I'd recommend this to anyone.

The Mutter museum is a must-visit for anyone in the medical field. The museum provides a fascinating exploration into both the natural and pathologic realm of the medical field.  I strongly advise most attendants to understand that some of the materials/display can be very sensitive, and may not be appropriate to a younger, and scarable crowd. I will say that one of the disappointing aspects of the museum is its modest size, but it has enough content to warrant 1-2 visits with something new to learn each time.

Meh. I've had better. That's the official terminology for two stars. This is just like any diner ive ever been to I suppose. Kind of run down and crappy looking. All the people who work here seem stressed out. The food is ok. I've been here many times in the middle of the night it's consiststant and it's reliable. But what I rely on it being is just ok. It's cheap. And you get what you pay for.
This place Should be voted "Sexiest Head Chef" and "Most ruggedly handsome kitchen crew."

Free wi-fi!!  OMG, how cool is that, given that I can spend hours poking around here.  12th Street Cantina was pretty good- they have prepared Mexican foods, can make something fresh from the menu, and have staples ingredients like posole and quesos that you purchase for home cooking.  yum.  They didn't have horchata- booooo!  Salumeria was celebrating their 25th anniversary and handing out delicious (free) cupcakes.  I bought an incredible 3 year aged cheese that was exactly what I wanted- sharp, calcified, memorable.  Great coffees, fresh juice, etc.  Don't dismiss this as a tourist trap.

We went to this place because of National geographic saying it has the best Gelato In the World!!???and because the menu sounded delicious traveling with kids. The atmosphere was lovely, the waiters were the nicest and most accommodating we've experienced visiting Philly and the food was just delicious. The appetizers were great, the bread was insane, the salad of microgreen Arugula was incredible and the pizzas perfect. We were skeptical of the gelato being the best but oH MY GOD it was! We have had gelato all over Italy and this Gelato was amazing!!!! Don't miss this place it's really that good!!!!!!

Really cool Brewery/Tasting room, but the beer is good, not great. I feel like every time I go they do seem to have a new beer on tap and they have consistently been getting better. 

That being said they do have the single greatest thing I have ever seen at a Philadelphia brewery: City Wide Flights!!! Yes, they do a pairing of a couple tastings of their beers with a 3 or 4 different liquors from local distilleries. And it's cheap! This along with a super cool atmosphere is why I'll continue to come back as they come out with new and improving beer.

I'm a pretty big fan of this place. Their Mexican street corn is phenomenal. I'd have to say my favorite entrée here is their Brussels sprout tacos. Lovely atmosphere. Really good margaritas. Pretty decent service.

My friends live in the neighborhood so I've been when visiting them a few times. Absolutely love it here. The bartenders are super friendly and welcoming! Love the neighborhood feel in the bar. They recently updated their menu and I definitely want to head back and try the pork wings and turf burger. Yesterday I got the chicken fingers with side of hot sauce and blue cheese and fries. The chipotle aioli that comes with the fries is super good. And these chicken fingers were delicious! Not too crispy/fried. Drink selection is good and prices are cheap! Definitely want to head back for wing night and quizzo night!

I was debating between a 4 and 5 because I think the mac and cheese balls were overpriced, but it's unique enough to the area that it deserves a 5. I'll admit I've never had "authentic" Cajun food, but the gumbo was spicy but not overwhelming. The Train Wreck (sausage/salami po boy meeting a cheesesteak) is delicious; not as spicy as the gumbo but still enough heat to keep it interesting. Very warm and filling.

The staff is pretty nice and they do samples of the gumbo, so give it a shot.

1st time I've visited terminal B since it's been remodeled.  Generally love All the restaurants...found the ordering system a little confusing.  I ordered at takeout  and sat at the table but you can sit at the tables and order from the iPad and a server will bring you food/drink.  

I ordered the tomato braised chicken sandwich.  The chicken was really tasty but the roll was stale.  It included fresh cut French fries which were delicious.  I also ordered an iced.  Pro:  it's fresh brewed Con: it tasted like it came from the coffee pot.  I always ask myself if I'd be willing to try the same dish again and I'd say I'd give them a second chance but if the roll and tea aren't better, I'll choose somewhere else...lots of choices here!

The prime rib sandwich is ......... AMAZING !!!!!!!!!?

Great meat, cheese, roll, light pepper and onions!

Go! This lovely treat in the heart of Rittenhouse Square really may be my new favorite steak house in Philly. Stephen Starr's luxury meat and seafood "boutique" really blew me away.

Everything is so chic and sexy. From the small, intimate bar to the couch seating with marble tables, just gorgeous. My family has a yearly steak meal on Christmas eve and this was the best I can remember. You must try the Barclay Bellini if you are a fan of Bellini's; best I ever had. It has some vanilla in it and it's so refreshing. 

Glen was a fabulous server. He was extremely friendly, informative and warming. He gave us great suggestions and told us fun stories, he was a treat. 

The popovers everyone is talking about literally melt in your mouth. Warm and crispy outside and velvety inside. The fresh sea salt butter was just the diamonds on top. 

We started with the Scallops, Ceviche and fresh Crab... all of them were mouth watering. The scallops were pan seared perfectly, the ceviche was crispy and flavorful and the crab was perfection. Just great. 

The Filet was perfect and the sides, creamed spinach, baby carrots with honey glaze and truffle oil mashed potatoes did not disappoint. I could have bathed in the truffle oil mashed potatoes. They accidentally brought out the regular whipped potatoes and those were velvety perfection as well.

For dessert we went with the Creme brûlée cake and coconut ice cream, it was the perfect end. The ice cream was clearly house made and I want to know if they can ship it to my house.... DELICIOUS!

I can NOT wait to return!!

over rated.  Sometimes pats is a 5, sometimes its a 4, other times its an abysmal one.  Went last week and had to give it a 3.  Bread as usual, is great.  Its fast, so thats also good.  But the meat is so dry as its just sitting on a huge pile of cooked meat losing its flavor as its evaporating away.

Great selection. Incredibly friendly staff. It's one of the best places to go in the area if you just want to sit and chat or get work done. There's plenty of seating and free wi-fi. Highly suggested!

Everything was stale , very disappointed bought cinnamon buns hard as a rock, the cream doughnuts were good. Everything else very old and stale.
Not going back

small place with not much for decor, but none of that really matters to me - all i care about is the quality of the cut for my wavy hair.  michele did a great job.  i gave it a 4 only because its shorter than i wanted, but it still has the shape that we talked about and i felt like she really listened to what i wanted in that department.

as another review said, they are gossipy (i forgot something and when i came back i basically caught them making fun to me) - but hey, we dont go to hair salons to make friends do we?  so im definitely going back!

plus - they text you the day before to confirm your appointment which is so much easier than the awkward confirmation calls.

A neighborhood pizza place that outdoes the average neighborhood pizza place. 

The pizza is solid and has lots of options, plus their sandwiches from the grill are very tasty.

A great option when you don't want something fancy but you also don't want to cook!
Moved from the neighborhood but still frequent this place. I haven't found pizza in the northeast that touches this yet! They never disappoint.

been using coupons here.  Now suddenly they are only taking  certain coupons. The ones from the red plum weekly ads are ok the one worker told me. I guess some intranet coupons are circulating? But beware of where you get the coupons from.

Best hot pot in town!! Ingredients are super fresh, soup base/dipping sauce is incredibly flavorful. If you looking for the authentic Sichuan experience, make sure to get their duck blood - one of the very few places that serve fresh duck blood (directly from farm) in the States. Most importantly, it is very reasonably priced. Great service as well. Can't recommend enough!!
Great place to have a drink if you enjoy being surrounded by douchebags, OMG girls and screaming babies
Would of earned 5 stars because the chicken is terrific. My only complaint portions a bit small for the price. But wonderful FOOD!

Came here with family from out of town. I had been here before and was seated upstairs where it is nice and quiet. When we arrived this time the hostess sat us in the bar...it was very loud and not a great setting for four people trying to have a nice and quiet meal. I then went back to the hostess and asked if we could be seated upstairs, she responded very rudely that the upstairs was not being sat anymore for the evening...mind you this was at 8:00P...during dinner time. We took our seats at the table in the bar. The server was nice enough but took 15 minutes to greet us. She went right into if we would like to start with cocktails...and being a patron before I knew that Tuesdays were half off of bottles of wine at this location. This was a Tuesday night and I asked if this promotion was still going and she had a very annoyed look on her face, rolled her eyes, and said that yes in fact it was half off wine night. This really rubbed me wrong that she was going to be comfortable NOT promoting this special. We ordered an appetizer and each ordered an entree as well. We also ordered two bottles of wine through the course of the meal. If she was worried about her tip or us not spending enough, she was wrong. All and all the food was very good! The service needs some work, if your restaurant has specials your server should let the table know. These are things that bring customers back into your establishment. The hostess was rude for no reason -- word to management, this is the first person your customers come into contact with, perception is reality. There are definitely some areas of opportunity here. I would go back, but with caution.
I like that this place has raw cookie dough. It's an ice cream shop with a lot of options but high prices.

I love Dirty Frank's.  It is by far my favorite bar in the city.

A story:
Once, last winter, the power went out due to some kind of construction happening in the street.  Most places would have closed, but the bartender (I think it was Sheila that night) lit candles, we put on our coats, and kept on drinking.

The only thing that makes me sad is that they used to have a Ms. Pacman game, and now they've replaced it with some crappy video thing.  It's OK, now I save lots of money in quarters.

This Pho place is the best pho restaurant in philadelphia. I've been to at least 3-4 competitors in the chinatown area and none are as savory or as cost conservative. I get a large seafood bowl and my girlfriend gets the beef.
Love this place!  Great sandwiches and very friendly/ polite staff.  Ordered online and picked up a Reuben and the Restaurant School Special...best sandwiches ever.  Give them a try...

I'm going to make the bold claims that Barbuzzo is probably 1. the best restaurant in Philadelphia and 2. the best deal for restaurant week.

The inside is small but adorable.  Nice wood and great lighting with a very long bar that expanses the kitchen.  Our waitress was so happy and nice.  You get extra touches here like having your napkin folded while you're in the bathroom, a constantly full water/beverage, and she was paying so much attention that she overheard that this was a birthday dinner celebration and brought out dessert with candles.  

The food was phenomenal.  Highlights included the chef's crostinis, the roasted beet salad, sheep's milk ricotta, grilled swordfish, gnocchi and of course the salted caramel budino.  That being said, absolutely everything was delicious.  They were just the best.  Portions were generous.  I'd probably go back just for the beet salad and the ricotta.  

The drinks were great.  I got the sanguinello, a fruity lady's delight.  They also offer large sized bottles of beer like Chimay blue.  

Barbuzzo is the best.

Although Matt the bartender was extremely busy, he made us a perfect martini and was very efficient with the tapas we ordered  
The food was delicious;  the lamb chops were cooked perfectly, and the dates and almonds covered with bacon were a winner
this is by far the best place if you are a fan of beer.  they have by far the best selection of beer in philadelphia.  second best would be tria.

Great hotel! Had our mothers 80th b-day weekend with all the kids.  Rooms were great, location to shopping, restaurants, museums and terminal market (very cool), staff was great.  Fountain restaurant awesome - had dinner there 14 years ago and was blown away - never forgotten.  Had dinner again and it was incredible again!  We live in Miami and get a lot of false promises from restaurants - the Fountain is the real deal!  I am very picky and have been disappointed at other four seasons as well as the ritz in different cities.  Philly has a gem!
Simply awesome. We got a half plain half pepperoni and it was on point. Staff was friendly and service was good. All around an easy 5 stars.

It's a cold and rainy night. Football weather and the Giants are playing the Eagles at "Lincoln Financial Field." The stadium itself isn't really special. It's your standard open dome stadium. I'm sure for the fans of Philadelphia this stadium is rich in history. It's three levels high and has two large televisions. Another knock against them is everything is extremely over priced. The only ray of light qare the cheerleaders. They are well coordinated and bring value to the stadium. Towards the end if the game the Eagles fans started throwing beer from the stands. Plus I was cursed out while I tailgated with friends. I don't know if I would come back. The city of Philadelphia is rich and history and beautiful, but the stadium isn't moving the needle for me.
Great service, timely pick up and delivery. Definitely going to use again! Highly recommend!
Great sushi place it just has that vibe. The sushi itself is amazing and the staff are very friendly.  I recommend any of the sushi and ramen as well

I took my friend here for her birthday on Tuesday night. The food was phenomenal. And our server was friendly and knowledgeable but the hostess failed to mention to our server that it was my friend's birthday so our server didn't put a candle in our dessert. He promptly brought out another dessert with a candle in it once I mentioned it but it kinda ruined the night. It was even more frustrating because when the hostess called the day before to confirm the reservation, I was promised a candle would be placed in the dessert. Love the food, but I'm not sure if I'll be back.

I am absolutely addicted to the bowls!  Have tried, and love, both the Sriracha chicken bowl and the flank steak bowl. Delicious! The order is always packed carefully, and arrives at lightning speed. They always seem to be their estimated delivery time. The delivery people have been terrific as well... Safe and friendly. Highly recommended

I'm glad to see that Monk's gets the positive recognition it deserves.  Kind of a hole-in-the-wall walking up 16th street, really it's just looks like another bar.  But go inside, and Belgium comes alive!!!!  Well, not really, cause Belgium is not really lively country, kinda boring actually.  Belgium is all about beer, mussels, and Tintin (you'll know him if you look him up).  But in that respect, this place delivers. 

In fact, the mussels are better than "authentic" Belgian mussels cause, I presume, they come from this side of the Atlantic, where seafood just tastes better.  Sorry Europe.  Beers are great and plentiful, but my one gripe is that they do a lot of domestic Belgian-style brews, which, IMO always never taste as good as one made by a real monk in Belgium.  

With places like this, why do people ever go to Irish pubs?
The classic was perfectly seasoned with just the right amount of sauce. Great price too!!

This place is fantastic!!  Food is great, atmosphere is intimate. It can get a little tight when there is a wait, and there really isn't anywhere to wait inside-- but the staff is super accommodating and really makes you feel welcomed.

A story of two kitties: 

Two kitties were adopted and needed to be spayed/neutered. In comes Dr Lowenstein & Wissahickon Creek Veterinary Hospital. A house converted into a business, the warm homey ambiance did little to relax the kitties, but reassured us human. Lowenstein, a kind and comfortably quirky veterinarian, gave special attentive care to the kitties. He sweetly did not charge us an arm and leg when the female kitty tried to cunningly undo her stitches and had to be kept overnight for several days.
After entertaining us with stories of her own creatures, the Vet Tech gave us a friendly smile and said "please do not hesitate to call to check up on her!", leaving the door wide open for us to be crazy pet owners we are.

Ordered for delivery several times and ate here once and the fish is impressively fresh.  The sushi rolls are very yummy and the restaurant is inviting and the staff are nice. My only gripe is the price.  This is probably one of the most expensive sushi places in the area if you compare price per roll or per piece.  Also the fish slices are relatively small in sashimi and nigiris.  I would suggest eating here when you want to taste good fish but are not so hungry.

First time in Philly so I went here to grab the original cheese steak. The cheese steak was honestly low quality and the eating environment kinda stunk. I had to go to a Dunkin Donuts just to take a piss.... Thriving on just its merit is the only thing about this cheese steak that keeps people coming. Jim steaks south street, Spataro's, or Molly Malloy's can make you a cheese steak that's 10x better. Don't make the same mistake that I made, listen to every local and don't go there. Being the first is great but it doesn't make it the best. The Wright Bros brought us the airplane but Glenn Curtiss made the aircraft what it is today.

Menagerie is one of my favorite coffee spots in the whole city! 

Staff and service is a delight. Their iced coffee is fantastic. They usually have a tasty, berry flavored iced tea as well. They have a limited food menu but all of it is awesome- especially the breakfast sammy and oversized cookies. 

Great environment to sit and work in with lots of seating and they play the best music! 

Prices are what you would expect for a small "non-chain" coffee shop but the service and quality makes it well worth it.

Great food and attentive service! The chilled beet soup has a refreshing taste with cucumber. The asparagus with beets was the most delicious plate. It was cooked well and the vegetables were quite fresh. I opted for scallops as the entrée and they were perfectly cooked. Definitely recommend. Keep in mind it is BYOB and cash only, got a gentle reminder as I was seated.

I've been back to Rita's a few times and after giving them a look and a talk, they seem to understand that large does not mean half a cup. So now we're on a friendship level. 

I get the vanilla custardy stuff with nilla wafers. Talk about delish! I highly recommend it! Now if only I could figure out what this other stuff is. I'd love to try something lemony!

Completely in agreement with Sean there, there's not much change since the name change. They did close off the kitchen area, added a sushi bar and eliminated all Chinese dishes but the end result left little to be desired, at least when it was Asian Fusion, they had kebabs and those Nan Zhou noodles which were quite good even though they charged a lot more than Chinatown. 

They advertised themselves as Thai but they are not. The dishes are Thai but the ingredients and the cook failed to be authentic. The fried Calamari tasted like it came out from a frozen back you can get at Super Fresh or BJs. Thick coat of breading, flavorless, they give you 5 squid rings for your order and some slices of tomatoes and an unidentifiable sauce. Fried tofu are cubes of mouth-burning blandness, fresh from the fryer, the fried tofu absorbed all the heat and it's really dangerous if you don't give your customer any warning. Instead of the Malaysian/Thai version where a big chunk of fried tofu are hollowed out with a peanut sauce and bean sprouts. The tofu are just cubes of nothingness, not even a bit of salt and pepper and served with the same unidentifiable bland sauce. 

Their Pad Thai looks to have all the same fixings, the whole dish is wet and had a naggingly sweet flavor, you don't taste any savory from fish sauce or soy sauce or salt to balance it out. Only the taste of diluted sweet chili sauce. It's almost a dessert. The saving grace of our meal was the string bean with beef, and even that is a bit too wet. I don't know what the change of a name can do to a business if everything else stays the same. Good news is that they have 20% off if you dine in, but I didn't even bother to check the bill when we paid. Is it really worth it? I can't imagine myself coming back or ordering take out from this place.

1.5 Stars
Go here to pay homage to the great Founding Father, Ben!

Wow, now I don't know whether Luhv or Goldie is my favorite moderate/fast food vegan place.  As impressive as Luhv is, I think Goldie is even better.  I simply have never had falafel this good.  In fact, I went a few years not being sure I even liked falafel, because at other restaurants, it is so heavy and bland.  Goldie's falafel is crisp, light, and delicious.  I loved it served atop Goldie's salad, which featured mint leaves, beets, chopped cucumber, delicate salad greens, and a drizzle of tahini.

My review is about my plant shopping experience! The owner Amin loves plants and is hosting houseplant pop-ups every Friday-Sunday 12-8 pm. While it's normally a teahouse, he says he is rebranding and plans to have these sales every weekend. Parking is a bit tricky in the area on weekends, but I found free one-hour parking on the next street over (Cresson St under the bridge). I first learned about the pop-ups from a local plant group on Facebook and messaged Amin to make sure the plants I wanted were in stock. He was so great at answering all my questions and I brought a friend along! We could tell he really loves plants and shared in our excitement, because he knew which plants had been repotted recently and told us not to disturb them (ha, rookie mistake I've made in the past after purchasing a plant). I'm not an impulsive buyer and had been looking around for a philodendron birkin for the right price, and this is where I got it! We're both really happy with our plants and within the next 1-2 weeks of buying our plants we both saw new growth. Would highly recommend getting your plants from here!

Dear Vito,

I can't thank you enough for making Jennifer's wedding the most magnificent night of her life. You exceeded all our expectations and beyond. Everything was so pretty I can't even express in words today now that the wedding is over. You are a very creative, talented person and work hard to please both the bride and the parents. Working with you was so much fun and you made  Jennifer feel so important right to the very end. Your dream team with Kathleen made it so special and completely trouble free. All I can say is all the work you put into it, magnificent chupah, lighting, tables and all your beautiful touches took our breath away. Thank you Vito you are a floral extraordinaire and made a beautiful venue even more magnificent. I can't thank you enough. Vito and Kathleen you are a delight to work with. Vito you talent is beyond words!

Love,
Randi, Rob and Jennifer

Great service. Treated well. Great quality. I got a gel manicure 3 weeks ago and I just got my first chip. Had to take it off bc my nails grew out and that's the only reason. Also, I work in healthcare and am constantly washing my hands and using antibacterial soap and it still lasted this long. Highly recommend!!

While traveling for business, I always try to find an affordable yoga studio for a few classes. The drop in rate at Maha was $9 and that was by far the best price in the area. A mat rental was $2.

I took the general yoga hour class. The instructor was a substitute, so I didn't get her name, but she was lovely! Her music complemented the flow. The city views and sounds from inside the studio were also lovely. 

Maha is inside a multi-level building and there's no marking for it at street level. I walked back and forth a few times trying to find the studio. I actually stumbled into the correct building planning to ask for better directions.

One of a kind sandwiches which satisfy your taste buds. However, 2 things that need to be addressed here. The workers are super nice and they give you the local deli/sandwich shop experience, but... I'm not so sure about the sanitation of this place. 
First off, they don't use gloves. They make sandwiches with their bare hands and handle money with those hands afterwards. 
Secondly, the sandwiches make you thirsty for water or a soft drink. 
Don't get me wrong. I loved the sandwiches and will probably go back for more, but they could use less salt.

Stopped by on Sunday before dinner at a.kitchen and  just loved a.bar. This place had happy hour oysters, handcrafted cocktails, a unique wine list and Matt, the bartender was knowledgeable and helpful.
We will definitely be coming back.

Not a fan of this place at night.  The large foyers at the top of the stairwells tend to have a few homeless people sleeping on the floor during the day, and a LOT of people hunkering down by nightfall.  Going in to catch a late train is a little daunting when you're by yourself.  Case in point: they've put up large mirrors on the wall, so that you can look to see if someone is following you.  Comforting.
Also, every entrance smells like pee.  All the time.  It's a giant wall of urine that you hold your breath through until you emerge above ground, into fresh air.  I see people mopping the floor or cleaning periodically, but it never seems to do any good.
Catching the train here at peak times is a bonus, though, since it's near the end of the line.  You're guaranteed a seat at Market East, whereas getting on at 30th street can often mean standing the whole way home.  
And if you need to pop into a store, the Gallery is huge... plus the access to the Reading Terminal Market.
Amazing food. They went above & beyond to make my moms birthday special. Our service was amazing!

The food is good but a little bland, especially the lemonade. The pilaf didn't taste like much, and i didn't get enough sauce at the end to make my dish taste like anything substantial. The actual hummus was good!

When I say "BOBA" you say "BUBBLES" ?... I guess its an East Coast thing. 

Being a frequent boba I mean bubble tea indulger, I judge them by the Honey green milk tea. I should have stuck to one of their top drinks, but I guess its not one they are popular for and I can see why. Lucky me, I also had the honey green tea which was real good. You can adjust your sugar level, so if you normally do not do sweet, be sure to ask for it less sweet! This hot humid weather in Philly has gotten me craving for it all the time. The bubbles are smaller than what I'm used to but it was some good grade balls I must say (pun intended). My friend also had the curry fish balls and for under 2 bucks it was worth it. 

In Chinatown you are going to find this place located right in that hustle and bustle, its a bit cramp inside when you order but the drinks come out fast. Oh did you ask about parking, its pretty much non-existent here in Chinatown... so hopefully you're dropping by because you're in the area, otherwise its not worth it. 

Customer service was efficient and fast ! I will be back for some more of that Honey green tea.

I had my hair done by Kim Tran. I have never had my hair as beautiful as when she did my color and cut. She is a blond specialist and the combination of colors she used to highlight my hair were just perfect. I was also surprised at how healthy and shiny my hair was after she worked on it. Regrettably I moved away and I haven't been back, which meant that I have been going to new places and none of them have even come close, thus my hair is not as beautiful as it used to be. I am seriously thinking on driving 3 hrs for Kim to do my hair again.

October 5th, 2018 my husband and I with two friends went to Devon Seafood in Rittenhouse Square to celebrate their 35th Wedding Anniversary.  Our server was  , he was very attentive, funny, pleasant and helped make our dining experience enjoyable.  We also met one of the managers, Sherie Mee. She is amazing!  We observed her working hard with the kitchen staff, she also greeted us and made our evening very special.  You can tel that Sherie loves what she does and is very good at it. This location is very savvy when it comes to customer service and handling issues, if any.  I do suggest you call in advance and make a reservation to help make your dining experience go without a hitch.  Ever since my husband and I have come to Devon, we feel at home and the waitresses and waiters are eager to serve.  They want you to have a great time and they are diligent about getting your food to you.  I recommend Devon for any event small or large.  They cater to EVERYONE!  When you sign up for their email, you'll receive special coupons that offer a free entree for your birthday, anniversary and periodic discount off coupons just to visit the restaurant.  Don't feel the coupons cheapen this restaurant, they are top-notch  and service oriented. I think it's a great marketing strategy to get folks coming in and being long-term customers.  If you have an issue, please kindly let them know.  The food and presentation was so amazing.  Our friends are still talking about their anniversary and shared it on social media. We had lobster, chicken, fried shrimp, mussels, fish and it was delectable.  Not to mention the honey and hot biscuits  that come with your meal.  You become obsessed with these before your meal comes out.  I'm a borderline diabetic and I had atleast four rolls before dinner.  When you visit Devon, go with every intention to have a positive experience and you will.  This is one of my favorite places to go.  Also, sit in the back area where there's elegant booth seating.  Some people prefer dining al fresco (outside), but I prefer the indoors.  They also have a great bar area that is friendly, savvy and they have a wide selection of wines.  This is my experience, I hope you will enjoy it to.  I don't offer my review for any restaurant, they have meet several basic standards. Atmosphere, service, quality, location and price. Devon is in the heart of Rittenhouse Square with beautiful panoramic views.  Give them a try!
Oh my lordddddd this place has the worlds greatest breakfast sandwiches!!! And you can get a hash brown on it. I am so so in love with Kimeys I wish it was closer to my home.

The best broccoli rabe and provolone sausage around! This Italian Market butcher shop is "old school", and definitely worth the stop for their homemade sausage. The rib eye steaks and bacon are very good as well.  Honorable mention to the Sicilian Wine sausage.

Drinks and food has always been great here.   Only reason why not 5/5 is because service can be spotty.  Its either the best service ever or you're sitting at a table to be cleaned for 20 minutes....
Anyway they have an extensive drink menu which includes whiskey flights.  I am a huge fan to come here for drinks.  The ambiance is very cool with the dark wood and industrial lights.  
My absolute favorite thing to get here is the ice cream- the guy makes it from scratch!
Hey for a whiskey flight and some home made ice cream, I keep coming back regardless of the service.

What a beautiful restaurant and meal. The location is perfect, a renovated series of Philadelphia brownstones. My fiance is a vegetarian, and I had been wanting to take him here forever. We were not dissappointed. Service was on point, and the food was imaginative and delicious.

not your fancy yoga, but solid. they bend over backwards (no yoga pun intended) to adjust the pricing to everyones budget. yoga is expensive and it should be available to everyone. hawthorne makes it available. lots of classes. good teachers who care about the practice not the spot light or the glory. they have gifts they want to share that fit every yoga level. there are lots of events and workshops and again, pricing is reasonable. if you get a chance, support the little guy. this is a great thing for the neighborhood and community. AND theres usually parking close by. its kinda a no brainer.

The line moved slowly but the staff was pleasant enough.  I didn't order coffee, buying a Philadelphia You are Here mug and bottled water.  The fellow dropped the mug onto the counter when he opened the box to scan the bar code. He did, however, show me that it wasn't damaged before putting it back in the box.  The mug cost $12.09, $1.14 over the blacked out price tag (I have purchased many of these mugs before) and the water was $1 more than the another airport vendor.  Although it was only a few dollars, I felt ripped off.

I just drove an HOUR because this place had such great reviews and they did not disappoint! TOTALLY WORTH IT! I had Jenne! HILARIOUS! Very comfortable! FAST! And personable! Loved it and worth the drive. Just TMI...... She used hard wax, which HURT LESS!! I was "grown out" long and my whole appt was 30 min.... And she was done. Very THOROUGH TOO! Made sure she got the back side. Loved this place, I can't rave enough. No questions, WORTH IT!

The food here is really good. The beer was a plus when they had it. My friends and I would go almost every day from school but the one owner was actually rude to us. He would not only yell at his employees in front of the entire restaurant but my friends and I who again spend money their every single day were watching a movie on the TV they have in the restaurant the owner came up and rudely changes the channel. Like come on man, we come here every day. The food is great I don't know what the complaints are about but it:s just a weird vibe when you kind of think of a spot as a hang out and the owner acts like that. Some of the staff is really nice it breaks your heart to hear them being screamed at by the owner.
Had to update after dealing with headches post-visit re: insurance.  I don't know how these people made it through medicial school, nursing school, or... any school.

These guys really saved my day. I ride a scooter to work and with the sudden onslaught of below freezing temperatures, it had engine problems. Long story short - I was supposed to get towed by another company (provided by the scooter company's service provider, Road America) who kept me waiting an entire day while they played phone games. Today they tried the same thing. Luckily after alot of back and forth the company booked South Philly Towing who was at my work in under ten minutes. Before they got there, South Philly Towing gave me a courteous heads up phone call. The driver who towed me to the scooter shop was friendly, competent, and even let me put my bicycle in the flat bed so I didn't have to ride it to the shop. Can't say enough good things about how professional these guys are.
Eric ALWAYS goes above and beyond, really went out he his way to help my family. Thanks Eric !

The service was perfect. The decor is amazing, fun and a ton of history. The food was delicious and certainly priced fairly. As an original Philadelphian I'm upset I haven't found this neighborhood gem any sooner. I can't wait to go back.

Even in an Airport I will not concede to subpar coffee, I will find the closest Starbucks and even if it's out of the way I will make a trip to get my morning coffee.  

My flight was leaving at 7am and I was definitely needing my coffee once I made it through security.  I was flying out of terminal D and this Starbucks was a short walk and was between terminals B and C.  

My coffee was made to my liking, the baristas were friendly and I was able to use my gold card.  I hope they expand their locations within the Airport and eventually add a Starbucks to terminal D, until then it's a short walk for my coffee.

What an awesome hidden gem on Passyunk!  I wasn't overly excited for Scandinavian food either. I don't know any fat swedes and never thought their food was particularly good but Noord was fantastic. 

This moderately-priced BYO had great food, quality ambience, and incredible service. Everyone that worked there was so nice, asking us to come back, and being genuinely tickled that we enjoyed the cuisine. 

We got the smoked fish appetizer, which was really good, particularly the scallop. Both entrees were delicious and the dessert was really good as well. 

We sat at the "bar" instead of a traditional table--mostly due to circumstance, but watching the three chefs handle the cooking load was fun, educational, and interesting.

Great experience, even better results. The staff is super professional and friendly, I felt relaxed and comfy throughout the whole experience, my lashes turned out incredible, and even left with a mini care package. The only problem is I'm going to be addicted to LashBee from here on out!

Being from New York, admittedly it is difficult for me to really agree when delicatessens boast the "New York Style" moniker.   Most of the time, the effort is greater than the product.  As far as Kaufman's is concerned, I will say that two things do remind me most of New York delis:  the matzoh ball soup, and the attitude.

The soup is truly a sight to behold.  First, it's huge.  For nearly $13.00, it really has to be.  When you get the soup to go, you actually get two quart-size containers:  one for the broth, chicken, veggies, and pasta, and one for the matzoh balls.     The matzoh ball itself is just the right combination of fluffy and dense:  light around the edges and harder in the middle.  The chicken and broth are also perfectly prepared.  I personally appreciate the pasta (bow ties), but that is definitely unusual as far as authentic matzoh ball soup is concerned.    The only change I would make would be to add a little more carrots.   

What has not been so great is that every time I go to Kaufman's, whomever is behind the counter (the owner?) seems like he is bothered or annoyed.  A smile goes a long way with customers.  Just last week, I came during lunch hour and there was hardly anyone there.  The man behind the counter took my order and then mentioned that the credit card machine wasn't working.  I didn't have cash on me so I told him I would come back after a trip to the ATM.  He shrugged his shoulders.   When I returned 5 minutes later, he acted like he never saw me before.  Then an elderly woman came in and asked about the dessert selection (there were hardly any cakes or desserts on display) and he gruffly told her that they were waiting for the bakery.   That struck me as odd since there were many signs around the deli indicating that their cakes and desserts were made in-house.

I do enjoy the food - especially the aforementioned soup - immensely, but I think I would be  likely to go to Kaufman's more often if I felt that it was a friendly establishment.   Something just doesn't feel right.

Since we can all agree this place isn't for cheap skates, I think I'll just point out how unique this place is. I have ate here a few times for lunch and overall, for a quick lunch bite, its pretty good. Not amazing... but Good. Again and again, I find that the sandwiches look better piled up in the store than they really are. 

This is the sort of place that you bring your Outta Philly Guests.... they will love it. It really redefines food shopping as more of an experience than a chore... for that reason... it gets four stars from me.

This place is really awesome, our favorite breakfast/brunch in the area. It's kind of small, BUT because of that they're expanding... And because of THAT, they're currently CLOSED UNTIL 8/21/2014 when they'll open a much bigger place right next to the old one. 

When they reopen, get the sausage gravy and Nutella strawberry waffles with a scone on the side. They're all great.

People here are stingy with their stars. After reading reviews and ignoring the rating, a friend and I decided to try it out. The food was cheap and delicious. Nothing like starting your day with a large meal for under 8 dollars. Service was quick and hospitable, and I would definitely come back.

I wish I took a picture, but I had a cheesesteak and it was very good! My mom always says the bread makes the sandwhich. 

Well this place makes their own secret bread and it goes so well with their cheesesteak. I never went to Pete's and those well known cheesesteak places so it's hard to compare. But I'd say give this place a try and decide for yourself!

Once you eat from Blaze, you'll always go back! I swear, Blaze makes it hard to enjoy pizza from anywhere else!!! When I tell you I LOVE this restaurant, I absolutely mean it!! The workers are friendly! The service is quick! The restaurant is very clean and I always have a great experience when I visit. The manager Rachel seems to inspire her team to be nothing but excellent! Now as for the pizza, the toppings have such good variety from sauce, cheese, meats, veggies, you name it! And the sizing is perfect...one of their "build your own" pizzas is great, I think, to enjoy a few slices and save a few for later! (Get your own, WAYY TOO GOOD TO SHARE) Not to mention the crust is thin & perfectly crisp, not too doughy at all! If I could treat myself to a Blaze pizza everyday, without drowning in self guilt, I sooo would!! (Haha, but seriously) So delicious! And don't forget to try some garlic pesto on the crust!! Yummy

We booked two rooms 6 weeks ago for a special weekend celebration with friends then were called and told over a month later after receiving a confirmation notice that a wedding party had booked the rooms earlier. Frankly I think they got the business after our booking and bumped us from the rooms. Poor business and customer service which will be reported to the better business bureau. I understand this has happened to others with a reservation at this hotel. And there was no offer to make good on the reservation at another time.

My fiancé and I went yesterday for Valentine's Day. They had a set menu, and everything we ate was awful. Cesar salad was overwhelming, pork belly was not Crispy on the side ( very chewy), the sirloin was way undercooked and cold, the pasta was cold and undercooked as well (not al dente) probably the worse pasta I've ever tried.They charged $90 for a set meal and the service was ok. The bar looks pretty and the restaurant has a good atmosphere.

Given the fact that Will was on the Top 20 restaurants in Philadelphia, I came in with high expectations. I feel really bad not giving them as high a rating as I initially would have wanted to but I really did not have that great of a time. It also did not help that one of my friends got slight food poisoning after our visit (after having the skate as she's the only one who ordered that)

We came in for the $45 pre-fixe on Sunday, wherein we had everything from baked snails (four stars), pork belly (five stars), lavender spiced duck (three stars), and caramelized banana (three stars). The sweet-potato-apple soup was also amazing (Five stars). 

We got served foccacia bread with butter and sea salt. However, the foccacia was not as soft as you'd normally get it. It was almost stale. But this may have just been from our experience, and it was our bad for not clarifying with the server if this is the consistency they wanted for the bread or was this a mishap on their part. 

Of the dishes, don't think a lot of them are worth breaking down fully. However, over-all, I got the sense that the pre-fix menu was trying too hard to be innovative. The pairings between lavender-spiced duck and squash did not work very well, as the tastes did not necessarily compliment, in the same way the caramelized banana was overpowered by its accompanying cinnamon cake. 

At some times, they did create amazingly innovative combinations. The sweet potato soup did that very well by marrying apple cider foam with sunflower granola/seeds and chestnut cream. This dish, which was a pour over, led to high expectations for the meal. The different textures blended well together and the creamy balanced the somewhat sour, and the crunchy brought everything together. The appetizers were definitely standouts, but it just seemed to have gone downhill from there. 

Though the restaurant's vibe would make for a great date spot, given its intimate and small size, the food really did not sit well with me. I would give this restaurant a shot again because there were some bright spots, but I was still quite disappointed over-all.
Yes! As good as everyone says. Lines are long at the hot spots but they move fast. Whoopie pies at the Flying Monkey are incredible- get one!

If you're looking for amazing, low key eats, this is a hot spot. The food is bangin and you can't find anything else like it in the city. Be careful, some days they run out of food by very early afternoon and have to close up earlier than listed.

Ordered delivery.  Waited an hour and a half only to get a call that my food would be another hour because of delivery problems.  I am usually flexible, but a call that late is unacceptable.  And for all that they didn't even offer a free meal...
The atmosphere is great, music, decor! The music was the right volume so you can talk. Our Server Nicollett was very hospitable and fun! The margarita tower was good, I'll be back for more drinks!

Nice selection of beer. Got a nice saison and friend had one of their porters. 

Atmosphere is chill, also serves food.

Sweeten your warm days with Weckerley's ice cream. I came to visit my Philly friends and one friend was very adamant about taking us here. It was his current favorite ice cream store. 

This is a very cute boutique ice cream store. Locally owned and locally made with organic ingredients. When you step in, the walls are painted a light baby blue color. I absolutely liked the simple and boutique design. Light blue and white with cute succulents to add some greenery to it. Very instagramable and very picture friendly.

The minute I stepped into the store, I was welcomed with a nice aroma of waffles and greeted by the friendly staff. The waffle smell was so inviting that I knew I had to get an ice cream waffle cone. 

I sampled the beard of bees, lavender flavor, and cookies n cream. I liked all 3 flavors but I was feeling basic so I got the cookies n cream sugar waffle cone. The ice cream was rich and very creamy. It had the right amount of sweetness. Enough for my sweet tooth and not overly sweet for my friends who don't often eat desserts. My ice cream waffle cone was 6$. A little more on the steep side but they hooked it up. I kept eating and eating but I just couldn't finish it. I had to take some ice cream out so I could eat the sugar cone. Had I known, I would've shared my ice cream so it wouldn't go to waste. It was too good to be thrown out. 

Ice cream doesn't have to be eaten only on a hot day. Anytime you crave for rich and creamy ice cream, stop by, get a sample and if you like it then ice cream on.

Wow. Is all I can say. Stopped here on a rainy and dreary day just to give it a try. My husband loves cinnamon sugar pretzels and is used to Auntie Annie's being a hit or a miss. This pretzel was 100% a hit! It was so soft and they made it to order. So delicious!! I had the twisted braid and that too was so fresh and soft. Husband said he's never going back to Auntie Annie's again! Customer service was also great too!!

The service at this place is crap.  My wife and I were visiting Philadelphia and came here on July 28 at 7:34 PM.  Since we don't drink, we love to go "restaurant hopping" when we visit new places.  We stopped in Tom's Dim Sum because we noticed on the menu that Tom's listed Fried Cheese Wonton on their menu.  We were very excited because most places have Crab Cheese Wontons or Crab Rangoons.  She hasn't been able to find a good cheese Wonton since she lived in Minneapolis and I haven't had them since I lived in Denver.  (with the exception of Panda Express)

We decided to order a couple of small plates.  We ordered some steamed vegetable dumplings.  We also ordered the Cheese Wonton.  My wife broke the Wonton apart and saw something inside it that didn't look right.  I took a bite and it was filled with crab.

I called the waiter over and pointed out that it had crab inside it.  He said that it does have crab and can't be made without.  I asked him to take it off the bill because the menu just listed it as Cheese Wonton.  He refused and said that because we took a bite that he couldn't take it off the bill.  I pointed out again that the menu said that it was a Cheese Wonton not a crab wonton.  After all - this should be accurate in case someone has a seafood allergy.  He refused.  I was taken back that he said that he couldn't take it off the bill because we took a bite out of it.  What was he planning to do?  Serve it to someone else?

I decided to visit this location because the one near where I live the staff is always so rude, I experienced quite the opposite here, the selection was huge by comparison, the live plants were just fantastic and the lawn ornaments were the best. I had gone in expecting to spend about 20.00 but ended up spending almost 45.00 cause the stuff was just that good, from now on we are only shopping at this location

Awesome! I've never heard of a sushi burrito before I was recommended to this place, but I'm super glad I now know what they are! The two ladies working when I arrived were extremely kind and helpful and got straight to recommending and making us two delicious burritos. They were extremely flavorful and delicious - and not too bad in price. 

I would definitely recommend this place.
This is a classic. Very much on the pricey side but the atmosphere is amazing the location is cool. The service is pretty quick

Almost exclusively order off of caviar, but this place always tastes great. The tofu is very flavorful and the salmon bowls hit the spot every time. I don't think they've been carrying mango or pineapple lately, which is a bit upsetting, as they were great in the bowls, but the results are still excellent.

Make your own salad, bowl, or sushirito. The rolls are meh, but the poke is great.

Parada Maimon is a new Latin/Dominican restaurant located in the loft district just north of Chinatown/Vine Street.  The building is the old location for Billy G's luncheonette, which closed awhile back.  The owner has a restaurant by the same name in New York City, and has opened his second location here in Philly.  They serve breakfast, lunch, and dinner and the menu is heavy on the dominican/latin/caribbean cuisine.  They usually have a few specials on the steam table, such as beef stew and roasted chicken.  The specials come with a choice of yellow or white rice as well as black or red beans.  They have an extensive menu including mofongo, patties, stews ceviches, platters, and sandwiches.  They feature a number of proteins, but seafood if prevalent on the menu.  The operation is a family affair, with the owner, his daugher, and I believe a few other family members.  The service has been very good on my two trips for dinner thus far.  They offer delivery and pickup as well, and take credit cards.  The owner is very friendly, and will take the time to answer any questions you may have and make small talk if you're so inclined.  Overall, we're very happy to have this new addition to our neighborhood.  The owner is a restaurant veteran, so we hope the place will do well!

Scott is extremely professional and knowledgeable. Both me and my client were beyond satisfied with the level of service that we received. Scott provided a conclusive report as well as tips throughout the inspection that my client noted to improve the home once purchased. A master inspector and highly recommended!

There are some people that like to chat and hang out at cafes.  I do, too, but sometimes I need a place where I can work leisurely, so I am always on the lookout for a good work or study cafe.  In Center City, Philly, I've found these hard to come by.  So far, after over a dozen cafes, I've found La Va to be best.  They have comfy couches, a moderately quiet work place, free wifi, and even tables, if you are so inclined.  Awesome loose leaf tea, too.  I also love how homey and "neighborly" it feels, but I did come on their 10th year birthday.  If you need a comfy place to work, give it a try!

This place came highly recommended to me by a friend and it lived up to my expectations. I came for Sunday brunch and ordered the chicken pot pie, Mac and cheese pie, and pear ginger crumb pie with a scoop of Bassett's vanilla ice cream on the side. 
The Mac and cheese pie satisfied my salty cravings and the crust was nice and buttery. The biscuit topping the chicken pot pie was delicious and the flavor of the pot pie was on point. They looked small but were surprisingly filling.
The pear ginger crumb pie was amazing. Definitely get the scoop of ice cream on the side (+$3 but totally worth it). The only thing that would have made the experience perfect was if the pear ginger pie was heated, I just love the combo of hot pie and cold ice cream. Both savory pies were good but I think the sweet pies are the real winners. I'll be back soon to try out the other sweet pies, my eye is on the butterscotch bourbon.

This is in my opinion the best sushi place in Center City. I do not get specialty rolls when I get sushi because they are usually expensive and not really worth it BUT here there are so good. There are so many unique sushi rolls and flavor that you can't really find anywhere else. All the sushi is fresh and the rice/fish ratio is great. Bonus: they let our party stay way past 11 PM (their closing time) while we finished our wine bottle and conversation. Also, our waitress was great: quick service, packed up all our leftovers for us, and gave us extra sauces free of charge. Overall, great food and awesome ambiance.

This is the 4th Omni hotel I've stayed at, the 3rd hotel in Philly I've stayed in and so far the best in both categories.

The location is absolutely great - right across from Independence Park, a couple blocks from Market and best of all, two blocks from one of my favorite bars, Khyber Pass. I've been suckered into those awful hotels closer to City Hall and let me tell you, that area is not fun (except for McGillin's). But at the Omni, you're in a great neighborhood, surrounded by good food and drinks and of course tons of tourist attractions.

The staff is very nice and accommodating and gratefully don't put on that super-friendly act. They're real people and they know that you don't want to have a whole conversation and you just want to get to your room and lay down.

The quality of the room was excellent - HUGE and facing west with a view of the park. It was also nice and clean, and the walls were thick enough that we couldn't hear any noise form the other rooms (tv's or otherwise...)

I'm a member at Omni so I tend to favor their hotels because of the select guest club, but I can honestly say that I would've enjoyed my stay without the free drinks.

Loved the atmosphere. The bartenders were very attentive and friendly. Apps were tasty. The draft list could be a little better I saw a few that weren't tapped yet that I would've like to have instead but that's my taste. Check it out!

Yes Lindsay Y., popsicle addiction is a real disorder because I now have it too.  I have not been to the store, but instead get my pops from their cart at the farmer's market.  I have had pineapple basil.  Couldn't really taste the basil, but I didn't care because it was just a slice of heaven the way it was.  Creamy yet dairy free.  And so fruity good.  I have also had the lime mint.  The day was a scorcher and this pop quenched my thirst like nothing else.  I have a full on addiction.  It is dangerous.  I look forward to Thursdays just so I can get these pops from the farmer's market.  I am glad I don't live near their store or my wallet would be lighter and my sugar intake higher.
Great place. I'll be back. Extremely fast service, and the noodles we got were delicious.

Good Mexican food. It is a BYOB. They charge a small corkage fee. Other than that, it is really cheap. And they do take out! The decor is great, the place is filled with all sorts of interesting and colorful mexican kitsch. They have a great selection of hot sauces...

Just had my first spray tan at Bronzed. The attendant was terrific, discussed options, darkness desired, and after care. I came in an hour earlier than my appointment just to see if they could fit me in - and she very graciously did. I'll be back!

Killer customer service. Had a bad experience yesterday and left a review. They called me almost immediately after the review and offered a complimentary meal to make up for the experience and change my perspective. Definitely did right by me and the food was great this time. Had the chicken bao buns and crispy pork dumplings and both were done very well but I did like the dumplings more. They tossed in a couple of extras, including chocolate covered fortune cookies, some kind of fried chip, almost like tortilla strips, and I believe it was a hibiscus tea which was very good. All in all a good experience and I will definitely return and recommend the place to friends. Shout out to Irene for doing a great job
British food which tends to be hearty. Always packed, make reservations. I loved their outdoor tables and the restaurant itself, when open is a cool labyrinth of small rooms.

$2.50 for a spicy barbecue pizza. It lured me. Better than Fame's? I'd say about the same. Just go to the one with the shorter line. The pizza won't blow minds but Temple is a food desert. 

Two stars in the normal world, probably three at Temple.

we had been struggling to find a good pizza spot that wasn't fancy. so glad someone recommended Mattei Family Pizza. we've ordered 3 times now and will continue to get delivery from them. the pizza arrives at the estimated time and the food is still hot. the pizza and salads have been wonderful. excited to try more things on the menu. 

100% recommend.

Wow, this place took me 2 days to find! I kept wandering up and down the street looking for it. Eventually, I found a place and went in and asked if it was CWAS but he said that was across the street. After a drink I didn't need I still couldn't find it. Eventually, I did and I am so glad. This place is a high temple to mixology! They really take their libations seriously. I can't quite recall the drink I had-it had chocolate in it, but they also do a peanut butter cocktail! I was fascinated watching them mix other drinks. They burned wood and then captured the smoke in a glass just to give it a hint of smoke, they also beat egg whites half to death and other wired and whacky things. My drink was superb. The place is chilled and very dark, which at my age is a positive advantage. The bar staff were super Friendly. A big city establishment that Philly should keep hold of. Excellent.

I very rarely write reviews...this was a terrible place to visit.  When I arrived at the front desk, 3 rooms that just checked in were getting refunds and leaving.  It was not dirty, it was actually FILTHY!  I don't think I need to say anything else.
I enjoyed getting take-out from the first day I walked in.  It's fresh, healthy and good!!

The POPE is a favorite for brunch, booze, and hangout-ery.

It has a sort of dingy, rustic, dark vibe inside, which is in my top 5 preferred atmospheres for bars and gastropubs. The bartenders are very nice, and the brunch food is great, with my favorite being the vegan French toast. 

There are plenty of vegan and vegetarian options, and a killer beer list.
