It's good for groups if you're going to eat, because while the entry and bar area does sometimes get very crowded (usually by people who bring in 15 of their closest friends ... I've been in one of these groups, so no hate), the table seating and the other side of the bar are generally very accessible. 

The POPE is hands down one of my favorite places on Passyunk, great for a single beer, a night out, or a group celebration.

We were so grateful to work with Alexander. When we moved to Phili the movers scratched and banged up a few of our wooden items. Alexander restored them beautifully and quickly with competitive prices. He also did pickup and drop off with no extra charge.  It's not easy to trust someone with loved furniture, but I would highly recommend O'harro furniture.

If I could give zero stars, I would. I ordered a home cleaning for my mother as a Mother's Day gift for Saturday, May 11. She received a text message from Antoine the Thursday prior asking for her address, details of the cleaning, etc and she was confirmed for Saturday at 2pm. She then received another text Saturday morning confirming her appointment for 2pm. 

It is now 5:30pm and the cleaners never showed. We were unable to reach them via text and phone calls were sent to voicemail. This is the most unprofessional experience I have ever encountered, and will never use them again and would not recommend them to anyone.

Had a late lunch here on a Saturday - we wanted to have a couple drinks and not fight for a seat so Molly's was a good choice !

The husband had what he said was a "solid" Bloody Mary. His entree was the beef stew which he had more of a soup quality then stew but was still flavorful.

Our son had the classic cheese steak served with fries.

I had fish and chips - the portion was surprising large and had to take half home. It was really good - actually one of the best I've had in a long time. The fries were good but not great.

Service was very good - our waitress was attentive even though it was very busy. 

Glad we came here!

Directions for a perfect day. 

Park your car on Packer Ave for free. Enter the Linc. Watch the Eagles WIN!! Exit the Linc, run to your car in attempt to beat traffic on 76 while giving high fives to anyone and everyone in green.  Stop into Celebre's and pick up a slice or 4 to go!

I love how this place hasn't changed through the years. Everyone is super friendly, and while I've only had their pizza, I love it every time. 

Thank you Celebre's!  You always make sitting in traffic much more tolerable.

The outdoor garden is serene. I enjoyed brunch here on several occasions. The ambiance is casual yet elegant with an array of diverse lighting fixtures. The restaurant is never overcrowded and reservations are honored in a timely fashion.

The food is always fresh, hot and delicious. Let's just say the assorted muffin/ pastry basket is delectable. I found myself wanting more but knew I had to wait for the main course. The omelet I had was moist, flavorful and succulent. I have never had an omelet that seemed to melt in my mouth :). My taste buds  were tantalized and teased to the point of sheer food ecstasy.

The wait staff was friendly, engaging and inviting. From the moment we stepped through the threshold until the time we left, they made me feel like a valued customer. This place is somewhere to go to enjoy a good meal, great atmosphere and hopefully equally as great company (that you bring with you).

Came for a family member's birthday dinner.  I'll start with the good.  I personally really liked the ambience.  Our waiter who's name I didn't catch because I arrived late, was well informed, and accommodating.  We ordered a number of apps for the table, and the octopus and beef tartare were my favorites.  Now the reason for the three stars.  I ordered a wagyu New York strip.  It was a lovely steak, but I firmly believe it was not wagyu.  For 70 some dollars a restaurant should not be trying to pass off lesser meat as wagyu.  I have eaten enough wagyu meat to know this was not that.  The steak was somewhat lean apart from the strip.  I didn't end up saying anything because I didn't want to ruin everyone's good time.  Just buyer be ware.

Been here countless times and you know what? It's time for a review. AWESOME craft beer menu, great brunch and everyday menu, not too loud, awesome music (thank you Dave), and great location with great parking. I heard that Steve Malkmus played here and if yr lucky, you'll catch a live music night of yr own. Try the Manhattan; it's like a homemade Fireball with a cherry. Good stuff all around. Come here, sit down, enjoy the sights and sounds and neighborhood, and remember what a good time it was. 
"The Abbaye. Philly's Best Kept Secret."

Went here during restaurant week and was very impressed. Food was delicious. Service was attentive but not at all pushy. Portions were large and very tasty. Save room for the budino-it lives up to the hype!

Such a hidden gem! This place is delicious, inexpensive, and best of all - open all night :) Best kati roll I've tried is the mixed veggie. Service used to be a bit slower but they've picked up the pace considerably. Now you can get your food in less than 5 minutes.

I recently went through a very traumatizing phone experience (with my iphone). Right after it occurred, I was upset, very shaken up, and honestly, didn't know what to do. With my roommate out of town, and my parents in another state, I honestly had no idea what to do. At first, it seemed like everyone didn't care at all (I get it, we're in a city, but still), including the employees where the incident occurred.

Not sure of what to do, I knew that I had to get a new phone. I had no way of contacting anyone and I was heading to another state the next day and was going to be flying out for a trip 2 days later. So I figured I'd walk into the apple store and check out my options. 

Seriously, I don't know what I would have done without the employees in the store. They were SO helpful. They asked me if I had the find my iphone app installed and we all watched my phone as it was taken into some sketchy areas of south philly. We were able to contact the police again and the employees let me wait in the store as we continued to watch where my phone was taken and as we waited for the police. They were also really helpful with keeping me sane and cracking jokes, etc to keep things lighthearted.

They were also great with letting me know what my options were and weren't just trying to sell me a phone. They were legit being helpful and wanted me to make the best decision for my needs. 

Everyone was really nice with letting me use the store phones as many times as I needed in order to get in touch with my family, as well. They even let me stay in there til way after the store closed. In the end, I didn't even end up getting the phone because I couldn't activate it (and then realized I had an old droid I could use), but they were still so nice and made sure to let me know that I could get in touch with them if I needed anything at all.

Seriously, I will be sure to buy any future apple products from this store after this experience. Thanks for restoring my faith in humanity, apple. You guys were SO helpful that night, and I really appreciate it!

Delivery should be this complicated. I ordered quesadillas for 1pm delivery to my corporate office and get a call from the grub hub person that they are outside my office at 1140am. More than an hour early and also not delivering them to the 28th floor as the order details included this. The person claimed the doors were locked to a corporate building that houses over 50 companies  on 37 floors. They apparently made a fresh order of quesadillas for the 1pm delivery but again the person did not come on the elevators. Instead I watched as he sat outside on his bike with the food. You didn't even bother to enter the building. If you are delivering and getting a tip (which you did with this order) then I feel it is only right to come up to the suite or the building that placed the order. You can leave your bike with the security desk as many other delivery service have done. Poor service and will not be ordering or eating from here again.

Great staffs, large selection of beer. We ordered some sandwiches and dine-in. You can get beer from their draft selection or bottled beers (which they will open for you once you bought them). Nice spot for quick lunch and on-the go.

Best Bah Mi I have tasted.  The combination of fresh warm South Philly bread and buttery goodness.  I ordered the BBQ beef Bah Mi.  Was everything and more.  The right amount of meat and veggies.   Mmmm ill definitely be going back for more.

Also the outside is deceiving as it is in a Chinese warehouse that has a grocery store and other Chinese stores,  but don't be deceived,  this place is a gem amongst the competitive Vietnam joints around Washington Ave.

This place is pretty rad.  Free standing in the middle of the square at Penn Center with outdoor seating, it's a pretty nice place to spend an afternoon (with free wifi).  

The regular coffee here is also pretty tasty, although kind of expensive.  You pay mostly for the atmosphere, and it's kind of worth it.  I wouldn't go everyday, but a morning or two are certainly in order.

Communication is ATROCIOUS here. Spoke with the PM front desk person who quoted $385 for spay and front declaw. Upon arrival, I was told that her quote was wrong.....by $200 dollars! They tried upcharging me a total of $600 for spay and front declaw. I then get the excuse of "well, they must have quoted you thinking you had a voucher". I asked what a voucher was....and have yet to be told....

Last year, we had a front declaw and neuter done for $350 total. No voucher. No nothing. Magically now it's $250 more that's sprung on you as you enter for the surgery that day.

Furthermore, they provide horrible customer service. Front desk attendant in the AM is a slob that doesn't have the decency to pull his pants up to his waist. The male doctor lacks any personality or ability to have a patient/doctor relationship.

The female doctor is very nice. That's about the only thing I can say about this place. We'll never return again and would never recommend this place to anyone.

Still solid as ever. Marcus knows how to serve his guests the best food and drink in the area. Hats off to the fantastic rotating tap selection and the rock solid tri-tip sirloin steak. Fall tap beers looking great!

Always wanted to check out this place, and when we saw that Heritage participates in the Restaurant week in Northern Liberties and Fishtown, it was no doubts. We went there and want to go back again! Really You won't regret! Food was amazing, the atmosphere was perfect. Also It was a good deal: dinner for $35 per person plus happy hour $5 wine (5-7pm). I can write a lot about food since everything was balanced out well, but the homemade bread made my experience here unforgettable. There are not that many places in Philly where they make a good, properly baked through bread. Heritage is one of those places! Chives butter was a perfect addition for the delicious homemade bread. While we were enjoying the food the band started to play jazz. What else you need for the perfect dinner with lovely person next to you? Now we are planning to come with our friends and try other items on the menu, I am sure they are all great!

Our group of six ducked into The Corner Foodery for some pre-gaming action before the Flyers game on Saturday. The set up is half bottle shop, half sit-down eatery. You can pick out bottles to drink there and just pay as you go and enjoy the seating. There are still and bubbly water taps and several TVs scattered around. I was surprised at the ample amount of seating and even more surprised at the lack of people. I think this place is still under the radar, but I can't imagine that it will be for very long. It's not often you can go to a restaurant and drink a beer at the bottle price.   

The menu is a mix of sandwiches, salads and snacks that sounds delicious. I'm eager to get back for lunch or dinner sometime soon.

I am writing this review as I'm enjoying some leftovers from the absolutely show-stopping tasting menu dinner I had at Bufad on my birthday a few nights ago. As I put another bite of the bolognese in my mouth, I think what I often do whenever I eat their food: 

"How do they do it?"

By "it", I mean consistently knock my socks off. Bufad is literally a block away from me so I go there quite often. It's my go-to for delicious food. But it goes beyond proximity. This place is perfect to me because they always keep me guessing with their offerings, switching up the menu according to what's in season. They always manage to surprise me with their ingredients, coming up with inventive combinations. They serve solid classic pizzas for fussy traditionalists but they've also got plenty for the more adventurous foodies. I can bring a crowd in there and I can be sure everyone will be happy and impressed. The desserts are unbelievable.  And the service is always impeccable. I have never once been disappointed with any server there, and I've been there a whole lot!

Oh, and they're BYOB, so going out to dinner here means meals are always affordable. I've been to Vetri's pizzeria and spent much more on pizza that frankly was just not as delicious as Bufad's. It is truly one of Philly's best kept secrets.

Awesome. Just plain fabulous. Reservations def required of this tiny locale. A friend brought us here for dinner and while the steak was cooked delectably and massive, the envy were the pork chops w/ fava beans. I can't even begin to describe how tasty they were! Definitely worth checking out. BYOB and cash only.  Beware of bad acoustics.

Having always been more of a Whopper, Jr / double cheeseburger kinda gal, I ventured into Village Whiskey to see what the fuss was about. I had had a good burger here and there (25 Degrees in LA, PYT recently), but it wasn't really a food I would actively pursue, like I would good sushi or steak. Garces had only failed me once so far (see my Chifa review), so I was fairly optimistic.

We went all out with the Whiskey King - 8oz burger, maple bourbon glazed cipollini onions, rogue bleu cheese, applewood smoked bacon, lettuce, tomato and a big fat piece of seared foie gras to top it off. I was a little wary of all the components - I'm usually good with lettuce, tomato, and onion, if that. But this was REALLY good. It was a mess to eat, but not a mess in my mouth as I had expected. The burger was juicy and cooked to a perfect medium rare, the intensity of the bacon and bleu cheese was tempered by the sweet onions, and the foie added just the right amount of richness. The lettuce and tomato was refreshing, although a bit lost towards the end.

The duck fat fries I wasn't too impressed with. I may just be partial to the thinner cut fries, or spoiled from the ones at PYT I'd just had. I was also a little disappointed that they didn't come with some sort of yummy sauce creation, as I'm not a big fan of ketchup.

We also ordered oysters - super fresh and tasty.

The meal is sort of an investment, so be prepared to shell out a bit. But the ROI is great. I'd come back again for sure.

I love trying new breakfast creations and this is the place to make it happen! I wish I could come back sooner to try more entrees, but I was only in town for 2 nights.

I decided to order the pecan pie French toast with a creme brûlée latte. My fam ordered the chicken and waffles Benedict and a bacon arugula Benedict. 

They did not disappoint on the pecan pie French toast. It was really heavy that I brought it home and had it the next day! It was def more of a dessert. The bacon arugula Benedict was also good, we choose mac and cheese on the side. I liked the choice of pasta they used as well as the cheesy-ness of it!

I wasn't the biggest fan of the chicken and waffles Benedict or the creme brûlée latte. The waffles and the chicken's breading could have been more crispy. It wasn't soggy, but not crunchy either. The latte didn't seem strong enough for me. Mayb there was too much ice that it diluted the coffee. Shrugs.

I'd def come back to try more things here!

I go here all the time for the following reasons

1) No sales, no special deal b.s. I don't feel like I'm being tricked by prices
2) Great service. They get packed and have an impressive register system. Line can be wrapped around every aisle and I'll only wait for 12 minutes
3) Good Food/Products.

The only cons I have is I'm not always in the mood to hear their 80's music and their super upbeat atmosphere is welcome on some days but not for others. Overall it beats the prison like feeling you get from big supermarkets. It's just annoying to listen to hippie music or something weird when you're tired from work or whatever. There are definitely days I said "screw it, I'm going to a regular store because I'm not in the mood" but that's not too often.

Also parking lot is WAY too small. They need to do something about that situation, especially with cars lining up around the corner. This is the only real problem they have going on in my opinion.  I'm sure it's not easily solved otherwise it would be. That city life. 

Good job guys keep it up. I rarely shop anywhere else.
Had their Colorful Fruit Tea with 30% sugar and it was absolutely delicious. The finely diced mixture of real fruits and berries was a refreshing touch especially on a hot day.
GORGEOUS space.   Tesla Tease's Opium Den show is phenomenal, and the staff there is so wonderful.  Amazing cocktail list and the ambiance is unmatchable.  Can't wait to bring everyone I know.

I live half a block from here and for some reason, never stopped in. A day off from work, extreme hunger due to lack of food in my apartment, and convenience I decided to stop in for a sandwich.

I ordered the turkey panini to go (smoked turkey, provolone cheese, roasted peppers, spinach, mustard and mayo) and waited for it. I got home, and though it was good size for the price- however, the center of the panini was cold which made it not as good as it could have been.

Considering the over abundance of sandwich places within a 5 block radius , not sure if i'll stop by to give them another shot. But who knows, a rainy day and hunger might change my mind.

Wonderful Valentines Day Dinner--Chefs Tasting Menu.  So rich, so good, so decadent.  What an experience!  The staff and service were terrific!  I can't say enough about them and the overall service experience.  The food...the food!!!

Came here on a late Friday night. We ordered a ton of food since we were starving and there was a bunch of different dishes we wanted to try! 
Some of the items we got the go was: half a roasted duck, beef chow fun, pork and mustard green soup, scallion pancakes, Hong Kong wonton soup, roast pork fried rice and sweet and sour chicken. 
After 15 mins we got our food and drove home to DE. Despite driving alittle over an hour our food was still pretty hot which is nice!

The good: 
Beef chow fun - lots of good stir fry noodles and few slices of beef. Overall delicious dish with lots of flavor
Duck - also delicious with a lot of meat but super fatty
Pork + mustard soup - lots of pork and good peppery flavor to the broth
Wonton soup - didn't taste the broth but shrimp wontons in the soup were delicious

The okay:
Roast pork fried rice was okay needed more seasoning
Scallion pancakes : needed to be fried more but decent once pan fried again
Sweet and sour chicken: just okay needed more flavor in the sauce 

The ugly:
So not sure if it's from there our international travels... (which we came back into Philly and got this food to go for quick dinner) or one of the items we tried here my husband and I both got some sort of stomach bug which has lasted for almost a week. Could be from the oily residue from all the food we had but it was super delicious (which is why it's a 3 star since we both got sick a day or two after eating this food). I haven't seen any reviews saying anything about getting sick but just a warning ! Maybe might try again but with less to see if it was the food/fluke.

Pretty unique concept of a meal - forewarning you are just eating a fresh pita bread and some fresh hummus (+/- some toppings if you pay for it).

Atmosphere: I was in the mood for a quick meal because I was at a local hotel and got into town somewhat late, so hopped in. The place is relatively small, quick-casual place to grab a bite. Server was friendly and helpful.

Food: The hummus was NOT the best I've ever had. It was good overall but I've had better. I added the eggplant topping which made it much like baba-ganoush but I'll tell you that it was not salty enough. The hummus itself had a ton of olive oil on it, which imparts a good flavor but it felt like too much. The pita itself was my favorite part; warm, fresh and hit the spot. Grabbed a local beer, can't remember the name but it was an IPA; good choice but served in a plastic cup, felt small and tacky.

Long story short, its a good place to grab some good hummus and pita, but I don't think it felt like I ate a meal. A good place to grab a snack and share with someone.

If you saw a clown in full clown gear, but also wearing football pads and holding a shotgun, you would ask yourself "hey, what's up with that clown?  Is he a clown, or a football player, or a hunter, or all three, or what?"

That's Llama Tooth, all over the place. It's an outdoor area that styles itself as a beer garden, but there are no beers on draft, and the vibe is vaguely Caribbean with fish sculptures and lite reggae music playing. I'm not quite sure the ambiance and the concept match up. To wit, beer gardens should not be playing the 1992 smash hit "A Whole New World" from the The Little Mermaid soundtrack.  That's beer garden rule one.  

Ultimately, the food was what did Llama Tooth in, for me.  It took a very, very long time to come out, and when it did it really left a lot to be desired.  I ordered the "Mountain of Pulled Pork."  Now, I'm a pig, and when I order a mountain of pulled pork I expect a mountain of fucking pulled pork.  What I got was a dry handful of pulled pork on top of even drier beans.  If you are going to be bold and say you're serving a mountain of pork, deliver.  Alas, no dice.  

Another member of my party ordered the Ahi Tuna salad and got through about a quarter of it.  Dryness was the culprit, again. My wife ordered the beet salad and said it was ok, and a bit expensive for what came out.  The MacNcheese entrees were apparently pretty good though.  

I know I'm coming off rough, and taste is certainly subjective.  But the consensus of my group of eight (including a professional chef) was that this place needs to make some tweaks to the formula. The place was only 1/4 full on a 76 degree Saturday evening in May, and that is not a good sign of the restaurant's overall health. I don't think I'll be going back.

Saturday night dinner, very crowded, poor layout, furniture is too large for the space.  Service friendly but slow, food was very mediocre.  Spring rolls were greasy and tasteless, soft shell crab was mushy, duck was good, chicken ginger stir fry was kind of boring and reminded me of ok Chinese food.  The owner is a lovely man, I hope they can work out the kinks.  I was under the impression that Thai food was fresh with lost of flavor.  Step it up a notch guys.

For Sushi Buffet, I had a great time. The price is reasonable. You shouldn't be expecting the best sushi ever with this price, but it's definitely good for sushi lovers who wants to do all you can eat.
Each order has 6-8 pieces, which can be crazy if you just want to tastes more, but I stayed there for two hours just to try things and enjoy myself, which by the time I finished, I had tried well enough things.
The waitress I met once wasn't so energetic, but she's not rude, so I was fine with her.

The best BBQ I have had in Philadelphia!!
The meat is very fresh! The taste is very delicious.
High quality Skewer BBQ!

My friends and I came here for dinner this past Friday since I heard a lot of hype about the new place in town. We set reservations via OpenTable but they weren't necessary - there weren't a lot of folks when we got in around 7:30. Like many of the other reviews have stated, the decor here is impressive compared to other places in Chinatown but also slightly gaudy and over the top in some areas (eg: restrooms).

The service was meh. The servers seemed to need a lot more training or a thorough review of the menu items. The English here wasn't the best either, I began ordering English but had to switch to Cantonese after a while.

It seemed that our server was incredibly hype on us ordering drinks to start. My husband ordered a Sapporo (which was listed as $8 on the drink menu but we were later charged $15 on the tab) and the table ordered a bottle of Charm soju to which the server asked us "Red or White?" in return (If y'all have indulged in soju in the past, you know that ish is clear). That was the first red flag. A few minutes later we were informed that the restaurant didn't carry any soju and that the listing on the menu was "old" and then he proceeded to ask us if we wanted red or white wine again. Bruh, I didn't come here for wine but ok. Red flag number 2 - my friend asked for a Dogfish Head beer because he saw it on the (outdated) drink menu and this request set the servers into a tizzy. The servers bounced the question from one to another and apparently noone at Canton 11 knew what Dogfish Head was so my friend ended up settling for another Jack & Ginger. 

On to the food - we ordered a variety of dim sum (shu mai, har gow, soup dumplings to name a few), skewers and a few dishes (chinese sausage w. chinese broccoli, seafood panfried noodles & general tso's chicken) to share. They also ran out of a few of the things we ordered such as the quail egg skewers and the eggplant w. shrimp. The dishes took a pretty long time to come out, especially the dim sum. Overall, the food wasn't impressive by any means. The skin on the shrimp har gow dumplings broke apart as soon as you tried to pick it up, the skin on the soup dumplings were either already leaking or busted as soon as you picked it up.  The prices on all the items were pretty steep too given the fact none of it was much to write home about. 

I can't see myself coming back here for a meal. The decor and atmosphere doesn't really eclipse the fact that the service is ok at best, food is subpar and either they don't update their  menus or they unapologetically overcharge you for drinks. There's plenty of other choices just down the street and around the corner for authentic Chinese food.

i come here a lot so i may be biased - but this is my favorite spot in center city for so many reasons. there's a dish on the menu for every type of customer, all of which are delicious. am personally a sucker for the lemon sole, short rib and the duck! the crispy shallots in the sole add great flavor and texture. short rib is perfect cooked with delicious polenta and veggies. the duck is accompanied by an awesome warm lentil salad... if you're feeling really gluttonous you can sub out your side for mashed potatoes, they're the best i've ever had!! 

warning: it can be a bit loud depending on the accompanying crowd, but the food will not disappoint. the staff is very attentive and very friendly. sometimes the meal can be longer if they're extra busy... but as long as you're with enjoyable company and have tasty wine, you should be just fine!

pro tip: they don't take reservations on the weekends. head there a little earlier than you want to eat. you can out your name in and then head across the stress to food and friends for a beer while you wait for your table. sometimes we bring cards and play with our friends if we know the wait will be longer! :) (food and friends also conveniently has an ATM machine, so you can grab cash for your meal while you're there!)
I've returned to this place many time. It's always great to be back. Great food but slightly pricy.

The chicken wings were fried crispy and seasoed
 The lady taking the order was a bit abrupt and they didn't say thank uou or come again. I  may not go back for these reasons

Went here with a pretty large group and had a great time. Service was awesome and everything that came out of the kitchen was great. We had quite a variety of their cocktails and nothing disappointed in that area.

All of the food was well above average, but the root veggie pot pie was probably the favorite. Super savory béchamel and great crust.


The live music is also a huge plus. Great atmosphere for a group dinner or drinks. I'll definitely be back if i'm in the area.

My boyfriend and I absolutely adored this place! Definitely recommend the guacamole and plaintain chips as well as the crab empanadas!!! We are already anticipating our next visit!!! Please do yourself a favor and check this place out.

Overall great place to live. It does seem like the building is still unfinished in some ways. Construction materials are occasionally left around, things that are so rudimentary in building construction are still not done, it takes a significant amount of time to get mission critical things addressed (elevators), and no matter how many times they say it will be fixed, the package system is a nightmare. I feel bad for the front desk attendants who have to deal with that thing. The nice amenities are the gym, roof with BBQs, TV, and patio furniture, and a fire pit outside. Some issues though. The gym consistently has broken equipment that is "fixed" and then breaks again (because most of it isn't commercial grade), or the temperature is sometimes in the 50s. The fire pit outside has not worked once since I moved in a year ago, but seemingly always has somebody coming out to fix it. The BBQs both on the roof and outside by the fire pit are ALWAYS broken in some capacity. The ignition switch is either missing, the gas is either turned off from the front desk (with no notice), or the grease trap is 100% full and nobody takes notice to empty it. These are all very easy things to fix if management would step up their game and actually provide the level of service and attention that my rent payment deserves. It isn't cheap by any means to live here, and for the price it would be nice if they were more attentive to these things. Oh yeah, and the gym could use a wall pad for ball throws and a fresh coat of paint. It's pretty gross.

The tofu banh mi is better than the chicken banh mi.
I wasn't a huge fan of the pickled mango.

It is worth noting that this is just as much a Vietnamese grocery story as it is a banh mi place. There's only one table there and it isn't exactly a "dine-in" sort of establishment.

This was my first time trying Pakistani food! Chaat is very interesting - every bite gives you a different flavor due to the different layers incorporated into the dish. I tried the samosa chaat (the samosa was delicious!) and the dish was great! Know that this dish isn't hot (temperature-wise), the samosa or whatever add on you get is warm but the base is chickpeas and potatoes and they are cooked but not hot. Very interesting dish and I'm so glad I tried it! I also got a mango lassi - very yummy! I tasted the fog lassi which I found delicious (it's yogurt based with chunks of fig!) 

The location is very cozy and the owner is extremely friendly and welcoming. We had no idea what we were ordering and he explained everything to us! Thank you so much for the hospitality and the introduction to a new and delicious food!

The Famous Fourth Street Delicatessen was founded in 1923 and it is no wonder why it is still open.  The food is fabulous.  The deli has four generations in it's history and they have  owned delicatessens in Brooklyn, NY. Since 1989 they have carried the tradition to Philadelphia and South Jersey. The portions are amazing and they start you off with a compliment salad.  Yes, they give you a free salad.  We got the Ruben with the Cobb salad with a slice of German chocolate cake.  We received the check along with two free cookies. 
When you go here...make sure you are hungry or be prepared to split.  The Ruben sandwich cost $18.50, but it is really a sandwich for two.  The sandwich has a pound of meat in it.

I was told by the employee (manager?) that everything served in the restaurant is organic, including the veg in the veggie burgers. 
Black bean burger= 5 stars
Beef burger= 4 stars
Onion rings= 5 stars
French fries= 2 stars, dry and crunchy throughout each fry, reminds me of a thicker version of the potato sticks snack in a can

If it were a couple years ago....XIX would have gotten a solid 4 stars from me....but it's not quite the same anymore.  I've only been for happy hour and my first time was fantastic--so you know I was hyping it up to my girlfriends.  

The ambiance is still very elegant, classy, and sophisticated.

However the drinks were just okay.  I also think they should have some discounted bar snacks because paying around $17 for an appetizer at happy hour is a little hard to swallow.  Good thing I stuffed my face at Qdoba beforehand--score!!

Definitely come to XIX to soak up some sophistication and get a nice view of the city, but you've been warned that you could probably make a better cocktail at home.  

I will say those complimentary wasabi peas are a nice touch-and highly addictive!

This food was so good it almost made me cry.  I saw the chef taking a break and thanked him when we left.  

The Shrimp appetizer.  Niagara Falls tears.  My plate of grits made with chicken stock were NOT my grandmom's grits.  Because if they were, they would be inedible.  It was her doing to make me not a big fan of grits.  This chef changed that for me.

The Beef made to fall apart sobbing.  I think we were all weepy as we were sharing our meals with each other.

The place was jammed packed that Friday Night (October 1st 2010)  in the front when we arrived (there was some big wing dig for some mucky muck political somebody from Philedelphia that I had no clue about coming in from Los Angeles) and our Martini Drinks (that we took pictures of they were so good and pretty) were a touch slow.  I totally get that, and it was worth the wait.

We were seated fast and when we informed the wait crew that two at our table had a show in a few hours, (we were all in town for DuoFest at The Shubin Theater)  they brought our food out speedy to accommodate us.

Super friendly service.  The food was make me cry Foodie Fan Good.

The kicker of it all was Saturday (October 2nd 2010) at a completely different establishment after my Show was where I randomly ran into the bartender.

Him: Shaun Landry?
Me: (sort of taken aback as I had no idea who he was and thought maybe he just saw our show) Yes? Hi.
Him:  My name is (the bartender of Chris Jazz Cafe) I just heard from a friend you came to Chris'  I'm the bartender.
Me:  Holy shit!  Your food made me cry!!  So did your drinks!

Turns out he is an actor also.  We stood outside and talked about Food and Theatre He gave me his card.

So folks, Go to Chris' Jazz Cafe for food and drinks...then catch the bartender doing Three Penny Opera at the Arden Theatre in Philadelphia!

Finally.  A place where I can write a great review and plug a theater show at *the same time*  :)

Definitely my go-to for a good spray tan in philly. The workers are always very professional and helpful. I typically do an express tan because I don't like sleeping with the solution on and it comes out great every time. Price is pretty good as well, under $40. They have a special when you first visit where you get a free tan which is also a great incentive to try this place out. They also have monthly plans!

When I moved to philly I had to find a great nail salon fast! I'm so so happy to have found this salon! Coco blue spruce is amazing! Staff is friendly and usually able to take walk ins quickly. I do recommend making an appointment for weekends. Even Fridays during the day can be crazy! I love that they offer complimentary drinks and give a mini massage for manis. I've been going here for a year now and they always do a great job on my nails! :)

Wow, I got a yelp to badly review a business. Aside from truly trying to resist doing so, I couldn't shake the experience I had here yesterday so here I am. 

I went in to get new jewelry for my tongue piercing which I recently had re-pierced after having it done for 4 years in the past. I am no novice when it comes to this piercing, its sizing, and how to change it. 

Instead of simply being shown to the correct ring, I was slammed with negative comment after comment after I politely said I did not need their help in changing the jewelry. Accurate facts about the dangers of tongue rings, yet delivered in ways that made me want to get a yelp. 
 
"You know that if you get an infection it will go STRAIGHT TO YOUR HEART"
"If it gets infected it will like, be an infection of like, your TONGUE, which you use for like EVERYTHING" 
"EW Don't PUT YOUR FINGERS IN YOUR MOUTH you'll get saliva on the counter!" (After trying to show them the ring)

I can't even properly articulate that I could not say anything right to these people. Instead of being helpful, they intimidated me and if I was new to this piercing probably would have went home and taken it right out. 

I will not be returning, and stress to WARRIOR that being knowledgeable about something does not grant permission to act pretentious and with condescension.
Place is very cool. Beers are terrific. loved the 100 IBU's, Dankstoyevsky, and the Grod is great too (coming from someone who doesn't typically enjoy Sours).

I gotta say... Nothing thrilled me at all on my recent trip to Penn 6. 

We went for restaurant week, so could that be why? But no... I have had some excellent service and meals during restaurant week, that cannot be it.

The beans in my soup weren't the tender, starchy goodness that I expected. The "cumin rubbed" flank steak that was served was bland. I had to put salt on it, I NEVER put salt on anything. And the dessert was just odd. I understood the concept, but the blueberry compote was too bitter, almost acidic. 

The only decent part about the evening was that we got there early enough for the end of happy hour. Drinks were pretty good. Other than that, don't waste your time!

Stopped in at 6 pm on a Saturday to find a mostly empty restaurant. Good quiet place to grab above-board sushi. The special rolls seem a bit overpriced, but the standard rolls like spicy tuna, etc. were reasonable. Sake nirigi was great. Tempura was also good. Awesome paintings in the restroom.

I read the reviews and decided to give this place a shot.  I needed a state inspection and an oil change.  I dropped my car off around 10am without an appointment and had my car back by 3pm.  They seemed to be pretty good about fitting you in if you bring your car in early enough. 

When they called me, they let me know that there were a few other things that I should get done now, but could wait a little longer if I wanted.  No hard sell here which was nice.  These guys were great.  I specifically dealt with Roger.  He was courteous and easy to deal with.          

Also, see the coupons on their website.  It offers a pretty decent discount on the state inspection and couple bucks on the state inspection.

George at Creative Silvers has been able to repair multiple watches for me that I've collected over the years. He really hooked it up and the turn around time was amazing. I went to get a new battery and he even fixed the band of my watch, all with a smile. 

   I've never had any issues with receiving the correct parts for my jewelry. I've even taken some of my mothers items there to be cleaned and serviced for years. She was more than impressed with the turn around time and quality of the work. 

Creative Silver = the Jawn.
Try the spicy ramen. The pork is so tender and the broth so flavorful. Sleek little shop with great service. Favorite ramen spot in Chinatown

Went to this Pep Boys and was very impressed by not only the work done, but also the shop's customer service. Josh got me all set up with the new tires I needed, kept me very well informed about my tire options, and didn't make me feel pressured like a lot of shops can. I was in and out in an hour and the car seems great. Even gave me free air a couple of weeks ago before I got the tires changed! Professional job - I'd recommend

Evil Genius is becoming one of my favorite breweries in the city. They are constantly putting out new and creative beers, including their recent Eagles themed beer Fly like and Eagle, a cherry wooder ice flavored IPA. Their Chocolate Peanut butter porter they call Purple Monkey Dishwasher, is an award winning porter that people really enjoy.

The environment in Evil Genius is very cool and relaxed a lot of seating and different places to hang with your friends and enjoy a very tasty and unique beer. On warm days they open up their beer garden which gives you a nice place to hang outside and drink some beers with your friends.

They offer a pretty unique food menu, including classics like peanut butter and jelly and grilled cheese and tomato soup, they also offer some desserts like their dessert nachos.

So if you're looking for a good place to hang with you friends have some beers and try beers you won't get anywhere else Evil Genius is a great place for you.

Easily one of the best Chinese places I've ever ordered from. 
They take a little while to deliver but the wait is worth the food, but I recommend not ordering standard "chinese" food.  They have an incredibly extensive menu of unique items which makes this place so good, try the unique stuff like the wood ear in chili sauce, or the house special spicy lo mein
And as I said really the only con is the time it takes for delivery, about 40 minutes can be a bit of a pain but it really is worth it.

wow,the reason for all stars? the service was over the top great!!

mustache guy suggested and served finest south street fare.awesome time!!
Customer service sucks! They ignore you and not trying to help even they see u waiting! If there is another store i can buy my son's uniform i will not step on this store!

Came in 2013 and had the highly lauded village burger, which I thought very good, maybe not the best burger I had. I figured that I would revisit this place to see if it's still worth the hype. The interior is quite small and I counted around 40 seats, including the bar. Because of the small amount of seats, I recommend that you come during an off-peak time so you're not stuck on an extended wait time. Either way, this place isn't exactly group-friendly unless it's closed for a private party.

For me, 3 for $3 deviled eggs are hard to pass up and they were quite delicious! It's a fairly light appetizer. I ended up with the $26 Whiskey King burger, which is not only the fanciest burger I've ever eaten, but it's also, very easily, the most expensive one. Thanks, foie gras. This is NOT something you want to eat if you want to be dainty. If you do, you should, *gasp* cut the burger in half. Although it does not outwardly say it on the menu, there is lettuce and tomato on it. This has to be one of the top 3 burgers I've ever had. It's just extremely gluttonous. It may have been the duck liver that set it over the edge...or maybe it was the bacon. You can't go wrong with bacon. I thought that both the duck fat fries with cheddar cheese and the tater tots were good, but I wasn't wowed by either.

Something I enjoyed here were the cocktails. Part of the fun at sitting at the bar is watching the bartenders put together the drinks. You can tell that they really want to put out great drinks, which can be pretty expensive.

Bartenders are extremely rude, to everyone. Waited for over 20 minutes for them to even acknowledge us. I'm surprised this place even has 2 stars. I'd give a zero if I could. Seeing these people being rude to older couples that probably JUST CANT HEAR YOU is infuriating. Would never recommend this place to anyone.

We headed over to KFN because our friend Jake was DJing and we wanted to show our support with some serious rump shake-age. My favorite things about this place: $1 PBR special, dancing on stage, Twin Peaks playing on the TV (the owls are not what they seem!), and pearly pink marker graffiti in the lady's room that says "you look pretty" and points to the mirror. Aw, shucks, KFN. 

Also, I talked up a new pal and had the best dialogue evs:

Me: "I want to be friends with the girls that were dancing creatively with the curtain; I love people that play off the props!"
She: "You are! That was me!"

Totes serendip. 

P.S. I almost forgot! For some reason I can't remember, they had a free bagel bar set up!! Extra star in the bag.

Food is decent. Ordered a variety of dishes to sample: nachos, traditional tacos, Spanish meatballs, coconut rice. Wish there menu was a little more extensive. Not a bad place to stop, if you're looking for a decent vegan meal.
I love the She Body Butter and the mixed fruits body butte.  I love, love, love these products and I have never had a problem with an online order.  I live in Baltimore, MD.
This place had the best tacos I've had in a long while.  Highly recommend the Super Chick and the Shrimp Gone Wild!

This was one of the most regrettable dining experiences I've had all summer; I've been regretting every penny I spent here since I signed the check approximately 10 hours ago. What upsets me the most, though, is that people seem to really think this is one of the best things Chintatown has to offer. I am utterly baffled by any review giving this more than 3 stars.

First off, the atmosphere is totally pretentious. Yuckitori Boy attempts to pay homage to the chic sushi dens of lower east side Manhattan, yet embarrasses itself by delivering neither quality nor charm.

The edamame had clearly been defrosted moments before arriving at our table, and I'd be surprised if the agedashi tofu, which was soggy, spongy and borderline flavorless, wasn't microwaved. I'm pretty sure that the 'Chef's Choice' Yakitori platter ($15.00!) contained less than a single chicken breast's-worth of meat, and the sushi, well, reminded me that it was the end of the week. I've had better Shumai from this hip little place I know about called Trader Joe's. I think theirs are frozen too.

I'm praying that on my way to work I'll find a twenty on the ground so I can pretend that my visit to Yuckitori Boy never happened and can stop feeling like I made the worst decision of my life last night.

What makes Percy Street Barbecue unique and appealing to me is their extensive canned beer list.  It is neat. Nowhere else in the city (that I know of) can you chose from a list of over 70 craft beers brewed from across the country...all in cans! 

Then you have the food.  Very good Texas style barbecue. I go for the brisket platter served with sides of burnt end (!) beans, coleslaw, onion, pickles and some good ol' white bread. 

Speaking of Pickles.  If you like your alcohol mixed with pickle juice or vice versa, I suggest you treat yourself to a Hillbilly Gatorade.  A Miller Hi-Life (poured from a can of course!) mixed with Pickle Juice and their Old Faithful BBQ Sauce.  It shouldn't be good, but it is.  Actually, redact that last statement.  It SHOULD be good, and it is.

For dessert, the Pecan Pie will do. Buttery, sugary, gooey, sweet, crunchy, caramelized pecans on a nice pie crust.

The atmosphere here is great too.  Coming in off of South Street, it's like you stepped into another world.  A world somewhere near Texas. Where the jukebox is playing good music and the air is filled with the aroma of smoked barbecue meats.  

Interesting fact, one of the employees actually handwrites all of the chalkboard menus (at the bar and all over the restaurant).  That shit kray! I'd trust them to be my surgeon.

I'll be back very soon to continue my work on dominating the Canned Beer List.

Okay so I don't know what happened in the last two years since I wrote that review but CHick-Fil-A has really stepped it up!  The employees are very nice, courteous, and service is fast and organized.
Great quality and so easy to pick healthy dishes. Loved my team lunches here.
Was here for the 1st time this past Saturday with some fam/friends for my birthday. The service was great & the food was absolutely amazing. I will be back.... A LOT.

Great new fusion restaurant. Hidden but super wonderful with a large seating area and delicious menu. Also great coffee. 
Had the chicken with waffles and it was a fun twist.

Really enjoyed my experience here today. I'm a non-smoker and came with no prior knowledge of vaping and the sales person was so willing to walk me through all the different types of pens and box mods available. He even offered to look in recipebooks to help me come up with combinations of flavors I might enjoy. Went above and beyond. Will be back and definitely taking advantage of the rewards program.

The decor and service were lovely but I did have to brush crumbs off our table when we were seated. However the evening was terribly marred by the drunken behavior of another guest. A loud woman was cursing at the next table. I politely asked her to be a bit more quiet and not use profanity that I could clearly hear. She seemed to be agreeable, but when I sat down she got louder and then flipped the bird at me. I asked to speak to the manager. He agreed to move our table but he did nothing to quiet this woman. Nor did he stand up for a guest who had been insulted. I also think he should have comped us our drinks or dessert. The others at my table really enjoyed their meal. I thought my scallops were overpriced and just ok. I won't be back.

Decent place for a drink and some billiards/Skeeball! I would say the best thing about this place is its great beer selection! Prices are okay. However, almost always super crowded and sometimes super hard to get drinks. People can also become rowdy as well, especially later on during the day. Essentially, kind of feels like a place where young adults go on a weekend night to get college drunk.

Pros: Great beer selection, SKEEBALL!
Cons: Loud, rowdy, crowded

I ordered the Tomato Pie, which is supposed to come with "chunky california tomato with fresh basil & extra virgin olive oil". I requested two additional toppings, bell peppers and mushrooms. The pizza had a canned tomato sauce that could by no means be described as chunky, no olive oil, and no basil. The additional toppings were the only things that came as promised. The only redeeming value of this place is that they deliver at 2 in the morning.

Came back today for dinner with my bf and our daughter. Once again we are totally pleased with our experience. We tried the pork sung onigiri this time along with the cooked maki platter and a mango milkshake. We also had the spicy maki combo, crabmeat onigiri and matcha milkshake again since we loved it so much the first time. 

Service was lovely and our server was kind and helpful.
We were even brought a complementary creme Brule which was so kind of the manager!

 I also can't express how much I love that they have a changing table in the bathroom!! I am so sick of going to restaurants and having to change my daughter on the dirty floors or on the sink tops. My bf and I always think of Bubblefish now whenever we want to go out to eat with our baby! We can't wait to bring my bfs family here once they come to visit in a few weeks.

I'm pissed, I called Old English pizza on 5th Street to order  large pizza with wings and pay over the phone like I have always done prior to the new owners , now they dont accept Master cards, as of today 3/21/2019 i will not patronize any Asians on 5th Street who only wants CASH..One thing for sure they don't patronize us......Wake up black people they come to our neighborhoods for our money only....they do nothing to support any cause of ours......

I can't believe this place gets on average less than 4 stars! I've dined here at least 5 times and get take out on the regular from here. I'm a whitewashed Asian so maybe my opinion counts less, but I love their dan dan noodles and dumpling sampler. The hot pots and pepper chicken are flavorful and the staff is always friendly and attentive. My only criticism is that sometimes spice levels requested don't always align with spice levels received. When dining with spice adverse friends some of them have been blown out of the water with the spiciness of the dishes they've ordered, but then when asked to be punished with heat I've gotten rather mild dishes... Seems like it's a bit of a toss up. Other than that no complaints!

I love this place!  The pizza is delicious, especially on a cold day when you want to be warm and toasty.  This is my favorite place to go when I have an out-of-town guest, because there are so many options.  We usually order wine and the portions are so large that we share different meals.

I have bought a fair bit of cheese from this shop, but strangely enough, I had not ordered any food from their lunch counter. The Inquirer's high ranking of their Italian hoagie was a little unexpected when I first read the article some months ago.  So I was eagerly anticipating the opportunity to try it, but also hesitating out of a little skepticism.

I have to say that the Italian hoagie is excellent. All of the flavors are completely balanced and well-rounded. Each one of the ingredients shines through on its own, but also works in harmony with the others. One complaint that I often have with Italian hoagies is that the onion they put in can dominate the flavor palate. But there was nothing like that here. It had the right amount of heat, sweet, tangy, and savory.

Finally!!  This is a great alternative to Superior since he is closing shop!  I do not trust many folks with my shoes( ferragamo, Bruno magli, metzlan you get the ideA). I wear my shoes hard and it shows but this place just brought two of my favorite most beat up pairs back to life!  Prices are reasonable and they take credit cards which is a plus!  Look no further they do a great job!!

Saturday Feb 15th I attended a magic show, along with my girlfriend and two friends,at the South Street Magic Theatre . This was our first time visiting and we were all very pleased with the show. From the beginning it was good, with a great warm up act by "Captain Swirly". Really neat stuff. 
    
   The main act hit the stage about 20 minutes after. This guy, Dan Hauss, was awesome. He wasn't originally scheduled on the bill, but there was some inclement weather that week so I believe they had to make some changes. None the less, I'm glad they did. Dan's show was top notch. He was funny, mesmerising, and interacted with all of the audience members. We even  got a chance to talk with him a bit after the show and he couldn't have been nicer.
  If you ever think about going to South Street Magic Theatre, don't think, just go. I guarantee you'll have a blast, we all agreed it was the most fun we've had in a while.

Stopped in for dinner before heading to a show on nearby Avenue of the Arts.  Had a great time here -- all of the staff were very friendly and helpful, the service was prompt and professional, and the food was terrific.  Will definitely return.

Ok, so not 'every' time I've been there has been a blast.  There's definitely some dull and/or awkward nights, but I do have to say that they're few and far between.
Be sure to check out Madonna Michael Prince night there and get there early because the place fills up quick.

Always delicious food, very convenient. They are quite friendly, but not in a pushy way. Affordable. I wish they have a more eco-friendly approach (even eat-in is served on disposable plates), but I can understand how that might not be affordable.

Brunch here was amazing! I'm not a big chicken and waffles fan but theirs was amazing. All of my friends were happy with their entrees and I'll definitely be coming back. I also hear they have good happy hour deals during the week that I want to check out.

I had my first gyno appointment at this office. After going through their website meticulously to make sure that it would be free under my insurance, as my annual gyno appointment, I came in for my appointment. The desk staff was professional and efficient, as was the nurse. I enjoyed my entire experience - something I never thought I would walk away from an obgyn appointment saying! My doctor, in particular, I found phenomenal. Dr. McGinty was personable and made me feel very comfortable. She was professional and clearly qualified. When I had some blood drawn for a test, the nurse who came in and took my blood was incredible. Albeit, it may sound odd to rave about a blood draw, but having had my blood taken previously this month by a medical professional whose only job was to take blood, the nurse who performed the blood draw at the Women's Wellness Center was much quicker, pain free, and all-around better! I couldn't believe how fast she worked. 

While I have seen reviews saying they had to wait on the phone when calling for an appointment, this was not my experience at all. I called and was immediately answered, and then penciled in for an evening appointment later the same week! In short, I found my experience pleasant and professional. The office is in a row home as well, which provided an aesthetic experience I much preferred to it's more typical overly-sterile feeling counterpart.

There is no discernible difference between Sabrina's the First and Sabrina's the Second. Well, foodwise there isn't. Contrary to what many of my friends would have me believe, the food is EXACTLY the same, but with a hint of new restaurant thrown in.

The only thing I don't like about this place is the weirdo decor. It's kinda art-deco, it's kinda 60's retro, and it kinda makes you want to throw up if you're nursing a hangover on Sunday morning. But I'd gladly suffer through bad decor for the sugar fiesta that is the Special Stuffed French Toast.

And don't forget, they have call ahead seating. You don't have to wait in that ridiculously long line!!

Crescent Cab used to be a fine company. After Avant Garde closed, and many of their drivers switched to Crescent, so did I. Well, I called for a cab at 8:27 last night for a 7:15 pickup. At 7:16, I called dispatch, who at first weren't able to find the ticket, then said she thought that I had already been picked up and that it might be 15 minutes to get a cab here (South Philly). When I called back in 15 minutes, she said that a cab was "on its way," but "not on the map." I never heard from them again. They will never hear from me again.

Will never be using their service again. Did a great job cleaning my carpets, however the customer service was horrible!! The customer service reps quoted me a price via phone and did not explain that it was just an estimate and when the technician arrive, he quoted me a price double my original estimate. The technician also arrived two hours AFTER my scheduled 2 hour hour block. Just completely ridiculous. If you have an option, choose another company that will commit to the promised time and accurately explain their pricing.

Came in for brunch, had asked the server for a different preparation in the eggs to avoid having them overcooked, was assured they would not be, came out really overcooked and was still charged for the plate anyway. The gravlax flatbread was just ok. Hommus was good, really enjoyed the mushroom hommus over the lamb.

I love Time - always have. Where else can you listen to live jazz or dance to house and pop music? The three rooms at time offer three distinctly different experiences for whatever peaks your mood.  Time for me is all about the atmosphere but it's food, beer and cocktail menus are all very good.

There is often a $5 cover but it's worth it for jazz in a lively center city atmosphere. There can be a line on weekends later in the night.

I am renting an apartment from Bluesquare.  Unfortunately, if I owned property, I would not have them manage it.  They don't take care of the apartments that the renters rent.  When I moved in, I had to tell them to clean and paint the apartment, even though they said they were going to do so before I moved in when I signed the lease.  When you look at the paint job, you see that they did a sloppy job, like getting paint on hardwood floors, not painting the ceiling, and leaving a mess afterwords.

There is an overall lack of professionalism from the managers all the way down to the workers that "maintain" the property.  When I moved in, Lloyd was the manager of my apartment but now Pam is.  Both of them have left a lot to be desired.  To get the same repair done, you have to call Bluesquare property managers multiple times to make sure they are on top of their own maintenance team.  As the tenant, you shouldn't have to remind managers to manage.  Maintenance workers have broken two of my ceramic items while "fixing" other things in the apartment.  There is no 24-hour maintenance service so if you are locked out or have some other issue on the weekend, you're out of luck.  One of my first interactions with the current manager, Pam, on the apartment was a shouting match in which she was sitting comfortably in her office and couldn't send someone from the office with the Master key to let me into my apartment two blocks away for another three hours.  This was despite the fact that the key that maintenance had made me when I was locked out did not work.

I have had a mouse in my apartment for 2 1/2 weeks.  It took them days to call an exterminator and another week to patch up the hole, which the mouse just pushed away anyways. To give them some credit, they decided that they needed to move the fridge to fix the holes and since it is built into the cabinet, it would be difficult to do.  However, after this assessment, it has taken another week for any action.    I put out poison myself which killed the mouse after several days, but the holes remain. 

Because I move a lot for work and school, I have lived in apartments for the last thirteen years and have had over ten different landlords.  This is by far the worst company I have had to deal with.  Overall, they are a horrible company to rent from and it is best to avoid them at all costs.  *Especially* if you want them to take care of your property.  Getting the condo owners involved is the only way to get them to step up their game, which kind of defeats the purpose of their managing the property.

Food is decent and filling but the place is so dirty it's enough to make me not go back with fear of how things are actually prepped. There's food spilled all over the counter where the staff put together your food. The floors are also dirty and you can see their work stations behind the counter are not kept clean or organized either. The staff is also under whelming, and robotic. No greeting, no emotion, let alone any type of eye contact. Might as well automate it with robots for cheaper production.

I am so disappointed to write this.  I love Greek food and have been dying to go here for a few years.  

Their Valentine's Day menu was not good.  The duck was ok.  The rabbit was like a standard chicken cutlet.  Both salad options were below average.  I couldn't take a second bite of the raw vegetable salad.  I am not even sure what was in it.    The raw salad was probably one of the worst things I have ever eaten.  The lamb and pheasant pie was good.  I particularly liked the yogurt that came with the pheasant pie.  The bureka's were good too.

The server was very pleasant and knowledgable.  The restaurant messed up our reservation, but luckily squeezed us in.

Unfortunately, they made a huge mistake with their pre-fixed menu.  It was way too expensive, especially since the dish selection was horrible.  Hopefully, this is not usually the case and I just had a rare experience.

I always thought FSC was over priced and not very friendly, but the food was solid and the upstairs seating was great for working. 

The last two times I was there service, and quality of food  was bad enough for me not to return. Last week my husband and I went on a lunch date. We ordered at the front register, paid (tipped) and let the person know we would be sitting outside. 25 minutes later I pop up to see what the hold up was. No one was at the counter. I waited 5 minutes and went back to our table. When I returned our food was being placed on our table. Our food was mildly warm. No apology was given. 

Ok, fine. An off day. I get it. 

This afternoon I host a business lunch with my team. We order and head upstairs. After quite some time, someone brings our buffalo cauliflower but tells us we need to go collect our food from the bar since there is no table service (or runners apparently). We had to then collect our own silverware sets as well. We were not given a buzzer to alert us when our food was ready. No one bothered to say "it should be ready in X minutes, so grab it yourself", or "Have you been here before? Here's how things work here" or "we don't have runners, so keep an eye on time". Nothing. To top it off: During our meeting I see an employee run an order of cauliflower up to another table. Is this a sometimes policy? What gives? I won't be back to find out. 

What's next? Will you have to wash your own dishes?? Next time eat at home. Your cat will give you better service

I ordered "sardines" a la plancha, but there was only ONE on the plate.......for $4, what? BUT,  I didn't complain, ate as it was fresh and tasty.  I also ordered their spicy bean and spinach soup, it arrived in a huge bowl that only had a *tiny* amount of soup in it, soup that had been overcooked AND over salted.  It was so over salted that I was unable to eat it, and have a pretty high bar for salty food. THIS I had to send back.  The waitress took it, didn't apologize or say anything (awkward and rude)...then, a little while later, brought me another soup, which was less salty, but still, the amount of soup in the massive bowl was offensive...for SEVEN dollars, they serve a such a small amount that it's not even worth it.

If you just want to drink and listen to music, go for it, but I wouldn't waste my money on the food there.

The chef just finished harassing me via yelp. If he does it again, this review goes down to one star.

Husband and I were on first date night in 2 years. Was very excited to try this place. He ordered the Chilean sea bass, I ordered the scallops. His sea bass was raw in the middle, like still 1/3 translucent pinkish. My scallops were so disgusting tasting. Im not sure if they were rotten or cooked in a strange oil but they were completely inedible. We are not complaining types but was so shocking that we BOTH had issues with our orders. Reported to manager who remove them from the bill. The point is, we left hungry and sceptical that a $$$$ establishment will serve this to any customers at all.
 I would LOVE to hear what the chef was thinking while preparing our meals...

Excellent happy hour alert! This place is a bit dangerous just because I happen to work right across the street. It lures me in with lovely cocktail, beer & snack specials...along with it's soothing sounds of an awesome jazz trio - I"m hooked!

Try to avoid renting from Solo if you can. I rented from them for 2.5 years in Fitler Square. While they probably aren't the worst mgmt company out there, they are far from the best. They don't seem to show much interest in their properties...or maybe it's just the owners they work with. 
Over the time I rented from them, they were inconsistent about responding to service requests. Sometimes they would send someone out within a few days, other times they would "forget" for months, after promising to fix something. The service contractors they use are definitely low quality. Over my 2.5 year stay, we had a new floor put in the kitchen (the plasticy "tile" was laid down crooked) and they had to rewire the thermostat (using blue wire that they staple-gunned across a white wall/ceiling). I could always tell when a contractor had been there, because they never cleaned up after themselves. Nails on the floor, anyone? Ouch. 
My favorite though, was the squirrel who lived in the ceiling (maybe still does!). At first, they responded and sent someone out, after I sent in pictures of the hole the squirrel was making, the debris they were tossing out of the hole, etc. They fixed some rotten wood on the roof deck door and said problem fixed. But problem not fixed...squirrel still running around. Eventually they started ignoring my emails about the problem, and that's when I decided I was done. 
Also, don't plan on getting your full security deposit back. My roommate and I left the apartment in MUCH BETTER condition than we got it. We cleaned, spackled, everything. They charged us $100 because they had to wipe down a window sill and the knobs on the stove. When we complained, the owner (Deborah Solo) was rude and just complained to me that they find it frustrating how many people get upset about deposits. Well, maybe if that many of your tenants are getting upset, might it be time to look to see if YOU are being unfair? Her email responses were unprofessional and the icing on the cake. I cannot recommend this company and am much happier renting elsewhere!
Unreal food. This is the only place I will go to breakfast in manayunk. Greg is the man and his staff is awesome and extremely friendly. Coffee is also out of this world.

I was expecting more but everything was just OK.  The portions are small and I expected that, but the food lacked in the quality and flavor department.

Oh wait, I almost forget the one thing that I did really like: the wine!  We ordered a nice bottle of Malbec and I think that was the only thing that didn't completely disappoint.

Good location, very decent accomodations, excellent service.   

Brian at the front desk was seriously one of the nicest, most helpful hotel staffers I've come across in a long time (and everyone I made contact with during my stay was friendly and accomodating).  The rooms are a little noisy, and could use updating, but they're clean, with comfy beds and nice touches like eye masks and ear plugs.

Not quite the hip experience of a W, nor the luxury of a Four Seasons, but dependable, comfortable accomodations at a reasonable price.  I'd definitely stay here again if budgets prohibited the afore-mentioned brands.

I just moved back to Chicago after having left Philly for 4 years.  I love this place and will definitely miss it.

Always entertaining.  I love the mix of people personally, even though I'm not a "hipster" or bike messenger.  You get everything in here.

The Drunken Spelling Bee is fun and amusing (so glad that blonde bitch doesn't run it anymore).  Porno bingo as well -- where else can you get a shami, anal beads and free porn (I managed to win all 3).

I love the jazz band, and have sang with them several times.  God bless, Will, who in his 70's still works his charm with the ladies.

The bartenders can be surly, but that's also their charm!  Love ya Butchie!

Here's the thing.  The food is great, the owner is a nightmare.  Yelp is all about a comprehensive review so I'm just being honest.

Visited the restaurant for brunch on a Saturday with a party of 6.  They were able to seat us within 15 minutes even though they seemed to be quite busy.  We all ordered something different and everyone seemed to be satisfied with their meal.  I had a coffee and a cheddar and tomato omelet.  The eggs were absolutely delicious, not over-cooked which tends to happen with omelets, but perfectly moist and tender!  The omelet was served with an ample amount of roasted potatoes and a slice of toast.  It was truly the perfect amount of food.

The only downside to the actual meal was the weird club music that was playing loudly over the already loud dining room.  That was confusing.

Anyway, my friend's credit card was charged three times.  An honest mistake by our courteous server.  My friend called the restaurant to report the charge and was told by the owner that "this sort of thing just happens with debit cards".  As if it was her fault for having a debit card...never apologizing for the accident and letting her know she'll have her money back on Tuesday.  Being a student with low funds and in-between jobs, this put her in an uncomfortable moneyless situation.  She was never offered any sort of compensation.

She called back a day later to talk to the owner and just make it known that she was unsatisfied with the way things were handled and was told things such as "this really isn't a big deal".  Again, she explained that maybe not for him but for her it was all of her money.  So anyway, as she kept her calm, the owner began yelling at her and eventually hung up on her.  This just seems inappropriate.  As someone who has worked in customer service for years, I know (unfortunately) that it's typically the irrational customer's job to yell at the worker, and am still baffled at the treatment my friend received from this person. 

So like, great food but really terrible management.  It's a toss up.

Just got my car detailed here today. After reserving a time slot, the team was able to turn my vehicle around in 2 hours. Full treatment cost was very reasonable and much less than the competition. I asked about a couple extra services and they were able to get everything done within the time allotted. I'll be using this location for future washes and detailing services!
I really like this place! Good fresh food and worth the price. There's European twist to their food - fresh squeezed juices and egg Benedict dishes are so tasty and fresh.

I'm reviewing this as someone who has planned at least 4 banquets here in the past few years.
Randy Schaller is our hero and Geraldo is his sidekick - as a team, they are superior in service.

They work with your budget and both buffet and sit down dinners, whatever you decide to choose have been delish, hot til it reaches you and very seasonal. The food (for a chain hotel and for large over 100 ppl events) is fantastic! Guests are always raving about the catering!

It's not your typical Hilton, it's clean, glistening in chandeliers, looks grand. They just renovated the bar and restaurant and it was a good move!

Parking is only $5. Coat check $1. Outside of the city, worth the drive due to the lower prices compared to Center City hotel banquet rooms.

Love the old fashioned, nostalgic atmosphere. This place can get pretty packed during the summer, even at night. I love getting one of their sundaes. During the winter, they have milkshakes with a warm slice of pie inside. My only qualm is that it's rather hard to find parking around here, but that's to be expected in philly. Overall a wonderful experience!

First trip to Darlings and enjoyed the experience. My wife and I were served by Terri. Awesome waitress who helped us with our selection and scored on each choice. My wife thoroughly enjoyed her crab cakes and likewise my steak was cooked just as I ordered. Terri added just enuff conversation to make you feel like a regular. And her suggestion to cap the meal off with the classic cheesecake couldn't have been better. Upon finding out that our wedding anniversary was that day, she decorated the cheesecake. nice touch from a great waitress. 
A return visit is a guarantee.

As part of an Olney Food Tour, organized through the North 5th Street Revitalization Project, our group got to savor Kilbi - grilled marinated beef ribs - and various fresh and spicy small dishes called Ban Chan. This was a full experience of fresh flavor and atmosphere, with plenty of choices for constructing our plates to match our individual palates.

My boyfriend and I came to Philly on a weekend getaway and came to butcher bar for a friday night dinner. Very busy so im glad I called ahead for a reservation. The lower part was a bar and the restaurant was upstairs. The olace was decored in a rustic kitchen theme with meat hooks (its cool looking i promise) hanging from the ceiling. We were seated in a busy area and served quickly. Our server was very friendly and helpful with menu suggestions. We ordered the bacon as a starter, it came out shortly and was absolutely delicious and flavorful. I ordered the game hen with vegetables which was a large plate. Very good. My boyfriend ordered the bison ribs and they practically fell off the bone they were so rich. For dessert we had an amazing carrot cake with heavenly cream cheese frosting. Would definitely come again the next time we are in Philly

Rolls Auto is a very unprofessional establishment. I am very disappointing with the service that I received this morning. A sales associate by the name of "Ace" informed me not to come in to look at a certain vehicle without cash in hand, instead he told me to look at a few on the street. i called back and he was very irate, unprofessional and hung up on me. I asked to speak to the sales manager and he informed me that he was the manger. 

What a piece of ...

Excellently top notch espresso.

Everything I've tried here is thumbs up across the board.

It was a cold 30 degree day in this brisk frozen winter of 2013, and I wanted to be warm deep down to my bones. So the roommie and I bundled up and headed to the Academy... where the butterflies were rumored to fly. Being students, we were able to get a discount and I think it only cost 13.50 to see the whole museum. I remember visiting Philly one day years ago and exploring the Academy, seeing their awesome collection of fossils and taxidermied habitats, and what i remembered wasn't even close! Such a thorough colleciton, plussssss THE BUTTERFLY ROOM! wow. 80-120 LIVE butterflies and moths live year round in this tropical escape! the room is small, but the amount of live beauty is amazing. the collection is obtained from farmers all over the world, from Madagascar to Costa Rica, and the cocoons are displayed so the visiters can watch the new ones hatch. When we went, the room was almost empty, an owl eyed butterfly landed on my roommates hat and stayed for a half an hour! The guide present was extremely knowledgeable, and showed us specific features and anatomy of the butterflies on the dead specimens he was collecting for disposal. We stayed for about 45 minutes, till our hair was curly from the 80% humidity, our clothes sticking to us from the 80 degree weather, and our minds blown. BEST ESCAPE from the frigid winter EVER! Highly recommend if you would like to exercise your mind and immerse yourself in beauty as well.

I've eaten at Amada several times, and it pains me to give it 4 stars. I wish, just once, that Yelp had a split rating system: one for food and another for service. If that were the case, I'd give Amada a 5 for food and a 3 or 4 for service. Here's why.

(I'll admit that my last experience colors what I'm going to say.) I got an early reservation -- 5:45 -- because my client wanted to have an early dinner. We took the $65 prix fixe Chef's Tasting Menu. After only a few minutes after ordering, we had four dishes on our table. As we were eating those, two more courses came. Less than 30 minutes after ordering, all but the last 3 courses were delivered to our table.
