One of my dining companions got too full, and couldn't eat the main items -- lamb chop, flank steak, gambas.

In total, we got 11 courses of the most expensive chef's menu delivered to us in 50 minutes. Seriously. It should have been two hours. I wondered if they were worried and wanted to turn the table for a 7Pm reservation...it seemed nuts. I mean, I have never been rushed that much for a premium dinner before. 

I'm going to keep going back. I've had some 5-star meals there, but the last time was really disappointing from a service standpoint. 

I didn't talk about the food. It was exquisite. Outstanding. Next time, I'm going to take the time to enjoy it and tell them to slow down. It's that simple.

This place was sssooo good! The restaurant is super cute and quaint and reminds me of being in someone's home. The food was super delish and they served huge portions. I had the dish with a blue tortilla and guacamole and eggs and damn those potatoes were amazing. I don't remember what it was called but I finished like half of it and ate the rest the next day. SO GOOD. like home cookin. I def will be back. But I won't order the iced tea. It was flavorless and nondescript. But yeah the meal was amazing.

Another place that didn't live up to the hype. Not bad but wouldn't rave. Got the breakfast sandwich which was disappointing. No real flavor, few flakes of cheese between the square of frittata. Pretty small, and I'm usually stuffed at most restaurants. Got the potatoes which were overall pretty good, but a few were a bit undercooked. The good ones had a nice crisp ish exterior and soft interior. Honestly needed ketchup which is too bad...just a bit more seasoning could have gone a long way. The salsa verde (I guess) was on both the sandwich and the potatoes--bit spicy.

Counter service where they drop off your food but you clean up yourself. Small restaurant had more of a cafe vibe. Having trouble picturing it as a dinner spot but maybe it's more impressive for dinner?

Nothing short of an absolutely amazing dining experience can be had at Alma De Cuba. 

Long story short... I happened to be in Philly over Columbus Day weekend with my boyfriend and he surprised me with dinner reservations at 'Alma De Cuba'. As soon as we got out of the taxi and were in front of the place I knew I was in for a great night out. 

First of all, from the outside 'Alma De Cuba' looks like a traditional home from La Habana that just happened to be transported to Philly.

Let me also say, the ambiance was great and very intimate. This is definitely a spot I would consider perfect for 'date night'. Dim lighting, comfortable seating, and ample space (parties of 2 were spread out on the first floor) made this a great place for dinner to start off our night. 

Upon walking in, we were immediately greeted by the hostess who sat us down at our table and handed us menus. I liked the variety in the menu because there were a couple of choices for meat lovers, as well as seafood lovers, and they all looked amazing. 

While we looked over the menus we ordered drinks, a passion fruit caipiroska (made with Vodka instead of traditional cachaca like a caipirinha) and a red wine sangria. I wasn't a HUGE fan of the red sangria and I ended up ordering a caipiroska like my boyfriend because they were tasty, the liquor wasn't overpowering, and the you could really taste the passion fruit. 

After much deliberation, I ordered the 'vaca frita' (fried cow), which brought skirt steak, white rice, black beans, and a sort of tomato salsa. The boyfriend ordered a seafood dish, the whole roasted bronzino. 

Before our dishes were brought out a server came out and placed a small basket of bread at the table with this amazing sauce that I can't describe at the moment. I kept the sauce once they took the basket away for my meal. 

After what seemed like less than 10 minutes of having placed our order our meal was brought out. The skirt steak was cooked beautifully along with my white rice and black beans. Being from the Caribbean and growing up eating Caribbean food, I have a particular preference for how I like my rice and beans cooked. Alma De Cuba did not disappoint and gave me an authentic Caribbean/Cuban meal. 

The service at this place is what I would consider excellent. Friendly, and more importantly helpful, staff is quick to accommodate patrons, as well as bring out dishes or drinks once you order. 

I can't wait to go back to Philadelphia and try a couple more items from their menu (food & drink wise).

Finding a new stylist after moving is such a hassle, and I'm lucky I found Josh at Jason Matthew Salon.  I have fine medium-long hair, and it is hard to find someone who doesn't want to add too many layers or cut a blunt edge.  Josh was quick and accommodating-- gave me a perfect cut in 45 minutes on a Monday morning.  I've received several compliments!
I also love that even as they are reasonably priced, they don't incessantly attempt to upsell you with products.  Conversation was great, easy, and I left with a huge smile on my face.

bought an expensive shirt there after traveling 45 min from the burbs on the recommendation of a friend - they wouldn't let me unwrap it before I purchased ... about a week later I opened the package to iron only to find it has small bb-sized black spots in about 4 locations.  Now, do I drive back in to Philly return it or just chuck it? - dry-cleaner can't even get them out ... tar or something.  Love the color and the fit, but can't wear it.

An Italian restaurant getting five stars from me?  That's a rarity.  I'm kinda bored with Italian food ... but Little Nonna's is awesome.  First it looks cute - especially its rear outside courtyard replete with a clothesline.  At night white lights illuminate.  Don't worry about the rain - it is all under Plexiglas cover. 

Now the food.  I ate the best meatball that I have ever eaten anywhere in the world.  This meatball is large and incredibly tasty and served over a bed of polenta.  The seafood mixed grill which consisted of swordfish, calamari, and shrimp was also tasty.  Add a little pepper and it comes even more to life.  For desert, a pistachio cannoli perfectly his the spot.

Service was friendly and competent.  Little Nonna's deserves those five stars,

not a lot has changed for the better at earth bread + brewery.  and you know, i live damn far away.  i think this marks my last uphill mt airy-bound bike expedition to source an increasingly-disappointing seed pizza (flatbread). 

first of all, the hostess, servers and food runners are dumb as bricks and they couldn't care less.  the hostess looked at me like i had two heads when i asked her to not sit us next to the 10-top of screaming, climbing munchkins.  why is earth bread + BREWERY so packed full of unparented children?  (see my last review from 3 years ago.)  common sense 101 for the hostess: a couple on a date does NOT want to sit next to little johnny's 5th birthday party.  i shouldn't even have to state this.  you have two floors.  make use of them.  

our server struggled so hard to open our wine i wanted to reach over and do it for her.  instead she took it to the bartender to open.  she also had no clue about the menu - it was obvious she'd never tried any of it.  really?  so we TOTALLY struck out with the hummus trio.  it was overly cold (too refrigerated), had zero flavor and not enough bread.  we also struck out on the special pizza - something autumnal, with brussell sprouts and squash, i think.  not i'm not sure: it was blackened to a crisp.  i was busy talking to my date when they sat it down; didn't look at it right at first so i didn't notice the char until i smelled it.  the burning smell caused me to look at our food, and realize we were served a black saucer.  we wanted to sent it right back, but no server or runner would make eye contact.  eventually it went back, and out came the manager with: our pizza is supposed to have the char bubbles; perhaps next time you can ask them to not cook it for so long.  oh get off it.  i know what a pizza is supposed to look like.  this one was 100% charred, including the squash, a pretty difficult vegetable to burn.  i should have dared him to eat it.  its replacement still came out too dark, but by that point we'd established that flavor combination just didn't work for pizza anyway, and called it a bust.  next up came our seed pizza, which was also too dark.  and i LIKE well-done pizza!   

our wine and food were long gone, and we were waiting for our bill forever.  it took so long, i just handed over my credit card without looking at it, before our server could disappear for another eternity (at this point, a table of kids had been seated next to us; we just wanted outta there).  she proceeds to keep my credit card for 20 minutes as i'm getting increasingly cranky and annoyed.  finally the card comes back - and she's charged me for the next table's food, not ours.  but she's off again, and i can't get her attention.  i looked at the bill, decided it was certainly less than what we'd spent anyway, signed it and went to sit at the downstairs bar for another drink and to give them time to realize and correct their mistake.  they never did.  they just have their shit together that little.

i'm giving them a break with two stars because they have a good concept.  the beers are good, wines are reasonably priced and good, and the seed pizza is good (though decreasingly so).  this place needs to examine their kitchen staff and just totally fire the entire front of house, with the exception of the downstairs bartender (pete?).  he was cool.  knowledgeable, friendly, excited about beer.  

anyway, overall - so, so, SO disappointing, earth bread + brewery.  i know you were one of the first fancy-topping pizza houses in the city, but with places like zavino and stella and nomad arriving on the scene... you've been outdone.

Came here with a friend, we both loved the food and the owners are so friendly and make you feel very welcomed. The menu is big, if you're confused asked them what they recommend based on what you like. The food is fresh, you won't regret coming here.

For how nice this place seems, I was sorely disappointed.

I came here for a work-related happy hour the other day. The lounge was nearly empty and yet our waitress was nowhere to be found for nearly 45 min. We ordered the sliders, spinach dip, pretzels, frites, and nuts/cheese plates. The food was just OK, I honestly expected better. 

The food prices were good for such a swanky place, but the cocktails were overpriced (I didn't like my pear martini much). Overall, I'd probably come back because they have a good happy hour, but I've had better service at XIX and the atmosphere is just as good with better cocktail prices for happy hour.
Earnie goes above and beyond to satisfy his customers. Great prices and great mechanics is why this place is number #1 in Philly. You can drop off your car before hours if needed they do a great job

DON'T DO IT.

Notice that the 5-star review is for a Uhaul rental. . . not at all this self-storage location. The other two reviews are for this actual business, and yep, they suck.

We have had units there for years because it's super-close and so seemed convenient. And once you've put your stuff IN self-storage, you'll probably stay, like we did. We're finally getting out of the last unit now and I'm so sorry it took this long.

We had the same water damage issue in one unit as Bill H. -- caused by a leaky pipe in the building. No compensation, no apologies, nothing.

The air conditioning in the storage area is wimpy at best. Very conducive to mold even when there hasn't been water gushing through your things.

The security is non-existent -- you need a key card to get in, unless the garage door has been left open, which it often has. There's no manager on site EVER in my experience, and there isn't even someone answering the phone most of the time.

There's a large garage to pull into -- except at least 50% is always full of maintenance stuff for the apartments (which is hilarious, since apparently they don't really DO maintenance in the apartments very often). Often the maintenance truck is also parked there, so then it's 100% full. Pulling in is difficult but possible when the truck isn't there.

The elevator only breaks occasionally, so that's something. There's a cart to move things at least half the time. That's about the best I can say for them.

I have never actually set foot inside The Couch Tomato Cafe but I have had their food many times. My company (in Bala Cynwyd) often orders pizza from them when we have meetings or training. Their pizzas are awesome! But a colleague of mine turned me on to the Grilled Cheese & Tomato Bisque Combo which has become my absolute FAVORITE! The bread is delicious, it's super cheesy and the tomato bisque is great for dipping. Also, you can get arugula on the sandwich which is great. You can get gluten free bread if you'd like, too. It may not be the healthiest thing on the menu but it's amazing - especially on a bitterly cold day like today. Order one, you will not be disappointed.
Also, big shout out to the delivery team: they deliver fast and are really friendly! Make sure to tip them!
Now I have to go finish my grilled cheese and tomato bisque before it gets cold!

UPDATE: I've tried several sandwiches since my last review. The last one was the "Kickin Chicken." It is not "kickin" what-so-ever. The chicken is in large chunks and can be a little on the dryer side because there is very little sauce (in this case, chipotle) to juice it up. I didn't even taste pepper jack cheese either and am not positive that it was on the sandwich at all. Also, I called my order in and asked for spinach instead of romaine and I was charged an extra dollar. You should be able to substitute a topping of equal or lesser value without being charged! I've also tried a buffalo chicken and the smokehouse. Again, good but lacking on the sauce. The driver was quick and courteous as usual. Overall, however, I'm down-grading from 5 stars to 3. :(
So happy to find this place right next to the hotel I was in. I admit it doesn't look like much but service was fast and food was delicious. Will definitely be back the next time I'm nearby.

Nice cafe on a quieter section of Chestnut st. They have a good selection of teas and bubbles teas. I'm not sure if it ever gets crowded but I've gone on Sunday mornings and it was very quiet. Good study spot too!

Best burger in Philly. PERIOD.
Awesome whiskey selection (obviously), fantastic service, limited menu, which is fine with me, because what they do, they do WELL!!! GREAT food!!!

This review is mainly for the lobster ravioli.

Every time I have ordered or been with someone who ordered lobster ravioli, I have always been disappointed to see a smooth, mashed filling. I want to feel like I'm eating lobster meat, you know? 

So imagine my surprise when my lobster ravioli came to me at Varalli (they were out of the gnocci, my first choice), and I bit in to chunks of lobster meat. I don't want to be over-dramatic and tell you that the heavens opened up and I heard angels sing...but I heard angels sing. It was divine. The pasta shell was thin, the filling meaty, the sauce rich and full of mushrooms and tomatoes. 

My dining companions were all pleased with their meals, the salads look to be pretty good, and the crisp zucchini appetizer was a hit. We enjoyed pleasant service from a guy who was kind enough to ask if we were on our way to a show and did we need to be in and out quickly. We had just come from a show and had all the time in the world, so he took his time and so did we.

P.S. Leave a little room for dessert. The tiramisu will blow your mind.
Great food!! Great atmosphere!! Love the Ishi Yaki Bop..that's a must get dish!!!... expensive but it's worth it!!!

It was pretty pricy on even a Sunday afternoon (4pm)... Like $50 an hour. I would expect some kinda happy hour but then again coming from nyc we're spoiled with happy hr deals for asian karaoke. However one good thing is that it includes food, so instead of going to a bar, you can drink $50 worth and get the room for free! Theres also a ton of food from chicken wings to ramen if you dont wanna drink

The karaoke system was definitely above average - it was a touch screen monitor, which i liked a lot! The mic kept running out of battery 3 times so that was annoying.

One tip is that if youre thinking about signing up for more than an hr, dont. When an hour hits, they give you the bill very slowly. They collect it slowly too. And they take it away slowly. So we got like an extra 30 minutes for free lol

Overall if you hv a group of 5 ish and wanna drink this would be a good place

I'm going to have to agree on the quality of this place.  An excellent wine bar for the area with an inviting interior.  Everyone is friendly and the place has the atmosphere a wine bar should have (not uptight).  

Great variety of wine choices, and the presentation of tapas was well done.  The cheese plates are great.

Although my love will always be for wine bars on the west coast, Vintage does a great job in Philadelphia.

A pleasant lunch outside in late May. The fried goat cheese ($12) was good and the bacon-wrapped dates  ($13) such a hit we tried them at home. Wine was typically over-priced at $14 for either Gruner Vetliner or Pinot Grigio.

Lovely atmosphere with beautiful outside seating and great food. My mom and sister enjoy their broccolini caesar salad and love their their pepperoni pizza because it is loaded with pepperoni. For dessert, they loved the assortment of cannolis. They also have a puppy park on the side where you can bring your dog and eat! They will bring water for your dog as well. We love this place!

I've been here a couple of times and have been satisfied with the burgers, but never blown away.  The burgers are a solid masterpiece flavors, but the fries always seem lacking.  I mean, just the sound of duck fat fries conjures up thoughts of delectable wedges of potato goodness dripping with flavor, but unfortunately the flavor is just not there...even with the sly fox cheddar sauce.  I am probably (definitely) in the minority here, but I much rather have a nice cheeseburger from Parc with those delicious, crispy, thin-cut fries.

If you are looking for results, this is the place you need to check out. I have tried many different boutique workout studios and gym in Philadelphia but nothing compare to F45. The workout are always different and challenging. Is a good mix of cardio and strength. I feel like my muscles are confused from the workout. The trainers are great and always there to help you and make adjustment so you do not get hurt. I highly recommend to take advantage of the free week trial and check it out. You won't regret it.
Decent spot. The bread was a little chewy. But overall does the considering it's the "only" cheesesteak spot in walking distance in the area.

I always get the roast pork w/ sharp provolone and brocoli rabe.  You can't go wrong.  If you have time, eat it here, it can get a lil soggy in your travels.

I've heard from a number of sources that their cheesesteaks here are even better.  They only serve them til around 2pm, after they "shut down the grill", so unfortunately, I've yet to sample one.

*Update* I want to express my appreciation of the follow up conducted by Nicolette. I truly appreciate her concern about my experience. I will continue to patron EWC, management stepping up plays an important role in customer experience. Thank you, Nicolette. Truly appreciate it.
After the initial review, the company offered to send the shirts free of charge.  They arrived a week after the event, but I appreciate the effort that was given.

What a treat, and right in our own backyard!

The Barnes has one of the largest private art collections. There are multiple paintings from Matisse, Cezanne, Monet, Renoire, Picasso - the list goes on! 

We went on a "First Sunday" special so the entry was free (and therefore the line was forming at an exponential speed!) Once you're passed the double door entry, you can go downstairs for coat check/waiting area until your time or straight through to the galleries if you wanted to hang out in the open space between galleries where they have a small cafe for coffee and snacks. The tickets have a time on them, and that is the time that you're able to actually enter into the galleries. 

I liked the organization of the rooms. Each room had a specific booklet, and each booklet was organized by wall - very easy to find the artwork you're interested in and learn more about the piece. The booklets gave us a glimpse of the history of the artwork, naming the artist and the titling of the piece. After that, it was all up to your imagination to peer and see what was intended (or not!)

During the afternoon there is usually a group activity (it was arts and crafts the day we went) so definitely a good idea to bring the kids for these events. The group activity is independent from the art gallery viewing, so you're able to enter just for that if that is your plan. 

Honestly, great way to spend the morning getting your culture on, and the afternoon activities really help make this a fun family outing. Enjoy!

A friend invited us to meet them at Devon Seafood Grill for Sunday brunch at 12:00 noon.  We arrived a bit early and waited for all parties in the reservation to arrive prior to being seated.  While waiting, I noticed that the restaurant was not very busy.

There is a large bar in the front room that is surrounded by small tables, as well as a dining room on a lower level.  The tables in the dining room were dressed with white tablecloths while the tables around the bar were not.

Despite having a reservation, we were seated in what I consider the "bar" area.  There were others seated in the same area including a party of 5 at a table that would accommodate 3-4 comfortably.  The other guests in this "corner" of the bar were seated at tables along the banquette.  

The three of us were seated at the table in the very center of this nook; sandwiched between the party of 5 and a party of 4 along the banquette.  SANDWICHED with no room for spread!

Before we ordered, I offered our server the opportunity to relocated us to another table as service to the other tables around us would be very difficult for the staff.  He stated that it was not an issue for us "blocking" pathways as they do this all the time.

The service staff could only access the other tables by walking behind me and a guest with the party of 5 through a very narrow walkway.  Throughout brunch, all I heard was "excuse me" from the service staff passing through and the sound of my chair being bumped and/or kicked.

Now onto the food?!?  If you've read this far, you may as well continue reading.

What was ordered:
- Calamari - Free with Yelp check-in
- Chicken and Waffles
- Quiche
- Eggs Benedict

The calamari was a bit greasy and included other items like fried jalapeño and other vegetables.  It was served with a nondescript aioli and a sweet and sour vinegar.

Chicken and Waffles consisted of three miniature, round Belgium waffles with three chicken tenders with "pancake" syrup.  Pretty blah plate presentation with beige/brown waffles topped with a beige/brown chicken strips each and a little white container of cheap syrup all served on a white plate.  No garnish, no color.

Quiche is baked in its' own individual portion casserole dish and served with a beet, poached pear and mesclun salad that was garnished with blue cheese.  The salad was tasty and the quiche was eh.  The casserole dish is lined with some type of flour "crust."  It was not buttery nor flaky.  They pour the egg mixture into the dough and bake it.  The dish is about 1-inch deep which makes a very shallow quiche-like entree.

Eggs Benedict is a traditional dish with a poached egg on top of Canadian bacon and English muffin.  The menu states that it is served with "home fried potatoes."  While this was not my entree, I will share that the hollandaise sauce seem a bit "baked" onto the plate (sitting under a heat lamp) and the potatoes wedges looked like oven roasted potatoes.  

Were my expectations too high based on Yelp reviews?  It is easy to see why the neighboring restaurant was much busier than Devon Seafood Grill.
Super clean and modern coffee shop. Great beans. Looks like they also have a wellness shop that's posted inside that has a number of different CBD oils and products.

The staff is very friendly, and they take level up. But the food is mediocre at best, and the options are very limited. Don't come here if you are looking for variety and something healthy; I don't think I saw a vegetable there. On the other hand, if you want a tasty cookie or treat and don't mind eating standing up, you might find something you like. At a minimum you will be greeted by very pleasant workers.

I think UPenn Vets are the best ever!! We had our 5 year old Dachshund there for an emergency visit due to him having IVDD(Invertebre Disc Disease)a back problem very common in Doxies.After trying to solve the problen with our regular vets through meds which only worked for a week(no fault of my regular vets) we were told to go to UPENN by a friend who went there for her Doxie.You could'nt imagine the fear I had when our Oscar went down on his back legs and we were 2 hours away from help.
I must say that Dr. Cross and his staff were excellent..not only was the surgery a success but they called every day for almost a week to see how he was and I must have called a half dozen times on Dr. Cross's cell phone..always patient with me and my overzealous fears!
I can not say that the fees are any more expensive than any other emergency vets I have encountered and personally you can not put a pricetag on your pets health..we wouldn't do it for our human family so why would pur pets who are art of the family be any different!Our cost was around $4,000 and I would do it again if need be..Oscar does have a 98% success rate..YEAH!!!
I wish I could take them with me when we eventually move to NC!
Our Oscar is running around and doing GREAT..he is better than new just as they said he would be and we are very very GRATEFUL!

This macys is the worst went in to try to pay my bill and all the registers was crowded with people the line was moving slow I had to walk all the way in the middle of a store to pay my bill. And their was one sale associate that was getting ready to do  something else and I ask her can  I pay my bill and she said yes but their is no coverage in Mary's . One person to a a register or none at all you had to find someone and the place is junky is this supposed to be upscale Macys is the pits.

This place is splendid. 

I have been here twice and both times I ordered the turista as it's a great value and then you get to try many dishes. While you will not find chain restaurant portions, you will not need that due to the number of courses you receive. However, each course is a good size still allowing one to share with tablemates. Everything I have had here is really flavorful, so just order what sounds good. I even enjoyed the desserts (I typically stick to savory items) and could not decide which one was my favorite. I would recommend this restaurant to anyone who wants an authentic and delicious dinner in a cozy atmosphere (yes the tables are close). 

The servers are enthusiastic, well-informed and the kitchen is accommodating. However, making a reservation can be a huge pain as you are often forced to leave a message and then wait for them to call you back. Why oh why can't they just join open table???!!! So anyway, my difficulty making two reservations accounts for the four stars as the food really is top notch.

The people at AJ's are super professional and truly understand the needs of our city row homes . I just had a delivery of a Speed Queen washer and the gentlemen (Darren, and I didn't catch the other guy's name) were EARLY, polite and quick.... Such a pleasure doing business with them, next a microwave.

Perhaps I just ordered wrong but the food was just kind of . . . mediocre? My server was adamant that we try the whipped cheese with honey which was alright but one-note, and the rest of our order with the exception of the gambas al ajillo was a bit bland. I would also recommend passing on the olive oil cake - definitely unpleasantly greasy.

I can't believe what I'm hearing here. These people are fabulous. I see them everyday. I wait in line and witness their goodness to each customer. I'm not an overly sensitive guy, so I don't interpret every move. To me the coffee is good, the food is good, the service is good and the employees are good, so 5 stars baby.
The food and service was incredible! Never been to a restaurant with such high standards to their service. Definitely will be going back in the future.
It's not the food its fine we'll good , it's literally the service  SUCKS like a Capri sun straw !

I had been alternating between plated/blue apron for the last year and a half. I'd recently run out of the time, energy or desire to make those meals, as "convenient" as they may be, when I came across Nonopreps and decided to stop skipping weeks (which I often forgot to do and ended up wasting food and money) and cut the cord altogether in favor of a real human, local chef doing this awesome thing. Its the same price point without the expectation that I'm going to mince my own garlic and chop my own herbs! 

I'm on my 3rd week and everything has been great. I ordered from the specials menu on my first week and now Noel just shows up on Monday with whatever he's chosen for me. The ultra customizable option is probably great for most but spare time and brain function rarely cross paths for me. As someone who would rather starve than make a decision about food, I'm so appreciative of our arrangement and how accommodating he's been. Every time I see the meals he's picked for me, it's like he's helping me to be a normal adult human for 2, allowing me to have a cheese covered or pasta heavy comfort meal for the 3rd. Maybe that's not the line of thinking, but I tell myself it is and I really enjoy it. It's incredibly easy and everything tastes good. 

Honorable mentions: Florentine lasagna, chicken enchiladas and I think the little extras in my bag last week are called the energy bites? If so, order like 30 of them.

Thanks a million times over, Noel! I'll continue to support this endeavor of yours for as long as humanly possible!

With all the hype of this place, I was not impressed by their sandwiches.  In my opinion, the price is slightly too high and the quality and taste of their brisket or roast beef is average.  

With all the other options in Reading Terminal, I'd rather recommend Hershel's for meats or a conservative cheesesteak by Spataro's.  

Lines are long but do move fairly quickly if you are still deciding to try it.
I love this place.  Margheritas are awesome.  Food is great!.  Love the Chille Rellenos!  Boyfriend loved the tacos.  I haven't had anything bad here.

Had Lil Pop Shop at a food festival and loved it.  I came to the new location opening weekend and bought 10 pops to bring for father's day.  Everyone loved the flavors.  Im a new repeat customer! I especially love the coconut hibiscus!!

I'll just get right down to the nitty-gritty!

Go to McNally's Tavern.
Order the Schmitter sandwich.
Enjoy the badass sandwich.
Check it off your sandwiches bucket-list. 

Yes, the Schmitter is made with traditional sliced, grilled steak, and melted cheese.
However, it's not a cheesesteak. 
The steak is served on a toasted Kaiser roll, not a hoagie, with fried onions, tomato, and grilled salami (which I opted not to have) and topped with the Schmitter sauce, that what makes it the ultimate perfect sandwich.

Four Yelps.
All of these reviews speak for themselves! Wonderful staff, and amazingly responsive. Loved the detailed discharge notes that I received via email. The best place to take your pet

I had a terrible experience with Inna.  I had high hopes because she came highly recommended from a family friend.  I came for a hair and makeup trial for my wedding.  She did not care to understand what i wanted and simply did what she liked.  Overall, a huge waste of money and time which i regret.  She charged $95 for an updo that she spent 30 mins doing.  I have naturally very curly hair and she did not smooth one strand.  Instead, she brushed out my curls and i was a frizzy poofy mess.  She claimed that was the most popular look in the magazines right now.  She told me that everyone is going to think my designer wedding gown is fake because i want a typical boring hairstyle.  She also told me that she doesnt mind if i write a negative review about her because she is so renowned in the beauty industry.  Oh well Inna, i gave you a fair shot.
Turkey corn dog! Yes please. This is so exciting to me as someone who only eats poultry.  The corn dog was made right in front of me and was hot a delicious.  Save room if you are at the market

Soooo, falls taproom is okay.  The reviews make it seem amazing, and, for East Falls, I suppose it is.  I think it used to be better with its old menu that had more options.  While I understand when super awesome restaurants cutdown their menu as their ingredients are much higher quality, a place like this needs more than a few burgers, sandwiches, and flatbreads.

Their beer menu in true hipster fashion written in chalk on the wall.  The price for the beers are not great, nor outrageous.  They are priced where you feel like you should order one, but if its not amazing, you'll miss those extra dollars. 

As for the food, I've had a few things here that are all, decent.  The bison chili nachos are, meh.  First, while making your own tortilla chips is a nice touch, do them right, these were too oily and thick.  Sometimes, exotic names make dishes seem like they should be more impressive.  Adding "Bison" makes this seem like an impressive dish, but since most people know bison, in ground form, differs little from beef save for calories, you end up with...chili nachos.  It tastes like, mediocre chili on mediocre chips.  Not great.  Nachos are one thing we shouldn't try to make trendy.  I asked for salsa, they didnt have it, but they did have cilantro-lime crema....the next obvious choice.

The burgers are good, but, just good.  They have exotic spreads like red onion jam, and olive tapenade, but the meat was just okay.  I will say the fried pickles are pretty delish.  Nonetheless, the falls isn't brimming with amazing culinary options, and a place that offers good beer and good food is a place that I will be coming to again.

UPDATE 9/'13
Had to re-review with a necessary 1 star upgrade.  They rolled out a new menu, again, this time keeping most of the go to dishes and adding some nice mainstays that were former specials.  They upgraded the quality as well, or maybe I previously just went on and off night.  I had the bison nachos again and this time they were significantly improved from last time.  Meatier, cheesier, hotter, just overall much better.  The wings were huge and delicious.  

Seems like taproom is coming along quite nicely.

This place was horrible. From the bread being cold and stale to the water glasses never being refilled. The calamari was rubbery and the meal in general was not very good. Let's not even talk about the service....it was non existent...The best part of the meal was leaving.

In my continued quest for the perfect Al Pastor taco my search today led me to Mexico Lindo. I first have to say that I lucked up and got a parking spot right in front of the place because trust me parking around this area is tight and there are tow signs literally every where so know before you go.

The restaurant is very small. You can eat in but there are only five or six tables. The gentleman who took my order was very nice.

On to the tacos. I was hoping for greatness but I just got okay. Like with Chiquita's Pizzeria and Mexican Grill, Mexico Lindo also puts sauteed onions in with the pork. I simply don't understand why you need cooked onions when you are going to garnish it with raw onions. Maybe it's a Mexican South Philly thing. In any event I took them out. There was plenty of pineapple included which I loved. There was no grilled jalapeno or radish on the side and the limes had definitely seen better days. The salsas were thin and watery but the green one had good flavor. The taste of the Pastor was acceptable but clearly my search must go on. Other than that, I would try other menu items.

This place is so cute and good for any occasion - studying, catching up with friends, bringing your kids, or just enjoying some drinks, pie, or popsicles. I love this place for their popsicles (matcha mochi, blueberry buttermilk, sweet corn) and their hours. It's perfect for studying. I love sitting in the back corner where you can still people watch but it still feels quite secluded. The bench is really comfortable and there are outlets. It's also a good balance between being quiet but not uncomfortably silent. My only complaint is that their pie slices are quite small :c 

Start a punch card and get a free popsicle after buying ten!

I do miss Vietnamese street food. I mean authentic food like stir fried snails and grilled prawns. I was pleasantly surprised that this place offered such dishes. I usually have to go to D.C to get this kind of food. The restaurant was very spacious and nicely decorated, nothing fancy but clean and comfortable, there's even a mini stage for karaoke I think.

The place seem to specialize in hot pot and grill, although they have many other items on the menu. We chose a variety of dishes to sample in the restaurant. We had their breakfast special on a sizzling plate that came with beef, pate, egg, fries and a side of meat ball. The fries were very good, crunchy and fresh, the beef was a bit tougher than I have expected. The meatball was very tender, although it does lean on the sweeter side, but I like sweet so that was good. I didn't expect to be charged for the bread however since this dish usually came with bread.

Next we had tamarind crabs, baked clams with scallion oil and garlic stir fried escargot. The tamarind crabs sauce was out of this world, you can put this on any seafood and it would blow your mind. The crabs themselves were small however and had very little meat, they were also overcooked. The clams were on point. The scallion oil topped off the meaty, juicy clams and with a bit of peanut on top for crunch, it was one of my favorites. The escargot was ok, while it was tender and they gave a lot, I think the seasoning could be more liberal.

The two dishes I had the most problems with was the crispy chicken with fried rice and their Vietnamese pizza. The chicken was not crispy at all and tasted lukewarm and there was no flavors in it. The pizza was not crispy enough and all I've tasted was Sriracha. We ended the meal with their purple yam sticky rice dessert, which was pretty amazing. The sticky rice wasn't too sweet and it had tons of flavor.

Overall, it was hits and misses. I still wanted to give this restaurant a higher rating because I believe they have a lot of potential. Personally I will definitely come back because I know exactly what and what not to order now. Also, the closest thing to this is 4 hours away so they deserve some credit for being one of the only restaurants to serve up these kind of dishes. 

If you are going to visit, try their hot pot and really stick to their street food. Their rice platters and traditional dishes may disappoint. Another bonus is that service was excellent and very accommodating. I do have to say I did have a good time here.

Amazing food! The ambiance and overall aesthetic is EVERYTHING.
Drinks were oooook. Portions are less than desirable. 
But overall, very good. Loved the food!!! Our waiter was MIA too. Kinda weird. But oh well.

As a woman I am nervous when getting my car fixed because I do not want to be taken advantage of. I felt like with this experience I was. 

The gentleman who helped me with all my questions and giving me my invoice details was great over the phone and took his time we revised my invoice a few times with what i finally decided on having done to my car. I felt taken care of and was excited that everything seemed to be so easy! 

When it came time to pick my car up I arrived 3 hours after they called me. As I was about to pay for the service that was done (and it wasn't a small bill either) someone from the service center (my car tech) came to me at the checkout counter to tell me that even though they fixed my problem/car it doesn't work (there is not another problem). How shocking is that! There was an issue that I came in for them to fix and now there is another issue that needs to be fixed. I was shocked that when they called me to tell me my car was ready that they didn't mention it and it seemed like they were about to let me go home after paying for something that was not working. 

The tech came to my car to show me what was wrong (my mistake for not testing it myself before leaving the lot) and asked me if I wanted it to be "unplugged" so that my car horn would not go off while I am driving down the road. So I said yes, and i would have someone else look at it (why i would need to do that when i am at a service center is crazy... but clearly my car was not fixed). I also had the nozzle to my windshield wiper fluid fixed (yes i paid for that) and BEFORE I drove off the lot I decided to test it out. Oh guess what - it was NEVER fixed... so I walked back into the store - told them about it and the same tech came back outside to look at my car, test it and then drove my car back to the shop to "check it out". About 10 min later my car is out front again and BOTH of my nozzles have been replaced. To make matters worse when i got home and plugged in the part to have my horn work again the horn DID NOT work. I had someone check it out and the horn had no power to it - so did i buy a broken horn or did the new horn break and they didn't even bother to replace or fix it and that is why the tech was so quick to ask if i wanted the fuse unplugged? So many questions! 
So - I went right back to the store the next day at 7:30am with a MAN who is more familiar with cars and everything seemed to be handled and he put the horn in for me as he told me not to have this location touch my car again. 

It is just so sad to think that I paid all of that money to have my car fixed yet it wasnt... is that because I am a woman and they tried to get one over on me or is it because the staff is lazy or because i am unfamiliar with cars... again, so many questions!

I do have to point out that when I returned the next day the Service Center Manager assisted us and he was very nice and helpful and didn't question anything that i told him he just took care of it (makes me think that these things tend to happen often?).

Side Note - I was at he store the day before to purchase a lugnut for my tire (I didnt even know it was called a lugnut) and I tried to explain it to the woman who was at the counter and she blankly stared at me. She told it it was a lugnut and and pulled it up on the screen - then asked me which one I wanted. Best part - she was ON THE PHONE (a personal phone call) while checking me out. How UNPROFESSIONAL! There was also someone who could clearly be seen eating pizza behind the counter and another woman walked up and loudly asked the employees what type of soda they wanted. Maybe that should have been my first indication that the employees at this place do not care? What about Management - how could they let that happen. She was way too comfortable with that white iphone speaker headphone hanging from her ear that it must be something she does constantly. Cool Pep Boys - you are paying your employees to talk on their phones?

Also, another side note... the hours on your website for the location that I went to say that the service center opens up at 7:00AM. I arrived to the location at 6:50am so that I could hopefully be the first person there. The store hours posted in the door window say open at 8:00am. Then there was a printed out sheet up paper also posted on the window that stated new hours are 7:30am. When I brought this up when I picked up my car and when I spoke to the sales rep on the phone throughout the day they mentioned that the times have been incorrect for a while. They did apologize but that doesn't help the fact that there are 3 different times posted for this location. Oh and there was someone else who also arrived at 7:00am to purchase a new battery and when i told him the store doesn't open until 8:00am he told me he checked the website and couldn't wait so he left. Your store LOST a sale because of the incorrect hours posted - how many other times does that happen every day?

It's an old building in a fantastic location , (broad and locust st). For all of you whining millennials who expect instant gratification, complain to your unit owners or tell your parents to move you to another building which will undoubtably be much more expensive. As to the management, Jamie and her staff are professional and attentive. I was there last week and was very impressed with how clean the halls are as well as the beauty of the grand lobby.

I'm living for the last six months in the Arts Condominium and spent Christmas and New Year's here. It was a pleasure, quiet and clean. Jamie our on site property management is amazing and had everything ready for the holidays. Everything is convenient to the Arts Condominium. I had a last minute party on new year's day. Nomads catered it and other essential were bought at a clean grocery store located at Watts and Spruce. Liquor store is one block away as is Schelingers deli. 
Management keeps the Arts Condominium spotless 
and very clean smelling. 
It great living here.

omg...if you like cupcakes, you must pay this place a visit
the cupcakes they have here are absolutely divine
their cupcakes have a few different cake varieties: pound cake, red velvet, buttermilk, regular cake
all of which have different icing combinations to make for the most delicious cupcake treats that ive had...maybe ever

the only reason i dont give them 5 stars, is cupcakes is about the only single serving size treat they offer
they have cookies, but theyre not nearly as special or diverse as the cupckaes
they sell cakes too, but its all made to order stuff for special occasions

but this place is definitely worth a visit for a cupcake dessert if your in the northern liberties area

This place is a must to try but idk if I would go for dinner/brunch unless the person I'm with really wants to try it. Food was ok, service was horrible, ambience is amazing. I love just looking out the window and seeing the Philly skyline. 
Going here for HH is amazing but it gets so busy and overcrowded at times so you'll have to just stand by the bar. 
Make sure you ask about the snacks they give at the bar the wasabi peanuts are so good!

Just stopped in today to share a chicken suya and steak suya bowl to go. One with jollof rice and plantains and one with uto rice and cornbread. Both medium heat which was perfect.

Both bowls had great flavors and were very filling.
We will definitely be back again next week.

Since Wok left, this is our go-to place for Chinese carryout. We've lived in the neighborhood for over 20 years, and have tried every place within 7 or 8 blocks. My spouse always gets the Pad Thai Shrimp and eats the large serving in one evening. Square on Square caters to her low salt preferences--never a complaint. I order over the whole menu; last night was red curry (excellent, big serving).
As I said, I like to experiment, but have found no nearby Chinese restaurant even close on quality. A few extra dollars (it isn't cheap!) is a smart investment in taste. I try every new place, but keep coming back to Square on Square.

Oh my favorite place to be when I want to be drunk.
And it's true.. they have closed the doors.

RIP venture Inn
So many good times.

We decided to give this place a chance while being in Philly for the day. We arrived a few minute before 12 pm and had to wait a couple of minutes to be seated. Today was pride day in philly and this place was in the route of the parade. We order the Steak and Eggs, Chicken and Waffle Benedict's and Short Ribs, every single one of the plates was DELICIOUS!!! The comment around our table was: It was definitely worth the wait to be seated! We also had coffe, Mimosa, Orange juice and smoothie. Only one advice to the girl dealing with placing people: It won't hurt to be a little bit more pleasant.! I know it was crowded today because of pride but that girl didn't smile not even one while we waited to be seated! Will be definitely coming back if we return to philly on a weekend.

October was Lobster Month at Route 6 and all lobster dishes were 1/2 price. Route 6 has extended their lobster specials for the month of November. For November, Lobster month is advertised as almost 1/2 price. I went last night, November 1st, to check it out. Well, my cold 1/2 lobster cocktail was 9.50 (versus 16.00), my crispy fried little lobster tails were 15.00 (versus 22.00), and my 3lb wood grilled lobster was 35.00 (versus 64.00).  Each dish was perfectly prepared and delicious - overcooking a lobster is a sacriledge (I am originally from Massachusetts) and Route 6 does not overcook them. So by all means go.  If you have dining companions that do not do lobster ...no worries...plenty of other seafood choices plus chicken and filet mignon are on the menu.
Cheesesteak was greasy and bland. The turkey and cheese hoagie was decent. Service was fast and friendly.

It's been 2 years and I'm still here!! 
Morgan is the new property manager  - she is professional, pleasant, personable, positive, accommodating and follows up immediately on all requests.   
My apartment is great, the complex is quiet and neighbors are friendly!
Awesome place to live!!!!

Happy 2015! Thanks for reading review. This is my first time posting ever! Felt strongly about experience and wanted to exercise my liberties as an American. My family and I woke this morning excited about the idea of sharing the dim sum experience with our 4 kids (age range 11-13). We eat dim sum once a year so it's a big deal for us. When there was a 20 minute wait for our first choice, it was my great idea to try the new craze in Chinatown, Dim Sum Garden. I'd walked by this place several times and always saw it packed; thought to myself, "this place must be good. Can't wait to try it."

So the 7 of us go in and are immediately greeted by a grumpy, impatient waitress. We were disappointed that we wouldn't get to share with our kids the full dim sum experience but since we were already seated, proceeded to order. In summary, the food is HORRENDOUS!! What are you all eating in here that you come in hoards? Don't believe the hype! I encourage you to not try and convince yourself that this food is good. It REALLY isn't! But to each his own. We just know this place isn't getting our patronage. My new year's resolution is to not complain. I really hope to honor this and set an example for our amazing kids!!

I have gone to this place for both Brunch and dinner.

Brunch was decent, but not great. The food was decent but overall nothing to get super excited about and not the best brunch I have had in Philly. The Blood mary was decent and they made it extra spicy as I asked. 

Service was good, the staff are all really nice.

For dinner, the mushroom sandwich was very well put together and the flavors just popped.The fresh goat cheese salad was good but the goat cheese was a bit too runny for our tastes. The dressing on the salad was really good though. We got the beef and onion pasties for an appetizer and that was pretty good, with a nice layer of salt sprinkled on top which really added some good flavors.

Dinner would have ranked 4 stars, but brunch brought it down a drop to 3.

Our experience at Almaz Café was a bit disappointing considering the very good reviews it received by Yelpers.  Besides one lentil dish, the other vegetarian and chicken, meat dishes were lacking in the taste profiles I have had at better Ethiopian restaurants in west Philly and NYC.
Worst service ever. Nobody came to seat us for about 10 minutes. Waited for our food for 2 hours. Staff is not friendly. Will never eat here again

I live generally close to here. I have heard about it for a long time so one particularly hot evening my friend and I decided to check this place out. THEY HAVE BANANA ICE CREAM! That is such a rare find in this day and age and I loved it so much. The only issue is that with a cone I couldn't add chocolate chips making it my own Chunky Monkey style ice cream. But I will definitely be back because this place has a ton of flavors that need to be tasted and it's a neighborhood hot spot. Support local businesses. :)

I tried Nan Zhou tonight for the first time and I am so happy that I gave this place a try. I ordered the Beef Brisket Noodle Soup and it was amazing! It was piping hot. The broth and hand drawn noodles were great. The beef brisket was so tender that it melted in my mouth.... Ugh, it was just everything! It didn't take long for my food to come and I noticed that everyone else in the restaurant got their food in a timely manner as well. You are given water when you are seated and the servers keep the glasses filled when they see that you're running low. The food portions are very generous. I desperately wanted to finish my bowl but I just couldn't lol. They take cash only but the food isn't that expensive anyway. The service was fast and the staff was nice. I will definitely go back and try something else on the menu.

How can you beat a BYOB nail salon that gives killer massages? I'll tell you... YOU CAN'T. The staff is so sweet and wonderful and the massages were incredible. Normally, I feel like the massage component only lasts a few minutes and they barely use any pressure at all. I went with a friend who raved about them and she wasn't kidding! I was so relaxed and the staff was incredibly attentive. I think I've found my new nail salon!

United has a great atmosphere and pretty great pastries and coffee. This location is always bustling with students and don't expect to find a seat, but it's great for a quick stop. The hot chocolate is delightfully crafted with rich cocoa, although it varies a lot between baristas. Try it out and grab a bagel too- if they're not sold out!
Intimate, trendy, cozy, little place. Go here for a cocktail or beer. It's a nice addition to the neighborhood.

My experience was pretty consistent with the reviews so far. The atmosphere and meals were just okay. Not terrible by any means, but definitely not something that would would keep me coming back.

Overall, we felt that the menu was pretty limited and the service was not very good.  We never received consistent attention from our waiter despite the fact that it wasn't really crowded in the restaurant.  If you want a cheap BYOB for a date night or to have a casual dinner with friends, this is perfect - but if you want a nice dinner that is going to wow you with a really good flavorful meal and terrific service, this probably isn't the place.

This is a little neighborhood secret (but they are always busy). The pizza is delicious, like pizza-snob kind of pizza. My favorite is the white pizza. The warm cauliflower salad is amazing as well. The salad is so tasty that it's actually a treat to have. And I'm a girl who likes fatty food.

I'm a reasonable person.  I realize that on a rainy Sunday afternoon the delivery time for a place like this will be longer than usual, but how is it that THEY do not grasp this concept?  First, given they knew it was exceptionally busy, they probably should have mentioned that it may take longer to receive my food than usual when I placed my order.  Second, when I call after an hour and a half of waiting they should not respond "You're food is done, it's all ready, we're just waiting for the delivery guy to come back.".  Uhmmm...so my food (pizza, salad, wings) has been cooked and sitting around under a heat lamp or something for over an hour?  Yeah, that really makes me want to wait for your delivery guy to come back, pick it up, and then bring it all the way to me? Thanks, but I'll pass.  Next, HIRE A COMPETENT DELIVERY PERSON! If you are directionally challenged, you should not go into the delivery business- my address is not even close to complicated yet he needed to call me for directions 3 times (it's a city, kid, it's on a grid...).  Overall the food itself was okay.   I've had pizza here before and have liked it, this time the food was cold...so it was difficult to really give it a thumbs up, but in the past I've enjoyed it.  Caesar salad is pretty good (lots of parm cheese, just the way I like it), Greek salad is good (even had olives, eggs, and stuffed grape leaves!), pizzas are pretty good (and very reasonably priced), wings are actually spicy (fire wings are not for "yeah, I like wings" people- crazy hot!).  So my suggestion is not to abandon this place permanently, just skip the delivery and eat in.

Decent pizza - the sausage is particularly tasty and not too heavy.  This is where the local Planet Fitness obtains their pizzas for their infamous Pizza Mondays and it's always a tasty treat.  But to be honest, I need to get my priorities straight...

Now that's what I'm talking about. A cheesesteak worth putting Philly on the map for. Thank God for this restaurant across the street from the stand, too. I walked over to Tony Luke's on a sweltering day in the summer and found a ridiculous line at the original stand, and was pleasantly surprised to find a quick seat for my group inside the restaurant (even less of a wait than the line I think). 

The special cheesesteak with broccoli rabe and sharp provolone is quite good, but a little too sharp for my tastes. Their traditional cheesesteaks are perfectly done - thin ribbons of deliciously greasy and juicy ribeye steak. Make sure to add a basket of cheese fries to really stop your heart.

I had eaten at the PYT at the Piazza one other time and thouhgt it was pretty good, but after my last visit, I'd have to say that PYT is inconsistent at best.  My friends and I wandered over to PYT on a Sunday evening and it happened to be a launch party for a vanilla liquor named Tuaca.  We were offered free shots, gave it go, and were not impressed.  We were also not impressed by the celebrity guest....some dude form "LA Ink" who nobody gave a damn about. Maybe it was the glow of the celebrity presence that distracted the kitchen staff that night? I ordered the cheese burger soup, fantasizing about a think and delicious piping hot cheese soup on a cool autumn evening. Instead, I was presented with a tepid bowl of cheese-flavored water with very strong overtones of ketchup and chunks of burger, which were as dry as the sahara despite being in a bowl of water!  It was filthy.  I also ordered the special that night: the lobster burger. Boasting the truffle drizzle over fresh lobster meat, the burger sounded mouth-watering, but left me disappointed yet again. The lobster burger tasted more like a Lobster Fart! And not the kind of Lobster Fart where you eat lobster and then have some tummy issues, but the kind of Lobster Fart in which a crustacean that has been dragging its ass all over the ocean depths eating the excriment of other species farts in your mouth. THAT is the lobster burger at PTY.  No more for me folks. I've had enough, but if you're the type who thinks you've tasted everything & you're looking for an unplesant adventure, try the lobster burger.

Went here for my sisters birthday dinner and it was a good experience. Its in a good location to bar hop around old city after dinner. On to the food, the lobster Mac and cheese is AMAZING! The calamari is really good too. Not to mention the martinis my favorite was the twizzle which is I think is bacardi, strawberry juice and lemon...it's delicious! They are a bit pricey at $9 for two servings in a small martini glass but totally worth it. And for the birthday girl we got a complimentary brownie sundae.

Eastern State for the day tour is amazing. The experience is really cool and informative and I would recommended that to everyone. 

The Halloween Nights was not great and I don't think I would recommend for the price. They advertise 5 scary attractions but there were really only 2 so once we finished those there wasn't really much for us to do.  Being in the penitentiary at night is definitely a cool experience but everything else felt like it was lacking. Parking at the zoo and taking the shuttle was convenient. There were long lines everywhere but they all moved surprisingly quick.

I was beyond impressed with their customer service. I ordered several items for Mother's Day and some of them did not arrive. I let Di Bruno know what'd happened via email and received a response and solution within an hour or two. The missing items were delivered the very next day and everything was still great. Would order from them again.

Cool little 3rd generation looking italian shop that I've never noticed an hve been in the Bayou (right next door) many times. lol I got a hot soppresata and capicola hoagie. DELICIOUS! My gf got the cunam panini. The quality and homestyle panini bread was great...i'm just not a big fan of cuban's. ;) Check this place out if you want high quality normal prices!

Dim Sum Garden is my go-to restaurant in Philly when I crave xiao long bao and can make the trip to Chinatown. I've been to Dim Sum Garden on three different occasions and have never paid more than $15 with tax and tip for what I order. Hell, the first time I went here, my friend and I spent about $15 for the two of us on three dishes that satisfied our hunger adequately. While the price is affordable and the foods are mostly delicious, the service is typical of authentic Chinese restaurant: abrupt and rushed, which can be interpreted as unfriendly or even rude, but that's just the culture. Therefore, I don't dismiss Dim Sum Garden as a bad restaurant simply due to the service.

After trying different appetizers, dim sum, and entree at Dim Sum Garden, I have come to the conclusion that I won't order entree there again. It's a hit-or-miss for me, and I'd rather pay for what I know will be delicious than gamble my money on something that may not be my taste. I highly recommend the Shanghai steamed pork soup dumplings (xiao long bao) and "triple steamed buns" (三鲜大包). I've tried the pan fried version of the xiao long bao, but I think the steamed ones are better. They're good without any condiments but even better with the dumpling sauce on the table. The triple steamed buns are filled with chives, egg, and glass noodle if I recall correctly. I tried the vegetable steamed buns tonight, but they were not as good as the triple steamed buns. Scallion pancakes and chicken skewers are also good although I'm not sure if chicken skewers can be considered authentic Chinese food. The chicken skewers come with peanut sauce if I remember correctly, reminiscent of the Thai/Indonesian "satay."

For beverages, I had Thai milk tea tonight that was pretty good. Can't wait to go back so I can try the fried cheese wonton (!) and xue mei niang because their pictures look really good on the menu. Although I take the pictures with a grain of salt now because my pan fried xiao long bao don't look anything like the picture... The flavors are most important, though, and when you're paying $10-15 for a decent meal I guess you shouldn't expect aesthetics to be the restaurant's main concern.

Well folks, I'm sticking to my guns!  I meant every word of my first review & to the "gentleman" who was so Hell bent on thinking my boyfriend & I were "Del Frisco's marketing insiders" doing acts of restaurant review sabotage because of our positive comments... I laugh.  Te.  he.  he.  

Sir, it's called a degree, lots of kids & hep cats out here these days are getting them.  Don't be afraid of the education bug... embrace it.  If you need to start small, that's fine.  (I'm not here to judge you, only the businesses.)  I suggest Scrabble or old episodes of Reading Rainbow as a jumping point.  (Beware though, that theme song is catchy as all get out!)

Don't forget folks, sometimes if you give off snooty & ignorant vibes others will reflect the same energy.  I've gotten plenty of upgrades, free stuff, & awesome service just because I was nice!  (A simple & forgotten concept, but that's why people are so appreciative when they are in the presence of someone who is indeed a nice person.)  Get your aura right & give it another go or stay at home, learn to cook (or have your chef teach you how), & quit whining!  

Vaya con dios!

I work the night shift at my hospital so I like to wake up early on days when I don't have to work that night. After stumbling around my apartment for about an hour trying to figure where I am and what the heck is going on, I like to jet right to the Chestnut Hill Coffee Shop for a massive cup of caffeine. After reading a lot of reviews for Infusion I decided to give them a try since it is also relatively close to my place. So, off I went exuding desperation for a good, strong cup of coffee. 
Unfortunately, I left Infusion feeling very unsatisfied in many ways. First, apparently I went on the first day that it was under new ownership (4/1). This surprised me because my first thought was that the place could really use some sprucing up. The furniture was way too worn even for a cozy coffee shop and I remember thinking that I didn't even want to sit in the chairs that were available. Second, after seeing a picture on Yelp of what looked to be tons of pastries I was excited to try something however pickings were slim and what was there didn't look overly appealing. Third, the coffee was just ok. I didn't really like that it was in those self serve containers and my first cup of the lighter brew tasted very watered down. My second cup of the darker brew wasn't much better and even tasted a little burnt. 
I definitely wasn't impressed and will only go back to see if the new owners make any changes.

I've been to a dozen or so Ikea locations up and down the east coast. One thing is for sure, one is not better than the rest.  They all offer the same stock, are about the same size, and have the same set up - displays upstairs, inventory downstairs.  In the 20 + years I've been a customer it's been a love/hate affair.

LOVE the displays providing room design ideas, the multiple ways they use the same pieces, and the fact that you can see and feel how each piece functions.  Love the options and choices.  Love the prices and abundance of inventory, the way things are packed to make transporting home in a car easy.  Love that they also have Swedish food to eat there or take home prepackaged.

HATE the return process (if you can even get them to take something back), the hunt for the merchandise on your list, the complex assembly with limited instructions.  Hate the poor quality and the fact that inventory and parts change often and chances are if something breaks you can't get replacement parts.  Hate the long lines that are present at every location, the lack of help loading.  Hate that you have to buy their bag if you want your items bagged.

One thing I have discovered is even if you scour the catalog for days on end, make a list and check it twice once you get to the store all will change.  You'll see a piece in a display different from the catalog and fall in love, or the opposite - you'll see the piece you came for and the display isn't how you envisioned.  Either way sometimes too many choices equal too much frustration.

This coming from a woman that once packed up my car, measured the space remaining, and went back and purchased more.  That drove home two hours down the turnpike smack dab against the windshield because the driver's seat held not only myself but a box.  Or that I once loaded my car so full I couldn't even see my children.  Yes, all this has happened and happens all the time.  If you want a good comical reality experience just sit in the parking lot and watch people load for a few hours....

Overall as I aged so did my style.  While the catalog and displays are nice, rarely if ever does that same piece look like that in my own home.  My daughter's bedroom was entirely Ikea and in less than a year we had to do multiple repairs on our own.  Drawer bottoms are week, side rails are plastic, beds and dressers are no more than particleboard wrapped in a laminate skin.  Wheels on trundle beds are undersized and the design faulty.  What works for the Swedes unfortunately doesn't work for American children.

As a middle aged woman I now come for the inspiration.  For ideas that I can take home and rework with more structurally sound pieces.  I come for the accessories, the candles in bulk, and the furniture pieces that are inexpensive and will only get occasional use.  I come for the unique - things that can only be found at Ikea.  For a day browsing with girlfriends or killing time in a vacation location.

So this love  affair for the most part is over, or maybe just categorized to friends with benefits.

I used to come here often like a couple times a week to get my weekly dose of bbt. But the last couple of times that I came here the past year or so, my orders were always made wrong or the drinks tasted different from how it used to. My friends that go here also experienced the same things; orders made wrong. I've heard from at least 4 people that I know complain about how this place mess up orders frequently. One of them ordered a milk green tea with red bean toppings and they received pearl milk tea with red beans (its a combination of tapioca AND red bean toppings). I once ordered a honey lemon gt and the honey was missing or lack thereof so it was literally the sour lemon taste. Keep in mind we go to mr.wish oftennn so we know what our favorite drinks tasted like. Maybe it's new staffing issue since the people we used to recognize isn't there anymore. But we are pretty disappointed in the drinks from here.

What a horrible experince! We were seated outside on what looked like an ironing board and little stools you might see at the Ikea childrens section. The food was unrecognizable. It you go there to find Banh Mi and Bottles quality, know that they seemed to change owners or something is going very wrong in the kitchen. Maybe it was Covid? The magic is gone.
I usually only buy the tortilla chips, but the regular tortillas, are so good, you can eat them plain.
The food was so so and quite expensive. flavor-wise, it is not the most authentic Mexican restaurant in town. It's good for a casual date or drinks with friends more than a real culinary experience

walked in for a state inspection in the afternoon and they took me right away. jim, who handled my inspection, was amazing and took care of everything. he even changed my plates for me. great experience, and will be back to see jim for an oil change.

Beer: great selection and very knowledgable staff. Bf ordered a beer that ended up being served at room temperature because it was in the cellar (???). Beer should be cold and ready to be served, no excuses. 

Frites: amazing!! Served with a delicious mayo. Yum!

Frog legs: served in hot sauce, chicken wing style. The hot sauce overwhelmed the meat. Would've been better off just ordering wings. 

Monks mussels: these were the reason we tried this restaurant in the first place. Monks has a reputation as the best mussels in town and these did not live up to that reputation. The mussels themselves were a good size and not fishy, which is a great place to start. But everyone knows its all about the broth. This broth tasted like a combination of watered down wine and old garlic with some herbs thrown in. It was tasteless and a huge disappointment. Don't waste your time. 

The three stars come from the beer selection, frites, staff, atmosphere, and frites. It's all about the frites!!

Unfortunately we will likely not be returning.
Rapid repair of cracked screen while you wait best price in town. Easy on street parking available I would definitely recommend this shop.

***** Cash Only*****

First of the bat, wasn't aware its a cash only business (there's a bank few feet away with an ATM, will charge $2 Fee). 

Wife and i split 2 sandwiches, the ever popular Paesano and Daddy Wad. 
Both sandwiches very phenomenal, but if i had to choose, i would definitely go with The Paesano with the must have sandwich at this place. The way the brisket, fried egg, roasted tomatoes, pepperoncino, sharp provolone and their horseradish/mayo concoction are married to that sesame roll is like a symphony. 
From the first bite you'll know you are hooked. Everything is fused together and plays very well. As with the Daddy Wad sandwich, you can taste the love in every bite. We also had Potato Arrosto, i can't really leave a review on them as the portion was way too small,  from what we had, it was good, but definitely felt robbed. 

So, why the 4 stars and not 5? Few things, first,place looks like a shack in a half way home. When i say there's limited space to eat, i mean there are probably like 6 chairs around the counter where you can sit. Thats it. And the major challenge is devouring this sandwich before it gets way too soggy to enjoy.... If you take your time eating the sandwiches, your last two to three bites will be a watery-bread-meat-cereal mess. So, i can not see how people would order take out, unless you ask for it sahara dry and liquids on the side. And as i mentioned earlier, the potato side was definitely fit for 2-3 teaser small bites. 

Otherwise, this place is a gem. Its what Philly is all about, tough/rough/hard exterior with a loving, mouth watering, soul good feeling, interior.

I just walked out of Cycles PHL with my bike and it feels like new after a tune-up. My bike was ready in less than a day, exactly as promised when I dropped it at the shop. They even cleaned it. 

Good price. Great work. The team is friendly and fun. I highly recommend this shop.

I was recently stranded in Philly due to the weather and of course ended up getting sick as well.   I found this place through yelp and was so pleased with the staff and the facilities. Everyone was so nice and helpful, including giving me directions and just generally taking care of me. Then when I got home, the doctor called to check up on me. Thank you!!

I've had pizza form here about 4 times in about a year and I must say its one of, if not the best, pizza joints in Philly. I mean come on ! Chicken parm pizza !? Are you f'n kidding me ? It's 2 of my favorites in one bite. The chicken is actually fresh and hand breaded ... Not that disgusting process chicken finger garbage. 
I highly recommend the follow : BBQ chicken pizza, pepperoni or as mentioned the chicken parm. I wouldn't steer you wrong.
I've never had the chance to eat-in but this place seems like it would be a cool place to hang out enjoy some grub and catch a flyers game.
To go is fast and never had any mix ups, I'm usually in and out in 5 minutes.

Luigi's now takes the crown over Mix (near 22nd and chestnut)

This restaurants ad came floating across an app on my phone and it offered discounts so why not try it out. It also had a fairly decent review on yelp which kind of solidified a reason to go. I was going to a movie at Center City Philadelphia, this particular restaurant was within 10 blocks so I gave it a try on a Monday. I'm glad I tried it. Because of the rain I was able to easily find a safe parking space within a one block. I mentioned Monday because they have a great special of, half of pizzas on Monday all day. There are so many choices that it took a little time to pick one that was just right. I had to make a quick choice, several pizzas flew past me that belong to others and my mind was changing rapidly. In the end I was pleased with my choice of pepperoni with sausage, it was delicious. As for the restaurant itself, the bar/restaurant had a nice  crisp and clean wooden decor.

My concern with this restaurant is the service not that it was bad it was just inconsistent and slightly dismissive. It lacked fluidity. When I arrived three people were standing at the host stand without really acknowledging me. It took a minute and I was given the 'will be right with you' by someone who was basically passing through. But I was quickly seated. However the bar staff doubled as the server.  My server was friendly but in between bringing me my beer and placing my order she was doing a lot of filling bottles and other task instead of taking my order or just bringing me my beer. When I ordered my beer she went back to the bar and stacked glasses etc. I was one of six people in the restaurant so they weren't crowded enough for any type of delays and service! But I will try it again on a Monday. I love pizza and it was delicious. The pizza had a nice thin crust without being too crispy. a great flavored sauce without being sweet, plenty of meat and cheese for half the price I'm in!

Packed, I'm giving it 4 stars because the patrons KEEP coming back.  Luckily I went before 11 so that helped.  By 12, expect a wait.  At the same time, you know what that means when you have to wait esp for a HUUUUGE restaurant such as this.  This is worth a try on a weekend if you can make it early OR if you can wait. 

The dim sum dishes were all pretty standard, some were better than the others.. but if you want the philly dimsum experience, I believe this is the most popular joint in that part of town.  So go for it!  Have some tea and dimsum.  What I noticed was that the carts were being refilled constantly... at which I immediately ask for the ones fresh out of the kitchen, still steaming hot.. . usually the ladies will give those to you first thing.. but just in case they don't, you can request for it with a point of a finger  (but please don't make a scene.. i mean if they don't give it to you, dont get upset now. haha it's not the end of the world.)

Also, the dimsum approach is sort of a distraction from a heavy Brunch with pancakes/waffles/french toast...   this was my way of just getting something different just for once.  YUUUPPP!!

Inept and rude service the first time I went.  Gave them the benefit of the doubt  and went back a second time...different person was not rude.  Third time I went, there was yet a different person who was also inept and rude.  Apparently the staff at rocket cat do not like it when they end up having to stop socializing with one another in order to take a customer's order.

These asshats are not the only coffee and locally produced edibles purveyor in the area, so I am instead taking my money to Reanimator as well as Steap & Grind.
Butcher Update: the best Italian sausage I've ever had.

If you look at the pattern of reviews they seem to decline the longer the restaurant is open. I hypothesize that the chef was probably present around the time of opening and then faded away to open a lame, casino style steakhouse at the Valley Forge casino? Google.
