Oysters casino were fine. The $80 Bistecca for 2 was just OK. Under seasoned and didn't have the flavor of the wood fired grill they have on site. Came with bland fingerling potatoes. Wine list is way overpriced as well.

Then there was the $9 side of Brussels sprouts with balsamic and sage. No discernible balsamic or sage. Just greasy, deep fried, almost burnt sprout leaves on a plate. $9? Yeah. They were terrible. 

The saving grace that warranted 2 stars was the bar/ bartender. Totally overpriced drinks ($13-14?? $10-12 is way more reasonable) but he doesn't set the prices. But. He was really friendly, made an off-the-cuff cocktail for me and dominated the menu cocktails for the wife. 

Pretentious dining blows. Wanna be pretentious dining is a million times worse. I can see why this wanna be place is almost empty at 8:00 on a Friday.
Long line, but this place is serious good food and worth the wait.. menu is a little confusing but the food is spot on...

Fabulous food!  We have been to Cafe y Chocolat 3x for brunch and 2x for dinner and it has been amazing every time!!!  Great huevos rancheros, enchiladas verde, fish tacos, Mexican coffee.    Never had a dish that wasn't fantastic.  Owner very friendly and he makes the experience wonderful.  Highly highly recommend!!!

More like 2.5**

I received a Groupon as a gift for Christmas and was waiting to use it until the last minute because I have a trip coming up right around the expiration. When I walked in, I was greeted gruffly by the one staff person there. I told her about my groupon and she demanded to see it and did not believe me when I told her what it was for. She practically ripped it out of my hands. Then she told me she would have to see if it was valid. I tried to explain that the expiration is at the bottom of the paper but she would not listen. Then she told me that it had expired in December. I explained that the Groupon was purchased December and expires in April. She begrudgingly agreed. 

I then received a luke warm express pedi during which barely anything was done. My calluses are all still there except the one she focused on. I asked for warmer water and was given a spritz if hot and that was it. When it was time for my mani I had to take my own items to the mani station. 

At this point more people were coming in so she called for more help. For whatever reason the woman who came in to work next to her needed lots of assistance and I was left to soak my hands in lukewarm water so she could help.  Even though the assistance was things like getting a bowl of water from the nearby sink. 

Even though I didn't like my experience I still tipped based on the regular pricing although I did tip less bc I was unhappy. It's been a few days and my fingernails are still looking good. My toes and feet are just average. I could have done the same job myself. I'm glad I got a gift Groupon here because now I won't spend my own money. 

This review probably makes me sound like a high and mighty b and maybe I am. But I feel like a pretty low maintenance gal who just wanted to get pampered before a trip and instead had a sub par experience for a service that should be luxurious.

Adorable store with chocolate made on-site.  Samples are available, and there is quite a variety of chocolates available.  Buy a box, or put together a customized gift.  There are lots of themed chocolates, too, like Liberty Bells and holiday shapes.

First, thank you to my friends. They raved about this place before I moved to Philadelphia. Next, I thought it safe to check with yelp. Thank you as well.
I was more than impressed with this place. It was Italian, but not the typical dishes you expect. I'll go to Ralph's for veal parmesan or spaghetti bolognese, but the menu is a bit more interesting here. And it's a BYOB.
The wife and I split the mussels for an appetizer and both ordered pasta dishes with seafood. The taste was fresh. The pink cream sauce on the farfalle delizia was incredibly rich. The farfalle positano was light, letting the natural flavor of the food (especially the crab meat) come through.
The service wasn't super-speedy, but they were rather busy (on a weeknight, that's a good thing to see - it says something). And honestly, I never sit down for Italian when I'm in a hurry; it's just not right!
I only give 5 stars to restaurants that I've frequented more than just once or twice, but I'd love to see a repeat performance. If I do, I will gladly return with a 5-star rating!
PS: Please, please, please don't bring your kids! Give us a break at least once!

Amazing venue I had the opportunity to visit during CCD Restaurant Week. The dining room is indeed loud with conversations echoing throughout the space but that helped mask my convo from that of my neighbors. So while seating is squeezed in, the noise level sort of acts like a cocoon to still make your dining table feel intimate and separate from the rest of the room. The staff was helpful and attentive and clearly work well as a team. One server clearing plates while another delivered the next coarse all while their colleague is uncorking my bottle of red blend wine. All of this is close proximity and with a smile. That's no easy feat during the rush of Resto Week and they should be commended for it.

I have to be honest in that I don't remember all that much about the food, because of the copious amounts of wine that I drank the evening I went.  I know I liked it. 

I do remember that when the waiter told us everything was organic and locally grown, he then mentioned the steak was from Tazmania and some veg was from Florida.  

It's pretty small on the inside, and we were definitely cramped.  I will probably go back without drinking a bottle of wine.

On a positive note, the repair was adequate. The negatives include the scheduling system... you don't find out your appointment time until 9:30am on the day of the repair. They will call at 9:30 and will then give you a 3 hour window. This basically means you have to take an entire day off from work (instead of taking a half day, which you might be able to do if you're given your 3 hour window in advance). Also negatives... the repair person and customer service scheduling person were somewhat unfriendly.
Ordered after waiting in a long line and had to wait 45 minutes for my food. Disgusting.
The tea infusion espressos are fantastic! The coffee is also delicious too! Love that they have a great selection of tea to choose from! Also great place to get your work done!

I have been searching for a hairstylist all over Philadelphia and I have finally found one I'm ready to commit to.  Marialana is wonderful!  She consistently gives me a great hair cut and fabulous color that actually looks natural!  I have long, fine hair and though I do not ever like to take time to style my hair, I still want it to look stylish when I leave the house.  I've paid twice as much at many other salons around the city only to be disappointed with the cut after I wash and try to style it myself.   I love my hair now.  Color was another difficulty for me.  People would dye it too dark or too light or the color would fade way to fast.  Marialana chose the perfect color for my hair the very first time I saw her.  It doesn't fade fast, the color is rich but not dark and doesn't show 'red' or look 'brassy' in the sun or certain lighting.  It is also ammonia- free which is really important to me.  I am extremely happy with this salon and with my hair.  Thank you, M's on the Square!

This place was great! it had so much personality! so much food so little time! I was just a bit annoyed because my bf and I were getting so confused in here! we were running around in circles trying to get away from the booths we've already passed by. But this place was great! next time i'll make sure to bring more money =P

My wife and I decided to keep our condo when we had our first child. JG Real Estate came highly recommended and they have proven to live up to their reputation. We recently had a tenant move out to purchase their own place. JG helped break the lease early and transition to new tenants, all of which required very little time on our part. We continue to be impressed by how seamless and independent they operate, but while keeping us apprised of the ongoings of our unit.

In the heart of Philadelphia's unassuming china town, E Mei delivers some authentic, spicy and tingly sichuanese fares. Lunch special portions are generous and very reasonably priced (at least by my typical NYC standards - though certainly pricier than the food trucks I frequented as a student in Philadelphia). The Ma Po Tofu and Spicy Sliced Beef specials that we ordered were both excellent, and a check in on yelp awarded us with a free order of dumplings. What more could we ask for!  I would recommend a weekday lunch visit to take advantage of the discounted lunch special pricing.

Great mechanic.  

I just moved to the area.  South Philly has as many auto mechanics as it does dive bars, so it can be overwhelming where to go.  

My knowledge about cars is about as extensive as my knowledge about nuclear physics, so I"m a prime suspect to be ripped off, and have been a victim of this in the past.  

luckily all tech is run by a really nice mechanic.  he's asian and i can see how some people might have difficulty understanding him but the prices are great and he will show you exactly what he has to do with your car.  i priced out the services he provided and found that they were better than any other place i could find.  they were also significantly cheaper than the meineke i used to go to for brake issues and the sort.  

Also very convenient scheduling.  The couple of times I've been there he's been working on my car within an hour or so.  Oil changes happen even faster. 

Finding a good mechanic is valuable.  sometimes you think you have one and then after a year or two you realize you were being overcharged on everything they did.  ive done my homework with all tech and the prices are great on everything i've had done thus far.  i'm also getting friendly and quick service.
This place has my heart. Truly wonderful food and selection ; they even have a book store up stairs !

This place was recommended and their coffee is fantastic!! The cold brew was incredibly smooth, I definitely recommend it. HOWEVER, avoid their tea at all costs. It was extremely rough and you can tell they use a poor quality of tea - but definitely stop by for coffee!
Order here regularly. Never had a bad thing from solly. Pizza in northeast philly is hit or miss but this one is well above average. Great steaks, gyros, hoagies, salads, and fries.

In Riva is an awesome restaurant.  The service is amazing, food is excellent, and the atmosphere is different.  It's funny because I never would have thought a pizza joint would be one of my favorite restaurants in Philadelphia!  Ha!  The only thing I would like to say is that the pizzas are larger than expected.  The first time I went, my boyfriend ordered a pizza and I ordered a pizza (i thought they were the size of pizza hut personal pans from back in the day).  I should have taken notice of the waiter given me the "put down the fork fatty" look because when we got the pizzas, it was entirely too much food and I can eat a lot!  I would suggest 1 or 2 appetizers and 1 pizza to start (if you are dining with another person) and if you are still hungry, then add another pizza!

THIS IS GOOD PIZZA. Really good pizza, in fact.

I'm new to Philly (from New York), and Gennaro's is the first pizza of this particular style that I've encountered here. It lies somewhere between your true neapolitan pie and your standard american slice joint. That said, it's pies-only, and cash-only, and on the pricey side, but oh so worth it.

Folks who are turned off by the soupy center of a traditional neapolitan pie (something I don't particularly mind, but can see why one may not care for it) may fare better at Gennaro's, whose super-thin crust is crispy enough to allow each slice to support its own weight while maintaining a noticeable chewiness and puffiness in the outer crust.

The sauce is fantastically balanced-a bit sweet, a bit tangy, and super fresh-tasting, over all-while the cheese is a creamy, whole-milk mozzarella that's low on grease, and perfectly browned in the oven. 

I got takeout this time, but the shop looked quite cozy, and I hear they are byob, so I'll be sure to dine-in soon. I'll definitely be bringing my out-of-town friends here when they come to visit.

One of the best beer stores that I went to during my visit. It's actually a food store, but their beer selection is fantastic with even rare gems like Russian River Supplication/Consecration and Firestone Walker XVI.

They don't have glasses, but you can grab something from the refrigerators and use their coffee cups. I highly recommend Siberian Night Imperial Stout if you want something really big roasty and creamy in a beer.
Joseph William Shaw Sr, said Joe's is the best if the best barber shop in Philadelphia

So I typically praise DD.. A definite go to place for food and drinks the last few years. That last few months things have changed . Service has been shotty (brunch specifically) they changed the menu, and it doesn't seem it's for the better.
It took an hour and a half for brunch. The waitress and the cook were screaming at each other, with the waitress ending with "this place is a hole. A HOLE!" And storming off.

I am a Tapas snob, having spent a fair amount of time in Spain, and having no good tapas anywhere in LA I came in dubious for meal at Amada.

I was impressed.   Very authentic, very well executed..    I do not think this is a straight  5-star tapas joint but the excellent service, cool atmosphere, and great drinks put it over the top. 

I would recommend against the traditional Spanish tapas short list.  We had croquettes and garlic shrimp and were just decent.   The Chorizo, which I forced the waitress to add to the Garlic Shrimp because I'm evil like that, was excellent.   The short rib flatbread was great, and really enjoyed the other items we tried.

Most definitely lived up to the hype! I enjoyed the tayim courses with 2 other friends at the bar.  So much amazing flavorful food and a great way to taste most of the menu!  The drinks were unique and inventive plus paired well with the food.  The customer service from all the workers I have yet to have seen matched by any other restaurant I've been too.  Being taken care of allows you to relax and really enjoy the moment.  Highly recommended if you are in the area.  They do have a short 5pm-6 happy hour with a few dollars off drinks and $6 hummus, but we waited in line outside at 4:15 to score one of the 20ish bar seats.  Otherwise you can chance it, or make reservations pretty far in advance.

Came here with friends for appetizers and drinks.  We ordered the mushroom arancini, Peruvian scallops, meat and cheese plate, and burrata cheese.  Every single dish was amazingly delicious.  The low sofas and tables lended a nice atmosphere and our waiter (Nick) was awesome.  Highly recommend!

This place used to be the shit. It fell off heavily. 
Every time I go lately the food is old and crusty. 
Not cool. 
And why do they have concert speakers blasting music in here?
Is this a Latino thing? I'm not trying to be racist. I don't understand the point of music so loud you can't hear yourself think.

Our food and service was not what we usually get this time. We went around 8pm & they close at 9, plenty of time right?! Well, they made us wait 10 minutes for a table when 75% of the restaurant was empty. Although our waiter was nice, he had a hard time with our order & it was pretty standard. My husband and brother in law, ordered salads, which he brought out with the meal. We waited a long time just to get our dinner. The food was subpar, my beef brisket didn't look the way it normally does, it was not shredded, it was dry with no sauce on it. The rib tips were extremely greasy/oily. Last but not least, was the check. He was no where to be found when we were ready to pay, since there were 2 couples we decided to split the check in two & pay with credit cards. No lie, 15 minutes later he came back saying there was some connection issue! We were there over 2 hours because of all of this & needless to say we will not be going back anytime soon, if ever.

Aloof snobs who are completely unorganized.  I  made an appointment for mani/pedi on a weekday for the weekend, only to show up and be told I did not have an appointment.  The owner, who is the one I spoke to on the phone to make the appointment, would not even look at me or acknowledge me because she knew it was her fault, and I knew that is was her who made the appointment.  Bad business practice to not admit you are wrong. Never again.

Sunday lunch. Our waitress was fantastic. The atmosphere was nice, reflective of a seafood environment. 

My wife's scallop platter was delicious as were the potato salad and beets that she selected as sides. 

My crab cakes were okay. Jumbo lump crab meat it is not. It was ground too small and the cake was too creamy for me. It tasted good, but not what I was looking for. The linguini with garlic and oil side that I selected had way too much basil, oregano or something. I could barely eat it. I'm still tasting it two hours later! 

Overall, this is a good restaurant. We will go back. I must say that the prices are great! The most seafood for the money in Northeast Philly.

Ah, the Trocadero.  Remember when i skipped out on our school trip, just to see Bad Religion? I got in a lot of trouble for that, but it was worth it.  Remember when we drank 40's before the GBV show? That place on 11th and Chestnut always served me when i was underage.  And lastly, Remember when i was kicked out for *passing* the crystal during one of your short, but brief periods of 'rave' parties?. The snow was driving hard, my friend and it was a cold street.  My life would never be the same without this debauchery, and for some reason, i'm thankful for it.
Never had a bad meal , ribs are tasty with lots of meat, macaroni and cheese is flavorful and sweet potatoes are always good

I've gone here for 3 classes so far on a 6 class Google Offer deal. I may not make it back in time for the end-of-August expiration date, which is a little bit of a bummer. I went to the Thursday barre boot camp class with Lauren, which was OK. She's a very nice person, but I think I was expecting a class that was more based around the barre, either in a ballet class sort of way, or in the way that Pure Barre/Lithe use barre. It ended up being a lot of strength exercises away from the barre, which were fine, just not what I expected. I think a lot of places are trying to piggy-back on the barre fitness trend (which PB does best in my opinion), and I think this class would be better off marketed as just Boot Camp, since barre barely entered into it.

I went to back-to-back Saturday classes too. One was Barre Stretch and Sculpt with Jae Hoon, a (guy) professional dancer/Pilates coach. It was basically like the first part of a ballet class, which was cool, because I haven't taken a ballet class in about 6 years, and I found that I actually missed it. It's more "straight" ballet than PB/Lithe, which was a nice change. Jae Hoon actually stresses proper ballet technique as a dance teacher would in a ballet class, so that was cool. It's also accessible to beginners, though--he only gives major corrections in form to those who seem to have a background in ballet, I think. He's very entertaining, so that was fun.

He wasn't scheduled to, but actually taught the next class (Pilates Wake-Up) as well because that instructor was running late. It was a good class, basically your typical Pilates class. I like that the instructors here are all very well-trained in Pilates and do not hesitate to correct your form in the exercises. They all seem very passionate about Pilates and fitness, which bumps them up a star for me. One downside is that the classes don't really fit in with my schedule (my work day ends at 4), so that's partially why I won't be redeeming all the classes in the deal. 

Overall, I would, and have recommended, Thrive to others. One neat thing is that they're partners with Philly Power Yoga, which is on the 2nd floor, so you can get your Pilates and Yoga at the same location, if you like both. Also cool is that both locations have special Yelp offers, and occasionally offers on other sites.

I came here because I noticed a new business on Groupon and brought it. I love that there is finally a poke bowl and boba place on Passyunk and gave it a try. I got the Big Catch and Passyunk Double Passion poke bowl, both are amazing! I added extra eel to both to give it a more savory taste. The ingredients are fresh and it taste great with brown rice. I couldn't even finish mine in one sitting. I will definitely come here again. Christine and the other girl are so nice and quick and knowledgeable. I really hope this place becomes permanent on Passyunk!

My husband and I went to Village Whiskey as a celebration, as it's been on our list of places to eat for a while. The hostess was very friendly, and we were told there was a 50 minute wait. This may seem long, but what can you expect when you go out to eat on a Friday night in Philadelphia. While we waited, we were able to get drinks at a standing bar towards the back of the restaurant. We started with a glass of whiskey and a "Spice Jam," both of which were excellent. After we were seated, we ordered an appetizer of fried pickles, the Whiskey King and the burger of the month, the Two Hearts Beef As One with a side of duck fat fries; all of which were delicious and definitely worth the wait. The fried pickles were one of the best we've had, and the burgers were both cooked to order. All in all, it is a cool, low-key place that serves high quality food in South Philly and definitely worth a visit. Be prepared for a crowded room though, as there is little privacy and given the relatively small space, all of the noise made clashes and can make it hard to hear.
Combination of professional, competent and friendly.  Everything that Penelope C said is true for me too.  I haven't had a bad class or instructor yet.

Apparently they don't accept out of state cards . The f I have my card and state issues ID what does it matter?  Add that I also have a military ID which is why I'm out of state...  just don't go here.

We ordered a margarita pizza and sweet potato fries through grubhub.  Pizza was crap and the fries were on the thin side and greasy.   We were given a menu with our delivery and saw that we paid about $5 more than if we ordered directly instead of through Grubhub. There's much better and cheaper pizza around Roxborough.

I highly recommend these guys. They were prompt in completing the work finishing days before the estimated completion date. They went above and beyond, even giving us a ride home when we dropped the car off. All the staff were friendly and courteous. Fantastic service.

Mad Rex is a cool, new unique dining experience in Philly! They are still working out some kinks which is normal of any new restaurant, so don't pay attention to any of these 1-star reviews. Their attention to detail on the theme/ experience is amazing, food and drinks were very good (free locally sourced carrot-squash hummus anyone), cooking your own meat is always fun and overall we had a blast. Check it out before everyone starts going here!

Michael Solomonov has done it again. Funky atmosphere with great tunes and cool decor. I had the tehina hummus and my tastebuds were just so happy, it's as delicious as Zahav hummus, which is so craveworthy. So glad we stopped in this afternoon on a whim for a great lunch. Luckily it wasn't too packed when we got there and we were able to grab a spot at one of the large tables. Will be back again for sure.

I am really distraught at having to award this place three stars.

This is the kind of place I should be gushing about.  GUSHING.  It is cheap, it has an endless menu with tons of variety, it is a hole in the wall that reeks of authenticity.  This is my kind of place, in short .

Maybe it's because Choo Choo is mad hyped ... but ...

all right, so C and I ordered vegetable spring rolls to split.  I am pretty sure we were given egg rolls.  No bigs!  I am not a fan of egg rolls, so C got an extra out of the deal.  I then ordered the BBQ pork with grilled pork balls over vermicelli, remembering a review that gushed about Choo Choo's BBQ anything.

The pork was tasty; the pork balls were stellar.  But ...

I don't know!  The ratio of vermicelli to lettuce to pork was stacked heavily with vermicelli and lettuce.  The vinegar-chili sauce had no punch.  It felt like I was eating a really big bowl of mediocre salad.

For an entree that was hugely filling and pretty okay (and 5.50$!), I will be back.  Maybe I just ordered the wrong thing -- for me -- and will be giving this place an update with five stars.

I really hope so.

Seems clean. Decent variety and choices. Nothing tasted amazing, but nothing was bad.

I was a little turned off when I found the hot food to be lukewarm at best. But I didn't get sick so I guess that's a win...?

I found this place about a year ago. I come here for my body waxes (underarm, Brazillian, lower leg) and I have never been disappointed. I make an appt with Lisa every time and she knows my skin and hair patterns well. She's very thorough and friendly. She's officially an honorary member of my family.

Issues with so called fresh fruit! Recently purchased oranges and 2 days later all 5 in the bag were moldy!! I returned the fruit and complained to the cashier who refused to replace them! While the owner stood near by and did nothing!!! Horrible customer service!! This is not my first encounter with their so called fresh fruit. I've noticed moldy blueberries and strawberries. Just recently they were selling expired (3/28) "Organic Girl" salads on 4/4. I have been a customer since they opened and will no longer shop here and will suggest to all family and friends and as many people as possible not to shop at A & N Produce!!

Came to Root for brunch and had no problem getting a table.  Root has such a cool mad-men vibe.  I had the omelette with goat cheese, mushrooms, and fresh herbs.  It was cooked flawlessly and the flavors were amazing.  My friends loved the egg sandwich on a fresh everything muffin and were raving about how tasty the bacon was.  Come to Root if you are looking for a classy delicious brunch!

So I've come here about 6 times now and while the food is five star and constantly wonderful, the hostess, service and seating ticks me off every time. 

To be totally blunt, with the exception of a male host on our first visit, the hostess of the day is always on the rude side. We went as a party of four at 11:45 on a Sunday for brunch. The majority of tables were empty but we were told there were 12:30 reservations and we'd have to sit at the bar. 45 minutes with empty tables?! I said we would be done eating in 45 minutes but the hostess acted like I had three heads for saying that and really was very "whatever sit at the bar or I'm sorry we can't seat you". My daughter really wanted to eat here (she's vegan) or I would have walked out. 

We were pointed to the bar with four empty stools crammed together in a small space. We couldn't even fit so I had to stand behind mine until the couple next to us was done eating. When I got to actually sit the bar is so narrow that there was about a two foot gap where I had to bend in half to reach my coffee. Absolutely no room for plates as you can see from the photos. 

Our order was taken by the bartender who was sweet but overwhelmed. Our order came out wrong but she corrected it. We had to keep asking for waters to be refilled and for more coffee. The hostess stood around glaring at anyone who came in and asked about the empty tables while she refused to seat anyone. 

At the end of our meal it was 12:20 so indeed we could have been seated in the dining room. 

I give 5 stars plus for the food, which was all amazing and worth coming for, just don't expect great service.

Nestled in Fairmount Park along the trail is this old landmark inn.   I went there a few years back and thought the food was grim.  They solely got business based on their locale.  Things have changed over the past few years.  While the back won't win any Michelin awards soon, the food is much improved. Em and I stopped there during a walk on a beautiful day a few weeks back, and our lunch was really tasty.  I had a fajita wrap that frankly, I could have had another right then and there if decorum weren't a factor.  Friendly staff and the occasional horse strolling by made it even more enjoyable.  

I always thought this place was a missed opportunity.  To have a great restaurant in the park would be novel and a destination dining experience.  While it's still nowhere near that, it has improved ten-fold and that gives me hope that maybe one day, it will be THE place to be.  Until then, get some exercise along Valley Green and then stop in for a nice meal.  A wonderful way to spend an afternoon.

Having moved here from the California / Nevada border just a few months ago, I was craving the ubiquitous Mexican taco dive with street tacos for $1.00 each, cheap eats, and loud mariachi music at full blast. The kind that you walk out and you stink of onions and cilantro. I never thought I'd miss that!

This place is just around the corner from work so I tried it out one day at lunch. After we ordered, we received warm chips and a spicy green salsa that had quite a kick.

My $12 chicken fajitas came with 3 flour tortillas and a side of fresh guac and sour cream. Delicious!

We ate here one night before doing Terror Behind the Walls at Eastern State.  The place was really nice inside.  Cool wood tables, plenty of windows to let light in, and lights hanging from the ceiling.  Plenty of outdoor seating too if it were warmer.  Had the soup du jour which was a spicy butternut squash soup and loved it. For my entree I had the pork tenderloin with mashed potatoes and brussel sprouts with bacon.  My husband got the steak and fries.  Everything was very good.

This place has been on my boyfriends bookmarks for a while now. While sitting in Rittenhouse part he looked to see how far away it was and to our surprise it was like 3 minutes from where we were. Really cute retro looking place with many options to choose from. I went with the basil lemonade and couldn't be more thrilled. Definitely a unique taste but it a great way! Boyfriend had some sort of fudge thing and he really enjoyed it as well! Price is decent for good ingredients!

Scheduled an upholstery and rug cleaning after reading these reviews. When I made the initial call, someone got back to me within the hour. The appointment was scheduled for 11am. They arrived around 9:30 am (no problem for me). Unfortunately I did not get the name of either person (yes, they told me their names but I have forgotten). The process was explained to me and they got started right away. Afterward, they gave me the quarter they found in the couch cushions (LOL!) and explained how long it would take before we could sit on the couches and walk on the rugs.
Friendly, professional, arrived earlier than expected. If you have pets, no problem - they love pets. I would definitely use them again.

I did not want to rate this establishment because I had a terrible experience. The bartender was good and when I ordered food it was terrible. No remorse from anyone instead another lady came upstairs and decided to get confrontational about why I came up to her to let someone know about the bad food. As a person that enjoys Max's cheesesteak and even the Eagles bar I expected better food and service.

I just purchased a cake for my sister's shower. The cake was not a traditional shower cake but a bit more elegant. They did a wonderful job. The buttercream lace design was perfect. My cake's flavor was a carrot cake and it was made apparent in the first bit that is was made from natural ingredients. All of my guests loved both the flavor and design.

I love this place.  I have visited on many occasions for different purposes from lunch meetings with clients to Happy Hour with co-workers to a big family dinner over the holidays.
We always have a great time and Steve, the bartender, always takes great care of us.
The food is delicious and worth every cent.  Their HH menu is fantastic, you can't beat getting a pizza and good quality wine.
I encourage anyone looking for a place to eat in the area to check it out.
Thank you Stella, keep up the good work!

A friend and I had dinner there recently and had a positively wonderful evening.  Attention was paid to the smallest of details, creating an evening of gourmet food, interesting drinks (which were packed with quite the kick), and an enjoyable, relaxed evening.  
We started off with a couple of the most interesting cocktails you could imagine.  We spoke with one of the managers who explained that he and the bar manager spent countless hours tasting various drink combinations to come up with a list of their signature drinks.  I'm not going to tell you what's in the LeDuck East Falls because you won't believe me.  Just trust me and try it for an exotic taste sensation.  We also had their version of a Manhattan which, again, was like no other I've ever tasted.  They weren't cheap but they were big, strong, and worth every penny. 
We then had the salmon with roasted vegetables, and the brisket in the richest of sauces.  It comes with a corn pudding which I cannot eat for dietary reasons.  They were incredibly accommodating and served me a Caesar salad in its place.  If you are a fan of anchovies you must try this salad.  Their recipe uses the exactly perfect amount of anchovies in this classic dressing to give it a wonderful saltiness.  I was told they make it fresh each day to guarantee a perfect dressing.  
My friend finished the evening with another cocktail: What's the Rumpus?  This is dessert in a glass that will make you fall in love with the bartender.  It's touted as a "rich dark chocolate rum espresso flip," and yes,  you will flip over it.  I finished with one of the smoothest cups of good ole regular coffee I've ever had.  A dark roast with no acidity?  Yup.  Dark but mellow.  Sounds like an oxymoron but that's the best way to describe it.  
The atmosphere was intimate enough to make for either a romantic evening or a fun night out with friends yet bustling enough to make you feel part of a big festival.  We never felt rushed yet we felt we never had to wait if we needed something.
Oh, and check out the art work which adorns the walls.  Apparently the artists get 100% of the proceeds.  So, not only do we have a seasoned restaurateur with a fine new venue but he's also a supporter of local artists.  Gotta love it.  
We will be back and we will bring friends.

Arch gourmet has EVERYTHING!!!! Their deli sandwichs are really great as well. My favorite is the southwestern panini. The people there are so nice. When I forget ingredients I need for groceries arch gourmet has anything I might have forgotten.

STAY AWAY 

I will start by saying that while I rarely write reviews, this place of business truly deserves one and should help to serve those seeking a gym in the Philadelphia area a nice outline for one that they should never spend money on.

My girlfriend and I were members here for roughly 3 months after moving into Old City over the summer. The facility is pretty large with a good amount of machines, a nice open area on both the 1st and 2nd floors, and classes offered every day. The check in system was simple but not very thorough or welcoming as the attendants either seemed very overworked or uninterested in human interaction. And while I was okay with all of this (I just wanted to get my workout in and get home) the next experience here I definitely wasn't okay with. 

One night after a long day at work I entered the gym to grab a workout as I normally did during the week. I went to the locker room (which was basically empty aside from a gym employee wrangling the dirty towels) changed, and packed my work bag and clothes into a locker while securely fastening my normal lock. I left the locker room with my phone and headphones in hand for roughly an hour and a half paying no mind to my belongings as they were safe in my locker, or so I thought... I arrived to the locker room at around 8:15 pm to find the locker door of my locker completely ripped off of its hinges, my clothes strewn about the locker, and my bag weirdly zipped back up in a different manner than I had left it. Additionally, the lock was still firmly attached to the metal fastener and only the locker itself opened (which PSC is fully responsible for making sure to be in strong working condition). 

Immediately, I opened my bag to find $60 in cash missing as well as a new Stainless Steel Citizen Eco-Drive Watch also missing. The thief had pilfered items that were not easily traceable and left my clothes, laptop, work phone, and remaining items in my wallet. 

I approached the counter to speak with the GM, Edward Gonzalez, who I took to the locker, showed the damage to, and followed up on next steps to understand the gyms course of action. Ed relayed that they would need to launch an internal investigation and work to identify who the culprit may be. He noted that the gym did not have an internal camera system at any entrance of the facility and that they could not track any unauthorized perps who may have entered the locker room at any time unless they cross referenced their sign in list which is barely adhered to when entering the gym as it is. He promised that a new system was being "priced" within the next couple of weeks but how would that help me now? What was his solution to fixing this very glaring issue that the gym had which not only immediately affected me personally but could affect others in the future? He had no answer for that and instead urged me to stay in contact with him and make sure that I didn't bring valuables to the gym moving forward. Again, this was a very disconcerting answer given that I come directly from my office and would need to travel home before going to the gym which would add roughly a half hour to my already stretched schedule each night. Instead of adding additional work for your customers, ensuring their safety and piece of mind should be your top priority!

All in all, Ed avoided my phone calls and emails for roughly 5 days after the incident with no update. I confronted him one night to understand what progress had been made at which point he hastily talked through the gym's liability policy and urged me to file a police report as they couldn't identify who did it and had no answers. My girlfriend and I cancelled our membership that night and figured to be completely done with the gym at that point, the police report was filed but to no avail. Just recently we received a bill for another month at the gym and are currently in a battle with corporate to make sure these charges are relinquished. Avoid this gym at all costs, they are poorly mismanaged and unsafe

I really want to leave a good review here. I found Garrison's on Yelp and was impressed at the quality and breadth of reviews; I live just a few blocks away and have been looking for a new mechanic since my last one closed a few months ago. I took my car in for an oil change and to have my brake pads visually inspected.

The good - Barbara and the team were very friendly, and I was impressed with the work that they did.

The not-so-good - My problem is that they did work on my car that I didn't authorize. Instead of visually inspecting my break pads as I had asked, Garrison's did a full inspection of the break system and undertook $100 worth of work that I had not agreed to - they did not contact me with a list of concerns and an estimate as most mechanics do. When I received the total, I called and immediately expressed my concern that I had not given permission for this work to be done. There was no apology, no offer for a discount, just a comment that next time I come in, I should ask them to call once they've inspected the vehicle. 

Overall - I'm really disappointed and will not go back. The convenience was amazing, but I have a severe lack of trust in a mechanic with this kind of mindset.

Visited Millennium Nails this past week and was very pleased with both the customer service and quality of my manicure. I learned that they were under new ownership which was apparent  through their effort for wonderful customer service, specials and candy bonuses! I will be a regular!

I'll be the first to admit that generally healthy food is not my favorite but FUEL is the exception. I've mainly stuck to their shakes and sandwiches and haven't had a bad experience yet. My favorite sandwich is their Protein Grilled Cheese. It's very cheesey and the bread is always toasted perfectly.  My favorite shake is the Rush but I also enjoy the Protein Buster when I'm in the mood for Peanut Butter. My girlfriend always orders the Protein Buster without the whey protein so they let you customize your shake which is nice. I recently got their Groupon for a 10 shake punch card and went through that thing pretty quickly. I usually grab one on my way home from the gym.  

I highly recommend this place. The service is fast, the food is fresh and healthy, and the concept is cool.

This pop up coffee shop serves its purpose. It's a nice place to stop in for a quick bite and a good coffee beverage. The atmosphere is fun, light, and playful. The outdoor seats are a perfect place to read a book or have a first date. Come check it out!

This has always been on my lists of restaurants to visit but I was underwhelmed by the flavors and portion size. The only thing that was worthy of a praise was the ricotta. We also ordered the meatballs, pizza, and budino. The rest was forgettable. 
The restaurant is very dim and loud which I liked for a romantic dinner. However, this is a very small restaurant so you can easily hear the conversation the person next to you, behind you and in front of you are saying. Overall I probably wouldn't return.

Great Iconic place for a Philly Cheese Steak. Ordered one with onion & provolone and another with Pepper Jack. Friendly and prompt service and the gentleman calling out order names was funny and very personable. Good amount of seating available and another server sets up a tray of peppers, pickles and ketchup for each table. Sweet!
A decent size overall with the steak to cheese ratio a lil less than I'd prefer (was hoping for a lil more really) bread was good, not as crispy on the outside as I like but still very good. I enjoyed them even more when I added the peppers and pickles which really kicked up the flavour profile. Well done A++

What a find. The food was delicious. Started with dolmades and falafel. Flavorful and so good. I had the beef gyro. I love the amount of lemon in their sauces. Perfect. The pita is a bit crisp on the edges, which I love. 

The two young women at the counter were fantastic. Sweet and welcoming. I believe they did all the food prep. Perfectly balanced. Cannot wait to go back. Bravo Smiley's!!!
The art materials store is close to Drexel University. There are lots of art materials to choose. I always come here twice a month.
Great selection and food is better than other buffets I have been too love the hibachi! The only complaint I have is some of the food was cold. Other than that I would visit again!

Before I criticize or act like I know anything about what makes a good restaurant, I will say this: Lunch special!

Amazing lunch special here...I promise if you order the nachos on that menu as part of your special you will leave happy and full.

As for the place itself, it is a little too dark (drab dare I say) for me.  Kind of like an old mexican diner.  The food is fine...standard mexican fare...tacos, guac, enchiladas, etc.  It is also relatively inexpensive, so a good spot if you are craving a sit-down mexican experience.  That said, nothing about this place is overwhelming.

Decent happy hour if you can make it in time...overall this place is a solid three in my world.  Not teetering on the brink of bad or great.

How many times has a tourist came to Philly and heard; "This place has the best cheesesteaks." Well, I think I had it here. Got the mushroom cheesesteak with fries and it was amazing. Had to get a slice of strawberry cheesecake too that hit the spot. Very laid back atmosphere with friendly service. Would suggest this place to anyone.

superb dining experience...small plates are the way to take a great culinary tour.   Had the cauliflower, meatballs, gnocchi, grilled dorade.   All were great.    Drinks we ordered were consistently made and very tasty.    The place is small/tight.    A few reviewers have indicated that it was warm inside the restaurant--all true.   If it's summer, dress lightly.    When we go back, we'll do so in the Fall or Winter, when the warming flames of the pizza oven and kitchen are more appreciated.

We just moved to the area and we were excited to try a new place. The atmosphere was just what we were looking for after work however we are not pleased with the service. We sat outside and waited 25 minutes for someone to approach us. We ended up having to get our first drink at the bar.

We were having a nice time and wanted to order another drink however the server came up to us and handed us the bill. We were extremely confused since we never asked for it.

The server we had was extremely rude and it's disappointing because it could have been a nice night. 


The atmosphere is great but I would definitely improve on the employees who work there.

The tiramisu here is utterly otherworldly. After buying birthday tiramisu cakes from termini for the past 3 years (which were quite good, or so we thought prior to today), we decided to switch it up this year.

We've had tiramisu in countless restaurants, here and in Italy, and never had one quite as good as Isgro's. It is impossibly moist & rich. I keep thinking the next bite might be a bit drier or a reprieve from the richness but that bite just never happened. Everything about this cake is unbelievable. One of the few cakes I've ever had that both looked amazing and somehow managed to taste better than it looked.

Isgro is known or their cannolis, which are super legit, but their tiramisu is seriously underrated. Looking forward to next year's cake already...

I've eaten at a lot of sushi bars and I'm super happy that the best one is right in my neighborhood. We had the omakasi in the back and it was lovely. Next time we plan to check out the pub in the front.

The service was really attentive and the back sushi bar was nice and quiet and peaceful. 

If you want a great sushi experience in Philadelphia, this is where you should go.

George is the best!!! I brought in 2 Movado's, 1 Gucci, and 1 Fossil watch, all with dead batteries. I have always went to Movado directly to have my batteries replaced.  After reading the reviews about George, he was the person I asked for when I arrived. He replaced the batteries on my Movado's, as well as my Fossil (which required 2 batteries) and charged me 5.00 per battery, plus tax, which in my opinion is a steal. I paid 22.00 total for 4 watch batteries. I also was chatting with George as he replaced my batteries and learned that he has been repairing watches since before I was born, so he knows what he is doing! (apparently he used to have a store located in the Gallery)

I had an issue with the Gucci watch and George had it sent out for me at no additional charge, and let me know the repair estimate when it came back to his store. I would highly recommend going to George for very inexpensive watch batteries, that he even gives you a warranty on.

Keep up the good work, George! :)
Gus makes great, quality food. His menu changes every day, and come early so he doesn't run out before you. Also, love the tunes.

This place smelled ! 
We went there as it is a meeting point for the big red pedal tour, I got a coffee based alcohol drink while my SO hot an old fashioned. He said that it was the worst old fashion drunk he has ever had! ( he has tried a lot). Plus the bar itself was shabby, the bathroom smelled like something died in there. 
I am sure it was just an off day for them. 
I would go back to try the other stuff. My drink was decent, but while there I read a review of another yelper and she too said something about the smell. 
Hopefully they have taken care of this issue.

I would give more than 5 stars if possible! I have never not liked a plate I ordered at Plaza Garibaldi. My friends and I affectionately started calling this restaurant "Taco City USA"... I have no idea why as we didn't even order tacos that often. That being said, the tacos are GOOD. *Everything* is good. My boyfriend loves the carne asada, my roommate and I loved the sopes. Next time I'm back in Philly, I definitely need to make a trip back here. It's been too long. They always had nice waitresses and cute waiters. Good service, and it felt like you were in someone's home.
beautiful ambiance!! feels like you are in a beautiful backyard relaxing under the lights.  The peach shandy was yummy!
Great experience! Recommend it 3 times over !!! I can't wait until my second appointment! It's a little weird when it first start great way to cleanse! I wasn't sluggish today

Bistro La Minette is the perfect date spot. Amazing service, food, and wine selection. 

I have been to Paris and the food here is reminiscent of my favorite bistro in Montmartre. I love that they have some of the French country style cuisine like Coq au Vin which is the best thing I have had there.

You will not be disappointed with anything you order.

I had been waiting all winter to get my car detailed, and I wasn't disappointed. It was like getting back a brand new car. Super-clean and sparkling, inside and out. They were so friendly, warm, and most important,  professional. They called when they said they would when my car was finished. 

I'm not going to wait so long next time. It makes a huge difference how you feel about your car, and it's so easy to do.  I will definitely be going back to the auto spa next time (even though I live on the other side of the city, but it was worth it.).

After trying a couple different places after moving into the area from the suburbs, Zestos is the best by far and I'm not looking back!

My girlfriend and I love their pizza and my girlfriend loved their wings as well.

Their veggie grinder (which I am a fanatic of and always get them everywhere I go) is phenomenal and I seriously have no complaints.

Fast delivery and the food is always hot and fresh.

I was in the area of Philadelphia (heading up I-95 back to NY) and figured that I had to make a stop to finally try a real cheesesteak from Philly. I looked up YELP and decided on Max's Steaks based on previous reviews. I pulled up to find the placed sandwiched in a rough part of town and especially wearing my Mets, Giants, and Knicks gear I was ready for some abuse.

I walked in and the place was super tight but the gentleman behind the counter was ready for business and knew what he was doing. His female counterpart seemed new and timid. He had a bunch of loaves ready for those steaks and he kept the line moving while memorizing everyone's order impressively. There was tons of steaks and onions cooking away on the grill while he pounded away at them forcing them to cook it seems. And here comes the abuse........at least we were Mets fans because he said if we were Yankee fans we would be out of luck!!! He happened to be a Celtics and Cowboys fan.....YUUUCK! At least that was the only yuck here, the steak sandwiches were packed with steak and onions. The lettuce was good but would have preferred the tomato cut thinner and more visual options in terms of fixings. The Mayo was a bit much but not excessive and wish there was more of a cheesy-ness to the sandwich, I envisioned a trail of cheese following some of the bites that I never got. The sheer size of the sandwich was amazing (the whole loaf I think) and seemed worth if for $15 bucks. I was very happy with the sandwich and it packed good flavor.

I would have gave the place 5 stars if there were more toppings or at least in view to know they were options. I would have really enjoyed a place to sit. Overall, it was a great experience and if I'm ever in the area I will certainly be back for another steak fix!! I finally had a Philly cheesesteak from Philly......LETS GO METS!!!
Great food, very little fruits  and vegs and the watermelons dont look real... a little pricey
Great burger and fried cauliflower but unfortunately I got the worst MSG headache I've had in a very long time. Beware MSG sufferers...
The food is incredible and Tina is the absolute best!! Delicious and would highly recommend!!!  Best place in town. Xx traveling across the country e&c

Renter BEWARE. We rented from Nancy Alperin in her flagship building at 1736 Pine Street location. We rented for two years and at the close of our lease, we never received our security deposit back despite the completed checklist. The in-flux staff in the office at that time (2002-2003) were unprofessional and gossipy. 
We needed to file in Small Claims Court after repeated letters asking for the $1000 to be refunded. Ms. Alperin never responded to our requests and sent her lawyer to work out her problems. I represented my self. Needless to say, the Judge agreed and we were awarded twice the security deposit (state law in PA) plus filing fees. I never was able to recoup the time and energy I devoted to reclaiming what was rightfully mine, including missed days of work. 
I found her caliber of leadership to be lacking ethics and morals, not in the service of her tenants and clients.
True leaders need to respect those that pay their bills, not just sit on boards, while acting unethically at the ground level. 
(Cross posted on zillow)
Love the food here, everything seems fresh and clean. Service is polite and friendly. Just everything you want in a little breakfast place.

The restaurant was decent, but the chicken breast that I ordered was very dry. I was surpised because I've heard this restaurant was one of the best in the neighborhood. The seasons used complimented the meet well but the yellow rice was bland.

Stephen Starr's restaurants continue to be painfully average, yet overpriced.

Summary: It's good if it's happy hour and you're hungry. 

Accidentally arrived right at the beginning of happy hour. The bar was dead, only 4 people scattered around the bar itself and the seating in the same room. 

I had the Lamb Shepherd's Pie, the "triple cooked chips" (fries), and the $3 Yards IPA for happy hour. The Shepherd's Pie was good, but it wasn't exceptional. I've had much better tasting dishes for the same if not lower price. The "chips" were good in a weird "these fries are almost burnt" way. I enjoyed my meal, but I probably wouldn't return for dinner unless it were during happy hour and I were hungry.

I'd come back for another $3 pint during happy hour.
Smiley's food is super delicious, we are looking forward eating it again. You just cant go wrong.

So delicious! I got the carne asada which was full of flavor and exactly what I was looking for. Very authentic Mexican taste, nothing like a chain. Staff was super attentive. Prices are pretty cheap! My three other friends got burritos and a chicken dinner and none of us had any complaints! Another plus: the portions are enough for two meals!
Roast Pork with sharp provolone and mixed peppers.   Damn good!
pan fried noodles and pork soup dumplings did not disappoint, a little more soup in the dumplings would've been perfect but still great flavor.

Even with the poor reviews, having a potential new ramen shop nearer to work was too tempting (and why not try?), so a group of us gave Ai Ramen a shot.

The $4.00 pork bao bun wasn't bad, but it was $4.00...unfortunately although some fellow diners' ramen noodles looked firmer, mine were sadly overlooked and the pork was fat-only, unappealing visually, and inedible. The egg wasn't seasoned, just hard boiled. About as expensive as Nom Nom, it was much lower quality. Would definitely choose another spot in the food court, and make the trek to Terakawa for real ramen!
Waste of time to go here.. the produce section is smelling of mold as the weeks go by, even the canned goods are expiring, the meat is always days from being poison.... the staff is the worst!

I tried their Super Green which was "fresh spinach, green tea, pineapple, soy milk, and 100% natural non-fat frozen yogurt." So good. I think anyone that doesn't particularly enjoy green smoothies would think twice after trying this. The interior is quaint and cute, though encourages customers to come in and leave quickly with their drink. The only downside is the cost of the smoothies which holds me back from visiting often.

Perfect for a quick bite when I only had about 30 minutes for lunch.  They do a great job with the space they have but it is tiny so it really is not best for big groups.  Everyone who worked there was SO nice and helpful.  I ordered the Power Chicken Salad and it was delicious, I really needed a healthy but fulfilling meal.  Would definitely recommend.

Unfortunately, they are very unresponsive and scheduling any appointment with them is a nightmare. Consistently took far too long to reply and made the process much more challenging than it needed to be. Too bad.

I really don't understand the elite reviews.  We should have trusted out gut instinct and left when we saw an empty store for a sat lunch.  Second warning,  the chef was picking his teeth with his finger as he was preparing our food.  

The food itself was just okay.  More on par to what you would settle for in an airport food court.  The hakka noodle soup I got looked as if I was served only half of the bowl.  It could have used more than 1 bokchoy and the small portion of beef and noodles.  My husband ordered Braised chopped pork dried noodle.  Flavor was decent but the portion again was tiny.  Also the egg served in the dish was still cold. We were not at all satisfied with our meal and came home and comforted ourselves with left over pizza from the night before.

First of all @Loren H. coming from Wilmington on Septa should earn Loren a free cup of java.

I've been a regular of The sweet spot for about a month now it is great.
Chocolate Croissants are awesome. I'm not a pastry person but I am a chocolate person and these croissants are using high quality cocoa. The kind of taste I experience from imported German chocolate.

The staff members are extremely friendly and they have a half off sale on most pastries after...(don't quote me) 7pm?

Sneakily placed in the middle of a menu full of deli sandwiches and American breakfast staples, are 3 options of Greek specialties: mousaka, gyro, and chicken souvlaki. For the random amount of 8 dollars and 30 cents, you get a plate of mousaka with a side salad and a roll. It is a pretty amazing deal.

Mousaka is a host of some of my favorite things. I enjoyed the varying flavors and textures of my layers of cheese, meat, sauce, and eggplant. There wasn't a strong showing of bechamel, but nobody is perfect.

Sandy's mousaka is definitely one of those hidden gem lunches that get me all warm and fuzzy inside.

http://midtownlunch.com/philadelphia/2010/09/20/by-the-beard-of-zeus-home-cooked-mousaka-at-sandys/

Confusing interaction from early on, but it seemed as if we had settled on an estimate and scheduled the service, only to be rescheduled and then get into many more questions about the work needing to be done.  That ultimately led to me canceling the work with this company.  I wish others better luck as it seems from other reviews it's a solid company.  Not for me.
The food was out fast, was delicious, and the service was good. what else do you need?

Oh man, this place is simply gorgeous. I headed over to Fishtown today specifically to grab a coffee and study here because of how nice the interior is. Old wood and white tile scream my name. Couple that with lots of space and nice music overhead, and you've made me fall in love. 

Besides enjoying the decor, I also grabbed a latte and some greek yogurt. The latte was pretty good and satisfied my caffeine cravings. The yogurt was delicious--vanilla greek yogurt with a pistachio granola and a little honey drizzled on top. Yum! 

I'm definitely coming back here (my new study-go-to spot?!), but next time I'll come with more of an appetite and will grab some solid food.

Everything about my dinner here was perfect. It's been a couple of years, although I do plan on going back soon, but all of my friends that went on my recommendation say their meals were delicious. I distinctly remember the snert and the almond cake being so flavorful. The chef came out and talked to us for a bit and just really completed the experience. One of my favorite restaurants in the city.

I am giving this place a 5 simply because i think that their cheesesteak is better then Pat's, and right now Pat's is way ahead in the rankings. 

The only other thing these two joints got going for it is that they stay open later like as late as 3am and Geno's has Birch Root Beer. Other then that i would avoid going to these places at all cost. Their attitudes use to be funny back when they were charging $6-7 for a plain cheese steak but now that it is $7-9 neither of these places are worth it. 
The only reason to go here is because the only other option is WaWa or some dingy diner, so compared to the late night competition it gets 5 stars from me.

Nice guy, clean work place 
I had a lot of work done there including several implants 
Dr Serang is not experienced enough to do implants mine were put in wrong because they broke dr said they were out of warranty and charged me to do them again, had a cavity filled the tooth broke the next day eating soft pizza, dr said you must be pulling trains with your teeth didn't offer refund or apologize 
Had several other cavities the doctor never told me about 
I stopped going there because he didn't want to see me again he said oh no it's you again when he entered the room and saw me in the chair.

Not to be punny (I am) but this hit the spot. Had a spot burger and a side buffalo chicken burger bc covid times means comfort eating. Coleslaw on the spot burger is excellent, gives some crisp freshness to an otherwise indulgent affair. My wife had a custom chicken burger and fries which she loved. We wanted to come because of the gracious policies of the owner, and we'll be back.

I'm giving this place three star, because when I came near closing time, the lady was so nice and accommodated me even though she was cleaning up and putting everything away. Their service is definitely good. The frozen yogurt I had was just ok, kind of runny. Somehow I feel their website looks better than the actual store experience.
Everything fresh and delicious. Arepa reina pepiada, pollo empanada, and chicken soup all delicious. Will have to return to try more!

Love Good Karma! I have been to each location and think the quality of the food/drinks is the same. The only thing that's different is space (how large, option of outdoor space, ect.) The sandwiches are decent, but not fantastic. Great pastries though and really fun seasonal drinks to try. I personally always get either the London Fog or the lavender lemonade.

This is my favorite of the three locations. It has the largest space and people there are generally quieter/studying. There are a mix of tables sizes available as well as a smaller room off to the side that tends to be even quieter than the main area.

Cuba Libre has been on my list for awhile and I finally had the chance to go this weekend. Due to COVID, the city has shut down car traffic on 2nd street to allow for more outdoor dining for restaurants like Cuba Libre. As a result, it was easy to make a reservation and you felt safe throughout your entire dining experience. The waiter informed us that they were known for their mojitos so I took his recommendation and ordered the coconut mojito. It had the perfect combination of mint and coconut and was so refreshing on the warm day. As an entree, I ordered the Tour of Havana. The tour included a Cuban sandwich, plantain chips, salad, and black bean soup. I certainly did not leave hungry and even needed a box! I highly recommend Cuba Libre for either a date, a drink with friends, or a dinner with the family!

I have had some bad experiences at Darlings.  So I went to check it out for the first time in 6 mos because PYT was too crowded.  I must say the service has improved a lot!  Our waitress was great and she had some good suggestions for the menu and the drinks were delicious!  One thing to improve is bussing the tables a little quicker.  Overall, a good experience!

Wasn't that great to me. Took a total of three times for bf and I to get to come play a game the first two times they were packed (after going, I'm not sure why). This time we went on a Saturday night and honestly I was not impressed by anything, not the drinks, not the food, not the bowling, definitely not the prices. Our server never even asked if I wanted another cocktail, which I did. I'll drive the extra 10 mins over the bridge to Pinsetter which has a bar, better prices, parking, and a lot more lanes next time.

Alcohol, tacos, nachos, burritos, happy hour , hipster ambiance , what more could you want ! This place is on a hidden corner in Fishtown, once we discovered it we couldn't stay away. It's always crowded with mostly college students so be ready to wait for a seat. But I promise it will be worth it . Like them on Facebook and you will receive notifications when they have dollar taco nights! They have the best nachos here as well, you will be picking the cheese off of the pan. They have nice strong margaritas as well. Highly recommended if your in the area.

Possibly the most genuinely nice and polite staff in Philadelphia!  The food is also quite good.  Definitely a step up from cheap Mexican... good full helpings, all entrees come with a tasty rice and beans, and they have a really nice assortment of maragaritas.  The free parking is also a really nice plus.  It's a little off the beaten path, but well worth a trip for a nice quiet meal, especially when the weather is nice as they have a nice outdoor patio.  Try the chorizo tacos... they're great!

Had a great brunch here last weekend.  Hash browns were a little over fried for my taste (they kind of just tasted like the oil they were fried in), but otherwise, an excellent meal. My omelet was delicious and my husband's bacon was fantastic.  The toast was even toasted, which is a rare feat in this city.

I was flying Philadelphia to SFO and the flight attendant kindly offered a drink and I kindly accepted a bloody mary.  I soon realized I made a mistake because I am trying to decrease my alcohol drinking.  I gave my drink to my friend that was flying coach because I didn't want the drink to go to waste.  The attendant went from super nice to super mean in a heart beat.  She earlier asked to take my coat to be hung.  When she asked if I gave my drink away, I said yes and told her politely that I didn't know I couldn't give my drink away and I had never heard or read this rule.   She replied,  "Why don't you use your common sense?" And  She said this in front of a full cabin so everyone in the 1st class heard this because there is only 4 rows.  I felt embarrassed that she would say this basically accusing me of stealing a $5 drink.  
I replied to her,  "Why don't you be more polite?"
I was so mad that I when back to the back of the plane in a lot of traffic,  asked my friend to give me back the drink and I slammed the drink on the galley table.  "here's your damm drink back!"  She reported me to Captain Leo and I told my side of the story to him and he told me that there are in fact rules (not that obvious) about giving away drinks. I apologized to him and told him that I didn't know about these rules.  He complimented my cloths and said that it was only a 3 and 1/2 hr flight and  basically just get along for a relatively short flight.  This calmed me down and on my way back to my seat I went to the galley and said sorry to "Nurse Cratchet."  (One flew over the Cuckoo's nest.)  She did not acknowledge me and just looked away.  I did not order anymore drinks or food.  I lost trust in U.S.Airway's representative for common decency and common courtesy.  I wouldn't put it past her to put a bugger in my meal.  Why does this ice water taste so funny?  Because she yelled at me in front of a full cabin and made me go get my drink (in a lot of traffic), she made me feel like a child.  More over, she made me feel like a thief.  She made no attempt to explain the rules and the logic behind them.  Even now, I still don't understand how a $5 drink could cause so much fuss.  I would have paid for the fricken drink.   No passengers should be made to feel like a thief.

So slow! By the time I finally got on a shuttle (every other rental car had multiple other shuttles go by while I was waiting) it took an hour from the time I finally got on the shuttle to getting my car. Not a cool way to start a trip.

If it weren't for Yelp, I wouldn't have discovered the delectable dessert shop that is Brown Betty Dessert Boutique.  I've been a Yelp lurker for quite some time, using it when I need to know where the best of the best is in the city, and as I was looking for a dessert shop to make a memorable cake for my 25th birthday party, I stumbled upon the reviews for Brown Betty.  

I'm originally a Southern girl, so I was excited to discover (via their sleek website) that Brown Betty's dessert repertoire consisted of sweets I'd usually indulged in at many a church social or family picnic growing up.  I ventured down there one evening with hopes of tasting a few cupcakes and talking to someone about ordering a cake.  The interior is cozy (read: matchbox), neatly decorated, and comfy kind of like my Granny's kitchen.  It was about 45 minutes from closing time, so the cake decorator wasn't there. Instead, I spoke with another employee.  I told her the theme for my party (A Dime Becomes a Quarter), and that I wanted quarters of some kind on the cake. She took my number and informed me that the cake decorator would be calling me the very next day to let me know if my idea was viable.  I bought 2 cupcakes before I left: red velvet and pineapple pound cake, both deliciously topped with cream cheese icing (my favorite).  The red velvet was too cocoa-y, but the pineapple pound cake cupcake left me wanting more.  

Now, down to the reason Brown Betty didn't receive a full 5 stars from me.  They never called me back.  I called them back a week later, only to be informed by the same employee I spoke with before (NOT the decorator) that the decorator wasn't sure if she could do the design I wanted and would get back to me before the week was over.  The end of the week came and went, and there was still no word from the decorator.  I called Brown Betty back as soon as they opened on a Sunday, and (thank goodness!) the owner (Linda) answered the phone.  I told her my story and about the runaround I'd been getting from her employees.  She was not at all pleased and came up with a solution to decorator's dilemma in all of 2 seconds (chocolate coins instead of icing coins, duh!).  She quickly took my order, told me the price, and called me back like 15 minutes later to apologize again and confirm that I knew I had to purchase the chocolate quarters and bring them to the shop.  

At the end of it all, the cake came out beautifully.  It was sinfully sweet, dense just like pound cake should be, and the chunks of pineapple (because that's the flavor I went with) interspersed throughout the cake were a burst of juicy sweetness.  And much to my surprise, the piping on the cake was chocolate, to match the chocolate quarters they had used.  Out of all the fun that was had at my party, the cake was definitely the highlight.

So I'm going to co-sign on the sauteed chicken here. It's seriously moist and flavorful and was rightfully named, "magic chicken" by my coworker. For the hefty price of $13 you can get a small salad with sauteed chicken and this is quite enough food to share between two. 

If you're feeling particularly hungry, a small salad (with chicken obviously) and a pizza will be perfect and you may even go home with a slice or two for later. I've had the margherita and the barese (with sausage) pizzas, both of which were delicious. Their crust on the bottom is thin and crunchy, but the crust on the edge is soft and chewy - it's like an impossible miracle!

Dave is a great barber. After trying to find a true barber in the area, and going to most, I tried Dave's yesterday. I was very impressed. Dave gives a great cut, good price, and is a classic barber, a tradition that seemed to be dying. I'm glad it's not, and I'm glad it's within walking distance of me now. If you go to the typical chain, different haircut every time, type places, I suggest you stop now, and head on down to Dave's. You won't go back to those places. I'll be a longtime customer of this shop from now on.  Support a hardworking, local business!
Their regular pizza slices are average at best. However, their chicken bacon ranch and other speciality slices are pretty good

Honestly, this wasn't my intended destination BUT... I am so glad for the chance to try Pietro's.  I was blown away is an understatement. The crust was perfect and I consider myself a harsh critic.  The taste of the classic specialty pizza with the warm pesto and the sausage.  WOW!  Please, do yourself a favor - pick a cool evening, sit on the outside patio, and enjoy.  You can not be disappointed - I promise!  Service was a bit 'interesting' but I was very comfortable and enjoyed a leisurely dinner.
Good food is undeniable, if you're looking for comfort food with an bukharian twist then Shish Kabab Palace is the spot. Plov is a must, have it with LAMB RIBS & VEAL LIVER kababs. BYO and enjoy !

Kudos to whoever runs their Instagram and photographs the food. Unfortunately the reality of what we got was far from what enticed us to come here. 

Came on Sunday around 4:30pm, not busy at all but service was slow, even to get beers at our table when no customers were at the bar. 

Our appetizer came out about 3-5min before our meals were dropped at the table. The buns on both sandwiches were sad, poor quality that you'd expect at a backyard bbq. Clearly not the same as what's photographed for their Instagram.  The fries were an absolute tragedy, soggy and saturated with oil.  

Overall this was a total letdown.

I wish I had taken a pic but i ordered half pepperoni and cheese pizza the pepperoni side was sooooo wet with grease it was inedible. 

Cheese fries were fine

Ordered a cake for my daughter's birthday on 9/3/19.   It was to be delivered on her birthday on 9/9/19.  We received a cancellation notice that they cancelled the delivery.   We live in Chicago and our daughter lives in the Philly area.   When we called they said that grubhub was to balme and that we could come 'pick up' the cake.   A little hard to do when we live half way across the country.   As of today 1/22/2020 I am still calling and trying to get a refund.   I have been told on mulitple occasions that they are going to refund the almost $50 they owe me but no luck.   Glad that Buddy is making so much money on TV and from his fame.   But he surely has no customer serivice!

I have curly hair and have cared for in so poorly that I didn't even know what my natural curl looked like anymore. My friend told me that I had to go see Erin at Fringe, and I could not be happier that I took her advice. When I got there I told Erin my goal was to "feel confident wearing my curls to an event" (I always straighten my hair to seem more put together). She did that and more. I cannot wait to return! Every curly person in Philly and the surrounding area should come here.

I was pleasantly surprised with the food. I'm from LA and the food seemed more authentic than other restaurants around. But I didn't like the service. No combining meats, no exchanging one ingredient for another, and no extra salsa or asking questions about the food. The portions were small for the price. Food is a four-star but service a two.

The interior of this place is really cute and definitely fits in Rittenhouse. Went at 7 pm on a Tuesday and it was packed with business and happy hour crowds. The waitresses kept refreshing our waters without being invasive, which was nice!

I am a vegetarian and wanted to try their mushroom burger. The flavors were good, but I thought they focused too much on presentation--it has several clunky layers of 2 mushroom caps, 2 layers of grilled onions, lettuce, mozzarella, and large zucchini. I took out some onions and mushrooms and could actually take a bite, but the bun also fell apart as I ate it :/ would be willing to come back and try the vegetable stir fry though!

The food here is so good me and my kids go here all the time the prices are great as well one of the reasons Why i actually go, it's always packed when I go But for some reason I get seated right away! Oh and the lunch specials are Awesome
You have to love and respect "the church." Best place in Philly to see bands that matter. I got to see the last ever Atom & His Package show here. Ah, the memories...

I'm not sure what's in the water today but society is irking my nerves. So I show up at 4:45 to get a gel manicure/pedicure. The girl who I have never seen before, some young girl with glasses tells me that I can't get a manicure today because she has a 5:30 coming in. A gel manicure takes exactly how long?! like 20 mins. WHAT IN ALL OF THE HELL DOES THAT HAVE TO DO WITH ME?! The audacity of you to even ask me to come back another day. I liked this establishment because the job done here is decent and it's not crowded ever. And now I see why it's not crowded ever!!! 

I've come here a few times and have never heard of such stupidity in my life. There's two of you working here. So you're both dedicated to one procedure???? 

I now have to find a new salon. I don't have time for this shit. and I'm mostly irked that I had to walk out of a salon with chipped polish.

Wow.. this place is kind of crappy.  I pass it every day on my way back and forth to work and I've never stopped in until last night, because I really had to use the bathroom and didn't feel like going to Friday's.  

Having experienced a fantastic dive that people complained about earlier this week, I had high hopes for Mace's Crossing.  blah. 

It smelled weird, the bathrooms weren't clean.  I lost my appetite when I walked in, which wasn't helped by the nasty french fries that I was served.    The bartender was nice and attentive and the regulars at the bar watching that 7 hour phillies game seemed to be nice and friendly as well.
