The best part of the evening was when this old hippie came in with a large knitted jester's hat and a stuffed monkey strapped to his waist and took 20 minutes in the men's room.  Dunno what that was about but it made me giggle. 

I might go back if I'm drinking or something but I wouldn't go back for the food at all.

One of my favorite new spots!  Love the smoothies, the rice vermicelli bowl, and the Tang Tang Chicken.  Ordering takeout is fast, they're very accommodating with any changes/delays, and the owners/staff go out of their way to make sure you're good to go.

I was there last weekend to check out the new space upstairs and had a great experience.  The staff was attentive and the owner, Craig, even came to my table and checked in. The pizza and salad were as good as they have always been, and the new small plate menu does well in offering an expanded variety for sit down dinner guest. The beer selections are diverse and tasty and waiting at the bar and quaffing a Mother's Milk Stout, is not a bad way to wait for take out. Overall,  I was very pleased with the food and service and if you go, try the crispy artichokes, they are special.

First, I will start with the service. The service was great!! The host/manager could not have been more accommodating and was checking in on us throughout the night. Our server was very nice as well. 

Drinks: Good! Not very strong, but not too weak either. I like a strong drink and usually like to taste the alcohol in my drink a little, and I definitely could ~ever so slightly~ taste it in these drinks, so I was happy. I got the Finca Vigia, and my friend got the At The Grave of The Forgotten which I tasted, and I liked both! My other friends got the Hornet and a glass of Syrah but I did not try those, but everyone seemed to like them.

Now I will get to the food: We were a party of 6 here for a birthday, so we tried MANY menu items! I'd say the two best things we got were 1) the corn ravioli (amazing, I could drink the sauce, wish I just got 2 orders of this) and the 2) the octopus (my friend LOVES octopus and orders it everywhere she goes and said this was the best she's ever had). The other things that were well liked were the heirloom carrots, burrata (not the most amazing/special burrata I've ever had but you can't really go wrong with burrata either way) and the gnocchi. We also got the beets, and I typically LOVE beets but I didn't love these. They were fine but again lacked flavor and were nothing special. As for the fish... I got the salmon as my entree after the server talked it UP and made it sound absolutely amazing, and my friend got the tuna app. Neither of us were impressed. My friend said she thought the tuna had a bit of a weird flavor. As for the salmon, it was overcooked, tasted quite gamey, and I honestly feel like I make a better salmon at my apartment. I also didn't love the side it was served with (some kind of finely chopped broccoli/bell pepper/horseradish salad that was eh not great). The salmon was my least favorite thing that I ate. 

Desserts: since it was our friend's birthday, we tried all 3 desserts which were a pineapple upside down cake thing with ice cream, a chocolate cake thing with PB chocolate ice cream, and a panna cotta with berries. The best of the 3 was BY FAR the pineapple cake (everyone agreed). The panna cotta was pretty good and I think the second favorite all around. It was vanilla-y and light and bright with fresh berries. The chocolate cake was not great. Dry, lacking flavor big time, meh. 

Overall, this restaurant is definitely good and worthy of 4 stars, and I'd like to go back in the future! It has a great ambiance, great outdoor seating with HUGE umbrellas that can keep your party *mostly* dry in a light drizzle (depending on which side of the table you're sitting on), and great service. But there were a few menu items that were just not anything special and that should definitely be improved upon.

Starter - arugula salad
Primi - fusilli with crab and uma cream
Secondi - salt cod and potato purée with pesto of watercress. 

Nice cocktails before the meal. A wonderful armorone to accompany the meal


Though the servers changed and the sommelier was gone at the end of the evening the service was exemplary.  The food was really spectacular, I tried all of my dining companions' foods too and they were likewise remarkable. 

The bistecca was presented amazingly for a friend and tasted wonderful. If you eat beef, splurge and get it. Desserts were good but not memorable. 

The one caver is that it's hard to get in. The only reservation was for 9 and even sitting at the bar beforehand was very difficult. 

The decor was fashionable but the acoustic was kind enough for all of us to easily speak to each other in a busy space.

The owner mills his own flour, which I think is pretty awesome. Way to be in control of the quality of the food, down to the last detail!! 

I am a huge (and will be literally even more huge if I let my cravings for this place get the best of me) fan of their almond croissants, choco croissants, chocolate SPELT!! babka, and whole wheat bread. 

They don't have a huge menu, so you won't be overwhelmed, and they keep it simple and make very well what they offer. 

They also sell raw honey and fair trade coffee beans!

Great service as well! I didn't have my wallet or anything on me, but I was a part of their buyers' club, and they let me owe 15$ (I came back a week later to pay it, and added more money on my buyers' club account)

Best bike shop in the city by far.  Friendly knowledgeable service and they can get you just about anything you need for either navigating the city or hitting the trail.  Don't waste your time at other bike shops - do yourself a favor and just go here first!!

This place never disappoints. I always order delivery and the good is always on time and the options are delicious. The bad: It's a cash only place. 

I've had many different menu items and the vast majority have been delicious. My friend is a vegetarian and she loves the options they offer. 

This place is affordable. The service is great and the people are awesome. Def recommend!

Best hot pot place I have tried so far. This is a new place and they did a great job making the place look nice and modern. Also, they did a good job spacing out the tables so that it didn't get too loud and each table felt very spacious. It is not an all-you-can-eat but the prices are reasonable for the quality. The original milky broth and the spicy broth are both so good on their own and so flavorful. That's probably why this place doesn't need a sauce station for their hot pot. You can get half and half to try both broths. The waiter also helped us cut down our selection because we went a little overboard checking off items on their menu. He was so kind to do that because it was the perfect amount and we didn't end up wasting food or money. Throughout dinner the waitstaff was very attentive and made sure we had everything we needed. I am very happy this place opened up in Philly!
The furnace went off, wouldn't restart. Green air came out right away, diagnosed a broken starter, and fixed it the next day for $220. Excellent service would highly recommend.

I ordered food from grubhub and hours later I still hadn't received it. I called the restaurant multiple times, but no one ever answered. I had to stay on hold with grubhub customer service in order to get my money back for the food I never received.

I recently as of today went to get a cheese steak from Geno's First the people who work there was not all that friendly Then when I went to eat outside on their tables A Panhandler decided to come to me abou buying candy when I didn't want to be bothered he started calling me names I went to the owner's and asked them to take care of it You know what the told me it wasn't their problem it was the city sidewalk after u spent $22.00 for 2 sandwiches and $7.00 for Frues And $3.00 for a soda. What Nerve!!! Go to Pats Across the streetMuch better Service
Excellent service in and out quickly and not a long wait for the airport shuttle.
I have found Maria's to be the best place to dine. Delicious food at a reasonable price with a wonderful atmosphere and a expert staff.

This place was amazing. The main door looks like the front door of a house and then inside is a beautiful staircase which gives you a very home-like feel. Came here with a group of people on Wednesday night it wasn't busy at all. We did family style food so the owner brought out all of the main dishes and smaller portions and everything was delicious. The Jerk Wings had a kick to them and the chicken was spicy. This place is BYOB. The staff are so friendly it's a father-and-son who work here and their story is inspirational. I would highly recommend this place to anyone. It's Trinidadian food and certain dishes are spicy but it's so good.

A Starbucks, is a Starbucks, is a Starbucks.... and this one is a Starbucks.  That doesn't say much, but simultaneously says it all.  I'm a purist with my coffee and always just order coffee.... no frills.  There should be a separate line for people like me who order, they turn and fill a cup and we are gone.... I don't even need to go to the separate counter to pick-up.  But I digress... this was coffee and it was good.

10 out of 10 would recommend Tufano ! As first time homebuyers, getting our roof redone was our first major project. As nervous as we were, Rick and his crew made this process totally
Painless and easy. 

We called and had an estimate the very next day. They did not call and hound us about booking and were very patient with us while we got our money together and made our final decision to book. Once we called to book, they fit us on the schedule within weeks. 

That morning,the crew was right on time and did not waste a second. Before we could even get out there and greet them, they were already on the roof and right to work. We took our dogs out for the day to get away and let them work and by 3 o'clock, the roof was totally complete and it looks wonderful. They did a fabulous job cleaning up, even went up into the attic to sweep up any debris that was left. Even our neighbors commented on how hardworking the crew was throughout the day.

All in all, they were totally professional and diligent. Afterwards,Rick came out to make sure everything was right and we were happy. You can totally tell they care about their customers.  If we didn't have an amazing warranty on our new roof, we would call Tufano again and again. I will definitely be recommending them to family and friends.

The Westbury is a really good place to grab a few drinks when you feel like you can't afford to go to Valanni, or if you would rather have beer than cocktails. Much more low key than a lot of other bars in center city, good beer list, and VERY good prices especially considering the area that it's in.

Got food there once before and it is not good. The crowd can be a hair on the older/creepier side from time to time but all around it is a really fun low-key bar. A bartender was rude to me once before but I didn't see him the most recent time I was there, and all of the other staff are extremely nice an funny.

Got take out from here. First time in a long time. Honey BBQ wings-crispy on the outside and flavorful and juicy. Also got a special with fries and cheesesteaks. The fries were good and the sandwiches were good too. Chopped steak and a soft roll. I don't know about anyone else but we loved the wings and will be ordering from here again.

Amazing amazing amazing experience. From the moment we walked in the staff was phenomenal and communicative. Everyone is so invested in the customer experience and it shows. The food was delicious and perfect and my only regret is my teeny stomach couldn't hold anymore of their delicious food. I had plans for the rest of the day and I don't even care anymore if we do them or not because this one trip to this one restaurant was so worth it.

WORST HOOKAH BAR EVER!!!! If you have smoked good hookah you know this isn't it. Our hookah tasted burnt making us suspect that it was probably an old hookah where they add a little bit of fresh shisha. The staff is rude and don't try to help you if you tell them you have a problem. Instead they keep walking by you, look at you and ignore you as if they do not care. As a female, the staff hits on you and only assists you if you flirt with them. Been here a few times because it's open late, while hoping each experience would get better. Nope I was wrong! Disappointed for the LAST time!

Little Pete's is a bare bones, honest to goodness, homey diner. Tucked in the ground floor of an apartment building mostly populated by the elderly, this spot deserves a gold medal for diner food. Massive menu, cheap prices, waitresses that have been there forever, the works. We've been a few times after a night out and their breakfast hits the SPOT. The 2 pancakes, 2 eggs, & breakfast meats combo is perfect. Plus they have amazing milkshakes. You really cannot go wrong. If you are in Fairmount looking for a cheap eats spot, check out Little Pete's.

I was looking for a restaurant that would satisfy my foodie requirements but not be too intimidating to relatives visiting from the suburbs.  I loved this place.  The Iron Lady was the first excellent cocktail I've had in Philly.  I had the heirloom tomato salad, deviled eggs, and mussels.  Everything was very tasty and well-prepared.  I also REALLY appreciate the hostess who cheerfully granted my request for a quiet table so my elderly mother could hear the conversation.  She gave us the window table on the top floor, which was perfect.

i heart this place. We take my son here. I go on girls nights here. I go on date nights here. I go with small groups. Always a great time. The food is fabulous, I have loved everything I ever ordered. The cocktails are delicious, something for everyone. The atmosphere is cool, its a bit loud but its meant to be upbeat and casual. The outdoor seating is fun, situated under the blvd along the river. I usually order a la carte but the last time I went was 2 couples and we did the 3 course tasting with wine (for 4 obviously). It was more than enough food, we had leftovers. Great variety and it had some things I wouldn't normally choose that I may now order on my next visit. Our server is often the same guy and he is absolutely awesome. Im not sure his name, I know I should remember it for all the times he has taken care of us... He has an equal sign tattoo on his wrist and is really helpful when you cant decide, which happens a lot bc everything on their menu looks good. Go here now! You will not be disappointed!

Sorry but I wasn't impressed this year. There didn't seem to be as over the top displays like they had in the past. In fact, there seem to be fewer. All I saw were the plants and flowers that had been entered into their contest with their ribbons. The other were too many vendors. I don't want to buy wood flowers or miniature cactus that i can buy at walmart for half the price. I think it's overpriced for what you see. You can go to Longwood Gardens for half the cost and see much more. The only upside is we did see the butterfly experience but again an extra $5 a piece. We probably could have spent most of the day there.

I probably would only visit here on a Tuesday. The tacos are great for 2 dollars. I also tried their knock-off version of Pho; average (but don't answer them when they ask them how it taste because they won't show up again). It was probably my fault because when I said the Pho was average, they told it wasn't an Asian restaurant, then she told me it was her grandma's recipe. So is your grandma a bad cook or not Asian.

All that said they had good beer and the other waitresses were nice.

I found Dr. Rozanov through Yelp reviews. I had gone to another dentist in the city just a few times before our insurance changed, so I had to find a new one in a relatively short period of time. I was annoyed to be jumping from dentist to dentist because I think it's nice to have a relationship with the medical professionals in your life. I didn't think I could possibly have more good luck finding another dentist I liked as much. Turns out I did. Dr. Rozanov is friendly, gentle, knowledgeable, informative and actually fun. I had two appointments in quick succession - a new patient visit, followed in a couple weeks by my cleaning and the replacement of two older fillings - and both times, I had my questions answered thoroughly, I felt very comfortable and calm, and I was allowed to pick my own music. The office is spotless and high end, and he and his assistant Jess were able to get me in and out during my lunch hour, which was exactly what I was hoping for. I've already recommended Dr. Rozanov and Jess to a few other people in my office. Highly recommend.

This is great dive-like joint with cheap drinks and good, affordable food.  The service is not really good but I guess you shouldn't really expect too much out of a place like this.  It is kind of like Grace Tavern but bigger if you've ever been there in the Graduate Hospital area.  But Grace definitely has way better service.

One time, we just stood there and waited forever before anyone acknowledged us and came to take our order.  Another time, someone tried to squeeze in on the table next to us and dropped our drink and the waitstaff kind of gave us a dirty look and charged us for the drink even though we didn't drop it and it was pretty much full still.

You definitely come here for the burger!  The burger is amongst the best in Philadelphia.  They also have a lot of really good vegetarian options if you are vegetarian.  We've had brunch here and they seem to make their eggs with butter instead of oil, I prefer oil but that's just me.  As far as drinks, they have awesome Bloody Mary's!  All their other drinks including beer are well-priced and pretty good.

Last night was a real treat. Between the food, the ambiance, and the friendliness of Hendra, the owner, we felt like we spent a day on vacation!  The fish was fresh and tasty, and the prices were extremely reasonable for the quality. Hendra came to our table and shared with us his journey to America from Indonesia 16 years ago, not knowing a word of English and starting out in Manhattan of all places! Not only does he have a successful business now, he speaks awesome English and he even has a great sense of humor!. By the end of the evening, we felt we had made a friend. Hendra was so kind to us. NO DOUBT we'll be back. Hendra, we've joined "the club".  :-)

I love eating at Hibachi. The funniest part is when the chef is cooking and when he plays shrimp toss and u have to catch the shrimp in your mouth. I love their rice and their Steak and Shrimp and their chicken and fliet milgon is good too. The salad and the dressing is good too. I love when your looking at the view outside especially since we went there at night we saw the and the lights on the bridge it was sooo cool. It was pretty cool that my friend ate there and she had a good time. :o)

One of the best takeout lunch spots in Fairmount! I've tried both their sandwiches and their poke bowls and both are excellent and priced reasonably. The inside is also extremely clean and lovely if you want to dine in. The staff has always been friendly and speedy. Nothing but great things to say!
the best japanese style noodle house in Philly. have to try "九州男儿" perfectly mixed noodle with pork and vegs. chicken katsu don is also good. have come here often since 2012. keep it up

Picked up an order for four today at 1pm. Service was prompt and despite a few changes/additions to our sandwiches, our orders were made correctly. Always a tiny victory.

Prices have changed since they first opened. Aaliyah P, veggie sandwiches are now $1-$1.50 than meats. Good stuff! I enjoyed the Roman Calvary for $8.10. Doubly soft + chewy bread and a fine amount of meat + cheese + arugula. Frankly, I appreciate the fact that these sandwiches aren't overstuffed. They go down easy and don't leave you feeling like you inhaled an entire pig afterwards. 

It's a lovely walk from my office at Broad and Chestnut. I'll be back for sure.

30th Street Station is to Philly as Grand Central is to NYC. The main atrium for Amtrak still has the old school time display board that makes the click-clack-click-clack noise as the letters and numbers change to let you know of the train arrival/departure times. Off to one side of the main atrium are the shops/restaurants/vendors/post office and off to the other side are the connections to SEPTA regional rail, trolley, and market-frankford lines. There are also a few drop off areas for the cheap intercity coach bus companies (such as Megabus).

I am often asked where to switch trains to go to the airport and if they should switch at 30th Street Station. IF you are coming from the north / east and your train reaches Jefferson Station before 30th Street Station, I recommend switching to the airport line at Jefferson Station. Why? 30th Street Station is outdoors (can be cold or hot), has 6 train tracks, and if you need to switch train tracks you must go up/down stairs. Jefferson Station is indoors, has 4 train tracks, and the airport train is likely going to be coming on the same track/platform. IF you are coming from the west, you will reach 30th Street Station first and you will probably have to switch tracks regardless.

In the immediate area outside the station is a nice open plaza type of area with some food vendors and live music in the summertime. The best place for lunch is the food truck alley on Drexel campus. The Schuylkill River Trail is also easily accessible by crossing the Market Street Bridge. However, there's tons of construction going on right now, but I'm excited to see the new transformed area in the future. The conceptual images for the "30th Street Station District Plan" show alot of improvements to modernize the area!

Awesome bar. I came in latenight on a Wednesday a couple weeks back for a beer or two. My bartender was awesome and we had some nice small talk. So impressed with the place that I've already come back twice and brought two new people. Really loved their lavender cocktail. Can't wait to go back
Best lunch I've had in a while and they're super nice. This will definitely be my new spot for takeout.

Despite being a picky eater and not being able to eat everything I wanted to due to dietary restrictions, I have to say, this place continues to ROCK. The food is just unbelievably, consistently delicious and the decor is top notch - it doesn't get any better than this - probably my favorite Garces restaurant.

I am new to the area and my dog was attacked by another dog. I rushed her here and they saw her right away. Crystal, Judy, and the rest of the staff were so kind and compassionate. I have had animals my whole life and this the best vet experience I have ever had.

Also, I am sure people have had negative experiences here in the past judging by the other reviews, but this place seems lovely and the people kind and courteous. It seems they have taken great strides to improve their practice and it certainly shows.
Yum! Nothing out of the ordinary; but definitely a quality creamery along the infamous South Street we all love. The new starburst flavor was pretty good.

I received a livingsocial coupon for my birthday. Everything I had heard about supper had been positive so I was excited to give it a try. After it was all said and done, my whole experience just felt like one big chore. If you are going to get anything, get the water. The water was cold, but not too cold.
Very tasty food. Everything we tried was excellent. Some very well seasoned dishes, juicy meat and great service.
One of the nicer Dunkin Donuts but they close so early!  I don't get the point of a coffee shop not being open during the second time of day people need coffee most...late night!

They have the best mojito's I've ever had. The food is phenomenal with their flavored packed chicken. The jazz music is very relaxing. I love the bartenders there they really care about the quality of drinks they make. I also like that majority of the staff is black at this soul food restaurant. It really makes me feel at home when I'm there.

The prices are high, they seem to try to fill the market between P.A.T and Green Street Consignment and they have unique finds. I shop there at least once a month but I had a bad experience recently. I found a great pair of shoes that were dated November 2015. According to information I found out from helpful store staff on a previous visit, when an item in the store is dated 2 months or older, it is reduced by 50%. Knowing this I asked about this but the clerks at the store that day were clueless and refused to honor the store practice. I went back about a week later and the same shoes were there but the older stickers were removed and updated to March 2016 so shoes that have been in the store for 4 months are back to full price. This is bad form and anti-customer, buyer beware.

I hesitate to give Zahav 5 stars based on just one dinner. Normally I like to try a place a few times before I give it my highest praise. I'm going to make an exception because my meal there was amazing (food, service and atmosphere) and at $36 for the tasting menu it is probably one of the best bargains in town. 

On a Wednesday night at 7:30pm Zahav was bustling with mostly young professionals. Immediately the place felt warm and cozy despite the high ceilings and large wall size windows on one side of the dining area. Lighting and other smart design choices do wonders for what could easily be a cold feeling space.

Things started out well with the hummus and laffa (flatbread). Without question I have never had hummus as good before. 

Next came Salatim, a selection of 8 small salads ranging from chickpeas to shredded beats. Not all of them were knockouts, but all were at least pleasant and flavorful.

Four small plates came out in rapid succession following the salad course. All were delicious. The fried cauliflower will make even the most hardened carnivore happy. The Moroccan veal cheek pastilla with dried fruit was a perfect combination of sweet and savory.  The mixed offal were tender and served with a charred onion sauce. The Yemenite fried flatbread paired nicely with a sauce made out of sweet crushed tomatoes.

The last two plates were lamb tenderloin and chicken (both grilled over coals) served with rice and couscous respectively. Neither dish was exceptional, but both were very good. 

Dessert failed to disappoint. The roasted peach with cashew baklava and white chocolate panna cotta was heavenly. So was the semolina cake with passionfruit and coconut sorbet.

Huge fan of this little spot! The last time I ate here I had an Al Pastor taco, and it was bomb. I've had all their tacos except the vegetarian one because, well meat.. although I'm sure it's delicious. 

I also love their chips and salsa. Their Guac is good, and the salsa is really to die for. I could eat that with just a spoon, nice and chunky. My only complaint is that their a tad pricey, but hey they're also the only place in center city to grab a quick taco so I'm okay with it!
Waited over an hour and twenty for our food to come out. Food was great but no compensation for awful wait, no water refills, and slow service. Sorry it was college game day, but prepare beforehand.

This place is a true definition of the sandwich shop can't go wrong with any of the sandwiches on the menu roast pork being one of my favorites, I think the cheesesteak is underrated, definitely want to go to this place when you're near the Italian market

Our whole crew the entire way was absolutely incredible! From the young woman selling us our tickets, to the people checking tickets for you to get on the bus, our guides, and our drivers; everyone was great. I can't say enough good things about them. The tour itself was very fun! Kudos to them for being able to drive in this traffic because even in my small car I don't think I could stomach it.
If you're going to the zoo you get off at the rocky steps and change buses (don't worry, they tell you this!!). Our guide Ray taking us to the Zoo was AMAZING! With the Covid-19 hysteria going on he called them before dropping us off to make sure they were still open.  Thank you, thank you, thank you, Ray, for being so kind, funny, and personable! 
We had a great time. Wish it ran longer but they weren't kidding when the city shuts down at 5.
I recommend if you're touring the city and going to multiple attractions get the Go Philly passes! They can save you money and we had 0 problem using them here.

I've always been a big fan of City Tap. While I do think that it's pricey - especially for a place that's located on a college campus - the food has been consistently solid when I've visited.

I ordered the mimosa flight and the Big Chick. The mimosa flight came with four juices (cranberry, grapefruit, and two oranges) and a bottle of champagne for $15. I ended up splitting the cost of the flight with two other friends, and it was more than enough for us to drink our fill. 

The Big Chick was a star. I substituted the hash for French fries (I firmly believe that City Tap House's fries are some of the best at Penn), and was pleasantly surprised by the enormous size of the Big Chick, which is a huge chicken cutlet covered in gravy and served between two biscuits with a fried egg and bacon. It was as delicious as it sounds, and I walked away from the meal feeling extraordinarily full and satisfied.

TLDR: pricey, but a nice place. Food is great, drinks are solid, and the atmosphere leaves you wanting more.

I was a fan of Brickbat Books when it was Big Jar Books in Old City. It moved to Queen Village and became Brickbat, and now that I'm in Bella Vista, I'm happy to have my favorite used bookstore so close by. 

I love Brickbat because its shelves look like my shelves. I feel at home with their oh-so-carefully curated selections and want to take each of them home (with the exception of the ones I already own of course). And because their shelves look like mine - I trust Brickbat's suggestions - I trust that everything that I grab off of their shelves will be enriching in some fashion - and it always has been. 

The store is small and to someone just haphazardly walking in, it may be momentarily baffling. It's almost like a gallery, with a significant portion of shelf space used to display individual books. There are no piles of yet-to-be-processed items and you don't have to turn your head every which way to read the spines of books placed upside down, sideways and backwards on the shelves. But spend some time with the shelves and you'll end up walking out with an arm full of books.

This is going to be my new go to lunch place! Fresh ingredients and I can get as many as I want to on my salad for no extra charge! That is unheard of in this city! Can't wait to try their pizza. 

Customer service was excellent!

The only downside is that the setup is not the best and it's kind of difficult when there are a ton of people but that's an easy thing to ignore, especially if you're getting take out.

Fine place to stop before you get on the el or go to the bar. There is an endless cast of characters behind the counter, some have teeth and some do not.

Weird things happen there too, last Friday the owner was rip roaring drunk and giving things away. Sometimes they will get thier tobacco license taken away and not have cigarettes.

Come for the 1.50 surcharge atm, and a huge drunk hoagie.

Always wanted to take my son here.  We finally went today (Friday afternoon before Christmas...busy but not crazy).  I wasn't expecting gourmet, but I was expecting some level of quality for the money.  I had the banana split waffle.  Don't do it.  I didn't mind the small portion at all, but the waffle was so dry and awful, an Eggo woulda tasted better.  My 9-year-old had a hot chocolate (pretty good, but more kid friendly milk-chocolate-y than high quality chocolate with any real flavor) and a brownie sundae served in a huge cake baking tin (kind of a mess).  Again, dry brownie-no taste - a little ice cream and lots and lots of whipped cream.  
For $36? Not goin' back.

I don't know where I stand on my star rating, right now. How can I get 3 Pink Lady apples and they all be rotten inside? I went to Bottom Dollar and got 3 (for a lesser price) and they were DELICIOUS! I'm not one to return fruit, because you just never know, but man.... I was disappointed!

Oh yeah and I finally made it to the deli without there being 90 people waiting in line. There was a lady ahead of me and one elderly lady after me. All of us were ordering the white turkey breast $2.99/lb (you can't beat that) and when I ordered my pound, I guess the lady slicing it took half of it and gave it to the lady after me. So when they gave me my full pound, the lady after me didn't realize what happened and thought it was hers. So she said,"Is that mine?" And the lady at the slicer basically bit her head off with a,"NO! That's not for you." (add heavy angry accent) I almost said something, but I wanted cheese so I didn't wanna push it. I do notice how friggin slow they run those slicers, but maybe it's a good thing because these angry Russian ladies slice meat better than those young kids at another deli that begins with the letter J and ends in ulianos. :)

So I purchased a Level-Up coupon for this place and when we went last Wednesday we were told we could not use it as it was Center City Sips night. There is no limitation on the coupon or on their website so this was not a god start. We figured we were already here, the prices were not bad so we stayed. The place was packed by 5:30 pm. We were told the specials and ordered a drink. Our server advised of a half price pizza special and when we saw them coming out of the kitchen we had a good laugh. They were $5 for a pizza that was the size of a saucer. We ordered the Hummus plate and a pizza with prosiutto. The hummus wasn't exactly hummus but wasn't bad as it was served with veggiies and toasted bread. The pizza was one step above carnival pizza. I am a pizza snob when it comes down to it but yuck! This place seems like a good place to hang out and have a couple of drinks and a bite to eat if you are already drunk. I'm stil stuck with this Level-Up coupon so I guess we will have a return trip.

Hands-down the best brunch in Philadelphia--and better than anywhere I've gone in NYC, too. 

The food is spectacular. My wife and I are foodies, and we could eat the Le Chef omelet (egg whites, hummus, black beans, avocado, pico de gallo, and fried cauliflower, with incredibly fresh fruit on the side) every day of the week, but everything we have ever tried there has been unreal.

Just as important, the staff and atmosphere are incredible. In particular, co-owner Gabby is the greatest: warm, worldly, endlessly energetic and hilarious. He treats every customer like a personal guest, and he treats his regulars like family. One in a million. We love him.

This is one of those places I'm reluctant to publicize, for fear of it getting even better known, but since they put their customers' needs first in every way, I think it's only fair to put their needs above mine here!

Go now! What are you waiting for?
-Josh and Carly

By far my favorite dessert is cannolis, but they are very easy to get wrong judging by what most restaurants and cafes serve. Many places fill them all in advance so the cookie gets soggy, or over-sweeten the filling so that it tastes overwhelming. Isgro is one of the few that does it right-if memory serves you can get them filled with your choice (mascarpone, ricotta or ricotta with chocolate chips) right there (they do have pre-filled ones as well, or maybe I just got lucky with the fresh filling?) Di Brunos is my favorite though still, even though they have fewer options.

I ordered food tonight and paid for it via my debit card and the delivery person never showed up. I work at the CH hospital, so after waiting more than 2hours and trying to call 10:45pm they did not answer their phone. I am pregnant and disappointed. Why did they steal my money? They better refund me in the morning!!!!

I will start out by letting you all know that I was already drunk by the time we got to Lucy's and had to wait in line...(wtf is up with a line at Lucy's?!)  I have to say I believe this was what added to my enjoyment bc once my buzz started to wear off I was seriously dissapointed.  

I began going to Lucy's when I was in college.  It was one of our go-to bars.  That was a much happier time...there was a warm and cozy feel with couches along the walls which had bookshelves filled with old classics.  It was as if you were drinking in the study of an old mansion or something.  I had only been there once before since like 2005, and saw they were making changes, now they have gone to far.  They got rid of the pool tables and put in another bar in the back...I can understand logistacly why they would do that, but it totally takes away from the fun and charm.  It is cramped and crowded and once I was sober not too much fun.

I cant praise this place enough.  These guys are the best.

By far the friendliest parcel place I have even been a customer of.  They will go out of their way to make sure you are happy..

Prices are cheap and their hours are great too.

One of the BEST lunch spots out there! High quality, delicious Indian fusion. But they still do the traditional stuff really really well.

Their express lunch for $15 is basically a steal. It comes with an appetizer and an entree. For appetizers, you can choose a chaat, salad, soup, or one of their super yummy fusion plates. 

Appetizers I like:
-The drums of heaven (battered chicken wings with blue cheese crumble, garlic, tomato, chili and scallions) are so perfectly flavored. Crispy on the outside, juicy and tender on the inside. And you get 4 of these amazing things
-Naan pizza! They have different flavors all the time. The one I tried had paneer, red peppers, red onions, herbs, and mozzarella
-Barbeque pork spare rib samosa rolls, you get 2 rolls cut into 4 pieces in total. It comes with a super yummy dipping sauce of tamarind barbeque and blue cheese wine.
-Stuffed long hot peppers. You get 2 big ol' smoked green peppers, stuffed with house-made paneer, mozzarella, deghi chili, spring onions, with a fresh tomato sauce on the bottom.
-Aloo makkai tikki are these 2 yummy spiced potato cakes with corn and apple chutney. 
ALL SUPER TASTY!

Entrees:
-The best one is chicken tikka makhani (butter chicken). The chicken is apparently free range, and obviously delicious. the sauce is beautifully spiced, and tastes right. Not like the nasty overly creamy ones I have to eat in New Jersey :( It's served with fenugreek, black pepper, saffron rice, which is so delicious, but still needs to be drowned in the beautiful makhani sauce.
- Lamb rogan josh is also really good! It's a less interesting tasting sauce in my opinion, but still delicious. Kinda like a lamb stew but spiced with an Indian flair. The lamb had a great, tender texture, and the sauce was so hearty and yummy. This also came with the awesome herb rice.

Overall, an awesome date place!! The ambiance is really nice, roomy, clean, and dimly-lit. The $15 express lunch is just unreal.

I had pretty high expectations going into my dinner at Cochon last night, it's been getting so much praise. And it was a good dinner, don't get me wrong. if you like french food (heavy heavy HEAVY) you will like Cochon. But a couple aspects of the dinner just bugged me all night, and that combined with a hefty price tag has left me with 3 stars for this BYO. I'll just run through the dinner...

Before we even ordered, the first disappointment happened: no specials. I can't even remember the last time I went to any restaurant that didn't have SOME special. I'm sure this is not the norm at Cochon, but we were pretty unhappy, especially because we heard they had very creative specials.

Apps: I had quail stuffed with foie gras, she had rabbit ragu on some pasta with a fancy name that I can't remember (on some level, isn't it all just pasta? Where do all these weird names come from...) My quail was very good, cooked well, quite tasty. I'm not even a big foie gras fan, but this was goooood. The rabbit ragu went over well, but we totally had the exact same dish at James not too long ago. Not sure whether chicken or egg came first on that one.

Main course: Probably the biggest stumble of the night. I had 36-hour short ribs. I fancy myself a bit of a rib connoisseur, so I was excited. These were most definitely subpar. I mean, not bad ribs I guess, but they were not very tender, certainly not 36 hour tender. I can make ribs more tender than this in 6 hours on my Weber in my back yard. I also had some 36 hour short ribs at Daniel Boulud's restaurant in Vegas not horribly long ago, and they were 1000 times better. My fiancee has the pot-pie of the day which was made with boar. I think more than anything this was just a poor choice for her, not that it wasn't necessarily made well. The boar was quite gamey, a little too much for her. The pot pie was also steaming hot for like 15 minutes, which made it kind of hard to eat. The whole main course was just "meh." 

Dessert: Best part of dinner. I had an apple-cinnamon bread pudding which was awesome, and she had creme brulee which was also incredible. A nice rebound after a subpar meal.

Overall, I don't know ... maybe my expectations were too high. I think if this meal hadn't cost 100 bucks, it would've definitely been 4 stars, but the steep price tag is forcing my hand on the 3 stars.

Love this place. Even though the name is better suited for a gay bar ;) 
The cave feel is awesome. The crowd is just about as diverse as you can get in the city. Their  drum & bass nights are a must go on the second Friday of the month. They do have a $10 cover. They have pretty good drink specials If you get there before 11pm, but it's a good idea to pre-game beforehand. Also, another tip. Watch the stairs leading to the dance floor. They can be hard to see and as I've experienced myself just about every time in there, very easy to fall on.

Just went on Friday for lunch, it's still really good. Holy goat cheese frittata and the stuffed challah French toast AMAZING...It's sooo good. Service, meh. It wasn't busy for that kind of lack of attention. Food is still good. I'll definitely be back though.

I'm very happy with the recent bike purchase experience at Performance Bike. The selection of bikes is large. They have everything that one could ask for regarding biking! 

My personal standout experience at this bike shop was with the top mechanic Mike M. He was a down-to-earth regular guy. Even though the place was busier than a long-tailed cat in a room full of rocking chairs, Mike M took the time to explain to me things that mattered most regarding my new bike.  People were tugging at him and firing questions at him and he didn't get flustered once. He and the rest of the store appeared very busy.  His knowledge stems from years of personal experience and many miles of riding. 

I hope this store will be here for a long while, but I'm really hoping that Mike M stays part of the team because he is the reason I'll be back for all my service needs.
Very good sandwiches, great beer selection.  Except for those who are new to the team, they all seem to have good knowledge about their food and a good many of the brews, to boot!

Another unique experience this past weekend. I was here Saturday to see the movie Ted. When we got to the movie, there was a man in the back sleeping and snoring. It was pretty funny. You couldn't hear him while the movie was on, but between previews and at quiet moments between scenes, you could hear him snoring away. Everyone was cracking up. He was still passed out snoring when the movie was over. I am not sure what was funnier- the movie or this guy snoring. I am assuming that they don't check the theaters between every movie because this guy was really out.

Wonderful decor, authentic Latin American food and impeccably attentive service. I fell in love with this place right as I walked in the door. I grew up in South Florida, so the architecture was familiar to me and I felt transported to tropical climes.

All of our food was exceptional and prepared to perfection. 

We also ordered mango and guava juices (with milk) and Sangria.  All three were delicious. 

Note the bill comes with a 15% gratuity, however I gladly added more. 

This restaurant is a real treat and rather unique. Highly recommended!

Dry, overpriced cakes. I've tried more than once with the same result each time. 80$ for what tasted like 4 day old cake. Terrible. Definitely don't taste as pretty as they look!  Sugar cookies are good but, DON'T waste your money. Right down the street is a lovely bakery with more reasonably priced moist cakes and other pastries.

haul zing is absolutely amazing.

i hatehateHATE moving - i moved 5x over 6 years & had incredibly stressful experiences each time, the final time with a moving company sent from the depths of hell to torment humanity (see 1 of my 3 past reviews). it was traumatizing, but it was also my bad: i didn't check yelp first & was seduced by a low quote. but i learned my lesson: this time i checked & rechecked yelp, carefully picked a company, & was rewarded for my due diligence.

from the phone set up to the move, things could not have gone more smoothly & stress-free. at first they were all booked up for moving season since i called only a few days before my move, but as soon as a spot opened, eddie contacted me to squeeze me into their extremely busy schedule & set up a quote over the phone that was incredibly reasonable.

my movers russell & daniel were just great - they arrived a few minutes early, were incredibly friendly (& entertaining), professional, and worked very efficiently. eddie had estimated my move to take 1.5-2hrs, and it took 1hr 45min. all my stuff arrived in perfect condition.

i have to move again in about 2 weeks but i'm not even stressed anymore cuz i will definitely be using haul zing again & i know i'll be taken care of!

Quaint little restaurant on the side streets of Chestnut Hill. The wait staff is great. They have good vegetarian options, but the portions of some entrees were small. I ordered the tofu scramble, which was not great; I should have ordered the breakfast burrito, which is what my wife had and I was jealous - but she shared.

I enjoyed a celebration here wirh five friends and truly enjoyed the experience. The restaurant is an iron four masted bark rigged grain ship from the last century!

The atmosphere is elegant. Wooden decks and glowing lamps warm you up for a dining experience. We were greeted cheerfully and guided to a table in the stern section. 

The menu is very simple but has a fine selection of seafood and some turf for those who enjoy land more. We began with a fine bottle of merlot and two dozen oysters from Olympia oysters Washington State, some Calimari and a a plate of octopus. That was just starters!

Moving on to mains, I enjoyed immensely the Bronzono A La Plancha while others had salmon and lamb. We all started oh my'ing and ah'Ing of each other's dishes and forced samples onto each other. 

Last was a glass a scotch and a walk about the main deck to enjoy the view of the Delaware River. 

So satisfying.

Had really delicious barbacoa this morning at this location. Also had the consome and tasted my friends quesadilla. For me the star of the show was really the tortillas - so delicious!! Also got to chat a little with Cristina Martinez and she was super friendly. It was a great experience. Be sure to try the coffee as well- really delicious café de ollo!

it's byob! We didn't even order much but It comes with so many sides. Nice ambiance , friendly staff. 100% would recommend and go back. Food was really good, tasted fresh. I have had better but this feels like the perfect home cooked meal.

This is one of the cutest and tastiest bake shops in Philly. The owners are so down to earth and they take pride in their ingredients and their products. The flavors of their desserts are some of the best. I definitely recommend stopping by and trying some of their desserts

Dry aged beef.  A proper steakhouse MUST have dry aged meat and the servers should be educated on it.  This place has not one type of dry age, but several.  And they are ALL good.

Service is professional.  Crowd and decor is a bit trendy and perhaps too young.  I'm accustomed to steakhouses in NYC, where tradition goes a long way.  Having said that, I did not mind the posh ambience, simply because the servers treat you with the same sort of respect and chivalry of a 1940s establishment.

Not cheap.  But if you want cheap, you don't want or know quality steak.
Its outdated, the weights are from the 1980s and the power lifting area is tiny- however, staff is awesome and if its not crowded like the one time I went you get a throwback old school workout

There was dried urine on my toilet, lots of it.  Clearly, the housekeeping staff is either rushed or care free; in either case, it's not good for the traveler.  There is a long wait to have your car retrieved by the valet despite the $42 per day parking fee.  Also, wireless is quite slow.  This is also the only hotel I've ever stayed at where the room did not have a Gideons Bible.  I wonder why.  The location is excellent.  That's about all that the Sonesta has going for it.

YUP!  The reviews are on the money. This place is the real deal y'all. 
And on WEST Passayunk. For fellow South Philadelphians, you got a bit tired, maybe a wee bit jaded with the East Passyunk vibe, wonderful at times, not so much at other times!  This place is on the other side of Broad St. Great Spot! 
Ordered take out and we walked over and picked it up. 
Love the outside and the open kitchen. 
I think it's a Marra Forni oven. Straight from Italy. 
The owners are part of why this place is special and gets awesome reviews. 
We hung out a bit waiting for the food. 
The Margherita pizza is exactly what you expect and crave. 
I also had to try the Pide. (You won't pronounce it right. I butchered it and the owner told me how to say it properly. I practiced a few times and promised I would show how to say it phonetically, but I forget....  sorry). Never seen anything like it before in the pics. So just had to try it. The spicy sausage one. A Moroccan folded contraption with spicy lamb sausage from South Africa. Topped with tabouleh. The whole thing is incredible. GET YOUR OWN. I can honestly say, I did not wanna share. (I did, I did, but I could've destroyed the whole thing by myself quite happily). 
Gotta remember to make reservations next time.  Def

This place is amazing!  Everyone is extremely nice and pays very close attention to detail.  My first time here was about a month ago.  I got a pedicure and gel manicure for an upcoming trip.  I got them done on a Saturday then the following Monday I had chipped my nail.  It was completely my own fault, but because I had just gotten them done I wanted to get it fixed.  Not only did Angel completely replace my chipped nail, she replaced another nail that was slightly chipped (I didnt even notice) and did a new top coat on all other nails for free!  They have great customer service and I have been back many times since then

SILK CITY! I Used to love you, but now I only come for the mac n' cheese, the fried chicken, or roast beef sandwich. Brunch is good as well. But some of the food is just okay and I feel like they need to pay more attention to quality and taste. I had pierogies here once and they were overwhelmingly flavorful, so much so that I had a headache.

Let's talk about their cocktails, they are not good. I haven't had one cocktail here where I go "Yeah, yup.. gonna get drunk on these they are so good." Nope, even the most basic drinks taste awful! I ordered a gin & tonic while my friend ordered a margarita. Mine was overpowered with gin while hers was WAY TOO tequila infused and salty. Not like the bartender had just lined the rim with salt but had thrown a handful of salt into the drink. Gross, tasted like the ocean. Now specialty cocktails, during the winter I had one of their spiked apple ciders that included SNAP liquor. I thought "They can't mess this one up, can they?" Nope, they did. It was so bad, the flavors did not harmonize what-so-ever.

In terms of parties, Thursday nights Mo' Money No Problems is awesome. Besides that I've only been here a handful of times for various events and they are all super fun.
Delicious fast food! We got the Chicken Kheema roll, the lamb roll, and the chicken korma rolls w rose and mango lassi - super tasty and friendly staff
Good selection of higher end comfortable shoes (Birkenstock, Camper, UGG, Mephisto etc). On the pricey side but they have a great sale section!

for me, there are really only two knocks on this place. The hours (don't open until 4PM) and the fact that they are cash only. However, looking past those issues, the experience was great. I walked in to place an order for take out. The staff was very friendly, and checked up on me and kept me updated on my order (they were very busy at the time). The pizza came out of the oven, was put into the box, cut and given to me very quickly. That was appreciated, as I hate when pizza sits in the open air too long. 

when I got home, I could immediately tell I was in for something special. The crust was thin, crispy and the pizza had a nice sauce to cheese ratio. I ordered a half pepperoni and half sausage and the toppings were plentiful but not overdone. The pepperoni, in particular, was fantastic. The sauce has a mild sweetness, but it was not overpowering. It was really a very fantastic pizza and a nice change of pace to the standard take out pizza that popularizes the northeast Philly area.

One thing I will make special mention of is this. While waiting for my pizza order, four customers came in. the people running the establishment knew them by name, and even knew what they would be ordering. This is a sign of a place that has a lot of repeat business. also, when the people dining in were paying, the first thing asked was if they enjoyed their meal. 

Will definitely be back.

It's a post tailgate tradition for my friends and myself to end up at a pho place instead of at the actual game (beer will do that to ya....). Being Temple University's homecoming weekend, this was no exception especially when my friend was visiting for the weekend! 

After dancing our butts off at Xfinity Live... we needed food. Food that was going to be a short walk or Septa ride away especially. I suggested pho to my friends and they all hungrily agreed! We hopped on the BSL Northbound from the newly named NRG Station and basically ran to here because we were FAMISHED. 

We decided on Pho 75 because the reviews looked pretty solid and my Vietnamese friends from Philly said great things about here. I've spent my whole life eating pho (the Cambodian version at least, which is essentially the same IMO) so I like to think I'm able to distinguish between a subpar bowl and a bowl made with actual love and ample umami. 

We were seated and honestly, the rest of my dining experience was a blur but Pho. 75. F**king. Goddamn. 

I'll be honest: I don't remember what I got or what my friends got but all I remember is my initial reaction once I started eating my pho.

I didn't know my body NEEDED pho until I slurped my first spoonful of the broth. I was in absolute heaven. I didn't know a bowl of noodles could cure every possible minor issue in my life until I ate at Pho 75 (kidding, kinda!). That was all it took for me to devour a bowl in a scary under-ten minutes time. The broth is everything you want from pho and what usually lacks at many other pho places throughout the city. The meat was tender, complimenting the noodles and broth. It can be hard to find a place that ensures that the broth AND the meat are delectable and Pho 75 knows the secret. 

Nothing about my meal was below amazing. I wish I had taken a picture of how empty my bowl was because you bet I ate every edible piece in it and drank all the broth. I haven't done that with a bowl of pho in years until now because it was seriously that good until the last drop. 

Philadelphia has the highest Vietnamese population on the East Coast (unless Wiki lied to me) and it shows through some of the amazing places that really represent the good of the country's culture such as Pho 75. What better way to enjoy a culture than to eat the labor of love that goes into making pho? 

(I did not know that Anthony Bourdain ate here and loved it, according to past reviews. I love this place even more now.)

Hi energy, a barista with an accent, and fast service make this place a win for anyone on a walk about of old city. New artwork is common around here. Open doors and windows let the air in and keep things fresh. 

This is a great place for a fast coffee, breakfast or lunch and can very easily support a slow morning.

The pasta and sauce were fine but not good. The price is only slightly less than a typical Italian restaurant so I would expect the quality to be the same since the service is takeout quality. I won't be coming back.

I really like coming to this Target, it's close to where I live.  I'm taking a star off my original 5 star review because recently I had an unpleasant interaction when checking out at the register.  The manager was very apologetic and said he would address things properly with the cashier.  I like the Target on 19th St better, much better customer service but they're a little further from where I live and that store has a good return policy.  They do have great prices and the selection is good.  I'm thinking of going to 19th St more often but I'll try this Target again.  The cashiers here could be more professional and friendlier.

Terrific dinner! I couldn't decide what to order because it all looked so good. To solve this problem our server recommended the house menu which has a little bit of several courses. Every bite was delicious! Great service and terrific food. In spite of the crowd all staff members were pleasant and knowledgable. Thank you for a wonderful and friendly dining experience.

Foods ok, magaritas are ok, everything is just OK.  Thats the problem though, nothing memorable.  It's nice and convenient if you're on south st and don't feel like the typical cheesesteak/pizza/ice cream vibe but you could walk east a couple blocks or off south street one block to Bainbridge and get better food.  Had the tacos and the beef was very, very, dry.  The corn shell tortilla tasted like freezer burn...YIKES.  I'd say for the location there are better options.  Service has always been fine and has never been an issue.

Tried this place for the first time,  quality of the lamb chops was dry and darn near burnt.  The rice was blatantly dry as well...and still uncooked and the salad was warm and wilted.    Never again will I order from here.    This looks like well beyond an off night.   This place is totally blacklisted to me.  Try another Mediterranean restaurant.   And using burrito shells instead of pita bread?  Thanks for ripping me off.
We just happened to find this place. The food was great, the beers were better, and the service was outstanding. The bonus is that they had real English pub food. Try the sticky toffee pudding.

Best steakhouse in town. A bit pricey but worth every morsel !!

Steak was cooked to perfection and our server Amy was amazing !!

We just moved in a couple months ago, right cross street with Liberty Pizza. The owner is a great person, always friendly, very nice service. The food there is also pretty good, you can find almost everything with a reasonable price.

The staff there powered through a pretty long line without losing their cool and we actually got our food pretty fast. I had the Philly steak which was super filling and delicious. I only needed half of it though.
Awesome food and service.  Especially liked the avocado dish, the tofu dish, asparagus dish...basically they were all excellent!   Highly recommend.

Changing the scenery for dessert in Center City is a great idea. And, with Max Brenner this is a must.  The look is awesome and the desserts are incredible.  With chocolate being mixed in multiple places throughout the restaurant and excellent desserts being served, it's a great place for a first or future date.

We had the Banana Split waffle and a Kangaroo cup espresso.  Chocolate is of course the theme throughout all the options on the menu.  Therefore, chocolate came in a small lab-like glass and is intended to be poured over the waffle - which we did do.  I like the combination of the food being prepared yet I get to be the one to pour the chocolate so I feel like the dessert is custom to my liking.

A great place overall.

I don't live nearby but the many times I am in Fishtown I always stop in to stock up on staples. Their prices are fair. Their checkout lines are always well staffed. 
Aisles are generally free and clear of excess items. The store layout makes sense( at least to me).
Every-time I shop here there is one item that is so greatly priced on sale that I leave with a cartful. Last year it was name brand coffee. I purchased enough for a year and saved 40%. 
Today it was freshly picked and flash frozen name brand vegetable steamers. Now a cart full of that would've been ridiculous but I can tell you I left here with 20 steamers that will fit in my freezer. And I easily saved $20. I have never seen this particular brand so cheap so I'm guessing the owner of the store has a good " In" with a distributor. And this is not an isolated occurrence as I see this every time I shop here.
If you want a place to shop for reasonably  priced items where shopping and the checkout process is a breeze, you've found the place here!

Came here to get fitted for a wedding suit. Had to return 4 times, after the forgot my size and ordered the wrong suit on the first attempt, had the wrong size jacket on the second attempt, had the left sleeve too long on the third attempt, and finally needed the suit to be altered again on the fourth attempt, all of which could have been done on the first and only visit to be measured. 

The people that work here are no better than used car salesman. they will smile, shake your hand and tell you the suit looks great on you. Meanwhile, they cannot measure correctly, don't know a thing about customer service, and do not take blame for their mistakes. They didn't offer me a free tie, or a pair of socks, but instead suggested I must have gained weight.

I am hoping this place goes out of business soon because any fool that walks in here will leave a bigger fool.

This place was awesome! Never had a cheesesteak this good! It was worth the drive into Philadelphia from south Florida. It's a nice little hole in the wall place that felt like a local spot.

The only thing I wouldn't recommend is the old bay fries... They are a little overwhelming on the old bay.

Overall OK! Appearance was nice however their level of service was nothing notable (not saying it was bad just not impressionable). We wanted to change rooms because we needed a kitchen and they were accommodating since it was the same price and available. 3 stars for great view, location, decent presentation, and breakfast options. Not a 4stars because they did nothing really to stand out and we used valet parking and upon getting the car back it smelled like marijuana. We did not call to complain but decide we will just go somewhere else next visit. Not a bad place to stay just need to step things up to match competition.

If I could give 7 stars, I would. This was THE best dining experience we have had in Philadelphia...and we eat out a lot. From the very beginning the service was excellent. They personalized menus for our anniversary, brought complimentary champagne, appetizer, and dessert. Each staff that dropped off food said Happy Anniversary....and I haven't event mentioned the food yet! Talk about an incredible meal. Each dish we ordered was absolute perfection from presentation to the first bite. The brisket was magnificent and short rib left me wanting more. This is a very very special please in our hearts and I am already wanting to go back. Absolutely incredible and highly recommend to all. Thank you for a fantastic evening.
Shoutout to Mattea- fantastic waitress!

I like this place and so does my pet.  The people who work there are friendly and really have a passion for our furry friends. The selection is awesome (awesome if you feed ur pet premium food) and if they don't have your brand, they will be happy to special order it!  I like the resident cat who hangs out there too.  He often sits in the window so if you are walking by, be sure to say "hello" to him!

We called John Synder Plumbing based on his yelp reviews. Of coarse there's always a few sour eggs in the reviews, but we over look those who just don't get it. John responded very quickly and had his team out to the house the very next day. Our case was not an emergency so there was no need for immediate response. We had some blockage issues and John himself was able to assess and had an estimate right away. They arrived at the scheduled time and quickly got our problems resolved. He had to dig up the main line outside the home and install a clean out. Big job! They explained everything they were going to do and it went very smooth. The yard doesn't even look like it was touched. I already have recommended him to some neighbors who were inquiring about his work. They were clean, efficient and extremely courteous. I am a very satisfied customer! Good honest working guys is a rarity these days, so I was happy to have found them and to recommend them too. Good  professional hard working guys!  Thank you to John Snyder Plumbing and Heating and his team. Good Job!

I'm kicking myself for not trying Animo sooner since I work so close! All my lunch prayers have been answered! 

My first time was everything I had hoped it would be. I got a fajita style chicken burrito bowl w guac, sour cream, lettuce and tomato; along with a cin city smoothie. 

It was so good that I wanted to inhale it all immediately, so filling that it took away the sting of the semi-high price tag, and it reassured me that I could pretty much order anything off the menu and not feel guilty about it. I will for sure be back, and extra props to Animo for the sweet potato chips that was just the icing on the cake of this dream lunch.

Extremely disappointed. Cannot recommend.
 
And this is the last straw. But here's my story. In April, I requested a quote from Yeureka. I'd never heard of them but was solicited via Facebook as they were trying to drum up business in my area. A gentleman came to my property. Very professional and courteous. I needed a new entry door, frame, threshold and transom. He quoted the door including capping. I said I would rather not have capping and wanted plain wood to paint. He agreed and adjusted the price. I was told the project would in start 3-4 weeks. In JUNE I finally get the install scheduled, I don't even remember what the delays were from April to June. The first install date gets pushed back due to rain. That's understandable. It's scheduled for the following week. The date of install I drive by the property and nothing is happening. I call and someone forgot me. It's rescheduled again and again another no show. I'm told they went there, but the door hinges were on the wrong side. No one called me to say this, they just..did nothing. It will now take more time to get a NEW door made. 

July comes and the door is installed. But with the following issues: No peephole that I ordered (and paid extra for), capping around the frame (I asked for none), the threshold is squishy and not supported underneath, there is bare wood that needs to be covered, and the caulking has gaps. I'm asked to but refuse to pay the balance because the job is not completed. Eventually this is agreed upon. A week and a half go by with no contact, and then I find the doorknob isnt working properly. I add that to my list of things that still need to be addressed. 

Another week goes by when I get a call that the doorknob is no longer working at all and someone is trapped inside the building with no way to get out. At this point, this is an emergency. I call Lin, whom I've been dealing with the entire time and has been very sympathetic to these concerns. This time I state it's an emergency that someone fix the door because we can't get OUT of the house. This is a fire hazard and cannot wait for Monday as they have suggested. (this is a Friday night)  I'm told that both installers aren't available, and the owner thinks we should have another way out of the house so we have to wait. (There isn't). No offer to pay for a locksmith, no offer for anything. I now cannot shut the door because the brand new door knob is unable to open the door from the inside. I had to call and pay for an emergency locksmith out of pocket to change the doorknob. 

This has been an extremely disappointing process overall, with no regard to my very serious sudden concern. 4 months and counting and this job is still not complete.

A small, mom and pop type restaurant, this is primarily a takeout place, but it has a few small tables where you can sit and enjoy a meal.
The menu is quite simple - bimibap made with your choice of beef, chicken, tofu, etc. Ordered the beef and was not disappointed! The dish was prepared quickly - fresh ingredients, great taste. Don't miss out on the vegetable pancakes while you're there.

Finally got around to trying Sketch. I have to say, I was a little bit disappointed, mostly because I think my expectations were set very high from all these great reviews. Don't get me wrong, everything we had was alright, but I wasn't blown away by anything, especially for the cost ($30.00 for two burgers and one order of fries?).

Anyway, I ordered a ground beef burger with mushrooms, grilled onions, pepper jack cheese, avocado, and chipotle sauce. My husband got a ground beef burger with grilled onions, cheddar, and green goddess. Both of our burgers were overcooked. There was no pink in them at all, not even in the very center. I also couldn't taste the sauce on either burger. That being said, the roll was fantastic. Definitely the best part of the burger itself.

As for the green chili cheese fries, which I only ordered because everyone was raving about them... they were ok. I asked for the cheese sauce on the side, and even though the woman taking our order wrote that down, somehow when we got home the sauce was all over the fries, which made some of them very soggy. Ignoring that issue, I didn't really find the cheese sauce to be too special. I actually thought the fries themselves were really delicious, and if I could do it over, I would get a small order of fries (for half the cost) and some other dipping sauce on the side.

At the end of the day, it was an OK burger. (If you want a delicious burger, try Union Jack's in Manayunk.) I probably won't be going back, simply because the cost was ridiculous for what you actually get.
I really love the pizza from Pete's. I have never been there in person, we always order a delivery. But this is definitely one of the best.

Megumi is the newest kid on the block in terms of ramen.  It's literally around the corner from Terakawa, which I'd say is the favored ramen place around Chinatown.  The odds are somewhat against Megumi in that it is replacing a prior ramen place (Naoki Ramen I believe was the name) in that exact same location, which obviously closed down.

Megumi has a much bigger space than Terakawa, so it stands to benefit from the overflow Terakawa will surely have.  Unfortunately, Megumi has no ramen options for vegetarians.  All the ramens are pork broth based.  That isn't a problem for me, but it obviously leaves out those who are vegetarians or just don't eat pork.  I got the miso chicken ramen but was a little wary of it having a huge salt load, as is often the case with ramen.  (I want the ramen but not the salt overload but the line between them is thin)  Happily, they were able to serve me one that was flavorful but didn't leave me gulping for water after the meal.  The menu doesn't give you the option of adding extras the way Yamitsuki does and there's just a single size of ramen. I did feel like the amount of noodles I got was pretty plentiful and the waitress came several times to check on me.

Megumi does serve sushi, like Yamitsuki, so I guess your vegetarian friends can order that since ramen won't be an option due to the pork-based broth.  I'd come back here if I wanted to avoid the wait at Terakawa 
or get a ramen that I find less salty than what I can get at the other ramen counterparts in Chinatown.

Great BYO place. Didnt notice it was BYO until I saw it in the window and went around the corner to Onealls pub to get some take out beer. I got an al pastor burrito, waitress asked if I wanted spicy. I like spicy things so I said yeah spicy. Man did she mean it. Had a big kick! Taco Tuesday, mix and match tacos for $2 a piece great deal. Good food and good service, our waitress was lovely, will certainly go back.

I wandered down to Dave's Salon in a near act of desperation.  I needed to look sharp for that 'oh so embarrassing' driver's license photo.  They've been in the same spot for a long time.  Lisa took care of the mop that was my hair.  One of the better cuts I've had in awhile.  Good price, great cut, highly recommended.

I once told a friend of mine that I never met a chickpea I didn't like, so you can imagine how excited I was when Dizengoff opened. I frankly think that Zahav's hummus is among the best things on the menu there, so this makes total sense to me. I met my best friend there for lunch today and after checking the hummus flavors on Instagram, was pleased to see lima bean available. It was incredible. I loved the pita, too - it was soft and not too salty. Perfect. Had to get the limonanna, too. Delicious. We went a little before 12 and got the last two seats before the lunch rush. I see an expansion in the near future.

There are definitely biases towards which FroYo shops you like due to how you like your froyo. (explained by the the highly proven frozen yogurt sweetness:tartness theory) I like my froyo more sweet than tart and Phileo's yogurt matches that perfectly. Only bummer is that it is more yogurt than frozen in texture. 

Their selection of flavors is fantastic. Try a little of each to find your perfect froyo love match, then fill your cup up! Plus, the selection and freshness of toppings undoubtedly surpass those of Sweet Endings.

Premier Pet is incredible. I have two Akita mixes who have "special personality's." My dogs are tough on people but LOVED the staff and actually look forward to their visit. 

As winter is approaching the investment in grooming them is well worth the cost of buying a new vacuum, swiffers, etc. & your dogs will smell and look amazing. My 10 year old Akita looks like a puppy!

The service at Cafe La Maude was spectacular! Gabby was super accommodating to our group of eight on a busy Saturday afternoon. Even if they do not take reservations, I promise you the food is 110% worth the wait. The Signature Dishes are delicious and unique and I would recommend this restaurant to anybody in Philly looking for a good brunch spot

This is one of my favorite pizza spots in UCity.  The owners are the nicest people.  They use the freshest bread for their hoagies and the pizza is good too.  They renovated the shop last year and the inside is more attractive.  Good food and good people.
Yes Roto - Rooter stayed at my home for eight hours until the job was done correctly and guarnteed their work.  Lol  my only problem is that they do not clean up after themselves .

Sushi was incredible! We ordered the pink lady roll and spicy salmon roll. Easily rivals the best sushi we've had in the city. Not only the taste but the size was larger than most rolls offered anywhere else. Don't overlook BenTai. We will be regulars here for sure!

2 stars for food, and 4 stars for drinks. because i had very high expectations, and they failed with their taste. I had the 'escape velocity' cocktail. They do their homework on the cocktails.The food tasted average. Their ricotta bread drip was good, but it did not tingle my buds. The chic pea fries were kind of a let down. There was no creativity in the taste. Crab cake sandwich was good, especially the burger bun (soft and tender). The potaote mushroom entree which is served and presented extremely well was a let down on the taste. I have tried a lot of vegetarian restaurants, but this failed to tickle taste buds. They have great ideas on the plates and named, the chefs might want to add some more work in to balancing the tastes and I will definitely come back again. Oh, and the water they serve is infused with mint leaves and that was something i liked.
Consistently slow service despite the sandwiches being pre-prepared and simply warmed prior to serving (as we were told).  Disappointing.

It is quite simple really...

Great, attentive, professional and cheerful waitstaff!

Perfect timing of each course!

A really warm and welcome environment!

An eclectic wine list!

Really superb well presented and wonderfully tasty authentic Italian cuisine!

My third visit... nothing changed. Spasso was perfect again! Bravo!

A very neat place to see. You can feel the history ooze from this place. Everything inside looks as if it would have back in the day. I wanted to see Ben Franklin's grave but that's not in the grounds. I couldn't find the cemetery:(

Wow is all I can say...  I LOVE these cupcakes and that would be an understatement.  

I first came across Sweet Box Cupcakes at one of the night fares.  They had awesome  and innovative flavors at the size/prince and they had a special that night for a half dozen to take home.  I had half normal flavors like red velet and innovative ones like orange creamsicle (taste just like the childhood pops), and Yard Brawler (which was really awesome).  I was HOOOKED.

I then went into searching them online and so happy to know they have a place that holds all these delicious pieces of heaven.  To make matters sweeter, they also have brownies, cookies, galore!  I stopped in and got their sugar cookie (which was the size of my face and doesn't let down) and a cookie dough brownie.  That's right a cookie dough brownie.  I can't rave enough about this place, I love the quality of their product, the taste, the right balance between rich frosting and semi-sweet batter.  Awesome, just plain awesome.  

I'm day dreaming of eating a cupcake from here while I'm writing this review.

I mean you can come here if you want a bunch of their staff to make you feel terrible about yourself for stopping by. The hostess' were extremely rude and degrading, might be a good spot but it's hard to say because the welcome was so poor we couldn't stand to stay. Good luck if you want  to risk it!

Went here with a girlfriend for lunch on a Friday a few weeks ago. It was not crowded so we were seated right away. Our server was also very accommodating.
