The food was okay but nothing special really. I ordered the jerk chicken with rice and mixed veggies. The rice was very bland and they did not have any of the desserts I wanted so that was disappointing. 

The fresh flowers on the table were a nice touch and I did like the décor but if I was management I would consider investing in non paper menus. 

Overall I'd probably give them another shot but I'm not rushing back.

I had a GREAT experience with this school. Nick is a fantastic teacher--patient, and great at putting even a nervous adult learner at ease. I passed on my first attempt! He is excellent at breaking down the mechanics of driving, and teaching you systems for maneuvers like turning and parallel parking.

Its summer, and there is an abundance of wonderful fresh produce available, so I wanted to see what the magicians at Bindi could conjur up. I went on the Thali tuesday and was treated to a nice range of flavors. I ordered the vegetairian thali which included "butternut squash" spaetzel.. balls of squash in a tomato and pea curry. It was ok. The rest of the dishes were even better though. The standout was a cold peach soup that was refreshing and spicy at the same time. The raita had a bit of avocado in it.. and the chutney was watermelon, with a bit of spice, reminiscent of my grandmothers watermelon pickles. The green and wax beans served with onions and a bit of heat were a hit too. The rice was a bit of a mush. I paired this with two very refreshing summer beers.. Son of a Peach from R J Rockers, and a Festine Peche from Dogfishhead.  They worked and didnt over power the food. The restaurant provided a great class for the beer and kept them cold in the fridge.

Service was fine, though as I walked in without a reservation I was told they could seat me.. but I had less than an hour to eat. Not the best way to great a customer but I understood.

I finished the meal with the cardomom cake. The cake was nice.. the chocolate pot du creme was too much and also lacked spice. I loved the salted caramel, .. personally I would reconceptualize the dessert and have the cardomom cake served warm, with a salted caramel ice cream and perhaps a drizzle of something chocolate and spicy.. 

The thali is indeed a bargain. With the dessert the total was 29 dollars before tip, and you are able to eat it all in an hour without feeling rushed.

After trying very subpar cheesesteaks in central city that were supposedly "the best" finally found the absolutely best!!! OMG! What flavor and not too much bun but even it was delicious! Pat is the King!

I had a great experience today at Bonchon, I got the vegetable fried rice with tofu (see photo below) and I had a really friendly and amicable waiter named Zach. The hostess was also very sweet and helped me with directions, told me about the city, and spoke to me like a friend would. It was a really nice and welcoming environment. Chic too. Being from NY originally, I can tell when a lot of money has gone into making a restaurant come alive. This place felt alive and yet like a club. I highly recommend going here. The portions were very generous, the place was clean, and the people were nice. 

I could've done without the blasting provocative Korean music videos all around.
Got a roast pork with sharp. That's a good f'n sandwich bro. Get cha self one already!

Okay. I recently moved to south Jersey to be near family. I have two brothers who live around Philly so I asked one of them where to get a cheesesteak from. To be safe, he said to go to Geno's .

I'm from Brooklyn so I familiar with driving in a city but there is no parking when you get to this place. I had to literally drive around for 10 minutes before parking 4 blocks away. I'm glad there was no line. My brother told me it will be a line and to choose the shorter between Geno's and Pat's (which is across the street) 

Was it worth it? No. 

I was expecting a big greasy steak filled cheesesteak, not a subway sized sandwich filled with steak-um sliced steak. Nothing special. Just throw some steak and cheese on a roll, there you have Geno's. 

Beware, they only take cash but it's an ATM across the street. 

My other brother later told me Philly residents don't go here. The name I can't remember but it sounded Italian.

I heard on ESPN radio that John's was the spot but they were closed on Monday. I will try them also.
Legit the BEST Pirogies I ever had with old school philly bar feel. Great service, clean joint, cool play 80's play list accompanied by 70's-80's memorabilia. 100% recommend this place!
Went to Sugarhouse last summer.  I had fun.  I didn't win anything, but they give you good comps when you first sign up for their rewards program.  I'll return someday.

Decided to try this place out with a couple of friends. It's located on the outskirts of Chinatown which I like because it avoids all the busyness. We got the Uji Matcha, Limon Tea and Milk Tea. My drink, which was the Limon Tea, had a whole lime that was cut with slits. This made the drink a bit too sour and I could not really mix my drink with a straw because the lime was taking up too much space. I probably would have enjoyed this better if the lime was cut into slices rather than a whole lime with slits. Nonetheless, this place had a cool, modern atmosphere and had some fun games to play while you enjoy your drink. So I will be giving this place another shot.

Not sure I'm a fan. The ambiance is nice and the outdoor seating was perfect but that's about it. Although my burger was beautiful to look at (the Betty burger) it was incredibly underwhelming. I will give the disclaimer that I am not a fan of most "bbq" food because I think it's a euphemism for burnt, so this place wasn't exactly the best choice. My friend ordered chicken quesadillas and they were delicious, they also have the option to order a full portion or a half which would be right up my alley. The Betty burger came with pork confit, a delicious garlic aioli, ripe avocado, a fried egg, and the standard lettuce and tomato. The issue for me was the burger patty itself, the burger was charred to the point of no return. It may be my fault for ordering a burger medium well, but it tasted like the poor cow died twice. The tab ended up being around $45 without tip but that did include two glasses of Riesling so that's not too bad, just not worth a second trip.

it is ripoff warranty service and most of the things do not cover and even when they cover. They low ball the cost from the dealership and pay only fraction of the total cost. Not a BBB +A company. I called AA Auto protection to find out why, guess what. It went directly to the voice mail.

I've eaten at pyt several times and although the food is good, it's the service I just can't stand.  Everytime I try to call them to make an order to go, the phone is usually off the hook and goes directly to voicemail.   

Even when I dine in, the service is bad.  You walk in and just stand in the doorway while a billion servers pass by you and give you a stare as in "what do you want".  and this is when they aren't even busy!!!!   Once you finally get seated, the service is slow and the servers have an attitude!

One of the things that make Philadelphia mention in any part of the world is the original Phil's Cheesesteak. The original Phils Cheesesteak is a sandwich made from thinly sliced pieces of beefsteak and melted cheese on a long hoagie roll, a course you can adopt it, as best you prefer.

Dalessandro's Steaks has become famous for making one of the best Philly cheese steaks in town maybe the world, even surpassed the famous Gino and Pat. 

I have to admit, that the place does have an excellent and tasty sandwich and that an amount of ingredients is a lot with each bite you will drop the meat, onions or other ingredients on the plate so if you looking for a real and full Philly cheese steak this is your place but like any the place in the world they are not perfect and you will found two major flaws which they are only cash and the staff is very rude, they have to take a course of customer care.

I've only eaten in once or twice - I usually grab a to-go order from the bar.  Danielle, the bartender, is wonderful.  She's pleasant, courteous, attentive and quick.  Food comes out speedily and well packed for travel.  My favorites are the fried mozzarella, petite crab cakes and the apple walnut spinach salad, though their specials do not dissappoint either.  The food is always in large portions.  Conveniently located off Broad St, a short walk from the Walnut Locust stop.  Love it and eat there a few times a month.  Defintely check it out!
Great little breakfast shop! Food is fresh and delicious! The service is great! It's a little spot around the way locally owned and I would definitely recommend!

I love this place. 

My regular stylist, Ahlee, is very talented. I've seen her four times now & have been pleased with every cut/color. She listens to what I want & will offer advice when I ask for it. The one time she disagreed with something I asked her to do, she was honest but polite about it. I ended up taking her suggestion & was happy with the outcome. She's also very enthusiastic about her job & just a fun person in general. That's important to me, because I'm in that chair for a long time!

The rest of the staff has always been friendly, too. And the one time Ahlee had to be out unexpectedly on the day of my appointment, I was able to get in to see Claire the very next day. She was also friendly & gave me a good cut. She still remembers my name & speaks to me every time I'm with Ahlee.

The customer loyalty card is a plus. After 5 services of $75 or more, I got a free product up to $25 in value. That's a great way to the products they sell without shelling out money for them before you know you like them.

I also like the fact that because the products they use are organic, they don't smell as bad as traditional products do--especially the hair color. 

I recommend this place!

Stopped in for lunch today with a friend. The food was decent but the service was the real let down. The server was not attentive and every time I asked for something it seemed like I was bothering him. The carnitas tacos were flavorful and the guac was fresh. Being in the industry I can be picky with servers but this guy honestly ruined my whole dining experience and I won't be returning. A little attentiveness and positivity goes a long way.

I felt like I got a good workout here and had fun, but I wasn't crazy about it throughout and here's why:

+ CLEAN STUDIO SPACE

+ FRIENDLY INSTRUCTORS

- MUSIC. I could only recognize two songs on the playlist as being from this decade and some songs were played more than once. While the tempo was great, I wanted something a little more modern!

- AWKWARD ENTRANCE. So online, it says to show up 15 minutes before to get the deets on how to use the rowing machine properly. I understood this and planned to show up ~20 min early because I had to change from my work clothes. Only problem is, the door is locked after the running store underneath closes (6pm) and there's no receptionist to let you in while the class before you is still going on. So you've got to wait outside like a sketchball until the instructor can hear the doorbell (just keep pressing until someone lets you in). Also, to get to the restrooms you've got to work through the space where they do the intervals, so don't show up more than 10-15 minutes early.

- NO WARM UP. We just jumped into the circuits/intervals without a warm up and there wasn't a proper cool down either. As a first-timer, it felt a little unorganized and being OCD it didn't jive well with me.

+ GOOD WORKOUT. I definitely liked the low-impact cardio and the intervals though. Lots of plank/arm/leg work interspersed with the rowing machine. I liked that we paired up so you had someone to commiserate with. 

I could see myself coming back here, maybe only once or twice a month with my class pass, but I'll definitely have to come warmed up and plan on stretching on my own afterwards.
Relapse closed down a couple years ago.
Currently dancing my labor day wkend awayyyy! Hey silk city

Crunchy Bengali
and saucy Punjabi Chaat:
Your crisps are delish!

Had a great time watching the Eagles with a neighborhood friend and a next door neighbor who is a regular at this local watering hole.  Attentive bartender, friendly crowd of regulars, reasonably priced drinks, and they even had free sandwiches/snacks during the game.  Will definitely be back for another round, happy that I stopped in to check it out.

I'm a transplanted New Yorker, so I know NYC pizza...and this isn't quite it. However, it's darn good. The best available in center city currently, in my opinion. 

One star removed because of crust-inconsistency. It comes out differently depending on who makes it, I guess. Sometimes, tender; sometimes a tiny bit tough. It's very good though and I've always found the people to be courteous and the deliveries prompt.

Stopped in on one of the first cold nights of the year looking for some soup to warm up with. Picked out the Spicy Ramen and the crispy pork buns and my wife ordered the Mayu Ramen. Of the two ramens, the spicy was definitely more flavorful. I always enjoy kimchee which it had though it was a bit of an odd sensation since it was cold kimchee in hot soup. The menu also said it had chicken and seafood broth with the bone broth and this is likely why the soup tasted more flavorful in addition to whatever made it spicy. The mayu ramen was good and had some nice leek flavor to it but the broth seemed to be lacking something and it could have been simply not enough salt. Pickled ginger and bamboo were nice additions in this soup. The crispy pork buns were good but less flavorful than I expected and the crispy bun itself was less exciting than I thought it would be, maybe I'll just stick with a regular soft bun next time. Any type of noodles can be swapped out for another regardless of what the menu says and I liked the egg noodles better in the spicy ramen than the medium thick noodles in the mayu.

I have to add that I really like the condiments they had on the table. The roasted sesame seed grinder was cool and added some nice flavor and texture. The chili oil had good flavor and a bit of heat. And the little bits of crunchy roasted garlic were great too.

Overall, the soup was good (particularly the spicy ramen), but it didn't amaze me and make me want to come running back. Plus the 30 minute wait in a cramped corner by the door maybe wasn't worth it. If there was a bar next door or across the street I wouldn't have minded the wait as much.
I love sprouts but I give it a 4 stars because I wish the staff was a little more knowledgeable about their products but maybe in due time they will...

Going to Dos reminds of my time when I lived in SoCal...if I really did live there.  I've had some legit Mexican food but not in the east coast until now.  You can't go wrong with any of the items on the menu.  The portions are huge and the chips and salsa are never ending.  The tequila is also really good.  Great for happy hr!  What more can you say?  Just give this place a chance and you'll come back for more

If you do go here get the Tuna or buffalo shrimp off the app menu. Soooooo good. The Tuna melts in your mouth With the dirty dust on the outside. The buffalo shrimp cooked to perfection. Both apps served with rice. O and i cant forget about the light and fluffy cheese puffs....I would recommend a this great place for a light meal. And excellent beer pitchers too

I've traveled extensively throughout Europe, and the food at this place is very authentic. The sausages taste just like the ones in Germany! I love the veal and pork sausage, and one of my friends really liked the spicy beef and pork sausage. The spicy mustard sauce that comes with it is really good, but also really strong, so I'd recommend against spreading generous amounts of it onto the bun (like I did - and then it got so spicy that my brain hurt!). 

We were a small group, but we couldn't find seating inside. However, while it was cold outside, we huddled around the fire, surrounded by outdoor space heaters. The atmosphere was warm, and we had a lot of fun hanging out. Overall, though it was very difficult to find parking near the restaurant, we had a very enjoyable experience. Both the food and drinks were great!

The food at Le Cheri was delicious. Highlights of the meal were the Steak and the Artic Char. The Tripe was a wonderful surprise too. 

Service was exceptional-- prompt and attentive. Even with the great dining experience I found myself missing the old Rittenhouse Tavern-- the original with chef Nick Elmi at the helm. The drinks were more creative and the food was uniquely exceptional.  Never the less you'll have a great experience at Le Cheri.

Finally what Philly has lacked for so long, fresh nourishing bread made here in our city!  Selections spread from whole wheat boule's to white "wonder-style" sandwich loaves, a 'Philly' Muffin (think English muffin) and truly the best bagels I have ever had, not to mention a fantastic baguette.   It is evident the tender loving care that goes into each loaf.

By far one of the best breakfast/brunch spots you'll find in Philly. My girlfriend and I visited the "City of Brotherly Love" late December and yelped a bunch of places to eat at during our trip. Green Egg Cafe was one of the most popular and highly rated restaurants we could find, and deservingly so.  

I don't remember what time we arrived exactly, but it was around noon. When we turned the corner, there was already a big crowd of people waiting outside and we looked at each and said, "there's no way that's for Green Eggs Cafe", but boy were we wrong. We walked in and asked for a table for 2, in hopes that it wouldn't be a long wait...wrong again. The young lady told us it was going to be around a 45 minute wait. Typically, we wouldn't wait 45 minutes for a restaurant but this was on the top of our to-go places, so we had no choice but to wait in the blistering cold. During this time, we looked at reviews, and pictures of what others ordered. We had to spend our 45 minutes doing something worthwhile...also as a distraction from the freezing cold. 

When they finally called my name, I thought "about time, this better be worth the wait". We knew exactly what we wanted so we just told the waitress our order, Creme Brulee French Toast ($13), and the Veggies Benedict ($11). The food was delicious, and I definitely recommend the french toast. 

The atmosphere is bright and lively, great place for a brunch date.

The food is good. Not great, but very good, especially breakfast. Prices are quite reasonable, given the area.

HOWEVER!!!!!!!!!!!!

The service is all but nonexistent. Twice we went there. Twice we waited ages just to be greeted. Twice we waited in limbo for both drinks and refills. Twice we waited endlessly for our food to arrive. And twice we languished as we waited to even have the chance to ask for the check.

The place is consistently underfilled.

There shall be no thrice for us. 

What shall we tell the God of the Down Home Diner the next time hunger calls?

"...not today."

First and last time at this salon. I called for a mani appointment for around 1 o'clock. They told me I could come at 1 so I came at 1. I was not seen until 1:25. No apology or explanation. Also no waiting area. The woman seemed annoyed that I had a gel mani that needed to be soaked off. Manicure was ok. I think it was rushed and each layer wasn't allowed proper time to dry. I noticed that other people were coming in and waiting a long time to be seen as well, even tho they had appointments. For instance, a girl next to me walked out because she also had a 1 o'clock and still hadn't been seen at 1:35. I sat at the nail dryer for a ridiculous amount of time- at least 15 minutes- without anyone coming to give me a massage or check my nails. I had seen earlier that other patrons got massages at the nail dryer but only one man does them apparently and he never came over to give me mine. I couldn't wait another 15 minutes under the nail dryer so I left. I was pretty disappointed because the massage is the best part of the manicure experience in my opinion. The customer service and actual salon service were both generally poor. I will never go back. First star is for convenience of location right across the street from my boyfriend's apartment; second star is for them getting my old gel off and cleaning up my cuticles.

Awesome local bar. I love the outdoor seating. The bar tenders are also all always really friendly which is nice and a change of pace from other bars in the city. The bar drawls a nice friendly crowd.

I have never had their food.

I come here at night for the bar scene. I am a fan!!! the drinks are moderately priced, especially if you get there before a certain time and have a wristband.

Good for groups of friends, and if you dance on top of the bar, you get $1 shots.

The music varies, a lot. they can go from playing hip hop, to country music all within about 10 minutes.

Go get your boobs groped by Mona. She takes one look at them, instantly knows your size, and physically man-handles them into a gorgeous bra. She'll even spew some bullshit science to you about how you need an Italian bra to hold up your boobs and prevent sagging in old age. It's wild, it's absurd, and it's crazy fun. Never felt so sexy in my life. Screw Victoria's Secret. I've got a Mona now.

Iron Hill distinguishes itself from other Chestnut Hill restaurants with its large dining room/bar area and its Happy Hour specials. The on-site brewery turns out beers that are mostly good, with the occasional outstanding beer along with the occasional disaster. They also have a 2-Pizzas-and-a-Growler special that is the best value on the hill. The menu features several vegetarian-friendly options. None of their fare is to die for, but the menu is loaded with quality options at decent prices, and the happy hour and ever-changing beer selection keep me coming back.

I am so grateful that Six Fishes has come to this neighborhood!  The convenience, great vibe, quality of care and incredible value is remarkable.  I have been to other community practices where I uncomfortable and somewhat exposed, but the set up of this office is ideal.  I had seen Cara Frank back when she was in Mt Airy and she continues to impress me.  Everything that Cara does is first rate!
Lucky Star is no more.  It was destroyed by a fire.  We'll miss you!  Good luck rebuilding!

"Hey, isn't this the place from that Nicolas Cage movie?"

That's what I actually heard a high school student walking by Independence Hall say.

Is that what they're teaching full blooded patriotic American school children these days? Is that what passes for constructive knowledge of our great nation's great historical record? Can't I get a Fast Times at Ridgemont High, Valley Girl or Wild at Heart reference at least? Maybe some Raising Arizona quotes or something? Don't they teach these kids which Nicolas Cage movies to watch? What's becoming of our educational system? Taxpaying American taxpayers are paying for what? Kids to recognize important historical landmarks as crappy movie sets? C'mon? Really? What's next? Soon you'll tell me that the original The Wicker Man is no longer required viewing for 3rd graders or something? Won't you?

How 'bout some better signage? Seriously.
Went here for a afternoon cap and late lunch. Had the shrimp and grits which had nice flavor. Good service and good. I would recommend for a quick eat, good service and cozy environment.

I have been here a few times and each time was great! The first time I came in for a balayage ombré/highlights and although it took some time- my hair dresser was very meticulous which is great when doing hair- it was well worth it. Their cuts are always great and there is rarely any waiting time. The staff is friendly and the salon is very clean!

My wife and I were in town on a Saturday to catch a Pirates/Phillies game and picked this for lunch based on the Yelp reviews.

We didn't have reservations and the restaurant had several parties going, but we were quickly seated at a table for two. The lunch menu is not as expansive as dinner, but still allowed us to choose an entree along with a side of either a salad or pasta.

My wife is a fan of Moscato wine and thought their brand was especially good. The waiter, who was very friendly and attentive obtained the brand name and wrote it on our receipt.

I ordered the grilled chicken and shrimp combo with grilled peppers along with a side salad. They didn't have ranch dressing but the balsamic was good and was similar to my meal. The chicken breast, although thin, was quite tender and easy to pull apart with the fork. It came with three shrimp, four large strips of red bell pepper and six or eight spinach leaves. Very enjoyable. She got the veal parmesan and was quite happy with it. We shared a slice of New York cheese cake for dessert.

Although the online menu did not include prices, our luncheon entrees were $11.99 and $13.99 (quite reasonable) and along with two glasses of wine, dessert and tax came to $55.

There was very little to find at fault, and could have given 4 1/2 if possible.
Hot chocolate was cold. Blueberry muffin was not tasty at all, actually to be more specific it had a funny taste to it...and everything is expensive (which we wouldn't have minded if it was good!)

Do not park here....flat rate of $25 that you don't see when you arrive. Arseholes.
NOT RECOMMENDED and I will not be using any of these companies ever again!!!

Definitely one of the best sushi places to go to in the city for the food and for the people. The place is small, so everyone cozies up with each other and we all talk and comment on each others' dishes. The price is very reasonable and I love the tuna dumplings and sunomono along with some, hamachi, salmon, and eel. 

The sushi chefs are super friendly and if they're not busy making rolls for people sitting there, people ordering take out or delivery, he'll chat with us. They offer pretty neat special rolls, which change every so often. Tried a special roll with crunch tuna inside and eel with avocado on top. I gobbled that up so quickly.
I love this place, great atmosphere and great service. Love the hookah and its always a chill time. You deff have to come here and try it.

Un hôtel magnifique à l'extérieur, et qui fait lever la tête, vu sa taille immense ! 
Le Residence Inn Philadelphia Center City a un emplacement plus qu'idéal, car il est situé en face de la mairie de Philadelphie... Il n'y a pas plus central ! C'est parfait pour sortir le soir, ou visiter la ville à partir de l'hôtel.
L'hôtel mérite pleinement ses quatre étoiles, tant il propose les services adéquats :
* Des suites spacieuses... J'ai bénéficié d'une suite de 35m² !
* Une vue magnifique, spécialement pour les étages élevés, vers la mairie de Philadelphie, et les buildings alentours.
* Un service et une attention aux petits soins. Notamment la personne qui a assuré notre réception a su nous indiquer les bons spots pour sortir près de l'hôtel ! Le service voiturier est super pratique, même s'il est onéreux.
* Un petit-déjeuner gargantuesque, regroupant les classiques du petit-déjeuner américain.
Un super hôtel, qui a qu'une seule fausse note, pour moi : un lobby assez sombre et petit, qui ne donne pas une atmosphère de bienvenue.

Went last night with a relatively large group of 5 for happy hour. We all enjoyed some sangria and happy hour tapas as well as some items from the regular menu. We thoroughly enjoyed everything that came out to the table, and even after tax and tip, we only spent about 57 dollars a person. And that includes having ordered the jamon iberico which was phenomenal. You could taste the hazelnuts in the meat, we were blown away. We ordered 4-5 of the flash fried peppers, a table favorite. And we also tried the lamb tartare, which even the squeamish among us dared to try and they found it simply amazing. We ordered probably about 20 tapas and none of them disappointed. The spanish octopus, the white anchovies, the chorizo bilbao, and the albondigas were also amazing. The concensus was that the tapas here at Amada was some of the best food we've ever eaten.

OMG - go, just go.  SO good.  

Service was a little rushed, but they were slammed and food kept coming out so we couldn't have cared less.  Everything was delicious and thoughtfully prepared.

I'm becoming more and more excited about Spool every time I go in. I went in for some fabric to make a couple of "softies" - plush toys popularized in Japan. Laura was really very helpful and enthusiastic regarding my project and helped me pick out the supplies I needed. Laura also had great advice for me on purchasing a sewing machine of my own. I like that they have pre-made bundles of fabric quarters that make getting materials for small projects easier. If you need larger quantities, their fabric bolts are prominently displayed along one wall of the store. There's no having to sift through piles or stacks, just let your eyes wander down the shelves. I really love the patterns they have for sale - who wouldn't love Amy Butler or Kaffe Fassett's fabulous and rich color palettes? I'm looking forward to my pillow class in January and I'm sure I'll be adding another star.

My husband and I drove to Philly for a weekend getaway filled with wining and dining. We splurged a little and went to this restaurant for dinner. The ambiance was romantic and cozy. The truffle fries side dish was honestly the best part of or meal. I couldn't get enough of them. My husband ordered the crab cakes and he liked them but they could have been better. I ordered the seared halibut with Maine lobster, organic mushrooms. I didn't much like the halibut; it was unevenly salted. But I liked the mushrooms and lobster. 

We also got dessert and dessert wine. I don't remember what dessert were ate but it was good. The wine was good but I thought the quantity was not enough for the price you pay. I was a bit disappointed in that. 

It was a nice restaurant but I'm not sure I would go back.

My new go-to dinner spot in the city for French food! Loved the atmosphere - music, lighting, service, and food were amazing - probably the first place I've been to in the city that creates the feeling you're in France. 

Atmosphere: This was probably the highlight. Servers were so friendly! The space didn't feel overcrowded, and the music was a great volume (not too loud). It's a great spot for a romantic dinner date - lighting wasn't too dark, but slightly dim. Seating was also very comfortable, and the interior was well decorated.

Food: Start off with bread as an appetizer - I've had better bread in the city, but decent; some pieces tasted a bit stale. Essentially, everything else we ordered, I loved. Recommend the escargot and the pork cheeks - both were very flavorful dishes! The pork cheek dish also was a very large portion size - combined with brussel sprouts, polenta, and mushrooms. I also loved the goat cheese flatbread. Their ice creams are home-made, and the apple tart paired with caramel ice cream was a delicious mix. These were all recommendations from our waiter as well! 

Overall: 5/5 - I haven't been to another French-inspired restaurant that I liked so much in the city. Highly recommend for a nice date, but not crazy pricey (around $35pp in total; with no drinks).

OMG, I had to write a review since I've been here and wanted to see if it changed since I have been there last. Sadly, it looks like it hasn't.

I recently became a Texas Holdem Bonus convert (I went to Sands Bethlehem last night, and while they play AC rules, instead of Vegas rules [like Mount Airy up in the Poconos.] I still love that game!) and I was looking for PA casinos that would offer that game. I see SugarHouse offers it, but Holy Great Scot, you couldn't give me a $1 million of your money to play there!! 

I'm born and raised in Newark, NJ. So the type of clientele that was at the SugarHouse didn't (initially) bother me. However, those people make Newark look like a lilly-white suburb. This place was beyond ghetto, even for me. I played blackjack for the couple of hours I was there and I took them for $50 on top of the $200 I brought in. It is small, it was smoky, it was trashy, and I had to tell one of my best friends who loves gambling as much as I do to stay away from this place. I have never been to Harrah's, but I have been to Parx and I can't recommend Parx enough!! You want ambiance, good looking people, decent to good food, and some gambling? Go to Parx! 

I was on Parx's website and they don't look like they have Texas Holdem Bonus. Still, I can play Blackjack for hours. Yeah, I am never, under any circumstances going to Sugarhouse the way it is now. If they do a complete revamp or something, I'll consider it, but PA has so many, many options. I might check out Harrah's though. But I've been to Mt. Airy, Sands, Parx, in addition to Sugarhouse and I can, hands down, recommend all of those casinos over Sugarhouse. if distance is an issue, then keep in mind that Parx is not that far away from Sugarhouse (I know, I immediately went over there after Sugarhouse.) 

Just like Nancy Reagan said in the 80s..."Just say no! (to Sugarhouse.)

Since I love Mediterranean/Middle East cuisine, I was very excited to find this place.  The charming and knowledgable server was so kind and helpful.  The food was pretty good, but not as spectacular as I had hoped.  The hummus was a bit bland.  The veggie salad was excellent.  I had roasted veggies on top of the hummus - slightly overcooked.  The place was spotless (which I love).  The logistics of the place can be detrimental to bringing in customers.  You have to go to the back of the restaurant and around an invisible corner to order...it should be more inviting to street traffic.  I will go back and hope that some of the opening kinks are worked out.

"Farmicia" in Old City, Philadelphia

This is my very first review and its a shame that its mostly negative. I live 45 minutes outside of the city and took my wife out to Farmicia for her birthday dinner, hoping for a lovely romantic night, what we got was one of the worst meals I've ever had. Farmicia was highly recommended by a friend and we had heard it was from the same person  who created White Dog Cafe, which is a favorite, so I was very surprised that we got an 8pm reservation for a Saturday when I made the reservation just a day before. We soon found out why this was so easy. Smiles greeted us at the door but we were soon put in the far back corner of the restaurant. Other tables were available so we found it funny we were put in the in the table which was quite cramped, uncomfortable and under a bright spot light. Still hoping for the best our waitess came to the table, and semi-pleasant and not over charmingly asked, "ya ready yet?". Less than classy, but an acceptable attitude to me, although my wife found it off putting when we had to ask what the specials were. She was average, and kinda going through the motions. We split the cheese plate which was really fantastic and now we were excited for the rest of the meal. But the rest of the meal wasn't what we'd hoped for and rivaled a cafeteria buffet. My salad was simple, lacked presentation, and dressing tasted like it was straight from the bottle. My wife found her pear and crab salad mediocre, with crab just slopped on and lacking flavor.  What topped every thing off was when my steak arrived, it was slapped down on some mashed potatoes, with a side of kale that was so overcooked it was almost disintegrating. But the kicker of my $28 "entree" was the steak; amazingly unevenly cooked medium rare on the inside, totally red rare on the edges, over tenderized, and barely warm. Barely warm was strange because our meal came out fast. Was it sitting a long time? It was it a left over? Not sure? Never the less, I've had better steak in a school lunch line. My wife's gnocchi was less than spectacular, mushy and over seasoned with so much pepper it was hard to eat. It was if they lathered seasoning on because the food itself was so bland. And also completely lacking in presentation. It was shocking how our food was just slopped on the plate.  My wife said something to the waitress about how unhappy we were and the girl offered free desert for us both but even with her insatiable sweet tooth my wife amazingly declined. That's something I've never seen before, and this meal accomplished just that.
The decor, the mixed drink, olive oil bread, and cheese were the only things worth mentioning that were positive. The rest; not including a $150 tab, is something I would expect from a mis-managed Sizzler.

I love this place. My girlfriend is actually obsessed with it. The pho is excellent. I usually get the Pho Tai. Some of their non vietnamese cuisine is great too. I recommend the pork dumplings and pad thai. Also, the service is incredible. Fast and extremely accomodating. It's really the best service I've ever experienced at a restaurant. Oh, and it's dirt cheap. Awesome food, great service and cheap... what else do you need to know?

Eating ice cream on one of the coldest days of the year is understandable when you finally make it up to Weckerly's. The shop is small and reminiscent of an neighborhood ice cream spot with all of the natural wood and soft colors. It reminds me of Bi-Rite Creamery in SF.

The flavor options are a nice selection of classic and inventive. Each one I tried was absolutely delicious and packed with depth and flavor. We ultimately elected to split a single scoop of Honeycomb (amber honey with almond clusters) on a waffle cone. The Honeycomb was decadent breakfast scooped into one buttery and light cone. It was never cloyingly sweet and you could certainly taste the care that went into every ingredient.

I eyed the ice cream sandwiches and other menu offerings for next time I find a good excuse to make it up to Fishtown. I also greatly appreciated the shop's focus on sustainability as noted by all their compostable items. I am a huge fan of Weckerly's!

Adore this local nail salon. I get a monthly Brazilian wax and pedicure here. Best Brazilian I have gotten in the city, and I've been many places. There is only one woman that does brazilians, and I feel bad I do not know her name. She's fast and friendly and you never need an appointment. It does not have all the bells and whistles of a spa, but that's why it's a $40 Brazilian. $23 pedicure if you pay cash. Inexpensive monthly maintenance close to home. Win win.

Been here twice already. I really like the food. Taste really good. Not too greasy. Place is a little small. Even during busy hours service is still amazing. I really love the Braised rice. Taiwanese specialty. This place makes it closest to when I had it in Asia. Love it.

The way I found this places is through yelp. I was skeptical only because there are a lot of places that say Persian and are completely Indian dishes. This is Persian food.and they deliver till 11pm. I got a few things grapes leaves which were perfectly cooked.lamb was ultra juicy and the spices weren't just burnt on you can tell they were marinated and slow cooked. Chicken was a little bit on the dry side but still tasty. Overall great food. This place is a keeper

Great night for a basketball game. Lots of changes to the stadium. More technical lights and effects. 

Unlike the flyers opener the Sixers came to play. Lots of action lots of scoring. Good the see the young players making shots, 

Good game good win 

Another great night in the skybox. Great win over Boston.
Good haircut for cheaper than most other local places. They are always friendly and are following the precautions needed to combat COVID

The egg waffles are average at best. It comes out pretty ugly though (small pieces everywhere). The one who makes it just shoves the little pieces back in the bag. Also, one of the girls there seems pretty rude because she stared at me after I said thank you and ignored me when I asked for a plastic bag.
The egg waffle (original flavor) has this weird chewy texture inside, and sometimes the batter is still raw. It's unlike what it's supposed to be: cakey and moist inside. It's not cakey at all! Not sure if I'll be coming back here, but I think it may be the only place in Chinatown that sells egg waffles. Maybe I'll give the other flavors a try.

I bought my first acoustic guitar here last week and the salesman (owner?) was very helpful.  They were out of bags for my new instrument, so he offered to put it in a box for me to carry it home.  He also showed me his cool guitar tuner app on his phone and tuned it for me!  They have a pretty great collection of vintage vinyl.  (Skip the dance section though, ughhh).

I was craving fish tacos and usually go to El Vez,but I figured I would try something new and Lolitas since it had good reviews.  Unfortunately, the fish tacos were Terrible!!  Though they had lots of toppings and what should have been a tasty crusting, the fish itself was unseasoned and bland.  I only made it through 2/3 tacos before I gave up because they were so tasteless.  For $14 (including tax) I was incredibly disappointed--if fish tacos is what you're looking for, skip Lolitas and head to El Vez instead.

If you're a fan of nerdy, scrawny Italians, then the Fire is for you. The beer selection is adequate, the environment appropriately divey, but it's the service that really makes the difference. Did I say service? I meant Fall-time hay bales. And the projector in the alley ain't half bad either.

I've had some disappointing wax experiences at Genesis, tho the new person Heather did a quality wax on face, legs and underarms, and I know my  hair can be a challenge!  The room was clean,  the and supplies seemed fresh, she had antiseptic spray, she was courteous and efficient along with being able to clearly state the price range for services.  A very good wax today.

Excellent spot. 

1) The atrium is nice. The first floor has non-permanent exhibits. We saw the FDA and LGBTQ exhibits. Both were nice. 
2) The second floor has permanent exhibit. It is great.
3) Overall, I will recommend getting a Groupon or come after 4pm to get the 5 dollar per person ticket. It is not worth the 14 bucks per person.

Pets比較好吃
口味選擇種類多多
牛肉香又多汁
不用加醬料即可享受美食
去的人也比較多
分量大
女生兩人吃一個也可以

Terrible cancellation process, will charge you for an extra month even after you have cancelled your membership. Took 3 tries to actually get the membership cancelled, ended up getting charged for 3 months that was unusable as we had moved out of state.

In the world of laundromats this is a winner. Change machine that works, friendly staff, and good machines. I am fussy about my clothes and would not say these take forever to dry as past posters have. Stear clear on weekends though because it can get busy. Tried the wash and fold once and it was fine and certainly a cleaper option than some other places.

So I will embarrassingly admit that I heard of this place from that movie In Her Shoes. Turns out a lot of Philly natives aren't even aware it exists either! Looks exactly like any place with the word "hut" in it should from the front. Inside has a bit of a cheesy islandish decor in an extremely tight space. there is even a tiki hut where the register is and an old school boombox that places random music.  

We lucked out on a Sat night and didnt wait long for a table. There was one waiter so I can see how people may complain about slow service. Totally laid back guy wearing a beanie but surprisingly attentive and just a likeable person. 

We ordered the empanadas,BBQ chicken dish and of course the signature Jamaican Jerk Chicken. All was fantastic. As they forewarn you there the Jamaican Jerk is definitely VERY spicy! Most dishes are spicy but this one in particular. So if at all sensitive do not even contemplate ordering. Cornbread as mentioned was a bit dry I guess but actually was perfect for mediating all the spice!  Portions are very generous, especially for the price.  And of course one of the great things about many Philly restaurants, its BYOB! With bigger parties definitely make a reservation otherwise will have issues with seating. 

Will definitely be back, especially to check out this outdoor space when it reopens!
Fantastic value! Great food and service with reasonable prices. I wish there were more restaurants like this in the world. I had the green curry and it was a filling and tasty meal.

Not impressed, and never going back again! This place seems like the testing ground where the so-so recipes are left behind, and the ok is brought over to V Street's older *posh sell-out sister* Vedge (mind, the younger of the two is just that as well). My server had an attitude problem, but I thought: well, it's been a long night for her, so I'll just take this one on the chin." But then everyone else seemed cartoonishly fake in their dealings... like smarmy college kids just sucking up their lousy labor to get by battling whatever financial burdens they've been saddled with. The bartender was incredibly short and impersonal with me - VERY dismissive, and when I asked for something a tidbit stronger the second time around he just sort of stuck up his nose and made it exactly the same way right in front of my very eyes as if to say "don't eff with my way of doing things", done with another customer's drink which was disappointingly mixed in the same shaker...plus, 7 dollars for a pile of poorly presented starch? C'mon people! My peruvian fries were broken segments covered in what looked like the last few withering squirts of aji amarillo desperately  farted out of a nearly emptied bottle, and the not-so-funky kimchi tasted saltier than I've ever experienced it in my life... as if anything could really ferment with THAT much sodium! Could tell they probably make it the day before, rather than wait a week the way real kimchi should be. NEXT!

This place is run unprofessionally.  After making 2 massage appointments a week prior to the day of the scheduled massages, I received a phone call the day of asking if we could come in several hours earlier.  We couldn't.    Later that day, I received another call saying that they had to cancel our appointments because they had not scheduled any therapists to come in.  So what exactly is the point of calling a week ahead of time?  Not exactly service-oriented.

This tiny theater  on Frankford Avenue  in the Kensington/Port Richmond  is a gem of a place to wickedly  witty and  sassy  & titillating ( pun intended) burlesque  shows.  I can only  review  Walking Fish for the burlesque  shows I have attended.  This 46 seat theater needs more patronage.  I  attended  last night ( July 16th, 2011) performance of  THIGHLIGHT ZONE  and the theatre had many empty  seats.   That really made me sad.  Not only was  this  original and hilarious burlesque play ( which was comprised of skits related to  episodes from the t.v. series TWILIGHT ZONE) outrageously creative,sexy  and bold, but it also  was one of the best theatre  experiences I have had (  BAT BOY at Villanova was the last one).  For $10 ( the price of a movie ticket),  you can laugh and  be inspired.  These performers  LOVE  what they do but I know all the hard work and  energy  that goes into just one performance.  The performers make their own costumes and   the troupe  has to assemble all the props.  Long hours and no pay ( I am sure none of the performers got paid) .  The least  they deserve is a sell-out crowd.  I know the performers have  other jobs  but their love of the theater and burlesque is why they come here to perform.  I left  Walking Fish  last night feeling  light and relieved  as I spent almost two hours laughing my troubles away.  For a "feel good" unique experience I highly recommend  ANY of the burlesque shows at Walking Fish.  Note  to out of towners and possible future tourists: the area is very sketchy at night.

How great is this place?!? Pretty great! Went in for a re-gripping on my racquets - got both done for $20! Not bad at all. And it only took about 15 minutes. I found out they have used racquets for sale and they also do clinics on the weekends. When I have more tennis needs, I will definitely be returning!
So excited to have this place in the neighborhood. Open super early and very kid friendly. Food is delicious. Highly recommend the freshly fried donuts and breakfast sandwiches are my favorite.
I used to come here to find random clothes and jeans, it seems that there was a better selection then then now. Now there appears to be a very... Selective selection.
We tried the frog legs as an appetizer and they were so good. The burgers were excellent! Overall, great restaurant. Nice atmosphere, great service and reasonable prices. Definitely want to go again!

I had the Jalapeno burger after a three-hour flight. It was super good but maybe that's because I was super hungry. I wasn't greeted right away and the cashier was on her phone while helping me but they definitely gave a shit ton of sweet potato fries so that compensated for her not being attentive. Like most fast food restaurant I think Smashburger isn't too bad. The salted garlic fries and sweet potato fries though,.. uhummm! :)
Food and service was great. Was not a fan of the BYOB but the food made up for the inconvenience.

One word - Nah.
Two words - Never again.
Selected D'Angelos for a Monday date experience because their hours were later than most, open until 12AM (great). We sat on the 2nd floor and were the only patrons in the restaurant. Service was swift and attentive. The decor was classy and elegant.
I inquired about their specialties and was told that the gnocchi and ravioli were made in-house (okaaaay okaaaay, let's get it). We started with an appetizer of the caprese salad, I ordered the gnocchi with red sauce/ side of meatballs...my date ordered the alfredo/ with blackened chicken.

Bread was brought to the table...accompanied by rough cut garlic and parsley in olive oil (literally, rough). Presentation was below average.

Caprese salad...tasty, presentation below average still.

The gnocchi was temperature warm, below average and nearly tasteless. The red sauce was reminiscent of store brought spaghetti sauce. The meatball flavor and texture was unappealing...come to find out...and I quote, "our meatballs are veal...that's how we make them here." I ultimately opted for the ravioli in place of the gnocchi, which was a slightly better selection...same "blah" red sauce.

My date did enjoy his alfredo, and I would have to agree that it was the tastier selection. 

4 stars for the waiter's authentic Italian accent.
1 star for the food.

I need to write about the stellar service and staff at ideal image liberty place.  The front desk staff is Always ultra accommodating. I had to cancel a few appointments because of a family medical situation and they were wonderfully understanding and helpful in rescheduling multiple times.  I'm always greeted with a sincere smile and offered coffee or a beverage.  I have been to many other places and they come nowhere near the great experiences/outcomes I have had here.  Though all of the above is wonderful, Melina, who is not only an esthetician, she is an actual RN, consistently embraces the artist in her when doing fillers/injectables.  She steps back to take a look at her work and discusses any adjustments she wants to make with me.  She is a amazing!  Not sure what I would do without her!!!  See you all soon!

We had a BLAST on this tour, it was so unique & fun. There were 5 in our group aged 15 through 72 and everyone enjoyed themselves. The guides were knowledgeable and we just loved the stops in areas off-the-beaten-path. May do it again!

I pick the spicy ramen. Nice ramen soup with less salty. The egg and pork was perfect. Love it. For one single person, it's really quick to wait for dining in; yet two people or more usually need more than 20 min.

I decided to give Mood a try this weekend instead of my go-to Ekta. Where to begin: let's start with the positive.  Online delivery and payment were super smooth & easy and my delivery arrived within the estimated 30 minutes. Then it was all downhill from there.

I started out with some chaat papri.  The papri were all soggy and disgusting! I was so turned off by the mushy papri that I stopped eating it after 2 bites.  I get it that it's delivery, but Ekta always manages to keep the papri crunchy in my delivery orders. Next up:  paneer pakora.  I pretty much love any type of fried cheese, but these were just average.  They didn't have much flavor and dipping them in the chutney didn't do much for them. My entree was the baingan bhartha (mashed eggplant dish).  I also felt this was lacking in flavor and spice.  My bf's malai kofta sauce was thin and sad and just not all that tasty. Neither of us finished our food.

Looks like I'm going back to Ekta for my Indian food cravings!

What a find! Before I moved to Philly, I came here to visit and wanted to check this place out after seeing it on Man vs. Food. On the show, they feature the roasted pork sandwich with broccoli and provolone. So we ordered one of those along with a brisket sandwich because the brisket looked fantastic. In my opinion, the brisket is better than the pork. To be fair, I've never really like pork much anyways. But that brisket is amazing, and somewhat surprising too, because I don't think of Italians for their brisket. When I think brisket, I think Jewish Brisket and Texas Brisket. I am a big fan of both. But man this brisket is also amazing! 

The first time I was here the line was extremely long, but the last time I was here, it wasn't bad at all. I think I had beat the lunch rush. The guys who work the counter are very nice, friendly, and helpful. I was surprised because the last time I was here I felt rushed. Maybe because it wasn't that busy this time. Each time I come to the Reading Terminal Market I've eaten here, and it will be tough next time because there looks like so many other great things to try too!

Second visit to this location and it didn't disappoint today.  Love the meat whether it be beef or chicken as it is nice and tender.  Decent amount of meat for each steak.  Fries are solid.  I liked the caramelized sweet potato fries but caution they are messy as the sauce drips out of the box into the bag.  I had sauce in my hair and no place to wash up.  The fountain drinks are rather small and for over $2 a pop I would have expected free refills on those.  I was told that refills were $1.

For a $9 buffet lunch, Tandoor is pretty good.  I really like palak and it was pretty decent here.  Their samosas weren't bad either.  Fellow diner enjoyed the chicken.  She had been a frequent customer a number of years ago and everybody working remembered her and was extremely friendly.  

The actual restaurant and buffet display leaves a lot to be desired.   I felt a little gnarly all day after eating here.  BUT, for a once a year cheap indian buffet lunch, I'd go back for the annual visit.

My hotel was next to Chinatown so we made various trips there to eat since I don't really get to have good Chinese food where I live back home. 
Everything I ordered was delicious. The siu mai was amazing! We couldn't stop eating!! 
The place is clean with large windows for people watching; the service was friendly; and the restaurant was filled with both young and old Chinese patrons.

Great location by City Hall. Friendly, helpful staff. Newly remodeled and clean room with a decent amount of space.  Really good water pressure on the shower.  Complimentary flavored water in the lobby.  Valet was a bit pricey and took awhile to get our vehicle. You might want to explore self park options nearby. I'd stay here again.

Now this is a bar. Dark wood, lots of handles, bathrooms downstairs. We piled in with 12 people on a Sunday afternoon. I had Palm Ale at the server's suggestion, plus a few Hoegaardens, a Penn Pale Ale, and...the rest is kinda fuzzy. The server was very friendly, the bartender not so.

This location used to be a crowded disaster on weekends, but I need to give them a 5-star shoutout for all of the innovative steps to maximize efficiency in their small store and parking lot.

1.  They completely remodeled the checkout, more than doubling the number of cashiers, and they did so in a tight space.

2.  They reinvented the checkout line.  It now wraps around the store when crowded, but don't let that intimidate you!  It moves lightning fast because they have crew directing the line and it's one line for all 14 or so registers.

3.  They have crew monitoring the inbound flow of cars into the tiny parking lot (which is free!) and directing traffic.  

Again, bravo to management and the crew for the most innovative crowd mgmt of any trader joes I have ever seen!

Whole Foods up in Fairmount...take notes!

You can't really beat a good sandwich, fries, and a buck fiddy beer.

We stopped here before a concert. My husband got the brisket grilled cheese which he said was good. Being a vegetarian, there weren't a ton of options for me but I got the tomato sandwich. It was fresh and interesting, although I do wish the bread was grilled.

Service was friendly. The place started to get packed on a Wednesday around 6pm. I'd stop here if I was in the area in the future.

I love this place.  I love their veggie grinders, though if you're like me you have to get them without green peppers and lettuce, and with onions.  Also, get mustard/mayo and have all the veggies cooked before AND after they are put on the grinder.  Delicioso!  This is the only food I get here, as I am trying not to eat animals that led terrible lives, and Philadelphia eateries don't seem too concerned with the living conditions of their beef.  

Also, the owner here is kind of awesome.  Any proprietor that enjoys talking shit about the Cowboys is ok with me!  And they have like a million beers, though they must be obscenely overpriced.  Seriously, some bitch got 2 six packs and a supersized bottle of Lambic and it cost $36.95.  And one of the six packs was of Yuengling!  I was kind of amazed that she didn't even bat an eyelash at that.  Clearly I should have mugged her after she left.  

Anyway, veggie faces and rich beer fans should love this place.  I guess I fall into one of these categories.

My friend dragged me over their to try their Shawarma fries and date tahina shakes.  The placed is small and packed but if you can snag a table, the wait is worth it.  The food is quick and delicious!  The wait is also worth it cause this place gets packed! You know the sign of good Eaton' is...a packed place! Their Shawarma French fries (3) are all dat.  Perfectly seasoned and hot and crispy! Try them. We went just to try these fries.  We also shared a Date Tehane shake (4.50) and it was sooo good!  It was very thick and had nuts and was so thick and creamy you almost had to eat it with a spoon.  I'd like to go back and try their Bag O' Falafel Balls (5) because everyone raves about them.  We were to stuffed to eat anything else and wished we would not have had dinner before deciding on the fries as our "dessert".  My friend loves their ketchup so much that she bought some to take home.  It's seasoned almost like a curry.  Very delicious!

Can't ever go wrong with Devon seafood but I will say that I wish the Lobster Mac N ' Cheese was a tad bit more flavorful but I think it was great as is, still could've been amazing lol. All the food we had was pretty much spot on. Had a great time. Great ambiance. I will say I'm upset I forgot the waitress' name because she was great, she definitely deserves flowers for her attentiveness and good communication throughout our meal.

I went here the other night as one of our stops while bar hopping. We didn't get any food so i can't rate on that but what I can say was the pretty bartender with the tattoos and red hair was AMAZING! She actually made me a hot tea because she could tell I was getting over a cold ! 
She also made the best old fashions we've ever had ! So 5/5 for service and drinks

Beat the crowd on a Saturday night getting there at 7pm and got seated right away. I'd never been when they were on Spruce, but their new space is really nice, very open and warm, sits a good amount of people but didn't get super loud. The waiter was very nice, though seemed a little distracted, food service was pretty fast and the food itself was really yummy. 

Between the three of us we got the pumpkin ravioli, the quail, and the clams spaghetti, and all plates were licked clean. My friend also enjoyed his house salad, and while it was very delicious, I was little surprised when my $8 mushroom-goat cheese-pinenut appetizer was literally a portobello cap with a lump of cheese and pinenuts on it... The tiramisu and creme brulee were also very good, but the chocolate cake was goooooooooooooood =)

I'm new to the city and was referred to Emma by my sister. While there is a certain no nonsense aspect to this salon, I was completely blown away by three things. First, the staff is unbelievably friendly. Emma was so sweet! I felt like I was walking into a family home for my brazilian. And second, the speediness of the service. I live a short 5 blocks away and it took me longer to walk to the salon than it did for Emma to do her magic. And finally, the price. You can't beat $35 for a brazilian! I would (and will) definitely recommend this salon!

Stopped in here over the weekend. I had seen this place on TV some time ago and made a mental note to stop in as some point. Well, that time was last Sunday(7/9). The place wasn't very busy. There was a couple eating outside and no one was inside, which was fine with me. The menu has several burger options and a variety of sauces. BTW, the garlic aioli is AMAZING. I think I need to come back just to try the other sauces. I had the truffle butter burger add bacon, cheddar and the usual accoutrements(lettuce, tomato, onion and pickle). No joke! This is one of the best burgers in Philly. I usually do mustard and ketchup on my burger, but I ate this one condiment free. It didn't need any condiments. A couple other burgers caught my eye(Cyclops and Kobe), so between the burger options and the sauces, I will be back.

If you hate American coffee (especially Starbucks) but shy away from gut-scortching Italian espresso, this place is for you.  If I lived in Philly, this place alone would convert me back to being a coffee drinker.

it's kind of like getting hooked on Motrin 800's...  
giving them up because you just we're "feeling it" any more...
and then having someone walk in and hand you a Percocet 10/350.

(I hurt myself a lot.  that was the best analogy I could come up with)
The broth is definitely what keeps me coming back for more. Great fasts service and super affordable. I would say this is one of the best places to get Pho in south philly.

Hands down,  my favorite book store in philly!  I love this store! Did i mention i love this store!  Yup that's how much i Love it! If you are looking for something positive to read, this is the perfect place.  The entire place has a positive vibe; the crystals, the incense, the beautiful jewelry, and the energy. It is a spiritual type of store full with knowledge and ancient secrets. Highly recommend it! Theres even yoga class iffer throughout the week downstairs :)
One of the most popular BYOBs in Philly, at least with students.  They have standard American fare - mussels are tasty.  Always loud and lively.

If you need waxing of any kind, look no further, Audra is your girl. I had waxed before but never a Brazilian so I was a bit nervous of the whole process. Well let me tell ya, Audra made me feel like I was just hanging out with a friend during the entire process. You are so personable, down to earth, kind, open minded and AMAZING at waxing. It was honestly a relaxing process, as crazy as that sounds because of Audra and how great she is. Thank you so much Audra for moving to Philadelphia and blessing everyone with your waxing skills and wonderful personality. I look forward to the next time I get to be waxed by you! Please move to Hawaii so you can wax my whole body

I'm sorry Natalie and Jose, but your response is blatantly false. We headed to the vet the day after I picked him up due to his incessant scratching - no way he could have gotten (lots of!) fleas in that 18 hour timespan. I called you on the way home from the vet and you offered no consolation, apology, or explanation. When I met you I did not see your facility, just the patio, and there was only one dog there - a bit misleading, but I can understand if there was some sort of miscommunication there. I didn't realize my dog would be one of ten or so - I was not informed and didn't think to ask. I'm glad you think Henry's experience was positive, but seeing as I can't exactly ask him, all I can do is assure you that my experience was not. I see that I'm not the only one, so best of luck cleaning up your act and getting back on track.

Bob our server was excellent! Food was great as usual and for it to be New Year Eve, everything went great! The chef even came and greeted us. We are frequent customers and they are always are on point. Love this place

Great Irish pub in the  old city. Great room with high ceilings and big old world windows does much to take away the traditional darkness and gloom of some older Irish pubs. Great selection whiskeys and beers on tap. Nice friendly staff and crowd. Worth a revisit next time we are in town.

The food was fantastic and made to order its small but everyone was great our waiter was personable and accommodating then we find out he was the owner john was just a great guy I recommend this place highly

Had dinner with my girl last Friday night.  It was a dead night downtown due to the Phillies game being on, but we still had a 45 minute wait for a table.  Glad we waited.  We had a great little meal consisting of an excellent prohibition cocktail, an enormous chilled tequila that filled a martini glass, a fried shrimp appetizer, and a heavenly burger with heavenly cheese fries.  The drinks were excellent and reasonable considering the neoghborhood.  The app was good, not great, but after betting on whether we were going to get 3 or 4 shrimp for the $7 price, my date and I were stunned to count about 15 medium sized shrimp.  After that hit the spot, the village burger with thousand island dressing, bleu cheese and carmelized onions ($16) came out, as did the $7 duck fat cheese fries.  The portions were big, and all of it was melt in your mouth good.  The burger meat was of excellent quality and very well ground, something I look for in a burger.  The bun was perfect, toppings just right, and it was cooked perfectly.  The fries were dreamy, probably the best I've had.  All of that excellence was served in a small but upbeat, quirky fun setting.  The menu is limited, but I'm sure everything the they do is done with perfection in mind.  I have a new go to spot in the RIttenhouse area.
Loved the cheesesteaks here, they aren't disgustingly greasy like most other places and they put a ton of meat in them. The line moves quickly so don't be discouraged from seeing a large crowd.

This is the first food truck I've encountered in Philly, and I was impressed. There are carts I like more in Portland, sure, but this is an hour drive from me, versus a four hour plane ride, so I'll take it. Had a pulled pork grilled cheese, and loved it. The texas toast was buttery and a good heft for the amount of meat and cheese and sauce that it contained. It was a bit pricey, but since I'd probably get it again given the chance, I guess that means it's priced just fine. Nice choice to park outside the immensely busy Yards Brewing, and good for them for letting their patrons bring these delicious sandwiches in to accompany their delicious beers.

This is a NEast staple and they do a great job with their steak and fries. If you're doing take out, beware, its gonna be messy and your delicously greasy steak might eat through its paper bag. And ruin your purse. But nevermind that. They don't chop their steaks, they do sliced steak and they have a separate line for fries, sodas, and other sides. It's good place to go when you don't have a lot of time and feel like having a fat kid Friday.

Great place for special occasions. The menu has a nice variety of choices. The atmosphere is pretty whimsical with an Alice in Wonderland feel. The portions are smaller, but it's filling. 

I love the cozy feel of the place even though it's upscale. There's warm lighting and flowers. It's such a charming place!

Philly has no shortage of Italian restaurants, so if you're new in town and offering Italian fare, and a BYOB at that, you better make it and you better make it well.

WIth Ulivo, also comes Monsu, Il Pittore and Alla Spina all in span of six months - lots of Italian cuisine, lots of interpretations and lots of options.  One must stand out.

With the Northern Liberties Sovalo pedigree, the offerings did not disappoint, but they did not sing either.

The space is ample, airy and roomy which is always such a departure from your run of the mill BYO, which I appreciated.  Great place to bring big parties.  Decor is rustic, basic and clean.  Service is friendly but a maybe spread a bit too thin.

As for the food, the cibo, my friends, the food was pretty solid and the portions generous.  Strongest options fiound in the starters and the lone pasta dish, and the best picks being the octopus and fried polenta starters, and as I remember the Sovalo specialty, the most dreamiest gnocchi: a ricotta gnocchi with spinach and black truffle butter - good to share as a starter (because of the richness), but you could definitely spoil yourself by just ordering it for yourself.  That plate alone could elevate Ulivo to four stars, but YELP being YELP we can never give half stars (seriously, I need half stars!!!).

But in all honesty, what prevents me from giving Ulivo a full four stars is for a couple reasons:  first, the desserts are really what sunk the ship for me, both a ricotta cake and an olive oil cake fell totally flat.  Both cakes were dry, crumbly and nearly flavorless, which was not a a nice way to end the meal.  Second, though we were offered a complimentary glass of wine when we waited over 45 minutes after our reservation time to be seated, and though I know it's the nature of the BYO beast, it was a bit frustrating to get such a late start and then feel like we were getting rushed at the end of our meal to leave as we were, evidently, the last table in there finishing up.  Lastly,  the other courses, did not seem to shine as much as the starters and pasta mentioned above - and had they been just as strong and tasty as the others, it might have propelled me into giving Ulivo at least four stars.  

In the sea of Italian offerings, this is just one more sailboat bopping in the water.

Stopped in for lunch and had some awesome wild boar tacos. The tortillas were the perfect crispness, still pliable but with crisp edges that had a crunch. The meat was juicy and delicious and the guacamole was creamy. The order came with two tacos, but I would have happily eaten two more. Mexican street corn was available as a special and I ordered that as a side. While the seasonings were good, the corn itself was small and tough and altogether not a good piece of corn. It was a bit of a letdown for this reason.

The beer selection is good with a nice selection of crafts on draft and a cooler with bottles and cans as well. I enjoyed a grisette from St. Benjamin on draft. I liked the atmosphere, a good local bar feel with better than expected food. I will likely return to try more food items and drink more tasty beers.

This is definitely one of Philly's best cheesesteak place. I came here once for my interview, which was also my first time having a cheesesteak, so I really didn't know what kind of taste I should be expecting. But after living in this city for over 6 months and experienced other cheesesteaks, I definitely can say "Jim" makes the best cheesesteak. I got the whizz mushroom cheesesteak with no onions and my friend got the cheesesteak with onions and american cheese. Including a can of soda, it was about $11 for me, with the additional cost of mushroom and $10 for my friend. The seating area is pretty small, but I definitely recommend eating there rather then to take it home. Initially I thought I would not be able to finish it, so I asked to have half of it packed to go, which I took a photo of how messy it becomes. This place definitely have a historical past, the walls are filled with signatures of professional athletics, actors, singers, and many others.

I really am not a huge fan of cheesesteak, but if I must have one, I would definitely go to Jim's.

FYI: cash only. There is an ATM inside.

This place sucks. The music is too loud that I had to scream to my friend while trying to talk. Oh and the servers are rude and takes forever just to get one hookah. Honestly I recommend you to go anywhere besides here.

Simply the freshest corn tortillas around. However, they make large tortillas (4.5") which is the common size you'd find in a grocery market... IF ordering a large batch, make sure to ask for pickup in the morning to get the tortillas FRESH out the oven.

Really good food but slow service, rarely check on you. Food is a little overpriced but authentic and really tasty and filling. The live music is sooooo overly loud and comes out of nowhere and stops. It's nice to have live music but not soooo loud that people can't enjoy their meal and talk. Also super cold.
The Kathy with an almond croissant is fantastic!! The waffles are also to die for. Delicious! Great service and an amazing atmosphere. Very laid back, and super affordable!

Go to Pat's. This place is not amazing. I also understand you should speak English, but if you are new to the country it is difficult most likely. Having a sign there with american flags and bald eagles saying to Order in English only made me want to bust out my thickest asian accent I can come up with and just F with the worker, but I'm above that. I don't know, I'm American, but my parents are asian. I've been in the US longer than these young kids working there and seeing a sign like that turned me off. I almost walked away, but my friend had already purchased his sandwich. Then we get to the hotel and I come to find out the place is Anti-mumia. Ok i'm never going there again. LOL. I'm a Mumia supporter and if you are against Mumia that is your opinion, but if you feel strongly for the unjust imprisonment of Mumia, why would you go to a place who feels he is a murderer and clearly support the officer? Now I am completely against killing an officer as everyone should be, but I can't support a place where they merely support this man because it's a cop hangout.

Cheap eats in chinatown as always.

I only had the the squid kebab, but my friends seem to like this place alot since they keep coming back.

Next time I will try the tofu because that seems to be really popular ish.

What a great place for locals and tourists alike. The sheer amount of food options you have will leave you astounded. There are a bunch of different gems you have to try - Beck's Cajun, Hershel's Deli, Beiller's Donuts, freshly squeezed OJ from Iovine Bros... those are my personal favorites. It's definitely a spot to hit if you're indecisive (like me!).

Displaced by Grey Hound 

On Sunday 30 people were displaced by BAltimore's Grey Hound location.

The passangers, myself included wanted to get to New York City, the busses out of Washington D.C. were over sold causing 30 passengers to be stranded in Philly overnight. Many passengers have missed their connections home. 

One of us has been on busses since Thursday.

The supervisior told us that Baltimore never called him on our 2 hour and 40 minutes bus ride. Sooo we have a two hour wait... 

The supervisior here at Philly has been helpful but the customer service at Grey Hound has been terrible round and round.
What's. Not to like. Just as cite as it gets. Everything in the entire store sang to me. Nice help. All just very special. I highly recommended Kent the staff re.

It's teeny-tiny but it has some great picks! I came in here after a noodle fix with a friend. While she looked for some dresses for work, I dove into the clearance racks for some exercise gear. There was only about 40 pieces per size but I was able to find four items for $7 each, so no complaining here.

The staff didn't come over to say anything to me, but checkout was surprisingly quick (when has that ever happened at a Marshalls/TJ Maxx?!). Also, the store was really clean and organized, which made the whole experience much better.

I've been wanting to try out this place for awhile and finally came here last night with my hubby. First the broth was delicious and very flavorful and the dipping sauce was good. The beef was very fresh as well as veggies. I would definitely come back again soon.

Drury beer garden was a pleasant surprise that we discovered en route to Bru.  It was the perfect spring afternoon to be outside, and we liked the selection of beers and available.  

The seating is pretty limited, but we were lucky enough to grab a picnic table before the crowd hit.  

One downfall was the willingness to serve food - when we first asked about food, they told us they weren't serving until 5, and then proceeded to deliver an order of fries to someone sitting at the other end of or table... SUPER FRUSTRATING. At 5, we asked again, and were first told we couldn't order food, but after reiterating that they said after 5 and it was after 5, they finally took our order.
I've been going to the salon for years it's always been the best in South  Philly

Came to medium full, friday at 9 am.  Waitress would not put in the order for my 2 yr old child without having our complete order ready.  We had to get a manager to clarify that this is not the restaurant policy and were given a wonderful new waitress.  The order for him went through right away with ours following 5 minutes later.  I do not settle for poor service from one lazy waitress and this restaurant came highly recommended.

Red velvet pancakes came quickly and were fluffy inside and crispy on the outside.  My son enjoyed them.  Theres definitely a bbq kick to the bloody mary.   The crab mac and cheese was yummy with a Dry finish on top, sprinkled with a heaping tbsp of crab, and a creamy skillet beneath.  The crab was sweet like diner cole slaw.  My wife got the breakfast burrito and said it had an indian kick to it.  The food is very filling and the portions are generous. I had to take the crab mac home and couldn't finish even half for brunch.  Would come back.  CASH ONLY.

I spent a rainy Sunday evening in the North Star Bar with my friend Leanimal, her roommate and a local drunk named Al playing cards.  There was some live music going on in the other room, but from what we heard calling it 'music' is a bit generous, although the people preforming were most definitely alive.

As far as dive bars go this one had no special characteristic (besides the live 'music,' I guess) to separate it out from all the other dives along Poplar street.
