I will say they were letting underage kids in to go to the show, which does give them some extra dive-bar points, but not enough to push it over the top.  

Since we were playing cards, Leanimal kept trying to turn the lights up, and the waitress kept dimming them.  Which we did not understand, because we were the only people in the bar who were not at the 'concert.'  After a few rounds of this game the waitress finally told us that the owner has cameras all over the place and watches the feed at his house, and he was calling in and saying that the lights in the back were too dim.  WTF?  The owner of the bar sits at home and watches live feed of the back room of the bar?  I'm guessing some pretty shady stuff goes on here after closing hours.

The PBR pounders were cheap and the french fries were greasy, but there is really nothing too spectacular about this place to warrant a return visit.

Oh, also, their website claims that they are 4 blocks from the Philadelphia Art Museum.  If by 4 they really mean 15 then they are correct.

Don't bother. I work around the corner from this resturant and we were short staffed so I called at 12:53 for takeout. I was told by the woman who took my order that it would be ready in 10 minutes. I got to the resturant at 1:07 and was told that my meal wasn't ready yet and it would be a few minutes and I was asked to sit down. I looked in the back to see if anyone was cooking and there were four men statnding in the back watching television. I waited for twenty minutes before I had to walk out without my food because my lunch break was over. The woman was upset with me for leaving but she had been in the back watching television as well and and at no point did anyone ever start cooking my meal.

Part 1 of a 2-part review:

I just realized that I've never written a review of Le Virtù, my favorite place for pasta in Philadelphia, so here it is...

Whenever I dine at Le Virtù ("The Virtues"), one of the mainstays of East Passyunk Ave, for some reason I have to keep asking myself, "Why don't I eat here more often?" The answer being that, with so many great restaurants opening on super-hot EPx in the past couple of years (my favs being Fond, Noord, Laurel, Will, and I'm sure upcoming Townsend), it's all too easy to get distracted.

But this was the first ever Philly Wine Week, and having decided to crash the "Unauthorized Underground Wine Week Industry Party" later that evening, four of us thought a little BYOB-pasta action would be a good prelude to any subsequent debauchery.

This was the second time I've been to LV in the past few months, previously having enjoyed several killer pastas with seasonal white and black truffles shaved over them (a $30-35 supplement). And I also remember enjoying some of their new cocktails. This time was no exception:

~SIRENTE (photo): Berentzen Pear Liquor, Stoli Vanil, rosemary, black pepper ($12)--This looks like a dirty rainwater martini, with bits of dead plant material and who knows what floating in it. Which might not sound very appealing, but it really was very tasty. Although I think they should back off on the vanilla a bit.

~LE VIRTU MARTINI: Stoli vodka, limoncello, splash of Italian lemon soda ($12)--I'm a sucker for limoncello, and anything remotely related to it, but this was a tad sweet perhaps for my taste.

~BOTTA: Abruzzesse Super Punch, rum, lemon, lime ($12)--"Super Punch" is the kind of Italian liquor that only my Italian grandmother would touch, assuming of course that she was actually Italian and not Irish, and had very bad taste. Hard to describe, but in this context it resulted in a fantastic cocktail. Just try it...

I love the comfortable wooden bar Le Virtù, ~10 seats right inside the restaurant's entrance. It is rarely too crowded, making it my favorite pre-dinner cocktail haunt at this end of EPx (Stateside at the other end). Plus they serve Cynar, which will definitely perk up your palate before/during/after dinner.

The menu is divided into Antipasti, Primi, Secondi, and Contori, but we decided to graze through the former two sections so as to enjoy the greatest variety, including what LV is best at: house-made, fresh, and often usual pasta.

~AFFETTATI MISTI: House-cured salumi and antipasti ($29)--Prepared in the basement by talented Chef Joe Cicala, this is a must order dish, with ~5-6 cured meets and a few pickled veggies. With a little red wine, I would be all to happy making a meal out of this. Plenty generous enough for 4 people to share, and served with excellent bread too.

~TRIPA IN UMIDO: Tripe braised in tomato sauce, grilled rustic bread, pecorino ($10)--I'm a sucker for offal, the offaler the better I always say. Bibou has a lovely, French variation of this dish, but I think LV's is even better, more earthy and flavorful, tender, tomatoey, just eat it with some crusty bread. Another LV must-have in my book.

~SALSICCIA NOSTRANA ALLA GRIGLIA: House-made Abruzzese-style sausage, polenta, peperonata ($12)--Loved this dish, the sausage was just so herby, but a bit on the small side for sharing, I could have eaten it all myself.

~MACCHERONI ALIA MUGNAIA: Hand-pulled, single-strand pasta, garlic, extra-virgin olive oil, hot pepper, pecorino ($18)--This might be Chef's signature dish (either this, or the head-on Porchetta he serves during his annual 40-course, 9-hour La Panarda feast), and if you have enough people, this could be a single noodle up to 60 feat long (see the video below). Simply prepared, and extremely al dente, I think this is frankly best when shared by a larger group. Not a flashy dish, but a fun dish, that exemplifies LV's respect for the history and tradition of Abruzzo.

http://youtu.be/wT9Kw72FEDQ

~TACCOZZELLE ALL'AQUILANA: Handkerchief pasta, Abruzzese-style sausage, porcini, black truffles, Navelli saffron ($20)--Perhaps my favorite dish of the evening, with perfectly prepared pasta, and a rich, incredibly mushroomy ragu.

~CIMA DI RAPA: Broccoli rabe, garlic, hot pepper, olive oil ($8)--

~WORD OF WARNING: Don't do what I did with the beautiful complementary basket of dried peppers, and eat one whole on a dare! Sort of like the Cinnamon Challenge, it's not the spice that gets you. Instead, use the provided scissors to add the desired amount of heat to your pasta.

To read the rest of my review, click below:

These guys were great. Early, efficient and a pleasure to do business with.

I moved from a third floor walk-up into a house in Philadelphia and they got the job done. Their hourly rate is also a bit cheaper than Mambo.

First off, I must say to disregard any review below 4 stars with comments about it not being their preferred style of food or a cuisine they don't typically eat. Those people should stick to McDonalds, Olive Garden and Cosi.

On to GTC - I have eaten here numerous times and the staff has always been very pleasant and appropriately attentive (but not like overly interruptive). Food is always great although I have never been there for, nor would I ever think to go there for, brunch/breakfast. Stick to lunch and dinner. Even went in at restaurant week once and the food and service were as good as usual (the desserts we got with this meal were incredible!). Based soley on the restaurant, they'd get a five-star but the market area, specifically the cheese is way overpriced. It is a good selection of cheese and charcuterie but pricing is better on cheese at DiBruno Bros. where they do have more variety. Pizzas are great

Love that it is sorta BYOB. Yet if you forget to bring something, you can go to the state store (although I heard recently they started serving beer so not sure if it is BYOB anymore). I will be going back again and taking my family next week.

Food is pretty good although inconsistent. The staff however never smiles and is the most unprofessional I have seen in philly. Today, as I was eating at the bar a waitress complained about the small tip she received while another was having a conversation about a sexual act (I would rather not say what it was). 

Classy.
Not bad for a small food truck like setup located in a gas station.  Price is very affordable.

My husband picked this place for a nice meal before going to see a ballet, and it made him look really good. We had a table at the bar, overlooking the open kitchen, which was fun. The multicourse set up was interesting, and almost everything was good, if not really good. There were one or two things I wasn't crazy about, but so be it. I might have preferred a bit broader set of options, and I was disappointed that the steak requires two people or a large surcharge (as you may know from my rants in other reviews, hubby is a vegetarian, so I don't get to eat as much steak as I would like). But this is a solid, elegant, inventive restaurant. We look forward to coming back. My suggestion is that it is a good place for out of town colleagues or your favorite aunt and uncle--I wouldn't call it romantic, but it is a very pleasant place. The service was fine, but a bit rushed and stand offish. There are other restaurants in town that have garnered more affection from me--Sbraga is not warm and fuzzy. Next time, I'm getting the steak.
Grace is the best! I got laser hair treatments from her and she was a little nervous going into it. She was so helpful and comforting. Everyone was also very nice and the office is great!

Went here for lunch with my girlfriend on Tuesday.

We started things off with a glass of wine for her and a General Lafayette Porter for me. Our waitress was proficient, but a bit distant and she didn't really seem concerned with our orders. Fortunately, she was the only downside.

We got the Claudio Fresh Mozzarella, Tomato and Basil Bruschetta appetizer, which was delicious, if a bit small and, for the main course, she ordered the Smoked Salmon and Lemon-Pepper Vinaigrette Salad and I got a Smoked Chicken with Lancaster Cheddar Panino. My sandwich was excellent and the salad was apparently pretty good as well. 

The beer list was also quite impressive, featuring several Belgians and a few special brews from local micros.

Overall, it was a nice lunch experience and I will definitely return sometime in the future. I guess my only complaint is the lackadaisical service and the not entirely welcoming atmosphere.

Everything we ordered here was good if not downright delicious, and the service was warm and friendly. I wasn't feeling too hungry when we went in, so I just ordered a Greek salad, but luckily my parents had eyes bigger than their stomachs and shared some of their soups and entrees with me.

The soups were the least impressive part of the meal -- both the lentil soup and the soup of the day were only okay. The lentil soup was punched up a bit with a squirt of lime juice, but compared to the rest of the meal, the soup was disappointing.

The Greek salad was great; the feta was tasty but not too salty, the dressing was nicely garlicky. The greens were quite fresh and crisp, and the tomatoes on both my salad and the salmon entree that my mom had were juicy, like they had come from a backyard garden. The olives on my salad were also really good, just a bit tangy. The only thing I would change about the olives was that there weren't enough of them.

My dad had the grilled Mediterranean eggplant, which is served with a tomato and olive sauce on top of Israeli couscous. This was a great rendition of eggplant, and the tomato sauce had more succulent olives to enjoy.

We rounded out the meal with some rice pudding (topped with tasty crunchy pieces of pistachio) and sweet mint tea. As we were finishing our meal, I told my parents they ought to move to Philadelphia so that we could enjoy good meals like this one more often.

I took my parents here for my mothers birthday last weekend. From the moment we walked in, Richie (Manager) made us feel at home. We brought two bottles of wine, both were opened for us (poured ourselves) and delicious house made bread was brought to our table. If it were up to my father, he would have ordered the bread all four courses. We chose the four course tasting menu for $40. We were warned the portions were smaller, but I was happily content after just the second course. For everyone who is complaining about the wait for food, THIS IS A DINING EXPERIENCE... It is not meant to be a rushed meal. The hits of the meal were the Pasticiatta, Agnolotti, and Pesce Arrosto. This is definitely one of the better dining experiences I have had in quite some time through and through. I highly recommend Modo Mio and will be returning next month!

While this place may not be swanky,* it's a decent place to usher in warm weather and get your toes did. Mani/pedi combo is only 25 bucks! 

*I don't find it 'grungy' as was stated in another review. Things can be sterile and clean without the help of an uber-modern interior decorator. To each his own, I guess.
Best sushi anywhere. Made me into a sushi snob. Better than places in bucks county and Philadelphia center city. Jay - owner and sushi chef is true artist.
I expected the bbq chicken sandwich to be more than a dry piece of chicken between two buns. No sauce or toppings. Highly disappointed considering how good the food looked in line

They are making another las margarita on old York rd in front of Abington hospital and let me tell you I can't wait on top of the 6$ lunches and great late nite margaritas I don't no what's better  So I love this place and Abington will too

Valentine's Day is always a toughie.  And after 20+ of them with my husband it can get REALLY tough.  But this one was fun! We went to the WCL wines of Itaplly dinner on the 13th.  Relaxed,  pleasant and interesting, WCL and Joseph Brandolo presented a delightful International Festival of the Arts Dinner. 

Prosecco had been already poured at the tables, as we walked in.  MIsto or Ribollita promptly appeared.  Each course, paired with a delightful Italian wine was carefullly thought out andthroughly enjoyed (though the Bucatini all" Ametriciana reminded me of spaghetti and meatballs...instead of rabbit loin served over Bucatini with Pancetta and Tomato).  

They've got more coming in April. I can't wait!

We stopped in on Valentines day at 11:30 PM after seeing radio Dept at Union Transfer. This place was packed at 11:30 which blew my mind. We waited a bit for a table which was soooo worth it. The foods were all really fresh and loaded with tons of flavor. We had the shreded pork and clams, wow that was awesome!!! and peking duck, Super tasty!!! Last was sweet and sour fish which i loved!!! Portions were pretty big, we had quite a bit to take home and the price was good too. 

Staff are friendly but work very hard here.
I enjoyed my latest visit to this establishment. The service was good and the food was delicious. It's a small place but myself and two others were able to get seating at the bar.
Excellent wine and beer (and I am a snob with regards to both). The food and vibe are also excellent. Only negative is it is a bit pricey, but, whatever. :)

We met the Marchiano family while on vacation in OCNJ.  What a terrific group of people!  From across our balconies we chatted over beers and they mentioned their bakeries in Manayunk and a newly opened bakery in Sea Isle.  They surprised us the following night with the gift of a fresh tomato pie, and a cheesesteak and pepperoni bread.  They were all delicious!  We can't wait to visit their new bakery in Sea Isle and and share with our family and friends!

I don't typically go out for what I'd consider fancy pizza. I'm the mom & pop shop that delivers to my door kind of gal; but, after a lot of nudging I took the ride down to Alice.

Oh. My. God.
I cannot think of a single thing from food, wine, desserts, & service that was not spectacular. My date & I tried 3 kinds of pizza which were each delicious. The charcuterie board with burrata was soooo good. We each got our own pastas & what we could eat by then was great. We weren't expecting such portions sizes!
Phillip, our bartender, was the sweetest! He explained everything we got, how it was made or where it came from, you could tell he was truly passionate about the food.

I took a s'mores dessert to go since I can't resist anything s'mores & didn't touch it till the next day. By then I was wishing I had grabbed a  2nd one since it was damn near one of the best desserts I've ever enjoyed.

I feel like I'm sounding dramatic or exaggerating but this was really what a good restaurant experience should be. Good food, great service, fun people to spend it with, & a lasting positive memory. This place has truly gained a customer for life

i am not vegan but i LOVE this restaurant! when i have vegan friends visiting i always like to take them here! the food is ALWAYS delicious and the ambiance is always on point! make sure to leave room for desert!

My team and I had our planning meeting there this past weekend. We had some billing issues the front desk could not resolve however after speaking with management, they were able to fix it quickly. While management was helpful, I had to speak with four people to make sure everything was on a master bill and if I didn't ask for a manager, it would not have been resolved. Other issues were my confirmation had the free wifi that ended up not being free. Lost luggages for my team, took awhile for my team to get checked in. Other than what I stated above, the hotel is a great place to stay. Very modern but the front desk customer service needs improvement.

The decor is great, the food was good, the lighting heavenly. The sexy sleek look of Pods interior would impress any out of town visitors or dates. 

But what dazzled me about this beauty were its restrooms. John, shitter, WC, or loo, Pod's bathrooms are proof of gods love of humans. These amazing unisex stalls are spacious enough for u-n-i-sex ;).  How progressive that men, womyn, trans, and intersex, can all share the same queue without waiting in inequitably long lines. This is also a great convo topic to chat up some eye candy in line. 

My mother always says that the quality of a restaurant's bathroom will tell you about the cleanliness of its kitchen. Considering I felt like i could have comfortably eaten in those neon stalls, I think that is the highest compliment I can give pod. :)
Mediocre service poorly prepared food that takes too long. Inattentive management. A real contrast to other Ruby Tuesday locations.

Quite possibly the worst meal/drinks/service I've had in Philly in years. There is Nothing redeeming about this place. Nobody there cares about things like flavor, quality, ambience.  Our server was borderline rude, didn't bother to care that my plate was left untouched because the food was so incredibly tasteless.  $16 a plate that was like canned beans and boiled potatoes. Drinks that were basically dishwater. Was there with two friends, and all three of us walked out in utter disappointment.  Don't bother. Total waste of money.

Great mac and cheese. Great service. We will
Be back as often as possible so we can try more of the menu.

We have much better options in the city as far as barbecue goes, but I have to admit the highlight of the meal for me was the crispy yet succulent (if not salty) lamb belly. A very innovative and interesting item, great for people who don't eat pork too. 

The extensive beer menu is great too. Lots of options.

Our server might have been more informed. The bread tasted a little stale. The sides were passable, but the mac 'n' cheese was barely tasteless. Pulled pork and brisket were done nicely, but not compared to their rivals (Sweet Lucy's and Percy St. come to mind). The sauces were nice, but we had trouble getting the mustard sauce out of the squeeze bottle.

This might be an odd complaint, but our servers were kind and warm, but wearing short shorts on a cold rainy spring day was a little unusual. But I don't prefer servers in costumes, it's a little diminishing of them and the place.

Food was great and service was excellent! I went there because Nine Ting was packed. The quality of the meat was great and the soup was especially amazing. I chose the half and half option. Excellent overall!

This place is close to my new home and it's my favorite restaurant in the area by far. Great beer selection if you're in craft beers. The food is also never dissapointing. On my last visit I had the short rib pizza and I was in heaven. It's a quality food spot for sure with nice people, good music always playing and a cozy atmosphere.

This was my first time ordering and I got a Stromboli special! Needless to say it was worth the buck plus $1.00 off Monday special. The only flaw is that it lacked flavor and sauce! Fast delivery also helped!!

Absolutely delicious. As others have said, the no reservation policy will become problematic soon, but our party of 5 arrived on the night of the great snowstorm of early 2016 and we were seated after about 15 minutes. The service was good, the space was appealing, simply and elegantly put together, and the food itself was fabulous, just glorious. For openers a green salad with pancetta was delicious, as was roasted cauliflower; in the second course the gnocchi with boar ragu was a standout; and the cod entree was just about perfect, beautiful and delicious, bright green kale contrasting with the soft white fish floating in a red paprika-colored poaching liquid. Drawbacks? It's loud, there were only two dessert choices (doughnuts and panna cotta, and even if you're dining alone order both!), and at least for now, no credit cards or reservations. BYOB.
Took a date to dinner here last week.  The food was excellent, and we had a sampling of pretty much the whole menu.  The lamb gyro was my favorite. Great BYOB place, and the ambience is fantastic.

Delicious!  We had the ricotta pancakes which were delicious!  The connolli cream was perfectly sweetened and the portions were large. Coffee wasn't hot and waiter didn't come back and offer more coffee. We are big coffee people. The clear mugs look cool but don't keep the coffee at an optimal temperature.  The restaurant is very urban and has a terrific industrial feel. The neighborhood is rough -- walk there with a friend.

being at cut is always great! i go to alex and he rocks the fade every time. great conversations with that guy. wow does he crack me up! cute doggos in here, too. totally recommend cut! see alex if you can!

Wanted to go to Green Eggs but line was too long.    This place is the BOMB if you care about your food.    Everything made to order, fresh and VERY tasty!    These guys here really care about making sure you get the best food in a friendly atmosphere.   You will not be disappointed!     Just a heads up...no eggs after Noon.

This isn't the first time I pulled up to the drive through (after waiting in a long line) and nobody talked on the speaker. I waited almost half hour last night and then could not even order my food. I left, came back, and the same thing happened. People were blaring their horns and everything. Get your sh*t together. Then you hear these establishments want $15/hr !!LMFAO. If reheating frozen food and dropping things in a fryer is too complex for you maybe find a new job.

I shopped here before and when I went to get my iPhone unlocked they put a R-Sim in there that rarely let my actual SIM card connect to my phone. Other times I went with a friend, and they tried asking her inappropriate questions. And most recently I ordered a phone from them because I had no way to contact potential jobs. Yet when my friend went to get it, they said they didn't have it. But earlier that day they told me they had it in the back. Then they said they mailed it out. Came to find out that wasn't true. They didn't mail it out till way later. I was suppose to receive it via mail 1/24/19, and it finally came 2/16/19. Yet when it came, only thing in the box was a charger, a iPhone case, and gloves that said MyPhillieWireless. I went to get my money refunded and was told they don't do refunds, and they started to laugh and joke about it. And when as of 3/15/19 at 10:38pm the owner of MyPhillieWireless Khalil Bashar, he texted me after weeks of not hearing from him when I politely contacted him about never receiving my iPhone... And he threatens to come to my house and attack me. Then he calls me outta my name a few times as if I'm suppose to be scared of him. This is a horrible place to go. And nobody should have to do business with MyPhillieWireless ever again. And today at around maybe 11:18am he texted me again calling me out of my name, and making threats because he was mad I got a refund for a phone I ordered yet never received. I will never again do business with this place, and urge you all to find another phone store to get all your electronic items repaired. No customer should ever have to go through this.

Worst customer service, flew from Philadelphia to LA, I was 40 minutes ahead of time at airport from my flight but apparently I missed the flight. Rep said it says on their website to be 2 hours early at the airport and was rude saying that reasonable to ask. but there is nothing on airline site or even in email stating that. I had to book another flight for additional amount which was connecting through Dallas. It was an hour late and no one once apologized for being late on their behalf. 
On flight back. flight was cancelled but no email or phone calls made. I happened to check the flight status and found out the flight was cancelled. I called the airline customer service, lady on phone was very very rude. I was beyond mad at her attitude. She kept on insisting to book new flight with no compensation what-so-ever for cancelled flight. WORST AIRLINE to fly with. Never going to fly this airline again, ever!!!
Their cauliflower parm is one of the best things I've ever eaten. The service is so friendly and engaging, making the entire experience very enjoyable.

Absolutely love this gym! I always get an effective workout in. You don't have to stand around waiting for machines because it is never crowded. The place is always clean and offers clean towels to utilize during your workout. The gym is about ten years old but everything seems brand new and is in working order. The locker rooms are spotless and have showers in case you're on the go. Membership is fee is only slightly more than sweat but it's worth way more. No contract, no initiation fee, easily cancelled my membership because I'm moving. I'm really sad to have to leave this place.
First I find a battery in my salad and now I find a hair in my sandwich. That is the last straw. I'm never going back. This place is Nasty!!

Stayed here for one night with my wife for a "staycation" for our one year anniversary. The staff were very polite. Our room included a "fuji soak tub" which is a delightful hot tub with jets and bubbles. The only hotel in Philadelphia that has a hot tub or jacuzzi to my knowledge. The view from the 8th floor was a mix of beautiful old architecture and modern sky scrapers. You are right in the middle of the shopping district and they even have bikes available (although I'm not sure if you have to pay to rent them.) I highly recommend taking the elevator up to the top floor to wander around the meeting/reception rooms to check out the view. Other pluses included complimentary wine for a limited time on Saturday night and free coffee in the morning. Downsides are the exorbitant prices on mini fridge items (a bag of m&ms was $9!) and no continental breakfast.

Really good food steak. We arrived when the place was opening and we're greeted by a grumpy host, who may not have had a good-night-sleep, who told us that there may be a table, but we'd had to wait. It was a gorgeous afternoon, so we did. Then, our amazing and friendly Venezuelan waiter showed up, followed by a delectable parrilada, well cooked and with abundant chimichurri, as it ought to be.

Came here for happy hour while visiting Philly for a few days and was very happy with my meal. Shared a (very) large plate of nachos that were served very differently (basically on a pizza pan) from how I've had them elsewhere (tried some more nachos in Philadelphia and they were served the same way so maybe it's just a Philadelphia thing). One order was more than enough for 2 of us and were topped with plenty of cheese, the right amount of sour cream, beans, hot peppers, and other toppings - the chips were still crunchy and not drowned by the toppings to the point of sogginess. Also had a solid house margarita at a good price. Overall, definitely a place worth recommending

Really solid dining experience here.  We had the following:

Steak skewers
Sample skewers
Shrimp tacos 
Chicken wings 
Kimchi fried rice
Brussel sprouts 
Edamame ravioli 

The steak skewers and shrimp tacos were my favorite of the bunch.   The edamame ravioli comes in a deliciously flavored broth and was tasty as well.  I wasn't a huge fan of the Brussel sprouts as I felt they were a little too heavy on the tart side and could be a bit more balanced in flavor.  

Our waitress was really nice and we left stuffed beyond measure.  Really nice meal.

Great spot.  Decorated very  60's (in a good swanky way).  I usually never have ciabatta sandwiches , but our waitress (Olivia) highly recommended the Grilled Chicken Ciabatta sandwich, so I went for it.  It was absolutely the best, the chicken was melt in your mouth fresh, the dressing added to the layering of taste and the bread was easy to eat (not tough as most ciabatta seems to be).  The fries were shoestring and seasoned nicely. 

My husband had the Cobb salad and he thoroughly enjoyed it!

I've patronized this shop numerous times since its opening, and can only say it's going downhill quickly! The bagels have become hard/stale and toasting it only makes it worse, as the settings on the toaster are set for burn!
They've gotten my order wrong twice in the last three visits, and while the staff is friendly, I'd prefer that my order was made correctly versus friendly service. I'll most likely give it one more shot, this time without toasting and checking my order before walking out, but I don't have high hopes. Really just wish there bagels were as delicious as they were a year ago!

Cheap, Yummy, and supporting a good cause. This spot couldn't make you feel any better. My hubs and I really enjoy stopping by here and we are not disappointed with the pizza. At 1.25 a slice and one of the most affordable options around for pizza, Its impossible to go wrong.

Good tasting milk tea, but confused why the hot tea is more expensive than cold tea. Workers were slow and she did not close the cap completely on my drink so when I picked it up, the drink spilled all over and burnt my hand. They did not offer any apologies or replacements.

Izumi never fails! The rolls are creative and delicious. The staff is knowledgeable and organized. If you haven't tried Izumi, put it on your list. The rock shrimp tempura are a must and any of the specialty rolls. My husband loves the generous sashimi. And because it's a BYO, you can enjoy more sushi!

Best Bar Food I've Everrr Had 

I've never drank here...but I go in and eat at least twice/week.

They're wings are DIVINE!! And during happy hour they're 10 cent/piece. 

The Vera Cruz quesadilla is heavenly....bacon, extra chz, carmalized onion, bbq sauce, chkn, peppers, side of ranch dressing. Can we say #WINNING!!!!

The crab fries are realllly good (a tad salty) and the cheese just melts in ur mouth

If u go here to eat u wont regret it. I'm going to get the buffallo chkn nachos next time i go...i'll keep u guys posted :)

I really, really want to give this place more than 2 stars. The sandwich wasn't bad. I think the flavors were there. But. I found a plastic piece in the sandwich. That's a huge no-no for me. In addition to the plastic, the bread was soggy.

I was surprised to learn that Matt & Marie's customizes your sandwich even after you order something off their standard menu (I ordered this online).

Plus points for using seeded bread for sandwiches.
We had their five course me for my birthday last year and it was out of this world. They are super friendly and accommodating to allergies too.
The service here is horrible. Their system for making drinks is completely disorganized and causes orders to be completely ignored. My order took fifteen minutes to be completed.

Hard to find. Circled the block 3 times before realizing it's tucked away behind a short brick wall and not brightly lit. Odd.

The vibe inside is super stoner-chill. The food was good and they have gluten free wrap options, but my wrap smelled and tasted like playdoh. Not sure what it was made of.

I ordered vegan cream cheese on my wrap that I also tossed, the texture was spot on but the flavor was super weird.

My husband got a normal bagel with normal cream cheese and smoked salmon, which he said was "pretty good!"
Small rooms but clean.  No frills hotel w a great location if attending a conference.  One block from Reading Terminal.  Good restaurant and bar.
eh. the clam chowder was salty and the seafood salad was drowning in dressing. plus most of the lettuce was wilted at best. won't go back.

Just got their takeout, delivered to Queen Village. We ordered and paid using an online form, which was cool. We got papaya salad, nice and crunchy. Green curry with chicken, tender, not too sweet, with eggplant, mushrooms, bamboo and squash(?). Also pad kee mao, with tender flavorful big noodles. Convenient, fast and tasty.

Hands down the best shop in the city. We have everything from concert posters to special art pieces framed by Trinity. Floating prints, beveled mattes, mounted 3D art. Always a fair price and excellent work every single time.

Savas has the best pizza in University City and possibly Philadelphia. If you just want to grab a slice to go, their slices are huge so usually one is enough. My other favorite menu items include their paninis, chicken salads, and fettuccine alfredo. They are a little pricier than other pizza places, but the food is much better quality.

Such a great experience! I was there a few days ago and Aubrey was my server. But she wasn't the only one that took care of me- The whole staff checked on me frequently! I sat outside and enjoyed people watching while I ate my many courses. I was very undecided so I eventually decided to just get quite a few items. My server was very knowledgeable and gave me accurate descriptions about every dish I was interested in. By far the mamas meatballs what is the best. Everything else was also tasty and I'm so happy to have found this vegan restaurant. Thanks for everything!

Had been a dentist for my 5 years old for couple years, until recently my son got a schedule for filling.
Instead worked on the filling, the dentist gave my son two baby root canal and two silver crown WITHOUT parent consent!!!

I had to call to their office asking them what they did to my son and they said they made a mistake and just said sorry... This is a terrible mistake for a well-known dentist and also illegal to do a surgery without any acknowledgment from the parent. And not to mention insurance fraud.

This kind of things has to stop, I don't want more children has to suffer.

Please beware and ask them what they gonna do to your kid, stay beside your kid. And find your self another good dentist

Tourist attraction. Not worth the hype, if you want to say you've had a "philly cheese-steak check it out but not worth the trip to south philly.  A little bit better than Geno's, but not by much. Want a great Philly cheese-steak go to a food truck, some of the best cheese-steaks I've had are from food trucks!

I came here to buy my first ever plant! I left with a pathos, two succulents, potting mix and a beautiful bag plant holder! The staff was friendly but I wish they would have been a little more thorough when explaining the watering process of the plant since I have never owned one before. Other than that, it's a beautiful little shop and it was the perfect way to end our trip to Philly!

my bf took me here on a friday night with some co-workers. we missed the appertizer time and had ordered 2 fantastic meals when we arrived late. he had triple tower with chicken, eggplant and vegetables. i had salmon (perfectly cooked!) laid on a flat wood and the risotto - mucho gusto! we had the table at the back of the restaurant outside where we can see the manayunk river and the bridge - a nice view from the top. the people are happy and this place is definitely not loosing customers due to 'recession'. why would they not? it's a wonderful place to meet friends or just have delicious food. i really enjoyed it and will definitely come back. ooohhh- the dessert is not great but it's still good. we shared an apple turnover in a burrito fried wrap. i was not crazy about it because the apples were dried inside and the crust of the burrito wrap was too thick. i think it's should be like a crepe - thin crust is better and the apple should be saucy and it could have caramel inside - if it was me that bake this it would be this way. maggiano's has a different take of an apple turnover and i like theirs better. oh, well...

Found this place using Yelp while on business in the area.  I like to find a new Irish Pub wherever I visit to try a Guinness.  Billy Murphy's might have the BEST burger I have ever had!!  Napkin dispenser came in handy and the fries were hand cut.  By the way, the Guinness was delicious.  Definitely coming back when I'm in town.

Yummy. There aren't really any korean food trucks on campus, so to see one was a wonderful surprise. I ordered the udon noodles with chicken. The wait was a little bit long, but what can you expect at noon? For 7 dollars, the amount of noodles and chicken was good. The chicken was delicious, the noodles had a good spice to it. I just wished that they can put a little more broccoli in it, there was only one ):

Arrived here because the store on Ogontz was under renovation and I was disappointed in them for opening this second store; they are under staffed and not pleasant at all. Walked in there was no at the host booth and non of the employees greeted us or even asked if we needed help until I called out after waiting for 10min. The wait was over an hour we will never come to this restaurant again poor service and poor quality food.

It is good. I had to say that before saying what I do not love about it...I want to like this place more but it is so old-school (and not in a co way). The ambiance just screams "1980s," and the style of the food is the same way. The food tastes good but it is just nothing innovative. It is a reliable Italian place though and it is conveniently located by the Academy of Music, Kimmel Center, and other theaters in the area.

Like the atmosphere, even though it's an "reproduction" of a British-style pub, a la Steven Starr's version of a Paris bistro in Parc.  It's A-OK, 4 stars.  Might actually be nicer than some "real" British pubs, the way Epcot can sometimes be better than Europe (less graffiti!).  The food is good, consistent.  They pay pretty good attention to delivering the goods, which says a lot, considering the number of plates they serve.  Someone is paying attention in the kitchen.  I had a traditional roast and it was excellent.  A hearty thing to eat in the winter.  Enjoyed the upstairs room, as it seemed a bit more private and less hectic than downstairs.  I would avoid this place at crowded times, as it gets REALLY crowded, unless you enjoy that kind of thing.
Great food I've eaten at the northeast location so I had to try the one broad. The food is just as good cool location I will definitely be back.
*update* Line out the door? 20 minute wait. Seated with coffee in less than five minutes, food in the next five. Delicious as always. Best bacon around. This place just does everything right.

I was hoping Avenida would be a great place to walk to on those nights when you're too tired to cook and craving some latin spice. Many descriptions about this place led me to believe it would be more interesting latin fare in a casual environment at family-friendly prices (I think one article actually said it was *budget-friendly*). 

Well, there isn't much on the menu for the budget-conscious, as most entrée items are in the $18-22 range, and pretty much all drinks are $9+. The tables are very crowded together, and the waiter's apologies for the 25 minute wait for clean glasses to prepare our drinks didn't really make up for the nastiness of the hostess and chaotic nature of this entire experience. 
The food was decent, but not worth the money on a regular basis.  The appetizers were a good size - we tried the "el Biche" soup and a vegetarian fondido. The fondido was good, but was also something I could've made at home with some Trader Joe's prepared foods, and the Biche soup needed a little something more to make it more of a signature dish (either more lime or chili?). The entrées were ok too, but just ok, which is not enough to justify the money we paid. I think our drinks were really the best part of the meal- the margaritas tasted fresh and they didn't skimp on the alcohol.

We will probably venture back sometime again, but will probably keep it to an app & drink or 2 at the bar.

My first experience with Gianna's was when I was staying in Philly and got  cake delivered. This was great because I get very excited when cake is vegan and it was pretty damn good. Also I didn't see where it came from.

Then I went to the actual place to eat and my perception changed a bit. The restaurant is not a pleasant place to be. It's scuzzy and there aren't many seats. Everything looks dirty and I was told by my friend to avoid the restroom since it was pretty gross.

That didn't stop me from going back however because sometimes I really crave pizza and at Gianna's I can get it. I do have some complaints with it though. It's wonderful that they make a Sicilian style vegan pizza but there isn't nearly enough cheese on it. It's more crust and sauce with little sections of cheese which I save until the very end and savor. When I make pizzas for myself at home, I take the time to thinly slice a block of soy cheese so everything is covered in wonderfulness. It's a pain yes, but really not that hard to do. 

I don't have any complaints about the cheese fries. The cheese melted and there was plenty of it and fries galore and it worked. 

Even though I don't like being in Gianna's I'll definitely stop back when I'm in the area. I can't really pass up the opportunity for a big piece of cake on the way back to catch a train. I don't think I'd go out of my way to eat here though.

I would give this 4 1/2 stars but it loses one and a half for the price.  The burger itself cost $11 but then when you add in cheese and stuff that you would normally expect on a burger it makes it a very expensive burger like 16 bucks.  The staff was good and everything else was pretty good atmosphere was nice but just too expensive for burger.

I grew up in Taiwan and immigrated to the US. This is the most authentic Cantonese restaurant in Chinatown, therefore it is my favorite. I have been coming to this restaurant for 20+ years. The food has always been delicious, the quality has always been great and the service is good. I like many of their dishes. Just to name a few:  "Assorted seafood bean curd hot pot", "Seafood and fish maw soup", "Pork chops Peking style","Sliced beef with black mushrooms and bamboo shoots", "Fried stuffed bean curds". Their interior and decor need to be modernized. For me, the food quality more than make up for the decor.
Quality service.  Cool atmosphere.  Small but good menu.  Loved the brueshettas we tried.  Great entrees and small plates.

The food was great but the customer service wasn't so good, waited a half hour just too order our drinks, we didn't get any napkins or forks we ordered a appetizer on the menu and apparently they didn't have it which is ironic because we came when the first opened up. But other then that it's a good place too eat

We were walking around the city looking for somewhere new to try for lunch when lo and behold, a girl was passing out a bottle opener with a 10% discount to Franklin Social which just opened that day. So we checked out the menu and then grabbed a seat inside! We were promptly greeted by everyone that saw us. 

Overall, the service was a bit overzealous. I felt like in an effort to do really well on opening day that they were "doing too much" which ultimately just made me feel rushed. For example, offering to take our plates away constantly, including when I was in the middle of putting a fork full of salad in my mouth, "can I take that for you?" No thanks, I'm still working on it. 

The other major no no for me was when I asked for a straw for my water and he brought me an unwrapped straw with his bare hand and then put it on the dirty table. Ummmmmm, I'm good on the straw...thanks anyway. 

The food was delicious. My Dad can't have spinach but we liked all of the other elements of the spinach salad so we asked to have that salad with arugula in place of the spinach which they did and it was delicious. They even plated it in two separate bowls for us which I always appreciate. I actually didn't love the flavor of the nuts which I was originally really looking forward to. 

We both got the turkey sandwich and it was really good. The bread was perfect. The aioli was good. The textures of everything on it worked well together and it didn't completely fall apart like some sandwiches do. It was a little bit messy in the sense that I felt like if I put it down it wouldn't necessarily stay together so I just held it until I was finished the half. The French fries were a little limp. They unnecessarily had parsley on them which adds nothing other than bits of food that could potentially get stuck in your teeth. I didn't finish them because they weren't that good.  I NEVER leave a French fry behind! 

We were given our bill without asking for it...or being offered dessert. I'm assuming it's because we were there around lunch time on a weekday and the business crowd likely prefers to get in and out without these offerings. We weren't offered any other water or beverage once our bottle of sparkling water was finished. 

The bathroom was clean. The toilet was wet when I walked in and when it automatically flushed I quickly saw why...the water was like a tidal wave. I was actually laughing out loud as I was watching it. 

This was opening day so hopefully they'll work out some of the kinks that made it a less than perfect experience but I would return and I would recommend people giving it a try. It was nicely decorated inside and it was spacious. There is a bar. They offer outside seating. There is street parking available and when in doubt, park on Front street and walk a bit!
Fast, cheap, and yummy. We grabbed extra buns for lunch on our way to the convention center.

I went here today for my first ever Brazilian. The place was very clean with a spa like feel and the staff was very friendly. The wax definitely hurt, not gonna lie. But I'm happy I got it done, and that everything is nice and smooth now. I hope it's true that my next wax will hurt less, we'll see in a month!
Good Poke' I enjoyed it.  They had a decent selection of toppings, and service was good.  The restaurant was clean.  I would dine here again.

I gave blood for the first time on Monday.  I wasn't sure what to expect, and I can't really compare this blood place with other blood places, but I really enjoyed the experience.  I went at 5 o'clock, expecting a long line or a lot of paperwork, but there wasn't really anybody in front of me and I only had to answer 30 questions (if you ever had sex for drugs, don't waste your time trying to donate).  I was a half-hour early for my appointment and I was processed quickly.  The vampires were really friendly when it came to donatin' time and was pleasantly surprised to see a little boomboxy-kinda radio playing old school R&B and  hip-hop including Digital Underground.  After you give blood, you go to the canteen where you can enjoy some juice, chips ahoy, pretzels, and water.  I got a sticker for my trouble, didn't faint and was sent on my way.  This blood center is right next to the Electric Factory, so if you ever feel the urge to get in a mosh pit after giving blood, you can...although I think that is highly highly unrecommended.

I fell in love with this studio the first time I went this past summer and haven't stopped going since! All of the instructors are wonderful and incorporate their unique styles into each flow. Juli, the owner, is super friendly, remembers the names of all of her students, and has the best yoga voice in Philadelphia! I would recommend to anyone and at any level in their practice.

My husband and I decided to give iPho another go because we had a less-than-stellar experience during their grand opening time. Definitely has made a 180 change from before. Normal to no-wait times, courteous staff. The food is as good as it always was :) We are very happy we tried it again. Looking forward to going back!

I picked up a couple of sandwiches here a week ago. We had the Mayfair and the Fox Chase. Delicious bursting with meat and flavor and huge!! And very reasonably priced also. Our only issue was that even though I had called the order in and it was ready and waiting in a bag, I had to wait behind 2 students who were just getting pizza slices and this kinda took forever until a guy came out from the back and took care of us. But over all really good!

This place has a cool set-up, and upon my husband's suggestion of going there I was happy to try it out. I ordered what I typically order from any coffee place, an iced chai latte, fully expecting it to be fresh brewed and awesome. Unfortunately it was literally the refrigerated boxed chai mix and whole milk, with way too much whole milk. I was actually quite surprised at their lack of detail to this normally delicious drink; I definitely expected more considering it took about 7 minutes for the barista to painstakingly pour small drips of water through the paper filter for my husband's Indonesia blend coffee (which he enjoyed, and that is the only reason for the two stars). I may go back to try an actual coffee drink but if chai is your thing you're better off going elsewhere.

Best nail salon in the neighborhood.  Consistently top notch work.  Because of this,  it's difficult to get an appointment.  Forget same day,  or even two days in advance . .. you have to plan ahead and make an appointment.  It's worth it though.. their dip powder never chips off. It's always perfectly done.  Debbie is a great tech there

I've never posted on yelp before, but I've had such a horrible experience with City Fitness that I had to post.

I signed up for City Fitness back in the summer along with everyone else and committed to a year long membership.  At the time it made sense because I am only going to be around Philly until this coming August, so a year was going to work perfectly.  But then with the delays into September, October, November it became less and less reasonable for me to commit for a full year.  

Anyway, I emailed the guy who was periodically sending out updates on the gym, jquinn@cityfitnessphilly.com, to tell him my situation and try to work out a half year membership or something in late November when it was clear that I wouldn't be able to sign up for a full year membership.  I waited for a month after sending the first email but didn't hear anything back.  Then, in late December, we received an email saying that the gym would finally be opening and they even announced a start date.  So, I sent another email and asked him to confirm that he would not begin charging me for a membership, to which I received the eloquent response of "confirmed"...

What happened literally a week after I received that email??  They went ahead and charged me anyway.  And then again the following month (I'm bad at checking my bank account activity apparently).  When I realized that they've been charging me, I sent now my third email asking them to stop charging me and return the money that they "confirmed" they would not charge me in the first place, but yet again no one is responding.  I've had to change my bank account information to make sure they stop.

At the end of the day, no big deal, I lost a bit of money and had to deal with this headache for a few months.  What concerns me more, and why I decided to post my little story here, is that either these people are so poorly organized that they didn't realize they were charging my account (not a good sign for the company going forward) or someone actively decided to charge my account anyway in spite of "confirming" my cancellation (a very bad sign).  

I guess this is fairly self evident, but I do feel like I should say that this is just my one experience with this company and maybe other people have had positive ones.  Also, I haven't even been inside the gym (I'm on an away rotation, yet another reason why this has been a pain) and maybe it's great, I don't know.  However, just consider that there are tons of other gyms in our neighborhood all within walking distance that hopefully are more reliable and trustworthy.
Fav Italian spot in the city! Must try, great vibes! If you are in south Philly it's the perfect date spot

Dined here this weekend to celebrate my best friends mother's 60th birthday. First let me just say I was really impressed by the restaurants following covid guidelines with only 25% capacity and well spaced seating. The servers Also wore both face masks and face shields. Service was great. We had a server in training but he did really well and we kept encouraging him throughout the night. Now onto the food. We shared a seafood tower which included cocktail shrimp, crab legs, crab claws, and lots of butter/cocktail sauce, and some type of hollendaise. Between 4 ppl it was barely enough to go around but I've dined here before and knew the portions for this would be slight. The seafood itself was super fresh and tasty. For my cocktail I ordered the berries and bubbly which is a cute little drink with dry ice smoke. It was also pretty strong so that was a plus. For my entree I got the lamb chops and the table shared lobster Mac and cheese, garlic mashed potatoes, and roasted Brussels. The sides are generously portioned for sharing (2-3 ppl can share one). We skipped the dessert but I did see a neighboring table with the warm butter cake and I will most definitely be back for that!

I would love to give this place 5 stars.  The food is great and the gelato is amazing!  While the portion sizes are small compared to "normal" american size entrees, I actually find the size perfect.  The only complaint I have it the service is at times a little slow.  If you have no place to be, then it's not a problem.  So, just plan accordingly!  You won't regret it.
I love this place. Cute pups, shady spots, and tons of space to spread out.
Was there for the first time. Love the eco friendly interior. Everything is neat.  Very nice people!!!! Going back again. Looking forward trying Hatha Yoga
Pretty standard American fare. The stand outs were the big draft selection & the OUTSTANDING veggie burger. It felt like biting into a garden of deliciousness!!
Hands down BEST BRUNCH IN PHILLY! All you can drink mimosas are served fast an very tasty with many types of juice! We love coming here!

UGH!  I nearly forgot to write my review of this place.  All computer systems mishaps aside ( it happens, and it happened the night we were here... can i close my tab PLEASE???)  This place just kinda sucked.  We came to see the boxing match/ UFC fight.  Cover charges sort of piss me off, and this one was ten bucks! I would rather spend the money on beer. I was really peeved by The Field House because of the fact that the layout and the staff made for a really clunky, awkward, not that fun experince.  Why not serve food upstairs?  It got WAY packed downstairs.. and I could not believe how loud... yikes- my ears actually hurt the next morning.  I guess it's sorta hard to pinpoint what went wrong here, but it just seemed like the place was poorly run. I guess I'll chalk my one star up to "bad vibes".
Neighborhood staple. Always right on time with pickup and delivery. Super consistent quality.

Absolutely massive sandwiches from this Philly staple. I was fairly certain our takeout bag for 2 weighed a good five pounds. Eating such a sandwich requires some imagination, a few engineering skills with a fork and knife, and a large plate. Corned Beef w/ Turkey, Russian Dressing, and Coleslaw on Rye was delicious. I would like a bit more of the dressing, so I'll get it on the side next time. You will pay dearly for the size though - we're talkin' $20 for a sandwich & side. However, I got three meals out of my order bear in mind. 

Props to the potato salad - not to mayo laden & nice tang of vinegar. I'll be back to try some of their pastries!
Great food, good beer, and a guaranteed joyous time. The crab cake sandwich possibly is the best in the city.

With a name like "Drinker's" I was a little skeptical about the food that we ordered to complement the (pitchers of) beer we were enjoying around happy hour. However, I was pleasantly surprised: the Nachos were light and crispy and generous smothered cheese and other toppings.  The Buffalo Fries were also smothered in cheesey goodness with a little spicy sauce to cut through it all.  Good bar food and great drink prices!

We went to Charlie was a Sinner for happy hours. The drinks and food were delicious, and the service was friendly. 

There is no parking available.

I am in Philadelphia for a girls' weekend and we went to Reading Terminal Market for breakfast today. The market is incredible! Literally every type of cuisine is present and it is all being made there, so fresh. We decided to each try a donut from Beiler's. We collectively tried the blueberry fritter (delicious), the vanilla frosted (sweet and moist), banana cream (amazing), and a maple nut (sweet and creamy. Not a one of the donuts we tried was less than fabulous. You can watch them make, fry and fill the donuts while you wait! This part of the market is not open on Sundays so keep that in mind if you are heading there for a donut fix! Definitely worth the calories!

Yass I think I found my new favorite neighborhood joint!

Cry Baby Pasta is simple, straightforward Italian food, refined in taste and presentation.  Menu is just right - not too much and not too skimpy.  Don't come here with expectations of massive South Philly slop portions.  It's a PROPER serving of pasta.  Trust me, I like a big ol' bowl of carbload like the next person, but sometimes it's nice to not feel pressured to finish or have to take a doggy bag home (especially when it's a late dinner and you're going out somewhere immediately after).

With that said, next time I come, I'm ordering a ton of apps and wine for the table and going that route.  We started with the grilled artichoke, smoked ricotta/mushroom bruschetta and the arugula salad, and then each got the tagliatelle bolognese for entrees. Everything was absolutely delicious (although I will say the very tasty bite of artichoke was pretty small to be considered a shareable appetizer, so it left me wanting more). The arugula salad was perfect.  Our tagliatelles were dope and perfectly portioned.  I've never seen or tasted a bolognese of that variety, almost like a blush/cream vodka sauce. I said to my BF: "I mean this is the nicest way possible...this reminds me of Hamburger Helper."  But like ELEVATED.  If HH went to Cambridge and got all bougie.  Effin' delicious.

But we both agreed that the best part was the bruschetta...it was FANTASTIC.  Plus, they have a few different varieties to choose from and I want to go back to try them all.  My mouth was watering for it the next day, and still is.  In fact, it was so good, instead of dessert we ordered the buffalo mozzarella bruschetta AFTER our entrees cause we needed another taste. The mushroom one was oh so salty and smoky and the honey drizzle on mozzarella one was a delight.  Both  were creamy and crunchy - truly the perfect bite.  Would I classify it as "bruschetta"?  Probably not.  But a damn good appetizer nonetheless.

We paired our meals with really good red and white wine (the menu is expansive in this area) and plus, our server was on point even for the super busy Saturday night crowd.  Prices were reasonable for the quality of what you're getting.  Will definitely return!
The roast beef sandwiches are beyond compare, and the only valid basis upon which to judge the mightiness of Nick's.  If you haven't gone yet, what are you waiting for?

After seeIng the post from a girl who wrote into Action News about her treatment I had to write this review as I was treated the same way. The people who own this store, and the girls running this store were rude and nasty. They were laughing at girls trying on dresses. These girls were literally body shaming a Customer. I will never shop at this store and would hope anyone that does not tolerate bullying or body shaming shops here either.

Spending time at any airport waiting for a flight is not usually in my top 10 must do activities but this particular Friday night in February was quite pleasant. I stopped at Independent Prime in Terminal B and had a cheesesteak (it is Philly after all) and a glass of wine. All ordering is done with tablets on tables or the bar. Easy enough, scroll through and order, me ext thing you know your wine appears before you! Cheesesteak was quite good, better than going to the food court. A bit pricey, $35 for food and drink for one but it helped kill an hour and the atmosphere made me forget I was waiting for a plane.

Just had their dim sum this weekend.  There are alot of components which makes this place the best in Philly for Dim Sum.   Again, let's do the good and the ek.. I've had better. 
Chicken Feet:  Very nice flavor.  Deep Fried just enough to make it the perfect texture.  Just enough Hoisin to make a very good sauce.  Wish it had less red coloring.  You know you put too much coloring when the chicken bone is red too...) 
Chiu Chow Fun Gor:  Very nice mix of pork, water chestnut, dried shrimp in a nicely wrapped rice paper.  Great flavor. 
Steam Chicken and Cha Siu Buns:  Perfectly steamed and very soft. Bun had just enough sweetness to compliment the chicken and BBQ pork flavor!  
Ha Gow (Steamed Shrimp Dumpling): Wrapped very nice and I tasted hints of bamboo shoot and water chestnuts.  Perfect filling along with Shrimp.
Siu Mai:  Put too much dried mushrooms.  As a matter of fact, why so traditional?  Just pork, pork fat, shrimp and binding agent like egg white and cornstarch.  That's all someone needs.  
They have other items that we see in NY's chinatown and Hong Kong.  Overrall, a nice gem off of Race Street!

My first experience with the Better Box was at their Food Truck in Olney when the menu was much more limited. I was able to enjoy their unique eggrolls from the truck, which were absolutely delicious. Since then, they have expanded to two brick and mortar locations and another location inside a Shoprite. 

Recently, I had the opportunity to stop by their Fairmount location and tried the General Tso's Chicken Cheesesteak and Crab Curly Fries. Due to COVID restrictions, I got my food to go and enjoyed it at home. The cheesesteak was interesting- consisting of shredded chicken breast, General Tso's sauce, broccoli, and cheese on a long roll. Personally, I found the sauce to be far too sweet, but the cheesesteak was robust and could easily serve two. It's a great option for those who enjoy a sweeter sauce. The curly crab fries were DELICIOUS. The crab meat was jumbo lump, seasoned well, and the fries were fried nicely. I just wish they didn't get so soggy in the styrofoam container on the way home. 

I absolutely recommend the eggrolls (all of them) and the crab fries. The cheesesteak just wasn't for me.

I'm really surprised at the low reviews for this place.  In my opinion, it offers the best valued Ethiopian food in the Philly area, a place where competition is steep.  The food here is a good $2 or so per item cheaper than in most restaurants, and the service I have found is consistently better than average.  The quality of the food does not at all suffer from the lower price--instead, you just give up the fancy atmosphere, and that's cool with me.

This place is a little divey, in a 70's cafeteria sort of way.  There's an eating area on one side, seat yourself, and a bar on the other.  The bar is dark and the seating area is brighter, but not exactly the most brightly lit place.

But I like it.  There's a positive vibe in here.  They're often playing energetic hip-hop music.  It's the sort of place that I feel very comfortable, and I'm not quite sure why.

But I always like the food, especially the vegetarian food.  I especially like the Foul and Vegetarian platter here.  Food is cheap, portions are good, quality is consistently good, service is consistently good.

Seriously, what is there to not like about this place?  The only thing I can imagine is if you're looking for something fancy...which I almost never am.

Epic win!

I stopped in Suplex when I was visiting Philly with my wife. I gotta say I was really impressed with the store. It was a laid back relaxed atmosphere and just the kind of place you want to hang out and talk sneakers. I can't say enough about the owner Chris. I wanted to buy a Duplex shirt to support the store but, they were out of them at the time. He actually took the time to get my address and send me a shirt when they got them in. I mean it's a class act of a store and the place to go if your a sneakerhead.

Went here for a birthday celebration with some friends. I thought that the food was very reasonably priced, the service was good, and it was definitely the hot spot on a Saturday night. Ordered the duck kabobs which were good, but not amazing. I guess I imagined kabobs to be on a stick, but they come as 2 small patties on a plate. The hanger steak was moist and delicious. The hummus and bread that came with it was also fantastic. The waiter did not hesitate to bring out separate checks for everyone at the table.

YOU MUST COME HERE! I LOVE IT. Okay, maybe I'm over excited, but the food is really great. I love everything on the menu. Its a great place for a quick bite to eat, great location, and great customer service. They have drink and food specials all the time. They have fish of the day tacos.The Shrimp glazed tacos are amazing. I love all the side items. I mean its doesn't matter what you get you'll love it.

negative 5,000 stars! i can't believe how horrible these people are. i better stop ordering things online, while i live in philadelphia. once again, these assholes didn't deliver my package and at 8:39pm claims that the "receiving business was closed." um...this is a residence. i double checked that my order didn't have the wrong address...nope, it's my home address. that's strange because there was another ups package sitting in the lounge of my apt building...i just can't with fucking incompetent assholes anymore.
Comfortable atmosphere, great prices, friendly service, decent food... my only complaint about this place is that you will definitely have to WAIT for a table if you go on the weekend...
Hands down one of the best burgers i have ever had in my life. The turf burger is no joke. Burger tuesdays with beer specials.. cant beat that

Took some colleagues to dinner at Kanella South last night. It is my fifth visit here. There is something about this place that just elicits a good time. Keep trying to figure out its secret sauce. Is it be the well-trained, yet very hip staff? Could it be Konstantino's dedication to his Greek Cypriot roots? How about the fact that he is a Greek God in the kitchen? Could it be the fire-grilled sardines? The grilled octopus? The lamb ravioli? I could go on and on. I guess I will have to go back and really narrow things down don't you think?

Jerry's Bar has been in everything I've read lately so naturally when a friend and I were thinking of where we should try, this was first on the list.  We showed up around 8 on a Friday night and were seating immediately upstairs.  The ambiance is great- more of a restaurant than a bar.  Very cozy and was a nice escape from the freezing cold.

Our waitress was great.  She was quick to offer suggestions and detailed descriptions about what Jerry's did an excellent job of.  They hand make all pierogies, gnocchi, etc.  We started off with cocktails - I had the hemingway which was amazing, and my friend had the gin one.  Both delicious although fair warning that they were pretty potent.  We ordered the pierogies which were fantastic.  Probably some of the best I've had, even topping ones from polish hill in Pittsburgh.  Yep, that real.  I got the Jerry's burger and it was legit the best burger I've ever eaten in my entire life.  Bold but true.  I don't usually indulge myself in things like that but I'm going to recommend that everyone gives it a shot.  My friend got the hanger steak which was also amazing.

All in all, A+++ experience at Jerry's.  I can't wait to go back and try out their much raved about brunch.

A friend and I have been dying to try Vedge together after all the rave (and difficulty getting a reservation!). We made towards the end of happy hour, so we indulged in a drink and app at the beautiful bar. For a high-end restaurant, the deal is pretty good, though still pricey for a frequent stop. We shared crispy potatoes accompanied with a nice herb sauce. 

Shortly after, we moved to a table for dinner. The spacious, modern dining room is fairly quiet, even at peak hours. Once seated, we ordered cocktails. I got the Black Hole Sun with scotch, burnt miso, and activated charcoal, which was smooth and smoky. My friend got the Mirepoix cocktail with vodka and a celery flavor, which was refreshing. We were also welcomed with an amuse bouche butternut squash soup that was  delicious. 

We decided to share everything starting with the rutabaga fondue. It blew us away with its savoriness and cheesy texture. Plus, the fresh baked soft pretzel was delicious. For a bit of green, we had the pea leaves which had a great umami flavor. Then, we ordered the wood roasted carrot. This was probably the most impressive dish of the night. A large roasted carrot was accompanied by carrot mustard and a carrot kraut. Each made the root vegetable taste completely different yet equally tasty. 

At the end of the meal, we were surprised by how full we were. Though, in the spirit of catching up, we decided to prolong our dinner and get dessert as well. Usually I don't opt for panna cotta, but my friend was convincing. I enjoyed the white chocolate flavor more than fruity flavors that I've tried. Paired with the fresh ginger ice cream and crunchy bits of snickerdoodle cookies, I was a big fan.

Miga is a nice date place. Relaxing atmosphere, cute deco, friendly staffs. Food however is not challenging. The little dishes are interesting to look at but not that good. The bimbim bob has lots of good fresh vegie, but the chicken is bland and tasteless. The soft tofu stew is disappointing, nothing is wrong with it, but it just does not do it for me. 

Now I can say been there done that and move on.

It was around 9pm and we had been walking around Philadelphia looking for a place to eat.  Nothing was hitting us and every time we got excited about a place, we discovered they were closed.  We gave up and went to pick up our car from Patriot Parking on Chancellor street.  The person who I was with said "Let's just grab a slice of pizza over there."  Pointing to Pizzeria Vetri.  Neither of us really wanted pizza but we both half-heartedly agreed and walked across the street.

A.  It was one of the few places that we went to over our 3 day stay that had air conditioning.  So, it was pretty much the only time we were comfortable during our stay (even the hotel had awful air conditioning!).

B.  The place was clean and cute.

C.  The service was excellent and REALLY nice.

D.  The pizza was out of this world.  It was blackened on the bottom just as it should be.  DELICIOUS!!  We ordered 3 different pies and there was NOTHING left when we were done.

E.  We wanted to go back the next night to try the Nutella pizza but we weren't able to get back there.

I was really impressed and wish I could find a pizza place this good on Long Island.

Over salted everything! Slow service,
And which is a sad, because the Devore was fabulous:( , looks can be deceiving !

I visited the cozy restaurant with a few friends and had a great experience. We started with a few of the Crispy Spring Rolls and Fresh Summer Rolls. The rolls tasted very fresh. I really enjoy the peanut sauce  with my soft rolls. We ordered a few items off the menu and shared them. They were all great. We had some Shrimp fried rice and it was hot and fresh with just the right amount of seasoning. We also had 2 of the platters with white rice. We all enjoyed the Grilled Pork platter we added a Crispy Spring roll for a little extra, and The combo platter, which was nice to get a variety. Again we had the peanut sauce and some of the fish sauce to go with it. As we were paying we were advised they just started selling Banh mi, and I'm a sucker for these sandwiches so I took a few for the road. I'll go back next time I'm in town for sure!!! If anything I wish I had a little more ice in my drink.

Came to Seorabol after work one day with a couple of friends for their new happy hour. We were seated pretty quickly and ordered all the food on the happy hour menu, which included the kimchi fried rice, mandoos, general cho chicken wings, fried rice cakes, jjajang myun (black bean noodles), and buldak (spicky chicken), as well as a bottle of soju to share (they have fruit flavored soju!!) All the food was really good, but I particularly enjoyed the jjajang myun, and the general cho chicken wings. The waiters were attentive and constantly filling up our water and tea. All in all, a nice addition to the happy hour scene, great food and great prices!

Best coffee shop in Philly, hands down. Inventive lattes (cardamom rose!) to activated charcoal bagels, it's a really unique place. Great customer service. I would recommend the vegan lox with cream cheese on a black sesame bagel. AMAZING.

This place has become my go-to for Chinese delivery.  They're fast and dependable for American-style Chinese food.  I usually go for a won ton soup and General Tso's combo platter.  Always consistent and decent prices.

Have not received a fair interview from this establishment after getting called back and scheduled for an interview TWICE. The first interview I had there was with a fast-talking man (who couldn't find the hiring manager) in the middle of the restaurant on an extremely busy day. My interview lasted exactly 4 minutes . No call back & when I did, they had no knowledge of me or the man I was interviewed by.

The second interview I had , went almost the same way, except this time, I was compensated with a drink at the bar because again, I was seated and asked to wait for the hiring manager, & after about 15 minutes of waiting, the manager never showed up. I saw NO people of color at this "Mexican-themed" establishment , or working the position I was applying for. Which was off putting.

After being rushed out of the restaurant the first time, I thought for sure my interview would go differently the second time around. Although the employees (hostess & bartender) were respectful, it was not their job to accommodate me instead of the hiring manager. Nobody ever called me back to even reschedule for a phone interview at least. Extremely disappointing from a place I looked forward to working .

This food court is a really nice addition to Chinatown. There's a variety of food, plenty of seating, and they serve alcohol. It's a convenient place to grab a bite to eat before or after a show at one of the several venues nearby. I recommend trying the Kurry Korner stand.

Here's the story: I needed a bikini wax, received the flyer for a free first visit and booked the appointment. Naturally, I checked Yelp in advance. Was I leery after the reviews? Yep. But it was free so I went for it. Here's my feedback:
- Very friendly staff, both reception and the aesthetician. 
- A little odd the aesthetician didn't leave the room while I changed.
- A bit annoying that all she talked about were the products & why I should buy.
- Totally pain free. Bonus!
- Very clean facility.
The worst thing I can say is getting a wax of your "bikini line" is an absolute joke. If you're like me and expect more hair removal then I strongly suggest booking the full bikini. She removed a little bit on each side and that was the end of it. Lastly, if you prefer the blue wax versus strips, definitely check out Body Restoration on Walnut St. One of my best experiences yet.

Ok. Sandwich? Good. 

Customer Service? Terrible. I politely asked for a cup of water with my fries at the "Fries, etc" window, and the guy said "No."
He was so terse/blunt that I thought he was kidding and laughed. Then I looked at his face, and realized he was completely serious. He followed that with, "Sorry, no can do, babe." (Babe?! Really?).

I'm sorry, but if you can't take 5 seconds to give me a paper cup of water after I just paid $13.50 for a sandwich and fries (overpriced if you ask me) with unheated outdoor seating in November, I won't be returning. But maybe I'm just missing the point of the experience?

I loved the wild Wild West bison burger! Better than I expected. The fries were good too. It was louder than I wanted for conversation. The music randomly got louder at times. Our server was nice. I will be back!

Good place, love the exhibits and friendly staff.  I do agree the presentation is heavily skewed to the left leaning progressive view but overall it's a good museum.  If you want a true study of the American Revolution go to Yorktown VA American Revolution Museum.  The Yorktown museum is a must see for anyone who loves 18th century history.  As for the Philadelphia Museum if you can filter out the left leaning agenda you will enjoy your visit.
I've been coming here for a few years now and I'm never disappointed. All of the technicians there are amazing but Andy is my favorite.

So I asked the guy at the register to give me the most popular Philly Cheesesteak. He preceding to have me pay a little under $14 for the block. The bell peppers overtook the steak. I went to ask him why he told me that if everyone near me had ordered the regular Philly cheese steak sandwich which only cost like $6. He said that the 10 people I asked were just locals and that they do not order the most popular. Hmmm ...sketch. Will not be returning. Wish I could have given them zero stars after a lady who overheard me suggested he como me a regular on, but her request fell on deaf ears.

Great place to sell your stuff. The owners are easygoing, and most important fair.

Not a pawn shop, they just buy stuff. And by stuff I mean everything...I've brought everything I have there. Star Wars toys from when I was a kid, jewelry, my moms collectible figurines ..I think they were called lladro, watches, my coin collection, model trains and I got paid for all of it.
