More than I thought it was worth too which was a pleasant surprise.

We had the tasting menu here for New Year's Eve and the food was quite heavy.  

- My fave was the Rissois de cogumelos, a pickled mushroom salad and creamy fried mushroom turnover.  The flavours in the salad were refreshing and tasty.
- Smoked pork consomme was fine, but unexciting.
- Raw salt cod was all right.
- Tuna salad was so-so; the tuna seemed overdone & I'd avoid this in future.
- Vanilla poached lobster was fine.
- Fried rice duck drummettes were very heavy & duck was overcooked -- I expect duck to be succulent.  This was not very appetizing.
- Octopus gravy was tasty.
- Quail stuffed with sausage was perfectly cooked, succulent and tasty with pickled grapes.

In general, I was disappointed after all the great things I'd heard about Koo Zee Doo -- the food was fine but I expected it to be better & more enjoyable.

Dedicating these five stars to Elisa.  She donated a hair cut to a breast cancer fund raiser which I bid on and won.  While I was there I got a whole head of highlights for 150 bucks.  She explained everything that she was doing to me and the nature of hair color...she was very professional and informative.  She was friendly but didn't talk the whole time, so I got to read a couple magazines from their wide selection.  I loved the cut...totally fixed me up from a dull and boring cut I got from Sevan salon in Conshohocken.  I will definitely go back to her and confidently recommend her to someone looking for a new stylist.

Whitesnake playing on the jukebox. 
PBR...in a bottle. 
Dogs. 
A clientele which seemed to run the spectrum of social characterizations. 

I found it eclectic on such a variety levels, it induced a mild level of confusion. I must confess, this place has the squid stumped.

It was cozy & friendly enough. I would come again.

[Edit 9/29/11: I forgot to mention the decks of Trivial Pursuit cards on the bar. It's been ages since I played TP, it was a cool touch.]
My favorite ice cream in Philadelphia. One must try - and probably can climb - FF's Mount Vesuvius. Also, hands-down best cotton candy ice cream the world has seen.

I'm still loving Tinto! This time I stopped in last minute and snagged a table at the bar for dinner. We opted for the chef's tasting menu, but requested the duck confit to be included because its to die for! 

Our tasting consisted of:

Chorizo and egg salad - deli delight!
Cheese assortment - deliciously paired with fruit, honey and jam
Green beans - vinegary spin on green beans alomandine 
Moules and frites - meaty chili-like taste accompanied by crispy duck fat fries 
Crispy potatoes
Duck confit - melt in your mouth duck perfectly paired with a cherry
Rabbit and Shrimp paella - creamy rice with hearty rabbit and a big juicy shrimp (head and all)
Chocolate fudge with sherry ice cream - nufsaid

I can't complain about anything. I love Jose Garces and want to have his babies.

Went here for sips. Their specialty cocktail was a cranberry pineapple vodka concoction.  I know it's sips, but I expected a little better.  It was premixed already and bartender served in a plastic cup filled above the rim with ice.  That's a lot of ice!  So if you exclude the ice, the drink so realistically a shot. And I mean a shot of juice - I couldn't taste any alcohol in it.

The outdoor patio is very cool, and the area for sips is great- outdoors, live DJ, good open space for the crowd. I wish I got to try the food, but there was nowhere open to sit.  It was that crowded.  

I'd come back to give the food a shot, but given the drink prices, I imagine the food could be pricey too. ($8/draft IPA)

Lets be real for a moment. The class is comprised of 10 or so students. The instructor yells out a bunch of exercises and shows you a bunch of exercises that they want you to do quickly. If your familiar with the exercises great if not then your lost trying to figure out how to do each exercise without hurting yourself. The instructors rarely check on you to make sure that your doing the exercise properly, instead they continue exercising I guess as an example too show you to what your supposed to be doing. You don't really get the full workout from the class and your left feeling like you missed a part of the exercises because you couldn't move as fast as the rest of the class or that you didn't get a full work out because you couldn't get the exercises right. Pretty big disappointment, since you don't get the personal attention to be able to exercise properly.

Yo...

This place is the truth. Since Megabus offers insanely cheap fares from DC to Philly, I'm always there visiting my line sister!! She lives directly across the street from "Saigon House" as I have dubbed this place. I've eaten here three times and have gotten the shrimp friend rice each time. Wait, my first visit I ordered the shrimp spring rolls too! Anyways...


My food is always piping hot and the portions are always big! I always have left overs and eat them later. You could honestly enjoy the rice with no sauce but the sriracha and the sticky brown sauce set it off! I always ask the cashier for extra! It's fresh, delicious, and CHEAP!! Paid $8.00 for the rice. $2.50 for the Spring rolls if I recall correctly.  BIG OH!! The service is super speedy! 

It's located in a trendy little neighborhood in West Philly, lots of hipsters and young adults. I gave this place a shot because I'm a sucker for hole in the wall joints!

I hope to try the bubble tea next time, but I gotta get over my phobia of the texture  of tapioca balls in my mouth. Creeps me out a little.  I would explore the rest of the menu but the rice is so damn good!!


I would've given it a 5, but the hours of operation are inconsistent making it difficult to plan my day (my days are planned around my meals!!).

I have to say some of these reviews about Lush don't do the place justice and frankly, make me wonder if the problem isn't something more personal (in some of these cases.)  I think very highly of the products and the staff is helpful and friendly.

From the sound of these other reviews, you'd think the employees were outright forcing you to buy products you couldn't afford.  Let me just ask: would it be fair to prejudge you on the amount of money it "looks" like you can spend?  To me, that would be incredibly insulting - far more so than showing you something you may or may not have looked at otherwise, or considered purchasing.  Besides, no one is forcing you to buy anything.  In addition, I don't think it's difficult to let someone know you'd like to shop peacefully without assistance, all you have to do is say so. You can't expect people to read your mind.

Shopping at Lush is a unique experience and gathering from other reviews, not too many people are used to receiving help from employees when they shop.  In Europe this kind of treatment is common.  In Philadelphia, it is not.  Perhaps it's this shock that is making people feel uneasy.  Either way, I'm used to this and find it refreshing to see in the states.  In fact, it's one of the reasons I'm drawn to the store.

I never have any problems, with the staff or otherwise.  In addition, I do believe the surfactants used are plant derivatives in most cases.  At the very least, I think you can safely say that the products at Lush are as all-natural as you can possibly get and have them maintain their appeal - which is to say that they are quite natural (some of the products have expiration dates because of the ingredient freshness.)

The bottom line: excellent soap, enthusiastic and helpful staff, and a well designed store.  I genuinely enjoy the products and can safely say I don't think I will be buying soap anywhere else.

It's right next to the Piazza, so it doesn't have to try hard. 

Best advice- head there for the happy hour. Cheap and strong margaritas.

Cozy is the best! They all take their time and do a great job. The massages are also wonderful and the equipment is really clean. Plus the staff is friendly. On weekends it's better to make an appointment. They have to turn away a lot of walk-ins.

Have not been here in a while but have fond memories of this Mexican restaurant. Ok food, great drinks. A bit loud and boy did it get crowded! I did not remember a day where it wasn't crowded. Great prices and service was quick when I use to visit!

eh, I've had them in the past, and it was good, re-ordered for the first time in about a year, and it wasn't good.  Not sure if they were just having a bad night? whatever it is, it enough to keep me away, and my stomach! Ordered a cheesesteak, tasted like it had no cheese on it, the roll was burnt! so burnt it was hard to bite into! the fries were so soggy they were stuck in a ball in the bag, my husbands Stromboli was pouring grease from it.

Hey Yellowtail,
Why does your menu "hander-outer" drop menus to areas you don't deliver in?? I've had your menu in my kitchen for months and was finally putting an order in and you don't deliver to my area? OK!

Just adding another 5 star review to the mix! 

But in case this is still news to anyone... Dizengoff offers a delicious shakshuka on Sundays only!! It comes with a foccacia type bread instead of the regular pita, and the tomato sauce has a slightly spicy kick. Perfect brunch dish. 

The menu items change all the time, but the radish and peanut hummus is one of my favorites! 

Also, you'll need the extra pita if you don't want your pita to hummus ratio to be way off... :)

I love this place. This was only my second visit, but it's a go to dinner spot when I get together with my Philly & NJ girlfriends. They have fabulous burgers & frittes with bourbon dipping sauce. Also an amazing beer list-Tap & numerous interesting bottle & van selections. Plus we can always get a table without a reservation. Service is fast & friendly. Parking can be tough because you have to search for street parking. They do have outdoor seating in the summer. The decor inside is artsy. Fun place to take out of town guests.

Far exceeded my expectations with inventive, tasty fare at reasonable prices.  Took my date here Valentine's day. We had delicious farro risotto with mushrooms and some delectable crispy artichokes as appetizers.  My main course was a tasty eggplant dish with pomegranite and kale served with some fragrant basmati rice.  My date had a homemade parpadelle dish- also good.  Nice selection of beers on tap, friendly with good service.  Highly recommended.

Love this place and come routinely for drinks with friends.  The old movies, the marvelous drinks and the pimento grilled cheese (only available at happy hour) are marvelous. I highly recommend a seat at the bar to watch Lou work his magic.

Delicious! Im glad Yelp led us here as we were in the mood for some authentic xian eats. This was comparable to our limited but previous really good experience with the cuisine in NYC. The portions weren't as big as we expected coming in cold and hungry but it was a good meal.

The cumin lamb skewers were well cooked but just felt light portion-wise for the cost.

The piping fresh spicy biang biang noodles (with garlic scallion) were great. I didn't do the best job mixing up the dish as it comes out pre-constructed with individual spices in different parts of the bowl but it worked out well as we were feeling a little spiced out by the end of the meal and appreciating the blander pieces of noodles :D

I liked the hamburger a lot but again wish the portion of meat was a little bit more.

Efficient service with food coming out fast. Definitely a nice new experience in china town.
Best Chinese/Japanese food in Mayfair and quite possibly anywhere in the city except for China Town. Sushi is always fresh and the food is always ready quick and delicious. Never a bad experience

Delicatessen has easily the best pastrami I've had in Philadelphia since the Koch Brothers died, and maybe the best ever.  But I made a mistake, and ordered a Reuben.  This pastrami is too good for a Reuben.  The other Reuben ingredients detract from the pastrami.

I also got noodle kugel, which was fine.  And they serve La Colombe coffee, so what's not to like?  Well, I did not care for their pickles.  I thought they lacked flavor.  But I will return for the pastrami.

2011-09-20: I returned and got a plain pastrami sandwich with mustard.  It was excellent.

Nice quaint spot, very convenient and accessible.​ If you'd like a tasty, healthy, reasonably priced breakfast, this is your place. I've eaten at more expensive locations whose food lacked much compared to this eatery. I would definitely make this my breakfast and brunch location for my visits to Philly.

Nice place but I got the impression they were seating us based on our race to the back of the restaurant. We objected to where they we seating us in that location and were given a better seat. Once seated we had a very pleasant waitress and the food was very good. I had appetizer of chicken wings which were unseasoned  but cooked nicely with great side sauces,i also had a delicious salad with Chicken which was cooked perfectly I had a very nice portion. I am rating this with 3 stars because the older lady hostess should have offered us an option in an half filled place where we wanted to be seated.

Great place to grab a beer and snack.  I had the eggplant parm sandwich.  Man that was good.  Wine selection wasn't so great but one does not come to this place for wine.  Really good beer selection.  I would definitely go back.

I was told by friends that when I visit Philly, I should try the pretzels. After a bit of searching, I found that a lot of people recommended Miller's Twist. I also tried their lemonade and it was very refreshing. Reading Terminal Market was bustling and there were a lot of different shops so I can see why it was so popular. All the different smells and types of food that were being sold really made me want to buy everything. Next time when I go back, I'm going to buy some more pretzels! It was a shame that I didn't have enough stomach room to try more of their pretzels.

I got a state inspection there.  After the inspection, they showed me I needed a new battery (old one was 6 years old) and front brakes.  Explanations were straightforward, and they showed me the parts they were replacing.  The service was timely, and all of the prices seemed appropriate to me.  Verbal quote matched the final price, and they threw in a back windshield wiper for free.  I'll go back next year.

VERY SKETCHY company! They were scheduled for 1:00. They called two hours earlier when I wasn't home and asked if they could come earlier, but I had to say no since I wasn't home. They didn't show up at 1 when they were scheduled, and no call. I waited a while and called at 1:30 because I planned my whole day around this and had other things to do. No answer, no call back. So at 2:00 I called again. Someone answered and when I said the technician was an hour late, he said "what if we gave you a three hour window like other companies?" I said I would have called someone else. He said he'd have the technician call me.  A few minutes later the technician who called to ask if he could come early called and said he didn't know about the job but he would leave from Philly (40 minutes away) to come. I said what the heck are you talking about? I spoke with you and you knew you had a 1:00 appointment. I said forget it, my day is shot. Then someone identifying himself as the owner of the company -- the same guy that answered around 2:00 -- got nasty and rude and claimed I was the one being rude! I'm on my way to rent a carpet cleaner. This company is the worst, not just in service but in the way they treat customers. (Also, I've gotten countless calls from them trying to get me to use their service, and when I finally did they screwed up my day.) Very sketchy company. Wish there was a "0 stars" option.

Really nice brunch spot in Northern Liberties. The staff were able to seat our large party quickly, probably thanks to the morning sleet keeping people home. They were also very attentive, as my group showed up the were promptly asked about drinks, and weren't pushy about waiting for everyone to arrive before ordering.

There are a lot of options on the menu, with both brunch standards and French-inspired specialties. The Green Shakshuka and Gypsy Eggs were highly recommend by my group, but I opted for the Parisian Zá, with ham, goat cheese, and caramelised onions on a croissant, which was fantastic. Most of the savory dishes were well portioned, whereas both the pancakes and french toast came in tall stacks.

I've been coming here for nearly a year and can say (even through a rebrand) that the service is always quick, attentive, and friendly. 

The cocktails are extremely creative and inspire patrons to explore unexpected flavor combinations they may have otherwise not considered. It's a lively, energetic place to grab a drink (or a few.) You can't go wrong sitting at a booth or the bar.

Behind the bar, the staff creates a family feel - as a repeat customer of Encima, this is one thing that keeps me coming back. If you ever have concerns or aren't sure what to order, or you're just looking  for some great Philly recommendations and conversation, these are your go-to people. While I do see a couple less-than-stellar reviews for Encima, I'd say these are not a reflection of the bar overall and I'd give it a try yourself. You absolutely can't beat this Rittenhouse gem, I'd recommend it to anyone looking for a great cocktail in a warm and fun environment.

Stopped in on a Tuesday night. We were really craving a car bomb or a pickle back. Realizing some places don't serve everything, we asked if we could do an Irish car bomb. Bartender called us racist for ordering Irish car bombs. I'm offended.

Came here months ago with my boyfriend and had such a mediocre experience that I still remember it vividly. When we first arrived, we were told to sit wherever we pleased, but the booth we initially chose was awkwardly L shaped (side by side seating). The second table we chose was fine, but the seats were uncomfortably low for the height of the table--I felt like a child with my elbows unable to reach the table. When it came to service, the waitress did not refill my water glass once, despite it being empty for quite some time, and I had to explicitly ask her not once, but twice for more water. Along the same lines, we were unimpressed with the waitress's thoroughness when we asked about how a certain dish was cooked. It was our first time trying traditional Senegalese food, and a little more guidance/information would have been appreciated, but instead I received an eye roll. When the food finally came out, we concluded that it was tasty but a little too greasy and salty for our palettes. Really wanted to love this place but likely won't be back.

Vic's is my favorite sushi place and it just happens to be right around the corner from my apartment. It's a small shop, just a few chairs for dining, and a wide window sill to sit on while you're waiting for your to-go order. The owner and his wife are friendly and they make amazing, inexpensive sushi. 

One night awhile back, I was hanging out with some friends, when we experienced a need for sushi. We spent $75 on take-out sushi. There were five of us, and we were all stuffed by the time we were done eating, and there were still a few pieces leftover (a fact that made my heart sad, but I just couldn't eat anymore). That is an amazingly good price per person for the level of sushi gorging in which we engaged.

The rolls artful and tasty. The pieces are generously sized and flavorful. The service is terrific and the atmosphere is comfortable. They are on Sansom Street, between 20th and 21st, on the north side of the street. Check 'em out.

I was rather disappointed with Hertz. The car itself was fine, clean, all that...but the service was terrible. It was slow and the woman did not explain the car, how it ran, or anything. I had trouble driving it initially, so called the service number on the pamphlet, but had to wait a long time until I just ended up hanging up and figured it out myself.

We came to Amis coz of Marc Vetri and Brad Spence, with high expectations, but left a bit disappointed. The interiors are classy even the noise level is over the top. The house made rhubarb strawberry soda was pretty good. The salami platter was good but it was a lot of sweet accompanying the meats with no piece of crunchy toast (maybe an in house bread). The Cacio e Pepe was, unfortunately, a travesty. The pasta, which was pretty good, was ladled in some cheese sauce which had a very grainy texture and grated Parmesan. 

I'm afraid to say we might not be back soon.
So good.  They have good vegetarian options and some delicious sandwiches.  My sister and I try to visit whenever I am in town.
Unbelievable.  Hostesses, bartenders, and waitstaff were awesome.  They served us free appetizers because we had a 20 min wait and 3 hungry kids.  Grilled Mac and cheese. Yum
Very friendly bar service :-)  Only a few beers on tap, but they had Hell or High Watermelon so I was satisfied.  Yummy fried green tomatoes.  We'll be back for more beer and snacks!

Philly local and I was not impressed with the taste of this cheesesteak. It's underseasoned/bland, the bread is way too chewy.. Not a good chewy but the type you can still be chewing way after the meat is gone. They give you very little meat. Maybe this is a great cheese steak to the hipsters? My sister did enjoy her vegan cheesesteak but I will stick to Carmen's Cosmi's or Jim's... Sidebar:They do have good wiz sauce though.

Found them through Angie's List and got a coupon for $15 off. Very easy to schedule with them. The girls arrived on time and did a good job. There was some mix-up about the amount they were going to charge me but 1 call to Rayleen, the owner, and we got that sorted out. Given this billing mix-up and the fact that I was shorted by 30 minutes of cleaning (15 minutes since two girls came to clean the place), I decided to give 4 stars and not 5. I do appreciate that Rayleen offered to make up those 15 minutes the next time I hire their services.

New to the area and ordered a large plain pie for delivery for my first dinner in the new house. The pie was excellent. Still steaming when delivered, crust had a little crunch to it, cheese portion was on point. My only complaint is that it could have used a touch more sauce but I'm an extra sauce guy anyways. 

The delivery arrived 10 mins quicker than quoted and the delivery guy was pleasant as well. Will deff order from here again.

I arrived VERY late for my check-in this evening, and Darren (hopefully I spelled that right!) was SO kind and accommodating. I travel for work, and am so used to being a pain in the a** when I check-in late at hotels, and this was honestly the first time I recieved energetic, kind service at 2:30AM. From someone who travels for a living, the world needs more happy people like Darren. Thank you!!

I haven't been to Landmark in over 5 years and I remember why. I always feel sticky when I dine in one of the restaurants (they have the Market St location and N 54th St).

The service was fine. Our waitress was nothing special. She did come and ask if we wanted anything else so the three kids at my table ordered more lemonade. 

I ordered the chicken quesadilla with old bay fries and chicken tenders. The quesadilla had more tortilla than chicken and didn't come anything; no peppers, no salsa, no guacamole. Other restaurants I have been to come with the extras so their version of the quesadilla fell flat. To their credit, their menu tells you that it comes with honey mustard, sour cream, and American cheese sauce.

The chicken tenders were basic and the fries were good. Not overly seasoned with old bay like Chickie Pete's so I enjoyed the fries.

I asked for lemon to put in my water but was told they didn't have any but placed an order for fruit so they will have some in the restaurant soon. This just don't make any sense. There's a Restaurant Depot nearby, a supermarket, produce stores, and 9th St Italian Market with a plethora of fruit vendors. Heck drive down to the Wholesale Fruit warehouse on Essington Ave near the AutoMall.

Out of town visitor came here based on a colleague's recommendation. The food was amazing! The Salmon was as good as I've had in years. Lots of flavor and cooked perfectly. The pasta was equally as good and the marinara was outstanding.

Extremely cheap skewers - $2 for meats/$1.5 veggies). Little gem is a byob and opened every late (to 12/1am depending on the day). Each table is stocked with containers of spices. You have the option of buying grilled skewers or a hotpot combo that comes in a metal tub. 

My favorite skewers to get are the beef and mini sausages. The grilled pork and chicken were ok. 

Only thing I wish is that for the hotpot combos is that you can make your own combinations instead of picking preset ones. 

Quick service. No one really bothers you. Although I wish they were more attentive.

Caveat -- no seating so takeout only, but so worth it!

I hail from Long Island and Iannelli's pretty much puts Long Island Italian lore out of business. Damn good!

I used a Living Social right before expiration expecting nothing of this joint. The sandwiches are ridiculously big. For $30, I got "3" sandwiches but it took 6 meals to finish them and I am a very big guy. The bread is amazing, thick, tasty, crunchy, just to name a few adjectives and the sauces, spinach, meatballs, chicken, wtvr, all incredible. I usually am so bored by Chicken Parms but theirs woke me up. My friends are annoyed with me at this point for orgasming aloud every time I bite into their sandwiches. Please have a try.

And to think, this place is listed as a bakery. Best damn Italian-friendly sandwiches ever.

Canullis is one of those awesome Philly spots on 9th street. We come here once a week, we know all the butchers by name, and we buy 10-15lb of meat per trip.

On Saturdays, with the exception of the end of the month, it will be BUSY. If you want faster service, just avoid Saturday. 

Charlie, the owner is also a really good guy. We've come to know him over the years and  they will happily give you a tour of the back if you ask! (Not during peak times)

Note: their bacon isn't good. Just FYI.
It's a must visit for Christmas. The streudels are very flaky and crispy. Very friendly staff. It's big and there is a box for 2 strudels.

This place is unreal. I am so happy with the food and the ambiance of the restaurant. I have been there 3 times and every single time I am happy with the food. Their dishes are so unique and every bite is amazing. I recommend the short rib. It falls apart in your mouth and has so many flavors it's unreal. The only negative thing I have to say about the restaurant is the noise level and the darkness. It definitely doesn't stop me from going, but if you are going with older people it might be an issue.

South Bowl has one of the best bowling happy hours.  Taco Tuesday anyone? $3 dollar bowling per game, and $4.40 shoe rental, so in other words, super cheap date with lots of fun. I've also been there for my birthday on a random Friday, they tried to accommodate us and did a pretty good job, considering I did not call ahead and there were about 20 of us.

That being said, the servers are a little lacking, I don't necessarily know that it's the servers fault, considering there is maybe one server for all the lanes during bowling happy hour.  Generally, when we go, we're able to order one or two drinks and our food, then...nothing, until we leave. :(

The food has been pretty good and as you've probably read the tater tots are super good - get them.

Saxby's is nothing fancy; and nothing sticks out in my mind about this place... only the lack of great service; and the pretty bad coffee. 

I ordered an iced coffee on a hot summer day. What did I get? Coffee that was literally poured over ice in a cup - a warm and watered down mess. This was gross. 

The food was okay. Nothing awesome.

I'd rather spend my money at our two local awesome coffee shops that are within a 2-block walk from Saxby's: Metropolitan and Lovers & Madmen... or even at Starbucks for that matter!

This is the place! Prices are a bargain. This is a Full service salon. Head to Toes . I've been to other salons where they don't handle waxing or scalp treatment that I wanted done with my regular cut. Do you know how time consuming it was to go elsewhere just for those single treatments? Having found this location I can ask for 3 different services and be out the door in less than 1.5 hours. They are so efficient and accommodating!

Terrible communication via Uber eats! This restaurant has good food (when what you want is in stock) but the communication with customers is horrible via Uber eats. This has only happened with this business so I don't blame UE. Twice we've ordered, received a message that the order was successful, time passed and we're waiting thinking any minute now our food will be delivered. The driver calls to say they don't have what we wanted. We should have known that shortly after placing the order. We could have cancelled and not wasted a hour waiting. Unacceptable and unprofessional.

Decent Italian food in northeast Philly? Is it possible? Well it is now that Via Napoli is in town! For those that remember the spot Before the current ownership, I suggest you go back. The service, menu and quality of food is much better now. 
As other reviewers mentioned, their breads fun and tasty. I really enjoyed poking it and watching the steam escape.  Bruschetta was good, calamari just ok.  Sangria was delicious.
Judging by the menu and decor, items including lemons are their specialty. I got lemon tilapia and was very happy with it. Their red sauce was surprisingly delicious.
The only thing that bugged me was their menu.  It is printed like a newspaper and for whatever reason resulted in complete sensory overload for me.  Keep it simple my friends!

My friends and I enjoyed dinner here a few nights ago. Overall, the dishes are amazing, and worth the price. However, the serving size is small, so getting  few different things off the menu is recomennded. Also, we order the veggie lo mein (not on the menu), and the first time we got it, the noddles were too salty. We sent it back, and the noodles that were served to us next didn't have any salt at all. Not sure what was going on, since we ordered that due to a previous recommendation. 
Other than the noodle mishap, Sampan can definetely expect another visit from my friends and I.
Ordered creamy shrimp for takeout, for 19 dollars literally got 5 shrimp with 5 pieces of walnut, the frying batter was really thick.
Good food. Great atmosphere! Definitely worth checking out. Best crab in Philly hands down.

It had been quite sometime since I last visited this place - so I was glad my friend suggested dining here, especially when he reminded me of the thousand layer bread.

We got two orders of thousand layer bread with both curry potato dip and vatana dip (I preferred the curry dip) - the bread was delicious, although I wish it were warmer when it was served.

Since it was lunch, I took advantage of the lunch special (quite a deal at $6.50) and chose the coconut tofu with white rice. It was delicious and not as rich tasting as I thought it would be (which is a good thing). The special also came with a negligible salad with seasame seed dressing on the side.

There's a good selection of vegetarian options - Monsoon Vegetable and Jungle Tofu are on my to-do list for next time...

Can I just say "ditto" to the below review? My husband and I stayed here this past weekend for our one year wedding anniversary. Employees were extremely knowledgable, professional and personable. After asking about our visit, we mentioned our anniversary. We were in our room for less than 5 minutes when they knocked at the door with champagne & strawberries and a very sweet note. The room was BEAUTIFUL, spacious and in mint condition. The park view didn't hurt either. The next morning, we texted the valet to bring our car around. At check out, the concierge recommended a number of great brunch spots in the city. When we got out to the valet, we waited less than 5 minutes for our car, despite just texting (it recommended to text 20 minutes ahead of time). The employee that handled our luggage was hilarious and sent us off in hysterics. We had such a positive experience and will continue to stay at the Monaco when in Philadelphia.

I came her for my birthday in February with a group of friends. The experience and food was good, but not particularly memorable.

I'm not one who minds expensive dining once in awhile, but that's only if the food is up to par with the price. Unfortunately, it wasn't. Flavors were there, but they weren't particularly interesting and not really any different than any other less expensive Asian cuisine places.

The inside decor of course was beautiful and that's probably what attracts most of its guests, but essentially I'm not paying for the decor--I'm paying for the food.

Absolutely love que chula! I could probably eat here every day and the fact that they're right down the street makes it so much more tempting to do so. I've had plenty of friends order from here during their visits and they all really enjoyed their food. Ingredients are fresh and flavor is on point.

I was very excited to check out this new place. The decor is very interesting and over the top in a good way for philadelphia. I liked the ambiance, but it's a little clubby for dinner (which I like). The food was pretty good - I had the Heirloom tomato salad, and had girlfriends who had the fig flatbread, salmon, and the mushroom salad. No one complained! 

The cocktails were pretty good, but very strong. 

Unfortunately, the service was lacking. Our server seemed nice, but slow and flustered. Took us forever to get drinks in, the food didn't come st the same time (one girl was waiting 15 mins after the rest of us... for a salad) and getting the checks took forever. Not sure if this new place is just getting its feet under it or what but I wished the night could have gone smoother. Cool vibe though!

Enjoyed my second visit to Pizzeria Vetri in Fairmount as much as my first! Arrived by noon on a weekday to beat the lunch crowds, so had zero wait time for a spot for four (community-style tables). We shared an arugula salad (with basil pesto, it's divine!), a Maurizio (mozzarella, rosemary, olive oil, sea salt), and a Pomodoro (baby heirloom tomatoes, burrata, basil), all of which were supremely tasty. We ordered the Napoletano-size pizzas, and it was just the right amount, allowing us room for cannoli and fiordilatte soft-serve cones, the latter were so creamy and delicate! Service was well-paced and friendly and complimentary sparkling water is always a plus!

I've been here twice and so far have had the open faced Mediterranean burrito, the Nutella french toast, beiridge (a warm oats and quinoa dish with nuts, berries and honey) and the Parisian Za. I distinctly remember the burrito was dressed with some savory green salsa that literally made the dish unforgettable. The other dishes were all excellent too so you can't really go wrong with any of them. But just a tip, the french toast may be a little too much sugar for just one person so I would recommend ordering it to share! I also ordered a chai latte here the other day on a whim. It's now 5 days later and I am still thinking about how it rocked my taste buds. 

Remember, they don't take reservations. So if you can, try to come here on a weekday. But if you have to come on a weekend, it's still 100% worth the wait.

French Lebanese fusion cuisine, ftw!!

I know a lot of people go to Sabrinas for the stuffed french toast but I'm never able to pull the trigger on that one knowing it is probably a weeks worth of calories so I usually opt for one of the egg concoctions (hopefully does less damage?). The egg/omelet category is a little limited which is a problem for me as I'm not in the mood to create my own omelet in the morning.  My omelet was ok but had too much cheese.  One of my guests ordered the lox platter and was disappointed by the small amount of salmon (the plate was mostly salad) and the fact that the cream cheese was in a small container and not put on the plate like at Honey's (who by the way is more generous with the portions on the lox platter). My other guest ordered scrambled eggs and was upset at the brown spots on the eggs (fire too hot?)
I can't help but to mention that the carry out containers are styrofoam which should have been illegal years ago.  
Unless you're part of the stuffed french toast following, I'd go to Estelles (which by the way also serves stuffed french toast) for a better breakfast.

Eh, went to Osteria for my birthday with family and friends. The waiter wasn't the nicest, I don't think he smiled once. The food was okay, but I feel like I could have eaten the same thing at a less expensive restaurant. Nothing really excited me. Which is surprising for me because I see the joy in almost everything.

Go find this place! it's like a gluten free chipotle. Great flavor even though everything looks plain. If you're a multi allergy person this is a great place to start! I ordered the salmon plate with brown rice, broccoli, and avocado. It was great! they season the avocado side with olive oil and everything but the bagel seasoning, the broccoli has parmesan on it. The sauces come in the side, i ordered teriyaki and put it on my rice and salmon it was all really good and a great portion. Starbucks is close too!
Awesome bread and soups to start with new twists on the fresh peace. Nicely sauced and garnish in shredded veg. Stay away from pasta which is not that interesting.

I did not go here on Valentine's Day.  

I did not get completely shitfaced on absinthe.

I did not throw shade to about ten different girls.

I did not grind all over my roommate to Ma$e.

I did not start yelling at my roommate's boss that he should just tell me if he liked me or not.

I did not insist that we all leave because I was freaking out.

I also did not get taken here by a gentleman who later performed a totally juvenile act in cutting off communication with me.

Thank God none of these things happened, or I could really hold a grudge against this place.  As it is, I find it a little dull.  Reminds me a little of Rye in SF.  Which I have reviewed, so you can go check that out.

We went here three days in a row. By Day 3 they knew our drink orders, offered us free appetizers, etc. Friendly and efficient service - plus every meal was superb. Fresh, farm-to-table fare crafted without pretension. Cocktails were imaginative, tasty and generous. Go.

Love this place. No bullshit- I'd eaten here while seeking out scrapple on a trip to philly and when I decided to move here, proximity to Lisa's was a factor. It's a simple, classic product. Perfect for a hangover or after a morning run out on the wissahickon. I tend to go either Big Country or Big Scrapple (add hot peppers to any sandwich), but would also say you shouldn't sleep on the Hottie. All sandwiches are a robust 2ish pounds and should probably be eaten sparingly. Luckily the folks at Lisa's make sure you can't over-indulge by closing the restaurant for half the year ‍.

Thing to consider- this is a grab and go spot. recommend calling your order in.

I love coming to the Trolley Diner whether it be alone, friends or/and family. It's a nice atmosphere. The service is great. I don't have to wait a long time to receive my food and it always comes out delicious.

David was a superb host, and seated us within 5 minutes. There were 4 of us, just back from a photo shoot and walk through philly, on a Saturday night, and we just wanted a quite relaxing warm place to sit and catch up.. the tables were intimately woven together, 30-35 people max downstairs and 38 upstairs, but very comfotable. I had the Chicken/Lemon Capers and pasta, and Fiance had salad, our friends had the Raviolli and Gnocchi - excellent!.. was incredible! Ricotta in olive oil was refreshingly different.. BYOB .. service was quick and friendly.. David came by to check in, and kept our equipment and belongings securely stored away. Hour later we left thrilled and thought to have our Rehearsal dinner there.. 10/10

Went here, based on yelp reviews, to get my engagement ring resized (a vintage family ring). The staff were very helpful and trustworthy. I was able to get my ring resized (4 sizes smaller) in a little over an hour, for a great price. I was extremely happy with both the result of the resizing and the service that I received. I would absolutely recommend them.

One word - AMAZING. Hands down the best brunch I have had in a long time and THE best biscuit I've ever had. I got the loaded potatoes and added every veggie possible. My friend got the special Benedict (spicy Italian) which was also delicious. If I could eat one breakfast for the rest of my life - it would be Hawthornes homemade biscuit with jam.
I had a good experience- took about 45 minutes on a Saturday morning to jump my battery.  The man was professional- I would recommend
Perfect neighborhood bike shop. Always quick, attentive customer service and quick turn arounds on fixes.

I love the roast duck! 
Reasonable price, good portion & delicious! 
I also like the vegetables (I forgot it was Chinese broccoli or water spinach) they mixed with salted fish! The waiter told me it's a dried fish & stink, she make sure that I know the food before I order. She thought maybe I wouldn't like it. 
One thing I don't like it..., cash only! Lucky! I saw on Yelp about this, so I have enough cash to pay, lol.

Best meal I've been out for in a long time. A group of 5, we ordered multiple dishes to share. The spicy jawn pizza, saffron spaghetti, short rib pasta and the lamb shank stood out the most. I would go back here for a nice night out with friends or for a nice date. Very good atmosphere. I'm trying to think of something that could improve, of I had to pick something it would be the comfort of the seating arrangement could have improved. The service experience was fantastic.

Such a fun atmosphere, good drinks and amazing guacamole. However, you get the feeling that the dinner entrees are just over priced because it creates a fancier ambience, not because the quality or the portion sizes are really worthy.

This is a very good restaurant.  Most of the items on the menu are good.   Their tasting menu is a good option.   Some misses, though.   The lobster paella has great potential (this is a paella negra - I don't know where else around here that serves this) but it was too salty.   This place is a notch better than Tinto.
Schlesinger's epitomizes a great jewish deli: tasty, plentiful, homemade food, traditional deli environment, tremendous attention to a great customer experience.  Check 'em out when you're in town.

Saturday 7pm - 3 people

Server and staff were friendly and attentive. Food was served in a timely manner, waters were promptly refilled, and table was cleared quickly. The three stars is because everything was too salty it was hard to enjoy the natural flavors of the dishes.

We ordered:

TORTILLA ESPAÑOLA - Spanish Tortilla, Saffron Aioli  7

CROQUETAS DE JAMÓN - Ham Croquettes 6

ALBÓNDIGAS - Lamb Meatballs, Shaved Manchego 12

WAGYU BEEF BROCHETTES - Royal Trumpet Mushrooms, Sauce Bordelaise 15

COSTILLAS DE TERNERA - Beef Shortribs, Horseradish, Parmesan, Bacon 14

ALCACHOFAS Y SETAS - Artichoke,Wild Mushrooms, Black Truffles, Manchego 14
DO NOT RECOMMEND !! Sushi rolled up badly. crumble. the service is long and not attentive. meat brought very long and very cold.

This WaWa location is spot on as it's only a 12min walk from 30th St. Station. So if you are traveling through and have a small wait, take the trip down the block and grab a treat. As Pumpkin flavored anything has been flooding the commercials and airwaves, WaWa hit a home run with the pumpkin flavored coffee and cream cheese!!!!
This morning on my travels, I took the trip there and grabbed a Pumpkin flavored coffee with a hint of Cinnamon powder and Vanilla Cream. The coffee was accompanied by a Cinnamon Raisin Bagel with Pumpkin Cream Cheese.. My GOD this was heavenly. It was so good, I gobbled most of it up before I was even able to take the picture.....

This is quickly becoming my favorite neighborhood bar after two recent visits. It's a great place to grab a quick drink or spend a few hours hanging out and playing games over drinks with friends. They have several shelves of various board games (Jenga!) upstairs in a unique homey space. It's never been overly crowded the times I've been there and you can actually have a normal conversation without yelling or straining to hear the other person. There is a bar with traditional bar seating both on the first and second floor and the bartenders I've had are all great. Haven't tried any food here yet but planning to come back soon! They also have a happy hour that I need to check out!

Basil's was quick to tow my car after it got rear-ended and Vanessa took care of dealing with the insurance for me. It was especially helpful since I was out of the country 2 days after my car was hit and run. I was kept up to date frequently and their work was quick and nice. I can't even tell where my car was hit. They gave it a good clean inside and out as well. I would highly recommend them to anyone - very fast and friendly

We were walking around and decided on Jamonera for dinner because it had a good atmosphere. The menu sounded promising and our server was great! We were rather disappointed with the cuisine though. There were just too many flavors happening in each dish. We probably wouldn't come back.

Sabrina's is undoubtedly my go to spot for brunch in Philly. One great advantage for brunch (which is always busy on the weekends) is you can call ahead to put your name in and they'll give you the approximate wait time.

Very cute environment and friendly wait staff. The challah French toast and frittatas are amazing. They have specials that change every week including a special Benedict, egg sandwich, omelette and pancakes/ french toast. Their lunch and sandwiches are also great. Unique menu options as well as the classics. Overall, the perfect brunch/lunch!

I've been to 2 of these in philly and 1 in NYC, and this is by far the worst.
The food was mediocre at best, with sugar overloaded into everything - including their sacred dan dan noodles.  (I ordered the dan dan noodles, the cold cucumber, dry pot fish, cumin lamb, and eggplant).

This place is supposed to be able to do more authentic spicy stuff but this branch can't do either, even the chilli oil is not hot. 

The wait staff is woefully untrained... They wear random clothing so you can't tell who they are, they're super slow...  I thought they'd hired highschool students with no previous experience, but they looked a bit older. One time when I came to this location for lunch I had to go up to the counter and ask them to take my order, and then again for water, and then again for the check. Nice people, But they've never been trained. 

The table management was pretty terrible, they have large tables to begin with, which are too big for 2 people but not an ideal shape for 3 or 4...  And half the time they had tables for 4 being given to 2, so the whole place was under-utilised despite having a line to get in. And because the wait staff are slow and bewildered, they don't turn around the tables  so quick. 

The other locations have had way better food, better trained staff, and were an overall better experience.

I decided to do everything last minute, but luckily everything went into place and the team was able to help me. It was an easy process and there wasn't anything that I needed to worry about. Strong guys  keep it up! Thanks again

Build the expectations you want, Tracy will exceed them. My coworker hit my car, I dropped it off with Tracy, and she took care of everything. 

Call them, if only to get their opinion on how you should handle whatever happened. They have experience and it's worth the time.

Maybe I am old school, but I still care about customer service. I absolutely love to support a local business, but this one will not be it. It took them 3 weeks to get some basic wiring fixed in a bass amp. They NEVER called back when I asked them to please call back and give me information. I called them about 10 times for an update, and not once did they call back. If I have to hound a business to give me my stuff back, that is not good. I was hoping that they would at least once, reach out to inform me of what was going on since it took them a record amount of time to fix the equipment. Def. not a music store for actual musicians.

Great hoagies, quick and friendly service.  I'm not sure how this can "tacky up" the neighborhood, since at least from the outside is the same as the prior french bistro.  No, it's not trendy like Middle Child, but it doesn't have an absurd set of rules just to eat an egg sandwich.  I like Lee's.  They make good quality old school Philly hoagies and cheesesteaks in a nice, clean setting.  Lee's is perfect when you want a good sandwich at a decent price without any pretentiousness.

Great food and great prices
Does get noisy on a friday and in the summer it is hot inside
The neighborhood  is probably not the best,
Mixto, has a better ambiance down town.

I used to live just outside of Munich and went to a lot of beer gardens. This place is takes the best parts of the beer gardens of Bavaria and reinvents the rest. The beer selection was nice, the games were fun and social, and the food tastes better the more beer you drink. 
This place is an excellent place, I am considering moving just to live closer.

I wish it was more actual pizza museum than fancy pizza restaurant, but overall it's not bad. I finally tried all three slices and as far as pizza goes it's pretty good. They have a tendency to burn the slices, which is kind of a bummer, but I think it's worth the $3.50 a slice to check the place out. I've only been here on off-hours and so far haven't had a long wait. The staff is always super friendly. Since the place is so new, they've been getting a lot of press and the last time I was there, they had a camera crew in there interviewing the owner, wich is totally fine, but kind of inconvenient since there isn't a ton of seating. I haven't tried one of their pies yet. They sound good, but honestly $22 is a bit steep. I'd definitely come back though.

Would like to give 5 stars. The only reason for 4-stars is we have not had a chance to try food while eating in. The atmosphere is authentic and the food is wonderful. Especially after 8 PM this is my go to place for Mediterranean flavors. They could make the food a bit spicier but it is always tasty. I'm a big fan of their hummus, falafels, and lentil soup. Check it out!

This is the best Mac and Cheese I have EVER had. The staff was very friendly and very nice. We explained we had come all the way from TN to try their mac and cheese so they treated us well. Good size portions that were very filling. 10/10 would recommend

Cute local spot. Great beer selection, from what I could tell. Bartenders were helpful and friendly.  Food was good, but not a whole lot to choose from. Check the menu before you go to make sure there is something you'll like,  but definitely give it a try.

The decor is fitting for the menu and gives off a warm and 'nice' vibe. Must mention that it was a hike to get to the restrooms downstairs. BUT it was an incredibly nice walk through what reminded me of a European Villa with warm tones, dark woods and beautifully tiling. Again....just my opinion here folks.

Sat at the bar while waiting for our table. This might seem like an odd thing to write, but the bar was almost too big and too deep. I felt like I was yelling at the bartender to order and that she needed a step stool to put our drinks down in front of us. As everyone around us were waiting on their tables as well, and were  nibbling on complimentary hummus and pita, we did not get offered any. I know, shame on us for not asking, right?

Would have been nice to have been giving a head's up that we would recieve the same said hummus and pita when we sat at our table, especially before we ordered a crazy amount of food. Again, shame on us for not asking.

Drinks were ok but not much alcohol at all. Like, I am a lightweight drinker only weighing in at 150 pounds on my 5'6" frame and I had SIX drinks. I was walking fine, talking fine and walked 4 blocks to the garage where the car was without assistance or a studder or slip. Again, just sayin' folks....these are things I would want to know!!

Food was good. Fresh fish was cooked well. Sides were average and wait staff again, average.

At the end of the meal, all my friend's ordered the "Greek Coffee" (not sure if that's what it is called on the menu, but it is there special coffee) and I opted for a simple cappuccino. Glad I did. This special coffee, although very amusing to watch everyone react to the intense muddy coffee-type substance in the cup was a waste. Ordered regular coffee for everyone after that.

Again, it was good food and the decor/furnishings were very nice. I would eat here again, one day, if I was craving good hummus and a fairly good meal. But nothing that I am jumping up and down about.

First time here, loved it... Absolutely loved it. Great good, flavors, service was quick and friendly. Huge selection of beer, and a really cool atmosphere, not cramped like a lot of other restaurants in area.   Coffee was stellar too.   Will definitely be coming back.

I came here with my friend on a Tuesday evening around 6pm and we were seated right away. There's quite a bit of seating, and the restaurant is clean and nicely decorated.

I ordered the Vegetarian General Tao's Chicken which had a bit of a kick to it and was delicious. I thought it was a little pricey, but the portion was large enough for me to take leftovers home. My friend had the Pork and Egg Congee with Mushrooms and really enjoyed it. 

Service was fast and the food was tasty. I would definitely return to Sang Kee!
Just an amazing place. Food was outstanding, staff was great and the history of this fire house just amazing. Thanks for everything. Highly recommend the Butter Cake!

It's hard for me to cough up money to a big record store that is NOT Tower records but FYE can have some perks.....some, but not many.

Get there early enough and you might find some cool stuff in the used cd section but other than that their CD prices are just ridiculous. I guess it's the price you pay since everyone download now, but still. 

It can be useful though when selling back your old CDs. We got some good prices on some shit we just didn't want anymore, but if it's not rap or emo you probably won't get more than a buck. Seems like that's the only thing that sells thee besides the movies.

I seriously miss the old Tower import section because I never can seem to find anything I want at FYE. And for everyone praising their movie section I can probably find the same things used elsewhere for a fraction of the price, especially if it's b-horror and sci-fi.
Not sure how are supposed to rent a car here when the people that pick you up the airport just drive past you and don't stop for you.

I had a friend visiting from out of town over the weekend and I brought him to Vedge (at his recommendation!) We could not have been happier with everything we ate. I am already looking forward to going back and trying out some different items. Great atmosphere and delicious cocktails as well!

Stopped in for some cupcakes and banana pudding the other day. The woman working (who may be the owner) was very friendly and helpful. The cupcakes were delicious, and fresh (we got several, I haven't finished them yet). The banana pudding was one of the best I've ever had (second to only Magnolia Bakery in NYC). I'll definitely be back!
Awful experience. The hotel dry cleaning service completely destroyed my most expensive and favorite suit.  The one arm was melted, and the threading is now coming undone in multiple places.

This place is completely awesome and let me tell you why. First, they have wireless. I can sit in a corner, get some work done, and no one judges me for it. Second, the food is delish. I had a pork bun, shrimp dumplings, and a steaming cup of milky tea and all hit the spot. Third, that meal put me back $3.85. It's so cheap I have no idea how these folks stay in business. And finally: the staff are nice! You don't go into any Chinese establishment expecting decent customer service. And yet at Mong Kok I experienced nothing but bright smiley faces and helpful service. I was very much taken aback -- in a good way. Totally happy at this place, will totally return.

I have been coming here for a couple of years now. It is my absolute favorite cafe in all of the city. It is inexpensive. You can come here just for the freshest dessert and a sharing pot of traditional mint tea (GOOD crepes, waffles and fruit, mamoul, baklava, smoothies) or for a complete meal (rice, meat, pita, salad, the most delicious hummus ever, and more)

There also make espresso drinks. One of the rare places that do a lot and still do it well.

The ambiance is relaxing and cozy. It is well decorated, tasteful, but casual. No matter when I go, it is always bustling with mixed clientele from all backgrounds.  It dies down for a moment during prayer time at the mosque down the road. There is also outdoor seating for when Winter finally gives up on harassing us. It's a great place to hang out for hours and no one will judge you if you break out a deck of cards (n case anyone was wondering). There is also free wifi.

It is a very successful business, and I hope it stays around for good!

It's very simple, Govberg (and The WatchBox, their affiliate) played the old bait and switch with me. After agreeing on a price for a watch, plus tax, they sent me an invoice that was, including tax, significantly more than it should have been. I got no satisfactory response from Jason and canceled. I have it all in writing, too, which makes it even stupider. 

Make sure you scrutinize the bill *very* carefully before you buy from them, if you do at all. I wouldn't.

*****UPDATED TO ADD*****

I'm grateful to the response below because it illustrates the problem. What he's saying is that a company that moves millions of dollars of inventory doesn't have a calculator. If they did, they'd see that we're not talking about $50. 

Watch collectors get into the hobby for a lot of reasons. But one thing we have in common: we're Type A about precision. The idea that a used watch dealer would be so sloppy with my money (I'm sure there's never any ambiguity about *their* money) bodes ill for the quality of the merchandise they sell.

Really convenient location. I was looking for souvenir shot glasses and stopped in as I was walking around checking out old city Philadelphia. Shop owner was very friendly and polite. Prices were great!

This store is very cute, but it's also expensive. If you're looking for a really unique wedding or bridal shower card, or if you want to get a thoughtful gift of stationary for a friend, this is the place to be. Just be prepared to drop a couple more bucks than you typically would at CVS or Target for the same type of item.
Convent for our family. Prices are great shoe department leaves a lot to be desired. Cashier's are often friendly. But crowded most of the time. It still beats Walmarts lines and parking.

I love the 3 for 10.00 meal.
Drink,salad and Entree.
The extra crispy chicken strips are delicious,  also the bbq bacon cheeseburger and the loaded mashed potatoes are delicious also
Circle thrift is right around the corner from my house and I love coming in here.

First time in philly and, by chance, Cleavers was the closest cheesesteak place to me. It's a very nice looking place with good staff and fairly quick service. I went for their "The Block" and it was pretty tasty. I would prefer some kind of sauce with it (restaurant at home has a sauce that really elevates a cheesesteak) but in its true form definitely good and worth to try. I can definitely see myself coming back in my next visit to philly

I posted a review for Govberg's other location here: http://www.yelp.com/biz/govberg-jewelers-ardmore.

It's kind of long so I won't repeat it here. In essence, I called them about Rolex After-Sales Service and they not only had a strange policy that doesn't match any other Authorized Dealer, but they were extremely rude on the phone.

If you're looking for a Rolex, I suggest you stay away. Any friendliness you get before you pay will be counterbalanced by the bad attitude you get when you need service or support after you give them your money.

Meh,  I expected alot more from this place the gauc was good everything else was so so. The menu is very limited I also didnt like the fact they said we couldnt sit where we were but a few seconds later someone sits there and it's ok. $15 bucks for nachos arent that good nope wont fo back here

My absolutely go-to for their delicious homemade spreads & bagels; i actually have a standing order every 2 wks cuz im beyond addicted to the lox spread and the whitefish salad which rivals any nyc deli...but then i tried their desserts, and just WOW! if ur looking for the flakiest, freshest, honey glazed baklava, its here...the rice pudding is so creamy & light, its just heaven, and the tiramisu is so decadent it will have u & ur spoon settled in for the nite, enjoying every last, scrumptious bite - sooooo worth every calorie ;) my thighs may hate me, but my belly is just filled with joy ;) talia's is a neighborhood gem!!!!!

This store is GHETTTOOOOO! as HELL... Just like all other reviews over an 1hr late for delivery. I used the app to schedule my order to be delivered by 7pm ... order shows up at 8pm and COLD as ice. Delivery driver couldn't have been more callous. Called to complain, store manager gave same excuse as all other reviews here "We're Busy, Lots of Orders" ... Well Hire More Damn Drivers.... He too couldn't care less. 

Domino's needs to shut this store down, or at least clean house of such incompetent callous people.You can tell these workers are just there for their paychecks, there whole demeanor exudes it.

DON'T! order from this store.

I worked at a Thai restaurant for a while and so I am pretty familiar with the dishes that are typically served at a place like this. I love spice, so I went for the panang curry tofu lunch special. I am literally eating it right now, and it is delicious. Only complaint is tofu isn't as crispy as I would of liked, but the flavor is amazing and portion is decent. Crispy tofu for 5 stars!

Fantastic experience.  They gave 5 star service and even with their great selection, they were not displeased with me purchasing frames from an online provider and gave me a great deal on new contact lenses.

We went with a huge party and they set up a pre fixe menu (which we decided on a few options for it beforehand) which made life a lot easier for 12 people. I had a delicious beet salad and goat cheese, crusted salmon, and an amazing bread pudding. The Old Fashioned I had was really good too. Love it here.

This was my first rolled ice cream experience, and overall it was fine - if not a little over-hyped. I've heard some other rolled ice cream places allow you to create your own flavor combos, whereas Seventh Heaven has a pre-set list of choices. 

I chose the Wild Berries - which was blended with blackberries, blueberries, and strawberries. I enjoyed it - it was refreshing and nice. 

It was definitely cool to watch them make your ice cream dish right in front of you. Definitely a fun treat! I will have to check out some other rolled ice cream places to compare.

When things come together perfectly, i dont mind giving high marks for everything. I was told about this place thru a friend and he told me about the crabs they used to serve. I almost walked passed this place. Its not a place that you see easily.  It looks small on the outside but it has big potential.  The food they have are mostly comfort food with plenty of vegetarian options. They also have a long wall of refrigerators with many beer options! I was in heaven. 

I went down the long corridor and a young guy named Nico greeted me and even though it was 10pm, he was very welcoming and he helped me choose what i ate. I ordered the grilled cheese brioche with cheddar and a tomato based soup. Wow, quite delish! I asked him to help me choose a porter. They have many varieties in the frig. He picked an Old Rasputin Porter which is 10% alcohol so you need to be careful...it was amazing! I also picked a few other beers and ciders and the 6 pack of anything comes at 10% off. Yes, this place had about 30 people sitting down, yes there is a dining area also, and the music was not too loud. I was wondering how come this place was not packed. I spoke to a very sweet lady named Mariah. She was so nice and i must have asked her 20 questions about the business. She had all the answers and also had ideas on how to improve the place. You can tell when someone is sincere. This place seems to hire them.  Unfortunately she was not the manager and cannot implement some of the changes to bring in more traffic. This place is a gold mine but i doubt it is advertised heavily at all. Everyone sitting in the dining area were quite content.  You would be too with the low prices on food and cold beer. They must have over 100 selections of beers in the frig. The food shuts down about 11pm and theu close at midnight. One of the few times i give any place a 5..i think they deserve it. I would be remissed if i did not mention Celia. She may same very serious, but she is quite sincere. She is only been there for 2 weeks and i suspect she will be there for quite some time.  

The food is good, the beers are cold and the people working there are super friendly. What else do you need!!

It's $8 for the tour and $3 for the museum. Go when it's not busy like my friend and I did. We went on a Wednesday afternoon and there were almost no other people in the building barring the receptionist and a few Masons in the library and at the museum desk. 
Neither my friend nor I were stupid enough to ask dumb questions about the Da Vinci code and our guide loosened up and answered anything we asked. There was a recorded blurb at the beginning of each Hall (all 7,) and certain interesting areas outside of the Halls like the Grand Stairway and the Benjamin Franklin Room where the tour began. The building is magnificent and has many significant architectural features. I particularly liked the glassed in ceiling that used to be open but now has stars painted where the florescent light above shows through. This building has many many lights which are not switched on until the lodges come in to use the building for their meetings at night. 
My Grandfather was a Mason and I wish he could have seen this place. I sent my Mom a folder card that contained pictures of all 7 Halls; I knew she would really appreciate now beautiful the building is. To correct Anna M's review, many Masons are not rich although I am sure many are white. And although there are many religious references and symbols the Masons aren't exclusive. The association is mainly dedicated to furthering the community through networking the way they did before the internet, locally and through businesses. And as my Grandmother told my Mother when she asked, they are more like institution committees than a mysterious cult. The Masons are as secretive as an old church from the 1800's. Many of the Halls actually look like some of the churches in New England (like the Gothic Hall and the Nordic Hall.) 
If you like architecture and enjoy history you've found a gem in this building. The museum is small but packed with history and interesting artifacts. The gift shop is small but also similarly packed with cool things to buy for a Mason, kid or anyone else you could think of. They have some gorgeous enameled boxes that are made into animals that are relatively affordable considering what they are. I only wished I'd gotten my Mom a DVD of the tour of this building for only $11. 
I wouldn't consider the price of this tour as expensive considering that the Temple has more to look at than many museums. The Halls are constantly in need of work because the building is so old and the materials that the Halls are made of is mainly wood and plaster. The building is so old that the original lighting consisted of gas chandeliers and lights. Everything was switched over to electricity but many of the fixtures were adapted. The small details that our guide told us about the floors and stairs and rooms on the top floor and the basement was interesting and held our attention. Both my friend and I agreed it was well worth the price and time to come and be dazzled by the history and the Halls.

I walked from gate 6 and got a disappointment. The burger was pretty good but it wasn't worth walking so far. I was holding an ice cold coke that was making my hands cold. Ally Mora told me to write this too.

This is the type of furniture store that I normally consider useless to even walk into.  Of course the merchandise is beautiful and the staff attentive, but prices at these types of 'boutique' outlets usually puts everything far out of reach.  This weekend and I went with my wife and had a good experience.  While not cheap, things weren't outrageous. We got a very nice dining room table and 6 chairs for $2k.  We were seeing sets of similar quality at similar stores, prices in the $3-6k range.  So, we were able to expand our small collection of grown-up furniture without going on a payment plan.

The only other Starbucks in this area is kinda far, so I came here again to my dismay.

Still dirty, no improvement since the last time I was here. Sat for over an hour working online, and when I went to refill my tea, they did not want to do it for me, saying, "We cannot refill your tea since you've left the premises."  I pointed to my open laptop 15' away and said, "...but I'm right here."  I will never forget this Starbucks, because they will actually argue with you in front of customers.

Where's Schultzy when you need him?  Very sad to see this.

A solid dim sum spot! 

Ocean Harbor did not disappoint. This was my first time having dim sum since COVID and so I might've been a little overexcited because the food I got was more than enough to last 3 meals for the two of us. I called ahead of time and was able to get everything I wanted (even if it wasn't on the menu), which included shrimp dumplings, shumai, bean curd rolls, sticky rice with lotus leaf, turnip cakes, chives dumplings, stuffed eggplant, shrimp rice noodle roll, and a big serving of flat noodles. I really appreciated how this restaurant had basically all the traditional & classic dishes. 

The order was ready for pickup within 20-25 minutes and the staff was very courteous. My personal favorite was the shumai (just so tasty! I can't say shumai has ever stood out to me before as a dim sum dish but theirs was great) and the flat rice shrimp rolls (can't ever go wrong with these); some of the let downs were the chives dumplings (didn't have as much flavor/freshness as some others I'd had) and the stuffed eggplant (also lacking a little in the flavor and texture departments). I also wish the beef flat noodles had a bit more greens to them to give it a bit more visual appeal. Overall, though, this was a welcome feast for my eyes and stomach and I would happily return!

Marigold Kitchen had such promise to be one of my new favorite restaurants in the city but that chance quickly scampered away alongside the mouse that ran across the dining room. That is correct -- a rodent joined us as a late night dinner companion on Friday night nearly brushing my foot as we were finishing dessert.

The fact that there was a rodent in this brownstone setting is not entirely implausible, but what was wholly surprising was the fact that the staff was tacitly apologetic, at best. "They come out when it gets quiet."  "This happens in the winter time."  "It's our neighbor's fault" were some of the justifications uttered by the Manager.  

I will say this --  the food at Marigold was indeed great, but beware that you may end up sharing it with tiny co-patrons that would made the Health Code board squeamish. (And for what it's worth - this is the first Yelp comment I've ever written. I felt that it was my civic duty)

I'm a Bourbon fanatic so Stateside is right up my alley...they even have Parker's Heritage (which is unheard of).  Small plates, so you can sample a bit of everything.  I reccomend the pork belly (in fact if you are anywhere that sells pork belly you need to order it).  

The service is phenomenal, not over intrusive and always right there when you need something.  We came around 5:00pm on a Friday and were surprised how empty it was.  There was a happy hour crowd but not too big.  

Give it a try, expect a high check, but if you love small plates and most importantly Bourbon, you will find Stateside is perfect for you as well.

Duke's Plumbing is awesome. He is responsive, thoughful, and goes the exta mile. This is about tge third or fouth time I have used them. My hot water heater stopped working. He came right out and after much legwork to help me avoid having to pay for a brand new one was able to fix it. It turned out to be a minor fix. Thank you Duke and your team for helping me.

Alright so Starbucks is usually Starbucks in my book. Went in here after lunch, got through the line quick with friendly service. Coffee was hot, and they have coconut milk here. Things seemed business as usual and what you would expect walking into a Starbucks. 

Remember: if you have the Starbucks app, open it while your in line to make sure your signed in and not when you get to the register (like I did #fail).

Loved it! It's nice to finally have a good Italian place in Manayunk. Food was delicious! Plates weren't meant to be shared, but the whole table had a little bite of everything and each meal was great!

Shall I preface it with "I'm not from Philly" because I just don't get the appeal. Fries, grease, fat, and more fries. Salad with fries. Soup with fries. A side basket of fries! There's a reason there's an obesity epidemic, and it starts with this restaurant. 

Could be a matter of unmanaged expectations - our friend from Philly kept raving about this place, and we drove faaaaar out of our way to eat here. But with a bevy of fatty options, I wasn't feeling it. Ordered the clams, and over half the shells in our basket were empty. *Shell shock!* What a rip-off.

Then my poor daughter got wretchedly ill for four days after eating the seafood chowder. Yes, I can pinpoint it to this item, and it was miserable to see her so sick for so long.

I would never, ever, go back. Good thing, like I said, I'm not from Philly so it's not even an option.

This place gives preferential treatment to different people.

I had an appointment at Shanti Medspa and was going to be late to it, so I called in to reschedule, but Shilpa (eyebrow threader) let me know that 30 minutes was fine and I should still come. I came exactly 30 minutes later, and arrived at the same time that the person with the next appointment and his friend did. That person's friend did NOT have an appointment and was a walk-in. If that walk-in needed her brows threaded, she should wait until I, who had an appointment was done. Instead, I sat and waited for BOTH of them. When I brought this to Shilpa's attention, she let me know that the walk-in came with her friend who DID have an appointment. That's not logical, since those people should then have made appointments for TWO people, not one person.  By waiting for BOTH of them, I was late for my next appointment of the day. 

It did not escape my notice that the only visible difference amongst us was the color of our skin; the person and his walk-in friend were white, I am a not. I hope this was not the reason why this establishment let someone cut in front of me in line.

A friend suggested this place for restaurant week earlier this year and I am SO glad that she did! The restaurant week deal is an awesome deal - one of the few places that I've been to that doesn't skimp on serving size just because they're offering a special prices menu. We ordered chips and salsa to start so we could munch on something with our drinks. I got the Margarita de Champagne and I thought it was pretty good! My friend got the South of the Rio and I thought it was better than my choice. For the first course, I went with the empanadas. That night they were filled with ground beef. I thought they were seasoned very well and pretty large for an appetizer. For the second course, I got the Mar y Tierra, their variation on a surf/turf. The tamarind sauce that it came with was AHmazing! I really liked it. Then for dessert, they were offering chocolate cake. How could I say no to that? I ordered it thinking it might on the smaller size. NO, it was huge and I am not complaining about that. I had so much food that night and I was stuffed until the next morning. I would definitely recommend this restaurant week and I hope to update soon once I've had the chance to visit for normal dining!

I hate this store. The only reason I ever come here is when I need an unusual item (e.g. special spices, wonton wraps, etc). Other than those rare occasions that I have no other options, I avoid this hell hole like the plague. I often come here only to find out they are out of the product I came there for in first place. There are probably about 7 isles of non-produce foods. The rest of the store is filled with food bars, personal items, bath and body products, clothing, plants, etc scattered throughout the store in nonfunctional sections. The flow of the entire store does not make sense. The isles are small, crowded, and never seem to be fully stock even though the employees are constantly working hard and quickly. I would hate to work here. I have always been helped when I ask but I can tell the workers are overwhelmed and tired. Although employees are usually polite and helpful, I often feel like I'm disrupting their workflow.

I can NEVER find products. It's takes so long to find things because the isles are poorly labeled and jammed-packed full of overpriced items. They really should reconsider the flow of the store. CREATE MORE ISLES SO FOOD CAN BE EASIER TO BROWSE AND COMPARE PRICES. How should they create more isles, you ask? Well first downsize the stupid clothing/beauty products and random family items (e.g. $50 children's lunch boxes) area I have never seen anyone ever browsing. 

The only credit I give the store is the nice produce section. However, I can get the exact same fresh products (including organic) at other groceries for cheaper. The prices for food here are stupid high. 

Lastly, the customers here are SO entitled! People standing around being completely inconsiderate of anyone else wanting to get to the product they are standing in front of as they scroll on their phone or let their kid do cartwheels while everyone else dodges out of the way.
