Enter at your own risk. Avoid if you dislike disorganized environments and clueless customers.

We did the "Leave it to us" option, in which for $50 per person the restaurant sends out small plate after small plate of lovely food. I would say that the vegetarian items were by far stronger than the non-vegetarian ones, but everything was flavorful and well done. We definitely enjoyed ourselves, even if we were sort of exhausted by the end of the meal -  you definitely do not go away hungry.  The wine/drinks are no better or worse priced than any other similar caliber of restaurant ($12-16 per glass, $40-80 for a bottle), and the selection was pretty well done. A lovely experience even if it is not one that I will seek out more than once in a while. For two of us with the "Leave it to us"  option and two glasses of wine each, after 20% tip, $200.

For nostalgia we stopped into The Olde Bar which is in the old Bookbinders building. Bar was dimly lit with these cool candles along the entire bar. Table tops and regular tables behind the bar. We were lucky to score seats at the bar. I got a Manhattan and hubby got Ketel One. They measure all booze, but it seemed like a decent poor. I got calamari and hubby ordered the lobster roll but subbed for fries instead of chips. The calamari was crispy and delicious. They were laying on a bed of puréed roasted pepper sauce and had dollops of garlic aioli, yum! Very well balanced ratio of squiggly to rings. Poor hubby didn't get food out for what seemed like an hour after mine came. When we asked the bartender said the kitchen was backed up. But then he got suspicious when it hadn't come out for a really long time. Finally, it arrived and they put an extra piece of claw on top- I guess because of the wait. The other bartender finally came around and said she forgot to fire his! Wow!! The fries were really good, and kept their heat for awhile. We had to ask for ketchup, I always find that strange. Hubby said it was good, but not worth the wait or price tag. The bartender gave us a free round because of the food foul up.. good customer service there. I would return, the menu is small but interesting. Lots of raw oysters too. I heard happy hour is a buck to to shuck. I'm sure it gets a good crowd!

THE BEST martini in recent memory! I mean, how can you go wrong with 3 olives and prosciutto!

 My husband and I chose Zavino for my sis-in-law and mom-in-law. They are not adventurous at all and we needed someplace close to Temple Hosp.

The place was hopping at 3:30 on the Saturday before Easter. The hostess on duty was very sweet and got us seated ASAP. But not before I was able to order THE martini. 

My in-laws were very happy. My husband and I shared various apps. I can't really remember anything more because I just keep dreaming of THE MARTINI....

Everything was wonderful from the easy online appointment booking to the very cost effective blow out that I got. Even with wild curly 14" long hair I was in and out in 45 minutes and couldn't be happier with my look! Definitely plan to come back next time I need to glam up!

I used to live by this shop but it was always so packed that I never stopped in. A few weeks ago, UberEats was offering free delivery so I decided to try their tacos! Overall, this place offers exactly what you would expect from a taco shop - fresh ingredients and flavorful meat! I found the portion sizes to be enough (got 2 pork carnitas to share with myself)! Love that their chips are freshly made. On my next trip, I'll try some of their taco sauces!

If you order right you can have some amazing food here....the truffle guacamole, melted manchego. nachos, fish tacos, tua ceviche and black bean enchiladas (substitute the red chile sauce it is so much better) were all spectacular.  
Only negative is the wait times.

I tried to like Pico, I really, really did.  There always seems to be some issue.  the food is delicious... everything I've tried is tasty.  But the wait staff is shoddy and menu items and prices seem to change inconsistently.  The place bounces back and forth from BYOB to having a liquor licence.  Sometimes the "5 taco plate" comes with 5 regular tacos, sometimes 3, and sometimes 5 mini tacos... all at the same price.  Who thinks thats a good value.  I went last night and it seems like the owner has shifted all his energy into The Baja Room, his place across the street (which is awful!).  They served us on paper plates with plastic forks and knives... who goes out to dinner to eat with plastic?? I ordered a bottomless ice-tea and not once was I offered a refill... I had to ask each time.  We ordered chips and salsa and we got chips, they were out of salsa, and I was charged for chips and salsa.  I won't be back.

My wife thought it was delicious--I'd say very good. So maybe that's about an 8.5 out of 10?  The service was very friendly but a bit slow in the second half of our meal--they forgot to ask if we wanted dessert. Whoops!  The menu is at tad  confusing

I think there must be some psychology to the fact that airport food establishments always have mediocre review averages. Something about the looming inevitability of being stuffed into a small metal box with dozens of other passengers, delays, TSA, and sky high prices just makes people frugal with the love in reviews. But even then (and trust me I've had my share of delays at PHL) this is one of my favorite places to come when I'm trapped in Terminal F with some time to kill (which is every damn time). There's lots of seating and I really like taking my time ordering on the tablet and seeing pictures of all the food.  Can things get lost in translation? Of course.  Take tonight.  I evidentally checked "a twist" instead of "olive" with my dirty martini. And the bartender caught the anomaly and asked about it.  And my brussel sprouts arrived sans the incredibly important bacon but my server brought me a cup of fresh bacon to sprinkle on top. I think that's pretty good service for an airport bar. Plus, hello, ample charging docs, tablet entertainment and big screen tvs.

This place gets busy fairly early in the evening. I love the Greek Salad (skip the Greek with Greens - the plate is to large for the tiny tables), Baba ganouch, and their Pita - right from the stove and crispy around the edges. The appetizer size of the Calamari is out of this world. Ask for a side of red sauce for this. The scallops are incredible. If you can't get a table.. sitting at the counter is a really cool experience. You get to watch this asembly line of wait staff manage all these moving parts of the restaurant. You'll also get an ear shot of some of the wait staff bashing the customers. While the restaurant runs very efficiently, there is this air of arrogance from the wait staff that can be diasppointing. They are not there to speak to you, only to take your order, serve you food, and impatiently wait for you to leave so the next in line can have a seat. They use very little spice in all their meals. It's basic, satisfying, with no frills. Bring a bottle of white, and enjoy.

I purchased the Volt heated gloves and heated vest for approximately $350 from this store. The first time I attempted to use the gloves, the connector from the glove to the battery kept slipping off, hence no connection. I tried the second battery and it connected properly. Now the fun part, I called the store to start the process for the exchange, first call was on hold for over 30 minutes and never an answer. I sent two e-mails and finally received a form letter response about the hoops I needed to jump thru to get their defective product replaced.
I mailed the defective battery back and will be pleasantly surprised if I get a replacement back without my attorney getting involved..... : )
Think twice before purchasing from this company....I do not believe a company with this poor customer service is lacking for complaints about their products or personnel.
Food is fresh and delicious and I'm not even a vegetarian.  I'm usually here 4 times a week.  Dean and Deb are the best.

I love cake donuts and this place has some of the best I have ever had. They have hot and fresh donuts that are available everyday and the specialty donuts that are rotated and NOT hot and fresh but still amazing.  I personally get a hot cinnamon sugar most days.  The specialty donuts are fantastic. I've tried flavors creme brûlée and lemon meringue.  

Federal also serves fried chicken which I have only had on the sandwich.  The sandwich is available after 11 am and has a special sauce and pickles.  Perfect for a late breakfast. 

There also beverages and even t-shirts.  If you're just stopping through Philly make sure to visit.

Wow. I have been craving Bing Bing Dim Sum all week so let's do this thing. THIS FOOD IS SOMETHING TO SALIVATE OVER. I'm not sorry for all caps because I truly mean it with all the enthusiasm within me.  

Red Curry Shrimp, 
You exceeded all my expectations with your creamy and dreamy coconut vibes bathed in subtle spices and peas. You inspired me to make my own curry at home which means I was truly inspired. WILL ORDER AGAIN. 

Dan Dan Noodles & Pork Bun, 
What can I say? I am a vegetarian so I had bites of you that did not contain meat and you were still delectable. I can only imagine how amazing a full bite containing meat was. My sister, who is not a vegetarian loves you so much with all your savory flavors.

Last but NOT least, I do not remember what you were called but I will call you Chocolate Dessert Dumplings. You, my friends, were filled with such sweet chocolate filling with your cool cat dumpling coat. My mind oftentimes drifts to you while I daydream on my SEPTA commute.

Green tea, 
You are always so reliable with a meal like this. Holding a cup of you reminds me to fully enjoy each part of the meal in a mindful way. I salute you. 

Service,
I do not remember my waitress's name but she was great, helpful, friendly and just as excited as I was that it was my first time eating there. She hooked us up with the goods. 

Atmosphere, 
You are quaint, trendy, and really fucking cool. I dig the community table and the art that is seen everywhere. "Come in and eat here!", the warm red hue illuminated by adorable string lights and paper lanterns calls to you from the street!
See you soon :)

Quick corner deli every neighborhood needs!
I frequently grab breakfast sandwiches from here, as they are best place in my neighborhood for a good deli sandwich. I appreciate their quickness & that they have hashbrowns for my sandwich. My classic order is sausage, egg & cheese on an everything bagel with a hash brown and hot sauce. Their staff isn't the friendliest, and the bagels aren't always the freshest. Regardless, still a frequent spot of mine. 
I recommend this place if you're looking for good deli sandwich quick, especially breakfast sandwiches!

Didn't even get the pizza yet and I'm irritated! Late night and were hungry only to be falling asleep because there 40min delivery time is already going on an hour and a half - don't recommend if ur looking forward to a fresh hot pizza within a reasonable timeframe ...

I give this place 3.5 stars. I rounded up because they are cheap, decent quality, and consistent. I order delivery from here regularly and you can get 3 rolls and miso soup delivered for 16.63 after tax and tip for DINNER. I haven't found another sushi place that has a better value during evening hours and can offer acceptable quality sushi. My favorite items are the salmon avocado and spicy salmon rolls, but the tuna avocado, eel avocado, and shrimp tempura rolls are also decent. I have had bad experiences with Sumo sushi and Crazy sushi, but iSushi has been consistently good over the past 3 years of ordering from them.

I love the atmosphere of this cafe. The sunlight brighten the room, makes it very comfortable for students to do readings or assignments. If you want to hangout with friends at here to do some chatting, this is also a suitable place. The chocolate banana walnut cake is one of my favorites ^^

We went there at dinner time on a Friday night so we weren't surprised it was busy. The food was AMAZING and the service was very nice and reasonable. My only complaint is that everyone working seemed so stressed out I felt tense watching them. Maybe I was just paranoid but it was hard to ignore. Anyway, I would try it again for sure. Food 10/10, service 9/10, atmosphere 7/10
The food here is really awful (lacking in seasoning), as is the vibe of the establishment overall.

TIME actually drew a pretty decent crowd for a Thursday Night; there are two rooms, the main one more open with a oval shaped bar in the center, a live band in the corner across from the entrance, and ample seating around the perimeter, though it was pretty full; the second room is nestled in the back, cozier and louder -- more hole-in-the-wall-ish -- also with a bar and kitchen service. If you sit in the back room and notice the wall of beer bottles, I wouldn't advise trying to remove ones I'm fairly certain they are not cemented in place. average age was around 25...Good Music! Table Service available and the full menu is offered until at least 1am. 'The Burger' was very good, though the salmon was just okay... and overpriced.

So as y'all see, I used to love this nail salon. But lately it's always understaffed and every time I come in I'm here for at least 3 hours. Great example of a business starting off strong and slowly trickling to some bullshit.

Stopped in tonight to pick up a few things. While, this is not a full fledged supermarket, this is a great place to pick up a few things when you can't get to a food store or Target. I needed cleaning supplies and found just what I needed. There seemed to be plenty of stock, however the pricing is a bit high. The employees were friendly and willing to help you find whatever you need. 

Fun finding for myself, they carry Mochi, my favorite dessert!

Pretty good food, but _TERRIBLE_ lay-out and super-slow checkout.   

Props for a good deli counter and a mostly good fish counter.

Pro-tip: Free garage parking with validation, although it's a bit of a PITA.

As soon as we entered, all I could smell was chocolate, and though I don't really like chocolate the smell of it baking, that comforting warm chocolate smell is up there on the list. 

"Pack sensible shoes," I told myself, "you'll thank yourself later." This may have been good advice to heed, irregardless the weather was 15-20 degrees warmer than I anticipated coming from Boston. I'm not always good at taking my own advice...

After what seemed like some sort of death march (read: I'm not a fan of doing a lot of walking in flip flops) from somewhere near the Ben Franklin bridge, zigzagging like someone in a strange city might do, The Sweet Box was like an oasis!

The women who were working were very nice and helped us procure a cupcake each and a bar for later. We talked briefly about how long they'd been in business, the food truck and how cute the storefront was. 

These delightful cupcakes were the perfect ratio of cake to frosting. Strawberry champagne (I think it was called) didn't even need frosting, the cake was so good, and the Vanilla Vanilla was good enough that I "forgot" to share it with my friends. The Raspberry Bar was the perfect compliment to coffee the next morning. 

If you are in the area, I strongly suggest you add "eating cupcakes" to your to-do list, you'll thank yourself later- I mean it, also, when packing, really, find some space for a pair of kicks.

This is adequate, cheap sushi.  Probably best for college students & people that aren't selective about sushi in general.  I'm sure I would have liked it when I was in my 20s.  But after living in Japan for several years & having my eyes opened to high-quality sushi (at all price points), it's tough to get excited about this place.  Sadly, I think you have to pay more to get high-quality sushi in the US...or at least outside of California.
Great customer service. They were able to move me a week earlier on late notice. Also made sure all of my belongings were safe during the move. Will be using them again!
This was a delicious brunch with a chill atmosphere. Best biscuits and gravy I've ever had outside of the American south. And $4 mimosas where the OJ to bubble ratio was perfection. Thank you!
When eating out I look for food quality and good service. They scored 5 on both. The menu has something for everyone and the portions are decent. I just wandered by this place and glad I stopped in.

One of my previous reviews was about another shop in the Italian Market that is keeping me alive during these unprecedented times so I need to write another about Superior because when I'm not stuffing my face with chips, I'm shoveling fresh pasta down my throat. All things considered, I should be be severely overweight.

The best thing about fresh pasta is that it takes like 30 seconds to cook (okay maybe 2 min if it's frozen - which it comes as). They have a multitude of sauces ranging from marinara to vodka sauce, Alfredo, mushroom, pesto - you name it, they have it. They have vegan pasta and sauces as well. I always grab a little bag of grated parm to top off my pasta (also to hit the $10 credit card minimum sometimes). My favorite is the tortellini, but that would be my fav anywhere. 

They are on Mercato, and you can go in person. They only allow one person in at a time now so you'll have to wait because I'll probs already be in there!

the shop is clean, 
the jewelry reasonably priced, 
the nostril studs are the best i've seen
&
the staff is extremely knowledgeable.

i got my septum pierced here, 
let it close up like an idiot, 
then went back and had it 're-pierced'.
ten minutes and roughly twenty dollars later, 
i had a smile on my face
& 
a shiny new stainless steel retainer in my septum!

I love reading terminal market! I've been going there for a while to get lunch on the weekends and it's always the first place I want to take guests who are visiting Philly. Lately, I've started buying more and more of my groceries - produce and meat mainly - there as it's super affordable and very convenient.

Had my hair styled by Nicole for a few events and she's been way awesome. She gave me exactly what I wanted - perfect curls that would not fall all night. She will def continue to be my go-to girl when I'm looking to get all fancied up.
People should really look into their food selection too. The food is pretty legit as far as I am concerned. Thumbs up!

Very very poor communication, posts on social media of my dog clearly not happy and harness on wrong, set off security system and did not notify me--I found out from the alarm company first, brought in new walker without telling me, didn't tell me my dog was peeing on the floor and sofa out of fear when she was greeted by them--had to find out from roommates (thank god for them or I would never even know of these issues). I work in a stressful ICU and want to be sure my dog is safe and happy, but these walkers only added more stress.

I have never been one for sea cruising, but after a dinner at the Oceanaire, I may have to reconsider.

We went on an invitation to celebrate the start of halibut season and their last delivery of king crab. The chef David Wiederholt presented each course with great detail on the preparation and sourcing of dishes. 

All the dishes we enjoyed were delicious and we were presented with intelligent and well balanced flavors. My favorite - the king crab, which was procured from the Time Bandit, one of the ships featured of the "Deadliest Catch". Its the closest you can get to celebrity crabs with having to expose yourself to Tommy Lee. The slow roasted halibut with bacon wrapped filet mignon was so rich and delicious, I had to stop myself from licking my plate.  

The reto-1930s decor is well executed and feels organic without slipping into the theme park realm (Stephen Starr, I'm looking at you). Its the kind of place you can call "classy" without being sarcastic.
You'll feel right at home ordering old school cocktails like a Harvey Wallbanger, which I did.

Its definitely a special occasion place. Parents in town, big client meeting, proposing to your girl, Oceanaire would help to make it memorable. I'm sure I'll find some special occasion to get back there soon.

Best local dive bar ever !!! Perfect way to end every weekend night!! And there's jello
Shots.... Nuff said

Ate here for the first time yesterday. While the food is good and the decor is stylish, the atmosphere is loud and the food is just barely-inspired enough to warrant its price points. The highlight of our meal were the Brussels sprouts, while the shoestring fries left a lot to be desired, and there was a lot of them -- a massive pile. The Thai chicken lettuce wraps were okay, but short on chicken portions, and the French onion soup dumplings were just so-so. Dessert was rich, but interesting. Overall, I wouldn't spend the money to eat here regularly, but it's nice once in a while.

I had a craving for dim sum, so I dragged my boyfriend here last week. 

This place is pretty cozy, upon walking in I could already tell it's a bit Americanized. I sort of liked that though, in terms of service. 

The food was average at best, but good enough to satisfy my cravings. I haven't had dim sum in awhile so I forget where the legit spots are in Chinatown. I just remember the service being bad and the servers being rude at the authentic Chinese spots. I chose Nom Wah particularly because I knew I wouldn't have any issues with service. I'm so used to my mom ordering for the family. But finally, I could get my shrimp rice rolls without there being an issue with a language barrier! My boyfriend and I ordered almost the whole menu, I don't remember what they all were. I just remember that portion sizes were a little weird, but not a deal breaker. The inner alcoholic in me loved how they offered cocktails, so you already know that made some of my experience. 

Overall, this is an okay place. Would visit again, but I am still on the search to find a dim sum spot with impeccable service, food and atmosphere in Philly. Will this be like finding a pot of gold? Stay tuned to find out!!

Not a bad shop at all, very nice selection of of authentic as well as clone/replica mods. They carry most of the high end premium juices available. Do watch out though, they alway seem to run out of stock or have juices to sample that aren't available to purchase. Their prices are on the higher end of the board, some not too bad, others are just ridiculous. Very friendly and mostly knowledgable staff. Id like to not see food out in the lounge area from employee snacking and loud COD zombie sessions by the employees when I walk in.
Overall, very solid shop, this is my go to place when I am in Philly.

Still...fighting... mental block ... amazing restaurant. At 2nd & Girard?! 
I was here last night for the menu turista and I brought a friend that is absolutely not a city gal, and may well have been terrified in all her cute lil suburban glory, but we were not disappointed. 

This spot is a little cramped, but it was kind of nice because our neighboring diners were awesome! 

My food was incredible, starting with the seared scallop appt and the potoato gnocchi with ox meat and chocolate - wow! The entree was the least impressive course - I got the lamb and something in the marinade was burning my mouth.

Of course, we did get a free Sambuca at the end though, always appreciated! And the staff was amazingly attentive and kind. We went through our wine completely blissful from a great meal.

I think Simon is right - this spot is a great great find, but it isn't quite a 5* yet.

This place is a great little secret on the edge of Chinatown.  No atmosphere and ikea decor but the soup dumplings are out of this world.
All the food is prepared fresh to include the noodles.  Prices are low and quality is high.  If you had to pick one place in Chinatown to eat before you die this is it.

Closed? 30 years too late. Rude service. Dirty tables. Horrible food. The best thing about the place -- the customers. Inflated ego of owners who thought they were the proprietors of some 4 Star restaurant. Totally delusional. Glad it will be obliterated from the face of the earth.

Went on December 9th, a chilly night, but was not much warmer inside than outside. Had our coats zippered up the whole meal. The quality of the food was very nice, what a good home chef would do. Food is served "family style", what that means is one serving for two people. The place seems to be popular with younger couples, maybe this is their introduction to French regional cooking. We paid about $200 with tip and extras. That amount of money will buy you a wonderful meal at many other places. We were so disappointed after the wonderful reviews by Craig Laban and Michael Klein.

I wanted to love Roy Pitz for so many reasons but my first experience just fell flat. 

The space, which includes a large room filled with a long bar, picnic style tables and standard four-top tables is awesome. The menu of sours looks great and the prices seem so reasonable. 

We made a reservation which was a good idea since it was packed. (Reservations available on opentable.)

We started with the pretzel and the fried persimmons. The pretzel was my favorite part of the whole meal. It was soft, salty and tasted like warm butter. Throw in the beer cheese and I should have just ordered a second one for dinner.

The service was a struggle. It took forever to even order our first drink and then even longer for it to come. The beer themselves were not awesome. I am not a sour beer fan so I tried the non-sour beers including the Ole Jail Ale, Cherry Hound, Schwetziber and Lay Down, Stay down. Not one of them were particularly memorable. 

The burger I ordered which was touted as their most popular seller was overcooked per my request and tasted like something from the freezer section. (The onions and gouda on it were pretty delicious though)

Overall, it was fine. Maybe it was just a bad night or maybe the service and the food are always subpar. Either way, I would probably avoid trying it again when there are other breweries so close by.

Julian's nice place to go.... Foods good nice presentation clean and Julian  himself is such a great guy with a big  smile .... Like home.....fish and grits my favorite...Jewish apple cake .... Good strong. Coffee!!!!! ............ staff nice too look for Rick! A real plus for the neighborhood...............Marlene

I love Toms. Really nice people that own and run the place. The food is always great and the prices are awesome especially when everywhere else is raising prices. 
Never had bad food here.

We visited to the Philadelphia conference center and we had a lunch at this Yamitsuki Ramen place where is located nearby the conference center.
The ambience is cool and clean. I liked the Japanese Izakaya theme. The menu variety is wide!  It has all kinds -  Shio, Shoyu, Miso, Tonkotsu, Curry ramen. There are many topping to choose.  It's great that you can choose the size (Regular/Large).
The taste was decent and the price, the service were reasonable.

Ambience/location: Excellent
Food: Good
Price: Good
Service: Good
My go to Pizza joint in Fishtown. Really tasty Pizza. Wish they delivered but glad they sick with quality then quantity.

I feel like I have to start by saying how bad my allergies got while I was at Double Knot. I was perfectly fine before coming here, but BAM--30 minutes after arriving I had all the itchy eyes and runny nose that I could handle. Maybe pop a Claritin if you have bad allergies before stopping in. 

Now that that's out of the way, let me swoon about how cute this place is. It's in the basement of Sampan, so it's dark and decorated with murals of whales and edison bulbs (aka my favorite things). 

Highlights of our meal: we went for the tasting menu, which is 10 courses and $55/person. I loved the duck scrapple bao bun, the king oyster mushrooms, and the tempura cheese curds. I also had two of the cocktails, the gripping hitch and the granchester knot, with the first being my favorite. 

Lowlights: the skate wing tasted perfectly fine, but is bone-in so it's a bit annoying to eat. The cod-fried rice needed a bit more flavor but wasn't greasy at all like your south-philly hole in the hole fried rice. And the japanese sausage is like a fancy hot dog. It tasted fine, but isn't something I would order again.

I am a complete sucker for Brisket. With that said, I decided to try the Beef Brisket Noodle soup (to-go) which was $8.50 before tax.

I really like the to-go containers that they use; it keeps the food quality and makes it easy to eat.. because noodle soup to-go is usually a pain. The noodles, vegetables and beef brisket were in a bowl container, and the soup was separate. Just pour over, and you're ready to eat. 

However,the actual soup itself was a bit sweet and the hand drawn noodles honestly looked too perfect. It kind of tasted/feel like lo mein noodles, in soup. I was pretty disappointed since they advertise themselves as a Hand Drawn Noodle & Sushi restaurant. Maybe next time I'll try their sushi and bubble tea.

Quick shuttle trip to the airport. Mike the morning shuttle driver was amazing. He came aboard with newspapers and cookies! Great way to start the day at 4:40 am. 

Check in staff were great and the Honors reception on weekdays was a nice option to grab a quick snack before an early night.

Doesn't anyone work in this city? If not, and they have a New York Times, a MacBook, and lots of black clothing, they're at La Colombe in the morning.

Generally, the line goes out the door. But the service by the 3-day-stubble beautiful boys is spot on. They make the orders lickety-split. And their full tip jar is testimony to how they rock and roll. 

Really great part? Real creme in a china creamer. Long spoons. Granular brown sugar. And a saucer even if you're getting a cup to go.

Get the cappuccino. Their froth is the best in Philly.

I am in here many times during the week. Whether it's for a quick sandwich for lunch, coffee in the morning with a breakfast sandwich, or to pick up some quick ingredients for dinner. The staff is awesome, friendly, conversational and fun. The deli is fast, clean and makes great salads and sandwiches to order.  This is the only place near by to get a simple breakfast sandwich, fast and easy. This is my go to corner store and will be for as long as I live here.

There are some good and bad things about this place and the experience I had. I can be a little picky with some things, so my review will be different from the mostly positive reviews here. Also, there will be SPOILERS below. So stop reading now if you don't want to be spoiled. (If you want to know my overall impression, its more negative)

SPOILER SECTION BEGINNING and main review below
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
We were let into a room after being told some brief instructions: find clues, solve clues, find method out. We went in to a relatively empty office and tore through a lot of the clues: inside books, underneath shelves, inside drawers, etc. 

So the first complaint I have is with the clues and the office escape. Even though we were in an office setting, I felt a thematic disconnect with the clues and office setting. We basically had to find papers that had riddle like statements on them. For example, one paper said A, B, C, K and some other letters and said "They're good for you". The answer to this is Vitamins, which is a fine riddle. But I see little connection between this and the office setting. I would've preferred a clue more closely tied to the theme of the location. For example, if the location was a Newspaper office and the clue was "What's white, black, and red all over?" the answer could be a "newspaper" if you read red as "read". This is a tame example, but I feel the clues could've been more thematic with the setting. Most others may not care about this, but I'm a fairly avid gamer, and I love games where the theme, setting, and mechanics go along well, and having random puzzles in the room felt slightly more disconnected.

Another thing I disliked was the price for the quality of the room. It was $28 for basically two rooms in the basement. The office setting was very minimal, with a few locks and switches and such, but I didn't think it was worth the price tag. I think a fairer price would be $15-$20 even, but $30 per hour for a puzzle of this caliber in a relatively tame, boring office environment was not impressive, nor did I feel I got my money's worth.

Another issue I had was with the clues. Perhaps this is just bitterness on my part for failing to complete the room, but I felt the clues were a little luck based. If you found a red herring too early and wasted too much time, it wasn't good. There were also clues that could be taken multiple ways and clues that could get messed up very quickly. 

As an example of the luck based part, we analyzed the books and overanalyzed one book that had writing at the beginning whereas just about none of the others had writing - this writing was ultimately useless and unrelated and we wasted a fair bit of trying to detect a hidden message. As another example, the painting had a number on the back that we tested out on some locks - it was apparently unrelated to anything and just an arbitrary number put there. Some may argue that this is a red herring, but there were many other clues which were just as random that, when followed through upon, lead to other clues. So how would newbies in this setting know what to follow through on and what not to? I see this as either luck or just rushing through, which would still require some fortuitous timing on our parts.

As an example of clues being taken multiple ways, one clue had a failed delivery day and it was a 3 letter word. This could be taken as either the name of the delivery company, which was 3 letters or as the name of the day of the week it failed upon, which seemed to be emphasized on the sheet. It turned out to be the company. Another such clue was the direction, which could be taken as either West or Left, for the 4 letter clue slot.

Perhaps my last qualm was the location of some clues and how some of the instructions could be misinterpreted. Our cluemaster told us not to take anything apart and emphasized not to damage the property. Thus, when I was checking things such as the two chairs, I flipped them over, turned them on their side, and examined them fairly closely. But I didn't try something like removing the top of the chair for fear of breaking it - it later turned out to house one of the clues we needed. I suppose some may argue that this was just an oversight on my fault, and it probably was to some extent, but I think I would've found that clue if I was more willing to do a bit more damage to the room, which I would have been if not for that instruction not to damage the room. 
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
SPOILER SECTION over

The above is most of my qualms. Some who posted here complained about the waiting area. While this was small, I didn't mind as much as I didn't mind standing and talking with my friends there. There were also hand/dexterity puzzles, which I thought was a nice touch, even if the place was lower-budget.

Some good things: was an interesting experience (though I don't feel the desire to do it again given I've experienced it now), and it did feel a bit exciting at least initially.
New Wave is best neighborhood bar in Philly! It's a great place to grab a beer, watch a game and have a good meal with friends. They have a pool table too!

Initially I was going to purchase a Groupon for the facial however Donna said she would honor the price which she did. I've had much better facials elsewhere and will stick with my normal spa.  She was nice enough however it was less relaxing and more I'm just ready to go. I get facials often and have loved each one. I came out feeling refreshed and relaxed. In this case however, I was disappointed and glad my husband did not accompany me as he initially was. He would have been highly annoyed at the service and the chatter. I am serious about my Saturday Self Care regimen. I was left on the table for 15 minutes to "relax" no mini massage like I'm use to nothing just me laying there while Donna answered a phone call and trained someone. I was aware of the training however the session seemed more about teaching the young lady then about making sure a new client was satisfied. Unfortunately I can not recommend Donna and I wouldn't refer her to anyone I know.  In hindsight I think the service was indicative of the cost I paid. If I had paid full price instead of using the Groupon discount I think I would have received a better service. I got what I paid for unfortunately!
This is my go-to sushi spot and it never disappoints. The sushi is very fresh and you can't beat the price! The ladies here are very friendly too!

VEGAN FRIENDLY
just to start. they know what you are worried about if you are vegan. they can explain all issues clearly. just a dream come true.

UHHHHHHHHH THIS PLACE IS AMAZING

Meh. I've lost enthusiasm for writing this. But truly everything is good. I would step out of your comfort zone and order the la ping as an app.

I would try to order things not in the noodle section first, just so you can try some of the truly amazing Tibetan food.

This review is solely for the dance party aspect of silk city, and I feel so disappointed because silk city has been built up by many. I can certainly appreciate the diversity. There are young people and ... more 'experienced' people there dancing and having a trying to have a good time. The ratio wasn't so bad so long as you counted the 'experienced' folks. 

I'll bet you the DJ really hates playing Kesha. again and again. The music was so bad I made an irish exit.

Amazing! My family and I worked with Tara (6/30/18) and I found my dress. From the moment I stepped I to the shop I felt welcomed. Tara was soo down to earth, helpful and positive. I can not begin to thank her enough for the wonderful experience. They also have a wide variety of dresses for all price ranges.

I usually don't write reviews but I felt ever so compelled to do so after my experience here. Let me start by saying ,   I'm not a picky eater at all.my friend and  I ordered a basil omelette which we didnt like but had no intention on returning it because we both hate  to be "that difficult customer."As we were getting ready for the check ,the manager stopped by and asked if everything was ok. We both were hesitant. But finally spoke up and let her know the omelettes were less than desirable.  She explained she would REPLACE the omelette with something else. We agreed. Our waiter then returned with the bill and our bill didn't reflect  what the manager said it would. She decide we were to pay for the omelette because, and I quote "nothing was wrong with the omelette "!!!! Keep in mind we took one bite and decided we hated it.  The waiter took the omlettes back because we were getting a replacement.  Once we told her we sent it back she said "you could have taken it home and shared with your friends " I felt like I was in the damn twilight zone.  (Lol) like are you serious lady. You've clearly only been a manager for 2 hours !! Lol. Long story short after I explained to her what the meaning of replace meant she decided to change the bill.  The waiter brought our new bill out. STILL WRONG. All she did was take off 8 bucks. I was having a really good day and I wasn't in the mood to go back and forth with this lady so I payed for the food WE DIDNT EVEN EAT, and we left. Never to return again. I just feel like I was bullied and I don't feel any customer should feel that way leaving a restaurant. That manger need to really go back and take some courses on elevating the experience of the customer because that was a horrible experience. Definitely lost a few customers with that one .

I am among those customers who have persistent doubts about Govberg and its watch repair and restoration service. Relying only on its internet advertising, I sent a rare Rolex Submariner watch (circa 1958) for basic service and for an estimate on anything else that might need work or replacement. After a shot delay, I received an estimate that included the cost of restoring the dial. I knew then that his service was not for me. The dial, which is original and is in nearly perfect condition, would have been completely restored for something over $900. I refused all service and asked that the watch be returned to me just as Govberg received it, but I was in for an unpleasant surprise; i.e. I took my watch to a local, certified, expert and honest watchmaker who opened the watch in my presence and said that it did not have the original case back but had one that came from a newer Rolex model that would significantly diminish the value of my Rolex. 

I am certain, but cannot establish to he level of certainty, that Govberg jewelers either unwittingly switched the case back on my Rolex watch or know someone who did so purposefully. Although I filed a  complaint with he Pennsylvania attorney general and with PeopleClaim.com, nothing in my efforts and assorted complaints has generated any investigation from the relevant players in this expensive drama. 

I wrote that I was more than willing to take a polygraph examination to validate the truth of what I have said above, but I was never told whether the test was possible or whether PeopleClaim.com was willing to review the facts in this case. To this day, nothing has been done to ameliorate my misfortune, and so I have turned to Yelp to tell my story. 

My suggestion: never send anything of value out of state for restoration, service or appraisal

Ok. I'm so annoyed that it's Christmas Eve morning and I come out to my car to find yet ANOTHER flat tire!?!  There's a ton of new construction by my house, which must account for all the nails puncturing my tires...grrrrrgh. Anyhow, after AAA comes to give me air I drive down to Pepboys to have the tire checked and this time Devon helps me, completely having the wheels checked to make sure it wasn't the same previous flat tire that was fixed by Jim (my favorite mechanic!), which it wasn't. They checked for any other punctures, checked all my other tires, and quickly got me on my way again. Devon was a great point guy to help direct everything to be done timely and efficiency. Thanks Devon! Have a great holiday season...

It's unfortunate to give this place a bad review because they have nice products here but the mechanics are the laziest self-loathing pieces of shit to ever be called mechanics. These guys wanted to blow off the entire day letting my car in the garage when all I needed was for them to slap inspection stickers on them. They came up with an excuse that they saw a nail on my tire, I needed new wiper blades, and my strut needed repairs. I took my car somewhere else and got my safety sticker done and they didn't mention anything about repairs or fixes that were needed for my safety stickers. It took one hour at the other place and these guys in pep boys burned through the whole day with my car.

If you like wasting your time, this is the place for you! I wasted an hour and a half there.

I'm new to the area and was looking for a new nail salon and this one is a block away. I decided to give it a try tonight. I got there at 6pm. First of all, there is no such thing as a Reception booth. There is just someone asking you what you need at the other side of the shop. I just wanted a manicure, nothing special just polish no gel or any of that.
She said 20 minutes and I knew that it was going to be longer since there was some people waiting already.

45 MINUTES AFTER THE INITIAL 20, I had still not been serviced, and no one was even acknowledging that I was still waiting or keeping me updated.

I gave it another 15 minutes and still had not been serviced. Nothing said to me, not even a "pick your color, we'll be with your in a minute" NOTHING. They were just doing their friends and cousins nails, chatting, eating, texting.

I do my nails every other week + some waxing so they just lost a potentially regular client.

DO NOT WASTE YOUR TIME THERE.

PS: I created a Yelp account just to post this review. That tells you how pissed I am.

Went here based on several articles on food in Philly but was definitely disappointed. The Whoopie Pies at the other end of the market looked much better. These tasted like mass produced commercial products rather than something from a bakery.
This is my favorite salon in Philly. The prices are great, and their work is even better. Staff is super friendly, and I look forward to coming here every time I need a refill :)

Amazing massage! I bought a 60 minute Swedish massage on Groupon and called right away and was able to book an appointment for the same day. It was all a seamless process. Clean place, friendly staff. Mandy was my masseuse and she was excellent, I would definitely go back!

Got some steak rolls from these guys. Basically 6inch sandwich rolls. Good price and absolutely delicious. My roommate picked them up and told me he had no issues. Hopefully the place can take cards soon. 5 starts for a happy customer

I teach high school in Philadelphia, and have meant to write this review months ago, but it got delayed until my summer vacation to do list. I had two good experiences at this pep boys. the first was on xmas eve, 2012. mechanic eddie got my ford explorer up and running for me just in time so I could drive to my parents for the holiday. the second, was mike took care of my state inspection in april. both of them were very friendly and helpful...it is definitely my new pep boys. thank you.
Luke warm, mediocre hot chocolate.  I like the decor inside the cafe. Good atmosphere.

I'm glad I stumbled upon this place while walking around Fishtwon -- super cute rustic setting with string lights, perfect for a night out with girlfriends. This may or may not have been our 2nd dinner because it was too good to pass up.

Keeping it short, here's what I loved:
- Hot Italian Sausage was spicy and dense 
- Beef brisket was fatty and tender
- Mac and cheese was thick with crumbles on top

We got complimentary rolls, cut the meats up, and made sliders out of everything. It was all perfect and just how I wanted it, I'll be back!

Changing my review based solely on the food- the food is always so fresh and tastes so good whenever I come, but the service is where they fall short. 

I came in on a Sunday with a party of 7 and the line to wait was out the door- with us at the front waiting to be seated. We waited about 30-40 mins just to get a table. 

While you wait there isn't much space between the dine in and take out lines so you are constantly in someone's way. The building needs more organization when it comes to waiting- I know the area is small but there has to be a way around it other then being in a crowd in the way of people who are taking out. 

Maybe they need to expand the building or there needs to be another building on another location- because the amount of people waiting in line to eat was insane! 

Philly needs another Nifty Fifty location, who can we write to, to make this happen? anyway the food was great- by the time we sat down I was in full beast mode cause I was so hungry- so I ordered a royal burger- a cheese burger topped with a fresh from scratch onion ring. I also ordered 6 chicken nuggets but they didn't taste like anything I remember- they breading lacked flavor so I ended up not eating them and giving them to my niece and nephew. We also ordered cheese fries and they were smothered in cheese! Just the way we like them. 

Everyone was chomping on their meals, and my niece and nephew were verbally expressing with mmm sounds as they ate. We were stuffed by the time we finished but we all ordered milkshakes- I got the mint Oreo which is my favorite, someone ordered a strawberry one, a birthday cake one and a plain Oreo milkshake. 

There was nothing but compliments about the food and how good it was. 

The only down fall like my previous reviews is the service. Waiting in the lines to just sit down was terrible, and our waitress kept forgetting about us and took forever to get us refills or anything else we needed. But since the food tasted so good I'm eating it 4 stars and for the fact that a table of 7 all ate and were stuffed and had dessert for only $115 I think it was an awesome deal!

One of the slowest services ever, even despite it being empty. Food was mediocre and the shrimp and grits smelled of rotting seafood and ammonia. So many better options in this area, save your money and piece of mind and stay clear of this place.

The service is very horrible, I feel rushed every time I'm there..it's like the severs want you to chum down your food and not talk..I mean hello I came out here eating to talk to friends and family it's all about the gathering and BQQ. I have to snatch the menu back everyone the older waitress takes it back when I want to hold on to it to order more food. Overall the service is HORRIBLE.

Went yesterday with some friends for a late night bite. It had been years since I had been. Last time I went we tried as much as we could. As usual, I was not disappointed. I had the chicken parm and it was probably the best chicken parm sandwich I've ever had. Can't go there without having the fries and I also got a 4 way drink I think it's called (orange, pineapple, cranberry juice and a splash of lime soda). It was really refreshing. Everyone enjoyed their wings, shrimp, cheesesteaks and more. Definitely will return since it's so close to home!

I came here for restaurant week dinner on a Saturday night as a party of 3 and the place was buzzing! We had requested an outdoor table, outdoor seating is picnic tables and are limited. The indoor seating was spacious and had multiple options including couches and a bar area. Parking will have to be on surrounding streets. I would think on a busy night with something going on at the Met, finding a spot might be an issue.

For the restaurant week menu, they serve family style and you get your choice of 1 raw, 1 salad, 1 starter, 1 side and 2 entrees. They then portion it out for your party. This is a great value!

We got the shrimp ceviche for the raw course, there were 3 shrimp for our party of 3. We all agreed it needed the flavor to be punched up more.

We got the tomato watermelon feta salad and they brought out two heaping plates. It was a great balance of freshness, sweetness and saltiness. The portion size seemed in stark contrast to the shrimp course.

We got the corn agnolotti which had a cream sauce. They served two small pieces of pasta per person. This is where we started to wonder the logic behind the portion sizes, as we wished we had gotten more of the corn agnolotti and maybe less of the tomato salad. We were scraping the plate, definitely the highlight of the meal.

Entrees we chose the hanger steak and boar cavatelli. The portions were VERY GENEROUS. A large heavy plate for the steak and two heaping bowls of the pasta. The mushrooms with the hanger steak were great but we found the steak quite chewy (ordered medium rare). The pasta was perfectly bouncy, the boar meat was very tender and the sauce was simple but played well with everything else on our plates. I don't know if I would order the cavatelli as a stand-alone entree, though, as it didn't blow me away.

The broccolini that we chose for a side came out late so we were too full to eat it all but it was cooked well. Nothing too special. We tried ordering other sides but they all had possible nut contamination which didn't work with my allergy. Double check with your servers on this.

Overall, the vibes were great, service was cheerful and attentive. The menu showed a lot of promise in its variety and flavors but would like to see more consistency with the portion sizes. Some dishes did not stand out as much, but the corn agnolotti will definitely be something to come back for.

I came in with stomach issues that had been on and off for a few weeks. Told them I thought it was a parasite and that I'd recently been to Mexico. They said it probably wasn't, then told me they didn't know what was wrong with me. I kept feeling horrible and throwing up after I left. Finally I went to another urgent care center who confirmed that it was giardiasis (a parasite that i picked up in Mexico). 
I cannot believe how lazy and incompetent Vybe is. I even told them what I thought it was and they just ignored me. They said it wasn't possibly a parasite (clearly with 0 knowledge about how a parasite acts in the system). 
Vybe was a waste of money and I could have started getting better a lot earlier.
Great food, great service! What more could you ask for. Can't go wrong with the shawarma. Decent prices for being glatt kosher. Super clean!!!!! That's the cherry on top.
Authentic.....it helps that the owner hails from Italy.  Squid Ink pasta is superb.  Bring your favorite bottle of red and get ready to be blown away.

The gelato here is 3 stars. The fact you can study and drink wine here makes it 4 stars. The gelato was decent. I had the chocolate hazelnut, but I've had better (creamier) in the North End of Boston. I'm not sure how this place got best ice cream in the world by National Geographic. I would say there are a few places on my list that would rank higher if National Geographic tasted them.

I've been going to Kary's Salon for about 4+ years now. She and all her stylists are great and talented at what they do. I started the process of going blonde here and I always get compliments on my hair. Every person that asks me who does my hair, I ALWAYS refer them to Kary's Salon!! Their prices are also very reasonable compared to other salons in the city.

With my full set of highlights, I usually spend about 2 hours here, so I'm always seeing the cuts they do. These ladies actually listen to what you want and they do what you want. When I tell Kary I only want an inch off, she does exactly that. They are great at cutting men's hair as well. My eyebrows are always on point because of them! If I could give them more stars, I would!!

Great burgers!! I had a veggie burger done the diablo style and I was very happy. Their veggie patty is one of the best I've ever eaten, and I am very very excited about trying out the veggie burger in a few different styles. The veggie burger is made of vegetables and not soy or some other protein - I can't stand those. The patty was sizeable, but not greasy or overdone.

I have never been a fan of burgers that overwhelm - the humongous variety that get monotonous or boring to eat after a while. And one keeps eating because its sitting there in front of us and we're not concerned about overeating. At Chewy's, the burger size is perfect - it is neither overwhelmingly massive nor small by any standard. It is a good meal-sized burger. I am not sure I would need a side with it, but I'm going to try it a few times with and without sides and see. In all, it is a filling burger - very very good for a meal. 

Another thing about Chewy's burgers - they pay attention to the condiments. Not like some places that throw in a slice each of tomato, onion, cheese and a leaf of lettuce. The condiments suit the burger and go well with one another. The variety available here adds to the excitement of trying out new burgers!

And the tots? The best ever - perfectly fried, crispy and not overdone. I got a *large* amount for $2.

Both the guys here are very good - they are friendly, seem sincere and hardworking. Respect.

Can't wait to go back. 

PS: I have never written so much about the humble burger before - but Chewy's burgers are far from humble.

I was blown away on my first visit to this market, there is so much to see inside, tons of  eateries and shops, I had a very hard time choosing where to go, what to see and what to eat !! Foodie heaven !

Cute place and great service. I found the prices very reasonable for the quality of the food. My Miso Chilean Sea Bass was perfectly prepared and with a portion so generous I couldn't finish it all. When is the last time you couldn't finish a restaurant size portion of this delectable fish?! The beautifully presented complimentary dessert was a sweet touch.

We love vernick  but this is even better great ceviche great raw food great entree I cannot say enough positive things we had halibut ceviche tuna carpaccio endive salad  two amazing entrees
Everything's a 10
It is not cheap but worth it

With all the buzz around having to try a Philly Cheese steak with a side by comparison of Pat's and Geno's I really had much higher expectations.  

To be honest I've had better cheese steaks not in Philly so the only big difference which people who live in this area seem to really love is the wit wiz option which is basically that nacho cheese whiz filling which you can get at places like 7-Eleven on your nachos or by melting that Velveeta cheese.  Prepare to put on the calories though.

The one thing I really enjoyed about this place over Pat's was their bread, it's soft and has a nice chewiness that goes well with the cheese steak however I found their steak meat to be overly dry and not as flavorful as Pat's.

I wouldn't return here in the future, I've had my side by side comparison and satisfied my earning to see what the buzz was/is all about so my experience is complete.

Everything here is ala carte as well, you gotta buy your cheese steak apart from your fries.  It's all cash too so at the end of the day for a combo meal you're paying more than $20 for fast food which is ridiculous.

P.S. I also had the cheese fries which weren't that good, it's the same cheese whiz they slather in the cheese steak.

CASH ONLY.

Authentic, inexpensive Mexican BYOB in northern liberties.

Tacos, burritos, sopes, tortas. I really like that they offer cebollitas and nopales (grilled onions and cactus).

They also give you a complimentary basket of chips with a bean sauce on it. Salsa here are really good - verde and and smokey roja.

Top to bottom fabulous.

My two roommates and I came here for our seasonal fancy dinner and could not have chosen better. I'm a vegetarian and they both eat meat, so we thought we'd find something for everyone to love.

We split the baked brie (amazing) and they shared the my favorite appetzier--sashimi, I think, and they also called it divine. I got the featured salad, which was goat cheese, spinach, and strawberries and it was very refreshing. Entree wise, they split the lamb tangine, which looked super tender, and the filet mignon and that was great too apparently. I got the mediterranean roasted veggies and couscous, and it was awesome! Honestly, it's hard to find good vegetarian food that I can't re-create at home for far cheaper, but even the couscous was flavorful and the portion was huge! 

Desserts were delightful--the brownie was great, and so was the peach bread pudding (not usually my thing). The creme brulee was just okay. 

Service was great! Our water glasses were always full, they came out right away to open our wine for us, and the owner (I think) came by to check on us several times. Would DEFINITELY come back here.

Worst tailoring and clothing experience I've ever had. Only nice thing about my suit is the fabric I picked out. After multiple botched fittings I gave up and took suit to another tailor to fix it. Tailor commented how bad the suit was: cheap machine sewn blind stitching on the pant hems that left visible dotting on the cuff, arms weren't tapered and length was too long, there was a tear on the interior lining I didn't know about that he showed me and fixed. This place's whole schtick is offering you a drink when you walk in and having hipsters with hand tattoos teach the "bespoke" suit making process. These are cheap imported suits (which is why they're often delivered late) that they order overseas and get mass altered in a factory. Unless your body proportions require custom clothes, custom suits are a complete waste of time and money. Buy an H&M suit (or name brand if you want) and get a good tailor. You'll look better and waste less time. Also bought a shirt during same fitting. they completely screwed it up, obviously lost my order and someone else's measurements were delivered. They refused to offer refund, only store credit. Requiring me to go back for new measurements and waste another afternoon fixing their mistakes. Don't be shy to call your credit card company and dispute charges if item you purchased is not delivered. This stores business practices are despicable.

Such a cute little place! Dined here with a friend of mines and we had a great time. Our server was Ethan, he was nice, and made sure we had what we needed. We sat in the bar area because the seating was filled outside--I'd recommend making a reservation if you have the chance as this place is very small. We started out with the vegetarian chili nachos which were fantastic! Then we shared the catfish wrap and shrimp risotto. The wrap was FIRE!!! The shrimp risotto was good too, but a little bit in the heavier side of things so I couldn't finish it after having everything else. Topped the evening off with the caramel pecan peach pie which I'm almost sure I'll be having dreams about. I will definitely be coming back here again. Thanks Chestnut Grill!!

Great brewery! Beautiful space with a ton of character! Nice fire place, good beers, and at $5 per beer, great value! Nice, adult hang out with a cool Biergarten. Leave the kids at home and enjoy this cute corner of Philly!

The General Tso's/sesame chicken is undeniable.

Take it from a lifelong connosieur of these delicacies, youre not getting your chicken more juicy, more flavorful, more perfect than here at Mui Chung. 
The balance these fabulous people strike in their fried meats between crisp and sauced is the type of balance that puts Kerri Strug to shame.

I've tried others in the area, at times multiple times per day.. I regretted ever straying from my true love.

Ill never wrong you again Mui Chung.  Youre so pure.. I love you.

Amazing food! Amazing hosts! Amazing service! Amazing environment! What more could you ask for? The best staff with food that will remind you what jaw dropping food tastes like! Definitely coming back again and again!

This is a great space with plenty of seating... the outdoor patio is especially nice! The menu is good... their burgers and omelettes are better than the huevos rancheros. Service was a little slow because they were busy.

Very disappointed in this place.I used to like it there until the last time I went.My father is a veteran  and I told them every Monday they give their veterans 50 % off their ENTIRE check. He was thrilled to hear about that and we live about 40 minutes away but he wanted to go there and treat the whole family.Before we got to Maggie's  we checked on the website to see if they still had this offer and they did.There were no Exclusions  anywhere! When we got there I even double checked it with a waitress and Confirmed that was correct. We ordered are food and ate.The food was good but the waitress needed to improve on service. When the bill came they did not take 50 percent off our beverages and they weren't  alcholic drinks and they took only 25 percent off certain meals. I went up to the waitress and she said the items we ordered don't count as the 50 percent off. Meanwhile no one even said there was exclusions nor was it printed anywhere.I just think this was terrible advertisement and wrong what they did especially a veteran serving our country. I was disappointed  in this big time. When we left men were yelling at my mom saying some rude comments since she didn't close the door correctly and the staff did nothing about it. So I would not recommended  this place to anyone and have told multiple people  about our disappointing experience.

I hate writing negative reviews but after Sundays experience I felt I had to.  If you've seen Oceans 13 enough perhaps you know where I am going with this. My wife and I and our 1 year old strolled in Sunday around 11:30 looking forward to a quick brunch because ya know...one year olds....The hostess greets us, asks how many and if we have reservations.  We didn't make reservations because the last 4 times we went for brunch the place was empty and never had issues getting a table. Although we always felt we were met with opposition when we rolled in with a stroller and diaper bag we never thought beyond that as we always got a table.  So I say "no" and she proceeds to tell us "Oohh sorry, all the tables are booked for reservations.".  I peek my head around the corner to see 4 occupied tables and 3 people at the bar.The place easily sits 60-75 diners at any given moment. So you're telling me within the next 30-45 minutes every single table is going to be full with diners who have made reservations? Doubtful.  What made this even worse for us was that the couple who recommended Heritage to us stopped going due to a similar experience. I really hope we weren't turned away because of our baby. But thinking back to our previous experiences and our friends relating a similar story it now feels that way. We will not be back.

Cheap. At writing, it's 15 dollars per way to DC. You'll be sitting next to questionable individuals. The bus smells a little and the stops deliver a powerfully pungent smell of piss. When I start making real money I'm not using it, but not terrible for frugal people. The trouble is, you never are quite sure where its going, so make sure you ask, and the driver rarely speak english. Also show up 15 minutes early, and expect it to be 30 mins late.

** UPDATE**
I was messaged by the NTP letting me know the owner would be calling me. 
I received a phone call today from Sarah and I explained what occurred. She stated that I would be refunded and credited with a free wax.


It wasn't my first time getting a bikini wax but it was my First time getting waxed at TNP. I made the appointment for a bikini wax via text message which i thought was pretty cool. Once at the appointment, I filled out paperwork and signed in (clearly writing "bikini wax"). I didn't wait for more than 5-10 minutes when I was called back. The wax began with the hard blue wax. I assumed the waxer knew   what service she was providing until I realized she was giving me a Brazilian wax. Although the waxer was quick, this is not the service I wanted nor is it an aesthetic that I like. I am very disappointed and doubt I will return.

After reading the reviews, I was hoping that this would be such a positive experience. I was very disappointed in my services here, but there are some pros and cons I think you should consider before going. I saw Dr. Jillan Rowbotham for a gynecology visit.

I'll start with the cons....

Cons:
1. Bedside manner is lacking....greatly. 

Here is an example from during my exam: 
Me: Can I ask you a question?
Dr. Rowbotham: Is it relevant to your appointment today?

She was abrupt, curt, and I felt like when she was talking, she was on autopilot, saying things she has said a hundred times over. If you have questions, it feels like you are interrupting her, and she literally will resume where she left off, and continue the speech. Of COURSE I am going to ask you questions 'relevant' to my visit...I'm not here to know what you think of kittens.

2.  The front desk staff is very disorganized. Very. 

Here are two examples:

a. I wanted to do some work, and could see there is a wifi signal available at the center...but not one person knew what the password was for their clients to be able to use their wifi. They giggled and said that they had 'no idea.' Ugh.

b. When they went to take a picture of me for the 'client files,' their camera didn't work. It was embarassing for them. It just seems like they didn't have it together at all. They tried multiple times to get it to work...so either no one could figure it out, or it was broken...either way, not good. 

c. Sitting in the waiting area, I had to hear about all of their personal difficulties...which truthfully, isn't very professional.

3. The RWWC is very self-promotional, and it feels like it. 

Here is an example:

On questionnaires they ask you how you feel about aging, your skin, etc...clearly to be able to push their dermatological services. They ask you about how you feel about your weight, your body, etc...apparently to push their nutritional services.  It seems they ask you things that would be 'typical' female insecurities to make money from you later. So, while it may seem like it 'helps' women on the outside...it seems more to be engaging women on topics to make money from their insecurities. Thankfully, I don't get caught up in that. I work out, eat well, etc...

Here are the pros:

Pros: 

1. Dr. Rowbotham is clearly a knowledgeable doctor, and for this, I may return. She seemed to be able to identify any potential issues, and she is clearly competent at her job.In the end, I suppose that is all that matters.  

Maybe she was having an 'off' day when I saw her, and I hope so, because this really does have the potential to be a great place. I originally scheduled to see the doctor everyone was raving about - Dr. Saltzman, who was on maternity leave or something.

2. The facility is clean, you don't have to wait long, and it's warm. As any woman knows, warm is very important when getting a gynecological exam, since you are basically without clothing in a paper gown the entire time.

I want this to be a great place...and this is just my experience. The problems they have can be easily fixed.
The staff is fantastic the food is fantastic and the presentation is fantastic. It's an intimate space it's BYO. just make sure to book reservations and bring your own sake!

This is a great little BYOB in Bella Vista. The service was great and fast and the prices were very reasonable. The food was delicious. I had a steak burrito that was delicious. Everyone that I went with was very pleased with their meal. The green salsa and guacamole were amazing. The salsa was good too, but a little too spicy for me!

Last time I was in the Pure Fare restaurant, I mentioned to the cashier that I wished the place was closer to my office in University City. She let me know that they were opening a food truck, hopefully in September. I got the news last week, from the U City facebook feed, that they were indeed opening on Wednesday. 

I made it out here on Friday at 12 just before a bit of a rush of people. They serve the same salads and sandwiches, pre-made and ready to go. I liked that the salad dressing was on the side, because I was picking up two salads to share for dinner later that night.

The salad with zucchini "noodles", edamame, bell peppers and grape tomatoes was delightful. I think it was the salad dressing, a fresh tasting light lemony dressing, was what made it really special. The second salad was a bed of arugula with fresh beets, goat cheese and red onions. I had buyer's remorse at first when I brought that one home, because I usually can't hang with the peppery arugula. I was surprised that I loved this salad as well. Again, the dressing was what made the arugula bearable. The goat cheese was a little bland, but perhaps it is a lower fat version than what I'm used to.

I'd love to give them five stars, but I think they're still working out some food truck basics. If you're going to sell from a food truck, please charge an amount that is divisible by a quarter. My total bill came to $15.12 and I had to dig for change at the bottom of my purse! 

I will be back. Probably twice a week because it's a 5 minute walk away, hooray!

I have been a loyal patron of Kabobeesh for many years. I've tried many other similar establishments and all I can say - Kabobeesh is the best! They capture authentic Pakistani flavors in their kabobs and curries. The "no frills" atmosphere makes complete sense when you understand their target audience, South Asians - this is normal to us! My favorite items are beef seekh kabobs,  tandoori chicken, and haleem. Chicken samosas are awesome too! I also appreciate the generously sized portions that have been enough to be devoured as left overs!

I live about five minutes walking distance from this place and in three years have been inside only about a half dozen times, and I love Mexican food. 

The menu is hit or miss, with about half the items (burrito, tacos, and quesadillas included) being full on awful and the other half being fair to good (soups, the chop salad, the entrees without taco bell analogs) but unless you are eating alone chances are someone at the table will be disappointed. 

The service is always great, but that does not make up for the pricey menu and the often grimey interior. There are usually flies buzzing around the high ceilinged dining room, even in winter which can't be a good sign.

Slightly less unbearable during summer months when there is plenty of outdoor seating.

Another uninteresting overly rich Vetri jawn. Can't get why these places are getting the traffic they are getting. There is absolutely no attention to detail in terms of flavor combinations and if the food is going to be this soulless I don't want to spend this kind of money. Plenty of other places that will give me way more satisfaction for my hard earned dollar. 

I'll dwell on one thing: the salumi. Please tell me why these already pretty uninspired meat products need horrible sweet "jams" to go with them. Whatever little flavor they have (serious, again, completely uninspired--someone is just going through the motions) goes out the window in this hideous fashion that has everyone sticking jam (sorry, fruit preserve) with cheese, with meat, with whatever. It's OK if it's Manchego and quince paste, you know, a classic. But it gets really old really fast, and when you are getting an incredibly bland mortadella smothered in honey or a liver pate smothered in marmalade it's just gross (bet Vetri loves the 8% food cost on that plate, though). 
Anyway, as you may have noted, I really wasn't impressed with this meal...not for the price or for anything else. Vetri is now a machine producing the same kind of crap in every one of his places. It isn't inspired, it isn't good, and most importantly, it isn't fun. 
Why did I go? Someone else suggested it. Believe me, I'm done with Vetri if it's my choice.

Rather than take up your time by waxing redundant, let me refer you to the other five-star reviews below.  To those remarks I'll only add that, to my perhaps limited knowledge, Sassafras is like no other place in Old City, or Philadelphia for that matter.  Further, as a bi-city dude who spends half my time in NYC (and as a Yelper who shivers with pleasure at the prospect of writing a bad review of anything), I can safely say that this is one of the warmest, most interesting, most authentic, most amiably presented neighborhood bars I've encountered in either city, ever.  From the sharp, friendly bartenders to the beer selection to the lighting to the fireplace to the typically-appropriately-volumed music to the special musical guests on Tuesday and Wednesday nights, to the fantastic burgers, chicken santos sandwich, Mongolian dumplings and other well-executed basic dishes, to the gorgeous eclectic musical taste of the bartenders themselves--this place quietly, beautifully rocks.  And those Irish accents just cinch the deal (I know, I know, sorry, can't help it).  My friends and I are engaged in a conspiracy of patronage.  We want to keep this place around for a long, long time (though luckily, and not surprisingly, it seems to be doing just fine without our help).  Note that the ownership changed a couple of years ago, so earlier, less favorable reviews may not be reliable.  Just go, and if you love it (as you are likely to), go, and go again!  Sassafras, thanks for being Sassafras.

MILK TEA REVIEW ONLY:  FOUR STARS

I think I might have found my substitute for Zhong Gang after hours.  Mong Kok's and Tea Talk used to be bearable, but lately it has been too creamy and left me with a bad aftertaste.  

I've had the milk tea hot and cold here already, and both times were great because they were refreshing and smooth.  However, the milk tea here is $2-$3 each and is definitely more expensive than Zhong Gang's, but you gotta do what you gotta do when you can't get your Zhong Gang fix, you know?

Can't wait to try the small eats the next time we visit!

There's a reason that Tria was nominated for a James Beard award for wine service, and Mike McCaulley was nominated individually.  Simply put-they run a great program both at the Fermentation School and at their cafes.

I've taken 3 classes at the Fermentation School and have never been disappointed, although the Italian wine class that Mike taught was a standout.

Get on the e-mail list to be informed as soon as the classes are announced- popular classes will sell out in minutes, as there are clearly some devotees who attend multiple classes a month.

Also be aware that the seating is at tables of three-show up 15 minutes early to make sure that your group is seated together.

I'm sorry, but this place just wasn't that great to me.  The banchan was pretty aweful.  They also served a miso soup broth which sorta confused me.

I ordered the dol sot bibimbap as people seemed to like it on this site.  That was ok, but not as amazing as it was depicted.  Overall the menu wasn't that extensive which is understandable for a place with a small kitchen I guess.

Staff was really friendly though and service was pretty good.  I'd recommend this place if you are in dire need of korean, but if you don't mind waiting a little longer, I'd recommend Pastoral instead.

So a couple of weeks ago my roommate dragged me out to this bar to meet up with a few of our friends. I have to say I was totally thrown off because right when I stepped out of the cab there was this huge porn shop/theatre right in view. I thought we had the block all wrong, but no, right next door was this cute little bar, in the most bizarre location.

We get in the bar and it was definitely slow but we were like 4 meeting up with about 5 or 6 more so it made the bar look full. We sat down and were greeted by the bartender Megan (pretty rad chick), and the owner Aristotle ( who was also pretty rad)! The drinks were inexpensive, there was live music from the 2 d-jays, my friends and I had a great time. Megan took great care of us, even gave me a free shot before I left. Which is 2 thumbs up in my book. Liberty bar is definitely a cool place if you can look past what you have to walk by to get there.

It feels like it took forever for this place to open. Thank God it finally did! I met a friend here on a weekday night and was immediately impressed by the outdoor seating area. There are lots of hanging plants, and the tile flooring really takes your mind off if the fact that you're sitting yards off of Moyamensing. The bottle selection is extensive and reasonably priced. They even have an 8-tap system running. One temporary drawback was that their computerized register system wasn't up and running, but I was assured that should be finished soon. Once that happens, cash OR credit will be accepted. With Caffé Valentino right across the street, you can even order some food!
