The owners were very friendly and accommodating, even offering a glass to drink my bottled beer out of. As with many places that offer outdoor seating, this place is pet friendly. My only disappointment is that they didn't open earlier. The propane and electric heaters will only take us so far into the colder months to come.

I walked in with what would eventually be a party of 13 picky eaters. The staff got us seated and fed. Everyone enjoyed what they had.

Thank you, Prohibition Taproom, for great service all around. I hope the tip reflected that.

Apparently, the larger pizza chains close after a certain time, leaving you in the lurch if you have hunger pangs in the middle of the night. Who will come to the rescue? Tower Style Pizza, that's who: they continue to take orders and deliver pies until 4:30 AM[1].

We called in a request for a large pepperoni pizza that set us back about $18[2]. The deliveryman arrived about 20-minutes early (we were quoted a 30 to 45-minute wait, I think?), handing us piping-warm goodness.

Even under the influence, we could still tell the pizza was bad for human consumption - it's greasy[3] as all get-out - yet we still devoured it in no time flat. With the exception of a pleasantly crispy crust, Tower Style isn't quality pizza - not even close - but they provide a valuable service, pizza pies delivered to your door after a long night of partying.

[1] Maybe I misheard the deliveryman? Their web site states they will deliver all hours.
[2] Are my notes lying to me again? Doubt it was that pricey, as their menu shows a 14" single-topping pizza to be $8.25.
[3] Even as I type this, I still feel the grease slopping through my veins.
Great vegetarian and vegan restaurant with unique small plates so that you can try multiple. The pumpkin dish is amazing

Happened upon Bud & Marilyn's for lunch during a visit to Philly and feel like I struck gold. Great bar experience with amazing and friendly service and spectacular food. The Nashville hot chicken buns are enough meet this Nashvillian's standards! Don't sleep on the cauliflower falafel bowl, either! Definitely worth your time.

Very creamy, less tart than Pinkberry. Very delicious.
I think this is finally a product that defines 'frozen yogurt'. There is a very yogurty taste to the treat.

minus one star - expensive.

For starters I agree with Emily! I walked in today and I felt uncomfortable saying hello even as they looked at me let I was a bother for coming in. When I asked if they had more indoor plants the gave me a short cut off yes and then turned away. "no, can I help you or what are you looking g for"
When I looked at the prices...wow! A standard rubber indoor plant that you can get anywhere was 45 bucks! Even walgreens sells them for 11.99.

I think I am sticking with Lowes. Always good service and they love to help you.

Best Thai in philly. Hands down. My favorite is the tamarind duck. The app sampler is a great deal. Curry usually isn't my cup of tea. But theirs are all great. The Baan Thai salad is the only salad I'll order anywhere. The peanut dressing on it is amazing. The Tom yum soup is the only thing I'm not too keen on. It's kinda bland and too sweet
We love this place!  We eat there every time we're in Philadelphia. I like the mango chicken or the moo goo gai pan. My son loves the shrimp lo mein.

Absolutely excellent! Everything we ate was flavorful! The wonton had pork and scallions, the egg roll was hot, fresh hot sauce and duck sauce,the noodles were fresh.  I ordered the moo goo gai pan it was the best ive had in years. My husband ordered the general chicken it was tasty and the rice was good too!!!the service was superb! I wish I lived closer!
It's a Popeyes, so the food is awesome, but they tried to charge me $1.99 for a cup of water, and said that they don't have "courtesy" water.

Legit Indian food buffet. Family members are quite knowledgeable concerning indian food, and they love this place!  Definite food coma afterwards, but it is totally worth it. Have been going here for years, and the quality is the same. Only thing that has changed is they no longer serve ice cream with the buffet. A shame too considering the hard-core sweet tooth at the end of the meal.  Soooo good, I can swim in a bath of their chicken tiki masala sauce.
Sushi is great and the delivery is quick. Their chicken dishes leave a lot to be desired. Good local spot for delivery.

If all you 're going to serve is burger and dogs, you SHOULD at least be great at it.  There is a nice, reasonable beer selection, and the service was affable.  If you are going to a beer bar, this would probably be four stars.. Bet after reading a stellar hot dog review in the local paper, we decided to try the grub.  We arrived on a 100F Saturday afternoon at 12:30 or so, so the place had just opened and was dead empty.  They did not have ice tea, which my wide requested (hey, the recipe is pretty simple .. happy to share it..)  Wife ordered a chili dog (which was decent..  New England buttered roll, and a long skinny grilled wiener (like a less garclicky Hebrew National.  No natural casing  or anything... It was the New England roll that put this at a higher level..  I ordered a burger (the Bleue) and I guess it was my fault for not specifying medium rare, but the twin patties arrived pretty gray on a nondescript roll. most of the flavor came from the salty onion straws which crunchified the texture.  onion straws as a side were OK.  Crinkle cut fries were generic frozen, out-of-the bag store-brand variety.  The garlic butter gave them a LITTLE flavor, but they were not crisp, and surprisingly (since we waited about a half an hour for our order) served almost lukewarm (with a sprinkle of grated Parmesan that didn't even begin to melt.)   As this was a ten-dollar burger (many of the other ones climbing into the 14 buck range) I couldn't help consider what $11 gets you at Village Whiskey.
 
To sum, probably a great beer bar, where a dog is a nice bar snack. Would not make this a lunch destination.

You'd never find this place it you weren't looking for it.  It's one of those restaurants in a slightly off-the-beaten-path location that results in the clientele being 99% from the local neighborhood.  Glad I happened upon it.

Good pizza, good hoagies with sarcone's rolls.  Worth making a trip.

(if you're familiar with Brother's deli in East Falls, this place is right around the corner and the rolls are better here)

the pluses: decent mussels, a huuuuge selection of beers
the minuses:  crowded, crowded (even on a weekday).  spendy for what you get.  loud.  and the fries are short, which means i have to either: a) dip my entire hand into that yummy sauce, or b) use a fork.  i don't really like either choice.
i'd rather go to grace.

I'd ordered out from here a few times before I had the delight of going to the actual restaurant, so I was familiar with the fact that their FOOD IS AMAZING. The menu is expansive and delicious. A few favorites: the hongo relleno (portabello mushroom with crab stuffing!! and cheese! it's creamy and delicious!), nachos mixtos, the enchiladas, their guac, and the chocolate cake (GET THE CAKE). 

Tip #1: Bring tequila. Margarita mix is free. I went there recently and arrived before the rest of my friends so the server had mixed drinks and poured shots for everyone before they even stepped foot in the restaurant. 

It's a wild ride of a place. The food is so worth it, as is (in my opinion) the (high) chance you will end up eating your entire meal wearing sombreros. As other yelpers have mentioned, the server makes it his personal mission to make sure you are stuffed beyond comprehension with delicious food, and makes you feel at home (home?) in such a bizarre and wonderful way that I've never experienced in another restaurant. He's probably bored, honestly, and restaurant patrons are an easy form of amusement. I cannot recommend him highly enough. I'm not even one for really hands-on waiters usually, but he is a gem. Even though he can't speak Spanish, he knows the menu like the back of his hand and will guide you as you will undoubtedly be overwhelmed from the sheer magnitude of choices. Fortunately, everything is so good, you really can't go wrong. 

Tip #2: Be open to wearing sombreros. It might happen.

A cool place to choose your food item from different restaurants. It's an air conditioned mainly foodcourt type of a place to cool off during summer month during my visit in Philadelphia. I went to Kari's tea bar for some tea at Bourse marketplace. You can pick a place to sit and eat wherever u desire. It's fun to check out.

Very expensive but very overpriced. The portions are really small like the small is the size of a Dixie cup. Everything bagel flavor is delicious! Wish they had fro yo and fat free or low cal options. The only vegan options they have are with coconut milk which is very high cal. Maybe that's why the portions are so tiny!
Good food and service. A bit loud, with two large parties there for brunch, but doesn't detract from everything else.

This comic store is so spankin' new that I popped in to watch the paint dry and got a little high!
Seriously though, BNW is a fairly large - and much needed comic store in Olde City. The guy behind the counter was a darling and there's a big foyer in front with chairs so when Wednesday rolls around I bet some of you will not be able to wait and will be sitting out there with your new comics!

Love sweet green! My only qualm is the price, but in my opinion the salads are huge and filling, and the ingredients are local and of awesome quality so it's worth it!

My go to is the guacamole greens, but i sub in romaine lettuce. This line vinaigrette is to die for!!! I usually order online for pick up and it's ready right at the time i request it and very fresh! I'll be

Ordered from here twice, have had ribs, burgers chicken and everything has been really well cooked and so flavorful. Fast delivery, never any issue with my order, or placing my order, and the prices are very reasonable for the quality of food you are getting. Really delicious, definitely recommend if you enjoy ribs or a really flavorful cooked outdoors tasting burger. So good.

My friend, Jess, partner, Kate and I were going to head to a concert at the World Cafe Live but wanted to grab a quick bite to eat before going.  I decided to look for somewhere near the World Cafe Live since parking in that area is a nightmare and I figured it would be easiest to park in one spot for dinner and the show.  Based on positive reviews on yelp we decided to try out Old Nelson Food Company and man it was awesome!  They have a little deli, grocery items, and beer.  We got hoagies and a beer (which we were allowed to drink there although we had a hilarious adventure opening it because it had a cork and they had no bottle opener so we opened it with a pen and a lot of determination...)  I got a ham hoagie with cheese, peppers, honey mustard, and onions and it was truly delicious.  Every ingredient was exceptional from the hoagie roll to the meat to the cheese to the mustard.  It was the best hoagie I have had in a very long time!  And it was very reasonably priced--a whole 12 inch hoagie for about $6.  You couldn't get a deal like that even at Subway.  I can't wait to go back this place was just so good!

Service was exceptional, but that's about where it ends.

While not opened till 2008, the restaurant overall looks and feels as though it's stuck in 1985 or 1990 and is in desperate need of an update.

Typical steakhouse menu lacks "top notch" execution--my older daughter's chicken main course was extremely overcooked; tuna tartare was virtually flavorless, slightly off in color (sitting too long in the fridge pre assembled?) and saved only by the sriracha sauce, and chilean sea bass, while well prepared, was hardly worth the ultra-premium price tag. Other daughter's filet mignon tips were pronounced "ok" by her.

In short, there are better steakhouse options out there, and this location, at least on this particular visit, paled compared to their New York counterpart.

Finally! A BYOB restaurant in Northeast Philly with both the food and the atmosphere worthy enough that it could be in one of the cities culinary epicenters. Dont let the unassuming strip mall it resides in fool you!

A friend and I went a few hours into the grand opening for lunch. The place was already packed. We were greeted and ushered off right away to one of the last open tables by partial owner Joe Callahan. 

The decor is fantastic. Its comfortable, modern, warm and a bit rustic. This isnt a place that will hang its hat on decor alone though. No, no..the food here packs a wallop. With each category on the menu including at least one duck infused offering, I felt obliged to order a basket of the Duck Fries. Absolutely phenomenal. I mean, literally one of, if not the best french fry dish ive ever had. The shredded duck and gouda marry together so well here. I also ordered the Fried Chicken Sandwich. It was crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside. In other words, perfectly cooked. Head chef Kris Serviss is going to do big things! 

Our waiter Ernie did a great job and the owner Joe came by at least twice to check on us. For being just 2 hours into their grand opening, things were moving smoothly..surely a sign of good things to come. I cant wait to go back and try out the dinner and brunch menus!

OH HELL YEAH. THIS PLACE ROCKS! I'm not going to say anything that hasn't been said before. I will keep it short and sweet with this: Awesome food (super fresh, amazing choices), reasonable prices, and great service! What else can you ask for?? I ordered the huevos rancheros and my gf ordered the tofu scramble. The fries that came with hers were off the hook! There are so many great options on the menu, I will have to come back. Yes, a little cramped and long waits are the norm but when I tell you it is worth it...IT IS WORTH IT.

This restaurant is just the not the same.

We have been there a handful of times since it's reopening earlier this year.  The food just doesn't have the same exceptional flavors it once had.

Food is now very ordinary!

After dining at over 400 restaurants in philly over the last 10 years, we think we know what decent service and food look like. This place took advantage of it's customers. We went here for a holiday dinner. The food was fair, regular Italian. Service was okay. The specials were marked up insanely - $40 for a lobster ravioli, $17 more than any other pasta on the menu, which is something that the staff should have mentioned but didn't. Additionally, the wait staff added a 23% gratuity automatically for our party, which is beyond presumptuous and excessive. If you want to add 15-18%, that's fine, but to add 23% is totally nuts.  I'd have tipped a usual 20% and not thought anything of it. I questioned them on the tip and they said they automatically add tip, but it'd have been awkward to debate it.

Mmm...coffee. One of my five favorite things in the world. And Green Line Cafe is definitely one of the best cups of coffee in Philly. 

Their Americanos rock, the espresso is roasted just right: rich, dark, and smooth. I haven't tried any of their pastries, but they have an assortment of sweet treats, yogurts and parfaits, novelty sodas and sandwiches. Nothing calls attention to itself, its standard faire, but maybe that's only because I normally am only in the mood for coffee. The staff's okay, they seem to get along with everyone, but since I'm not here often, I feel out of the loop so conversation stays business casual.

The sitting area leaves something to be desired though. Don't come here if you've got more than just one or two buddies. The tables are small and a bit flimsy. You might have a coffee spillage if someone lifts their elbows off the table too quickly. The outside seats are a bit uncomfortable. They are also small and a bit cheap and ironically feels a bit claustrophobic. You might feel like you're surrounded by more people and their bikes and dogs, with even more passerby walking through. If it's summer, pop a squat across the street at Love Park and chill by the hill while you watch West Philadelphianites play soccer and frisbee and football. See if you can guess which hipster took a shower most recently this month, too. Nah, I'm just kidding, don't do such self-injustice.

One of those rare, honest mechanics who cares about their customers as well as their cars. I would just go to them now but if you need more convincing, read on below. 

I needed to get my bumper fixed and I didn't have time to go to my trusty mechanic all the way in the suburbs, so I took my car to A.O.K. (a recommendation by the guys on 11th street. I brought my car in early on Monday without an appointment. There was no hassle dropping it off AND they fixed my car (which happened to have additional damage inside the bumper and they fixed that as well) by the end of the same day. I didn't get my car until the weekend because of the insurance dragging their behinds, but A.O.K. was on time and on point with everything. My bumper was beautiful and back in shape. Wonderful job!

If I could give them an additional star, I would. Because when I got home, I noticed that the hub cap of one of my tires was missing. I called A.O.K. to ask if it might have fell off in their shop and they offered to order me one, on the house because they felt bad that my car was in their shop longer than it had to be. Great guys and I would absolutely go back there again! Its quite a relief to find an honest mechanic around South Philly.
Best meatballs I've ever had. There was a spice in them I could not identify. I need to know. I must know. Little Nonna's meatballs- I will dream about you until we meet again.

Sunday night buffet between 6-8pm. The best (well, only) vegan buffet of which I am aware. Everything is delicious and I want it constantly and it is sad because I am not there right now drowning in their food. I am hungry while writing this now. :(

People complain about the price at $15, but you literally can't eat this much vegan grub for cheaper. Assuming this much translates to the infinite-turned-later-dying-of-the-itis amounts that I can stomach :). 

Not even sure what to mention since I don't really know what I ate. I literally took multiples of every single dish and just devoured. So good. Licked plate clean. 

Oh, and this is KOSHER! That is a big deal, since Philly is limited to maybe three decent kosher restaurants. This is amazing. Must go back every day...well...Sunday!
I like five guys but my friend will not go there again. It was like $20 for 2 cheeseburgers and fries. The soda was extra also.

Their cream donuts are by far THE BEST EVER!!! I've tried cream donuts from numerous places and by far Holmesburg bakery has THE BEST!!! I used to think their buttercake was average, but they must have improved it because the last time I got it it was out of this world!!!!

Decided to head over to Continental for a night cap after another great meal at Village Whiskey. I ordered two drinks for my GF and I only to find out there was a little bit of leftover lipstick on her glass that was not hers. The bartender did the right thing and made us another drink, but it was rushed and lacked the TLC of the first one (no alcohol), which is a bit of a disappointment when you're paying $13 for a drink. Perhaps the most disturbing part of tonight's experience was the fact that the bar hadn't been wiped down since the Great Depression and was very sticky. Amateur hour was in full effect. I hear the food is great but the bar was not.
Great place. The Asian taco was fabulous and the service was excellent. The place maybe hard to find but worth the visit.

Delish, We called ahead for snapper and jerk chicken.  The flavor is amazing. Yes, the peas and rice are spicy, just the way we like it.   We loved everything and the prices are good.  We will definitely be back.

I'm underway on a bathroom renovation and realized that the type of vanity we want won't work with the sink plumbing coming out of the floor. I left Mike a message in the morning, he calls me an hour or two later, scheduled for later that day, spent 2-3 hours at my house, and voila! In one day of realizing an issue, my plumbing is now sticking out of the wall instead of out of the floor. His price was more than reasonable, and he even had to troubleshoot a bit since there was an unexpected steel plate in the way behind the wall. 

All of that warranted a positive review. Thanks, Mike!

Lobster roll is so good the bread is so soft and everything is delicious. The service is great and place is pretty big. It seems like a hidden spot when you're walking around in the city. Definitely recommend.

I had been here several years ago and was not impressed, but some recent great reviews on Yelp made me check it out again. What a waste of my precious dining out money. Being a vegan I always like to go to veg places but Govinda's didn't seem up with the modern vegan times. I personally was hoping for some Daiya cheese on my "chicken" cheese steak but instead I had their homemade cold unimpressive vegan soy cheese sauce (which they did not know the ingredients for, bummer for my wife who has food allergies). The service wasn't great with the counter person not really knowing what was what and getting my order slightly wrong. Ok so the cheesesteak was edible.. The fake chicken was fine, bread was ok, cheese sauce I wouldn't get again and I had to pick the peppers off (I had asked for the sandwich without). My wife got a burrito which she said was bad. They also had a weird selection of beverages: bottled juice or water. The only good things were the fries. They were really good! And there was plenty of seating. I won't be going back. There are lots of other vegetarian restaurants that are much better.

The atmosphere is nice, dark, and relaxed. 

Overall, the food wasn't the best. 

We ordered the following items: 

- Vegan buttered popcorn w/ cajun seasoning
- Fried green tomatoes
- Grilled chicken wings 
- Fried chicken poyboy comes w/ sweet potato fries
- Pork ribs w/ mashed potatoes & smoked cheddar fries 

The vegan popcorn and grilled chicken wings were delicious... I would go back just for these items! 

The fried chicken poboy was dry and lacked flavor. 

The pork ribs were ok... you can definitely get better ribs elsewhere.

Killer selection of Beer!  Excellent food too.  Bangers and Mash, Shepard's Pie and Fish and Chips were all top notch!  Excellent service as well.  Next trip to Philadelphia will include another stop to The Freehouse for sure.

Cute, cozy, great menu options (some items you generally do not see at other cafes, so that is a huge plus) and outstanding service. Short, sweet and to the point --- Great place to grab something delicious and relax or work!
Not good for delivery. Food was cold and soggy. I ordered the salmon bento and stir fry soft shell crab. Horrible. Threw most of it away. Now looking for something else for dinner.

Never thought I give any restaurant a 1star.  This is the worst restaurant I been to yet.  It started off with the staffing kicking out a young black male who plead I am still waiting for my mom.  Should of left at that moment, but I had to try the food.  The shrimp was sour and smelled horrible.  We told the hostess and she took it away quickly.  We were the second customer in the place, thinking everything would be fresh.  Now my wife and I feel sick to the stomach and walked out.

This place is quickly becoming my go to for more expensive sushi places in the city.  Raw used to be my go to- but either they have fallen off or I just like this joint better.  The rolls are good, the ambiance has a nice feel to it, even though its small and your basically touching elbows.  Go check it out if your around there, you won't regret it, its better than Raw and Crazy Sushi.

If you are looking for a good braiding salon, look no further. The reviews on this site are accurate. This salon has good and fast braiders. Every stylist did a good job in a timely fashion. Prices are reasonable.

My services and technicians were pretty great. They were trained and professional. However the time between my two services was not satisfactory. I found myself in their weird family room type waiting room for 30 minutes in a tiny chair against a wall looking at the hideous contemporary (?) decor. Never once was I asked if I would be interested in water or tea or whatever. I felt so utterly uncomfortable in that room, not spa-like at all. I originally thought when I was first being escorted to that room that it was a small sore spot of the spa and that I'd be escorted to a more spa-like relaxation room. Nope not the case at all. Haha. Idk maybe I'm a spa snob having had a lot of really good experiences but to me the name gives it an air of going to be a luxurious spa - it's not.
Good pie. Especially like the three for $14 on Friday nights. Would recommend. A little on the expensive side but it's nice for a treat every now and then

Fun BYOB with reasonable priced Sushi.  This place was the freshest Sushi I have ever had.  The Oyster Shooters and Chocolate Churro's are a must have!!  Suggest that you get reservations this place is quaint and small.

Went to monk's last night.    It was packed as always. We were told a 30-40 minute wait, and that we would wait at the bar area... well, they skipped over us even though they were right next to us and we never heard our name called.  Long story short, we got our table and a friendly server.  I forget his name. He was helpful with menu choices and gave us great recommendations. Had the frog legs, they were very good and tasted like chicken wings with buffalo sauce and a blue cheese dip. Entrees we had basic stuff, wife had the trappist burger and fries. Awesome. I had the mussels with the red chili oil. I forget the exact name. They were some of the best I've had.  Very good! Beer selection is killer and hard to choose. This is place is great, we go every year right before xmas. I love that it's not a chain restaurant!  Only downside is that the bathrooms are awful.  Think of the movie trainspotting! Regardless,  we will go back

I have had this mediocre, overpriced food before, but today I really wanted Indian food. They deliver, so I decided to order through their website.  

They call and tell me my card is not working. I ask him to do it again. I call my bank and nothing is wrong with my card, so they do not understand the issue.  He said he is getting a CVV2 error code, by bank rep says that he needs to enter the 3 digits on the back.  He proceeds to argue with me about his system and says it's my card, etc. This went on for 10 minutes, until I said FORGET IT.  Now I am starving, pissed off, and my voice is non-existent.   

I WILL NEVER EVER EAT HERE AGAIN. I WILL NEVER PATRONIZE THIS LOCATION OR ANY OTHER. You better believe that the next time I want Indian, I'm driving.

Ok, yes, this is apparently one of those Iron Chef spots...

But take that out of the equation and indulge in all that is tapas and sangria. The darkly lit ambiance compliments the wooden decor in only a way like the warmth greets your from the cold. Its a wonderful experience coupled with wonderful food and amazing service. The staff humored us for countless pictures and pitchers! 

Yes, pitcher after pitcher of sangria... amazing sangria that tasted like grape juice that so wonderfully creeped up on you till blissful intoxication. I think I can best describe my enthusiasm with a quote from the movie Beerfest that I shall apply some literary license: 

"I wish it were winter so we could freeze it into ice blocks and skate on it and melt it in the spring time and drink it!"

The tapas... from the garlic shrimp to the baby squid to the octopus and to the lobster paella... 

i have to stop now or i will risk drowning in my own drool...
;D

Over-priced garbage. Geno's are better, but not by much. They both suck.

Chink's Steaks on Torresdale Avenue is still the best cheese steak in Philly.

Came for Restaurant Week lunch with my lady friend after passing this well known Gayborhood favorite many times over the years. After passing through the curtain it was surprisingly more snug than I had expected but now it makes sense why a reservation is essentially mandatory here. Very crowded on a freezing mid afternoon Friday. Service wasn't mind blowing and we did have to ask to have our waters refilled but all things considered our server was as attentive as you could ask for given how busy this place stays. 

I agree with other reviewers that say this is an excellent place to share plates and indulge in as many options as possible. Straight out the gate, I was extremely impressed by our starters: Sheep's Milk Ricotta.. I found myself constantly munching on the crisp yet soft bread slices after I drenched them in the creamy seasoned ricotta sauce.. but this didn't even compare to the burst of flavor of the Vegetable Antipasta that didn't have the greatest presentation I've ever seen but more than made up for it in the taste department. Cleaned my plate up which was a perfect portion size by the way leaving room for the 2nd course: a beautiful pie of Maialino Pizza accompanied by oregano and peppers to spread at your discretion. My date especially enjoyed this delicacy topped with tasty pork, perfectly placed provolone and prosciutto shaped like a human heart (this is a compliment). Not overly cheesy with the perfect amount of oil, fold up the large slices and take a bite. My personal favorite though was the Campenelle Pasta, The combination of the squash, broccoli and another round of ricotta has my mouth watering while writing this review. 

I'm not a big dessert guy but my date tells me that the Tiramisu was superb and the Caramel Budino was overly salty. 

Overall Barbuzzo did not disappoint. I will come back here for the chill ambience and delicious food.

I took my family there again. Why?  Because I forget how bad it was the previous time.  My husband ordered a hot sandwich, which he says is safer.  I ordered the prime rib special- medium.  It looked like it was cut then grilled well done.  It had grill marks from a flat grill surface on both sides and not one sign of pink anywhere on the meat.  I would have sent it back, but the waitress said that is the way medium looks.  I gave this to another person on the table, since he ordered a very tasteless piece of brisket.  When asked how the brisket should be cooked, medium was ordered.  That, too, was very well done.  If you cover it with enough mushroom sauce, it might be edible.  Thank goodness, the salad bar was filling.  We took the meat home for the pets. The other entrees were fine.

The server was nice, but be prepared to ask for every drink refill.  This seems to prevalent with every server.

This place is OK as a late night or when everything else is closed.

I went here for Quizzo on a Sunday with some fellow yelpers.  I didn't get any food, but everyone seemed to enjoy their orders.  I just went with some Yards Pale Ale.  There's a decent amount of seating in the bar area, plus a dining room area, and some outside tables.  

The service was a little spotty, but once we pointed out to the waiter that we needed napkins and a bunch of water re-fills, he was apologetic, and much more attentive.  

The quizzo was pretty fun, a good variety of questions, and a cool final round.  There was just a little too much time between questions.  I get that they want you to buy food and drink, but most people get to quizzo early and have already eaten, and ordered at least one drink by the time it starts, so there's no need for it to be that slow.  I like that it starts at 8 though, although really it was more like 8:30, because I was home by 10.

Come to this place if you want a refreshing drink to washed down your food after a delicious meal from the restaurants around the south philly area. There's no powdery flavored drinks offered here like other bubble tea places. All their drinks are made with fresh fruits.  I personally like their avocado drink.

The staffs are polite and friendly.  The décor at this location is nice, clean, and bright. It's a nice place to chill to digest your food or meet up and hang out with friends.  There's also free Wi-Fi if you want to hangout and do some work.

Will definitely return to try out their other drinks on the menu.

Great addition to the area! Cute bagel shop serving up an eclectic offering of bagels, spreads, soups, and sandwiches. Tried the togarashi bagel with the recommended scallion lime cream cheese and the fennel seed sea salt bagel w/ bacon, egg, cheese and their aioli spread. The togarashi was definitely the standout & the lime in the cream cheese enhanced all the flavors. Will definitely return for a bakers dozen soon!

We heard a lot about this restaurant so we had to try it.  It was good but the crowded and small nature took a star away.  The food was good but average.  We got the meatballs and Gnocchi.  It's a long and narrow building so moving around is TIGHT.  I actually got hit on the head with a plate as a server was trying to reach the people next to us.  And with just one bathroom, there was a huge line.  With all the great restaurants in Philly to try, I doubt we will go back.

Went here for lunch yesterday to be surprised that they were running the restaurant week specials on a weekend. While the choices were very limited in scope, the food nevertheless high quality. 

I started off with home made mozzarella and bread, coupled with the free samples of balsamic vinegar nearby (Felt like white trash getting it).  It was quickly devoured.  Then, my sandwich came out next....

Bear with me when I say that I literally had the best chicken panini in the world.   I regret not seeing the ingredients, but I'm pretty sure there was some spicy aoli involved, goat cheese, roasted red peppers and chicken.   

For dessert I had a a butterscotch pudding with apple purée, cinnamon crumbs and a white chocolate wafer on top.   I swear these guys make every meal with love- even when they have to mass produce it during restaurant week.

Basically the best french toast we've ever head. The bacon egg and cheese baguette is also to die for. The draft latte is SO GOOD. I'm holding the 5th star hostage until La Colombe gets over it and offers wifi to its customers. We are parked here on our computers regardless... come on people. WIFI.

Everything else, on point.
Asked for the Deluxe Burger medium rare and got a medium-well burger with burnt toppings...the house salad was bland as well. Not impressed

Cold Hiyashi Ramen (regular with Cha su or Vegetarian) is a summertime treat.
I get mine with the sauce/dressing on the side.

Blissful carbo-loading.

I got there at 11:45 just before the lunch rush hit and got in and out with my take out bag.  Sweet!

Dr. Zhong
My friend and I were in your Race St location yesterday for acupuncture Just wanted to thank you so much for all your help. Not only is my shoulder and neck so much better. I feel less anxious and nervous about taking care of my mother 
Your advise was very warm and welcoming.  I really appreciate all the time you took to encourage me. 
We will both be back, hopefully soon - the acupuncture was amazing 
Again thank you so much for your encouragement and time
Regards
Linda C

I don't write many reviews but I couldn't be happier with Amber and her crew. The response time was lighting fast. For my place they sent out 3 people who all did a great job. Thanks so much!!! I now having a cleaning company in Philly.

Order from them a couple of times, but never again.  Always something wrong with delivery.  Either really late or cancel orders on you when they are busy after telling you that the food is on its way.  Just not worth it.

This place is a joke. The staff is completely unfriendly and unhelpful. It's taken me many small annoyances such as not getting what I ordered or waiting 15 minutes to finally write a review. I just placed an order on my mobile app and they claimed they never received an order and didn't offer to do anything to fix it like...I don't know...make the coffees I ordered and paid for? And I'm a neighbor and frequent  customer. Not anymore. Avoid.

This was a cute little brunch spot. We went around dinner time but they still served their signature breakfast dishes. The atmosphere was very cute, quiet and cozy. Our waitress was very friendly and patient, since we had a tough time choosing what to order. I would definitely recommend Sabrina's to people looking for a good quality meal and welcoming atmosphere.

Coach Dina and Coach Ashley are legit the two best kids coaches in the world. I had brought a friend's kids in a while back for a trial and Dina took the time to really put them at ease and teach them techniques in age appropriate ways. The youngest girl ended up becoming obsessed with jiu jitsu and went on to sign up at a local gym all because of that one glowing experience.  And Ashley is just a fun-loving person that all the kids instantly gravitate towards.

My own kids are still a bit young to train but I can't wait to get them in kids classes with these two amazing women.

For my 100th review, I wanted to go a little nuts.  The Whiskey king burger in my mind was all out.  $30 for a burger is extravagant...and better spent towards a steak in my opinion.

This wasn't my first encounter with Village Whiskey.  I had previously stopped by to dine on their lobster roll (during my lobster phase).  I was sad to see that it was taken off the menu because I really enjoyed the lemon aioli that came with it (a break from the 'traditional mayo/butter' lobster rolls).  

As with most Jose Garces restaurants, there was a long wait (an hour in my case) to be seated since VW does not take reservations.  I love their iPad station; they would take down your phone number and text you when your table was available.  Way better than those buzzers since you can walk more than like 10 ft away from the restaurant.  

The wait wasn't an hour and we were seated outside.  We ordered the deviled eggs and the short rib fries and the Whiskey King burger with a fried egg, med rare.

The deviled eggs were disappointing.  I've had and made better ones and for $3 for 3 half pieces..I can get a carton of eggs.  The short rib fries were pretty good though.  The cheddar sauce really made the dish.  The short ribs reminded me of pho...mmm pho.

Now for the Whiskey King...well it looked pretty darn amazing.  But it was a disappointment.  Good thing I decided to share half the burger (I probably could not finish it on my own).  Now I love bloody steaks but this patty was too rare for me.  Everything was just so mushy.  The blue cheese was unfortunately pretty much all I could taste.  $27.50 for this burger is pure injustice!

This further reiterates to me that Jose Garces is way overrated in this city.

I stopped by for a quick drink as I work in the neighborhood and I missed my train. The staff was very friendly and welcoming. My martini was perfectly prepared and the ambiance in the bar area was intimate and conducive to conversation. 
The bar staff were well supplied and very professional. I'll definitely make this a regular stop. I can't wait to enjoy a meal here!

My gall pal Meredith B is the known as the "Banh Mi Queen" at Rotisseur so naturally this has been on my list of places to try. Thankfully I found myself in the neighborhood on a hot day at lunch time and went for it...I was not disappointed. 

I got the Chicken Banh Mi and the chicken was well done. It was tasty and well cooked but I'd like to come back and try a chicken leg and thigh combo some time before judging it too much. What made the sandwich awesome for me was the perfectly cool and crunchy pickled cabbage. It added the perfect texture and bite to the sandwich that made me love it. 

My only complaint was that I wished the crispy skin was crispier. I love myself some crispy skin and it just wasn't what I was expected. 

That being said, I still totally enjoyed the sandwich and absolutely plan to come back. Thanks for the recommendation, Banh Mi Queen!

BF and I come here for the following reasons:

- GREAT food and GREAT beer selections (hard to find a neighborhood bar with food/drinks being equal). More on food later.

- Cool atmostphere- It is a dimly lit bar with plenty of seats at the bar and maybe 10 tables. It is not the best place to go if you are looking for a quite and bright atmostphere. It's a fairly small place so it can get crowded during dinner time.  Service is always friendly and attentive.

- LOCATION- right in the loft district where there aren't many bars in the vicinity and right by the orange SEPTA line if you are looking to meet up with friends. 

FOOD:
We've had everything from appetizers (you must try the Polenta Fries and Green Beans), Sandwiches (Pulled Pork is always hot and juicy and Pub Burgers), Salads (I like the Chicken Paillard) and the Crab/Shrimp Riscotto Cake. Prices are VERY reasonable..

DRINK:
With so many beer (on tap and bottles), liquor, and wine selections, there's something for everyone. They have a great happy hour (m-f 4-7?).

It's a neighborhood gem for a quick bite, after work drink or simply a night cap.

I don't think I have ever before in my life seriously used the word "swanky" as a describer for anything, but hey, that's Pennsylvania 6.

We arrived about ten minutes ahead of our requested reservation time last Saturday night, and were seated in one of the half-booths in the upstairs portion of the fancy-pretty restaurant. The tables are somewhat close together, but there was breathing room and no server-bumps, so it was fine.

Speaking of servers, ours was great. She stopped by often to check up, but not too often that it would've been annoying. I wasn't drinking that night, but the cocktails looked delicious (and allegedly were, as reported by my friend.)

We split the duck fat fries with lemon aioli -- try saying that five times fast -- and they were different but fantastic. Not one remained in that basket by the time we were done with them.

For my main entree, I chose the Lancaster chicken, which was reminiscent of a.kitchen's chicken dish, but better. It didn't look too big until I stuck my knife into it. It turned out to be filling, though I didn't bring leftovers home.

For dessert, my favorite and most savored portion of the meal, I had the bourbon vanilla gelato. They gave three little, incredibly rich scoops. It took me forever to finish the plate since it was so flavorful. Certainly not your "vanilla" vanilla!

PA6 is also conveniently located near PATCO and next to Go Popcorn (love), and that makes me happy. I'd make a return trip.
Overall I give this place a 4 star. Cool atmosphere and great beer selection. Food is decent, def recommend the pizza Margharetta

In these weird times we're in GR is doing right and best they can, adapting and whatnot. I don't these guys personally but what I can say is the two other options in a walking distance are a crap show- they have coffee now which is cool and cause PGW got me up at the butt crack of dawn I woke for a nice light breakfast- I wouldn't normally write a review but the homemade yogurt and simplistic peanut butter and jelly bagel with a coffee hit the spot, and also eat local be happy!

I got the kawan this Monday n it has the expired date dec 2018 when I open the packet it has fungus and it stinks extremely bad they have refused to return the money ..... I have filled a food safety complaint... on them I strongly recommend people to avoid this store .... and the way owner responded to my call is extremely unprofessional

This is an excellent neighborhood edition.  Much cooler and better as a bar than as a restaurant.  Exciting and ever-changing beer selection.  Their nachos are perfect (not too greasy and not too much) but I haven't really been able to find another item on their menu I love.  That said, I still really like this place.  Great for an after work dinner, apps, drinks....but let me warn you, place is loud.  Expect to be shouting.

I am still making up my mind about Amari's.  I have been there twice and have not yet had an outstanding meal, either good or bad, but I keep feeling that one must be coming.  When it comes, I hope it will be outstandingly good.

The first time through I got fish and grits, which I do not remember clearly, but they must not have been very unusual because I normally remember food.

The second time I got a chicken cheese steak.   This would have been excellent, except they put it on a spongy roll, not a chewy italian roll but more like a giant hot dog bun.  I really regret that bun, because it would have been such a nice cheese steak otherwise.  It's not hard to get a decent roll in this neighborhood so I don't know what went wrong.

The service has been friendly and prompt and the prices are reasonable.  

I think I'm going to give Amari's one more chance and then see how I feel about it; it might end up anywhere between 2 and 4 stars.
Had a strawberry bubble tea and the vegetarian bahn mi. Both were delicious and I would definitely go back!

Are you kidding me? The bagels i ordered were not good. This meal was the closest i've had to legitimate prison food, at least prison food is free. No, i spent $8 on a bagel with lox with maybe 2 fingernails worth of lox... and that was the best part of the sandwich. The bagel tasted like the underside of a brand new Payless shoe. My girlfriends bagel was also of the rubber consistency and the cheese on her sandwich was burnt black. The cream cheese was not terrible but did not save this bagel from utter catastrophe. If you enjoy Cos-playing as an animal and want to eat "human" dog food to complete the look and add legitimacy to the fetish, this is your place.

Split a godfather and cheesesteak with a friend. Godfather was good, but cheesesteak was great. Originally had some trouble reaching them on the phone to place a pickup order, but someone picked up on our second try and pickup was easy.

Sooo good.  Finger licking good!  I'm so glad they have a place for K.F.C around here and I don't have to go too far to get it.  Can't beat the combo of K.F.C and beer/soju.  Price is a bit high so 4 stars instead of 5.  If anything at least try it once.. you won't regret it.

Worst delivery of pizza I have ever had. Pizza was cold, not even luke warm. Also ordered the arancini balls and they were terrible. It tasted like a grocery store bought frozen version. Will not be ordering from them again.

Love to come here on dollar taco night! They're tacos are pretty good and so are their margaritas. Only thing is you have to get there early because it gets PACKED and it's not that big of a place. Would definitely recommend for some quality Mexican food!
Excellent! People are friendly and kind, always a pleasure. . Best cheesesteaks in Philadelphia!

Me and my friend bought a Groupon to use for some gel manicures here that we were saving to do together when we both had availability. When we called to make an appointment they told us 1.) They would only accept 1 Groupon per hour and 2.) They would only accept Groupons Mon-Thurs 11:30-2:30 pm. Neither of these "rules" were in the Fine Print section of our Groupon order. When my friend asked why they had these rules but they weren't stated, they answered: "Because Groupon doesn't let us say it in the fine print."  I get it that most small businesses aren't jazzed about Groupon users, but adding in non-existent fine print to make it hard to use the vouchers is straight up shady. At this point we're going to steer clear of this place indefinitely and work with Groupon to get refunds.
I could eat this food every day and never get tired of it. If everyone had access to their food on a regular basis I guarantee the world would be vegan. Spread the love! :)

The cashier was rude and pushy. He thinks he's funny. Acts like a really big deal. He may be the owner or something. He was in a lot of the photos. Food was OK but there are a lot of other good choices.

The Reading Terminal a MUST whether you're visiting Philadelphia or have lived here your entire life. You want Chinese? Boom! they have it. In the mood for a delicious roast pork sandwich? Well you're in luck considering Dinic's has one of the best in the country. Or maybe you want an Amish pretzel, a half rack of ribs and a delicious ice cream cone for desert. What do you know, its all here too. Jokes aside, this place has everything you could want to eat as well as a produce market and small knick knack shops. Make sure you come hungry because you'll leave satisifed and, believe me, you'll want to come back again and again to try everything it has to offer.

The Signer's Garden is located across the street from the Kimpton Monaco, where I stayed on a recent trip to Philadelphia. I didn't get to experience in its true beauty (I visited in winter) but I bet its lovely when its in bloom.

Its centerpiece is a bronze sculpture of the "righteous Signer" which was modeled after George Clymer, a Philadelphia merchant who signed both the Declaration of Independence and the U.S. Constitution. The statue is an ode to all of the signers of both document.

There's a few benches in the park and you'll see lots of floofers peeing in the grass. It's not huge, but worth a stroll through. I'm consistently impressed at how clean and manicured the NPS keeps Philadelphia green spaces.

Came for NYE dinner. The meal was okay. The grilled octopus, potatoes that come as an entree side and the bread and olive oil were probably the best dishes (we ordered the whole menu minus two pasta dishes). You could tell the pasta was homemade but even the $20 larger portions were small. We did feel very rushed through our meal though. We were told upon arrival that they needed our table at 730 and we needed to order everything at once before 6pm. The other odd thing is when they called us to confirm the reservation 5 days in advance, they wanted to know which entrees we would be ordering... overall it was okay but I won't be rushing back anytime soon.

Went to meet an old friend for lunch and was pleasantly surprised. The inside is quirky cool and the menu fresh and inspired. Had the chicken duck mousse which was a huge portion. Enough for a meal. Bread slices were yummy but way too thick for my taste. Couldn't finish. Also had salad which was abundant and tasty. Will come back to try dinner. Service was spot on.

I called Phoebe's BBQ and ordered the Family Meal Deal with beef ribs.  After about 5 minutes, I got a call from someone at Phoebe's saying that "the boss" said I am not allowed to get beef ribs with a Living Social deal and have to change my order to pork.  Nothing in the fine print on the website or on my voucher says this, so I asked to speak to "the boss" and was told he would call me back.  After at least 20 mins, I still hadn't received a call yet so I go to pick up the food and to my surprise NO FOOD WAS PREPARED.  I asked what happen because I never got a phone call and was told "the boss was supposed to call me."  I asked to speak to the boss now, and one person said he is upstairs and another person claimed he was not there.  Both employees refused to call "the boss" and I was left empty handed.  I am disgusted and would never recommend this place.

A really excellent CVS only a couple of blocks from me-so nice to have a reliable neighborhood spot like this so close by.

Honesty, the reason this place stands out is the exceptional staff. You are always greeted by a staff member at the door, always someone to ask you what you need when you're just creeping around the aisles looking lost, and always with a smile on their faces. It really shows a strong management, a happy staff, and a comittment to good service.

There are literally thousands of CVSs, Walgreens, RiteAids, whatever pharmacy/general store you choose-and several within just blocks of my house-so it's hard to stand out, but this location really does. I was initially pretty disappointed when I saw that this was what was coming to this spot on the corner of South and 22nd (I guess I was hoping for another gastropub, beer store, or sushi spot?) but I now see the beauty of having this reliably friendly spot nearby. Only docked one star because their hours need to be extended... we need a better 24 hour spot than the crappy 7-11 down the street!

I'm surprised by the low reviews.  I love this place.

It's the rather unusual (although not for this neighborhood) combination of Dim Sum with Vietnamese food.

Very user-unfriendly, typical for Dim Sum, but people familiar with it will see many of the classic items and a few less familiar ones.  This place also has a full menu of the usual (and many less-usual) Vietnamese offerings, which can be great because Dim Sum tends to be heavy on the starch and meat, and the Vietnamese items offer more fresh vegetables and can make the meal more balanced.

Price?  Astonishingly low.  I've had items of similar quality elsewhere for as much as $1 more per item, which when you are ordering a lot of small items, adds up very quickly.  My friend and I stuffed ourselves, got some pricier items like clams, and it was under $20 for the two of us...I imagine big groups could make out even better.  And BTW?  The clams were outstanding.

Really really good.  Best value for Dim Sum I've had in Philly yet, contends with the better places I've had in other cities.  I definitely want to come back, but I'm still trying out other places too.

The only downside?  The chaos of the parking lot in this plaza, with the tiny spaces and ubiquitous double parking (prepare to get parked in!) makes you feel like you're actually in China, although that would be a tad more chaotic.

We went there today for my birthday brunch and it was just perfect. We arrived at 930am sharp to be seated quickly. Our waiter was welcoming but not too cheerful, which is about right. Why would you want to be bombarded by that at 930 on a Sunday morning. The potato pancake benedict was so good that my dad basically inhaled it. I had the shakshuka which was the right balance of flavor with their in-house buttermilk biscuits. My daughter had the stuffed french toast which was a big dish for a 6 year old but we helped her finish it off. We will def be repeating this meal.

In Fishtown, so if your coming from outside the city be prepared to leave your car on a random street and walk a few blocks to get here.  Lots of old school arcade games but on a Friday night the place is so packed that there are lines for ever game.  

Since they didn't have the beer I wanted the bartender recommended a craft beer that was the most like what I was looking for which was pretty tasty....but the taste of the beer wasn't worth the crazy process of getting a drink, its annoyingly hard and so many people just cut right in and get service.  

I feel like if your looking for drinks and video games, just stick to Dave and Busters.  Yes...I realize I just said that.

This restaurant was alright. I wouldn't be too eager to go back. I thought the food was a bit overpriced for what it was. The nacho appetizer was actually really good, as were the drinks. But the entrees were a bit underwhelming and the service could have been quicker.

PROS: Tasty sandwiches, cool beer selection
CONS: High prices, small food portions

Overall, U-Bahn is a nice place to chill out with your buddies, if you are willing to spend. The food is solid, they have live performances from time to time, and it isn't terribly crowded.

Maybe I should have had fish or maybe they just aren't good with groups? There were about 20 of us. They served a very sad looking Caesar salad that was doused in dressing, and the gnocchi was overcooked, as was my filet mignon. The staff were clueless and inattentive.  Sorry, just telling it like it is. I definitely wouldn't suggest this restaurant for groups, but perhaps as single tables from the regular menu the experience is different.  It is also windowless and in the basement, so not suggested for beautiful spring days.

Terrible! The person named Marissa is very unprofessional, rude and will tell she will call then never does. She will set an appointment then disappear. Lacks professionalism and is inconsiderate and needs a lesson in HR. Stay away!

I've been to Vivi a time or two before in NYC, but the Philly one is the most recent - so it gets the review. We stopped by for a quick post-dinner treat before heading back to our hotel, and even though it was Thanksgiving night and Chinatown was pretty deserted, there were still a fair amount of people looking for bubble tea at night. Service was still pretty quick, though, and we only had to wait a few minutes before we had our sealed plastic cup full of goodness. That said, while the flavoring on the tea is pretty standard, the tapioca here is definitely on the subpar side - reasonable size, but way, way too much squish to each bite for my liking. I don't like very firm tapioca either, but too squishy is just a bad texture.

In all, Vivi is a solid shop, and they have a lot of different varieties. Compared to some of the other bubble tea chains I have been too, though, it's not really a cut above the rest.

I was so excited when I first read about Kennett opening up that I ended up walking by it almost on a daily basis to see the progress to opening.  I live so close I could check in there from home, but since opening I have only been three times.  I'm trying to be good to my liver.

The atmosphere is dark yet inviting, with TVs but music on quietly in the background.  I usualy choose to sit at the bar, and easily found a seat on a Friday night (not sure that'll last).

First off, the staff was so welcoming and friendly.  They introduced themselves, asked me questions, and we very informative about the plan for the restuarant. They want to be certified a green establishment so they were filling me in on all they are doing.  Being a beer guy, I was glad to see a beer engine, but slightly disappointed to hear it will exclusively pour Yards ESA. The beer selection is almost all local, which is nice, but I wonder of time if this will get old.  They will need to ensure they are getting some variety.  I'm not a wine drinker but am told the list is good, I tried one of the cocktails as well, and found it delishious.

Food wise, i'm still working my way through the menu, but have had the cheese and meat platter (good size portions, nice selection of both) and the parsnips and a burger.  All met expectations, which were pretty high to be honest, knowing the chef's background.  

This only get's four stars now, but betting it would be five if I had eaten more of the food.

The largest collection of Renoirs in the world--what more do you need? Over 60 Cezannes? Check. Over 50 Matisse's? Check. Picasso, Van Gogh, Monet? Check mate. 

Beautiful building, excellent staff offering assistance and a very good restaurant, too.

Wow. I was impressed the first time I went to The Bridge. My bf REALLY wanted to go see a particular movie on its opening night, and once I saw the tickets available online for this theatre we immediately bought some. Seat reservation included? HELLS YEAH! That is such an awesome feature, and it makes buying tickets early really worth the extra effort. That way, I don't have to show up mad early, possibly wait in a line, make a dash for the seats and have to submit myself to medieval torture by stretching over all the seats I need to reserve for my group. Only to be seated a wee bit too close to the screen for comfort.

When we got to The Bridge, I was a little surprised by how nice the exterior and interior are - and that they have a bar/restaurant there too! In addition to the typical movie snacks, of course - which they also serve from a cart inside each theatre. The seats are nice and comfy, nothing special though. There was a huge line at the ticket counter when we arrived, I'm amazed anyone just shows up instead of going online but the extra fee might be the issue. Of course the theatre only filled up in the five minutes right before the scheduled start time - the beauty of reserved seats - so there's no point in trying to score better seating until the movie has actually begun. 

I can't get over the reserved seats! I love it!

Ugggghhh!! Frosting was DISGUSTING!! Tasted like raw shortening without sugar. I see most bakeries are doing this which is a disappointment. I took one bite and and bounced it off the trashcan.Wasted our time an money and had to look at a man with a crusty handle bar mustache tell us he could microwave our ice cold cupcake to make it more palatable. Please learn how to use sugar!!
A great little Chinese bakery with all the usual favorites but the real delight is the Roast Pork Buns.

amazing pizza but it is a little pricey
Tried a lot on their menu and they are all delicious!
you wont go wrong with any of the choices

calling ahead and ordering pickup is awesome, no hassle waiting and amazing pizza with little wait.

It's also a great pizza museum!

They used to have gluten free beer for me but they don't carry anymore.   I was trying to contact the restaurant directly but couldn't find any contact info.  Please carry some gluten free beer for us celiacs.   It's hard to find a place that does and you were a great place that did.   Omission is not gluten free and still hurts celiacs.
Did a great job repairing my leaking roof. Responded quickly and gave me a fair price. Thank you!

Rarely do I write reviews... but this was just beyond disappointing. For a chain "surf & turf" restaurant that's sandwiched between a few other chains, "Capital Grille," "Ocean Prime," and "Del Frisco's," this comes solidly in last place. So what did I have? A center cut rib-eye (mind you, I ordered the bone-in). Average, at best. Salt and pepper might be satisfactory for a "TGIFriday's" diner, but... I expect some type of care and inventiveness with my steak. Mashed potatoes -- (these might have been the worst I've ever had). They took my plate back to add cheese (they only had parmesan) and brought it back with "melted" cheese. The issue here is that the cheese (and potatoes now) all taste burnt -- I suspect beause they torched the cheese, although they claim it was put under a heat lamp. I know what gas and torched food tastes like... and It doesn't taste like food under a heat lamp. The "side" of vegetables (which comes with the steak) -- *sigh* let's be clear, 4 non-descript vegetables (lacking in color and flavor) does not make a side. My dish isn't brighter by their being there. In fact, it's made even sadder (because they're all that weird boiled/steamed and salted color). I didn't know what to do with them, so I took a bite and left them. The sauteed mushrooms -- compared to everything else, weren't bad. But they aren't something you should pay $10 for when you can go to Reading Terminal, grab some crimini's and do the same thing yourself.  Underwhelming doesn't begin to describe what I feel. Lastly, the Oyster's Rockefellar -- 5/10. I've had better, particularly if you've had them on the coast or in the South. If you're contemplating a steak dinner... go a block up to Capital Grille or a few blocks over to Del Frisco's or Ocean Prime.

What can I say that isn't good about this place. The food, the waitstaff, the prices....all fantastic. I've gone multiple times and everytime it just gets better. I've brought many people here for their firsts and they assure me dekes has not seen the last of them.

I recommend the ribs with the xxx sauce, homemade corn bread ( please take into consideration that I do not like corn bread, that is until I had theirs), and the nilla pudding.

I have been to this club twice under different circumstances. The first time I went, only the first level was open. It was extremely crowded and dark and difficult to move around. However, the last time I went all three floors were open and it was GREAT. There was a $10 cover and $3 coat check on the first floor. My friend got extremely ill and the bouncers could have been extremely rude, but the male bouncers outside were helping and and trying to keep my friend safe. The music was fun, the third floor is 21+ only (the entire club is 18+) and the bar is on the 3rd floor. I thnk this used to be called club 27.

This is a great, cozy independent bookstore.  They have a great selection of suggested books towards the front the of store and a cozy window seat complete with coffee, I believe.  This is a good place to shop if you like to browse a bit, without being overwhelmed by titles.

Chipotle has awesome food, so if you're feeling Chipotle go grab some! Just not from here, the service is terrible. They are awfully rude, and every time I've gone so far they've messed up my order AND forgot to give me drinks that I paid for. 

After politely asking for my drinks they forgot (not even mentioning my messed up order) I was given a terrible attitude for no good reason. No matter what the situation is, customers shouldn't be treated this way. I won't be going to this location again.

I am not sure why i bother to order from Slice. Sometimes I get a great pie, other times, eh. 

We ordered a Americano Pizza (American regular pie with pepperoni) and it was the most blah tasting pizza I've had in awhile. What made the flavor so blah was that the sauce tasted as if they just placed a can of crushed tomato on top, a few shreds of mozza, then quite a bit of non-spicy, non-descriptive pepperoni that was also on the greasy side. 

Quite frankly, Marra's down the street does the same brick oven, thin crust with much more zest. 

We also ordered buffalo wings, and they were very dry, it looked like they used a dry rub and then sprinkled hot sauce over them. Thank goodness we asked for extra sauce- or not- because it seemed to be a cup of hot sauce, not wing sauce. I come from the holy Buffalo Wing ground of Upstate NY, and worked in a few pizzerias. I know wings. I know how they should be done. This is not the place to buy them. Thank goodness for Wegmans and my ability to have Anchor Bar wing sauce (the original!) on hand to at least give the dry wings excellent flavor.

With all that said, I do like some of their salads, especially in the bread bowls. Yes, as other reviewers point out, their salads are lacking, and I believe given the competition in the city, expensive. 

I will order from here again- but I will be selective, and now that I know they skimp on certain salad toppings, ask for extra of what I like.
they overcharged what is seen on the menu. For two scoops, it should be $8.95 + tax but my total was $10.75? i wish they gave more sauce as a lot of my rice had no sauce and the raw fish was warm?

I invited friends here for my fiancés 30th birthday. We got here early enough (thankfully) bc the place filled up very quickly. We sat outside on the picnic tables. I like their outdoor seating with the tall heating lamps. Made it very cozy. Played some ping pong there and we brought Cards Against Humanity along. Beer selection was great. The food was very mediocre. Blah. Boring. Tried a little of everything hoping to find that one "diamond in rough". Wouldn't eat there again. I would grab a beer and hang out there which looked like what most people were doing anyways. If you were never there before (like us) it was a little confusing that their were no servers. You have to get everything yourself which is fine

Ocean City is probably my favorite dim sum place in Chinatown. For the most part the food is hot and the service is fine - unlike Ocean Harbor where the service is TERRIBLE (they are outright rude) and Joy Tsin Lau where the food is often times cold. 

There's an upstairs, so if the bottom looks packed you can just make your way up to see if there are tables there.
Authentic Chineses food, very delicious! Clean restaurant and fair price. Very nice waitress good service! This is the top one during my 9 days' trip in New England area.
Really good pizza...and i would have given 6 stars but they are small... To feed a family with two teenagers its gonna cost you 50 bucks...

This place came highly recommended by a friend of friend. She said it was her favorite restaurant when she lived in Philly for a project. All I can say is, I don't get it. The place was packed, so clearly a lot of people love it, but we thought the food was totally average. And my friend told them it was my 40th bday and they brought a dessert and sang... then charged us for it. Lame. We wouldn't go back.

Seen and heard about this new place from reviews on local news paper. Walked past it a few time and been wanting to try it out. They specialize in Thai chicken and rice a dish to me that have a few versions. And heck can't go wrong with chicken and rice.  I had Hainanese Chicken rice before from Penang and Banana Leaf both a few blocks away. Both Restaurants is good but I prefer Penang. Both those restaurants chicken and rice dish are Malaysian style. Cily version is Thai style and it's known as a street food. 

Well today was the day I finally got the chance to try it. The place is not very big but very bright. Could fit probably 20 maybe more, maybe less if it wasn't for Covid. But now they only do take out and delivery. Took a few minutes to look over the menu. Not a lot of dishes but to me that's a good thing. That means they're focusing on their key dishes and it's freshness. Their whole menu is all Thai dish a lot of which are what Street vendors sell. Pad Thai, pad Seewel, and skewers. 

I got the Fried chicken over rice. Sounds simple nothing special right. Man was I wrong. Got home as fast I can because I didn't want the chicken to get soggy. Open the box and thank lord it was still hot. Took my first bite of the chicken and I was amazed. Not only was it still tender but I knew right away they didn't use day old oil. There was no funky old grease taste and the chicken was fried just right where it was still juicy. The rice wasn't no ordinary hainanese chicken rice. It that with a nice hint of ginger that elevated it to the next level. It was fluffy, fragrant, and tender. Their sweet chili have just the right amount of heat with a light sweetness from pineapple. Not sure but there is a fruitiness element to the sauce. 

This spot did not disappoint and it satisfied my craving for chicken rice. It also gave me a little taste of Thai street food. Definitely going back to try the rest of the menu.

I wish I could give her zero stars.  I've been going to Dr. Shangold since 2011. 

To start, her new patient/yearly forms (better now that they are online) are a pain to fill out. Her website isn't user/patient friendly.

Her receptionists are grumpy and provide poor customer service. The office itself is run down. All patient charting is paper. 

The phlebotomist/medical assistant is friendly.

Now on to Dr. Shangold - I learned early to always be scheduled as her first appointment, because she always runs over her time with her patients. While this should be comforting, it isn't, because during my appointments, she doesn't listen to what I want or how I feel, and repeatedly tries to put me on birth control even though I don't want it (I'm over the age of 30).  Due to my acne, she wanted to place me on a medication for my acne in addition to the birth control. Every time I go to her office she pushes medications on me. She also diagnosed me with a health problem and fails to be supportive or caring, and reviews her findings and plan of care too quickly to comprehend or take note. I have continued to return to her office because of my health issue, however I have reached the end of my ability to work with her office. I've found another provider.

When I have gone in for lab results, every flat surface in her office is stacked 6-12+inches high with patient charts. My chart is filled with yellow note pad paper charting. If a stack of patient charts were to fall - how would she tell which is mine?! 

Poor beside manner, poor customer service, poor office practices. Save yourself a headache and find a different provider.

Gotta keep it real Honest Toms is just not the same since it went vegan. There's no meat!

In all seriousness I enjoy vegan and vegetarian food and do think that the tacos and bowls have a fresh taste BUT that being said I do think that the level of tastiness (yumminess? Idk) has fallen since it went vegan. I do miss their meat options and think it did taste better before the switch. 

Regardless, I appreciate that they don't try to replace meatless with faux-meats but keep true to earthy flavors instead of trying to emulate a different taste. The chickpeas taste fresh as do the sweet potatoes.

Love the atmosphere and there is one girl who is always working when I'm there and she is so sweet and always gives me great recommendations. 
They also take Venmo and WeChat Pay

The food is great and the staff is cool. The fried chicken and the catfish with the Alabama white sauce is delicious. I'm not a big meat eater and they offer a hummus that is really good too. 

I was fortunate to perform at their grand opening which allowed me the free food but I would have happily paid for what I ate and I will be going back early to eat before my next gig there!

New place, great people, great food!
Really cool unique spot. Amazing gourmet dogs, nice bar, solid draft list, a pool table and baggo/corn hole for entertainment. Hope this place does well.
Best cheesesteak in Philadelphia. If you want a steak sandwich this is your spot. Always quality always fresh.
EXCELLENT food and even better service!  We had a wonderful family celebration dinner and being at Zorba's made it even more special.  We couldn't have picked a better place

As of right now I believe there are 334 reviews of Sabrina's.  It doesn't need another brunch review, it needs a DINNER review.

Enter, me.

I've been dying to eat dinner here ever since I knew it existed.  It's a BYOB, so our new "Built" 6 pack holder came in quite handy.  We had a few Harpoon summer beers to accompany our feast which consisted of Shrimp Scampi for the boo, and RIBS for the goo.

First, when you go for dinner...you don't have to wait outside for an hour or call ahead or give others around you the dirtiest looks if they dare take that bar stool you were eying.  Heads up, you can still order their brunch menu into the evening hours.  By the time we ate, at 7, brunch was over.  But if you're timely, you can avoid the crowds and still get a french toast.

My ribs were outstanding.  A rack of 4 baby back ribs sat on top of garlic mashed potatoes topped with a jicama and watermelon slaw.  The ribs were smothered in a blueberry, dr. pepper barbeque sauce which also accumulated around my mash.  Two slices of blackberry corn bread rested next to my ribs pile and wedged behind it were fresh veggies.  I couldn't have been more satisfied.  The bbq sauce was so original and the ribs weren't too fatty at all.  I went home with my mashed potatoes and corn bread because I couldn't fit it all.  They do dinner the same size as brunch.

Boo's shrimp scampi was plentiful of shrimp and had fresh spinach and tomatoes throughout.  We abide by the rule of not ordering the same thing, and I decided that Friday night I was going to be the man at the table.  

The dinner menu is much smaller than the brunch menu, but it has some choice specials that they also post on their site.  It's a little pricey but it's so worth it.

This is Omoi Zakka's second shop in Old City and it is just as perfect. I love that Select Shop has much more space to walk around and browse. A majority of the items here can be found at Omoi, so it's not so necessary to visit both stores in the same hour like I did. The carefully curated products are of highest quality and the prices reflect that. Items range from housewares to stationery, greeting cards, posters/framed prints, select clothing items, skin care, and jewelry/accessories. I do wish, however, that this location had a larger variety of locally made items from the area. I truly believe that is something that would set this store apart from Omoi. The staff is incredibly friendly and helpful, and that's what I love most about this shop. Oh, and follow them on instagram for the latest updates!

The Indian store has pretty much every Indian stuff that we are looking for. Compared to the international spices located on walnut street, this place is a bit cheap. But sometimes the attitude of the person at the cash counter irks. He doesn't posses a pleasant attitude which is kind of very bad.
Our FAVORITE cheese steaks in Philly, we usually go more than once during our trip. So delicious and reasonably priced! Just writing about it makes me want one..

This place has deteriorated so quickly .
When was initially opened as Flying Fish Crafthouse. It was so good. Creative, great menu with amazing staff
Then unfortunately seemed like for whatever reason the crowd wasn't coming.
Then after the rebranding it all went south.
Mediocre food. Went from spotless with cool vibe to kinda dirty and not well maintained 
So much promise. Just a shame.!!

I've been getting my hair cut here for 4 years or so. I come from DC to see Tony who always gives me a great cut. Sometimes i come in with a picture and other times I just tell him to go crazy and I'm always happy with what I come out with. They've done some renovations lately and the place looks great. I can't speak to color or other services but the place is always bustling.

The taste was good, the price was high, the portions were average. Nothing amazing, just good food at a high price. Service was adequate. Nothing stands out, it was just average. Got a medium  well steak that was closer to well and a medium that was closer to medium rare. They almost got it. To give them a benefit of the doubt it was packed on thanksgiving night. Sweet tea not an option.

Stay away!  They ruined my couch and I have been trying to get the insurance money to pay for the damage for over 8 months!  They are NOT honest or trustworthy.  Reported them to the BBB, have called, emailed etc and they refuse to make it right!  Their insurance covers .60 per lb, out couch is 191 lbs.  which is a grand total of $114.60 and yet they refuse to respond to emails or phone calls. Run! Ru very far away from this moving company, they make all of theses promises and claims of being professional and trustworthy but a They ARE NOT!  Don't put yourself through the pain of dealing with such a company!  SCAM artists is what their company name should be.  I sure wish I had these reviews to read before I used them in July 2014!  What a huge mistake!

They don't even deserve one star!
This place rocks!!!!  If you want a relaxed atmosphere and genuine hospitality from the entire staff, plus excellent food and beers than this place is for you.

A friend of mine and I discovered La Fourno one day when walking down South Street, trying to kill time while waiting for a table at another restaurant.  I was being picky about where to have our pre-dinner drink and after several blocks, I got annoyed with myself and decided we were going into the next place that had a bar.  Seconds later we stumbled upon La Fourno and their sign announcing "Bar Open". We stopped to read their sidewalk sign (mostly because their window sign was so helpful) and were greeted by their owner.
