We stopped in for a slice for my hungry 2 year old who ended up devouring an entire slice.  The service was great.  The waiter came over and sliced up the pizza for my son.  The price was excellent for 2 HUGE slices and a bottle of water and soda it was like $8. 

The pizza was fine.  Crust could have been thicker, could have been more cheese.  These are fairly nit-picky details.   But the price is right and we will come back!

I had always wanted to go to Ranstead Room and when I finally did it became my favorite bar without a doubt.  I sat at the bar (which is the best seat in the house) with every intention of experiencing as many different cocktails as possible without becoming my usual ultra sophisticated self.  haha.  

I made a note in the captain's log that the 'Pinky Fizz' was especially delicious.  It was the first time I saw bartenders as artists, different ice formats, raw egg and alcohol as a plus and a special glass for every drink.  All these pleasures were enjoyed in a room straight from all the coolest vampire stories ever written.  I mean that in the darkest, suavest, sexiest way.  

This place makes you feel like a rockstar.  

That is all

It's a cool place and I'll probably go back, but the service was pretty awful. We went on a Monday night, not crowded at all, and yet it seemed that they ran out of many things and the service was slow....... in the sense that our waitress was not very attentive; when we did get a chance to order the food did come out in a timely manner. The food was okay. Drinks were decent. The atmosphere is pretty awesome, and probably the only reason I'd go back.

Great atmosphere and amazing selection of beers, but ran out of seitan before 3PM. Seating is somewhat limited, but comfy. Loved the food and the shuffleboard table upstairs. Definitely would come back!

While the ambience of the place is nice and the location is convenient, particularly for guests of the Aloft Hotel, the drinks are subpar. I ordered the strawberry cocktail and when the bartender pulled out the box of strawberries to slice the fruit, I noticed that half the box contained moldy strawberries. This obviously put me off immediately. A friend ordered the margarita without the jalapenos, which she said was not of good quality. The only saving grace (at least in my mind) are the various board games and yard games available (life size jenga, connect 4, and a pool table)!

We were here for a Philadelphia Orchestra concert, and I have to say that the building is super impressive as you first walk in. It's absolutely beautiful from top to bottom, and I couldn't stop looking around everywhere. We didn't have a whole lot of time before having to head inside, so we soon headed up the stairs to our seats.

Verizon Hall is another beautiful room, shaped like a violin and with great acoustics. We could hear everything crystal clear even though we were at the very top tier, furthest from the orchestra. We could also see pretty clearly, so it didn't feel too removed.

One rough part was that it was very warm in Verizon Hall. It was nice to get out during intermission because it was so warm in there, and we bought a drink to get refreshed before going back in. Prices on drinks aren't cheap, but not too outrageous either.

I would definitely check out other concerts here!

I was really excited to try this place out after reading all of the great reviews. Most importantly- if you are looking for Mexican-this is not it, this is more Mexican influenced dishes. Not in any way a bad thing. What unfortunately was a bit of a let down is food itself, not something you would go back for. Ok to try it once, but not in any way something that would set this place apart.
Deke's may be the best ribs in the area. You can't go wrong with a rack of ribs and some of their cornbread. The brisket is on point too if that's more your thing.

Our new favorite spot in Manayunk! The food is delicious and fresh. It's great that they have sushi as well as great entrees. My favorites are the new leaf roll it is huge a delicious! The dry pepper chicken is amazing, the chicken is so crispy and never dry! We also love the fried rice and the lo mein. They also have delicious cocktails; lychee martini is delish! I highly recommend for take out or eat in.
Summer was amazing! One of the best servers we've ever had. The restaurant atmosphere is amazing too! Love love love!

Brunch was a frustrating experience.  Our server was very sweet, but the service and food was lacking.

Wait times were long at each stage of our meal.  And tables were being served out of order.  The portions were skimpy and meals were low quality.  

The coffee tasted burnt to me.  We also got the fruit bowl with yogurt.  It was missing one type of fruit and it came with only about two tablespoons of yogurt.

I'm no restaurant manager, but I feel like if they added a couple servers and bussers, they'd be better off.  It seemed like servers spent so much time refilling waters.  Maybe they can have bussers do it or put pitchers on each table.
So excited to have such a good Vietnamese restaurant in Rittenhouse/Grad Hospital. Went twice in a week and will definitely be going back again soon.

Previously tried to find on Saturday but it was a 30 minute wait. We left. Tried again on a Wednesday at 5 and there were plenty of open tables. Food was fresh. Service was excellent and the food came out quickly. I stopped by for a quick bit to eat before my appointment and was able to make my appointment with time to spare. I love the quesadilla which was vegetable. Very tasty. The house salad was fresh and a good size for 3.99. The chicken taco salad was good and my dinner date has leftovers. I will most definitely return for dinner or lunch.

Shanks was another stop in my continual quest for Philly cheesesteaks.  

Very slow and a bit chaotic; decent steak. We may have just hit an off day, so I'll say the steak was decent enough where it might be worth a shot, but this place isn't high on my cheesesteak list.

I'm writing this review while laying on my back on their treetop hammocks, staring into the tree canopy and blue sky above!
It's so peaceful and I feel like the air has extra oxygen from all the plants. :) you can definitely smell the flowers while walking around. 
Beautiful place to stroll around, enjoy being outside and see a huge variety of flowers, trees, and plants. 
It's very well cared for, with many paved walking paths, and many offshoot trails. Sculptures, flowery displays and informational signs everywhere you look.
There are a couple spots our two little kids (5 & 3) enjoyed: the treetop nets and giant birds nest, the log cabin, model train area, and the swan pond. 
There is a small gift shop. 
If you want to walk and see everything, you can prob do it in 3 hours.

This salon isn't very accommodating and the women have attitude problems. As I was leaving the stylist who did my hair decided to talk shit about me like I wouldn't notice it and made sure I knew her prices were going up. 

She wasn't friendly and any time I have called in past they don't have anyone available for services. So when I get annoyed they blame me

I won't be back for anything at
This place

I love the Obama's seat lol. Delicious food with huge huge portion. It's been around for a long time so definitely a place to go. I love their almost everything I had. Once you tried cakes here , you won't wanna go to other places :P
There was a little wait but we had coffee at the bar while we were waiting and it was really good and the breakfast itself was just so fresh and theor specials are a must try
Can't really ask for more in a shop- nice people, fast service, fair prices.  Have used them for my last two windshields and will continue to use this shop.

Unexceptional and average.  Our calamari appetizer was chewy and the sauce watery.  The tagliatelle was a bit gummy.  As is customary in virtually every restaurant, the specials were over-priced and did not contain any ingredients that were particularly special in a seasonal or cost sense.  We also overheard our waitress list the specials to a table next to us and noted she omitted one when telling us, a dish which one of our party would have enjoyed despite the price.  Finally, we had to ask the staff to turn down the music twice.  Frankly, I am tired of restaurants whose staff play music they prefer and at a volume that is unnecessarily loud.  Even were the food exceptional this self-centered behavior ruins an evening out, but when the food is average the noisy self-indulgence lingers.

Completely overrated!

Very mediocre food.  Wine was way over priced and small pour based on what they described.  Very disappointed.

We rented a car from here because it was the only place on Priceline that accepted my low bid.  This was the only place for miles where we could pay under $25 per day for the rental. Given that we didn't pay a ton, my standards weren't very high.  

We got to Hertz around 11 pm. There was one person ahead of us at the counter. We waited in line behind one person for 45 minutes before being helped.  We were finally given the car and then went to the lot to get it and the car was locked.  Not only was it locked, it was the wrong car. We had to walk back across the parking lot to get everything sorted out.  Such a painful process, especially after a long day of travel.  When we finally got the right car, it was downright filthy! But we were too tired to switch cars again so we just left.  

On the plus side, we did end up getting an upgrade for our trouble.

I've been going to Nikki for over a year- actually moved out of philly for a hot minute and couldn't wait to come back. She knows what you want and absolutely kills it! You may be undecided on what you want going in (men's hair), but you will leave very impressed GUARANTEED!

What a great experience here at Independence Prime. Roseanne, was outstanding with her attention to customer service and customer centricity!  That means a lot for travelers. 
Now about the food. Excellent selection of freshly made meals . I recommend it!

We (my husband & I) ended up here right before 11 on a Saturday night, altho we did call ahead to see if they'd seat us. I wouldnt recommend going that late, but they treated us well & I didnt feel rushed. The waiter was great with explaining the menu, suggesting # of plates, and we got a great Lebanese red wine recommendation from our waiter, who knew the wines well, however i tend to like to see the food menu before choosing (since i had never looked at their menu, ever). We shared a little, but since he's not veg he got 2 meat dishes (a burger kabob & salmon). I didnt eat those (but he liked the kabob better) so i'll talk about what I ate...
-Turkish hummus: a hot hummus with butter, SOO good... "Small" is a good portion size for 2. And next time I'll know it's really hot & won't touch the sizzling plate, or burn the top of my mouth. Served on an israeli newspaper.
- Crispy Haloumi: Basically fried cheese in fig sauce with pine nuts and mmmness. It's less tasty onces it cools off, but still good! This was plenty of crispy cheese for 1 person... im glad i was sharing.
- 4 Baby stuffed rice peppers with dill yogurt sauce: very tasty, very small peppers.
- Chopped mushroom in filo dough (you get 2). Good but nothing super special.
- Dessert! "The New School" (crispy & chocolate, i think icecream?) & Chocolate Laffa (warm chocolate delish).
Overall I think the food was good, I felt full after all that but we did have dessert too.  At the end of the night (and a $60 bottle of wine) it was ~$75 person.  Great place to try for a special night out.

HUGE line at the Phillies' game, but it was surprisingly efficient!  Crab fries were pretty good (pretty standard, crinkle fries).  The cheese sauce was the best part (consider getting two containers).  Wish the fries were bigger / wider to scoop more of the cheese sauce into my mouth. 

Make sure to grab the fries they are just putting out or you'll get cold fries like I did.  

185/365

The worst experience ever. At first everything seemed fine easy pick up & return...then I receive a phone call asking when I'd be returning the car. WTF! The car had been returned the day before. Unfortunately I used Hotwire & the rental was prepaid so Alamo failed to give a receipt because the account was already closed. I immediately left work & came straight to te rental car counter b/c I refused to be charged for a car! Now I'm still sitting here waiting on the administrative supervisor to find the car. NEVER, EVER, AGAIN will I rent from Alamo or their affiliates. It's way too many other places to rent from at PHL. In fact I rented from Hertz just 2 weeks ago without any problems. This is my FIRST and LAST time renting from Alamo. Now I see why the corporate account remains at Enterprise!
service was great from the moment I entered   Lobster Mac was good but not the best I ever had. The blacken snapper was awesome.

I'm having dental work done at this location. I've been there several times the school is very clean the students were very professional after the student who is a senior does the first examination then the doctor reviews. And the student does the work.

I came to the Sonesta Philadelphia Rittenhouse Square recently for a wedding. The rooms were clean and I had no issues checking in or out... which is the reason for two star rating versus the one star rating this place deserves based on the staff's attitudes.

We had a pre wedding family celebration in one of the suites. As a result, there was a heap of trash. One of my friends figured since we were already heading downstairs we could bring some of the trash down on the luggage cart versus littering the hotel hallway. One of the staff members, Raul, gave my friend an attitude and asked what he was supposed to do with the trash? Then he proceeded to hand my friend a trash bag to fill with the trash as he watched and snickered with another staff member. 

I was not present when this happened, but my friend came outside to tell the rest of us what just happened.... he felt very degraded. I went inside to talk to Raul and the other staff member (unfortunately she did not have on a nametag). I let them both know that my friend was just trying to help... they didn't have much to say in response.

I would not recommend Sonesta Philadelphia Rittenhouse Square and I would not come back here.

Wow! This place has scarred me for life! I can't enter a nail salon without feeling sick to my stomach!

I went to have acrylic tips filled in/replaced. The woman actually RIPPED the old tips off when the chemical dip failed to dissolve the adhesive!! Not only that, but she took her very rough nail file and proceeded to file down the surface (not the edge) of my nails!

I was very intimidated by her rough treatment, kind of in a paralyzed state of disbelief. I found it hard to just get up and leave. When I eventually did, she had drawn blood from several of my cuticles, and told me that if I can't handle the pain, I should not be getting my nails done.

It took a couple of days for the throbbing in my fingers to stop. Because she had filed the nails down almost to skin level, it took months for them to grow out.

DO NOT GO THERE!!!

This happened a few years ago. If Yelp was around, or if I'd thought of it, I'd have written this bad review sooner. (I was reminded by an invitation to visit a friend's salon. Even though it's a friend who I trust, I still will not go. This experience has turned me off for life.)

Barely approaching mediocrity in terms of quality and harsh on the wallet (relative to what you're paying for). Don't forget the obnoxious lights, large crowds and the fact you'll probably either be sweating or freezing your ass in line for something you're going to feel bad about. If you want the full miserable experience make sure to head on over after last call.

Philly Gainz has a great variety and always have new items available whenever I go in. I like to try new types of protein bars and they have a wide variety. Prices are lower than other stores and the people that work there are knowledgeable about the products and helpful!
Passed by Philly and recommended by a friend to try this restaurant.  Prices and food are average. Not as good as my friend said.

Some of their pastries are good but their service not so much. My first and last time coming here was with my friend on her birthday. We ordered a bunch of pastries and bought a box of candles they had at the counter. I then asked if they had a lighter or a match and they said no. So then I wanted to return the candles because the whole point was obviously to use it at the spot for my friend's birthday. However, their response was that they could not process the refund because they already charged it on my card. What kind of ridiculousness is that? The candles were like $2.50. They couldn't just give me back in cash? And her attitude was beyond obnoxious. I will never come here again. There are so many pastry shops in philly with excellent quality and service.
Good pizza, nice family restaurant. There has not been a pizza I haven't liked here. The only annoyance is that its cash only.

Reading all of these glowing reviews, this must have been an off night. This was my second time here, and after having a great experience once, I was longing to return. Unfortunately, I don't think I will be back. My soup was almost inedible with the amount of fat that was on the brisket. I had to pick it off with my fingers. The complimentary pickles tasted stale. The hummus was super salty, as was the bread. The other small plates we ordered were nothing to write home about, and both, again, were very salty. I don't think the food was worth the money. Our waiter had absolutely no personality. Both me and my collegue were confused and disappointed.

Ordered in by using UberEATS. The delivery was set up well because the map shows you the ETA just like the ordinary Uber. Food came and was amazing, just how Italians like it! Pizzas are personal size, light and fresh! Margarita was my favorite, along with the flatbread in oil. Can't wait to dine in on the roof deck soon!

Absolute worst customer service I've ever encountered. Clothes are great, employees are condescending, lazy and rude. I waited for 17 (!!) minutes waiting to purchase my item, while 4 bored employees stood around talking to each other. Apparently only one guy was a 'cashier' but he was running to the back getting something to another customer.

I first found this store (and their detailed, comprehensive website) when I was looking to get my nose pierced. I went there for the first time yesterday to get my tragus pierced. I had read reviews of the counter staff having an attitude so I was a bit nervous. When I entered I was greeted quickly greeted and warmly. Once I told them what I wanted, they sterilized the jewelry. This gave me enough time to eat my leftover salad (they suggest you eat no more than 4 hrs before hand, since otherwise your blood sugar might be low and you might faint). Then I was called back to the piercing room, which was a bit reminiscent of a doctor's office as some had said, with the piercing equipment on that thing with wheels that dentists use. 
Jay explained the whole process to me and answered my questions while he prepped the equipment. The piercing itself was pretty quick, did hurt a little when the jewelry was being put in, but I didn't hear a pop like some say. I did get a little faint after, so I stayed seated in the chair and Jay for me some Gatorade. We talked about aftercare until I finished my Gatorade and felt well enough to go rock my piercing in the world. All in all, very pleased. Hardest part of the piercing was picking out the jewelry and then figuring out how to get my piercing submerged for the salt soaks (fold whole ear into a shot glass of salt water).

I've been to Bru a handful of times and I really enjoy this bar.  The draft list is excellent and the food is delicious.  During my last trip a few days ago we tried the burger with a side of the spatzel and the Bru Fries.  Not the healthiest meal, but it was absolutely delicious.  I really love the burger here, I've had it twice and it stands out.  

The beer list is pretty robust, they feature a long list of craft labels to choose from.  The list has a lot of variety between beer styles, I'd be surprised if you couldn't find anything you'd like.  Bru also offers a long list of bottles and cans to choose from if you need more options. 

The only thing I don't like about Bru is how jam packed it gets at night.  This place essentially turns into a club around 9pm, they even remove the chairs and tables to accommodate the crowd.  I don't want to fault Bru too much for this, it's their business model and I assume it's working if they continue doing it.  That said, Bru is so chill when it's just a regular bar, I wish it stayed that way throughout the night.

When I searched gluten free restaurants that are "good for kids" in yelp, this came up. Please know there is only one item on the entire menu that is gluten free and it is pork. So if you don't eat pork, there is literally nothing on thus menu that you can eat. They also don't care that this is the case. That's fine. Just don't say that your restaurant is gluten free if it's not. Also, the kids menu is meh and they do nothing at all to accommodate kids. So again, that's fine. Just don't say you are "good for kids" when you're not. If you look at my reviews they are almost all 4 or 5 star reviews. I don't like to give a review if I can't give a good one. However, our service was not great & the food was also not what we expected. So, I recommend changing your status to say "not gluten free" and "not Good for kids."
They have so many different options for food. They have the best crepe place and donut shop!!

I will preface this by saying 'NEVER AGAIN'. I listened to people rave about this place and show me food pictures for TWO years. Or however long they've been open. I really thought I was missing out. So finally I decided to go check them out since I didn't want fast food for dinner. 

I ordered the salt and pepper wings and seafood fried rice. I figured you can't go wrong with that. In the words of Charlie Murphy, wrong......WRONG!!!! Chicken wings are my favorite food hands down, so I kind of consider myself a wing connoisseur. First of all, never in my life have I seen chicken as small as these 'wings'. They look like they came from Cornish hens. I've seen mice with bigger arms than these wings. Have you ever had a Vienna sausage? Those are bigger than the wings at this place. I'm not lying. If you don't believe me, go place an order. They shouldn't even be allowed to put a price tag on these wings. $10? $10!!? One bite and done!! But I digress. Let's talk about the flavor. Fail!!! If you tell me your food is 'Thai salt and pepper' wings, that's what I expect. Not greasy wings covered in red peppers and onions with little flavor. So if you go, order the wings with a lot of caution. They were not remotely worth $10. 

As for the rice, it was just ok. I thought it was slightly overpriced at $16 for six medium shrimp, eight mini scallops and a little canned crabmeat. Maybe I would have felt better at $12. Either way, the taste wasn't good enough to order again. 

As for the restaurant itself, it is small and the layout is weird. I wouldn't want to go there to dine in. It looks like someone's dining room was converted into a restaurant. It's tight, and the tables are very close. You could hear the details of everyone's conversations as if you all came together. The staff were kind of nice. No, they did their job. Nice is a stretch. In all, my Star Fusion experience just wasn't what I expected. But as long as others (with lower standards for food) like it, that's all that matters. I won't be going back.

I was curious to try Mood Café, but had no idea where to begin. The owner quickly stepped in and provided the best customer service by explaining the different types of chaat and combining my food preferences into an amazing dish. There is truly something for everyone as each dish can be a custom order and the owner excels at cooking for everyone's palate. I had a refreshing vegan mango lassi and my friend ordered the mint one -with fresh mint picked from his sidewalk garden. Mood Café is a welcome addition to the Baltimore Avenue shops and I can't wait to see what mood I'm in/food I get next time I'm there.

Oh lawd!  I just ate the most horrifc lunch at E & C.  A meatloaf sandwich - wherein the meatloaf contained multiple bone fragments.

Sooooooo disgusting,

Fortunately they took it off the bill.

I used to tolerate E & C - I will now NEVER go back on my own accord.  (Alas, some co-workers insist on dragging me here.)

BLECH!

I heard about how good the cheesesteaks were, so I decided to try one. The steak was tender and seasoned well. I had fried onions and hot peppers. The best. Can't wait to get back there. Wait time was short.

Edit: I went back the next day and arrived at 10am when they opened and was taken care of and out of there by 11:30.  So that's my takeaway: Get there at 10am when they open or forget about it.

Imagine driving 30 minutes to arrive at 10:38am, they open at 10am and them telling you it is a 90min-2hr wait and 2hrs 10 min have gone by and there are still 8 people in front of you which is at least another hour making it a 3hr wait and now you have to leave and drive 30min home to drop off your dog after never even getting the service you needed because they should have told you it was a 3hr wait to begin with and you have another appointment at 2pm.  And they were all: We'll just take you off the list.  They won't make an appointment for you it's walk in only but they just wasted your whole morning and afternoon and didn't even get the shots your dog needed you came there for after driving 30min each way and waiting 2hrs and 10min! People have lives and jobs and if your employees had told me from the beginning it was going to be a 3hr wait I would have just left and went about my day!  You should also take appointments like every other place does instead of doing this to people! Unreal.

We went Tuesday for Opening Night.  Our meal was uniquely delicious, and we found the service to be wonderfully pleasant.  We were actually thanked multiple times for coming in, which i found to be very charming.  Laurel will certainly be a great addition to the Philadelphia Restaurant scene.  We will certainly be back.  The gnocchi is out of this world!

Pretty standard Chinese menu in Cantonese style. Servers are friendly and efficient. They take your order quickly and food comes fast. Their lunch specials are huge and cheap. They have one of the better shrimp rolls I have had. Their wonton and sweet and sour soups are solid. Sweet and sour shrimp is a little greasy and I did not like the overly sweet sauce. Not bad not great portions are large prices are cheap.

I had no idea what to expect, but I was definitely pleasantly surprised. I had no idea that it was associated with Sampan before seeing it in person. The bar is a small alcove behind the restaurant so you're outside, but you're sheltered. They also have heaters it seemed over the weekend. It's loud because there's concrete everywhere, but it wasn't overwhelming. The air smelled delicious because of everything good coming from Sampan. Drinks were standard, and the crowd was fun!

Wasn't sure what to expect because of the name of this place.  But it's a totally awesome bakery/coffee shop/candy store.  Service is excellent, the coffee is amazing and so is the treats.  Plus, it's a nice place to hang out and kill some time.  I love this place!

This place was so so so cute !!!! The workers were also so cute and kind ! I got a strawberry banana smoothie and double chocolate muffin and they were soooo good. The atmosphere was great and the second floor had a good view of the street. Definitely will be visiting again !

The Lemongrass Tofu hoagies here are really as close to perfection as a you can get. Everytime I unwrap one, I feel like it should be in a Banh Mi photo shoot - they're perfect. The Banh Mi Bunnies - mmmm. 

Ok, too far. Anyway, the rolls have just the right amount of crunch & chewiness. Tofu is lemony and delicious, with just the right amount of spice and the texture from the cucumber & carrots makes the whole thing taste fresh. In all - delicious.

Just writing this review, I am excited to go back here. Candidly, I felt like I just had an all-around okay experience. But since I have been there, so many friends have come to me and said how much they love it, so I am dying to give it another chance.

The food was good, no complaints, but my meatballs just weren't thaaaaat impressive. The eggplant parm was solid though. 

I loved the restaurant though - the aesthetic was cute and they have a quaint outdoor patio which is a nice breathe of fresh air being located in the think of the city.

Do NOT bring your car here for service!  I have had my car in for the transmission several times and I know all about the problems with Ford transmissions.  They do NOT care and will do NOTHING to help. I have to beg the operator to try and get someone on the phone and it takes hours and even days for someone to call back.  I have never had such a frustrating experience.  My family has bought multiple cars there and I will never step foot back in that place again.  WORST customer service!

Fresco tortilla is an amazing place the workers are friendly and always remember a customers face and are very fast, the food is amazing and very inexpensive I eat from here daily not only do the sell breakfast and desert but they sell some of the best soups that I have ever tasted and a plethora of quesadillas, tacos,burritos and salads  not to mention amazing fish tacos for a small place such as fresco tortilla they have a very large menu and very good service. I love fresco tortilla it is the best place to eat in the hyatt

I had a ring that had a stone fall out and took it in on a Saturday afternoon to have it fixed. Service was great and I felt very comfortable. My ring is as good as new and services were priced very reasonably ($10 for sizing and about $30 for ring repair/cleaning). I would and will recommend this store for purchasing and repairs- staff is friendly and seems to really know the business!
This place stinks, overrated, I got my cheesesteak in 4 seconds, before I got my change. Expensive and the lady refused to give me a cup of water, I'd go back to watch the place get demolished

Good spot for some Middle Eastern fare on South Street.  Its small and cozy, service is relatively fast and menu is well priced.

My only problem there is that there is great variation in quality of food prepared.  I like turkish/arabic coffee, but most times it comes out bland there, while on few other occasions when older cooks were making it, the taste is simply superb.  Same goes for some of the food, falafel last time was overcooked and hard :-(

Still, if you want to lay off the usual pub food of South Street and not spend much, it is a good place in a good spot.

Delicious "yellow sign" Chinese food. Ordered online and food was ready in 15 mins. Ordered two dinner boxes - sesame chicken and boneless ribs. Both with fried rice, one with wonton soup (mediocre) and one with egg drop soup that was delicious. The food is exactly what you expect from a cheap hole in the wall Chinese food place - big portions and a little greasy but overall just what I was hoping for. We'll be coming back here frequently.

We saw Andrew the chef on Goodday Philadelphia and wanted to give it a try. We were not disappointed! Great food and a funky, fun filled atmosphere. All the waitstaff and management were extremely friendly. We met Andrew and told him we would definitely be back. We are from Jersey and come into town every other Friday to try something new. Great place!

I have definitely found my new go to spot!
The environment is A1. A combination between fun and relaxed. It's family owned and you can tell that they are invested in the clientele. 
I was greeted so warmly when I walked in (that's rare in nail salons in the area). My nails came out perfectly. Jennifer is one of the women who specializes in acrylics. She never ran my cuticles, was gentle with the sander so it did not get too hot and made recommendations that fit me to a T. 
The salon itself is beautiful inside and the atmosphere they set encourages the clients to engage and be friendly with one another. 
I've gotten so many compliments on my nails, it's incredible!
Thank you Jennifer and Modish!

I got an acrylic refill and gel polish (mood changing so it gives an ombré effect).

The Amish make everything from scratch. Bought a six pack of sticky buns for a gift. $5.95 for 6 sticky buns, a bargain. Had one sticky buns for breakfast, sugar overdose!!! Ha! It was good though. I gotta give them credit for making everything fresh.

I was here with my 5-year old son and husband a few days ago. It's a clean, well-situated hotel with nice staff. This hotel would be perfect for a single or couple with no children because it's not designed to be kid-friendly. There were no other kids there during our stay. I totally understand if families with children are not the hotel's targeted audience (which is perfectly fine), so you should not be expecting that. I felt the need to write this review however so those families, looking for a Philly hotel, would be fully informed.

To be specific about this hotel not being kid-friendly, there's no kid's meal in the menu, or simple things like kid's robe, step stool, and the walls are pretty thin so you can hear everything happening in the adjacent rooms and in the hallways. The cleaning persons were nice, but I hate to tell you that they don't care how much noise they make in the hallways. They were loud while cleaning all the other rooms. If you have a napping child, forget it. 

For business folks thinking of booking here, please note that the wifi is public or open. No password required, which is almost negligent . You shouldn't be conducting any business that involves personal information in the hotel's "naked" wifi.  (TIP: if you're going to do so, get a software called PrivateWifi. I have no relation to or financial interest in PrivateWifi.)  The so-called business center is a little shelf with two computers; no chairs, so you have to stand while you're on the computer.

This was my first time eating in at this place. I have ordered food to go a few times and it's great. The place itself is a little small but I think it makes for a fun environment. We came here on a Sunday for brunch (well really lunch bc it was about 1pm) and it was a short wait- I don't think it's typical of a Saturday but we were grateful.

The upstairs space is up a flight of stairs that are kind of steep- just be careful coming down those things. It's smallish but cozy. Definitely makes for a louder environment but I wasn't bothered by it.  The service was great- everyone was friendly and quick. We were given free muffins and our choice of a free drink- OJ, mimosa or Bloody Mary. I had the Bloody Mary as I nursing a hangover. There's soo many good food options !!! I opted for the sweet waffle sandwich- fried egg, pork sausage with melted Brie stuffed between two wee waffles. Literally the perfect combination of sweet and savory. Delicious. Reasonably priced as well. Also, it's cash only!!! 

They have a quite on the front their menu that I truly believe they live by as evidenced by their service & attentiveness : "We are in the people business serving food" 

I can't wait to come back and try more fewds!!

as other reviews said food is nothing special.  i'm giving it a 1 just to kill the hype of this place.  again NOTHING SPECIAL and perhaps just a step above olive garden.  it's also cash only when the yelp profile says credit cards accepted.

Very stuffy. Very good coffee. It's nice to sit outside and people watch. The outside chairs could be used as torture devices (uncomfortable is an understatement). If I am paying ten dollars for coffee, can they provide a comfortable place for me to sit and drink it? Seems like a fair deal. Anyway, it's impossible to say the coffee isn't quality. It is.

Cool lounge modern space. Professional and efficient servers. They offer a host of ceviche items that are artfully prepared and fresh along with some their small plate options. The price points are a little high but not out of line with other Starr restaurants. Be advised that they do a very nice hh with great specials.

The atmosphere is great! The servers/front desk cashier people are the absolute sweetest! Like everyone else said, the Turkey panini is delicious. I've had it twice already! I haven't tried their coffee, their Japanese Green Tea Matcha with Soy Milk is delicious. I order it every time :)
They have assorted baked goods that seem to change daily/seasonally depending on the item.
Space wise it is a little small. There aren't many outlets but I'm sure if you asked someone to share they wouldn't mind. The wifi here is pretty solid and fast.
This is my second time here and I am definitely coming here more often!

We have been to Bourbon Blue in the past for happy hour and after today's visit we realized why it's been so long between visits. Service was good and staff was pleasant but the hot garbage they are spinning out of that kitchen leaves a lot to be desired. Today's breakfast consisted of French Toast and their Southern Benedict. French toast was awful and dry and the Southern Benedict tasted like it was a Jimmy Dean microwaveable breakfast. Needless to say an unwanted colon cleanse followed this meal within 30minutes .

This review is for brunch. Really delicious food-well balanced and well executed dishes, cute place. Can't wait to try it for dinner. Had the charred heirloom carrots with house made yogurt, foie gras (so good), egg benedict, and crepe mille feuille with blood orange and mascarpone gelato. Only thing I wished they offered was cappuccino/latte and not just regular coffee to enjoy after a great meal.

They're chicken Caesar salad is so good filled with a lot of chicken. the buffalo chicken Pizza is also good. And they  have specials every day of the week but not for Saturday. But Sunday is deals on pizza and every night is busy up till closing. Temple students and police officers and businesses far and near eat at this location. The parking is not good if you park in the wrong spot they will tow your car or give you a ticket. If order a pizza that cost $7and you come back to your car with a ticket on it  for $35. And you say to yourself damn that was an expensive pizza.

i AM definatley going to say that I never had a bad time eating here. The service was nothing but friendly and the setting was very nice and clean.  I do think for what theyre doing there the food is great as well. Also their desserts are really good as well. Green Tea Creme Brulee? yum.

As one of Philadelphia's oldest bars, McGillin's is a must stop for a quick bite or a fun night out. During my times here in the evenings, it is usually packed with people and plays the top hits music. What you may not know, McGillin's is one of the few places with a fireplace! In the dull winter, the ambiance of old architecture and design will warm you up! 
The menu is very extensive and is very budget friendly. Whenever it snows in Philadelphia, they have drink specials, and if you feel really excited, you can take your saved dollars and splurge on a "Snow Daze" t-shirt! The drinks come out delicious and they have a massive beer selection, and the cocktails are heavily influenced by the season. During the day when I went, it was fairly open around 1 pm, so you shouldn't feel too concerned about getting a table/bar seat. If you're making your rounds through Philadelphia's bars, you shouldn't omit McGillian's!

I rarely write reviews but I have been meaning to do write one about my experience for quite a while now. I have been seeing Shannon now for over a year for monthly facials and she really is the best!  I struggled with acne and acne scars for years, about a year I decided to try facials. By luck I was booked with Shannon and she was just awesome. She is really knowledgeable about the skin and has helped me so much. My skin is looking great. Not only has my acne and scars faded but my overall skin condition is so much better.  

PS I recently started seeing Becca for my brows and she is awesome as well!!!

This store is an absolute disgrace and the manager should be fired immediately. I went in to get a covid test after two days of waiting, and it was completely unclear where they were located. I called ahead of time, and the person who answered was rude and ended up hanging up on me. I then waited in several different lines, first at the pharmacy and then at the front of the store. Then, I see a man with a bunch of boxes yelling "covid tests" near the self checkout, as if he's  Santa Claus throwing presents to people. It isn't clear whether there is a line to get the tests, or if the line is for the self checkout, and also zero clue as to where any of these lines should be formed. Please, for the sake of everyone facing the beginning of a long apocalypse, learn how to run your store properly!! And don't hang up on people, dickwad!!

I'm a Philly native. And this was an excellent experience well worth the $14!! 

Being a native, I paid to see the deck only to take in the views and read whatever information may have been available around venue. But then tour guide Andrew approached and asked if we'd like a tour. Andrew's knowledge and expertise on all hinge Philly made this a 10-star experience. 

Andrew explained the origins of philadelphia, along with the histories of William Penn and Ben Franklin.  He explained how the "squares" of Rittenhouse, Market, Washington... and 3 more which I don't recall were the foundation of building the city. 

Lest you think the Observation Deck is just a boring history lesson (even if it was all history, it's worth it), Andrew also knew what was happening in the city now. He answered questions about new buildings under construction, the use and fictions of newish buildings that are now open, and the upcoming projects in the city that haven't even begin yet. It got me thinking about living in Philly again. 

This tour and deck are well worth the time and money. I'd suggest you get here asap before it becomes popular (there were only maybe 3-4 other people there the whole time we were), prices go up, and it's not fun anymore. 

And ask for Andrew to give you a personal tour!

So, I literally just walked into this place- I have never eaten here before and was super excited! My husband and I walked up to the register and the employee behind the register motioned to help us- My husband noticed one of the cooks baking something behind her and asked "oohh what is that?"  (and okay it was a little loud because of the heavy cooking going on) BUT- there was a microphone in her face and she said something and we couldn't hear her- so she said once more- and lastly again but she screamed at us "BACON STEAK"- LOL i felt like she was going to beat us up- I thought this place was halal? also okay well shove your bacon steak up your you know what... Saad's is where it's at!

I loved this museum.  It's a beautiful little space with an impressive number of Rodin's snuggled in (as mentioned, the largest collection of Rodin outside of France).  It's airy and light, there is plenty of space to sit and ponder the sculptures all around, and a nice little garden out front with the Thinker chillin' out front.  Awesome.

I came to Silk City New Years Day morning, and the wait was about an hour, but it was worth every minute! I ordered the Huevos Rancheros and O-M-G. SO DELICIOUS! I've never mixed my eggs and made them Mexican style with guacamole and sour cream and all that, but it was one of the best breakfasts I have ever had.

My friend ordered a belgian waffle and it came topped with fruits and ice cream, and that looked amazing as well.  

Also our waitress (I don't know her name) was incredibly sweet and friendly!! She called everyone honey and was super attentive. I wish I knew her name because she deserves some credit to my great first time Silk City AND Huevos Rancheros experience!

Castle Roxx is your typical corner causal bar not a total dive but nothing fancy either.  I recently went here on a bar crawl and had no complaints.  One of the people in the group need to go home and fell on his way out the staff brought him some ice which I thought was super nice.  

I use to come here more frequently when I lived in the area, I remember the food being ok bar food.  Probably wouldn't go out of my way to come but if I am in the area I might stop by again.

First I hardly ever give a 5 star review! But this place right here! Soooo when I go to restaurants I expect good service and good food. What is one without the other?!

Well I got both today and trust me it's hard to get that around the way. First off, the website for this place is on point! It's up to date, super easy to use and they even give you a free order after you order 10 times. So I placed my order online, then I called to make sure they got it (I have had bad experiences in the past with online orders). They got it and told me the turnaround time and I was on my way. No attitude, no being on hold, no being told we don't have this or that or we are not taking phone orders. 

Biggest test - will my food be ready when I get there. It wasn't BUT they were busy and didn't wait that long for my food to be served. And they kept checking up on me. Plus after seeing the platter of the person before me oh I was waiting!! Got my food and they apologized for the wait. (Maybe about 10 minutes). Got my 5% Yelp check in discount and met Tosha and I forget the other guy's name but they were cool and professional. I even tried the soul juice. 

Now on to the food. 

Delicious!!!!!! I got the chicken/fish combo with Mac and cheese and collards. Lemme tell you (and don't tell my mom in law) this was some of the cheesiest Mac and cheese I've ever had and the greens were on point, no gritty, not too juicy or too spicy just right. My kids got the honey BBq wing platter and I didn't hear a peep while they ate. I didn't try the soul juice because I don't really do juice but my girls love the strawberries in the drink! 

I highly recommend you give this place a try. The food is "my grandma made this" good  and I'm pleasantly surprised. Hard to find good food "around the way" so I'm telling everyone plus they just gained a regular customer.

mmmmmm. 

I work around the corner from this place so if I'm hungry i'm likely walking here if I'm too lazy to head up to walnut.

Their deli is super well stocked with lots of different options for sandwiches that are absolutely delicious. I had the turkey club on potato bread and it was sooo good. Could have used a little bit more bacon though. 

For breakfast, their options are a little bit more slim, they have breakfast sandwiches wrapped in foil and sitting under a hot light, but that's not how I roll so I can't give a definitive thumbs up or thumbs down on those. 

however! Their bagels remind me of the kind you'd get at any bodega scattered over the five boroughs in ny. Not toasted, pre cream cheesed, and HUGE. Plus they're DELICIOUS! I'm not one for untoasted bagels, but sometimes I just get that craving for one... I have a bagel problem, honestly. 

Their coffee is great and fresh as well.

Go get some.

We went to Keatings for New Year's Eve, at a cost of $95 wasted dollars a person.  2013 was a pretty dismal year of my life, and Keating's NYE dinner really rounded it out.  
Here is the good - the food was decent.  My entree was a little cold by the time it got to me, but that wasn't the worst thing in the world.
What was really bad though was the service, and it had very little to do with the poor young man who was doing his best, and more with whoever did the staffing and decided that one guy could handle 9 tables on NYE.  We didn't get a drink, beyond the initial one we ordered and struggled to get even water throughout the course of the meal.  
There are many more things I could point out and complain about, but I don't really feel the need to hit on the minutiae.  However, if anyone is even thinking about going here next year for a fun, festive and upscale NYE dinner and fireworks, forget about it....don't come here, go somewhere else, as this was one of the worst NYE celebrations thrown by a restaurant that I have ever seen.
Sorry, I love Keatings for outside drinks in the summer, because although pricey, it has one of the best views, but avoid this place for a NYE celebration at all costs...and certainly for a $95 a head dinner...that's money and time I will never ever get back.
Love this location, staff super friendly, smaller menu but the drinks make up for it !

Came here on a Saturday afternoon. Friend and I were walking by when I noticed the cafe/restaurant. We were both craving coffee, so we stopped by. The decor is here is pretty awesome with a relaxed vibe. When we got there at around 2, they were serving brunch still. Since we had just had lunch, we just got iced coffee and a bagel. While the iced coffee was pretty well prepared, the bagel was toasted rock hard (and they ran out of cream cheese). Price was pretty reasonable though. I think if the stars had alined and things had worked out, I would have given them four stars, but the bagel episode was not unnoticed. They do have real food also, which others around us ordered and smelled good. I'd give them another chance probably. (I'd add a half-star if I could.)

Went for brunch last weekend and the service was atrocious. We were told we couldn't substitute egg whites for regular eggs on one order. The waitress just said no. Didn't ask the chef or anything, just no. My wife ordered the vegetarian gyro, but it came to our table with meat on it, so we sent it back. The waitress apologized a few times, but it took about 30 minutes for her gyro to come back without meat on it. By the time her food came, the other three of us at the table had finished eating, which made it awkward. We also had to ask the waitress multiple times for more water before she brought it. It wasn't particularly busy in the restaurant. The waitress halfheartedly apologized a few times for the service, but didn't actually do anything to fix it. Just bad service.

Nom nom nom! I love this place and it's great that it's right around the corner from my apartment. I've come here for brunch many a time and can't really say that they disappoint. The only downside is the small space that they have. I know they have a backyard patio which isn't a bad addition to their space but it would be a lot nicer to have other spaces to sit. I've heard a big hub bub about their sweet potato fries but I must say that I was very underwhelmed...the fries were soggy and seemed like they were a day old. I will say that their sweet potato hash was really good and the sandwich I've gotten which has maple bacon and avocados were also very appetizing. Coffee is good but what kind of coffee isn't good besides the overly burnt ones I suppose! Overall, it's a good brunch place if you don't mind waiting and get any of their egg dishes or sweet potato hash. Maybe I'll give the sweet potato fries another time but for the time being, it was a bit disappointing.

I work down the street from Honest Toms. This place is legit. I can honestly say I have never have a bad meal from this place. We get burritos from HTs once a week.  I have had every different combination of ingredients in the burritos. Meat, no meat. No cheese or cheese. It is never bad. Add onto the fact they are happy and polite and always nice is the icing on the cake.  The sause is the best. Yum!!!

"You should check out Fiume."

That's advice I'd been given for the last five or so years. Somehow, I never made it out 'til a couple weeks ago. That's because my friends haven't learned that, when something as cool as Fiume exists, they should probably threaten me with knives and junkyard dogs or something until I end up there and thank them for their foresight.

For real. How have I missed this place?

Important things: it's small. It's cash only. It's not super cheap.

Even more important things: worth every penny, amazing selection, and a vibe that reminds me of drinking in someone's grandma's upstairs living room. Kinda like Tattooed Mom.

For real, though. If you want to spend forty bucks on beers you can't find pretty much anywhere outside of a specialty store (where, really, they're not much cheaper), come to Fiume and partake.

Their whiskey selection is also pretty top notch, and they have a whiskey of the week special for $7.

So, yeah, this has been my twice a week thing for the last couple weeks.

Bring yourself, your crush, and maybe your grandma to create a scene that's cooler than anything you've ever done in high school.

Soooo... I've used Yelp for about 5 years without utilizing the review feature. Just preying on others reviewing spots and making my own private reviews. Well today is the day I break the seal and say THIS PLACE IS GOD DAM AMAZING. 5

I've been going to SKH for many years and it still remains one of my favorite places. It's not fancy by any means, but don't let that deter you because the food is amazing. The chicken kabobs are probably the best I've ever had! I pretty much get it everytime I go. The chabli kabob is also amazing. The meat in both of these dishes is tender and loaded with flavor. However, I would skip on the beef and lamb kabobs because the meat is a little too chewy and they don't taste as good in comparison. Also don't miss out on the bulani, burani and hummus appetizers, which are also incredibly delicious. The hummus is also super tasty and goes great with the Afghani bread they give you. If you go during the day, ask if you can have the cucumber salad instead, it's made fresh and the yogurt sauce is lovely. The chicken chalaw is also really good, but I'm not a huge fan of the tomato-based sauce. The prices are really reasonable and the portion sizes are good. The Afghani decor and wall color give it a nice ethnic vibe. This place is really a hidden gem and I highly recommend it!

Yelpers for this one right.  It was quiet for lunch at 12 in old city Philly. Possibly  being off the beaten path. Fried calimari was light and tasty.  The cold veggies with bread was a new and interesting change from butter.
